name: Enjoy The Mountain
categories: Motorcycle Rental, Tours, Hiking, Mountain Biking, ATV Rentals/Tours, RV Rental, Hotels & Travel, Arts & Entertainment, Active Life

yelp_reviews.text: We set up an online appt, meeting and transaction was very simple and painless. We opted for a 2hr side by side ride where we had a four-seater with our guide Matt (who by the way was super awesome and informative).  Throughly enjoyed our ride with some superb views.

This was my first ATV ride ever and it was amazing! Nick was the best tour guide we could ask for. He was patience and super informative. He really made the tour and the experience great! I would definitely recommend this for anyone looking for a unique experience.

This is the place to go if you want to experience the Santa Barbara mountain trails and excellent views via ATV! Our guide; Jorge, was very respectful, fun, and ensured our safety was of the utmost importance. We didn't have much experience with ATV's so the confidence of having him around was an excellent feeling.

As for the ride, Jorge leads the pack and even takes pictures during the ride. When there is a reason to proceed with caution, he indicates it to have us slow down accordingly. I could go on but for the sake of space and readers sanity, I'll finish this with simply stating that if you want to rent ATV's this is the place to do it. You'll encounter extremely helpful people who have your best interest in mind. Great company I hope they're around for a long time to come!

Had a wonderful time with Nick, our guide! He was super friendly and knowledgeable about the mountain and can put any fears you have to rest. After walking us through the basics, he made sure we were comfortable on the ATV before pressing on. 

Since we were a small group, we ended up going to the top of the mountain during our hour-long ride.  The previous group had gotten back a little later, but Nick made sure we made up the time and since we rode the double, he made sure that we both had a chance to drive and that we all felt safe and comfortable while enjoying ourselves. 

Would DEFINITELY recommend this to anyone looking for an adventure off the beaten path! Only hiccup would be finding the meeting point (it's up in the mountains, not in downtown SB) and since we didn't have cell phone service there, it was hard to figure out if we were in the right spot, or if we had missed the group (we were a few mins late..)

Nick really took care of us, my boy and I, on the trails. It was our first time scaling the Mtn side..scary but he definitely made us fee safe and assuring we were always behind him. Thanks as well for capturing some pics for keeps. Hang tough and take care Nick! We LOVE nature and crazy fun experience..

Great experience. Nick, our guide, helped us find the meeting spot and was very accomodative since we were running a little late. Nick thoroughly expained to us how to operate the ATVs properly and all of the safety measures.

The views were awesome, overall great experience. Nick took some great pictures and sent them to us right away. Highly recommend and Nick was a great guide.

I'm so happy that we decided to go atving during a last minute getaway for the weekend to Santa Barbara!! Nick was our guide and he was so wonderful.. Highly recommend booking this tour. We opted for the hour session which was perfect for us. Make sure to fill out the waiver before you get there and print out a copy. Connection up there is very iffy. 

I was really scared riding but Nick was very patient. I did not feel rushed during the tour at all. He let me go at my pace (even though it's a snails pace) lol. He would stop and take pictures for us and let us stop at the scenic routes. I really appreciated that he could tell my husband wanted to go faster than the pace I was going and took him up the mountain for an additional 10 mins. 

I'd highly recommend doing this tour if you're looking for something fun to do in santa Barbara.

We had an amazing tour with Nick! He was super friendly and knowledgeable about the mountains and wildlife. This dude breathes the mountain! We loved the tour. He took some awesome photos for us. Very warm and personable tour guide, we would definitely recommend this tour to anyone. As long as you're not a bonehead, safety is not a concern. ATV and provided equipment was all up to par. Big thanks to Nick again for an amazing tour. 

Wildlife spotted: Lizard (Nick tried to catch it), Baby Squirrel, and Deer (about six feet from us, very cool)

My wife and I did the ATV tour in the Santa Barbara Mountains.  It was fantastic!  The ATVs were clean and well maintained.  The tour was a lot of fun and the scenery was beautiful. Our guide Jorge was great at showings us the different trails and more importantly keeping us safe.

Our guide was Jorge, his directions were just on point for a fun filled adventure. 

Notes.. Make reservations by either calling or booking online, they will give you directions on how to reach the upper oso campground in the Los Padres Natl Forest. Come dressed for an adventure, you will get dirty and thirsty..  bring some Water lesson learned
Best day ever!!! Jorge kept us safe and made sure we had fun! Just a tip: sign your waivers before getting to the mountain. There's no reception up there!

My girlfriend Gia and I went ATV'ing with Nick for two hours at this beautiful Santa Barbara location. The weather near our hotel was a little cloudy, but once we went on the other side of the mountains to Enjoy The Mountain, we had clear skies with spectacular views. I love how it is a private event with one instructor so it a more personable experience. Nick is a great, outgoing guy who is an even better ATV guide with his personable traits. Thanks so much for the experience! Will definitely be back.

AMAZING. wish we could have booked Jorge for the day. Myself and bf rented 1hr atvs each outside Santa Barbara. First day we were to meet there was a mix up of location but Jorge made it up to us by giving us an extra half hour for free. Our first time on atv and he was amazing with us. Especially me. I was terrified. He put me at ease and made sure the entire time I felt comfortable. Giving me the thumbs up as we went along to boost my confidence. The views were outstanding. He stopped along the route and took pics of us. It was a great ending to our trip and will def organize a repeat with Jorge again if we are around that location again. I am seriously recommending to friends and family. Do yourself a favor and go for it. What an experience to have a guide to ourselves and enjoy the views.

Most AMAZING adventure! My boyfriend and I did the 2 hour tour each on single quads with Travis and it exceeded our expectations! Travis was AWESOME, would not have been the experience that it was without him. The views are unbelievable and Travis knows all the great spots to stop and take pictures. We are definitely recommending to all of our friends!

It really is as good as it gets. Due to the Santa Barbara fire, our appointment had to be cancelled. It was a bummer because we made shirts and everything and wanted to surprise our bachelorette with it. BUT Jorge provided another option and had Nick pick us up, drive us 2 hours away to their Santa Clarita location and drive us back in time for our spa appointments. This was scheduled the night before and Nick came promptly at 7am the next day. Talk about being there for your clients. These guys went above and beyond to make sure our experience was great even though the roadblocks were out of their control.

We had a fantastic time and even though Nick and Jeff (our tour guide) seemed a little nervous about driving with a bunch of Asian women, we finished the tour unscathed and happy campers.

These guys are legit and really care about their business. So glad we got to know them. Gotta give more props to Nick though. He played all the jams we knew and loved in the four hours we were in the car with him. It was amazing.

Enjoyed an hour long trip through the Los Padres Mountains with my mother. We each had our own 4 wheeler/ATV. All the equipment was in great shape. Our guide, Jorge, was amazing, patient and knowledgeable. Sylvie was also very helpful in getting us coordinated and started with our gear. The trail was gorgeous. There were incredible views from the top of Los Padres Mountains. Jorge knew the best places to stop to take scenic photos of us and later emailed them to us. We both felt completely safe & enjoyed the experience. I can't wait to return for more!!!

We went today for a 1 hour ATV through the mountains with Tim as our guide and it was the best!!! We went up the trails of the Santa Ynez Mountains and the ride was not easy but also not crazy challenging for all you first time riders. No need to be afraid, the guides go at whatever pace you are comfortable with. The ATV's are really easy to operate too. 

Tim was a great guide and took tons of pictures for us and was really engaging and showing us all the beauty in the mountains. It was incredibly serene and just so much untouched gorgeous land in the Los Padres National Forest.

I highly suggest it if you are in Santa Barbara and looking for an adrenaline rush :) It was only 25 minutes to get back to our hotel in downtown.

This adventure was above and beyond, beginning with how great our guide, Nick is. He went the extra mile for us from the get go, he waited very patiently for our arrival since we arrived late, nevertheless we were missing a waiver and he went beyond to solve the issue, he's an amazing guide and person. Thanks Nick, you represent how great your company and adventure is.

Stop everything you're doing and book an ATV Adventure with Nick.  After some confusion with the waiver, these guys found a solution and treated us like humans and not tourists.  Nick was a fantastic guide and allowed us to ride fast enough to really enjoy the adventure, but not too fast where we'd all fall off a cliff.  He was thoughtful, and professional, offered to take pics, and ensured we all had a great time.  I can't speak about any of the other guides, but Nick is a great frontman for the company and my only regret is not booking a longer tour!!

We've been on other ATV tours before and hands down, Nick is one of the best tour guides we've encountered. He's full of energy and super down to earth. We could tell he enjoys what he does and cares about his riders having a great time and truly "enjoying the mountain."

My boyfriend and I are from the east coast, and we've never gone atv driving. We've never even been to California! So this trip was a first time for everything and it was amazing!!! Nick is the sweetest and makes you feel so comfortable during your trip. If your a newbie to atv driving, it'll feel nerve wrecking because you do have to adjust to the machine but you'll learn quick. Overall it was a great experience and I highly recommend it!

If you're in the Santa Barbara area, this ATV tour is a must! Nick was our tour guide. For only being on the job for 2 1/2 mos, Nick really knew his stuff. He was very knowledgeable and patient. He took his time explaining everything to us and made sure we were comfortable the entire time. We highly recommend this tour for anyone looking to have some outdoor fun. As an added bonus, Nick told us to stop by Cold Springs Tavern for their tri-tip sandwiches. They were delicious!

2nd time riding with them (1hr tours) first time in March with Tim and this weekend with friends and we rode with Nick. Both times the guys were great,  very knowledgable of the area and their first priority is safety ! Nick was super welcoming , made sure everyone was comfortable and enjoyed themselves . They are very accommodating to everyone's ridding styles. Clean equipment , nice ATVs . Beautiful scenery.  Sylvia in the office is awesome! Definitely coming back for 2 hour tour . Thanks guys!

I took my boyfriend for his birthday. We did the ATV for an hour. If your a beginner and may be going slow I recommend getting 2 hours but for us the 1 hour was perfect. Arrive early to ensure you get there on time as we were a bit skeptical if we were going the correct way. The service goes in and out so I would recommend you have the directions saved just to be safe. Also, sign the wavier ahead of time bc my boyfriend forgot to and we were sitting there for a bit trying to get service so the info can get submitted to take off. Maybe, they will get some printed out so this wouldn't be a problem and you can get started right away! 

Tim was an amazing tour guide! He was very professional and very helpful through this whole process. He made a couple stops so that we could get a few pictures and enjoy the view, as we were high up. He definitely knew his way around. 

Overall, this was a great experience and I highly recommend it. We want to come back with a group of friends sometime soon!

We had the best time today. We've done several ATV tours at other places (Cabo, Arizona, etc)and this was by far the best. The equipment was high quality, the tour we had was 1 on 1 (rather than being the 10th person behind the guide), the views were unbelievable. It was like something out of LOTR. Highly recommend. Tim was great, knew what he was doing, and left everything open ended. Asked how frequently we wanted to stop, where we wanted to go, took photos, gave some history, and in general showed us a great time!! You won't regret shelling out the money to take this tour. It was an amazing time.

We love the experience with the Enjoy the Mountain team. Jorge, Sylvia and their crew were amazing! We never rode ATVs before so this was quite an adventure. We always felt safe and watched and the hour we spent was simply amazing!

The tour was incredible!!! The ATVs ran very smoothly. The Terrain, View, and Atmosphere was amazing!!! Nick, our fantastic tour guide, was very knowledgable, safe, and professional. His guidance really took our adventure to another level. The whole experience was incredible and totally worth the money. PS Nicks newly renovated office was very inviting and clean... We love what you've done with the place :)))).

I surprised my girlfriend with a 2 hour ATV ride for our one year anniversary. And man, was it a blast! Jorge was our guide and he was professional, patient, and very knowledgeable. I highly recommend Enjoy the Mountain for anyone looking for an adrenaline rush and unique group activity. 

A few things I would like to mention to have a smooth and wonderful experience like we did:

- Sign your waivers online ahead of time
- Verify the exact location of the meetup point and lookup directions before you go up the mountain (you will likely lose service halfway up)
- Dress accordingly. We wore long pants, jackets, and gym shoes. (and bring clothes and shoes to change into after since you will be getting dirty!)
- Keep water in your car so you won't be thirsty going back down the mountain!
- Don't be afraid, these guys know what they're doing!!

Also, if you can, book on a day with good weather and clear skies. The only issue we had was that we had gone on a foggy, rainy day and it was a challenge driving up the mountain to get to the meeting point. We still had a great time regardless of the weather!

We had an unforgettable experience and are looking forward to doing this again soon!

I can't say enough about my adventure with Enjoy The Mountain and our ride with Jorge. First and foremost Jorge is the BEST ever. We had such an amazing ride! I booked this on a whim and this memory will last us forever. Thank you and I will be booking for additional friends to join me!  If  I could give more stars for price and adventure I would!! Thanks for providing everything needed and our photos. Penny

We met with Nick for the tour. He is a great tour guide. He is fun, creative, friendly, and professional. It was our first time and we came out as pros. I did like that we were able to stop at the top to take pictures and listen to nature with breathtaking views. We got the 2 hour tour and it was an incredible experience. We had a blast riding the ATVs. We will be back!
Since we were famished he recommended that we go to Cold Spring Tavern which is on the way back. It was a great recommendation. Tri tip sandwich was exquisite!

I'm so glad I stumbled upon Enjoy The Mountain while planning my birthday weekend.

Nick was our tour guide and he was more than fantastic. He called me few minutes before our appointment just to make sure 1. We were on our way, and 2. We didn't get lost. As soon as we arrived he was very friendly and and ready to get us on our adventure. At first I was worried that having a tour guide would've made it less fun, but honestly, Nick enhanced our experience through some of the stories he told us on our few small breaks. 

This overall experience was super fun! I highly recommend Enjoy The Mountain for anyone who wants a real adventure!

Just a few tips:
1. Sign your waivers ahead of time! The reception up there is spotty, and you'll  waste lots of time waiting to get enough signal to access the waivers online.
2. Take screenshots of confirmation email they send you when you finish the waiver. That way when you arrive, you won't be trying to load your email with the half bar of reception you get.
3. Plan to arrive 30min - 1 hour early. The path there is kind of tricky, and it's very easy to take the wrong turn and waste time. Follow the directions they send to your email. 
4. You WILL get dirty. Take long pants, and old shoes. My boyfriend and I took a change of clothes for afterwards so we wouldn't get back in our car with all the mud and dirt on us. 
5. Have fun!
Amazing trip. Nick was awesome. I recommend asking for him if you are planning to book this trip!  He knew a lot about the area and was great about taking pictures of us and emailing them later.
Very great and unforgettable experience with ATV's, and wonderful and helpful tour guides, Tim & Drew. Very beautiful scenes too.

My boyfriend (Joe) and I had an amazing time in Santa Barbara!! This was a complete surprise to me and it couldn't have been better. Jorge and his team made us feel protected during this Covid time by implementing proper safety measures. Our tour was beautiful and Jorge made sure we felt comfortable and safe during the ride. Additionally, he took some really great pics!! THANK YOU! Totally recommend!
Best birthday experience as it exceeded my expectations. The staff was amazing and I would highly recommend to anyone. What an unforgettable moment in time!

Found this company on Yelp in preparation for a weekend trip up to Santa Barbara. I was a little worried that the people might not show because it seemed like a small website and company, but there were no issues with timing at all. 

Overall, it was a pretty fun experience! Our guide was already there when we arrived, taking another group of people out on the mountain. He was professional and helpful, but it would have been nice to get off the ATVs for a few minutes near the top to get some nice views of the valleys below. He was more than happy to take pictures for us, and he definitely seemed to know his way around the trails in Santa Barbara.

The ATVs that we used were pretty new, and we didn't have any mechanical issues at all. They were a little muddy from the previous group that went out, but it wasn't anything too bad. You have the option of getting a single person ATV, or having 2 riders on 1 ATV. If you really don't want to be in charge of driving, I guess you could book the double occupant ATV, but if you're going to go, you might as well drive on your own right? All their ATVs are automatic, so it's literally just a gas and go type of deal. Super easy to use. If you can ride a tricycle, you can ride an ATV. 

After booking the trip online, I got a lot of information on when and where to meet via email. The company does a good job of giving you that information before your trip. A little on the expensive side if you ask me, but if you're looking for a fun little activity to do in the area, I would recommend these guys!

My husband and I had a great time. The adventure was eye opening and beautiful. Silvia was the perfect guide, she was fun and very professional. She made us feel very comfortable being it was are first time. We will definitely enjoy the mountain very soon thanks!!

My two brothers and I spent the long Thanksgiving weekend in Solvang and decided to end our trip with an ATV tour in the Santa Barbara mountains before heading back to L.A. Best decision ever! Although getting to the meeting point was a bit scary as we drove 15 mph through winding roads next to steep cliffs, the views were nice and the ATV experience was totally worth it! We gave ourselves plenty of time to get lost and it took us about 1.5 hours to get to the checkpoint. Luckily Google Maps was on the entire time and we even had an hour to kill on top of the mountain before our 12:30pm appointment. (Also had screenshots of the directions in case we lost service).

Upon arrival, we met with the two girls who were wearing face masks and face shields so we felt safe. They had all the sanitizing stuff. They checked us all in, gave us each a mesh covering for our head, pairs of gloves, goggles and helmets. They offered different insurance plans (just like a rental car company), each ranging in price. Then, they walked us over to the ATVs where Jorge was our tour guide. He asked us if we've ever rode before and taught us how to use the automatic ATVs and made us feel comfortable and safe. (Throttle was on the right handle bar and brakes on the left. Second brake was a foot pedal). We drove on wide and narrow trails, over some fun rocky areas, kicking up lots of dust, and he took photos of us the entire time. This was a great family bonding experience and we're all still talking about how fun it was and want to do it again. 

Know Before You Go: It gets dusty so wear clothes and closed toe shoes that you're ok getting dirty. Give yourself plenty of time to drive to the location because the last thing you want to do is rush through the scary mountain roads.

10/10 would definitely do this again!

Amazing experience in Santa Barbara! Jorge was awesome...we couldn't have asked for a better guide! Being my first time riding an ATV, I was a bit nervous riding up the mountain...but Jorge's calm and professional demeanor put me at ease. 
I would definitely do it again! 

Tips: You will get dirty...it is highly recommended to wear pants and a long sleeved shirt. Also, if you are afraid of heights or get motion sickness this is not the place for you. If you will be riding for long take a backpack for water etc. The location is a bit tough to find so make sure to allow yourself enough time for travel. Happy trails! :-)

I booked the one hour quad session for my boyfriend and I last weekend. While riding the quads was a good time, the customer service once we got there was one of the worst experiences of my life. 

To be clear, I paid for this months before our appointment.. Within those few months, I lost my wallet. When we got to the meeting point, they wanted my same card number for incidentals, to which I told them I had a new debit card because the other one was lost after booking and the session but that it was for the same bank account. The manager was so rude to us, while in turn calling me a liar about losing my wallet and saying "well then I'm trusting you that this debit card will actually work" that we were tempted to leave before even riding the quads. It made the entire experience very uncomfortable. 

The ride was fun, but we will not be booking again with them.

You have to do this! You will have the most amazingly fun time!  Book your appointment now as they are sold out months in advance and once you ride with them you will understand why. 

Jorge was amazing and his confidence in me allowed me to ride like a pro! This is truly an exclusive event, as if it is just your party and your guide.

The views were stunning and it was delightful to get into the back side of the los Padres national forest.

Riding straight above Montecito gave you a birdseye view into the neighborhoods and out to the ocean. 

Be sure and plan on staying for the rest of the day as there are great hiking trails and picnic spots with unparalleled views.

We are so blessed to live in such a beautiful part of the country. Thank you Jorge for guiding us and making the experience over the top FUN and exhilarating. 

Can't wait till next time!

Had a great time quad biking in Santa Barbara this morning. We were lucky as there was only myself and my friend booked so it felt like we had our own private trip with our awesome guide.

The views were spectacular and direction about how to use the quad bikes was really straight forward. It was also great to feel so safe about our ability. I definitely didn't feel pressured to do anything I didn't feel comfortable doing.

If you're in Santa Barbara, I'd definitely recommend giving it a go. I'd do it again!

WARNING 

We saw all the awesome reviews from this place and thought to give it a shot. My wife has been wanting to go ATV'ing awhile, we were staying in Santa Barbara for the weekend so the timing was perfect.

We live almost 2 1/2 hours away from the location (Santa Barbara)but gave ourselves a 40 minute cushion before our reservation. We past through 2 counties and caught anADDITIONAL hour of traffic on the way and completely missed our reservation.

Now before your reservation you sign a agreement  with  a reschedule disclosure attached. We didn't make the appointment, didn't reschedule and we were forced to forfeit our prepaid $250 for two riders.

This is an online business so you won't be able to talk with a single soul. I reach out via email, and was surprised how quickly they responded in NOT caring about helping me with my issue.

Bottom line; no they didn't do anything wrong by not helping, but they could care less about having satisfied customers or anything outside of collecting our money. So if you're planning on going with this company then please be on time cause you'll not be shown any leniency, or to cough up an additional 40% of your initial cost PER PERSON  just to reschedule.

Had a great Time.  Showed up early and they were ready for us.  All the paperwork and waivers are done online...so no fuss there.  The guide was Jorge...(owner I am guessing from reading all these other reviews?.  He was a cool dude.  Gave us some simple instructions and away we went.  We did the one hour ride.  My self and two boys.  The terrain was not easy nor was it over our heads....perfect for the 3 of us.  We had a great time!

It is a nice experience because you get a nice view during the ATV ride, but in 1 hour it feels like time flies. The views don't last too long because then you have to get back to the starting point due to renting out hourly periods. The worst part for me was the crazy drive just to get to the Meet Location. I recommend you get your own ATV's and plan your own trips. $230 for two people isn't that bad tho I have to admit.

We really enjoyed our first time riding. It was a great social distance friendly activity. They had Lysol spray, wipes, sanitizer and disposal face/head coverings to wear under the helmet. Really like how the waivers are done online and getting them done beforehand made check in very easy. 

You definitely need to plan your travel time getting to the meet up location. It's a mountain and the road is narrow so you have to plan for driving slower going up. We stayed in Solvang and it took us a little over an hour to get there. 

We did the beginners ride for 2 hours and it was very fun. Our guide, Jorge, was excellent. He gave us clear directions on how to operate the ATVs and took us up the trail and helped us when we got to narrow paths. He was very patient with us and took tons of great pictures of us. 

It was a super fun way to spend the morning. Great activity to do with older teens/young adults.

You can't call these guys. I was trying to book a group for Santa Barbara, but the website didn't show it as an option at all. When I Facebook messengered them, they just said there are no reservations at this time. I never put in any date and it didn't show up as an option, so all I wanted to know is if at the SB location they even do group tours. I could not get a straight answer from whoever was replying. GET A TELEPHONE, PLEASE!! LOL So frustrating! I will try somewhere else. Terrible customer service.

We went today for my birthday with Nick.
The tour was amazing!!!
Nick was a very professional, friendly and fun guide. We took our 8 year old daughter with us and she rode by herself! Nick made us feel very comfortable about her safety.
He also showed us a snake and fossils on the way up, which made our trip even more special and fun:)
Thank you so much, Nick, for an unforgettable birthday tour!!!!!!!!!

This easter instead of going to Lopez Lake we went to Los Padres National Forest which is really pretty but if you go to white rock the staff needs to cut down all the dead grass that invades the rock and the water. I would wish the staff would also clean or get rid of the dead grass that's also living by the water. The water is really green and dirty. Los padres national forest staff need to work hard to maintain this forest looking pretty and not like a dumpster. Even Cachuma lake looks like they put a bit more effort.

I work with a college group that does camping retreats up in this area and has been doing it for the last ten years or so. It's a beautiful place with some great hikes, access to the water, and far enough away from Santa Barbara where you really see those beautiful stars but close enough where it doesn't take forever to get there! Pretty good deal compared to camping in some other areas too!

My SO and I love exploring new trails and so it was no surprise when he suggested we try Los Padres NF. Once we arrive and got passed the gate, we were detoured by the signs of a previous campsite (I think they should just remove the signs to stop the extra stops). Once we got on the trail, it was angelic. The trail follows a stream of ponds or swimming holes. We were surprised at the number of people taking the break from the summer heat. But that's not why we came, so on we went. As we walked we found signs along the trails of balancing rocks - very zen-like and very comforting knowing you're on the right path. The terrain varies and every turn is breadth taking. Definitely going back.

I can't believe there's only one review for this gem of a national park.  Does no one else venture into the range for some of the best adventuring in sunny Southern California?  Are we too taken with ourselves in vanity and trendy fashions to go outside for some fresh air and traipsing amongst that which is the Santa Ynez Mountains?  No, this cannot be.  Not if memory serves me correctly.

My dearest friends, in just a little under two weeks from hence, I shall be back in good ol' Santa Barbara to take care of some business; and during my visit, I shall defy these preposterous tendencies and galavant all over this beloved range once more.

There's something to be said for getting to live in such a great state as ours.  Do you take the time to go out and discover these wonders we call a part of home?  Truly blessed are we to have such amazing majestics in our own back yards.  Hiking and off-roading along the foothills and peaks of the Santa Ynez Mountains were things that we geology students did nearly every weekend - rain or shine.  I've missed this range, with its knifebacks and snakebacks, sheer drops down to the bases of peaks so high you can see the Catalina Islands from the tops, and the reddest sandstone formations you may ever see.  In summer months, you'll see parachuters vaulting from the tops of these jagged peaks to float aimlessly down to the sandy white beaches of the town every weekend.

This park is especially special as it stretches from the sandy beaches of SB, all the way to I-5.  I won't be going that route this time, but Route 33 takes you directly to the back door step of the Hungry Valley State Vehicular Recreation Area (see my review for that park).

A bit of trivia for you, lovies.  The Santa Ynez range is the only mountain range in California that goes East-West; all others traverse North-South.  This uniqueness is due to the famous San Andreas Fault.  Santa Barbara is the exact location of the kink in the fault where the North American plate and the Pacific plate continuously bump against each other, causing this phenomenom to occur.  Crazy, right?  I love unique things.

Next time you're in Santa Barbara, take a day hike amongst the high peaks, and maybe even try parachuting off the top of one on a warm sunny weekend.  The views you get really can't be beat.  I'll wait at the bottom with the car.  Then we can go get some chowder on the pier afters.
I love this area for hiking! I go there all the time with my family. The only thing I don't like it is paying the $5. It is a public forest and yet they charge you! :(

All over Ojai there are maps that can be purchased that detail hikes in Los Padres National Forest.  The Boyfriend and I are not athletic people, so we looked for a hike that was both rated easy and was short.  We drove into the park and bought are day pass.  While there, we were informed of a couple of events going on at the ranger station.  After checking their website, it looks like they have events every weekend.  The staff was very friendly and helpful.  

We then headed to the trail-head of the hike, which was at a campground.  Our hike was both easy and short.  And included several small waterfall-type parts, climaxing with an amazing waterfall at the end of the hike. Locals told us that this is often where calendars are shot. 
 
The Park itself has lots of camping facilities and a small like in which people were fishing a boating.  We were only in a small part of it, so I am sure there is a lot more to discover.

Los Padres National Forest is a beautiful forest to come to, nestled in Central California with scenic views. When my husband, our little girl dog and I visited this national forest last month, we saw patches of poppies and other beautiful flowers all throughout our drive and brief walk to take family photos. 

There was no admission price to pay when we came, but have read from other Yelp members' reviews that you may need a forest adventure pass. But there was no one that was selling the passes on site, nor did we see signs that indicated that we needed to have one when entering the forest (we also did not hike or engage in any type of activities when we were in the forest so I am not sure if we needed it). I may be wrong but I just thought I would mention this information. 

Anyways, it was a nice day when we arrived. Unfortunately the visitor center (where there are restrooms but did not see any where else where there are restrooms so be sure to use the restroom before coming here) was closed and one of the entrances was closed as well (which is probably why we were not charged to come in here or were required to purchase the adventure pass). 

There did not appear to be a lot of shade so be sure to wear a hat and bring water to stay hydrated if you plan on hiking here. 

If you want to see Mother Nature in action, this is the place to check her out in all of her glory if you happen to be in this part of California.

The entrance by Los Olivos is where we went -- FIGUEROA CAMPSITE-- great place to stay if you're tasting and camping! Easy to find just go straight as you leave the town into the mountains. 

Get your $5 adventure pass at the local Los Olivos Grocery. Camping is just past the fire station-- stay on the paved road. 

Way better deal than Angeles national forrest.

The Los Padres National Forrest is just another one of the wonderful examples of the diverse costal mountains that we have access to as Californians. Although I am in no ways an expert on the Los Padres, what I have seen has convinced me that I want to go back. However, I have spent a good amount of time in the Santa Ynez National Recreation Area, a special area of the Santa Ynez river on the western boundary of the Los Padres forrest.

I camped at the Los Prietos campsite, one of the many campsites that dot the recreation area. Although not one of the premiere campsites I've been to, Los Prietos has 3 very important things: 1) Easy river access, about a 5-10 min walk (depending on where your site is) to the White Rock day use area, 2) flush toilets (although smelly ones), and 3) shade provided by big, sweeping oak trees. However, there is very limited privacy - those big oak trees are great at providing shade, but not ground cover. Also, the campsite has a reputation of attracting the type of crowd that doesn't believe in camp etiquette, but I think that purely depends on your luck of the draw. The river does attract a TON of UCSB kids when it's flowing well - we'll leave it at that. Personally, I didn't have a problem. If you can, snag the campsites #15, 16, 6, 11, 17, 19, 20, and 22.

The actual Santa Ynez river was by far the best part of my stay, especially after we found one of the many prime swimming holes. Just remember, it's definitely in your best interest to check ahead with the ranger station to determine the water level. If you catch the river at the right time, there are numerous legit swimming holes throughout!

I am definitely on my way back to Los Padres; next trip will be to Ojai and the campsites around there like Wheeler Gorge. If you want to read more about my trip to Los Prietos you can check out my blog at www.tenttalktime.com, or follow the permalink: tenttalktime.com/los-prietos

My husband and I backpack in national forests quite frequently, and our goal is always to leave them cleaner than we found them. Most recently, we went on a car camping trip with friends. These are the same friends we car camped with near Mommoth, Sequoias, and most recently the Los Padres National Forest. 

We were quite disappointed by the amount of trash we saw everywhere on this trip. The very first night it was just my husband and I and we camped in the lower Los Padres. Just as we were setting up our tent, we look down to see a pile of human feces. Upon looking around, we saw other areas of used toilet paper. We figured it was because it was so close to the road.

However, the next day we met up with our friends and camped closer to the Big Sur area, still in the Los Padres N.F. Again, it was impossible to find a camp area out of sight of open piles of feces and mounds of used toilet paper. Very disappointing. Also, gas on U.S. 1 sold for $6.50 gallon and ice was $4 a bag. So be prepared. I'm not blaming the forest rangers, but we certainly saw plenty of them. They seemed to be oblivious to the sanitation problem, unfortunately.

WOW!! So beautiful!! But watch the heat and BRING PLENTY OF WATER!!!

The Sespe Creek Trail's water is very low until you get about 8 or 9 miles in.  Even then, use a filtration pump just in case!!

Night hikes are great because you avoid the heat and get to wake up to this amazing view of the mountains, the valleys, the creek, the rocks, etc.

Adventurers, GO WILD!!

Provided there are no fires nearby, this is always a fun place for a hike.

Long hikes or short hikes, this forest has them all. Be sure to research your hike (or other activity) beforehand for safety reasons, to ensure you know where you're going, as cell reception is spotty at best. You also want to make sure it's at your preferred skill level.

Great place to explore nature and get some exercise. Excellent way to get kids away from the city and electronic devices for a while. It's the type of vividly beautiful environment that inspires deeper admiration for our planet and all she has to offer, with rich forests and mountain views all around. Depending on where you are, you may even get some views of the ocean from up high.

I admit, it's been awhile, but this is THEE place to visit. Here in So Cal we are so blessed to have so many natural wonders within a couple hours drive; beaches, mountains, Vegas, lakes, rivers, parks, walking trails, many big shopping centers. But Las Padres is a gorgeous place, if you drive up over the mountain guess what you'll see?  The ocean!!  You can purchase Pistachios!!!  Good one!!!  Olives. Oils. Wine!  Or, you can remain within the Park, be sure to check out PMC (Pine Mt. Club) and see all that this areas nature has to offer. Some high peeks for star gazing!  Lots of wildlife. If you're into off roading, like me, you can find MANY surprises!!  Find the tree canopies!!

name: Santa Barbara Surf School
categories: Surf Schools, Education, Paddleboarding, Beach Equipment Rentals, Specialty Schools, Active Life, Surfing

yelp_reviews.text: Kaitlyn was a great instructor!  I was there at the end of July and it was a very baby wave kind of day with lots of beginners so I was nervous it wouldn't even be big enough to get anything. But it ended up being a super fun day and she had such a great attitude the whole time. Highly recommend!

:) :) It was my  first time surfing AND my first time in the ocean !!! Everyone was so helpful! I was surfing in no time. Cute boys teaching me how to surf ?? YES PLEASE !!! I recommend this school over all the others. I cannot wait to go back!! I already miss warm weather, beach, waves, and good people soooo much!! Why do I live in CHICAGO ?!?!

Went today to learn with Greg and Heather. It was a group of four so there was a good amount of one on one time. We had about ten minutes of on the beach instruction then hit the water. I was able to pick it up quick so after a bit Greg let me do my own thing and threw in some pointers as needed. At first I was worried that the 90 minutes in the water was going to be on the short side, but it felt like just enough after the lesson was over. 

Price was $65 each, which is more than reasonable considering the board and wetsuit rental would be about 2/3rds of that somewhere else. Also, we were able to call Greg around 915 to set up a lesson for 1100 which was super convenient. Overall a great experience!

I had a great experience with Owner/Instructor Greg Lewis. It's a small outfit, but he provides all the gear you'll need, a pre-beach lesson, and a gaurantee you'll stand up and actually ride a wave. 

Before my lesson, I warned him that putting me in the ocean was like trying to bathe a cat, but that I really wanted to take a lesson...and asked him if he was honestly up to it. He said, "No Problem!" And kind of chuckled. During the lesson Greg was very present, and very patient. 

In a nutshell: If I can do it, you can do it.

We booked a private lesson on 4/13/17 for my three kids (ages 17, 16 & 11).  Communication was excellent and immediate for location, directions & time to arrive. When we arrived the instructor Sean was already there and got them in their wetsuits. They had instruction time at the beginning out of the water and then they went in. This was the kids 2nd time to surf (last time was Hawaii) and it was awesome! Each of them was able to get up multiple times and Sean was constantly assisting, helping them improve. An hour & half lesson was perfect for these beginners. The kids are already asking when we can go back! We will definitely use this company again. Sean was fantastic!
My kids had a great day with Jeremy.   He had them both surfing right away.  The beach at Santa Clause Lane was perfect.    Thanks, Jeremy!!

My eight year-old son and I took a lesson with Greg and he made us both feel so comfortable in the water. He broke down the steps and voila, we both stood up and caught waves our first lesson. We had one of the best afternoons we've had in a long time. My son said I looked good, "for a mom." Thanks Greg!

I found Santa Barbara Surf School in a Google search and after viewing the website I emailed about my first surfing lesson (at 50) and my just turned 9 year old daughter doing a semi-private lesson.  Greg promptly got back to me and his attitude and outlook sold me immediately.  I really wasn't nervous about it at all.  They provide all the gear and first do a lesson on the sand so you know how to get on the board in the water.  Matt was our instructor and Greg had another family.  We had a great time, my 9 year managed the maneuvers based on the clear instruction and got up time and time again!  She LOVED it and although her anticipation was to be living "Teen Beach Movie" she really came close :)  We had a great day and they use a nice beach that
is manageable with other surfers.

Highly recommend the experience.

My son and I surf all throughout OC and San Diego and we are looking for some new spots to check out this summer.  Instead of calling a local surf shop I thought I would call the guys who help people ride waves for a living.  Thank you to Mitch for helping me  with a few spots to check out in the Santa Barbara area for the summer of 2018
Awesome company and very awesome instructor, Kaitlyn G.  I treated my son and his daughter (7 years old) to surf lessons Father's Day 2017. Fantastic experience and Kaitlyn rocks !

John was my instructor and I had an awesome time today. I'm new to surfing and had been out once with an experienced buddy before and several times on my own. Those times, I never got up on my feet and felt wobbly in the water. After about 30 min of instruction today, I was on my feet and riding waves. It was an awesome experience and I would highly recommend this surf school to everyone.

I really wanted to learn to stand up and Ally got me up my first try! I got a good foundation to keep practicing, which is what I wanted. It was super easy to book last minute, there was good communication between booking and the lesson to make sure I came to the right place prepared, prices were good for the area, and the 1.5 hr ween by in a flash! 
Definitely recommend!
Nick took my 13 year old nephew out to surf for his first time ever...and he was so great that we're going back tomorrow.  My nephew said that Nick was "savage" and the "lesson" was lit.

We couldn't have asked for a better first surf experience for our 9 year old twins. Carson and Ally were both amazing, fun, knowledgeable and focused on safety. You can tell they love surfing and love their job! They had them standing up right away and they caught so many waves. We live in the Bay Area but will definitely be back for lessons and/or surf camp.
Greg and his guys are great. My wife, my two teenage daughters and I all were riding the waves almost immediately, and spent the rest of our lesson falling in love with surfing. Highly recommended.

Amazing group of people. Had Brody has my instructor and he was awesome. Super nice, helpful, and fun. Got up multiple times during my first lesson and am hooked. Will definitely be renting a board and suit from these guys moving forward.

Nick was my instructor and this is my 3rd lesson with this surfing school. Nick was a great instructor. He encouraged me all the time made me feel comfortable. I was able to be on the wave by myself. Highly recommend!!

If you're interested in picking up surfing or just want an intro to surfing, then this is the perfect place to get those lessons!

My instructor Nick was great! He was so friendly, personable and a great instructor; helping me find all the right changes to improve, feel confident on my board and finally catch a few waves.

I've taken surf lessons elsewhere and by far enjoyed my lessons here much more. Highly recommend it's a blast!

My husband and I chose Santa Barbara Surf School to try out surfing for the first time on a trip to California. Directions to their van were easy to follow. They had all the equipment ready to go when we arrived including wetsuit (necessary for November). Abe made our lesson easy to understand and fun, especially for two novices. He helped get us in the right spot in the ocean to be able to catch the best waves and instructed us on when to stand up. We had a great time and hope to return.
My kids 16 and 9 had a lesson with Daniella today and she was amazing!!!!! The boys had so much fun and stood on the board quite a few times. Highly recommend this surf school!  Thanks daniella!!!

I arranged a surf lesson for 5 relatives who had never been on a surfboard. SB Surf School sent out two instructors and insufficient equipment. Both instructors (only one was competent) initially attempted to "launch" my family members on blue water faces instead of queuing them up in the white water so that the challenge of popping up would be limited. Their oversight resulted in a log jam. At least one relative on soft top board bull dozed another relative in the impact zone after take off. It was a real flea circus. I, with many years of experience, waded out and began launching in the white water with good results. Equipment: the adult boards were all the same size. The adults were not! This was a no-no. One adult in particular would have surely benefited from a longer board with more volume. He was unable to pop up on the one size fits all board provided. I requested a larger board for him, none was available. Long story short, I would not use Santa Barbara Surf School again. These people need to really sharpen their game.

My friend and I spent 2 hours with Randy from SB Surf School & we were pleasantly surprised to find that although never having tried our hand at surfing before, we were both able to catch waves & experience the fun & thrill of surfing for the first time. We found SBSurfSchool to be very helpful, personal as well as occomidating to our schedule in order to help squeeze in a class for us. It was loads of fun & we'd definitely use them again.

name: Dustie Wagens
categories: Tours, Hotels & Travel, Wine Tours, Campgrounds, Car Rental, Active Life, Wedding Planning, Surfing, Event Planning & Services

yelp_reviews.text: Awesome company -- love their vans and Melissa has been so accommodating! She is very professional and always followed up with our group.

So...we haven't rented from them but we met them in a taproom and what an amazing family they are. If their business practice is half of  what their family dynamics are it will be a great experience. They are down to earth great people. Next trip to SB and will be using them for sure to rent a bus.

Who knew you could cruise and camp along the California coastline in a vintage Volkswagen Bus without having the headache of buying and restoring your own!? Dustie made my dreams come true. We took George a fully restored 74 VW Westfalia up to Big Sur and had the time of our lives. Why rent a normal car when you can rent a Dustie!?!? See you again summer 2018!!!!

One of the best weekends I've had in a while was camping with Dustie Wagens.  My friends and I camped in 2 VW buses at Lake Cachuma. Good times by the fire before we retired to sleep comfortably in the buses.  

We also got the chance to make it to the Dustie Wagens kick off party and see the office in downtown Santa Barbara.  Adam and Mel are great and made sure every detail was taken care of.  

I highly recommend this cool company.

Dustie Wagens took us out for a 65th birthday and we had the best time! Adam was an excellent driver and host. Melissa helped us plan our trip before hand and was incredibly flexible and accommodating in helping us select a mix of wineries and breweries. Highly recommend and think this is a great day for adults of any age!

Nostalgia at its finest. I own a Honda Element (which I love), but had another couple joining us on a camping trip to Lake Cachuma. We were debating whether or not to pitch a tent *rim shot*, but came across Dustie Wagens randomly while wandering around Santa Barbara. After a very brief deliberation, we went for it!

After chatting up with the super helpful and friendly owners Adam and Melissa, they showed us to our van and gave us a rundown of all the fun quirks of an early 70's vee dub. I couldn't wait to get my grubby hands on the keys fast enough.  We tossed in our gear and fired her up and I was instantly transported back to my 5 year old self shifting my dad's old squareback while he steered and hit the clutch.  I couldn't wipe the grin off my face as I worked the shifter with those long throws and spun the oversized bus steering wheel. 

Anyhoo, after getting used to punching the gas and working with only 4 gears, the drive up and down the mountain was easy and the VW was surprisingly nimble.  Sorry, I digress, hahhaa on to the details. 

The rear seats easily fold down for about a full size bed that was surprisingly comfortable. The gem of this sucker definitely was the Westfalia upgrade. After a few easy snap buttons and pull latches, we pushed the top up and ta-daaaaaa instant bunk beds!

The lake was beautiful as always, but renting the bus was definitely what will stick around in my grey matter for many years to come. I can't express how much I loved the experience and recommend this anyone who knows how to drive a stick shift, loves classic cars, and just anybody who wants to have a good time. 

I am looking forward to checking out one of their upcoming beer and wine tours. I have nothing more to say than...farfegnugen!

Smoochies!

We wanted a local company to showcase at Fork Fest 2018 & Dustie Wagens was the first one that came to mind, due to their unique pop-up shop & vintage restored '70's era VWs. The Dustie Wagens team was great to work with & brought the Harry, along with some locally designed surfboards and other small items for sale. Lots of attendees of the festival expressed how interested they were in renting the VW & had no idea they could do so - it was a great educational experience for our festival attendees. I would highly recommend the Dustie Wagens team to be invited to other festivals as a unique addition. Thanks for being at Fork Fest 2018 & we look forward to working with you again!!

I surprised my husband with a California Road Trip in a Dustie Wagen for our anniversary. Told him to pack a small bag of casual clothes and that's it, so he had no idea where we were headed until he saw the yellow Bus in the driveway.
We just had three days, so the rule was stay on PCH as much as possible. We opted for hotels instead of actually camping to save setup/breakdown time and be able to cover more territory.
Melissa and Adam were so cool and the bus was such a blast to drive. I learned to drive my Dad's '65, so the 4-speed & clutch felt pretty familiar.
Total flash back to cruise down the coast at an easy pace and feel connected to the ocean and the wind. We didn't even mind getting passed by pretty much everybody because they all either waved or honked hello and snapped pics when they passed.
And it's a proven fact that a bright yellow 70's VW bus makes EVERYONE smile. 
We made friends at every stop and as we charted our course from cafe to restaurant to hotel the days flew by waaaay too quickly.
We were actually a little sad to give back the keys but we made a plan for our next Dustie adventure on the train home from Santa Barbara. 
Next time we will bring our boards and do the beach camping experience and have friends do the same!

name: Milpas Rental
categories: Truck Rental, Automotive, Home Services, Machine & Tool Rental, Contractors, Local Services, Trailer Rental

yelp_reviews.text:
We have rented from Milpas Rental for over 12 years to include major rentals for our vacation home remodel. I rented a carpet cleaner from them today as I could not get a carpeting cleaning company to come out on short notice. Upon using the carpet cleaner I run into a glitch. When returning the item I explained the situation and they were most accommodating.
Thanks again!

Here's the truth about Milpas Rentals, at least what I've seen over the last 30 years of my residency.  They have a ton of tools to rent.  Pretty much, if you need it, they most likely have it.  I've rented from them several times as your basic homeowner and as a contractor.  They have been polite and very fair about their service and rates.  I've found their prices reasonable.  Far cheaper than buying the product!! But that is not what sold me.  Recently I rented a tool and then became ill and was unable to return the tool on time.  They showed me grace and mercy.  They had every legal right to require full payment of the multiple extra days.  They were VERY kind to me and concerned about my well being.  I thank them very much not just for their forgiveness but more importantly for their compassion.  Absolutely I'll rent from them again, and again.

My husband and I have been renting from milpas rental for many years now. If I could give it 6 stars I would. They have anything you can imagine to rent. From scissor lifts to great quality power tools. Their fleet of equipment is always in top shape and never outdated. You not only get amazing customer service every time, but definitely the lowest prices around. Their staff is super knowledgeable and super friendly. When we had our wedding a few years back, we needed extra power for lighting and kitchen equipment, and they were so helpful with leading us towards what size cord we needed and even connectors for all of the lighting.  Keep up the good work Milpas Rental!

My boyfriend and I had a great experience with Milpas Rentals. The equipment (floor sander) we rented performed like it should. The staff was very friendly and on point! I had to return this very heavy machine which I was a little worried about but two strong men came out to my car immediately after pulling up to handle it for me. Thank you guys and Megan! We will be back!

Not cool. I needed to rent a jackhammer for some demo work and was pleased to see they advertised the new Hilti, however the only thing they had available was an older Hitachi. It worked fine but the power cord had a very worn plug that kept falling out of the extension cord socket. I lost way to much time stopping to replug the jackhammer back in. When I went back to to the shop to return the tool I arrived 5 min. before close. The man on duty inside had already locked the doors and did not respond to my knocking. I waited for him to come out at which point he refused to take the jackhammer and told me to come back in the morning. I explained that I live two hours North of Santa Barbara and wouldn't be here in the morning. He still refused to take the jackhammer. Terrible and unprofessional customer service. This was at the Sate Street location.

These guys are the best! Justin, his sister, and his father have been very kind to our community. We so appreciate the help and the support that they have given us. If you have to rent pretty much anything, they have it! And it's nice to know that the people to whom you give your business are giving back to the community! I highly, highly recommend these guys. They are wonderful. They stand by their work and they are nice, friendly, and kind. 
A+++

I have nothing but great things to say about Milpas Rentals. Meghan was taking care of us the entire time. Meghan went above and beyond to accommodate to my situation when parts didn't arrive and it went through the weekend. She didn't charge me any extra fees and was sincere and understanding the entire time. She deserves a raise for that kind of customer service. Thanks again Meghan and Milpas Rental Staff. P.s the drop off and pick up driver was great. Super friendly and polite we enjoyed each other's quick conversation. Milpas rentals for the  W!

Today was my first experience with Milpas Rental, and I have to say, this company has really got it together. It was super easy to rent out and return the equipment. And the people were all very attentive and seemed very knowledgeable. My rental( gas powered pressure washer) worked perfectly for the job I had. Will definitely be going back if I need any other equipment rentals.

Probably my 4th time over many years renting from Milpas Rental, and first time in a while. I needed a 2kW generator early the next day. I took a chance and called after closing at 4:10pm. Megan answered the phone in a chipper tone and I was able to quickly reserve the generator. Pickup at 7:30am the next day was a breeze; drive through, they show you how to operate the equipment while you are in the car, load it and you are off. Returned it the same way and final bill/contract in my inbox. What could be easier? The equipment works perfectly and is well maintained, and the staff is pleasant and friendly. Need to find me something else to rent!

name: Santa Barbara Jet Boats
categories: Boating, Active Life, Local Flavor, Rafting/Kayaking

yelp_reviews.text: I have been coming here for many years - great operation and great people!  I have been to many places across the globe and this operation has the largest area to jet around in and have fun.  For the best fun, go as early in the morning as you can - just like water skiing, when the water is like glass.  Don't forget to bring your cell phone with you and take pictures - great pictures and great screen savers to remember your fun!

This review is for both Sea Landing and Santa Barbara Jet Boats (we checked-in Sea Landing's building, but our jet ski rental had a label for "Santa Barbara Jet Boats"). All the staff members that helped us including the girl that booked our reservation as well as checked us in, the dude who gave us the safety and boundary spiel, and girl who got us our wetsuits, life vests, and launched us into the water were all very nice and chill. Took us like 5-10 minutes to check-in and get prepped before we were on our jet ski and out on the ocean. We paid $135 for 60 minutes of use. We got the 1-3 person Yamaha WaveRunner jet ski that can go up to 55 mph. That thing can really fly. We were only daring enough to take her up to 33 mph max out there, but if you got the guts, you can definitely max her out to fully make your money worth it. Nice people working there and easy process to go jet skiing.

We rented a jet ski there in May.
Friendly staff, reasonable prices with tax included. The necessary equipment is provided. Moreover the suits are included.
My wife and I rented their 19ft Yamaha jet boat. Stewart was friendly and helpful. Boat was basically brand new. The low price included fuel and taxes. Highly recommend.

My sad update....

DG and I drove 30 miles from where we were staying to rent jet skis on the Saturday that I told Stuart we would be coming up.  We didn't have reservations, but he had said that he would be there so it wouldn't be a problem.

I called Stuart as we were leaving, and left a message saying we were on the way.  I was a little concerned when I didn't hear back from him, and it was drizzling slightly when we got close to the area.  But he was so friendly and positive when I talked to him the day before, that I figured it would be fine.

When we got there, two girls were scheduling whale watching boat trips, and were very friendly, but told me that Stuart wasn't there.  They tried to call him on the number I had, as well as his partner, and couldn't reach either of them.

We left and figured we would come back the next day, and that we would get a call back at some point.

Unfortunately, Stuart never called me back.  : (

I'm open to trying him out another time, and raising the stars - but it would have been nice if he would have returned my call - especially after being so friendly on the phone and surely hearing my message that we were heading out to him.

The upside:  we took an AWESOME ultralight flight along the coast from Sky Rider Ultralight in Camarillo instead!!  Woooohooooo!!

When I was planning my vacation to Santa Barbara, I knew I wanted to do something out of the ordinary that I couldn't really do back home in Las Vegas. I searched around and when I saw jet skiing, I knew I had to try it.

All of the equipment that you need is provided by this business. You don't need any experience operating a jet ski; they will explain everything you need to know. All of the workers here were super nice and helpful. If you happen to bring anything with you, they will hold it until your return. One hour on the jet ski cost us $135. They provide a watch for you that can be held in a water safe compartment of the jet ski. They let you go out pretty far, so you can go pretty fast on the ski without worry of going out too far. 

Jet skiing was an awesome experience. This was something that was totally out of my comfort zone, but I'm so glad that I tried. The water was nice and I got to see a couple of seals. If you're looking for something fun to do while in Santa Barbara, I recommend this 100%.

I called and spoke to Stuart today to get quotes on jetski rentals, and he was SUPER friendly!  I can't believe this business isn't even on Yelp yet - it took me SO LONG to even find jetski rentals in the Ventura area!

Located in the Santa Barbara Harbor, in the Sea Landing building, just off the Garden Street exit in Santa Barbara, they rent multiple types of recreational water vehicles:

* 1 to 3 person sit down Sea Doo, Yamaha and Honda watercraft
* Single person stand-up Kawasaki Jet Ski 
* Twin engine, Sea-Doo jet boats (14' models that hold up to 5 people and 18' models that hold up to 7 people)

All rentals include fuel, insurance and wetsuits, plus instructions, so you're good to go even if you've never been on one of these things at all!

Stuart said that it's pretty common to see dolphins and sea lions when you're out on the water, and that an hour is pretty much going to feel like a long time.  

They're open from 9am til sunset daily, so give them a try when you're in the area!  I know I will.......TOMORROW!!  : D
You must rent a jet ski right here! People are extremely sweet...love the way they treated me and how they explained everything...next time I'm in Santa barbara I will stop by

Stopped by this place last Thursday to rent some jet skis. I was originally planning to rent at a location in Marina Del Rey, but I read some horrible things (in addition to learning about a surcharge for 3 people) and decided to try this place in Santa Barbara instead.

I made the right decision.

The experience here was pretty seamless. Walked in (with no reservation), and stated I wanted to rent three jet skis. Signed a form, was made aware of the deposit and charge, and shortly thereafter was acquainted with the gentleman who would be setting us up.

Didn't get his name, but he was friendly as can be. Explained the rules and regulations, provided some advice, and we were off.

It was a fantastic time.

Arrived back, collected our belongings, and headed home. Couldn't have asked for anything more.

Definitely recommended.

Loved it! 
The staff was amazing! My family had so much fun! We rented the jet boat for an hour and had a blast! Definitely recommend!

The general management of this place is more than adequate, however I found the workers get hangry around noon and can be somewhat grumpy. *DO NOT FEED THE SUN BUMS* Overall this is a very functional business and while every move there puts in the effort. It's Alex who really runs the she-bang. She rocks all your favorite suits and puts the boys to shame. It's worth the visit just to see her. Alex is essentially the wheels on the bus . She can raise the roof just as much as she deserves a raise.

We had a blast on the jetboat -- seats 5 people. Great price and awesome staff. We called 2 hours ahead of time to make sure a rental was available. They hold any belongings you don't want to get wet. Definitely would go again!
Just fun. Get the sea doo for two. Otherwise your gonna get very wet.   Try to go when ocean is calm. Service is wonderful.   They accept tips.

OMG!
Ok this is what you need to do when in Santa Barbara!
The price is right, the water is beautiful and you might even see some dolphins. The staff is so nice and helpful. 
Try and get a mid-afternoon reservation. The water is more calm then, but you still might have some of that morning fog. It's burned off by noon or 1pm, but the water can get a little choppy then.
Great for families! They have boats and jet-skis. A must-do!

Pretty cool place. Rented the paddle boards for 2 hours. It cost $20 and is a great way to get some exercise and sun while being out on the water. The staff was helpful and nice and if I rent a stand up paddle board again I'll probably get it from them. 

The only reason they don't get 5 stars is because we originally went to rent the jet skis and the rental prices for the jet skis are absolutely insane. $137 per hour.....

Amazeballs! This is THE thing to do in SB. 

Reservations were made easily and everything was ready to go upon arrival. The staff is super friendly and just wants you to have a great time.

We rented a jet ski each for an hour. The $135/hr rate includes fuel and everything, which I think is very reasonable. The equipment is top notch and well cared for.

I will definitely be back for another adventure on the ocean.

Go for it!
Great experience with a Jet Ski hire! They don't take the deposit off the card and the jet skis were great. Fast and beautiful scenery.

It ok, other take 15mins get out of the harbor and come in 15 mins at 2 miles hour , ask for receipt for a refund for the deposit $500 per jet ski , they tell you see it online with your bank . The jet ski were old but ok . $135 per hour to  rent .  Should bring up my own jet ski . The service was bad .

I'm in Santa Barbara for the weekend and I thought that booking a wave runner would be a great idea. I considered going to Oxnard for the place with more reviews, but the rate here in SB was the same. These guys were the only option on Yelp in SB that I could find, so there aren't many choices available locally. That said, I had an excellent time here and would highly recommend it to anyone who is looking for an adrenaline rush. 

The staff is all friendly and they explain rules, best practices, etc. thoroughly. Once you are debriefed, they send you off into the open ocean. Luckily for us the ocean was calm like a baby in the womb, so we were able to go full throttle as we pleased. We enjoyed every minute of the trip. Don't forget to say hello to the sea lions.. arf arf!

Great experience with my son. We rented one of their small jet boats. Surprisingly fast. Cost was basically the same as 2 jet skis. All of the employees are very friendly. Everything was carefully explained to us.

Came here during 4th of July and they were open. We rented 2 jet skis and everything was smooth and easy to follow. All staff was friendly and helpful. We had a lot of fun and I would recommend to anyone.

We did a Jet Ski rental on one day and Jet boat rental on another.  The only complain, which is why I am giving 1 star less, is that it takes 10-12 min to get out of the harbor where you cannot drive above 4 mph and similar time to get back in. So for a 60 min rental you truly get to drive fast for about 35 min.

It's been 2 years straight of me and husband coming here every August. We love santa Barbara and they have everything you need to enjoy yourself on the water! We rented the jet skis and had a blast. Even saw some dolphins in the water which was a super nice treat! Great customer service as well!

Stu and his crew are top notch. We highly recommend renting the jet boat and or a jet ski. Pricing is reasonable for not having to do anything but listen to a 2 minute safety meeting. Get there when they open to have the best experience. Will definitely rent again from them when we are back in town.

We tried calling to ask about reservations and pricing for a jet ski, no answer and the mailbox is full. We decided to risk the time and drive up to go jet skiing on a Friday morning. Arrived well after they opened but was told we could not rent a jet ski until the owner arrived about 1pm.  We will go elsewhere.  Southern California jet ski , located in the Channel Islands Harbor not only takes reservations but they are priced substantially lower.

We were so exited to do boat sailing. On the day of our reservation a guy called at 9:30am to let us know he won't be there till 12:30pm. Our reservation time was 10:30. Having to delay and change the appointment It pushed our whole day back and we had to cancel another reservation that we had booked for 12:00. Once we got there for our boat there was apparently one guy working the boats. He said we would have to wait an additional 30-45 minutes. Absolutely ridiculous so we patiently waited. Once it was the time he said he is still not ready to wait 15 more minutes. Finally he went over the rules and guidelines but there were so many people no one social distancing and he wanted everyone grouped all together to go over the information. There was like 18-20 people and half with no masks. Finally got on the boat half way though the boat was smoking and wasn't picking up speed. We brought it back took about another 10 minutes got it fixed and back out. 

I think customer service is very important for a business and when there's a complaint and all of a sudden there's no manager available or owner available to talk to it's extremely frustrating. 

I don't think we would come back to rent from this particular boat rental company.

Our boat died way out at sea and our hour was lost. Called the emergency number given and no answer. We were in real trouble. Eventually called the harbor and they relayed a message.  No refund, no discount, no concession what so ever.  Stay away from STU and from this place. Dangerous and unreliable

This is the best thing to do in Santa Barbara. Called and talked to Stuart, he is super friendly and informative. The prices are very reasonably and the jet ski worked great, there's nothing like zooming around the ocean on one of these, very exciting. We even went back the next day and rented a boat, toured the santa barbara coastline and had some food. We made a whole weekend out of it, this place is AWESOME.

You will love it! I would have pictures to post here, but I could not take. 
You need to sign some papers and make them sure you are not gas in the forbidden areas. 
You have to pay 135 bucks which I think is too much! 
Because you have to not gas for 10 min to pass all swimmers and other people and also when you get back! So, you lose 20 min at least!!! 
But anyway I love it

Great support and service from the staff. They were flexible with my kids and their needs, we had a great time riding the beautiful Santa Barbara coast line on their jest skis. We were able to get on a jet ski within 5 minutes of arrival and all of their staff was super friendly and easy to work with. We had  a great afternoon of fun in the sun thanks to this company.
Very strict with their rules, but definitely a nice spot to go jetskiing. Parking is great, most staff friendly.
Won't answer their phone and voicemail is full...have been trying to reach them for days! Highly unprofessional!!!

What are YOU going to remember from your visit to Santa Barbara? 

1.  Stuffing your face at a restaurant?  
2.  Riding a f@#cking jet boat, THEN stuffing your face at a restaurant.

Highly recommended.

Pretty easy renting a Kayak from their shop that's right next to the harbor. We rented 2 kayaks and one of their guys helped us get onto them. Kayaking in the ocean is different than on a lake. The waves definitely make it more exciting. 
Getting out of the kayaks was a little hard. We did not have a phone on us to contact the shop for help. We basically had to get the kayaks to their harbor, tie them and get out. Which was pretty hard.
Professional, efficient service with fun boats. We rented a jet boat for a family of five and had a great time!

This Place is Awesome!!!! We went last May and rented a twin engine 60mph jet boat, and two brand new jet skis (hourly rates everything included). We had a very pleasant short safety class telling us where to go, what to see, and most importantly how to operate the jet skis and boat. The staff set us up with really nice gear, and was extremely helpful and knowledgeable, which in past experiences is rare for any rental business. we then navigated out of the harbor about (5 min) into the open ocean. The machines handled so well and they had a lot of power. Once out in the open ocean, the boundaries were huge. A couple miles wide and a few miles out which is more room than any ski rental facility I have ever been to, and I have been to many! Sightseeing is amazing with dolphins 5 feet from our jet skis and sea lions on every buoy. They also let us have a little extra time to compensate for the navigation in and out of the harbor :). Highly recommended A+++++ we will be back this summer.

Have rented a sea-doo twice and paddle boards once. 

Paddle board experience was really poor, not their fault but they definitely didn't help. Got stung by a sting ray getting off the board at the sandbar area, called them to help out, instead of helping they gave me the harbor patrol phone number rather than either hopping in one of their boats or calling for me. Then when I returned(I had been on board maybe 5 min before being stung) I was not offered a refund, it's only $25 but the gesture would have meant a lot considering how miserable I was at that moment. Just kind of expect more from people who deal with the ocean so much. 

But alas they're fairly priced on jet-skis so I'll probably be back

Omg this was my 1st time & instead of getting the jet ski we got the speed boat Celine the receptionist was awesome very knowledgeable & the rest of the staff was great I don't understand why they don't have 5 stars thank you guys for everything best birthday
Stuart and his team are top notch. Super easy process and great equipment. We will definitely come back again and again. These guys are really great to work with and may the experience awesome!

I have used the service before and the boats are fun. However trying to reach anyone is impossible. You can't make a reservation by calling the Sea Landing , they just give u Stewart's number. Mailbox is always full, Stewart doesn't return calls or texts. Going on over a month with multiple requests made.

RENT HERE! We rented a jet ski here for Labor Day 2018 ($135/hour) and had a great time! The staff was helpful and courteous with the rental paperwork, including upfront disclosure of accident-related costs. The water-safety instructions were simple and clear. They got us out on the water quickly with a few pro-tips to hang out in the water, and how to locate our boundaries. Give it a try!

name: Santa Barbara County Courthouse
categories: Courthouses, Active Life, Event Planning & Services, Landmarks & Historical Buildings, Public Services & Government, Parks, Venues & Event Spaces

yelp_reviews.text: The court house is very old and the art is great. The spiral staircase is funny. One can get view of entire city from 5th level. The tower clock room is really surprising with all mechanical gears inside!! You need a prior appointment to get into clock room. Worth spending 2 to 3 hours of time here.

The courthouse was voted on TripAdvisor as the top attraction in Santa Barbara. Yes, it doesn't sound very interesting, but it's a must visit if you're in town! 

Make sure to make it up to the view and climb the stairs  (resist the urge to take the elevator) to watch  the clock/bell at work. The view, although not super high, is breathtaking and gives you a good look at the beauty of Santa Barbara.
