We saw high schoolers doing some sort of club activity here (maybe MUN?) but that didn't stop us from enjoying the courthouse. You are free to walk he grounds--no intrusive security guards or large tour groups. It didn't feel so touristy/museum-y either which for some reason made me appreciate the experience all the more.

The best part of all--it's free! You won't need to set aside a ton of time here (maybe 30-45 min). There's ample street parking around the courthouse, at least on a weekend so get over here stat!

Most beautiful place to get married, we chose the Mural room and it was great except some people came in were rude. We knew it was open to the public but I expected a little more courtesy from them. Just as I was about to walk down the aisle people were trying to go in and we asked them to wait but they snap that they were allowed to be here. True but let the bride walk lol other tourists sat down and were very excited for us! Linda was a pleasure to email with when reserving the room. The lady at the information desk accused us of stealing a pen, it was returned I found out but she was too busy on the phone during the time and didn't notice it appearing back on her paperwork lol minor things that didn't ruin my perfect day but to be aware of. Wouldn't change the location. And bring a pen to sign marriage license  and remember no a/c or heat
By far the best we experienced in Santa Barbara and it was the only free historic visit. Highly recommend. The view from the tower is beautiful.

This is one of the most beautiful sights to see when visiting Santa Barbara.  The building is amazing......it's so majestic night or day.  The grounds are gorgeous.  

my husband and i got married in the mural room on the 2nd floor.  it is just spectacular.  the hallways all have tile floors and colorful Mexican-inspired tiles on the walls.  It is amazing.  So much detail is put into every nook and cranny.

Visit the Courthouse and hang out....it is worth a special trip!

So far the most amazing court house I have ever seen. Great for weddings, though beware there are tours going on, and it's open to the public. Probably the most beautiful building in Santa Barbara. If you can go to the tower, you can see the ocean, and a beautiful town. Love Santa Barbara.

Read a lot of reviews that mention this is a must see while in Santa Barbara. It certainly was. The architecture and landscaping on the property was beautiful. There is a tower you can climb up to get a 360 view of Santa Barbara. 

There were showing free movies in the evenings.

For some reason in high school we would all loiter disreputably outside the courthouse after school.  I don't know why we chose the courthouse as a good place to look like we were going to do some crimes.   Probably because they got a nice lawn.

This place is beautiful. Do you want to know the best part? It's free!

The courthouse is open daily to the public, so expect crowds and tourists if you're heading there on the weekend. You can amble around the inside, checking out the paintings and beautiful tiles and various rooms, and then make your way to the top for awesome views. They have an elevator and stairs to the top. The observation "deck" is't huge but it has 360-degree views so you can see the ocean, downtown Santa Barbara and the mountains on the other side. Aside from the awesome view the grounds are absolutely gorgeous with lots of green grass and shady trees for a picnic. 

Note: You can park right across the street in Lot 7. Convenient!

How can I give this less than 5 stars? Admission is FREE! (of course, they casually ask for a donation but no pressure at all). What a beautiful landmark. My wife and I were talking about how it would be awesome to have a wedding here. But not in the Fall/Winter when it's windy freezing cold. Such as how it was when we went. We went up to the 4th floor viewing tower to get a good view of town. So nice... made me want to live in SB for about 10 seconds. Then more of that freezing cold wind started up again. I'm sure it's not like that all year long :) Plus, we had to go because we were 5 minutes out from the end to our 75 mins of free parking.
If you want something cool to check out then go here for the free 360 degree view of Santa Barbara and the beautifully tiled court house hallways.

I feel real fortunate to have had this as a stop on a recent bus excursion; as during my only other SB visit we went right to the mission and blew outta town.
As the pics show, this really an architectural gem I envy the folks working here daily to deliver justice.  Don't miss going to the top of the tower !

This is a must go to place! I came here for the first time for something special. I came with my mock trial team for the annual "Mock trial competitions." 

Anyways, you guys probably aren't interested in that. Although if you are, don't be, you have no idea how draining it gets after a while. Anyways...

Parking and walking through the front doors you immediately get this feeling of awe. The front doors look to be almost a hundred feet tall! You feel like you are walking into this important place, which you are. 

Getting to walk around, especially in the main courtyard, was just very cool. It's green, photogenic and overall, enjoyable. The first thing that you might notice is that there are so many people here all doing different things. While I was there, people were taking wedding pictures, being tourists, I was there with hundreds of other high schoolers all dressed up for our mock trial tournaments, there was even a couple of Disney princess there. Everyone comes here. 

If you are fortunate enough to be able to go into a court house like I was, that's where the trip becomes even more magical. I won't give too much away, but it is unlike any other court I have ever seen. They have banners, high ceilings, all the good stuff. It gives the place a sense of majesty, like you are walking into a royal conference or something. 

Final verdict: Come check this place out! You will not be disappointed. There is a lot to look at and definitely take pictures. You can take some good ones. I would know, my dad's a photographer.

Beautiful 360 views of Santa Barbara--for free :-)

Highlights:
- Views: after a short elevator ride, you can walk around the square and see views from each direction. Mountains and the mission on one end and the ocean on the other. Cool birdseye spot to check out the little city. 
- Free: not your typical big city spot where you have to pay $20+ 
- Nice sb style building 
- Just few blocks from action on State Street

Lowlights:
- Not much else there so more of a few minutes activity than a destination

Glad my local friends gave us this heads up.

*Interesting art work
* nice scenic view
* friendly tour guides (who are volunteers!)
* best of all, it's FREE admission!!!!!!!!!!!!

MUST DO IN SANTA BARBARA!!!

Visiting this courthouse was one of the highlights of my trip to Santa Barbara. It was stunningly beautiful - all of the beautiful hand-painted tiles, the "mural room" w/ hand-painted murals on the walls, and the hand-painted ceilings.  The architecture is beautiful, and the view from the tower in all 4 directions over the red tiled roofs to the ocean and out to the hills, is incredible.  It's neat that it's still an operating courthouse, too, and to see people on the weekend picnicking on the grounds. An incredible Santa Barbara landmark.

Santa Barbara County Courthouse, which is really really close to Downtown SB (or actually in there) is really really cool and surprising. It's a courthouse, yes, and functioning. But so why would anyone want to go in? On their own free will? Because it looks really cool and...old.:O It has Spanish roots there, spiral staircases, courthouses, there's a room where you can have a wedding reception but can be seen of all the incredible artwork and decor in there. There's also, on the 5th floor, a beautiful view of parts of Santa Barbara. There's also a clock tower on the 4th floor that you can see a part of (but can't be open/gone in unless by appointment).


Let's go to court guys.

Enjoy the history (:

Stopped by on a Sunday and the courthouse was open for tours and self walk throughs. People can take the stairs to the 5th floor to the viewing balcony in the tower. An elevator is also available. 

The views were spectacular! There's a 360° viewing from the top of the tower. You can see out into the ocean, the surrounding mountains, and the mission. 

The 4th floor is the clock floor, you can see the clock's mechanics. When we went the room was closed, but you can see through the glass wall. 

The 2nd floor has a big courtroom with artwork covering the walls all around. No flash photography is allowed in here.  

The surrounding grounds were lush and great for photos.

This courthouse is amazing! I love the architecture and the feel of the place. I wouldn't mind serving jury duty here. My favorites were the spiral staircase and the library. It was unfortunate that the tower and the mural room were closed off for renovations. Well that only means another trip to visit here again the next time I'm in Santa Barbara area. Park on the street and you have plenty of time of walk through the grounds. A nice free place to explore.

A must see!  This is one of the most unique public buildings.  It is a great history of Santa Barbara and a beautiful piece of design and architecture.  Be sure to go to the top of the bell tower and walk down to see every level.
Beautiful tiling and a nice view on top. No entrance fee.

What a beautiful historic landmark! And going up the the top deck to get a roof top view of downtown and the ocean is so special. I'm happy that a friend told me about it. You can easily spend an hour here and I think it's a must when visiting SB, which is why it can be a little crowded, especially on the weekends. I would love to be there when they have movie night on the lawn.

Parking downtown is relatively easy, although most if not all street parking is limited to 90 minutes.

I recently got married in the mural room. It's a little tricky on how to navigate on what to do next after you talk to Linda and book the room. Luckily a family friend knew a judge and he took care of of some of the confusion. 

Just remember if you get married on a Saturday, people will be around! July brought a TON of tourists. It was giving a bit of anxiety. Luckily I had a day of coordinator who was able to keep people out of the room for the whole 10 mins we were in there.  It's a beautiful space and makes for gorgeous photo ops!
Five stars for the view. A spectacular panoramic view of Santa Barbara is available from the courthouse tower.
It's so cool! The view is awesome and I'm so thankful that this FREE thing to do is in Santa Barbara.  Brought my friends and they all loved it too.  Gorgeous facility.

Beautiful place for a wedding. Parking right outside (first 75 minutes free) or park across the street in the parking structure (first 75 minutes free and $1.50 for every hour after).
There are many floors and hidden gems in this beautiful space. I attended a wedding in the Mural Room and it was stunning.  The judge who married my friends was stellar and even came around to say hello to the rows of attendees before the start.  They have a clock tower here and the history behind it is fascinating. You can also go to the very top and see a beautiful 360 view of Santa Barbara. Truly a great space for guests to entertain themselves and enjoy Santa Barbara. This is preservation and history at its finest.

Had my wedding ceremony here. Really beautiful place!
Open to the public so we just picked a spot and had a quick small ceremony. Luckily there was hardly anyone in the garden area. Sadly there was a private event inside and we could not get any pictures inside.

This court house is amazing! 
Just wow!
The garden is big and full of flowers. 
You can married here ok. Hahaha 
Also, the interior and the designs are amazing.
It doesn't even look like a court house to me.
Go to the top of this court house where the bell is and the big clock and you will see a nice view of Santa Barbara.

P.s.
The parking is not bad.
Street parking is 90mins

What an amazing place. I got married outside one year ago as of yesterday and the weather was perfect. It smells like flowers everywhere. We couldn't have picked a better place. Santa Barbara is awesome and so is this place. The people that worked here were all really nice to us and it made it even better. You almost always run into a jerk or two in life but I didn't find one here. I guess why would you be working in such a beautiful place? Awesome memories.

The views are even more beautiful than I could have imagined. I was in town for business with a weekend free, and I had the courthouse on my list of things to do. I can see why so many couples choose to get married here!
There are elevators that go up to the top or you can take the stairs.
Wow wow wow. How is this place free? I'd pay at least $7 to go again. Amazing views of the city from the clock tower and amazing art throughout. This was our favorite activity in Santa Barbara.

This is possibly the most beautiful courthouse that I've ever been. I love that people can go up the tower for the amazing sweeping views of Santa Barbara coast for free. Walking around the halls, I was impressed by the detail and beauty of this place. Even the meticulously manicured lawns invited people to relax and enjoy the sunny day. Truly a place that harkens to the Renaissance versus the modern drab gray buildings of other cities.

I might give it this a lower rating if I had actual court business but just as a tourist I like it a lot!

Voted the "most beautiful government building in America"...by some magazine. The local joke is that it was Santa Barbara Magazine. :-)
Actually it was on Tripadvisor. 
Spectacular example of early California, Andalucian architecture.
GO!!!

To me, this was the highlight of our road trip to Santa Barbara. Great place to visit, ad the best part IT WAS FREE!!! 

Check out my Road Trip to Santa http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=18oVdfa2nyU&feature=relmfuBarbara video. Some Clips include the

My husband and I were walking downtown and stumbled upon this little gem. While walking around the building I kept thinking this can NOT be a courthouse. It's way too pretty! Where am I? I was really confused as I walked around the garden, the room with the gorgeous mural, admired the beautiful tiles, the interesting architecture, and trekked up the stairs to the observation deck. The best part it's all completely free!

If you get the chance, take the free tour.  This is an amazing property and structure with a very interesting history. And don't forget to climb to the top of the tier clock for some exercise and amazing views.

We came in Sept 2016 to visit the grounds and the tower for some great sight-seeing. The views from the top are gorgeous and great for photographs. 

The grounds were nicely maintained and wedding ceremonies happen here all the time (in the mural room). In fact, they had a wedding going on when we were there that wknd! You can only pop your head into the mural room when there is a wedding ceremony, but everything else is open to the public.
The Santa Barbara court house is so beautiful. Perfect place for photos on their lovely grounds. All of the employees are very helpful and friendly too.
I love visiting this place (on my off time of course!)!! Don't forget to head up the clock tower - it's FREE!

Didn't get to have the whole experience as we popped in here to kill some time before dinner and they were closing in 10 minutes at 5pm. The gentleman at the desk was more than happy to let us scope the place out and didn't make us feel unwelcome for showing up so close to closing.
The interior is pretty cool to check out and see the old courtrooms, beautiful architecture and the relaxing lawn area outside. Didn't get to go to the clock tower which will give u a great view of city for FREE!. Next time!!!

The view at the top of the tower was great. They have helpful plaques to show what you're looking at. We went on a Saturday and there was a wedding going on. Beautiful architecture. Lots of mosaics. Some paintings.

They don't make courthouses like this anymore.  This was recommended by a guy working at a nearby skate shop.

This beautiful complex has a clock tower, sunken garden, Spanish mural room.

The clock tower has breathtaking views of downtown Santa Barbara with wide mountain vistas and low hanging clouds.  This a small local picnic area that accessible by a few flights of stair.  There are alot of goodie on the way up like an 18 century weight clock.

The sunken garden is quite and big area for picnics.  Here is a preview below.

This has to be one of the loveliest buildings in California. It isn't grand, it isn't minimalistic, yet it's somewhere in between. 

Tip: Visit it at 2pm so that you can take the free docent tour. Without it you'll miss many of the hidden decorative and architectural gems inside.

Parking: You can find free street parking a two blocks away.
Very nice place, with great view of Santa Barbara from the top. The docent was also very enthusiastic.

I couldn't believe that this is a courthouse. So beautiful! 
There was a lovely garden, an amazing view of Santa Barbara from the tower, the mural room, etc. We loved it. I would highly recommend seeing this when you're in Santa Barbara.

My husband and I made our second visit to wander around the Courthouse yesterday on a very beautiful spring day.  We brought his parents here at the beginning of the year and I knew I had to go back with our camera one day.

Every detail of the Courthouse is amazing! The details, the colors! Ornate tile work. Intricate paintings on the ceilings and throughout the Mural Room. Check out the Spiral Staircase down the ground floor corridor. (I took pics but the color doesn't work right; it's hard lighting to get a good shot.)

The grounds are wonderful for walking, laying out in the sun to catch a nap and some rays, and looking at beautiful flowers (huge wisteria on the east side) and varieties of old trees.

Plus all this beauty is free!

When we bring guests to Santa Barbara, we'll definitely be bringing them here.

Beautiful architecture. Unfortunately, the top was closed to the public which I was told that you could view the whole city of Santa Barbara.

When I came here, there was a wedding going on which was pretty awesome.

Had a short photoshoot here. The architecture definitely was European-influenced.

We arrived right when the courthouse was closing BUT we were able to enjoy and photograph the spectacular scenery outside. I've never scene a courthouse this majestic and ornate. It resembles the spacious lawns of a renowned university. Enjoy your visit.

Wow this is a beautiful building and a courthouse to boot - kinda unbelievable!  The view from the tower is great and the clock gallery is cool (we happened to be there when it was open from1-2pm on Saturdays). The murals on the second floor are beautiful (we saw a wedding wrapping up there). Worth a stop for this free attraction. Also at 2pm they have free tours. We did not have time to do this.

I've been here a total of two times and both times I was able to see some amazing views of Santa B! Gorgeous views and quite chilly sometimes so maybe bring a light jacket! There are really nice spots for photo shoots and the area is just so quaint. Parking is on the street (Free).

Tip: The very top closes at 4:45PM!!!!!!

A few years back, I had to attend the courthouse for a work-related issue. The construction of the building was simply breathtaking. Last weekend, I took my GF and she was stunned by the majesty of the building. We were able to enter one of the courtroom and the murals was simply beautiful! A must see for all tourists!

SHIT LOOKS LIKE A PALACE
but apparently real court "business" goes down here too.

I always brings people who come to SB to visit me here. go to the top of the tower if you wanna be all romantic n shit with your honey from out of town who came up to party. show her that there's more here than just sloshy nights in IV. I mean, they have a bunya-bunya tree. who even knew that was a real thing? all this you can learn just reading the plaques.

back in the summer when i was unemployed and not in school my bro and I used to bring thermoses of whiskey and hang out on the lawn at like, noon. its a great place to hang out, but there are motherfuckers getting married all the time and you have to be careful you dont drunkenly traipse into the background of someone's wedding photos. not that -I- mind being in them.

my favorite part is the staircases. you gotta see the staircases.

Really beautiful historic building with gorgeous architecture! Don't miss the main courtroom. Beautifully painted walls within it, and you are even allowed to peek inside during a court meeting or wedding! Also, the fantastic views from the tower are a MUST. Quite a climb if you take the stairs, but definitely worth it :D

Along one of our food tour stops, we got to check this beautiful structure out! It is definitely a must-see. It is a courthouse that still has events and court-related activities running today! The architecture is amazing and there are barely any doors here to have natural sunlight and air go through. One of my favorite fact I learned were that the tiles created in the kiln were stacked and they were separated with three small round clay balls between - so when you look closely to each tile, you can see those imprints to show that they were real! This also has a huge courtyard inside and a staircase to walk to the top to view the city! Breathtaking view! Must see!

Whenever we travel and see and interesting building we have to stop and take a look. This building was no different. 

We were surprised to find out that this was their courthouse. Wow! This is the town to get in trouble in if for no other reason than you get to visit the courthouse!!

Parking wasn't a problem. We explored all over the grounds inside and out and took lots of photos. I'd post some here but it looks like every part of this beautiful building has already been posted.

The courthouse is an impressive historical building with lots of pretty murals. The murals depict scenes from California's past. The view from the tower makes this a must visit if you happen to visit Santa Barbara.
Beautiful architecture and gardens.  Check out the mural room and clock tower.

I used to work here and I never took this place for granted.

It's such a beautiful building and I miss it so much. It's such a good example of Andalusian architecture.

Had a lot of fun walking around the court house looking at the amazing architecture as well as their infamous clock and the view from the top is gorgeous because you get to see the entire city.
Parking is available only on the street. The inside of a court room is filled with paintings on the wall. So beautiful!

Too many unsolved murders dating back to May 1963 and other crimes. The sheriff's department is dysfunctional and has very slow response times. Deputies and staff are conceited and arrogant. Some of them think they are above the law. When will we see arrests and convictions in the murders of Vern Smith, Linda Edwards and Bobbie Domingos in May and June 1963?

This courthouse is a must-see if you're in Santa Barbara. Its exterior is beautiful, but it pales in comparison to the architecture, artwork and views inside. 

There are plenty of interesting courtrooms to explore, and you have to check out the clock tower lookout on the top floor. 

Oh, and the group of chained convicts upon entry really set the tone that it's not just an amazing site to see but a functioning court of law.

This is an amazing building that is a must see in Santa Barbara.  The Spanish-Moorish architecture is incredible and the murals are fantastic.  Take the elevator (or use your legs and climb the stairs!) up to the top of the clock tower.  Incredible views of the entire city.  The sunken gardens were not as exciting as I was hoping (essentially lawns lower than street level) but this spot shouldn't be missed.

Breathtaking inside and out - truly something as grand as one could see on TV. We walked to the top (clock tower) and I think I saw one of the most beautiful views to date - I have not visited Spain, but this is how I imagine it would look like from above. Even though so close to what used to be subsidized housing, the Courthouse building stands out in the most glorious way!

Definitely the most beautiful and unique courthouse I've ever been to. Spanish style with beautiful grounds and a tower with great views of Santa Barbara. It's a fun place to wander around and explore. We've seen a wedding ceremony/photos every time we've stopped by.

Are we in Andalucía or California? Walking through the corridors and arches made me feel like I was visiting Sevilla. The top of the tower will reward you with lovely views of Santa Barbara. It's a gorgeous place that will make you feel like your in Spain for an all too brief amount of time.

Amazingly beautiful Spanish architecture! Stopped by today [first-time visit] to survey the location for our wedding & was surprised at how many people were hanging out there for their work-day lunch. It's unfortunate that we didn't get to see the mural room or go up to the clock tower due to construction. But according to their website, construction should be done sometime in July of this year (2015).
This isn't just any courthouse...it's one of the more beautiful historic places you'll see in Southern California. Anyone in the area should stop by and visit. Parking (across the street) is free for the first 75 mins!

Really beautiful setting. 

I didn't fully experience everything, but the grounds are well kept. It's understandable why weddings are always using this venue for photos. There were folks up at the clock tower - I'm not sure how but it must be on their website.  There were other families having a picnic on the lawn. It was very picturesque. 

Street parking can get tough, but we managed to take in and photograph the pretty view of the building and grounds after a quick walk around the corner.

I was looking around on TripAdvisor for things to do in Santa Barbara, and the courthouse came up as the #1 attraction. A courthouse?? Weird...

I decided to check it out anyways to see what all the fuss is about. This courthouse is amazingly beautiful. You can freely walk around it and there is a tower where you get really nice views of the city. There's also plenty of free parking around here and it's by State St. where there are a lot of places to eat and shop.
It's an absolutely beautiful venue! It literally transports you to a different time and the ambiance is serene. If I were to ever marry, it'd be here! =]

It's a little hilarious that this building is a courthouse.  Those things tend to be utilitarian and ugly.  I know Santa Barbara is a gorgeous place, but come on, it's being ridiculous here.

I went with my boyfriend, my mother, and her massive camera.  She was snapping pictures at every corner, taking in every marvelous detail.  The building is an architectural beauty and the inside is equally impressive, with high ceilings, pretty stairs, and a second floor courtroom decked out in grand muraled walls.  The lawn is another sight to behold, wide and lush with green.

The best part of the courthouse, though, is the rooftop.  The building isn't massively tall, but it's tall enough to provide panoramic views of Santa Barbara.  On a clear day, you can look off of any of the four sides and have your breath taken away.  The rooftop wasn't crowded when I visited either, and my mother had a field day.  I don't blame her.

So let me start by saying that I'm an attorney and come to court quite often in different counties and cities. With that being said, the old courthouse is probably one of the most beautiful courthouses I have ever been too. There's painting on the ceiling in the courtrooms.and the hallways have this renaissance feel to it. You don't see courthouses like this any more, as many of them feel drab and cold when you walk in. Not here. 

There's a courtyard area you can walk around in or take pictures. This place does not feel like a courthouse. This might be good if you are a defendant in a criminal case, as it might put you at a sense of ease.

The courthouse is in the older area. This is good as there is a lot of character to the buildings. There are also a lot of nice shops in the area so if you get out of court early, it's a great place to walk around. 

It's not too far from the free so it's easily accessible. 

I have never ran into a judge or prosecutor here that was not nice. Everyone here seems to be very nice and professional. I've had nothing but good experiences here from the judge, prosecutor, and clerks. Everyone is super friendly, nice, and professional.

If you would have told me I would visit a courthouse while on vacation I would have laughed. Infact a week before our trip I looked up local attractions in SB and I did laugh when one of the top five was the courthouse!
We had just parked and planned to pick up sandwiches from Three Pickles to take with is the the Botanic Garden when we found ourselves in the shadow of the courthouse and thought... well let's just look - if it's lame, we'll leave.
It was the best Santa Barbara specific site we visited! 1. It's free 2. It is from floor to ceiling gorgeous and covered in history. 
Even my teenage son was mesmerized by the spanish style beauty of the building.
i imagine someone could speed through in 15 minutes and get an idea of what the building is about or take half a day to explore the all the nooks and crannies. We were there for almost 2 hours and I fell in love. 
The rest of our stay in SB I told everyone I spoke to how wonderful our experience at the courthouse ended up being.
My experience in Santa Barbra was most certainly amplified due to the beautiful historical Court house building . I can't see how anyone can rate this any less than a 5 star. Love the details!

Are you here for an actual trial? If you are and have never been, you can find the location of your courtroom on the bulletin board on the left as you walk in from the corner of Figueroa and Anacapa. There aren't too many courtrooms so it's an easy find. We were in small claims court so our names were used, if you are in criminal court the defendants names are used.

The final decision will be mailed to you.

Absolutely a must-do in SB. Best of all its quick and free. You can take an elevator to the the top of the tower and the views are incredible! We got amazing shots of the surrounding area and the ocean was cool too. Glad we made it here!

This review, like most, is for the striking architecture and manicured grounds of the courthouse complex.  I'd heard for years about the sunken gardens interior, site of many weddings. 

Last weekend we were visiting nearby historic sites.  Seeing that it was only a few blocks away, I suggested that we walk over.  We came in via Santa Barbara Street, strolling over lush grass.  Moving into the extensive sunken area, I could see the appeal to bridal couples.  The intriguing architecture against a blue sky must make for amazing photos.

Given how precious fresh water resources are today, I'm surprised that some of the massive lawn areas haven't been converted to native plantings, saving some water for the impressive fountain out front.  The fountain is currently dry, for want of a better balance of choices on available water.

We walked around 3 sides of the complex, finding each to sport unique facades.  All are grand and photogenic.  One day I hope to return and see the view from the stately clocktower.

Checked another landmark off the to-be-explored list!

Who knew that i would ever fall in love with a courthouse.

Well, it happened. My friends and i didn't want to leave. And we didn't for quite a while, haha. 
I don't quite know how to explain exactly why it was such a great place, it just was. And you have to go experience it for yourself. You can almost feel the history of this place as you walk in and out of all of the court rooms, and through all of the long, epic hallways, and up and down all of the beautiful staircases. The architecture is stunning, and so is the courtyard. If it wasn't so damn hot we definitely would have laid out on the grass for a few hours. 
But by far, as others have mentioned, the best part about this whole place is the tower and the views that it has to offer. I'm pretty sure there's no other place in Santa Barbara that will give you views as beautiful as the ones from the tower. So even though you have to take the super old and super sketchy elevator up there, and even though for some odd reason the emergency staircase is closed, it's worth it and i suggest you do it if you're in the area and want the full Santa Barbara experience. Just cross your fingers before you step foot in that elevator because there is a good chance it will crash and you will die. 


Did i take that too far? oops.

Very cool architecture and generally a beautiful building. The views from the top are unbeatable. Definitely take your time and walk around the whole place. It really is a must see place in Santa Barbara and it won't cost you a thing.

Such a beautiful space! The courthouse is much more a piece of art than a government building. It's free to walk around the grounds, inside the courthouse (be quiet if court is in session), up the stairs to the mural room and up more stairs to see the bells and walk around the top of the bell tower. I don't know the history behind the courthouse, but the people behind this gorgeous building were brilliant-- they made a government building that can be appreciated by everyone. Great space for photos shoots, weddings, and special events.

Such a beautiful building. My friends got married here and the Mural Room is truly amazing. You can take an elevator up to the top of the tower and see gorgeous views of Santa Barbara. 

You might think it sounds weird that a courthouse is a tourist destination... but this has to be the fanciest courthouse in the country!

Anywhere where I can get an awesome view of a city, I'm there! The first time I went to Santa Barbara, I went with my then boyfriend and his friend who grew up in Santa Barbara. This was one of the first places hhis friend took us, and it was amazing! The building was amazing, and the views at the top were rad! It gets really windy up there though...

The second time I went to Santa Barbara, it was a girls trip and my friend had never been to SB. I was determined to find this place so I could show her the awesome view. After driving around for awhile, we ran into it by chance. There was a wedding going on at the courthouse that day too, and I must say they put on a beautiful wedding (we watched it for a few minutes).

This is a great place to visit if you ever stop in SB!

As someone who loves history/architecture, this was right up my alley. No, more than that. It made my trip. If you just looked at the courthouse and nothing else around you, you would feel as if you were in a different time and place. That is rare amongst buildings, and a magical thing. Everything about it is spectacular, from the chandeliers to the Mural Room to the tile floors. But my favorite is definitely the clock tower. From there, you have gorgeous views of Santa Barbara on all four sides- the ones of the ocean and the hills are the best. The building itself is not the only masterpiece here, though- the grounds are just as stunning. I couldn't get enough of the Sunken Gardens and the flowers that abounded. Coming here was a very enlightening experience. I've also heard that they hold weddings here (as they should!). Having been here, I have to say that this place is a serious contender for my wedding should I ever decide to have a destination wedding.

Beautiful! This is definitely a place you'll take a few pictures! The building is gorgeous outside and in. I'm a particularly big fan of all the beautiful tiles. And don't forget to go all the way to the top so you can look out over the city! There's only one small elevator, so plan on waiting if there's even the slightest crowd.

Just came back from a visit to Santa Barbara, and they're doing a lot of construction on the Anacapa side.  They must be doing some major gardening.

And a little history about the building (esp. for Steph C.): The courthouse is modeled after the Andalusian castles of Spain.  The original building was Greek-inspired, with columns for a more authoritative look.  An earthquake destroyed it, so the city decided to make it the way it is to not only blend with the rest of the city's architecture, but to also embrace its Spanish heritage.  So it's not ridiculous at all.

This building is so beautiful, it's no surprise that many have their weddings held there.  I've even seen a small ceremony done at the clock tower.  Now that's romantic!

Must see in Santa Barbara.

After checking out the Mission, we were hungry and got some subway in the downtown area. On our way back to Mission Park, we decided to stop by the courthouse since it looked like a nice play to have a picnic. Since it was a very nice, sunny yet cool day, we ate on the steps facing the courtyard. Peaceful and quiet, there was actually a chairs setup for a possible wedding closer to the building. 

Wanting to see what awaited us at the top, we took the staircase up since the elevator was a little slow. If you want to take the staircase all the way to the top, before you turn left and see the third floor elevator, there's a brown/black door that will lead you to the stairs adjacent to the clocktower room. 

Once we got to the belltower, it was an amazing view of everything in Santa Barbara. You can pretty much get a 360° view of everything if you walk around the top (which is like a 20x20 spot).  You'll get the ocean breeze from up there which will feel wonderful. 

If you go down tot he mural room, the walls from top to bottom are one gigantic mural. 

Overall, a beautiful place, inside and out and definite must-see in Santa Barbara.

One of the must-see tourist place in Santa Barbara! The view from the top of the building is amazing! You can see the mountains, trees, ocean and several key points in Santa Barbara! The mural room is very interesting, it shows history of the city. Sunken gardens are very beautiful and well maintained!
Definitely head up to the top for the views... They also give free tours but the views alone are worth the visit.

The 360 view from the top of the clock tower can't be beat. One of the best photo opps around. The clock mechanism one floor down is fascinating, and the mural in the courtroom is absolutely incredible. There is beautiful artwork throughout the building. As it's free, there's no reason to miss this.

To help appreciate the Santa Barbara Courthouse, go to some other city's courthouses. Probably you'll see some crappy, utilitarian, 1970s building, 50% of the reason people shudder when they get a summons for jury duty.

SB's Courthouse, built in 1929 and designed by William Mooser III, is an example of how government used to construct public buildings, with a sense of grandeur and awe. 

During the hot days, the architecture keeps the building cool. The Sunken Gardens are lovely and a host to many events (currently this summer UCSB are screening Universal Monster Movies!). And the view from the Tower is one of the best of Santa Barbara.

(Another recent note: the tower is currently under refurbishment, so you may see scaffolding, and as of this writing there are no hands on the clock face...so, er, bring a watch!)

Yesterday, my nephew and his new bride got married in the beautiful Mural Room but not without their share of drama! After arriving at the appointed time (2:30-4:30) another group came in and started setting up. I'm a Wedding Planner so I started to ask questions. To make a long and stressful story short-the Courthouse had double-booked the room! Luckily the security guard stepped in when we had email proof of the room/time and that our wedding was set for 3pm and theirs was set for 4pm! The bride arrived a bit late and in tears at the Courthouse's mistake, so we took a few deep breaths and got everyone down the aisle asap. We didn't stick around after the wedding for photos in the Mural Room which was disappointing but quickly moved everyone outside (dodging other weddings on our way) and took photos on the grounds. I helped set up for the 4pm wedding so they wouldn't have the same stressful experience that we did! Beautiful buildings and grounds...a Courthouse wedding shouldn't have had this much drama or stress though!

Rating for staff only. Building beautiful--from what i could quickly see. Staff incredibly rude & unhelpful! Man at desk told me building closed in 12 minutes. We RUSHED up the stairs--passing another staff member COMING DOWN along the way-- only to find the door at the top locked along with a sign which said it closed at 4:45. Got back down & asked man why he (not to mention the woman who probably locked the door & passed us on the stairs) didn't tell us that the viewing portion closed before the rest of the building. He said he did. When I disagreed he said "I'm not going to argue with you" and turned away. Really?!
Great architecture.  The detail in the wood, ceilings, and ironwork.  Staff was very welcoming... Beautiful clock tower.   Great glimpse into an older era...

Such a beautiful and historic building. Absolutely one of my top 3 in the SoCal area. Their grounds are lush and green with plenty of areas for picnics, reading, taking photos... it's just PERFECT! The tile work and stucco art is impeccable and well kept. The mural room is the most beautiful I have seen and my husband and I were lucky to be married here. Definitely a must-go if you're in the area and even if you're not, plan on heading that way! It will not disappoint!

One of the most beautiful public buildings I've ever seen. My son is obsessed with clocktowers, and we had to visit every day we were here in SB. The view at the top of the tower can't be beat, and the mural and inner workings of the clock and the bells were big hits with both kids. But don't miss the rest of the building, make sure to walk down. Beautiful tile work, amazing sprial staircases, paintings and murals and ceilings...so much beauty. We even saw two weddings in the main courthouse! Simply can't be missed!

A gorgeous building in and of itself, but the views really make it amazing! Climb the stairs if you can, you'll thank me. It's not too tough and it's worth it. The mural on the second floor are lovely, the clock room is intriguing but hitting that roof shows the true beauty of Santa Barbara.  Definitely the highlight of the day!
What a wonderful introduction to Santa Barbara! Loved the beautiful historical architecture.

The courthouse garden is used for Friday night movies, last night they showed some like it hot and it was a perfect night! We pic nic'd with friends and watched a great film with the beautiful courthouse as the amazing back drop.

I LOVE the SB Courthouse! I always make sure to visit whenever I'm in the area. The architecture and decor is beautiful, lots of places to take great pictures. There's usually a wedding or quinceanera photo shoot going on when I visit. I love waking through the halls and up the spiral staircase and going up to the clock tower, where you can see the bells ringing on the hour as well as an amazing 360 view of the city. The grass area in the back is perfect for sitting or taking a nap in the shade. Highly recommend stopping by, they have free tours as well as free parking across the street.

Almost as visually pleasing and relaxing as the beach.  The architecture is just beautiful, parking is easy, and the view is breathtaking.  I'd like to take advantage of the thick patches of grass for some reading pleasure or a picnic.  And I'm going to bring any guest that comes to Santa Barbara to the clock tower view.  Ahhh, bliss.

Beautiful interior, great 360 degree view of SB, restoration is close to perfect to original time period, still functions as courthouse today (awesome!), iron gates (and their are a lot of them), woodwork (including doors), tiles, and plaster work are all absolutely gorgeous.  The arched and vaulted ceilings are quite a sight, as is the ceremony room with the nailhead trimmed benches and wall-to-wall murals (this room is located next to elevator leading up to 360 degree SB view).  It's definitely a must-see in SB.  Other important info: 70 minute free street parking, sloooooow elevator to 360 degree view of SB, sunken garden is basic but appealing, exquisite interior.

My husband and I took the guided tour at 2pm.  It is free and worth it.  We learned way more on the guided tour than if we had just walked around on our own.  It meets upstairs and then goes every where.  It was around two hours but the man would have talked forever if we wanted him to.  Then we went to the top and enjoyed the views.  Amazing history and lovely architecture.  Better than any old courthouse we have in Texas.  Highly recommend people make the time for this while in the area.
Best views in town! Don't miss the mural room.

Amazing courthouse and historical landmark with beautiful views and greenery! So glad we found out that this was a place worth visiting while in the area. A wedding was going on while we were there so we had to wait before we could go see the inside of the main courtroom which is also amazing. The artwork throughout through the courthouse is very memorable with a remarkable amount of Spain memorabilia that is very well maintained for such an old building. There was a second wedding outside of the courthouse on the grounds which was beautiful. 

The clocktower offers a cool view of the Santa Barbara from above and also is exciting to climb up there and see the old restored clockwork. Definitely worth a visit while in the area! 

Staff and mini-giftshop are very helpful. We got there a too late for the tour but seemed like they are pretty popular!

Parking is easy to find as there is plenty along the street and is mostly free.

I was so happy that their tower was open on Presidents Day. This was a highlight of our trip. You can see everything from the tower, including the Ocean. The lady working at the window was very knowledgeable and answered all of our random questions.

Santa Barbara County Courthouse is definitely a top place to visit when in SB. The court house is open to the public and has gorgeous views of the town as well as open spaces to chill and to enjoy the weather. The clock tower is probably the most popular thing to check out and the views up there do not disappoint. The tower gives a complete 360 view and is absolutely beautiful. The tower entrance is not hard to find once inside the main building. The tower can be accessed through the flight of stairs or an elevator. Parking to get here can be tricky, but the surrounding blocks have free 75min parking! There are some spots that are 75 min free parking Mon-Fri, but just make sure to read the parking signs. The courthouse was one of my favorite places in SB and I highly recommend people to check this place out!

The popular thing to do when visiting Santa Barbara is to go to the mission. It was the first place I went to on my first time in Santa Barbara. If I had the choice of going to the Santa Barbara Mission or the Courthouse (assuming it's not due to a summons), I'd pick the Courthouse.

On a Saturday morning, the Law Library on the second floor, the Clock Gallery that is midway up the clock tower, and the sunroof-domed Hall of Records that adjoins the main building were all closed. That meant the only things left open were the Mural Room on the second floor and the Observation deck on the clock tower.

Even with the limited access, it was well worth the 30 minute visit to see the high decorated ceilings with heavy cross beams, the tiled floors and walls, the view from the tower, and the big staircases including an indoor/outdoor spiral staircase under a turret.

Conveniently close to the Santa Barbara Art Museum, park once for free for 75 minutes in the parking structure between the two to visit both.

Drop. Dead. Gorgeous. If you appreciate California Spanish-style architecture, design, tile-work, lighting, typography, murals, etc. this is the place for you to visit in Santa Barbara. 

I spent 9 weeks on jury duty last year in a crappy institutional building with low-pile carpets, fluorescent lights, acoustical tile ceilings, etc. If I'd been in this building, I would have been so much happier. 

Stop by, and drop some cash into the donation bucket, it will be time and money well spent.

Attended a wedding here.  Place is beautiful and open to everyone free of charge.  Place is a working courthouse but on the weekend you can walk around.  The clock tower is definitely a spot to go up and look out over the city.  Great views of the city free of charge.  The clock mechanism is also worth a look.  The art murals and architecture are awesome.  The grounds are well maintained.

Who knew??  Wow, what an incredible view from the top and then so much to see as yu begin your decent from Tower clock room, staircases, floor tiles, wall paintings, step banisters, etc.  An amazing display of Illuminati symbols hidden in plain sight.  Wonder what we are really looking at here!  Seat of power, law library was amazing.....What an architectural gem as well as symbolic representation of who has the power...Must See!!

Well, I guess this might make going to court a bit easier, for work, jury duty, any worse and you might not appreciate it quite as much.
Beautiful, everywhere you look it is amazing, the views stunning, Santa Barbara is magical and the courthouse a must see while visiting.

Our wedding day was perfect. We eloped this past weekend and everything was so easy. We made our appointment online, filled out our license application and showed up ready to start our new life together. Our officant made us feel special and our ceremony had us in tears. 

The grounds are amazing. I didn't know we would be allowed to walk around freely afterwards, I wish I had researched better. I paid to to photos at another location after the wedding but we didn't go. My feet were done after our photo shoot at the courthouse. (Check out the pic in flip flops)  

Anyone thinking about getting married here, DO IT!

When you think of courthouses, you don't often think about it being a place for a wedding venue. But that changes with this particular courthouse. The Santa Barbara Courthouse has been made popular by the beauty of the interior and exterior Spanish style set up of the court house and how accommodating it is for weddings. A beautiful place for your next wedding with plenty of Kodak moment locations on the property. Recommended.

Worth visiting for the 360 degree views from the watch tower if you are touring Santa Barbara. There is a parking lot just opposite the court house which is a very convenient place to park and then explore the surrounding areas on foot.

If you visit Santa Barbara he need to come by and check out the courthouse. One of the older buildings that is still standing. As great views from the tower of the town and the ocean. Seems to be a tourist destination spot and I can see why. If you have some extra time and your close to it I would suggest coming by and taking some pictures from the tower. Also gives you a good layout of the city.

If you're a tourist, come check out this historic courthouse.  You'll notice the clock tower so you can't miss it.  Take the elevator to the top and enjoy the beautiful views of Santa Barbara, Montecito, and the ocean.

So pretty! This was a really pretty place. I can see why people get married here and there was going to be a wedding here too. (People setting up). 

It has so many Spanish historic buildings and was just a beautiful sight. The grass is so green and cut, you just want to have a picnic here... well far from the wedding. 

The interior was pretty and it's open for the public. Very many photo ops!

GOOD FOR:
Weddings
Engagement photos
Glamour photos.

Love this building! Beautiful historic gorgeous court house that is still in operation but you can also tour and walk around in and go to the top of the clock tower and overlook the entire city and ocean. My husband and I got married here in the tower 8 yrs ago. Beautiful building and outdoor large courtyard, wonderful for pictures. I also have come down here for the 4th of July patriotic orchestra concerts free and live in the garden, wonderful! We laid on the grass lawn and had oodles of dragonflies hovering over us a few yrs ago, it was so magical to the music.

Wow!!! Beautiful Spanish architecture. It is very well maintained. I love all the details and bold ideas of the building. The building is very nicely designed to get a great view of the hills and area. 

The garden is very beautiful too. 

Friendly staffs inside the building. 
We also enjoyed the murals and arts in the building telling histories of the area. 

So glad we stopped by!

Santa Barbara holds a very special place in my heart and the Courthouse is an icon in the city. The grounds, clock tower views, and architecture are unmatched - try finding something so stunning that is a court house some place else.  

This review pertains to the Hall of Records where we obtained our marriage license today.  The line was short with just 2 other couples in front of us and the staff were knowledgeable.  They were super helpful and kind at answering our questions regarding obtaining a marriage certificate in an expedited fashion (you can get it the same day if you turn in your signed marriage license before 10am and pay a $12 fee).  They supplied us with a packet organized with all the information we need.  All in all, an easy process for an exciting moment in a beautiful environment.

What a beautiful place! I really can't believe this is a courthouse. I ended up in Santa Barbara via Amtrak Surfliner on Friday 6/9/17 on my day off and wanted to get out of LA. Hopped on from the Glendale station and 2.5 hours later, I ended up on State Street. I always end up talking to a local (everyone's pretty friendly) and someone asked if I had ever been to the courthouse. I thought...'no, why? it never occurred to me to stop by there.' I completely regret my decision in life to not explore more than the 1 x 1 mile radius of the train station. :( 

I took the trolley (50 cents) and got off on Anapamu St and walked the one block over. I was so impressed! The huge arch in front of the building with the beautiful fountain is something to admire. The courthouse is open to the public and when you walk in through the small door on the right, you enter into a very old entrance lobby. There's an elevator to take you to the top or stairs (careful, they're narrow). 

Once at the top, you can see a 360 view of Santa Barbara. I went around 12pm and it was such a clear day! (photo attached) 

We walked around the property and there was a wedding being set up. It was so beautiful and had me thinking that this is more my speed if I chose to get married at this location. Such greenery and impressive architecture. 

Once we were done, I went back on State Street and continued on with my day. That was a huge plus and nice break on my daycation in SB. I urge those that visit to make a quick stop. There's plenty of parking structures (from what I saw) around the area, and street parking. You can beat visiting..it's free and those views should cost us!

I'm willing to bet that most people have an overall negative connotation of courthouses. It's one that is associated with jury duty, being sued, divorce proceedings, etc. Therefore, unless you've been rewarded in a courtroom in some way; it isn't your idea of a relaxing, beautiful, almost-vacation like spot. Well, think again because the Santa Barbara Courthouse will crush all of those preconceived notions!

The Santa Barbara Courthouse is an architectural wonder which can't be missed when in the downtown area. The lumbering, tall structure with fountains, a bell tower, palm trees, and green grass will make you feel that you are at some Spanish resort. Oddly enough, there is an instant striking feeling of calm and serenity (not one you expect at such a place!). The Courthouse offers FREE tours and let me just say: this is highly recommend!

Upon entering, there is much to see and behold at the Courthouse and all of it is breathtaking. The corridors dazzle the eyes with archways, winding staircases, high ceilings, wooden beams, tiles, and Spanish (almost Moroccan) details and are perfect for photo-taking. One can visit the actual courtroom which combines elements of an art museum (with beautiful wood and murals) with an antique courtroom. It is awe-inspiring.

If that isn't enough for you, take the elevator or steps to visit the clock and the Bell Tower. You don't have to be the Hunchback to appreciate the jaw-dropping views from a bird's eye. One can see all of Santa Barbara, the mountains, and even the ocean. It seriously feels like you are on vacation in Europe or something.  There is NO reason to pass up the opportunity to take photos which will look straight off of a post card. Warning though, it is very crowded up there with all of the tourists jostling for photos so definitely have some patience and time-management skills as the deck closes earlier than the courthouse. 

Once you've gotten your fill of the sky; head down to the gardens where birds chirp, trees provide shade, and the soft, greener-than-a-crayon grass will calm your mind. Also interesting are the views of historic parts of the building such as the solitary confinement rooms of the old jail from the 1920s.  The Courthouse is nothing short of a beautiful gem (ask the staff about ghost stories!).

To be noted is the great customer service (at least during my visit). The woman manning the Help Desk was kind, peppy, and answered any questions we had. There were also various younger (college-aged and most likely students at UCSB) walking around as curators throughout the building who were clearly more apt at socializing with each other and didn't really care to provide service but because of this; they left us alone to venture by ourselves and I would rather have it that way anyway. 

I could rave about this place forever but the point is that the Courthouse is gorgeous, peaceful, provides amazing views of Santa Barbara and is FREE! You really can't beat that either for a special occasion (my visit was on my 30th birthday) or just a casual day in Santa Barbara. So although you may cringe at the thought of a courthouse; make sure to visit this wondrous spot when in town.

While the building and surroundings are nice and the clock tower gives you a 360 view of the city, be sure to explore visit the inside of the building too. And don't park in front of the book drop-off box!

We reserved the Mural Room for our wedding in Feb 2017. The whole process was very easy and reasonably priced. We booked about 4 months in advance with no trouble. 

The Mural Room is gorgeous and thankfully indoors. It rained our wedding weekend but we had no worries bc everything was inside and warm. The room holds about 100 guests seated. Acoustics were no problem - we spoke a little above regular speaking voice during the ceremony and everyone could hear us fine. One warning is the closing time. Our reservation was 2:30-4:30pm and we wanted to take photos for an hour with our photographer after the ceremony. But, the courthouse closes at 5pm. The guard was strict about getting us out at 5. We took photos outside after that, which worked out fine. Try the giant tree with pink/purple flowers right outside the front door. It worked perfectly for photos.

Overall, the courthouse is beautiful, unique, charming, everything we could want for our wedding.
Beautiful views of Santa Barbara from the top of the tower. You can also see the restoration and cleaning taking place in one of the courtrooms. Great free way to start the day in SB!

Beautiful. Seriously. 

Outside people are playing catch, reading or sleeping on the lawn. Wish I was a local to join them all the time.

Inside you'll see hand painted tiles everywhere. Do yourself and your body a favor and take the damn stairs and not the elevator. It's only 4 floors. You'll find the courtroom on the 2nd floor with painted walls. There was a model doing a photo shoot on the stairs against the tiles too. Oh and if you take the stairs you'll see the mural they're painting too. When you get on top of the tower you'll find a beautiful 360 view of SB. 

The best part? It's all free.

One of the big cultural highlights to any Santa Barbara trip. The old courthouse has outstanding, unique architecture that gives the city some real character. You can roam around the interior gardens and the outdoor collonades. There are free, guided tours that I highly recommend. You can also climb to the top of the bell tower to get some spectacular views of the city. Easily walkable from the main shopping district.
I love coming here to sit in the garden to relax and going up in the tower for the view of Santa Barbara.

I decided to come check out the Santa Barbara County Courthouse, as many Yelp threads said it was a "must-see" on a day trip to Santa Barbara.  I did not really understand what could be so great about a courthouse, but I trusted Yelp, and I'm so glad we did! 

This is so much more than a courthouse - as they lady explained at the info desk, it is a magical building! This building has such amazing architecture with amazing design and art throughout the whole building. There are so many paintings, tile details, large wooden doors, and other small details that make this building amazing and so fun to walk through. You could probably walk through this building several times, and continue to find new amazing details.  Also, they work very hard to preserve this building, as it is quite old, and while we were there, there was a whole room that was closed off because they had specialists in there that were working to preserve the beautiful mural on the wall. It is so awesome that they put in so much care to ensure that people can enjoy this building for years to come.

I heard that there was an amazing view from the lookout point on top of the building, so we decided to make the trek to the top. On the journey up, you can see the mechanisms of the clock that chimes of the hour, and right as we walked past it, it began to ring! It was so cool to see it happening, and we were lucky to be in the right place at the right time. Once we reached the top, it was truly amazing. With the help of the picture guides on each side of the lookout point, you are able to see all the different landmarks throughout the area. You can even see the ocean from there! I love lookout points, and I was truly amazed being up here. 

Outside the building, it is still so beautiful! Large fields of green grass, beautiful trees, and even more art and architecture to admire. I'm so glad that I was urged to come here, and I seriously encourage anyone new to Santa Barbara to check this place out. It's free and such a wonderful experience!

This is probably one of the most beautiful buildings I've ever seen in my life. The whole place is stunning, and we even got to look in on a wedding in the mural room! Seriously, if I could get my whole family and friends to fly 5000 miles, I would totally get married here someday in the future. Maybe I'll just elope anyway--- it's that beautiful!
The view from the clock tower is incredible, I could literally spend hours up there. A perfect place to see all of Santa Barbara. 
The people working here are incredibly helpful both about the building itself but also about general guide information about the city-- or even just where to find the nearest post office etc.! 
The grounds outside are a perfect place for a stroll or a picnic. If I ever get my family to visit SB this is the first place I'm taking them. 
AMAZING!

For a place that condemns and stigmatizes people this is an extremely well kept and beautiful courthouse with a relaxing atmosphere. Nice details include handmade floor tiles, vintage art paintings, a ceiling painted by Italian artist John Smeraldi using copper and zinc, the great clock tower view and a lush garden. A lot of design work was copied from Spanish castles. If you appreciate antique Spanish architecture from the 1920's this building is worth a visit. Read the signs before parking on the street. Citations are constantly issued. Admission is free.

Came early hoping it'll be open. Stayed till 10:30am and still was closed. Sad but ended up enjoying the outdoor. 

Came early and dodged the crowd. 

Clock tower was shut down due to construction ...BUMMER!

Indispensable when you are lost in Santa Barbara because you can see all of downtown from the clock tower. Before you re-orient yourself, check out the art along the hallways and the mural room on the second floor.

This has got to be one of the best places to view SB. The tower provides 360 degree views of SB and all it's beauty. You might want to bring a jacket during those windy days and a camera to capture the surrounding because it is very nice. 

The inside had some cool things as well. I got to enter into one of those courtrooms but I found it odd they still had sessions running on a Saturday. It is definitely a good spot to go visit if first time at Santa Barbara. Be sure to go to Coffee Cat across the street.

Though it's a somewhat niche tourist attraction that we didn't actually plan on visiting, we saw a notice for a free tour and thought 'Why not?!' We were very glad we did because the history of the courthouse and its work tells a lots of the history of the area. The guide was very informative and spoke very passionately. The building is exquisite and the view from the roof is well worth the visit.

Make this a stop if your're  planning on visiting Santa Barbara.   We toured both the Santa Barbara Mission and the Santa Barbara Courthouse and although they were both great we enjoyed the Courthouse more. It's hard to believe this is an actual working courthouse.   Beautiful Spanish-Moorish architectural style.   The inside contains beautiful murals and ornate tiles.   Beautiful gardens outside.   I have a fear of elevators so I took the stairs all the way to the top of the clock tower where you can view the entire city.   It's quite a hike but well worth it.  It's a 360 degree view of all the city has to offer.   A different perspective from each side.  This courthouse is a signature example of Santa Barbara's style.  Don't miss it.

The courthouse is a wonderful place to get married!!! My husband and I said our vows here in November 2013 and had the best time. We inquired about the Mural Room in September (we planned our wedding pretty quickly) and were able to reserve it for a few hours on a Saturday morning in Nov. Judge Brian Hill married us and he was absolutely everything we hoped for- funny, engaging, wonderful at vows (we were not up to writing our own!) and he got everyone all teary eyed several times. We highly recommend him- he is a judge at the courthouse and was very sweet; my entire family loved talking with him. 

I could not have asked for a better experience. Linda Van der Wyk helped me book the room and was very kind over the phone. She explained that there was no Plan B if it was raining which is why we chose the gorgeous historic Mural Room instead of the outdoor gardens (also beautiful!). Our guests were mostly from LA and the east coast.. east coasters were especially amazed by this historic building. There's nothing like the SB Courthouse over in NJ, CT, or NYC.. ;) 

The best part.. the price. We paid well under $300 to rent the room for the allotted time. Our photographer got amazing shots in the Mural Room and in the gardens after our ceremony. Guests loved visiting the tower at the top too! Highly recommend this place for a headache free experience and wonderful wedding day.

Me & my husband got married here outside where the stairs are at, I must say this is beautiful & well maintained I would recommend anyone to get married here if youre on a budget & planning a small wedding ! Great photography to , memories always cherished !
Must visit the top to get a great view of all of SB.. Free tours or great for a quick visit.  Wasnt that busy on a Sunday, so not bad.

This place is a must to visit. Very historical; amazing views of the city; real judge houses :) 
Loved it.

Sure jury duty sucks, but I'd gladly do my civic duty at this courthouse!

What a beautiful, educational, and above all FREE thing to experience in downtown Santa Barbara! 

The architecture, the gardens, and the views from the tower can't be talked about enough. It's right off State St. so you have no excuse not to stop by and check this place out.

We parked at the Paseo shops and after walking around for a bit we decided to walk to the court house which is a 8 min walk from the shops. I instantly fell in love with how beautiful it is and how there were so many different areas to explore and take pictures. It's a great place to explore it have your lunch on the grass while enjoying the beautiful view of the courthouse.

Beautiful and way worth the 30 minutes it takes to explore. My wife and I came at 9am after our morning coffee and the place was dead. Just some mild activity in the court rooms. On a beautiful mild summer Santa Barbara day you just can't beat the ambiance here. Plus it's a block off State Street. You really have no excuse to pass this up.

This is a surprise...  I work in politics so I spend a LOT of time in Courthouses and I don't generally find them places I want to visit on my free time.  But this one is actually pretty interesting.  Well worth a stop if you're doing a Santa Barbara trip.  

The views from the tower are spectacular.  And some of the stained glass and light fixtures are awe inspiring.  We didn't make it in time for the tour but turns out a talk was being given in one of the court rooms about the history of the building.  Spend 15 or 20 minutes listening to this...  it's fascinating.  And if you show up when they give the free tour, bonus!  The fountain outside is gorgeous.  Lots of folks want to get married here so no matter when you show up you're likely to encounter a bride or two.  It's a romantic place...  I admit....  we did a little kissing here.  It somehow gets you in the mood...  

I'm all about Frank Gehry and modern design, but this structure is definitely something to see.  I dare say it might even be a good start to a romantic dinner somewhere nearby...

This building is absolutely GORGEOUS!  Definitely worth the trip if you're visiting SB.  We came on a Sunday, so court wasn't in session, so it wasn't very busy at all.  You just don't see buildings like this anymore.  All the details from the flooring, walls, even the stairs is just beautiful.  Unfortunately the clock tower was closed when we went.  Was looking forward to seeing those views from up there.  Maybe next time when I'm in SB.

My good friend was providing us a tour of the Santa Barbara County Courthouse which is located at 1100 Anacapa Street, in downtown Santa Barbara. If you are a lover of architecture, this Spanish Colonial Revival style building is quite an enjoyable visit. I always was in great belief that Greeks had a hand in some way in almost every new architecture structure that exists and they definitely did with this structure. The new building replaced a smaller Greek revival courthouse at one time long ago.  

We visited the clock tower, which has many views from all directions. The clock tower mechanics some levels below was surely quite impressive engineering. I also enjoyed reading the history painted on the walls in the beautifully architected main court room. This is an active court house to this day and it is surely worth the visit and especially for those love creative architecture.

I came while waiting for folks to get ready based on a recommendation. This place is gorgeous! I loved all the architecture and art. I wished I had the foresight to hang out around the clock tower room to see the bells go off. I felt like I was in a Spanish Mission-style Hogwarts. Totally worth checking out!

PROS: Most beautiful courthouse i have seen (a must see), Free (doesnt get better than that), Theres a display of a old courtroom that is too cool (no flash photography but you can take pics), Must also go up to get a tower view.

CONS: No guide (or at least i dont know of or where to find them).

Im in love with this courthouse. its no doubt a very lovely place to be.

I loved this place when I went here for Valentine's Day with my bf. Surprisingly, there was not a lot of people here that day so it was easy to walk around. Parking is no problem in this city it seems, there's a public parking structure very close by the courthouse.

The interior is just architecturally beautiful. I counted no less than four couples getting their engagement pictures done in the building. The view from the tower was absolutely gorgeous! It was a breezy and sunny day, perfect for seeing a FREE 360-degree view of Santa Barbara. 


It was a nice experience that I've recommended to other friends who might be coming here. I'll definitely take others here if I'm in the area.

Great architecture, beautiful gardens. You can get a brochure for 50 cents at the visitor station that shows you the points of interest and also the plants outside. Note, this building was built in 1925 so note as old as one would thing based on the old Spanish style.

I thought we would briefly wander through the courthouse and make our way off to the "more exciting" things we had planned.  Quite the opposite, friends.  Keep looking up at the ceiling and you will find that this place is amazingly beautiful, filled with tiles and paintings.  It is Free, but see the lady in the window in the lobby and she'll sell you a self-guided tour brochure for 50 cents.  It's a steal, people, and it supports the courthouse.  This place has great bathrooms, FYI girls---finding a nice public bathroom is very difficult, but this is a great place to stop in.  We took about two hours here and loved every minute.

Stunning. What a shock to wander in here and see one of the most exquisitely preserved  Aundalusian architecture you can imagine. Everywhere you turned, there would be gorgeous colorful mosaics in little crooks and crannies, stunning iron-wrought lamps with horse feather glass, wonderfully crafted mission-style furniture.  We walked up the stairs and peeked into any areas we wouldn't be arrested for trespassing and oohed and aahed over the oil paintings and al frescoes.

What a joy.

Yes really, go to this courthouse even if you are not in legal trouble! Even in the midst of the beautiful Santa Barbara, this courthouse is a fine example of Spanish colonial revival architecture. Unlike other courthouses in the country that tend to be more brutalist and intimidating, Santa Barbara Country Courthouse emits an air of humanity through its beautiful structure.

Aside from its beautiful interior especially the elaborate mural in the courtroom, this courthouse also has a wonderful sunken garden and a tower where you can get perhaps the best vantage to see the whole Santa Barbara against the backdrop of the Pacific Ocean. All this is free too and street parking allows 2 hours or more for free as well! 

Get your camera ready because there will be so many stunning photography opportunities here. Even if you aren't here to photograph, take a moment to admire this beautiful building. Trust me, the view is worth going to court for!

---
tl;dr version:
1) Beautiful Spanish colonial revival building
2) Wonderful interior plus a sunken garden and an observation deck
3) FREE to access

The prettiest courthouse I've ever been to. We went to a wedding in the mural room and it was easy to get street parking and the gardens had a great space for photos. It was perfect for our large family of 50+ for a group photo on the stairs.

We loved the tower views and took some great shots up there, the walk up was well worth it but wouldn't suggest doing it in heels.

While this is an active courthouse, most people set foot inside or on the grounds because it is so pretty.  Very well manicured grounds, lots of green grass and foliage, not to mention the courthouse itself, make this one of the major tourist attractions of Santa Barbara.  We came here on a Saturday, and while the building was open, obviously court was not in session, and we couldn't see inside the courtrooms.  We came too late for the 2pm tour, so we just walked around on our own.

If you love old world Spanish architecture then you must stop by to visit this place. Walk up the stairs to the bell tower for a nice view of the city, it's also free to go inside. The place closes at 545pm daily.
The views from the top of the courthouse are spectacular! We also enjoyed strolling around the area. Highly recommend adding to your list to visit.

My family and I really enjoyed our visit to the Santa Barbara County Courthouse.  This Courthouse has been around since 1850, and is filled with beautiful murals and tiles throughout.  We found a free 15-minute parking off of Anacapa Street, which was more than enough time for us to snap some pictures of the main grounds, check out the chapel, as well as take the elevator to the Viewing Tower (on the 4th floor) to catch a beautiful view of SB!  Everything is free for you to roam around to - the Mural Room (on the 2nd floor), the Law Library (also on the 2nd floor), Hall of Records, the Grounds, the Clock Gallery (you can see it behind the casing while coming down the stairs from the Observation Tower), as well as the 4th floor viewing tower.  This place is opened everyday except Christmas, so you can check it out on any of the 364 days :P  Definitely a neat place to check out while you're in Santa Barbara!

+Viewing Tower (Observation Tower) on the 4th floor (elevator access available)
+free 15-minute parking off of Anacapa Street
The most picturesque courthouse in the country. There's not many that even let you wander the halls... but it's encouraged here!
I'm sure people would love to see the picture of my dog at the court house, if I could I would give it 6 stars!

BEAUTIFUL.  I am SO excited that I was able to see this gorgeous historic landmark. 

I KNEW this was a must-see for me while staying in Santa Barbara and I only had one full-day here - on my birthday - which was a Sunday.  As this is a still functioning and in-use courthouse for the county of Santa Barbara - I didn't think I would be able to see it on Sunday - but it was open - doors thrown wide to welcome me with a friendly lady sitting at the welcome/info desk to advise me that I was free to roam around to my heart's content.  Yay! 

I did just that - I roamed, I took photos of small nooks and crannies, I saw the historic chambers, I marveled at the light fixtures, I walked to the lookout and saw the beautiful view of the city, I walked on the lawn, I did it all.

I probably spent an hour or more inside - and easily found parking on the street as it was a weekend and no courts were in session.
