This is a beautiful spot --- be sure to see it if you are in town.  I'm jealous of those who get to use this as their actual courthouse.

The Santa Barbara County Courthouse is a place you have to visit! Hopefully you visit here as a guest, and not a defendant, or being charged with anything. :)
It is best to visit here on the weekend when court is not in session. The grounds are beautiful and well maintained. Most of the SB Courthouse is open to the public on the weekends, and it is worthy of your time to visit here. Most of the time you will see people taking wedding pictures, or even having wedding ceremonies on the grounds. 

Tip: Be sure to take the stairs/elevator to the to Clock Tower, the 360 degree views of Santa Barbara are amazing! Best part, it is free!

Something to consider: During the SB Fiesta's, the SB Courthouse sets up a stage on the grounds and you can watch Spanish dancing while you sit/lay on the grass area. If you do come to this event, do come early as it gets really crowded. It is a really cool experience! 

Street parking is a challenge, and I suggest parking at one of the nearby parking garages.

I've been fortunate to have attended a few weddings at the beautiful Santa Barbara County Courthouse. Some of the best views of the city are offered here and inside is filled with terrific photo opportunities throughout its exquisite Spanish-style architecture. On our last visit there was some construction going on so unfortunately the clock tower was not accessible. 

This is a must-see for visitors and locals alike. The grounds and the building are gorgeous and make for a pleasant afternoon diversion; just show up and explore for free. 

Pro tip: during summer evenings people gather at the courthouse's Sunken Gardens to watch free movies under the stars. Find the showtimes online (usually Friday nights) and bring a blanket or low-back chairs, snacks, and beverages, and you're all set.

It's simply gorgeous here. If you're ever in Santa Barbara, this is a must-see.

I think the best way to enjoy this is just to explore. You can take a look inside, along the hallways and rooms, to see the beautiful architecture. Or, you can go outside and take a walk around the streets or the lawn. Don't forget to go up the clock tower and check out the views. I can't say enough about how great it is, you're going to have to see for yourself.

I saw a wedding going on there, so I think you should definitely consider it for your wedding venue if you're into that.

We parked on the street, but I'm pretty sure there are other ways.

This historical courthouse has an amazing view at the top. The building itself is also full of colorful tiles and has well kept greenery all around. Definitely worth a quick visit since there's no admission fee and there's free 75 minute parking in nearby walking distance garages anyways.

Located in beautiful downtown Santa Barbara, the courthouse is truly an amazing place to visit with its Mediterranean style architecture. Beautiful artwork and tilework deck the floors, walls and halls of this still functioning courthouse.  As beautiful as the view is from ground level be sure to take the elevator up to the clock tower to get a birds eye view of courtyard, the sunken garden, as well as gorgeous panoramic views of all of Santa Barbara.

One of the things you should do in SB if you're a tourist, just because it has the highest view of the city -- just climb to the top of the clock tour. Bonus points -- it's free. There's also a mural room which is gorgeous. Other than that though, not much else to do.

Beautiful views of Santa Barbara and the ocean from
the top floor (don't bother seeing the actual clock- lame!), but lots of annoying foreign tourists. If you want to be bombarded by picture-taking obsessive people, please, by all means, go on the guided tour  Soooo annoying to me when people are crowding and snapping 24/7! Definitely worth seeing though. Got some great shots.

This is a beautiful location for photo shoots.  I love not only the gorgeous greenery and landscaping but the beautiful, old, funky and historic architecture.  It's also great that you can walk (some elevator access) all the way to the top of the bell tower and get a great view of the city.  I've taken photos up there too.  Pretty much guaranteed to be a wedding going on here every Saturday or Sunday.  Some convenient street parking in the nearby blocks which is nice too.  I love coming here - it's just incredibly beautiful.

My husband and I just visited Santa Barbara.  His criteria for travel includes 3 things: outdoor activities/wildlife, entertainment and local culture.  When he suggested a tour of the Santa Barbara County Courthouse I had visions of wandering around a sterile office building for 5-10 minutes then cutting out and heading to the beach.  No such thing.

This is an absolutely gorgeous place.  To call it a building is misleading, it's a trip into the past.  The docent at the front gives a quick history with the highlights, the mural on the 2nd floor is not to be missed, as is the view from the tower.

An interesting sidelight, you may end up being part of someone's wedding video.  The beautiful hall with the mural is where weddings are performed, all the while it is still open to the public. 

We saw other beautiful site sin Santa Barbara and the outdoor activities and entertainment were first rate.  But our favorite is the tour of the courthouse.

Such a beautiful place to explore when you are in Santa Barbara. You can walk around the courthouse for free. There is a tower that overlooks the beach and a bit of Santa Barbara on top of the courthouse which is a definite must see. The lawn is absolutely stunning. This is a great place to walk around or to take really incredible pictures.

Beautiful building! Take the elevator or the stairs to the top tower (I think they close this at 4, so go before then) and you get an amazing view of all the red roofs, the mountains, the sea, etc. Beautiful murals and a huge, cool vintage map of Santa Barbara County.
This place is beautiful!!! There is an amazing view of the city in the tower. It's simply breathtaking and the courthouse seems like a Hacienda. Hard to believe court is held here.

Yelpers: this is an actual, working courthouse. Visiting here Monday was the first time I ever wanted to be a lawyer. The beauty of this building inside and out is enough to turn me to the dark side. There are free guided tours at 2 o'clock (which I missed) and 143 steps from the ground level to the viewing tower on the fifth floor (which I didn't miss).

Tip: starting in July, the cloud cover burns off in the afternoon and provides a clearer view from the tower to the coast. That makes for a better viewing experience than the mornings.

A beautiful landmark in Santa Barbara, it's definitely worth visiting and going up the tower to see the gorgeous views of Santa Barbara. If you're little more adventurous, you can grab a few snacks and have a quick picnic at the sunken gardens, which is at the center of the courthouse. 

There is a nearby indoor parking structure that offers 75 minutes of free parking and it's a much better option than parking on the street, which most likely will take you quite some time to find. 

While you're here, you might as well visit the Santa Barbara Museum of Art which is walking distance from the Courthouse. While we were visiting it was under renovation and entry was free, which was convenient.

Breathtaking 360-degree 
A must see
You can either take the stairs to see the insides of the tower or take the easy, relaxing elevator

Great view of SB from the top of the clocktower ("El Mirador" clocktower). The actual room with the bell was closed when I went on a Sunday towards closing. It's 85 feet up.

When I went here I thought the view of SB is the only thing to expect from the whole visit BUT the details and charm of the architecture inside was outstanding. I did not expect beautiful chandelliers, great art and details on the walls and floors. Great bonus for visiting this place. Most importantly they have restored and maintained this place beautifully as it used to be a prison in the past!  They are still restoring certain parts of the buidling. Spanish influence is clearly evident.

It is free to enter and they do accept donations downstairs before you climb up the stairs or take the elevator.

Stupid young know-nothing guard closed the tower at 1 minute before closing time.

Dude, this is tourist place.  Close at the time your supposed to.

Yea, I should've come earlier, but when you're a tourist, you lose track of time going on a $0.25 bus that takes your $1 and doesn't give you change.

This place deserves a 1 star because the guard could've told me just closed it when I got up to that floor instead of just let me find out that he had just closed it a minute ealier. 

The guard should know better than to threaten me about calling the cops when I'm complaining that he closed earlier than when it was supposed to (even the lader at that desk/booth on the 1st floor said I have a could minutes).

Stupid young guards...they think they have some power when they get a wannabe cop badge.

Tourist place...people come from afar...open till the last minute.


*I went the next day, but only because I missed it this day...and luckily this place was on the way back to LA from Sycamore springs*

yes, the scenery was very nice,but didn't have a good time.

Great historic place to visit while in Santa Barbara. We went on a Wednesday, so it wasn't crowded at all. There's not much to do, but it's fun to check out the architecture, art, take pictures. We sat on the grass inside the court yard, which was relaxing.

Somehow tourists visit "America's Riviera" (our lovely town of Santa Barbara) without visiting one of its most well-known landmarks, the Santa Barbara County Courthouse. How this is possible, I just can't quite put my finger on it! So I have decided to give it a shout out to hopefully bring it to the top of the Yelp search list when searching:
"Must See" / "Tourists Must See List" / "Must See Places" / "Local Flavor" / "Sightseeing"...and beyond.

Instead of a long-winded review about the in's and out's of this historical landmark, I'll refer to my fellow Yelper's 240+ reviews and focus on some tips and facts for your visit:

- Hours are Monday through Friday, 8 am-5 pm and weekends 10 am-4:30 pm. If you happen to be available for an informative tour, they are FREE and start in the second-floor Mural Room daily at 2 pm on the dot, and Monday, Tuesday and Friday at 10:30 am. Inside the main arched entrance, there is always a docent available for questions.
- An important place to begin my "Yelp tour" is with parking. Parking is plentiful and free for 75 minutes in the nearby city parking garage/lot #7 (1115 Anacapa St, Santa Barbara, CA 93101) or all around the rectangular city block of the building.
- If the county is not in a drought, stop at the main entrance off of Anacapa Street to see the beautiful fountain. The main archway also has the Spanish inscription of "Dios nos dio los campos. El arte humana edificó ciudades." which translates to "God gave us the fields. Human art built cities." There is a detail topographic map of the Central Coast valleys that make it credible how Santa Barbara is one of the only places where the Pacific Ocean is actually directionally due south versus due west.
- The Courthouse was designed and built in the Spanish-Colonial style and completed in 1929. It remains purposefully the tallest building in downtown Santa Barbara at a whopping 4 stories. While this does not seem very tall to those of us who grew up in cities, it does offer amazing views from its four-sided clock tower known as "El Mirador." The free viewing deck does close a tad earlier at 4:45 pm but allows 360-degree swooping views of the Pacific Ocean with almost all the Channel Islands, Santa Ynez Mountains, downtown theaters, Stearns wharf and the famous Santa Barbara Mission in the distance.
- For a short history lesson, this courthouse is fairly new since the original sat on the same lot from 1872 to 1888 but was destroyed in the 1925 earthquake; the exact location is where the Sunken Gardens now resides. The current courthouse is 150,000 square feet in size and has four wings, one of which is a Jail Wing that no longer holds prisoners.
- On the third "story" there is the Bisno Schall Clock Gallery with visibility of the clock mechanism which was installed in 1929 and is still functioning. Few know but the ceiling features the exact constellations from the night sky using fiber optic lights from when Santa Barbara was named in 1602.
- The second level has the "cherry on top," the Mural Room. While it used to be the actual courtroom (which is now behind it), this room has gorgeous handpainted murals that walk you through the history of Santa Barbara starting with the Canaliño/Chumash native tribe. The curtains and pews are original too.
- Walk down the hallways to see how the arches, shapes, designs, tiles and decor are purposefully not uniform in order to carry on a Moorish-style. On the first level, there is an memorial flag made of various firehoses for those who assisted in the destructive Thomas Fire and Montecito mudslides. Nearby, there is an old payphone that still functions and a spiral staircase with the seal of the Native Sons of the Golden West from 1929.
- Head outside from the back entrance toward Santa Barbara Street to see the time capsule, massive castle-like doors, and neat dragon stances. After all this walking, relax by laying down in the Sunken Garden which has beautiful palm trees and plants from more than 25 countries!
- If you fell in love with the building, you can always rent out the Sunken Gardens for your wedding, quinceñera or other events. In the summer they have free public outdoor movies on the lawn too. Otherwise, if you are visiting and want to take home a memoir, they sell postcards and historical brochures on the main floor at the entrance. Tips are always welcome to assist with necessary restorations.

Please enjoy our beautiful beach town of Santa Barbara and please support small businesses along downtown State Street!

During our short stay in Santa Barbara, my wife and I had wandered by the enormous and beautiful courthouse building several times, always stopping to admire the architecture. Then a local mentioned we could actually walk the grounds and go inside, so we made a beeline here the next morning. The highlight was the clock tower section, with beautiful mural rooms, the clock & bell mechanism itself, and fantastic views at the top. I was pretty much snapping photos the entire time.

Note: this is an operating building, with occupied offices and active courtrooms, but stay-out zones were well-marked and we were able to wander and explore pretty freely. It made for a wonderful morning adventure.

This is how courthouses should operate.  Nice clerks, nice staff, no metal detectors, ornate wooden doors, It's like living in 1954 all over again; I could get used to this.  

Free parking is nice too.

The Santa Barbara County Courthouse is a gorgeous venue for a wedding ceremony. My fiance and I visited the Mural Room first on our list of wedding venues. Nothing could compare after, so we wed in December and the rest is history! 

We got married in December 2014 at the Santa Barbara Courthouse Historic Mural Room. The room is glorious with Spanish style architecture, high vaulted ceilings, spectacular original mural paintings from the 1800's, wrought iron work, wooden pews, colorful Spanish tiles, and natural sunlight shining through every window, hallway, and door. It is truly awe inspiring for a wedding ceremony. 

This is a highly sought after venue for the ceremony component of a wedding. I called and spoke with Linda Van der Wyk at the County of Santa Barbara months in advance to try and book a preferred date. She was so helpful in discussing possible dates and times, which are booked at least 6 months in advance so be prepared! It's so worth working out. I had planned a fall wedding, but opted for a December wedding just to be married in the gorgeous historic Mural Room! My only complaint (4 stars instead of 5) is the strict time structure they have on renting the room. Due to the fact that the room is booked up well in advance (on Saturdays) it made it difficult to find a great date and time to hold a wedding. We're thankful we made it work though! 

The price is totally reasonable for such elegance. Our family and friends enjoyed wandering the courthouse grounds, taking photos in the clock tower, and enjoying the ambiance before and after our ceremony. We are so happy we chose this location for our ceremony.

Santa Barbaraを訪れたら是非立ち寄って欲しい場所です^ ^
裁判所ならではの静まった雰囲気を体験しつつ、時計台から360度街並みを見渡す事が出来ます。結婚式を始め週末にはイベントなども行われておりますので、事前にチェックしてみてはいかがでしょうか？
なお駐車場事情になりますが、市営のパーキングや路上パーキングがあり、75分までは無料で停められます。

I could not of asked for a better wedding ceremony. First off, this place is incredible! The clock tower, the architecture, the spiral staircase, the long hallways, the giant door. Breathtaking! You have the choice of exchanging vows on an indoor balcony or outside in the gardens. Highly recommend outside although its a public space and there are lots of other things going on (this particular time people were setting up their blankets for movie night -- adorable!). But it was just perfect. The staff are just such helpful, sweet people who almost felt like family. The officiant who married us was a nurse for years before she decided to work at the courthouse and marry people. She was just so warm and made our fifteen minute ceremony very intimate and special and memorable. I highly recommend that couples who plan to marry here to write some of their own words to each other -- which apparently not many people do. It just makes it that much more beautiful.

It is definitely nice to check this place out, go in and go as high as you can. The lawn in the back is a nice picnic. 

gosh its hard to describe this place when everyone else already have..

go and enjoy definitely!

Who would think that a courthouse could be so beautiful?  Or that it would be a key place to stop, as a tourist??  Well, it's all that and more.  Free to visit, I highly suggest a casual walk through the grounds and public areas... take time to look at the Spanish tiles, or the paintings.  Beautiful!

Stunning views from four sides, free parking across the street (up to first 75 mins), beautiful tiles, murals, and a tower bell that goes off every 15 mins- not to be missed. They do offer an elevator if you can't handle the stairs.

One of the must see places if you spend a whole day in Santa Barbara.  Beautiful architect, and you can walk or take the elevator to the top of the tower and took a view of Santa Barbara.  You can also look at the mechanism of the clock while climbing the tower.
Wicked place to visit.  Make sure you see the tower, the clock room, murals and roam the halls.  Beautiful place to be enchanted by.

The drive from Ventura here is about 40 mins with light traffic on the 101N. Things you want to know about the courthouse:

1. Free!! Yes people, it is free. We are lucky to have this hidden gem in the heart of SB

2. Parking is free too if you can find street parking for 75 min or the parking lot across the courthouse.

3. Closes around 4:30/5 pm everyday so make sure you make it here before they lock the doors

4. Pet friendly! My pup loved it up there

5. Lastly and most importantly, 360 degree view of Santa Barbara! The view is just fantastic from the clock tower

Marvelous!
This is the sort of landmark I expect coming to a "small town" like Santa Barbara. Like aged wine, I can almost taste the richness of the history fermented in the paintings and walls of this courthouse. As I gazed on each wall panels, the murals, and the clock tower, I was teleported back into another century, another time, another America quite different than our own. 

Any one touring through SB should spend some time walking through the courthouse. Go visit the mural room. Stand on top of the clock tower and take some photos. Gaze at the marvelous paintings lining across the walls. Enjoy!

I DON'T NEED NO STICKIN' STAIRS OR ELEVATOR TO GET TO THE TOP!

BACKGROUND:
Since 1850 this site has been the home of local government and a place of civic pride and celebration. Visitors from around the world come to see the courthouse, mural room or grounds; and hundreds get married in the Mural Room or in the Garden every year. The County Board of Supervisors ordered the construction of this now historic courthouse in 1926, after the smaller Greek-Revival courthouse was damaged in an earthquake the previous year.

STATS:
Street parking (read the signs)
Great place for a picnic 
Peaceful place for relaxing
Free to explore

EXPERIENCE:
Visiting a friend recently we took a little look around the places that I've seen, but never experienced. The Courthouse is one of those places , where the architecture and history makes me go ah!!! 

The Spanish Colonial Revival style building from the Spanish tiled floor, to the murals and pictures up the walls to the decorated ceilings offers so much old world Spanish art, and so much history to go along with it. 

Although there is a prisoner wing, it is no longer used or accessible and unfortunately we reached the entrance to the tower too late, it was closed. Pfft, I don't need no stickin' staircase or elevator to get to the top, too bad there are security cameras all over or I would have just climbed to the top. I've seen pictures Santa Barbara from the top and they are amazing. 

I will almost definitely be back if only to visit the crown of the courthouse for the breathtaking sight. I'm sure the trip up the tower will give this review a 5th star.

#ART #ARCHITECTURE  #CULTURE #SANTABARBARA #COURTHOUSE

Review #10

Ermahgerd! This is the best view in Santa Barbara! This has to be one of the prettiest free things ive ever been to.

It looks like a giant spanish castle fortress complete with murals, paintings, chandeliers, century-old drapes, tiles, and grand architecture! One of the most beautiful buildings I have ever had the pleasure of visiting!

Seriously, If I havent  persuaded you to visit here then I must go on...

Spiral staircases, a "mural room" showing the early spanish exploration and settlement of the area & important historic figures, multiple levels to creep around in, a beautiful expansive lawn with sunken areas and gardens to compliment the "castle-ness" of it. 

A few story climb to the tower. yes, there's a giant tower that "towers" over the courthouse and the city, with a marvelous view when you reach the top. There's a bell tower exhibit that I didnt check out because it was closed, but it looked cool from the window. Wanna know what you're looking at? there are signs that point out everything in the view. You can see Santa Cruz island in the distance.

Oh yeah, this is a functioning courthouse. So you may see people wandering around taking care of their courthouse bidness. You may see a wedding in progress like I did. Id totally do a courthouse wedding if the courthouse was a giant castle!!!

So many brides, so little time.  Our parking time (75 minutes) expired and we decided to break from the tour a bit early.   

Price is very reasonable (FREE) and it's worth 10x what we paid!  Hahaha.

Make sure you visit the Bell Tower, it's the highlight of visiting the Court House.

Nice outdoor location for summertime fun. My sister brought us out on a Friday night for a free movie screening of Goldfinger. Earlier that day she laid out a picnic blanket to reserve a spot for our family. Unfortunately the screen wasn't set up when she arrived around noon so she didn't realize that it would be positioned diagonally / facing outward from the corner of the courthouse. Sitting along the outer edge of the plaza, we were still able to enjoy the movie with a decent view.

No food was sold at the venue, but plenty of restaurants were open nearby. Most movie-goers brought pizzas, popcorn, and other theater foods to enjoy on the lawn before the movie started. Also, the courthouse bathrooms are closed in the evening so the only available facilities are ports-potties located at the far end of the plaza.

Word to the wise, place your blankets close together or else you'll get some random yoga mat shimmying in between you before the movie starts. Sort of unnerving, but hey, I guess that's what the locals do

In my opinion the coolest place in downtown Santa Barbara. The building is beautiful and there's plenty to see inside. There's also a nice courtyard where people can hang out and bask in the sun. The best part is the Mural Room, which has gorgeous murals on the walls--it can also be rented out for events, like weddings. Before you go, don't forget to check out the tower, which is free and offers beautiful panoramic views of Santa Barbara.

Brilliant view of the whole area, from the mountains to the ocean, once you climb up (or take the elevator up) to the top of the Courthouse. One floor down you'll find what looks like a fascinating floor devoted to the clock (unfortunately, it was closed on our visit). Murals throughout the main floor (in both the hallways and the chapel) are nice, though the subjects frequently involve the Spanish conquest of the native Americans.
Santa Barbara County Courthouse is a fine example of architecture.  It's free to view though they ask for donations to help defray the restoration costs.  Nice view from the tower.

The Santa Barbara County Courthouse is worth visiting when in Santa Barbara.

The grounds are lovely. . a great place for a picnic lunch or a good book or people watching.

The building itself is beautiful and fun to explore. Great photo ops!

DO THIS: Ride the elevator up to the clock tower. The view is fantastic!!

This place is one of the most historic buildings in the area. It's a gorgeous example of Andalusian architecture and has a huge garden in the back. During the summer they hold large outdoor movie nights on the grass. 

The interior decorations are exquisite, and if there's no wedding happening, it's worth it to see the old courtroom. 

Go up the clock tower for a 360 view of Santa Barbara. It's worth it to take the stairs, mainly because the elevator is really slow, but also because from the stairwell you can look into the clock gallery on the third floor and see the clock's mechanisms.

Beautiful view of the city of SB when you go all the way to the top! It's free, and it's a couple flights of stairs where you'll see a clock tower on your way up.
It closes at 4:45 though so make sure to get there in time!

Architecturally stunning. Stroll around during the daytime and make sure to see the mural room. On a summer night, pack a picnic and spread out on a blanket in the garden while you watch a show during fiesta.

Definitely a beautiful building with a unique, historical architecture. The interior and exterior features of the building looked really nice. There's even a tower that you can walk into, and once you reach the top, you will see a lot of enjoyable views (as shown in my pics). Keep those cameras handy when you're at the top. 

A must see for any visitor in this city!

This is a must-see if you are in Santa Barbara. This working courthouse has beautiful historic architecture throughout the building. And as a bonus, the grounds are very lush and green and have great landscaping. The icing on the cake is the 360 degree view of Santa Barbara from the clock tower. Definitely one of the best sights in the city, and even better... It's free!

This review is based on hosting a ceremony in The Mural Room.  If you're thinking of booking it, please read:

As a venue, this place is pretty unique, and well worth the 'cheap' booking fee.  The room itself is beautiful and really an amazing setting for a courthouse wedding.

5 stars for venue

The main hurdle here is getting a reservation on the calendar w/ General Services (GS).  The ONE lady that handles all the bookings, Linda, is sweet, but it's obvious she is overworked and does not have the capacity to ensure reservations/details are set accurately.  She answers the phone probably 30% of the time, and maybe replies to 20% of your emails.  To put it bluntly, you have to get pretty lucky to get in contact w/ her.  

Long story short, my fiance and I were notified one month before our wedding date that we were accidentally double booked.  Even crazier, this error was not discovered by GS, but by an outside vendor we had luckily contracted and then informed us.  To Linda's credit, they tried to make good by opening up a different time slot on the same day, which worked for us.  Paranoid at this point, I continued to triple and quadruple check w/ her regarding the new time slot pretty much every week leading up to the wedding date.  She ensured us each time that she had spoken to all the necessary parties that would be there that day, and they had been made aware of our special arrangement.

As you can probably surmise, come our wedding day, we were approached by an overaggressive security guard 10-15 minutes before the start of our ceremony threatening our guests and I that he wasn't made aware of the schedule and that he would have to shut down the place and 'kick all our guests out!!', his exact words.  Around 10 of my guests had to step in and convince this guy that this was obviously an error on their part, and that I had all the necessary paperwork.  In the end, the security guard was able to verify everything and we were able to go thru the rest of the day, but this nightmare should not have happened....especially for such a simple booking.

If you're thinking of booking here, just be aware the booking/reservation procedure here seems really outdated and prone to communication errors/mistakes.  

0 stars for the booking process

My best friend and her now husband got married at this courthouse and it was beautiful! They rented the mural room and that process was really quick and easy. It was also considerably cheaper than other wedding venues we were looking at. The parking situation was super easy too because 1) there were lots of available spaces in their big parking structure and 2) it was cheap (approx. $1.50 an hour). There were tourists walking around the courthouse, but that didn't bother us because they were respectful of our celebration and gave the bride lots of compliments haha.

Beautiful architecture and worth going to the top for a breezy view. Free 75 min parking in front. Worth going to the docent your that starts at the mural room on the 2nd floor. I wish sharif were a bit livelier and engaging, and open to questions - I still don't know why there are little branch looking things in the middle of the rose window. I'm thinking he didn't want to just admit that he didn't know ;)

Kids couldn't sit through it much but I'll be back if I'm here for work or something on my own.

Santa Barbara County Courthouse is a functional court. But it's also a beautiful piece of art located in the heart of Santa Barbara. It has high ceilings. It has been remarkably preserved throughout the years.

There are tours that go through this courthouse. There's even an information booth.

So glad we stopped at this historic landmark. You wouldn't think this place is worth going but we were really surprised! 

Love the beautiful architecture. You must take the stairs up and take in the arial views of SB. Wow!  There are 4 different views you can take in - whether it be mountains or the ocean. The outlook is so amazing and what's even more awesome - IT'S FREE!!! 

We saw a couple taking their wedding pics in the lawn with the courthouse in the background.

I went on a beautiful self guided tour of the courthouse.  On a Sunday afternoon, there was a wedding and quite a few personal photography sessions, in addition to tourists strolling throughout the property.

The building is beautiful, with lots of ornate wrought iron, murals, concrete carvings and painted tile inside and out.  The gardens are well manicured and the lawn is a great place to sit and relax.  There are stairs and an elevator to go into the tower for a 360 view of Santa Barbara, including the ocean, mountains and other old buildings.  There are maps which help you locate points of historical interest in the town below.

As far as accommodations, we parked on the street and walked less than a block.  The building has ac and bathrooms.  There is a visitor center where you can get more information, purchase postcards, or sign up for a guided tour.

The Santa Barbara County Courthouse is gorgeous! It was my first stop in SB and I enjoyed walking around and taking in all the views. Street parking is free for 75 minutes, which was plenty of time for my mum and me.

We walked up to the Observation Tower (don't worry, there is also an elevator, although no matter what you do have to go up a few stairs) and got to enjoy the Mission View, Mesa View, Riviera View, and Ocean View. It's helpful that they had placards explaining what we were looking at. The Clock Gallery looked cool, but it was closed off. The sign said that you had to make reservations if you wanted to see it. We also checked out the Mural Room which is beautiful. The entire room is painted and it's pretty amazing to see all the work that went into it. There is a large grassy area in the middle of the courthouse which is a great place to hang out and relax. I think that is probably where they hold their special events.

The SB County Courthouse is beautiful, clean, and a great place to take photos. Definitely worth checking out when you're in SB!

Like other reviewers have stated, you have to visit here whenever you are in Santa Barbara. Again, let me reiterate. You have to visit here. Why? First of all, it's free to visit here and there are guided tours which are also free. Secondly, if you go all the way to the top, there's an observation deck with a great view if it's a clear day.

Now onto my actual visit. When I first walked by, about to enter, I admired the architecture of the building itself. Being from the East Coast, architecture styles where I'm from and Santa Barbara are completely different. Compared to the courthouse in NYC, give me Santa Barbara's any day of the week. Granted, given the number of criminals on trial on any given day, it might be a problem if NYC had this courthouse but ah that's a problem that could be fixed at a future time.

Entering the building, I was immediately greeted by a docent or volunteer. To be honest, I'm not quite sure what exactly he was. Nevertheless, said docent/volunteer welcomed me and informed me when the next tour was. Since I was not in a rush, I decided to do the tour. While waiting for the tour, I spoke with the docent/volunteer, who in addition to having information on building tours, also had information about nearby attractions. While waiting for the tour to begin, I conversed with the docent/volunteer who encouraged me to explore on my own and to go all the way to the top for the view. Since I had some time left, I decided to do just that. I can't remember how I got up there but I think I took an elevator and walked up some stairs.

Unfortunately, on the day that I went, the weather called for clouds early on then sun later on. I was hoping that the clouds would clear fast (although when I arrived in Santa Barbara in the morning, that wasn't the case) but when I reached the top, my wish had not come true. It was still cloudy but not as bad as it had been before. I'm sure that if it had been a sunny, clear day then there would have been nice views of the surrounding areas. For me though, the views were somewhat limited due to the clouds (damn weather) but I still agree with other reviewers who say the view is wonderful. I definitely snapped a few pictures while there and savored the view as best I could despite the clouds.

On to the tour itself, the guide led me and a couple around the building, pausing in various places to describe where we were exactly and to explain the significance of each place. Definitely worthwhile as I wished I had eyes on the back of my head so that I could see what I had missed- namely, the artwork all over. During the tour, if memory serves right, we even went into a courtroom (I think I went on a weekday).

Overall, a definite worthwhile experience and I cannot stress this enough. You have to stop here if you are ever in Santa Barbara.

The mural room will blow your mind.


What a great way to spend an afternoon.

This has been my favorite quiet place since high school. 
I used to come here after school and sit in the grass while I read my horror novels and did my homework. The large buildings and scenery creat a feeling of solitude around the whole courtyard. Stroll the tall, long, echoing hallways and take the stairway to the tower to get a scenic view of santa barbara. 
This place holds so many memories for me. Oil painting, walks alone, and even my mom just got married here.
Beautiful, elegant, quiet.

Please stop on by to explore this courthouse with clock. Art work and beautiful old fashion style of overall design. I took the stairs option to each floor however elevator is avail. The top floor overlooks Santa Barbara and is gorgeous breath of fresh air. This place is functional and when I visited there was a wedding taking place without interference from those wanting to explore clock. Restrooms avail. Overall clean, maintained well. Enjoy!

To get a rather impressive view of Santa Barbara, I suggest you go to the top of the Santa Barbara County Courthouse, known as the clock tower.  Santa Barbara is a pretty area to begin with, but a better perspective is gained when its viewed from above in the clock tower.  The clock tower is just one of the many impressive features of this historic building.  Take a stroll into the courtroom to view the detailed craftsmanship.  If you opt not to use the elevator in this building, notice the beautifully colored and ornate tiles inlayed at the beginning of the staircase on the first floor.  This building has been well-maintained and complements Santa Barbara well.  Please bring your camera when coming here because you may be quite upset if you don't.

This courthouse is used as a traditional courthouse, but it also is a great historical site! The building is all open air and in Spanish mission style. You can do self-guided walk arounds throughout the day, but they also have guide-led tours. If you walk to the top, they also have an observatory tower that gives you fantastic views of Santa Barbara, the ocean, and surrounding mountains. This is a great free attraction in Santa Barbara!

Me and My family thoroughly enjoyed our visit to the County Courthouse.   
Courthouse is historic building with some very nice rooms,  beautiful tiles & murals, a nice garden and a clock tower with wonderful views.  
Entry is is free and there is some free parking on Anacapa Street.

Only gripe is that it closes at 5 pm and we had to take stairs up to observation tower but view from top make the trip well worth it. I can imagine that you can watch beautiful sunset view from here.

This is a really cute spot if you're looking to fill 1 hour of your time in downtown Santa Barbara. The Courthouse is beautiful and surrounded by green grass. The clock tower was opened on Saturday afternoon when we stopped by. You can either take the elevator or the stairs to the 4th floor where it opens out to the deck. It's actually not that tall but you get a good view of downtown, the mountains, and all the way towards the beaches/ocean. There are scenery panels on each side of the deck to show you what you're looking at. Great place for pictures both on the deck and outside on the grounds. Bring a picnic basket or food if you're interested in eating outside.

Prettiest courthouse I've ever visited- with daily free tours at 2pm, it's worth a stop! Shout out to my fellow history addicts 

The architecture of the building is the traditional Santa Barbara, Spanish mission style. The white stucco walls, the tiled roofs, the large rafters. The building is a walk in history.

It's very outdoor indoor style, with the spiral staircases that looks immediately out onto the lawn and garden below being completely open to the air, not enclosed. 

Although we missed the daily tour, we still enjoyed walking the site. The large paintings and adhered tiles are beautiful works of art. We also took the stairs up to the clock tower which was such an interesting room to view. The mechanics and craftsmanship is readily apparent behind the glass. 

Panoramic views await you at the top- you can see the majestic riviera, meandering homes up the Santa Barbara mountains & the ocean in the distance. The tiled roofs below reminded us of an Italian overlook, reminiscent of Florence or Rome almost, just modern. Even on our cloudy visit it was worth the stop in.

I've been to Santa Barbara several times but never bothered to visit the courthouse - until yesterday.  Wow! This is a real gem...and admission is free.  Walking through the courthouse brings you back in time a bit. There is such attention to detail - everywhere - wall, ceilings, doors, etc.  A spectacular view from the clock tower is a must see.  If I had to chose between visiting the courthouse or the mission - I'd choose the courthouse.

Let me preface this review by saying I'm not leaving a lower-starred rating because I'm spited by law enforcement or because of a ticket I disagree with. That is not the case. I am a full supporter of our law enforcement and have no issues whatsoever with them. The premises are clean, historic and beautiful. However, their entry-level staffing choices leave a bit to be desired. 

Let me also state that this isn't an anger-fueled post written immediately after an incident. This particular incident occurred three months ago. However, it has not yet mulled over after 90+ days, so I feel it's necessary to review. 

This review is strictly based off of a single staff member at your traffic division kiosk: Stacy Aguilera. All I can say is, 'really, SB Superior Court? This is the best you could do?'

I stopped by the kiosk to ask a general question, and this woman's customer service was atrocious. Not only did she not look me in the eye once, she was unknowledgeable and listless. Sure, she probably collects something around minimum wage, but knowing that my hard-earned tax dollars support her livelihood, I'm a bit disappointed over this department's HR practice. 

My frank advice to the HR: sit next to this woman for a day and note the level of customer service she provides. You, too, will be shocked and disappointed. As someone who has worked in the customer service industry for 15 years, a woman with Stacy's demeanor would be terminated immediately. I implore management to pay closer attention to how this kiosk's attendants interact with the general public. Two stars it is.
We took the 2pm tour after eating a huge lunch - perfect way to walk some of it off.  Make sure you go up to the clock tower, the views are spectacular!

Heads up...the whole inside of the courthouse is closed to the public. We took a Lyft to visit only to see a sign saying "tower closed". We thought that we could still enter the groundfloor but the guard said the whole inside was off limits since Covid. They need to update their website and have signs out side that reflect you can't come in at all. Only able to walk around the building, and there is surprisingly very few places to sit in the ground surrounding the building.

Bring a sweatshirt, as the tile and open space make this building always freezing cold on the hottest of days. A must see if visiting Santa Barbara. There are no words to describe the garden and old-Spanish architecture style of this historical site. I recommend all to stop on by.

A courthouse isn't usually a place most of us want to end up ... and almost never on a vacation ... except when you are in somnolent Santa Barbara.

Courthouse records designate the 80-year old building a California Historical Landmark and a U.S. National Historic Landmark. It's a popular location for weddings, tourists, and anyone wanting a birds-eye view of Santa Barbara.

 * Free to the public 7 days a week except Christmas Day
 * Open Weekdays: 8 am - 5 pm; Weekends/Holidays: 10 am to 4:30 pm
 * Note the Observation Tower closes early (times vary)
 * Parking is street or nearby public parking
 * Free guided Courthouse tours everyday at 2pm (except Sundays) and 10:30 am Monday, Tuesday, & Friday
 * Or explore the hallways, rooms, tower, and gardens on your own
 * Construction was completed in 1929 to celebrate "Old Spanish Days"
 * Designed by architect William Mooser III in iconic Spanish Colonial Revival style
 * Replaced a Greek Revival courthouse damaged in a 1925 earthquake *
 * The large sandstone fountain near the entrance is called "The Ocean"
 * El Mirador is the name of the 85-foot Observation Tower
 * Visitors have an option of stairs or elevator to ascend the tower
 * It offers a 360° view of Santa Barbara including the Santa Ynez Mountains, the historic Mission, the Pacific Ocean, and nearby Channel Islands
 * Murals throughout the courthouse depict scenes from Santa Barbara's history
 * The 2nd floor Mural Room is popular for weddings and historic romance
 * Also, check out the Law Library on the 2nd floor
 * The Hall of Records has a skylight, balconies, and heavy Spanish-style furniture
 * The Sunken Gardens are the most used wedding location in the city
 * The Clock Gallery is only open by appointment
 * The bells in the tower are fake, but sound perfect every time

Painted ceilings, heavy wood doors, large murals, imported tiles, decorative medallions, iron chandeliers, and the red terra cotta roof of the Santa Barbara County Courthouse harken back to a rustic era while providing a reason to actually plan a visit to a real courthouse.

"Sir, please tell the jury what you witnessed on the eve of the event"

"I saw people. Happy people using this courthouse for their phones and their instagrams. A sight to behold indeed sir"

I attended a wedding here in the spring, the weather was great and the venue is unique and cute. The outside especially has some beautiful gardens and architecture for wedding photos and backdrops. It's also walking distance from other points in santa barbara.

This lovely Spanish Colonial Revival style building was built in the early 1920s. The building is surrounded by lush green lawns and a beautiful tropical garden. I love the architecture of this building, it is so intricate and detailed. The ceilings inside the building are hand painted and one can see giant murals on the walls. The tiles on the floor and the old style phone booths give such an ancient touch to the Courthouse. It made me feel like I'm completely in a different era.

There are a lot of weddings and special events that take place here all year around and I can see why. This place is picturesque and it is given that all the pictures are going to turn out to be memorable and beautiful. This Courthouse is still fully functional and is an office space for many lawyers. There are many law offices around this building too.

Definitely, make sure that you see the beautiful view of the city from the topmost floor. Every angle of the view gives you something different to see. It is beautiful!

Take the time for a quick stop at the beautiful Spanish-Colonial style Santa Barbara County Courthouse. Built in 1929, it is an iconic landmark with a distinctive four sided clock tower, Mediterran red tile roofs, graceful arches, fountains and lush sunken garden. Although tours are currently closed, download the app for a virtual self-guided tour of the exterior and grounds.

The Great Arch leading to the sunken gardens was inspired by historic Roman triumphal arches. The adjacent "Spirit Of The Ocean" fountain was designed and sculpted of porous sandstone at a cost of  $5,000 in 1929. It did not weather well and was resculpted as a replica in 2011 at a cost of over $730,000. It signifies Santa Barbara's closeness to the ocean. The four-sided clock tower, known as "El Mirador," houses one of the last remaining working tower clocks made by the Seth Thomas Clock Company. 

The sunken gardens and grounds are filled with magnolias, palm trees and birds of paradise. Beautiful and peaceful for a quick stop on a sunny day.

The best place to have a courthouse wedding! The staff is extremely helpful and friendly. The ceremony is intimate and beautiful. Would absolutely recommend having your civil ceremony here! 
Thank you Conn for marrying us and recommending all the best spots around the courthouse to take pictures! Couldn't have asked for a better experience.
I starting coming to this iconic location when it was The Bakery and I loved it. Unfortunately, nothing that tried to replace its unique location and menu was successful until now. Thank you!

My next stop after eating at the moby dick restaurant was the courthouse. 
It was not that far away. 
We parked at the 75 min free parking spot, which was convenient.
I really enjoyed looking around the courthouse.
Such a fine, beautiful place! And it is free too!
There were many tourists here other than my aunt and me. There was a wedding here when we were present.
One of the door opened all of a sudden and this couple who wed came out and started making out in front of me. And people were blowing horns and making noises. Having a wedding here would be very nice.
I enjoyed the fancy, decorative tilework and i also liked going up the view tower. 
What a view!! Viewing tower closed at 4:45 for us.
The courthouse is a nice spot to take many pictures, and it was a serene, quiet, lovely place. 
I really liked it, and recommend this place for anyone. Whether you are a tourist or not.

I had the privilege of jury duty and had a really good time here in this old building. 

During breaks you can get your exercise walking around the grounds and up and down the many staircases. There is art on the walls, old furniture and fixtures, beautiful tile work, and pottery. I walked up the stairs to the clock tower and they have a cool area where you can see the mechanics of this grand old clock actually working. Once you get to the top you have a 360 degree view of our beautiful city.

Now, the case was civil and kind of boring but the judge was funny and filled empty time with great old stories. I met some very interesting folks, got educated, plus I got a paycheck that might pay for lunch for two at Paradise!

If you get the chance to serve...do it!

One Of The Most BEAUTUFUL Public Propertys In The World!  
A must see on your  visit to S.B.   Free, easy access, plenty of parking, a 360 view @ the top of the clocktower.   Breathtaking photo ops everywhere !   if you're lucky you might come across a nice deputy sheriff that can also give you some facts.
 *tip- donate .50 to the very informative & friendly lady behind the desk for a colored guide book w/map,   titled.. a Historic Masterpiece.

A hidden Santa Barbara MUST SEE.

The price of admission to view the glorious murals, clockroom, and viewing tower? Free! My favorite price.

We cannot show you the murals because of their age, they cannot be photographed with flash photography.  But they are beautiful and a gem in Santa Barbara's crown.

While there is an elevator up to the third level, you will get a great calf and quads muscles workout to make the climb yourself all the way up to the viewing tower and back down.  You definitely want to bring your camera long with you.  The views are spectacular and you will encounter visitors from around the globe whilst up at the top.

The courthouse also has a beautiful sunken garden in the back area which locals like to eat their lunches at.  Super tall pine tree and palm tree back here, taller than the flag poles. Peaceful and beautiful.

Its entrance is right across from the back of the Art Museum and Public Library.  We happened upon it while parking to visit the art museum.  Glad we left room for serendipity and adventure on vacation or we would have missed this unique gem.

Vintage-looking in style, this courthouse dates back to 1929, when there was an architectural movement in Mexico and the U.S. that brought back Baroque influences seen in Spanish colonial-era buildings. Since there was a large earthquake here in 1925, it's no surprise that many structures in Santa Barbara date from the late 1920s and reflect this then-popular Spanish Colonial Revival style.

While the gems inside like the Mural Room and the clock tower aren't open to the public at the moment, there's much to appreciate from the outside. The main entrance features a grand archway with a Spanish inscription (there's also equivalent English text off to the side) trumpeting God's creation of the land and man's creation of cities. Ornate figures and an elaborate fountain complete the entry. As you pass through the arch, note elaborate tiled mosaics and a beautiful wooden ceiling. While artistically significant, some of these works might not have stood the test of time, given society's slow but ongoing evolution towards a more enlightened and "woke" consciousness.

It's not just about the building, which remains a working courthouse to this day - go through the archway and you'll discover a vast, beautiful courtyard full of gardens. Even in current circumstances, it's a perfect, large spot for distanced outdoor weddings and events. Thanks to Richie D. for pointing out this local treasure here on Yelp!

What an extraordinarily beautiful building.  The architecture for this courthouse is unbelievable.  So many intricate carvings!  The ceilings were beautiful!  Then going up to the tower - what a view indeed.  Stairs, stairs and stairs - then the surprise and reward of a splendid view of Santa Barbara from the top.  This is a must for everyone - it is worth your time.

The Courthouse has one of the best views of Santa Barbara. The outside of it is so beautiful and has a huge grassy lawn on which is perfect for picnics. The day we went there was a huge wedding going on. 

There is a lot of history and there are many floors to it. It's actually still functioning as an actual courthouse as well. Every hour the clock still rings. There is also someone also down in the section where the mechanics of the clock are. They are there to explain everything and how it all works.

Once you past this section you will be on the observation deck. This deck provides some of the most incredible views of Santa Barbara. You can see everything from here. Sometimes you can even see the Channel Islands! Even as a local I come here sometimes just to relax and remind myself that I live in such a beautiful place!

Such a beautiful piece of architecture in Santa Barbara. I think it warrants a must-visit if you are in SB. The Spanish Colonial Revival style is so prevalent in the city, but the courthouse is the paragon of all the buildings here. The main facade of the main entrance is absolutely stunning and monumental. The surrounding area  has lush gardens to stroll through and a large grassy area that is perfect for afternoon naps, picnics with friends, and scenic backdrops. 

I saw a lot of people stopping by to enjoy the sunshine after work hours or sitting in the grass to enjoy a quick lunch. A wedding photo shoot was going on nearby.

There's some free parking around the area. However, tours and access inside the courthouse is currently unavailable due to the pandemic.

Love going here when we can, my Dad considers it one of his favorite places - going to guess that has a lot to do with it being free! The building and grounds are beautiful. Do what you can to check out the clock tower in the Bisno Schall Clock Gallery and see the clock. You can only see it Monday, Wednesday and Friday from 11:45 a.m. to 1 p.m., and on Saturdays from 1 p.m. to 2 p.m. There's a great mural that surrounds the clock.
Also make sure and take the stairs (you can also take the elevator) all the way to the top, you get a lovely view.

Ive been here walking around and it's a beautiful wedding site. my friends will be getting married here tomorrow. 

lots to see. it looks very historic and its so clean. i love the look of this place and wouldnt mind getting married here. we walked from our hotel and its so pretty. 

can't wait to update to let you know how it looks when there is a wedding taking place!

i planked here too.

We decided to take a day trip via Amtrak to Santa Barbara to see the city and enjoy travel via the train. We searched on Trip Advisor for things to do and the number one listing was the County Courthouse? What? So we took the little trolley, only .50 per person unless you have Amtrak receipt and then it's FREE. We got off at the 1200 block of State St and walked a block over to the County Courthouse. The grounds are gorgeous. After walking around the grounds we went inside to the Mural room. It was beautiful and very impressive mural of the history of Santa Barbara. This is actually still a court room and we lucked out to see it since there wasn't a trial going on. Then we walked up the stairs to the tower stopping on the 3rd level to see the clock room. Very cool! It went underwent an extensive restoration after being locked away as a storage room. Then we walked up to the tower, (you can take an elevator) but you'd miss the clock room. Anyway, the top is stunning with 360 degrees of vistas of Santa Barbara and beyond. The building is full of ornate decor including art, tile, lights, woodwork, etc. This is a visual feast to be had! AND it's FREE to see!

TIP:  It's closed on Sunday, not sure about Saturday!

How to Get Married at the Santa Barbara Courthouse: http://bycherryphotography.com/blog/how-to-get-married-at-the-santa-barbara-courthouse/

Santa Barbara Courthouse is one of the most jaw-dropping gorgeous courthouses that you can get married in. I have shot full weddings and small civil ceremonies/elopements in which the couples have come in from Canada, the East Coast, and even England! Everyone has said that as soon as they saw photos, they HAD to get married here. The building architecture celebrates the city's Spanish heritage, is filled with natural sunlight and grand open hallways, you can walk around the grounds for over an hour and get so many photo ops that you'll feel like your photos were taken at 10 different locations.

I love this place!

Luckily, I've never been here on business  :)

I believe it's one of the most beautiful places in Santa Barbara.

-The garden area is wonderful.  A great place to relax and lounge for an hour or so.
-Parts of the building are open to the public, you can take a walk around and enjoy the beautiful building.
-The view from the tower is probably one of the best views in the city.  A "high tower" with 360 degree views of the city.

I highly recommend spending an hour or two here if you visit Santa Barbara.

Peace and harmony is what you'll feel when you visit this area. There is so much to see and do. You can bring your lunch and blanket, hang out and enjoy the quiet surroundings of beautiful architectural buildings. This is a Santa Barbara gem, and a historical landmark you don't want to miss. If you're in the area, stop on by! It's metered parking, so make sure you bring change. Worth the drive.

Love this place, It's like a small museum that is open to the public. Not sure who built it or designed it but they got it right. Been here 3 times and all 3 times somebody was getting married. Reminds me of a Mission. They keep the grounds immaculate. Must stop.

What a gorgeous piece of architecture and so far I consider this my favorite spot in the city!  The perfectly lush green expanses of lawn surrounding this grand building are calling my name for a picnic and a childlike roll in the grass, LOL.  The view from the top is incredible! Got lots of Instagram-worthy shots from this vantage point high above Santa Barbara and visual proof of why this expanse of the coast is referred to as the American Riviera.  The (free) elevator takes you right up into the tower, otherwise if you are not hanging with a whiney 7yo, you can walk up the 5 flights of stairs for a little workout with your view.

Definitely must-do if in SB!

I never thought about reviewing a place like this, which makes me think.  I must bring a notebook with me "everywhere!"

The grounds here are incredible.  So nice you'd love having a picnic here.  Trust me you will.  Last time we were here we saw a bride and groom having pictures taken.  How romantic!  

Also make sure you get up to the top of the tower and get a gander of the city.  Truly a sensory treat.

The Santa Barbara Courthouse is beautiful!  Definitely take one of the docent led tours.  Not only will you hear about the Courthouse, but Santa Barbara history.  The tour is free, but do leave a donation.  We took the 2pm tour after eating a huge lunch - perfect way to walk some of it off.  Make sure you go up to the clock tower, the views are spectacular!

This place is one of my favorite buildings in Santa Barbara and (why not?) in California.

The Spanish style of architecture is breathtaking, the lawn is spotless, the vegetation is lush, and the views from the top are amazing. You can go up to the rooftop daily till 5 pm. It's something you need to do if you visit Santa Barbara. Also, check the first level, the decorations, the paintings, the cool Moorish style balconies and chandeliers. This place is one of the kind.

There are nice restrooms inside, it's free to visit and an elevator will take up to the terrace to see great views of the ocean and of the City.

This place has good energy, sometimes there are events on the lawn, a nice wedding, a festival, a zombie dance around Halloween, an outdoor screening in the summer or just a cool guy playing flamenco at his guitar, because...

The building reminds me of Spain and each time I visit Santa Barbara, I always stop by. This place never ceases to amaze me with its beauty!

I recently have discovered the serenity of SB Courthouse and Sunken Gardens. Santa Barbara is a beautiful  town, and even our courthouse can be the site of romantic nights and movies under the stars. I have thoroughly enjoyed the UCSB Arts and Lectures Hitchcock movie series this summer, and will make sure to enjoy the sunken gardens for picnics with friends while venturing downtown.

Gorgeous historic facilities in Santa Barbara! So nice that they allow visitors to wander the echoey halls at leisure to enjoy to architecture, artwork, and the placards throughout that explained the historical significance of the various pieces and spots in and around the buildings.

Take the stairs up to the clock tower. The spectacular views from up there are well worth the exercise!

We enjoyed the visit, but I was a little disappointed that there wasn't a tour or docent around to answer questions for our group.

Just got married here.  There were no lines and in fact they accepted us a few minutes earlier than our appointment.  Our officiant was Gabe.  He was very professional and was such a help to us.  He was even able to provide us with official copies of the marriage cert THAT DAY! 

The grounds are beautiful and we took ample photos inside and out.  Be sure to be out of there by 4:30-5 p.m.  The custodial staff was YELLING at us to leave.  We were in too good of a mood to let them get us down.  We just sent our guests across the street to the French Table for a break with appetizers and drinks.
Threw a baby shower there! The whole staff was amazing and super kind to the whole party!

This is my favorite pool ever, and I have a swam in a lot of them over my decades of life. 

One of the things I love is that I always get my own lane, so I have no idea what the person meant about having the share lanes'. And 5+ years I have only ever had to share a lane twice!

The staff is friendly, and the pool is partly treated by O-Zone so my swimsuits last longer and I don't smell like chlorine when I leave.

The water is always super clean and just the right temperature and I just love it there.
Ha, a swim pass is $550. Its a tiny pool at the Junior high in Carpinteria, who is running this place? Seems very poorly managed.
Love swimming here, just, I have a hard time getting out of the pool but the step ladder helps!

No kids allowed?? Not cool. Can't find a pool anywhere that will let me bring my daughter for a dip. You guys should really change that. When i was a kid we swam in every public pool from goleta to carp.
If only it wasn't so crowded when I get off work - between the swim masters and the Waterpolo team workouts it's usually two per lane and isn't relaxing to swim

name: Surf Happens
categories: Summer Camps, Shopping, Surf Schools, Fashion, Fitness & Instruction, Surfing, Active Life, Specialty Schools, Surf Shop, Education

yelp_reviews.text:
Had a surf lesson today for my 11 year old twins and their friend, Chris Keet owner.  He was terrific, kids attended surf camp last year (5 days) and said they learned nothing compared to what they learned in two hours from Chris.  He was funny, validating and informative!  I would love to come back to one of his camps! What a great guy and my kids are happily surfing!

He also loaned us his fabulous camera, I have 343 pictures of my kids surfing PRICELESS!

These guys are the best. I've done a surf school before and it was all a bit disorganized. These guys are well staffed, well prepared, great with kids and adults alike. They are super careful with the children, as well as enthusiastic and energetic. They have enough instructors to insure that everyone is well attended and no one feels ignored.
My party of 8 had a fabulous morning. We couldn't be happier.

We did a 2 hour family lesson. My 10 year old was able to stand on her own after an hour.  My 4 year old would not get into the water.  We had 4 different people trying.  Finally Chris  came over and he was able to get him to ride two waves.  He was so good with my son.  We had a wonderful time. Thinking of coming back with my daughter to do a women's week in the future.  Our two instructors, Ryan and Glen were very nice, and funny.

Excellent customer service! I was in town for a short visit and called last minute on Friday and was amazed they could fit me in for a private lesson on Saturday morning. Ryan was a terrific instructor. The wetsuit was warm, clean, and dry and I didn't get wet at all. Ryan brought me two different boards so that I could use the one that suited me best and move to a shorter one when I was ready for greater maneuverability. I will definitely use this company again if I am back in Santa Barbara.

My 13-year-old daughter and I arranged a lesson with Chris on May 11, 2013.  We called on short notice and he was able to meet us for a 2-hour lesson right away.  Chris was great.  He was friendly, knowledgeable, and an excellent teacher.  He quickly recognized areas we needed coaching, and gave good advice that made a difference.  He took pictures at the end of the session and emailed them to us, just as he promised.  We will definitely want to take another lesson with him next time we are in Santa Barbara.  Highly recommended!

This is a wonderful company. My son has done the stationery camp and I plan on doing the woman's lessons. The instructors are wonderful young people that surf and they are very accommodating, knowledgeable and the gal that runs the office is awesome! We will come back as much as we can. He's done both spring and fall sessions.

I moved to California to be a rad surfer chick and this was my first stop enroute to realizing my dream.

I signed up for the Adult Clinic on weekends in August, I think it ended up being $400 for 6 sessions. The sessions were intimate with just about 2 other surfers and one surf instructor. Tom is great, friendly, patient, passionate, and encouraging. He taught us how to look up swell and read the waves. We would do some yoga and strengthening moves before hitting the water, and once in the water we'd work on paddling, turns, and turtle rolling before catching waves. The instruction really helped me feel more comfortable surfing and I felt it gave me great basic knowledge to step it up to the next level.

There's a surf divas camp for people like me so I'll definitely have to check it out soon.

My daughter took surf camp for a week with Surf Happens in July 2013.  Pros: she learned to surf (which is great!), they have a nice location on a good beach.  Cons: the counselors were really underwhelming, the camp seemed disorganized and not well-tailored to different ages and personalities, and I am not sure that the instruction was really all that good.  This camp was MORE expensive than a number of others in town, and they nickel and dimed us the whole week (no lunch provided, parents bring all food for last day BBQ, pictures are $40 if you want them of your child surfing).  Sorry, but I think A-frame or other SB surf camps would be a better camp AND less expensive.

Brought more than half my Brownie Girl Scout troop to a four hour surf school today and it was AMAZING! Every girl had a blast and now they all want to surf! 
Seeing the joy and confidence on all their faces when they stood up (they all did multiple times!) was the best! Plus they got valuable lessons about how to care for the ocean. 
I was jealous I wasn't enrolled! Can't say enough good things about them :)

We LOVE Surf Happens! My husband and son first took lessons here, then my son did Surf Camp. The staff is incredibly friendly and patient. My son went from not sure about surfing to LOVING it - awesome given we live in Santa Barbara. Going to try to squeeze in another week of camp this summer. Highly recommend!!! Thanks SH!

First Class operation.
They know how to teach.
Take great care of our kids.
No skimping anywhere.
Wonderful staff.
Exceptionally high standards for the lunch they serve and the products they use.

We absolutely love Surf Happens and are so grateful to Chris, Jenny, Ally, Jade and all the other amazing employees and instructors for giving our kids such a great experience at their camps and after school programs for the last few years. They run a well-organized business, are just great people, and have really inspired and nurtured our daughter's passion for surfing.

On a recent trip to Santa Barbara, we had the most amazing experience at Surf Happens. The owner came out to instruct our lesson personally, and not only was he SO engaging with my kids, but also allowed our two other friend's kids to join free or charge, even though they hadn't signed up. 

During the lesson, the instructors were so attentive and fun, it left such a wonderful impression on me and my girls! In fact, my youngest daughter was afraid to try surfing again after a previous bad experience, but the owner was such a natural with kids, and was able to connect with and encourage her, so she decided to give it another try at his urging, and ended up having a great time! 

It was truly such a great experience, and we can't wait to go back again!

We booked SurfHappens for a company event and were so impressed! They took care of absolutely everything for us, from catering to activities to equipment. Our team had a blast and would absolutely book with them again for future events.

I wouldnt personally book them. I made a booking and gave them all my details but did not get confirmation via email for a whole 24 hours inspite of calling them three times after making the book.. Every time i called i was assured that it will be emailed soon..

My husband and I had a great surf lesson with Nick at Surf Happens.  We booked the 90 minute private lesson for two people.  The location was great with waves perfect for us as beginners.  The online booking process was also easy to use with clear directions.  Would love to do this again next time we're in the area!
We  booked a private surf lesson for 3 kids and 2 adults. We got 3  instructors and everyone learned a ton and had a great time! Instructors  did a great job for us! Thanks!

They turned an old guy into a surfer

OK, sort of, but I rode the board on my own a couple times until I stuck the back fin into the beach... This was a bucket list item for me. I tried a Surf Happens neighbor 1st but couldn't get them to follow through on a lesson. In just 2 hours Surf Happens got me on my way to becoming a real surfer.

name: SEA Landing
categories: Event Planning & Services, Boating, Rafting/Kayaking, Active Life, Boat Charters

yelp_reviews.text: the employees were all nice and respectful to their customers. I rented out a two-people kayak for $30, and we were allowed to ride it for 3 hours, which is a pretty decent deal. I paddled the kayak around the port. it's a great place to kayak because there are areas where the waves are calm, and if you want, you can go out into the ocean and ride along the shores. I also saw seals just relaxing on a deck, which was a cool experience (wished i brought my phone to take pictures). Overall, it was a fun activity to do, especially during a warm sunny day :D

I love this place. our first time here. Cool, young instructors and very friendly. I rented a Jet Ski for 1 hour. it cost $ 135 with all the gears and tax. You get to take Jet ski out by yourself and enjoy up to 55 MPH on the open sea. Although I could not get past 30 MPH due to the waves or I would have flipped over.  they have Kayaks as well. it was $27 for 3 hours of tandem Kayak or $ 10 for 1 hour.
+ parking cost $ 3 with Validation for the whole day at the Marina.
bad service and they alway change the schedule without telling you ahead. They.do.whatever they want to do because they are the only one in SB

I've rented kayaks all over California and Hawaii, and they all typically charge you at least $25/hour and up.

SEA Landing: $15 for 3 hours...unheard of!!!!! That's a deal!!!! :D

The caveats? There's no lesson, but ocean kayaking in sit-on-top kayaks is pretty self explanatory.  

Staff is friendly, but no service.  You just go up to the desk, pay, get a seat back and lifevest, and then you walk down to the dock where you grab your kayak, set the seat, and pull it into the water yourself.  But if you've never kayaked before and/or are out of shape, I can see this being a problem, though I would assume one of the staff would help you if you asked. 

There's one 2 Star Yelp review because one Yelper got pissed that they don't allow dogs...I love dogs and own a dog, but personally I would rather not get my hands, legs, or butt on doggie pee or dried poop.

Solid place to rent kayaks, jetskis and more. Friendly and helpful over the phone as well as on land and in water, they are competitively priced, and easy to work with.  Be aware (and this goes for all such companies in the Harbor), that they close by 7pm. You'll have to wrap up your 3 hour tour in time to bring it back. 

Kayaks and oars were in good condition, and so were we upon our return to the shore.  They offer fishing tours, too, which look awesome. Whale watching, too. Plan ahead, though, because these things book up.
This place is great. The crew are awesome, makes for a more relaxed fishing experience.

I just spent four days living aboard Sea Landing's ship TRUTH during a Sierra Club trip to the Channel Islands, 5/5-5/8, 2018.  There were 26 of us guests on board. FYI, we had a variety of ages on the boat - from folks in their 20s to their 80s. 

First, being a diver, I do have a gimlet eye for the quality of the piloting and crew teamwork on any ship I board. These would be the keys to our safety - and fun.  And it's not something you always see... On the TRUTH, the quality of both were set at the highest level in my experience.  

Captain Davey's low key, humorous style, and his highly skillful leadership, extended through to his happy and obviously experienced crew. Everything was carried out well... And the TRUTH was a very functional ship for our trip. It was clean and ready to roll on arrival - and kept clean throughout our trip. 

The weather was tough on the way out. Through savvy piloting by Captain Davey, the hits and swerves were lessened as he skillfully gave us the steadiest ride possible.  He proved utterly devoted to our comfort - and our desire to have the experiences planned - as much as the sea and weather would permit us. 

Many of us had kayaks on board and there were also many transfers to different islands. Great skill was shown by the crew in getting us on and off the ship every time - including with folks who needed extra help. We were skillfully piloted close up to amazing sea caves and sea lion haul outs at every opportunity.  Captain. Davey is intimately familiar with the Channel Islands.

What particularly delighted me was that I was so generously welcomed to the bridge, where it is cool to hang out both for the view of the running of the ship - and the location of the moment. That time gave me even more insight into the ship's operations and Captain Davey's excellent leadership style. 

In closing, the entire crew of the TRUTH was a joy to work with.   To my surprise, the food was superb!  Kudos to cook Jasmine and her helpers! To a person, again and again throughout our trip, people spoke of their great satisfaction with Captain Davey and the crew of the TRUTH - and how great a trip this was to the Channel Islands. 

In closing, I would also like to say that the ground staff for Sea Landing was also exceptional.  James, I believe, was so helpful in arranging for my kayak rental.  The entire operation at Sea Landing impressed me greatly.  If you are wise, you will travel with the folks at Sea Landing.

The crew of Coral Sea is truly exceptional. Everyone was really helpful and encouraging. This was my first time fishing and I knew nothing about it beforehand but they will teach you and are more than willing to help and give pointers on what to do. Mad props to the crew for really going above and beyond to help everyone the best they can and making it a really smooth experience.

5 stars!! It was $15 for three hours, everyone was very helpful and was asking if we had any questions. Not the typical LA "I don't give a shit attitude". The only minus was that we had to lift the kayaks and place them into the water ourselves and when we came back we had stack up the kayaks ourselves. But for $15 for three hours is well worth it! Definitely recommend this place if your looking to save some money.

I have only ever taken advantage of their kayaking rental. Singles are $15 for three hours. Doubles are $25 for three hours. What great deals! This is very inexpensive and I have never taken up the entire three hours. They are very hands off and they just give you a lifevest and seatcushion and tell you where the kayaks and paddles are. It is all very easy though (bring someone who has gone kayaking before). I like to go with friends and kayak in the singles - it is so much fun and the scenery is especially bright and beautiful!

I left Santa Maria 1 1/2 hours before the boat took off and ran into traffic on 101. Tried to call them to let them know what was happening but no one ever answered. By the time I got to SB I had about 4 minutes left. Called again and spoke with someone finally. Told her the problem but that I was just entering the parking lot. About a minute later she called me back and said she didn't know if they could wait.
