I told her we were running across the lot as there wasn't any place close to park and that I could see the boat to please wait. My grandson made it to the dock and told the men that we were here to wait but he said that the captain (or someone) told the guys to just leave! Can't believe 30 seconds would have made such drastic difference. Needless to say my 2 grandsons were very disappointed. The people inside said I could re-book for tomorrow! Like I will ever use them again for anything... I THINK NOT! I should specify this was for Condor express whale watching boat.

Kayaks are cheap (2 stars)! Thats a plus, though location isn't so great (lost a star) cuz you spend most of your time navigating boats and coming in and out of harbor. They dont give you a map or any real information what to do when we are out at sea (lost another star). I gave another star because when we did ask for help (a group of 9) they came out and helped us unload and setup each kayak without any hesitation. And someone was there to recieve us as well.

Great place to rent kayaks.. 25 for three hours. took my friends granddaughter. she had a blast! couldn't get her out of the boat. we saw sea lions and some fish in the water. boated all over the ocean. calm waves and friendly people. its a must do and good exercise

Took a 3 day trip with Capt. Dave, Crew Alex,Eli,Larissa (sorry about spelling),& Maggie. Wow how great they were. Answered all questions, shared so much information. What a wonderful trip. 6 stars and deserving.

I like coral sea very much.  And I am Coral Sea's fans.  Especially the deckhand "Christian",  "Larry",  "Rafa".  They always did the good job.  Their action are both very quick and helpful.  

More over,  the captain is excellent due to he always bring us to the good fishing area.  And he try his best to find the fishing area.  

In california,  Coral Sea is the best fishing boat which I recommend.  Due to I have tried lots of the landing in LA county,  Ventura or Orange County.  NO BOAT IS BETTER THAN "CORAL SEA".  Always make us enjoy fishing and make us full tank. And always make us feel very very tired due to we catch lots of the big fish. 

Last word I want to say : Coral sea,  we love you... haha
A good place to rent yacht, speed boat and jet ski. It seemed a bit overpriced than other places, but the horse power was great. Have fun on the water!

Love this place I been coming in for a very long time. Tandem kayak $25 for 3 hours or $45 all day amazing. They have all prices on their website and they rent more than kayak. They also have whale watching and other fun stuff. Looking for something fun to do in Santa Barbara I totally recommend this place.

We love to kayak and actually paddleboarded for the first time with this company.  Great experience!  Prices were very reasonable, staff was so friendly and easy returns.  We were out on the water in no time, enjoying every minute of our time with the equipment we rented and they were so easygoing with the return.  They didn't question our time at all.  We just dropped out stuff and went on our way.  If only all businesses could be so laid back!!
Great place to rent a boat. Friendly staff. Me and my family rented a boat and had a blast doing so. Prices are reasonable and would definitely come back again.

I took my 3 grandkids fishing a couple for the first time fishing on a boat. We fished on the Stardust, with Corey as skipper, Larry  and Gavin on deck.  My grandson is  7 years old so fishing in deeper water was a challenge, both Larry and Gavin were very encouraging and helpful to not only my grandson but to all of us. My grand daughters and grandson caught  plenty of fish. Corey worked hard to find the fish spots and the results were everyone on the boat caught as many fish as allowed. Good memories were made for our family, I can't thank the Stardust crew enough for making our trip special. I highly reccomend  the Stardust if you are considering a fishing trip and bring your kids or grandkids or a young neighbor kid. Oh , yeah Larry makes a great breakfast burrito and a super good cheese burger.

minus several stars for the no dog policy. I was pretty upset my sweet little dog was not welcome to ride on the kayak. Other kayak businesses have allowed me to have my dog and I couldn't understand why it would be a problem. =( The price is good and the hands off approach is nice too. So 3 stars for my first visit, but 1 star for my last (due to the no dog policy) and not being able to go out.

Went kayaking the other day and it was incredible!  I went on a Saturday afternoon around 4pm.  You simply give them a credit card and they hand you a life jacket and send you on your way.  The boats are sit-on-tops so be prepared to get a little wet.  We made our way to Ledbetter Beach and about half way there a group of dolphins surfaced about 15-20 feet away from us and then continued to jump and play in the distance.  I managed to catch a wave onto shore (surf-kayaking they call it) and then left the boats for a stroll.  On the way back we circled a buoy piled with snoring and barking sea lions.  I got about a kayaks distance away from them (they are huge up close) but they paid little attention to me.  All in all we were out there for about 2 1/2 hours and it was only $15 each.  Great experience!

My fiancé and I love kayaking and paddle boarding every now and then. When you walk into SEA landing they make sure to take care of you right away! Usually you pay and you're on your way. It's great.

I am giving three stars because the service is a mixed bag. Today we were yelled at in a rather abrasive way when getting instructions. There is a way to give directions to people without being rude and condescending. While we had a great time out and the price was good my family definitely did not enjoy being talked down to.

Having the good fortune to have been raised in Santa Barbara I was lucky enough to stumble across this gem.  The Sea Landing not only has sport fishing but also scuba diving, whale watching, and island camping.  Every encounter with staff at the landing has exceeded my expectations.  If you are looking to explore the ocean in any capacity, do it here for a great time and a fair cost.

We rented out scuba gear from Sea Landing for a trip with Truth Aquatics. They were super helpful and all the gear was perfect. When there was confusion on our bill, it was quickly made right. They are steps from the Truth Aquatics boats and provide carts to get the heavy gear down to the boat. I wouldn't hesitate to rent from them again.

Came with a big group for my good friend's bday this past Sunday and they were very helpful and accommodating. Normally the kayaks are self serve because the place is small and cheap but they made an exception with us since there were so many of us.

We had so much fun kayaking and all for $25 for 3 hours!(we got the double instead of the single which is $15 for 3 hours)
Will definitely come again!
These people are morons.  The whale watching trip on the condor express was perfect but dealing with their contracted ticket brokers was a joke.

I had a great time on the 3/4 day boat Coral Sea. While the boat was sold out to their "Covid Capacity" of 30 people so we had enough room to social distance and fish at a distance from each other as well.

John, who was the Registration Host / Deckhand / Cook / "Over-Gapher" had us laughing all day. He and the captain (who was also funny) were roasting each other and the guests. 

To help keep guests out of the galley, due to Covid, they walked around to take food and drink orders and delivered it as well. 
The crew delivered every single fish to their guest's fish sack for them to help keep accurate fish counts and keep people from congregating too closely.
Rather than having guests wait in line to have their fish cleaned as some boats do, the crew took my order on how I wanted my fish cleaned and my bags of fish filets were chilled and waiting for me at the end of the trip. I had a BLT and nice nap on the ride back to the dock.
Both the stern and the bow Bait tanks were stocked with bait. Most boats only stock the stern tank.

Fish Totals: 10 Rock Fish (limit), 2 Ling Cod (limit) 4 Really big whitefish including a personal best.

I fish party boats about 8 times a year in Sand Diego, Long Beach, Oxnard, Ventura and Santa Barbara. This was definitely one of my best experiences

I love these guys!!  They took us out deep sea fishing over spring break, and we had the time of our lives.  We had two young boys with us, and the crew made it super fun for them.  The entire crew was attentive and interactive, and they all clearly love their jobs.  It looked like they were all having as much fun as we were.  They answered all of our questions thoroughly and helped out with whatever we needed.  They had to untangle our lines countless times and never made us feel silly for our clear lack of skill and experience.  Untangling the lines of inexperienced fishermen cannot be a fun part of the job, but you wouldn't know it when working with this crew.  This was a very fun experience, and we plan to visit Santa Barbara again just to take another trip with them.  I always love being around folks who love what they do.  I never knew fishing could be so much fun!

They do not respond to emails to let you know they won't have jet skiing available when you tell them you are heading up there in a week with a big party to rent for your daughters birthday and when I call the people gave me the owners number to contact him because they said maybe he would come in for a big party. Asked him to let me know either way! No response! Very unprofessional. Saved a thousand dollars cash I was ready to spend . Won't even try to go there again ever! Bad business! And I emailed three times . I couldn't call because I'm a NICU nurse and was working in the day to make money to spend there!  Next time we will stay in OC for jet skiing!

I've been on a number of trips with the Sea Landing over the years, including whale watching and sport fishing. The staff has always been helpful, professional and courteous. I'm not sure if there are any other tour operators that compete with the Sea Landing, but with the way they do business there might as well not be.

My group came to have a fun sunday!
We wanted to rent a bout and a jet . 
But  Unfortunately the staff and the owner was not nice! 
No  Patience impatience, very rude.
Never coming back there!!!!

I came here last weekend with my boyfriend. We had a great time. The reason for the 4 rather than 5 stars is solely based on price. 
To jet ski it is $135 an hour with a $500 hold on your credit card. They offer no discount for going over 1 hour. But they are also good about giving you the needed time to taxi back into the harbor. 
I just felt the price was very high when you can do the same thing in other cities for cheaper. 
The people there are super friendly and helpful. 
We had a very fun time on our day trip to Santa Barbara.

In my opinion it starts with the person who answers the phone... The girl that answer was rude and just acted like she didn't give... I would say some words to describe her but I don't think the review would go through. 
She would be gone so fast if this was my business her head would spin... Not helpful and just pushed potential clients away... Lost my business.

If i could give them 10 stars, I would.

Been here twice for 3/4 day fishing. Both times were exceptional! Staffs don't play around here and they are very professional. Yesterday's trip, my fiance and I went on the Coral Sea and I loved it! With 22 anglers on a 58' boat and awesome weather, it was perfect! They even have bunks!! Caught up on some sleep since I had to wake up 3 am to head towards Santa Barbara. 

Service: Deckhands were running around like headless chickens to make sure their customers got what they needed (unhooked fishes, untangled lines, hooked your baits at times- serious!). Two deckhands on standby at the stern and one at the bow. I am amazed! Josh helped us out a lot!! Rafa (I am so sorry if I spelled anyone's name wrong), was great, too! 

Cook for the day, John. Came out to help during fishing times! When it was time to head back, he came around and took orders. 

Captain was great! He knew what he was doing! 

Thank for the awesome trip, guys! :)

Great place, great service, awesome deal. For $25 for a tandem kayak, for 3 hours (!!), you can kayak all around the beach and shore-lines of Santa Barbara, the pier and beyond. There's no hand-holding here: you pay, get  a life-vest, a paddle, a kayak, you're pushed in the water, and away you go. The service is excellent--very nice and attentive. You can leave any belongings with them, and make sure to dress to get wet. Can't wait to go back!

Sunday a group of friends joined the Cast and Crew of the Condor Express for the last Whale  Watching  Tour of the season. We had such a Great time. 
Once we were underway I made my way to the Galley for Food and Drink. Kelly our Galley Master took such Great Care of us. I started with the Condor Express Burger  and a glass of wine . We were able to enjoy the sightings of several Whales  and hundreds of Dolphins. The crew did such a awesome job explaining everything that was going on, making the Journey so much Fun. 
Thanks so much to so many who made this such a  Memorable Trip

If you wanna get racially profiled and get disrespected, just go there. Horrible customer service, they grabbed our bag out of our hand to see what's in it. And the owner cussed at us too for no reason.
Auch tolle Motorboote zum Ausleihen, sind gestern ein gute Stunde unterwegs gewesen, bei bestem Wetter und ruhiger See. Das Personal ist sehr freundlich und hilfsbereit.
The blonde lady is the most disrespectful person I've ever met. Fun time but bad attitudes by the employees

They have done it again! Always great going out on the Coral Sea with Rafa as captain. We have been on this boat too many times because of the great service received each time. It makes a big difference when you have a captain that knows what he is doing! 

I have got to give credit to deckhand, Martin. He made the trip fun with his nonstop humorous jokes and very quick on his feet to help us out when needed. Thank you!

Thursday, Aug 23rd 2012... 3:30ish pm 

My tip: RECOMMENDED!

We walked right over from our hotel and right onto the beach. (see my review of West Beach Inn) 

The price was a little high I thought... but hey, we're on vacation! For some reason, you want to spend your money like you own an oilfield. It was $135 per hour for a jetski (that's the -1 for the rating) and a $500 deposit on your credit card. But it is a large, expensive machine... I get it.

Every person on the staff we spoke with were super nice... so friendly, happy and polite. After we were explained the rules and regulations, which takes about five minutes total and even that was upbeat and friendly... it scares the hell out of you to even touch one of those things let alone ride it. (Speeding fines, proximity fines, bumping fines, capsizing fines... shit!)

We put our wetsuit on, strapped on our life jackets  and doubled up on a single large jetski. I highly recommend doubling up... it was a blast to drive and a blast trying to hang on.

One hour is all you need because it will beat you up from laughing the whole entire time! We zipped all over that ocean and once we got familiar with the beast we had that throttle wide open. You stop worrying about flipping it eventually, because it seems nearly impossible to do, although we weren't trying.

We returned to home base after one hour to the same smiling faces. It was a little hard to get off the jetski because our legs were like spaghetti noodles!

In fishing boat, you expect to have a fun day out in ocean whether you catch a lot fish or not.       The worst thing to experience is having a deckhand that has issue with Anger Management around you that you don't know when and what may happen, especially there are a few fillet knives so easy for him to reach.........

Considering Murphy's Law, I will think hard next time I want to be in any fishing boat.

I have been outdoorsman for 55 years. Hunting and fishing, mountaineering and diving.    I took a friend to fishing onboard Coral Sea Fishing Boat September 3, the sad day that a diver's boay had horrible accident.

It is also a day i will never forget how a pleasant fishing day can be ruined by a deckhand, Shawn.

Shawn is not only lack of people skill, he is lack of common couetesy and basic upbringing for least respect to elderly customers.   He has been rude and give unnecessary ridicule to customers, often to Asians and elderly.

Sportfishing boats are supposed to offer pleasant fishing experience, but a good day out in ocean could easily be ruined by an uneducated low level customer service.  

I could tell the chef Howard in Coral Sea was very gentle and was trying to be very helpful to offset the derogative behavior of Shawn.  Howard is good crew Coral Sea has to make me believe the skipper Rafa does try to maintain reasonable level of reputation.

I wish I picked Stardust fishing boat.    I had much better experience with Stardust.     A pleasant fishing day refreshes me for a month, but watching how Shawn destroys a nice Labor Day weekend of fishermen confirms to me how important good education is to all of us to understand how to be a good person.
Great crew and great Captain, Great Trip. I will fish here again later in summer!

If you're a novice angler this boat might be OK, but as an experienced angler no so much. The boat and crew was fine, but what ruined the trip was deckhand Shawn (condescending to many with zero  people skills). He's pretty drip dry with a bad attitude, kind of like a fish drill instructor in boot camp and the main reason why I'd never go back.

Why they won't let you put your fish in your own sack makes no sense to me. In all my years fishing I've never seen this, they just let the fish flop around the deck. Eventually they get picked up and hopefully put into to the correct sack however; errors occurred in this regard. My partner ended up with extras putting him over the limit while I was missing three fish. Had Fish & Game checked his bag he would have been cited.

I observed several small rockfish being filleted and then stripped out for bait. The landing says this practice is legal so I spoke with a Warden to clarify, unethical as it may be, as long as it's with in boat limits it's not a violation, just a shame. You could be Bill Dance, Zane Gray or even the World's greatest fisherman but if you fish this boat be prepared to be treated like a greenhorn, no thanks.
Absolutely a disgrace. TOURIST do not go to this place for a rental unless you want bad service and a jackass manager

I went here several times and it was all great in the past until right now they change the skipper

of Stardust.... ( I think the skipper's name is Correy? ) The whole crew sucks... When I catch

 fish no one come to help me unhook the fish and no one come here to pick up my fish. There

 were 5 fishes bouncing under my feet. So I finally open my mouth to tell them to pick up my

 fish and he finally did... And I HAVE TO OPEN 

MY MOUTH ASKING THEM TO PICK UP MY FISH AGAIN AND AGAIN BECAUSE SO MANY 

FISH KEEP BOUNCING UNDER MY FEET SEVERAL TIMES.  


The skipper were lazy... he just

put us in one spot and wait for like 30 minutes. NO FISH WERE BITING HE STILL PUT US 

THERE FOR NOTHING. He was sitting there and playing with his phone......  


My first time in Stardust gave me the best experience ever ( skipper keep changing spot to find 

the fish and everyone hit rockfish limit )  but right now Stardust change 

to a bullshit skipper he is really lazy and the deckhands are lazy as well. I was lucky I caught

3 rockfish that day. Some of the people next to me caught 0 ( one guy caught only a small 

rockfish and he release that little thing and thats it for him that day , and another guy just caught

0 , not even a fish ) 


I WILL NEVER GO TO STARDUST AGAIN IF THIS SKIPPER STILL WORKING THERE.

The people who work here are really nice and easy-going. Rented paddle boards here and they helped us get set up and on our way. They even gave some really helpful hints to us on how to manage the paddle boards. The prices are very affordable as well. One star off for not returning my phone calls and RSVP requests (via their website) ahead of time.  We ended up just showing up the day when we needed the paddle boards and there was no problem, but I would have liked some kind of confirmation beforehand. Either way, I recommend using them for your rentals.

So awesome! Girls weekend in Santa Barbara. Terri was great and so helpful!!! We paddle boarded and it was easier than we thought it would be. We even saw sea lions and dolphins swam right by us!!! A must do!!! So glad we did it. Thanks, Terri

The absolute best experience!!!

So much fun- we took a girls' trip, and this was a huge highlight of our Santa Barbara experience!!! We were all beginners- first time paddle boarding and Ryan was so patient and helpful through the whole trip. He helped us find the perfect spot with amazing scenery and great wildlife!!! We saw dolphins and seals and got a great workout too! We will definitely come back for more fun next time we are in town!!!

Amazing Experience! A must do for anyone living or visiting Santa Barbara. I took my friends Paddle-Boarding for the first time and they absolutely loved it. East Beach Rentals had a ton of selection for any skill set and we got some great tips from the rental guy. Thanks for such a great time!

East Beach Rentals is where to go! Get an umbrella a chair and a paddle board! Ryan and crew are so helpful and fun! Locally owned and operated! Bocce ball was the best! Paddle board yoga was amazing!

I called East Beach Rentals twice, just a few days before a long weekend with my wife in Santa Barbara (It was in July). I left a message both times, giving them my number and telling them day/time we were interested in renting kayaks. They never called me back. Eventually I gave up and called Paddle Sports Center in the harbor. (They set us up for reservations right away).
So fun and the gentleman was so helpful!  It is a beautiful beach and SUP is the way to experience it!  We will be back for sure!

Top Paddle board/ Kayak rental spot in SB. Great prices, beautiful beach, and all the employees seem to love what they are doing. Was hesitant to try paddle boarding but after a couple pointers by Brenyn I was up and feeling confident. Even got to see some dolphins as well. Overall great experience. Will definitely be back!

Really nice employees, we back over here from Santa Clarita
We are happy found this place , beach is super clean
Great staff! Helped get the girls going on standup paddle boards. I got to sit and relax with an umbrella and beach chair! Thanks for making this a wonderful start to our vacation.

So much fun!!!!!! We rented a double kayak and paddled out to a barge packed full of sea lions. Beautiful views. The best time we've had in a long time. Amazing service, super fun and memorable. Can't wait to go again. Definitely a must for everyone!

name: Santa Barbara Adventure Company
categories: Tours, Boating, Hotels & Travel, Rafting/Kayaking, Parks, Travel Services, Active Life

yelp_reviews.text: The kayaking tour at the Santa Cruz Island was great.  My husband and I had a wonderful time.  Jen, our tour guide, was helpful and informative.  We had a lot of fun exploring the caves.  We didn't expect much but this tour definitely exceeded our expectation.

Spectacular trip all around.  Erin was a phenomenal guide - we had one member of the group who felt sick from the boat ride out, so he did half the trip, then we picked her up and she got to enjoy the second half with us.  He really worked with us to ensure that we had the best time possible.  The Channel Islands are like visiting CA a hundred years ago - teaming wildlife, crystal clear waters, air so sweet and clean your lungs rejoice.  Was a memorable trip!  Reminders:  you pack in and out all your food and drinks, so be sure to bring garbage bags.  Don't forget the dramamine and sunscreen!
The Tasty Cupcake & Wine tour was the best! Their customer service was great, wine was great & so were the cupcakes. We even got to take cupcakes home as a part of the package!!
Hector was an awesome guide through the sea caves of Santa Cruz Island!!

First time paddleboarding. 3 women from the East Coast..so we were nervous. We were newbies to paddleboarding.

Grant was our instructor and he was amazing and patient and has the best Aussie accent (it's real too!). We had great instruction on the ground and safety instruction before hitting the water. We did really well and owe it all to Grant and his individual attention and not pushing us beyond our capabilities. We ventured farther out to sea where the waters were a little bumpy and we were just determined to stay standing. 2 of us fell numerous times but had the best time. Grant brought lunch from Trader Joe's. yum. we loved it. We woud recommend this company and Grant to everyone; in fact, we already have shared it with others.

These guys rock! I originally try to book with aqua sports, but they were booked solid. They referred me to Santa Barbara Adventure Company and I could not have been more satisfied. Our guide Adam was professional and extraordinarily informative. You could tell he had a real passion for his job and shared his knowledge of the history of the island and its local inhabitants. I seriously considered bringing my own kayak and going it alone but I'm so glad I didn't. I went in areas that I never would have considered, but never felt forced to do so. Our group had approximately's five kayaks and all of us were novices. I consider myself an intermediate just because I'd been in kayaks before but after the trip I realized how much I really didn't know! Adam was very patient and never forced anyone to do anything they weren't comfortable doing, and I never felt rushed. These guys deserve six stars. I give Santa Barbara Adventure Company my highest rating!

I went on the wine tour in June with 4 people and we were all very pleased. The service was amazing, the tour was fun, and the company was very accomodating. I highly reccomend using SB Adventure Company for your wine tour!

We had a great time on the sea kayaking tour by Santa Cruz island! We were lucky enough to see TONS of dolphins swimming by our boat on the way there as well as a whale. The instructors were great and the caves were pretty cool.

My GF and I went up to Santa Barbara for a vacation and some wine tasting.  I found the Santa Barbara Adventure Company online and booked the Cupcake and Wine tour because my GF really likes cupcakes.

We had never been to Santa Barbara before, and the SB Adventure Company tour was great.  They picked us up at our hotel promptly and we drove around to get 2 more couples.  The driver, Adam, took us to the Santa Ynez valley and 3 different wineries.  Adam took care of all of the arrangements once we arrived, and after the second tasting we had a delicious lunch.  

The cupcake tasting was actually paired with the third wine tasting.  While I enjoyed the cupcakes, it was a little hard to focus on both the cupcakes and the wine (as the previous tastings were have some accumulated effects).  That being said, I would definitely recommend this tour and getting some extra cupcakes for the drive home.

We went to Santa Cruz island for sea cave kayaking on Memorial Day. Amazing experience! The guide is nice and professional. We kayak through many cool caves! Also saw many cute wildlife. They provide all great stuff/equipment we need and taught/guided us useful skill. When you come to Channel Island National Park, you must kayak to see all those amazing sea caves and definitely consider SB Adventure!
Excellent! Kenji was thoroughly professional and safety conscious, very knowledgeable about a wide variety of topics, and a lot of fun to hang out with.

We booked the coastal Kayak tour around Santa Barbara on July 3rd, 2016. 

Pros: Our guide was great, very knowledgable and had lots of great facts to share. The  gear was great and kayaks themselves were well taken care of and easier to handle than others.  Was expecting just a simple Deli lunch, but was surprised to get salads, wraps, fresh fruit, chips and cookies that were all pretty good!  Kayaking under Stern's Pier was a unique perspective and fun. 

Cons: We felt that the tour wasn't really as advertised.  Website says the whole tour would go from 10-2.  In reality, we didn't start with instructions until about 10:15 and didn't get into the water until closer to 11. We weren't too worried about the delay once were told we'd be back between 2 and 3, however, we were back on the landing at 1:55, pretty disappointing. The "secluded beach" was actually the Leadbetter Beach, which is next to Santa Barbara Yacht Club...which is about 1/2 a mile from the Sea Landing. 

We did enjoy ourselves, the conditions were good, and our guide was great, but it definitely wasn't what we thought we were signing up for and would not recommend this company to others.
Went on the Refugio State Beach kayaking tour. Guides were kind, prepared, knowledgable, and interesting. A nice adventure!

My friends and I booked the bike wine tasting tour and it was a great experience. The staff was very friendly and accommodating. The complete bike ride from one winery to another was very pleasant and the scenery was beautiful. I highly recommend this tour! The first winery stop was awesome and the staff there was very informative and helpful with their wine selection. We made our way to our 2nd winery where we had lunch and the scene there was beautiful and it gave us a lot of opportunities for pictures. 
I highly recommend comfortable clothes, tennis shoes, and girls might want to consider braiding your hair or something because the bike ride was a nice workout.

SB Adventure Co is amazing! I first posted this on tripadvisor, but I was so happy with the experience I thought I'd add it here, too:
I had always wanted to try kayaking, and SB Adventure Co. made it a really fun, safe, and exciting experience! The guides were attentive but laid back, and they made sure everyone felt stable and secure before heading out for adventure. I had a great time navigating around the harbor, checking out the pier, and playing kayaking games facilitated by the guides. In fact, I have kayaked every weekend since I first tried it with SB Adventure Co. I highly recommend trying out any activity offered by this company - in fact, I plan on doing the bicycle wine tour next!

SB Adventure is amazing!! I went on the Santa Cruz Island sea cave kayaking trip and it was AWESOME!! 

They took care of everything for us from the the kayaking gear to offering snorkels and wet suits for us to use after we went kayaking.  

Our kayaking guide, Aaron, took us around the island and through the caves.  He was super knowledgeable about all the wildlife on the island, from different kinds of birds to the various types of kelp.  Our group had a pretty big age range but everyone had a blast!

I've been visiting SB since I was a kid and this was my trip out to the islands.  Will definitely make a point to try and go back each time I go back down there, with SB Adventure of course.

My fiance and I went out to Santa Cruz last week and booked the afternoon 1:30 kayaking trip with Hallie (Halley)... So awesome! Weather was perfect and our guide was super chill and knowledgeable. I'd recommend these guys if you're looking for a sweet time without breaking the bank. Ana-Crapa!

Booking is easy.  Staff is helpful and quick to reply to questions.  Pre-instructions leave no surprises.  There is ample free parking at Island Packers near the ferry dock.  Santa Cruz Island takes about an hour by ferry.  Kayakers are provided with a mandatory helmet and life jacket.  There are secure storage boxes for all the extra stuff you brought that you won't need.  A dry pouch is recommended for cash (ferry purchases and tipping), granola bars, sun block and underwater camera.  You may want a water bottle with a clip to hook to your life vest.  The guides are helpful and knowledgeable.  Hector fascinated us with history of the Island and its wildlife.  The caves are amazing.  There is time after kayaking for lunch, hiking and snorkeling.  It was such fun that we are thinking of making this a yearly event.

We went on the bike/wine tour through the San Ynez Valley and it was really wonderful.  The trip was organized very well, the wineries we stopped at were fantastic, and the bike ride was pretty easy and relaxing and lots of fun.  The scenery is really wonderful and I can't think of a better way to see the valley then on a bike.  I highly recommend this trip! Only thing I would change is adding one more bike riding segment!

We had a great time kayaking along El Refugio Beach with our guide, Hector! It was an easy ride along the coast, complete with lunch on a small beach in the middle of our ride. We even saw some dolphins along the way! Hector was also very knowledgeable about wildlife in the area, which was great. Thanks!

What a fantastic excursion experience! We booked the day before (whew!) and were set up for the cave kayaking tour on the Channel Island of Santa Cruz.on Well organized overall and the transition from the ferry to the instructional portion of the excursion was well executed. Brina was our guide and she's fabulous. Smart and funny, she is very well informed of the ecosystem of the island and took us into some beautiful caves along Santa Cruz's Cavern Point & Scorpion Rock coastline.  I always felt safe and secure paddling with her! She was attentive to those that needed her and let those that seemed more experienced have room to paddle freely. Overall wonderful adventure and well worth the cost! Thanks SBACo & two thumbs up to Brina (I'd give more if it was anatomically possible ;)!

Had a great time sea kayaking around Santa Cruz Island.  The sea caves were amazing and we had a fun time going through them.  The Santa Barbara Adventure company provided everything we needed to keep us warm, etc.  The tour guides we had were great as well (Kenji and Kelsey were out tour guides).  We had a group of 11 teenage boys and 3 leaders and everything went as planned and exceeded out expectations.  Would go again for sure, and would use Santa Barbara Adventure without a doubt!

My husband and I are in our 60's and we have some kayaking experience.  We did the Refugio state beach and we just loved the adventure.
Our young personable and very experienced guide Hector was so caring and attentive to each and every one of us!  
We loved the exciting launching and arriving onto the deserted beach where we had our tasty lunch.
This trip is highly recommended and we are looking to go with SBAC....for the Scorpion sea caves suggested by our guide.

We used this company for a 14 girl bachelorette party, and we were blown away with how amazing this company was! They went above and beyond for us! Planned everything to a T, we just sat back and enjoyed. Our driver seriously made it for us, best personality and service! Thanks guys! I cant say enough about them, book with them!!!

I was lucky enough to have my girlfriend surprise me with a guided kayak tour of the Channel Islands sea caves a few weeks ago. We had an absolute blast! 

Matt, our guide could not have been more informed on native marine and  plant life. He taught us how to "pinwheel" turn among other beneficial kayaking techniques.

I would definitely recommend this experience to tourists and locals alike.

Had a great time on our tour of the Santa Ynez wineries. Our tour guide, Ash, was very informative and provided us with a perfect outing. We enjoyed the chosen wineries and the wonderful lunch he set up for us. Thanks so much for helping to make our mini vacation complete!

This is our third trip with this fine company and as usual we weren't disappointed. This time around instead of a trip to the Channel Islands we stayed coastal and kayaked the shoreline around Refugio State Park. The thing that makes Santa Barbara Adventure a world class touring firm is their employees who are very passionate about what they do. Our guide Brendon was a very likable gentleman from the get-go. His attention to detail be it the history and geography of the coastline to the safety of his participants was impeccable. He put out a wonderful food spread halfway through the trip that gave us time to explore the tidal pools and wonderful stretch of central California shoreline where we beached and a little time to swim and relax before heading back. Thanks Brendon we all had a great time. Next year a sojourn to Anacapa!  SBA gets our highest recommendation.

My family came from Utah to visit us in California for Thanksgiving, so we wanted to give our dad a special thanks by taking him to do something none of us had done before.

Having traveled to many different destinations, and always trying new things, it was a bit of a search for me to find a new adventure for us to celebrate what we came up with as "Awesome Dad Day." 

I had tried several searches of "things to do" in Southern California when I came across Santa Barbara Adventure Company's site. I never knew there were sea caves to kayak around in the Channel Islands - so when I saw this as an option, it was my first pick. 

The office Manager, Mallory, was incredibly helpful and friendly when I called with my questions and to make the reservation. She offered ample information and got us excited about our new adventure. When we arrived at Ventura Harbour, we met our guide, Brina. She proved to be both knowledgable and experienced in not only the kayaking part of journey, but also the birds and wildlife that surrounded us. Once we arrived at Santa Cruz Island, she kept things very fun and casual. We felt no pressure to get suited up or paddle anywhere in a hurry. We felt confident she would only have us do what us first-timers could handle, but she always offered both easy or challenging options. 

Next time I'm looking for something different and fun to do, my first check with be with Santa Barbara Adventure Company to see what else they might offer. Our experience was unforgettable and I highly recommend them to anyone interested in a great adventure.

Went on the Sea Cave Kayaking trip;
The entire process was very smooth.
The guides were helpful and friendly, and we all had a blast kayaking
Highly recommend

So before I start this you should know I am a very picky high Maintance girl, but I got the wine tour on bikes for my husband for our anniversary ....  And we had a wonderful experience ... The trail is not hard at all the scenerie is GORGEOUS, the food they serve is outstanding and the people serving the wine are very friendly and nice, and not to ever forget our poor tour guide,Adam, who had to put up with me and all my bathroom breaks,lol!He is so fun and hilarious.. He may be one of their best tour guides there so if anyone goes I would say ask for him, although I don't know any of the other ones.. But to shorten all this up if you haven't gone you should go very romantic, and a LOT of fun.. We will be going again!

We had a great experience with Santa Barbara adventure company from the start. Mallory was very helpful getting our trip set up and quick to respond to my emails with any questions I had. We booked the sea cave kayaking trip on Santa Cruz island. We had an hour long boat ride out to the channel islands then grant got our group together and got us all the info and equipment we needed to get into the water quickly. He was an amazing guide and made sure all 8 of us were safe and got to go in our fair share of sea caves! One thing that really impressed me was that right off the bat he knew all of our names and that small detail made the trip more personal. I didn't think I'd care about anything but going into sea caves but he had so much knowledge of the island and all the wildlife we saw. He even had recommendations for hikes when we got back and extra places for us to stop on our road trip up the coast. Overall a great time and I hope we can make it back and try something more challenging!

We spent a wonderful day on Santa Cruz Island kayaking through amazing caves.  Our guide, Janelle,  was knowledge,  friendly and fun! 
The whole adventure was a 5  experience from the booking through the end of the trip. 
Impossible to recommend more highly!

I had a great time kayaking at Refugio Beach with our guide Matt. Kayaking was surprisingly easy to pick up after the brief tutorial -- although it's definitely a workout! 

It was awesome to experience the open waters from the kayak. We paused in several kelp beds and had a nibble as we watched it sway beneath us. We encountered several harbor seals and pods of pelicans skimming the water. Most excitingly, we had sea lions leaping out of the water right next to our kayaks! Our guide was great and had so much to share about the natural history of the Santa Barbara & Gaviota coast. 

I highly recommend SB Adventure Company & will be planning future trips with them. The trip was well organized, and I felt safe even while outside of my usual comfort zone.

We took a sea kayaking and hiking trip to Santa Cruz island this past Saturday.  We had a great time.  We went out of Ventura harbor and the entire trip went incredibly smooth.  Everything was very well organized, boat tickets, kayaks, wet suits, life preservers and activities.  Our guide was awesome and worked well with our group and my kids.  I would strongly recommend Santa Barbara Adventure Co to anyone looking for a kayaking trip to the Channel Islands.  We will definitely use them again.

Lots of fun!
We went on the coastal cruise in Santa Barbara yesterday & had a great time. Our very polite, knowledgeable guide was Spencer. 
After kayaking around the harbor, we ventured out to look for seals and then pulled in on the beach for lunch. They provide a nice lunch with wraps, chips and veggies. 
Really a nice day and just the right amount of 'sea time' novice kayakers. 
I would definitely do another tour with them.

My family and I (including a 14 and 11 year old) had a fabulous trip with SBAC on our recent kayaking trip in the Channel Islands.  The person I spoke with to sign up was very helpful, and I appreciated the call the day before, making sure we understood the details.

Our guide Ryan was amazing!  He was incredibly knowledgeable about the history of the island, and the flora and fauna we saw while kayaking.  Our adventure turned out to be really educational, but in a fun and interesting way.  

We would definitely book with this company again - they were complete professionals, from beginning to end.  Thanks for a great trip!

Some friends and I from LA went kayaking as a fun bachelorette weekend activity and we definitely enjoyed every minute of the tour around the Harbour - we even got to see sea lions!! Our guide Chris was amazing and had great energy throughout. He was also really informative in, not just sea life and its surroundings, but also the best places to frequent in town. We would all definitely recommend them to anyone looking for guided adventures!!

My boyfriend Jeff and I went down to Santa Barbara for his birthday weekend and decided to look at the Adventure Company for some activities. I highly recommend their coastal kayaking trip. They were very friendly and thorough during the booking process. They offer to pick you up (on time, I might add) and drop you off after the trip. Lunch is included with the cost, and so are the kayaks (we opted for single kayaks, free of charge), paddles, wetsuits, and life vests. 

Grant C., our guide, was patient, non-condescending, attentive, and very knowledgeable. After a brief yet informative orientation (seriously, although none of us rolled over, just the thought of tipping over in the cold water made me pay attention), he guided us around the harbor towards the open water, through the Stearns wharf pier and little canals, under footbridges and even under a catamaran! He guided us near the barking seals on a buoy... one jovial seal even went with us, threading through the water and under our kayaks. It was so playful, loud, and so close I could've touched it! 

Despite being quite windy the day we went out, there was nothing like the view from the kayak. The sunny sky and the salty sea. The mountains, the beach, the pier, other kayakers, even the gargantuan cruise ship floating still in the distance all made it worth it. Made the sunburn and sore upper body worth it. 

Make sure you have a decent breakfast, a nice slathering of sunblock, a waterproof camera, and a healthy sense of adventure. Thanks to SBAC, I could finally check this off my bucket list.

I am a native Californian and have never been to the Channel Islands. We were visiting Santa Barbara and were looking for an adventure and saw great reviews about kayaking the Channel Island sea caves. We didn't  know what to expect. We caught a ferry from Ventura to the Channel Islands. We saw several pods of dolphins and we were lucky enough to see a blue whale while crossing the Channel. The ferry ride was super bumpy and many people were getting seasick. We happened to sit towards the back of the boat, so we didn't have any problems. Little did we know the ferry ride would actually be a whale watching experience. It took around 90 minutes to get to the islands. When we got to the Channel Islands, the outfit gave us an orientation, assigned us a guide, and gave us wetsuits. There was a place to change into wet suits (changing room) and go to the bathroom (private drop toilets). We were in the kayaks for about 4 hours, seeing a ton of sea caves, a bunch of wild life with great tour guides! They made sure we were safe and explained the history of the caves, their given names and told us all about the birds, fish, sea lions, crabs, sea urchins and were given the option to eat some fresh seaweed! When we got back around 3 pm we ate the lunch we brought, (no food to buy) and had the option to hike around the islands, however we had to catch the 4 pm ferry back to Ventura. If you miss this ferry, you have to stay the night! What a great trip. I highly recommend this adventure with Santa Barbara Adventure Company. On a side note, I was super worried about great white sharks, but we stayed really close to shore and were told the sharks are on the other side of the islands.

This is a first class organization. They are so helpful with all the questions I had and so thoroughly prepared me and my daughter for a great day in a truly amazing place, Channel Islands National Park. Our guide was incredibly knowledgeable, super friendly and very much focused on ensuring we had a wonderful safe experience. It is not every day you can kayak through caves in the middle of the ocean. Great time and Santa Barbara Adventure Company is a super organization to explore with. Enjoy!

We went on the Painted Cave excursion and it was fantastic! Quite a long day. Left the harbor at 8am. Had some breakfast on the way over and saw two different pods of whales and lots of dolphins! The captain slowed the boat down and we hung out with our new sea friends for a bit. One of my favorite parts of the day. Arrived at Santa Cruz around 10am. Jumped straight into the kayaks with Hector and Cooper as our guides. They were awesome. Hector had few cool stories and lots of fun facts about the island. We kayaked in and out of caves down the coast and seals and sea lions were swimming around the kayaks. Hector also pointed two Bald Eagles! Painted cave is the last cave and the grand finale. Then back to the boat for some hot lunch, which was delicious. The cook made vegetarian meals for my sister and I so he gets a thumbs up for being awesome. Not to mention he baked chocolate chip cookies on the way back, and so won over my heart. We got back into the harbor around 6:30 pm. 

I recommend bringing medication for sea sickness!! I never get sea sick and felt a little weird on the way over. Ginger candy helps calm the stomach and is a nice distraction to chew on while you're on the boat. And don't forget sunscreen and a warm jacket because it gets chilly. Thanks SB Adventure Company!

My boyfriend and I did the "ultimate" kayak tour on Santa Cruz Island yesterday and had a great time. Our guide, Marc, was very entertaining and knowledgeable. He took us in a variety of different caves and told us all about the Island's history. We also saw a bunch of wildlife like the CA state fish, sea lions, seals, and a falcon. 

I didn't notice until we were a few minutes into our tour, but I accidentally received a child's paddle that was a great deal shorter than a regular paddle. However, Marc was nice enough to paddle back to shore to get me a regular sized one that was much easier to use. 

We started kayaking around 10:30 and had lunch at Potato Harbor around 2:00. The life vests have pockets so I suggest you bring a light snack to eat along the way as I was pretty hungry by the time we stopped for lunch. Overall we had a great time and would definitely recommend this trip!

Had a blast out on Santa Cruz Island with the Adventure Company this summer.  The trip planning is simple, bring some non-cotton clothes for kayaking, water and lunch.  Everything else is taken care of.  They have sit-on-top boats out on the island, super simple to paddle and very stable.  Both singles and doubles are available.  They provide wet suits, paddle jackets, PFDs and helmets, so it really is just your base layer you need to worry about.

The kayaking itself is fun, tailored to both the weather conditions and the abilities of the kayakers.  We had Ryland as our guide, and he was great.  He took us through a number of caves, including a few through caves.  The weather was great for the trip, the seas not too rough, and the Island Packer boats that get you there are back are very nice.

If you are looking for a different day adventure, this is definitely a lot of fun.

Went kayaking in the caves this weekend, it was amazing! Book the earliest trip you can, ours was a 7:30 am check in, I think we got on the water close to 10am and it was still pretty empty on the water so it felt a little more private. Bring your own food/water and lots of it, you'll be there all day, we got back around 6pm. Ryan was our guide and he was great, very knowledgeable and fun. You'll see sea lions, fish, crabs and dolphin (on the way there) so it's good for older kids even though their minimum is 5 years old. I brought my 7 year old and he was exhausted, but he loved it. I'll be back for more!
This company lived up to its reviews and our kayak guide, Ryan, was the best!  Thank you SB Adventure Company for a wonderful experience!
Awesome day kayaking the Brendan today at Refugio Beach! Girls loved it and we saw a seal, sealions and lots of Pelicans!

What a way to start the New Year!  

My husband and I really wanted to make it out to the Santa Cruz Islands and when we learned about sea cave kayaking, we were sold.  Our guide was Grant, a lovely Australian who was very clear on what to expect from the experience.  He was cautious and would ensure that entering the caves was safe by going in himself before inviting us to follow.  He is a world traveler who has an appreciation for the environment and shared a lot of information about the surrounding areas.  He truly loves what he does and so that too makes the experience a wonderful one.  

On our way back, the boat captain spotted 4 gray whales and stopped near them for us passengers to view.  It was at the end of the day and he could have rushed the experience but was patiently searching for them, following the group, and then allowed us to take pictures.  The crew was great too, making sure to guide us on and off the boat.  

It might be pricey to some but I reassure you that it's an experience worth having.

The wine and bike tour was absolutely fantastic. It was fun, active, and relaxing all at the same time. My girlfriends and I loved it all. 3 wonderful wineries, 12 miles that seemed to fly right by, and beautiful scenery & weather. Cannot be beat. Our guide Jennifer was a jack of all trades. There are not enough words for her overall sweetness & incredible random knowledge. If you're even thinking about it, just do it. You won't be disappointed. :) Oh, and the lunch was delish (healthy sandwiches, fruit, & veggies)!

I can't even tell you what a great time I had with this company.  I decided to go kayaking with my girls for my "60th Birthday Bucket list adventure" and settled on Santa Barbara Adventure Company.  I was really excited about exploring the caves and I was not disappointed.

Our guide was Adam.  He was knowledgeable, funny and ALL the guides were extremely helpful.  I was a bit nervous about being an "elder" in the group and never having kayaked before, but they made me feel right at ease and helped me with any needs I had, which fortunately, weren't many.  What I really liked about this particular adventure, was that it wasn't just kayaking.  The kayaking went anywhere from 2 - 3 hours, but opportunities for hiking and snorkeling were also available for the remainder of the day.

If you are unprepared for doing anything, no worries, they had everything in case you wanted to use their stuff.  Water shoes, wet suits, wind jackets, snorkeling equipment and of course, life vests were among the many items they supplied. All you needed to bring was lunch.

I recommend 3 things...1. Wear a ball cap, as the floppy sun hats don't fit well under the helmets; 2. Be sure to wear shoes that you can walk over rocks on and 3. READ the info they e-mail you with your confirmation.

This adventure was educational as well as exciting.  I completely enjoyed this trip and would highly recommend it to anyone, as well as doing it again.

In short:  Positive experience and I would use them again.  Our tour guide Tam immediately made us feel at ease, went over what to expect, and gave us a great tour.  I give them four starts (instead of 5) because our tour was cut short.

But, when I contacted the owner, Michael Cohen, he responded to my letter almost immediately, didn't make any excuses, and made an offer to make-up for the short tour, even though I didn't ask for anything.  I haven't seen this level of customer service from any company, large or small, in a very, very long time.

The Santa Cruz Sea Caves tour was the best kayaking I have ever done.  The guide let us go at our own pace and was very knowledgeable about the sea life and geology.  

The water was crystal clear and looking over the edge of the kayak you could see for tens of feet to the bottom.  The water was full of sea stars, kelp, seals, and sea lions.  It was much better than a trip to the zoo!

What a great adventure! The entire team on Santa Cruz was awesome and informative, but Elise was truly the best! She took us to some amazing caves (and navigated our group through Marge with ease), and shared a lot of incredible information about the Channel Islands' history, marine life, and environment throughout. I can't wait to return and do it all over again!

My husband and I went on a 3 hour guided kayak tour of the sea caves on Santa Cruz Island in the Channel Islands National Park last weekend.  Our guide, Adam, was the type of guide I always hope to get:  He was a true naturalist with extensive knowledge about the ecosystem, geology, biology, culture, and history of the area.  He told awesome stories that were almost as amazing as the spectacular scenery, caves, and wildlife that make up the Channel Islands National Park.  He was patient, calm, helpful and encouraging to every person on our trip. I've been kayaking a few times previously but the information that SB Adventures gave in the brief preparation session was stuff that I didn't know and that made our experience easier and more enjoyable.  I commend Santa Barbara Adventure Company for offering such a great trip, for employing guides that are passionate advocates of this special area, and for structuring and experience that's accessible and enjoyable to the fullest. I would do it again in a heartbeat!

We went on a sea-cave kayaking trip on the Channel Islands and it was AMAZING and well worth the money. The online tips were really helpful with what to bring for the day. 

We met our guide, Kenji, at the dock for the ferry out. The ride our was great - saw a pod of killer whales, and the ferry staff would stop and make announcements about cool things to look at. Really a very fun and informative ride (if a bit early!). Plus you can buy hot food and drinks on board. 

When we got to the island, another couple realized that their tour guide (from another company) never showed! Kenji was so nice, called SBAC and had them join us so as to not miss out on their day! Kenji was just so nice and informative - very friendly and helpful. SDAC had wetsuits in every size, and the gear was all clean and in great shape. They locked all our stuff away while we were out. Kenji is clearly a hard worker and did just an amazing job telling us all about the island - history, wildlife, and especially lots of cool info about the birds and sea life. He is clearly passionate about the area and really has a wealth of information. It was SO great! I learned so, so much. 

He was also a fantastic kayaking leader - we paddled a substantial distance over the course of several hours, but frequent stops to check things out, and plenty of caves meant that it went by quickly and felt like nothing! He was also very careful with safety: we did go through several caves and tunnels - scary and fun!! - but as the tides got higher he was sure to only allow those caves that were safe. In addition he made sure that we didn't disturb any of the nesting birds (spring time). 

It was just a PERFECT day! Adventure, nature, gorgeous turquoise water! We saw: killer whales, dolphins within a few yards of us, seals within feet, tons of colorful urchins and starfish, and the most amazing birds. Highly, highly recommend this tour and Kenji! He was awesome, and made the day even better :) Not to mention the islands are amazing, and after your kayaking you can hike around or check out the museum and historic buildings. 

tip: buy a cheap waterproof disposable camera if you want pictures of the day - EVERYTHING on the kayak gets soaked!

We did the Santa Cruz sea cave kayaking trip.  They had the best pricing for the trip and the guide was excellent.  Grant was very laid back but informative.  He took care of the group and of course, his Australian accent doesn't hurt either :)

My friends and I originally booked Aquasport kayaking tour.  However, due to rough ocean condition, we had to cancel. Aquasport guy were not available the next day, but they coordinate with this company, Kenji, and he took us kayaking tour. Very flexible and so nice!
Sea cave kayaking was awesome. Kenji is very experienced and nice, and know every single cave. We enjoyed cave, and sea animals!
Higlhy recommended!

First of all, wow! Just wow...this company did everything that they promised they would. I had concerns and called/emailed before our day trip was planned. They reassured me of my concerns and promised I would have a fun relaxed time.

Our party arrived and we were directed by our guide, Mark to fill out the necessary paperwork and ushered to the boat that would take us to Channel Island. On the boat, we had a funny guide who pointed us some of the sea life that were swirling around us. The sea lions resting on the red buoy. The whale that was swimming nearby. So much fun information about each of these animals. It didn't feel rushed and I felt comfortable being on the boat...even though I am notoriously seasick on boats. (One of my concerns! But advised to take dramamine an hour before we got on the boat)

We reached the island and we were herded by our guide to a separate area where they did introductions of our second tour guide, Adam. They were funny, reassuring, and taught us the basics of kayaking and what to do if we fall out, over, or through the kayak. And off we went. It was a beautiful day with calm waters and never did I feel like I was in danger or not under the watchful eye of the guides. We were a large party that were doubled up in kayaks. We explored caves and taught how to maneuver through tight spaces and choppy waters. We were never pushed into doing anything we didn't want to do. They told us where to stay safe while they took a group into a cave. And then it was sadly over.

It was an incredible experience that I hope I can do with my daughter and husband soon. I would fully recommend this company to anyone for their caring and professionalism...but most importantly for making it a fun, happy experience for the entire party.

We had the BEST time with Santa Barbara Adventure Company! Our guide Victoria was amazing.  She was very friendly and loved to have fun. We were constantly laughing and having a great time! 
It was a beautiful day as well, sun was out, skies were blue and there wasn't too much wind until the afternoon.  
Victoria also made us the most awesome spread for lunch. 
We can't wait to do go do the painted caves tour!

SBAC has a great crew. I've enjoyed several kayaking trips with them in Santa Barbara and out to the Channel Islands, and then had them organize a team building day on a high ropes course at El Capitan Canyon for 15 people at my company (great if you like to climb up and stand on telephone poles). I've been on a lot of guided adventure trips in many countries, and SBAC really stands out with a staff that works hard to ensure everyone is safe and has a great time! I'm thinking surf lessons are next.

Cupcake and Wine Tour was amazing. We wanted to find something different to do while in Santa Barbara and this was definitely it.  All of the wineries were great and the picnic lunch that is provided is wonderful.  We would definitely take this tour again.

I chose Santa Barbara Adventure Company for the Wine & Cupcakes tour for my bachelorette party.  Nine girls went, and we had the best day!  Christie had great information for us about the area and the vineyards, took us on a little detour to see miniature horses, and kept us on track all day.  She was pleasant and fun, and the wineries were EXCELLENT.  If you're like me and are just starting tasting in Santa Barbara, you can't go wrong with this tour.  The cupcake pairing is brilliant and fun, and the experience was overall 100% lovely.  I cannot recommend this tour and this company highly enough.

Our family of 3 took a kayak tour with SBAC yesterday. Our original route was going to be too windy so they gave us an option to get our money back or do an extended harbor/beach tour. We had a great day with wonderful guides. Shay and Abby we're both professional and personable. They have a great way with people and made us feel safe and right at home. They recommend bringing plenty of water and applying sunscreen frequently. I second this as our son came away with a hefty sunburn on his feet! I highly recommend this company for your next kayak adventure.

The SBAC team was amazing!  They led our University staff group through a fun "orienteering" program, customized with a Survivor theme.  Each of the facilitators was engaging, warm friendly, and professional. I would definitely use them again for another exciting team building experience.
Awesome time today, Matt was great! Three wineries,Firestone, Zaca Mesa and Saraloose (known for cupcake paring)informative, easy going and a wonderful lunch! can't beat it! Awesome experience!

SB Adventure Company is in a class all its own. The only problem you face is deciding which adventure to choose as there are so many exciting options. 

We went with the wine tour by bike and we couldn't have been happier. The guides were very knowledgable about the area informing us on the drive up to the wineries about the unique geography of the Santa Ynez Valley...this was all new and exciting information to me...and I've lived in California my whole life. 

We stopped at 3 wineries and they were all beautiful. The bike ride in between was a great chance to view the scenery and get some fresh air. The ride was not strenuous and was actually very relaxing. The guides took very good care of us and provided us with fresh snacks and a generous deli spread for lunch. 

This experience definintely made me want to come back to the area and experience another one of SB Adventure Company's unique trips. We didn't want the experience to end! After we were all done, it felt as though we had known the 2 guides (Ryan and Joe) from way back and felt as though we had just gone on a trip with a couple buddies. 

A definite must do!

We had amazing kayaking along Santa Barbara Harbor with Nate. We were the only ones scheduled to kayak that Sunday morning, so it worked out perfectly. We were able to kayak at a comfortable pace and check out the sites for as long as we wanted.  

Nate was an excellent guide. He mentioned he studied Outdoor Education, which explains how he knows so much about the animals, plants, and their habitats. He made it very easy to communicate with him throughout our kayaking excursion. He taught us when and how to use the proper strokes during certain situations. We also learned a lot about the sea lions and harbor seals we paddled past. If you can put in a request for your guide, I highly recommend Nate! 

Definitely worth putting off our drive home to the afternoon!

Tips: 
- Dress in waterproof layers! That water is freezing. 
- Bring a long a waterproof camera 
- Use the bathroom before you go! (If you have to go again, there's a restroom near the area you'll have lunch) 
- Have a decent breakfast and BRING WATER - the paddling can take a lot out of you, especially if you're under the sun most of the time

We had a great day with Santa Barbara Adventure Company.  Christina showed us sea caves, blowholes, seals, birds, and a kelp forest.  She was knowledgeable and enthusiastic.  The waves were big the day we went out.  At one blowhole, a sizable swell tipped me out of the kayak.  With the help of others, I was able to get back into the boat.  Need to work on that upper body strength.  After kayaking, we hiked the Cavern Loop Trail and enjoyed the view of an impossibly blue ocean.  We saw several island foxes hanging out near the campground - cute little things with pointy noses and bushy tails.  Overall, an exciting day filled with gorgeous scenery.  The price of this tour was high but worth it.

What a wonderful day with Aaron and Lindy! We booked the four hour harbor kayak because that was the best fit with our cruise  port time. We came in on a Century cruise. The tender boat let us off right at the dock where we were to meet our guides. After a safety briefing and suiting up (they had plenty of extra gear to keep us dry) we were off. Saw sea lions on our way out to the  beach park where our guides prepared a fresh and delicious lunch on the shore (cutting up fresh vegetables and fruit to serve with wraps and salads and cookies mmm). A harbor seal pup swam with us a bit on our way back. Fun navigation challenges in the harbor on our way out. Fun, safe, friendly welcoming and instructional guides. 

A great shore excursion for independent cruise passengers. I recommend the company add some information on their website welcoming cruise passengers to confidently book excursions with them. I wasn't sure it was feasible until I called and spoke with a representative.

So fun!! My boyfriend and I signed up for kayaking at Channel Islands and decided to go for the Ultimate Sea Cave kayaking, which consisted of kayaking for 5 hours (something we didn't know!) and visiting all the caves from our beach to Potato Harbor. Even though I had only kayaked once in my life before, my tour guide, Ryan, was so encouraging and helpful to everyone in our group. Also, before we kayak, the company has a very organized way of grouping everyone together, making sure everyone has their gear, and going over safety precautions and rules beforehand. I don't know how to swim, but always felt reassured that I was going to be safe because of them. Would definitely book another trip with them again! :)

Excellent. 
Surfing instructor devoted much time and effort to make sure that we all had the best time. Definitely a thrilling experience. I'll always remember my first time learning to ride waves. Fun times.

I booked a trip several months ago for my Trailblazers (4-6th grade kids and their dads) group for Sea Cave Kayaking in the Channel Islands. From the word go, everyone at Santa Barbara Adventure Company was amazing.  The staff in the office were great at helping me put together my trip and provide me with all of the information I would need to get me going. 

When we arrived we were greeted by friendly staff, and our time on Santa Cruz Island was incredible. Our guides were patient, friendly and super knowledgable.  We had an amazing day learning about some California history and seeing the beauty of the Channel Islands up close.  

I would highly recommend working with Santa Barbara Adventure Company - I can't wait to go back!

We went for a kayaking tour at the Santa Cruz Island. We checked in outside the island Packers office in Ventura and then boarded the boat for the island. 
After arriving at the island, we were given some instructions at the beach near the dock. Then we changed into wet suits that were provided by the SB adventure company. Our guide, Rye started us off with some instructions and finally we started with our kayak tour. 
This was our first kayaking experience in the ocean and Rye took great care of us. The sea caves were amazing, we got to see some seals and some rare birds as well but my favorite part of this experience was the Scorpion Blowhole!! 
It was an amazing and unforgettable experience overall! The guided tour is highly recommended specially for people who are a little scared of the ocean (like me).

Rye, our tour guide for the 2-hour tour of Santa Barbara's coastline via kayak this past weekend, was very patient and informative. After a slow start, the other two members of our tour group never showed, my party of three had Rye's complete attention. It was a lovely excursion along the pier, beach, and marina. Rye never rushed us and was always on the lookout for boats (for our protection). 

I never kayak before and I was surprisingly pleased with our experience.  I recommend this activity for older teens and active adults. Be prepare to be sore immediately afterwards - you will be actively utilizing your arms (for strokes) and legs (for balance) - but 2 pills of OTC pain reliever medication will help alleviate the pain.  Also, the $35 price per person is a relief, too.  

Side note: I never flipped over, but there were many close calls!

I haven't gone to the trip but already disappointed, I booked the painted cave kayak trip four months prior to the trip, and they call me one week before the trip and told me that my trip was canceled, and needed to change to a different one that is more pricy and leaves at different harbor, which means I need to cancel the hotel I booked before and find a new one in such short time for a big group. And after all these troubles they caused their attitude was like... "after all we're the only company that has access to the islands, you have no other choices, so either come or cancel, no discount. " so I paid more, and changed to a different trip, I hope it's worth it.

Great outing.  A must if you spend time in santa barbara.  A boat ride out to santa Cruz,  kayaking thru a bunch of caves then  a swim followed by a great lunch and freshly baked warm cookies.  A full day.  Take a wind breaker, a waterproof bag for your phone and lots of sunscreen. Aaron was our guide and he was great. Going to do the trip out of Ventura so that we can hike and kayak next time.  Really well organized outing. Thank you

We did a sea kayak tour on Santa Cruz island. We got the boat from Ventura. It's quite easy to find and plenty of parking. Arrive a little early so that you can complete forms. There are other kayak tours and campers and day hikers leaving from this spot so you have to find the right check in person. But that is no problem at all.
The boat ride over is about an hour. If u get sea sick take some Dramamine before hand. There are snacks and drinks to buy on the boat.
Once you arrive you head to the kayaks where they will go through a safety and education piece. Don't worry if you've never kayaked before there are all levels here. 
You can store your stuff in a big container while you kayak. Bring some water and maybe a snack for the kayak tour. Depending on the temp u may feel cold and they have jackets and bottoms u can borrow and wear as u will get very wet and inside the caves can be chilly. We did not feel we needed them and it was overcast when we were there. 
You'll kayak for a couple of hours. It's tiring! Be aware of this :) but so much fun. The caves are incredible and the guides are knowledgeable fun and safe. They will help you if you need it. One of our party got exhausted and one of the guides just towed them. 
When you get back you can then hike or snorkel. They provide wet suits and all the gear. 
There is nothing on the island, so bring food and water with you and a change of clothing, again if the temps are low u will want to have something warm to put on. 
I really enjoyed this trip it was probably one of the best kayak tours I've done.

We booked the Refugio Beach Kayak trip recently.  We went on a Sunday and were the only two people on the trip so had a private tour.  Scott was our guide.  The weather was low overcast when we left but a beautiful sunny day on our way back.  We paddled about a mile and half up the coast for lunch at a different beach and then back.  We saw lots of brown pelicans and cormorants feeding, kelp beds and associated wildlife, harbor seals and a grey whale breached about 25 feet from our kayaks on the return trip.  Scott was a great guide, with lots of information on the local area and we had a nice lunch.   Working with the SB Adventure company was easy and efficient.

Went for a Cupcake&Wine Tour! The company was very accommodating when we wanted 4th person to be added to our group and there were only 3 spots remaining!

Christina called us and updated us on when she'd be picking us up and was very knowledgeable and attentive.

Wine and cupcakes were awesome! I'd definitely recommend this to anyone and go again!

Channel Islands Sea Cave kayaking in August was perfection. We were blessed with calm waters ( lake-like conditions!) and we're able to enter the "back office" cave, 300 ft beneath the island! Absolutely incredible. My mother and I went tandem on a double kayak, with her in front, and me steering in back. It was a great set up, as initially she was nervous about being in the kayak. She had a wonderful time and was up for anything once we got out on the ocean. 

A bit of advice: If you do the full day trip (10 hours), be sure to bring extra things with you to use on the island after the kayak trip. You will have about 4 hours to kill once you're done kayaking. Bring a book to read on the beach, extra shoes/socks to go hiking, etc.

My best friends surprised me for my bachorlette party weekend with a bike ride through San Ynez wine country.  

We road 13 miles, stopped at 3 wineries and had a picnic.  The experience was so much fun!  

The bikes are excellent quality and the guides are really nice and helpful.

We did the Santa Ynez wine tour and had Tamara as our guide.  She was PHENOMENAL and worked so well with our bachelorette party! The tour took us to three different wineries and our guide was knowledgeable during the entire experience.  A few examples of the exceptional service we received are:

1.  We had one member of our party that wasn't feeling well.  Tamara went out of her way to cater and help our friend get back on her feet.

2.  She took us to some beautiful scenic locations ( Cold spring bridge) to take fun pictures of the bride to be.

3.  One member of the group was early in her pregnancy.  The company reached out to the wineries ahead of time to let them know.  Tamara then brought in different types of juice, which the bar tenders poured into glasses.  So the secret could stay hidden for another month.

Overall this was one of the best experiences on tour we have had.  Great work to Santa Barbara Adventure Company!!

Three of us had a great time on a guided kayaking trip.  Our tour guide, Billy, was more than prepared when we got there.  Our equipment was ready to go; he also had extra water bottles, sunscreen, and water resistant containers for us to store our stuff.  He gave us a clear rundown of procedures, led us on a nice tour, including getting fairly close to some sea lions and seals.  A nice lunch was provided - wraps, popcorn, veggies and dip, fruit, and tea.  Billy set it all up while the three of us played with the frisbee he brought for us.  One of the best parts was in the second half of the trip - navigating through somewhat of a maze under a pier.  Billy certainly made this adventure a lot of fun.  I highly recommend!

We booked our tour to horseback ride on the beach  for 2 hours, it was excellent.  Sara was also very nice.  Great horses and  amazing beach to ride on was expensive but we'll worth it,now can cross off bucket list, would highly recommend.  Horses were great also.

We were a group of 5 kayaking for the first time in Santa Cruise islands. The experience was awesome and Santa Barbara Adventure Company is highly recommended to anyone. 

They took care of all the rental kayaks, cruise reservations to the Channel Islands in a seamless fashion. Reservation process prior to the trip was very smooth, special thanks to Melissa. 

The kayak tour guides (I dont remember the names now) were very professional and helpful. Overall, the experience was worth the efforts and I would highly recommend anyone to tour Channel Islands with SB Adventure Company.

This is a great company, my class and I are in town from the Bay Area for a class trip and we went kayaking with them. They were very very professional and safety/fun oriented. We did some cool team building activities in the ocean and learned a lot while having a great time.

We did a painted cave tour with Santa Barbara adventure company. Amazing experience, friendly team and even great food on the boat. The boat they chartered had very comfy two person bunker beds which we got to use on the way back from the Island.

AWESOME!!!  GREAT TEAM WORK delivers a FANTASTIC EXPERIENCE.

Fantastic end to end experience! It really felt like the guides from Santa Barbara Adventure Company loved their job. Their team work and enthusiasm made all the difference in providing us with a fantastic experience. 

We had a family of four with two kids 10 and 13 years old for the full day sea cave kayak trip. I booked last minute, so I called to secure a booking. Brooke was very helpful and helped us look for available ferry tickets. She was very responsive both in calling us back with the status of the booking to post booking questions via email.
