The ferry ride (from Island Packers) was really good too. The Captain was professional, yet kept it fun for the passengers. The on-board food and drinks were reasonably priced. The landing pier at Scorpion was non-operational at the time, so all passengers needed to take small boats (6 passengers each). It took some time, but I must say, the staff was really good. It was a lot of extra work for the staff in having to get all the passengers to the island one-boat at a time. They even hauled the boats and timed the tides to give the passengers the best experience possible. It was really hard work, and we appreciated it. (I think Island Packers should place a bigger tip jar at the entrance so passengers may better show their appreciation for the staff's work).

The kayak trip was adventurous. Ryan is da man! He was really knowledgeable, professional, fun, patient and attentive to our group's needs. I flipped over twice with my 10-year old (I wasn't very good at this) and both times we were promptly helped by Ryan. On one of the flips, another guide from Santa Barbara Adventure Co, paddled over and helped us a bit. It was great team work. At all times, it felt adventurous, yet safe. Ryan guided us for a really great adventure. He guided us through different caves, showed us points of interested, discussed a bit of history, and was really enthusiastic the entire time! He was awesome.

We went into about 6 or 7 caves, saw wildlife (shrimp, fish, sea lions). Two sea lions swam towards us and even did a flip right in front of our kayak. The ferry ride took about an hour. After getting shuttled on the island and receiving instructions, we started the kayaking at around 11am. We finished up around 2:30pm. The kayaking wasn't extremely physically demanding... my 10 year old was able to do it quite easily. But you should feel comfortable in sitting for about 3 hours and actively moving your arms and have a bit a leg strength. This was easier than a 90 min horseback ride.

I highly recommend going with the Santa Barbara Adventure Company.

This company is awesome!! I booked the cupcake and wine tasting tour for a bachelorette party.  I called a few other places prior, but SB Adventure Co had the best customer service BY FAR.  We even had 2 girls cancel at the last minute, but SB Adventure Co still refunded me the majority of the money, minus a minimal fee which I was cool with since technically we were already outside of the cancellation period. 

The wine tour itself was great. We went to 3 different wineries, with a cupcake tasting (so delicious) at the last stop.  I'm not much of a wine drinker so I can't comment on the wine.. but everyone in the bachelorette party had a good time. Our tour guide, Tam, was the best!! He usually does the kayaking tours for this company but we were so glad that he decided to do our wine tour that weekend too. He was funny, played good music in the van, and was pretty knowledgeable about the surrounding areas (most of us were from out of town).  The company provided food (cheese, crackers, fruits, veggies, good quality deli spread) at every stop, which Tam so kindly prepared for us. So as soon as we were done with the tasting, we'd walk out and the food would be waiting for us picnic-style. We were all stuffed by the end of the tour! 

This company also offers numerous other activities in Santa Barbara. The bride loved the wine tour and Tam so much that she booked a kayaking tour with Tam for her and her fiance soon after. 

Overall, excellent excellent EXCELLENT customer service. Next time I'm in Santa Barbara, I'm definitely going to try one of their other activities. You should too!

Went on a van wine tour, it was excellent! The van was comfortable and spacious, plus they picked us up directly from our hotel. They drove us an hour out to the Santa Ynez wine region and we visited 3 unique wine tastings, all with their own cool vibe. Our guide was extremely knowledgeable, accommodating, polite and was a safe driver. The picnic style lunch was good also. Los Olivos is an adorable little town with quaint shops. We loved every last minute of our trip. It takes you to amazing vineyards and also is informative. Plus, you don't have to worry about the drinking/driving aspect of going out to these wineries. I seriously  recommend doing this if you want an affordable and fantastic way to experience wines near Santa Barbara.

We did the harbor kayak tour in the afternoon for about 4 hours. The guides were friendly and helpful in preparing us for kayaking. On a short cliff lined section of the beach, we had a awesome lunch that the guide laid out for us on a folding table.  We made our own sandwiches and had cookies too.  It was much more than I expected on a Kayaking tour.  The guides were very friendly and pointed out some of the Kelp forests, and sea lions that were swimming around us.  We even had the opportunity to Kayak Surf!  It was a perfect day and a great experience.  I would definately go again.

The sea kayaking Santa Barbara coastal trip was an amazing experience! Our guides, Amy and Jen, were patient and great. They were really well informed and made sure everyone was having a good time. The whole day was planned out really well, including the picnic on the secluded beach. A wonderful company to go on adventures with!

My boyfriend and I went on a wine tasting tour by bike using this company, and we really enjoyed the experience. Our tour guide was extremely friendly and full of information about the area. She did a fabulous job purchasing food for the picnic lunch that accommodated my food allergies, which was incredibly appreciated. The bikes were in excellent condition, the ride was the perfect length, and the three vineyards that we visited were all unique in their own ways. We really enjoyed the tastings and ended up purchasing wine at each of the wineries (which we then picked up with our guide in the van on the way back). I would definitely do this tour again.

We were in Santa Barbara, and in our hotel found a leafelet about sea kayaking, especially visiting the caves in the Channel Islands. We just called, and even though no trip was planned on the wednesday, they managed to get Ryland, one of their guys, to bring us to the Channel Islands for a few hours of paddling around the coast and in the caves.

It was a great experience. Ryland met us in the harbour of Ventura, and one little hour of sailing further, we got to the Island, where Ryland prepared all the gear. There were no other people with us, so we were having a private paddling sessions.

It was just great. Ryland was passionate about the island, the sea caves, the nature. He explained us so much about the area, all we could see, and not see ;-) The caves are just great to visit. He would always make sure to enter the caves first, to check if it was safe, and then we could follow.

At one point we also got to do a bit of snorkelling, accompanied by a small sea lion. That was a wonderful experience, having this little cute animal following you and checking what you're doing there.

Only regret, not having stayed for the night on the island ;-)

After the paddling, we still had an hour or two to walk around the island before heading back on the boat.
The boat trip itself is great as it slows down and manoeuvres to check out the animals we could see during the sailing between the coast and the island.

So thanks for a great experience and having been so easy in arranging a trip last minute for us.

We took the wine tour through Santa Ynez for my sister's bachelorettes party this past weekend and it was AWESOME. The wineries were gorgeous and while some other places offer 4, 3 total was plenty. We were never rushed out of one spot and we're basically allowed to design the day for ourselves. Everyone who worked at the wineries were incredibly friendly and knowledgeable and our driver Pat was the absolute best! He was such a sweetheart, gave us a ton of information, let us play our obnoxious music and hung out with us as opposed to being weird and just sitting in the van. 

SB Adventure company responded to all of my email inquiries quickly and it was awesome that they picked us up from our AirBNB in SB and dropped us back off there. Thanks for everything!!! It made my whole sisters weekend.

Went last Sunday for a Kayak tour and CODY was our guide.  I was on a "date" and wanted to impress my lady friend so I booked this tour with no expectations other getting out in the ocean, kayak, have some fun and be active together.  Cody was the coolest dude. Super surf beac bum laid back chill dude.  Super cool and took us out to see the sea lions, prepped a little lunch and then took us kayaking under the pier as well as look at starfish.  It was a perfect day and a perfect date. I had a great time and would totally do it again.  Great time for sure!!!

I took fifteen Boy Scouts kayaking at Scorpion Anchorage, Santa Cruz Island. It was incredible! The caves are amazing and the guides were fantastic. They even provided wet suits. After kayaking, SBACO allowed us to help ourselves to their rental snorkeling gear. Great experience.

My unusual experience kayaking at Refugio State Beach.  Also, some tips for first-time sit-on-top kayakers, especially if you're tall, over 180 pounds or have a long upper torso which can make kayaks top heavy and unstable.

We selected Refugio beach to learn surf entries and exits.  A tour guide held the kayak in the surf zone as I got on and helped me past the first couple of waves.  After he let go, I immediately rolled the kayak which felt unstable.  Attributing this to inexperience, I got back on and paddled past the surf to the waiting tour guide offshore.  Five minutes later I was dumped again by a small swell in deeper water. This time it was much harder to re-enter the kayak.  The tour guide told me to relax, move with the kayak and that I'll be less likely to tip as we get further away from shore.  About ten minutes later a small swell dumped me again. This time it was extremely difficult to re-enter the kayak since we were in open water half a mile away from shore, my leg was tangled in seaweed and my arms were tired from the first two recoveries.  Although the guides were extremely helpful, I decided to head back as this was no longer fun.

Michael Cohen, the owner, called me after the tour to apologize, see how I was doing and reassure me that this wasn't the type of experience I was supposed to have.  He evaluated my instability problem and said a larger and more stable kayak should have been provided to keep me from tipping considering my height and weight.

Wanting to make this up to us, he invited us to re-take the tour promising our next experience would be enjoyable.  That's great customer service and we will definitely give this company another try.

KAYAKING TIPS

1 - Although tour guides give some basic instruction, I highly recommend watching these short videos by Ken Whiting to see what's involved in kayak re-entries should you tip over:

 How To Re-enter a Sit On Top Kayak
 http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NmaNBXEa_yQ

 Launching in a Surf Zone
 http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0LLg3Z6GHjo

2 - Professionals recommend that you practice kayak re-entries in shallow water before heading into deep open waters.  Wear clothes you can get wet and practice this a couple of times.  A dry beach demo won't prepare you for this alarming event.   

3 - Make sure your tour company asks for your height, weight and other relevant information necessary for providing the proper equipment and kayak.

4 - Practice lifting yourself out of a swimming pool along the edge without using ladders or steps to ensure you have the upper body strength you'll need to get back into the kayak if it tips over.  Re-entering a sit-on-top kayak can be exhausting and intimidating if you're not used to swimming far from shore or in deep ocean.  If you can practice with a kayak, all the better.

5 - Why my kayak was so unstable - In addition to my inexperience, a kayak's maximum weight capacity varies according to water conditions as one source warns, "When your weight approaches the maximum weight for the kayak, stability is reduced.  While it may be stable in calm water, it may be unstable in rough water at that weight.  It's a good idea to allow at least 100 pounds extra weight capacity."  Common rental kayaks such as "Ocean Kayak Scramblers"  have a maximum weight of 275-325 pounds.  That means for "maximum stability" on the ocean,  your weight including any gear should not exceed 175 pounds.

Have Fun!

What a cool, yet local SoCal trip you can take! It's only 3 hours from San Diego, 2 hours from LA and wow it's so worth it! We checked in at the Ventura Harbor which had plenty of parking, and boarded the Island Packers Cruise Ship for about a 45min cruise to the Channel Islands. The boat DOES have a snack bar, just FYI! We had a nice guided tour in which we learned about the sea life, and yes saw a whale! It was a gorgeous boat ride to the islands.

Once we docked, we easily found our tour guide and he explained a lot about the Channel Islands. I had no idea that there are 16 islands in total- 8 US, and 8 owned by the Mexican Government! There were also plenty of pirate stories to hear! It is a national park, camping is allowed with permits.

We decided to do the Sea Cave Kayak tour.  It was about a 4 hour tour around the islands. Our guide was very informative, and he really helped direct us through some tough sea kayaking through the ocean caves! The sites around the islands were amazing, even the Go Pro couldn't capture the beauty of all the nature that surrounded us. So much sea life, and the seals seemed to love to swim right next to us!

At the end of our sea cave excursion, we had a chance to hike up the mountains on the island that we docked on. Gorgeous 360 views, and a peaceful step onto the untouched nature, we really found inner peace just being there, besides a great days workout!

I definitely recommend putting this on your bucket list of things to do, and Santa Barbara Adventure Company was a fantastic company to guide us on this fun weekend getaway! 

LYL- Live Your Life! LYLSD.com

perhaps any teacher's greatest fear is a student getting hurt whilst on an outdoor education experience. while this remains true for me, it is also resoundingly true that if any student is hurt while on an outdoor education trip with the santa barbara adventure company, your fears, and that of your poor student, will be promptly alleviated due to the impeccable level of care the staff provides.

i recently took my seventh graders to el capitan canyon for a biking, ropes course, surfing, hiking adventure led by the phenomenal adventure company team. during the biking, one of my students (yes, she was wearing a helmet!, tumbled off her bike and ended up with quite a few abrasions. brina and ry, 2 or our adventure company guides, are certified wilderness first aid responders, which basically means they can clean or treat pretty much anything remarkably well. as soon as my student was bandaged, michael, the owner of the company, drove us to a local hospital for some additional inspections of my student's various road rash. thanks to how well brina and ry had tended to her, the hospital really didn't have much to do other than prescribe some pain meds and rebandage her.

we all emerged from this experience--me, my student, her family, my administrator--even more head over heels for the santa barbara adventure company. it is my goal for this year to bring them more business, as much as i possibly can. i truly just cannot speak highly enough of this company.

oh, and as for the rest of our trip--it was perfect. the team work inspired by the low ropes course was impressive, the high ropes course was varied and challenging, the hikes were lovely, and the staff were all knowledgeable and efficient. in fact, as the other teachers chaperoning with me mentioned, we have never encountered a stronger team than that established by the adventure company. it was also really nice to see the same faces this year that we saw from our previous trip the year before; it's actually the first time, on any of the many outdoor ed trips i've led, that i have seen the same staff from year to year.

thanks and appreciation to the utmost for grant, eric, landon, matt, ry, brina and, of course, to michael, who created such an incredible team.

I booked the cupcake and wine tasting tour for my bachelorette party.  There were six of us total.  Santa Barbara Adventure Company called to confirm the day before, which is a professional touch I highly appreciate.  They also picked us up directly at our hotel, which was very convenient.  We were the only people in our van so it was like a private tour.  We had an absolutely wonderful day.  

Our guides, Adam and Jenn, were very friendly to us the entire time and put up with our antics beautifully.  We visited Sanford, Lafond, and Saarlos & Sons (with cupcakes there by Enjoy Cupcakes).  I will review each of those businesses on their own, but for this review, I cannot speak highly enough of the service provided by Santa Barbara Adventure Company.  They played our obnoxious playlist for us on the van's speakers, took photos for us, chatted with us about everything random that came up, and gave us a truly VIP experience.  We were taken care of wonderfully by them.  The lunch they set up for us was very nice and accommodated the vegetarian members of our group perfectly.

A few days later, I received  a follow-up e-mail a few days later from Rachel asking me how my trip was.  This is a wonderful courtesy and too many vendors/businesses don't make this extra effort.  I go on wine tasting tours a couple of times a year in Temecula, Malibu, Santa Barbara, and Napa, and in the future, I will ALWAYS use Santa Barbara Adventure Co for Santa Barbara wine tasting sessions.

Adventure for sure!  So much fun.  Went for a friends birthday celebration.  Me my girlfriend and another couple headed out to Santa Cruz island.  The guides did an amazing job!  Provided everything you need for sea kayaking.  From wet suits to safety gear.  Also gave us some very solid suggestions on hikes that would fit into the time table we had.  

I highly recommend using SB Adventure company.

If you can I would totally ask for Adam as your guide.  Very knowledgeable of the island and kayaking.

I took this tour on my 30th Birthday and it was amazing! The cupcakes were such a nice addition to the tour and the best part was we got to take some home! Eric was a great guide. Saarloos & Sons was definitely the highlight.I would recommend this tour to anyone who has a sweet tooth and who enjoys good wine.

This tour was wonderful! We were in SB for a Bachelorette and we definitely got our taste of SB and wine-tasting. The tour guide was prompt and on time, very friendly and accomodating for the group. We loved the winery wtih the cupcake flight, and in general we were satisfied with the whole experience.
We highly recommend this espeically if in a large group and ready to have fun!

The actual "adventure" was only 3 stars, but the excellent customer service bumps it up a bit. 

This is a great company to use if you're unfamiliar with basic adventures like biking and kayaking in the Santa Barbara area. The staff is really accommodating and helpful. 

But if you're experienced at all, save yourself the cash and just rent your own bikes and kayaks. Our wine tour was supposed to be 10-12 miles with 3-4 stops, but it only totaled 9 miles and left us wishing we didn't have to get back in the shuttle so soon.

My husband and I did the Sea Cave kayaking trip and had a fantastic time. Paddling wasn't difficult (definitely ok for beginners) and our guide Ryan made the whole trip very educational and interesting. While I am familiar with lake and river kayaking, this was my first time in a sea kayak and in one with another person. Ryan made the whole experience easy and fun. On a side note, I have always been curious about the Channel Islands since first seeing them as a student at UCSB and I'm really glad I finally got out to explore them. It was a ton of fun!

Our tour took us to Firestone, Lincourt, and Saarloos wineries and gave us more than enough time at each one. In fact, they could've easily fit in one more winery but alas this tour only came with 3. 

The lunch was a "make your own sandwich" spread which I'm sure didn't cost too much to put together however they charged a bit too much for what you get. We were charged a little under $120 per person and considering each winery really only costs about $10 per tasting, we were left wondering what the other $90 per person was spent on. 

Firestone and Lincourt are worth visiting again in the future...the views from each are really beautiful. The wait plus the "talk" we got at Saarloos was a bit over the top and almost felt like a strange therapy session. The cupcakes were tasty there however and to be honest, more enjoyable than the wine.

Nice guide overall but next time I'd go with a tour company that charges under $100 for a 3 stop tour and skip Saarloos despite the cupcakes.

Excellent experience all around! Booked our tour over the phone and they were terrific answering all my questions and making sure we were choosing the right tour for our group of six. We went on the day kayak tour on Santa Cruz. Our tour guides were Adam ad Laird and they were both great! Very information and attentive to the needs of the group. 
They provide wet suits and water jackets for the actual kayaking time so we just needed our swim suits and water shoes. Some of the guys chose to just use the water jackets, but most of us gals wore both.  You'll definitely want that change of clothes for after the tour so that you can enjoy the rest of your day, dry!!  We had enough time after a lunch break to take the short hike up the hill to see some amazing views!! Walking shoes were great!!
If you have never done the Islands, this is a great way to get to experience a little bit of everything!!
Thanks SBAC for a memorable day!

just returned a few days ago from an outdoor adventure experience with 22 7th graders courtesy of the santa barbara adventure company. having been on way too many of this sort of venture over the years, i can only heave a deep sigh of relief at finally ending up with a company that gets what we need. not only is the customer service beyond reproach, but the staff was amazing with the kids--in control but never patronizing or too granola the way some of these places can be, and the activities were stellar. 

we took the kids sea kayaking, on a high ropes course, a great long hike, had some night hike action, a camp fire, heated swimming, team building--and it was all super flexible and super competent. we just were so clearly in good hands. michael cohen has put together a great company with an excellent staff. Brina, Rachel, Matt and Ry all clearly knew what they were doing. 

such a magnificent experience that i already rebooked with them for next year!

Our bachelorette party went on the wine tasting trip with cupcake tasting. Who knew the two would go so well together? In total we went to the Firestone, Lincourt, and Saarloos wineries. Eric was an excellent guide, he was extremely knowledgable and made the trip super fun. Thanks Eric! Will definitely be back to try the kayaking tours next.

The experiences they offer are fantastic!!! If I could give more stars I would. We went on the kayaking trip from Refugio to El Capitan. We saw a whole pod of dolphins swim by and a sea otter who came right up to our kayaks and sniffed around. Seriously it was better than what you see of the nature channel. Our guide was the best. She was so sweet, very knowledgable and really catered to our needs. She brought us a fantastic lunch and picked out a great place to stop and enjoy it. My fiancé and I had the best time and it was well  worth the money. In fact, we are planning to go on their longer kayak trip to the channel islands next. I highly reccommend. No need to look for another place to plan your activites when in Santa Barbara. The staff is friendly, they go through all the activites with you AND this was the cherry on top of my experience. Pretty much why I decided to get on yelp and tell you about them. I had forgotten my favorite yellow sweater in the water proof bag after kayaking. The guide called me that night to say she found it and would return it to the office. The office called and confirmed my address and sent my sweater right away. This is exceptional customer service and it needs to be recognized. With so many companies trying to cut corners these days, I am very impressed that they go above and beyond to make the customer feel appreciated.

Just finished this kayak tour. The island itself is beautiful. However, our guide Jerry was a total mess! He was clearly disorganized, flustered, and unable to manage a small group of 4 kayaks! He repeatedly complained about having to use his "outdoor voice" to guide us (hello, we are outdoors), and was strangely rude and impatient. He ruined our tour completely. At one point, I kid you not, he hissed and gritted his teeth yelling and accusing us of back peddling (when we literally synced our strokes with his). We were really shocked at his unprofessional behavior. If you have ANY other ocean tour options take that and SKIP this company altogether. They do not employ capable people, at least in our case. You have been warned!!!!!

My 10 yr old son and I just got back from what will be a day that we'll both remember for the rest of our lives. We spent a gorgeous, gorgeous day on Santa Cruz Island with guides Landon & Bryn. They lead us along Scorpion cove and through some amazing sea caves. The guides were super  knowledgeable and very friendly. The weather was awesome, the equipment was in great shape and my son and I felt safe 100% of the time! Highly recommend!!

oh, i'm sorry. you didn't have a perfect day yesterday? that's so sad. perhaps you should have been me yesterday, enjoying my perfect day. yes, perhaps that would have increased your happiness quota. alas! you are not me, and so the day of perfection remains mine and mine alone, but you can vicariously enjoy my perfect day simply by reading this review! lucky you. 

the mister and i headed off for santa barbara relatively early yesterday morn for a day of WINE BY BIKE, courtesy of the sb adventure company and its stellar guide eric. eric picked us up ever so promptly at the scheduled time and off we went! 

ever so knowledgeable about the indigenous flora, fauna and history of the area, our guide eric pointed out mucho nifty goodies about the area as we made our way into the santa ynez valley. i honestly don't know how he managed to spy so many birdlets of specific varieties while driving that big van, yet spy them he did, often in enough time for us to also catch a glimpse of the turkey vultures, falcons, woodpeckers, etc. 

our adventure in wine commenced at zaca mesa. a totally yummy experience! lovely wines and quite a few tastings. loved their rousanne, which they described as a white wine for red wine drinkers. 

after this, we received our biking safety talk, helmets and instructions to the next winery some four miles down the sunny road--which brings me to an essential point! don't just bring sunscreen along for the ride! actually apply it! silly girl that i am, i made sure to pack it, but did i bother to use it? well, the answer to that would be a no, as today i am a crispy girl... 

the ride was superb, curvy and gentle with a perfect breeze keeping us cool. eric, ever the safety-conscious gentleman that he is, followed us in the van in case we needed anything. 

our next winery was foxen, where we were given temporary tattoos! always a boon. the wine here was nice, though not so nice as zaca mesa. however, we then biked down to the old foxen tasting room, some 400 feet down the road, which they refer to as the shack, and the wines here were fabulous. we loved the casual funkiness of this tasting area and were super happy to enjoy our tasting here while chowing down (delicately, of course) on the perfect picnic eric had laid out for us. seriously, a really nice luncheon! we all had different meal requirements (me--vegan, my mister--gluten free, and the other gent on the journey--open to anything), and all of our plates were aesthetically arranged, tasty and nutritious. 

seeing as how we lingered excessively over lunch, combined with the fact that we started our tour an hour later than is typical, eric ended up driving us to our last winery--kenneth volk winery. delicious. truly. 

and so, there you have it. a perfect day, with perfect weather, a perfect guide, perfect imbibery, perfect gastronomy, perfect biking, perfect company. 

so what are you waiting for? go grab yourselves some perfect days right now! call the sb adventure company and sign up for the wine by bike tour and ask for eric!

What a great group of folks! I booked a Kayaking tour of the Painted Caves on Channel Islands off the California Coast with Santa Barbara Adventure Co and they provided an amazing and fun filled day! Our tour guides were Hector, Billy, Pierce and Scott. Not only were they great guides for our kayaking adventure, they were great guys that were considerate and fun. With Scott on the Ukulele, they made the time traveling to and from the Channel Island lots of fun.

Santa Barbara Adventure Co took care of everything for this trip. All you have to do was show up in the morning. The morning started off with a breakfast of coffee, fruits, yogurt and assortment of breads, jams and peanut butter. The lunch was a delicious meal of chicken pasta (cooked right there on the boat) with salad and sour dough bread. They were even careful about our dietary restrictions.

As for gear, you literally don't need to bring anything. They do however provide a list of items that you may want to bring and the boat had plenty of room for whatever gear you wanted to bring. My suggestion is to at least bring a towel (to dry off after kayaking), a camera, and a container for water (to take on the kayak). Santa Barbara Adventure Co provided just about everything else: sunscreen, jackets (to keep warm on the kayak, life vests, helmet, wetsuits, ginger candy, ginger ale, water, snorkeling gear, and of course kayak, guides and loads of fun.

The whole trip and tour were so fun. The weather was perfect and we were accompanied by dolphins almost all the way to the islands and back.  The painted caves and the wild life around the islands are definitely a must to see. We were in Hector's group of kayaks and the whole tour he provided such interesting info about the islands.

Next time? If anyone wants to go, I am game to go a second time.

We did the harbor kayak tour, about 4 hours. The guide, Dave, was very helpful in preparing us for kayaking, answering all of our questions, and pointing out features of the harbor. On a more private section of beach, we had a delicious lunch that the guide laid out for us on a folding table that he brought along with him. The guide was very good at keeping track of all six of us as we tended to disperse in our journey.

We chose the sunset kayak trip based on some Google searching and the reviews were a little over-rated.   We had expectations of kayaking out and really seeing the coast and seeing it under the stars. Here is a summary of the Pro's and Con's

Pro's
The guide did an excellent job of explaining the process, equipment, and kayak techniques. 

Seeing the coast

Some good stories from guide

Con's
Two hours is too long to sit in the kayak.  Our butts hurt and my hip flexors started to cramp up.   We are in pretty good shape and we were uncomfortable and ready to call it a day about 30-45 minutes before the trip was over. 

If you are looking to kayak the coast, then you are better off renting and going on your own.  If you are looking for a tour and learn about the history, this is a good option.
First time kayaking and we had a great time . Matt was a great tour guide . Would recommend using this company. Our next kayaking will be to the Channel Islands .

We did the Adventure Sea Cave Kayak. Highly recommended! Three of us had no kayaking experience, we all did fine! Chuck and Simon, our guides, were awesome! They gave us history and facts about Santa Cruz Island which was a plus. I liked how the SB Adventure Company was super organized, which made this experience an A+. Will definitely try again! I'm ready for the Painted Cave experience! Thank you SB Adventure!

I wanted to thank Whitney, Janelle, and Billy for making our trip to the Channel Islands memorable and safe.  My students were from landlocked Oklahoma and most had never been on the ocean before.  Some of us were a little scared and I even got very seasick and capsized my kayak.  They did not blink an eye and kept on going, giving their all for us.  I was most pleased that after the sea caves adventure my students reported having "the best day of their life" on this trip and I am SO thankful they were safe with their guides.  Janelle stayed with us from the time we got on the boat until the time we disembarked for home, even through me being seriously seasick.  It was a overall great experience for all my students and staff and I was so relieved that I was able to know I could have an off day and they were still totally on, encouraging, and positive.

This review is intended more for the service of the trip.  My wife and I went on a kayaking tour for our wedding anniversary and she ended up getting terrible seasickness.  So if you get seasick easily, this is probably not for you.  

But, the guide, Ry, was very helpful and towed us back to shore. He even dropped our beach towel back at the hotel we were staying at.  Not for us, but really great service.

Greatest adventure of all time for my wife and i on our anniversary!!! Pablo is the best teacher ever!!
Thanks Pablo and Santa Barbara Adventures. 
YOU GUYS ROCK!!!

We booked a sea kayaking tour on Santa Cruz Island (Channel Islands National Park) through them about four months in advance. 

Customer service over the phone and through email was perfect: friendly and expedited. We made a lot of changes to the reservation without issue. 

Once the day came for our tour, Santa Barbara Adventure Co had its own desk out in the grass adjacent to the Island Packers office and convenience store. They had their passenger manifest all in order and had our tickets to the ferry at the ready. We did a quick briefing and orientation. 

Once on the island, we had another orientation for the sea kayaking. Our guides were Spencer and Spencer (yup), and both were excellent! We learned a lot about the wildlife and plants on the island along with technical skills required to safely navigate the sea caves that dot the Scorpion Ranch area of Santa Cruz Island. 

Overall I couldn't be happier with this tour company. Highly recommended!

We took an afternoon sea kayak trip and it was amazing.  We talked about everything we experienced long into the night once we returned.  Our guide (Mark I believe) was very informed about all the caves and made us all feel very safe.  

My husband and son shared a kayak and unfortunately fell into the ocean.  Mark was very helpful and got them back in and was extremely patient. There were 5 in my party and another party of three joined us for the tour of the caves.  The group was small and it made it easier for the guide to handle.  

It is the ocean that you are in, not a lake so be prepared for an adventure!!

My husband and I and another couple booked the Santa Ynez Wine Tour by Bike.  We had Havah as our guide and I just had to take a moment to tell you what an amazing guide and person that she is.  She picked us up at our home promptly, and as we drove to the destination she filled us in on local info and got to know us too.  Everything was handled smoothly, we thoroughly enjoyed each winery and the beautiful picnic lunch -  Havah was great with showing us info on our ride and did a great job coordinating our trip so we had a chance to make an extra stop along the way.  Really a turnkey tour!

You are so lucky to have such a wonderful young woman working for you with such maturity and knowledge.  She really enjoys what she is doing and it shows.

Thank you for a great day.
Sincerely,
Kellie & Mike Z.

If you're looking for an amazingly fun outing, go kayaking with Santa Barbara Adventure Company! I went with 50+ girlfriends to Santa Barbara this wknd and we booked a variety of activities, including the "fun paddle" kayak tour. 

Any large group outing can be challenging, but they were a dream to work with. They were organized, so accommodating - including with respect to additions to our group, exceedingly knowledgeable, totally on top of it, and they tailored the outing to our needs an interests. The guides were phenomenal. Not only were they focused on safety and fun, but they were hilarious. Everyone had rave reviews! I can't wait to do it again!

Such a great experience! We did the sunset tour with Erica yesterday and it was great! I can't say more nice things about our guide she was great. She was very patient and informative to talk to about the area. I will definitely be back to kayak in the area. It is also a great COVID activity since you are so far away from everyone you really only come into contact with your guide and whoever else you are on the kayak with. 10/10 experience!

Our wine tour was amazing!!  Perfectly paced for a satisfying buzz that lasted all day.  Plus the cupcake pairing at the last winery was AMAZING.  So delicious.  

Big kudos to our guide, Eric, for putting up with us and letting me put on my own music in the van (his music was great too).  

Highly recommended!!

I found their company on Yelp and their staff was fantastic. I booked SBAC for my sister's birthday weekend with 8 friends to do the kayaking and it was great.  They basically let me book everything over the telephone, give the credit card info and done. Such an easy process and the team that took us kayaking was great. I nearly hurled but still had fun! 

I would definitely go back and use them for the surfing lessons or the win tours. Definitely worth it & all of the other Yelp reviews were spot on! Thank you!

I went kayaking with Adventure Company of Santa Barbara to the Santa Cruz Islands and it was a wonderful adventure! The weather was perfect that day and everything about the trip was great. The painted caves were breathtaking and the trip was only made better by our resourceful and wonderful tour guide. They took the time to explain how to kayak, what to expect, and during the trip our guide was always available with any questions. The scenery was amazing! The trip would be perfect no matter what your skill level was. I had never kayaked before but felt like an expert at the end. It was just challenging enough, but not too hard. Our guide even took the time to let us all hold a star fish and stop to (quietly) take pictures of a sea lion. Overall, I would recommend the trip to anyone. Great company and great tour!

My friends and I went on the wine tour that they offered. We went on a lovely Saturday and ended up with a few other people on the tour. They were on time picking us up. Amy, our driver and guide, was great. She was very knowledgeable about the area and the wineries that we went to. Her energy was great and she was very upbeat. We visited 3 different wineries and stopped for lunch at the second one. We visited Gainey, Sunstone and Kalyra. Sunstone, where we had lunch, had a great outdoor picnic area. Amy spread out a good lunch of sandwich fixings and drinks and we ate more than enough. 
My only drawback to the visit was that these were not the best wines I have had in the area. The vineyards were beautiful but there are some better tasting wines to the north of here.

Very pleased with our tour this past July. We had a group of Boy Scouts working on their kayaking merit badge and SBAC was more than accommodating to us. Brooke in the front office for group sales made life easy with getting everything set up. Caco, Jerry and Paige were excellent guides who were patient and informative. You could tell Caco was passionate about his job and he shared a lot of facts with us. Jerry was also excellent at his job and was funny. Paige was excellent too and very patient. We booked the half day tour and it was worth the money. I think there were six sea caves we went through. We saw parts of Santa Cruz Island you simply can't see unless you're in a kayak. Best of all Caco and Jerry took extra time to demonstrate some of the kayak skills and concepts needed for the BSA kayak merit badge.

As a side note, two days before the trip, I realized we were really 18 people, not 16. I called SBAC and they made the extra two spots happen and got us the third guide. Very impressed with the customer service. 

This is a very safe operation with skilled and passionate employees. I highly recommend them. While not the cheapest activity on the island, you get your money's worth.

We did a Kayak Tour of the caves in Channel Islands and it was amazing. Simon was our tour guide and he great. I have a sister with a disability and Simon helped the perfect amount while still allowing us to participate in everything we knew we could do.

My boyfriend and I booked a sunset kayak tour.  During the booking process, you specify the type of kayak being requested (singe or double).  We of course selected 2 singles.  When we arrived and met our guide, we asked about the kayak and was advised that the office organizes the kayaks and was told that we were on a double.  The guide was very apologetic and called the office to see if there was anything that could be done.  Long story short, their wasn't anything that could be done.  Due to a clerical mistake, we were stuck on a double kayak.

The office contacted me a few days later to again apologize for the error and offered a full refund!  They also stated that they are working on a process to ensure this issue wouldn't happen in the future.  This goes to show how much this company cares about customer service!!!  

Other than the mix up, the tour was lovely and the guide was wonderful.  The harbor was gorgeous and the group we were in was very small, which allowed us to ask a ton of questions and gather 'locals' knowledge.  

Next time we are back in SB, we will absolutely be booking another tour with this company.

SB Adventure is awesome.  Did a wine tasting and mountain biking excursion with Kyle and have an amazing time.  Fantastic service from a very knowledgeable guide, he took care lunch and all the gear.  I highly recommend the guide and company!

Did a 2 day kayaking trip with 10 other folks and had a blast!  Our guides, Adam and Brin, were very experienced, friendly, and most importantly, made us feel safe!  If you're looking for a kayak tour to sign up with, don't hesitate to go with these guys.

I'd give this company more than 5 stars if I could! From Leslie, who answered the phone and answered my multitude of questions (being a first time kayaker wanting to do the sea cave tour) to Sean and Christian who led our Coastal Kayaking tour (because the sea cave one was sold out - apparently you need to book about 2 months in advance!), everyone I encountered was super friendly and helpful and patient. My husband, son, and I thoroughly enjoyed our first time ocean kayaking even though my husband and I were TERRIFIED before the launch. But Sean and Christian were very thorough with explaining what to do through every phase of the tour. And though neither hubby nor I are very fit (we can manage one flight of stairs all right but 2 flights makes us a little out of breath), Sean and Christian kept the pace very mellow with frequent stops to look at scenery or kelp (we quickly learned not to look at the kelp underneath us too long because then we started getting seasick, which got better by looking off into the distance) and to regale us with stories about Santa Barbara that physically, the tour was quite manageable. It was a wonderful experience, and next time we're in Santa Barbara, I definitely want to do the Sea Cave tour!

What a fantastic experience! I had the opportunity to work with the Santa Barbara Adventure Company and plan a kayak tour to coincide with our novel study of Island of the Blue Dolphin with my third and fourth grade students. Everything from the planning and preparation with Brooke to the actual trip was top notch! Our guides, Kyle and Aaron were awesome! They were so patient and knowledgeable and went out of their way to support us even when one student got seasick in the middle of the kayak tour. I would highly recommend working with the SB Adventure Company and look forward to planning another trip in the future!

We did the Sea Caves kayak tour on Santa Cruz island (7/1/2015). The whole setup is great. The kayak tour is guided, and can be followed by self-guided snorkeling and/or hiking. They even provide snorkeling equipment! I learned a lot about the island, and had a great time doing all three of these activities. 

Another huge plus is the boat ride. We saw blue whales and a huge pod of dolphins. The boat (from a company called "Island Packers") slows when wildlife is present and the captain talks about whatever type of animal you are seeing. It's really great that they don't just speed by the wildlife. They take their time and teach you about it. I am truly appreciative of that. 

Our Kayak guide was named Ry. He was awesome! A really happy, skilled, fun-loving, and knowledgeable person. He let us take our time thru the caves, and even took us in a more rarely visited cave (since we were lucky enough to have calm conditions that day!) The other guides (Janelle, Laird) seemed like they would've been great too. All guides were very helpful in making sure we had properly-sized gear. They were all super cool- the type of people you wish you could hang out with in real life!

Thanks Santa Barbara Adventure Company for one of the best days of my life!

We had a great time kayaking around the harbor. We booker the tour online and we signed the waivers online so there was less contact. Our guide was friendly and helpful. We received the instructions in like 3 minutes and we started kayaking. It doesn't need experience but It can be tiring. It is definitely was a good activity to do.

We booked a sunset harbor cruise on a recent trip to SB. Ty, our guide was knowledgeable, funny, and overall an amazing tour guide. We somehow were the only two people who booked that evening, so we got a very private tour! We saw dolphins, seals, sea lions, and tons of birds and sea life in the harbor. At one point we were surrounded by a pod of dolphins.. so majestic! About half of the tour was in the harbor with learning about the history of the harbor, types of boats and use, and looking out for sea life. Then we went into open water (felt very safe!) and paddled to a buoy to see sea lions. On the paddle back we saw lots of seals and were lucky enough to spot some dolphins, too!

Entering the water was easy (I've had some less than fun experiences with other ocean kayaking trips were you enter/exit the ocean from the beach..) and from a dock. Would 100% recommend this trip and this company. A great experience that we will talk about for years!

The 6 of us took a kayak sea tour around Santa Barbara harbor with SBAC guide, Chava. She was so much fun, keeping us laughing and telling us cool stories about Santa Barbara, the harbor, and the local sea life. She really took care to make us feel safe, teaching us how to get back into our kayaks should they turn over (mine and my husbands did, near a group of sea lions.) Chava was very attentive to everyone's needs on the trip, and made sure everyone was paddling efficiently, drinking enough water, etc. When my Mom got a little tired, Chava tethered her kayak to her own and helped with the work load. It was greatly appreciated. Thanks again for such a great time. My husband and I liked it so much we're talking about getting our own kayaks. :) We hope to take another tour with you guys, and when we do we hope Chava is our guide. Thanks for everything!

Did the Channel Islands kayaking tour in May 2021 and it was wonderful. Everything was well thought-out and well-organised; our guides Ben and Sean were lovely and very knowledgeable about the wildlife and the islands. This is a terrific way to see the islands in a day

My fiance and I were in Santa Barbara for a wedding and decided to make the weekend even more memorable by enlisting Santa Barbara Adventure Company to take us on a kayaking tour. Neither of us had ever gone and were excited to learn.

We scored BIG TIME! Our tour guide, BRYN, was incredible! I mean PHENOMENAL!  He taught us all about kayak safety, the harbor rules, and  he's extremely knowledgeable on local history and wildlife. We were the only ones going, so this  ended up being a private tour. It couldn't have been any better!

The tour took us around the inside of the harbor where BRYN taught us how to maneuver the kayak using various oar stokes, then around the breakwater into open ocean. He lead us to a beach where he prepared lunch for us. Everything was fresh and tasty. 

The whole time BRYN showed his concern for us by reminding us to drink water and use certain strokes. It was an added comfort knowing that he has extensive first aid training and years of kayaking experience.

All in all, our experience with BRYN as our tour guide with Santa Barbara Adventure Company was the best ever! I highly recommend that you book a tour with them, and once you do, ask for BRYN to be your guide.

When I realized it would be nearly impossible to visit Painted Cave on my own, after extensive research and trying to save money, I booked through SBACo. It was worth every cent. The staff was very professional, knowledgeable and friendly. They came around on the boat and introduced themselves. I have a decent amount of experiencing kayaking but we had various skill levels and with the rough ocean, some people were struggling. I appreciate that the guides kept in communication with each other other and the boat so they could let the boat know to come around to pickup some of the kayakers that were tired. 

The boat staff of the Truth was also incredible and it made for a great day.  There were nice bunks with pillows and blankets to take a nap, a freshwater shower, delicious lunch of pulled pork sandwiches and coleslaw, plenty of coffee, and fresh baked cookies. I feel like I just came back from summer camp.

PS Be sure to let them know if you have any dietary restrictions and they will be happy to oblige.

Yesterday was my first time to Channel Islands National Park. I wasn't sure what to expect there so I decided to add an activity to my island visit. I booked the 9.30am Discovery Sea Cave Kayak tour (the shorter one - about 1 to 1.5hrs) since it was my first time. Got to Ventura - island packers venue and Clay quickly helped me with the check in process for kayak tour + ferry ride- which was about an hour long to Santa Cruz - Scorpion Anchorage. The ferry crew were very helpful. The galley had food/snacks as there is no food or water on the island and you need to get it yourself from the mainland. Got to see a lot of dolphins and sea lions. No whales that day. The first glimpse of these secluded islands + a pelican flying overhead reminded me of Jurassic Park - talk about a cinematic experience :)
Once you land, you immediately get a view of the sea caves, hikers, kayakers, snorkelers and scuba divers. Guides welcome Kayakers on arrival and instructions are given on how to proceed from thereon. You will be provided with all the safety gear - life jacket, helmet, wet suit, paddle jacket. There are lockers provided to store your belongings/food, picnic tables, changing stations and restrooms. There is a small store on the island where you can buy dry bag/mobile pouches etc.
I did panic before getting into the water on the solo kayak. A million thanks and shoutout to my guide Jeremy for not letting me back out at the last minute. He made sure all of us were safe and had a good time. I thoroughly enjoyed my time Ocean kayaking and made new friends. Paddled through sea caves, sea lions swam to us up close, saw jelly fishes and tasted kelp for the first time. I also got to see island scrub jays and island fox - which was so small and cute like a cat :) 

After that I had lunch, went to the visitor center and completed one hike to Potato Harbor (the views are stunning!) and got back to catch my 4:30pm return ride to the mainland. 

I am definitely going back probably for backpacking + kayaking here.

The sea cave adventure kayaking tour was amazing!! Our guide, Nikki, was so kind, fun, patient, and knowledgeable! They provided all the equipment we needed for kayaking (wet suits, jackets, helmets, crocks) and a safe place to keep our belongings while we were enjoying the ocean. The caves are amazing and the island is beautiful. We also had time after kayaking to go on a 1 mile hike before our ferry.

We saw hundreds of dolphins and a humpback whale on the ferry and a few foxes on the island. Everyone was helpful and wanted to make sure we had a safe and fun day. I highly recommend!
Thanks to Hector for making a memorable and safe trip!   Kayaking in Channel Island was unforgettable; beautiful Island, beautiful weather and nice people! Loved it so much! Thanks

Top notch experience! With our party of 18 paired into nine kayaks for a sea caves tour on Santa Cruz Island, we had three awesome guides. Half of our group was comprised of 10-14 year olds, and we all received detailed instructions and training in advance of our tour, plus we received additional help to ensure our gear fit properly. 

We chose the half-day kayaking tour. Our guides Caco, Jerry and Paige made the adventure not only fun, but educational, too. Caco has extensive knowledge about birds and shared with our group. 

The highlight for me: The Green Room sea cave! The sunlight on the water was absolutely stunning. I can see why people fly from all around the world to experience this sea cave first hand. Photos just don't do the scenery justice. 

As a bonus, it was impressive how Caco, Jerry and Paige worked as a team to investigate each cave in advance of our group to ensure safety by checking the tide and rocks inside. They went through before we did and returned to give us insight each time on how to best navigate the particular sea cave we were entering. 

Three hours on the ocean was a bit much for my 10-year old but he made it. This was a fabulous adventure and I hope we can do it again. I highly recommend SCAC, Caco, Jerry and Paige! 

Tips: 

** Book in advance
** Bring water, snacks and sunscreen that can fit in your vest pouch 
** Wear a long sleeved, comfy shirt and a hat (if it's windy, Choose a hat you can secure under your chin) 
** Wear closed toe shoes you're okay getting wet (or borrow Crocs from SBAC)

They were great. They put on a wonderful camp out for us at Ocean Mesa campground near El Capitan. They're staff were very good to work with and we were impressed with the professionalism and the fun that they brought to the event.

Don't hesitate to book them for your kayaking adventure tour in the Channel Islands!! They exceeded my expectations with amazingly funny and knowledgeable guides, who are ready and willing to take you into the caves! They always put safety first by going into the caves first to make sure it's safe, then gives us instructions on best way to navigate our way thru. Our guides were Sam and Scott, super fun dudes with a ton of info about the island.

Showed us different bird species, took us into a cave to view harbor seals from a respectable distance, and gave us history of the islands, all the while paddling like skillful watermen. 

The ferry ride to and from the island was unlike any ferry ride I've ever embarked on; felt like we were on the whale watching expedition! We were approached by a several pods of dolphins swimming and jumping in our wake, plus a couple of feeding basking  sharks right off the bow, so the captain slowed and stopped to make sure we all had ample to to soak in the magical sights! Even had knowledgeable conservation guides on board who gave all of us great information on what we were seeing and what they were doing. On the way back to Ventura we saw 3 basking sharks and more dolphins. An experience that I didn't expect to come across on a ferry. 

The island packers visitor center/gift shop is a must visit before u jump on the ferry. They sell t shirts, souvenirs, and different Sun hats, towels , sunglasses, etc... just things that you may need for the trip that you may have forgotten to bring. People in there are super helpful too. 

After this experience, I'm planning on bringing my kids to do a camping/kayaking adventure on the island that they offer!

Unprofessional and unaccompanied. Booked a reservation and then California shut down wineries and other attractions again due to covid 19. After a scare of covid 19 in our office I called to cancel my reservation about two weeks before the service. They told me they wouldn't? Ended up still taking $300 dollars without providing a service... hospitality spot on with the business. Go elsewhere unless your fine with people taking money, and being rude on the phone.

My husband and I  took a trip to Scorpion Anchorage on Santa Cruz Island with this company and we had an absolutely amazing time. I highly recommend the sea cave kayaking. Our guide Adam was very knowledgeable and experienced and we had a really unforgettable experience. We will definitely use this company again for our next adventure around Santa Barbara.

I went kayaking and it was amazing. The drive to ventura was beautiful.  The location was a pretty sweet harbor great for photographs. The guides were all super friendly and easy going. The ferry trip was a blast feeling the wind on your face and even animal sightings. Once we got to the island we broke up into groups with our guides which they do through experience.  My guide was Adam . he was pretty laid back made sure we got a good understanding on how to kayak and that were were comfortable once on the water. One thing he did. In the begining which i thought was pretty awesome and realistic asked each and everyone of us what expectations we had from the trip . once he knew what we wanted he took us out and gave us all exacly what we came for and more . even though i didnt get to ride a baby whale on my first day he did point out all the beautiful wildlife and told us some history about them . The whole experience was pretty rad and worth EVERY PENNY. after kayaking and going through the safe caves . you have the option to hike throught the island or go snorkelling . which i did but the water was freezing . Adam pointed out some spots worth checking out.  I did and ran back to shore. To dry off. AWESOME TRIP OVERALL

My husband and I had the best day kayaking! The tide was really high when we went out, but our guide, Ryan, went in to every cave before us and told us exactly what to expect and how to maneuver the kayaks. He made us feel very calm. Seriously one of the coolest experiences I've ever had! "The Green Room" cave was my favorite :)

My family and I (seven people) recently went on the Santa Barbara Harbor Kayaking tour with the SBAC. I booked online so I can't say much about the customer service in the office, but the site was extremely easy to use.

We got to the harbor and met our tour guide with no trouble at all using the directions provided. Our guide, Laird, was extremely helpful, funny, and pretty much all around awesome. After some basic instructions and a paddling demonstration, we set out around the harbor as a warm up, and then paddled out into the ocean to check out some sea lions. We rowed through the open docks and to the beach, where we tossed around a frisbee while Laird prepped an excellent lunch.

After lunch, we rowed through the piers, got a little ecology lesson, played some trust-requiring balance games, and finally made it back to the harbor. All in all, the trip took about 3 1/2 hours, and was an amazing time - and I can say that despite the fact that I was the only one on the trip who fell in. Definitely worth a try if you're looking for an active afternoon out in Santa Barbara.

Went on an amazing wine tour with a great guide Matt.The Mercedes van was clean and comfortable.Started out seeing a beautiful rainbow over incredible views.We visited 3 different winery's and enjoyed an awesome lunch set up for us .It was nice to learn about the area.For a fun day of exploring choose this company for sure.We will definitely use Santa Barbara Adventure for all of our future trips.Thanks again Matt.
Cheers Rich & Jamie

An absolutely amazing adventure! We booked the 2.5 hour kayak and snorkel tour at Scorpion Anchorage. Our guide, Aubrey, was super awesome, very knowledgeable, and easily rescued us when we tipped our tandem kayak over!  Would go again in a heartbeat!

My adult daughter and I did the sunset kayak with Jose as our guide. We saw seals, sea lions, and a lot of different ducks and birds, including western brown pelicans. The tour lasted about an hour and a half and took us around the harbor and out a ways to a bouy that had sea lions draped on the base. Enjoyable trip, Jose was knowledgeable about the sea life, history of the area and had good recommendations for eating and imbibing.
Highly recommended for a easy and manageable kayak trip.

Really awesome!  We did the sea cave kayaking at the Channel Islands and it was pretty rad dude!  Aaron, Bri, and Kenji guided us and they were great!  Really knowledgeable and friendly.  Patient and supportive as well.  The day starts early w/ a 8:00 AM 20 mile ferry ride to the islands... a bit choppy going out (very still coming back), but we saw dolphins, seals, ocean sunfish, etc and the crew is very funny and helpful.  Once on the island we get geared up and given an orientation by our very capable guides and then it's off to kayaking!  It really was an adventure... saw so much sea life and the caves were pretty intense/fun!  It's not just a boring paddle around... the caves can be dark and the swell can push you around.  We flipped over.  Fun stuff.  But I thought the coolest part was seeing a massive feeding frenzy w/ dolphins and seals and divebombing birds.  We were probably 5 yards away at one point... very cool to see seals and dolphins and even birds swimming right underneath you.  The water is so clear you can see crabs and urchins at the bottom near the islands.  We got lucky w/ the weather being perfect.  Afterwards we did a little hike and it's back on the boat by 4 pm.  Just a real fun day all around.  Thanks to SBAC!

We went on the painted cave kayaking trip but because of wind it ended up as just the regular sea kayak trip. They couldn't help the wind but apparently it's pretty normal for them to not be able to get over to the painted cave side of the island. The tours did not cost the same, which seems pretty dumb if they often bail on the painted cave trip which is more expensive. The guides were nice and informative. Lunch was really good. The boat was fairly large and had plenty of bathrooms and places to change into wetsuits. The sea kayaking itself was pretty limited. We didn't have that much time on the water and the sea caves were small. There were about 20 people on the tour so we often were waiting in lines to get into the caves, which was boring. The water and day was beautiful though. Also, the tour was supposed to include the opportunity to snorkel but there was no mention of this. Also, because of time of year we didn't see any whales and we also struck out on the dolphin front as well. In the end I can't say much more than it was a fine trip but not awesome.

This review is for the Wine Tour only. 
We booked a group to go wine tasting for a girls day out. 
It was the most AMAZING Experience ever! They pick you up (at our rental house) in an air conditioned van, drive you to the Santa Ynez Valley where you visit 3 wineries. I was amazed at the information that we were given on the valley and wineries. We had a private guide who prepared a delicious lunch at one of the wineries. He was super informative and very generous with time. We were a quick group and didn't linger for a long time at the wineries. We had some extra time before we were to head back to Santa Barbara, so we stopped at one additional winery (which we paid separate for) We asked to go to some wineries that were off the beaten path, and our guide got us there. I would highly recommend Ricardo as your guide. He was amazing! Would definitely recommend the tour. WORTH EVERY PENNY!!!

My girlfriend and I are knee deep into nature and love danger, so we decided to try out the Ultimate Sea Cave Kayak tour that they offered. Prices in my opinion were pretty reasonable considering how fun it was and how much it included. Our tour guide was Ryan and honestly, he was pretty awesome! Super helpful and funny. There's this secret beach in one of the caves that he brought us to that ass also pretty darn exciting. The reviews on here are legit and the trip definitely met my expectations and was well worth. 10/10 would go again (but probably the shorter one). Just be ready to be sore the next day!
This was a great experience!! Super fun and recommend for anyone to do! I did it and I was 4 months pregnant! Worth every penny :)

We had an incredible sea kayaking trip with Santa Barbara Adventure Company. We took the tour out of Refugio State Beach with guides Caco and Kami and we couldn't have been more pleased with the trip. We had a group of thirteen mostly made up of high school seniors and recent grads, and Caco and Kami really made the trip fun for the group. They had excellent knowledge of the area and provided great instruction. We ended the tour with a killer game of kayak water polo that our guys really enjoyed. Highly recommended!

Had a wonderful kayaking tour of Santa Barbara harbor.  Our guide, Hallie, was fun, enthusiastic, knowledgeable. Learned the "ropes" prior to setting off, they provided wet shirts to keep your top dry. (I had on bathing suit and shorts-your butt will get wet, of course) So fun to row out to open sea, see sea lions, seals, birds.  We watched an outrigger race nearby!  A nice lunch is provided and set up on the beach: wraps, fruit, carrots, hummus, chips, cookies, water.  Got to go under the pier to check out star fish.  It's a perfect way to see Santa Barbara from the water!  A really fun day. Thanks, Hallie!

I haven't gone to the trip but already disappointed, I booked the painted cave kayak trip four months prior to the trip, and they call me one week before the trip and told me that my trip was canceled, and needed to change to a different one that is more pricy and leaves at different harbor, which means I need to cancel the hotel I booked before and find a new one in such short time for a big group. And after all these troubles they caused their attitude was like... "after all we're the only company that has access to the islands, you have no other choices, so either come or cancel, no discount. " so I paid more, and changed to a different trip, I hope it's worth it. 

6/13/17
So I just came back from the trip yesterday, and it was awesome! The manager gave me a discount for the inconvenience, and the tour was pretty good. Our guides Tony and Scott were very helpful and friendly, and we saw a lot different kinds of wild animal during the tour, such as fox, dolphin, whale, sea lion. Very interesting tour, I recommend it!
Great place for camping get really busy over the weekend but doing the week this place is perfect.

Over the last ten years I've taken about four trips curated by this outfit. The owner, Michael, has built a one of a kind company. They have both some stock trips (Channel Island sea kayaking, Wine Country biking) but they will also create bespoke trips to fit your group. 

I've been on trips bike riding in Santa Barbara, taken surf lessons with them in Carpenteria, been rock climbing and hiking, and sea kayaking in the Channel Islands. All were top notch.

The naturalists are friendly and knowledgeable; they are also great with kids--patient, kind, encouraging. 

Let them plan everything and just show up and challenge yourself and have fun. You won't be disappointed.

Very fine day on the water. SB Adventure provides equipment, a guide, and a great day trip to Sta Cruz island. Our guides Landon and Laird were very thoughtful. And we didn't tip over as we shot thru the caves. Highly recommended.

My brother and I did the Discovery Sea Cave Kayak Tour. This was definitely a trip highlight for us! This company was very thorough and safety conscious. Everyone was provided with sanitized mandatory safety equipment (helmets and PFDs) and optional attire (wet suits, paddling jackets, water shoes). Everyone was given instruction and made to feel comfortable. Our guide was Dustin and he was excellent. He was attentive and knowledgeable, his enthusiasm for his job contagious. 
And finally, kayaking through the sea caves, seeing the marine life, learning about the flora and fauna, and just being out on the water were wonderful. We would definitely come back.

Took a sea cave kayaking trip to Anacapa Island and it was incredible! Our guide Adam was extremely knowledgeable and made the experience fun and memorable. Saw plenty of sea lions, fish, birds, caves, hidden grottos, snorkeling, lighthouse, etc. Too much fun!
Kayaking the sea caves in Channel Islands was awesome! We also did a 6 mile hike to fill time before the ferry ride back. It's a fairly long day in a remote spot but totally worth the trip!

I highly recommend this tour. If there's more stars, I would give more because of Hanna And Kyle. My sisters and husbands and I had a great time with them as they took us to the best wineries, served great food, patiently took pictures of us (oh we love pics!) and just made the experience one of our best. We left our hotel today and we also found out that they dropped off something we left in the van. Wow! They even went beyond their jobs even after the tour. Will definitely take this tour again sometime soon. Maybe different wineries but same team. Yay to Hanna and Kyle!

I'd give 5 stars for service, but only about 2 stars for the "adventure" itself. My husband and I took the painted cave tour on July 15, 2017 and discovered it's not for everyone.  I'll give you the good and the bad of it and a few tips.

The Good:
Great Guides that are friendly and super helpful! They go around the boat regularly throughout the 2+ hour ride out and back to check on everybody.  They are knowledgeable and watchful of everyone's safety and will fish you out of the water if you fall off the kayak.
They point out the dolphins and whales on the boat ride and even circled around to follow some pods of dolphins.
Breakfast and lunch options were good. Lunch is served while the boat is anchored.
They have all the equipment you need for the tour.
There are 2 bathrooms and one changing room.
They offer time to snorkel by the anchored boat after the tour.
The boat ride back was nice and smooth.

The Bad: 
The boat ride out to the island was SUPER choppy and they say this is more common than not. It's hard to walk anywhere on the boat on the way out. Several people were seasick because it was so bad. 
Breakfast is served as you are departing so you have walk around to get your food and drink while trying to keep balance and hold your plate. Then you have to eat in a warm cabin while the boat is rocking pretty bad. (It's no wonder people were sick!)
The bench seats on the boat deck are really uncomfortable after about the first hour. 
Once on the kayak the rough water and swells were nerve racking (this is not comfortable for a novice, but it's not tiring either). We kayak regularly, but mainly in harbors and lakes.  Open water is challenging to say the least! You will get a break at each cave you stop at (3 or 4 of them).
The caves are super dark and the guides (you get two per group) only have on a headlamp. 
The painted cave was not as impressive as we expected.  It was so dark you couldn't see much (forget good pictures while inside) and the barking sea lions prevented us from going in very far. 

Tips:
Travel light! Windbreaker, hat, towel, change of clothes, sun block, water bottle. 
Don't eat too heavy at breakfast and don't drink too many liquids because you do not want to have to use the restroom when the boat is rocking all crazy!
After you eat go on the deck and sit on the bench with your feet against the side of the boat and it will keep you locked in no matter how much the boat rocks.
Don't bring anything on the kayak with you except one water bottle.  You won't have time to use anything else and the you won't want to let go of your paddle while fighting the waves.
If you bring a waterproof camera make sure you have it well secured to your person.
If you have a headlamp, bring it. Those caves are soooo dark.
Use the wetsuits provided unless you love freezing cold water. You are in the shade most of the time and it's cooler by the island.

Conclusion:
I can say that we never would have had this adventure if not for this tour.  I can now say I have been to the channel islands, kayaked through turbulent waters and entered several sea caves!  That being said, we would never do this again.  Maybe the water was extra rough the day we went, but I'm not taking any chances. Some people love it. There were a few people in our group that had been on more than one cave tour at the channel islands. 
Even though the tour required a lot of kayaking against rough water and waves, you row with the current and have breaks at each cave so it wasn't too bad.  My husband and I were not tired at all, but most of the boat was asleep on the way back.  I guess youngsters need their naps! LOL! 
If you are up for the adventure and don't expect too much of the caves then by all means don't let my perspective stop you.  That's why I offered some tips to make your adventure a little easier.  I think if I knew in advance what to expect I would have been better prepared for it.  So, I hope this helps!

I had organized an offsite for our department. When we voted on day trip ideas, sea cave kayaking on Santa Cruz Island was the most popular choice. A rarity was that there wasn't a single no-vote.

There are about four guides operating the tour. I sent an inquiry to all of them with questions. SB Adventure was quick to reply and gave a good answer. Other guides never replied or replied over a week later with an informative "call us." Upon calling and only getting the voice mail. Another guide upon asking, if there would be any kayak games for our group, while on the water, replied with a shocked 'definitely no.'

On the morning of the trip, everyone in our group was very excited. The boat took us past a pod of hundreds of dolphins. They had babies with them. Dolphins served in the wave that the boat through up. Some of us had serious cameras and got amazing photos.

The guides were well organized. I have done rafting and other guided activities, these guides had the most gear to offer. They had extra water for us. They had straps for glasses to avoid losing them. They had snorkel gear, wet suits, spray jackets etc. They were very well set up.

Kayaking through the sea caves was super fun. The guides would give us instructions like 'stay left, sharp right turn, avoid the submerged shelf there, enjoy the green light...' The whole navigation thing was super fun. Quite a few kayaks hit walls or got stuck. The guides would talk them through the steps to get out of those situations. And, yes, the kayak polo game was super fun.

Two little things. I'm so used that guides usually have a ton of jokes during orientation. These ones were pretty serious. I suspect, because we were a company party, they might have left out the jokes. The other thing was that on the way back the boat didn't stop for whales, dolphins, oil rigs or anything. In past trips with the same boat, they used to always give us at least one attraction to see on each trip or up to four on lucky trips.

Mountain to sea bike ride was awesome.  The crew was attentive and fun - lots of good info tidbits, history and conversation - and the view down the mountain was awesome.  It's one time I was glad SN was fogged in because the view above the clouds was epic.  Not a lot of exertion but an experienced.  Was a great preface before our MCConnell's ice cream!!!!

Santa Barbara Adventure Company is AMAZING!!!!! We used them for our community-based organization's middle school summer program and took three trips with them and all of them were outstanding. We did an overnight camping trip with sea-kayaking and two beach team-building trips and all were great. The staff was always so helpful and accommodating--shoutout to Kim, Gabe, Kyle, Ty, and everyone else who was so awesome with our students, logistics, and making sure we all had a great time!

I'm giving it a 4 because this start was a bit rocky. We didn't know where to go and we kept asking the sea landing people but they kept telling us they were a different company and did not know where/when our instructor would come. When the instructor arrived we were angry/frustrated from the confusion and waiting. She was a very sweet girl and said they told her she needed to come at 6pm but they had told us to be there at 5:30pm. After that initial issue everything else went smoothly. We got to see sooo many sea lions it was super cute. Only recommendation would be for them to put a sign where they want people to wait at because that would have also been helpful. As well as telling everyone the same time they should get to the location.

We finally decided to go for a short getaway during this Covid time. To keep activities outdoors, we booked the ocean kayak tour from Haskells beach. What a great time! Not only was the weather conditions optimal, our guide Marc was fantastic. His knowledge of the local history and review of the ocean biology kept us entertained , especially our 11yo.  We particularly loved the row under the pier- not a vantage we get to experience all the time. We will definitely book with this company for our next visit!

Today we went on our first sea kayaking trip. We drove from Santa Barbara to Ventura were we caught the boat to the Channel Islands. The ocean was very rough but the captain did an great job getting us out of the harbor and safely across to the island. The pier at the island had been damaged by the last set of storms so we had to take a skiff to the shore. On the island our guides Caco and Ryan gave us lessons on how to maneuver the kayaks in the ocean, ocean safety, general information about the island and the sea caves. They were both very friendly, experienced and professional. With their help we were all able to launch our kayaks safely through the high surf. We leisurely paddled along the beautiful shoreline viewing sea birds, seals and sea lions along the way. At the caves our guides gave us a quick lesson on how to negotiate the sea caves and helped three kayaks through the narrow archways and into one of the large caves.
