The couple ahead of us were hit by a large wave and their kayaks capsized throwing them into the rough surf. Caco and Ryan jumped into action retrieving their kayak and helping them to get back on. When our turn came to enter the sea cave we were hit by another large wave and sent us off course and onto the rocks. We were able to stay upright and with the help of our VERY professional guides be were able to get out of a potentially dangerous situation. We are very thankful that the company hires such talented guides. We all paddled back as a group to the beach were we had lunch and free time to explore. As we hiked around the island saw the Island Fox that is only found on the Channel Islands. On our boat trip back we saw a pod of whales and a school of dolphins!! This was a PERFECT day filled with thrills, excitement, the beauty of the island, wildlife and friendly people!!! I would HIGHLY recommend this trip and our guides Caco and Ryan. FABULOUS and worth every penny. A++++

We did the painted cave kayak tour, and left santa barbara harbor around 8am for a 2.5 hr trip to Santa Cruz Island. Thankfully the waters were calm enough this day, which allowed us to go to the painted caves, as it is a seasonal and limited tour. 

The entire tour included breakfast and lunch. you can also bring your own *beverages to store in the fridge for the day. There are bathrooms and outdoor showers on the boat, and a bunch of  comfortable bunks, that I crashed out on for a bit on the way out to the island. 

Throughout the day, we saw a good amount of sealife. Tons of dolphins riding along the bow waves, a bunch of sea lions, all types of birds and fish during snorkeling. It was awesome!

The island itself and experience is 5 stars, but I gave this place 5 stars, as all the guides, captain, workers and such are all so nice and friendly. 
Our kayaking guide kyle was awesome. He knew a lot about the island, and shared old chumash tales about the island.

5 last minute tips:

*Dont forget to bring your parking ticket to the check in/ sea landing for validation ($3.00 total)

 *Bring swim fins if you want to be efficient at snorkeling (they provide snorkel gear)

* bring a waterproof camera(gopro recommended)

* use the tip jar

* Beverages = alcohol

My daughters (10 & 14) and I had a great time surfing with Mark and Rachel on 4/29/16! The weather wasn't the best, but we made the best of it.  Mark was a wonderful teacher, and very knowledgeable about the California coast.  Even though we only booked the 1/2 day lesson, they invited us to share their meal and provided snacks and water.  Mark even provided special cookies, since it was his birthday! It was an honor celebrating his special day with him, and I would DEFINITELY recommend the Santa Barbara Adventure Company!

If you're wondering what you're going to do in the Channel Islands and if - as the name says - you're up for adventure, look no further.  The Santa Barbara Adventure Company (aka Channel Islands Adventure Company) well solves that dilemma.  

Not being a camper, I booked 3 consecutive day trips to Channel Islands National Park and was looking for something to break up the hiking routine.  I'd never before been ocean kayaking but reasoned that were I going to, doing so in a national park's marine sanctuary would be the place to try it.  Also, they promised the exploration of sea caves via kayak and that sounded amazing.  

It was.  

SBAC offers full day (about 4 hours total) sea kayaking adventures providing you with guides, kayaks, paddles, wetsuits, water shoes, and helmets.  For an additional fee you can order what I heard was an excellent lunch (about $35) from someone they source through.

Arriving on the island around 9:30 a.m., I was in the kayak around 10:30 a.m. and cleaned up and back on the beach around 2:30 p.m.    
Midway through the adventure, after kayaking in some open sea waves and into and out of some caves, guests were given the option to end their time kayaking.  What came next was the real adventure: about 90 minutes of even more challenging and interesting caves to explore.  It's a ton of fun.  

Our safety-conscious and knowledgeable guide (Marc) pre-scouted each sea cave, reported on how to handle it, and gave us a choice of opting out.  I opted out of none but came close to regretting it on the small cave called "Doggie Door".  Flat in the kayak with my helmet wedged against the cave's roof, waiting for the water below to recede I had a brief "what was I thinking?" moment.  

You'll learn a whole lot about the islands and marine life while on this adventure but what you'll likely remember is that it's an experience unlike any other.  By the time we finished and got back into our regular clothes there was just enough time remaining to sit near the rocky beach at Scorpion Cove and nibble at some food before it was again time to board the boat back to the Ventura harbor.  

If you've got a little adventure left inside you (and $149 per person to spare, boat ride extra) I think you'll have a lot of fun with these folks.

This company offers a LOT of activities which include: kayaking at the Channel Islands, kayaking in Santa Barbara, surfing lessons, sunset cruises, biking, hiking, camping, wine tours, paragliding and tons of other things.  Just check out their website.

Our group of friends decided to go kayaking at the Channel Islands.  For $165 a person, this is roughly what our day looked like:

8am- Arrive at the port, pay, get ready, meet our guide
9am- Leave the port on our 2 hour boat ride
11am- Arrive at Prisoners Bay at the Channel Islands, sign waivers, briefing on kayaking 
1130am- Start kayaking
1:30 pm- Stop at a cove (Tinker's) for lunch
2:15 pm- Continue kayaking
3pm- Get back to the dock, swim around for a little
3:30pm - Boat arrives
5:30pm - Arrive back to the port

I really enjoyed our day except I wish there was more time for kayaking!  We didn't get to kayak through the sea caves which is what I was looking forward to the most.  Our guide, Jennifer, was EXCELLENT.  She was friendly, patient and so incredibly easy to hang out with.  It was almost like she was just another friend in our group as opposed to any formal guide.  She even brought her sister along who was visiting.  Lucky for us, it wasn't a busy day so it was just our group and her which allowed for a lot of flexibility on where we wanted to kayak, when we wanted to stop and our overall pace of the day.  

Remember to follow any suggestions/advice for the day:
-wear water shoes or old hiking shoes (it was so hard to walk on the pebble beach)
-bring a light windbreaker (it can get cold but our guide brought extra)
-wear swimsuits (so you can swim/jump off the dock but you will get wet no matter what)
-pack a good lunch (kayaking will make you very hungry)
-bring a small backpack for your stuff (your guide will have waterproof bags to put them in)

I would definitely go with this company again for the other activities!

My husband and I were doing a tour of the West Coast, and decided to take a day trip to the Channel Islands. I honestly think SB Adventure Company is a company full of highly skilled and trained individuals who truly love what they do. Every person from SB Adventure Company that we came in contact with on our trip was both friendly and helpful. It was easy to set up since they handle the tickets for the ferry ride to and from, as well as book your activities with them. We did the Discovery Sea Cave Kayak Tour and also rented snorkel gear. It is truly a full day - it's 1+ hour to get to the island, then you are escorted to their camp area, then you can embark on your tours. There seems to be different departure times in the late afternoon, but you do not get to pick what return ferry you are on, so expect to leave any time between 3pm and 5pm. Also to note: the parking area is moderately busy, so if traveling with suitcases in your trunk, I would say this is a safe spot for them to stay for the day.

Our ferry ride experience may have been unique in the sense that we were told it was one of the choppiest waters they had gotten all summer. A bit of advice: Try your best to sit inside by the door positioned in the direction that the boat is moving. You can sit inside or out but do be warned it will be cold, and you likely will get wet if you are outside. 

I'm sure you are aware you need to bring ALL food and ALL necessities (sunscreen, phone battery pack, etc) with you for the day. There is no cell service whatsoever on the island. SB Adventure provides water to fill up your water bottles, but that is it. No food. Anywhere. I HIGHLY recommend using Channel Island Provisions, especially if you are visiting from out of town. The man who handed off our food supply for the day was exceptionally knowledgeable about the island (and motion sickness). We both got the Deluxe Day Pack because like I said, its a FULL day on the island - the more nourishment the better.

In regards to kayaking, my husband and I are experienced in kayaking on rivers in Boston. Note that the Channel Islands are in the Pacific Ocean - we didnt do that math. Also note: bring a change of clothes for your day of adventures. Its not "will you get wet" it's "you will get wet." I didnt understand that going into the kayak trip. I also recommend getting on the first kayak tour of the day, as it seemed like the later kayaks started to get backed up. 

Our guide Nick could not have been more knowledgeable, friendly and helpful. He was consistently checking in on the group, giving people a tow when needed, and being respectful of everyone's limits. He was such a great tour guide! We were clearly the dunces in the group of 4 tandem kayaks and never once did he shame us - Thanks, Nick! He truly wanted to make sure we had a great time!

I had the best experience exploring Santa Cruz Island and it was all because of the SB Adventure Company. I drove up from LA to catch the morning ferry and my guide greeted me at the ferry. We had a lovely sail over, saw dolphins and then were greeted by the most majestic, BLOOMING island on the other side. 
This company has this all down pat and I was so happy that I elected to go with them rather than on my own. They prepared me with what to wear, what to pack and even set me up with a delicious breakfast and lunch which I definitely needed after all that kayaking!

I did the Sea Cave Adventure and it did not disappoint. It was so cool to go inside the caves and manuever around. My guide Pablo was the BEST. He was so laidback yet very professional and knowledgeable. He knew a ton about the island's wildlife, flora, history, geology and answered all my questions. The water was crystal clear and the coves and beach stops were so memorable. 

I only wish I could have stayed longer! Our ferry ride back, we caught sight of 3 gray whales plus a MEGAPOD of dolphins--unlike anything I've ever seen. 
This company it is all smooth sailing. Thanks guys!
Muy buena diversión y entretenimiento para mi y mi familia. Mis hijos quedaron fascinados con la experiencia.  Un sueño cumplido

My husband and I have loved to kayak in the ocean and on lakes for many years. We wanted to tour the caves for as long as we can remember only we went after a storm and it was ruff seas the day of our tour. Jeremy Carberry took good care of us. I went into the water twice. He kept calm and made sure I got back on my kayak. Never was so happy to be back on dry land. We will go again but next time it will be a beautiful calm day. Thank you Jeremy for keeping us all safe and sharing the beautiful caves around Scorpion anchorage. 
Hope and Shane Reeves

This review is for the Discovery Cave Tour on Santa Cruz Island.  I took this trip with my husband and our two kids, ages 6 and 10.  This was an amazing experience, well worth the money. 

I found the online booking a little confusing, so I called in, and I am glad I did, as booking over the phone was an easy experience, and I was certain I had everything I needed.  

We booked the hour and a half because we weren't sure if we could handle longer with our two kids, especially since we have done this before, and our daughter isn't much of a paddler (in fact, on this trip, surprise, even though she has paddled before, when the guide handed her a kid-sized paddle, she says, "No, I am not paddling. Daddy will paddle."  I guess she had decided that she just wasn't up for it. I think she was a little scared and felt better just being a passenger). My 10-year-old son and I shared a kayak, and we probably could have handled another hour of paddling, but my husband carrying my daughter as cargo was ready to be done after the hour and a half. 

The caves are amazing. Talk to your kids about it first though, show them some videos.  Our kids were cool with it, but we felt really bad for this other family with us on the tour -- the youngest child in their party, probably about 9 or 10 years old, wouldn't go in any of the caves at all, which meant they paid for this cave tour and instead basically just kayaked around the edge of the island with no caves.  Sad because the caves were the coolest part. 

Word to those of you who get sea sick (I do, so does my husband). We both took Dramamine for the boat ride to the island, but towards the end of the kayak tour, it was starting to wear off... right as we got to the windier side of the island. Both of us started to get nauseous.  Fortunately, I had put snacks and more Dramamine (the chewable kind, works faster) in my pouch, so I had it on us, and that helped a lot (although not fast enough. I wish we had taken more right before we left on the tour). 

Our tour guide was great and full of knowledge. We learned about the birds, about the fish we saw, all while kayaking. I expected to learn a lot about kayaking; I didn't expect to learn so much history.  This was a wonderful trip.

Superior in Adventure Excursions.
East to work with, sometimes a bit difficult to get a hold of via phone.
Ruby, who works at the rental station on Santa Cruz Island, was very helpful and friendly. Very good customer service experience here as we rented snorkeling gear for my group.

I booked the Coastal Kayak tour in SB to surprise my bf for his birthday. It was AWESOME! And we BOTH LOVED IT! It was my first time kayaking and it was a GREAT experience. I can't wait to go kayaking again!

We went on a Friday so the harbor wasn't as busy luckily. There were only 2 other people so it was just us 4 plus our guide, Grant. He was really chill, knowledgeable about SB, and allowed us to choose how far and how long we wanted to kayak... VERY flexible and accommodating. It made the experience very relaxing and enjoyable. We got to see sea lions, DOLPHINS (we got lucky, one swam right under us), kayak under the pier, and see star fish. We had lunch on the beach and the included lunch was great! They get major points for having a vegetarian option! We had lentil wraps, hummus wraps, guacamole, chips, apples, crackers and drinks. I was surprised all that food and a pop up table was on Grant's kayak. Grant guided us into the beach, giving us hand signals when to paddle, stop or go backwards... that part was easy. Going back into the water from the beach was an adventure in itself. I got soaked by some waves but I felt really confident with Grant's instruction and my bf's strong paddling abilities hehe :) I can't say enough how much fun it was!

SB adventure co. makes sure you're prepared for the day by giving you a list of things to bring.. that definitely helped since I had never gone kayaking before and couldn't ask the bf. Bring a waterproof camera! :) They provide tandem kayaks for no extra charge, wet suits, life jackets, and a wind jacket. Making the reservation was a breeze. I'll definitely book another trip when we head out to SB again. The channel islands kayaking trip is next!

We had a great half day Kayak Tour on Santa Cruz Island with Santa Barbara Adventures! Our guide, Tony, was awesome. After a short paddling lesson, we hit the ocean. Tony took us to five or six sea cave and gave us detailed instructions on how to maneuver through the cave. It really was an adventure!

We did the afternoon tour. The water around Cavern Point was really rough due to high winds. Tony gave us the option of turning back, but our group went for it and made it to one last cave. The waves were high but it worked out ok. We heard later the morning tour is usually calmer.

The tour company has a nice set up near the visitors center on the island where they outfit you with a wetsuit and/or paddle jacket, PFD, helmet, and paddle. There are places to store your extra gear and someone on hand to keep an eye on your gear so no one, including the foxes, swipes it.

Everyone involved with the company was really friendly and helpful. The half day tour was perfect because it allowed us time to experience the island's hiking trails. We made it to Potato Harbor and back before our tour seeing plenty of foxes and even an Island Scrub Jay along the way. Thanks for the great experience!

So let me start by saying that I made a complete error by booking two kayaking tours for my husband and I on the same day. Santa Barbara Adventure Company was completely willing to work with us, which they did not have to do. We ended up doing two kayak tours both at 1 o'clock with Chantal and Tyler.  They were both completely different tours, very unique and we had a beautiful time on the water in gorgeous Santa Barbara for two hours. What else could you ask for. We even got to see a pod of bottled nose dolphins while kayaking out to the green buoy we saw a lot of natural wildlife including sea lions, harbor seals, went directly through a group of 60 or more pelicans resting in the water... (What a blast) and had a great time hearing about all the different yachts, boats and harbor stories from the local area.
Guides were on time, and very professional.  They made sure that everybody had the skills they needed before we hit the water.  I would recommend this to any level of kayaker, even beginners. An extra shout out to Chantal... She made the tour very personal and delightful. Make sure you give it a try. We got our tickets through GROUPON too!  THANK YOU, All!!

This is a great company, my class and I are in town from the Bay Area for a class trip and we went kayaking with them. They were very very professional and safety/fun oriented. We did some cool team building activities in the ocean and learned a lot while having a great time.

I had a really great time. The guide was knowledgable and made the whole day fun. It was a small group, so we each got one-on-one attention. Usually with these things you get PB&J and a high five, but the lunch was ample and plenty, even for a vegetarian (which they asked about ahead of time). One hundred dollars seemed a little steep, at first. If you are a first time surfer, I would definitely recommend spending the extra dough for the extra attention. Definitely recommended!

We booked the Amazing race for a team building exercise. We had 3 groups of 4 people and had a blast.  Our guide (Brina) was awesome, and each of the other guides made it a fun experience. Highly recommended

Matt and Dustin were AWESOME!! They were extremely patient when we were on the mountain and explained some cool facts about Santa Barbara's history. They were incredibly sweet and prepared a lovely lunch for us. The sites we saw were incredible; it really was a mountain to shore adeventure!! I highly recommend working with them in the future

My fiance and I had the pleasure of doing the sunset tour last week with Addison (?) (hopefully spelled it right!) It was our first time ever kayaking and we had the best time!

A day before our tour, we were apprehensive about going down to SB from OC, due to the fires. We called and Brittany informed us that unfortunately, they would not be able to refund, but would reschedule if we had major concerns. She also sent a detailed email about the current weather conditions and general fire assessment of the areas. That reassured us, so we decided to go. I am so happy we did!

Addison was waiting for us at the dock and was super patient and helpful with setting us up for our gear and giving us instructions. We were off and away and met so many beautiful sea creatures along the way! She educated us on the areas, recommended places to eat and go and she was so easy going, I thought I was talking to a girlfriend!

Overall it was such an amazing time and I can't wait to try the other tours! Thank you!!!

Beware: your arms do get sore, but it's worth it! The company provides dry bags for your items and vests and jackets! It does get pretty chilly out there, so wear a jacket!

I would highly recommend their tours! Very professional, patient and so much fun!
Beautiful! Can be very packed and busy on weekends but it's still super fun! Coming back again here soon! Thanks
Nice little beach cove here, neat old staircase to shore get off at San yasidro exit, not much parking,  water here is always calm !

Horrible place, way too crowded and my car was broken into and my wife's purse was stolen.

Trash all over , Malibu beaches are way better.

Beautiful beach with clean shorelines. This particular beach has become one of my favorites because it has never been too overcrowded when I have visited over the years. The water is rather clean from sewage or debris, which makes it much more enjoyable when taking a dip.

There are several seafood restaurants within close proximity, which makes the task of choosing a spot to eat delightfully toilsome. It is rather easy to drive to, as many other California coastal getaways are, thanks to the Pacific Coast Highway. 

Pleasant ambiance in the fabulous Montecito area. I shall be returning as usual.
Miramar beach, is just a slice of heaven. Clean, private and majestic. Perfect place to escape all the every day, stuff
A slice of heaven in Montecito. I love coming here with my family to unwind after a crazy busy work week.  Perfect place to escape to. Bring a blanket and your lunch, and forget about the world.

Miramar is a small, private beach in Montecito that has just as much parking as it does beach. It's never very busy and the waves generally stay pretty calm. The beach is in a small curve like shape and bordered by private residences. The sand is usually pretty soft but not many shells are to be found here. They technically allow dogs on leashes, although I see them off leash very often and no one says anything.

I come here when the tourists crowd all our other nice beaches or to watch the sunset as it is a lovely location for that. Bring a blanket and/or jacket, though as the breeze does pick up in the evenings and it gets pretty chilly. I love seeing all the dogs play and taking a short walk along the waterline. 

Miramar is conveniently located very close (basically walking distance) to the Coast Village Rd. shops, restaurants, and bars. This makes it a prime location to go for a romantic stroll post steak dinner and cocktails at Lucky's on Valentine's Day. (There's a Vons up the road too for all your flower and card needs ;) No need to thank me..

name: Miramar Beach Bar
categories: Nightlife, Active Life, Bars, Hotels & Travel, Beaches, Lounges

yelp_reviews.text: Service is everything...it makes or breaks the experience. Beautiful grounds, waited 20 minutes to get a drink at the beach bar. Dined at Caruso afterwards and the dishes were spectacular but the service was NOT.  When half of our group has entrees served but the other half waits 10 minutes, that is totally awkward and not cool.

It feels like you are in Abercrombie store. Lots of cute servers , but no service. We ordered 6 items. Two came and one of them wrong and 4 others did not show up at all, while 10 cute servers stood around like they are on vacation. 
The manager was kind and was the only person working, but the HR in that hotel needs a revamp since they hire the wrong people. Beautiful views but everything else sucked. Why these guys don't get it , Santa Barbara is not Beverly Hills.
The host was great!! (don't know his name but Italian guy with 2 boys.) Took the time to teach my kids how to play curling game. Drinks were great and atmosphere was perfect! Can't wait to come back.

This place has an amazing view from the cocktail lounge outside but would be nice to have a little bit more shade outside to enjoy your drink. Some umbrellas would be nice. Customer service was horrible the only person that was running around and practically taking our orders and catering to us was a sweet man I believe he was the bus boy named Mario. I feel like every customer out there were flagging him down for drinks or asking him to find the server. I really wanted to stay there longer but the service was horrible. I will come in a few moths and hopefully the service has improved by then.

Over priced terrible quality, margaritas taste like they are made with artificial lime juice/pre made margarita mix and are $24 before tax and tip! 
The seafood is poor quality and pricey too. 
The only good thing is the location. 
My advice, come drink water and avoid everything else

Gorgeous.
The decor is reminiscent of a beautiful, classic yacht. You feel like you could be yacthing when adjacent to the bar since the overlook is just there. You cross the train tracks to arrive and it's a quick walk through garden grounds after passing through the main lobby. 

From an aesthetic standpoint, Every detail is attended to here. 
When we were there, the hostesses were feuding and it caused a minor delay while awaiting a table. It's worth a small wait though. The ocean blue waves were sparkling and the weather perfect. 
We had two rounds of drinks, tacos, impossible burger & a salad at our table. It was dog friendly and we sat at a high top. Food wasn't exactly memorable but everyone loved the tacos and fries. 
It was quite expensive but worth it. We'd return in a beat. It's so elegant and completely our scene. Loved it!

Gorgeous views, delicious food and great service! Sauro is the manager and was very welcoming and pleasant. Edgar was our server and did excellent! He recommended the sushi and it was fresh and delicious. There is guest parking for those of us that come for only food and drink.  If you want to enjoy great service and a relaxing time then go during the week. The weekends will be slower service and packed with people as most beautiful places with water views.

Dana from Guest relations is probably the rudest guest relations person i have ever spoken to in my 15yr career. I will never go back or recommend it to any of my clients ever. Disgusted by her attitude. I hope management can get back to me on this.

Had a great time at this spot. Wonderful views of the ocean. Excellent mixed drinks. We came on a weekend when the BBQ menu was available and got the salsa and guacamole which was very tasty. Would definitely recommend for a nice afternoon setting. Austin is the man!

Awesome service by the water. I ordered the poke bowl, which was really fresh and perfectly seasoned. Austin, our server, was excellent and very accommodating. As a designated driver I wanted a half pour of wine and he not only made it happen but put it on the house. A class act!

The Beach Bar is on the beautiful Rosewood Miramar property and has an amazing view. It's the perfect place to grab a delicious cocktail to enjoy beachside. The Purple Sky is my favorite drink there. The female manager (whose name I forgot but wish I could remember) provides incredible service and checked in often to make sure everything was going well. Highly recommend to anyone passing through Santa Barbara or Montecito.

This property is GORGEOUS but the service at the beach bar is subpar. I was alone in the early evening waiting for a friend who was running late. I was at the bar for 1 hour and not one person asked if I wanted a drink or checked on me after I ordered my own drink at the bar. The only people that assisted me were the bartender and a "busboy" who brought me water and an olive/nut assortment. While I'm sure the service will improve with time, it was an unexpected disappointment at such a beautiful (and expensive) resort. That said, I'll be back and hope to see an improvement.

My husband and I walked over to get information to book our daughter's engagement party.There wasn't a host or hostess present so we proceeded to the bar.A young man in sunglasses ran up to me and asked where I was going.The bar, I replied and he walked away.My husband and I got to the bar but the available seats were in the sun and it was hot.We decided to move to a less sunny spot.The same young man in sunglasses appeared and literally started yelling at me.He said you said you were going to the bar and now your walking around looking for a table.We didn't know he was an employee/host.Havent you ever been here, he asked? We have, I replied.When he asked.It must have been a while because things work differently around here now.I said do you chastise all your customers?The place had three tables taken and everything else was open.I asked if we could sit at the two top and we were granted permission.I went to sit my purse down and the host said his wife would never put her designer bag on the ground.We should have just left.We ordered 2 Mai Tais. They were $24 a piece and watered down.Forty five minutes later it took several attempts to flag down the young female server and she brought us our check.We don't want our check.We wanted to order another round and get some food.The setting is great but it is so poorly managed I don't think I'll ever go back.Deep Sea does only wine but it is a lovely ocean setting without the bad attitude and you can rent it out.For a wedding, the El Encanto was fabulous.
Don't understand why this place not a 5-star review.. incredible service, food and spectacular views ! what else could you ask for?

Many kinks need to be ironed out. We moved our party to The Manor House bar after the 3rd Person could  not find another person to light the heat lamp. By the time someone came, who I regret not getting his name, because he literally tried Eagle Scoot moves to light the damn thing, my party already retreating to The Manor Bar. I commend you Eagle Scout Man. Literally zero other people gave a shit. The worst thing a service person can do is delegate a task to someone else.... and then not even do that. We waited 45 minutes. We had to ask three different people to take our drink orders.... because "we weren't in their section." This might be acceptable at a truck stop diner, but not here. I've been doing this for 20 years. You can take a drink order, put it in the POS system and keep the table for yourself or transfer the order to whoever the table belongs to AFTER you put the order in. Don't ever tell a customer that their experience isn't your priority. It's not professional.
Just had a couple glasses of sunset drinks with my wife.  My server was Andrew and was VERY nice and accommodating.

Miramar Beach Bar is the classy beach bar at the recently remodeled Rosewood Resort in Montecito. Rather than beers, bathing suits and flip flops, you are more likely to see martinis, Birkins and Manolo Blshniks are more likely to be seen at this beach bar. Service and drinks are impeccable. The view, perched above the beach, is amazing. The view alone is worth the price of the cocktails.

Matt B. grew up right by the Miramar, so we decided to check it out last time we visited Santa Barbara, walking over from his childhood home with our dogs. It was around 5:30 when we got there, still sunny out but early enough to drink freely, and we hit up the beach bar for cocktails with a view.

The bar was over past the train tracks that go through the hotel. We had to step across them to get there, and I found this more charming than inconvenient--the Miramar has done pretty well incorporating an active train track into a luxury property. The beach bar was attractive, an open air wood deck, handsomely furnished, overlooking the glittering water.

It was already busy when we got there. All of the tables were occupied, so we stood at the bar and watched for an opening. The bar just got more crowded instead, though not with a raucous beach crowd. There were a lot of families with kids and dogs. Our bassets received plenty of pets and compliments, which they enjoyed immensely.

We were able to get a first round of cocktails without too much trouble, but by the time we were finished, the bartenders were slammed. We ended up talking to another couple at the bar, and seeing how long it took them to get their drinks, we decided to leave before getting a second round. Not the bartenders' fault--they were friendly and professional and doing their best. The place was just not equipped to handle the sunset beach bar crowd.

Our cocktails were okay. I had the Italian mule, made with Cynar, Amaro Montenegro, ginger beer, and lime. It was kind of medicinal and sweet. I should've just gotten the Moscow mule. Matt had the Sea Mist, a light, refreshing drink with Toki Japanese whiskey, Yuzuri yuzu liqueur, citrus marmalade, lemon, simple syrup, and plum bitters. We sipped our cocktails, snacked on some mixed nuts, and soaked in the view.

I'd recommend Miramar Beach Bar if you're looking for a lively place to get drinks with an ocean view. Expect slow service at peak hours, though, and maybe don't bother with complicated cocktails.

I have had the best experience here with friends. Every weekend we meet at the restaurant to enjoy great view and talk about business. Everyone is very nice and the food is delicious. I can't wait for the weekend to hace my meetings and feeling like in a vacation. I highly recommend The Miramar.

I came with my family including 1 year old baby on 4/4/21. We checked in for a table and was told we would be seated in 10 minutes. After 20 minutes, I asked when our table would be ready. I was told by the hostess that she "forgot that others had checked in before us" and there would not be a table ready for at least 20 more minutes. We left. Very unprofessional service. Too bad.

My mom and I spent the holiday weekend in Montecito at this resort,  was exactly what I expected ..just a  wonderful get a way.  Amazing breathtaking views ,  staff is very polite and helpful ..Emily at the bar above Caruso's was so kind and wonderful!  A must stay if you're ever in this area ..best Resort I've been to in years..

I'm a local and have been here several times since it opened.  First, the

Upsides:
Location is fabulous!  Even the train that runs through it is fun and Disneyesque!
Yep.  That's about it.

Downsides:
Service is always slow
Food is horrible!  Absolutely stay away from the hummus!  It is served with jaw-breaking thick chips and not pita...it is also doused in heavy chili oil.  Okay for adults, but not for kids.  Chicken quesadillas are equally inedible.
Drinks are good...but hardly worth the price.

Overall assessment:
Go there for the sunset.  Get a cocktail.  Don't bother with the food.

I love to chat with people and have found that about 80% of the guests are from LA.  I've come to think that it's just a place for them to come and be seen.  As for us locals....we'll go elsewhere!

Horrible !!!
I'm a 35 yr resident of Montecito. 
Seriously get over your self Rosewood ...
If your a local stay clear ..
I've been to the finest places in the world and it all starts with hospitality.
This place is a joke.
Anyone reading this that's visiting ...
Go to .. The Ranch , The Biltmore , Honor Bar ,
Tre Luna ... 
Brofey Brothers ,
Even The funk Zone ,
Not a big fan ,
But better then this silly wanna be chic ,
Yada , yada .. 
please !! 
Give me break . 
Just stay away .....
This place sucks .
Could go on with details .
Just take my word .

I visited Miramar yesterday afternoon , around 1 pm, me and my friend Strolled around the property , very pretty and well taking care. We want to try the food truck. Apparently they advertise to be open for service from 11:30 am .It was a windy day and by the looking of the manager, things were not under control. He took my order and got upset because I was not familiar with the ingredients, I felt like I was an inconvenience to him. He asked me 4 times about my drink and failed to give me the correct one at the end. Also I purchased a hot dog with regular French fries and they forgot the hot dog bun and gave me sweet potatoes fries instead. Bad service and bad attitude. Good luck with your reopening this Thursday. Make  sure you train your personal and be aware that good customer service is as important as your food and please be nice with customers, especially locals. I am not coming back ever.

The only good thing about this place is that it by beachfront. This restaurant being owned by the Rosewood resort where we were staying at was very disappointing especially for an establishment like Rosewood. There was no standard in the meals that arrived. 1st night We had the chicken sandwiches some drinks and the fried pickles. It came w the 3 sauces and presented well which we loved and homemade- we ordered a 2nd one and it didnt come w any at all so we asked for them. Of course it took a while and id already gone halfway through it. The following day we ordered it again as a snack and had drinks on the beach. The standard was again subpar as it first came w/ 2 of the sauces only and the 2nd and 3rd order only came w the spicy orange aioli. We asked for the white one over and over and by the time it came out we were done. Had high hopes bc the restaurant is nice and appealing but there was no standardization.

Miramar's views are indisputably the best in town! Yes it's overpriced and moderately pretentious, but the views trump all of that. I promise that watching the sunset at one of their high top tables is worth a $20 glass of Rose. 

Food/Drinks - Besides the fact that they're about twice as expensive as they should be, their margaritas and cocktails are actually pretty good. I would avoid the food unless you're starving, price/portion ratio is laughable.

Service - When I moved to SB I was going here at least twice a week and the staff was so inviting. Even the GM would come chat me up for a few minutes and make sure I was being taken care of. Very professional and friendly.

Ambience - My pictures don't do it justice, you have to see it yourself. This is by far the best ambience of any place I have reviewed. Great place to relax after work or to have a drink if you're having dinner at Caruso's or The Revere Room.

Great little spot at Miramar Resort! We didn't stay at the resort but just stopped by for a quick bite at the Miramar Beach Bar. The weather was beautiful, the view right by the beach was amazing! Listening to the sounds of the waves was just so relaxing. They didn't have a full food menu just appetizers and cocktails. The drinks were perfect, not too weak and not too strong. Had the crab cakes and really liked them, the hummus plate I wish they would of given a few more veggies or chips to go along with it. Service was great! I'd go back again!
First visit while in Santa Barbara for a month, was quite delightful.   The light bar food and well made drinks were just  perfect  while overlooking the ocean and Miramar Beach.  Give it a try...

The views at Miramar are debatably the best along the Santa Barbara coastline. Miramar Beach Bar, located on the upstairs deck at the Rosewood Miramar provided glorious views at all times of the day with great finger food. My girlfriends and I came here for lunch this morning prior to checking out from Rosewood. We ordered the following: 

Two orders of the tortilla chips, guac, and salsa. (One order is good to share as an app for two people). Two people ordered the fried chicken sandwich and rated it a 4/5. One ordered the cheeseburger and rated it a 4/5, and I ordered the Italian sandwich (recommended to me by the waitress), 5/5. Overall, great lunch spot overlooking the beach if you want quick finger food. I also recommend the cute coffee truck parked out front to get any drinks (I recommend the iced oat vanilla latte).

So while staying a month in Santa Barbara we tried the Miramar Beach Bar a couple of times.  So to be clear...you should go there if you love the views, the ambience and a good cocktail.  With that in mind, you will love it.  Service is not rushed; it is a cool vibe and it is very friendly.  You WILL pay, but do not let that get in the way of enjoying yourself.
If you can come here and try to catch the sunset! It's so amazing with a view of the beach and you can go down to the beach and enjoy  a expensive cocktail

Stunning and exactly what Montecito needed! I've spent a lot of time in Montecito and it was such a pleasure to come up for a weekend and experience Mira Mar. I look forward to staying here next time. Breathtakingly beautiful, relaxing and posh but, not uptight and stuffy. I'll be sure to take more photos next time.

Very uninviting. Manager Sauro Moist might need to learn how to speak to people politely. Definitely lacking good customer service. Very pretty there which is great. Would only recommend someone there based on looks alone.

We got the Purple Sky, Pearbellini, and Miramar Margarita. They were all sooo good! The purple Sky was probably the best drink I tasted all weekend (and also super cute). Drinks are veryyy expensive. Our total was close to $90 but definitely worth the experience. The view is beautiful and the service is unmatched! Would definitely come again :)

Jewelry stolen by housekeeping, we will definitely not be returning. Noticed immediately after dinner and the hotel clearly not taking any responsibility. They have still not offered anything other than an apology. The unprofessional handling of security issues will not bring me back.
Loooove this place, the view is spectacular. Jared is THE BEST  Mixologist and the service was top notch. He made this experience so special! I'll definitely be back ASAP:)

I visited on a hot Saturday 10/3/20 for lunch with a friend. There was a 20 minute wait and the hostess directed us to a small cart serving a choice of two beverages: spritzer and margarita, we ordered one of each. The cost was $51.00, which I would not mind if it was not in plastic, no where to sit and no shade umbrellas while waiting. We ordered lunch from small menu: a hamburger and bratwurst with pretzel, waitress suggested fried pickles as well as there was no sides. The burger was average, the bratwurst juicy but the pretzel was stale. In both of our opinions the food was over salted and service below average.  The only redeeming elements were the setting with beach views, manicured gardens and smartly dressed staff.

I want to leave a review for my bar server at the Upstairs beach bar at the Rosewood resort in Montecito. Heather is her name and she was happy, conversant and personable at the same time very professional. She told me about the Rosewood and how beautiful it is here and what an amazing place to work. 

It made me think that the Rosewood takes very good care of their employees because she loves it, or they just hire happy well adjusted professionals   Either way , I will come back. The wine was delicious and the view couldn't be better.

What a shame that the food and service at this truly beautiful location have gone from good to bad.  Service has always been slow but yesterday it took an hour to get food.  And the result was not worth waiting for. Am I chopped chopped salad.  Really a bowl of lettuce with some strawberry slices and shrimp on top. 
The new menu leaves off the great ceviches and fish tacos. And why?  Those are faster to prepare, and clearly the little airstream kitchen can't prepare burgers and salads quickly.  Go back to ceviche and tacos.  And train your wait staff. Our server did not even know what was on the menu.  Rick Caruso, you are much better than this.

If you're at the Rosewoood and want to just chill, watch the sunset and have some sushi, it's a fun relaxing place. There are no TVs though so don't  expect to watch any sports games. Your main focus should be the relaxing ocean and whoever you are with. All they have for food on the menu is sushi. If you want other types of food, you have to go to the cafe at the pool.

Beautiful location, awesome views, staff are friendly, professional and welcoming.  I have been here a couple of times and each visit was totally enjoyable.  Customer service was 5 stars they really made us feel comfortable and welcomed. This outside bar is a few steps from the beautiful resort and steps away from the beach.  Should you visit this bar you must try the Dungeness Crab Fritters DELICIOUS

Friends came up from LA for a post-COVID visit and stopped by the beautiful Miramar Resort to enjoy a gorgeous sunny Montecito beach day.  We decided to sit up on the Beach Bar and have a round of cocktails.  The ambience is casual, comfortable and takes in the cool ocean breeze.  The people watching is great, and the waitstaff friendly and attractive.  Why only 2 stars?  Simply put, the cocktails were too expensive to be as watered down as they were!  WTF...$24 for a Margarita that was mostly mix and slushy ice.  The black salt drawn artistically on the side looked like the charcoal from an aquarium filter and unless you planned to lick the glass below the rim on the outside, total fail.  The Aperol Spritz was more color than taste.  The Moscow Mule came in the usual cool copper mug, but had no taste.  And actually, the non-Alcohol Pear drink for the designated driver was delicious-- and priced the same as the tasteless weak cocktails.  We won't be returning if its a good robust cocktail we are looking for.

Beautiful place for cocktails  nothing more  perfect than sipping a refreshing drink right on the beach. We went for drinks for a friends birthday and loved the atmosphere. It had a hampton's feel to it!

Something so calming about how picturesque this whole hotel is. I personally loved it and think it's worth it to come by to enjoy purely the atmosphere, as well as the drinks. Came here with a friend around happy hour time while I was visiting her in Montecito. The beach bar is situated right above the sand with amazing views and is just hands down boujeee, but the service was still pretty friendly and attentive. There's different kind of seating options and we were lucky enough to get a comfy couch facing the water. We both ordered an aperol spritz, that had some kind of fruity twist to it (passionfruit?) that I can't remember, and it was refreshing but nonetheless a standard spritz. It came with a little trio of bar snacks that was a nice touch. I completely skipped the olives but the mixed nuts were herb-y and yum, and the pickled veggies were good too. 

We only had time for one drink before dinner, but this was the ideal spot to catch up and I could've hung out for hours. When I visit her again I definitely want to return for HH and try some of the food I didn't even know they had! There's also the cutest pink Fiat Jolly in front that I honestly regret not getting a photo op with. :)

Disappointed again. We had been to The Revere Room Previously and it was a wind tunnel/morgue with bland food.

It was a nice day so we decided to go back and try the Beach Bar. Although the ambience was lovely the food was sadly lacking.

We ordered the slider which is a single small slider that came with fries for $20. It was unexceptional and flavorless and the fries were borderline McDonald's style.

Equally disappointing with the halibut ceviche. A slimy mess it's flavor can't even be described as ceviche like, it was like some old fish you had scraped off the bottom of a shoe worn by a Scally wag from a doomed troller.

Our disappointment led us to try and order something else so we can get some type of satisfaction from one of the dishes so we ordered. Next up the shrimp and fish bites. Again, just kind of blah.No flavor and the fish is somehow indescribable as a part of an animal it is more like mush that was squirted out of a tube prior to being fried. The puzzlingly weak sauce that came with the fish was a yogurt base that was trying to be some type of tartar sauce but fell way short

Our server was very pleasant and the lemonade was pretty tasty but overall this restaurant as seems to only depend on its location at a ritzy hotel and proximity to the beach to exist.

This food served anywhere else would truly doom any restaurant

Ocean front white water waves are in full view from the top deck of this loungey bar where the comfy chairs are situated to strategically face the views of the true blue waters, LoL!

You must cross the rail road tracks on the grounds to get to this ocean side building from the resort. 

Lots of sunny or covered and shaded seating on this outdoor deck. Of course, I always opt for the SHADE  (no UVA or skin damage for me, LoL)!! Aka shady lady..

Plenty of cocktail options and 2 pages of fresh gourmet light bites, ceviche, tacos, or a burger.

My seared halibut tacos were light, fresh, citrusy, delightful with the pickled onions, micro greens, and specialty made sauces. Please keep in mind that they are very small sized portions. Quality, not quantity when dining here..

Conveniently open for afternoon delights. Service was wonderful, no rsvp needed, first come first serve. Obviously, note that it gets very crowded on weekends.

The stairs towards the lower deck are where Caruso's (open for dinner only) & restrooms are located.

In a few words, "no value here, just ambiance". We were informed upon arrival that our valet charge at $17 would not be discounted or waived for eating there. My wife had a Mai Tai for $24 from a mix, without traditional dark rum added, which our server acknowledged. Our luncheon, prepared in an adjacent motor coach and not a kitchen, consisted of a "Surfer" burger at $28 which was too large to eat properly and dripping with juice and soggy on the bun and an unappetizing fish taco for $18. In all, this "luncheon" with gratuity was $121 in addition to the $17 valet charge. There is really no dollar value here, just ambiance, which might be acceptable if  you only wish to visit and have a brief meal or cocktails. Consider parking outside outside the complex in one of the many spaces, walk in, and enjoy the visually pleasing grounds and ocean view.

name: Tunnel Trail Hike
formatted_address: Tunnel Rd And Mission Canyon Rd

yelp_reviews.text:
Nice hike with some great views over looking Santa Barbara and the Channel islands. The trail starts on a paved path for about a 1/2 Mile then turns into a wider fire road. Shortly after it becomes a single track hike. It isn't well marked. Being my first time there we needed to ask a couple of people which way it was to the top as there were other trails along the way.
Wear good shoes and expect large rocks on the trail.  It can be strenuous for those who are not used to hiking or don't excersize but overall a great hike which can be done in a couple of hours up and down.

It was a really nice 5 hour hike. It would have been nicer if it wasn't so hot. We took our dog. It might be too much for a dog that is not used to hiking. We had to carry her down in a backpack because of the heat. The views of the city, the ocean and the mountains were really nice. The only reason that I am not giving it 5 stars is because of the road and mountain bike area that you go through at the top. It isn't as nice as the rest of the hike.
Incredible view in the amazing Santa Barbara Foothills!! On a hot day be sure to bring LOTS of water. Dog friendly, which makes it even a better hike.

Great hike.  Took me about 5:30h roundtrip, but that's b/c I did take lunch and take time to eat at the top.  A tough, sweaty 3h up, and a relatively easy 2h down.  I did go all the way to the summit of La Cumbre Peak at 3995ft from sea level, about 3000ft from the Tunnel Trail trailhead.  The description of the trail on santabarbarahikes.com was surprisingly accurate and did help me know which way to go more than once.  The only part where I went the wrong way for a bit was at the very beginning -- the fork where Tunnel Trail goes to the right, and 7 falls & Jesusita Trail go to the left, so watch out for that.

I am in pretty good shape and do a decent amount of hiking, and I would rate this as a tough hike -- it was very much like the Bright Angel Trail coming out of the Grand Canyon, a continuous 30 degree ascent with lots of step rocks (but about half the total distance as the BAT).  I only took 3L of water and I think that was about 1L less than I should have.  I took my water filter but this was a bone dry hike -- no water source to filter and drink on Tunnel Trail.  Also, I started at 12:30pm which was fine, I got back with plenty of sun left, but I also hiked during the hottest part of the day.  

My recommendations for anyone considering this hike:
1. Definitely plan to go all the way to the summit, that will be about 11 miles roundtrip, plan on that taking 5-6h at least.  The summit is cool b/c there are picnic tables, a pit toilet, and an old abandoned outpost of some sort.  And you get amazing views of not only Santa Barbara but also of the vast backcountry and of the Gibraltar Reservoir (see pics of all).  
2. Take food, have lunch at the summit on one of the picnic tables.
3. Take at least 4L of water per person.
4. If at all possible go with someone.  I went alone but there a lot of potential things that could go wrong on a long, tough, and relatively remote hike (I didn't see another human after the Rattesnake Trail fork and the remaining 3 miles or so to the summit) like this -- sprained ankle, fall, snake bite (see pic of snake I saw on trail).  But if you're smart and tough you could do it alone.

I have to comment on Kali's critique that you can do easier hikes in CA for equally good views.  That is very true.  But me I hike for 1. The workout and 2. The view (as a reward for the tough workout), so this kind of hike is my favorite -- tough, but well worth it.

Highly recommended.
Such a beautiful hike! Deff one of my favorites. Especially in the spring time - tons of wild flowers and great views of the city and ocean.

Just finished hiking the entirety of Tunnel Trail from Tunnel Road to East Camino Cielo Road--about 9 miles round trip. If you're a runner and used to measuring distance by road miles, be advised, you will not be completing this in the 75 minutes or so it takes you to do 9 miles on paved roads. We hiked at a pretty decent clip today and this took us close to five hours (without more than a two minute break to enjoy the juncal formations at the top). The first 4.5 miles climb steadily uphill, reaching an elevation of about 3,700 feet.  It's up up up and mostly exposed to sun. Trails are single track and rocky, so you'll need to stay focused and tread carefully. This is a strenuous trail. Prepare yourself accordingly. You probably won't enjoy this hike if you're not in at least semi-decent shape. And even if you are, you'll feel it.

Though difficult, the rewards for this effort are well worth the time and fatigue. Tunnel Trail is a hike through the fascinating geological history of Santa Barbara. You'll see plenty of Matilja and Coldwater sandstone, Cozy Dell Shale and (my favorite) juncal formations near East Camino Cielo Road. These are formations from the Eocene Age. That is, you're looking at stuff that formed about 50 million years ago--pretty amazing.

Be advised, the descent is a bit of a relief, but no walk in the park. You can pick up the pace, but on tired legs, stay focused as there are a lot of slippery rock areas and it's SO easy to misstep. At 9 miles out, bring water for sure and maybe even a snack. We didn't and probably could have used a little boost at some point--you'll expend a lot of energy on the way up.

Send me a message for more specific directions to the trail head. Happy hiking!

The Tunnel Trail in Santa Barbara is a lot simpler to find than it is to hike. While "Santa Barbara Hikes's" site considers the trail a moderate one, I'd argue that this one is for the well-seasoned hiker. 

Remember this caveat when you read hiking guides: Many of the posters/bloggers are off-trail/climbers-hikers, and just about anything with an actual trail is "easy" to them. I stand firm on my rock that *any* trail requiring one to cross over a stream by hopping from boulder to boulder as a trail that demands a level of physical agility and expertise. Of course, a band of rugged teenage boys--who may not be hiking experts--would do well here, assuming they don't care about whether or not they fall in. It is this crossing of which I speak that made the trail more cumbersome than exhilarating for me. 

The range was developed, flanked by homes, and--with this development--came a need for ginormous towers of pulsing radiation, towers that robbed the view of its awe. There are a plethora of paths, all pulling hikers into different directions that seemingly make no sense. Twice, I reached what I thought would be a peak only to find another metal troll (cell tower). There are no public facilities here either. The Golden State offers too many more kaleidoscopic scenes to warrant a return to this trail.
Be sure to not leave valuables in your car.  Our car had a window smashed, and by the look of all the piles of glass in the dirt, it was not the first!

If you can find the trail head it's well worth the drive! Best at sunrise for sure! 

Easy hike very short distance from the road to the ruins.

Easy little one mile walk if you take the long, winding driving route. (You can do a longer hike from down below if you choose). The short version is perfect for my pug who has a short tolerance for exercise, lol! Scenic view. Would love a romantic dinner date staged up here.
Absolutely beautiful! Definitely more of a walk than a hike but the scenery is gorgeous. We loved the drive, as well. Highly recommend!

This place was great! It is small and not an actual castle, but it had beautiful views and plenty of cool spots for photos. I got there around 10 am on a Sunday, and had the place to myself for at least half an hour. People started showing up after that. 

I followed the coordinates (34.51855, -119.793234) that Carol L. posted, and found the place perfectly! Many thanks to her! There were also large spots for off-road parking, so no need to worry about parking. I even took photos to set your mind at ease. To some, the drive up may be scary. But I thought it was kind of fun. All the twists and turns, climbing up higher and higher until you're even above clouds! 

Once I made it to the trail entrance, which is marked by a gate, it was very straightforward to get to the castle. You can even see it at a distance after walking for a few minutes. This was more of a walk than an actual hike.

Enjoy the drive up and have fun at the castle!

Hard to find trailhead! Short hike but a beautiful and mysterious abandoned site since 1940! Great views of the Pacific Ocean and Lake Cachuma!!

Directions to KNAPPS CASTLE
From the 101 Freeway in Santa Barbara, take Route 154 north for ten miles. At the top of the mountain, turn right on East Camino Cielo and drive three miles east to a small pullout on the right opposite the gated dirt road on the left (the start of the trail). The trailhead is located on a C-shaped bend in East Camino Cielo, one mile past Painted Cave Road, the turn off for Chumash Painted Cave State Historic Park.
RED GATE
Gps
(34.518155, -119.793234

This is a great Nature Walk and not much of a hike. Very moderate elevation change and a great place for the family or a walk with the dog. Beautiful views of the valley floor and gods beauty. 15 minute drive up a switchback style road which is paved with ample parking for any size vehicle.

0.8 of a mile to and back. That's the 'hike.'

Anyway, Knapp's Castle is not much of a castle anymore. However, it's quite nice to see the ruins along with the nice view of the Santa Ynez River Canyons. It's a beautiful location if you want to take photos. 

Here's the coordinates that might be useful if you're looking for where to stop your car. (34.518155, -119.793234)

On your way up or down, you might as well stop by the Chumash Painted Cave since you're in the area... Not much to see, but it's a nice cave painting. Bring a flash light to see inside the cave since it's gated off.
Wow!! What a great time with the kids. Easy 10 minute hike beautiful surroundings. Views for days.     If might a little tough for older folks.

This place is more pretty in picture than in person, so don't get your hopes up too high.  Best time to come would probably be at sunset, but due to time issues, we got to see this place in the early afternoon. 

We followed a Yelp review to get here. Someone wrote that it was 10 miles past the Painted Cave road, but it was not.  We drove 10 miles up the winding path and did not see it.  Later on I read that it was closed because the owner was doing construction on the site.  Disappointed, we decided to head back out.  Suddenly, our guardian angel appeared, standing just 15 feet away from us. (I think he kind of followed us, then he stopped where we stopped pretending to enjoy the view. He stood close enough so that I could ask him if he knew where Knapp's Castle was. He explained how to get there and said that it wasn't closed off, so I jumped for joy! He ended up guiding us down the winding road to the gate that was about 1 mile past the Painted Cave road, (not 10 miles).

Besides our group, there were 2 other younger guys there, so it was quiet and serene. 

How to get there: Coming from L.A., take the 101 to the 154. Turn right into E El Camino Cielo road. When you see Painted Cave Road, keep going straight on El Camino Cielo for about 1 mile (that's what the guardian angel said). You'll see a red gate with the sign "Right to Pass Revokable by Owner" on the left (very easy to miss...see my pictures). Park somewhere and hike about a half mile in. He said the hike was very easy. Yes, it was easy, but you'll still huff and puff on a hot day, so be prepared. 

Our guardian angel also reminded us of rattlesnakes, so be careful when you're not walking on the path. 

Would definitely come back here again at sunset.
It was really nice up there until someone broke into our truck and got my bag. I just want everyone to be careful when you park your car.

I have been here three times and it is absolutely stunning . Also the  hike/ walk is pretty easy going down but once coming back up it is a bit more steep. However, only a 10 minute walk. The only concern is that you need to be very careful what you leave in your car . We had someone break one of our windows and take my bag. Make sure you don't leave any valuables in your  car because it seems to be a reoccurring problem on this spot. There was glass from other cars on the floor and also previous reviews mentioned getting broken windows and stolen  items. Other that it really is beautiful.
A nice drive just past the painted caves. It's .5 mile walk from the road. Once you walk down the dirt road you'll see a beautiful view and what's left of Knapp's Castle build in early 1900's.

Awesome views, easy walk.  Took my kids around 5 pm and there was no one else there...it felt a little creepy actually.  There are shipping containers and tools on site and a make shift bunker...felt a little horror movie-ish.  I think my mind got the best of me.  Go with a few ppl and it's great.  A picnic would be even better.  We just felt like we were being watched.

This is an absolute shame, because this place is beautiful. I had been waiting for quite some time to do a little maternity shoot at the castle. I grew up in the Santa Barbara mountains and thought it would be a great homage to my roots. After many reschedules, my photographer friend and I were finally able to lock in a date. When we got there we were immediately approached by the caretaker who questioned what we were doing there. This was before any camera equipment or anything was out. Photographer had her camera backpack on and we had one bag of my outfits. Although we knew it was private property, we had known many people who have taken professional photos there and there was nothing posted against it so we didn't think anything of it. The caretaker was immediately rude and spoke to us with a very demeaning tone. He mentioned many photographers took advantage of the place with leaving trash and getting in the way of other visitors and we assured him that would not be the case.
He finally obliged to us shooting, with the notion of being 'quick'. We got going right away. As we were taking some shots in an area that was completely empty, he came back around and basically told us we weren't being quick enough and to leave immediately. For. No. Reason. There was another group up the way taking photos with a professional camera as well and he didn't make them leave, but all of the sudden he was on some power trip and told us to leave, following up with how beautiful I looked (We'll I did spend $300 on hair and makeup for this shoot!) It was all incredibly strange.
I am sure this review will not do much, but I felt it was necessary for others to know that photography may, or may not be allowed - depending on how the caretaker feels. Again, if there was any sort of signage regarding no photography, we wouldn't have even tried. But to be based off of one mans 'feelings' is quite inappropriate. Even the other group of people taking photos was apologizing to us and was clearly confused at the situation. Such a bummer.

we've done this hike about 3 times now and it's about a half mile to get up to the "castle". It's good for kids, elderly people, and dogs. the trail is a little rocky at the beginning and slightly inclines but it's pretty easy. It's a lot cooler up there than down is SB so i recommend bringing jackets.
Arrive by dawn or dusk to catch a breath taking view of the valleys :)

Getting there was a little confusing cause we drove right passed it. There isn't a big sign that says "Knapp's Castle" so reading the previous reviews helped. The walk or hike if you wanna call it that to the ruins is really short. Keep in mind the weather when you go. Once you actually get there, the views from the site is really spectacular. It was quiet when we went so it was perfect. The actual site of the ruins isn't massive. Almost all of it was pretty much gone but you can use your imagination once you stand in the grounds what the place might've looked like. I can imagine the place being really beautiful during sunset or sunrise! But this is one of those places where if you see it once, you don't need to see it again.
Check my photos  it's a nice place to get awesome shots  don't expect to find parking , just park on the side road and walk about 5 minutes and you will see the castle

Was a very short hike the castle was interesting to see can't believe someone built that. The view is amazing . Their were alot of people there. Alot of wild life even saw a snake and many different lizards.

My first experience here was four years ago and I remember the place being ravaged by graffiti, which was disheartening to see. After returning here years later, the caretaker and owner did a great job renovating the place to remove the graffiti and make the place much more appealing for the public to visit. If you are a photographer or a person that just admires scenery, this is a great getaway spot, and one of Santa Barbara's hidden gems.

The owner and caretaker are mellow with visitors as long as you don't vandalize the area and that you respect the property.

The real beauty of this place happens at night when you can stargaze with friends on a moonless night and see everything clearly. Rarely does anyone come here at night, which means you will have the area to yourself.

The place is hard to find initially, but there's a red gated entrance that you should see as you're driving through East Camino Cielo. Once you see multiple dirt turnouts with a red gate, that's where the trail starts. Follow it for 0.8 miles and you are there!

So glad my family and I checked out Knapps Castle! It's a short walk from the main road on a dirt road.

The view was spectacular! It's incredible to think of what the Castle would have looked like if it did not burn down.

If you want to check out a cool, historic place with a great view, I would recommend checking out Knapps Castle.

Pretty nice place! It's a good place to relax and enjoy the view 
Now there are people working there but it's not a big deal and a good place to take some beautiful photos!

Amazing view, short hike, great place for photos! 

*Look for the giant iron gate. The trail begins there going DOWNHILL. If you're coming from SB, it will take awhile.
*Park on the street. No "adventure pass" needed.
*The place is still open as of this posting. 

There is construction material strewn throughout the ruins, which is unfortunate. Visit and enjoy it while you can!

If you like off the beaten path surprises, you should stop here. It's a short walk from the car to the castle and getting there around sunset is ideal. Mr. Knapp built this in 1916, sold it to a woman in 1940 for $10k and it was burned to the ground in a fire 5 weeks after she moved in. Mr knapp was one of the Union Carbide founders and his personal mission was to make the forested areas of Santa Ynez assessable to everybody so he supervised the building of roads thru the dense areas.  Interestingly he built his mansion facing the vast hills and Lake Cachuma, not the ocean. He donated much to the Cottage hospital, local churches and was instrumental in recruiting Dr. sansum. When you see the view I hope you feel the soul of a wonderful man. Take your kids there. Tell them the story about how hard it would be to see the national forest without roads and Knapp made it happen.

Awesome view. When driving up to Knapps castle. Your GPS will tell you to turn left. You'll notice there is a gate blocking the dirt trail and you cannot turn left. Park your car on the right side of the road and you will have to walk 5-10 minutes the rest of the way to Knapps.

It is a beautiful view. Just be careful walking along the edges!! Get your pictures in and enjoy the view.
Easy to get to; lot of history to the place. Google it, and read up before going there. Worth the drive and the walk. Great views.

Hike is very short(for those beginner hikers or people who don't like to hike). Once you reach the castle don't expect anything too extravagant, there's a couple of pillars here and there. One thing that amazes me was the clouds. I highly recommend coming here early in the morning. It is beyond beautiful

Its hard to called a Hike since .8 of a mile round trip But the views are amazing and the ruins really cool, unfortunately there is construction equipment and random stuff (like a bathtub) other than that it is a really cool place to go and officially check it out of the bucket list. BTW since its a private property you are technically trespassing.  The parking is really limited to just the side of the curve in a narrow road.

Summary: A hidden gem, but it takes a very narrow and difficult driving route to get to and it is difficult place to find. 

Do not enter the yelp address into waze. It will take you to down a longer route and it will take you to a middle of nowhere. A lesson learned for me and my boyfriend, who have never been here before. 

Tip: Thank you, Carol L. for the coordinates! Enter these direct coordinates (34.51855, -119.793234) into Google Maps and you find the gate entrance, which is easy to miss. 

Despite the troubles of getting to Knapp's Castle, it is honestly a beautiful place with an interesting story. You also get a beautiful view of SB, which is breathtaking.

Beautiful hidden castle! We did our engagement session here! Everything about it is gorgeous! Not easy to find and a lot further away than we had expected, coming from Santa Clarita. I'd come back again to just relax and chill out. If I get the chance, I'd come back here for more pictures. My only suggestion is to get out before it gets pitch black! There were bats flying everywhere! And even some flying near the ground!!

We visited Knapp's Castle this past weekend and it was extremely foggy & windy. We didn't get to see the grand views everyone else talked about, but did get to see the remaining pieces of the castle itself. Definitely crawled onto the arches for some fun photos too. It's about a 10 minute brisk walk from the parking area.

Came here and was told I wouldn't be able to see the castle due to construction that was going on. Such a disappointment, I drove 2 hrs and couldn't even see I.T 
We did go down a different trail and attempted to see the cave but I.T was closed off as well.

Other reviewers have the history so I won't repeat that.  

What I will say is that this is a really cool place to visit and very accessible. 

I wish it had been more peaceful and quiet.  Unfortunately easy access means there are a lot of other people visiting too so try and come during the week!  

From where you park (along the road) to the ruins is less than a mile hike.  I did it in a skirt and flip flops but there were some uneven areas and rocks so tennis shoes probably would have been a better choice.

Word of caution - there was a lot of broken glass from cars being broken into along the road (people that break into cars to steal people's belongings are loser a**holes and I hope they get caught) so don't leave your valuables in the car. 

It was really hot and not as much of a breeze as I thought there would be so up high. Bringing water, sunscreen and a hat are all good ideas.  There were also a lot of bugs so bug spray is a good choice too.

There isn't too much to explore, but the views are amazing
A short hike (walk) to a beautiful visit. Well worth the side trip. Make sure to bring water and use a bathroom before you get out there.

Super cool. Who knew there'd be a ruin in SB?!

The drive up (i think depending which route you take) has a brief part in a mountainous residential area that is a bit nerve wrecking  but it's not long and not as bad as Id been led to believe. Found the spot where to pull over and park near the gate, but had there not been a couple cars already there we totally may have missed it. 

The walk to the ruin is very brief, about 5 minutes. Do beware of snakes on the path, as my husband nearly stepped on one! Didnt appear to be a rattler thankfully. 
The site has incredible views, makes for great pictures and even has a cool rope swing over a slight cliff slope for the daring.

Pretty solid place when you are visiting from out of town, especially if you have never seen a castle in ruins. It is a popular place to go during sunset (and I bet sunrise too). 

To put you into context: this castle was built over 100 years ago (1916) by George Knapp, the founder of Union Carbibe (a subsidiary of Dow Chemical). In 1940, Frances Holden bought it and allowed her friend to move in with her. The castle was destroyed 5 weeks later by a forest fire. Today, it still remains private property but it is open to the public. it is a great place to view Santa Ynez River Canyon, Lake Cachuma, and San Rafael Mountains. 

It is ~20 min drive from downtown SB to the Castle. The roads get a bit windy as you go up the mountain. Make sure to put the trailhead coordinates (34.518155, -119.793234) on Google Maps ahead of time, as the reception is spotty/non-existent as you go away from the city.

Once you get to the coordinates, you can park on the side of the road (no parking lot) and the trailhead will be on your left. The "hike" (I would say short stroll) was less than a mile round-trip (0.8 mi). There was some elevation changes here and there, but it was not bad. The views on the "hike" will make you forget that you are even hiking.

Once there, the view was magical. Even though the sun was setting in the west, I couldn't stop staring at the mountains in the east. 

Only downside is that it is a bit crowded and noisy from the tourists trying to get the right Instagram photo. But I would still come here again to just chill and look at the view.

1st time in the Santa Barbara area!

Pros:
-10 mins walk to the castle from your car.
-you can climb on top of the broken buildings at your own risk. 
-good spot for views.

Conclusion: a must see when you are in the area!

Beautiful scenery, nice to stop by and just hang out. Some renovations currently happening, would be nice to see some information about the origin of the castle! The landscape is begging for a panorama haha. The little walk to the actual ruins isn't hard and it's definitely worth it!

So let me just start off by saying this review is for getting engaged at Knapp's Castle, and not necessarily for general recreation (though parts of this may apply).

When we first arrived at Knapp's Castle--my then-boyfriend Chris had told me it was recommended to him by his best friend, and so we stopped by as a quick side trip on our way home from Solvang--I wasn't too impressed with the outside. There's just a metal gate, and if you're wearing sandals, you have to be careful not to get gravel or sand in your foot. Subtract one star! And the main foundation was a little ways from the shoulder on the highway where we parked (but not too bad, since he had planned ahead of time that we wouldn't have to go too far before we reached the ruins).

We arrived on the small campus, our presence virtually ignored by the few European tourists hanging around on the outskirts. At this point, there is not much to recommend Knapp's Castle over other hikes, since most of what is immediately visible are some construction vehicles and lots of chaparral overgrowth; however, we continued farther in, and soon reached a series of tall, ampitheatre-style stone tiers. We descended (Chris assisted me), and found ourselves facing a breathtaking view of Lake Cachuma and surrounding mountains, framed by the property's original sandstone arches. Great photo opportunities here, for sure! Chris rummaged around in his camera bag to set up the shot--and when I turned around, he was holding a ring instead of his camera.

I'd love to relay the details of what was said, but in my state of shock, those specific words were lost to the void. (Full disclosure: I have since contacted Chris regarding this issue, and he was able to remedy it to our complete satisfaction.) I do remember him sinking down to one knee and (probably?) asking me to marry him. My initial expectations of crying, smiling, etc. were misplaced, as engagements apparently reduce me to stunned silence for several critical seconds at a time.

(Oh, and did I mention the dog? Knapp's Castle is ostensibly pet-friendly, and while I don't believe in omens, making eye contact with someone's debilitatingly handsome Weimaraner after I said "Of course I will!" certainly felt like one.)

So, main event finished, we toured a little more of the grounds, littered with shy lizards and lovely sandstone stairs. We also took advantage of additional photo ops in front of an abandoned Jeep just to the left of the trail on our way out.

All told, I would definitely come here again. A small wrinkle in our (Chris's) plans occurred when his digital camera broke the previous night, but since he needed his 35-mm camera bag to hide the ring, it turned out that using our film camera worked out just fine.
