After winding through what seems to be the backyards of some very lucky Ellwood residents, a grove of tall eucalyptus trees rises in front of you.  Look up!  At first it looks like eucalyptus leaves are just fluttering in the wind... until you notice that they're not leaves, but masses of monarch butterflies dangling from the tree branches.  Every now and then a pair would flutter down from the branches and land on the ground in, what I'm assuming to be, a mating ritual.  They toss about in all their carnal glory on the grove floor before one of the butterflies flutters away, still holding onto its mate.  

Watch where you step, as keeping your neck craned up the whole time makes it easy to miss all the toddlers, dogs, butterflies, and downed branches underfoot.  If you're hoping to get a good shot of the butterflies, take a along a really good zoom lens, or else just wait until they flutter to the ground.  

The grove only shelters butterflies from November through March, so soak in the spectacle while you can.  After living in Santa Barbara for four years I can't believe I waited until five years after graduation to come back and see this place in all its glory.  A great place to spend a Saturday morning before a walk on Ellwood Beach.  Oh how I miss the wonders of Santa Barbara.

I'm going to thank Nick G for taking us to this GEM of a spot.  It seems like a locals only secret kind of place, open to the public of course and parking is nearby on the neighborhood streets.  

We came here on a recent day trip to Santa Barbara one weekend.  The Butterfly Preserve is a short and easy ten minute hike with barely any elevation gain/loss.  Your grandmother can make it here and when she does she'll be glad she came!  

When we got to the area with the butterflies it was amazing!  Tons of monarch butterflies flying all over you.  Tons of butterflies just hanging out in the trees, there would be so many of them on the tree branches that they'd look like leaves!

 I have never seen so many butterflies in one area in my LIFE!  The best time to visit this area is during the winter around December or January so go NOW!

Incredible, wonderful paths, outstanding views from cliff side, dog friendly (if you have a friendly dog) great photo op, look for the Monarch butterflies clustered in the Eucalyptus trees in February. Run Walk Bike Walk Dog Photography ( i didn't notice any restrooms)
What a great preserve. Along the way the eucalyptus trees give such a great aroma to the trails. Bluffs were the perfect place to pop the question

The Coronado Monarch Butterfly Preserve is lovely all times of the year but especially during the fall and winter months when the Monarch butterflies migrate to the preserve. I love to ride my bike here and it's a great place to take a date.

When visiting the preserve I highly recommend good tennis shoes. The trail is covered with shade and is relatively short so bringing water along isn't a necessity. There are a few hills, possibly difficult for the elderly and out of shape; but easy to moderately difficult for a healthy adult or child.

The preserve also allows beach access. The beach below is full of tar like most SB beaches but the other day (early July 2013) I found a 26 perfect sand dollars. I left them in the sand in a peace sign. Check them out if they're still there :) Hope you all enjoy the preserve as much as I do.

Absolutely beautiful. Lots of little trails, so MAKE SURE you know where you're going. Hubby and I thought we could walk all the way round the beach and ended up walking 3 miles back to the car lol. user error. 
I will say, I've been 3 times since December and not once have I seen the butterflies. Maybe they've moved and no one's realized it? It's a beautiful walk anyway. nice little light hike with plenty of great views and bluffs.

I found this place on Yelp and it is exactly as described! I'm not much of a nature person. I've never been camping. I don't like bugs, mud, too many trees, dark places. You get the point. Well, to my surprise, all that went out the window. This place is a hidden gem! It's beautiful!!

The Butterfly Preserve is  located smack in the middle of a residential street. When you first walk in, it's more a forest. Pay close attention when you see big clusters of brown leaves. Those aren't leaves, but hundreds of monarch butterflies. If you walk up further, it'll lead you to a wide open space with little trails. If you keep walking up, it'll lead you to the cliff that is right above the beach.  I had to 2hrs to kill and tried to stick around for the sunset. I was able to take a few pictures but didn't stay long enough. I'll be back next time. 



P.S.  As I was walking around the preserve I noticed that there were houses all the around.  Can you believe these people have as their backyard? I'm so jealous!! I saw alot of people jogging and walking their dogs (most off leash). All the dogs were well behaved and everyone seem very polite. Damn.... I wish I lived there. I would so jog every morning if I had a backyard with such  breathtaking view.

There are times you come here for the butterflies, but mostly you come here for the ocean, the beaches, the views. There's really never a bad time to come here. Always a beautiful and serene place for a jog, cycling or brisk hike along the tranquil coastline.

It's so beautiful here, I saw about 5 Monarch's on this trail.  The trail will lead you to the beach and you can keep going along side the ocean bluff.   There's a donation box at the start of the trail too.   There's many split off's to this trail.  Lots of locals running and Mountain biking through here.  You can't ride through the butterfly exhibit area, you will have to find the entrance to one of the split off trails maybe the next street over- there appears to be an opening.  The Exhibit itself is more of a trail and it's a small area that you will be able to just look up and in between the trees will you be able to see the Monarch's.  It's all green in here with lots of shade, it backs to the residential houses in the area.

Absolutely gorgeous place for a walk! I ended up driving down Coronado and parking In a residential area. There is a sign that shows the trail maps and up you go on a small path that leads you through some eucalyptus and in to the Ellwood Grove, where the butterflies will be (of course IF you go at the right time.) Unfortunately, it had been a rainy morning and/or maybe still too early in the season? 
Never mind, the paths were still well worth a gander and it was quiet, peaceful and felt quite remote, even though you're very close to nearby housing developments.

After walking amongst the tall trees still dripping with rain and the sun beginning to shine through, my boyfriend and I wandered up to the left, after we passed Ellwood Grove and headed up a small hill towards the coastal bluffs for some dramatic ocean views-it was perfect as the rain clouds were clearing and we were rewarded by clear views up and down the Goleta coastline. If I were a local, I'd definitely spend a lot of time hear meandering the various paths. Something about this place was downright magical. We had a lot on our agenda so only spent 30-45 minutes here. However, if you've got some time to spare, there are lots of paths to follow. Bonus that every person we walked by on the paths said "good morning" and seemed genuinely friendly-something I'm not used to in Northern California....
No butterflies but spotted an Owl. Planned a trip for January hoping to catch the migration.

I was staying at the Ritz Carlton when I saw the sign that said Butterfly Preserve and was so intrigued but didn't realize that the time of season I went, all butterflies were gone. Overall the preserve is beautiful. Signs point out directions to butterflies. Be mindful as it is a public area and on your own. It is not too strenuous of a walk/hike. There is a parking lot but the GPS took us to a dead end house and the preserve was behind the house so we parked on the street in a residential area.

RUNNIN' WITH BUTTERFLIES!

A pasture, as far as the eye can visualize, the eco-richly filled with lush green grass, and butterflies flutter here and there. That's what I envision when I think about butterflies. The Monarch is the only butterfly known to make a two-way migration like birds and November through February is the time of year the Monarch butterflies migrate to Goleta. 

Free admittance, but the City of Goleta accepts donations to support the Monarch Butterfly Docent Program are welcomed. Open from sunset to sunrise, the 9.3-acre preserve includes Devereux Creek, woodlands and meadows as well as trails, an outdoor gathering area and classroom.
 
The grove is shaded with blue gum eucalyptus trees, the same place the Monarchs have returned to every year since the early 1900's. The number of butterflies visitors will see vary from year to year, ranging 20,000 to 500,000 and flutter around in the warmth of the sun.

If you're so inclined, after walking through the preserve, follow the trail to the buffs and enjoy the view of the Ocean. Better yet, head down the bluff and take a walk along the beach. If you like walking barefoot on the beach, be warned, there is tar on this beach, which is not so easy to get off. The upside, if you get tar on your clothes, WD40 is a removing agent, which may ruin some fabrics.
Well marked trails. Though several trails were closed due to due to seasonal dry spell and risk of falling trees. Caught a glimpse of a few early rising butterflies late October

Easily one of the most peaceful areas of Goleta.

Coronado Butterfly Preserve is an excellent place to escape the mundane trials and tribulations of everyday life. I see the space as a total oasis and while it's adjacent to a residential neighborhood, most definitely feels completely secluded. 

You'll find acres of wildlife, paths, and natural vegetation including a large number of eucalyptus trees. The trees are not native but were planted over 150 years ago; that said, I couldn't imagine Goleta without their presence. The aroma, the leaves, and the myriad butterflies they attract are nothing short of breathtaking. 

The walking/biking trail is lovely and goes straight out to a very secluded and clean beachfront. It's a total hidden gem of Goleta. I admire how carefully preserved it is, and how clean the trails are in general. 

If you are truly looking to unwind and become one with nature, check out this mild and beautiful preserve!

I really love this spot! Plenty of open space to walk and beautiful beach views. Hike through tall Eucalyptus and end up at the beach or bluffs. Very easy walking terrain and if it's butterfly season be on the lookout for Monarchs. Parking can be tight, but if you can find a spot be sure to check it out!
The trail is an easy walk toward the ocean and back. It's very clean and not too many people. There are plenty of parkings on a weekday.

This was a great place, but you can't appreciate it without the timeline of events...

First we get there, and there is a beautiful forest of older trees, a burbling stream and some very nicely laid out paths... But no butterflies.

We get through the forest area, into an open grassland, and still no butterflies... 

We made it all the way to the beach, a most beautiful view of the islands.

On the way back from the cliffs, we made it back to the preserve, and saw two butterflies.... Wow, and then another one, and another one...

We started seeing more of the sun come out and then suddenly the handful of butterflies turned into 10's and hundreds, then we saw clusters...  

What is a cluster? So imagine looking up and seeing what you think is a branch with a bunch of leaves on it, and then realizing that the leaves are moving... When you zoom in to the 'leaves' you realize that every single one of them is a set of orange monarch butterfly wings.

Very cool place, very fun for kids to run around, but the butterfly experience was amazing, you just have to wait for it.

Tis the season: Don't miss the Monarch butterflies while they're around!  Coronado Butterfly Preserve, also known as Ellwood Preserve, is one of the select few places the beautiful creatures choose to stop and rest on their long 3,000 mile flight south to Mexico. Coastal plants and eucalyptus trees on the 9.3-acre preserve are a safe haven to them with moderate temperatures at the meeting point of the Pacific Ocean and winding Devereux Creek. They normally visit during the short, peak winter window between December and February and are most active during mid-day. The monarchs can easily be confused with fall leaves because they are often clustered together. Since they are 'cold-blooded,' they do not create enough heat from their own metabolism to keep themselves warm and build up enough energy to fly. When the sun hits them, they flutter their colorful wings to energize and warm up. Whether or not you're lucky enough to witness monarchs, the Elwood Preserve and Grove's cliff's and trails are absolutely breathtaking and worth checking out.

Notes: 
The park is open every day of the year from sunrise to sunset and is free of fees. To access a parking lot, use GPS coordinate of 34.429018, -119.897017.  You can also enter through a residential neighborhood by turning off Hollister Avenue onto to Coronado Drive until it dead-ends.
Due to the recent droughts, the city of Goleta has decided to close off many of the forested areas to avoid human injury from falling trees, however, some areas still remain accessible. Please use caution when visiting and mind all closure signs. Wear boots since there are some muddy spots. Recently there has been more and more trash left behind by visitors. Please pack out what you bring in.

Love this place for butterfly watching or for a nice run! I love that at some points you're walking under Eucalyptus trees and the other points of the walk you're on flat land looking out over the ocean with storkes roaming around! Its just magical. There are two beach access points along the trail. Its also a fun place to play hide and seek :)

Such a fantastic little hidden gem of hiking trails!! I came in search of the migrating Monarchs but the trails were absolutely bursting with all sorts of birds too! Definitely some very easy hiking - hardly any elevation gain and the trails are very well maintained and obvious. 

The trails are all dirt and a little gravel - I wouldn't suggest they're handicap accessible. The trails start in a residential area - across the street from houses - so be mindful for the residents. You get nice views of mountains all around in the distance. And it's not long before you find yourself in the middle of a eucalyptus forest - which is a favorite of the Monarchs! 

There are numerous trails that take you all in and out of the eucalyptus forest and this is where you'll find most of the concentration of butterflies. I only saw one moderate sized cluster and the clusters are hard to see - so it's nowhere near the scale and accessibility as the grove at Pismo Beach - but still awesome! There were tons of the Monarchs flying around and sunning themselves in small groups. 

But for me, the highlight was all the birds!! I even saw two on my life list that I'd been dying to see but hadn't yet (the Cassins Kingbird and the Common Yellowthroat)!! There were so many different species everywhere!! Especially once you get to the coastal oak trees by the beach cliffs. 

Which was another pleasant surprise- such lovely ocean views from the cliffs! There are several trails and lots of offshoots that take you all around the preserve and out by the coast. It seems like you can go down onto the beach possibly - but I saw signs saying it's a Snowy Plover habitat so I'm not sure it's allowed - just be aware and read the signs. 

There's lots of shade around the forests but not outside of them so I recommend sunscreen for sure. Parking is street parking and mine was the only car. I believe there's another parking area further down the Main Street as well. Parking and entry is free but there are donation boxes at the trailhead. The trails have some fun information signs along the way. I didn't see any bathrooms so plan ahead. Such an excellent trail system! Will definitely be back!

Awesome little hiking area but I came here when it wasn't butterfly season. It must be a beautiful thing to experience. I'll definitely be back to check em out. Hiking path is for beginners as well so give it a try don't be scared. There's also plenty of parking.

Loved the hike to the flat area, entry is deceptive but you pass eucalyptus trees and a stream(dried when we where there)  very short hike to ocean views. Parking is by the side of the road in a residential area.

Definitely one of the highlights of visiting SB, would recommend morning/late evening activity though since it can get hot during afternoon/early evenings.

Little nice nature preserve close to UCSB. Although it was supposed to be butterfly season, we didnt see one during our small day trip hike here. Its a very nice serene and obviously well kept place. The trails were very clearly demarcated and there werent too many obstructions. 

Its a very easy trail and that you can use to bring kids or the less hiking inclined for a brief bit of physical activity. The eucalptus trees were also wonderful as they provided a bit of shade and escape from the sun-exposure. As other have told you, this trail can get a little windy with all the small trails about so make sure you snap a photo of the trail map at the trail head before you go too far. 

A water bottle and some small snacks would also be great idea. I am not really sure when about the butterflies come out--but again, I've never seen one during the hike.

name: Santa Barbara Outfitters
categories: Fashion, Outdoor Gear, Sports Wear, Shopping, Sporting Goods, Active Life, Climbing, Hiking

yelp_reviews.text:
Pretty nice local outdoor store that isn't part of a chain (i.e. REI, Sports Authority, Sport Chalet, etc)! They appear to carry more products related to hiking and camping activities, but selection is still very good. They do sometimes have really nice sales that offer higher discounts than you would typically find at chain stores. However, without such sales, most of their prices are still at MSRP. They carry a lot of Columbia, Marmot, Life is Good, Brandy Melville, and Smartwool apparel. 

Staff can be a little hit or miss. Sometimes they will greet you, but from my visits, they are often taking care of things around the store. But they are usually friendly when you ask for their assistance.
I have to say, that outdoor jacket purchase came in very handy during the unexpected 4.5 hour hike yesterday.  Light, rainproof, warm, cute in pics.  Check, check, check and check.

I like this place.  Finding an outdoor jacket to keep you warm that doesn't make you look the Michelin man is no easy feat and yet I found the perfect one.  I have become much more outdoorsy than I ever thought I would be and that has prompted new additions to my wardrobe via visits to clothing shops I never thought I would enter. 

I am still not at the camping stage so water filters and titanium dinnerware are still not on the list, but then again, I never thought I would own a Camelback or a wet suit so you never know.

Cute cute cute outdoor clothes.
If you're on the hunt for TOMS, try here.  My friends saw my silver glitter TOMS and wanted pairs of their own.  We received very patient service here as multiple people tried on multiple pairs.

Friendly staff, cute clothes, hiking titanium dinnerware, and water filters!  I love filtering water at a cool river while camping!  It's my new favorite hobby.  There's even an indoor wall climbing wall.  I love this place!  I got a pair of sustainable flip flops from here (Green Toe from Simple).

The Santa Barbara Outfitters store is a very cool place to browse as well as get what you went there for. I recently went there for my first pair of Five Fingers shoes (if you can call them shoes) and the gal spent a lot of time with me while I tried them on (slowly:-) and made sure it's what I wanted. 

I'm happy to say I plan on going back next month to get my second pair! Everyone in the store was happy and friendly and well, it was just a very nice experience to walk out with happy feet!
This place sucks! Outrageous prices, snooty owners,no returns,  BAD business! Go to Mountain Air if you really need sporting attire or climbing gear.

This place is GREAT. Size-wise its no comparison to your typical ginormous REI and such, but they have an amazing selection of shoes, packs, clothes and other gear. For climbers they carry a broad range of the La Sportiva line and also an impressive selection of trail running shoes. I picked up a pair of Vibram Five Fingers there the last time I was in and will be back for more. Super friendly staff too.

I originally wasn't a huge fan of this place based on what I thought was poor customer service. I was in there last month and was really impressed with their selection and we got a super cute pair of Sanuks (they have the best selection for miles). 

There was a mix up on the price though and it took awhile to track someone down. Turns out they have a new server and there was a glitch, I wasn't being ignored. Once I actually got a hold of someone it was the owner who in minutes made sure that the situation was made right.

This store has a lot of really great stuff to choose from and an owner that wants to make sure that the customer is happy. That's the best combo if you ask me - we'll be back next time we're in the area.
Wow is this place a rip.. Going out of business anyways.. bye bye
Extremely friendly staff, good selection and nice sale selection too. I was very pleased with the service I received here and would definitely return.
Santa Barbara Outfitters has wonderful sales, great back country equipment and a Prana yoga clothing department.  If the staff isn't overwhelmed by too many customers, they are extremely helpful.

SB Outfitters has GREAT sales! I love to travel and am a bargain shopper and I have outfitted my self for trips to Europe and South America here. I am 24 and work retail so I am all about customer service and have little money so sometimes I am treated poorly because I don't look like I have money to spend, BUT this crew had always helped me out and I feel like they check in with you and let you have your space if you want to browse! PLUS they are always hosting local events! I will NOT be shopping at REI because SB Outfitters has everything i might need and if I can't find it there then I'd rather order online at Sierra Trading Post and get a really go deal.
Super store! Friendly staff, great selection and prices are not that bad. My hubby found two pares of shoes that he has been looking everywhere for, so we were stoked!
Love it here! Perfect place for some family fun and family pictures! www.alamodephoto.com

Love this simple carousel. It's so cute. The ride is cash only though, so don't forget! The ride itself is nice and long. I was getting a bit dizzy actually at the end! Hahaha. I like that there is history here too.
I always enjoy going here. It's so beautiful and just across the road from the beach.
Really fun and worth your 2.00. Well maintained, long ride, and short line doesn't get any better than that.

Incredibly rude attendant, ignored me as I walked in despite it being not busy at all. When I directly tried to talk to her she continued to look in the other direction and answered in flatly with one word answers. I just walked out. Wouldn't recommend.

The park has a little of everything: green open space, a shipwrecked themed playground, a carousel and a small garden/walking path. Conveniently located across the street from the beach and Stern Wharf.

Love Concert In The Park on Thursdays in the summertime. Great fun for friends and family to all enjoy right near the beach and Sterns Wharf. Very clean, can bring food to have a picnic on a blanket while watching bands entertain and people dancing the night away. Very fun little playground for children as well.

Beautiful antique carousel set in a gorgeous park with an amazing view.  This park has a really fun shipwreck-themed play structure that my kid's could spend all day playing on if we had the time.  Nice clean bathrooms available too.
Nice park to stop and rest with plenty of shade and a little playground for the kiddos to burn off energy.   The carousel adds for a little nostalgic fun.

We stopped here after going to the visitors center right across the street. It gave the kids a break from a long road trip. The employee that was there was not enthusiastic at all. Seem like he hated his job, maybe just a bad day. 
Check out my Road Trip to Santa http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=18oVdfa2nyU&feature=relmfuBarbara video. Some Clips include the carousel.
Nice old school feel. Worth the $2 a ride. Son had a blast. A bit away from the pier but worth the extra walk.
Great carousel and a long ride. Nice views!

Really old carousel from turn of the century. Beautifully restored. In a very nice park but quite a few homeless people sleeping there. 
$2.50 per person for a ride.
We had a wonderful time riding the carousel.  The attendant was very friendly and helpful! My two y.o. wanted to ride again and again.

This carousel was a lot of fun. We came on a Monday and were the only ones there for several rides. The love all the hand painted details. My daughter loved it too! 

Cost is $2.50 per ride. The rides were 4-5 minutes long. Parking was a bit of a challenge and I recommend parking across the street at the beach on Stevens wharf. 

Make sure you stop at the visitors center across the street for help on planning your time here.
What a beautiful antique carousel!   $2 for a 5 minute ride.

Anyone who either lives in Santa Barbara or is just visiting has definitely spotted this carousel, located in the small yet pretty Chase Palm Park, across from Sterns Wharf. 

Lucky for us, it's in operation. Well maintained. Little to no line. & quite the lengthy ride (5 minutes). 

Cash only, $2 well spent.

Perfect for kids or kids at heart.

I give this place 5-stars because it does the trick every time. If I'm in the area and my daughter is with me, it's a guaranteed stopping place for us.

My daughter leaves here happy and satisfied every time we come. We usually ride twice. You can't beat this type of entertainment, and it's a really cool spot. 

Are there homeless people in the adjacent park sometimes? Yeah, but they've never bothered us.

This Carousel is pure magic!  I heard "they" were going to take it down and remove it.  I think that would be a mistake!  Small children and those of us that love our child within would be sorely disappointed!  This Carousel is a part of what makes Santa Barbara unique.  If we continue to take apart what makes us special, then we will be just like everybody else.  Why would we want to do this?  There has to be a way to save this incredible, beautiful, magical piece.  
And come on!  Every time one rides it fills one with magic and wonder.  I don't know about you, but I need this and I need this gorgeous antique Carousel to stay.  If you haven't ridden it, you are missing out on a wonderful experience, and you may not have time to ride, so ride today!
Definitely worth stopping by. $2 for a 5 minute ride. I was able to share a horse with my 4 month old.

This review is not for the actual park rather an event that takes place at the park.  The "Art Walk" from what I understand happens every Sunday at the park.  You will find beautiful art work, crafts, jewelry from local artists/designers on display for you to purchase.  The grounds of the park were well maintained, East Beach was clean and inviting, and on the main walk way is the "Art Walk".   Each vendor has his/her space to setup and show off their talents.  There was so much to see and enjoy and everyone was more than happy to share information about their creations.

What a gem.  Located in Chase Palm Park - love this antique carousel.  Reminds me of my childhood.  Just a few bucks for a ride, which lasted at least 5 minutes.  Great deal for a little bit of nostalgia.

Beautiful park across the street from the beach. 

Highlights:
- Carousel: built in 1916 with paintings all around the top edges, it's a nice ride for the kids. $2 for a decent length of a ride. The attendant was really friendly giving my kid a high five.
- Playground: multiple boat themed play structures as well as some sea creature formations on the ground. Also a mission-like area in the back complete with a yellow arch where you can ding the bell 
- Ponds: nice bodies of water with a few koi fish and mini waterfalls 
- Grass: large area with a small hill to run around 

Lowlights:
- No parking: have to park at the general pay lot across the street

Loved this beautiful fun park and will be back
Cute place, only $2 and the ride lasts for like 5 minutes!! You definitely get the old school feel for the carousel. Not too far from the pier...it's a good 10 minute walk.

name: California Trike
categories: Sporting Goods, Shopping, Health & Medical, Medical Transportation, Beaches, Active Life, Bikes, Bike Rentals, Scooter Rentals

yelp_reviews.text: Took a two seater for a ride on our 33rd wedding anniversary. We had so much fun in the hour we had it, that we came back and bought one!  Yes, bought one!  We live on the Westside in SB and have used it almost every day since.  It's a slow sweet ride and it has allowed me to run errands and make great use of the bike lanes!

Super friendly staff. Great prices. Easiest way to get around Santa Barbara.  They gave us a map and suggested that we take a scenic tour on the coast bike path towards "Butterfly Beach". Except for one tricky intersection it was very enjoyable. Just be careful on the downhill streets as maneuvering can be tricky.
Most amazing nice people work at the California Trike Company , This place made our vacation amazing , let us see all around the beach. and there customer service was amazing. Will come here again !
This was my favorite experiences in SB so far. It's super fun and so much to see! Staff was amazing and gave us suggested routes to go. I had a great time.
Best way to get around when your in Santa Barbara!! Great staff ! Totally worth it ! We are definitely coming back while in town

So, zipping around on one of these was definitely the highlight of our trip to Santa Barbara!  It's the absolute best way to enjoy the town and nearby montecito. The tips they gave us about where to go and which beach were spot on!  It just makes it so easy to get places that you don't quite need an actual car to get to but don't have all day to walk. And with hills everywhere who would pedal when there's these?! This is something you must do if you visit.
Was really interested in renting a trike from them, but as I walked up the store workers were smoking a joint so I turned away and found other stuff to do

Walked past this funk zone hidden gem before they were actually open. I was with a family friend who recently had surgery and was using a cane to assist walking. These cool scooter bike things are awesome, she was able to tour Santa Barbara with us, without the stress or physical strain of walking! Totally recommend!

We had so much fun this weekend at the pier, great recommendations for places to go with our dog! My dog was so happy ridding on my lap. The owner was so professional and friendly! Great prices! Can't miss it when you visit Santa Bárbara.

Best costumer service! We went to a different rental places and we did not get half the service this place gave us! They even allowed us to extra time to go get ice cream! I cant stress what a wonder experience we had, you'll just have to go and find out yourself! :)

Spent the weekend in Santa Barbara last weekend. Was walking near the beach when a three wheel vehicle past me. It looked so fun. I called the number on the back of the vehicle and found the California Trike Company to be 2 blocks from where we were.  Let me tell you, this is the way to go!!! We drove all around Santa Barbara.  My daughter loooooved it. I highly suggest renting one of these.
I wouldn't tour SB any other way a MUST do to sight see.  Why peddle if you don't have to. Great rates super friendly staff. They even give you a map of things to see and water!!!!
Super fun!! Easy to drive, best way to see the sights in Santa Barbara. Definitely will do again,  can't wait to see the off-road model!
This was really fun and convenient because I have two little ones.  Good way to get around.  My kids loved it.  Will definitely go back again.we had a blast cruising all over SB.

This place rocks!

The guys inside are great, explain everything in detail, give you suggestions on where to go, a quick tutorial and test drive and off you go. Helmets are provided

It's a blast cruising around on the trikes, they go fast enough, you use the bike lanes and can just take off and explore. 

You really need to give this a try if you are in Santa Barbara and want to have some fun

We had a blast!

Customer service was excellent they went above and beyond to accommodate the customer. The lady at the register was awesome great customer service skills offered us drinks for example Waters and sodas at no charge all she wanted was to make sure we were hydrated. I will definitely be coming back to this place Upon returning to Santa Barbara

Amazing fun! Mike and his staff are so great. They routed us to butterfly beach, just so pretty.
Scooter was great, great value for the price. Great way to see the city. Will definitely be here again!

Rented a solo electric scooter and cruised around Santa barabara safely and efficiently I was out for 3 hrs and covered a lot of ground. Stoped shopped went to the court house..went o. The ward graves some chocolate ..and had a damn good time . The drivers share the road so that made it easier..

This place rocks!

The guys inside are great, explain everything in detail, give you suggestions on where to go, a quick tutorial and test drive and off you go. Helmets are provided

It's a blast cruising around on the trikes, they go fast enough, you use the bike lanes and can just take off and explore. 

You really need to give this a try if you are in Santa Barbara and want to have some fun

Ride in style..hahaha. Awesome service and super friendly employees! Definitely a very enjoyable experience taking these trikes out for first time explorers checking out Santa Barbara. Very easy to operate as well. We were given a map along with several suggestions on which route to take which was very helpful as we came here without much knowledge on what to do. Prices are very reasonable. I would recommend these trikes to anyone wanting to try something different! It's so fun whether with friends, family, or as a couple. The "snuggle" trike goes up to 25 mph and actually feels very quick.

Overall, a great way to take in the Santa Barbara coast, especially if you're too lazy to walk or pedal but want to see as much as possible!!! The Butterfly Beach Route is very pretty although the route to get there can get quite bumpy.

This scooter beats pedaling around Santa Barbara on a bike. We had so much fun driving around on the scooter we rented. It's easy to drive and perfect for parking bc it can fit just about anywhere. The guys at California Trike are super friendly and can tell you where the good spots are to visit. We enjoyed it so much! We will be back:)

I have to say This was the best investment in Santa Barbara! My boyfriend wanted to rent bikes, but then we saw the electric scooter!  

It was the most fun I had in a long time!  We drove down the street and was able to see the beautiful beach at 30mph!

The owner also told us about a Beautiful butterfly beach! The was a beautiful bike path where we got to see beautiful flowers and the beach!

Make sure to rent scooters from here while in Santa Barbara!

Great experience and prices! We almost rented a manual bike next door. Instead we were able to ride up to the mission and easily park with no problems. If we were on a regular bike, no way we would of madeit to the Mission. 

We rode in the bike lane, car lane and my husband got a little crazy and drove on the side walk when no one was around. Parking this was easy. 


$45 an hour for 3ppl. Or $120 for 3 hours.
Me gusta este lugar encontrar el agua caliente y disfrutar de el una experiencia inolvidable

Got information from trail walking group.  We then started off from Los Angels on a Tuesday afternoon.  It took more than two hours drive due to LA traffic.

Following the GPS, we arrived at 4 pm.  We followed the trail direction to walk uphill for about 10 mins to see a sign saying we have 0.7 miles more to hot spring.  We kept walking uphill.  While waking up, there were pipelines and we could feel they were hot but the water through those rocks was cold.  We did believe there was somewhere hot uphill.  As it's not daylight saving time, it got dark so quick.  When we were up after almost an hour, we saw a left and right direction.  While we couldn't decide which direction and it was getting dark, we gave up and walked back down for safety reason.

Coming back down was kind of slippery due to the steep and dusty road.  we had a strong flashlight to help.  It was good experience even we had not found the hot spring.  We will try again next time.

AMAZING!!!! This is absolutely the best hike ever and so worth it! It's around a 3.5 + hike and there are a lot of rocks. Make sure you are wearing comfortable shoes. It is easy to get lost if you are new because once you get closer to the hot springs, you will need to cross over with a ton of rocks. It's easy to just keep going up and following the trail. We followed some people and that was helpful. I recommend downloading the app; AllTrails app as it was helpful tracking the route. 

Hot springs: Amazing!!! It gets hotter as you climb up. Be careful because you can get easily hurt by stepping on the wrong rocks or slipping on a rock. Other than that, you can just relax

Parking: Horrible if you do not get there early enough to get the parking located in the front of the entrance! There had to be around 9-10 parking spots. You can park a few miles down in the neighborhood but be careful because there was a sign on the trail saying that parking tickets were being given out. I encourage you to arrive early! 
We drove around for 10 mins until we actually got a parking spot.

THIS IS A MUST IF YOU ARE IN THE AREA!

Hidden away in the Montecito mountains is this beautiful hot spring!! It is around a 3 mile hike round trip. To the hotsprings is mostly up hill but going down is all downhill! When you get to the hot spring, the highest one is the hottest and the lower you go the cooler it gets! Perfect place to bring a face mask and relax.

I love hiking in Santa Barbara, the weather is usually cool and overcast, there is plenty of natural beauty to enjoy. For this particular trail, I enjoyed this hike more than the destination therefore I'm unsure if it defeats the purpose since everyone goes for the hot springs. I've only been once so maybe it was just a bad day considering all the reviews seem rather positive! The trail was easy for me and I'm a beginner hiker. The entrance of the hiking is on private property so remember to be polite! Parking is terrible, thankfully I got a spot but parking enforcement came by and gave at least 20 cars tickets for parking on the side of the street so be cautious and read the signs. 

The hot springs were beautiful to see! But the people there gave off such an unpleasant energy. I went with my boyfriend and dog and as we approached the hot springs we received plenty of nasty glares in our direction. apparently the higher spring, the hotter it is. We saw two empty springs in the middle so attempted to hang out there. The way towards the middle you basically interrupt and walk by the other hot springs. This didn't appear to be an issue but as we tried to get in, the top three springs were full of naked middle aged women that seemed upset that we were there. Definitely don't want to make anyone uncomfortable and felt like we were breaking into something, we walked back down. As we tried another route to get to the springs, they continued to glare and rub dirt all over their naked bodies. Some of them even laid on top the rocks with their legs wide open, again they were completely naked so I'm not sure why they're acting like we're making them uncomfortable. As we walked back down, we decided to just leave since it seemed like we disturbed their nudist, mid life crisis ritual and a couple of them caught up to us and loudly discussed how rude we were. Ummmm.... I never intended to make anyone uncomfortable, we didn't even get in the springs because their angry fixed gaze. 

Overall it was a weird experience. I have nothing against nudists, I have been to several other hot springs that attract plenty of nudists but i don't know if I'd return here. But the hike itself is breathtaking and there's more to see than just angry naked women.

The springs are great if u go at a time when it's not crowded.  Seems to be crowded all the time!! The hike is nice. Very rocky and a takes about 45 min to get up there. An hour if u have kids.    Last time we were there was about 2 months ago. Unfortunately there was a very intoxicated man on drugs and alcohol that was destroying the rock formations.  He had this idea that the water should flow more. When he pulled the rocks, all these bugs and spiders got everywhere and my family and friend and I got dozens of bites all over us.  He continued to pull the rocks all the way up and was endangering people for some of the rocks he was pulling were big. He ruining the experience for all that were there that day. We were there in the early afternoon.  He may have been homeless.  It's very sad when people can't respect the hot springs.  People drink and smoke there as well so I don't recommend going at night.   

Overall it's a great spot with lots of different water temperatures. Mostly very hot which is great.  It's a journey to get there and it's really hit or miss, which I guess is expected from a place that is free.

Amazing place go early like 6am if you want QT or alone time. Some people (me) may be nude. Parking is easy off the street and hike was about an hour for me but it was safe and worth it to the hot springs.

This trail is kept authentic. Nothing to make it any easier, no port a potty, no steps etc, and that's the way I like it. The terrain was very difficult at times such as when you have to navigate among big rocks, and focus on not to slip on loose dirt etc. once at the top you will end up at the hot springs- actually you can smell the sulfur before you arrive. The water was HOT!!!! Omg- really hot. Remember to pack snack and cold water to enjoy while soaking in the "boiling" water. 
The hike was easy to descend! I saw families with young kids on the trail and few dogs.

Super duper easy hike on a beautiful day with some toasty hot springs. When you get there, the crowd varies. Some times people like to get lit there and some are just there for the vibezz. I would recommend trail shoes instead of big OL hiking boots, but that's your instinctual decision. Pro tip: the hot spring towards the top of the hill are hotter than the springs on the bottom. Loved the experience.

I am writing this for the people reading the reviews here and wondering if they're actually fit enough to handle this hike. I'm in my mid-forties, and based on the reviews, this sounded maybe a little strenuous, but doable. I cannot stress this enough: this hike is ALLLLLLLL uphill to get to the springs. I brought three younger people with me--two eighteen year-olds and an twenty year-old--and they struggled almost as much as me. So if you're a gym rat (and we saw many people wearing gym clothes on the way up!) this is probably absolutely no big deal. However...if you're middle-aged with a few extra pounds...this is gonna take some time and going to be pretty damn strenuous. It's probably NOT going to be a two or even three hour round trip hike. And unfortunately, there are no benches to rest whatsoever. By the end of two hours we were dying and turned around...which sucks, because I think we were 80% there. We just couldn't take any more effing climbing.

And coming back in the dark?? Some of you are a lot braver than us. There were a few places with some sheer drops off to the side, poison oak and tonnnnnnnnns of rocks to navigate (omg so many rocks). So I while I guess it's possible with enough time and a flashlight, I can't see it being a pleasant experience. Especially since signage is lacking in a few key spots. Again, this "hiking down in the dark, nbd" vibe is coming from fit locals that do it regularly.

Tips: don't bother bringing much with you except good trail shoes and a fully-charged phone with offline trail maps. Anything extra will have you hating life, and there's a reason you don't see people carrying much on the trail. Maybe some bug spray.

Parking is pretty bad, and getting creative is risky. We also came on a weekday--I suspect parking is nigh impossible on weekends.

But it was definitely a beautiful hike! Will try again when we're a little better prepared.

This place was insane!!! 

I had no idea there was a hot spring so close to me, but it was such a unique hike and experience that made for a fun weekend trip! In theory, the hike itself is relatively short (~3 miles round trip) but it is STEEP! Definitely recommend wearing proper shoes with good grip for this hike. Halfway through, I was pretty burnt out and was wondering if the hot springs would even be worth it. 

IT. WAS. 

While there were a decent number of people around, the hot springs had individual pools for people to be separated in. I figured the hot springs would've been a nice temperature to cool off in after the hike, but it was almost like a jacuzzi in terms of temperature!

Quick word of warning - for those with silver jewelry, the hot springs have sulfur in the water and WILL react with your jewelry and turn them into a rusty orange shade. I found out the hard way, but you just have to brush them with toothpaste and they were back to being good as new!

Amazing spot!! Just be mindful they will tow if u stay too late, no overnight parking.. just re-park and Uber in .. late night soak is epic!!
(I think it's called lower hot spring trail on all trails and aka Montecito .. ps make sure u know when to cross the creek!!! )

*DISCLAIMER* Sterling silver will get severely tarnished (can be fixed - google it), and some stones/gems can even be permanently damaged by the sulfur & hot water, so probably best remove any jewelry prior to soaking, just to be safe.  

*DISCLAIMER 2* If you go at night (which I'd highly recommend!), be sure you have been given solid directions, and be sure everyone has a flashlight. If using your phone's light, make sure you start with  a full charge, cuz you will NEED to see where you are going! Though this hike (2-2.5 hrs. round trip) is pretty easy and safe, there are still several spots that could potentially be very dangerous, if you can't see clearly. 

Aaanyway (finally lol), this place is simply AMAZING! Absolutely the highlight of our trip. It could not have been more perfect. If I could, I'd rate this place as many stars as we could see in the gorgeous night sky (basically infinity), as we reclined in soothing wonder at mother nature's jacuzzi (highest/hottest pool was at least 100-103 degrees F, fyi). If you are thinking about going... STOP, and just GO!!!!!!! ;)

Best hike we have ever done! Pretty easy with some tricky part, good for kids also. The hot springs is a nice goal to hike towards! Lots of different scenery, good amount of shade. About 2h30 round trip. Must try!!

One of Santa Barbara's true hidden wonders!

I navigated this trail yesterday and it was easier than I had anticipated. We took the "long route" up, from the San Ysidro trailhead. The distance to the top was 2.9 miles and around 1300 feet of elevation gain per my Strava app. After hearing from other hikers, we decided to take the "short route" back down which was closer to a mile - but then walked for over a mile up the main road back to our vehicles. 

The trail was very well maintained and was just gorgeous the entire time. There were areas toward the base which were littered with horse/dog feces so be cautious where you step. I also saw patches of poison oak but it was pretty well cleared off of the trail itself. 

The San Ysidro trailhead ventures off into a handful of different hiking trails. I know that the most advanced of them was the route to East Camino Cielo which takes you up to an elevation of over 3000 feet and adds an extra 3.4 miles. 

The hot springs were breathtaking and very clean. There were about 10 different pools in a row, and each were on different levels which made for a more private experience. The higher the pool, the warmer the water temperature. The pool we soaked in was the third from the bottommost one and felt like a bathtub. 

While the pools are all ages (there were children and adults hanging out), we did see a handful of nudists so keep that in mind. I'd also recommend starting your hike before 8am as the pools will become rather crowded later in the day. Definitely will hike up here again.

You know when you're doing some kind of meditation class, and you're told to picture yourself in your happy place, and you picture yourself on a perfect, quiet sandy beach -- only to realize you've never actually *been* to that beach? 

Well, this is that happy place beach: the imagined, beautiful, sandy beach, with water and sand stretching out beneath dramatic cliffs. Best of all, it's not crowded: there's plenty of spots to set up camp without anybody crowding you with their own umbrellas and boom boxes.

The only disappointment was that we didn't actually discover any tide pools, which was our reason for heading here in the first place. We came at the higher of the low tides, so perhaps that was the reason for our disappointment. Or maybe we just didn't walk far enough to find them.

I should note that *my* happy place beach includes lots of dogs prancing happily through the waves. But if your happy place beach is dog-free, you won't love Arroyo Burro the way we did.

Only beach in Santa Barbara that faces the sunset. The first time I saw the sunset here it literally gave me butterflies, so you pretty much can say I'm in love !!!! It's so gorgeous, you will fall in love too !!!
I loved this dog beach, how easy it was to get to from the parking lot, and the friendliness of people and dogs. My dogs had a blast plus it was also our first time to Santa Barbara so it was great.

This beach is great, I love taking my dogs here to run along the beach when the tide is low. There is some tar but not too excessive. I have never had a bad time when it comes to dog interactions, everyone is out to have a good time. Definitely recommend it to anyone wanting to take their dog out to the beach, if the tide is low enough you can walk pretty far in the off leash section.

Was crowded with people and their designer dogs when we were there.  Interesting cliffs, good picture taking at the end of the day.  mussels and anemones on the rocks that the kids enjoyed. Easy to get to and lots of parking.  We didn't try the restaurant.

I came here in September and finally I'm getting a chance to write a review and maybe in a couple more months I'll find some time to upload the pictures to go with it. :p 

In any event, this wasn't anything special especially since we made a drive from LA to SB.  Not worth it but i'd also like to note that we might have enjoyed it a bit more if it was low tide and we could see a bit more.  Otherwise I would have hit another dog friendly beach closer to come.  

We started out with the hike through the Douglas Family Preserve and made our way past the sludge then on to the beach.  Overall it was a nice walk but I'd never do it again because at the end of it all is a long set of stairs probably about 150 steps up to take us back to the residential streets where we parked.  I didn't like the 10 flights of stairs but I didn't mind, the problem was I had my 3lb dog in my bag and my 63lb bulldog gasping for air as he struggle at each and every stair.  He took breaks but he was also a fighter, at the end of 10 minutes we made it to the top.  He couldn't stop panting for the next hour.  He has never been worn out to that extent.

Something worth seeing is the trees that have disengaged from the overhead cliff.  The trees are draping into the sands and waters of the beach, its overwhelming.  My photos are no match for the true immensity.  If you go now it might not be there anymore due to the tides that may have dragged them away.

This was a nice stop on our way up the coast.  No charge for parking and a good beach location that if you have dogs is perfect. We took our little Boston Terrier who enjoyed the company of the other dogs there.

I would recommend this place for anyone coming or going from Santa Barbara, the location was easy to get to and it had a lot of nice features. We saw a pod of dolphins playing out from the shore which was also very cool.

Nice beach to spend a sunny day with fam. Eventhough we come during weekday, the beach is still full of people. It's a little bit tricky to find a parking, specially if you use van. 

There's a public restroom available, and a good restaurant; perfect for breakfast or brunch!

I had the chance to visit Arroyo Burro Beach on my last morning of a long weekend in Santa Barbara. During a weekday morning, parking was ample but I actually had walked down from a short nature trail that begins at the Douglas Family Preserve. Gorgeous beach with soft sand and dramatic coastal bluffs, it's not particularly wide but was long. Great spot for walks or runs on the beach, as well as some rewarding beach combing. I found a number of shells and sea glass pieces-more than any other beach area I had visited that weekend.
Friendly locals seem to use this beach for morning walks and everyone said hello as I passed by. This seems to be a less touristy area and was the perfect way to end my vacation in the area.
Other noteworthy points:
-Dog Friendly
-Free parking (but smallish lot)
-Tide pools in some areas (I got to see them at low tide...yay!)
-Seemingly tar free (at least at the time I visited)

Overall I wish I had discovered this beach sooner but glad I made it here last minute. Wonderful stop to walk, beach comb or simply sit and enjoy the view. Very relaxing and scenic area.

It's "real" name is Hendry's Beach, but you won't see any signs proclaiming this. At the same time if you ask a local where "Arroyo Burro Beach" is you might get a funny look. Nobody, I mean NOBODY, calls it that. 

It's a good beach for walking and a great beach for dogs it has little else to offer. For dogs you want to turn left, after you cross the slew it's a "No Leash" area. For walking I recommend turning right and watching the sunset. It's a chilly walk into the wind but when you turn around the wind and sun are at your back and it becomes much warmer. Better chilly on the way there than the way back I say. There are also some outdoor showers if you want to rinse off your feet. They are behind the boarded up restaurant. 

It's generally windy and the sand is rocky and hard making it a crumby place to lay out. The water is generally clean but can be some of the worst due to a sewage overflow in the slew that empties into the beach. Which reminds me .... 

Attention Parents: DON'T LET YOUR KIDS PLAY IN THE SLEW!! 

I don't care what beach you are at, it's disgusting and not good for their health.

There used to be a really bad restaurant here called the Brown Pelican but it's boarded up now. It has amazing views and a liquor license so it has potential. Knowing Santa Barbara it'll probably just become another tourist trap though :(

Oh, it also has a small park with some picnic benches and BBQ pits.

Disgusting, overcrowded, sea weed everywhere & SO MANY FLIES!!!!! I see why dog lovers like it.... it's a beach for dogs and their flies. Not for families to enjoy. The ONLY part we liked was the Boathouse restaurant.
Gorgeous, picturesque, and not crowded on a weekday. Quite windy today. Stunning view. Great for families (weekday afternoon). A restaurant shares the parking lot.

Stopped here on a trip to get my dog some exercise.

Large stretch of beach that gives your dog a ton of area to roam. Our little beagle Sidekick had a great time chasing crabs and adventuring in the rocks. The beach is divided into leash and off leash areas. We only ventured to the off leash area because what is the fun of a leash at the beach. 

The reason for 4 stars and not 5 stars is for the lack of dogs for Sidekick to play with. This is good because it means less people but not as fun for the dog. 

Parking was easy and painless.

What a hidden surprise it was to randomly see this beach ! We were lucky to find parking as it was really busy. When we walked towards the beach we see a beautiful creek for the kids to explore. Although the parking was an issue,the beach was very spacious and not crowded at all. Amazing spot ! 

Highly recommend to check it out !

Beautiful Beach! You don't have to pay for parking! Off the leash area + a washing station for your dog! My corgi had a blast! (Do not use the YELP directions to this place, it took us through a residential area, use the GOOGLE MAPS app)

Absolutely lovely. The beach is so beautiful and the people that come here are so nice too! My dog is usually very quiet and shy but whenever we come here she's running like crazy! She loves this beach and so do we. Also the dog showers are so nice! A dollar a minute for ten minutes is a little steep for a couple of college kids but the choices of bathing shampoo (flea and tick shampoo, regular, etc) is such an awesome feature! The only other down side to the beach is the sea weed and the flies that accompany it.

On the way back from a doggy road trip, we discovered Arroyo Burro (Hendry's Beach to the locals) when we did a Yelp search on dog parks. We found parking to be limited and had to "stalk" someone to get a spot (flashback to my college years), but once parked - what a find!  
We're talking a full service dog beach including mutt mitts and a coin operated doggie spa in the parking lot.
Upon entering the sand you hit the off-leash area to the left.  Although open for all sizes, we saw that it was mostly larger dogs, a bit scary for our 1 year old pups on their first beach visit.  We headed right, to the on-leash portion of the beach and peaceful serenity.  Go far enough down and you find yourself alone with no one to notice you went off-leash (shh).  This was the boys' first sand and surf adventure and they loved it, although they ran from the water like those little birds you see.
After romping around we thought we'd check out The Boathouse restaurant that's right on the beach edge.  They offered great patio dining, but ironically, did not allow dogs.  There was a "Doggie Tie Up" area just off the patio and in view of any tables along the patio edge with water bowls and (limited) shade depending on the location of the sun.  We passed.  The point was to be with the pups.  They should seriously reconsider their pooch policy.
Overall a really good location to play, we just wish it were a little closer so the boys could go more often.  We also decided we have to time these outings with their grooming schedule to get all the sand out.

I love this beach! Living in LA and not being able to bring my dog to the beach really stinks, but dogs are allowed on the beach in Santa Barbara! This was a great find. We went mid-week and parked in the lot for free (I imagine it fills up quickly in the summer and on weekends). There is even an off-leash section of the beach a little further from the parking lot. Wish we lived closer to this place!

I love this beach because it is one of the only dog beaches in Santa Barbara. I wish they would allow dogs off leash at other beaches. Sometimes the dogs are not very friendly and should not be off leash but most the time dogs are eager to play and hang out with my dog. The parking lot is always full and spilling into the neighborhoods because it also shares a parking lot with the restaurant the boathouse. They provide dog bags and an area for dogs to be leashed/hang out if you choose to dine at boathouse after a day at the beach.

Check out the tides before you come.

Best dog beach in Southern California. When you have responsible dog owners and well behaved dogs, it's impossible to give it 5 stars. 

I've been to Coronado dog beach  and I was very disappointed.  Being next to a naval base, we would hear helicopters to fighter planes fly over this beach every half hour. Not this beach, you can only hear happy dogs barking or birds chirping.  My dogs can't wait to go there again.

What an awesome place! Huge stretch of off leash dog beach where our pup could run free and splash around, without having to hike through rocks to access. We stopped here when we were camping at El Capitan and we loved it. Not too many other people, and everyone we encountered was friendly (and so were their dogs). Wish we lived closer - we'd definitely be regulars. We will stop here anytime we bring our dog up towards Santa Barbara.

This is Disneyland for me (of course I'm a dog).  Running off leash (left side), sand between my toes, surf, and lots of dog friends to play with, it doesn't get better than this.  Wish I could go every day.

Great place for dogs.  Owners watch and control their dogs extremely well.  Big open area and clean.

What could be better than a FREE DAY AT THE BEACH.! There is a FREE Parking Lot here. This is a great dog friendly beach. There is a little restaurant right on the beach here. This is a Great place for dog walking or people walking too. It's Very picturesque. I love the large rocks & those magnificent cliffs at the end of the beach.

It was a nice location and our dog enjoyed the time at this, however, there were not many dogs when we visited here.  I visited Roise's dog beach in long beach, I prefer Roise one because more dogs and my dog had much fun.

What can be more fun than watching dogs frolic in the waves! This beach is very well-organized: free dogs on the left, leashed dogs on the right; swimming on the left, surfing on the right. The day we visited, all the dogs were well-behaved, and I saw only one turd on the sand. I'd give this beach 5 stars if parking were easier. The lot is pretty crowded and at 5 p.m. on a Sunday, we circled round for about 15 minutes until someone left and we got a spot.

This place is dog heaven.  One side of the beach is for off-leash dogs and another side is for on-leash dogs so you can pick which direction to go based on your dog's temperament.  The beach is pristine and beautiful.  Our dog had the best time meeting all of the other dogs and exploring the beach.  The water is great and the waves are fairly calm.  It can be tough to find a parking spot but be patient and you will find one.  Parking is free.
Wonderful off-leash area for dogs.  Our little dog loves it there!  We can't wait to go back!

Arroyo Burro aka Hendry's Beach is where I spent most of my teen years. Oh the memories I have of wearing down my Joshua Tree cassette here. Even when crowded it never seems that way.

Now that I've introduced my dog to the beach I'm looking forward to taking him here. I know he will enjoy it as much as I have and its a chance for new memories to form.

The Boathouse restaurant had a takeout window last time I was there. They served hot dogs, frozen candy bars and other overpriced goodies. I do find it ironic they use the unofficial name Hendry's for the restaurant. Perhaps because not all locals use the official name?

The dog washing stations have been redesigned and opened. They are quite expensive. I will continue to bring and use my Blue Dawn here for my little pooch.

Free parking and plenty of well behaved dogs and responsible owners, what more could you ask for?  Best doggy beach in California!!!  Dog wash stations that takes credit cards and bills but no coins near the parking lot is awesome!!!  Everyone was friendly and it's a long stretch of beach for the dogs.  I love this place!!!

Wow!!! What a fantastic beach that I had the pleasure of visiting on this Memorial Holiday weekend. It was a cloudy day but there were plenty of people coming and going. I like this beach because of its clear & easy pathway to the beach. I even got to see some dolphin. This beach is located in Santa Barbara, CA off of Cliffs Drive. You drive into the parking lot and you walk onto the beach. Also, there is a wonderful restaurant where you can eat and have drinks. I've attached some pics. Enjoy!!!

This was a perfect get away day trip for my fiance me and our dog! A client of mine suggested this beach; and i must say it's absolutely beautiful! The people were friendly and nice to walk up and down the beach to let my Fur Child ( yes dog ) Run freely and play with others and run in the water; i took a lot of great pictures of our day adventure. Something we will do again very soon!

I love this beach! Also known as Hendry's or The Pit. It's my favorite spot to take my dogs or to just hang out by the water. It's a very popular spot so I wouldn't recommend if you're looking for solitude. There is access to bathrooms close by, dog washing station, Boathouse Restaurant and snack shack, super kid friendly and depending on the tides, can be great for finding shells. 

If you're planning on taking your dog here, PLEASE make sure that your dog is friendly enough to be around other dogs and children while off their leash. My dog has been attacked a few times. 

Have fun!

This was a nice stop on our way up the coast.  No charge for parking and a good beach location that if you have dogs is perfect. We took our little Boston Terrier who enjoyed the company of the other dogs there.

I would recommend this place for anyone coming or going from Santa Barbara, the location was easy to get to and it had a lot of nice features. We saw a pod of dolphins playing out from the shore which was also very cool.
Great beach for dogs and humans alike. Lots of shoreline to walk and forrest preserve next door. Also has a dog wash station

Absolutely beautiful beach and a great place to bring your dog. We drove up from Santa Monica to spend the day in the area and finished off with a couple hours at the beach. Our dog LOVED it. There were a lot of people there with their dogs, but it didn't ever feel over crowded. We ran around, played fetch, and our pup even got some time in playing with the other dogs that were around. The beach really is gorgeous and was well worth the trip. Parking was limited, but there's a residential neighborhood nearby where we were able to park and walk out to the beach. Highly recommend! Don't forget your camera ;)

Such a beaautifuuul beach! My BF and I came here over MLK weekend in January 2015 on a spur of the moment vacation to Santa Barbara.  The weather was great - in the mid 70's and this beach (aside from the horrible parking) was perfect.  Long, flat and walkable. If you walk about 1/2 mile away from the parking lot, you will encounter some large flat boulders that made a great place to lie down and take some risque pics! Up near the parking lot, there is a restaurant with outdoor seating; we didn't go in but there are some great people watching benches just outside.  Overall we'd go here again.

Love this beach! We have come here multiple times and have never encountered any problems. All the dogs seem to be very socialized. Have never experienced any poop issues. Sure there is tar but it's SB so get used to it for the foreseeable future! Bring snacks and drinks. Don't forget water for your dog! The dog wash station costs $10 by the way.

Arroyo Burro Beach is VERY dog friendly. Make sure you check which side of the beach you are on before you take off the leash from your dog.

Parking is free at their lots (there are two) but the place is popular and fills up early in the morning.

Doggie showers available. 

Very nice restaurant present for your beach-side brunching needs. I know: very first world problem-- "brunching need". 

Outdoor shower to wash off sand at the end of the day.

Shallow tide pools present.

Long and narrow allows for a very nice run. And while you're running, you can look up at the Cliffside homes and marvel at some of their landscaping.

My friends an I joked about reviewing a beach or even checking to see if a beach would even have reviews. well it did :D an it happen to be a 4.5 star. :D well I can agree! I will round up an give it a 5. the 4.5 I would deem the huge body of water/sess pool with green algae, an the poor parking. The reason for the .5 under the full 5. 

Features : 
DOGS! DOGS! DOGS! dogs to the left. (but if you dont mind dogs the whole beach is yours!) 
Full Service SitDown Restaurant , with clever side beach food window for the beach goers with no shirt no shoes. 
Very Great atmosphere! everybody was really cool we played a 3 on 3 2 hand touch football an a time or 2 went wide with the throw on accident. an everysingle person smiled threw the ball back or we went an grabbed it real quick. without any complaints all smiles an waves ! we dodged dogs, an even some dogs played with us! it was great!
AWESOME VIBES! truely! 

NEgatives :/ 
Parking, Joke. narrow tight parking. line extending to the street. 
Huge water sesspool with algee. on the dog side(Left) so they drink out of it( NOT GOOD) standing water!. what is it from? its gross.

more positives than negatives thats forsure ! 
Yes i reviewed a beach! it must have been something if I reviewed it wouldn't ya say?

easy to get to. lots of free parking. not crowded.  dogs can go off leash. no waves. the water seems slightly warmer than in la. the restaurant nearby makes a descent tuna melt and fries. it also has a bar. my dog loved this place so much more than the malibu (leo carillo) dog beach. there's even a self-dogwash area, where you can wash your dog with tea tree shampoo.

I love this place. It has a desolate feel where it's like a secret beach. Free parking!! Is booze allowed on all Santa Barbara beaches? cause that is unheard of in LA or The OC. The bar at the restaraunt makes some great blood orange margaritas. 

The waves were much calmer than at Huntington. There was quite of bit of seaweed & luckily I didn't experience any of the tar.

I can't wait to go back. It was calming & relaxing. I felt like I was so much further away from home than I actually was. I like the 2 hour drive. It was my escape.

Dog washing station..definitely dog friendly. Beach is dirty and swamp smelling. Dog area is 100 meter to the side. It's a solid 3 stars for just barely passing. Was hoping for it to be cleaner and bigger. Parking ...is free but have to keep circulating the 2 lots.

Great beach for dogs! Clean and everyone is friendly. Bathrooms are ok as beach bathrooms get. I wanted to wash my dog off after but I guess they are re modling the dog wash station, just note that it's not there right now.
The sunset views are OUT OF THIS WORLD.  I couldn't believe I was on earth... I felt like I was in heaven.  You MUST go.

Came here late Thursday morning with our 2 labs and they had a blast!  There was plenty of parking! Really enjoyed walking up the beach with them.  All the dogs and owners seemed friendly and my pups enjoyed running around and playing with them!  We went down the leash free area.  We didn't have any issues with tar either! I would definitely come back!
Dog and family friendly beach. Love this beautiful beach. Just FYI, parking gets full quickly especially on weekends and after work during the hot summer days.

I've been coming to this beach forever now and my dog and I are one of the regulars and we just love it! Honestly, I think my dog runs the pack because they really just follow her around all day, but thats besides the point

Such beautiful sand and such a beautiful play area! Love it here!

Really beautiful beach, next to the cliffs, there are a lot of dogs here. Still it is a very clean beach. It can get quite busy, it can get hard to find a parking spot. Also has a restaurant right on the beach, will have to check that out soon.

This is a very neat beach for dog owners. Some beaches are better than others, and this beach really is pretty--it has large cliffs on one side, lots of interesting rock formations dotting the shoreline and pools of water for dogs who like to splash around. 

Parking is free so on the weekend expect a serious crowd. The Boathouse at Hendry's Beach is right there, and as soon as you enter everything to the left is an off-leash dog park. To my knowledge they don't have a bag dispenser, so be sure to bring your own bags for your dog's business. They do have special trash cans for dog waste.

The beach goes on for miles so it's a great workout for your dog. On the weekends there are a ton of dogs there so be ready for a little companionship.

Note: Check the tides before you visit because apparently at some times of the day there is very little beach.

Great beach for a small party, to take a stroll, or to bring your pup. There is an off-leash section for your dog to socialize and a small restaurant that fills the air with delicious food smells. The air here is fresh and crisp compared to many other beaches in Southern California.

I just love this beach. Though I don't have one of my own at this time. I love dogs, and often go day tripping with friends with dogs. LOVE LOVE LOVE this place. Even when I've been there without a dog, it just makes me happy to see so many dogs playing in the water and running around. But I'm odd that way.

-Quiche Out

Love this beach! It is typical beautiful Santa Barbara and a great place to let our four legged friends run free and play. Mine love to get out and play with the other dogs at the beach. Its like a supervised doggy day care and such a treat to watch!
Notes: 
This is a great environment for well tempered dogs. If your dog has any aggression issues this may not be the best. location for an outing.
Doggy bags are provided at  the beach. So let's keep this beautiful treasure clean and not forget to pick up after our pets!

This pretty little beach off the beaten track in Santa Barbara is not all it cracks up to be.  There is a Resturaunt at the end of the free parking lot and a strip of grass / park area with a few picnic tables and BBQs on one side while the beach is on the other.   There is no view of the beach from the picnic area.  The beach area is ok.  Very busy and dogs are allowed on one side of the beach.  Many locals are constantly coming and going with their dogs.  The parking lot is busy.   The grassy area is right under a road way, there are dumpsters from the Resturaunt nearby, as well as bathrooms and showers too.   It is awkward to set-up a picnic on the grass as you cannot enjoy the beach from there at all.    Better to just eat at the cafe and enjoy the beach.  Better beaches are out there for picnics and BBQing.  This one is great for dog lovers though!
Dog friendly with a restaurant & restrooms right in the beach...perfect for a roadtrip resting point

What an excellent beach! Dogs are allowed, which is rare in SoCal.  There's plenty of room to run, it's very clean (i saw no dog poop at all), and there's a restaurant right there if you're hungry (but no dogs allowed.)
Beautiful beach. Somewhere you want to take your dog. But the water is freezing! Could swim in it, but it was a great view so 5 stars anyways :)

Honestly, has anyone ever been to a clean dog beach that doesnt smell like piss? I think not! There's some seaweed on the sand but come on.. this beach is so clean and my doggies love it! 10/10 recommend

I have been to a few dog parks but this "dog beach" tops them all. There was a large variety of dogs out there, none of them aggressive and with friendly owners. Everybody seemed to pick up after their dogs as well. The beach looked just as good as any other to swim in if you want to go in with your dog. It is situated against a cliff and the cliff eventually meets the ocean so your dog can only escape if they go back towards the parking lot.
The thing to do looked like to bring a six pack to the beach, pretty much everybody there brought a few beers with them in their bag and was drinking responsibly. I wish I would have known and brought some myself.
Even though it is a heck of a drive for me I will definitely be coming back..

One of many beautiful spots for visiting the shorelines of Santa Barbara County. It was a bit confusing at first to get there-- the parking lot conjuncts with a restaurant I forget the name of, but as you approach the coast signs for the "Off-Leash" section become visible. Lots of people in one area. It's very refreshing to be outside in the sand with all the dogs running around & watching the surf, just be mindful of all the bustle on the beach. It was worth the 2 hour drive, and I would definitely return for the trail nearby.
Love the dog beach... a lot of people and owners who watch their dogs.. only thing is the restaurant on the dog beach is not dog friendly

Great beach for taking a long walk. Check the tides first!
There is a section to walk your dog on leash should you need to. 
Parking can be difficult as early as 10am +

Hands down, the most beautiful and picturesque dog-friendly beach that we have ever been to! Just be careful if you have an extra curious dog because there are the occasional dead sea animal carcasses on the shore.

Beautiful. The sand is so soft and pleasant between the tows. There were surfers and even a sea otter playing on the waves yesterday. Dogs and families are welcome. Just a pretty beach! 

Bring a blanket, umbrella, snacks and drinks, and just enjoy the day!
Really nice place to take your dogs. there is a section for leashed dogs and for off leash as well. lots of different types of dogs great experience.

We had the most amazing day here. This beach is absolutely gorgeous and a very special place for dogs, kids, and everyone.  

We had a stunning walk so be sure to stroll further down the beach. There are some really stunning views, rock formations and cliffs.

Parking wasn't too bad in the lot, and I don't think I've ever seen our dog happier. She was joyful in the off leash area and started squeaking with excitement as soon as she realized we were at the beach.  There are also restrooms right by the parking lot which is helpful if you have kids.

Stumbled upon this beach while we stopped in Santa Barbara for lunch. We did a spontaneous road trip with my two dogs, so after lunch I yelped for a near by dog beach. Arroyo Burro came up, after reading the reviews and seeing the pictures we just had to go! Definitely glad we did! Once we pulled into the parking lot, we can see a lot of locals take advantage of this awesome dog beach! My dogs were more than ecstatic to meet some new friends, play frisbee, take a dip in the water.  Highly recommend any dog owners to check out this off-leash dog beach! It's worth the trip to see my babies play happily in the sand!
