We take our dog to this beach if we want the freedom of an advertised off leash dog beach (compared to Rosie's in Long Beach, this is much better in our opinion). No matter if it's the weekend, this beach is much less chaotic than Rosie's albeit much more quaint. We love how the off leash doggie side extends down the cliff into more of a private walkway. The views are gorgeous. Then there is a beautiful Oceanside walking path from Santa Barbara all the way down to Montecito that is perfect to start or end the day with.

Jean Baudrillard must have been standing on this beach at sunset when he referred to Santa Barbara as a paradise. 

Arroyo Burro, or Hendry's as us locals like to call it, is one brilliant stretch of coastline--moss covered cliffs, soft wet sand, a gentle sea. In the late afternoon, the landscape takes on this radiant, golden glow which is, in itself, the stuff of poetry.

As a proud dog owner, I am very very very pleased with this beach. It is pretty clean, offers doggie bags, and also has a nice off-leash area. The dogs and owners that I have seen on the beach are also very fun and friendly! My german shepard loves it here and is always exhausted after a long day of running around with the other dogs. Its always a good time at this beach.

Stunning location. Off leash dog beach with lots of dogs of all sizes. Weekend is pretty busy. The dog cleaning area is an awesome plus. 

My only real complaint and it's definitely almost enough to knock it down two stars is the sand flies. 

They are everywhere and annoying. It's a shame because we were considering changing to this dog beach instead of Rosie's in Long Beach because of the nicer location and beach restaurant. Sadly, the no bugs at Rosie's makes it a bit of a better beach especially if you are like us and drive over an hour each way just to come

What a fun place! We parked at the top of the hills in Douglas Family preserve and hiked down to the beach. Such great views. Not too crowded. Public restrooms and water fountains with feet rinse off station. Some tar in the sand- stains your shoes just a bit. Truly heaven on earth. The hike was easy - even for a very pregnant lady. :)

Half dog beach half human beach, water runs into ocean from mainland, semi private local scene, nice restaurant available, amazing sunset, soft sand, beautiful in canyons/cliffs. I loved this spot for sitting and relazing and looked great for a long walk!

I went to this beach for Easter Sunday. Wow, what a gorgeous place! There is plenty of room to set up an area to hangout here all day. This beach is a great place for swimming, boogie boarding, and relaxing in the sand. I would recommend staying to watch the sunset! The only close bathroom is in the fancy restaurant right by the parking lot so take care of business before you go to the beach.
Dogs, waves, & and super friendly people! The best beach around!!! Kids had a blast and parents were able to relax. Perfect day!
The beach is okay. Not too long. Probably would try a different place next time. Parking was very challenging.

Can't say enough about this place. Great atmosphere,  always super clean.  The dog community here is great.  We have never had a problem with any dogs.  Love coming here to relax and let our dogs have fun. So convenient that there is a restaurant right at the beach.  Phys they have a doggy wash station.  It a a little pricy... About $10 bucks.... We don't use it but a lot of people do.  Don't wait bring your family here to have fun.

I took my 9 month old puppy to Arroyo Burro's off-leash dog area (Henry's Beach) and HE HAD A BLAST!! 

He normally fears water (he runs the other way when he sees lawn sprinklers going off), but when the soft waves came rushing in, he ran through and enjoyed the waves! It was such a joy to watch him!

In the same trip, I also stopped at two other off-leash dog beaches (Old Port and Morro Bay), and this was by far my favorite. It was clean, people were friendly, dogs were friendly, and there was even a doggie wash ($10).

We are most definitely returning to Arroyo Burro. DOG FRIENDLY APPROVED!

There was not a lot of parking, but we just parked in the neighborhood near the beach. There were so many dogs, so if you're a dog lover, i'd definitely recommend it! The water was nice, and the beach was clean. There is a restaurant right next to the beach which is perfect if you're a foodie like me. The restrooms were also extremely clean, and there was a self dog wash!! Overall, I would definitely recommend this beach!

Best dog beach in California!  Our dogs have loved going here for years.  I'm always amazed by the friendly dogs and their people, and have only once or twice had a negative interaction with an overzealous dog- much better track record than every dog park we've visited.  Parking has become more problematic in recent years due to the restaurant, which is a real shame, but the beach truly is a gem.
Fantastic park, my dog loves it. it's beautiful at sunset and there's a very nice restaurant.

I am going to keep this simple.  There is too much seaweed, shells, and rocks on this beach.  If you are a dog, great, who cares.  But as the human taking my dogs, I care.  We did not stay long.  I walked the dogs for about 20 minutes before going back to the car.  I forgot my beach shoes, big mistake!  I wouldn't go back unless it was cleaned.

1) The sand literally has no rocks or stones, so it's perfect to go swimming in
2) It's and on and off leash beach; there are dogs everywhere 
3) Easily accessible bathrooms, but no showers 
4) There's a cute, little restaurant right on the beach

We probably didn't pick the most ideal day to visit this beach (4th of July), but it was the only day we were in Santa Barbara. We planned this trip in advance and was told by a SB local that it was a pleasant beach to hang out. She even commented that she had never seen so many people there as it was that day.

Parking was outrageous. There's a very long, narrow lot to park, but it's shared by Boathouse restaurant and this popular beach park. When we arrived around 10:15-10:30, it was already packed and we got lucky with a parking spot at the very end. It was a bit of a walk, but we were grateful to even find a spot since the line of cars behind us were SOL. At least parking is free!

This beach area isn't very large, so there aren't many places for a lot of people to go. After lunch, we got to the sand around 1pm and were fortunate to find a nice spot not far from the main walkway. But, within minutes of situating, a group of rowdy young adults decided to set up almost directly in front of us, blocking my nice view of the water. Boo. We lay out for a couple hours and headed to the water before leaving, but it was too gross (little rocks, lots of dirt, seaweed, etc) to want to swim. The sand wasn't very nice either, but it was whatever. This is definitely not one of the nicer beaches I've been to and I would most likely avoid it on my next trip.

Side note: You can bring your dogs! There's a section of the beach where you can unleash your dog too. Oh, and there's a dog cleaning service in the parking lot!
My dogs love this place. Half of the beach is off-leash, so it's puppy paradise!

Absolutely love this place! It's so hard to find a dog friendly beach that is both beautiful and clean. We spent a few hours every day of our vacation here...my dog and I loved it. Parking is free, too!

Such an awesome beach!  Sand is super smooth, and the rocky areas are smooth too!  Lots are areas to walk & explore.  The parking lot is pretty large and free!  There's even a doggie wash in the parking lot area that we used for a small fee.  My pup came home tired, clean & happy.  I just wish it were closer!

so a long time ago, on a lost and unknown journey through Santa Barbara,
my family and self found this beach..unknown to us the name, location, route, and from years until return, unknown what exactly this beach was

so now we return, march of 2012, the weather just right on the second saturday, perfect afternoon of overcast, warm delight, the swimmers in the sea, the sun bathers on the beach, the dogs running and playing across the sand

this is one of the best beaches i have ever been to in southern california.
not for the cleanliness of the beach or sea (no california beach is clean anyway)
but for the exclusivity, the seclusion, the serentiy, the joy, relaxation felt for a few hours here

it is a beach not to miss

What a beautiful beach, quaint and scenic surrounded by hills and a cliff.

The views afforded by his spot are clear, full of wildlife and relaxing.

A restaurant is also right on he beach so you can enjoy some fine dining with a view.

While here I was able to watch many birds diving in to the water to chase fish as a school of them was moving up the coastline.  

The only thing I was surprised and or disappointed by while here was the amount of oil rig platforms out in the ocean that you can see from the coastline. I did not realize we had so many of them off the California coastline.

Wonderful spot in SB. Dogs run off leash, always beautiful, and there's food and a bathroom close-by.

The only not so great feature is parking but that's not a big deal.

We had a great day hanging at this beach. It has a nice off leash area for dogs but it's big enough that you're not on top of each other. All of the owners were respectful and seemed to have control of their dogs. Parking is a challenge since there isn't much but if you wait long enough spots seem to open up.

My 53 yo husband grew up going to this beach with his grandmother. He absolutely LOVES it. It is a little local dog friendly beach, that if you have spent enough time in SB, you know is called Hendrys Beach. I never even knew it wasn't really the name until recently. The sunsets are beautiful and most everyone is pleasant and their dogs well behaved. 
I have never in the last 27 years seen any dog waste left on the beach. There are always going to be responsible, considerate dog owners and good dogs and owners that are checked out and lack consideration for others and their dogs behavior will reflect that, but even with that and the amount of dogs, I have never seen dogs being aggressive, I have seen excited and crazy, but I have never seen a dog fight, which is pretty great. The parking lot is tough, but persistence pays off. Walking from the parking lot towards the beach there is a strong dog urine odor, if you can get past the smell, I struggle with it, because it is gross, but I forget about once I am on the sand and watching all the different dog owners and dogs and enjoying the view, the dolphins and the ocean breeze.

Pet friendly!

We had so much fun with our dogs on leash walking around the rocky beach taking pictures of the amazing rocks and gorgeous beach.  It is the perfect place for rock enthusiasts.  Not much sand to lay on, so mostly a good place for taking walks.
Amazing SoCal beach that allows dogs!  Two sides...off leash and on leash.  Family friendly with facilities and parking.  Beautiful scenery as well!

My hubby & I decided to go down to Santa Barbara (one of our faaaav towns) for the Labor Day weekend. We did a bunch of reviews on the beaches and wanted to come to one that wasn't touristy and packed with families (not that we hate kids or anything *lol* we just wanted a more chill day at the beach).

We decided to bring our dog Lucy along for her first beach day - as we usually leave her at home. Boy was this ever the best becah we could have introduced her to! There were dogs all over the beach at the section where you can take them off the leash. She ran around and played until she was completely wiped out. There are even doggie wash/dry stations there for if your pooch gets too dirty (luckily for us our dog avoided the water so we didn't need them).

Overall the beach was set in a good enviornment and was def packed with more locals that tourists - which is what we look for on vacations. It was nice, but not our favorite beach in SB.  

Some cons: the beach was quite kelp-y (lots of seaweed washed up all over). Also parking can be a bit difficult - we lucked out and got there right as someone was leaving but saw many others waiting for quite a bit. 

RATING AS A 'REGULAR' BEACH: 3/3.5
RATING AS A DOG BEACH: 5!!!

This is the KING OF DOG BEACHES!  Since there are zero dog beaches in Los Angeles we always hit up Arroyo Burro when heading North on the 101. Large parking lot that was nearly full on a November Saturday at 11:30am -- but we waited and found a spot. FREE by the way!
After a short walk you head to the left as that is the off-leash area which goes at least a half mile where you can take a walk and watch your lucky pup at play. Tide was out so lots of room for running. "Daisy" had the time of her life and got soaked. They do have a restaurant but it's NOT DOG FRIENDLY!  What's up with that -- don't they want the extra business?  Patio is huge so why not divide it up and have a section for dogs? (Ok, owners can come too). 
Instead we drove 5 minutes South on Cliff Drive and found this fantastic Vegan place called Mesa Verde where doggies are welcome on their patio.

Doggie heaven ! I drive two hours to get there because my doggies are worth it . There's an off leash and on leash side , and all dog owners seems friendly.

There is a doggie washing station by the parking  and a barbecue area with shade .

Best place ever!!

Parking was a big challenge today.  We were heading for the Boathouse restaurant.  They share parking with the beach goers.  It meant Dear Hubby dropping me off to seek a table while he circled both lots.

As he did that, I did secure a patio table, and got to watch the folks on the beach.  This is one area in Santa Barbara where dogs are welcomed.  There are even areas where canine companions can go off leash.

As parking is free, there were lots of families hanging out, plus couples, groups of friends, surfers, and dog-lovers.  Great people watching spot, too. 

I didn't have the right kind of shoes on for a beach stroll, nor were there any towels in the car to clean up with had I gone beaching it.  There isn't really a sidewalk or boardwalk area here for walking or bike riding.

We have walked the beach during other visits, when better prepared.  It's definitely a popular destination, away from the main areas immediately north and south of Stearns Wharf.

Just be patient on parking and enjoy the rest of the vibe.

My sister's dog loves this beach!  I do too.  It's lovely and it has a dog spa.  You can walk up to the wilcox preserve too, it is a really nice walking park/path that smells like eucalyptus.  On clear days you can see UCSB from the park.  This beach is a great place to relax on a weekend.

**a dog spa is one of those coin operated machines for diy doggie shower

Rowdy. Dog friendly. I would find another beach. Way too many people. Sort of messy. There are far prettier beaches to walk. Try north beach ..just north of the harbor at low tide. We also walked arroyo dog park up on the bluff.. Also a little worn from way too many people.

A great beach for walking (both with your dog and without).  Miles of sandy beached right up against the tree dotted cliffs of Santa Barbara.  The parking lot right in front of the beach fills up quickly, so do yourself a favor and try to get there early (before 10am) and go to the parking area on the other side of the dog wash.  There's more parking and it's easier to get in and out of.

i LOVED this beach!!! it allows your dogs to be off leash and on leash if you prefer... either way its perfect to bring your puppy here.. yes there are a lot of rocks... obviously you can tell because the beach is like right off a cliff... our dogs ran in and out of the water and played along the cliffs and tried to rock climb. its a very relaxing beach and I LOVE IT... and i know my puppies did too!!

Great place to let the dogs run loose and tire them out.  The beach was clean and all the dogs seemed well behaved.  Parking was free and it was easy to find a spot.  

As one reviewer mentioned, there was a lot of seaweed, but it didn't bother us and my male terrier actually enjoyed "marking" all the piles.  

We camped at the Fremont Campground in the hills and it was too dog gone hot (see what I did there?) about 90 degrees, so we decided to escape the site during the day and hang out at Arroyo Burro instead.  It's at least 10 to 15 degrees cooler down below which made me and my fur babies very happy.

Great beach with free parking. Dog friendly and has areas for dogs on leash as well as off leash, which is wonderful for me and my dog who I just adopted and does not yet have enough training to be let off leash.

Lots of kids, families and dogs make for an energetic atmosphere during the day. Lifeguard on duty during open hours.

I think it was the combination of cute dogs, awesome hang-gliders and fantastic weather that made this beach so great.  The farther you get from the parking lot, the quieter the beach.  

Past many of the dogs, there is some fascinating geology in the cliffs.  We also really dug the unknown hang-gliding birds.

This is a great beach for walking with your dog.

Just wish some folks would observe the rules regarding leashing your dog on one side of the beach.

Also called Hendry's beach this is a beautiful and flat beach, great for taking a stroll. I was looking for a new walking beach and a few years ago an employee at Lazy Acres market up the street highly recommended this beach which less than 5 minutes away from the market. 

Pros:  Free Parking and beautiful. 
There is free parking which is a plus and the parking lot is decently sized, I've never had a problem finding parking.  This beach is located just below the Douglas Family Preserve which is a beautiful and serene preserve of land with large Eucalyptus trees on a cliff overlooking the ocean with a dirt walking path that is opened to the public.

Cons: Occasional and unpredictable dog poop and tar.
So I do love this beach, and was ecstatic the first few times I visited, but there are some downsides to the beach.  First off if you have a dog this is a great place to walk them, there is one section of the beach for unleashed dogs and one for leashed dogs.  The downside is some owners let their furry friends poop in the water or even right on the sand without picking up after them which is just disgusting.  My husband evidenced both incidents on our last visit and dodging dog poop while walking and trying to enjoy made me never want to return.  I love to go barefoot when I walk on the beach and I shudder to think about stepping in dog crap or its remains, and frankly its the last thing I want to think about when walking on the beach.  Another downside but somewhat inevitable as most of the SB beaches have tar.  We spent literally 20-30 minutes removing each others tar from our feet after a less than hour visit to this beach.  I guess the simple solution would be to wear shoes here, but in my opinion that defeats the purpose of getting your toes in the water if you are going to the beach.

I visited California from Arizona and stopped at this beautiful dog friendly beach with my 11 month old pitbull. Everyone was friendly, the beach was clean and all the dog owners where on top of what their dog was doing which is always a plus. I would highly recommend this beach to dog lovers and owners especially pitbull owners.

This is my favorite beach for a walk and for interesting photography. It was my dog's favorite beach when we lived in SB. The tide pools at low tide are fun to explore, and even if you don't have a dog, it's fun to watch the dogs play together. I miss the Brown Pelican, if only for the location. I hope the new restaurant is at least as good, because the view is spectacular.

This is a wonderful off leash dog beach! Get here early - I arrived at around 7:30 AM on a Saturday and the parking lot was already almost half full. When I left around 8:30 AM, it was a little over 3/4 full. There weren't that many dogs there at that time though, so it did not feel overcrowded with dogs at all.

The left side of the beach is for off leash dogs, while the right side is for on leash dogs. The beach also has restrooms for humans and dog poop bags available. If you're visiting in the morning hours, I would suggest bringing a sweater or jacket. I vastly underestimated how chilly it would be, even though it would get very hot later in the day. 

One of the best things about this beach is that most of the off leash area is backed by tall cliffs, so dog owners won't have to worry about their dogs running off.  The beach was very clean (or the unpicked up poop was washed away by the waves?) and I did not see a single piece of stray dog poop. The dog owners and dogs there were friendly and well behaved. I definitely wish I had found out about this beach sooner. I will be bringing my furry daughters back soon!
The best dog beach in southern California. Worth the drive. Clean! I've never seen a dog fight. Just heaven

Arroyo Burro translates roughly into "dry ass river gully" and nothing could be less true about this beach.  There is a small river running through the beach that you get to jump across, and there is nary a dry ass in sight.  Great beach for running barefoot a short distance, (which I did) and I was able to lay out with a friend on a fine January day that was in the mid-'70s recently.  That alone is worth a 5-star rating.

I grew up on this beach, learned to swim in it's waters and had tar covered feet.  Attended S.B.'s Jr. Lifegaurd program for years and became certified but ended up guarding at a pool ;-(
In the old days (early 70's) there used to be a cool snack shack that served the best tortilla strips, and we sit in the sand munching them down.

This is a beautiful chunk of grass stuck in between the magnificent Hendrys Beach and the big ol Pacific Ocean that borders it and the overlooking bluffs there only to watch a sunset if they were for anything at all.

I have an eight month old pup, mixed breed, that's 17 pounds. I wanted to turn him on to his first beach experience. We went on a Sunday afternoon, weather was about 75°, and just like others have said, parking was an issue. (Sunday afternoon DUH). Circled around for about 20 minutes before we finally found a spot, but boy was it worth it!

You walk down to the beach area and make a left, and it's all off-leash for the dogs. The beach itself is really nice, with well packed sand, very clean, easy to walk and run on. We walked down the beach for about 40 minutes and never saw the end. The tide was tame and safe for the dogs. Lots of dogs enjoying themselves and each other.  We were there for about an hour and a half and probably encountered 200 Dogs of all sizes and breeds. Not a single one was rude or disrespectful in any way.  Had a wonderful experience, we will be back. - 1 star for limited parking.

We love coming to this beach it is difficult to find parking because it's always busy. 
My dog doesn't run off but it's nice that we don't have to leash him so he can relax.
Beautiful beach. Nice weather. We stop by every time we drive to Santa Barbara.

I thought this place was going to be just like Malibu filled with climbable rock formations and urchins in pools of water fed by the sudden drop into whale-friendly waters beyond, adjacent a swimmer (and dog) friendly area. But, this place is actually mostly swimmer (and dog) friendly---sort of. It's gorgeous to have some beach up against some hills (see pics), but as for picture-perfect scenery or clear waters, you need to look elsewhere. After finding a parking spot in the *free* lot, and perhaps getting tempted to eat at the restaurant on the beach, take a gander to the water and notice how much damn seaweed there is and how much the sand is whirled up into the water making it "dirty". Not the biggest fan of that. 

That said, the dogs were cute, and I did like the northern California feel of this place plus the milder water I could actually see myself swimming in. And, I liked that, despite being a modest beach, it has all you need for a full day out... From a green field with 2-3 grills to the restaurant, to a shower to bathrooms to free parking, it's an easy place to come visit for your next beach craving.
ABB is a nice segway between the two and definitely worth a look or quick walk through as it's always overlooked by many due to its location between the more well known spots that surround it.

Absolutely beautiful!  This Santa Barbara beach is wonderful and offers gorgeous views of the ocean.  

-Nice restrooms (!!) 
-There is a cute looking restaurant, which I did not try but it looked nice.
-Parking is available!  Be patient.  A spot will open :)

A short drive from the touristy pier area of SB.  Worth it!

Great beach for a dog to run and play! 

Warning: lots of tar on the beach so make sure you have a good way to remove it from your feet after you leave.

Really enjoyed this small beach and watching all the doggies play. 
The beach is divided into two parts on leash and off.

YES! Dog beach!!! I lived in Hawaii and this beach is super lovely! Happy dogs, parking, poo bags.....

*LOVE that the beach is "mostly bordered" by a cliff to make is safer for dogs....

Great day, nice happy people and pups.
The beach is lovely! It's a dog beach, leashed and unleashed areas! Great atmosphere.

I really love this beach.  There is a dog park area to the left and a restaurant right on the beach.  Nice crowd.  This beach is long and you can walk for quite a ways. Usually quiet and very relaxing.  Great place to watch the sun set and many people do that.  Clean and nice!

For a couple of years I've been trying to find a dog beach for my Chloe and I was so excited to visit Arroyo Burro. The locals refer to it as Hendry's, but it's easy to find by it's formal name.  I think this is the only dog beach in Santa Barbara and here dogs rule!  The moment we got out of the car we knew we were in the right place.  Dogs are allowed on all portions of the beach (leashed in the parking lot and the park), however if you follow the signs just to the left of the entrance there is a vast area where you pooch can roam free UNLEASHED.

I felt like a mommy sending her baby off to school on the first day.  Chloe was in HEAVEN. The dogs (and owners) are behaved, nice and polite, and I was blown away by how picturesque and clean the beach was.  Post up here for a couple of hours and your dog will be worn out and happy as a clam.  There are plenty of driftwood sticks to throw around, but just be sure to come prepared to clean up after your dog, as the success of this beach depends on the owners respecting it.  

For any dog lover this place will warm your heart, plus there are other things to do in the area. There's a beach front restaurant and a hang gliding launch in the hills above the parking lot.

I loved this place so much I almost didn't want to write this review so I could keep this little gem to myself.  It's Santa Barbara's best kept secret!

We LOVED visiting this beach on our recent vacation in Santa Barbara.  Parking can be difficult certain times of the day so be prepared to circle around (or not find a spot at all) based on when you are visiting.  

The beach itself is great though.  It's dog friendly.  It even has a doggy washing area to get the sand off of Fido before he jumps back in the car!  How cool!

Lots of room and soft sand - great for kiddos who like to dig and play in the stuff!  There is also a back bay type area (another body of water) that runs along next to the parking area.  Pretty neat! 

There are also picnic tables, BBQs and a restaurant on site.  Great amenities!  Would totally recommend this place for anyone looking for a family friendly spot to swim and spend a day.

I've been to Rosie's dog beach in Long beach ( so dirty, do not take your dogs there) and Huntington Dog Beach (way better than Rosie's, and bigger too). However, this beach is by far the cleanest. Came here on 4th of July weekend and it wasn't that crowded. We parked in a residential area, and had to go down at least 50 or more stairs to get to the beach. Only down side is that a lot of ppl did not pick up their dog poop up by the starting point of the stair area. So it smelled pretty bad. Therefore, I can only give this place 4 out of 5.
Our dogs love this beach and so far we visited this beach more than 5 times. Great views and nice sands. Other dogs and owners are also nice.

Stayed in Santa Barbara for the first time, just one night, and we stopped briefly at Arroyo Burro Beach. Very beautiful! We didn't get to stay long, but I would definitely return so I can explore this beach more than I did. It's beautiful here! We lucked out with parking as the lot was on the smaller side.

Just got home from this amazing beach!! Max and Marley had the best time playing with all the other dogs. This beach is clean because of the thoughtful owners that are very friendly as well. You can walk for a great distance as your pups find new playmates along the way. My only question is why arent there more dog beaches in so cal?

BEWARE OF THIS WOMAN AND HER DOG ON THE BEACH. HER DOG ATTACKS OTHER DOGS. 

so here's what happened last Sunday when we were at the beach. Our golden doodle puppy Jordan was so happy going to the beach for the first time this summer until this woman's dog( who she claimed it was her mothers dog) attacked Jordan and bit him that caused two deep cuts on his tongue. after this happened she was trying to walk away so we went up to her and told her what happened and she needs to take the responsibility but all she said was Jordan would be okay and refused to give us any her contact information bc she said she's not responsible for what her dog did since it's an off the leash dog beach and that's what dogs do. dog owners like this woman should honestly be banned from going to the beach with their dog off the leash.
Our favorite kid and dog friendly beach. Watch out for tides and rock slides from cliff but other than that beautiful!

We took my friends dog to the beach after we ate at Hendry's and it was so nice and relaxing. She doesn't go off leash but she had an amazing time and ran her little grinch paws through the water! She was so happy. We'll bring her back again! All the other doggies were really friendly too and it's nice to have a dog bath and free parking! Santa Barbra is pretty great!

We and our dog loved this beach!  We have been hesitant to take him to a dog beach since they tend to be wide open and he likes to run off, but this beach felt safer since one side is a tall cliff wall and the other is the ocean.  He had a blast.  Lots of friendly dogs and people.  No poop in sight.  My dog isn't a swimmer but enjoyed playing with the other dogs as well as attempting to rock climb.  I highly recommend this dog beach!

Went on a Thursday around 11:30 am in June and it was packed. The parking lot was full and the beach was filled with people and dogs. To the left, dogs are allowed off leash. To the right, dogs should be on a leash. 

We went to the right to avoid an incident as there were so many big dogs to the left and my dog is only 29 lbs. 

The beach itself was not that nice as compared to other beaches in SB. Lots of small rocks made it hard to walk on and the sand wasn't as nice or vast.

Some pros: There is a dog wash machine near the parking lot and the parking is free. Benches at the top walkway area. 

My dog is my gauge as I go to the beach for him and he really didn't enjoy this beach. He seemed anxious so we left after 10 minutes which is a shame because he loves the other beaches like Summerland, East Beach and Butterfly.

One of the best off leash dog beaches in California! (That we've been to yet)

There are 2 sections to the lot and looks like there is a fair amount of parking spaces, but we had trouble finding a spot even on a Monday at noon. (Though I suppose it is summer now) We for super lucky and got the last spot. Expect a line to get into the parking lot, as well as in the parking lot. Also, parking is FREE! From the parking lot it looks like the trailhead for the Douglas Preserve is also here. 

Disappointingly, The Boathouse at Hendry's (the restaurant here) does not allow dogs. :( There were signs prohibiting them except for service dogs.

Anyway, back to THE BEACH! On the left side, dogs are allowed off leash, on the right, must be on a leash. I am really picky about my off leash beaches because I have a greyhound that likes to chase ground squirrels, and if she gets started chasing one, there's no way I could keep up. For that reason, I was great full this beach was long enough I could safely get her away from the "on leash" area, and the cliffs are HIGH up so there is no way she would be able to climb up. That and I did not see any squirrels this time. 

We loved it so much we'll probably be back tomorrow! (But earlier this time!)

I found this dog-friendly beach when we went for a walk in the Douglas Family Preserve.  I saw the nice view of the beach with owners and their dogs frolicking by the shore.  I told my husband, "I know we had a long day and you must be tired, but we have go here before we call it off for the day."  And I'm glad we went.  I'm impressed that this beach is so clean, compared to other beaches in Santa Barbara and very nice views.  It's a good place to take photos of your dog or other dogs playing fetch and splashing  around by the shore.  Very relaxing atmosphere, a good place to hangout at the end of the day.

Awesome!

Took our Golden there for Thanksgiving, she loved it.

Great beach for hiking, lot's of DOGs...Shannon was very happy.

A real plus is the dog washing station in the parking lot. For a couple bucks you can fully wash your dog. There is shampoo, showers and blow dryers.

We will be back.

First beach that we went to while visiting Santa Barbara. This beach was very long with a great cliff background. The pacific water is ALWAYS cold so you just have to brace that aspect, but it's comfortable. I liked the ambiance and they have a restaurant on the beach, but it is very expensive don't go and pack a lunch!!! The only draw back to this beach is the amount of dog owners who completely don't care about picking their dog crap up!!!! C'mon I don't care if your materialistic or fancy, clean after your dogs because your maids aren't here to help you spoiled brats!!!

So happy to take my dog to his first beach outing ever at this beach! Loved seeing his face as he got to be off-leash and have the ocean touch his little paws. I wish there was a place somewhere along the Westside of LA to take my pup to like this. 

The only negative, which I guess you would get used to, is all the seaweed that has washed up and with that seaweed are thousands upon thousands of those little black seaweed flies. It was quite overwhelming to see them all over my puffy, fluffy white furball dog! I was constantly fluffing him to get them off. It would have been perfect had the beach been raked with one of those beach raking machines. I KNOW I'm overly sensitive about that and I also know I have bug issues... so other than that (for me) it was great! 

I was able to get a few photos of the cleaner spots on the beach :)

Dog friendly beach with a designated off-leash area for your four-legged friends!  Lots of pups bouncing around here.  Pet owners were responsible for the most part, but we did find some rogue turds in the sand, so beware!  Free parking, but even on a Monday afternoon the lot was pretty packed.  There's a sit-down restaurant that also has a walk-up window for quick snacks.  Public bathrooms and showers are located on the back side of the restaurant.  And there's even a coin-op wash station for your pet if you like.  Just bring your own towels.

Absolutely positively the best dog beach I've ever been. 

Well, comparing it to Rosie's in Long Beach and the Huntington Dog Beach is just not fair. This one is far more beautiful, with the cliff and large rocks. There's also a tide pool that gets warmer than the ocean, and is perfect for those dogs that love water but   are weary of the tide. 

I came during the week and there was hardly anyone else there. I'm not sure what the crowds are like on the weekends, but the weekday was perfect.

I've been living in SB for almost 5 years. How come I haven't come here before? This beach is great. You get free parking, and lots of beach space. There is an off leash and a on leash area to the beach. We (me and my dog) went to the off leash area. My dog loves it here. All the dogs and people here a super friendly and know how to interact with dogs. other beaches I have gone to have people who don't seem to understand that dogs are dogs. Everyone is super friendly, the beach is super clean (everyone picks up after their dog), and I will be coming here for many more years to come. Also, if you forget to bring a ball or dog toy, there are plenty of pieces of drift wood around for you to play fetch with your dog.

The place to take your dog. It's off leash to the east of the parking lot. Lots of dogs! All have been really well-behaved. Nice owners. It's not the prettiest beach in the area, but lots of fun for dogs. The bizarre thing is that the restaurant right on this beach is not dog friendly. The beach has lots of free parking and a dog wash.
It's too bad the Brown Pelican is gone as that was my favorite breakfast spot, but visiting the beach for a walk is still a must.
Great beach. Ample free parking and a nice little restaurant where you can get snacks like a corn dog. If you squint, you'd think you were in Waimea.

Wonderful place for free-running fidos!  Owners seem to be responsible as the beach remains clean and poop bags are plentiful.  Parking is free. Snack bar and bathrooms available.  And to top it off, a coin operated self dog wash is available in the parking lot so dirty dogs never need to enter your car.

Stumbled upon this gem of a beach during a recent visit to UCSB. We were traveling with our dog, and we were looking for an interesting dog-friendly place to take him to play. Arroyo Burro Beach worked well for all of us. Clean beach and well-behaved dogs (and their owners). We went back later and ate at the one restaurant on the beach. My dog and I both give this place two thumbs up :)

Bella loved it!

We spent 2 hours playing fetch in and around the surf. It was a cool 60 degrees this afternoon but the sun was out and the waves were gentle. The beach was clean and uncrowded, the people were friendly, and the dogs were having a great time! Dog wash station was a nice bonus!

Definitely worth the drive up from LA, we will be back soon.
This beach wasn't anything special but it was still nice nonetheless. Very dog friendly beach, clean bathrooms by the parking lot, and even a restaurant right on the beach if you get hungry.

Holy shit, this is the best dog beach in the world.

After a handful of visits, our dog would beg to get in the car to romp around this beach.  It goes for a mile, there are countless friendly dogs, the water is gentle, and the place is canine heaven.  We've never encountered even one aggressive dog.

The bonus....on the way out, there's a dog washing station with a soapy wand, power hose, and a giant blow dryer.  I couldn't believe it.  

Why does Santa Barbara get all the best stuff?

The best dog beach I have ever gone too. Everyone is friendly, the beach is clean and they offer a dog wash. 

It's so relaxing knowing my dogs wont be sandy when they get in the car. 

I like how there is a restaurant close by and you can bring the food to the beach.

Beautiful dog friendly beach, there is a leash & non-leash section.  The cliffs are stunning and you do not have to walk on the sand to get to the water far, unlike beaches in LA.

Parking was ugly, we had to park far away, not enough meters.  There was also lots of seaweed which brings flies.

Nice to stop in if you are close.
We drive from LA at least once every summer to bring our dogs to this beach! It's our favorite and this time we came prepared to wash them at the dog wash station!

This is a great place to bring your dog, but be forewarned: they have started aggressively enforcing leash laws south of the fallen eucalyptus trees, which is the official end of the off-leash dog area. A lot of joggers in the area, too, and many of them carry mace. My lab has been maced here twice in the past two years, and both incidents required visits to the vet to clean the stuff out of poor Nero's eyes. 

But a beautiful beach!
Awesome beach for  dogs, they can be off leash :) Finding parking was difficult in the main parking but we easily found a spot in the adjacent residetial area. FYI drinking and smoking prohibited.

I've seen the parking shituation from the seat of my bike and on weekends it can be a cluster f-word--sometimes on weekdays, too. 

If you're sans vehicle or don't feel like driving it, there are a couple of bus lines that will get you here; also, you can pedal your way to AB via bike paths that wind through Hope Ranch, down Las Positas Road or cliff drive.  You can also park at Eling's park and walk/bike your way down.

So, about Arroyo Burro/Humphrey's beach: it's smallish, I've seen surfers catch some baby waves; there are little tidepools, walking/hiking/bicycle paths, and showers for your doggies after they've been running around in the sand and surf :)

The best part about being at AB/Humphrey's is that it's a tiny hike away from one of the most breath taking views you may ever witness: the view of the coast from the Douglas Family Preserve.

This beach allows dogs off-leash south of the creek. It's a wonderful, flat, large beach with plenty of space to let your dog run around and play in the water. Just check the tides before you go - because the beach is so flat, high tide leaves little room to walk.  

North of the creek that lets out, dogs are allowed on-leash. It's frequented by surfers and bodysurfers too. And there is a restaurant (Hendry's) serving decent beachside fare. Dogs aren't allowed on the restaurant patio, but they have ties to clip your dog to outside.

It's a little haven, what a beautiful place and was such a pleasure to see a dog beach. Both of my puppies was so happy to play in a water, clean and romantic place, friendly happy people with friendly dogs....

Love it, best weekends ever, have to plan a weekend stay around just to spend more time on that beach....

Super Important Update!  We have been back to Arroyo Burro Beach several times since my review. On one occasion, we had a completely different experience.  The tide was high at the time, so naturally most of the beach was taken by the tide.  Duh! We we're only able to walk about 1/2 mile down the beach, not the customary two-plus-miles we normally walk.

We love walking with our dogs on the beach (off leash). If you're taking your best friend (doggo) to the beach for walk and play, do it at low tide.  I found a lot of "Tide" apps in the app store, and use one regularly. We arrange our visits based on the tide. Smart! (picture me patting myself on  the back) Wink!

Arrived on a heatwave day (YESTERDAY). Left the hotel at 9 and arrived in 15m. Prior to arriving we were warned by the gas attendant that every beach would be massively crowded (at 9am???). We arrived and it had a pretty decent sized parking lot. We found a spot and there were still half a dozen spots, no problem.

i think what makes this beach popular is that it has a restaurant on site. any beach with concessions or food would be popular. It appears to be a very popular dog park, too. Next time, we will skip this beach. While we love animals, it just was inundated and at 9am there was fresh nasty poop on the ground leading to the beach. Shame on you, whoever isn't picking up after your poops. NO BUENO!!!! 

We were informed by a surfer that one side is for the dogs and the other side is the 'no dog zone'. There is a guy on a golf cart who patrols and there were a bunch of dog owners who were in the wrong zone, either intentionally or unintentionally. Perhaps signs at the entrance may be helpful.

The walk once you get away from the entrance is nice. I would imagine any of these local beautiful beaches would be awesome for sunsets. unfortunately, we are in and out and won't be able to experience that. For sure, next time!

I can't say enough good things about this beach.  Happy people, happy pups, beautifully serene....paradise.  This beach is exponentially better than any in the greater LA area and will forever be my "go to" for Wheaties & myself.

Having been to my fair share of California beaches, it's safe to say that this is one of the best. After getting lost trying to find a different Santa Barbara beach, Yelp led us to this gem where we spent all afternoon sitting oceanside, chatting, napping, and playing guitar in the calm ocean breeze. There were groups, couples, joggers, dog-walkers, families, and even singles along the beach... everyone just having a good time relaxing on the sand and living in the moment. 

It was a beautiful beach, and not at all hard to find. I would definitely go there again!

I love the off-leash area for dogs.  Only problem - my dog went crazy and took off running down the beach!  I couldn't catch him... he was running in the water, past the point where the beach ended.  Climbing over rocks with water up to my waist (wearing jeans), I must say that I didn't have the best experience here.  Maybe I'll go again once I have a better behaved pooch.
Great dog beach. Clean and you can walk for a ways. Milo loved it!

Our first time at a dog beach. They have an on leash and off leash portion which is clearly marked. It's a nice large space and even though there was a lot of people around on a Saturday afternoon, it didn't feel crowded. The space is pretty clean too and everyone seems to be very respectful about cleaning up after their pets. I was worried that it would smell there since most dog parks I've been too with lots of dirt tend to smell like pee, but it wasn't bad at all! It was really nice to walk my pup along the beach and see how he reacted to being at a beach and seeing the ocean for the first time. It was a fun experience and I would recommend bringing your dog to this beach if you are in the area for a nice relaxing time.

PARKING HERE SUCKS!!! DO NOT GO HERE!!  

So there are maybe a total of seventy parking spots between two lots but the Boathouse restaurant uses the public lots as their own.  It will probably take you about forty minutes to get through both lots on a Saturday afternoon, only to not find a spot.  

Why?  

Because the a-hole drivers in front of you will stalk people walking back to their cars.  Then those people will proceed to take FOREVER to get into their car and leave.  Also, that being sai,d people will have passed a spot where someone is leaving and expect you to back up 20 feet so they can have said spot even though there are twenty cars backed up behind you.  No.  I can not back up.  Why?  Because you are past the spot.  I am past the spot.  And no.  No one behind me will back up either.  

I could not get out of the parking lot fast enough.  And much to the dismay of the stupid lady behind me, no, I can not go anywhere.  Why?  There are cars in front of me.  Keep honking.  And honking.  I can not get out either.  Yes.  Yes I will get out of my car to yell at you if you keep honking.  BTW she finally got fed up, went down a one way area and almost caused an accident.  I pray karma will get that witch somewhere down the line.

The spaces in the parking lot are small and not well marked.  The lot is in need of resurfacing.

I did come back two hours later to try to go to the restaurant.  Don't.  Overpriced and terrible service, but alas that is another review for later.

I did find parking after another twenty minutes of circling the lot.

So this is a dog friendly beach.  It is not however, a dog owner friendly beach.  I did not witness anyone pick up after their dog.  Not once.  Even though there are dog pick up bags available.

There is also a service dog wash area, but it is near the street which is  a little far in my opinion, but I appreciate that someone did think about it.

Overall, the beach is fairly clean.  It could use a few more trash cans.  But if you want to lounge on the beach, I would recommend going elsewhere.
Great beach to take your dog to since you can go off leash if you walk to the east. My dog loves to come here!

Omg awesome beach that is super dog friendly ! On our last trip to SoCal , we were so surprised that with the  miles and miles of coast line and numerous accessible beach fronts, that the majority of them were so against dogs !! Super disappointing. On our way up the coast , we were excited to find that Arroyo Burro beach welcomed dogs openly.

We arrived around 4 pm , so parking wasn't too much of a challenge because some people were already leaving the free lot , right in front of the beach front . Just a few steps from the parking lot the beach presents itself. As soon as our pup was free from his leash, he went straight to the surf to get his paw wet and then did a double barrel roll in the sand . It was awesome . We played for over two hours and watched the sunset as well. 

Afterwards we were able to rinse our feet at a little wash station. Bathrooms are available, and there is also a nice restaurant on site, which we got to try out and I will follow up with a review :) Looking forward to coming back again for a visit !

We visited here with our pup and our friends' pups, it was a delight to see the puppers run around off leashes, exploring the water, sand, and rocks, prancing and playing with other pups there. It was a large area and plentiful spots to sit in the sun as your fur babies splash and play.

Be careful when visiting this site. Had my car broken into and all my luggage stollen out of the trunk of my car. Stopped by to kill some time on my way to Pismo Beach on Friday July 13th, around 1130 am. Parked my car on the east end of the parking lot. After about an hour of visiting the beach front my girlfriend and I returned to our car and realized the car had been broken into via the driver side door. The door lock was forced open and once inside the car the trunk was then opened and all of our luggage was taken. Not a good way to start our weekend trip. Police report was done in hopes we could some how recover several of our personal and priceless items. Worst thing place was crowded but yet no one saw anything. Totally sucks.. anyway not a safe place to visit.
Nice beach if you want oil in your feet . Yes they are some oil tanks that's spilling in the ocean going into the sand .. very pretty beach and relaxing not busy at all..

Koa Bear LOVED this beach! 
Everyone was super friendly and all the dog's were too! 
We will definitely come back when we do another roadtrip from SD :)

I love this beach and my dogs love this beach.  Free parking lot.  Restaurant on-site.  Bathrooms too.  Dog on-leash side.  Dog off-leash side.  Soft-packed sand.  Sun.  Waves.  How can anyone not love this place!  Oh yah, and they have a (pay) dog-washing station on the way back to the parking lot.  What an ingenious idea!

Parking here sucks! The bus is infrequent but goes right there. Uber/Lyft is another option, bicycle, beam me over Scotty, etc.

The beach is great! Sometimes you can see dolphins. Low tide is great for walking long distances.

I was very happy to have discovered this beach while passing through Santa Barbara.  My charming pit/lab mix Ava absolutely loved the dog beach and got a lot of play buddies.  She was so ecstatic to run and play that her feet barely touched the ground (pictures coming up shortly).

The dog owners were well-behaved, stayed with their pups, and I didn't find a single random log of either the beach-bound or free-floating variety (yes I'm calling you out, OB dog beach people...)

Don't wear shoes.  To reach the long strand on the west part of the beach you have to cross a shallow, narrow, inlet.  Jesse Owens might be able to leap it.  You probably can't.

There's a restaurant on the east side that's gotta have some magnificent sunset views, but we didn't stick around for that.

Everybody seemed to be circling the lot right by the restaurant and beach for a space.  I found plenty of spaces in the lot that runs parallel to Cliff drive.

When in SB, Ava and I will be back.

This is a local gem. Great to walk, bring your dog, surf, or eat dinner at the Boathouse.  Note that if you take your dog to the beach the Boathouse has a special place where you can tie your dog up and give them water while you go and enjoy a wonderful meal. So add dog friendly!

Wonderful sunset views from the top of the lookout. You can find the stairs to the lookout behind the restaurant. I love to walk towards UCSB for a sunset and the beach has always seemed safe.   Be sure to check out the picnic area for small parties and picnics. There is also a environmental center there

I wish I could give this beach 10 stars!!!!  Hands down best dog beach in California.  You take a left at the sand for the leashless area, and then walk FOREVER!  No cones to say "dog area ends here."  It NEVER ENDS!  We've walked for hours on this beach and never seen the end of dog heaven.  Patrons are very responsible dog owners who love this beach and clean up after their dogs - no poo anywhere.  About 30 minutes walk and you will come to a staircase that leads up and out of the beach.  Head up here and go left into the residential area - you will come to Douglas Preserve - a forested LEASHLESS area!  Amazing views and a great loop back to the parking lot where you started!

Fabulous dog beach!

Comes with a dog washing station. How can you go wrong here? 

Sure parking is tricky but bring your pooch, hit the sand & hit the dog washing station complete with a blow dryer on the way out. It's a great set up and whenever I'm here, the dogs all seem to approve; I don't think I've ever seen more dogs running through the sand.

Arroyo Burro Beach is THE place to give your dog a good time when you're in Santa Barbara!

Sun, sand, and gentle surf combine to make a wonderful dog-friendly environment for your furry friend to play.  The beach is uncrowded and clean, the people are friendly, and any dog that comes here seems to be guaranteed a wonderful time :-)

Tip: There's even a dog wash station so your fur baby can get clean before heading home.

Whether you live in Santa Barbara, or are just passing through, bring your pup here and they'll thank you for it.

Since I first moved to Santa Barbara, all the locals kept telling me to come here. I dislike touristy, busy, or too cheesy of spots, so I discounted this place and decided to visit someday but not in the near future. Just a drive through the busy parking lot and trying to find a parking space explains what I mean. After several unsuccessful attempts with parking in the last few months, visiting Hendry's descended lower and lower on my list. After the third attempt, I finally forced myself to suffer through the sea of poor parking, half naked swimmers, surfers, restaurant goers, and dog owners. (As you can see, it's an eclectic mix of people). 

I'm so glad I did, because as the beach opened up and you walk away from the parking lot, you start to find some peace and beauty. The cliffs to the right hide all the homes and the road behind it, and you are left walking on a beautiful, glistening sandy beach with an unlimited ocean experience. There are showers but they were broken, so be prepared to have your car and home full of sand, but this is definitely a beach experience you won't want to miss. I need to be more open minded to recommendations from the locals. This one didn't disappoint.

It's my favorite beach since high school. In my 50's now... that should say something. 

It says... great place to body surf, people watch, dog walk, dine at a decent restaurant on the beach, watch sunsets, and close by access to the Douglass Preserve is a bonus. 

And if you're Jewish, you can wash your sins out to the ocean on Yom Kippur at this beach. 

If that's not a winning beach, I don't know what is.
Fantastic beach right before Santa Barbara on PCH going south. Highly recommended!  Free parking and a restaurant right on the beach were icing on the cake.

Arroyo Burro isn't so much a "dog beach" as it is merely a beach that allows dogs. What I mean by this is, unlike some of the beaches my girlfriend and I have visited - not everyone visiting this beach has a dog with them. This is in stark contrast to some beaches where, virtually every single person has a dog, or two, or three. 

What I particularly like about this beach is that there are sections for both "off-leash" and "on-leash." We've become accustomed to off-leash only, which means we have to spend all of our time watching our little chihuahuas very, very closely - especially with so many big dogs around.

Sticking with the on-leash section, there are no such problems. Parking is pretty limited, but the beach itself didn't really feel overcrowded. There appears to be a little restaurant that overlooks this particular area of the beach. Additionally, there's a self-service bath/wash area for your pets as well - which can be used for a fee.

For dogs, whether on-leash or off-leash, I'd say it's a solid choice for a day at the beach in Santa Barbara.

Beautiful beach, especially at sunset! Parking is kind of difficult because there's a restaurant there too, but the beach itself wasn't too busy. Lots of dogs but their humans keep the beach clean :) we will definitely come back to this beach when we're in town!

What a lovely dog beach! Really, the best all around. Dogs unequivocally rule here!

The place is easy to find. There's abundant parking and a large space for the dogs to run around. Some tides are shallow.  

The beach is kept clean. It's always busy so your dog will be able to find someone to much her personality. 

The best thing is, however, DOGGIE SHOWERS. Just for a few bucks you can get your pup sparkling clean and fragrant. 

The showers used to take only the special coin you'd have to buy so we had a few of them saved from the last trip but now it's all credit card. 

Love this beach and wish it was closer to my home!

Absolutely beautiful beach, perfect dog play area. friendly people and pets all around. It's one of those places that make you feel so grateful you live in California. Parking can be tricky, just be patient, and nothing beats the self-serve dog wash.

What a neat beach! 

The parking is awful, but if you time it right you'll be able to find a spot in the front lot. It's on the smaller side so beware. There's also a restaurant on site. However, what makes this beach so awesome is how accommodating it is for dog owners. Yes, it's a dog beach! But how many dog beaches have designated dog-leash and off-leash sides? And a dog washing station? (It's not free though)

It's great for dog owners and dog lovers (just cause you don't have a pet dog doesn't mean you can't love them am I right). The beach is also clean, although there was a little too much seaweed for my liking. Overall, it's a good beach to check out!

It's certainly a nice beach to walk on and my wife and I have had a few picnics there as well.  If the dog owners could learn that the off-leash area is on the south side of the slough and NOT the beach where you first arrive, then this beach would be a lot more plesant.  Maybe the city can make some extra money here by enforcing the law.

Other than the dog problem, it's a nice beach to walk on at low tide.  Wonderful rock formations!
Excellent beach.   Really close to the beach and there is a small restaurant next to the beach.  The problem is parking and that they turned off the showers.   I'd come back.

Probably one of my favorite beach in Santa Barbara and is also known as Hendry's Beach! There is a huge parking lot for this beach which is really nice but can be busy. You can always park in the neighborhood nearby which is always nice too. There is a famous restaurant at this beach called Boathouse and is always nice for a happy hour, brunch, or any special occasion!

This beach is huge splits down the middle when you walk down.  Since this beach is dog friendly one side is if you want to let your dog off leash and the other side is if you want to keep your dog on leash. Either direction is beautiful! It's a great place to take a nice beach walk at all times of the day and even a grab place to layout to grab some sun!

If you are lucky enough you can grab some good waves here and have a great surf. I've also seen some dolphins here a few times as well! This beach has some of the most beautiful sunsets that I have seen in Santa Barbara. They even have a public bathroom and picnic tables which are super convenient. 

I recommend this beach for anyone who is dog friendly and wants to enjoy some sunshine! Great place for walks, sunsets, surf, and more! :)

We came here today to grab a bite to eat at the boathouse and found some benches to sit on while having our lunch. The beach was quite busy for a Tuesday afternoon. We were lucky enough to find a 2 hour parking spot right in front of the boathouse restaurant. Saw a pod of 3 dolphins swimming out in the ocean too, that's pretty cool. Doesn't feel like we are still under shelter in place restrictions in SB!

We spent the Friday night of Labor Day weekend in Ventura so we could specifically visit here on Saturday.  We arrived at 9:30am and every single parking spot was taken.  Wowzer.  I didn't expect that.  Thankfully, a car pulled out and we were able to snag the spot.  The beach is set up with two areas, off leash and on leash.  The on leash area is super close to the off leash area.  The beach was loaded with flies too.  Super annoying. There are no shower areas either by the restroom.  I was not super impressed with this beach.  I guess if you live in the area, its awesome, but considering we drove 84 miles it wasn't that great.  We left at 12:30 and it took over 3hrs to get home.   Oh and a dog rammed into my wife, knocked the wind out of her and gave her multiple abrasions and bruises.

We love this beach.  We have to travel from Pinon Hills to get there and I am trying to find out if it is open.  I keep calling the number and there is no answer.  Is the beach open?
Thank you

Done with this Covid scare! We needed to take control of our lives back and the kids needed to stretch their legs !! This place was amazing! Super friendly pups to play with and everyone was nice yet still very respectful of the social distancing.. did not make new hooman friends but King Louie and Mia sure did make new pals .. totally worth the drive! We will be making these visits more regularly!!

I found this spot (aka Hendry's beach) while taking a scenic route drive and what a find it was!
Busy around 2 on a Wednesday afternoon.  The parking lot was full but I found a spot fast.  Lots of in and out traffic.

You have Bluffs as you are next to/ below the Douglas Family Reserve (which is a must visit). Also, the Boathouse restaurant is here so perfect for a meal and cocktails.  

Took a short walk and took in the scenery while waiting for my table at the edge of the beach.  Don't miss this one when visiting Santa Barbara!

It's nice being able to go to the beach with the dogs. There's doggy water stations next to the restaurant, and a $10 wash/dry station which does a great job. Parking is very limited, but there's usually a few spots in the residential area across the street.

This is our favorite dog beach. We sat on the no leash side and everyone was very friendly. Also everyone is very respectful of keeping a social distance. Our pooch had an amazing time socializing with other dogs and nice people.

Absolutely stunning. Nice scenery around to take in and photograph. Must love dogs, or at least tolerate them. Minimal sand walking distance to get to the water. I had such a nice time here. Laying in the sun like a dead fish is my specialty. 

Bring your kids 
Have a great day 
Would recommend

HAPPIEST PLACE ON THE PLANET IF YOU ARE A DOG....AND PERSON TOO!:
OMG.  This beach is so much fun to come to.  I'm sure I'd come here even if I didn't have a furry friend to accompany me.   But as the only public-access and Dog-friendly beach in Santa Barbara, it's heaven for our four-legged companions.    
The beach is actually called "Arroyo Burro", but al the locals will forever know it as "Hendry's Beach."
It's designed so that as you descend the stairs to the beach, you have the option of turning left and walking over a small wash.  If you chose to turn right, you will have entered the part of the beach designed for PEOPLE only.  However, if you turn LEFT and cross over the small wash, you can take your dog OFF-LEASH.   
I smile ear-to-eat when I come here.   The dogs run full speed and "smile" ear-to-ear as they socialize together.    Dogs dart in and out of the water.   It's just hysterical to watch.   I'd even come here to watch them if I didn't own a dog....it always makes me smile and elevates my day.   It's so nice that SB has this precious gem as access for our pets.   Many places do not. 
They also have a dog wash station (nominal fee) as you exit  the parking lot, so you can clean your "creatures" before you head home. 
The public restrooms are clean and you can wash your feet off at the top of the stairs after leaving the beach.  
If you should find yourself hungry while visiting, don't forget to check out "The Boathouse" for  fresh seafood selections (just be advised that they do have a dress code and you cannot simply wear your shorts and bathing suit).

Also known as Hendry Beach, still one of the best dog-friendly beaches I have visited along the Southern California coastline. There is a good stretch of the beach where dog owners can walk their pets, under close supervision, without a lease. 

Near the top of the beach, there is a grassy recreational area with picnic tables and barbecue grills. There even is a restaurant and sports bar next to the parking lot.

A smooth, sandy beach area makes it a popular destination for families. The area stretching to the southern tip of the beach, however, becomes increasingly rocky. Lots of seaweed washes ashore and become lodged along its crevices, attracting water beetles and flies. An eyesore and definitely an area dogs and people should avoid.

The highlight is definitely the beautiful sunset overlooking the Pacific Ocean and gorgeous Santa Barbara landscape.

A nice beach for families and ideal one for dog owners to take their pets.

Possibly the best thing about this beach is that it has a self-service dog wash in the parking lot. For ten bucks, you can clean up your sandy, dirty little pooch before they turn your car into a sandy, dirty mess. My dog, of course, remained pristine (#Virgo), and trotted past the self-service wash with a haughty little over-the-shoulder glance at her friend who got tossed into the tub.

I know this is a picky little thing, but there were lots of rocks and sticks in the sand, so it made walking barefoot on the sand pretty uncomfortable. That was my only complaint. Otherwise, if your dog digs the beach, come here!

Always a great local spot to enjoy the day, sunset, a walk with your dog (doggie beach!!), Lazy day with loved ones/friends, or even eat at a restaurant. Parking can get packed on sunny and clear days, but most of the time there's always some available. You can even hike on the other side of the beach on the cliff too! So much to do at this simple local stop!

I love this dog beach. There is free parking. There is a dog wash station. There are clean public restaurants. There is an area for dogs on-leash and a huge and safe area for dogs off-leash. My dog and I are always on the off-leash side.  There are huge mountains, so you don't have to worry your dog running into the parking lot or street.  It's never too busy, so we have always been able to find a spot that gives us space.

I absolutely love this beach. It has an off-leash and on-leash section. I have only gone to the off-leash side. 

The life guard is always helpful. I am new to the coast, so I probably had seemingly silly questions, nevertheless he answered me kindly and without judgement about the tide and the little sea life that washes up. 

The beach is clean, minus the seaweed that washes up in big chucks,. That being said I feel comfortable letting my dog run, dig, and roll around here. She absolutely loves it. She knows we are at the beach just based off the parking lot.

When the pup is done playing and is filthy  you can wash them at the dog wash. It is a bit pricey, $10 for 10 minutes, you really can do two dogs during that time. It is a full wash, flea wash, condition and dry, so the beach doesn't have to go home on the dog. 

The view of the cliffs is breathtaking and on a clear day you can see one or two Channel Islands. Plus the sunsets here are amazing. 

The only issue is parking is limited, but circle back if you need to, this beach is worth it.

Amazing place to bring your 4 legged friends. Frequently see dolphins here and the sunsets are simply spectacular. Please grab a poop bag on your way down! Most owners are really courteous about cleaning up after their pets.

Parking can be a hassle so I recommend parking in the neighborhood on the right before the headache of trying to wait for parking. 

Also please note that the left side is a OFF LEASH DOG BEACH. This is one of the few areas that our pups can act like dogs and yet people who choose to jog down the beach or just don't like dogs still get annoyed. As long as you don't mind a happy wet dog you will have an amazing time for sure.

Our new favorite dog beach, too bad we live in San Diego!  Our pup had a great time.  We came here two days in a row and she couldn't have been happier.  We  visited the off-leash side and will definitely be back when we're in the area.  Highly recommend - people and dogs were so nice, beach was clean and lovely, and parking was no problem.  Even grabbed a nice dog friendly lunch with drinks at the restaurant located at the beach.  Doesn't get much better!!
Great park for dogs and kids! Will defiantly be back with my dogs....they loved it. Also, dogs can play unleashed.
Parking toward the lot on the left is convenient versus going right because of the Boathouse crowd

BEST DOG BEACH EVER!! I almost Don't want to talk about how awesome it is to keep it a secret but I would love to share this wonderful spot with every dog lover. The beach is also great for those who do not have dogs and just want to enjoy a clean pristine beach with a nice bathrooms and great parking. Cant wait to come visit again!

I wasn't planning on going to a dog friendly beach, but we stayed because we ate the Boatyard restaurant. Parking is a pain if you come late and the beach was already busy by 11am. 

We say plenty of dogs running around, but there wasn't any smell. We stayed on the right side of the beach (facing the water) and there were no dogs at all. There were plenty of spots so it wasn't crowded at all. The only downside was the seaweed washing up. They wash up in piles and there are tons of flies. Lift one up and see for yourself!

The public restroom was clean and there is an area to shower off. Popular beach with the locals and tourists!

We were in Santa Barbara for a wedding and brought our dog on the trip. He deserved some play time after being cooped up in a hotel room so we brought him to Arroyo Burro after seeing the great reviews. It's definitely as big and free as they describe! Parking looked crazy but we actually found a spot on our first go around. If you can't find one in the lot there are residential streets near the entrance and the walk didn't look bad. As others have mentioned as you walk up to the beach the left is designated for off leash and the right on leash (there are signs to verify). It can look intimidating with so many people and dogs but if you continue walking you'll find plenty of room for everyone. Our dog LOVED chasing the other dogs and learning to chase balls in the waves for the first time (until he got pummeled by a wave). Make sure to grab poop bags on your way on to the beach as there aren't dispensers or trash cans on the beach itself. I saw quite a few pooping dogs both in and out of the water... Clean up after your pet please. I saw a couple people trying to bury the poop in the sand. 

The only reason I'm docking Arroyo Burro one star is that after we got back to our car my boyfriend realized his feet were covered in tar. This is the black stuff washed up along the water line of the beach - avoid it if you can. We stopped at a CVS on our way out of town and washed it off with some soap and a sponge but I've read that vegetable or olive oil works as does ice. Our dog had a few splotches of tar as well and his feet smelled like gasoline so we had to give him a good scrub down as well. Maybe this is the reason for the dog washes in the parking lot. I'd definitely come back to Arroyo Burro but with more caution and awareness of the tar.

Probably my favorite beach in Santa Barbara. Is also known as Hendry's Beach. Great place to walk along the shore ride the surf watch whales and dolphins as well part of it is dog-friendly. Also a great spot there for breakfast lunch happy hour and dinner called The Boathouse which used to be the brown pelican. Definitely worth stopping by and checking out and getting some food or drinks after or before you hit the beach.

This is definitely dog beach central. The first thing you see once you park in the limited parking lot is the sign that instructs patrons that dogs are to be on leashes to the right and allowed to be off leashes to the left.  It's great. Dogs are frolicking joyfully off their leashes on the beach while their owners watch and play with them. 

I walked down the beach on the other side.  The hard wet packed sand stretches for quite a length. It's perfect for a long walk on the beach to get some exercise during this pandemic lock down period, with nothing else to do. I saw a handful of large shells - half the size of my hand, along with interesting large rocks strewn throughout my walk.

There is a restaurant here. It's a shame it's only open for takeout right now. It looks like an awesome place to enjoy a meal, right on the beach. 

Parking is free and there are onsite, clean public restrooms.

Such a stunner! This beach is truly gorgeous. It's clean with soft white sand and gentle waves. I feel like I enjoyed this beach a lot more than I usually would simply because I arrived here around 8 am when there was virtually nobody there. Had this baby damn near all to myself for a  good few hours and it was absolutely lovely!

It's definitely a popular dog beach, and you'll get to see lots of cute pups running around. But even if you don't have a dog, you should come here because it's beautiful. It got crowed right around noon which is understandable. I feel like if I had arrived any later than I had, I probably wouldn't have enjoyed it as much, but I truly did. 

The parking sitch can be a bit tough. There's a lovely restaurant called Boathouse at Hendry's right next to this beach, so the parking lot is shared with both beachgoers and restauranteurs. When I arrived around 8 am, the first parking lot was just about full. There are two parking lots, but they will fill up quickly. I actually had breakfast at the Boathouse restaurant and then made my way to beach right after. Thank goodness that I got there early because there was a long line of cars waiting to find a parking spot around 9:30 am. 

The weather was perfect and there was a wonderful breeze. The water temperature was perfect too. Coming from the Bay, I'm so used to freezing cold water temps. But Arroyo Burro was just perfect. There's plenty of space to spread out too. Took a very relaxing nap while I was there and also enjoyed getting my legs and feet wet for a bit. 

There are restrooms next to the parking lot and a few picnic tables near the entrance. If you're not a fan of crowds, I probably wouldn't come here past noon. I ended up leaving around noon which was perfect for me because it felt a little too busy around that time. I'd say this was my favorite beach to visit while out in Santa Barbara. I can definitely see myself coming back here again on a future visit.

Great dog beach! 

Wasn't sure what to expect coming here for the first time. But the pup had a great time!

Parking seemed that it can be pretty tough. Luckily I found a spot. 

There's two areas Off-leash (left) & On-leash (right). 

It was pretty packed on a Sunday giving the situation we are in (COVID). Majority of the people were wearing mask that I saw. 

Most people picked up after their dogs but some people just left their dog bags on the sand.

I knew my dog was going to be wet and dirty from the beach but it was awesome that they have a dog wash ($10) in the parking lot. Plus, all proceeds go to county parks.

BEWARE! We drove from Las Vegas to go to this beach with our dog, for my birthday. We went,spent 2 hours there and then 2 hours cleaning up all of the tar and oil off of our handsome Border Collie and off of us! We had to use Dawn dishwashing detergent on him and baby oil on us!
So disappointed, as we've been there before and it was so clean. A SIGN posted with a warning would have been appreciated.
First time in Santa Barbara and this is our favorite public beach by far.  They have a restaurant and public restrooms.  The neighborhood is beautiful.  Will definitely come back here again.

I think my expectations may have been a bit high, so I found Arroyo Burro Beach somewhat disappointing. First of all, parking can be a pain. There are a good amount of spots but on a busy day (like the day we went) many people had to circle looking for parking and some eventually left. We found a spot but it was a long walk on hot asphalt for our pup to finally get to the sand. We only stayed for about 90 minutes and were happy to give up our spot to a waiting, idling car. 
Also, this beach is not really a "dog beach"...it's a beach that allows dogs or is "dog friendly". I know how that sounds, but to people whose dogs are family like mine is, we know the difference. We have been lucky enough to frequent some great dog beaches, so this beach was a bit of a let down. 
There was a lot of debris on the beach and many of the people with dogs weren't really paying attention to them, so we wound up making lots of dog friends...including an adorable pup that we thought something might be wrong with for a bit because he clearly wanted to play and was affectionate but then would act bizarre when we walked or moved.  After about 20 minutes his owner finally came to get him and explained that he was "attacking" our shadow on the ground. I had never seen a dog do that before lol. 
Anyway, I gave it three stars because it's "A-OK" and we might give it another try if we were in the area again but I would definitely look for other options as well.

If you love dogs and the beach, this is the place for you. There's an off leash and leashed portion. Not everyone has a dog, but there are plenty running around. There's a free parking lot right in front of the beach - it's just steps away! It's pretty sizable but sometimes you just need to be lucky to grab a close spot.
