This has been one of my happiest beach memories.  It's a great stretch to walk or jog down. Plenty of photo ops and space to relax. I'd recommend visiting during the late afternoon to catch the sunset.

Took my dog there today. I love the people, the dogs and free parking however my feet and my dog were covered in tar. Spent about an hour to get us both clean especially my dog, he had it all over his legs, feet and stomach) Will never go there again.
It was just a really cool spot was a lot of dogs here both off leash and unleash and there was a cool restaurant called Henry's and I just recommend it highly
Pretty small beach. Can get crowded on the weekend. Although it is dog friendly so that's a plus if you like dog watching haha.

My dogs love the beach, but when there is a leash-free area, my Sheltie, in particular, is really in her element. The only issue is that she does what she often does when happy: she barks. She seems to think it necessary to urge the waves back in, or back out as the case may be.

This is a must visit beach. Not just once but many times. The look of this  beach changes from week to week with mostly sand to mostly rocks and tide pools. At low tide at times you can walk for miles. One direction of the beach is off lease dog beach, what's not to love about that. I can get my dog fix and not have to be responsible for one. Just be aware this is off leash area and you may get jumped on . There is a restaurant here, (The Boathouse)right on the beach with full bar, so next time maybe see you at happy hour
Great breakfast. Right next to a beach. Dogs are welcome and in the beach it will let you have your dog run free.   Great service too.

Two Words - DOG. BEACH.

This is technically "the only" off leash dog beach in Santa Barbara and definitely one of the best beaches there is because of it. Located in front of the restaurant, Boathouse at Hendry's Beach this is an awesome spot to park your car, have a classy dinner, drinks, and a long walk on the beach after dessert. Perfect date night in my mind!

While yes Arroyo Burro has it's own private parking lot it does get quite crowded on nice weekend days. In this situation you can park in the neighborhood across the street, up the hill past the beach parking lot and walk down, or by Mesa Lane Steps if the tide is slow. My favorite thing to do is park at Mesa Lane Steps and walk the beach from there by going down the steps and turning right until you hit Hendry's - Probably takes about 7 minutes. 

Awesome beach especially for dog owners and/or dog lovers! Bring your pet, a frisbee, and a poop-bag for a solid, safe time. Also a wonderful spot to watch the sunset or bring the kids for some sandcastle making and little waves to boogie board in. You'll catch me doing all of the above here about once a week at least!
One of my fav beaches in Santa Barbara. Dog friendly. Clean. Great for relaxing, swimming, or long walks around sunset time.
This is a good beach if you bring a dog. Otherwise, you'll feel out of place. Expect non-stop barking, dog waste, chaotic dog behavior, lot's of flies, overcrowdedness and very limited parking.

A pet owners dream come true is right at this beach. Dogs can be left off leash to the left of the lifeguard shack and on leash to the right. The water is calm, great for beginner surfers and not too crowded. There's a small private parking lot which can get quite hectic so it's best to find spots along the neighborhood across the street and walk. 

If you're not a fan of dogs, do not come here. It's possible that you might step on poop or be approached by other fur-babies around the off leash section.

The best dog beach I've been to. It's fairly clean and there are several poop bag stations available. Signs say leash on but it seems that off leash is common practice. 

There is a $10 self-service dog wash available in the parking lot. Also free parking! It does get pretty busy though. Hours are from 8 am to sunset.

There is a small staircase where you can take selfies and/or just soak up the view. The whole beach has a very chill vibe and the dogs generally seem well behaved.

Also known as Hendry's Beach, and Hendry's Boathouse is a cafe on the property. They even have a little window for food on the go. Very convenient and would definitely recommend.

BEST DOG BEACH EVER!  I love this place and my Dog is in heaven every time we come here.  The dogs have a blast the owners are respectful and clean up after themselves.  Its absolutely stunning, my favorite place to take the pup on weekends

Omg! My new favorite beach in California! 
So warm and welcoming. Very well thought out. CLEAN! Everyone is so thoughtful and friendly. All I know is Im ready to move to Santa Barbara. 
FREE 2 hour parking?! Yes! I drove 2 hours with my two pups and I'm ready to do it again this weekend! As soon as you pull in you notice they have a doggy wash station.  How incredibly perfect. This is the sole reason I don't take the dogs to the beach more often. NOTE TO all LA beaches: Install a doggy wash station! It's really not that difficult.
They have water fountains for humans, for dogs and bottles. They have a foot wash station as well. 
I was excited. It took me a few rounds to find a spot but I got one. Arrived on a Sunday at about 2pm. 

There are two sections to the beach. On leash and off leash. 
I think this is genius. 
Everyone was so lovely and happy. 
Beach +dogs=  I can't say enough about this stop. 
My dogs thanked me immensely and smothered me with kisses! They were so happy I almost cried. It's been triple digits in Valencia and we've been stuck inside with the a.c. Blasting for weeks. This was well overdue and worth the trip. 
All the dogs were friendly and fun. All sizes all ages all breeds. The beach is so clean. I am impressed. 
We stayed for a few hours and around 5 I was ready to start packing it up. 
I was also getting hungry and anxious to order at the onsite restaurant. (Awesome too)
Stopped by the doggy wash station on my way back to the car. $10 for 10 minutes. Wash, shampoo, rinse and dry. The whole sha-bang! 
There's nothing better than getting home after a beach day and the dogs are already bathed. 
I loaded the car, called and ordered my food to go (thank you yelpers! ) and drove up closer to the restaurant entrance (scored a spot right in front!) and was on my delicious way home in 25 mins. (Restaurant wait took a bit longer than it should've. But you couldn't be waiting in a more beautiful place. So all is forgiven.) 
Can't wait to go back to this beach this weekend.

My choco lab loves this dog beach!!!  Parking is free and limited.  It does get pretty busy, especially on the weekends.  The people are nice and the dogs seem happy and friendly.  I haven't encountered any problems here.   My dog loves going in the swimming pool but yet she's afraid of the ocean waves.  Luckily at this beach they have a creek side.  That's where my dog would rather swim at.  It's perfect for her to go fetch the ball.  The beach is clean.  The dog owners have been picking up after their dogs do their business.  I highly recommend this beach for big and little dogs.  By the way, the best part is it's off leash!!!

If you own a dig this is the beach for you. When you walk out of the parking lot, if you go to the left there is no lease requirement. Your dog can run free with all the other dogs that are enjoying the beach and the waves. If you turn to the right, leashes are required. So, however your dog enjoys the beach the most, this is the directions you can take.

The beach is not simply a beach for dogs, however. It is a truly spectacular beach, bordered by cliffs. You can find all kinds of great sea shells and rocks as you walk along the shoreline. Or, just sit back and enjoy the sun and the sound of the waves.

A lot of people, who are not dog owners, come here for the exercise. It's a great beach to get in that power walk or that jog. Or, to just stroll from one end of it to the other.

Overall, this is a very pretty beach and a great place to enjoy the southern California beach lifestyle.

I stay at the main beach, Santa Barbara beach, beautiful boardwalk but almost no surf, no surfing n much too quiet for me, a city slicker!!
Imagine my shock seeing how smack g this beach was, had I known I would have brought my swimsuit. I came here just to dine at the one n only boathouse.
The surf was perfect here, I had the last free parking right in front of the beach. The weather was perfect n it was a family friendly beach, nobody smoked pot here! I would have given it 5 stars if it had another eatery.

If my dog could talk, he would tell me that this was one of his favorite days ever! Clean, scenic, nestled, calm beauty. Of special note was how sane the other dogs were. We normally frequent Huntington Dog Beach (proximity). We love it, but might I (we) just say the dogs there are a bit....nutsos. This is a much more elegant experience, which we both appreciated. I'm even a little hesitant in giving this review because I want to keep it that way!

I also loved that they had a Dog Wash in the parking lot! Adorable. Just note that a $3 token gets you a stream of warm (nice touch) water for what seemed like 40 seconds. To wash down a Golden in that amount of time is impossible. $9 later, I got a couple minutes of warm water, and I couldn't figure out the shampoo situation. Anyway, cute thought.

We'll be back! Hopefully it remains a well-kept secret gem! Keep it classy, Santa Barbara!

Arroyo Burro Beach is definitely the top dog beach I've visited throughout the years. 

I used to frequent Arroyo with my father and our childhood dog, and have continued to keep this tradition alive with my Australian Shepard, Sheila. 

This beach is absolutely gorgeous, sits on a cliffs edge in Santa Barbara, and includes walking trails, white sand, and a whole lotta doggos! 

The beach is split in half, dogs on leash vs. dogs off leash. There are poop bags available, trash cans, and a very convenient dog washing station. Unfortunately the restaurant on site is not dog friendly. 

While there is a number of free parking spaces available, the beach gets pretty crowded in the warmer months. You might have to get scrappy and drive a few rounds, but I promise the experience will be worth it!
Beware of oil on the beach!! Clings to feet and shoes. Toxic and v difficult to remove.

A very nice beach adjacent to Hendry's Beach and the Boathouse restaurant. It's a great beach for your furry friends and the only legal off leash beach for dogs within the city of Santa Barbara. It can get slam packed on sunny weekends and even sunny days during peak season. This is certainly for good reason. The beach is nice and flat and there's lots of real estate for the dogs to run absolutely crazy. This beach also attracts surfers when the swells are up and has a nice break. Also a spot that's frequented by surf anglers looking to hook up a tight line with an occasional fish. I have to say I have yet to see too many surf anglers have a prolific day. Then again I think it's more about how the day is being spent on the ocean with the wonderful surf breezes and on a good day a view out to the Channel Islands. 

While there is lots of parking it does fill up fast on popular times of the week and in high season. Certainly worth the walk even if you have to park a ways off in the distance. An experience certainly worthy of your time.

Important things first, Beautiful beach and an even better sunset make sure you stay till a little later in the day and try and ve there the same time the fishing men are there...

Arroyo Burro is one of my favorite beach of the area. I really love going with my famille, friends and dogs.

There is a plenty of space when it's the low tide. You have in the left of the beach for dog free of leash and at right you need the leash. (See pictures I added)
The view is wonderful.

Practicing surf and observing the dolphins are a good things to do here.

You can find free and accessible picnic tables, restroom, a seasonal lifeguard and a restaurant (expensive but with a beautiful view)

Concerning the parking : open from 7am to the sunset (so no overnight parking) and NO bus, RV or truck + trailer accepted.

It can be full of people because it's a popular beach. But once you get parked, you can have a long walk on the beach easily. It's a long beach.

Waiting to be seated at the Boat House, we explored the beach area. This is a beach that has cans for recycling, trash, and dog waste. The beach also has a designated side for dogs on leashes and with no leashes. Benches are plentiful for sitting, but with strong winds, no one sits too long. Too cold. 

We saw lots of different dogs, and people, while waiting. All were interesting, especially the folks in shorts in 57° weather. The seagulls were plentiful - enjoying the wind currents and looking for handouts. This beach in the warmer weather must be packed! Also liked that one could get an accessible wheelchair for wandering on the beach. Regina would love that! 

Bathrooms are available and we almost went in there to wait. No wind! But we didn't. I did find an ideal spot to wait, in the sun and out of the wind - behind the restrooms!! Then Niece found secret staircase, next to restaurant, out of wind with stone benches. Wonderful find. Look for it! It's magical.

Arroyo Burro Beach, is a really nice beach with hikes and fun things to do. Next to the beach there is a really nice river following. Also at night you can hear the frogs go "rrribyit" at night. There's a really nice restaurant on the beach but it's really pricy.

If you have a big dog, medium or small dog that you love- this is the place to go on the weekend! This beach has an off lease and on leash portion. I am a frequent goer with my fiancé and Boston terrier! We absolutely love it. It is a hidden gem. 

Once you get there they have a smaller parking lot (for how busy the beach gets). In the summer I'd recommend parking down a nearby street- but in fall/winter seasons there are usually open spots. (It's free). 

There are public restrooms, a beach front restaurant and miles of off leash beach. 

All the people and dogs are friendly, the beach is clean and beautiful! I'd definitely recommend coming here and having a beach day with your pup! If you go without a dog (some do) just expect a lot of dogs running around and that your towel may get a bit sandy!
We love this beach. We spend the entire day here, most do not so the parking may seem full but people rotate out so often you will get something with patience.

Who doesn't love dogs and a sweet sunset. Whether you're at the Douglas Preserve or walking on the beach with the sand between your toes this is the place to go. I love to drop by to catch sunsets, set up a blanket and read a book, or to take my fur babies for a walk. If you walk to the left, dogs stay on leash. If you walk to the right, dogs are off leash and having the time of their life. I also love this beach because most of the dog owners are responsible and dogs are well-trained!

Arroyo Burro Beach, also known as Hendry's Beach, is the local "off leash" beach for dog lovers. Steep bluffs (that can/will drop rocks!) tower over a mostly flat beach characterized by jutting rocks, tidepools and a band of pebbles at the waterline.

Best times to go are several hours into a receding tide, when the beach widens up for your pooches. Doggy cleanup bags are provided and the beach was fairly clean when we visited in April 2015. From the parking lot, go "left" at the beach for the off-leash area.

Dog washing station is a huge plus! There's also a bar/grill with outdoor seating if you get the urge.

Bottom line: Only come here if you have a dog, skip otherwise! I am a beach lover. I came to this beach on a Sunday morning while visiting SB to go for a morning run. The beach was full of flies (I think I swallowed some while running). It also has a bad stench in the air, not the salt water and clean smell one usually breathes while going to a CA coastal beach. I mean it isn't awful, it is still beautiful and I didn't see any trash so it is clean. There is a decent amount of parking and it is great to go for a walk, run or to take your kids/pets. But, I am a CA girl and I have been to a lot of beaches and this is definitely my least favorite.

We took a roadtrip from LA up to Pismo Beach with our dogs and Santa Barbara was apart of one of the stops. We wanted to lay out and beach bum it with the dogs but it was kind of chilly the day we went so, we decided to take a long stroll on the beach instead.  It was nice to have my two dogs off leash and let them roam around and play with other pups around.  We walked really far down and after a while it felt like we had the beach all to ourselves. This beach and the Huntington Beach Dog Beach are my top faves to bring my pups.

Parking may seem tough, but there are enough people constantly leaving that you will find a spot.  There are bathrooms and showers (to wash off the sand) here which is a nice feature.  It is one of the few beaches where you can bring dogs and there is an off leash area and an on leash area that is separated by water (a stream that comes out to the ocean).  The off leash area is pretty hectic with dogs running around everywhere.

The water has mostly sandy bottoms and nice smallish waves that you can both body surf or body board.  There were a couple of smaller kids surfing a little on the first day we went, but the rides were really short.

There is a restaurant if you want to stop in and eat and they also have take out.

If you have a dog, you definitely want to come to this beach.  None of us had a dog, but we all still had a great time.
A really great dog beach! Lots of parking. Great for humans too though. There was some grassy areas & a restaurant there too! We will be back for sure if in the area.

A slice of dog heaven for pups and family alike! We've been here with two very different dog types: a clinger and a runner. Both did really well here.

The strip is split basically in half for leashed and unleashed dogs. Either side is naturally closed off by the landscape meeting the water. On the occasions we've been, the dogs are friendly along with their accompanying humans. 

There's a cafe if you have money to burn at the entrance. There's also a doggy wash in the parking lot if you want to bathe your pup before going back home.

My only gripe is the parking. Possibly a victim of its success, this beach is really popular and parking is limited....really limited. Just be patient! And if you have the flexibility, avoid peak times like evenings and weekends.

Two paws up for Burro (also known as Hendry's) and highly recommended!

Popular dog beach, I was hoping with the recent rain the creek would be connected the to the ocean to make the scenery even more amazing. It's a long beach with tall bluffs along all sides for relaxing views and great photography opportunities. There wasn't too many people when we went and it was pretty peaceful.

Dogs can go unleashed east of the creek, we stayed on the left side and the kids loved throwing seaweed into the ocean and running away from the waves. There is a small sand ledge so be careful when running away from the waves, my wife tripped over it when taking videos of the kids.

There is a small parking lot and a popular restaurant so it does fill up fast, we came early and parking was fine but by the time we were leaving cars were waiting for out spot.

The creek has bacteria warning signs so don't go jumping into that water :)

So far, my favorite beach on any coast.

I loved it because it wasn't over crowded with people or dogs.
And it was nature at it's best. There's nothing more disappointing than walking onto a man made beach with No rocks or SHELLS. 

I only wish I had arrived earlier so that we could have gone further along the shore before the tide cut us off.

WATER WATER WATER! Thank god for the rain it has blessed our mountains with beautiful waterfalls and wells that u can hear all throughout this trail! It was amazing did a little bit of bouldering here as well. And I saw families taking a swim in the deeper areas. Definitely bringing my son here for an adventure next time!

Absolutely a let-down.  My kids and I are quite athletic and avid hikers.  Compared to trails like the ones at the Pinnacles in Northern Cal, this place is just plain pathetic.  Pretty much all uphill with no rudimentary signs whatsoever to show you which direction to go.  Scenery is mediocre at best unless you go all the way to the top.  I see nothing spectacular with this trail especially if you are coming from out of town.  Don't waste your time here.  I would recommend driving up Gibraltar Rd instead and stopping at certain spots to go off those trails instead for much better views of the Santa Barbara area.

Loved it. Its a gradual moderate steep short hike. Water and Trail shoes as must, loose rock, so good shoe support helps. Wear pants or leggings, some paths are more narrow than others and I noticed there is a lot of Poison Ivy or Oak, not sure which but I do know they are either one. We hiked it when the weather temps were about 80, nice cool breezes throughout the hike. Vegetation and rock formations gorgeous. There are some very nice streams. We only walked to the Meadows due to the limited time we had.

We will be back next time to go to the top and spend more time and have a picnic.

I came here to hike because I heard that there was a creek that ran by the trail. At this time, it's mostly a dry hike. Nothing too special. Some nice scenery, but there are hikes with better scenery. Also, parts of the hike have a lot of brush against the legs, so it's better to wear leggings.

It's an okay hike, but I would Not recommend going out of your way to come here.

beautiful hike and many nice people along the trail. There was water along the trail for the dog to drink and cool off. We only made about a mile down the trail as we were carrying 9 month old twins in packs. So that extra weight slowed us down. Looking forward to going back when the twins can walk or when we can get a baby sitter!

Undoubtedly owing to it's name, this trail is relatively unknown and pristine. The trail splits at times but follows the course of the creek. There are huge boulder sized rocks along the creek side and plenty of trees which help provide near continuous shade throughout your hike. 

The trail is about 3.5 miles round trip with a difficulty level almost anyone will find easy. When you reach the end you are greeted by a large open field of wild flowers with several boulders around the circumference. From here you have an unadulterated view of the city far below, mountains, and the channel islands. IT IS SIMPLY THE MOST BREATHE TAKING VIEW IN THE ENTIRE COUNTY. 

And unlike 7 Falls, there is ample spaces for parking because there are much fewer patrons to this hidden park. Did I mention it's only 15 minutes from downtown? 

Near Skofield Park.

Love it here!! Beautiful hike w/ gorgeous views of SB. 

The drive to get here is an adventure / beautiful in itself!

We hiked to the meadow, and then continued to make our way up to the Gibraltar Road. 

First, the trail to the meadow consisted of large rocks and crossing of the creek at least three times. To me, this is great, because I love rocks and creeks. However, some parts of the creek were dried up, so it was not as lovely as it's supposed to be.  I expected the meadow to be a widespread of green grass where I can just run for hours without reaching the end, but it was yellow and smaller than expected. Maybe it's due to the drought.

We continued up to Gibraltar road for about another mile. This was a steep climb similar to the Gaviota trail up to the top, but it's relatively short. Within the first 2 minutes or so past the meadow, you'll reach a nice shady spot with trees and nice rocks you can sit on. It's the perfect spot for a picnic! The rest is uphill with little shade. The last ten minutes up has no shade. Once you reach the top, you will get a nice view of S.B. county. 

Overall, from the beginning to the Gibraltar Trail, it took us 4 hours and maybe 5 miles roundtrip. But we took plenty of breaks. It should only take 3 hours.
Had a really great "herb walk" with Emily Sanders yesterday. Gorgeous hiking spot - so nice to see it after almost enough rain... water in the creek again

Rattlesnake is especially beautiful right now after the rain. The drive alone is stunning. Every afternoon I get off, my dogs and I are hitting this trail. We usually hike for about 1 and a half and turn around and head back so you can go as far as your want. I love hearing the stream trickle as we walk and it's shady right now so we don't overheat and get sunburnt. I recommend wearing hiking boots right now because it's been muddy and you will be crossing streams. I would categorize this hike as moderate since you are walking uphill half the time. Btw, we typically park in Skofield Park. You can use that address to put in your GPS to get there.

Rattlesnake canyon has always been one of my favorite places to visit to observe all kinds of reptiles/amphibians and other wildlife. Newts mate heavily in the larger pools during the spring and it's always a treat to see them at work. The only thing that bothers me is that I often saw people letting their dogs run around in the pools. These pools are essential to the survival of this species, so I hope by seeing this review hikers will keep their dogs on leashes and keep them on the path so that the wildlife will continue to be able to breed without disturbance.

Clocking in at just over 2 and 1/2 hours (around 5.5 miles round trip), the hiking in Rattlesnake Canyon was a decent workout, with quite a bit of climbing starting out; the last part of the ascent was the steepest.

Tip: All told there's an elevation gain from the bottom to the top of around 1500 feet.

We took the trail all the way to Gibraltar Road, and were rewarded with some truly stunning views of the ocean and the islands.  As you ascend, you'll encounter a number of nice shaded areas along the trail, along with several good stretches with no shade at all.  After a while you'll come to a creek, which is quite lovely after the generous spring rains.

The return-portion of the hike is almost all downhill, so you'll make better time on the way back than on the way out.  There's also a park near the trailhead that looked like a great place to have a post-hike picnic.

Since I tend to hike a fair amount of long and challenging hikes I tend to forget this 4+ mile out and back trail can be a little challenging too however it's definitely doable. The trail starts near Skofield Park, sometimes you can actually park in the Skofiled lot or right along the road leading to the trail.

The trail is fairly easy to follow with some decent elevation gains along the way. While the views might now be as nice as inspiration point there are a few spots where you can get a nice view of the ocean and the surrounding mountains that you're in.

The actual trail itself is only about 2.2 miles with 1,600 of elevation gains, where the trail ends you find a sign point in the direction of other trails and roads so you can add on to make your hike in the area around 6-8 plus miles round trip incase you feel the 4.4 miles isn't enough for you. *side not to get to the top of the trail, one you get to the meadow there will be a fork toward the right. Stay left to go up the trail to reach the end of rattlesnake trail.

Great hike with great views! Best part of it all is that it's dog friendly! Parking is terrible. You definitely want to be parked inside the white line to avoid a ticket. If you're ON the line, you're at risk for a ticket. The roads can be a little narrow so I understand the strictness around this.

You get a little everything: streaming water, great views of the mountains, lots of shade, tons of picture opportunities, and a meadow of flowers! We didn't quite make it up to the meadows because we were short on time. 

Great place and easy to find. There's no signal up here so plan accordingly.

Scenic and beautiful trail!

I was able to take advantage of today's random sunny weather sandwiched between two rainstorms. As such, I knew I was in for a wet trail. Wet, check. Muddy, check. Slippery, double check!

The trailhead is maybe 100 feet away from the front of Skofield Park in Mission Canyon. It's a peaceful and hidden part on the outskirts of town. While there's no private parking, I was able to find a parallel street spot close by. 

The trail is mostly shaded and meanders through four separate creek beds. On a typical Santa Barbara day, this would be a non-issue as our city is notoriously dry. However, all beds today were teeming with rushing water. I must admit that it was kind of exciting balancing from stone to stone in an effort to make it across. One of the creeks didn't have equally spaced stepping stones and one of my shoes had to step in the water. The sun dried it out during my trek so I didn't mind all that much. 

One mistake I made was bringing my AirPods. I was afraid they'd fall out of my ears not only when crossing the creeks, but also during instances where I'd accidentally slide in the muddy terrain. Not only that, but there was pretty much zero Verizon cell reception. The sound of rushing water was peaceful and provided a wonderful natural soundtrack during my trek. 

The trail itself is wide and mostly clear. As you get past the first half, it gets exponentially steeper and rockier. I didn't make it up to the very top but am pretty sure I made it the majority of the way. My Strava app clocked 5.5 miles round trip.

This hike was not strenuous and while there were a good amount of others out there, still felt very secluded and peaceful. I'm no expert hiker and will say that this trail was not intimidating. The only iffy part was the muddy terrain but I don't imagine that this would be the case the majority of the year. Definitely worth checking out if you're looking for a nice way to spend a few hours.

All it takes to witness the natural wonders of Santa Barbara is this one hike. Walk along butterflies who glide past you through the air. Follow the sound of crystal water creeks all while meandering up the mountains. Chew on some sour grass like the local kids do. Hop in a refreshing 'poza' or pool to cool yourself down. Observe paragliders dive from peaks and mountain climbers escalate cliffs. Welcome to Rattlesnake Canyon!

Get Here:
Drive up Mission Canyon Road and turn onto Las Canoas near the botanical gardens. Follow it until you reach Skofield Park and a stone bridge built by a mason in 1919. You can't miss the sign for Rattlesnake Canyon & Skofield Park that looks like this (https://www.yelp.com/biz_photos/rattlesnake-canyon-mission-canyon?select=UdZte62iVm6ih1c4KNOEhg).

Find Your Route:
The trail is pretty well paved by humans. When in doubt, follow the creek. The GPS on my phone's Google Maps app was helpful too since the entire trail is mapped out from beginning to end; service is decent (with T-Mobile) but can be sketchy in spots. (See here: https://www.yelp.com/biz_photos/rattlesnake-canyon-mission-canyon?select=8T8_Vwyfa1Hs6-U4gKsLdw)
Look for these key points: Remain creekside initially. If you're observant, you may find pieces of the old dam and aqueduct built in the early 1800s by the Chumash along the creek that once brought water to the Mission. Cross over the creek twice. You'll eventually find yourself in a beautiful grassy meadow known as Tin Can Meadow (image here: https://www.yelp.com/biz_photos/rattlesnake-canyon-mission-canyon?select=sHNBj-8JY6EvzZAVE11fVw)--named after squatter William O'Connor who once called this plot of land home until it burned down in the 1925 wildfire. You'll eventually reach a split in the trail with a sign leading to either Tunnel Trail to East Camino Cielo (more difficult) and Gibraltar (less difficult yet still steep). The half mile hike with multiple switchbacks to Gibraltar has a pay off of spectacular downtown Santa Barbara and ocean views, in addition to paragliders and rock climbers. 

Take some tips:
-This is one of the few trails not impacted by the Thomas fire and also very shaded, so get here early to avoid crowds. Thankfully parking is plentiful.
-Even though the trail is very shaded, you will still need plenty of cold water to drink along the way.
-Be aware of Poison Oak along the route--stay on the trail!
-Take your antihistamine allergy pills before starting the trek. Pollen blows every which way.
-There is a lot of walking on wet, uneven stones and rocks so wear steady hiking shoes with decent tread.

Thanks to the Santa Barbara Independent news for the history tips and lesson!

The only way to describe this hike is: magical! I loved every minute of trekking through this trail full of trees, a stream, boulders, and the fresh mountain air. My friends and I stopped by here after being in Santa Barbara for a work event. Since it's such a long drive back to SD, we decided to get in some exercise first. I'm sooo glad we did. Hiking in SD is very much sub-par when you grew up in the PNW. I didn't think Santa Barbara would be much better, but it totally was. Felt like I was in another state lol. I could describe it more, but I'll let you see for yourself in the pics. 

PS- it's roughly 4 miles round trip, and dog friendly :)

As a non-avid hiker, this was a pretty challenging hike. I've only done inspiration point in town, so I was excited to try a new trail. Parking wasn't too bad on a Saturday late afternoon in the winter. Most of the parking is parallel on the street along a drop off with no curb so be prepared for that. The trail was pretty easy to find and most of it runs along the creek and under the tree cover so the shade was nice. I appreciated the peaceful sounds of the creek along the way. 

The incline itself was substantially steep. Lots of rocks and uneven footing. I would recommend this hike for someone with at least an average level of physical fitness. It took about an hour to get to the meadow with the fork, and then you can choose to turn back, go left for Tunnel Trail, or right for Gibraltar. Since we heard of the stunning views at the top, we decided to go right. This took about another hour to get to the top and it was much steeper than the first part of the hike. Not to mention the end of this trail connects to an actual road so you COULD drive up if you just wanted the view (we considered taking a Lyft back down since the sun was setting).

The view at the top is gorgeous, so worth it for sure, and heading down was much faster. Be careful if you're heading down around sunset. Once it gets dark, it's easy to get lost off the trail under the tree cover. 

I might do this hike again once I've tried some other local ones.

I did a nice 6.8 mile loop going up the shaded side of the canyon in the morning and coming down the partially shaded canyon in the afternoon.  October weather was fine, but still a little warm for this walk, so bring at least a liter of water.

This walk is unique for the desert plants you will see on the way.  Coming from Northern California where there is lots more water, the trail has an interesting collection of plants and variations of the Northern oaks that have adapted to the dry environment.  The leaves are smaller and the acorns are also tiny.

I met another hiker on my way who hikes the canyon 6 days a week with his three standard poodles.  He pointed out a known rattlesnake den that is on the trail.  "I often see them out sunning or with their heads peeking out the den when we go by.", he said.  So, be alert and you may see one of those reptiles for which the canyon gets its name.  My trail friend also showed me how high the water can get during the rainy season in the canyon - VERY high and swift - so if there are rains much of the trail is impassible and dangerous.

The trails are well enough marked, but you can use your phone to see exactly where you are using various maps.  Reception was outstanding for me!  ( Project Fi ).

My Runkeeper app kept me updated as to where I was on the trail and how slow I was going.  The trail is quite steep at the 5 mile point of my loop.  I was glad I took my cane!

name: Summerland Beach Rides
categories: Tours, Beaches, Hotels & Travel, Active Life, Horseback Riding, Attraction Farms, Farms, Arts & Entertainment

yelp_reviews.text: Went here as a Valentine's Day gift and it was a great experience. Charlie was a pleasure riding with . Who wouldn't love to take a horse ride down to the beach? Highly recommend trying out. (They also have minis!)

Paco led my friend and I on the most gorgeous beach ride. He knows horses and is a wonderful people person. He's always there to keep you safe and educated on what to do and is so much fun to be around. We'll be back for sure!  Thank you!!
Very bad experience, this place is so unprofessional and not worth the price will not go back

We had a wonderful morning ride with Charlie along Loon Point Beach.  Horses were wonderful companions, and I couldn't have asked for a nicer way to start the day.  This is a must do for horse lovers visiting Santa Barbara.

Thank you Charlie and Gabie for setting us up for the perfect beach ride for my wife's bday! It was perfection and we all love loved it! We can't wait to come back for more.... Charlie was awesome  and a great trail guide. I highly encourage anyone who has ridden, or not, to get outdoors and give them a call.... A 5 Star Experience!!!!
We had beautiful weather for our beach ride and it was a memory that will last a lifetime.   Great organization, and would go with them again!   Thanks!

* Erica and Rudy were fantastic from start to finish

* We had a great two-hour tour of Santa Barbara and the beach 

*  The kids loved it, and we will be back.

First off, Santa Barbara is beautiful. We did a horseback riding tour with Summerland and learned a lot about the local geography, its famous residents, and the scenery.

Rudy was our guide, and he was terrific the entire time. I would strongly recommend if you have 90 minutes to two hours. It will be memorable, and in a good way.

My fiancée and I loved our experience at Summerland Beach Rides! Our guide Ava was great. She was very friendly and made us feel at home, did a great job teaching us the basics, and told us fun facts about the houses along the ride. Our horses were very gentle and well-behaved. I felt totally comfortable and, having never ridden a horse before, that's saying a lot. The trail itself is very picturesque and of course riding on the beach is spectacular. It would make for any special occasion, or even for no reason at all! It seems like they take good care of their horses too. 

I have a few suggestions for the owner to make the experience even better:

1. Put up a sign at the ranch that says Summerland Beach Rides. The ranch is small and unassuming, and we were not sure if we were at the right place.

2. Invest in more helmets. Some of the current ones looked a bit busted and dirty, and I had difficulty finding a size that fit my medium-sized head.

Imagine yourself on horseback cantering down a beautiful deserted beach, the sun gently warming your skin, a light breeze in your hair, and the refreshing salty mist of the crashing waves. To your left is the expansive Pacific Ocean with the Channel Islands capping the horizon, to your right a shifting landscape of sandstone bluffs peppered with palms, agaves and yuccas, backed by the sharply rising mountains, all contrasting against the bright blue sky. This was my experience a couple of weeks ago with Summerland Beach Rides, and it could not have been better. 

I have lived in the area for a decade and had for years thought about looking into a beach ride but I wasn't prompted to do it until I had friends plan a visit -one of whom adores horses. My only regret is waiting so long! Our guide Charlie was entertaining, and also knowledgeable when it came to handling the horses. He may everyone feel comfortable, even our first time rider. 

I highly recommend Summerland Beach Rides! Great for an outing with visitors, a romantic sunset experience with your someone special, a treat for the kids, or just a fun way to mix up your day.
My girlfriend and I took a trip from LA for a ride. I planned it out with the owner, Charlie, to propose on the ride. It was such a great experience and I am so thankful for it.

I'm a horse person, I've grown up around them and been riding for 15 years, but I've never gone riding on the beach before. A friend and I went today and it was an experience for sure. 
Charlie is a character, depending on his mood, he came off rude a few times, but then would joke around with you 5 min later. 
The horses were fine, nothing spectacular, but again, given it's a trail riding business, I didn't expect much. Some were skinny and could use some weight, but nothing terrible. 
Because my friend and I were experienced, Charlie let us canter along the beach a few times to make the experience more enjoyable. We definitely had fun, the only iffy part was Charlie and how he came off a few times. I get it, horseback riding is dangerous and people don't always acknowledge that and it makes it worse that we had to ride across streets with traffic, but he went a little too far yelling a few times.

I like the experience and location beautiful scenery.
Unfortunately there was a few improvement things was missing and could be better:
We arrived at the place at time and we waited 30 minutes waiting.

We were me and wife and they added 3 ladies to us as well. Which make it big group and we couldn't hear the guide and when the persons ahead is not controlling their horses they effected us.

No one help or advice us on helmets sizes or put on.
They did not adjust the saddle perfectly with size of person.

If you have never ridden horseback on a pristine beach this is a must!  My wife and I, among a few close friends were thinking of an outdoor activity we haven't done together, so we chose to do a horseback ride in the late afternoon on beautiful Summerland Beach.  What an exhilarating experience!  Charlie, the owner and his right hand man Rudy were very helpful and selective about matching the horses with the right people based on a myriad of variables and personality types.  Once we started our ride, Charlie gave us interesting insights about the peripheral area as we descended down Padaro Lane into an equestrian arm that deposited us onto the beach.  It was breathtaking!  The glistening blue water coupled with sun kissed skies and warm ocean breezes made a ride on horseback very relaxing and extremely enjoyable.  The ride lasts approximately 90 minutes and it takes you through the upper canvas of avocado and citrus orchards before it concludes.  I would highly recommend this activity with a group of friends, close family members or a romantic date with your significant other.  Spectacular!

Wonderful place!!  Our daughter's dream was to ride a horse on the beach for her birthday, and Summer Beach Rides fulfilled that dream.  Paco took my husband, our daughter and me on the most beautiful ride.  Paco was fantastic...such an enjoyable and friendly guide.  Mina, Erica and Charlie gave our younger daughter the most amazing experience at the stable while we were on our ride.  They did so many fun things with her and were so sweet.  I spoke with Gabi a couple times to set everything up, and she was so informative and kind.  Thank you Summerland Beach Rides for such an incredible experience!  We hope to return this summer for lessons or camp!

My husband and I had such a wonderful experience. Our guide, Rudy, was very friendly and knowledgeable. Even though we both had no riding experience, we felt at ease. Rudy gave great instruction as needed. 
The ride itself was beautiful. Great views! 
Definitely coming back with our kids next time.

We had so much fun riding on the beach in Summerland!  Rudy was our guide, the horses were great & Madison & Brady helped to set us up!  It was low tide & a beautiful beach day  We would do it again & highly recommend Summerland beach rides!!!

Okay so I'm hoping this is the right horse riding site, and I hope if it isn't someone let's me know so I can direct this review to the right sight. My boyfriend and I had a horse back ride through the mountains and the ONLY thing that came close to the beautiful views, was the customer service offered by Erica. Not only was she knowledgeable, kind, loving and warm, she made sure to give us the best experience possible. She was informative and just lovely to be around. Honestly, she made the ride! I had a lovely time, the horses are calm and tame and SUPER WELL trained. I can't rate this experience highly enough. If you are looking to meditate and relax in nature with one of the earths most beautiful and awe inspiring animals, and an amazing group of staff... look no further. This is it!!!

fantastic experience!! I booked a two hour ride with my millennial kids that came out to visit me, home from college. It was so wonderful, we made such a wonderful memory for a lifetime. The views were spectacular, the horses were so gentle, yet really fun! The guide asked us if we wanted to trot, and we said yes a few times! Charlie and his wonderful team were so welcoming and kind and made it very simple to do something we started out as nervous about. This was an amazing experience and I plan to do this with friends that come to town, and more family in the future.

I've ridden horses my entire life but I've never had an experience quite like this. Walking the horses over the highway through the mountainous trails then down the beach was spectacular! Breathtaking and magnificent!!!  Paco led us through amazing trails lined with beautiful flowers... bucket list stuff here!!!

I have never gone horse back riding before and so as for a beginner I would totally recommend this place! The tour is about 1 1/2 hours and they go very slow which was great for me since I was a bit nervous. My horses name was Kota and he was slower than the other horse which made me feel more secure. The scenery is so beautiful! And the beach you go to is not crowded in fact we barely saw anyone there which makes for a very relaxing experience. There's a local coffee/bakery near by called The Red Kettle and their baked goods for breakfast were amazing! We did the morning ride and the weather was nice and cool even tho later in the day it definitely warmed up! Such a relaxing experience! I'd highly recommend! And our tour guide was very friendly and talkative, he is a local and was able to make suggestions on what we should do after. Great company!

My ride was scheduled at 8:30am. After driving 2 hours, i arrived 12 minutes early and received a poor greeting from an obnoxious seemingly "out of sorts" person who identified himself as the owner. The staff was awesome and continued to apologize for the owners behavior. After returning from the ride, the owner must have "regrouped" or something,. He then tried to overcompensate for his rude behavior. The group complained about him the whole ride. FIRST IMPRESSIONS DUDE. Note: people don't care that your ex-wife was a Black woman, but happy she got away from your obnoxious ass

My new FAVORITE place to go ride! My
Girlfriends and I had such a blast, the staff was so kind and they had hand sanitizer! I would highly recommend riding here if you want a beautiful view, beautiful horses, and all around great time!
Such a good ride. I love this place and the bikes are very inexpensive. Definitely recommend

There are no words to describe our experience touring the sea caves by kayak. My friend and I kept saying that it was definitely one of the best days of our lives. Our guide, Matt, was wonderful and so was the group we were with. We saw seals, dolphins, octopus, kelp forests, and simply being in nature surrounded by the ocean was unreal! Thank you Channel Islands Adventure Company!

We had a blast on the 3-hour kayaking tour at Santa Cruz Island with Channel Islands Adventure Co. A significant fraction of the national park is actually underwater, so you have to be in or near the water to experience it - you can't see it from the hike-to vistas.

Our guide, Hector, was incredibly knowledgeable and made the experience awesome. He took us through caves that challenged our rather elementary kayak skills and taught us about the island and the ocean life. His energy was infectious. Thanks for an awesome time, Hector!

- Milos and Carolyn

My husband and I went on the Adventure Tour with John in November 2018. We had a small group (5 kayakers). It was a GREAT trip. We were a little hesitant about ocean kayaking since neither of us have a ton of kayak experience- but it was totally fine! John adapted for the skill level of our group. The caves were incredible and the guide shared a lot of information about the SB Channel and the island itself. Super informative. We were able to take our time, never felt rushed, and the guide always made sure we were comfortable with the activities. Booking was super simple. Kim was very helpful answering lots of questions by email before our trip. It's nice that they have a little store on the island that we could stop by and get more info before our tour. Kayaks and all supplies- wetsuits, jackets, etc were clearly high quality and well taken care of. So happy we decided to go kayaking!!

We've just done the 3 hour adventure cave kayaking tour at Santa Cruz Island.  Everything about it was great.  The kayaking, the gear, the guides, the caves.  We ordered a packed lunch through their website, although it looked a little on the pricey side.  But it was worth every penny.  Delicious, and obviously made with high-quality ingredients.  And the trail mix was perfect for snacking on while we were kayaking.  After kayaking we had some time to go on a hike.  We took the 9.00am ferry out and returned on the 5.00pm.  The ferry journey was interesting because we stopped when we encountered dolphins and sea lions, and the captain gave us information about what we were seeing.  We're so glad we made the trip.  CIAC is a class act.

PERFECT TRIP!!! Went with 24 people on the sea cave adventure from Scorpion Anchorage on Santa Cruz Island.  At booking, they spent a LOT of time on the phone with me (Kim in particular) working on our group needs, gave me options for trips/costs/groups/etc., and advised me on how to book the ferry for a discount as well since we were a larger group (and the ferry booking people were fantastic as well). Explained everything in detail including what to bring to make our trip perfect based upon what I was asking for.  Everything was organized and set up at the mainland dock in the a.m.  Once on SC Island, Pat and two other lovely girls were our guides and they were perfect. They went at our pace.  Pat was in charge of my group and he was incredibly knowledgeable, paced us correctly on the water (and that is not easy given the variable skill levels of our group), and was not a clock watcher.  The others in my group expressed the same feelings about their guides. We are certain we stayed out longer (guide theory was "hey, it's longer on the water for us too" - they clearly love what they do) than what we paid for as we were eager for an amazing adventure. They were efficient, kind, easy to work with from initial booking to the goodbyes.  They give you everything you might need to make you comfortable with a huge smile.  The kayaking and the sea caves were breath-taking and the kelp forest and the ocean/wildlife were beautiful. This adventure company is the only one (and I did my research) that allows you to stay and explore the island (it is related to the island iteself as well) after the kayaking.  We brought our own ice-chests and enjoyed a feast afterwards. Then we snorkled, swam, and hiked for the last couple of hours to make the day full.  We left on the 4:30 p.m. ferry. Some of the deals you see on Groupon have you over on the ferry, on the kayak at a different island, and then back on the ferry with no additional time. (They identify that there is no "beach") so no place to hang.  Just be aware of that as I had to figure that out.  CIAC was happy to show/refer me to the other options/companies on other islands to confirm my understanding.  I agree with other reviews that you do have to have some  level of fitness -- you carry your own kayak to the staging area and, no surprise, there is quite a bit of paddling! :)  Can't believe we haven't done this before -- it's incredible and not even 90 minutes away from LA (no traffic).  BOOK EARLY!  We booked in April and went in August -- get the date you want!  Seriously the perfect trip.  Excellent company and excellent experience.

Definitely worth a day trip if you are in the area. Very knowledgeable and friendly guides took us kayaking through some shallow sea caves. Some experience is good so that you can see more. Finished the afternoon with a nice hike along the cliffs.

I was trying to introduce my friend to kayaking. I promised him a great time. It was not great. It was phenomenal ! From the beginning, John, our guide, was helpful and patient. Once he helped us gear up, we walked over to get our kayaks. He went over kayaking basics, launched each boat individually and made sure everyone was comfortable before paddling away. If you have never been in an ocean cave, you have never been kayaking. However, ocean conditions made some caves more challenging than others. 

Before entering each cave, John would enter each one to make sure it was safe. In some cases, he chose to not let us enter. In one situation entering a cave, a swell lifted my kayak, turned it sideways and I ended up in the water. My kayak was stuck. John paddled over, and calmly directed me what to do. No luck. It was stuck. John then helped me get my boat unstuck. As I swim backwards out of the cave with my kayak, another swell does the same to him and his kayak. We both finally get back on our kayaks and John is calm and smiling ! Talk about an adventure ! 

During the trip, four other people ended up in the water. In each situation, John reacted the exactly the same -- calmly and professionally. I cannot tell you enough what a professional John showed the entire group he truly is. On top of his professionalism, John was knowledgeable about most aspects of the island. Where he was not sure, he admitted it. That goes miles in my book. I have kayaked with other guides in California, Hawaii, Costa Rica, Canada and Mexico. I cannot say enough about John. Without question, John was the best ! Thank you John for a great adventure.

Our Venture Well Ventura Yoga group of 18 kayaked with Hector, Marc, and Rebecca. Entertaining and knowledgeable.....wanted us to have a great kayaking and cave experience. Thehy love their jobs. Some in our group had not kayaked before and the guides made sure they felt comfortable and safe. We saw many seals and sea lions and birds. The water is a beautiful blue in the caves.

Me & my husband had some much fun kayaking at Santa Cruz Island. Special shout-out to Pablo who always helped us cover-up when we lagged behind the group while kayaking. And, also Patt who gave us detailed information of every cave & took care that the cave was safe to kayak into. Patt & Pablo also helped us get back on the kayak, when we fell-off it & the kayak over-turned. 

It was really an awesome experience & Channel Islands Adventure Company is very caring!

I've only been kayaking a few times, never with a tour. So would it be bias if I say, BEST KAYAKING TOUR EVER?! because it TOTALLY WAS! went 08/13/18 and was told it was the best day this whole season! clear skies, clear calm water, everything was so beautiful!! arrived at the island and hikers and kayakers/snorkelers were split into two groups. we walked to a campsite, took about 10 minutes, where we were to store our backpacks and receive our PFDs and helmet. After we were all set and ready to leave, we had to make the walk back to the shore for kayak instructions and to carry our kayaks to the water. took about an hour from arriving to the island to actually sitting on kayaks in the water. the water was perfect. we went into many caves and our tour guide Chuck shared lots of information about the island and the animals. Chuck taught us how to kayak through the caves safely that even my 9 year old sister didnt have any problems! even though we technically took a long amount of time to get into the water, the tour was still 3 hours long. we got back later than scheduled, they definitely make sure you get your money's worth!

i definitely recommend this tour. without a tour guide you wouldnt know which caves are safe enough to go into or how to even navigate in the caves. the people were wonderful and if you have any questions they answer very quickly! i also suggest applying LOTS of waterproof sunscreen! any inch you miss, you will get burned!

We booked the Discovery Sea Tour since we did not have any ocean kayak experience and it was perfect! Spend About 1 1/2 hours in the water seeing caves and learning about kelp. Our guide Marc was funny, informative and helpful.  
Everything we needed was supplied which made it so easy. Would definitely do it again

Adventure Kayak Tour on Santa Cruz island:

This company is the exclusive provider of guided kayak tours through the sea caves around scorpion anchorage on Santa Cruz island in the channel islands national park.

After landing on the island (via Island Packers ferry service) we made our way about 1/4 mile down the canyon to where the company makes shop. There we were outfitted with safety equipment (helmet, personal flotation device) as well as optional dry bag and wind breaker. Then we made our way back to the beach and after a little kayak safety information, headed out into the water from the beach.  Our group of six individuals in 3 tandem kayaks were guided by Marc through around 6-8 caves over the course of 4 hours. 

Some caves can be dangerous to enter depending on the weather and the tide, so Marc was careful to inspect the conditions of each cave and instructed us on how to proceed through them. Some of them are very narrow, requiring you to lean back in your seat to squeeze under or rotating your paddle to squeeze through.

I have never experienced sea caves like these before, and while the tour isn't cheap, it was definitely worth it for a once in a lifetime experience.

Personal favorites were the "birth" tunnel and the green room, in the scorpion rocks. Marc also did a great job explaining some of the local biodiversity of kelp, fish, birds, and seals as we paddled along the coast.

I would recommend the Adventure Kayak tour to anyone who likes adventure and to whom the idea of visiting sea caves, both large and small entices them.

This is probably the worst adventure kayaking tour we've ever been on. The channel islands adventure company lacks communication and professionalism. DEFINITELY WOULD NOT RECOMMEND, but if you're looking for a rude/unorganized staff than this is the place for you. 

ONLINE SEARCH
When you look online they have a lot of wonderful pictures and testimonies that they showcase to get you interested. It is no question that this tactic works. It's the reason we decided to try it out, but the tour is nothing like what you see online. For starters the island packers ferry that the adventure co. recommends you use is just as unprofessional.

ARRIVAL
Upon arriving the check in staff did not acknowledge us standing in line to check in for 5 minutes. The on boat crew was rude and abrupt. Definitely not engaging nor interested in making an experience for their guests. They weren't clear on how long the ride would be either. What we thought was a one hour ride was almost 1.5 hours long. When we got off of the boat a staff member ripped my Go Pro out of my hand and broke my strap around my wrist. Extremely unprofessional especially if you know how much the equipment costs. 

THE TOUR ITSELF
When we arrived at the island we were taken to a campground further from the shore. Our tour guides were Ben and Caitlyn. While Ben seemed to be nice the tour wasn't as engaging or fun/ light spirited as we thought it would be. Caitlyn rushed us to get ready and leave the campground constantly asking things of us and not giving us time to put on sunscreen and suit up. If you're a tour guide and you say we have time to get ready than stick to your word. The tour itself was more physical than it was fun. Like I mentioned previously, the online version of the tour is much better than the tour itself which says enough. I also feel like we didn't see that much or have enough time to enjoy the caves/ experience it all. It felt rushed and unprofessional.

THE RIDE BACK 
Online the description mentions that the tour ends at 1 and we leave the island at 3:00 p.m. Naturally we expected to board a ferry fairly soon after our tour since the tour was late and ended at 1:45 p.m. However, our tour guide said that a boat wouldn't arrive until 4:30 p.m. and that there was no way around that so we were on the island for an additional 2.5 hours than expected when we had plans back on the mainland that we could have been doing with that time. So the boat finally came and the staff was again rude and unprofessional. 
WE WOULD NOT RECOMMEND THIS COMPANY, especially because we've done plenty of tours and have never experienced one this bad.

Our kayak tour of Santa Cruz island was the best part of our California vacation.  We met our guide Ben as soon as we checked in.  Check-in was super easy.  The ferry ride out was chilly, but otherwise pleasant and smooth, and we even stopped to see some seals.  The sun came out as soon as we made it to Santa Cruz and the weather was perfect.  Once on island the guides led us the campground, helped us suit up, and we hit the water!  We spent the next few hours popping in and out of caves - really unlike anything I've done before.  Ben was a fantastic guide and we felt very safe with him, and he answered all of our questions about the history and ecology of the island.  After the kayaking was over we still had a few hours to hike before the ferry ride back.  

Although we had very little flatwater kayaking experience we did fine on this trip.  I definitely would recommend a day trip to the Channel Islands, and be sure you do the kayaking!!

Took my 9 year old on the discovery sea cave adventure from Scorpion Anchorage on Santa Cruz Island.  We left from Ventura Harbor on Island Packers Ferry to cross the channel.  The Channel Island Adventure Company is a well run division of the Santa Barbara Adventure Company that leads all the guided kayak tours on the islands.  Our guide Kyle was great.  He made the adventure safe and efficient while making it fun adventure.  The kelp forests are amazing and teaming with life.  The whole process from booking to return was well executed and smoothly guided.  These guys have all the knowledge and great gear to successfully lead you and a great day on the water.

We were concerned about the weather because there was a high chance of rain. We stopped by the day before after setting up camp to discuss what we could expect, and our guide Jerry was there to talk to us. I didn't feel thrilled about going out in the water in the rain and Jerry addressed this by assuring us that if we feel unsafe or uncomfortable about kayaking, we don't have to. That, to me, was a sign of good customer service. Fortunately, it did not rain the next day and we were good to go! 

Jerry continued to prove to be an all around outstanding guide. He was very knowledgeable and an excellent storyteller as we explored the caves. He demonstrated a great expertise in kayaking, always ensuring safety while making it fun. And finally, he added a wonderful personal touch by having the group sing happy birthday to my partner in one of the caves. 

Thank you Jerry and Adventure Company for a memorable trip. I can't wait to book another.

Ignore Emily G.'s review; she sounds like a vindictive wench. These people are the kindest and most adaptable variety. I accidentally booked the wrong day and they waived the rescheduling fee, then when we finally arrived they were both communicative and adaptable. What's morem the kayaks were actually comfortable to sit in (at least for me). Cannot recommend them enough. Oh and the sea caves were incredible.

The tour was nice and our guide was outstanding. Many extra points for how positive and energetic the guide was the whole time! One person in our group was unable to engage in the activity, and the guide couldn't have handled it better. Not only for the individual, but also for the remainder of the group.

The whole situation with the individual made me think that the company should do a better job screening its customers. When we signed up for the activity there was no questions in regards to our physical ability to join the group. This is a physical activity after all. Quite frankly, it didn't look like the individual could complete the activity from beginning.

We bought a 1 adult, 2 children Kayak Tour in Nov 2018. Total cost: $427 + separately $59/a and $46/c for the ferry tickets to get to the meeting point where the tour departs.

This trip was confirmed while we were on the road, booked one day prior to our arrival.
We made several phone calls to the hotline and asked questions about the itinerary.  Due to the high cost, we were on the fans to proceed. After calling to get as much details as we could over 2 different days and speaking with employees on the phone, we were led to believe we would not be able to see the painted caves unless we go with a tour like them or unless
1. we are guides ourselves who know the area well or 
2. we are very experienced class 4 white water rafters. 

We are competent kayakers who have kayaked in different parts of the world, but we're neither the above so reasons they gave us tipped our decision to buy the tour.
 
Although we lucked out on the nice weather and the guide was good, we were extremely disappointed because

1. we did not see the Painted Caves 
2. we did not spend 4 hrs kayaking 
3. Poor logistical setup and management

The ferry arrived on time and between waiting for our group of kayakers to gather and walking inland toward the Basecamp where equipment were, it took almost 20mins. I began to worry as I vaguely recalled the confirmation email indicated 10.30am tour begin, which is 15-30 mins after the ferry arrived.  We had not even suited up yet.

There was a meeting to split up the big group then an equipment briefing by our assigned guide.

After spending time suiting up, locking our stuff, we walked back towards the coast where the kayaks are. A 2nd meeting followed with an intro of everyone, safety briefing and kayak operations.  

No designated launch point/ ramp. 
The beach could be seen from the storage point but it's awkward and quite a distance to walk the kayaks over the soft sandy and rocky beach (and carry back after tour ends). I consider myself fit but there's no way I could carry the heavy double kayak by myself and with someone helping, I needed to pause multiple times which was when the guide and a kind customer offered to take over. Since this is a commercial outfitter, I would expect the business to have a roller cart to assist in the transport to minimize time and improve efficiency.

By the time we hit water, it was past noon.
After another round of water drills, we finally started the tour. Actual time spent on visiting the caves was less than 2 hrs.  

We spent a huge chunk of time running through briefings and logistics. We were grouped in a team with level "3" experience which is above average, meaning no one was inexperienced. I'd done a lot of kayaking and this was the longest ever prep time to get in the water - especially for only a small group of only 7. (I was in a kayak expedition with 40+, our prep time totaled less than 20mins). If it was summer, I would have melted in my wetsuits and PFD.
  
The problem:
I understand the importance of safety briefings but the Basecamp was such a long walk away which was a complete waste of precious time that could be spent on water. Note the walk is not far, it is just out of the way of the coastal activities and wasted relative to the limited time we had.  It does not matter if you are able-bodied military guys - as there is no way to cut down those time with the distance between the 4 points: ferry drop off, Basecamp, Kayak storage, launch point. Perhaps they had limitations by the Island regulations where to build their sheds but when we bought the tour, we were led to believe it was a 4-hour tour on water, not hour plus of walking around from point to point plus briefings. 

Essentially, I felt we paid $427 for a 2-hr guided tour.  There had been no breakdown of the itinerary on how the whole "trip" will be like. I felt short changed.

Half way through, we learnt from our guide that we weren't going to see the painted caves.I was shocked. My first reaction was perhaps weather conditions around those caves or the experience level of our group is a limiting factor. Then, he explained Painted caves are on the other far end of the island and only conducted during summer. I was crushed and now felt totally cheated.During the tour, another family shared with me they were also surprised when the guide mentioned we weren't going to the painted caves.

The caves the guide showed us were nice but we bought the tour to see the painted caves.  Yes, I wish we had more time to research and read every fine print on their website on my phone but we'd based our decision on the information given to us by the reps who knew we wanted to see the painted caves.  

The manager, Kim, followed up insisted we had great weather and saw nice caves. She missed the point. Had we known what we were going to get, I would not have paid $578 for a 2-hour kayak tour. We were misled and they were not picking up the responsibility of false and inaccurate information relayed.

Amazing friendly tour guides and office people 
Amazing caves. 

I had accidentally booked kayak tour for a Saturday for 3 people and the ferry for Sunday. The whole booking process is confusing to begin with. And the adventure website is not very user friendly. When they called me on Saturday and asked where I was I stated that I wasn't leaving until Sunday. The girl on the phone said maybe I would be able to get on the waiting list. 
What I didn't appreciate was that when explaining to me that I'd have to be on a waiting list for Sunday it wasn't until the very latest second when we ended the conversation that she said that we might have to repay for the entire experience because we didn't call ahead to cancel. 
If we were aware of the mix up we would've obviously called ahead to fix it. But we weren't so we didn't.

When we arrived they said we would be able to get on the tour that there was enough space for us (only 2 of our party of 3 was able to make it due to food poisoning) and AGAIN not until the very late second when we were about to get all our gear they brought up that we would have to repurchase the experience because the one we had booked yesterday couldn't have been sold to other people. If this is a company practice - it sucks. 

The frustrating thing was that if today's kayaking had been full we wouldn't have been able to get on anyways.

It was rather irritating to hear "you didn't call ahead to cancel" when obviously in our scenarios we weren't in a position to call ahead. Who plans for food poisoning? Sorry it didn't fall in-line with the policy? 
As irritating as it was I didn't want to be rude to the people behind the counter/over the phone though because clearly it's a line they have to say fed from whoever is higher up. 

Either way had we not mixed up the dates they still would've had a couple of unsold tickets on Sunday. Because if Sunday was full they wouldn't have let us join. 


So no beef against the tour guides or the views they did a wonderful job. This review is not against them at all. I'd give them 5 stars. 10 stars. 
But we have to pay for 5 tickets for 2 people to go on 1 kayaking trip and it ended up costing just under $700. Absolutely ridiculous.

We had an excellent experience on our Discovery Kayak tour with John as our guide. He was very knowledgeable and safe. Our tour was 1.5 hours and because the conditions were good we had a little extra time and were able to paddle through 5 sea caves. After the tour we had enough time to picnic and go for a short hike. Amazing day out and I highly recommend this adventure!

We booked our kayak tour separately from our Island Packers boat ride to the island but you can make it easier and do it through this adventure company.  From checking in to the boat ride home this trip was awesome, I was a bit anxious bc it seemed like a lot of moving parts but everyone helped us get where we needed to be and make this a very memorable experience for my husband, me and the kids.  
The boat ride to Santa Cruz is about 1 to 1.5 hours one way depending on weather and also how many whales, dolphins or other marine life we encounter.  Bring your own water, lunch and snacks for a full day... we didn't go camping on this trip but we are seriously wanting to book another trip that allows us to stay on the island overnight.  Also there are so many hiking trails, short and long options!!
The kayak tour was the best part of our trip and our guide Caco was so knowledgeable, fun and just awesome.  Ocean kayaking originally made me nervous but it was a lot easier than I thought, we travelled over kelp forrests , paddled inside caves, saw bald eagles and its babies, other birds and harbor seals.  
Next time we'll add snorkeling to the adventure but we played in the beach and took so many amazing pictures. 
Truly recommend this company

This was a really special tour. We did the adventure kayak tour that was about 3 hours of kayaking. We explored the sea caves which was such a great experience. Our guide was Marc and he was so knowledgeable about the islands. He prioritized our safety but also wanted to push us out of our comfort zone. The tour group was also small so it felt like a very personal tour and we got to know the other people in our party. We also saw dolphins, seals, and even one of the endemic island foxes! Overall, would 100% recommend this tour and company.

This company offers an amazing kayaking experience.  If you want to explore sea caves off the shore of Santa Cruz Island (Channel Islands national park) - this is the way to do it! Such great service all around - from the quick call back to confirm details of my ferry when I just booked online to the friendly chat with several of the guides before and after the tour. Our guide Ben was welcoming and encouraging. I had a great time and got to check out caves I most certainly wouldn't go into on my own. This kayaking adventure was definitely a highlight of my time at Channel Islands National Park!

Our family group (young adults and a few oldsters) had a great time spending mid-morning to noon on 8/22/2018 with our guides Elise and Sarah. They bring a real joy to what they do, and led us into, out of, and through the caves on Santa Cruz Island with just enough instruction and more than enough encouragement. Very knowledgeable about the history, geology and wildlife of the Channel Islands, and fit that into a memorable "tour." Will definitely do this again in a few years, and would recommend this activity to anyone who wants to see AND experience the real beauty of the Channel Islands.

An absolutely fantastic and unforgettable 4-hour kayaking tour. We couldn't have asked for a better guide than Sean. The ferry ride was also an adventure in itself! Definitely give this place a go if you haven't!. If you feel hesitant to go, just know there are people of all ages and the guide caters to you. If you feel halfway through the tour you've had enough, you can definitely stop halfway!

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pjll1GZhBM0

We decided on a whim to inquire about kayak trip availability while on a one-night camping trip. Kyle kindly stopped cleaning up to talk us through the tour option and to book us after hours for the next day. We did not regret it! What an incredible way to see the park! The trip is a nice blend of paddling, exploring caves and floating. We even got to get out on a beach to explore a cave by foot. Kyle was professional, personable and very knowledgeable about the area. He was very intentional about safety and an all around tremendous guide. Great day on the water!
Very well run organization.  Easy booking and check in.  Extremely knowledgeable and friendly guides who ensure your adventure is amazing.   Highly recommended.

Had a great half day of kayaking with Channel Islands Adventure Company!! Our guide, Ben, was super fun and informative and the company was super well organized. I highly recommend booking a trip with them any day - really made my labour day weekend a thousand times better!

I've been on plenty of guided tours and my kayak excursion with Channel Islands Adventure Company ranks among the best! I received clear communication beforehand, and connecting with our guide once on the island was a piece of cake. Our guide, Chuck, had almost 20 years of experience guiding on the island, and it showed. His paddling instruction was easy to follow, as were his instructions once we hit the water. I felt comfortable with everything we did, even with very little ocean kayaking experience. Plus, it was fun to learn more about the flora, fauna, geology, and history of the island (and its waters), plus hear a few awesome stories of Chuck's own paddling in the area, which made the experience even more rich. I came away wanting to immediately book another trip - the sign of a fun day, and a grade A operation :)

I elected to do the Prisoners Harbor Kayaking Tour after coming down to vacation in the Santa Barbara area on a whim. I was a little nervous as I hadn't done a ton of research before buying my ticket nor had I kayaked for. But the instructions of what to bring and what the likely conditions, as well as a call into the company beforehand, helped allay my concerns. Check-in was easy and the ferry ride was great, with our captain Luke stopping on several occasions so we could observe groups of dolphins, sea lions and even a couple of blue whales!

Once getting to Santa Cruz Island, we were given a quick but informative orientation and were out on the water after being fitted for our protective gear. While I found kayaking challenging (this was my first time), my guide Sarah was patient and gave great energy. She also was super informative as to the history and ecology of the island. We also got to see a few harbor seals and went on a short hike with some amazing views. Money well spent!

I am amending my review to 3 star because the company's director responded to my complaint in a letter. I am glad someone in the company acknowledged our poor experience and apologized. 

Channel Island Adventure Company's initial poor customer service response to our episode compounded our dissatisfaction but the director turned it around and make things right. He made me feel that they do value their customers and it matters to them more than just running a profitable business.
