They also noted my comments and agree to work on improving things I pointed out. I like to add, after looking at their website that has a page listing differences between the caves: it will be more helpful to have a table instead of a long essay for the comparison.

The extra star is for Pat, our guide whom we were glad to have as he was friendly with my kids. 

I now learned that the Painted Cave trip does not involve landing so no walking around to launch kayaks. We hope to give them another try in the near future (to see the actual Painted Cave) and hopefully have a better experience. I will update my review at that point.

Great day exploring the coastal waters and caves of Santa Cruz Island. Perfect weather conditions no wet suit needed! They give us great information to prepare us for the day's event! Packed our lunch with a bag of wine! Perfect ending to a perfect day! Thanks CIA
Pablo was on his game, he almost had a three person rescue, luckily it turned out to be a single rescue! All is well nothing was lost!
Thanks 4 a great adventure!!

My fiancé and I wanted to visit Channel Islands National park while on vacation in neighboring town Santa Paula. I thought kayaking would be a great and unique way to view a National park! The reviews are all true! The park is amazing but the kayaking and crew really made it even more special! Although Dustin was our guide for the Discovery tour and was extra awesome, so was the rest of the crew! Everyone was kind and had a great attitude that made me safe but also that I was going to enjoy an unforgettable, exciting experience! And it sure was. Our group was able to see 4 caves and I loved going over the kelp forest. We even tried a fresh piece of kelp which I actually liked! Overall amazing experience and hope to go back for snorkeling/longer tour next time!

Great experience with an awesome crew of people.  The people at Channel Islands Adventure Company were organized, thoughtful, informative and fun.  Reservations were easy, they reminded us what gear they recommended us to take with us - check in was a breeze when we got there.  We also took their recommendation to order lunches from their partner (great food!).  Once we got to the island the fun began!  Once our guides Pat and Marc made sure everyone in our group had the equipment needed we set out for our kayak / sea cave tour.  These guys were fun and very knowledgeable - they knew a lot about the area we were in and you can tell they were very experienced in open water kayaking.   Super helpful with those that needed it and patient while keeping the whole group engaged.  Some of our group did a hike after wards and others relaxed in the picnic area.    I am a Santa Barbara local - and we often miss some of the things that are closest to us.  I highly recommend this to locals and visitors.  It's fun for all ages but I do suggest that all parties in your group have at least a moderate level of fitness.  You will need to carry your own gear from the boat to the staging area, help get your kayak to the shore and back as well as paddle.  Plan to be hungry after!  It is so worth it!

In my opinion, the Adventure Sea Cave Kayak Tour is the best thing you can do when visiting Santa Cruz Island (and probably the best part of the Channel Islands in general, but we didn't visit the other islands). 

It was incredibly foggy the day we visited which was unfortunate. The good news is that this didn't really affect kayaking through the sea caves at all. 

We kayaked through 10 or so caves. It's definitely the coolest kayaking we've ever done. Our guide was great too. They take tip through Venmo, so don't worry if you don't have cash. 

It's $160 per person, so it's definitely not cheap, but it was worth the price. With the fog, we definitely wouldn't have seen anything had we not done this tour. 

One thing to be aware of with ocean kayaking is that you might get motion sick if you're prone to it. I took Dramamine before the ferry ride and I still started getting sick about halfway through the tour (especially when we weren't moving).

The tour was a little long for my preference. It's 3 hours which is a little longer than I usually like to kayak. At about 1.5-2 hours kayaks start getting really uncomfortable, but there was a lot of good stuff to see so we kept going. 

My biggest complaint is that they make you carry your own kayak down to the beach and back up over tons of rocks. They're incredibly heavy and this is no easy task. With how expensive the tour is, they should do this for you. We've done a lot of kayak tours and this is the first one that made us lug kayaks around. 

All that being said, the tour was amazing, the guide was great, and I highly recommend doing it!

We could not have asked for a better experience! We signed up for a snorkeling tour, but since we had a smaller group were able to kayak to a better location before we jumped in the water. It was so nice to be able learn about the local environment from our guide prior to getting in the water as it was a little harder to communicate once we had all of our snorkeling gear on. Our guide Elise was fantastic - she answered all of our questions very well and dove down to the bottom a few times to show us Sea Cucumbers and Starfish that we couldn't see as well from the surface. We saw a ton of fish and a whole army of lobsters when we snorkeled through one of the Channel Islands' famed sea caves. Our tour was on a colder day in early June, and even with the brisk temperatures we stayed warm throughout the tour due to their heavy duty wet-suits.
I would highly recommend this tour to anyone interested in marine wildlife!

Matt B. and I are aspiring dog owners and as a general rule enjoy dog-watching more than people-watching.  One sunny afternoon, Matt took me to this awesome dog park to indulge in this little hobby.

We entered from a residential street, and at first I was unimpressed with the canine presence.  What I didn't realize was that the preserve stretched far beyond what my eyes could see from the entrance.  We walked all the way to the end and back, and saw not only plenty of awesome dogs, but also beautiful views of beaches and ocean and tons of lush greenery in the preserve itself.

Matt mentioned that the preserve was at one point bought by a development company and that many locals went through great lengths to collect the money to buy it back and maintain its natural state.  The largest single donor was apparently Michael Douglas, hence the name.  I'm not sure that alone is enough to make me watch Money Never Sleeps, but still, kudos Sir Douglas.

Brought a dog with and it was awesome.  May have seen poison oak towards the front, so watch it.  Pretty much has everything you could look for in a hike. some hills, some sand, and some beach views.  Also, hendrys is right near by, and you can cruise through hope ranch if you go left when you leave.

What an incredible place. A huge preserve of land on a bluff overlooking the beautiful Pacific and the Channel Islands. The place is dog friendly. Due to the drought it is a bit dusty, however that will all green up when the rains come, soon I hope. The views off the cliff are jaw dropping spectacular and unbelievable all at once. I love looking down from the cliff and seeing the turquoise green water with great clarity. Now there are ways to get down to the beach but I didn't investigate those I had a short time, and just got to hang on the cliff, which is mesmerizing for me. I guess I could have been there a long time and never known it. I'll be back to find this one again and just roll in the luscious view . A real winner for me.

This reserve is one of my faaaavorite spots to visit in Santa Barbara. It is so relaxing and has amazing views. There's a few different trails to enjoy...wander where you feel led. Parking is on the street. The walk can get dusty, so note that when choosing which shoes or car to drive. Also, like anywhere in Santa Barbara, a light jacket wouldn't hurt either. Dogs are welcome, and there are designated areas that indicate where they can be left off the leash. 

Between the views, eucalyptus trees/scent, random birds, and negative ions, it is really difficult to not enjoy this spot and see how real God truly is. It's a great place to relax and reflect or even connect with a few like-minded hikers and enjoy the breeze. Great place to take an out of town guest. Plenty of nice photos can be taken here. 

I look forward to visiting again. Check it out!

It is gorgeous and peaceful here. Just keep in mind it is a dog park, and if you sit for any length of time I've found dog owners have no problem letting their dogs invade your space and run all over your things. If people talked a little more quietly and kept track of their dogs it would be the perfect place. Other reviews say it's usually not crowded so maybe I've just come at the busiest times.

What an incredible gift for Santa Barbara, thanks to Kirk Douglas and family.  Few tourists know of it- well worth finding for the best dog park anywhere with breathtaking ocean front views and para gliders launching and landing.
It's so pretty here and dogs can run around.  Santa Barbara at its best.

TRULY a hidden gem for you and your doggie!

I am so in awe of this place. Quite an amazing idea for preservation. The Douglas Family created this space for everyone including their four legged pals to enjoy!  Locals and tourists are walking the trails with their dogs (and in most parts off the leash!) taking it all in with a gorgeous view of the ocean and the Santa Barbara coast. My fam and our precious pooch had a great time. The dogs love to run, sniff and explore! My dog had so much fun, she slept the entire way home back to oc. LOL 

This place is awesome and definitely a spot to stop and see.

Travel TIP:  If you are traveling with your dog here, poop baggies are provided - so be sure to clean up after them and bring your pup some extra water just in case. 

Overall: Beautiful. Its worth the adventure and fun for you and your dog :-)

If you're a temporary transplant, like myself, you cannot leave this city without coming here. It's amazing. The views will compete with *Love* for your soul's affection.  

You can bring your dog and let him/her off the leash; also, there are light  trails for mini explorations...that is, once you hike that hill to the top.  It's a small hill, bout a 5 minute hike--the length of a song on your iPod.   It's kinda a b-word if you're rusty, but trust me--so worth it.
Nice park to experience the mesa and the ocean and a bit of naturalness in Santa Barbara! go for a walk with friends. Or just stare off by yourself and figure out all that vexes you.
I've gotten really into sunset watching in Santa Barbara, because well duh it's sb how can you not !!! All the places I've been to, I have to say this is the best view for watching the sunset !!!
Loved this location.  My GPS found the location.  I found free parking in the neighborhood and my dog loved playing with the other dogs.  The view is amazing.  Enjoy a nice morning with your puppy!

If you hate dogs then I don't recommend it, but I love dogs and find "The Wilcox Property" (as it's locally known) simply amazing. The sunset views are some of the best in the area and the dirt paths wind through the area with a natural grace you won't find in a highly manicured park.

There are multiple entrances to the park, on at the corner of Medcliff rd & selrose ln, another at the end of Mesa School Lane, and yet another at the intersection of Las Positas and Cliff Dr.

I love to come here to meet my little brother for some quality family. I bring burritos and he brings his dog. We walk for half an hour and enjoy catching up. This place always has other walkers with their dogs but everyone is well behaved. You can look out in the Channel Islands or down below to Hendrys beach to check out the surf/visibility. A perfect spot to spend your lunch hour.
Great place to walk the dog off leash largest park you'll find that's off leash in Santa Barbara overlooks the ocean beautiful scenery I must visit for any dog owner

Hands down the best off leash dog park in all of California. Breathtaking views from a cliff that overlooks the Pacific. Happy dogs run about & play. Nice flat loop of a trail to walk with your two legged and four legged friends. Bring poop bags & be considerate and clean up your dog's mess. This place is a gem.  Have been going here for many, many years.

After reading all of the great reviews for this place here on Yelp, I knew I had to make the drive over here during my stay in Santa Barbara. 

People primarily bring their dogs here and there are areas where dogs can be let off the leash. It was nice to see all the cute dogs interacting with each other and having fun. It made me miss my furry friends back home. 

My hubby and I just walked a short way down the trail. We found a slope with a broken down tree. We just sat there for awhile and watched the ocean. It was really peaceful. This is a beautiful place with spectacular ocean views.

This is a great area to walk with beautiful views of the ocean. It is primarily untouched so in the dry seasons expect some dust from the dirt paths. It's located in a residential area so find parking along the street. Also, there is no fencing on the cliff edge. 

I heard that this area was slated to be developed as a subdivision but UCSB alumnus Michael Douglas bought it to save the land for public use, and that is why some foundations exist in certain areas. I'm not sure if that is true though. All I know is that it's a beautiful walk at sunset.

Off leash dogs through-out entire park.  

The city has posted signs that waives their liability in case of unwanted contact with dogs or their owners in anyway; see photo.   

The park is a giant dirt pit.  There are endless holes from digging dogs.  I always find several plastic bags full of dog feces littered about.  

To utilize a natural resource like this for dogs is absurd.  This habitat needs protection.  

Thanks

A good friend of ours drove us to this Preserve. First and foremost, I was so moved by the Douglas Family to have made the effort to make this gem of nature available to the public. The view of the ocean and the sunset were just amazing. We were a bit surprised by the dogs running around freely but they were all friendly.  We were filled with gratitude and joy.  Thank you the Douglas Family and our amazing friend to take us here.

I love bringing my puppy here.  The people and dogs we have come across are all very friendly, the park is clean, and there are beautiful views of the surrounding beaches from the top of the cliffs. Definitely a favorite off-leash dog spot.
LOVE this place.  I walk my dog there at least three times a week.  Just stunning.  Perfect alternative to a beach walk and there are always plenty of dogs around.
An amazing place for a morning walk with your pup! Fresh ocean breeze right a long the cliff, I will never enjoy a dog park ever again as much as I enjoyed this one!

Solid off leash dog park (trail) with breathtaking ocean views. The trail itself itself too long - you can walk the loop in about 30 minutes (or less) but the path along the water is really something special.  It's located in a residential area and parking isn't too hard.

I'm lucky because my grandmother lives right around the corner from this amazing place. As a family on Easter we went for a walk, took in the fresh air and smiled at all the dogs. The ocean views at the bluffs are amazing and remind me of the Coronado Butterfly Preserve also in Santa Barbara. If you haven't been there yet and love this place I highly recommend it. It took a lot of effort to keep this wonderful place so please take good care of it and pick up after your pups!

Beautiful place to hike in Santa Barbara. 
Lots of dog walkers hiking here. 
The view is really great. 
Parking lot was kinda big, crowded to the beach side.
Recommend to everyone!
Always enjoy walking my dogs off-leash through this beautiful area!

Douglas Family Preserve is a great place to take a stroll with the entire family (of course that includes the four legged furry members too!).  The preserve is dog friendly.  Most of the park allows dogs off-leash and there are poop bag dispensers/water stations all over. The view is breath taking. The trail overlooks the ocean and the preserve is a great location for family photos.

Fabulous views of the ocean.  You can stay there and look for hours.  Amazingly serene.

It's a little hard to find the entry point, but it's worth the effort.
My favorite place in all of Santa Barbara ... I have not one complaint! I visit this place at least 4 days a week, highlight of my days!

My boyfriend lives a block away from this preserve...Although I still call it Wilcox, the original name from many moons ago!  I would consider the terrain flat for not being paved, but has many minitrails to go all around the lot.  The bluff is amazing, they did have a California commercial done there after all (you know, come to California?!)! Kobe (my dog) loves running around with his dog friends without having to feel like you are somehow being a delinquent.  There are poop bags for those who forgot some at home, so there are no excuses to pick up after your K9.  Beautiful at all hours of the day, easy access, clean, and friendly.

Unbelievably beautiful and so amazing that they kept this area for nature, people and their dogs. The views are incredible and the paths are great for dogs of all sizes. They even have a water station for the dogs. It is so nice to have places like this available. We will definitely be back when in the area!

What a treasure find!!!
Walking path with off leash doggy privilege.  
Sparking Water View on one side, Beautiful Lush Mountain Ridges on the other.
So many photo spots!
Parking will be tricky.  Street parking in residential area.  But well worth the few loops around the blocks.

What a beautiful park.  Thank you Yelp for leading me there!  Had a lovely afternoon with my dog walking around the preserve and walking down to the dog beach below, which was hands down the nicest dog beach we've been to.  A must visit if you've got a dog or just love dogs.

Visited here with our dog today and I think we liked even more then Henry's Beach. The views were spectacular and the area is very serene. Our puppy had so much fun romping through the tall grass and passed out in the car on the way home. It was well taken care of for a park that allows dogs.

Douglas Family Preserve is a piece of preserved land opened to the public during sunlight hours, a true gem in Santa Barbara.  The landscape is beautiful and serene with a grove of Eucalyptus trees and dirt walking path approximately a mile long.   Located on a cliff with the ocean below (no beach access), you will find some beautiful stunning views overlooking the ocean.  You won't be disappointed with a visit to this unique piece of real estate in Santa Barbara.  

Wear closed toed shoes if possible due to the dirt path, also no restrooms are available here to my knowledge.  This is a popular spot for local joggers, walkers, and especially dog owners to walk their dogs,  If you have small children, keep a close eye on them as there are no railings at the cliff's edge but the path is definitely far enough away from the edge of the cliff where it is not dangerous at all. Parking is located on residential streets adjacent to entrance.

Very nice walking area. Its not a difficult walk at all. It over looks the ocean and houses. Its wide open so kids and dogs can run around. I was recommended to stopping here while i was looking for a nice hiking or walking trial.  I will definitely come back. 
Its a beautiful view.

Marvelous.
And my dog seems to like the place. 
His first glimpse of the ocean.

He seemed confused at first, but he got the flow of it. 
Perfect in the AM. 
As of late the brisk temps have been a great wake up.

Everyone around was nice, cordial and their dogs were well behaved. I've been to other dog parks in other cities, and I always think I'm about to be asked to place a bet and who do I got? 
As there are some real growlers out there.

This is my favorite off leash walk with my well  behaved and friendly dogs. I have had problems with people bringing their aggressive dogs and turning hem loose. Some people seem to think off leash is for every dog. My dogs have been the victim of unprovoked attacks on two occasions. I now carry protection with me. I would not let small children run around here.
Beautiful, mellow, mystical little preserve.

came here with my friends and we absolutely love it!! It's so pretty and the view is GORGEOUS. plus, we are all dog lovers and its sooo cute and fun to see dogs interact and explore! There's alot of people that go and its a nice place to take a walk or hang out with your pets! The people are friendly and so are the dogs! parking is in a residential area but its easy to find parking and the view is just amazing! We saw the sunset and everything. you can walk through this in an hour. I'd come back again and this makes me want to get a dog now! so cute!! gorgeous place with a great view of the sunset!
Amazing piece of property!  Gorgeous cliff views of the ocean & divine off leash spot for our doodle to run & play ball!  What a gem!
What a beautiful place to hike! That's great if you have a dog and want to take him or her off the leash. The views are breathtaking.

Douglas Family Preserve is a wonderful place to relax, enjoy the scenery and bring your dog. My boyfriend and i came here last week .....everything was truly amazing about the park. I was able to have my dog off the leash and didn't have to worry about other dogs because the other dogs there were well trained as well.
 Out of the whole experience the only thing I can complain about is irresponsible dog owners. I noticed consinstly that multiple dog owners choose to not pick up after their dog or if they did take the first step, they would choose to leave the doggie bag on the trail. Why, WHY would you have a dog and think it is okay to not clean up after them. People shouldn't be so lazy and irresponsible like this...first tip if you decide to bring your dog: clean up after them. Dog bags are provided on the right side when you first enter the park.

Awesome place to take your dog for a little stroll off-leash.  Love coming here around sunset.  Only drawback is parking since its in a primarily residential neighborhood, but we never really have a problem finding a spot.
A five star park for Santa Barbara Mesa! Wonderful views, dogs, and people! Not only for locals, visit next time you want a beach view walk without sand in your shoes!
Went on a Wednesday morning and there was plenty of parking. Easy trail to walk/jog/run that provides beautiful views. Dog friendly. This is a must if visiting Santa Barbara.

Bring your tennis shoes! Lots of soft floor, places, walk paths & roads to hike. Lots of dogs off leash and also uncleaned up poops. No places to sit & enjoy view, a foldable Seat would of been nice.  Not cleanest still yet nice.

Absolutely gorgeous, but I can't comprehend the "fenceless bluffs" coupled with the "leashless dogs" equation. Hazardous, no? Just like you would never allow your children to wander around there with wreckless abandon, I can't imagine allowing my dog to roam free there. The potential danger makes my stomach churn. What's to stop them from flying right off the cliff....in an attempt to chase a wayward ball or bird? In a world where we can't even open windows in hotel rooms, I'm surprised that there aren't more safety measures here.

My personal fears aside, the scenery is beautiful. Dogs seem happy. My doggie will stay on a leash until we get to the beach below.

This was one of the best spots I have found in SB so far. Stumbled upon it by accident. It has a terrific view along most of the path and has an area where dogs can go without a leash. Basically everyone there has a dog with them. It is a very well maintained treasure. When you get to one of the edges of the path you over look Hendry's Beach.

My son used to live down the street from this place, beautiful!  Love the beach, the trails and the creek restoration.  Of course I didn't forget my camera, makes a good webpage for later  http://www.caopenspace.org/douglaspreserve.html
If I still had my dog, I'm sure she would love it there.  Great dog friendly beach and preserve--there is even a dog wash station there, which is kind of cool :)
Live nearby and this is a great sunset walk.  Ocean views, whales and dolphins.  Highly recommend it.
Very beautiful place. It's easy to find, popular for dog owners, and joggers. I'll be back again. This seem to be a popular place to watch the sunset.
Fun little walk! Great for dogs :) wouldn't consider it a hike but its a cute way to see some nature. Lots of fallen trees that are fun to sit on, and an amazing view to The beach.

Glad I used yelp to find this place. This is a perfect place to take your dog for a stroll. We picked up some breakfast sandwiches and parked ourselves on a log and enjoyed the beauiful view. Highly
Recommend visiting this spot if you'd like a breathtaking view.

Read about this place on Yelp, saw it on an episode of the Dog Whisperer and had to take the pup here to try it out for ourselves.  It totally lived up to its reputation and was well worth the drive from Pasadena.

This park is the perfect place to take your dog if you want to tire them out while giving yourself something to enjoy in the process.  We'll be back again soon.

If Rob Lowe likes it (he's in a "Go California" commercial sitting at the preserve), then I like it! And of course my dog likes it too. It's especially beautiful right now (Spring) when the meadows are lush and green and the wildflowers are blooming. This is the place I go to at least twice a week, and I constantly think to myself how lucky I am to live in Santa Barbara. Whether I have my dog with me or I come to meditate, I always leave feeling peaceful.

I almost want to review this place again and again so that I could give it all the stars it truly deserves.  This place is AMAZING.

My fiance and I stopped here on our way from LA to SF for the weekend.  This was our first road trip with the puppahs, so I wanted to make sure that we had good places to stop and let the dogs go out and relax a bit during the long ride.  I looked up dog parks in the area on Yelp and good lord, thank god I did.  This place definitely lived up to the hype.  It was so beautiful.  If I didn't have a dog, I would probably love it here anyway!  It doesn't even feel like I'm in a big city like Santa Barbara.  It feels like I'm a million miles away in my own little paradise by the coast.  It's quiet, the only things I hear are my puppahs, my fiance, and the rustle of the trees.  It's sooo perfect.  I am seriously considering finding my next job in Santa Barbara simply for this park.

My dogs LOVED it.  We walked in and a few yards in from the entrance, we let them off the leash.  I was constanly afraid that they would tumble into the cold harsh ocean over the cliffs, but they never did.  They actually listened when we called them back.  They loved sniffing around every single tree and every single bush and every single flower.  I was thinking we would never make it to SF because they just couldn't get enough of the park, but they got tired and we eventually herded them back into the car.  They were tired and happy happy dogs.

We are definitely making our way back to this little paradise sometime soon, and for way longer than an hour or two.
Great for a easy stroll, great ocean views, dog friendly and breakfast st Hendry's after

I've been looking for a stroller friendly park or trail since moving up here and so far this is the best one. The trials all loop back towards each other and parking seems easy. (parking is in residential areas) the trails are hard dirt trails so it's not a completely smooth ride if you don't have the right type of stroller. (We have a jogging stroller with big rubber tires.) There are some beautiful views of the ocean but you are up on a cliff side and there are no fences so don't let your little ones or off-leash pets  get too close. But overall it's a nice walk with an even nicer view. It seems very dog oriented here however with most of the trials being "off-leash allowed". My little one loves dogs so it's not a problem if they get too close for her and owners seem conscientious of where their pets are wandering so I never felt unsafe or worried. Still, it's always good to be aware. We will definitely be back. Great spot to walk.
Perfect place for a quiet walk or to hang out with your four legged friends. Incredible views.
Love the walk here. It is so beautiful and a great place to walk your dogs off leash.

Love this place!!!:) If you go down to the beach watch for tar. If you get it on you feet rub some baby oil and it will come right off :) it's naturally occurring...no biggie but just FYI :)! My dog loves this place :)

After years of visiting the Arroyo Burro beach and wondering what was on the cliff above us, we finally made a visit to this preserve. We had no idea it was primarily a dog park, so we were pleasantly surprised that our dog was not only welcome, but in plenty of good company. As first time visitors we kept our dog leashed while on the bluff side of the park, as we didn't trust her not to run off the cliff under the spell of a passing pelican. 

The only parking is on the streets in the surrounding neighborhood, and we were impressed with the absence of angry signs that usually pop up when a popular destination opens up in a residential area. My heartfelt thanks go out to this neighborhood and the Douglas family for making this space on earth available to everyone.

My Vizslas and I love going here weekly, we'd go daily if the park was closer to home. It's a beautiful and enjoyable area to walk with an incredible view of the channel islands, frequently spot dolphins and at times whales. Wonderful view of the surf and Hendry's beach below at the farthest end of the walk. There are a couple of resident hawks, numerous woodpeckers and a horned owl. The mountain side of the trail has a beautiful view of Los Padres and Elings. We usually walk the full circle 3 times, takes us about an hour.
Beautiful hike. Great for a nice stroll with friends. The view was amazing saw a couple dolphins off the coast. Very easy
Great place for sight seeing, hiking, dog walks, etc. The short trails lead to a cliff side view of the beach.

People who have been here a year or two call it Douglas Family Preserve, people here for a longer time, the Wilcox, and an even longer time here- the Nursery.  Sometimes its a fact of life to save something, you have to expose it.  For a looooong time, the only people who came here were the people from the neighborhood, and a few who drove here from other parts of town.  When a L.A. developer threatened to make the whole property a retirement community, the whole town broke out in fundraisers, car washes, bake sales, and all sorts of ways to try to buy the property, we were a million short, and at the eleventh hour,  the Douglases stepped in with the money, hence the name.  It will be different things to different people- If you have lived here a long time, before it was on T.V., Yelped, and advertised by the city to the world. You will remember it as an uncrowded paradise at one time, now just pretty darn nice.  If you just drove here 2 hours from.L.A.., the land of dog parks that look like concentration camps filled with pitbulls, you will be overcome by the amazingness of it all.  (although, years ago, there was a time when people dumped cars and other junk out here)  Since it has been exposed to the world, it can get pretty crowded-come  around sunset on a nice day, and elbow yourself a space on the cliffside!   Anyhow, if you have any interest in being kind to the neighbors that used to have a quiet neighbor hood-Please- Don't let your dogs bound straight out of the car into a neighbor's yard, take a crap, and walk away as if you didn't see, don't park with your car blocking a driveway.  To the right of the entrance, there is a dispenser for the dog leavings-use them.  A lot of people out there now use all sorts of creative dodges to not pick up crap.  The most popular seems to be mobile device use.  This technique consists of staggering along the trail, chattering like a baboon into the little box, talking to nobody about nothing important, thus allowing you to ignore your dog and its leavings.  There is one woman who rides her bike out here, screeching into her handsfree, ignoring her dog as he craps here and there.  I walk there twice a day, seven days, and more often than not-crap on my shoe.  The other strange half-technique gaining in popularity is to bag the crap, and leave the bags here and there, as if you were going to return for it- I see squished, stepped on ones all the time.  The ones i see at night, are always there the next morning, and there are locals who pick up the bags for those who wont. So if you are going to take all the time to drive here, at least pretend  you care about the place.   Oh, and lest you think I am a grumpy guy who hates dogs, I am a dog lover who walks mine twice a day here- and cleans up after them!  It takes very little effort.

We are so glad we had a chance to visit this beautiful hidden place! If you like touristy places stay away, but if you like low key nature with lots of happy dogs running free check it out. Amazing ocean views w the Channel Islands in font to frame the setting! Just gorgeous! Easy walk lives you wishing the preserve was twice the size!

Like taking walks with beautiful ocean views? Got a  happy outgoing dog, that likes to play outside with other dogs?  Than the Douglas Family Preserve is the place for you.  dog poo bags are provided, please use them!
Amazing space! Loved up to everything written by other commentators. I went at sunset and enjoyed a beautiful cliff side view if Hendry's beach. Highly recommend!

This is our first trip to Santa Barbara, looking for some dog-friendly trails, since its also our first time bringing our dog along.  All I can say is thank goodness me and the hubby found this place.  It has a spectacular view of the Santa Barbara coastline and it also overlooks the Arroyo Burro Beach area, which I will cover on a separate review.  

We went here on a Saturday afternoon and we encountered fellow dog owners with their pets off-leash.  I like the fact that even on a Saturday afternoon, this area is not overcrowded and leaves a lot of room for both dogs and their owners to explore the beauty of the preserve.  Will definitely come back again next time we visit

I absolutely love going to this park with my dog. The view is unbeatable from every corner. It is quiet, clean & safe to go with a friend & chat sitting on a tree trunk. Thanks to the Douglas family for preserving this piece of land :)

I came upon the preserves by accident while I was apartment hunting. This is by far one of the best places I've been in SB. The preserve is on a cliff overlooking the ocean. Absolutely beautiful. I was able to chat with a few friendly artists painting the view. This location is perfect for a stroll or trail run. Dog friendly. I look forward to bringing my mountain bike up for a ride.
Great views!! Kid and dog friendly. Parking in residential but not far and easy. Very open. Beautiful place to visit.

Excellent place to walk your dog off leash. Great scenery, friendly people around, and great weather. Water available for dogs and poop bags for your K9 at the various  entrances. Free parking in the neighborhood surrounding the preserve.

My Australian Shepherd pup loves this place, and so do I! The view is amazing, and it's very well kept. All the owners that take their dogs to this beach are incredibly friendly, and all the dogs are (for the most part) well behaved. Parking is easy enough but can get crowded on the weekends.

We came here in Sept 2016 for a nice weekend hike. It's got some nice views at the top of the cliff. Once you park in the parking lot, there's a good 20+ minutes of hiking to get to the top of the cliff at which point you can walk along the top of the cliff area and see the ocean just below. Gorgeous - great place to take your dog too! :)
Gorgeous scenery, flat no real challenge or workout as far as hiking goes. Lots of people here with their dog & kids. The vibe is very respectful, really clean & pretty quiet.
Can be a little crowded, but a lot of space, and great views of the ocean and up at the hills behind SB

Truly beautiful place for a walk.... wouldn't call it a hike, as it is mostly level. 

Gorgeous ocean views from the bluff (you can see Arroyo Burro beach below), then the trail circles back into the woodsy meadow.  The cool breezes from the ocean and peaceful atmosphere are wonderful.  (We were here on a weekday and it was busy, weekends must be jammed.)

One warning:  there are LOTS of dogs being walked here - most of them are off leash & running free.  We are not dog owners, and had several enthusiastic dogs approach us to sniff & check us out up close.... so if you are dog-phobic, this may not be the best place for you.

Parking is on the public streets, so be quiet, aware and courteous when you park (don't block driveways etc.)

Beautiful trails!  We enjoyed a quiet walk there with our dog.  Nice dogs and people.  The trail was serene and the atmosphere was very relaxing.  Feels like you are in the woods yet you get to overlook the ocean.  Great at sunset.

Douglas Family Preserve is a big outdoor expanse with easy to walk trails and a dog-friendly environment. Parking seems to be mostly residential (some reviews mention a lot, but the closest one may be the one next to the dog beach). Otherwise, there's easy to find residential parking next to the gate entrance. At one end of the preserve, there's a very cool looking log that's great for sitting and looking out/down onto the dog beach and beachline. There's also a roundabout way to walk down to the dog beach with some slight elevation. Overall, cool non-intensive hiking/dog area.

I remember coming here as a kid, and I loved it!  Beautiful views, friendly people, dog friendly.... I think my mom used to pay me 10 cents for every dog poop I picked up!  (with a baggie of course!)  Such a good (and flat :)  ) walk on beautiful trails!

Douglas Family Preserve is a gem of local little park. Situated in a residential area, there are a few entrances with street parking nearby. I've been here a couple of times during my trips to Santa Barbara and never had an issue with parking. Overall the visitors seem to be locals out walking their dogs, but as a tourist, I found the preserve to be a nice respite from all the more crowded spots in Santa Barbara. The trails are mainly flat (with the exception of the path down to the beach) with views of the coast as well as meadows filled with wildflowers in the spring time. The bluffs do not have any fencing so be mindful of your steps and take in the views at a safe distance. 
There are lots of birds to view & listen to and overall it's a peaceful way to spend some time strolling through nature. 
Arroyo Burro beach is located below and there is a short nature trail that you can follow that takes you down to the sidewalk below. Make a left towards the beach parking area and you get a great "two for one" park experience-both woodsy and beachy all at once :)
Lovely spot to visit when in Santa Barbara.

So very beautiful and so mellow.  We hardly ever encoutered other people (or dogs for that matter!) as we explored this lovely bit of land (thanks Douglas Family!).

If you are in the area, it's a nice place for introspection & peace.

Handsdown THE best off leash dog park in all of Santa Barbara. Beautiful, untouched wilderness that deserves a full walk thru. Thank you to the Douglas family for keeping it public land iunstead of it going to McMansions and condoville--we are forever indebted to you!

Great views of the ocean
Challenging hike
Dog friendly

Dog friendly!

Dogs are allowed off leash here (within certain signs) but I would be cautious due to the cliff edges. For that reason, I kept my dog on a leash because she likes to chase squirrels and is sometimes not all that bright.

I parked at the lot for the Arroyo Burro Beach/The. Boathouse at Hendrys. At 7 in the morning there were plenty of spots, but when we left at around 9am it was starting to get busier. There are 2 decently sized lots with free! Parking. Also there is a dog wash station in this lot. I was going to try parking the residential streets but I wanted to take my girl to the beach again so this was a good middle parking lot where I could access both. 

The preserve itself is nice, there are forestry looking areas and you can also walk along the cliffs edge to get gorgeous views of the coast and beach below. Walking around the trails and back totaled to be around 2.5miles (this is also the walk from the parking lot). You will meet other people with off leash dogs. I was able to walk the trails in sandals (I was too lazy to wear my hiking shoes, knowing I was going to the beach after) and the trail is relatively flat, from the arroyo parking lot though you are going uphill a bit. 

Would definitely recommend anyone visiting, bringing their dog to come to this lovely area of Santa Barbara!
Gorgeous views and dog friendly. Lots of little trails to have your own privacy and make the hike special

What a wonderful place to visit! This natural preserve has fantastic hiking. You can even get to the beach from the trails. The views are breathtaking! We even got free side of the street parking really easily. It's a treasure!

We went there with our 2 dogs. Lots of dogs and at a certain point they can roam leash free! It was a great place to enjoy the view from the cliff down to the ocean. We went in the evening and saw a school of dolphins swimming and also saw 2 whales! We watched a beautiful sunset and took great pictures! So serene and peaceful!

Heaven on earth! Stunning views from above. Perfect to go at the end of the day during "golden hour". A must see if I'm the area. Can extend it into a long walk by going down to arroyo beach or the Mesa steps.
An amazingly beautiful place to jog or walk your pets. Bring a camera! :)
Awesome trail! Love the views! Love that you can bring the furry ones with you of course!

Locals still call this The Wilcox Property. Sit above Hendry's beach (aka The Pit, aka Arroyo Burro). Great place to take the dog off leash. Beautiful views. Drinking spot for dogs and humans alike. Wear shoes, your feet will get dirty if you're in open-toe footwear.

Awesome place for dog to play! There are both on leash and even more off leash areas for the dog to explore and run around. We encountered many dogs to play with. Breathtaking views. The entire family had fun, including the kids. We will be back when we visit the area. I am so glad we found this awesome area.

It's not often you could have access to cliff top views of the Pacific Ocean in SoCal. Great rewarding view for such a short and fairly easy hike.

How convenient is this to the residents at the top of the hill?! I ran across several locals taking their dogs for a walk.

Parking available and accessible when you follow the parking signs for Arroyo Burro Beach.

I hate how this is such an awesome park but SO FAR from me! :( 
I'm glad I got to experience this place, it really was a dream  come true for people and dogs! If i was a dog, this would totally be on my bucketlist. All the owners and dogs are very friendly and my dog had a great time running around, sniffing things, and drinking a bunch of standing water (after a rain storm). Gorgeous views! Definitely a must even if you don't have a pupper!

I was driving back to Long Beach after spending the holidays with relatives and wanted a place to stop to let my pooch, Cody, stretch his legs and well, take care of business. 

Looked online and the Douglas Family Preserve seemed a perfect fit. It's a bit of a drive off the 101 and you have to drive through some housing areas, but it's worth it.

If your dog needs to run, this is a great spot with an awesome view of the ocean. Not sure if there is beach access because the areas I was in was pretty high up a cliff.

Most of the dogs I saw there were large and super friendly. They seemed to love the chance to stretch their legs. Also, the owners seemed to be considerate and had control of their dogs off the leash. This is not always the case in some of the dog parks I've been to.

Parking is in a residential area which is fine . It is not a really special place. Its quite dusty and it is more for dog owners. The view is nice but not amazing. The breeze was really nice though. I wouldn't recommend this to visitors. I feel like I wasted my time here. I did enjoy taking pictures of some trees though. It was busy. Saw at least 10+ other parties of 2-8 people in our 30 minutes there.  No one said hi back even though I smiled and said hello good evening . So def not a friendly place. 

Note-if you have really young ones it's not really safe since it's on a cliff. This is not the reason for the low rating. Low rating is just Bc of nothing novel or great about this spot.
Just awesome. My pup and I had a blast walking the loop around up high and down to along the beach. Puppy heavy. She's snoring in my car right now with exhausted happiness.

One of my favorite places to take my dog. Most of the park is off-leash with poop bag dispensers at each of the entrances, and a doggy watering station. Beautiful views overlooking the beach and I have often seen whales and dolphins swimming right off the shore. 

We also haven't had any issues with ticks, like we have at some of the other off-leash spaces in SB around this time of year (late-spring/early summer).

FattyMatty introduced me to this place and for that I shall be forever grateful, I'm a little bitter he got 1st review.  Quite possibly the most beautiful location in one of the most beautiful cities.  With expansive views of the beach a hundered feet down and the islands in the distance, this is THE place in SB to view a sunset.

The Douglas Family Preserve offers a great short hike/walk to enjoy the outdoors and views of the ocean & cliffs! If you get lucky, you can catch a whale or dolphin swimming by. Such a peaceful place to just take in the sights and sounds of the water.  

I also come here often with my dogs to get their energy out.  If you do have dogs, I would highly recommend this spot as there are often other dogs around to socialize with and even trails that allow your dog to be off leash!

This place is so picturesque, great for taking photos of the shore. It's also a dog owners dream come true, there are little fuzzy friends everywhere. A very easy hike. I wish I lived by this place as I would go everyday!

Visited this preserve over the weekend and we were not disappointed.  We just wanted to visit, enjoy the views, and get some fresh air and exercise. The dog walkers were all very considerate and helpful with directing us around the preserve and down below to the beach. The preserve was easy to locate, following the directions linked with yelp. We parked on the street, began our walk, and then took the trail down below and walked on Henry's Beach plus checked out the Boat House Restaurant.  What a lovely area to visit and one that we did not know was there.  We were visiting from Ventura County.

Brought our dog here yesterday and not only was it fun and exciting for our dog, we were also treated to some gorgeous, stunning views of the ocean.
Was very clean and plenty of room to run and opportunities to meet and play with other dogs. Would definitely return.

I loved this park! There was so many dogs out and about, and all of them so behaved. We brought our dog here and let's say he had a great time as well haha. Amazing views of the sunset and plenty of places to stop and take a picture. You can smell the salty sea as you stroll through this park. Parking is easy and the park is huge. Can't wait to come back here next time I'm in SB!!

Really great spot for a quick off leash walk.  Stunning views of the beach below and lots of fun nooks and crannies for my dog to run around in and play.  I wish other owners would be more considerate about picking up after their pup (especially since bags and trash cans are provided for free) there's a lot of "we think our mom works here" going on with people bagging the poop then leaving it there on the trail (rude)

Other than that its a fantastic place, I just hope these inconsiderate and disrespectful dog owners don't ruin it for the rest of us.  Clean up after your dog ppl!

Nice place to take a walk and enjoy the ocean views! The entrance is located in residential area with plenty of street parking. 

A lot of people brought their dogs as well!

I almost don't want to write this because it's such a nice little "hidden gem" amongst locals on the Mesa! Douglas Family Preserve is a nice, local dog park with a walking/run path option and incredible views of the sunset of Hendry's and Mesa Lane Beach. It's located inside a private neighborhood and parking consists of only street options. 

If you are a dog owner with a friendly pup who loves to run freely, burn energy and socialize in public with other fur-babies, this is a great spot for you. They have special areas for the dogs to run around and others that are more private just for walkers/runners. Though the views are absolutely unbeatable, there is no beach access here and the cliff's are not fenced off so it's not the best place for young kids or drunk adults. 

I think this would make a beautiful first date spot, mid day walk with friends and pups, family photoshoot location, proposal or small wedding venue, sunrise and sunset viewing spot, and running area. Don't hype it up though, what makes it so lovely is the beauty in it's privacy and peaceful environment.

Beautiful views a plenty!  While it isn't much of a hike exactly.. it is so worth the walk.  You will be making many stops just to look out at the amazing views especially at sunset.  My boyfriend and I went here yesterday and had the best time.  There are definitely many dogs but they are so fun to watch at play.   As far as we could see the dog owners seemed to be pretty responsible with their dogs doody.  Which is what we were initially concerned a little about after reading some of the previous reviews.  I highly recommend stopping by if you are visiting.  We will be back without a doubt!

The most prettiest place in Santa Barbara.  It is spectacular, and also there are dogs.  

Thanks for saving the Wilcox Property, Michael Douglas!  I will never speak ill of your fine films!

Ta place is a great dog park!  Dogs can come and run off-leash. They have an area for water for your four-legged friend. 
  The views are beautiful, great spot for a sunset. 
  Be careful there are some steep drops and no ropes or fencing so beware where you're throwing the ball for Fido to fetch. 
  It's mostly dirt, no sand or grass, so the pups will need a bath after but the joy on your dog's face when playing here is so worth it!
Beautiful spot for a nice walk with my pet. It's all mulch and dirt and long. Will be back!

Super cool off-leash dog friendly trail that is kind of like a dog park! It's on a cliff next to the ocean so you get to hear the sounds of the waves and see the views of the ocean. Everyone there was very friendly and practicing social distancing. It wasn't crowded at all. The dogs were very friendly as well. Parking is in a residential area! It's not really a hike but it's a dirt trail so be prepared and wear shoes! The trail is as long as you want to make it! You can walk the entire trail or walk parts of it. It's a nice place to explore and let your dogs explore.

Douglas Family Preserve is located in a residential neighborhood right by the corner of Selrose and Medcliff.  Its pretty easy to find parking.  Its the perfect place to watch sunset and I am envious of the people who live right next to the park.  There are also many mature trees, so you are can walk mostly in cool shades with refreshing salty ocean breeze filling your nostrils.  The reason I am deducting a star is because there are too much dog poop on the ground.  There are as many dogs there as people, while most owners are responsible, some do not pick up after their dogs or leave little bags of dog poop everywhere.  Come on, the trash can at the entrance isn't that far away.  My 5 year old picked up a bag and thought it was candy.  It mauled the otherwise perfect experience.

I visited there today with my pug, if your looking for a quiet nice walk along the cliffs with nice views this is not the place. It was so over crowded and way to many dogs without training. My pug was bitten twice I had to yell at the dog then had to find the owner who was at least a quarter mile mile back from their dog to tell him to leash his aggressive dog. In the 15 min there I saw two dogs fighting not playing. This place is full of careless self absorbed people who don't pay attention to their animals or their behavior. All I have to say about this place is that it is dangerous if you have a small breed dog.

The preserve is on a coastal bluff within Arroyo Burro Beach County Park. The walk through the entire park with common ocean views and vegetation of mostly trees took 45 minutes. Not designed for picnics, the dusty space was mostly used by dog walkers. Multiple entrances into the park but none have parking lots which required parking in the residential neighborhood.

We just moved to Santa Barbara, and were initially going to go to the beach below the Preserve (also dog friendly). However, the line to get in was horrendous, and we didn't want to have to deal with traffic, so we made our way up to Douglas Family Preserve. Since then we've already been back a few times! It's a great little walk for us, and there are so many dogs for our pup to play with. It has gorgeous views, right above the water - definitely worth visiting.

The spot was absolutely stunning. Sadly shorter than I would like -- absolutely gorgeous view. But it was the people that really made it for us - a couple gave us directions down to the beach. Where our dogs ran - and there was a great restaurant. We went back the next day. Highly recommended for dog lovers!

This place is a very short walk and when you get to the end you can see down below to the beach. It is really nice however, there isn't too much to so up there or things to see so I found it somewhat boring. We quickly got to the end, circled around, and then went back to our car. I did really like that it was dog friendly and they had doggie bags and a water bowl with running water with it. That's really cool.
Just awesome place & view & my dog loves it!!!!!!!!!

My husband and I spent a beautiful time here during the golden hour. We drove up and parked in the neighborhood rather than walking to it as we were already dressed nice for dinner. 

This is a beautifully romantic spot perfect for watching the sunset. We loved seeing the dogs play as it made us miss ours back home  

Definitely a nice spot for pictures. My hubs took some beautiful ones for me. If we lived here I could totally see us bringing our dogs here to walk or just walking up ourselves for a romantic view or a picnic. Must view spot!

This place is magical. I was hesitant to come because from the reviews it looks as if it were just a dog park. But JUST a dog park is not what this is. Love it. One of my favorite places to mellow out.

We love going to this amazing open space! Plenty of trails to enjoy! You can hike to the top from Hendry's or park off one of the side streets from Mesa Lane. 

Make sure to walk along the bluffs for spectacular ocean views. Dog friendly!

Easy to find parking near the entrances.

Super dog friendly (and lots of dogs visiting the park) with water available for the dogs

Clean

Near view of the ocean and nearby beaches
Beautiful place to venture around with a great view of the beach and accessible street parking

Beautiful easy hike. The ocean views overlooking the steep cliffs were beautiful . Taking the trails headed inland gives you a different scene of greens, creek, a bridge, and some lovely trees. . Dog friendly . Some awesome logs of trees where you can just sit and take in the beautiful scenery and views of the ocean. It's a great hike for all ages.

We visited Douglas Family Preserve a year ago with our two small dogs and they loved being off leash! There is so much space and our dogs aren't into other dogs, so we never had an issue the whole time there. They could just do their thing sniffing around!

Great views, great photo spots of the coast. Parking is free in the nearby neighborhood.

An absolute must for anybody visiting Santa Barbara. Directed my GPS to the address listed on Yelp, and easily found the entrance to the Douglas Family Preserve. You only have to walk a few feet before seeing stunning views of the ocean and beach below. The cliff itself was very green with a nice walking path and trees to look at, and there are people walking their dogs as well! What a great place to have in the community.

On top of it all, you can take a short walk down a path (upon entering the preserve, steer right) towards the dog beach! So worth it! It was so fun watching the dogs play on the sand without their leashes. There were also a lot of families with kids playing in the sand and water. 

I took my sweet time and watched the dogs play, and spent a good two hours here. Not a hiking destination, more like a leisurely stroll! No bathroom at the preserve, head down to the beach for access.

Breathtaking view of the ocean! Very pet friendly! Ran into a Golden Retriever, a great Dane, and a Pitbull all in 15 minutes that we were there. Our mini poodle loved the view as well. Of all the places my fiance and I went today to sight see (botanical garden, art foundry, Franceschi Park) I would have to say that this is the best of them all.  Unfortunately, because it was our last stop we were unable to walk the whole trail or see the cliffs edge so we will be returning to this preserve next time we visit Santa Barbara - a true gem.

One of the only off leash dog areas in Santa Barbara. You must love dogs because there are tons around! The cliffs make me bit nervous because my dog once successfully attempted to go down the cliff to the water but that is not their fault. The views of the ocean are beautiful. The path is flat and good for a short out and back walk along the cliff edge or loop around the preserve. We took our engagement photos here and they are beautiful!
Highly recommend this trail. An easy hike with kids & the views are gorgeous! I'm so glad we hiked here!
Such a gem especially after all the rain. Such a fun place to run around with your dogs or take a moment to look over the ocean. I like to park off of Mesa School Lane.

This is a fantastical off leash nature preserve with coastal views.   So gorgeous and many friendly dogs and people to walk with !   The surroundings are beautiful and there's a water source for dogs to drink.

A large, flat walking area with BEAUTIFUL sunset views!!! A good amount of people around (and bonus: a lot of dogs too!) so it feels very safe, especially as it gets darker.

Tip: If you're here to look at the ocean, when you enter the preserve from the neighborhood street parking, go left on the trail instead of the trail straight ahead like Google Maps shows. The left leads you to the same ocean/cliff overlook in a shorter amount of time!

Parking: Street parking in a residential area, safe and not hard to find

Aka, "The Wilcox Property", Aka "The Dog Park"

I used to go here almost every day, as it is the only park in Santa Barbara where you're allowed to walk your dogs off leash.  It is a fairly large park, and when you have small dogs, as I do, it is an awesome place to walk your dogs.  They can get an excellent amount of exercise here, and there a lot of other dogs, so it's nice to let them get out and socialize.  You can also see people who take off and go para-gliding here, and that's fun to watch too.

This park is located directly above Arroyo Burro Beach, and the views from here are utterly amazing. Being Santa Barbara, the weather is always nice, and the sunsets here are spectacular.  You are doing yourself a disservice if you live in Santa Barbara and don't come here at least once. 

Plus, I have a secret bench here, that is nearly impossible to find...I always loved sitting there, Newcastle in hand, dogs running around...good friends...makes me miss Santa Barbara quite a bit...

Beautiful views of the ocean and nice walk for the whole family If you follow the main path it's about 20-25 mins and not many hills. My 8 year old does great but I would be careful with younger kids since it is basically on a cliff

Lots of dogs and they could be off leash so if you don't like dogs don't go.

Such a great place to hike along the coast. Parking is located on the residential  streets and is FREE.

This had serveral hiking trails and the one along the coast is dog friendly and your furball can go leash less. My pug Iz heart the place and the coastline views were spectacular with a pleasant breeze mid am.

If you're in the Santa Barbara area and are wanting to hike a easy trail with solid views I would definitely suggest this place.

If you're a fan of the views at Hendry's/Arroyo Burro Beach, you can elevate the views even more. Head straight up the neighboring Mesa to Mesa Lane, turn right and continue until you reach Medcliff Road, then take another right until it dead ends. Park in the neighborhood and stroll the beautiful preserve. The park is perfect for all ages and dogs (off-leash area), though no bicycles (go to Elling's Park 5 minutes away instead). Keep in mind that at the peak of summer it can be extremely dusty. Follow the short trail along the bluffs all the way to the end where you find yourself on a cliff with an old downed tree to sit on and stellar views of the Pacific Ocean, Hendry's beach, and Hope Ranch. It is a perfect spot for watching the sunset. If you're lucky, you may see paragliders taking off from Elling's Park to the north.

Getting there with google maps was flawless- no problem at all. Parking is in a residential area. What would be a dead end is where the park sign and entrance is and it's hard to miss. Upon entry to the park what you'll find is that It's a trail along a bluff the view of the command view of The Pacific Ocean is surreal. 
 Sunset was a great time to go. As mentioned it's a big spot for people walking their dogs off leash so there is a lot of awesome dogs in the area as well. 
 It's not very big and not enough for a whole day trip. Kinda just a nice sunset / sunrise quick visit and totally worth your time.
Found this place randomly searching best sunset views in SB and boy was this the spot. You feel on top of the world and it's so easy to access!

Heaven meets Earth! I love Douglas Family Preserve because it's a BEAUTIFUL easy hike/ walk with family. The views are breathtaking and it's a very clean. Perfect place for dog owners. Parking seems to be in the neighborhood.

Great easy hike with beautiful views! We were very impressed with this place, we just wish the hike was longer and a bigger place. It's dog friendly so beware of unleashed dogs here although I thought dogs had to be kept on leash. We constantly kept our vigilance since our small dog doesn't do well with other dogs. A couple of dogs came up to us randomly when we were sitting around and scared us - fortunately no bites happened. Most dogs seemed well behaved and the owners were able to call their dogs quickly. 

The hike takes at most 25 min to walk all around. We parked in a residential area and easily found parking Sunday morning shortly before 10AM. After a few min walk towards the left side of the entrance, you'll see grandeur views of the beach. Further down you'll hit a cliff and down below you'll see a beautiful view of the entrance to the beach with the hills in the back. Some views were IG worthy so definitely bring your camera here! We were lucky and ended up with perfect weather in the high 60s. A must see if you're looking for a super easy hike and great views.

I probably would have missed this spot had I not looked up while enjoying Late lunch/Happy Hour at the Boathouse Restaurant below.

I saw a couple of people looking out from the top of the Bluff and I asked "What/Where is that and how can I get there?"  I got directions and was pleasantly surprised it was only a 5 minute drive away.  Yes!  (found out hiking up is an option also) 

Got here around 4:30 on a Wednesday afternoon & not crowded at all except there were a few dogs running around.  Guess more than usual as even the dog owners were commenting on the pooch abundance.  (Off leash pets are allowed in certain areas)

Billed as a park/reserve where you can ride your bike, hike & view wildlife.  Over three miles of park trails as well one of the park areas has Hang Glider opportunities.

All that said, there's barely any mention of the pacific coast and Santa Barbara area views.  Breathtaking and amazing are understatements!  

I parked and entered from the Mesa neighborhood side-multi-million dollar homes with pacific coast views so be sure to keep it down ("Quiet Zone" signs posted on the streets-& rightfully so).   

Maybe the views are understated in the description so this spot stays a best kept secret? 
Either way, when in Santa Barbara, a must visit!  

Review # 500

One of my favorite spots in Santa Barbara.

It's beautiful here.
Great date spot or place to take a romantic walk with your boy/girl.

This property was slated for development but was saved by local efforts.  I heard street layout was already in progress and if you look, you can see some remaining signs, like an occasional fire hydrant.  

I have never heard of an official parking area.  I just park on the street in an adjacent neighborhood.

If you walk to the north end, you come to the cliff overlooking the Boathouse restaurant at Hendry's Beach.  http://www.yelp.com/biz/boathouse-at-hendrys-beach-santa-barbara#query:boathouse%20restaurant

I love that beach & restaurant.  I recommend driving down, having a drink and or snack while looking up at the cliff that you were previously standing on.

Beautiful views a plenty!  While it isn't much of a hike exactly.. it is so worth the walk.  You will be making many stops just to look out at the amazing views especially at sunset.  My friend and I went here yesterday and had the best time.  There are definitely many dogs but they are so fun to watch at play.   As far as we could see the dog owners seemed to be pretty responsible with their dogs.  Which is what we were initially concerned a little about after reading some of the previous reviews.  I highly recommend stopping by if you are visiting.  We will be back without a doubt!

Probably one of the best hidden gems in Santa Barbara! Well not super hidden to the locals, but hidden for travelers. This place is great for long walks and beach views (especially during sunset and sunrise). You'll also see so many friendly doggies roaming around! Most of them walk around unleashed but don't be afraid, they are always so so sweet! 

The park is huge! So many different paths and different directions to go, but that's what makes it fun. The landscape is just a beautiful place. There's also a short hike to Arroyo Beach if you go all the way to the right and down the mountain(:

This place is incredible! One of the best coastal hiking spots I've visited! It seems huge and has tons or trails and offshoots to explore. I can't wait to come back and check them all out!

There's a decent map of the trails and park right at the entrance. I suggest taking a pic of it to reference while hiking as there are so many trails, but it definitely seems like an easy place to find your way out of even without the map. I was kinda short on time so I opted to head straight to the coastal part. After the entrance, I turned left and made it to the ocean cliffs in no time! It's a very shaded path - lots of lovely coastal oaks and pines! And lots of huge old fallen trees by the cliffs to sit on and enjoy the view. And supposedly you can walk to Arroyo Burro beach from the preserve but I didn't try due to time constraints. Will definitely give it a go on my next visit!

This place is super dog friendly...there are areas where they can be off-leash and they provide water spouts for them at some points as well! For being so dog-friendly, it was very clean so all the dog owners are very considerate and respectful! 

I entered at the Borton Dr entrance...parking is in a very residential area and is all street parking. You most likely will be parking in front of someone's house. I didn't see any signs to indicate parking was not allowed and there's no designated parking lot. Based on the map, it looks like there's another entrance at Medcliff Dr.

Great for a little hike or to picnic. Especially good to take my parents since they are at the age where climbing and jumping is too hard. So for a hike this is the perfect difficulty and isn't too much work or too far. Plus you get cell reception which is a big plus just in case something happens. Where as up at red rock or other hiking spots you don't get cell reception.
One of my most favorite thinking spots...I miss being away. No matter the weather, I always found it peaceful and beautiful here.

What a gem! If you want to be near the water but not on the beach, this is the place to go. We loved that it was an off-leash dog park. My dog is far more sociable and friendly when he's off-leash, which is so relaxing. There are lots of scenic spots along the trail so bring your camera. This is a very easy nature walk and not a hike, which was perfect for us. I love that they supply poop bags, and also have a place for people to donate plastic bags to be used as poop bags in order to reduce the costs. This is a very special place and I can't see myself visiting Santa Barbara without stopping by.

Talk about a dog park on steroids! We wanted to take a walk that wasn't on a beach but where we could enjoy ocean views and this bluff provided just that. We didn't even have our dog with us and we still enjoyed it very much.

I have to laugh at people saying it's kind of dusty and lackluster how much do you need to enjoy walking along the Pacific?

YouPark in a little neighborhood and there are clear maps posted for a loop it's very enjoyable and level in case you're like me with a little post vertigo balance issues. We enjoyed it very much and it's right around the corner from a great little restaurant below that was recommended to us.
I really liked it here! The cliff seems a tad dangerous for our dogs to be off-leash, but even on-leash, we all had a great time.

I have always loved the Douglas preserve and was excited to see what they were going to do with it. Unfortunately they didn't do anything, and coming here has sadly become a danger pit. So many dogs have fallen off the sides of the cliff you think they would have put up fences by now. I understand it might look bad but hey put it half way this place caters mostly to dogs they should worry about there safety. It makes it a stressful walk for us as the owners having to constantly yell or be nervous our pets are close to the edge. This place has not yet done anything and it's just a dirt park. It would be nice to have some grass or flowers in there. This past red hearing a dog had passed away after eating a poisonous mushroom put me over the edge and I will never return unless they get some Gardner's in there and make the place more pet friendly. This place does have great views of the ocean but maybe think twice before taking your pups out there. NOT SAFE NOT WORTH IT! Check out tickets grove in the meantime while they fix this issue.
Very pleasant path for the family to ride along.  It can get a bit more crowded near the pier, but otherwise a great activity!

We were able to experience this bike path from East beach all the way to Shoreline Park! Lots of fun to bike along the path and enjoy the different sites & ocean views. Were able to use rental bikes froM our hotel to bike along this path!

The Cabrillo Beach Bike Path is an easy & scenic bike path that follows the coast of Santa Barbara's downtown area. With excellent beach access along the way and loads of picture perfect vistas to admire, this is great way to enjoy and appreciate the beauty of this central California town.

There are bike and tandem bike rentals in the immediate area. Many of the hotels near the coast offer free bikes to ride, which is where I try to get mine from. Though it can get congested during the summer and on the weekends, this bike trail is worth any hassle. I love the rows and rows of tall palm trees swaying in the breeze that line the route. The fresh smell of salt water and pretty sea views on one side and the dramatic cliffs curving alongside on ahead of you give you that real nature experience rush. 

I can't believe a Santa Barbaran has not already reviewed the trail. I am sheepishly doing it now, because this ride is always a highlight of any trip to Santa Barbara. The distance is approximately 4.5 miles long from Sterns Wharf to East Beach,  and then back to Santa Barbara Harbor. In the past, I have taken the trail further into Montecito but I am just as happy to do the Cabrillo route.  Bathrooms can be found along the way & there are restaurants on Sterns Wharf, at East Beach and in the Harbor area. This is a good and safe ride for kids to enjoy..

The reason I gave the Bike Path 5 YELP STARS is that drivers in Santa Barbara are so considerate of riders. They go out of their way to stop to allow you to pass and never honk or act aggressive towards bike-riders. This chill attitude is so cool and much appreciated by this rider.

This is one of my all time favorite spots in Santa Barbara. No matter the weather, the views are always breathtaking. Up, down, side to side - The palms, the ocean, and the mountains are all in eyesight's viewing. I love walking, biking, running, and taking pictures while strolling down the Cabrillo Beach Bike Path. 

You can either pay and park in one of the lots or do temporary free parking along the street. Parking is definitely not an issue. Some also like to come tie their hammocks to a tree and read a book or maybe set up a slack line and test your balance. Really the opportunities are endless!

*Tourists: This is a very spacious, totally free, and dramatically beautiful place to come hangout, have a picnic, take pictures, and make a nice (affordable) day out of it.

Santa Barbara's official beach boardwalk. 

I've been coming out here with my bike almost daily this summer. The coastal climate is just too dang nice to be cooped up indoors. I see the ocean as a respite from my full time indoor job and try to take advantage of its beauty as much as I can. What better place than the Cabrillo bike path?

For as much traffic as this path gets, it's surprisingly tidy. I'd have to assume that a grounds crew maintains it. It's in one of the most heavily-populated tourist sections of town but I've never encountered a major issue. There will be instances where food cart vendors or pedestrians are blocking the right-of-way but those times are fairly rare. 

If you're looking to engage in some mild cardio and desire a beautiful view, look no further than this path.

name: Rancho Oso RV & Camping Resort
categories: Campgrounds, RV Parks, Hotels & Travel, Horseback Riding, Active Life

yelp_reviews.text:
Gotta give them 5 stars since any negativity I experienced was not the fault of Thousand Trails nor of the staff. Great people, clean facilities, good looking horses, pools, adult lodge, cabins and covered wagon abodes.  Thousand Trails. Have to give them credit for how they run their places. 

Could stand to put in a cool dog run!  Got the room folks. 

Negative? HOT, very hot, not mountain hot, just hot!  You put dry river beds and hot together you have gnats!  But that is the way it is. This place is for sun birds and horse lovers, providing you don't believe in riding a horse in 100 degree weather then you aren't a horse lover anyway. Wait until fall or early spring. 

But then it ain't near as hot as Paso Robles is today.

Stayed at a full hookup site which had little shade, but water-elec only sites would be shadier.  Friendly and helpful staff.  Rancho Oso also offered a quaint chapel with a guest Minister who has been coming here with his wife for over 15yrs every Sunday to preach.  God Bless them!  The kids loved the pool, playground and of course, the horses!  however, the flies and yellow-jackets are vicious so beware!

The people running the facility are extremely friendly and the campsite was always very clean. The kids loved swimming at the pool and playing at the park but as far as a campsite goes it was just okay. On the weekends they have breakfirst served at the quaint old stone lodge and it was delish. 
The campground has horses on site for going out on the trails but with the horses brings lots of horse flies. The flies were so aweful we chose to eat inside all the time. If your looking for a get away that's in close driving range to lots of other interesting things than this is the place for you. If your looking for magestic scenery and trees than look elsewhere. The other major complaint was the road leading into the campground is very narrow and nearly impossible at times to allow another car to pass. It's a one lane road up and down and it's a little unnerving to drive wondering if another car will be going the opposite direction than you.

Trip 1

Stayed here twice in last 10 months. First time it was new to me and my family of 5. We had fun checking out the old stone building and wondering around. 
We stayed for 4 days but by the 2nd day we were over the flies, the heat and the close proximity of the other campers. We decided we would return and give it another try. That maybe it was just not a great time to go. 

Trip two. 

Planned this trip with my family of 5, the inlaws and my cousin and his 3 kids. We were packed in the camp sites like sardines. The heat was horrible so we spent the trip at the pool everyday. Staff was unfriendly and had a vibe of not wanting to be there. At one point we were so shocked by there rudeness we contemplated just leaving. We got over it and decided to keep smiling and be the bigger people. I have a disability and have to use the handicap bathroom for showers. Unfortunately I had to return around 5 to 10 times to try to use the bathroom because non handicap campers apparently liked the bathroom/shower better than the others. I noticed why when after waiting 2 hours I gave up and had to use the regular bathroom/ showers. They were to put it nicely, third world looking littered with dirt, paper towels and just funk. 

We will not be returning, we will not be recommending anyone we actually like to go visit rancho oso. I'm sorry to say but your better off looking elsewhere for relaxing camping.
