I can go on and on about our experiences with the workers at the gate trying to double charge us as well as other bad encounters but I won't waste my time. You shouldn't either, find a different place so you can enjoy your time in our beautiful forest.

First, I must acknowledge that Rancho Oso is a very unique, beautiful and charming property set in the Santa Ynez mountains.  The drive in is breathtaking and the campground itself has tons to look at and experience.  We definitely enjoyed our time over Thanksgiving in 2016 and even took day trips to Santa Barbara and Solvang.

The Good:

- Beautiful mountains, hills, trees, hiking... great location!
- Stone lodge is a historic, charming stone building that's been converted into a recreation center and dining spot with dinner activities.  We even caught the Chargers on TV (Go Bolts) there on Sunday
- Propane service, country store
- Staff was friendly, tons of activities, great pool area, 
- Cabins and covered wagons looked awesome (we were in our RV at the partial hook-up sites)
- No internet / wifi at campsites

Unfortunately, we did run into issues at the campground and we decided to check-out early on Sunday rather than stay through Wednesday to migrate to El Capitan State Beach just outside of Santa Barbara.  The main issues stem from the overall "camping" experience that hampers most of Thousand Trails campgrounds.  This one in particular had so many rules and regulations I nicknamed it "Rancho No'so".  Everywhere you turn, there's a "No [insert rule here]" sign.  No campfires, no charcoal or duraflame fires (sometimes), no music, no bikes at certain points, no petting the animals, no parking, etc.  The list goes on and on.  I'm not sure what causes this level of restriction but I can't help but wonder if the permanents in the park complain about the vacationers often.  In the end, we went rogue at times and ignored the signs and still had a good time (while feeling a bit like rule breakers for shallow reasons).

The other problem this campsite suffers from is the insane site booking process that Thousand Trails employs at all its properties.  There's nothing more stressful than arriving at a Thousand Trails resort at night and searching for those last few campsites while other vehicles are doing the same.  In the day and age of internet booking, I just don't understand why you can't go online, enter your dates, see what sites are available (with pictures) and book it (check out santeelakes.com, muy bueno)!

The Bad:

- Too many rules and restrictions, "No" signs everywhere (hence the nickname "Rancho No'so"
- At the core of camping are campfires!  How about investing in campfire rings?
- Hiking trails covered in horse poop (granted I love horsebacking riding so it's a minor complaint for me)
- No internet / wifi at campsites

Overall, I would not be opposed to returning to Rancho Oso again but I would definitely invest in a portable propane campfire grill and would still play my music (at appropriate levels of course) on our awesome porch light/speaker regardless.

One thing I will not do however, is renew my Thousand Trails membership until they upgrade the site booking technology and improve the campfire policies (seriously, Pilot campfire rings like the state parks only $200 a pop for a lifetime of awesome campfire memories).

--Corey

We stayed one night In Jan. We rented a cottage and qween bed and pull out beds were not the best. My back hurt when I woke up. The heater works great. The Dinner meal at the Stone wood lodge was average. The chicken tendern were hard, but the Chicken meal was good.
The pool did not look well kept

We loved the Horse back riding, it was amazing. 5 big stars for that

Next time we will bring our own food for dinner

Will come back  again

I made a reservation for my daughter, grandson and myself to go horseback riding. I was told to be there at 12:45. We arrived about 10 minutes early and told "Go sit over there while we finish our lunch". At about 1:30 they got us on horses. I observed other grandparents bringing their younger kids only to be told we're busy just wait. The ride was okay but since I was at the rear I could never hear anything the trail guide said. Then half way through she gets off her horse and says she has to walk it back and we should just follow slowly along...Great fun! There was never any welcoming or fun attitude about any of it.

We love rancho oso. We've been there twice. The first time we went tent camping and the second time we rented a trailer. The staff is friendly and the place is beautiful. We will be going back every year. You don't need to be a member of 1000 Trails to stay here.

After you get used to the staff telling you they can't help you because they haven't had their lunch break or to look at the board on the wall instead of telling you about a weekly scheduled activity you may decide it's the way of the cowboy and they are trying to be authentic. The employees are strict with their rules if it is a convenience to them even if it is a guest disadvantage...do you make a profit from selling me a $6 bottle of wine for $20? But will you let me buy it at 4:02 pm when the store just closed?  NO!!  It would be customer service....this place doesn't do that!  

Several employees live onsite.  The restrooms are cleaned 1x per day if you are lucky.  The employees who I observed sat side by side not saying anything to each other smoking one cigarette after another.  

Our camp site was littered with debris.  I would not complain a bit if the cost per night was $20 but it was closer to $100 with taxes over the holiday weekend.  Oh, and they must allow day passes to locals to come BBQ and swim because it was so crowded in that area but very few camp sites filled. I'll be looking for another place to stay.

I won't come back again. We have over 20 people and stayed in the 5 wages for 2 nights in Aug. It's too hot (over 100), dirt is flying everywhere with strong wind. During the night, it's cooler and ok.

We could not believe the long winding narrow dusty road into this god forsaken place. The drought might be to blame but it was a tender box just waiting to blow up into a fire storm. It should be closed for safety. 100 degrees and not welcoming. The gal at the gate was very nice as thousand trails staff has always been and allowed us to cancel our reservations.  Is not "Santa Barbara" but a hot hole.

My parents are avid campers and because of this, I grew up camping. Most vacations when I was a kid was revolved around going somewhere to camp. My camping days seemed to taper off around the age of 14 when I started High School and we started doing more adventurous vacations (Europe and Hawaii to name a couple). Than, out of the blue, my parents bought an 18 foot trailer in 2010, my dad bought his first truck and they joined the Thousand Trails camping "club". So, here starts my new found camping adventures if I decide to go on vacation with the 'rents. 

I came to Rancho Oso with my dad for three days and three nights. Rancho Oso offers Dry Camping sites (no hookups) or sites with hookups for your water, sewage and electricity. The campground is about 360 acres and they offer horseback riding for $35 an hour, they have two pools, a spa, numerous hiking trails, a basketball court, a tennis court, miniature golf, a horse shoe toss area, an Adult Lodge that has a TV and free WiFi, a mini mart that actually sells BEER (if you know my dad and our relationship, you know I was there every night for my nightly beer fix....), a playground and surprisingly clean public bathrooms. I was surprisingly happy! Plus, it's nestled in the pretty valley outside of beautiful Santa Barbara!! 

I was more than impressed with this campsite. I was able to go horseback riding (awesome!), go hiking, spend countless hours lounging by the pool and spa and meet neat people. This has been one of my favorite campsites that I've gone to with my parents (have gone to two prior to this place the past 1.5 years). 

Come here if you love camping. Or if you want to get away from civilization or to just relax. You won't regret it! Most relaxing vacation I've had in ages. It was way overdue and well deserved!!

Nice older park. Can accommodate big rigs. WIFI in adult lodge. Some reviews complain of the heat. We went the end of June so it wasn't bad. My complaint in lack of staff to enforce runs. Irresponsible parents take there kids into the adults only lodge and swimming during the hour long adult only swim time. Did attempt to go to an activity and there was no one there. Wish it was closer to Santa Barbra but it's doable. That's why we stayed there. Looks like it's fantastic for horse lovers.

Great for KIDS...as an adult I probably wouldn't come more than once.  My kids love this place.  Animals, weekend meals, treasure hunts, bingo, pool, waterfall walk.

My main disappointment is that they don't heat the pool over Winter and kids aren't allowed in the spa/hot tub.  

We stay in an RV, the cabins are nice,  like a 2 star hotel.  A lot of people bring their horses here.   Phone reception is bad or non existent, so don't come if you are "on call" or something like that.

It was an experience. People were nice, place was clean the cabins had air conditioning and wee equipped with pots and pans and bedding, toilet paper and basic towels. But bring your own enough for your family. . Lots of mosquitos so take your spray. Unfortunately for us it was less than a day after arriving, the Whittier Fire took place. We could not get back into our cabin. We left to Solvang in the  morning but trying to return after a couple of hours we were stuck unable to return.

Friendly staff. Fun activities. Clean grounds. Beautiful scenery. Don't go expecting a five star resort. It is a ranch. A little dated but well maintained and adds to the charm. We had fun watching the dog and cattle show, hiking, horseback riding, going on the wagon ride, going to the two pools (which were a bit crowded) and exploring the area. Food offered for breakfast and dinner was pretty good and reasonably priced.  Cell service was very limited but amplified at the adult lodge which also had wifi. 30 amp hookup so had to rough it and only run one of my two ACs at a time. The road into the ranch has lots and twists and turns but it was no problem with our 38ft motor home with tow vehicle. Bring bug spray for the yellow jackets which are just part of nature.

If you are looking for an awesome campground, Rancho oso is it. It was our first time camping so we did not know what to expect.  It is safe and their are so many activities for the kids.  Make sure you take mosquito repellent, candles .. the kids really enjoyed their experience.

Absolute gem of a campground, even with all the dirt, dust and flies. Very uncrowded this weekend! Watched Shrek in the Stone Lodge, two pools (one warm, the other not so much but refreshing), plus bingo, wine tastings, hay rides, ice cream socials and trail rides! RV slot #8 is an easy walk to the Adult Lodge for a quick internet check for work, and close to pools/spa and store (which has a nice ice cream selection). Really fun 'show' on bull riding. The cowboys were informative, experienced and had as much fun as we did. Also Sonny Boy (the new baby horse) came out to play with a giant ball - very sweet! Drove up Paradise Road to discover a swimming hole which was a easy walk in. To rent, they have wagons that sleep 4, cabins that look like the wild west and even a teepee! It's a short drive to Cold Springs Tavern for music on Friday and Saturday nights or stay put and play board games under a very still and lovely dark, star filled sky! Dogs are allowed too! Very fun place, in excellent condition and worth every penny on a very, very uncrowded weekend!

We took my horse-loving daughter here for her 8th birthday present in September.  We rented a cabin that came with everything- including air conditioning!  A plus as you all know how hot it's been lately!  I can't believe the negative reviews.  EVERY employee was more than friendly- from Bill who took the reservation to the guy in the chuck wagon to the wonderful staff at the stables.  We arrived early Saturday morning- just in time for the wagon ride around the property.  The place seemed packed and I couldn't believe that just a handful of people took the wagon ride.  It dropped us off at the corral for the dog, pony, and bucking bull show.  A few more people showed up for this, but not many- it was fantastic though- I couldn't understand how people staying here wouldn't want to see this!  and the show is only offered on Saturdays so it's not like they could have seen it before.  Anyway, don't miss it!  After that we had lunch at the chuck wagon, went swimming, then while my daughter and I took a 1 hour trail ride, my husband and 6 year old son went to the ice cream social.  At 6pm we went to the lodge for the tri tip dinner- good for $12.95! kids had chicken nuggets for $5.95.  All meals came with salad, rolls, beans, and lemonade.  On Sunday, we went back to the lodge for a full breakfast before heading out on a 1.5 hour ride with my daughter while the boys packed up.  We'd definitely go back.  This is a dusty horse ranch- sort of like old fashioned summer camp- not a 5 star resort.  If you go with that in mind, you will love this place.

"I really had fun at camp Oso with my family I got to play ping pong and other fun games. I also got to make crafts and I got to sit with my friends and there is a lot of space to play. I hope I can to camp Oso again," Is exactly what my daughter said about Camp Oso. There is a lot of space and mostly dirt but the restrooms are clean and free, with warm water for showering, there's two decent sized pools and they have different sports courts such as golf, volleyball, basketball, tetherball,ping pong, and a playground as well. They have a chapel as well which is very clean and pretty and they have picnic tables near it and a lounge area with board games and television and set up art activities. Overall a good place to camp with family.

Myy boyfriend and I got here for a 3 night trip to do most of our sight-seeing in Santa Barbara. We came upon the campground with no shade in 100 degree heat in August (with a GPS that doesn't show this place). Needless to say it's over a 40 minute drive to Santa Barbara. First mistake. We reserved "full hook-ups."  We may not be the most experienced campers yet, but perhaps someone..like say, the Ranger, could of mentioned if we wanted just water/electric, we'd have the shady cool spots nestled quietly back in the park near the pool, etc. We ended up in the back in the middle of the sun, hot as Hades. 
So... the only reason this place gets 2 stars is the pool is ice cold! Nice! and the Adult lodge is air conditioned,  wi-fi, a tv, and games, etc. You will be desperate for it to beat the heat! 
I am thinking if you're a horse lover in April or the fall/winter, this is your place.
A wonderful place for camping and horseback riding! The staff is so wonderful! The manager, Bill, is always friendly and helpful! I would totally recommend this place for an awesome family getaway!!

Rancho Oso is NOWHERE near Santa Barbara. In fact it is 21 miles away and takes at least 45 minutes to drive into town (the roads are narrow, curvy and goes over a pass). We reserved this campground because we are members of Thousand Trails (a camping club), which allows to camp at its campgrounds for free. Their brochure claims Rancho Oso is 5 miles from Santa Barbara. This is clearly an exaggeration.

The two swimming pools were cleaned and well maintained. If you're just looking for a relaxing weekend among oak trees this might be OK, but if you want to enjoy what Santa Barbara has to offer, this is not the place.

Here for a week over Easter break. Absolutely beautiful setting. Stayed in water/electric camp site. Nice and shady, surrounded by giant oak trees and rolling hills. Went on two horseback rides. First one was great. Second one just ok. A lot depends on your guide. The guide for our second trip had a rough morning and it showed. Still worth it but like anything, can be hit or miss. My 8 year old loved it no matter what and that's all that matters.  Anyone that has stayed at a Thousand Trails facility knows that some are better than others. This one shows a little wear and tear but is generally in good shape. Bathrooms were clean, pool was clean and heated, no complaints from me. Our manager at the gate, Jim, was awesome.  Helpful, pleasant and informative. Yes, some staff were not the cheeriest but in general they were all nice and somewhat helpful. I have stayed at nice hotels where staff is less than helpful...it happens. I'm guessing some people complaining may not have been the friendliest either...in other words you get out what you put in. Yes, it's a 30 minute drive to Santa Barbara. More due to the windy nature of the road and not actual distance. If you are planning spending all of you time in town then this is not the place to stay. I have to ask, though, why would you camp In the mountains if you plan on spending all of your time somewhere else?  If you want shade and more of a natural setting, stay in the trailer and tent area near the pool and store. The area with full hook ups is great if you have an RV. those folks tend to sit inside there rig, watching tv with the AC on. Not really camping. All in all had a great time and would definitely go again

Enjoyed a one hour trail ride on Pepper, a quarter horse gelding, on the 9am ride on 6/26.  Wonderful guides and the trail was interesting and my horse was very sure footed.  Plan to go back there again perhaps for a week tent or cabin camping in the not too distant future.

We just arrived home from a 4 night stay at this. Wonderful RV campground. The best/worst thing about this park is the limited to non-existent WIFI or cell phone connectivity.The kids did a 1 hour horseback ride ($45), we hiked twice to the waterfalls  (the 2nd on is much taller/better, saw wild turkeys and hundreds of ground squires (they're everywhere). Restrooms and showers are well maintained. Looking forward to our next visit.

My main complaint was that the GPS puts this place alot closer to Santa Barbara than it really is. Its at least a 40min drive to the pier. Beyond that it is a pretty good place. Be sure that if you want a shaded spot get one with just water/elect. All the full hookup spots are un-shaded and very hot in the summer. Pool is nice and cool and the Adult Lounge has free wifi. Short drive up to Solvang.

I loved my stay at Rancho Oso!  Tucked away in the Ynez Mountains, 25 minutes from Santa Barbara and the ocean -- it offers a wonderful nature getaway including wine tasting, smore gatherings by the fire, tennis courts, horse back riding, hiking, jacuzzi and more.  I look forward to visiting again soon.
Awesome experience. Beautiful setting to unplug from the stressors of work and technology. Welcoming workers. Nice that they have a store and meals! Will be back!

We loved staying at this TT. The perfect place to come back to after a long day of playing and exploring. We tent camped and only had one other guest in the tent area for the duration of our Spring Break stay. The tent sites were private and had beautiful views. The staff on hand was wonderful! Jerry was so great to our little surfing family. He took the time to help with questions about the different surf spots so that we could make sure we were going places that would work for our young kids. Will definitely be back :)

Fabulous location. Nature all around. Very quiet. The cottages are clean, pretty, very well equipped and with enough space for our 4 people family. Perfect environnement to relax. Santa Barbara was 20 Minutes drive, an easy drive from the ranch. Our kids loved seeing the horses. We arrived at midnight and got a very nice welcome from the staff. We stayed only 2 nights in the middle of our road trip and would have loved to stay longer!!!!
Thank you Rancho Oso!!!

We went camping here for 1 day. This place is not exactly camping site, more like a park.
the staff are very friendly, the pool was very nice. But in the weekend this place is crowed and a lot of kids play around on camping site, so it's more like a kids playground. If u come here with ur family and kids this is perfect, but if you r regular camper don't come here unless you wanna woke up by a soccer kicked to your tents by kids.

Went here for 2 nights out of a 7 night trip, it was one of the nicest places we stayed and was VERY reasonably priced.  We stayed on weeknights, so we paid extra for a cabin with a kitchen. Well worth the extra.  The facilities are rustic and for a person who grew up camping and going to horseback riding camps, it was nostalgic.  Go with that in mind, you're not going to a "resort". The facilities and a people were wonderful. My daughter and friend went for a 2 hour horseback ride, and enjoyed. Great instruction from Emma, and it was her birthday...happy birthday Emma. My 7 year old did the 15-20 minute hand lead with Sarah, who was awesome!! I will definitely be back.  The pool at this time of year was great, nice breeze in the shade, and water felt great. The country store is well kept and loved the adult lounge. Next trip we want to stay part on a weekend to experience the chuck wagon and the ice cream socials. Highly recommend this place for a great experience for kids and adults. Go with the right mind set, or you will have a review like some of the other reviewers who expected this to be a luxury resort. How silly, it's a working ranch people!?!?

Worst horse back riding experience ever! We took our two kids (ages 8 and 11) who hated it even more than we did. The staff was very unfriendly and there were so many rules about how to ride the horses. It seemed like every minute the leaders of the ride were  reprimanding you for doing something wrong. What a drag! The leaders also hardly engaged with us and made the ride truly boring. The trail itself was hot, dusty and windy and not even that beautiful!  We all couldn't wait for the ride to be over. The horses were in great shape and well cared for. I will give them that. Don't waste your time or money. We also went to Circle Bar B and I would highly recommend it!

This place was okay, at best... yes there were some nice features but nothing that would make me ever want to come back here. We didn't like that you cannot reserve specific spots ahead of time, you just have to see what sites are available when you arrive. Not great when you are with a group and you want to be all next to each other. That really put a huge damper on our trip from the start. 

3 families stayed in full hook up sites and we stay in a cottage, you can get a specific cottage number at the time of reservation but the on-stie ranger (at the time we made our reservation) put us at the end of a row that had all cottages, knowing we wanted to attempt at being close to the rest of the group with RV's. There were other cottages at the beginning of each full hook-up row which would have worked much better but it was too late to get one of those once we arrived. Very frustrating! 

Our cottage was small with very little storage space. We couldn't put anything away, all our clothes had to stay in the bags we brought them in. The bunk bed mattresses were extremely hard and our kids didn't get much sleep for the entire week. Our bed was at least comfortable and it was nice having a bathroom. But only having one room key presented other problems as well. 

We also missed having nightly campfires at the campsites. You can reserve one of 4 campfire rings scattered around the property but that really takes the enjoyment out.

There is hardly any cell reception or wi-fi on the property, you can get spotty connection at the Adult Lounge. 

We did use the pools almost every day and played miniature golf. 

I think the Rancho Oso website makes it look and seem much nicer than it actually is. We were expecting a nicer place than what we got. We won't be going back.

Stayed here recently on a 19 person + 4 dogs camping weekend.  My Aunt & Uncle are Thousand Trails members so they set it up.  I have no info on the prices.  They stayed in a "Getaway Cabin" with a living/dining area, kitchen, bathroom, bedroom, and loft.  It also had a large deck and yard big enough for 3 large tents under the shade of oak trees.  Our full hook-up RV site was large and shady.  Very easy to park the trailer.  The pictures online show lush green landscape but since we were in the heat of summer it was all dry and brown.  The Santa Ynez River was also down to a trickle. My sister's RV site was next to a small tree so they didn't benefit from the cool shade. The campground is gated and most guests are Thousand Trails members so there is an overwhelming feeling of safety. The kids loved the place.  They were free to ride their bikes around and explore or go to the playground or mini golf area.  There are 2 pools that were both packed, tennis courts, tether ball, and 2 lounges.  We watched the ranch dogs and bull demonstrations while the kids went on horse rides. One of the best features were the restrooms.  VERY clean.  It seemed as if the staff checked them every few hours. And since it is in the Los Padres National Forest we did see a few deer and foxes and heard owls at night.  A few negatives: They don't allow "amplified" music outside your RV and campfires (in guest-supplied approved covered fire pits) must be out by 10:30pm. Also, the flies are vicious during daylight hours.  Make sure to bring citronella candles and mosquito coils.  Did a pretty good job of keeping pests away.

This place is camping perfection. I took my children here during spring break, for a week (March 17 - 25, 2017). The weather (even though it rained some) was not a hinderance at all. The staff is friendly, helpful and pleasurable. We especially adored Kim, who made my children's experience awesome, with crafts, the horseshoe hunts, the wagon ride and just generally helpfulness. The spaces in both the cabin, trailer and tent areas are spacious. The bathrooms are clean. The breakfasts and dinners were plentiful. (Get the calendar of events so you don't miss anything.) Santa Barbara is 30 min in one direction and Solvang is 30 min in the other direction. It is a single lane road down to the camping area. So be advised and alert. The 360° views were outstanding. We really wanted to do on site horseback riding but the weather didn't permit it. The pools and hot tub were open and clean. There is plenty of hiking to be done. There is miniature golf, a playground, an area for horseshoes, movies on the weekend, ice cream social, a church...this place has it all. Want to stay in a real covered wagon or a teepee...yep, they have that too! They have a barn with chickens, rabbits, goats, horses and there's even an awesome dog and pony show (detailed demonstration with bulls). I have nothing but good things to say about this place. I can't wait to go back. Warning: There are tons of bugs. Bring bug spray!

Camping for those who truly can't live without a jacuzzi near their tent.

A lot of California camping has bewildered me by how convenient it is, which for me means anywhere that has a convenience store. This place has taken that concept to its logical conclusion by providing you with more amenities than a hotel. Were it up to me, we would have eaten nothing but GORP and not seen another human for two days, but for everyone else, this epitomizes convenient camping.

A pool, jacuzzi, tennis courts, basketball courts, a lodge with wifi, billiards, meeting rooms, books, and satellite TV, hot showers, fire pits, mini golf, horseback riding, a plethora of social activities and probably twice as many other things that I forgot about all await you. They try to make most things cheap or free. Of the list above, horseback riding is the only thing you pay for (and for some of the social activities). Even loaner tennis rackets are free. The convenience store is well stocked, and doesn't gouge you too badly.

We stayed in one of the "river" spots, which are, you know, near the river. It was expensive ($52/night). Huge oak and cottonwood trees provided shade overhead, and every spot had a charcoal grill. These had a reasonable amount of space between them, though you should be sure to find out what your site is like beforehand, as it looked like some of them were super-close to each other. They also have cabins and covered wagons (yes, seriously) that you can rent to stay in. Very dog friendly -- our little beasts both got doggy treats from the guys manning the front gate.

They have a cell phone booster which allows voice/data coverage for Verizon, and voice for AT&T. Depending on where you are, you may need to walk a short way to be close enough to their booster though. As others have said, this area can get hot during the summer months. Also keep in mind that this is very family-friendly, so there are a lot of kids. I suppose this is good if you have kids and bad if you don't, but wasn't a factor for us since the river spots are a little away from the main area.

Employees were great. Not just helpful/nice, but full of personality, which is a huge difference.

Every summer our family & some friends looking forward to have a nice & unforgettable camp. I guess wishes do come true. But this time it's the WORST experienced we had.  The bathrooms are so dirty & disgusting. Showers have hairs all over, bathrooms are clogged & foul smelling.  Campsites are dusty.  Staffs are very unprofessional & very rude.   Once you did your reservation online for the number of people/guest.  They will still charge you for every member come within your group.  If you didn't ask for a refund they will surely ripped you off.   
Coming to this place was a Nightmare!!!!!!!

My Husband and I came here and stayed in a cabin. We got to bring our two dogs (german shepherds) which was a huge plus for us. The cabin has a kitchen so we got to cook our meals which was fun and a nice alternative to processed foods for every meal on your vacation. We went on hikes with our dogs, horseback riding around the land (including crossing a river! FUN!) and got to see the farm animals including a 1,200 lb pig and an over-sized goat I could have ridden. It was fun to pet these animals and see the cute Chinese chickens walking around. It was nice to drive a short distance to Santa Barbara, or Solvang and go Wine tasting or visit the beach, and then be able to come home to a warm cozy cabin.This is a fun place if you're looking for something different than everyday life in the city and your typical beach front vacation.

We stayed in the cabin # 93 rated as sleeping 4, there was only two of us. Nice and cozy cabin with a/c, Gas Bar-B-Q on the front deck Queen size bed in the bedroom, and it was quite comfy  although the sleeper sofa was garbage, very uncomfortable to set on I can't imagine sleeping on it's bed.. Rancho Oso is a bit pricey for what it is,but I found Great friendly service, clean room, nice pool with an hour in the morning and one in the evening with no kids allowed in the pool (Adult time), but some kids still in pool area during that so called adult time.. & hot tub,( don't get me wrong I love kids but adult pool time means just that! Some quiet time would of been nice too). Clean grounds & horses too. The wifi was not good at all. It is a 35-40 minute drive to Santa Barbara but worth the drive in savings and scenery. What ever you do, do not follow Google's GPS  directions! You will never get there. Stay on 154 until you see paradise road turn off, then go right.... Do NOT turn right on Camino Cielo like the GPS says !!!! Especially if your towing a trailer.. If you do you will be sorry and you will never get there... We will go again. Thank you Rancho Oso but please do something with that wifi or at least don't claim to have it, as it is very poor at best.

My family and I just got back from what I would call the best weekend of my life. After being under a lot of stress and a heavy workload, my husband knew I needed to get away from the city before I went crazy...er 

Ok so he found this little piece of heaven on earth through Groupon and he reserved us a wagon a couple of days in advance. I was a little scared after ready some horrible reviews but I gave it the benefit of the doubt and I'm so glad I did. Our booking turned out pretty smooth.

The Road: I'm terrified of curves and my worst fear is driving off a cliff...yeah I was praying at some point driving up. Next time we're definitely leaving home early in the morning. Visibility at night is minimal. Don't. I repeat. Don't roll down your window at night because mosquitoes of all shapes and sizes will get in your car and terrorize you. The address was either wrong or we entered it wrong on google map because it said we had arrived like a mile before actually getting there leaving us confused in the middle of nowhere and there is no connectivity going up so screenshot your directions instead of relying on turn by turn directions .

The Staff: I have zero complaints of the staff. Everyone was very friendly and helpful. Kim is such a sweet gal , I think that's her name... she hosted the wine tasting event and the easter egg hunt oh and she's also at the stone lodge during breakfast. 

The Camp: I saved the best for last . This really is paradise on earth. Nothing  beats breathing the fresh air and enjoying the mesmerizing views. The wagons are so cool, there is a tipi there too if you want to rent it , and there are also small cabins if you don't do the outdoors. They have restrooms ( thank you Jesus ) and they have showers !! Yaaay! Which were kept clean all weekend long . They have a lot of activities which we didn't get to enjoy all. We didn't reserve horseback riding so yeah that didn't happen :( we did go hiking , the place is huge ! We also did wine tasting and we won a bottle of wine in a raffle :D 

So to wrap it up , one last piece of advise; they do sell tickets for dinner which we didn't know we had to get  before 3 pm , but thankfully a very friendly camper suggested we went to this little tavern down stagecoach called The Cold Spring Tavern..  omg this tavern is so cool and also in the middle of nowhere. In the end the whole experience was absolutely memorable and I couldn't be any happier

Super place to stay and enjoy ,only one problem was food option which is very limited and need to order before 3 pm it is difficult if ur check in time is 4 pm. They have shows but Saturday morning only, can not catch if u r reaching on Saturday night, however other things are very good , we stayed at safari tent which is spacious and cool way to stay, overall very good place to enjoy vacations. They have to work on showtimings.

bottom line, dont come here if you want to have a great time with friends and family...drinking, chit chatting, etc. My family kept getting bothered by the old rangers that we were being too loud or not being compliant. We ended up getting kicked out 2 days before our initially planned check out. I guess it only works if you really want to just camp, getaway, and whisper to each other. ALSO place is super outdated, everything is old...they should at least get a new paint job around the place.

We had the worst experience at this 'resort' and we wont be returning. The water was off for 90% of our 3 night stay, the staff was exceedingly and constantly rude. We picked this spot as it was half way in between Solvang and Santa Barbara and as a nice relaxing vacation but it was the worst experience. I don't recommend and won't recommend this park to anyone.

The good and the bad...

Good - Friendly and professional staff, quiet location, scenic area (good hiking, mountain biking, and horse trails in the immediate area), clean cabins, good breakfast (on weekends), fairly close (30-45 minutes) from Santa Ynez and Santa Barbara, and pet friendly with a lot of open areas and a dog park.

Bad - Very limited cell service (could be a good thing for some). You need to drive about 10 minutes out of camp to get a good signal (Verizon seems to be best service in this area). The WiFi service that is advertised as available in the Adult Lounge is terrible. It barely works, and when it does, is extremely slow. The cabins/cottages have satellite TV, which I realize is better than nothing, but they just have the very basic channel package (no ESPN for you sports fans). The "New Cottages" can sleep 6, but 2 need to sleep on a fold out couch, which isn't very comfortable. The master bedroom and bunk beds aren't much better. It is pretty tight for 6 people. If you do have 6 in this type of cottage, you may consider bringing an air mattress, or looking at some of the more deluxe options (we saw 4 or 5 larger cabins that looked pretty nice). The common area facilities (like the Adult Lounge) are pretty dated looking, and have "musty" smell.

Overall, if you are just looking to get away from it all and completely disconnect from the world, this is a good option. If you need to stay "connected" to the world while on your vacation, you may consider other options.

My Husband and I came here and stayed in a cabin. We got to bring our two dogs (german shepherds) which was a huge plus for us. The cabin has a kitchen so we got to cook our meals which was fun and a nice alternative to processed foods for every meal on your vacation. We went on hikes with our dogs, horseback riding around the land (including crossing a river! FUN!) and got to see the farm animals including a 1,200 lb pig and an over-sized goat I could have ridden. It was fun to pet these animals and see the cute Chinese chickens walking around. It was nice to drive a short distance to Santa Barbara, or Solvang and go Wine tasting or visit the beach, and then be able to come home to a warm cozy cabin.This is a fun place if you're looking for something different than everyday life in the city and your typical beach front vacation.
We plan on coming back!
Place was clean and the personal was friendly. We stay in one of the covered wagon. Had lots of flying pincher bugs. The food was excellent. Plenty of activities for the kids. We had fun.

We had such an amazing time here. First, the location-- it's in one of the most gorgeous spots in the country. You are close to Santa Barbara and to Los Olivos/Solvang/Santa Ynez (wonderful if you like wine tasting), but this spot is remote. It is heavenly and very beautiful. 

Every staff member we interacted with was lovely -- very pleasant and helpful. We enjoyed the weekend bucking bull demonstration, which gave us a look at the other side (bull training) of the rodeo industry. We are rodeo aficionados, and this was a treat for us since we hadn't ever considered how the bulls are raised and trained. 

The highlight for us was the horseback ride-- we went on a trail ride back through the national forest, and it was one of our favorite things that we've done in California since we've moved here. In fact, we loved it so much that we are considering brining our horses out here to board, or, at the very least, bringing them out to camp with us from time to time.

I cannot recommend this place highly enough-- we will be back, and soon.
Fantastic campgrounds! We rented a 6 person cabin..it was clean, updated, and had everything we needed! Closest RV park to Yosemite entrance.

Cabin review - 3.5 stars - 
It was never our intention to stay at a camping resort but it came up as an alternative option on Hotels dot com.. After some research, it actually sounded fun to rent a cabin at Rancho Oso. So that's what we did.

Located below Knapp's Castle, this out-of-the-way campground is actually a large park with RVs, trailers, tents, chuckwagons, and various cabins. We stayed in the Cottonwood cabin. One bedroom downstairs and a loft with two small beds upstairs. This cabin has a bathroom and shower with a kitchen. The cabin was actually quite nice ( perhaps not as nice as some glamping cabins) but it met all expectations. If you're thinking of booking a family hotel in Buellton or Santa Barbara think about renting a cabin instead. It's a totally different and exciting experience for the kids. They do have a tv with cable channels.

My only regret was that I should have stayed longer because there is so much to do in this area.  Unfortunately we just couldn't justify it because it was price prohibitive. Another reviewer mentioned that their cabins just felt overpriced and I'd have to agree as they were nice, but they were dated and the main bed was old and the loft mattresses were foam. While this park was full of RVs, all of the other cabins were vacant. I would love to come back, but probably not unless the price is more affordable ( even if it was just $20 a night less, especially when all other cabins are empty). Nice park tho...

Pros -
A well organized park
Nick the staff member was friendly and helpful 
Nice location

Cons - 
No cell or WiFi (not very strong at the adult lodge)
At least a 30 minute drive to Santa Ynez for lunch/dinner
Cabin price + tax and fees

Tip -
The Aliso hike is family friendly and a good workout. You can also see the Rancho Oso campground from the top of the trail

Stayed here for a quick weekend while wine tasting in Santa Ynez based on a recommendation from my Dad who had done the same thing. LOVED IT! So much so, we are staying again a month later for my birthday weekend. Easy way to get out of LA (2 hr drive) without needing all the planning an equipment that goes with a more hardcore camping trip. Example, we forgot the coffee (could have been awful) but were able to wander off and find the adult lodge that had a coffee machine, grinds, and filter ready for us! Even though we are big campers and brought our own food and "bathroom accoutrements", we ended up eating in the lodge and using the showers because it was nice to be part of the community. You can choose to go as fancy or as rugged as you like because there are different types of sites from byob tent sites to turnkey cabins. Dow was an awesome host, he's the membership guy there and gave us great advice on trails to take and the best way to enjoy our wine-tasting adventure. Cookie and Julia were awesome horseback riding guides and made us feel super comfortable. Can't wait to go back!

I feel like these reviews do not reflect this place! Rancho Oso was great. Amenities are amazing, and keeps everyone happy. It's very quiet bc they have a strict no music rule. You definitely unplug when here.  Very little reception and there only wifi is in Adult lodge. Call ahead if you want to go horseback riding. Oh, and bugs weren't too bad, but it's only spring. =)
This is definitely off the beaten path. Full hook ups. Too many places to explore. Lots of nature all over. Thousand Trails.

It was just ok. On the upside, very quiet for the most part and sites were large enough for our 36ft 5th wheel + the front staff was very friendly and helpful. However, bugs were hungry & plentiful. The playground had seen better days. The mini golf was covered in leaves. The store was closed by the time we got there nearly every day, so getting equipment was a problem. Finally caught it when it was open and wasn't even able to get our fourth person through the door in single file line before getting barked at to close the door because the AC was on. Needless to say, we didn't stay long or ask about golf clubs, etc. The pool was packed with very few chairs at all times until closing. Signs said no glass bottles, yet people were in there drinking away. Don't expect we'll return.

Had a great time here with my family . The only set back was when we first arrived the older man working the entrance booth was extremely rude to us because our whole party wasn't there yet . Pools and jacuzzi area , very fun . Nice trails , beautiful horses . Don't even bother with the activities list very unorganized. Cabins were comfortable, but beware there are a lot of bugs !!!!!!!

My family has  gone camping at Rancho Oso for years and we have never had a bad experience until this last weekend, a tree fell which I understand Mother Nature, but several bee hives were found and the exterminator that went gave us literally 10 minute warning for us to leave the area we had 5 children with us a group of 18 of us total it wasn't long before he started with the poison my 5 year old niece got stung by a bee, also we were told a large group of church people would be attending so we couldn't camp out on the area we normally camp out at, that was a bummer  because no church group ever showed up we were there from Thursday-Sunday and then last but not least there was a power outage from around 5 pm Saturday until the time we left on Sunday at 11 am it was difficult taking the small children to the restroom in the middle of the night in absolute darkness, this was not a good experience like we always have had at Rancho Oso sad to say

A REALLY FANTASTIC PLACE.  We wanted to go horseback riding but due to it having rained the day before we were perfectly happy just visiting the horses. They were so nice to let us hang around and the horses looked beautiful and very well cared for. 

Well worth a visit!!  We will certainly come back on a dry sunny day to ride!!

I had a fabulous time horse camping for a weekend at Rancho Oso. We had a cabin with a double bed and bunk beds which fit three comfortably, although we did need to move some chairs to make room for our saddles. The cabin was equipped with a small fridge, ceiling fan and coffee maker. There is an outlet to charge devices in the cabin. We trailered two horses up with us and they stayed at the pipe corrals nearby. The trails are nice and relatively flat, although they can get quite rocky at parts. They are mostly shaded with lovely views of the forest, mountains and riverbed. They also loop around so there is very little chance of getting lost while riding. 

The grounds are very beautiful, very serene and pastoral situated next to scenic mountains. We were able to buy firewood at the front gate and roast marshmallows both nights with one bundle of firewood (available at gate for $10.80 with tax). They do require that the fire be out by 10:30 which felt very early on a Saturday night.

In addition to cabins there are covered wagons with cots and a tepee which would probably fit two people comfortably. 

We brought snacks and beverages but ate at the Stone Lodge both Friday and Saturday nights. The Saturday tri tip was very tasty. 

Overall I highly recommend Rancho Oso for horse camping.

My family has  gone camping at Rancho Oso for years and we have never had a bad experience until this last weekend, a tree fell which I understand Mother Nature, but several bee hives were found and the exterminator that went gave us literally 10 minute warning for us to leave the area we had 5 children with us a group of 18 of us total it wasn't long before he started with the poison my 5 year old niece got stung by a bee, also we were told a large group of church people would be attending so we couldn't camp out on the area we normally camp out at, that was a bummer  because no church group ever showed up we were there from Thursday-Sunday and then last but not least there was a power outage from around 5 pm Saturday until the time we left on Sunday at 11 am it was difficult taking the small children to the restroom in the middle of the night in absolute darkness, this was not a good experience like we always have had at Rancho Oso sad to say.

What a disappointing experience.  This week my husband "surprised" me with a 5 year anniversary trip to Rancho Oso.  The draw was the opportunity to give our dog a lesson to herd sheep.  We were very full of anticipation for that opportunity.  When we first arrived at the Ranch, they directed us to a trailer that would be our accommodation.  Now at $120 a night, you expect decent but not spectacular.  When we opened the door to the trailer, I felt physically sick.  I told my husband there was no way I would even sit on the sofa much less sleep in the bed - the place was disgusting and it was about 120 degrees inside.  And if we could not get our money back, I would sleep in our vehicle for the  night.  When we explained our displeasure to the guard at the gate, he showed us a cabin that we could move into.  The cabin is new and very nice and we were pleased that they were able to accommodate us. (Beware -  most of their accommodations are trailers - not cabins - so be sure to ask exactly what you are getting.  If you are okay with grungy accommodations, the trailer will suit your needs just fine.  If you want a clean place with a decent bed, ask for a cabin.)  After two nights there, I turned to my husband and said I could actually come back to this place (but only if we stayed in a cabin). It is beautiful country.  But then today we had our "herding" experience with Adrian Lopez, and I knew I'd never return.  Adrian was obnoxious and rude from the beginning.  Mind you the lesson - if you could call it that - was all of about 10 minutes long - at a cost of $20.  He took great pleasure in making you feel  like an idiot because you didn't understand what he wanted you to do at the moment he told you to do it. He barked commands rather than talk you through what he wanted the dog to do.  And when I didn't immediately do what he told me to do he hurled insults such as, "Do you understand English?"  "You don't speak English do you?"  "Do you know what your rear-end is? I told you to move your rear-end against the fence.  You obviously don't know what your rear-end is."  In between, he made some rude comments about women and "liberals".  I would have walked out of the ring after his first round of nastiness but my dog was clearly enjoying the opportunity to herd. If you enjoy being continually ridiculed while you're trying to learn something new, take Adrian's "class".  He's guaranteed to ruin your day. Otherwise, spend your money somewhere where they don't take your business for granted.  I would never go back to Rancho Oso and I cannot recommend it.

This is the worst campground ever.  I grew up camping so I know it comes to finding a great campground.  And this place surely isn't it. There r a ton of nasty loud crows that join in with some kind blue birds to serenade u at the crack of dawn. Sounds like they r having a brawl of some sort.  The wasp that come out after that r super dangerous, especially if anyone r allergic to them. They come so close to u while u cook and eat. So uncomfortable. 

It's cool and all that they have running showers and toilets. But seriously do they have to look so old, run down and dingy? I mean come one now, it doesn't cost much to give them a bit of a face lift. Especially if ur gonna charge $60 a night for every sight.  

Camping should be a way to get away from the heat during hot summer days. Why is it so damn hot and dry there? I know it's that's no body's fault (same goes for the birds) but man it sure doesn't call for a nap in a hammock. Ur better off driving down everyday ur there to the beach. Which defeats the purpose of camping.

CAUTION  you must go over a steep mountain pass to get here. The toad cautions that trucks / vehicles with trailers over 26' NOT ADVISED. Make sure if you do attempt it that you have good breaks and are not overweight. This park has no usable cell service (Verizon). It's 35-45 minutes from town/ANY amenities both ways, again, over the mountain. 45 minutes to the beach. Sites are small and packed together pretty tightly. The W/E only sites are in the valley and there are less people down there, but the bathroom is older. The laundry is outdoors and costs $2 wash/$1.50 dry, and they will not give you change, so bring quarters. The bathrooms have 2 stalls and 2 showers each. They are well kept and not run down. There are flys EVERYWHERE, I'm guessing because of the horses. You cannot sit outside or have your windows opened unless you want to get swarmed - and they bite! We were booked for a week and left after a night- it just wasn't worth it. Headed to the Palm Springs Oasis TT Resort instead.

I really hope that word never gets out about how incredible this place is. It is correct that it is on Paradise road. Just so you know, GPS can't find it. Also, if you are a cell phone, iphone/pad, Blackberry addict, then this is not the place for you. You can't get reception here. 

There are great site for tent camping. Lots of shade. There ae also hook-ups for rvs. There is a great playground for kids, a pool, and mini golf. This place is very kid friendly. 

This place used to be a dude ranch back in the day and a lot of old Hollywood movie stars used to come here in the 20's and 30's. Part of the original house on the property still exists and is in use.

We stayed here April 6-8 2021. The only reason I'm giving a 4 star is because I feel they should have let us know in advance the place had no power to give us the choice to cancel the trip, thankfully we got the power back at 10:00 pm on the 6th. The other inconvenience is you need to reserve a horse at the time of booking but they don't let you know in advance either and my grandkids couldn't enjoy this amenity. They give you a specific one hour time in the swimming pool which I feel it's a very good idea considering the Covid situation.
The place is fun and the cabins are well organized and super clean. Petra does a very good job.
I hope to be able to return sometime in the future. Highly recommend it.

On December 22, 2019, I made an online reservation for Rancho Oso through a link on their website for March 25, 2020.  Do to a family matter, I called the next day and canceled Dec 23, 2019.   I was charged a $25 cancellation fee.  In my years of camping experience and learnings from  other campers this policy is very unreasonable to be charged for a cancellation 3 months in advance !  Be wary of making any reservations here ;(

We visited Rancho Oso on an RV trip around California and it was by far the worst campground we visited during the 2 week trip. The place is worn down and the bathrooms are dirty and old. There are millions of flies all over the place because of the many trees in the area. No wifi or phone signals unless you drive back to the main road.  
The folks that works there are really nice and was helpful and the view surrounding the campground is amazing.

This should be 4 stars, the folks here are friendly, the cabins (more like a tiny house) and very homey and cozy. It is about 20-30 mins from Santa Barbara (a nice contrast from the Ocean).
The only issue was my with my daughters cabin, the water smelled like sulfur and they couldn't shower, make coffee, NOTHING!  Both times we spoke to people they said "just run the water awhile, it will be OK"... This was not the case.  As a result they had to have coffee with us every morning and shower elsewhere (a hassle).
On checkout they gave her 1 night free (which was very nice) but honestly it was more about enjoying the stay, not about the money. Honestly, it should cost them more than 1 star, but it was a nice break from the city.

We have a membership at thousand trails.  Unfortunately we have the campers pass which allows no benefit.  According to them you need to have there adventure pass to have priority.  FYI if you get a reservation with full hookups, your still not guaranteed a  
spot.  Its  first come first serve basis, even if you have a membership they try to persuade the people without a membership and give them full hookups and send people with memberships to area without full hookups. This has been our experience at Rancho Oso.  It's been impossible to even get a reservation within 60 days, it's always full.  Your chances are better if you are not a member.  Your chances are better if you have  Luxury RV.

We drove all around to check out camp sites and campgrounds for our RV. Very nice, clean facilities, and plenty of activities for families! Horses to ride, farm animals to watch, pools and hot tub available. Very attractive cabins to rent also.

Address is 3750 Paradise, my Srint HTC EVO Stopped us at the RO Headquarters miles before the actual ranch, but keep driving through the National Forest til you see the large sign on the right. The driveway is windy & long itself too.

Bring your horses, or ride theirs. Staff warmth is hit & miss. Seems like there's been some recent changes with management &/or staff? 

We came & spent Easter weekend.

One way to reconnect with nature, without leaving all amenities behind. We had no internet connection and it was a blessing! On our way to the ranch we hiked the Hot-spring trails and visited Solvang since check in time is 4pm. We brought coal for the grill and enjoyed dinner before it got (pitch) black outside. Even though you stay in a little cabin pack as tho you'll stay in a tent. Bring everything you possible need for camping. No stores close by. We did buy firewood onsite for the community fire pit. The pool was very nice. Horseback riding available too. The skeleton staff was extremely friendly and helpful. The place is tranquil with its surrounding mountains and horses "everywhere". It felt as we were in a movie set :)

I will say this has been one of the better thousand trail parks we have stayed at. Most of the way up the mountain it's nice paved roads. However 1 mile before the park entrance you have to go up a nasty little road. Not fun especially if you have a large rig.

That being said the park is beautiful. Most sites are full hookup although only 30 amps. Good water pressure and voltage though. The sites are in rows up a cliff and all have great views of the mountains.

Our row had bathrooms and laundry on it. Did not use the bathroom but laundry was decent. Couple of machines outside. They all worked and were reasonably priced.

Gate staff was very friendly as usual for the thousand trail parks we have stayed at. Overall great experience, could be 5 stars if they paved that entrance road and had 50 amp service.

We LOVE Rancho Oso! 

First of all -- you need to have appropriate expectations. This is not a 5-star hotel or glamping resort. The cabins don't have high-end mattresses and swanky amenities. But that's not why you go to Rancho Oso. My kids and I LOVE Rancho Oso, and we always stay in the cabins, which are very basic but have everything you really need. We can take our dogs with us and the kids can just be outdoors and just be kids. My kids' favorite activity at Rancho Oso is picking their way down the creek bed next to the buildings -- that seriously is what they would do all day long if I would let them. They love the horseback trail ride too! The lack of Wifi is a real blessing, because my kids and I have to spend time together, outdoors, in the most basic of circumstances and there is really nothing better. We have come to Rancho Oso many times and will come back many more times! 

TIP: BRING YOUR OWN FOOD -- like a lot of it. The kitchen cooks breakfast on the weekends and it's delicious, but you really do need to bring your own everything else. 

TIP#2: BRING VEHICLES for kids -- bikes, scooters, etc.

I know thousand trails it's a lot of crap for reasons beyond their control. I really love this place. It will be 65° in Santa Barbara, drive over the hill only 11 miles, and it is 95 or 100°. Though the road is winding, 11 miles is not far to go to town. Get supplies. Play at the beach, then drive back to the camp. We are staying here for four nights, and there are two pools. One pool is warmer than the other. Six person hot tub. There are horses to rent, teepees to rent, covered wagons to rent, a restaurant, a country store... This place has it all. It is very clean and everyone is really friendly. We have stayed at a few other thousand trails, and this is definitely one of our favorites.  The laundry facilities, the bathrooms, and the showers are very clean. 

Highly recommend this thousand trails property.

We had a wonderful 2 night stay in cabin 85. Perfect size for our fam of 4. Cabin had all the essentials. All equipment was in working order. Staff was friendly and courteous. Nearby hiking with beautiful views. Short drive to Cachuma Lake and just over the hill from Santa Barbara beaches. Definitely recommend this place as a beautiful and relaxing getaway. Dec/2019

Rancho Oso Thousand Trails- February 17-24, 2020

Bring your cowboy boots and hat. Rancho Oso Thousand Trails is a beautiful historic horse ranch/campground near the Los Padres National Forest. Nestled between Santa Barbara and Solvang off Hwy 154, Rancho Oso is a lovely campground with a western feel.

Reviews said the road was only one lane with nowhere to pass. The road is a narrow two-lane windy road and it's slightly more than a mile from Paradise Rd to the campground. Take it slow and there is enough room for RV's/cars to pass each other with no issues.

An attentive friendly staff keep the park in good condition. Everyone we dealt with were nice and helpful with any of our questions. Each took an interest to make sure our stay was going well.

On 310 acres not only are there places to camp in tents and RV's, you can stay in one of their many cabins, Conestoga covered wagons, or even a teepee. Being a working horse ranch there are plenty of activities to keep guests busy. Horseback riding is available by making reservations with one of the wranglers at the barn. Some of the previous reviews we saw stated a problem with flies. You do need to understand this is a ranch and there are flies on ranches. There were some flies during our February 2020 stay but they were not too bad. I imagine during the summer they could be a problem. We were told that the flies are from the many oak trees in the area and not from having horses.

Visit with the horses, goats, pigs, and ducks at the barn and surrounding pastures. Or go hiking on one of the many trails. We did the short waterfall hike up the hill behind the water tank. It's a nice little  1/2 mile hike that crosses a small stream multiple times up to a 30' waterfall.

With the national forest so close there is some wildlife in the area. During our stay we saw a couple flocks of wild turkeys and a few deer.

Two swimming pools and a hot tub are on the property for relaxing. Play miniature golf, horseshoes, basketball, volleyball or pickleball on the campground courts. Our dogs loved smelling around the small dog park area. It is just a fenced in sandy area but a good place to let the dogs stretch their legs and run a bit. Near the store is a nice area to barbeque/picnic and a playground for the kids.

Plenty of washers and driers located outside under the patio covers at each of the restroom areas. They looked new, all worked and were in good shape. Priced $2.00 per wash and $1.50 to dry. You can add quarters for additional drying time.

The camp store provides any necessities you may need along with videos and recreational equipment to rent. The store also has a propane filling station.

Friday and Saturday evenings there are dinners and Saturday and Sunday mornings breakfasts available at the Stone Lodge. We had burgers, cowboy beans, and potato salad on Friday night for about $8 each. It was an ok meal. Saturday evening, I had the Tri Tip Dinner ($16) and my girlfriend had the Grilled Chicken Breast Dinner ($14). Both came with salad, mashed potatoes, and green beans. The Tri Tip was pretty good, but my girlfriend's chicken was burnt and dried out. 

Our T-Mobile phones had no phone service and we had very limited Wi-Fi with our Verizon Hotspot. While there is some Wi-Fi available at the Adult Lodge, we found it slow and hard to get much done on. With our boosted HDTV antenna, we were only able to get about eight TV channels.

We joked that the only thing missing at this campground was a lake. While they don't have a lake of their own, just a short drive down Hwy 154 is Cachuma Lake that is a perfect place to get out on the water if that's your thing. A short drive to the little Danish Village of Solvang makes a fun little day trip. It's a nice little town to explore the windmills, Danish stores and restaurants. Also, a short drive to Santa Barbara gives you anything you need from a good-sized city.

If you're looking for someplace to get away from the hustle and bustle of city life Rancho Oso is a the place to do it. This is one of the best Thousand Trails Campgrounds we have stayed at. We wish all of them were this nice and well taken care of. Our stay at Rancho Oso was great, and we would happily stay here again in the future.

Cabin was clean but when we ran the water, it smelled like rotten eggs. We called almost daily for a week prior to our trip, trying to book a horseback ride. When we got there, we stood at the horseback riding office and the worker hid behind the barn. We had to go ask her to help us make a reservation for a ride. She did not want to take us on a ride though we were ready and there was nobody else coming to ride, and could not even give a good reason as to why (other than it was 2 pm and they don't *usually* do rides that late). She was very rude and unapologetic. The next day was my birthday and she said they were not doing any rides that day because they were under-staffed. The horseback riding is 90% of the reason we come to Rancho Oso, so we were very disappointed. Poor customer service, not worth your money!

This was our first trip using our Thousand Trails Zone Pass. Since this area of Santa Barbara Off the 154 is usually beautiful, even when it's bone dry like it is right now, we were hopeful it would be pretty. We are not fans of TT Rancho Oso. First of all, we couldn't score a full hookup site online and settled for water and electric only. These sites are small and rocky, and the general area is a dust bowl. We didn't even have a place to sit at our campsite and our hauler isn't huge. Hardly any shade. Not much of that being in nature vibe here. We asked an employee about the full hookup sites and he said they re long term "leases" which just mean they are always full. We are disappointed after our first experience. Trying Idyllwild in a couple weeks. Praying that one is better. Pretty basic RV life at Thousand Trails so far. Kind of ghetto.

POSITIVES: Huge resort secluded in a nice valley in the hills above SB, very spread out layout. Some green areas, and fun things to do- cool playground, horseshoes, nice pickle ball courts (bring your own equipment), tiny lil mini golf course. Ranch animals including horses you can ride for a fee. Our W/E spot was very level even though was in dirt, and it had nice shade tree. Staff are chill and leave you alone which is peaceful :) cute lil store with necessities if you forget anything. Dump station had a hose to wash your hands! 

NEGATIVES: Drive in is pretty steep so be prepared for that.. and it is WAY OUT in the hills, despite the Santa Barbara address. There is barely any WiFi at the lodge, none elsewhere, though the website implies WiFi park wide. There was old nasty abandoned 5th wheel trailer in the spot next to ours, and trash all over the grounds. The safari tents are busted up and eyesore need to be torn down. Dirt roads in back half of park leading to the W/E Rv spots and tent spots. Porta-potty and it's lingering smell next to pool.

My family along with about SIX other familes decided to plan a group camping trip at Rancho Oso; this is only the begininning. Upon arriving to the property, my mother was told by the man at the gate that they were fully booked despite the fact that we had reservations months ahead of time. From the start we had an uneasy and unwelcoming vibe. When we and the other group members arrived, we were surprised to find out that every camp site is first come, first serve. We have gone camping every year for the past seven years and each place we've encountered was considerate enough to allow us to reserve our own sites. That way, it is much easier for those who plan on camping in large groups. At Rancho Oso, each family in our group was split into several different sites instead of staying together. Later on in the night, our neighboring campsite told us that we were in their "land" even though we came there first. When we asked one of the workers to clarify the boundaries of the sites, he refused to take the time to even help. He seemed skeptical as to which side was which and just got back on his golf cart and left us to our own devices. 

The staff. The staff, when each family came at different times, charged each and every one of us EVEN THOUGH we paid for our reservations in full. Another family was coming in a day later, so we told the man at the gate that they would be the second family in one of our sites and still, they charged them thirty more dollars.

We went back to the entrance to discuss with the workers as to why we were all unresonably charged $20, $30, $60 more than necessary. Every time we spoke to a different worker, each person came up with an explanation completely different from the other. For example: Each site is allowed two tents and two parked cars. When one of the ladies got frustrated talking to us, she said that we can only have one site and one car per. 

They kept making up things that were opposite of what the advertised on their website and brochures. When we went back a second time to talk to them, the manager refused to refund us our money and ended up telling us that he would refund our stay as long as we leave that same day. He (Bill) was also threatening to call the sheriff on us EVEN THOUGH they were the ones who were wrongfully charging us for the reservations we already paid for IN FULL. We stated that we had multiple witnesses and he specifically stated, "I don't care if you have witnesses!"

All of us peacefully stayed there and obeyed the rules that they set out for each family. It's not about the amount of money that we had to pay, but the specific reason WHY we had to pay (again). 

Additionally, there is nothing exciting or enjoyable at Rancho Oso. Almost every single building is run down, the available activities are tedious, no one is allowed to listen to music, there is only one fire pit for about 20-30 sites, and the most of all, the workers are rude. 

10/10 would not recommend to come here. If you want to get ripped off, then by all means, go to Rancho Oso.

I like the campground. The staff is very nice and make you feel welcome. But, I probably will not be returning. The WiFi in the clubhouse is very poor and no cell service or internet or TV service. They have leeches that live full time there that think they own the TVs in the clubhouse.  They need to do some repairs badly. They say they have no money for repairs and are short of help but Equity lifestyle made 7 billion $ last year. The front door of the club house has a big hole in it that a little putty and paint would fix. Just a small example. First impressions are important people!

Ranch Oso Rv and camping located off Paradiae Road approximately 15 minutes away from Red Rock-Just off  Highway 154.  This resort offers all kinds of camping sites for tents RV and cabins.  
The weather was wonderful, the hottest day was 100 degrees however the resort did  offer 3 pools to help keep you cool.   Among the amenities was a volleyball  and basketball and picker nickel court.  

Depending on your location in the resort you may not get Wi-Fi reception or be able to make phone calls so for those trying to get away from video games, cellphones , endless calls this may be your perfect escape! 

For those with a more adventurous spirit there is horseback riding and hiking trails along wooded trails.  Due to the extreme fire danger we were not able to enjoy a fire pit at night but I am certain those traveling in the late fall and winter or early spring would not come across this issue.  

I want to explain my rating was lowered solely for the purpose of kick of  cleanliness of the bathrooms.  The restrooms and showers were cleaned 2x a day and with the high traffic I felt they needed to be maintained more frequently.   Aside from that I purchased a yearly membership through Thousand Trails which includes this site and 16  others in California.

If the definition of camping is loading too much of your crap into your car/truck/van and stressing all the way down the road that you've forgotten something - and then it turns out you did - somoananitch !..  Well, then - I camp. However, I only camp within the geographic triad of my needs. Those being; 1) 20 minutes from home 2) 20 minutes from pizza 3)10 minutes from a B&B - do the math. It has always worked well for me. 
Located within the Los Padres National Forest, Rancho Oso works nicely in the confines of my triad - though even if it didn't, you might be able to get me drunk enough to agree to go again. Provided its not summer - it gets hot out there in the Santa Ynez valley Nancy. Full on full service camp sites. Good for tents and maybe better for RV's. As you mosey around you'll no doubt be intrigued by the five little houses on the prairie and wished you had reserved one. It's a Horse ranch with horse riding. Big Bulls lounging about in the fields. A restaurant, an air conditioned lounge. adult lodge area with fireplace. Great for kids. 2 pools, playground, mini golf (OK that's a stretch, but check it out and discover what you can and can't do with re-purposed ping pong tables) I've only done the group camp site. Which by the way, hopefully our paths never cross up there, because I go with our preschool families that includes about 65 kids five and under.."does that gun above the mantle work or is it just for show?".. 
The bathrooms are clean with hot water showers etc. The staff is very nice and accommodating. You could argue it's a bit tired in places, but you're camping for crying out loud. So no complaints, other than there were no mints left on my pillow in the tent - so minus one star. (as well as the fact I'm not emotionally prepared to give anything with the word "camp" within the subject matter a 5 star rating).

We only lasted 2 nights of an intended 5 night stay. The reason: flies, flies and more flies!

I spoke to many workers who all replied, 'yes they are really bad this year'.  

They literally swarmed around us as we were setting up. Kids had to go sit in car they were so bad. . 

Also, the wireless that they advertise only works in one specific location and was spotty at best. 

Lastly, we made horseback reservations for our kids and grandkids. When they showed up at 10a, they had our reservation but were confused about it and offered something at 1:30p. 

Was final straw! Packed up and left. Won't be back either and would advise any future inquirers to think twice.

Rancho Oso RV, a scenic rv park and campground tucked down in a valley in the mountains above Santa Barbara.
We have stayed at this gem located on a horse ranch with views on all sides a few times and have always enjoyed the location.
Most of the sites are on a terrace with a beautiful view of the canyon and mountains. There is a separate section with water and electric with tent sites as well.
Great place to walk your dogs on the dirt road or trails within or outside the campground.
Amenities include two pools, hot tub, pickleball courts, two lodges, and some other games.
They have a well stocked camp store.
There is also horse camping area with corrals and cabins.
They offer trail riding at a fee, and special events on the holidays. The kids will enjoy visiting the barnyard animals.
The park is about 35 minutes drive to Santa Barbara, Solvang, Santa Ynez and wine country.
Hiking and biking trails are close by.
Night provides a full sky of star for you gazers.
We have two hotspots and only Verizon works 50 percent of the day for internet or streaming Netflix. People with boosters and Antennas seem to have a better signal. 
The turn off into park can be one lane in places, so do not arrive before 1:00 to miss meeting an rv leaving.
I recommend this park for a weekend or a longer stay.

This review is for Rancho Oso's 1 hour horseback riding.  I made a reservation for myself and 4 other family members about a week in advance.  The reservation process is a bit inefficient, because the staff does not immediately respond to your phone call, you need to leave them a voicemail, and they call you back.  So if you get unlucky, you're going to be playing some phone tag.  Overall, we had a great experience, and we would all come back.   

Pros: 
-knowledgable instructors (firm but still friendly) 
-absolutely beautiful trail, nice relaxing pace
-calm, well-trained horses
-fun and enjoyable for all ages and experience levels 

Cons: 
-you can't bring phones / cameras on the trail ride 
  note:  If you ask, they will let you take a few pictures at the end, before you get off your horse
-the helmets (optional if you are over 18) are really dirty

We came here to visit my brother and his family during their cabin camping. Their amenities are swimming pool, mini golf, basketball, horse back riding and probably more. We got to do most of it  except for the horse riding, no riding on Tuesdays. I must say it is not a bad place at all. Wide variety of things you can do. I visited their tent sites, smaller scale but it is doable. You cannot burn fire woods except for charcoal. Their public restrooms are clean which is good. You need to have a verizon or att to get a reception, I have verizon and still had issues making phone calls, so be ready for it. For sure there's a way for it if you are staying there for longer time. The cabin we saw was small but good for family of 4-6. You will have to make sure it is clean prior to checking out or you won't get your $150 deposit. The staff were courteous. The place is far, and really have to pay attention coming here because of the skinny road. Overall, a good place to visit.

This quiet and isolated campground is nestled in a gorgeous scenic valley.  Morning and evening views of the surrounding Santa Ynez mountains are simply breathtaking!  We took CA-154 up to Paradise Road on our drive in and it was an absolute breeze.  Several online posts had mentioned concerns about the 1.2 mile paved road that leads into the campground, but our 28 foot class A RV with tow car had no issues.  It is a bit narrow in spots so take your time and be careful.  Warning: there is no cell service in the park.  We had to drive 7 miles out to CA-154 to get a decent signal on our AT&T and Verizon phones.  The lodge is closed but free wifi is available on the deck.  Unfortunately it is quite slow.  We stayed in full hookup back-in site #47 for 3 weeks and it was quite comfortable.  Rancho Oso has 7 parallel rows of 30 amp FHU sites.  I recommend going up to the top if possible to get the best views.  Restrooms were clean and washers and dryers are located outside.  The machines worked great and they take quarters.  Recycling is available for all items except cardboard.  We ordered 3 different packages during our stay and they all arrived without issue.  Large groups of wild turkeys roam around the park and we really enjoyed watching them.  The entire staff was wonderful but Len at the ranger station was especially kind.  He always had a smile on his face and was ready to answer questions.  Len also said that horseback riding is available at Rancho Oso for $50.00 per hour.
