Not really our thing but some folks will undoubtedly enjoy it.  Onsite country store had a decent selection of products and DVDs are available for rent.  The pool is open but you have to reserve a 1 hour timeslot.  Thank you Rancho Oso team for a wonderful experience!

I came to Rancho Oso from San Diego. We stayed in the new cabins. I had expected the same sort of supplies that a hotel might have. I was shocked that their was only a bottom sheet and a thin blanket on the queen bed and only a sheet on the bunk beds. They did supply "extra" sheets/blankets but you would be charged an additional fee for using them. They also required you to sweep and wipe down your cabin prior to leaving. One of our cabins the hot water didn't work. We camped in December and it was a bit chilly. The heaters didn't provide adequate heat to keep the small cabin warm. The horse guide we had was wonderful and all the animals on the property looked well cared for. We enjoyed a nice wine tasting and was able to meet several other campers. The campground is very peaceful and quiet. In fact several times we forgot there were so many other campers as you never heard them. The cabin was nicely built and new. I brought horses to trail ride with and I was glad my horse had front shoes on as the trail ride had a lot of rock. All in all a nice campground but a bit over priced for what you get.

I spent $378.56 for two nights in a TT Cabin. The cabin was old and very small. The sofa bed was broken, with completely broken and loose springs, almost impossible to sleep. The Gas Grill was out of order. The water in the shower went down so slowly that it took 1 hour to take a shower.
Camp amenities were a joke. The pool was dirty full of dead insects and leaves.
And you know what? When you check-in they give you the camp rules where is stated that before check-out you have to clean the cabin by yourself or they will charge you a fee!!!

Stay away from this place!

It was kind of a first come first serve when it came to RV spots which was nice. Staff at the front gate immediately tried selling us a membership before we even pulled in. It was very very hot. Make your horse riding reservations before you even get there. It books up very fast. The pool. Where do I start. No one monitors the pool. Ever.  The rules are very clear at the pool. Common sense rules. Every day there was dogs, glass bottles, alcohol and food. I even heard about a near drowning of a 2 year old during the 4th of July weekend due to the mom being too under the influence to properly take care of her child. The pool was crowded and kids running. I was just waiting for someone to slip and fall. I spoke with the store employee and she said there wasn't enough staff to monitor the pool and if I saw a problem to go to the start of the park and call a ranger. She stated that the other thousand trails have someone watching the pool, but they don't have the staff. Even after talking to the store employee and making my concerns known on the first day we were there, I never once saw a ranger and we were there for 4 days. The worst. My husband took my 9 year old daughter to the bathroom. Waited outside and while she was in there a couple,a man and a young woman came out of the bathroom together. Maybe he was just changing or maybe something worse, I can't take that chance with my daughter. The bathroom next to us was not kept up either. 2 out of the 4 days it was not cleaned. It showed and was obvious. I can definitely say I'm glad we did not sign up for a membership and we will never go back to this site.

name: Goleta Butterfly Grove
categories: Active Life, Pets, Hiking, Pet Services, Amusement Parks, Parks

yelp_reviews.text: The park and some trails are still open, but don't expect to see any butterflies even during migration season. We drove over an hour and only saw about ten butterflies. We left very disappointed.

I've had this place bookmarked for quite some time! So when the Pump planned a birthday trip to Santa Barbara for me, I figured instead of seeing the seals we saw last time in Carpinteria, it would be great to see the butterflies this time! (And it was!)

If you come here, thinking that you'll be surrounded by butterflies flying all around you - you'll be disappointed because it doesn't quite look like that!

You'll see some butterflies flying about high above you but the majority of what there is to see is this - high up in the trees! Clusters of butterflies! 

This area is covered with butterfly clusters that are harder to see (and quite honestly look like leaves - but you can barely make out the pattern) but the trees and foliage are just storybook and breathtaking! I love it! And it's just so relaxing! 

And don't miss checking out the sea cliffs when walking out a little from the wooded butterfly grove! It's seriously breath taking! 

What a beautiful place to take in!
Tranquil trail surrounded by eucalyptus trees! Visited in January and there were lots of flocks of butterflies hanging from trees.

I had always wanted to see Monarch butterflies and for the past few months I have had my eyes on this place. Finally we were in Santa Barbara!

We set out in the morning on a brisk walk towards the grove and sat down their for the first 10 minutes without being able to spot any butterflies. Then slowly you see one, two, three fluttering overhead. You start following their movements to see where they are going and then all of a sudden BAM, you find a cluster. Look to the left BAM, another cluster. Before we knew it we noticed the entire tree was made out of butterflies. It was unreal! We spent a good 45 minutes watching them and showing others before it became a bit too busy for my liking.

The only thing that bugged me was the amount of dogs that are off-leash. Apparently it's allowed, but I feel that it should not be at the grove as they run in areas that people are also not allowed in. Plus I saw (and smelled) people not picking up after their dogs, or leaving the filled bags along the path. Such a shame to an otherwise fairytale location.

I have a 5 year old and a 3 year old and this was a wonderful way to spend time outside. The walk is very easy and pretty much flat the entire way. We did not see any butterflies but the weather in mid-February was delightful. It would have been easy to send even more time outside if we had decided to head down to the beach. If you have a dog  this is a nice walk for them. The trail markers do make it easy to stay on the path.

I went in early November of 2013. The scenery was beautiful, however, something big was missing------ BUTTERFLIES!!!! We were there for about 15 minutes and saw 5 butterflies. I know a gal who went to the one in Northern California and loved it. She said the trees were covered in amazing butterflies. I expected to see the same in Goleta but unfortunately I did not. I you are looking for a nice walk in a pretty eucalyptus grove without butterflies then you will love it.
Good easy flat hike through eucalyptus trees to the beach. Dogs welcome. Butterflies in spring. Be wary of tar on beach.

If you want to visit the Elwood Grove and see lots of beautiful monarch butterflies, this is the time to do it. They come to hibernate here from Canada in November and they stay till the end of February.

Check their site before you visit, they have all the info you need. The grove is easy to find, off 101. The parking for the place it's right off Hollister Ave., to the left. Across from the elementary school. Free to park and free to visit.

Once you park, you'll see a big sign and a big trail. Get going! Bring your walking shoes! It takes 10-15 minutes walk through the woods, to get to the sanctuary.

Follow the signs with the cute butterfly design on them and sooner than you know you'll see the sign that will lead you to the grove. We ran into a couple who missed the grove, they haven't seen any butterflies. The place is easy to find as long as you follow those signs. When you get there, look up, and you'll see the eucalyptus branches full of butterflies hanging down.

There are docents at the grove after 11 am - 2pm in the weekends. The docent we talked to was a nice lady, answered our questions, offered us her binoculars, gave us useful info. She said that it's great to visit between X-mas and New Year's.

We were very luck when we got there, we saw thousands of butterflies, some flying,this grove is a special, unique place. It's worth making the trip to Goleta on a nice day. Skip if it's raining.

If you like to hike and walk, keep following the main trail to the Bluffs to see some incredible beach views from high up.

Very enjoyable excursion. I love this place, it's pure magic!

This place was very impressive.

I'm told that the only time to visit is November thru February, and the best time to go is in the morning or early afternoon when it's warm/sunny outdoors.

It's a free park, and the "grove" is located just off the beach in the eucalyptus trees.

If it's cold, the monarch butterflys aren't flying around and can be hard to find, but once you notice "those aren't dried leaves," you realize that there are thousands and thousands of butterlys resting on the trees.  If it's warm/sunny, I'm told that many of the butterflys will also be flying around.

If you're in the area, you should definitely take an hour out of your day to check this place out.

TIP: BRING BINOCULARS or a camera with a good zoom.

It's the little things! Having an injured butterfly feel safe enough to land on me just made my day! #monarchbutterfly #beingonewithnature

On top of that - the area itself was welcoming for locals and guests alike. There was a group of four super friendly/ energetic dogs with their owners that were playing in the creek.

It's about a half mile walk to the grove, and it's fairly flat most of the way.  Not wheelchair or stroller accessible the whole way, but an easy hike.  
When you arrive at the grove, look very closely at the clumps of leaves... they might not be leaves at all.  Plan to arrive around 9:30-10, the butterflies will just begin to become active when you hike to the main site.

It looks like several other trails meet up with this trail, but we had two five year olds with us, and didn't want to risk having to carry them back. A very interesting and fun 90 minute excursion.  Bring binoculars if you have them!

Beautiful short walk. Didn't see many butterflies. But it leads you into the beach. Real easy. About 10 minutes. Nice view on top. And you can go down if you want. Great for biking. Be sure not to leave late like we did because not much like when coming back. Worth a short sunset trip :)

Really neat place. The docent, Mary Jane, was very informative and answered many questions. We even got to see a chrysalis. It's sad when she explained that only 4500 butterflies were counted this year when  there used to be 300,000. She recommended that everyone plant milkweed in their yards.. I wish I could help but I don't live here though

Alert: Monarch Butterflies missing. Last seen near a trail with kids running with dogs, teenagers whizzing by on bicycles, and businessmen and women walking cats. Okay, I made up the last part.

I searched high and low here (by "high and low" I probably mean walked .75 of a mile on a trail) and saw zero butterflies. The trail is very, very rough going. It's as if someone drenched the dirt with water (making it mud) and then allowed it to dry into nearly unusable walkways.

This is one uncomfortable walk. It does seem perfect for a mountain bike, but given the plethora of folks I saw this late afternoon and the paucity of butterflies, I'm thinking that Happy D. is on to something.

We had such a great time here today. Even if there had been no butterflies, it was easy to park in the parking lot (right across from Ellwood Elementary), and the walk was beautiful. But, in fact, there were butterflies! Tons of them! It was surreal to see thousands bunched up together on branches and hundreds flying around. We will definitely be back and will bring our extended family when they come to visit in a few weeks.

We had visions of being ambushed by thousands of monarch butterflies and having them land on our outstretched arms! Well, that wasn't quite the reality when my family & I visited the butterfly grove. We hiked a fairly easy trail and discovered a few butterflies floating around, but upon closer examination, we noticed a couple of branches that were covered with monarch butterflies. We resisted the temptation to throw something at the butterfly branches to make them fly all over the place! The experience wasn't exactly what we had expected, but we were happy we got to see some butterflies.
great access to the ocean. I didn't see any butterflies but I didn't mind because of the ocean and bluffs.

Nice find on the way back to LA from Solvang Area. We came in November so didn't see any butterflies -- so I guess we were a bit early.  I think they arrive in December through end of February.  Still enjoyed a nice walk through the trees which do keep you quite cool in the shade.  You can walk for awhile and while it's mostly flat, you can go all the way to the beach.  Good for the whole family and saw lots of dogs -- many off leash which is fine by me as long as they are friendly.  Even a few bikes on the trails which are pretty wide.  Will be back for sure.

I've lived in Ventura County for 2/3 of my life and never made it here until this past weekend. Shame on me.

It's not migration season, but there's quite a few monarch butterflies roaming around.

We started our stroll close to midday.
Luckily, it was a little cloudy so we didn't have to suffer with the heat. It was a bit of a bummer because it was so beautiful in Santa Paula when we left and when we got to Goleta it was all gloomy.

We parked in the lot facing the tall trees on the left and apartment buildings to the right. Basically just followed the trail and made random turns until we hit the beach. Then we trekked down to chill in the water for a bit.

The views from the top can't be beat. It gets even better when you climb down to the beach. The only downside is the further down you walk, the more tar you'll be covered with. I didn't really pay attention to it until the smell got stronger. Then I noticed there were small black spots everywhere. At first I thought they were rocks or shells, but nope. It's a such a shame since the area is so nice. It specially sucks for your pet children who end up being covered in tar.

With that aside, I highly recommend this area. We did get to see some butterflies at the very end. They were over by the tall trees towards the exit/entrance.

There's quite a few areas where you can stop and chill so bring some snacks.

I'll definitely be back.
Probably next week.

I always love coming here to go walking.  There's a little something for everyone.  If you're staying cool, there's a ton of shade provided by the eucalyptus trees that the butterflies love.  It's sad that there are so few monarchs these days, but that's all the more reason to come here to see if you can spot them.  I came here a few times as a young kid (before I lived right here) and remember it being absolutely full of monarchs.

I have to say that it is also easy to keep your distance from people here.  I tend to come here during what I perceive to be low volume times during the weekdays in order to have the fewest chances of seeing people, and it's definitely a large enough place that you won't see very many other people.  It's always nice to be able to enjoy outdoor spaces during a pandemic, and I'm glad that this place is within easy walking distance.

Not what we expected at all especially since we drove over two and a half hours to see monarchs migrate and unfortunately there were hardly any butterflies to see :( It might have been too cold or too late in the day because we saw at most ten or so of them. The parking lot was tiny as well and we ended up parking in a nearby neighborhood instead--although, we did see other grove-goers park at the elementary school (Ellwood elementary) across the street. In spite of the lack of butterflies to see, there were redeeming qualities to the place. After we exited the grove we came upon a grassy slope that led to cliffs that provided a beautiful view of the ocean! I'd say we spent more time on those cliffs taking photos than in the grove looking for monarchs. So, while the experience wasn't worth the drive, it would make a nice stop for your next road trip or a casual afternoon walk.

One of the best spots in Goleta to take a walk, a run, or let  your dog off the leash. I come here all the time for meditative walks. The butterflies are beautiful for sure but the vast open space and multitude of walking trails makes this place really special.

Lovely lovely walk and hike. We didn't see too many butterflies. We just saw a couple  white butterflies. but I would definitely come here again because the walk was so beautiful. It looked like one of those painted scenes where a victorian woman with a parasol would be sitting on one of the many logs in the field of flowers.

Nice easy scenic hike for the whole family to enjoy  that goes all the way to the ocean cliffs at the end of the trails.

Dogs are allowed but they need to be on leashes.

Cyclists also come here too.

We didn't see many monarch butterflies here; probably because it wasn't the season too but the hike was very enjoyable.

This place is so beautiful, I am from goleta but moved up to Oregon many years ago. Santa Barbara will always have my heart, so my husband and I chose to drive down and have our wedding at this majestic place.
It was perfect.

Easily one of the most peaceful areas of Goleta.

The Ellwood Butterfly Grove is an excellent place to
escape the mundane trials and tribulations of
everyday life. I see the space as a total oasis and
while it's adjacent to a residential neighborhood,
most definitely feels completely secluded.

You'll find acres of wildlife, paths, and natural
vegetation including a large number of eucalyptus
trees. The trees are not native but were planted
over 150 years ago; that said, I couldn't imagine
Goleta without their presence. The aroma, the
leaves, and the myriad butterflies they attract are
nothing short of breathtaking.

The walking/biking trail is lovely and goes straight
out to a very secluded and clean beachfront. It's a
total hidden gem of Goleta. I admire how carefully
preserved it is, and how clean the trails are in
general.

If you are truly looking to unwind and become one
with nature, check out this mild and beautiful
preserve!

name: iSurf
categories: Surfing, Active Life, Surf Schools, Specialty Schools, Education

yelp_reviews.text: I've probably had 25 different surf instructors on my lifelong quest to ride waves, and so many of their lessons are contradictory, ill-suited to different body types or vague ("just feel the wave"). I went out with Thomas from iSurf on a small day and within 5 minutes of listening and looking, he had a half dozen specific, non-obvious tips to help me catch waves, improve my technique, and have more fun. We paddled out in Ventura and I probably caught 8+ small waves within an hour and had an amazing time. A good day for me is usually 2-3 waves at best. Thomas had me feeling comfortable and confident and eager to get out there again. Needless to say, he was incredibly friendly and considerate as well as thoughtful. Definitely one of the best experiences I've had surfing anywhere (including Hawaii, Costa Rica, NorCal, SoCal, and East Coast). Thomas and iSurf are also incredibly communicative and accommodative with scheduling.

Exactly what I was going for when I signed up for the lessons. 
Thomas is a really cool guy, the private lesson is really great for learning a ton about positioning on the board, paddling, standing up on the board, and maintaining balance. I was standing on the board on the first day, and hopped into group lessons the next day. It was super easy to coordinate, and Thomas even recommended the Airbnb place I ended up staying at in Summerland, which turned out great. Definitely was a major highlight on my trip to Santa Barbara. Would recommend iSurf to anyone wanting to learn how to surf.

Just had the best first-time surfing experience we could have possibly hoped for with iSurf!  Our group was four mothers and our four teenage daughters, all surfing novices.  We were each a little nervous, but Thomas and Mariah spent time instructing us on the beach beforehand, taught us some surfing basics very clearly and concisely, and addressed all of our trepidation with kindness and humor.

When we hit the water, we felt prepared and excited and Thomas and Mariah were great about pushing us into the waves and letting us know right when we needed to pop up, then giving specific feedback after almost every wave so we could keep progressing.  All eight of us first-time surfers caught plenty of waves, felt successful, and most importantly had a blast out there together!  Everything about our experience exceeded the expectations of all four moms and our daughters and everyone left wanting to surf again.  I am now planning on taking my whole family down for a family surf session with these fantastic instructors.
ISurf is amazing!!  I have the best time learning to surf with ISurf!!  I'm having the most amazing surf trip in Mexico with ISurf!! I recommend ISurf for sure!

I had such a great time surfing with the crew at iSurf! Travelled to Mexico for a ladies camp and everything was pretty perfect (despite the small waves).  I felt safe and well taken care of the whole time. The lodging, meals and surf excursions were well organized, and the instructor was super knowledgable and accommodating. Not to mention the camaraderie - all the ladies were fun and upbeat. A very enjoyable experience all around!

We signed up our 8 year old girls for a Saturday session at the last minute and they were able to accommodate us without issue.  The girls went out with Zach and had a great time.  Both wanted to go back.  On Monday we called to get all 4 kids in and were initially were told that there was no way that they would be able to accommodate us on that short of a notice.  Thomas called back and was able to work us into a lesson that day.  When we finally met him to thank him he told us that he just couldn't disappoint the kids.  Great experience all around!

I've shied away from surf schools in the past because of the unfortunate douche-y element that seems to pervade the industry. After learning the basics from an accomplished boyfriend, I figured I just needed to put the work in to get better.  That's not exactly how it played out and despite tons of practice, I ended up frustrated with my lack of improvement.  A friend recommended iSurf and I could not be more grateful!  I typically prefer private lessons over group but the group lessons here are relaxed yet effective.  I discovered I had developed some bad habits in my technique, and I learned how to make small adjustments that have resulted in sessions that are truly satisfying--I really feel like I get it now.  The best part is there is zero douchiness.  If you're interested in experiencing the rewards of surfing, but you're not trying to get caught up in the scene, check out iSurf!

Had a great lesson today with Zach and Perry. There was a ton of good waves today at Mondo's beach and they were able to safely instruct us how to get the mechanics down behind surfing. 

They were both pretty observant throughout the entire lesson and gave me pointers after every attempt of me popping up and riding the wave. They were funny and kept the entire lesson fun and enjoyable. 

I'm not the strongest swimmer and surfing out here was a good starting point. The water was up to my upper chest where we were standing (I'm 5'8"). 

Definitely recommend starting off with some lessons and getting them from iSurf! The groupon deal made it even sweeter :)

Zach was an awesome teacher. He was knowledgeable and patient. He put the right amount of challenge on you and really helped us improve our surfing skills.  This was our third time surfing and definitely the most enjoyable one so far. Highly recommend!

p.s. We booked the private lesson at a one-day short notice on iSurf's website. The process was super easy but be sure to reach out to confirm lesson time if you have not heard back from them.

I've been taking lessons with Thomas and Zach for about two months now and have seen so much progress in my surfing! I started off with a fear of two foot waves and basically no knowledge of the ocean and both guys have helped out me so much!  Every time, I've finished riding a wave, or fall of my board, or don't even get up, both Thomas and Zach are able to quickly asses what I can do to improve. They know exactly what works! All you have to do is listen :) If you truly want to understand the waves, how to move and really become a true surfer, iSurf is your best bet!!

This company is beyond AMAZING! I found them when I googled paddle board yoga in Santa Barbara for my sister's bachelorette weekend.  They emailed me back immediately and let me know that they no longer offer that but that they had something else in mind.  They picked us up from our rental house in SB right on time, took us to a beautiful secluded spot on the beach where they lead us through a private yoga session (one of the best yoga experiences I have ever had), followed by a gourmet champagne brunch picnic that was being set up for us while we were doing yoga.  The brunch consisted of delicious pastries from a local bakery, fresh fruits, cheese, chocolate and a bottle of champagne per girl!  The friendliness of each of the staff members was remarkable. They were very patient and sweet when some of us were still recovering from the first night of bachelorette debauchery! The rest of the group and I were floored by how smooth and great everything was ran.  They even took photos of us during the yoga and brunch and emailed them to me promptly.  I can't say enough good things about these guys, I highly recommend isurf to anyone looking for a one of a kind experience!

As my daughter approached adolescence, I really wanted to find an athletic activity we could do together. Except that I'm not very athletic. Thomas O. of iSurf had been coaching my daughter for about a year, and she loved him, so I decided to join his women's group. Thanks to his gentle, "you can do this" attitude, Thomas had me standing up on a board my first day. I am now actively surfing. I love the activity, but I love the connection with my daughter and the friends I've made through iSurf even more. I can say that joining this group has been the best investment in my physical and mental health I've ever made!

I researched a bunch of places to take my first surfing lesson and I ended up selecting isurf, and I am so happy I picked them!!!  I spoke to Thomas (the owner)when I made the reservation and when we got to Mondos beach he got me and my boyfriend fitted in wetsuits and introduced us to Zach our instructor. Zach taught us some rules and tips on the beach first and then we spent about and hour or so in the water. Zach helped us so much and was super patient in helping me especially. Having never surfed before I was not sure how I would do.  I can't tell you how much fun we had because it would not do it justice and we both managed to ride a couple waves!!!  If you are thinking about taking a surfing lesson when you are in Santa Barbara I will tell you that Zach and Thomas are absolutely the right people to take lessons from.  They are extremely knowledgable, patient, kind and funny!  Thanks Guy!!!! What a fantastic Birthday Present!!!!

My boys had the best surfing camp experience with iSurf. The great instructors (Thomas, Zack, Nicole, Ashley, Victoria, Malea), the small groups (1:4 ratio), and the convenience of dropping the kids off near the wharf yet the flexibility of traveling to different beaches so the kids can experience the best waves makes this surf camp stand out from the rest. My boys had so much fun that we decided to try too, and now we're hooked! We're all big iSurf fans, and will surf with them whenever we can!!

Where to even begin! I feel like I'm still riding the high that I had after my first lesson today with Thomas. 

First thing, to anyone​ reading this review, do it. Just simply schedule a lesson and get out there. You won't​ regret for a single moment doing something outside your comfort zone and yet being so amazed at what your body can accomplish. 

In regards to the lesson, Thomas was so professional, nice, funny, laid back and really gives you the confidence that people like me (totally new to surfing) need. I never for a minute felt out of place and really appreciated​d all that he did to make me feel welcome both at iSurf and in general in the surfing community. You could tell right off the bat that Thomas and iSurf as a whole were really well respected in Santa Barbara's little surfing world. I met a few of his students (who were all awesome and really nice) who seemed to be out there putting his advice to work and practing what they learned. 

The lesson it's self was wonderful. Starts on the beach with some basics that he goes over, and then you put them to use! Safety is key which I really appreicate, but dont let that fool you into thinking that it's easy or lackluster! You will get your adrenaline and blood flowing yet feel like your well taken care of and safe in this environment. I must have cought at least 10 waves my frist time (and was exhausted, surfing is hard work!) and am already itching for more.

iSurf offer's a little something for everyone, from private to group lessons to surfing trips to some amazing places around the world (think Baja, Costa Rica, and even New Zealand!)

Overall the only thing I wasn't​ happy about was that I didnt start doing this sooner and found iSurf earlier. Would recommend to anyone and everyone, you never know if you'll discover your happy place like I did today.

I have been with iSurf for almost 2 years now.  I'm turning 60 this year, and have done the membership package and what a great value!!  I did the annual membership, so for $100 a month I can sign up for a lesson 7 days a week if I want.  I usually go 3-4 times a week, great group of people, super fun, my surfing has improved, I've made great friends, what a fantastic way to learn this sport, and what a fantastic value.  Couldn't give a higher review!!!

Isurf is an incredibly ethical, well run business. Professional, committed instruction is at the heart of their mission, and has been since the owners started their own business. Sadly, all of the excellent reviews that iSurf receives on Yelp are being taken down almost as soon as they go up. Can anyone explain why?

iSurf is amazing! If you want to learn to surf the right way with an instructor who is knowledgeable, encouraging and has a ton of patience then iSurf is for you!! I have had lessons with other surf schools but nothing compares to iSurf!!! I am actually surfing now!  We go to different surf spots and experience different surf breaks!! ISurf is awesome and I recommend any woman who wants to learn to surf go here!

My friend and I took a surf lesson with Zach this past week and it was awesome. We both had been out before but were really looking for some direction and help on improving (and learning to stand!). We worked with Thomas, who was responsive and easy to communicate with, on finding a time to go out. The lesson itself was wonderful, super helpful and Zach was awesome. Would recommend to anyone looking to begin or improve at surfing.

Thomas is one of the nicest people I've ever met. Such a great lesson, tons of fun and learned a lot! I was able to stand up and surf multiple times, which is a huge deal. I had an absolute blast. Highly recommend!

I am a physically active person. I found iSurf because the winter snow season was over and I needed something else to do. I cannot say enough on the professionalism that I have experienced with the lead surf instructor, Thomas. If the session in Santa Barbara isn't looking good, he is kind enough to text me. I like that, especially since I live an hour away. I have learned a GREAT deal from Thomas and wouldn't be where I am today without his knowledge, expertise and patience. I HIGHLY recommend you give iSurf a call if you have the desire to learn how to surf.

We are visiting Santa Barbara and just had an awesome time surfing as a family, 3 adults and 3 kids age 6-9. Thomas was easy to set up the lesson with and instructors Zach and Patrice were awesome! We had a fantastic time and we all got up! I would highly recommend issued to anyone! Thank you for the great California experience!

Thomas was a fantastic stand up paddle board instructor! As first timers, Thomas brought the perfect balance of helpful tips and relaxed enjoyment. We made it all the way around the bay and even got to see some seals! I would recommend it to anyone.
Really great operation -- communicative and well organized. Kids did a summer camp and really enjoyed it. Safe and friendly.

Great experience. Thomas had my girls,
7 & 9, up and surfing right away. Highly recommend.

Very highly recommend booking a surf lesson with iSurf! My two kids and I had the best time improving our confidence and catching some waves. After a discouraging experience a couple years back my kids and I didn't want to try surfing again. Though after some thought we decided to book a lesson with iSurf and had an amazing time with Thomas. He was an amazing instructor and taught us the basics we needed for some great rides. Definitely worth it.

Zach was super rad and helped me practice my skills in the water!! Such a fun experience and great energy. Highly recommended for anyone who wants to get into surfing. They make the experience very comfortable and I will definitely come back again to this company!

I'm from Texas and was on a solo road trip along the west coast when I decided to try surfing in Ventura.... that's how I ran into Thomas.  It was toward the end of the day and he was packing up his van to leave when I aimlessly asked if he has any surf board to rent.  To be honest I was not expecting him to accommodate some guy off the side of the street.  To my surprised he said, "Sure, I can let you take one of the wetsuit as well".  I asked when do I need to get them back to you?  He said, "Whenever you're done, just drop them off at my place, I'll text you the address."  I asked him how much?  He was like, "Whatever xx bucks each if that works for you."  I was genuinely taken aback by his hospitality and epic chillness.  That's just the start, once I dropped off the equipments at his place when I was done surfing, he continued to help steered me to the best local restaurant and a parking spot for the night.  He even offered to let me parked in front of his house if I had any trouble with the parking spot.  Needless to say I rented his board and wetsuit suit for the next day too and he was just as accommodating.  I bought him a case of beer for all his help and just to let him know it's really cool to meet a real life surfer who embodied a surfer's philosophy of life.  Although I didn't sign up for his surfing lesson, but I would imagined his approach would be just as legendary as his hospitality.  Cheers Thomas... thanks for the encounter!
Thai from Dallas, Texas

We are visiting Santa Barbara and just had an awesome time surfing as a family, 3 adults and 3 kids age 6-9. Thomas was easy to set up the lesson with and instructors Zach and Patrice were awesome! We had a fantastic time and we all got up! I would highly recommend issued to anyone! Thank you for the great California experience!

I stood on the board for the first time! I had taken a surf lesson two years ago and wasn't able to stand but because Thomas explained everything so well I got it super fast. He made sure to watch each time and give me feedback on what I did right and what I did wrong so I could correct for next time. Highly recommend taking a lesson from him!

iSurf Santa Barbara is perfect for any female who wants to learn to surf or just needs some coaching to enhance existing skills. I wasn't sure what to expect and I was a little tentative as I have never surfed before. I just knew that I always wanted to learn but never did and it's never too late to try, right? The group was small class size to number of instructors and the ages ranged from twenty-somethings to age sixty. Everyone was so positive and really encouraged each other. We didn't know each other before the lesson, but afterword we all felt like a sisterhood.

The instructor gave an excellent, easy-to-comprehend lesson on land before you even get in the water. No matter your level, the instructor makes you feel very welcome, very comfortable and stoked just to be there. In the water, same thing. Very attentive to what you are doing right, and what you may be doing wrong so you can eliminate any bad habits right away. AND they make it fun. That's how it's supposed to feel... like having fun, even when the waves are breaking over you. I highly recommend this surf school.

iSurf Santa Barbara's instructor helped give me all the tools necessary to begin surfing, even on the first lesson I stood up! He watched and gave feedback on every aspect of my attempts at surfing, which was wonderful .You couldn't ask for a more understanding instructor either- I have a fear of waves (which can be problematic when surfing) but he helped me get over that too, and really worked with me. I would recommend iSurf to anyone at any level who wants to learn. There were some intermediate surfers in our class too who also got a lot out of the session. Hang ten!

My kids have done the summer iSurf camp for 3 years now and it is amazing! My 7 year old son is fully surfing after just a few days of camp and I am so impressed with how much he has learned in a short period of time. My daughter feels more comfortable boogie-boarding but she has a BLAST at camp and it is all they talk about. The instructors are all super fun and outgoing and Thomas definitely knows how to teach kids (and adults) how to surf. The camp is really organized and it is by far the best camp every summer. Thanks iSurf!

Five stars for our instructor -- but two stars for the idea of "group" surfing classes. iSurf's group class is a great way to get a taste of whether you'd like to surf, but not an ideal way to actually learn to surf.

That's what I concluded after my 12-year-old daughter and I used iSurf's Groupon offer to try surfing for the first time. That Groupon is a *fantastic* deal, and I'm really glad we were able to try surfing for $30/person rather than $100/person, as with just about every other option I looked at. 

That class was enough for me to realize five things:
1. You can't learn to surf alongside your kid -- at least, not if you are the kind of parent who worries about their kid drowning. I realized I was too nervous to take my eyes off my daughter while she was on a board, so I waited on the shore until she tired. HUGE kudos to our instructor for the attention he paid to her -- he stayed so close by that I wasn't concerned for my daughter's safety at all, and she actually did get a chance to try (but fail at) standing up.
2. Surfing involves getting a LOT of water in your face. Honestly, I felt like I was being waterboarded. If I were serious about trying, I'd spend a few days in the heavy surf, wearing a wetsuit, so that I could get used to being whacked in the face by waves before I tried getting on a board.
3. Surfing is HARD and scary. I couldn't figure out how our instructor came by his crazy intense upper body musculature -- until we started paddling out into the waves. It is WORK. I love swimming, so that wasn't too daunting, but it *was* daunting to swim right into massive waves. This is why I think I'd do better one on one: the only time I got far enough out to try surfing back in, I'd accidentally ended up far from the rest of the group, and I had no idea how to get back to them without getting slammed by a wave from behind, so I didn't even try. One-to-one instruction would be worth the investment, particularly if I did the work of getting used to big waves in advance. That said, two of our fellow classmates did great and were up and surfing by the end of the class.
4. The instructor REALLY matters. Ours was terrific (I'm so sorry I've forgotten his name!) and made a real effort to get both of us in the water. I particularly appreciated his gentle, confident approach to encouraging my daughter to keep going even after she got whacked by a few waves -- even though she didn't end up actually getting up on her board, she felt great about her efforts, and loved the class.
5. The day REALLY matters. To keep our spirits up, our instructor didn't tell me until *after* class that the waves that day were exceptionally strong -- and he offered us a make-up class so we'd have a chance to try again. We weren't able to take him up on that, but if I were trying again, I'd be sure to book a class with the proviso that we could check in that day and rebook if the waves were too rough for beginners.

The two things I think iSurf could do to improve the experience are (1) setup their online registration with clearer instructions on how to use a Groupon, if you have one -- I had to get Groupon's help in order to get in touch with with iSurf and actually book our class, and (2) provide more info on where to park and meet up at the beach. My daughter and I spend fifteen minutes looking for parking and twenty minutes walking the beach to find the group -- only to discover them in a van by the side of the road. Someone else in the class was even later, for the same reason. It would be wise to tell students to look for the group in a van on the street, and not on the beach itself.

All in all, it was a great experience, and I'm glad we gave it a shot.

Booked a last minute private lesson while visiting Santa Barbara - the ocean looked too inviting! 

Thomas confirmed the booking details by text and we met up at Campus Point. There were a couple of other lessons going on but Thomas had us positioned so the other surfers weren't a problem. 

The private lesson was great in allowing my teacher to focus on my technique and picking the best waves for me. 

I felt like I got a decent introduction, was able to ask lots of questions and now know what to focus on as I keep practicing. I'd take lessons with iSurf again as I continue to improve.

I have had a wonderful experience with iSurf!  I came to iSurf having learned the basics but getting frustrated not catching waves.  The instructor really helped me piece everything together.  Not only that but his teaching method put me in a place where I felt supported and able to trust his knowledge to get over my fears.  ISurf got me over the hump between beginning and intermediate surfing and I'm ready to keep going!

We wanted our 16 year old to learn to surf while we were in CA from the east coast. After booking online, Thomas texted the evening before our lesson to arrange a time and place that was both convenient for our touring schedule as well as ideal for surfing. We ended up not being able to go at the ideal time, but Thomas made sure it still worked. Thomas started the lesson with lots of verbal explanation on the beach, which we weren't sure would work for our son, but once in the water, Thomas had our son up on a wave by the third attempt! Our son said his instruction in the water was clear and helpful, and he had a total blast for the full 90 minute lesson. I would highly recommend iSurf and Thomas to any beginner!

My first lesson I booked by my friend's recommendation, now I'm recommending Isurf to my friends!  Thomas has different options, I tried few and they all were very beneficial for me. Learning surfing is fun, specially if you have professionals next to you who make you feel safe in a water!!

Thomas, 
Thank you so much for our lesson on Saturday.  Kathryn and Hannah couldn't stop talking about it and I scored a "coolest Aunt" comment on the way home:).
Have a good day and maybe I'll sign-up again.  I really enjoyed it too!
Karen

My 6 and 7 yo daughters attended the summer camp. It was their very first experience out in the ocean. The team: Zach and instructors are absolutely amazing. They made girls feel comfortable and were able to get them in the water despite my girls (huge) hesitance and fear of deep water. My kids spent a few hours on surf boards on the very first day which is totally unbelievable. My kids can't swim but team was able to alocate an individial instructor to each of them. My girls had a wonderful experience and want to go to camp again. Big pluse: camp provides thermal suits and boards.

Thomas is experienced, knowledgeable, patient and so much fun to surf with and learn from. He encouraged and challenged me as our session progressed, gave me feedback which yielded some solid improvements.

Highly recommend!

My husband LOVED his surf lesson with Zack. He had always wanted to try surfing but never pulled the trigger to book a lesson himself. Upon planning our baby moon I contacted iSurf to surprise my husband with a private lesson and to ask a few questions. I spoke to Thomas who was extremely helpful and answered all of my questions. After the call I knew this was the company to book through. We received a text letting us know the location of the session the day before as well as a text the morning of. When we arrived at the location the team was super friendly and energetic. The lesson was thorough and my husband spent quite a bit of time in the water. I was able to spot him catch a few waves as well! Thanks Thomas, Zack and the iSurf team for such a wonderful experience! Looking forward to going back to Santa Barbara and taking a lesson myself!

Three days before Christmas 2021 our family took surf lessons, most of us first timers, with Zac and Thomas. They were so helpful and good instructors. I was the most questionable being 65 years old, but I have been around the ocean my whole life and swim a lot. Zac helped me to get up on the board first time!  It was thrilling to ride a wave and especially be out on the water with my son, his girlfriend and my two nephews. We had a great day!!  Thank you!

Best Surf Instructor Ever!!  I found iSurf through Yelp while I was in Santa Barbara for a few days.  I've had a lot of lessons in the past and Thomas is hands down the best instructor.  He broke me of some bad habits and taught me lots of little maneuvers that helped me tremendously to be more efficient with my energy in the water.  I am not a beginner, but I had to go back to the basics to reprogram my muscle memory.  Thomas was great about taking me out during the right conditions and to the ideal spot for the type of lesson I wanted.  He is really good at reading the waves and where to position yourself in the water for optimal rides.   If you're a beginner, start with Thomas so you learn the techniques correctly.  If you're a frustrated surfer like I was, take a lesson with Thomas to get dialed in correctly.  I absolutely plan on getting more lessons from iSurf.  Worth every penny!!  And the service is great too!  On time and convenient.  You just show up and he has everything ready for you.

Our half day group lesson was an amazing experience for kids and adults!!  Thomas and Zack kept all the kids (ages ranged from 8-12) entertained, safe, and well-educated about surf/ocean conditions so they could truly learn the fundamentals of surfing with confidence.  With over 16 years of experience working with kids and a lifetime of surfing wisdom, I guarantee you will not be disappointed.  Thomas takes very good care of all his clients!!

Thomas and his crew were FANTASTIC with my daughters age 5 and 10. They participated in their summer surf camp and loved every minute. Neither of my girls had much experience surfing prior to this week and I found that they were easily up and riding the shore waves by the end of camp. Thomas and his employees were extremely attentive and made sure that no one was left behind. We'll definitely be signing up again.

My girls (ages 7 & 10) had an amazing time! We signed up for drop-in day of surf camp, and they pleaded to go back the next day. They were nervous to surf, and they ended up surfing and playing in the water the whole day, even catching waves and standing up. The instructors were great, and nice to communicate with. Most of all, my kids had so much fun. I'm so glad we found iSurf! Plus, they got cool hats to bring home. :)

I took private lessons with Thomas since this was my first time surfing and the entire experience was incredible! He really prioritized safety and made sure I felt prepared before getting in the water. He was fun, encouraging, helpful, and patient and I even caught a few waves before our session ended :) Highly recommended!

Good times!   Thomas  of iSurf communicated promptly and clearly before the morning on the  water. He let me know what to expect, answered my questions, and learned  what I was looking for.  Once we met at the shore, he set me up with the gear I needed to stay warm and safe and taught me what I would need to know on the water. Out on the water, Thomas was super helpful and had  an excellent eye for what I was doing right and wrong... and a fun demeanor I could match.  Heck, I am good with falling into the ocean a  lot and calling it fun!  I did fall, but I also surfed a wave many times and rode a few right into the beach.  Super fun, safe, and I learned a  ton.
Stay here for daughters graduation from UCSB.  Allot of long time campers. They dont like visitors much. VERY noisey because of hwy. 101. Never again will i camp here.

We booked a reservation for 5 nights via phone and talked to Debbie, one of the owners along with her husband Terry. Super friendly and helpful people! The park itself is located directly off Hwy 101 and Salinas which was clearly listed on the website and, although there was traffic noise, it never bothered us at all. It is a small park with 31 well marked spaces and was always super clean and tidy. It has full hookups, 4 bathrooms with showers, a coin operated laundry facility, and wifi. we used the bathrooms/showers daily and they were pretty clean. I noticed they were maintained daily but there were campers who sometimes left it very messy and dirty which I don't feel is the owner's fault. We brought our bikes and were within 10 minutes from the bird refuge, zoo, restaurants, beach, with Stearn's Wharf and Harbor, and State Street, a little further down the road. The park was always full but we never had any problems with loud neighbours. The price per night was very reasonable and the flat fee included taxes and all amenities. We had an excellent first experience with a travel trailer and would highly recommend this park to everyone.
Four night mid April 2018 in a 23' Airstream.  Cramped sites with full hookups.  A bit pricey ($60) but the only game in town.  Convenient.  Friendly staff.  We will stay again.
Look at the place on Google earth and you'll understand the negative comments right away. Should have done it myself. Wont make that mistake again.

What a lovely run RV park! The facilities were clean, the grounds well manicured and the owners were super friendly and helpful, upon entering the RV park there was just an all around great atmosphere. We have spent the last month in over 12 RV parks and we were the most comfortable here!  We were so happy to be located in the centre of Santa Barbara that we had no issue with the slight traffic noise. The people complaining about it being close to a highway should go to a campground in the middle of nowhere if they would like pure silence.

I liked this place.  It's kind of funky, but bathrooms super clean (big on my list!) Think some folks live there several  months at a time so kind of homey.  Really nice lady (Judy?) checked us in and knows a lot about SB.  I think its the only rv park actually in Santa Barbara.  Way better than driving all the way down to Carpinteria or up to Refugio.  We rode our bikes all over twon.  Will definitly come back next time we want to stay in SB

O'kay, so our RV is up and running, and we finally got the chance to stay Sunrise RV park. Again, I have never met more friendly people like Debbie and Terry. Their hospitality and local information was truly appreciated. Bathrooms, site and laundry room are clean. Yep, you will here the freeway noise, but we brought a fan to drown it out. 
Bonus, we met people from all over the world and future friends, Wendy and Clay, that are avid travelers. Check out their blog: www.GoingInZane.com. 
Yes it's a small park, but very quant!!! We look forward to our future stay.
Thank you Debbie and Terry! Enjoy your son's upcoming wedding.
Cindy and Brian Dow

It's a good spot if you want to stay close to Santa Barbara, but that's almost it. $50-60 for a night.

Pro: Direct on the 101, so very easily reachable. Decent spots with full hook-ups and some restrooms and showers. Friendly staff.

Con: Direct on the 101, so very noisy. It looks like a parking lot, not a lot of grass or trees here. And it seems that some trailer folks are living here on a daily basis. Not the most cosy atmosphere.

While this park may be convenient in the city and is clean, it is cramped for space and the noise from the highway is overwhelming and never stops. We planned to stay two nights but left after one because of the constant noise.

Yes Judy is a nice lady but this park is horrible. The bathroom and showers are very dirty and don't appear to be maintained on a daily basis. After 5:00 there is no one available to help if you need some assistance.  The location is in the middle of a neighborhood and next to the very noise 101 freeway.  If you forget something you can walk to a nearby market in a not so safe or friendly neighborhood.  The photos on their website do not represent this so called RV park and this is not a very good representation of the beautiful city of Santa Barbara.

Ich und meine Freundin fanden diesen Campground per Zufall, wir haben dann auch gleich online reserviert. Es ist der einzige in Santa Barbara und dies wird hier ausgenützt.
Wir bezahlten satte 60.-$ pro nacht.
Die Frau vom Besitzer war überaus zuvorkommend und sehr sehr freundlich. Der Besitzer selbst musste sich schon sehr zwingen um auch nur ein bisschen zu lächeln. 
Das ganze Areal befindet sich ca 10 Meter von der Autobahn weg, zwischen uns und dem Highway befindet sich nur de dünne hohe Mauer. Der Lärmpegel ist natürlich hoch, aber das störte uns nicht gross. Die Plätze sind alle nah aufeinander, also nichts für Leute die ihre Privatsphäre behalten möchten.

Das Wifi wird hier hoch gelobt, selbst die Frau sagte noch selbst das sie das beste Wifi hätten.
Ich habe keine Ahnung was hier der Amerikaner gewohnt ist, aber für mich war es schlechter als ein Mcdonalds-Wifi. (Siehe Bild)

I hate to give negative reviews, but I want people to be fully aware of what they getting at this campground. This is basically a parking lot on the freeway. You are literally at the the off and on ramp of the 101 freeway. Be prepared that is extremely noisy all day and all night because of the freeway traffic. Bring earplugs because even with the windows closed and air conditioning running in our rv, it didn't drown out the noise of the constant flow of traffic. Secondly, the spots are extremely close together. The hook ups and sewage connection for your neighbor will be right next to your picnic table.  I didn't use the restrooms or showers at the campground so I can't comment on those. Campground was kept clean. If you want more of the "camping" experience vs camping on the 101,  I would recommend going a little north to the campgrounds up there.
Close to the beach, downtown, well maintaned, clean & friendly!  What more could you ask?  Older park but very cool.  Reasonable rates.

imagine our joy of spending a weekend in santa barbara.for an affordable amount of around 60 a night.sunrise rv park.we didnt stay here.we drove in our car to check it out.can you say trailer trash.run down part of town.campers who look more like residents.and a view of  the old homes and a  freeway .      lovely

This is a tiny park in a small city - understand that much and you will have no issue.  The owners are hands down the sweetest most accommodating humans that you will come across - they will take care of you and actually care about what your needs are and what's going on.  Be a good driver and navigate into a tight spot, then greet your neighbors and then Lyft or Uber to the nearby shops, restaurants, bars, museums, beach or whatever.  This place is clean, convenient and limited which offers exclusivity - I'll take that any day!

I stayed here a couple years ago and it was a "so-so" experience as you can probably can tell from the reviews a couple years back, but since the new managers have taken over it has really turned around!  The bathrooms, showers and the park in general are 10x cleaner than I remember.  The managers are just more involved than last time I was here which has made quite a difference.  I'm lookin forward to our next stay in January.

5-stars for customer service, they were very helpful.  The RV "park" is a gravel lot with hook-ups (and close to the freeway), so if all you're doing is looking for a place to hook-up for the night it is perfect.  As a place to camp it wouldn't be my first choice.

The Santa Barbara Sunrise RV park is a great, cost-effective place to stay right near downtown Santa Barbara. 

Debbie was really gracious, accommodating and flexible regarding my reservation, which I really appreciated. They were having the sites re-graveled when I was there, and she gave me all the details re: the work schedule so I knew when I could drive in and out and not get blocked in by the work that was being done. The bathrooms are clean, and I like the fact that each bathroom is its own private space. There's a laundry room as well.

The RV spaces are close together, but it seems like that's the case at almost all RV parks these days. This is not a woodsy RV park with trees and a campground-like feel, and it is right next to the freeway. (The RV park is separated from the freeway by a high wall to help a bit with noise.) But the freeway, although a bit loud, was just white noise to me, so I didn't mind.) And I was just looking for a low cost place to stay near downtown, not necessarily a campground atmosphere or experience, so it was perfect for my stay.

I'll definitely be staying here again!

Customer service at this park is very good.  They respond to emails and phone questions promptly and the people in the front desk are very friendly and personable.  Though we only stayed one night we enjoyed their hospitality and the location of the park.  This is truly a small park.  We were amazed to see a 5th wheel trying to get out of the park and maneuvering through a very tight corner. We did not think that it was too loud even though it's next to the freeway, and I don't wear ear plugs. There are two sets of bathrooms/showers.  I only went to the one closest to our spot and it was clean. I would definitely stay here again.

Huge shout out to Debbie for having awesome customer service skills!!!!  Love the trailer flag outside as well!!  ;). 

Kevin and Lisa

Ok. Let me start by saying that when we made first contact with the park, there was so much energy on the other end that we knew we would enjoy our stay. The park is small but it has clean bathrooms and laundry facilities. Each day, someone comes in to professionally clean them. Upon arrival, we were greeted with a smile and personally directed to our spot. They have water and electricity hookups. The best part is...they have cable and internet. Internet that actually works...fast! We even watched Netflix with no interruptions. 

If you are looking for all the bells and whistles of a large RV park, maybe this park is not for you.  It is next to a neighborhood which my husband and I felt safe both at night and in the day time. We biked..A LOT!

This park is close to food, shopping and the beach! That is a dream come true! We rode our peddle bikes everywhere we wanted to go. Lots of bike paths through the neighborhoods and near the beach.

The front desk is very friendly and always ready to help.  

This is a military friendly park!  If you don't mind freeway noise then this IS the place for you...convenient and clean and friendly...with fast internet!

We checked in on the 4th of July and met the managers Debbie and terry. They couldn't have been nicer or more accommodating. In fact we had a mechanical problem with our RV and between the two of them we got in touch w the right mechanic and our trip was saved. They truly care about their guests. Just be aware that the park is next to the freeway and their is some noise. We found the location to be extremely convenient as we were coming n going while exploring Santa Barbara. We have no complaints and look forward to staying w them again.

We stayed here for 5 nights with another family. We had a 32' fifth wheel + 28' travel trailer. The owners/management were very friendly and accommodating! We made reservations for 4th of July on short notice but they are able to squeeze us in. They were honest on the phone in telling us they were next to the 101 before we had even asked, which was appreciated. The noise didn't bother any of us though since there's a sound barrier. You can't beat this location to the beach, zoo, wine tasting, mission, and so many more locations within minutes away. The entire park was kept very clean with larger sites than expected and low nightly rates compared to the area. If ever in Santa Barbara again we will definitely stay here.
Horrible place park manager is an absolute jerk . Too many other quality places in the area with better hospitality

We stayed overnight at this friendly campsite on our road trip along the Pacific Coast Highway with my son, his fiancé, myself and my husband. We are used to towing a caravan in UK, but unused to driving on the other side of the road, my hubby did a fantastic job with my son as navigator. We had a  lovely welcome from the kind lady at reception. THe showers were fantastic, a very enjoyable night in Santa Barbara.

This park is clean and friendly, close to freeway and some noise because of it, but makes up for it by it's great location.  A few minutes to some of the best beaches in California and very close to downtown.  The manager Judy was extremely helpful and accommodating.  Santa Barbara is awesome!
This location is awesome. I like how the bathrooms are separate locking stalls with a shower. Very friendly management too!  We are here in July and it is beautiful.
What a great place......Thank you to Debbie and Terry for your hospitality.....the park is clean and well kept....Perfect!!!!!

Judy was so friendly and helpful.  Yes, this IS the only RV park close to beaches and State Street, BUT very noisy (and not just the hum of freeway noise, but very loud trucks, trains, etc).  Also, extremely small sites with hardly any grass or extra space.  The next site's hook ups were practically under our small picnic table.  We came with our two young children and were very uncomfortable:  no space for them to safely play outside, right next to the sidewalk of an old neighborhood with an old apartment building, plus a parking lot right behind us for all of the neighborhood cars/residents.
Maybe if we had another spot, more within the park and not next to the street, we would have felt differently.  We will not be staying here again.

This is a tough place to review because many of the negative reviews are based on the reality that this place is cramped, although based on our stay & observations several reviewers go way beyond reality to stereotypical Yelper hysteria.  The good and the bad ...

GOOD
* Location ... location, location, location!  This park is very central to downtown Santa Barbara.  I thought the surrounding area was fine, not fancy but never did we feel unsafe.
* Clean ... my experience at all time of the day was that the bathrooms and park in general were very clean
* Booking & check in ... booking online was easy, and they had our packet ready for us even though we arrived after hours.  They also communicated by email several times before and after our trip.  The website needs to say that there are no fires however
* Price ... look, its hard to say that $60 to $70 is a good price.  But most private RV parks are in that range, although yes most are more spacious.  You have to take into account this is Santa Barbara -- one of the most expensive places in an expensive state.  Check out motel prices in the area and the bottom line is that $60 was a big savings for us versus any other option on a holiday weekend.

BAD
* Location ... yes it's central but its directly adjacent to US-101.  There is a constant noise of traffic.  We were in an RV and we could not hear it at all inside.  That being said, if you are in an RV and want to leave the windows open all night ... or have a trailer/camper that will be open air, you need to understand that it is not a serene environment
* Cramped quarters ... I've posted some pictures but these are probably the smallest spaces I've ever parked in.  There's not a lot of privacy at any RV park but this is a whole new level.  In my view spots 7 & 8 are terrible ... they are awash in unshielded lights from the bathrooms all night long and are so close to the also unshielded bathroom doors.  Personally I think spaces 6, 7, 8, 15, 16 & 17 should be discounted very heavily.
* Hookups ... the cramped spaces lead to some very strange placement of hookups.  In some cases the sewer line is in downright odd or practically unusable spots.  

SUMMARY

I give this place four stars based on them working within the constraints. They really need to consider how to create some privacy around the bathrooms.  But I admit this is not a place I'd want to stay for an extended period of time or for an entire vacation.  If you are looking for a week's vacation in a beach destination, a two star review is reasonable based on the overly tight spaces & highway sound.

This is a perfect representation of the,problems in Santa jBarbara. The prices are dirt cheap and it shows. Still they are too expensive for the crap you get. Once speaking to the manager and he said most were from  foreign countries. It books quickly near major local holidays like Fiesta and Solstice. 

Two stars for the manager. He tries to be aware of everyone and helped us solve a problem when a drunk group of English citizens who entered our mobile home park instead and hit a car here so badly the insurance wanted to total it. The ignored this and kept attempting to leave although they clearly were intoxicated and admitted they never had driven an RV

I know some people don't like the noise here, but it IS the only RV park in Santa Barbara and access is very easy (especially since they just finished some free way construction) and there is a new sound wall that is better than the old one.  The place has a homey, friendly feel to us and we like the easy accessibility to all the attractions SB has to offer.  Don't be put off by snooty reviews.  The people here are super friendly and it feels like or home away from home.

This RV park is very noisy. You hear the train and the 101 freeway all day and ALL night long. The park does not allow campfires and the spots are narrow. It's a couple of miles to State Street, Zoo, beach etc. The surrounding neighborhood is questionable, we didn't feel safe at night leaving the park. The only positive comment would be that the park managers were friendly and the park does have wifi with a good signal.

Great little spot to stay for a couple of days. Hosts were very friendly, clean bathrooms and showers and the area is close to downtown and beaches. Little neighborhood store right up the street that sells everything from coffee and pan dulce to hand saws and pantyhose. Will definitely come back and stay here.

We really enjoyed our stay at Sunrise RV. Small park, clean restrooms/showers, friendly owners, and less than ten minutes from State St. 

We would absolutely stay here again.

Well I knew it was near a freeway but I didn't think I was actually on it!  There was just a wall between us and the freeway was right there. Very noisy and did not sleep very well! When I arrived they couldn't find our reservation and we even had a number of the lot, luckily they had one left! I was not happy about that! Then the lots was so small our awning nearly touched the other RV. It's not in the nicest part of Santa Barbara either, the streets around us were a bit run down. I think they have a nerve to charge $70 a night. But they are the only RV park in Santa Barbara so they get away with it! The bathrooms were at least clean!

This park is small and compact and doesn't offer anything other than a clean place to stay in your RV when you're in Santa Barbara.  It's not expensive, and it's convenient to the freeway and it's close to downtown SB.  It's not a place to go and hang out and look at the view, but it does let you enjoy downtown Santa Barbara.  The people who run it are wonderful.

Pro's: Friendly staff, laundry room open all night, bathroom with shower (no coins required), wifi works great, off 101.  Near Downtown.

Con's: UGLY parking lot in question questionable part of town.  Did I say UGLY!
Really super dreary but serviceable for one night or if you have really big RV that's fully equipped and you don't care about atmosphere.

This was the only thing we could find.  We pull a tiny teardrop and this was a kind of scary place to be especially as we had to use the restrooms in the middle of the night and there are no gates or anything to prevent entrance by whatever riff raff might be in the area etc.  

It was also the most expensive rv park we've ever stayed at for 50.00night with almost no amenities besides bathroom/shower and laundry.

Stay here only if you need the location more than any other luxury.
We just stayed at this park for 5 nights.  It's a little gem.  Good home base for visiting all of Santa Barbara area.  We will definitely return. Friendly managers and very accommodating.

You must be kidding. 60.00 for spot in a sketchy neighborhood , 50 ft from the freeway  in pretty much a parking lot. Our fault. We were heading back from Big Sur and needed to stop for the night.  Should have done more research!

Oh, how I wish I could give this park a better review, b/c they totally helped me out and got me in a few days earlier than my reservation in the middle of July, and the managers, Terry and Debbie, are THE BEST.  BUT, the park is just okay.  WAY too small of spaces for the price you pay.  No picnic tables, no fire ring.  We couldn't even bbq b/c I'm not kidding when I say I could reach out and touch our neighbors' rig.  Really tight and really no privacy at all. No green area, just gravel.  We had about (I'm not exaggerating or kidding here) 6 inches on either side of us when our slides were out and our neighbors' slides were out.  No way we were putting our awning out or our chairs or rug.  REALLY noisy freeway noise.  The neighborhood isn't the best, but it was no problem for us.  GREAT Mexican food down the street!  I gave it 3 stars, 2 for Terry and Debbie and 1 for location.  It's a really great location - very centrally located.  Although you're not walking to the beach from here.  But the owners need to wake up and realize that people need SOME space.

We have stayed several times at this RV Park.  It is a small, urban park within Santa Barbara City limits.  The owners, Debbie and Terry are super nice and make reservations and check-in a snap.  

We have found that many of the RV Parks we have stayed in have "attitude" on check-in and reservations.  The park restroom and laundry, although older, are the cleanest that we have seen in our RV travels.  This park is perfect if you are visiting Santa Barbara. The park is located in a residential area next to the freeway.  It is a very short car ride to the beach, State Street shopping and restaurants, and also the Coastal bike path which is an excellent bike path.  There is also hiking in the area.

Also worth mentioning is that the sites are pretty level, hookups are easy, in excellent working order and close by. Most sites have a concrete pad and an adjacent picnic table that is not broken and is clean (unlike the state parks).

We were initially confused by some of the reviews.  We found the surrounding neighborhood and park to be completely safe.  The park is a "family" park and the prevailing attitude is friendly and relaxed.  Both my other half and I are light sleepers, and true there is some noise from the freeway, but we were easily able to sleep just fine.

Smaller, private RV parks in urban settings are very hard to find.  This one is a gem and we have it on our list of parks we will use at least several times per year.  Enjoying the comforts of our RV, close to all of the nice things that Santa Barbara offers for $60 per night is an excellent bargain.

I would also note that there are obviously some really nice State Parks close by the Santa Barbara area, however, they are all on Reserve America with the "7 month book on the 1'st of the month at 7:55 AM" drill that we are all so familiar with.  For us, this park has been able to accommodate on bookings even in the busy summer months.

Please note that the park has a total of 33 spaces.  The small size contributes to the overall relaxed, close in, urban style of this park.  If you are looking for a park that it is in the mountains, has a pool and hot tub then this is not your park.  If you are looking to see the sights in the Santa Barbara area, this is a excellent one of a kind small urban park.  We wish there were more of these as they are hard to find.

Good little RV park in downtown Santa Barbara. Stearns Wharf and State Street is about a 5 minute drive up (or about an 8 dollar Uber). We stayed two nights and went to Solvang and downtown Santa Barbara.  It's ideal for location if your looking to explore the city. The park is managed by a husband and wife who were so nice and helpful. We'll definitely stay again for our annual trip to the area for its ideal location. They advertise it as it is. A small RV park close to freeway, but everything else.

You'd be better off in a random parking lot for the night than blowing $70 here. Never again.  Now I can see why their website show actual real pictures of the sites.  Completely deceiving site map which makes the park look amazing.  Wished I had checked Google Maps satellite view before arriving.  First it has the worst set up for entry and exit. Sites are super duper tight.  And they are right smack next to the sound wall to the 101--so LOUD!  Other side there are small houses literally butted up right onto the property.  People in there yards are staring right at you.  The PULL THRU sites are a joke!  If you have a large fifth wheel, hang it up.  Tightest spots ever.  They are right on top of each other.  All the lots are gravel rocks, not a patch of grass...only cement is next to bathroom.  The Bathrooms were tiny and not clean.  There was one for men and one for women.  Total joke.   It was like the most random RV park I have ever seen.  Wedged in a residential neighborhood literally next to the 101 highway.  Sound wall does nothing.  I do not recommend staying here unless you are in an absolute pinch and are only staying one night.  If your RV is average size, you might be ok.  If you have a huge fancy fifth wheel, keep driving and find somewhere else. The park is impossible to get in and out of and the pull thru sites are an absolute scam. They might as well be back-in...

We love this Family owned RV park. It is just a place to park and shower and do laundry if needed.  It's not a resort. It is very responsible especially comparing hotel prices in santa barbara. It close to beach, zoo and State Street.  Yes the freeway is in top of you but like I said it's cheap. The owners are so nice and helpful.  
We have booked for next weekend.  See you soon

We came here in August 2018.    Soon as we got here, we were greeted and told where to go to checkin.  The people who run this park are super nice.   I'm going to get straight to the point.   This RV park is the ONLY park centrally located in Santa Barbra.  The next closest park is 25-30 mins out of town.   Yes it's next to the 101 freeway, and it's a little noisy but not bad.  We had site 28 that is next to the trash cans and sound wall.  The noise is not bad at all.  But if you are a light sleeper this place is not for you.  The trash can dumpsters are locked and you can only throw trash in the small trash cans.   His is nice so you don't have the smell of the dumpster every time someone throws something away.  

The best thing is, you can ride you bikes from this place to the beach.  Also the bathroom are individual.   There are four total, and it's a private bathroom and shower combo.  Super clean. The sites to my surprise are actually really wide I thought.  Enough room for the slide and outside room.  Remember to book online in advance, because summer books up quick.

My wife and i just stayed at sunrise Rv. I have read reviews about the size of the lots, well some are small but most were more than adequate the park and bathrooms are very clean there is one tight turn but not a problem at all they are however next to the highway, this was not a problem for us. Very close to the beach, I wouldn't say you could walk, we rode our bicycles from the park all the way to the wharf its about 4 miles each way, an easy ride, and it is well worth the ride lots to see and absolutely beautiful. We will stay here again for sure. I gave 4 stars instead of 5 only because they are so close to the highway

What nice park hosts!

I visited this urban RV park over a weekend in April 2013.  The new park hosts (Terry and Debbie) were absolutely wonderful.  I have a used 32' motorhome that I just bought and it was my first time taking her out and hooking up.  It was quiet when I arrived so Terry was kind enough to show me around, help me get my rig leveled and hook up.  I was traveling solo so having Terry help me was a big help.  

The park is very clean and well cared for.  The toilet and shower were convenient and everything worked well.  The park is right by the freeway and I read about that in other reviews, so I was prepared with ear plugs and slept like a baby.  There is road noise during the day, so just be prepared.

You can't beat the location as the only place I found that's convenient to downtown State Street.  I brought my bike it was about a 7-10 minute bike ride to lower State Street.  I did go for a run along the waterfront and that was convenient leaving right from the park (about 5 miles round trip if you run towards Montecito and then over to State St and back).

There is a small bodego up the street, but it's mostly a residential neighborhood all around.  The spaces are adequate and fairly level, there is on-site laundry.  Each spot had a picnic table and hookups were right there (used 10-15 ft hoses for my rig).
