If you're looking to be close to the action in downtown, this is a great place to stay.  If you want quiet and secluded, I wouldn't recommend Sunrise.  It's definitely urban, but super convenient if you have a car or bike to all the great restaurants, beach and shopping on State Street.

Santa Barbara has amazing weather and is one of the most beautiful spots on the Central Coast (no wonder it's called the American Riviera).

I'd stay here again.

Hope you have a great visit, too!

Clean park, friendly owners, but the noise from the freeway made it a little difficult to get to sleep. The owner did tell us about the freeway noise so I can't complain. The shower and bathrooms were clean, as was the laundry room. The only problem I had, we were in the back of the park and I didn't care for the people hanging around later in the evening. That made me uncomfortable.
Worst of the worst. If you like the smell of toilets or the noise from a highway go here! Otherwise try to find another spot.

We read the reviews before booking it. We wanted to see it for ourselves.We just spent one night in this RV park...  It's the WORST we experienced during our 10 days-rv trip... No one for checking the evening of the arrival. We pick up our envelop with instructions. We found our spot but our friends didn't have a spot per se to be able to park properly, way far from their hook up. 
Bathrooms were disgusting. Since shower curtains are too short: there was water everywhere  and therefore it gets dirty quickly. There was barely toilet paper in every bathroom... The following day at 11 am, still no toilet paper and no bathroom cleaning...
60 per night is definitely too expensive for this kind of service.

I'm going to start with this. I only gave them one star because I had to give a star. They call this place "sunrise Rv park" because you will be up until sunrise with no effort at all. It's almost like you are sleeping on the freeway. Worst camping experience of my life, and believe me we have crossed the country twice. Small spots, overpriced, no sleep, I mean what's not to like. Wifi sucks on top of that. This place should honestly be shut down. I don't know how the owners have an opacity to charge $70 for this horrible thing that they fraudulently call an "RV park"

One star because I cant give zero..... Not a "park". Its a gravel parking lot with the spaces far too close together. The ground is uneven, sloped front to back and right to left. every class A or c I saw had to use wood blocks to get level. My front wheel were actually 4 inches of the ground... ridiculous...The managers were on vacation and the woman at check in was a nut case.... Will never stay there again. They advertise cable... 4 channels... CW, PBS2, a religious station and some backwater channel I've never heard of. 
WiFi was intermittent at best which is very frustrating when you're trying to work.
The worst thing though is the noise from the freeway and the 2 am trains...Earplugs if you want to get any sleep. Did I mention it was the worst "camping" experience ever??? The parking lot at Walmart is better.... Its a dump and an overpriced one at that. Good Sam has no business even saying this place exists.
the only good thing I can say is the restrooms were clean even though they are at least 50 years old... Beware the hype....
This park is right next to the freeway but what RV park isn't most of the time. That said, the Managers are spectacular to deal with and the price is beyond fair. We'll be back : )

My husband And I planned to stay at Sunrise RV for 1 night and end up staying for 7 nights. 
We are new to RVing so we had no previous experience to compare with but we really had a wonderful stay. 
The location of the park is awesome. The showers were always clean but the best part was the wonderful treatment that we received by Debbie and her husband. Very friendly and accommodating and MOE is adorable. Will totally stay again.

Most convenient to Santa Barbara proper, Sunrise RV Park is a gem. Very clean. Hands on managers. Gracious and very accommodating. We'll stay again when we're in SB. Would be a 5-Star review were it not for the freeway noise. Hardly a bother after the first hour :-))

We booked our reservations about 3 weeks in advance. Their website was easy to use and had no problems. On arrival, keep your eyes open because this RV camp is directly off the highway...like feet off the ramp. We drove in, no one was at the desk but our paperwork was posted and directed us to site #7. Which had full hookups. These are smaller spots, gravel floors, and have a bench available. I didn't see any fire pits anywhere. We were very comfortable and weren't bothered by any freeway noise whatsoever. Bathrooms are clean, showers are clean. Washers and dryers work great and clean as well. We stayed 1 night as we were just passing through and most other places required a 2 night stay. Really liked it and recommend staying here. Manager said there was a liquor store/deli about a block away but we didn't go looking for it. No playground for the kiddos but overall a nice, clean facility.

We have been staying at Sunrise RV park for a couple of weeks. I can not tell you how nice and responsive Debbie and Terry have been. Their level of customer service is amazing!

The park is spic and span clean. The sites, the driveway, the laundry room and the bathrooms are consistently taken care of. 

The office is always available to answer questions or to get quarters for the laundry room (I seem to do that a lot). 

Our dogs are comfortable here and are very welcomed. 

The park is centrally located to all that there is to do in Santa Barbara; the Zoo, beach, Funk Zone, downtown, etc. 

There is noise from the freeway that comes over the wall. We wear earplugs when we sleep and it doesn't bother us. Small price to pay for camping in "The California Riviera". 

Santa Barbara is a beautiful place to visit or live. Next time you are in the area in your RV, look up this park.

We love this little RV site!  We have camped in a lot of places with our travel trailer which is only 12ft, but the location is amazing with regards to accessing downtown Santa Barbara.  I have read some comments about the neighborhood, but I always felt safe ( just because the site is located in a mostly Hispanic neighborhood, doesn't mean it's a bad neighborhood or someone is going to steal your tour bus of a motor home).  

The site is directly off the freeway, so there is highway noise during the early morning hours; but that doesn't bother me since I live in a city. 

The managers are super nice and very helpful.  If you are visiting SB for a couple to few days, this is a great spot!

We couldn't have been happier with this park.   From my first experience with Debbie on the phone right through the minute we left.  When we arrived, we were prepared for the location (close to freeway) which we thought was excellent..easy on/off and close to everything.  We were never bothered by the noise from freeway.  Debbie and Terry greeted us and were so, so nice and helpful.  Terry even offered to help my husband unhook our tow car from the dolly.  We thought that was really nice.  The park, we thought, was so homey and welcoming and I loved the bathroom.   Instead of a big, cold commercial looking and feeling one, it was like being at home in your own bathroom.  Great shower and very clean!   Unfortunately, we didn't get to spend much time around the park because we were only there 3 nights and had never been to SB before and had lots to see, much of which Debbie gave us recommendations for and we loved!   But when we were there we felt safe and comfortable and we look forward to going back again.  Thanks Debbie and Terry...sorry we didn't get to say goodbye before we left.

This place has been an RV park for 65 years! The same person has owned it for about 60 of those years. So, yes, it's old, but it's clean, well-maintained and the people who run it are super nice and helpful. There are beautiful mature trees to shade the very wide sites and they have full hookups and all the necessary amenities if you need them--restrooms, showers and laundry. There isn't a pool, but it's in Santa Barbara where there is plenty to do. There are motion lights and cameras for safety measures. In the daytime you can hear the train and freeway traffic, but you can at the beach RV parks, too. It ain't the wilderness, but it's a good place to stay.

Great location to get to town, the zoo and the beach.  Very friendly host that will give you restaurant and activity suggestions.  A bit of freeway noise is noticeable but freeway access is excellent for entrance and departure.  Definitely will stay there again in the future.

RV Park reviews are always a bit difficult to gage since so many are based around Wi-Fi or other issues totally unrelated to "camping". However I'll give it two stars (now at one star for reasons noted below) for the cleanliness. That's all.  The freeway, although with a sound wall between the park and the 101, is loud, make that LOUD! 

I can smell the neighbors breath. Too close to each other did I say? Although I must say this seems to be a fairly common complaint at RV parks in general. By parking off the pad we were actually able to have a little room. 

Location is everything they say, and this location and lack of any security makes on a bit nervous. The neighborhood uses this park as a public walkway. Plus how would you like to live next to an RV park that has month long tenants. 

I will say the management does what they can. Will hold nothing against them. (Edited, see below)  But you can't always make lemon aid with rotten lemons. 

We understand now why they have such a restrictive cancellation policy. No one would stay!

UPDATE:  when we decided to leave this park a day early it was painfully obvious they had seen the previous review. "Good" was the managements comment when I told them we were leaving early and "Better yet" when I told them we hadn't slept the night before due to the freeway. 

I also forgot to mention they are a member of the "Good Sam" Camping Club but they refuse to give the Good Sam discount!

My wife and I stayed one night at the Santa Barbara Sunrise RV Park and it was great. The people who run the place were friendly and even called the day before to explain issues with our spot and offered up to other spots in exchange. Upon arrival the person in duty showed me two spots we could use and she made her recommendations. We took spot 11 which borders on a neighborhood street but hey it works. 

Most of the RV Spots that we saw you had to back into, it's not easy especially if you are new to hauling a Camper in the back of your truck (like me).

Yes the RV Park is next to the 101, the noise isn't that bad and the neighborhood itself isn't bad at all either.

It's just a quick drive to State Street in Santa Barbara or the Zoo (across the freeway). There isn't a safe way to really walk to the zoo or the beach which sucks.

But can't complain to be honest with you.

I can not give enough positive stars. Owners were incredibly helpful and kind. The campground is small but we personally felt it was perfect. This was our first camping trip with our new RV and our kids and we had a great time. Tons of kids riding bikes and scooters. Site might be on the smaller size but we did not have any issues with space. WiFi was great too. Took a day trip to the beach; which is not too far away. We were here for 2 nights and had great neighbors... very helpful. We utilized their bathrooms & showers and they were very clean and stocked. Thanks Sunrise RV park for making our first trip a great experience.

Pets Welcome (While we love all animals, we regret that we cannot accept pit bulls.)

Okay first off, if you don't accept all dogs you do not love all animals. That's like saying "Oh I love humans but unfortunately we don't accept black people." You are racist against dogs. Pitbulls are not bad dogs they just get a bad rap. Why not ban chihuahuas? They have a worse reputation than pitbulls. As a Santa Barbara local and native this saddens me. Get your head out of your ass!

Great park,clean friendly,your next to the freeway so there is noise from the trucks,but thats  it no big deal.clean laundry rooms.2 bathrooms with hot showers..full hook up.wi-fi. easy in and out .the owner was very nice. The people leaving these bad review are not real campers..Thank You :-)

Good points 1. , very accessible from 101 2. very convenient to town and beach.3. toilets clean.4. do you have a choice?
Bad points 1. close to 101, very noisy 2. internet seems to work only in evenings. Not sure why but office never opened when we there( mondays closed, tuesday limited opening etc) to ask. Had to ask fellow customers for internet code.3. Wolf ( sort of dog) chained next to  a caravan ( emblazed with wolf cub on side) howled through the night. Had to put on fan system to limit the traffic and dog noises.
4. workman  planting trees by 101 took frequent  short cuts through camp from 7  to 9 am on diggers with caterpiller tracks made sure you did not lie in. Point 5. 4 toilets with showers inside not enough if camp full with say 80 people, particularly in mornings. Point 6. remember your torch otherwise cannot enter code to toilets at night.
Not sure about late night returns to camp, neighbourhood not ideal.Otherwise  Santa Barbara great for 1 or 2 days.

This is a tiny park, very hard to maneuver in. My husband and I pulled in with our trailer on our way to the mountains hoping to fill our water for a fee. We've found that many RV Parks offer this service, usually $20. We were unable to locate the manger so started to fill the tank expecting to pay after. The manager found us and and was so angry! We apologized profusely and offered to pay money as was intended but he refused and threatened to call the cops on us for trespassing. He was scary angry and saying very rude things despite us trying to be as nice and apologetic as possible. You couldn't pay me to stay anywhere near this manager.

Great little RV park in town. First spot #30 we got on the first day was so tight we just parked our cars around the neighborhood to give us extra room to hang out outside. It was tricky trying to hitch up while other cars were driving through. We still made it work and had a great time. 

The next morning we moved over to #1 and man did we get lucky! This one was right in front of the office but great because it was spacious and we were only next to one other rig. The people were so nice there and their restrooms and laundry rooms were spotless. Piping hot water so be careful. 

Definitely noisy on this site because it was right next to the freeway but we played music and white noise so we were fine.

This isn't a RV resort or nor do they claim to be but it's right in town and there so much to do around. It's a close bike ride to the beach and restaurants. We rode bikes to the beach to watch the sunset and it was a beautiful evening. 

We drove one day to Gaviotta Beach and had a great hiking adventure. 

My kids also did distance learning using their wifi and had zero problems. We streamed movies and it made for a nice little getaway. Clean, nicest people who run this place... we'll definitely be back.

I had a short stay here, last week. The owners/ managers we extremely accommodating, communicative, and helpful! It's close to the freeway but I slept fine. I did not get a chance to explore the local area as I was working - but the park provided everything I needed. I'm grateful for the pleasant experience. I would recommend, and hope to return here. Thanks Sunrise RV!
What a great experience. East check in. Super clean bathroom. Great management. Quiet (well the 101 is right next door) but the park was super quiet. We will be back.

You'd be better off in a random parking lot for the night than blowing $70 here. Never again.  Now I can see why their website DOES NOT show actual real pictures of the sites.  Completely deceiving site map which makes the park look amazing.  Wished I had checked Google Maps satellite view before arriving.  First it has the worst set up for entry and exit. Sites are super duper tight.  And they are right smack next to the sound wall to the 101--so LOUD!  Other side there are small houses literally butted up right onto the property.  People in there yards are staring right at you.  The PULL THRU sites are a joke!  If you have a large fifth wheel, hang it up.  Tightest spots ever.  They are right on top of each other.  All the lots are gravel rocks, not a patch of grass...only cement is next to bathroom.  The Bathrooms were tiny and not clean.  There was one for men and one for women.  Total joke.   It was like the most random RV park I have ever seen.  Wedged in a residential neighborhood literally next to the 101 highway.  Sound wall does nothing.  I do not recommend staying here unless you are in an absolute pinch and are only staying one night.  If your RV is average size, you might be ok.  If you have a huge fancy fifth wheel, keep driving and find somewhere else. The park is impossible to get in and out of and the pull thru sites are an absolute scam. They might as well be back-in...

We wanted to be close to downtown so we made reservations at this park. The owners are friendly but instead of giving verbal instructions on the details for entering their park,they sent an email that I did not receive. The park entrance is immediately off an "off ramp " from busy highway 101. We entered at the Office sign only to learn we entered the wrong "alley". The space is VERY narrow. We had to unhookthe tow car and drive the RV around the block and enter the correct "alley" to park. The exit from the site back to the freeway is impossible with a tow vehicle. We have a 36 foot class A RV and would gave been forced to drive into the "off ramp " of highway 101 inorder to enter the "on" ramp to the highway. Too dangerous so risked getting a ticket and crossed a double yellow line to go around the block and enter the freeway correctly. I would not recommend this site for anything other than small trailers. A class A without a tow car might be ok.  The sites are very shallow. Also appears to have long term "guests"

How could anyone give this place less than FIVE stars?? Yes, it's near the freeway, but it's immaculate, bathrooms are close, showers are HOT, and management is very nice, albeit busy. They are helpful, understanding, and pets are not only allowed, well-behaved ones are appreciated. Location, as they say, is everything. They're minutes from Santa Barbara's Old Town and beaches. I sure hope to come back, someday!

Outstanding service and great little RV park!  We enjoyed a much needed getaway to Santa Barbara during COVID Summer.  They were extremely helpful when I called to book my reservation and when I had to change it to accommodate the size of RV we ended up renting.  The park and facilities were very clean and kept in good condition.  The park was really close to all of the sites and we really enjoyed riding our bikes around the area.  Such a fun getaway!

One of the cleanest RV parks we have been in. Although it is a small park they do have a few sites for larger units. We had site 20 but when we came in they offered us site 22, which was easier to get in and out. Only negative is the location. Right at highway 101. Lots of traffic noise.

We stayed here for a brief one night stay, on our way up the coast. The camp hosts were so helpful and pleasant upon check in. The manager even helped us pull into our spot. :) The bathrooms and showers were also very clean and well kept, which is nice for those who need to utilize them. The distance to downtown and the beach are in pretty close proximity, but the actual neighborhood that the park is located in, is very questionable. I never feared for my safety, but I didn't want to go out of my way for a dog walk or a jog. The price is steep at $70/night...but it does beat any Santa Barbara hotel nearby. There is no view, just a nice big wall that separates us and the 101 freeway, which was a bummer. 

Ultimately, I am deducting two stars because of the location, price and view.

This is located in a great spot fairly close to downtown and the beach.  Both are walkable, though NOT right next door.  Wear your sneakers!!  The park is very accessible, right off the 101, with nice sized spots and full hook ups.  We easily parked our 34-foot class A with no trouble.  The staff is awesome!

Ok, so a lot of people complain about the freeway noise and the trains.  Yes, it can be noisy because it's RIGHT on the freeway and near the train tracks.  Suggestion: close your windows, run your a/c, and you won't hear it.  Property in Santa Barbara is VERY expensive, with the average home price over $500k, so you can bet an RV park is not going to be located on prime property.  Nonetheless, the park is in a convenient location, well maintained, and run by very friendly folk.  

This is NOT like Newport Dunes, but then again, it doesn't cost $300 a night for a parking space!  The price is reasonable for Santa Barbara.  

It's not a beautiful park in any way, with little greenery and it IS in a part of town you might not want to walk about in, especially at night, but it's the only park in Santa Barbara.  

We stayed here for two nights in September and are staying again for three nights this spring.  The only reason I didn't give this park 5 stars is I didn't use the restrooms, so I don't know what they're like.  Based on the staff, I'm sure the restrooms are fine.

If you choose to waste $50 just so you can stay in Santa Barbara - well, you deserve what you get. This is NOT an RV Park... This is an old backyard with hook-ups and it is NOT near anything! It looks more like a low-income residence parking lot - Walmart is much nicer!   This place is a dive. Period. We drove in and found that our "neighbor" had extended their shade into our spot (yes, they are that narrow) so we couldn't back into our spot without hitting the shade.  The people doing drugs in plain view in the space behind that location didn't seem to care (duh). Well, I went to the office and explained to the woman who answered what the problem was and her response? "What do you want me to do?"  I asked if she might have a cell number so I could find out when the people might return... And she said "maybe you should just leave - I really can't help you." I asked if I might get another spot - and she offered me one where my hook-ups would be driven over by cars on the road (she blamed CalTrans when they put in the wall). I am dead serious - this place is a dump and people who rate this higher then "1" ( I can't put 0) must be friends or relatives of the woman who obviously has no concept what customer service is.

Great location ideal for going to downtown and beach. 
For an RV park this place has the cleanest facilities.
We had such and amazing experience!! Bathroom were super clean and a friendly environment!! I'll recommend this place

Our son graduated UCSB and we used chose SB Sunrise RV as it was the closest camping facility to the campus.  I can not express how perfect this location and experience was for us, and the four other family members who camped in the same location.  

First my rig is 34'9" long.  Although the park is relatively small (that's a good thing) the spaces are large, accommodating, and clean.  
The first night we took an UBER from the campground to downtown for dinner and drinks.  The UBER ride was about $7.00 each way.  It was quick and easy.  Downtown Santa Barbara is beautiful with plenty to see, eat, and drink.  

Another night we held a dinner time graduation party for my son, his girlfriend (she also graduated), and all of our family members.  We reserved space #23 to afford us plenty of room and to be clear of other's campsites.  It was PERFECT!  The managers took extra effort to clean the space and put extra picnic tables at the location in preparation for our party.  Completely a success!  

I have seen many complaints on this thread reference traffic noise from the US-101.  There is definitely traffic noise but we frequently camp at beaches and in the desert.  At the beach the surf noise is about the same.  In the desert, the off road toy noise is MUCH louder.  So, I would say, you just need to be realistic about the fact you are in a camper and it is near a freeway.

And finally, the managers are as good as they get.  They greet everyone and make sure your experience is as good as possible.  They helped me back in my rig to the spot saving us from the typical marital argument (haha).  They made sure all of my equipment was of correct size as they have some adapters to help you if you come up short with your sewage tube.

Again, I was very pleased and would strongly recommend this location for any camping adventure if you are in Santa Barbara.

I came up to the sunrise rv park after calling them many times for advice and general info. They were always gracious and helpful. I came with my grand kids 3 of them and we had a super time. The park is safe clean and the owners are genuinely nice caring people.
The park is located near the freeway so there is a bit of noise through the night if you a super light sleeper it may be an issue but my kids and I didn't have any issue.
I recommend it for a much need get away. Super close to the beach too.

Such a great location! The spots are a bit tight so I recommend getting here early. The noise from the freeway isn't so bad. The manager, Terri, lives on site and is super friendly and helpful. This is the only RV park in Santa Barbara city.

When I made reservations, I spoke to Debbie. She was very accommodating and worked to make it happen given my schedule.  The email communications make it very clear what to expect upon arrival after office hours.  It was very easy to find our packet so that we would know what site number we had.  The brochure included the wifi password so there was no problem connecting.  

The campground is small and you are fairly close to other RVs.  There are a few sites that have more space.  The grounds are well-maintained and the bathroom is clean. The campground is next to Highway 101, and the website for Sunrise RV Park does state that fact.  The noise was not bad.  There is a wall and it does help.

When you think of Santa Barbara you think of beauty. This park was not at all what our vision. It's all gravel and no amenities. The view out of our front window was a neighbors messy back yard. The sites are sandwiched in and no place to really enjoy a night. Cars from over RVs are parked behind yours or to the side making it hard or impossible to get out when you want. We had a neighbor who made us wait 30 minutes before he would move his vehicle. I would not suggest this place unless you are desperate to park. Owners are very nice!!

Stayed here for Presidents Day weekend, seeking an escape not to far away from home but in the comfort of our rig.

Driving in, it would be helpful to know in advance which driveway to use depending on your site number.  Some of the sites are easier to navigate from the first driveway off the freeway, even though the second driveway is identified as the main entrance.  

The manager onsite, Terry, was extremely helpful in helping us get our rig situated.  Sites are level.  We didn't have to use any leveling blocks. Sites are a tad squishy.  We have a small picnic table outside our rig. Full hookups too.  Our rig was too far away from the cable tv line to be able to use that feature - so helpful to have a coax connector in case you need to extend the length of the cable TV line.  There are onsite bathrooms with showers.  

Perhaps the only con is the proximity to the freeway and its white noise which is constant.  That said, we are still satisfied with our stay here.

I highly recommend staying here, management is very hospitable and willing to help through kindness and compassion. We ran into issues with our RV and management happened to have the resources that we needed and were kind enough to allow us to borrow and use what was needed without hesitation. If you are looking for superb customer service with down to earth people I highly recommend the Santa Barbara Sunrise RV Park for your vacation stay. Not to mention that the facilities (bathroom) were extremely clean and well organized.

First and foremost, I want to say the managers of this park were very nice, pleasant and accommodating.  

It's all down hill after that. 

This is a very old park. I'm gonna guess 40 years at least. The site are extremely tight.  Not much in upgrades since it was built. 

Now for the worst. It is literally next to a very busy in-ramp to the very busy 101 freeway. We've been here 3 nights visiting our college granddaughter so the option to move wasn't an option. 

We have not slept in 3 days. The traffic noise was HORRIBLE !!!  There is no place in the park to get away from the noise. 

If you are looking for a peaceful getaway, this is not the place. I encourage you to look elsewhere for you sanity

there is no where else to park your RV in Santa Barbara.
had a good spot closer to residential area.
4 bathrooms were really clean.
grounds are clean, no trash laying around.
no activities but zoo and beach and shopping are a short bike ride away.
yes, freeway is right there, traffic noise but didn't bother us at all.
felt safe but still locked up bikes.

this was my first ever RV trip, not as hard as I thought to do all the hook ups - electric, water, sewer, cable.

will consider coming back.

There aren't a lot of options in Santa Barbara. The spots here are very small, and the freeway noise is LOUD. The people in the spot right next to ours are in this humongous RV, and when they opened their pop out, it hung over our picnic bench making one side unusable. If all you are doing is sleeping here and spending the day doing things in town, it's acceptable. It is what it is, and the price isn't bad. Ugh.

name: Lovewater Surf School
categories: Beaches, Specialty Schools, Adult Education, Surf Schools, Travel Services, Education, Active Life, Kids Activities, Surfing, Hotels & Travel, Swimming Lessons/Schools, Gyms, Fitness & Instruction, Paddleboarding

yelp_reviews.text:
I didn't really know what to expect with surfing. I'm a first timer from Texas, but my experience was amazing! 
I was a little hesitant bc it was cloudy and rainy...and it didn't meet my expectations. I had always imagined the sun shining if I was gonna be on a surfboard in the water! 
But it was perfect...surreal. We were surrounded by the most beautiful mountains covered in yellow flowers, clouds, fog...it felt like a dream. 
Matt and Brendan made us feel so comfortable in the water. They helped us catch waves and made sure everyone got up and had a great time! It was SO FUN! I'm coming back and telling all my friends they need to check it out too!

Had a great time learning how to surf with my 9 year old twins. Signing up on their site was nice.  The staff met us at Coastal Access near UCSB with wet suits and boards.  They gave us a short demonstration on the boards before we got in the water and then we were off to give it a shot. After just a couple tries we were successfully riding the waves. Their team were extremely patient and friendly.  I'd highly recommend them to anyone.

A friend and I met up in Santa Barbara and did 2 days of surf lessons with Matt. Matt really knew what he was doing and was very helpful with tips on how to improve our surf skills. I felt a lot more comfortable on a surfboard by the end of the 2nd and definitely plan on going to Matt again the next time I am in Santa Barbara.

A friend of mine and I met up in Santa Barbara for a weekend of surfing.  We booked our two lessons with Matt off of his website.  Overall it was a really great and fun experience.  We learned a lot and were able to improve upon our surfing skills.  Matt is very friendly and very knowledgeable. We were able to pop up very quickly into the session and then catch waves the rest of the time.  Matt was phenomenal at wave selection as well as positioning us to catch the wave just at the right point.  He was also great at predicting the surf and adjusting locations on the beach to make sure we followed the best waves.   We surfed on Saturday and Sunday and had a blast both times.  We will definitely be signing up with Matt again the next time we are in Santa Barbara.

My niece and her boyfriend visited me in Santa Barbara. I treated them to surfing lessons. Matt was a fantastic teacher - patient, encouraging and fun. They had a blast! And, he was very sweet to me. I'm 51. He carried my surf board out for me. Helped me navigate some waves. Let me just hangout on my surf board in the waves and watch. He's great with all ages if you're older and want to try. I had a great time. Highly recommend!

Austin was so great with our teen daughter and her friend.  They had so much fun and managed to stand up on the board a few times.  They surfed at the campus point beach, right next to Santa Barbara University.  It was great because my husband and I did a nice walk up the cliffs while girls surfed.  It was just them,
a semi-private 2 hour lesson.

Matt was very helpful and patient answered all my questions promptly.  Very reasonable price will definitely use them again

LOVE WATER SURF : A PERFECT 10!! As good as it gets!! Matt Coleman has been coaching my son for about 9 months now. The change I have seen in my son's surfing level as well as self confidence is phenomenal. My son went from riding a 7'2" board to a 5'0" in 9 months. He has been instructed on how to position himself in the line up for maximum wave catching. I can honestly say he is now entering into the intermediate / advanced level after only 9 months of coaching. There is no turning back for him, he is a surfer for life.

Matt Coleman is as much as a mentor to my son as much as a coach. He is truly invested in my son reaching his potential as a person. He has taught my son to reach for his personal best and to stay far away from comparing himself to others. He has also taught him the very valuable trait of staying humble, even when your ripping. At the very heart of Matt's philosophy on teaching surf is to have more fun then anyone else in the line up...thats the key to success.

I would recommend LoveWaterSurf to anyone at any level of surfing. Matt is an incredible role model for teens and young adults.

We just moved to California from the east coast with our two boys (11 and 14), and we decided to take a surf lesson as a family.  I wasn't sure how it was going to go-- different ages, different learning styles...I spoke with Matt on the phone, and he assured me that we could take a lesson as a family and it would be great.  We had a WONDERFUL time.  Communication with Matt was excellent-- he emailed the day before to have us meet him at the perfect location based on the weather/water.  He and Austin met us at the beach, and after a ten minute safety talk and prep on the beach, we hit the water.  It was a perfect day of surfing, and we all had such a fantastic time.  Most important, my kids had a great first experience and they left the beach asking when they could go surfing again.  I couldn't have asked for a better day, and we will definitely be back for more lessons!
Our boys took a lesson with these guys and absolutely loved it. Austin was patient and fun to be with. Campus beach was a perfect learning beach for them.

We drove all the way from Sacramento for surf camp and we would do it again in a heartbeat. Such a wonderful experience. Matt was wonderful with my kids. He taught them how to surf in one week! And most importantly, my kids came away from surf camp with a giant love of surfing and being in the ocean. I wholeheartedly recommend this surf camp.

I've wanted to get into surfing for some long time now, and decided I'm just going to do it. I yelped a few places, even called multiple ones but I'm happy I ended up with matt! He was super awesome and really helped me get up and ride some waves. I'm obsessed and want to buy a surfboard already and get out there.. but baby steps. Definitely booking again! Can't wait for the pics! Thanks guys!

My boyfriend and his daughters, ages 12 and 8, came to visit from Minnesota, and they wanted to have a surfing lesson more than anything else. We called several places and, fortunately, found Lovewater and Matt. Matt told us to meet him at a great location, one that he knows well and has excellent waves for surfers of all skill levels. He was punctual and had everything the girls needed for a successful lesson: suits, boards, enthusiasm, and a gift for teaching.
He started out by giving the girls "the basics" on the sand, then they got into the water and started to go. Both girls were happy to get into the water in the first 15 minutes to have lots of time to learn and practice. He made them laugh, gave them excellent information, and made the whole experience fun. When I asked the 12 year old if it was as good as she'd hoped it would be, she said "It was even better!" 
He gave us his number and we arranged to rent boards from him the next day; he, again, met us at the location and gave us suits and boards. He even gave my boyfriend and me some of the techniques he uses in order to help the girls ourselves.
Being a teacher isn't easy. Lots of people can surf, but it takes a special person to teach someone how to surf. Matt is obviously passionate about both teaching and surfing, and he has chosen to blend the two and serve his community and tourists by offering a safe education on the water. 
An overall incredibly positive experience, and for a price very competitive with other surf lessons in SB.
Thank you, Matt!

Had such a great experience surfing with Matt! He was so responsive and accommodating in booking my lesson to fit my timing while I would be visiting Santa Barbara, and made sure we found the spot with the best surf on the day we were going out.  Matt was so attentive during our lesson and made adjustments to his coaching along the way based on how I was doing, and as a result I caught so many waves!!  A great guy and goes out of his way to make sure the session is comfortable and enjoyable no matter your experience level. Will be back! :)

Matt was so patient and helpful. This was my first lesson and with his guidance, I was able to get up and actually sort of surf! A great experience all around. He did all of the work that a hesitant first-timer doesn't want to have to think about -- which waves to take, when to paddle, how to avoid crashing into people! I would highly recommend these lessons to anyone looking to learn to surf or further develop their skills.

Great, super nice local business.  We came down for a week vacation with a checklist of activities our boys wanted to try for the first time, including surfing.   Ian was, in his words, "super-stoked" to teach youngsters just learning and did a great job.  In a 90 minute lesson he had our boys up on boards and our older son had such a good time he signed up for the day camp 11-2 later in the week.

While our boys are pretty athletic, I was amazed at how quickly they picked it up (especially our eldest) under Ian's teaching.  At camp he was surfing long waves for minutes.  Of course these were gentle waves but still.  An awesome experience.  Highly recommend Ian and Matt's whole crew for first-timers for sure.

I did the 1-on-1 surf lesson with Lovewater and could not recommend it more highly. This was my second time doing surf lessons, and my instructor (Austin) got me more comfortable with all aspects of surfing: popping up, balancing, timing the wave, turning down the wave, etc. When family visits, I will be bringing them back for more lessons.

Matt has been coaching my son for 4 years now. Having my kids surf with Matt has been one of the best decisions I ever made through out my son's upbringing. Matt shaped him into one of the most solid surfers in his age range out in the line up. As well as giving him priceless mentoring throughout the process. Thank you to Matt and the staff at Lovewater.

Had a lesson with my wife and a friend.  Matt was just super.  Totally calm and polite.  Explained the basics in minutes and had us in the water practicing successfully.  Never thought I'd ride waves on my first lesson.  Offered great tips specific to each of us to help us improve and incredibly patient.

Oh my gosh! I can't even begin to describe what an amazing time my little cousin and I had during our lesson with Nemo. We got there a bit early to figure out where to go which was a piece of cake since they had a sign up, after getting suited in our wetsuits we headed down to the water to get started. We got on the board after some instructions on how to tackle the waves and how to catch a wave. I was able to get up a few times but my cousin was a natural and enjoyed getting to ride the waves. We're definitely planning on taking more lessons with Nemo! He's seriously the best!

I had zero expectations (skill wise) for my 6 year old daughter going to surf camp with Lovewater, and I was blown away that she was standing up and surfing the first day. Matt and his staff are beyond amazing:  patient, kind, positive, encouraging, happy coaches who are teaching her some really solid surfing fundamentals. 

I'm so grateful to the whole Lovewater team, and can't wait to continue lessons in the future.
Matt and his crew are the most genuine people you will ever meet. They know there stuff. I recommend them to anyone who is wanting to learn to surf and have an awesome time  and experience.

We had a great semi private lesson with Tyler. We were able to get lots of time in the water and they took us to the best spot with waves for beginners. We learned a lot and even my 7 year old was able to get up easily- he had a blast. Thanks for a good experience. Definitely recommend!

Attention everyone! This is a great surf school! I can't say enough how very pleased I was with the way this school accommodated our really large group and gave each and every person the opportunity to experience the rush of excitement that happens when a person catches their first wave! The owners name is Matt and he was a pro-surfer and you can tell by the way he is in the water and the way he's trained his staff. All the instructors are phenomenal, well-trained, Great communicators and help every person who might have reservations about ocean sports to feel at peace in the water. You show up and that's it... they have all the boards and wetsuits you need. If you're concerned about them having a small or large enough sized suit, you can just call or email.... I highly recommend Lovewater for all your surf instruction needs, young or old, aged five to 85, it wouldn't matter.... they're THAT good!
Such a great lesson and Robert was great working with us! Definitely a highlight of our trip!

Our group of 8, 4 adults, 4 children went to surf lesson with Loverwater Surf School.  Nemo, Austin, and Michael are awesome instructors.  They were very positive, professional, and encouraging to all of us.  We had a blast!  Can't wait to take more lessons.

My husband and I had never stepped on a surf board before, so we were not sure how it was going to go. It was awesome!! Thanks Matt for a really fun experience!! We will definitely be searching out surfing on the Great Lakes!

Matt is the best instructor I ever had. He is very polite, considered and a real gentleman.   He is a very good teacher, patient and very up lifting.  He sees your every best effort and even if you fail, he still finds something good to say.
Me and my nephew we were thought by Matt. We had a blast. 
I loved it. If I ever go back to Santa Barbara, I will look for Matt to have anather surfing lesson.

I have very high expectations and searched up and down the coast of CA looking for a GOOD coach /mentor for my sons MORE THAN THANKFUL we found LOVE WATER. We homeschool and love adventure! My boys started surfing for the first time in September, and it is now February. They are getting ready to make the transition to short boards & I can't believe the progress they have made in just a few months. Matt has put in a lot of time & effort, with my sons and they ADORE him
Wether your looking for a new life style with your kids and wanna join the love water homeschool surf program or just wanna go for the day you definitely dont wanna go anywhere else
m

We had Nick, Lauren, and Michael, they were the BEST instructors I highly recommend them. They were very engaged and were super helpful. Even if I fell I didn't feel discouraged because of their supportive nature.

Beautiful peaceful beach, albeit mildly crowded for a secluded beach. There were people twenty or so feet away from me in each direction. 

It's part of a community beach for a wealthy gated suburb just outside of Santa Barbara.
Scenic views in every direction and lots of friendly folks who politely mind their own business.

I understand not wanting any strange cars parked overnight at the beach hence the no parking from 2am to 6am signs around, but I felt like it certainly limited my time out there. I had to leave before sundown to find a place to sleep. I'm not homeless but my house is a far drive from SB and I made a trip out here specifically to go to this beach.
Great clothing optional beach.  People are friendly.  Never seems packed.  No bathrooms or stores.  Lots of beautiful sand and calm sunbathing.  Great clothing optional get away.

I've never actually been down to this beach, but the surrounding trail is beautiful and well-maintained. 

The trail can be accessed from a few points: South Patterson Avenue directly adjacent to the "More Mesa Shores" sign, or from a side street off Puente Drive which is where I normally access from. There is no private parking lot, and the side street off Puente is strictly a no-parking zone, so make sure to park on the main road where street spaces are plentiful. 

The trail is about three miles in a loop. It's ideal for walking or mountain biking. I like entering from the Puente side, walking 3/4 the distance, exiting at the Patterson/Shoreline side and then walking back via the Obern path which will add an extra two miles to the route. 

The trail is clean and mostly level with several spots to meander off and enjoy. There's a designated paragliding launch area (and to that, I say NO THANKS!). A good amount of the trail is up on a cliff against the ocean so be prepared for some great views. Note that the adjacent beach is nude-friendly and seems to be occupied predominantly by older men. The tide is high and there isn't much of a sandy area to relax on, so I'd recommend avoiding it if you aren't into that sort of thing. 

If you're looking for a mild way to kill an hour or two, check out this scenic trail!

This was very challenging to find. I was heading to it by bike because yelp said it was the closest beach from where I was (I was at this pier at Goleta ). Anyway, I got really twisted and turned around and eventually found what yelp said was the address, but this was not a beach. It was just kind of a cliff. I believe there is a beach, but I think the address posted here on yelp may be incorrect.

More Mesa reminds me of Douglas Preserve but with more space and less people. We spent about two hours wandering around until we got the cliffs. We took our dogs who loved running around. The cliffs are especially beautiful (note: they do overlook the nude beach below). There were people on horseback or biking around. It's very peaceful out here.

The walk started with mas mesa then headed down to the beach for menos mesa. In other words, crossing the grassland More Mesa is required to reach the beach via stepped trail.

Two trailheads exist. The shortest path to beach access begins at the northeast corner of the mesa at Mockingbird Lane that is a straight half-mile dirt road. The longer version starts at the northwest corner of the bluff at Shoreline Drive. This 1.5-mile long trail goes behind homes towards the ocean and then turns left along the edge of a cliff to the beach access at the southeast corner of the mesa.

Due to limited and inconvenient ingresses, this section of beach is not heavily trafficked. The mesa had lots of dried native grasses. In the middle of the mesa is an excavated section carved into bike ramps.

One of the best beaches in Santa Barbara is More Mesa, it is unregulated by the government pinheads and people can enjoy themselves in nature with their dogs and just have their own personal responsibility.

More Mesa Beach is a narrow beach below the bluffs of More Mesa Park. It's pretty secluded and because so it has the reputation as a clothing-optional beach.  If you turn right at the bottom of the stairs it is the clothing-optional beach. If you turn left it is not. If you continue on left you will be at Hope Ranch Beach. 

The main entrance is at the end of Mockingbird Lane in the Vista La Cumbre development.

Nude Beach appears to be a haven for gays and pick ups. 
I imagine they need an outlet and place to meet. 
I enjoyed the experience ad being nude. 
Wonderful place and even more if you're not straight.

Probably my favorite place on earth. I love the beach but I'm not personally a fan of actually going onto the beach. Here you can walk or mountain bike (or road bike if you're feeling daring) the coast of California. And when I mean coast, I mean be careful because a strong wind will send you off the edge. It's fucking amazing. Just wish there was a way to make solo female travelers feel comfortable at a nude beach. I wasn't even nude but old men would not stop creeping and following me around. Not this place's fault but I'm full of wishful thinking.

Here you have a perfect example of the tension between development and public access.
An Arab prince bought the property and is seeking to develop it, in spite of both it's history of public use and it's importance as a biological preserve.
Foreign money needs to be driven, with great discretion, from our common ground.
It is not OK to buy your way into limiting access and the privatization of our coast.
This is a great place to swim, watch whales, and enjoy the benefits of access.
Ignore that at your peril.
Went this past weekend and it was really nice but I think it's actually a dog beach. My group love dogs so we enjoyed watching them playing around. Vibe is chill and no one bugs anyone.
The hike is fantastic. The views are suburb. The beach is great but be prepared for some naked dudes. It is a clothing optional beach so I would avoid heading down with kids.

There is a beautiful open space on the way to More Mesa Beach! So many trails to choose, every day is a different. if you walk toward the beach trial you can enjoy the spectacular views along the bluffs! It is just heaven. Santa Barbara has many options, and this space is in my top 5! Don't miss an opportunity to get some fresh air and experience one of SB's Gems!

name: Lake Los Carneros Park
formatted_address: Los Carneros Rd At Calle Real

yelp_reviews.text:
Most of the property is scrub, but has one very interesting feature - a zig-zag bridge across the lake's marsh flowers (see pic). Unfortunately, the flowers weren't in bloom when I was there. The lake looks like a waterway that feeds mountain run-off to the ocean, but it's land-locked and there are trails that surround the entire lake.

I entered the bridge by walking behind the Stow House (see separate Yelp listing) and found this sign (see pic) which leads to the bridge.
Great hidden spot in Goleta. Although swimming is not allowed in the lake, this is a great place for a run, a bike ride, or bird watching.
Don't know how I didn't find this hidden gem sooner! Such a nice place to go on a calm walk.

We visited with our toddler-age children and spent about 20 minutes feeding the ducks. My 17-month-old daughter tried to walk into the lake a couple of times so we had to leave. While feeding the ducks was enjoyable, the water isn't circulated so it's very dirty. I think this would be a fun spot for school-age children. I would bring my kids here also to ride their bikes or scooters since the path is closed off to vehicle traffic. I will definitely come back to explore the area more.

I kept hearing that there were some good places to jog/ walk around the Goleta area, and this is a hidden treasure. 

Since I live close by, I walked there, but I have read online that you can park in a lot close to the fire station of Los Carneros Rd. 

It's a beautiful place where people take their families and dogs to go on walks and ride their bikes. I have also read that they allow fishing if you have a license. Some of the path is paved, and some of it is dirt, but the trails are well maintained.

I'll definitely be making use of this park.

Had an amazing picnic on the grass here yesterday. What a gem. I can't wait to hike to the lake and see more of the railroad area.   It was quiet and well kept. There were people walking and running the trails with their dogs. Nothing to star about really.

Lake Los Carneros Park is a great hidden spot that you wouldn't even know was in Goleta.

The park actually contains several children's attractions (the Stow House and Railroad Museum), but once you park you'll find access to several dirt trails. There is also a gorgeous old house with a beautiful lawn I saw people picnicking on. The whole thing is a large loop around the lake--there are dirt trails through wooded or brush areas, a wooden deck over the marshland and a blacktop portion of trail with great views of the lake and mountains. I would say the whole length of trail around the lake is about a mile, but there are several offshoots leading into more grasslands. 

Tips: There are several doggie bag dispensers and benches. Signs say dogs must be on a leash, but I saw a few dogs off-leash. There are bathrooms behind the museum shop (I saw the sign but didn't try them out). There are lots of ducks, swans, birds and other critters. 

Directions: Take the Los Carneros exit off Hwy. 101 and head toward the mountains. Go straight through the roundabout and make the first right at the fire station. Parking is free.
An excellent park with a variety of trails and paved paths perfect for walking or running.  Beautiful nature preserve with friendly outdoor enthusiasts.

When seeking out a place near home to enjoy Mother Nature, walk off some stress, or take a bike ride, Lake Los Carneros Park is one of my favorite parks to frequent.  This preserve is super special and unique to Goleta and let me tell you why:

- The South Coast Railroad Museum (presently on National Register of Historic Places and the California Registry of Historical Resources) is here on the grounds right as you enter the parking lot. You can't miss the bright orange and red original Goleta Railroad Station which was relocated from Old Town Goleta in two pieces after grassroot organization raised the funds to save the building from demolition. The museum features a miniature railroad, a Southern Pacific caboose, and a even model train set. Children can catch a ride along the miniature railroad on Fridays, Saturdays, and Sundays between 1:00-4:00 pm.

-I frequent the well-maintained, easy 1.0 mile walking, biking, running trail (half cement, half dirt) that goes around the circumference of Lake Carneros with a few small hills. The path is well maintained and has multiple paths that lead to the nearby surrounding neighborhoods. You will often see red-tailed hawks, turkey vultures, geese, swans, ducks, rabbits, lizards and occasionally snakes. Although the lake varies in depth due to the changing seasons, I can always expect to see birds enjoying the water. They do not allow you to swim or boat in the lake. You will feel as if you're far from civilization with mountains in the backdrop.

- The Stow House (U.S. Registered Historical Landmark) remains on the property with its original gardens being maintained. It reminds me of what I would see in Savannah, Georgia with a "Carpenter Gothic Victorian style." The home was previously part of the Rancho La Patera but has since been turned into the Goleta Historical Society headquarters. A little known fact: This was the site of California's first commercial lemon orchard. Tours of the house are available on Saturdays and Sundays, 1:00-4:00 pm. The house and grounds are used for many local and cultural events.

Check out this beautiful park right in Goleta's backyard!

Great spot to walk/run in Goleta. I like seeing all the dog walkers (leash required). Lots of ducks in the lake at spring time and you see the occasional cotton tail rabbit or crane. It's a small lake so I usually do a couple loops around, each loop only takes about 20 min at a leisurely pace. There are several little side paths that weave into the trees and benches on both sides of the lake to sit and relax to enjoy the view.

I grew up just a few blocks from here and we would ride our bikes, or walk there everyday. It is such a beautiful getaway in the middle of goleta. The trees and vegetation are absolutely beautiful. It's also a great place for a run to clear your mind.
We used to go feed the ducks all the time. I've seen a lot of wildlife throughout the park which is so awesome. Definitely recommend.

This open space is just about as historic as Goleta gets!

As a Goleta local, I have been fortunate enough to patronize this large park on a number of occasions. It's situated in a large undeveloped section of town and offers beauty as far as the eye can see. 

The park borders a quiet residential neighborhood, a few main roads, a couple of churches, and the historic Stow House, the latter of which offers a limited parking lot. I typically enter through lower La Patera Lane and park on the street. There are a handful of entrances on both La Patera and Covington Way. 

This park is home to a meandering trail, a neat bridge, and an awesome small lake. As a child, I would come here to fish. The open space has been improved with benches and is very dog-friendly. It butts up to the 101 freeway but feels like a private oasis. Definitely a spot to check out in Goleta.

name: AutoCamp Santa Barbara
categories: Hotels & Travel, RV Dealers, RV Parks, RV Rental, Vacation Rentals, Event Planning & Services, Campgrounds, Automotive, Hotels

yelp_reviews.text:
My husband and I really enjoyed our stay at Santa Barbara airstream. We wanted an adventure vacation in Santa Barbara and dislike staying in stuffy hotels. The airstreams have everything you need for a nice stay it even includes bikes so you can literally go anywhere! I would recommend this to anyone looking for an adventure.

Btw the dive bar next door is a hoot. The bartender thinks and acts like a pirate. Stumbling distance.

Fun place but the pictures are very deceiving. Very small. We had airstream 3. Does not sleep more than 2 and a child. Location not great but SB is a beautiful place. Heater did not work the second night. Nice sitting area outside with cute chairs.

This place is awesome. Having graduated from UCSB and not having visited in some time, I wanted to a quick visit. The airstreams are pretty nice, recently updated. The bathrooms are a bit tight and lack privacy from anyone else in the trailer, but hey, you are camping. Bikes are included, which is the best way to visit downtown.

Was very excited to stay here, saw lots of cool pics online. In real life though, the experience was....OK. I didn't think the Airstream was super clean, signs of mold in the bathroom, cushions in sitting area were filthy. Website states that bedding is "plush", not really. The pillows were thin and the comforter felt synthetic/cheap. For the price per night, really expected the bed and bathroom linens to be top notch, and they weren't. My second big issue was with the kitchen supplies. The drawers were an unorganized mess of randomness. We had to ask our neighbor to use their bottle opener - if nothing else, please put a bottle opener in trailer #4. Last issue, location. It really is on a busy street and you hear EVERYTHING. Totally agree with other reviews that list the location as a let down. No view and you literally open your windows up to your neighbors trailer. Remember you are vacationing in a trailer park with lots of foot and car traffic.

Hi Yelpers... so here is the scene. (It is our everyday life and we want to get out sometime to do whatever our heart desires.) Mainly this is a bar with friends getting straight faced right? I had 2 days off and I needed to get out of the city. My wife and I went up to Santa Barbara and I was wearing my man pants so of course she obliged. She was wearing her women pants when she found this place AutoCamp. AutoCamp doesn't look like much and for the price you can get something better. But it is the experience and it is different, not to mention the top of line quality material they used. Swag in a camper, seriously and they had monopoly that my wife and I enjoyed. I crushed.... Yea. My wife is amazing and she let me win, and the bed is so comfy yes comfy. 

Here is the best part. During check out we called the manager and said, "where did you get the material to decor the interior of the camper?" She laughed, we laughed and she gave us some insider info. 

If you want a getaway in beautiful Santa Barbara makes some memories and enjoy some awesome time together then find AutoCamp and go! 

My wife and I will be traveling to their other locations. Best, Kruger
what a great concept! step back to simpler time. The camper was great very comfortable, nice bed, linens and towels. Sitting outside at night was dreamy with trees and twinkling lights and stars.

The Autocamp is a hilarious and creative, fun place to stay. It is adjacent to a trailer park on a busy street but they have done a great job to bring charm to it with colorful Adirondack chairs, bicycles, wood fencing enclosures and potted succulents. Inside the airstreams are decked out with everything you could need from flatscreen TVs to small kitchens and warm showers. There is a mini bar stocked with delicious local snacks and restaurant suggestions. Down the street is a great coffee shop called the daily grind,  a couple of excellent Mexican restaurants called Los agave and el citio, as well as a Ralph's. Trader Joe's is only two blocks away also. There was a near - monsoon while we stayed there - torrential downpours - and our unit leaked a bit. I was really impressed by the level of customer service and would recommend this day to anyone in search of a fun quirky getaway.
Loved, loved, loved this place...cute and convenient. Made the best anniversary/birthday weekend ever.

Stayed here on 6/24 & 6/25 in Trailer 1 & 3 and had the best time! Truly a unique and must-do experience! The bathroom was a bit small in trailer 3, but trailer 1 made up for it! It had a claw foot tub!

And the staff was soooo nice and helpful! They let us store our drinks and food in our next reserved trailer until we could check in and let us change bikes so we could ride bikes that were more suited for our lack of height, LOL. (Thank you Lucy and Jennifer!)

The outside electric grill is such a great idea - we grilled some steaks and veggies for dinner and invited friends over. There's a dive bar next door (Jimboz) and we went on karaoke night, Maggie the karaoke dj and the bartender were cool, they even came over to ask us to come back when we were done eating dinner, haha!

Christi from autocamp was super attentive too. Can't wait for my next adventure there!

Scheduled this quaint lil site a few months early and ended up wanting to stay a few more days.The Airstream itself is what you'd expect small and tight especially the bathroom.But super clean the AC and fridge worked great.Their was a courtyard adjacent to Airstream with an awning and comfy chairs and a lil electric grill.Their were two bikes but if you your party needs one or two more the folks are pretty friendly Their and will share theirs.My only real complaint was in Unit 2 the parking space is unusually small.A truck or SUV will not fit.Their are numerous places to eat and golf course a 15 min stroll from the camp.And Mc Connells Ice Cream a bike ride away.For those who indulge a bar next door.You are very close to road but very little noise from cars.A very nice spot that I will use again and super family friendly.You truly get the camping experience.ENJOY FOLKS.

Depends on what you're seeking ...

We stayed at AutoCamp Santa Barbara this past weekend and it was not the "best" glamping experience I've ever had - nor was it "so terrible" that we had to leave and find other accommodations. Typically with places like these - that have reviews ranging from "amazing" to "awful" - it really comes down to what you're looking for. 

Would not recommend AutoCamp Santa Barbara if ... 

You're looking to somehow have a serene "camping/glamping" experience two miles from State Street.

You will be bothered by a bar/lounge next door that plays loud music with echoing voices out front.

You're fearful of staying in a "less than desirable" neighborhood adjacent to a trailer park.

You're looking for an "experience" beyond a cool Airstream in an unattended park.

We checked into AutoCamp directly from another Aistream park. The Airstreams at AutoCamp are modern and spacious but to be spacious, they are missing a few things (i.e., cabinet space, full kitchen, table/seating, etc.). The bathrooms are huge and this was a major plus. A real flushing toilet and a lovely shower, nice toiletries provided. We lucked out with sites #1 and #4, which are the only two with a huge tree that provides excellent shade. #1 is closest to the street. Though in my opinion, it doesn't matter how far or near you are to the street - they are all close to the street and I would venture to guess that everything #1 hears, the Airstreams situated further from the street will still hear. It appeared that the downside of an Airstream situated in the back is being window to window with some of the trailers from the trailer park. 

No table/chairs or booth in the Airstream but there are two Adirondack chairs in front of each Airstream along with a little table, situated on a small patch of turf. Two small folding tables in the Airstream that worked as a laptop stand. Electric BBQ provided out front. Though there is plateware, silverware, etc., the setup is just not too ideal for cooking. The fridge is very small (and freezes everything) - there is a microwave. But there's also easy access to a couple of Mexican restaurants across the street, sushi, Handlebar coffee shop (excellent), bakery, Chicken Ranch restaurant. The bikes were excellent and we rode 20+ miles each day with them, locks provided. Also walked to State Street and the pier, which was a few miles away. You can park right in front of your Airstream.

All in all - it was fine - would I go back - probably not. We are looking forward to AutoCamp Yosemite and would definitely try their Russian River location as well. Santa Barbara is just lacking in scenery, ambience, experience. But it met our needs and we had an enjoyable time regardless, as we were not looking to be hanging out at our accommodations all day. 

Customer service was very prompt via email.

The Autocamp in Santa Barbara is a truly special place. Came here with my girlfriend from Los Angeles to celebrate our one year anniversary and we felt right at home in Airstream #4. The staff is extremely helpful and were able to accommodate a late checkout for us. They even paid for shipping when I forgot two shirts in the closet.

Can't wait for the one in LA to open!

The idea is great.  But the space inside is very cramped so don't expect to bring your whole house here.  It is part of a trailer park, just know going in.  The airstream we stayed out was perfect on the outside.  The only real grip I have is the bathroom, it smells like urine, the drain does not work well so the water fills up in the shower and sink.

@SBAutoCamp

3.5 stars. Very cute and clever concept! Retro look with modern conveniences.

My hubby and I stayed in the 1959 blue/green décor trailer in space 3, 11/28-11/30 with our small 18 lb. dog. We enjoyed our time here, but these are tight quarters, so not quite as comfy as we had hoped. Granted, it's not meant for you to stay in all day long, but if you are claustrophobic, I wouldn't recommend staying here.

If you are used to camping in a tent, this will feel like a nice upgrade. However, if you typically stay in a hotel, you might feel a bit disappointed. I'll admit that based on the website and reviews, I was expecting a bit more. But it wasn't horrible, either. 

With respect to the trailer's web page at http://autocamp.com/sb/airstream-three/, some notes for the marketing dept: 
- Bathroom is decent, but definitely not luxurious as stated on the website
- Was expecting a bit more decor based on the pics, but no biggie.
- Cooking utensils were in drawers vs. in holders on counter
- Dining set shown on the website not accurate. Table is same, but actual chairs provided are two folding lawn chairs that don't go with the table.

Pros: 
- Great staff (Christi and her husband)! Very attentive and responsive. There was no coffee when we arrived, and after letting them know via email, they drove over to bring some to us! Very sweet!
- Love the little patio area! That was my favorite part of this place! Was nice to chill out there with a glass of wine in the evening.
- Nice toiletries (Malin + Goetz)
- Great location; several restaurants and markets nearby
- Love the guest book with the history of the place and ways to make our stay more convenient and enjoyable 
- Heater and A/C worked great and we had plenty of hot water

Cons:
- A bit spendy for not being a hotel and being in off-peak season. We paid $191/night + a $25 pet fee 
- Full size bed is no bueno if you are used to a queen or king bed. Appears cozy at first, but you have no room to move around and may wake up with neck and back pain. The mattress sits on a wood platform and is smaller than its space, leaving gaps around the sides. My pillow kept falling into the crack during the night.
- You feel every movement of the other person. If they're walking and you're in bed, you will feel the whole trailer shake. 
- Not sure why we paid $25 extra dollars to bring our dog. There are no poo bags or bins in the Autocamp and there are no amenities for pets. 
- Area is a tad noisy (you're in a city, not out in the country), especially if any Autocamp guests are celebrating. Some of the trailer park residents on the other side of the Autocamp can be loud as well, but they live there, so you can't really do anything about it.
- There was dust on the tops of many items like shelves, blinds and wall hangings
- Very cramped toilet area, and we're not large people
- Agree with the other reviewer who said the toilet paper provided is too thin. Glad we brought our own :-)
- Fridge is tiny...don't expect to fit standard square Styrofoam restaurant leftover containers in there
- Fabric shower curtain was a bit mildewy at the bottom and didn't stay in place well, causing a bit of a mess
- Bathroom sink faucet is set too far back from the bowl, which caused a water mess every time we washed our hands or did anything else in the sink. 

Suggestions for management:
- Things to add: 1-2 hooks for jackets/sweaters in the closet and a few little shelves or hooks throughout for drying bath and dish towels or other stuff. We had to get creative to be able to hang our towels up to dry; dish drainer; small bottle of disinfecting spray under the kitchen or bathroom sink for quick/easy cleaning; scrubby sponge for better dish cleaning
- Make trash and recycle bins available outside each trailer. Trailer 1 had a trash can in their space so not sure why we didn't. 
- Use a plastic vs. fabric shower curtain. Too flimsy and didn't hold back water very well.
- Suggest replacing games with traditional ones like Scrabble and Monopoly 
- Guest book shows this trailer has a Blu Ray DVD player in it, but we didn't see one.

Maintenance needs:
- Broken blind holder in kitchen area
- Window frames need cleaning. Some showing signs of mildew.
- Some of the screens were bent or had holes in them
- Stain on kitchen counter to left of sink
- Rear patio light (closest to fence) is too bright, but we couldn't reposition it because it would have shined into the homes of those who live there
- Louver windows next to front door need shades to help keep out draft and tone down the brightness of the outdoor deck lighting 

Notes for guests of this trailer: 
- There are helmets in the closet for usage with the bikes, which is cool
- Bring a bottle of Febreze or Lysol for the bathroom. Trust me on this. ;-)
- There is no maid service unless you want to pay extra, but I'm unsure of the cost.
- Note that you can't turn off the outdoor lights--they are automatic

Stayed here last weekend in unit #3. I had many questions before checking in. I had emailed the manager Christi a half a dozen times. She is very laid back. Knows just about everything you could ask about the area, and fired responses back to me almost immediately. This is in a trailer park. ( at the entrance). Which I had worried about a little considering I was there with my lady and we were looking for a romantic experience. Didn't want to hear drug addicts arguing and kids running wild. This park is not like this. The residents looked like retired folks possibly seasonal live ins. It was very quiet at night and peaceful during the day. I was comfortable there wasn't going to be anybody sniffing around our unit while we were out. The unit was surrounded in bamboo and avocado  trees. Very quaint little deck and a nice little patio set up. Very cool lighting at night. Inside there is everything you could possibly need for cooking and relaxing. Super cold ac too for those warm SB nights. The linens were amazing. I thought they were more cozy than some 5* hotels I've stayed at. It comes with 2 beach cruisers and a lock which was a very cool feature. It's just about all downhill to the wharf. Super easy ride to the funk zone. If you're not in the greatest shape or just not in the mood I'd suggest just tossing them in a cab to get back. That's what we did. It was easy. Those van cabs are sitting everywhere on State. I would recommend this place for people who appreciate adventure and don't necessarily want to give up those little touches that make a can make a good vacation great.

I booked a few nights at AutoCamp this weekend for my birthday before I had checked out the yelp reviews. After looking at some of them I had a lot of anxiety about the place. When we pulled up I was pleasantly surprised. Everything looked like the pictures. It is off a street that drives some traffic but we stayed in Airstream 1 which is the closest one to the street and I didn't even notice the noise. You can't see the trailer park unless you walk past it. The interior of the airstream was exactly as pictured. The fixtures all worked, the wall heater in the bedroom worked pretty well too. We just assumed there would be an iron included but there wasn't so if you think you might need one, you should bring it! Everything seemed really great! We also locked ourselves out the first night because I attempted to change the code to get into the airstream but didn't do it properly. Someone answered the phone right away and was able to reset the lock for us. I would definitely stay here again and I look forward to a stay at the Russian River location soon!

Already made our next reservation!!!! This place is an A+!!! Owner came and personally asked how our stay was, trailer was in immaculate shape, clean, cool, and inviting... Nice outside sitting area with new adirondack chairs, hoping a warm fire pit will be here on our next visit :) overall not one complaint!!! If there were 6 stars I would give it!!!!

Beautiful spot to get away from it all. The lot has a half-dozen or so classic Airstream travel trailers tucked away off a main street in fairly central part of Santa Barbara. The trailers are surprisingly spacious inside and well laid out. 

Awesome restaurants nearby and a Trader Joe's around the corner accessible with the camp's provided bikes. Definitely worth a visit.

This was a surprise Christmas gift for my husband. We had been dying to go ever since we'd heard of it. But what we didn't know was that it was a shiny air stream in the front of a trailer park. On a busy street. With a dive bar a couple of doors down that you could hear into the early hours of the morning. We were so disappointed that we left early. This was a very expensive mistake since they would not refund any money at all. The airstream was clean but that's the only positive. Very disappointing experience. Sadly we cannot recommend.

The novelty of staying in an airstream is pretty cool. That being said, I think over $200 a night for a very small space (we stayed in three), might be a bit much. The location is great though, with restaurants and bars walking distance. The bikes are awesome, but keep in mind the slightly downhill coast to the water feels like all it's three miles of incline on the way back. The bathroom is cute but if you are a bigger person you will not be amused by the small toilet cubby. And the toilet paper- you know the scratchy toilet paper from grade school? Yep, same stuff. Maybe it's me, but why have Malin and Goetz bath products and then cheapest possible tp? Overall, if the rates are lower than a hotel, I would stay here, if not, I would probably choose a hotel.

Cute. A bit small for comfort. Is a sweet idea, but the location is loud. The ear plugs don't do the job, perhaps the liquor store next door if you drink enough to pass out. Good for teenagers. And consider that is in a trailer park. Is clean and the little outdoor patio is smart; but did I mentioned  the avenue and the constant trucks and cars...

My mom and I were fortunate enough to extend our last minute day trip to Santa Barbara and stay in one of these incredibly designed airstream campers for a night. I found our  stay to be a dream, the camper was cozy comfy and extremely lovely I only wish we could've stayed longer! the campsite was very quiet which made it easy for us to wake up bright and early and hit the road feeling rested. Everything was practically perfect from the luxurious flat screen placed oh-so-perfectly above the bed, to the fabulous malin+goetz products provided ( which by the way I brought home) I was thoroughly impressed! The only critique I would give is that the bed had a tiny bit of a gap from the back end of the camper. It might be a smart move to customize the mattress so it perfectly fits the curve of the airstream, much like the little love nest nook on the opposite end. I will most definitely be coming back hopefully for much longer than just one night!

We were not able to make it up due to the 101 being closed, which worried me because I heard they have a strict policy regarding cancellations, but we were very pleased they offered to refund us. 
Way to go, AutoCamp! :) I cant wait until the 101 is open again and we can stay.  I am sure with customer service like they displayed, it'll be an awesome time!
