My husband and I stayed in Suite #3 at Santa Barbara AutoCamp November 7-9, 2017. We were pleasantly surprised when we arrived and saw the beautiful Airstream, and the layout outside.   The trailer was completely renovated.  It has a "modern" feel inside.  There was plenty of room inside to move around for the two of us.  The bed/bedroom was laid out efficiently.  There was a queen sized extremely comfortable bed, plenty of pillows, extra blanket and two nightstands on either side of the bed.  There were plenty of electrical outlets for charging cellphones, etc.  The flat screened TV was great.   Luckily my husband had grown up camping and using Airstreams.  He knew how to operate the ceiling fans.  The windows in the bedroom were a little tricky to open, but once again my husband knew the trick!   The bathroom was very well laid out.  The shower was wonderful.  The water pressure in the shower wasn't like at home, but did the job beautifully.   The sink was great.  There was an adjustable make-up/shaving mirror too.  The door was an awesome barn-door style.  We BBQ'd on the electric grill outside, and it worked out just fine.   The little patio area was fun to sit.  There were lights in the trees that came on.  Great for sipping wine and or cocktails.   The area was very quiet on the nights we stayed.  Suite #3 is the second Airstream in from the street, so the street noise wasn't that noticeable.  We would definitely stay again, and try the other location.

If you have a chance to stay at the airstream trailer park DO IT! It was super cute and fun when we stayed at the auto camp over Memorial Day weekend. The Airstreams are really cute inside, very nicely designed and restored. Also the "parking spots" for the trailers are nice, with electric grills, beach cruisers you can borrow, and pretty little lights in the trees.

I imagine during the height of summer it can get a little noisy in this location (busy street, bar nearby), but I still recommend it for people who like different experiences, especially if you can go at times other than summer. BTW we booked on AirBNB.

We stayed for two nights last weekend. On a positive note, I would give five stars to whoever did the website photography, as they could make Amy Schumer looks like Kate Upton. However, to be fully transparent, the photos of the ocean, should have been replaced with ones of the busy street, laundry mat, dive bar, Mexican grocer, and the mobile home park itself where it is situated. The camper itself was extremely small. I am 6'4 and could not fit into the camper without ducking around corners. It was clean, which was nice. Also the outside area was situated nicely, however given the area and surroundings, you didn't exactly want to lounge outside. In reality, we avoided the area and spent our days off site. We only returned late at night to crash. All being said, if this was an economy rental, it would have been totally adequate. However, when you position your property as a luxury experience and have very misleading photos, you set an expectation that this property cannot fulfill. Especially at the almost $300 per night rate. Likewise, it left us feeling both mislead and disappointed.
Worth trying. Clean camper. Complimentary bikes. Just a mile or so from downtown. Bakery and store across street. Dive bar next door. Trader Joes prob a quarter mile away. Clean. Safe. Thumbs up.

What a neat quirky place to stay during a visit to Santa Barbara!  I have no idea how they got a claw foot bathtub into an airstream but it's awesome.   Good rates when  compared to hotel rooms and a great bar with no name next door!

Thank you SB Auto Park! What a great visit! This is the latest trend and the coolest hotel stay I have ever had. From the awesome shower with recycled tile walls; to the amazing sheets at 1000 count; the filtered drinking water; and the dual flush toilet for those environmental types that are interested in conserving water - it was a great experience. Actually, it is design with a heart. What I really loved was the complimentary beach cruisers (yes, with helmets); the neighborhood restaurants; and even Trader Joe's is a short walk away. I began my day with fresh ground coffee while sitting on the porch in the sun reading about the local events in SB. The newly planted grass, custom driveway entry were enhanced by the bamboo-framed trailer site which added complete privacy, all making it a delightful stay. Kudos to SB Auto Park; I will be back! - debo

Had a fabulous stay at the Auto Camp! My boyfriend and I stayed in Santa Cruz airstream last weekend and just loved it. Such a unique and fun experience if you're looking for something outside of the box, not your average hotel stay! Loved the mason jars for glasses, we enjoyed sitting out on the patio underneath the giant Oak Tree, with some wine and good music on a lazy Saturday afternoon. There is a gorgeous view of the mountains behind Santa Barbara from the Auto Camp. You're also within walking distance of an amazing Thai restaurant, if you're looking for a good dinner spot, would recommend checking out TAP Thai. We will definitely be back next time we visit Santa Barbara!

I stayed in the Santa Cruz unit with my boyfriend and we loved it so much we came back a few months later and stayed in the Santa Rosa unit. Both experiences were awesome. Accommodations were modern, very up to date and super comfy. We loved the claw foot tub in Santa Rosa, the complimentary beach cruisers, the plush beds and the fully equipped kitchens. We enjoyed cooking dinner in the units, hanging out on the patio, and the delicious breakfast spot across the street. Will definitely be recommending this place to out of town family and friends.

Vacationed with my family and we stayed a night in this auto camp in an airstream (spot #2). There were only 3 of us, and it was very comfortable! It was also very quiet and relaxing. Instead of going out for dinner, we bought and cooked a meal on the grill that came with the airstream. It was such a pleasant experience! We were able to sit out on lawn area and just enjoy a quiet evening! 

The bathroom was kind of small, but that's to be expected, of course. Very tastefully remodeled, and the beds were very comfortable. I'd definitely go back and stay here again.

A super fun way to experience Santa Barbara! The airstreams are nicely done with modern amenities. Don't expect a lot of room but it's a nice experience away from a hotel. We really liked that we could bring our dog and we all enjoyed the little outdoor sitting area. I surprised my husband with this for his birthday and he really liked how unique it was. We would definitely do this again and also want to try their other location on the Russian River.

My daughter gifted this stay to my husband for his bday and I got to tag alone accommodation were like a tiny hotel on wheels without the room service although Sage was their when we needed him had a great stay ... Glamping at it's finest .

Such a charming little place. Don't expect the Ritz. It is a trailer with tight space with above normal room amenities. 
If you have Camped before you will love it. The grounds are incredible. Fake grass, nice lines, great big oak tree with lights for night.
Full kitchen, bathroom and very cozy bed. 
We brought our corn hole game-and had a blast. Happy hour, corn hole, barbecue, bath tub, lawn chairs... what more do you need!

Stayed in the one on with the clawfoot tub... (can't recall the name of the unit, sorry!).  Great idea, well executed, and more comfy than I expected -- I'm 6'2" and generally prefer BIG spaces, but it was bright and not claustrophobic at all.  My favorite parts: Free bikes to use; very cool bath products (I'm a big Malin & Goetz fan); and a cool dive bar within stumbling distance.   Only problem I had was that it took me awhile to figure out how to turn on the heat (hint: it's on the wall by the heater in each room, not on the main thermostat, which apparently just controls AC).  In short, it was a memorable, comfortable experience and I will for sure be back next time we're in Santa Barbara.

Have reservations, but they will not let you cancel even with the Thomas Fire happening in Santa Barbara, real classy AutoCamp.  Their cancellation policies are ridiculous, so don't book unless you can avoid a major disaster.  Serenity now!

My bf and I stayed here a few weeks ago for my bday.  I was so excited to stay here cuz I'm obsessed with airstreams. It was everything I thought and more!  We stayed in trailer 5. It was actually more roomie than I expected. The bedroom was  so comfortable and cozy.   I was surprised that a full size sofa fit comfortably.  The fridge was small but fit the essentials.  The bathroom was so cute.   My only complaint was it was a little awkward showering in the jacuzzi tub. No comfortable place to put the shower head.  The neighbors were quiet and no noise from the street.  I would definitely stay here again!!!

This was a girls weekend in Santa Barbara and we decided to give the Auto Camp a try.  The Air Stream trailers have been tastefully modified and appointed with modern features.  The shower/ bath is cleverly functional and the kitchen sink is stylish and clever.  The accommodations would work best for a couple or a couple with a child - it was a bit tight for three adults and the foldout sofa bed was not sleep friendly.  It is clean and easy to arrange online.  A couple recommendations would be an additional mirror in the living space (only one in the bathroom) and knowledge of the wifi password.
In addition, the grounds are well maintained and a small lovely outdoor area is provided.  Also two bikes are provided although we didn't use them.

My wife and I stayed overnight in one of the airstream trailers last December on our way to Big Sur for Christmas and we loved it! The trailer was very spacious and clean as well as very cute. We enjoyed our night there and will definitely stay again when we visit Santa Barbara.

Don't want to give so many stars because there's no airstreams left I have rated it so highly we've had the best experience of glamping  ever  daughter barbequedA wonderful wonderful wonderful experience I suggested highly so highly we can't even get two nights this year that we want it's a certain experience for those you like camping with 800 thread count sheets and robes and slippers and air-conditioning yay

Just returned from a perfect weekend in Santa Barbara. We lucked out with perfect weather (in January!) and the SB Auto Camp proved to a great spot for our stay. Saturday morning we unlocked the beach cruisers (provided by the auto camp as part of the rental - awesome!) and took a ride up state street to our favorite local breakfast spot. After breakfast, we made the short trip to the beach, and then some shopping downtown. The location of the SB Auto Camp couldn't be more convenient. We will definitely be making another trip, but next time we'll book all 4 of the airstreams and bring a group to take over...bunch of friends staying in side-by-side airstreams, utilizing the provided beach cruisers, BBQ's, and even croquet set...I can't wait!

I give it 3 stars because there are good points and bad points.  The good things are it's in a great area.  There are lots of restaurants and markets within a block or two.  The grounds are very nice.  The trailers are refurbished and are very clean.  The bed is comfy and the bathroom is huge.  The cons are the tv doesn't swivel, so you have to watch it in bed. And it doesn't have a table inside or out.  To eat inside, you use tv trays at the couch.  Outside, you sit on the edge of adirondack chairs with the tv trays.  There is nowhere to put anything while cooking outside on the electric grill.  Everything must be cooked there.  There are no burners inside.  A picnic table would be great.  Other things needed are:  grill brush to clean it between days, a dish towel, a dish drainer, can opener, and a pot.  Luckily, I brought a pot and can opener.

In spite of everything, I did have fun.  But I wouldn't go again until they get some of the things I mentioned, especially a table.

It was okay.  We were in campsite one very small lots of ants everywhere in the bed all over the counters in my girlfriends ear LOL. very small parking spot. I have a pick up truck had to back it in to make it fit.we were on a camping trip up PCH so we had a lot of valuable Camping equipment in the pick up it was in a bad neighborhood right on a main street was very worried and concerned someone was going to steal stuff out of the back of the truck

Long overdue to write this review. We visited in April 2014 and thoroughly enjoyed our stay. We stayed in trailer #2. It is the perfect size for a couple and possibly one child, but anything more than that might be a tight squeeze. Yes the trailer is close to the sidewalk/road, but it's quiet come evening and night. We had planned to go up to Santa Ynez during out trip, but ended up just hanging at the trailer. We didn't even leave for dinner like we had planned. We used the grill that was provided for dinner. The two twin beds were very comfortable. The bathroom was tight, but fully functional. I also loved that they used Malin and Goetz products (anything in their rum scent is amazing). Kitchen was pretty much fully stocked with everything needed. 

One huge thing to remember is that this is a trailer, not the Ritz or Four Seasons. Yes, the trailers are beautifully redone, but it's not a Five-star resort. If you want amenities, this isn't the place for you. If you just want to relax and enjoy "Glamping" then Autocamp is perfect.

Glamping at it's best. We stayed in the San Miguel.  Very comfy.  Since it is a  pretty new set up  they will figure out the small problems.  TV in bedroom limits usage and lights on the bright side.  Dimmers would help.  
No doubt we will be back for a longer stay.

My husband and I rented #4 and we found it to be a fun and romantic experience. Rarely do we get to relax just the two of us, and staying the night in a renovated and modernized trailer is a great experience! I was impressed by the bathroom space, it was clean and modern yet also luxurious--there are bath robes, down comforters and nice sheets which I did not expect. The units come with two bikes and helmets that you can use. There is a BBQ outside, a very cute porch, and lawn furniture. The auto camp is conveniently located across the street from Edomasa, a tasty Japanese food restaurant, and Our Daily Bread that has amazing breakfast/lunches, mimosas, espresso and pastries. Across the street is Ralphs, which is very convenient and the trailer has a mini fridge to store things in. My husband did a late-night ice cream and popcorn run which worked great because the trailer has a microwave.
There also is Trader Joe's three blocks away, a liquor store on the block, TAP Thai which has great pad thai noodles. I highly recommend staying here for the experience of freedom and adventure when you are visiting a new place.

Having owned Airstream trailers since 1990, we were very excited to experience AutoCamp's modified units.  We were extremely disappointed and had a very difficult time reaching management when the unit we were assigned was uninhabitable due to lock mechanism falling out, toilet not working, kitchen faucet very wobbly, patio chairs that were unstable and more issues that indicated poor maintenance and housekeeping.  Upon finally reaching a manager who said she was out of town, she agreed to refund our money then sent an email indicating it would be made according to their refund policy (it's two weeks in advance of reservation date).  At that point we were compelled to contact our credit card bank and request them to intervene with AutoCamp.  We did actually receive the refund of the extremely high price for a night in what was previously an Airstream (quality trailer) and now only a shell of an Airstream with amateurish modifications.  Very disappointed with the AutoCamp experience in Santa Barbara, including the location of the five AutoCamp trailers in a much larger operating trailer park.  The pictures were wonderful, but the actual experience full of unpleasant surprises and very disappointing.

Just checked into #4 airstream for a two night stay. Very happy with our accomendations although window cranks and window coverings really need some attention. Some cranks are broken or not there at all. Most shades don't roll up and one window is missing a shade. We used the bench cushions for privacy. Enjoyed our walk to dinner tonight to to the Canary Hotel at Finch and Fork, absolutely wonderful.

My boyfriend and I stayed here over the weekend and had a pretty nice time. Overall, this is a great novelty sort of experience and I'm glad we tried it out. However, I don't know if we would necessarily do it again..

Pro's:
-There is something really fun and interesting about staying in an Airstream. It's a cool thing to be able to say that you've done.
-Space 2 was pretty clean and well stocked with most of the stuff you would need, such as coffee, pots and pans for cooking and towels.
-The AC worked well, it never got too stuffy inside.
-The bikes that you can use for free are a great feature.

Con's:
-The location and layout of the camp are terrible! There is a very rowdy dive bar next door where the patrons come out shouting throughout the night. We definitely saw a fair share of shady people walking up and down the sidewalk, which was just a foot away from our Airstream. The camp is not removed from the street at all, so all the street noise kept it from being a really relaxing experience.
-Not all of the spaces have an outdoor dumpster, you have to pay extra to have your Airstream cleaned, and our garbage can inside the trailer was tiny. Basically, we ended up with our garbage piling up to the point that we had to ask the guests in another space if we could throw our garbage in their bin. I found this to be one of the worst aspects of our stay.
-Our stove didn't work all that well. It would have been good enough for maybe frying an egg or doing a quick sautee but it barely got hot enough to boil water. Don't plan on making pasta during your stay!

I would recommend this for another young couple with no kids who plan to get out a lot and are not necessarily looking for a relaxing stay. Even though the Airstreams can technically fit 4 people, it will be a really tight fit. Be advised that what you're really paying for is the novelty of staying in a tricked out Airstream and not a whole lot else.

We were not sure what to expect but the Autocamp sounded like a fun adventure & it certainly was a fun getaway to Santa Barbara. It was like a well appointed motel room but in a much more enjoyable setting.  Coupons in the Airstream got us a great discounted brunch near the park.  A fun way to travel & enjoy SB on the bikes.

We enjoyed our stay in the airstream, and loved Santa Barbara! I'm giving the Autocamp 3 stars because we had no hot water, and with no one there to help us we just had to stick it out. We called the 1 800 number several times, and we got a call back after we checked out. For the amount of $ we spent I feel hot water is a non-negotiable.

If it is super hot, the AC doesn't work. We stayed at the Russian River AutoCamp and had such a good experience. This is nothing like it. Bordered up to a main road and found within a trailer park, we would not stay here as a family. We did for one night and checked into a nearby hotel for night two. For the price of a Four Seasons Hotel, your expectations are grand. This was not even close for what was sold to us. At a minimum, a working AC would've been fabulous.

Great Company to work with! 

"Guys Trip" rented one of their units for one night on a wine tasting expedition. 

Lovely experience and very responsive front office.

If you are looking for something off the beaten path, this is your ticket. 

If your looking for luxury, look elsewhere. Units are very clean and really well designed. 

Highly recommend.

My friend and I stayed at Autocamp (#4) this weekend during a wine getaway to Santa Barbara. The airstream was awesome!  It was clean, had a mid-century vibe, and almost all the amenities we needed were inside including dishware, a microwave, and nice bath products. There was no lotion which I found strange but besides that it had everything we needed. The bed was comfy and we couldn't hear the neighbors which was nice. 

I've seen others complain that the trailers are at the front of a legit trailer park, which is true, but it didn't lessen the experience at all for me and i would stay there again in a heartbeat. In fact I really want to try the camp at the Russian River this summer!

We spent the weekend here for my girlfriend's birthday and couldn't have had a better time. She loves the style and retro feel of these airstream trailers and they were better than we thought. Each one (we got two for three couples) and they were better than we had hoped for. Super cute and cozy with all the amenities you'd expect from a boutique hotel. The staff was so nice and accommodating they helped us with an extra pair of bikes and we rode to the funkzone for lunch, antique'ing and wine tasting. We cooked our own meals with the grills and played board games under the cute lights in the trees. Amazing, unique and wonderful weekend at Autocamp! Next time we are going to do the Russian River ones next. Love this place!

Service was awesome , every questioned was answered and if I had any comments and concerns they were quickly taken care of! Pictures do not do this camp site justice! It was so cozy, clean and spacious for a aero trailer! I stayed there for my friends birthday weekend 4 adults shared the camper and it was perfect! All amenities included, microwave, fridge, oven, utensils, towels and even local brewed coffee! There is even an adorable patio deck layout! Makes you feel like you are away but still in the heat of down down Santa Barbara! I will definitely be booking there again in a month or so.

Nothing like the marketing. Located in a seedy trailer park in a questionable neighborhood in Santa Barbara. We were so disappointed, we only stayed one night although we paid, no over-paid $866.80 for the two nights. We will never go back and I cannot imagine anyone would. They must have only first time guests because no one in their right mind would go back for more of this none sense. Their business model is to over-promise, over-charge and under-deliver. 
The adventure is not worth the effort. The "electric bbq" is a George Forman Grill. No cooking facility inside. No room service. No maid service. The water pressure in the shower was a trickle hooked up to a garden hose outside. 
I gave them 1 star but that is only because I did not want anyone to think I forgot to mark the star. That one is a gift.
Don't confuse this with the photos on their website. There are only six trailers and no other facilities or big trees. Don't say I didn't warn you.

I would give more stars if the location was different. The airstream itself was nice but I feel as a whole it's over priced. The main reason for me is that it doesn't have a camp feel because of the area. To me it seemed out of place. Had it been in its own little airstream community in a more private setting I would like it better. Also a fire pit would be amazing but I'm sure it's a safety hazard. I guess my disappointments  was that even though the outdoor areas are cute you still feel like why am I sitting here in a residential camp ground of a busy street. I personally would rather stay at a hotel with a view or some sort of scenery. Also glamping type of place with the whole camping vibe would be better for me too. Now that's all my personal opinion and with that said if you are used to regular campground camping and roughing it than this place will be great. 

Space #3 airstream was nice and well stocked with kitchen supplies. Plates, cups, silverware, grill supplies and even a cork screw. It was clean and the ac worked great. The bathroom is tiny and a little annoying but it served its purpose. If you are staying with people you don't want to see you naked you have to dress in the tiny bathroom which is not so easy. I wish they had s privacy curtain in the back near the sleeping nook. Also the only tv is in the back sleeping nook. Other than those minor issues the airstream itself was good. Take it and put it in a better more quaint area and I would have given it more stars. This review is based on my person preference and I'm all about location and vibe. I prefer ambiance where I stay and this place didn't have the feel I was looking for. 

Tip: if you stay here with kids because there isn't anything for them to do. There is a park in walking distance. It's called oak park. It's a 2 minute drive end a ten minute walk. Google it. 

Also some parking spaces are small I had a hard time fitting my suv into space 3.

What was Sunset Magazine thinking?

After reading a Sunset Magazine article about this Airstream trailer "camp," we booked a trailer for 3 people and a dog one year in advance.  We had to pay in full.  We were given the Trailer 1 spot, which is so close to the sidewalk, you can almost touch it.  This is no camp, my friends, this is an existing trailer park in a busy and noisy commercial area which is not especially safe.  The site is only slightly larger than the trailer.  While the interior is up-to-date (nice sink in the bathroom) and clean, it is much smaller than the photos make it appear.  The "shower" was a tub with a shower hose.  As soon as we walked in, I started looking up other pet friendly places to stay (Fess Parker's Doubletree) and once that reservation was secure, we shut the door on the trailer and never looked back.

So Awesome!!!
ALLLLLL is GREAT!
Me my Ex and our 8 year old stayed in the smallest streamer close to the road.
So super cute and so functionally done.
Great beds,both the twin beds and the "simple to set up" double ( by lowering dinner table).

Tastefully renovated ( the kitchen is better equipped than my home kitchen!) and all what you need is right there.
The miniature lawn felt like "ours" even if neighbors are close by, it felt "private".
A great additional detail - Croquet!!

The bath room is tiny and CUTE..a minimal minus was a bit of  mold  smell in it which I understand..maybe a lovely air freshener or something and It'll be PERF!
Towels just fine.

We took a short ride on wheels to Trader Joes around the corner, got some food,barbecued on the perfect little electric grill had some wine and slept like babies - no noise from close by street.

I also love the fact that you get the cruiser bikes !
I have actually rented three of them for me and my very best friends.
No kids then so cruising around for beach hang and wine tasting!!!
Thats just as good as it gets!!

Liz B

Awesome experience! Keep in mind that this is more for the experience, so if you're one of those pretentious folk who are looking for a luxury stay at a unique boutique (and any other word that has a "q" in it) place, then take your snobby money elsewhere.  Accomodations were delightful. I especially liked the comfy bed. The claw-foot tub and beach cruisers are definite pluses. We stayed at space #1. We were warned of the possible noise due to the adjacent street and bar, but we didn't notice any noise. Perhaps it was because we slept like babies in the plush bed. It was situated in front of a trailer park, but the neighbors were very nice. Just be respectful of those residents who have been there for over 40 years. Great experience. Glamping for sure!

this place is so cool! glamping at its finest! the airstreams are all super modern and clean. overall, comfy and cozy. good for two people. they also give you 2 beach cruisers to stroll around town and its in a great location....right next to a cool dive bar with trader joes and coffee bean right down the street!

Great experience, was a little nervous about the noise issue due to previous reviews but it was very quiet and we forgot we were around other people. It's a cute spot, clean, and was very comfortable. I loved it and will definitely return and hopefully try other AutoCamp locations.

I found this place through Trip Advisor.  We have wanted to purchase an Airstream as we reach retirement and thought this was a great way to try one out.  Thankfully we booked before they made some list as a place to stay.  We were able to book the one we wanted (Santa Cruz)for the nights we needed.  The old reservation system always defaulted to one Airstream and you had to pick the one you wanted twice.  As I have recently been out their site they seem to have resolved that issue.  Last minute we needed to remove one night and they were happy to help us do it.  The Airstream itself was very clean and well maintained.  The nights we were there the WiFi was not working but we were on vacation and trying to limit out time on line.  Fully furnished with all the kitchen items you may need but again we were on vacation and not wanted to cook.  The chairs outside were a nice touch.  Being from TX it was nice to sit outside and enjoy a glass of wine.  My husband is 6' 3" and had no issue with the shower height.  TV had so many channels.  Bed was comfortable. The trailer did get warm in the day but we were out exploring the city.  Cooled down nicely at night.  There is a Trader Joe's down the street and walk able.  Two bikes which we used to bike to Harry's for drinks. There are now 5 Airstreams in the park.  The park is a real RV park with residents.  The SB Auto Camp part is in the front.  We had no issue with noise while we were there.  If back in the area then I am sure we will stay again.  Something different.

Disappointed. Based on the pictures, we were expecting a quaint atmosphere, not being part of an existing trailer park. Another thing to keep in mind is that there is no early check in. They send you a code which does not allow entrance until 4 pm.  We were unfortunately in one of the units next to the street and the noise from the bar next door was ridiculous. It is so bad that they even provide earplugs. Additionally, the parking space is tiny. Our very small car (911) barely fit and we woke up to the freshly detailed car soaked by the sprinklers. It is 7:30 am and after a sleepless night am on my way home. In all fairness, the trailer is clean and comfortable, hence the 2 stars.

We loved everything about what AutoCamp SB has to offer, except for one thing: the loud bar right next to our unit (#1). I'm not sure if all the other units have this issue, but the more we tried to ignore it, the louder it got as the night progressed. We stayed during Christmas Eve and Christmas. Last night I was woken up by the extremely heavy bass coming from the bar which was at 4am, way past bar operation hours. 
As I write this review on our last night here at AutoCamp SB at 12:15am, with the loud music shaking our unit, I pray that it stops soon after 1:30am so we can get some decent rest. 
Based on most of the negative reviews, I find it hard to believe the city has not imposed some sort of noise ordinance on this establishment.

We discovered there was an Auto Camp in Santa Barbara....so we drove up from Pasadena to check it out and WHOA NELLY!! Are you kidding me? 
Passé Vou (and that's not even French) I have 3 words 
"Oh The Deception!" You think you're getting a cool airstream only camp arrangement but not here. It's in a trailer park for crying out loud with a dive bar next door. 
Save your money and plan a trip to Auto Camp Russian River -- we paid $320 per night x2 for a Premium Airstream by a creek and it was WORTH EVERY PENNY! 
I'm talking bucket list type location! 

ACSB- not samuch! If you want a local experience I recommend Caravan Outpost in Ojai for the experience you think you're going to get here. 

I seriously wish ACSB would work diligently to find a better location and duplicate what they've done in NoCal 
in SoCal. They would make a grip of cash if they did! 
Auto Camp SB is a great idea poorly executed. 
Spend your hard earned dollars on something more worthy.  It's sincerely not worth the pri

** In response to the Mgmt. I'd visit SB Auto Camp but not for $700 for a 2 nite stay. There are better options.

This was our couples vacation and it was perfect! The vintage airstream is located in a neighborhood camp ground with 5 other airstreams and behind are local RV camps! Beautifully maintained camp site, clean modern airstream, perfect size for couples and the backyard ideal for cooking ( electric George Foreman grill) and quiet even though it is off the street. Comes with two bikes that you can use locally - everything so conveniently available i.e Handlebar coffee shop, daily bread, yoga, Japanese restaurant, Mexican bakery and meat store all across the street. Didn't have to use coffee shop since airstream comes with complimentary coffee grounds from Handlebar coffee.  The weather was too hot and humid to bike around town - ideal biking between 6-8pm but really everything walking distance including grocery outlet and Trader Joe's where we shopped for our dinner meals! Weather was perfect in the evenings where we could open all the windows and enjoy the natural breeze! The airstream was very roomy and comfortable for us and we stayed three nights four days. The built in music system was wonderful - you can have the door open in the evenings and listen to the music as you chill outside with glass of wine. The outlets set up for USB to hook up
Your phone for the music system and enjoy your Spotify playlist. There is also a laundromat conveniently located down the street along with many other small good review restaurants if one does not feel like cooking. We enjoyed our breakfast and dinners cooked at the campsite . We loved our experience and plan on autocamping again but next up will be Russia River! Highly recommend!!!

Was disappointed that the location for the Santa Barbara AutoCamp as it is basically in a trailer park, off a busy road, with 6 other units for their "camp". I still gave it 3 stars as the inside and outside were cute and well kept but looking at the pictures I obviously did not do enough homework to see the actual location. I anticipated room around to hike and sit quietly under trees. This was not the case at all. 
There was a great coffee place across the street and a delicious Mexican restaurant down the street. But it is much further from the beach than we anticipated and needed to drive down to enjoy the beach activities. 
I wouldn't stay here again and for the price could have stayed at a 4 star hotel and been much more near to the beach and State Street.

If there is a change in your plans or an emergency and you need to rebook, I suggest that you plan on staying somewhere else!
They refuse to return your payment. They will offer to let you reschedule for an additional 25.00 charge and then will charge you a higher rate than you originally booked. They gave me an outstanding balance of almost double of what the original booking was.
I had to dispute with my CC Co.in order to get my money back.
They have a 10 day cancellation that they stand on.
They are hard to contact. They do not return messages when there is a problem. 
Very poor Customer service and very Disappointed

The other hotels in Santa Barbara were booked up and we ended up staying in one of the Airstreams at AutoCamp.  It was such a pleasant surprise.  I kind of want to keep this place as a secret for myself but I try to not be selfish.  The insides are completely modern and have all of the amenities you'd expect.  They're hooked up to the water, electric and sewer mainlines so they act just like a normal hotel room.

My favorite thing about the stay was the water filter beside the sink faucet.  It's the kind you see in an upgraded home kitchen.  I never have enough water in hotels so it was a nice and unexpected treat.  The other thing that stood out was the bath products.  I'm a bit of a bathroom products man snob connoisseur.  I don't remember the brands they use but they were better than the ones I own.

Outside of the Airstreams are a nicely landscaped area.  I saw that each one had two beach cruisers.  My friend and I biked to a couple of the different brunch places that are right in the area.  There's also a great dive bar down the street called Jimboz Lounge.  Stop in and see Jack, the owner with a pirate patch and accompanying sailor's gruff but oddly welcoming persona.

I'm guessing I'll be coming back here to do it all again.

Great concept - horrendous location

I have to say I was extremely disappointed w the location and 'camp' for the price.  It was over $430 a night for the weekend we went which is the price of a nice resort in the area. The trailer park was extremely busy and not at all the type of atmosphere described on the site.  Autocamp is literally at the front area of an existing trailer park (not a fancy RV resort) but a residential trailer park.  There are random people walking and driving through constantly....keeping you a bit on guard.

Yes - it is great to be in town and walking distance from coffee shops and stores but not ideal for young families with kids.  You are steps from the street and next door to a bar.  The outdoor space is limited and We literally had to watch kids every minute for cars coming through to the other trailers in park and a couple times there were some close calls.

The images are VERY misleading there are only 6 Air streams and the daily charge should be at least half of what it is.  The website should be clearer that it really isn't suitable for families as that it is not very private.

The airstream was very comfortable, nicely equipped and a great concept.  Terrible in execution and location was worse.

Was at the Autocamp in Santa Barbara for two days last week!  What a fabulous alternative to a hotel room!  We sat outside on our own little patio, we had our own fridge and a small burner stove and dishes!  The bed was amazing! We will be back so worth it!  And Marty's Pizza was awesome as well loved it!  Last time we were in Santa Barbara we paid the same price for a small hotel room and the toilet overflowed and my husband had to go down the street to use the bathroom!  
Everything worked perfect in our Airstreams and there was plenty of room for the three of us! Loved it!

This was our couples vacation and it was perfect! The vintage airstream is located in a neighborhood camp ground with 5 other airstreams and behind are local RV camps! Beautifully maintained camp site, clean modern airstream, perfect size for couples and the backyard ideal for cooking ( electric George Foreman grill) and quiet even though it is off the street. Comes with two bikes that you can use locally - everything so conveniently available i.e Handlebar coffee shop, daily bread, yoga, Japanese restaurant, Mexican bakery and meat store all across the street. Didn't have to use coffee shop since airstream comes with complimentary coffee grounds from Handlebar coffee.  The weather was too hot and humid to bike around town - ideal biking between 6-8pm but really everything walking distance including grocery outlet and Trader Joe's where we shopped for our dinner meals! Weather was perfect in the evenings where we could open all the windows and enjoy the natural breeze! The airstream was very roomy and comfortable for us and we stayed three nights four days. There is also a laundromat conveniently located down the street along with many other small good review restaurants if one does not feel like cooking. We enjoyed our breakfast and dinners cooked at the campsite . We lived our experience and plan on autocamping again but next up will be Russia River! Highly recommend!!!

Take yourself back to the mid-50's and stay in the ultimate travel trailer without the hassle of towing one in and setting it up! These Airstream travel trailers have been retrofitted to resemble the 50's look, but with the all of the amenities one would expect today... Flat screen TV, full size stainless steel sink, refrigerator, A/C & a walk -in shower (at least that's what ours had). We also had a nice patio, Sun shade and even a BBQ (electric). There were a couple of beach cruisers (helmets too) should you feel like a ride to town or the beach.

Our 'Land Yacht' (#3) was in a semi-secluded spot w/ a small grass side-yard and patio. The patio area kept to the retro look with a couple Adirondack chairs and matching table. A great place to relax at sunset w/ some finger food and a nice local Chardonnay. Good place for a morning coffee too.

There's a great bakery (Our Daily Bread) and a fantastic carniceria/tacos place (Mayo's) right across the street, but we went just down the street just a bit to Los Agaves (highly rated on T/A) for a table service dinner. It was fantastic!

We really enjoyed out stay at Auto Camp and are looking for any excuse to return.

We loved our experience staying in an airstream.  The neighborhood is great, a true neighborhood, but a really good walk into downtown Santa Barbara. Parking in Santa Barbara seemed to be easy so we would recommend that over taking the long walk.  There are grocery stores near by so you can get stables to cook in your kitchen. Our kitchen was extremely workable. 

We stayed in Airstream 2, which was so light and airy.  Because of the skylights I always thought the lights were on, so if you can't sleep with the sun shinning this is not the airstream for you.  Of course the bathroom was small, but to be excepted.  We loved the little outdoor seating area and they provide bikes to use, which would help with the long walk/distance.  The communication was amazing with e-mails sent out a week out with instructions on how to check in (no check-in by person all by code).  We also got regular e-mails checking up on us and when we were getting ready to check out and after check out time.  All in all we loved our experience here and would recommend it.

We discovered there was an Auto Camp in Santa Barbara....so we drove up from Pasadena to check it out and WHOA NELLY!! Are you kidding me? 
Passé Vou (and that's not even French) I have 3 words 
"Oh The Deception!" You think you're getting a cool airstream only camp arrangement but not here. It's in a trailer park for crying out loud with a dive bar next door. 
Save your money and plan a trip to Auto Camp Russian River -- we paid $320 per night x2 for a Premium Airstream by a creek and it was WORTH EVERY PENNY! 
I'm talking bucket list type location! 

ACSB- not samuch! If you want a local experience I recommend Caravan Outpost in Ojai for the experience you think you're going to get here. 

I seriously wish ACSB would work diligently to find a better location and duplicate what they've done in NoCal 
in SoCal. They would make a grip of cash if they did! 
Auto Camp SB is a great idea poorly executed. 
Spend your hard earned dollars on something more worthy.  It's sincerely not worth the price.

Stayed for one night and it was great! Super cool Airstreams that are very nice and modern on the inside. Convenient location near San Roque and upper State with a lot of great restaurants and some cool bars near by. It was great having the two beach cruisers to use and a small grill to use outside the Airstream. Would definitely recommend for a great place to stay in Santa Barbara that's not your usual boring hotel but not quite camping in a tent...it's the perfect combination.
So things have cooled off, and Im happy for that. Have gotten to skate this park several times, and enjoy some of the actually good boarders. There are lots of creative lines to learn here.

Quite the intricate little skate park! Have been here while visiting Santa Barbara and I always have a great time when I come. I usually skate the smaller stuff, but there's really something for everybody here. From rails to a mini bowl, fun boxes and everything in between, what more could you ask for?
Great park..open and free...right on the beach. Great views all around. Fun in the California sun.

This is a fantastic little park, right on the beach. Smooth transitions, lots of lines for multiple people, quite a lot variety for a small park. 

The only thing it lacks is a proper pool-style bowl.

For the record, I skate.

Honestly this park is a great place to be so close to down town and very close to the beach. But the environment for the younger generation isn't the best place to be. The reason I say this is my younger brother was going here till I understood that there are two major skater gangs that control the park. If you are wanting your kids getting into drugs, gangs and fights then by all means. But if you really care about who your kids skate with please understand that this is a huge issue. Please be advised if your kid or friends are skating with Sutter keep him as far away as possible with anyone you care about. He is the ringleader of one of the gangs and won't mess around. Don't recommend going here unless you are wanting to get into drugs and fights.

I'm not a skater but have Skaters in my family and they enjoyed this park. It's really nice that it's on the beach as well so there's other things to do for the family members that don't skate. The pier is also walking distance and fun to visit as well. 

If you're a beginner skater, this park may be intimidating but definitely a great place to practice.

Really cool little skate part located right on the beach.  Lots of skaters, bikers and scooter riders of all different levels enjoying the boxes and rails. My son and daughter really enjoyed getting to try out the space and even with a large amount of people at the part it seemed to work nicely as people waited for one another to complete a motion.  Great spot worth checking out!

Most excellent park with amazing features. I'm a beginner myself and appreciated the bowl and also the ramps of various angles. Come here early in the day if you are as experienced and you'll be just fine. Come later in the day to witness the pros.

Wow, really cool there's a free public skate park right by the beach it's so quintessentily Southern California. It's well maintained and clean, no graffiti in sight and seems to be safe, mostly used by young kids. Skaters and BMX riders share the park well, with people taking turns without any hassle. Gets crowded starting from the afternoon when school lets out so if you're on vacation and want to skate, go mid-day and you'll have the place to yourself.

Ah a bright spring day in Santa Barbara off Cabrillo always a good thing after a long week on the tread mill called trabajo. Boys liked the sun and weather was a perfect mid 70's with a nice breeze. Lovin it and skate park ain't too bad, but some minors were medicating up, they must have migraines too. LoL..

name: Evergreen Open Space Disc Golf Course

yelp_reviews.text:
I love this course for its open holes a as well as for its en-forested ones.  I've just started playing again and will definitely be trying to make this more a regular event!  There are a few holes that can get muddy and trails that can be blocked off by water (if it ever rains again...) so just be prepared to go out there and tromp around a little bit.  Searching for discs in trees and ivy just adds to the adventure ;)

The best time to get out there is in the mornings, and during the week if you can, but it's not that crowded on the weekend mornings either.  Holidays are kinda crazy, but that's to be expected.

It's seems like a great community of disc/golfers as well. People of all ages, playing solo or in large groups have all been super nice and respectful of each other in my experience.

Frisbee golf heaven. Beautiful open space. I used to bring a six pack of beer , some discs and end my day here, 

There's also tennis courts where you can play for free. The south end has a baseball field as well.

Perhaps the greatest concentration of Bros and frisbees you'll find anywhere in the United States. 

This was a really nice setup, and despite the concentration of Bros, our group with both males and females/adults and kids, felt welcome.  A lot of fun, even for beginners. There is a website with etiquette tips and a map of the course that you should check out before heading out.  Some of tees aren't that well marked for first timers, but we found that others playing the course were very happy to help us out.

Bro-tastic!

Good variety of open long holes and tightly wooded ones. Some nice elevation changes. Complicated course; map at website.

I played here about three years ago and lost a disc.  I was excited to play again when the family went camping at El Capitan in Goleta.  My friend Pete and I dropped the wife and kids off at the playground that is adjacent to the disc golf course.  We played all 18, but it took two hours because we kept losing discs (fortunately, this time we were able to recover them).  Be careful of the poison oak as you climb through the jungle.  There is a small creek at the bottom of a gulley.  The whole course has great elevation changes and obstacles.  Even a few dog-leg baskets.
We had to ask the locals where the tees were eventhough they were marked with a number in the slab.  No real rollers.  Very fun course nestled in a quiet neighborhood.

While I've never actually disc golfed, this open space was adjacent to my childhood home and my elementary school. I've always admired its beauty. 

Evergreen Open Space is pretty sizable and meanders through an entirely residential neighborhood. It's probably over a mile from one side to the other. As a child, I used to love seeing the adults playing a round of frisbee golf while I was playing on the elementary playground during school hours. The course isn't mapped to my knowledge, but locals will know which way to throw that disc. 

The park consists of a huge green belt, a few tennis courts, a children's playground, a walking/bike path, and lots of natural landscape. There's a large eucalyptus grove with a seasonal stream. I remember there being a tree swing out there for a good while when I was a child, and also recall the dirt bike mounds back in the 1990s. Those were a great time and I'll admit that I was a bit sad when they were flattened. 

I love that this space offers a touch of natural beauty in the midst of suburbia. It provides solace for neighbors and tourists alike. The path along the eucalyptus grove is just breathtaking and I'd recommend checking it out if you're looking for a mild way to spend an afternoon.

name: East Beach Batting Cages
categories: Batting Cages, Sporting Goods, Shopping, Parks, Arts & Entertainment, Swimming Pools, Active Life, Fitness & Instruction

yelp_reviews.text: Took my wife here today really really fun place to go the gentleman behind the counter even suggested a more affordable package so that we can get more for our dollar.

Pretty much the only game in town but I'm impressed that they've made some nice upgrades to the cages. You can now choose from slow pitch/high arc softball to fast pitch softball with variable speeds (in each cage) and the usual 50/60/70 mph hardball cages. Staff is really nice but there always seems to be a few technical glitches. In any case (did I mention its the only game in town:-)! Its now a better game with the changes and I really do appreciate them, thanks guys. We'll be seeing you a lot in the near future :-) :-) :-)
$2.75 for 20-pitches but they no longer validate a free token :(, still good price but ya don't get a full 20-balls, maybe 15-18.

$2.50 for 19 pitches ranging from softball to 35 MPH hardball to 50 MPH hardball. Secretly my back was sore for 2 days after 80 pitches...

Still awesome! Worth the drive from Ventura. Our new favorite date!

Six years and two owners later, batting cage No. 1 still messes up.  The single employee - 20 year old laconic dolt in a wife beater T - seemed to go out of his way to be obnoxious.  We were two kids, two young adults and parents, the only customers there at noon on Friday and this guy did everything he could to either ignore our concerns -- bat/helmet sizes, package deals on tokens --  or literally give us the mad dog stare.  What a disappointment.

The downside is that usually the guy working there is more involved in whatever he's watching on TV that what's happening in the cages....what's happening in the cages is that frequently the machines get jammed and you lose your rythym.

The upside is that usually when you're standing in front of them, they are helpful in finding appropriate headwear. Also, if you have an SB Axxess card here, you get one free token (a $2.50 value based on individual token rate). 

The thing about it is, is that I'm pretty sure these are the only batting cages of this sort in SB so, what else are you gonna do?
Good for the kids to practice and great for family entertainment!

I usually really enjoy coming here but lately the machines have just been breaking more than usual. The balls don't shoot out entirely so you end up wasting about half of your shots. Today especially was saddening because the Asian guy who was working here was clearly giving away free tokens to what seems like his girlfriend. Which is not that big of a deal but then he himself WHILE ON THE CLOCK starting taking over the cages while paying customers were waiting to use the cages. Extremely rude and frustrating.

Good place to practice in town and a lot of fun. Pretty cheap for how many tokens you get, and overall fun place I know I will return to again and again. Down side is it looks a little old and overdue for some maintenance. For example, a guy was practicing baseball fast pitches in the cage next to me doing softball slow pitches, and the ball went through a hole in the fence, narrowly missing my head. Nets need to be updated, but I will still go again since it's a fun pass time in town that I'm really glad to have in SB.
These batting cages are a solid place to come to take some swings, whether you're on a team and need the practice, or if you just want to have some fun.
This is a pretty basic batting cage but they just upgraded to be adjustable high this on the pitches both softball and baseball. If you have a Ventura axxess card it's a free round everyday.

This batting cage changed my son's life. He wasn't going to make the high school team because my husband couldn't figure out how to teach Brandton how to lay down a sick drag bunt. An employee took my son Bradenton under his wing and after  thousands of tokens in the slow pitch softball cage my precious boy made the team. His jersey number is "Bat Boy". Thank you so much , I think the person's name was Sensor, baseball names are so funny. Great tacos next door too!!!

We had our game canceled due to weather so brought some of the girls here to work on some batting. Gabe the guy that was working. Was A++ great service and accommodating to our needs. Thank you Gabe we will be back.

I would be writing this post to say how pleasant my first experience at any type of batting cage was, which is mostly true if the weirdo alone in the shed that is this location's front desk/office wasn't such a h*ck, so the customer service seems to be non-existent if you approach the employees with a question. Anyways I blew some steam while using the batting cages but the bozo at the front desk was less than helpful with his long winded "simplest answer I can give you"

I took my wife to the taco shop here as I remember them being SUPER awesome. They still are incredibly awesome. We definitely will be back for that but just after, we hit the cages for a little BP. Definitely a good time.

It's so nice to have this place down the street. And what a great way to release the pressures of the day while waiting for traffic to thin out. I bat for hours but have never stepped onto a baseball field. As for complaints about the machines jamming up, there is just as much chance that it doesn't shut off after 19 pitches. This is a real old fashioned business and I love it. Thanks for outlasting everything else that has sold out on Milpas st since my childhood. You gotta love local business that stays local over the years.

Cages say open at 9 am. Nice to know I waited around with no sign on the door saying open later or closed until this time. People go missing and no one ever knows what happened to them. Never forget that

So it is the only cages in town that I know of. You know the people that work there are great - willing to help you out. But, a couple of the machines are tweaked. The 60 MPH comes in double time when I was there last so the only way to hit both is to swing at one and bunt the other. Also, one of the 50's will hit you. Other than that, good times.

It's relatively easy to purchase the tokens and they're at a fair price. You then decide which cage to go to I believe it runs from 40-80MPH. Some of the cages pitch too low which makes it really difficult to hit. Occasionally (probably once per visit on average) you will need assistance because the feeder gets jammed and you're stuck waiting for a pitch. 

It's not a terrible place to do batting cages, it's better than the options I've seen throughout the 805. Just don't expect the world out of this place.

I once hit a ball out of the net so park at your own risk.

I love this place!

I've been visiting batting cages since I was a youngster playing Little League baseball.  I don't play baseball or golf much but I do enjoy batting cages and driving ranges - is that weird?  Anyway, after so many years of visiting batting cages in many states, this one is my favorite.

It's easy to use.
It's clean and always kept in good repair.
It's in the beautiful city of Santa Barbara not far from the beach.
The staff is friendly, polite and do a great job.

They have baseball and softball machines.  Slow pitch to fastballs.
They have bats there that you can use.  You don't need to bring any gear.  There is a great taco shop on the property:  East Beach Tacos.

Came here for the tacos next door & decided to challenge my bf to who can hit more balls. We're totally amateurs, but it was a lot of fun! If it's your first time I recommend getting a couple tokens, and go with the softball setting. My arms were sore after just a few rounds lol. Can't wait till we're in town again, this was a fun lil date spot.

name: Chase Palm Park Plaza
categories: Public Services & Government, Active Life, Parks

yelp_reviews.text: This park is so nice, it's big and has grass!  It's in such a nice, open area.  The kiddos can run around and it's fun to see the SB lifestyle and culture.

The free concerts at the park are a higher quality than I expect for no charge. Two years ago these concerts helped me survive a hellish summer. I still have friends with those who I bonded with over the fantastic tunes.

Having moved back into the area I'm already looking forward to Thursday nights this June. From an Eagles tribute band to Gypsy rock I can't wait to hear all the bands. 

The green atmosphere adds a large amount to this venue. However, I will not down vote a location based on Mother Nature.
This is enjoyable park, we should go for a walking,cycling,running,anything at anytime. Everyone's happy time.people enjoy a lot in that park
Awesome! Great beach path for walking or biking.

Chase Palm Park is a beautiful park by the ocean, with long strips of green lined with palm trees on palm trees on palm trees. Matt B. and I took our bassets for a walk here on Saturday during a weekend jaunt to Santa Barbara. This was a wonderful way to spend time on a warm afternoon.

Parking was easy, in a public lot right by the park. It cost $2 an hour, which is not crazy, and honestly easy parking by the water is a real novelty to two people visiting from L.A. We didn't walk the whole park, but we got a nice stroll in, taking in the lovely views as well as the sweet dogs and interesting people. Our dogs got some welcome attention and sniffed a few friendly butts. There were a lot of people out, picnicking and listening to music. One guy was walking a bouncy-looking tightrope thing stretched between two palm trees.

We enjoyed our walk in the park, and the next day, we met a basset hound named Roger, whose parents asked if we'd been walking by the beach in front of the Hilton--they'd seen two people with two bassets but couldn't holler effectively from their car. If you want to see and be seen while hanging with cool dogs by the ocean, get you and your pups to Chase Palm Park.

Don't listen to the poor reviewer.
 During the summer they have concerts in the Park every Thursday.
 The line up for 2014 has been awesome so far, captain cardiac, and the Beatles tribute band were pretty epic.
 During concert intermissions they have a free raffle where you have a chance of winning a cd, swim passes, or tennis lessons.
 Depending on the concert this place can be pretty crowded, they allow you to drop off chairs/blankets early in the afternoon at noon, this is always not fun, you would think older people would be more kind, and relaxed about putting their chairs out, but it's like the amazing race, and a mad dash to see who can get the best seat (some pushing has occurred). You're not allowed to park in the lot near the park when setting chairs up either, which sucks! However if you don't mind the walk, there's a place you can park that has 15 min free parking
Santa Barbara beach area was an okay stroll but everything is not lively. The pier had like 7 people max. The rest were racist hobos making Mexican jokes. Not cool.

Curious as to why this listing refers only to the plaza here at Chase Palm Park when it could just as well refer to the entire park (or when there could be a separate listing for the creek/pond behind the plaza).

As for the plaza itself, it's a nice, circular, amphitheatre-like venue with benches and walls around the circumference where folks can sit. The plaza gradually descends into a grassy lawn in the middle, complete with a decorative fountain. It can easily be used for public concerts and gatherings or private events and receptions. Indeed, there's a villa towards one side of the plaza that folks can rent for events.

Behind this villa is a beautiful creekside oasis, complete with footbridge for those event photo-ops. The park then extends further into a playground (covered separately here on Yelp). From end to end, it's a pleasant 10 minute walk. A space well worth exploring if you're not solely into the beaches here in Santa Barbara.

I will never go here to rent paddle boards again. we arrived an hour and a half prior like it said online and MIKE told us that it was absolutely impossible for our group of 10 to go out because he just recently sent out another group. We told him that it was my birthday and he just kept saying "it's impossible". MIKE then sent us to the downtown location. By the time we parked and arrived at the shop it was 5:25. I told them that MIKE sent us here because he couldn't accommodate us. They two workers gave each other a look and said "bs, he wanted to close early" we also found out that this was the 2nd time that MIKE has done this. Nothing but praise for Jordan and his co worker who took us out and kept their shop open and extra HOUR AND A HALF so that my friends and I could go out on the water. They were so nice and didn't even rush us, they even apologized for the whole situation multiple times. Bottom line: Go downtown where the service is above and beyond opposed to dealing with MIKE who just wants to close early.

First off, the actual kayaking experience was nice, decent price and well built kayaks but they could use a hose off between use. But, the staff member mike really needs to work on his customer service skills. When we walked in he didn't greet us at all and told us to "come to the window" as if we should already know where to go (it was our first time) then when the iPad was not working properly he made no effort to apologize or give a solution. He was also rude and off putting when I asked for a rash guard. I wanted to stay warm in the sometimes chilly ocean and when I asked for the shirt his reply was "you won't get rashed up kayaking" as if that was a dumb request to make. Like I said before, kayaking was a nice experience but mike really needs some work.
Great experience, great staff, great vibes. An hour was a perfect amount for a small group of friends to rent tandems to go to campus point and back.

The time on the listing is wrong. We came an hour and a half before the closing time listed here only to find that they stopped giving out paddle board rentals. No where on the website does it say that they would stop giving out rentals at a certain time. It only says that you must return the rental before closing hours. We were really disappointed...

Having graduated from UCSB some years ago, I still love coming up to the SB/IV/Goleta area every once in a while.  I've been to this Paddle Sports Center location twice now for tandem kayak rental sessions.  Both times it has been well worth the drive up from LA, though sadly, both kayaking partners have not been able to handle the full rental hour.  This is a bare-bones operation but all the equipment is solid and the nice young man working the trailer is very friendly and helpful.  The beach itself is not typically too crowded and the smaller waves make for easy launches and landings.  I'm likely to come back on my own in the near future.

Grandmother Sycamore. She is an old and wise tree. She is magical. She is a giant yet she is gentle and offers a lovely place to sit and be. I hope she is left to live many more years and that she is treated with respect.

What an amazing tree.  I come here every trip to Carpinteria because it reminds me of the journey of life and what is truly meaningful and lasting about it.  This tree has been there a long time and I believe it has the embedded knowledge of all these years it has lived in that same spot, unmoving, ever-growing and undaunted by all that has passed since it began as a little sprout.

I go to commune.  I go to touch this ancient being.  I go to be awe-struck.  I go because my friend goes and loves this tree too.  Its special and being in its presence is special.

I never get tired of visiting this tree.  A city historical landmark, this tree is almost 300 years old. It's is so huge, it could no longer hold its branches up and they are down on the ground and the branches are larger than the trunks of most trees.  There are other beautiful trees on the property, but the Portola Sycamore is special.  And yes, you can climb the tree at your own risk.  This is one of those types of places my Dad would take us to when I was a kid and I'd wonder what all the fuss was about.  Go and take your kids and force them to look at the tree until they appreciate it as much as I do.  They'll thank you one day.

Legends say that if you touch your hands to this tree, close your eyes, and tell it what negativity you want to let go of in your life, it will absorb that heaviness from you. Being over 300 years old, this tree is full of wisdom and life. Simply being blanketed under the soft glow of its enveloping canopy, I feel lighter. It truly is a Healing Tree and I could spend an entire day resting and reflecting under it's timeless branches.

Such a magical tree. 

The fact its almost 300 years old is mind blowing. 

It has grown so big that it has rested its branches on the ground, which have grown upward from there. It looks like a grove of trees but its really just one!

A perfect spot for a picnic or date to connect with an amazing tree!

Another playground, nothing more nothing less.  The kids had some fun but when we got there we were actually quite unimpressed.  Spent about 20 minutes there and moved on.  If you are coming here and expecting a cool and fancy play area, you might as well skip it.  Have been to multiple neighborhood playgrounds with better facilities and play structures.  If you are a kid under 7 years old, any play area is going to be fun, so this review is purely for the older kids and the adults that have to stick around while the kids play.  Enjoy.

What a great playground!  I loved the nods to local history included with the row of adobes kids can play in and the mural featuring the mountains.  My kids loved the interactive walls that spew cool stem and squirt water from sea life sculptures.  We spent a good three hours here before the little ones were hungry for lunch --- wet and covered with sand, they had to be cleaned up before I could take them anywhere though! 

Obviously well cared for, this playground is clean, has available bathrooms nearby and a carousel adjacent to it.  Don't be alarmed by the crowd of homeless on the other side of the large grassy field, they don't seem to want any problems and they kept their distance during our entire visit.

So cute. A little shady break from our walk along the arts and crafts fair. Bathrooms are nearby, though someone thought it would be fun to pee in the large shell-like structure.

Bench tables are available, but seems to lack some shade. May be difficult for smaller kids, but the 5+yr olds seem to enjoy it.  With my toddler, it was more of climbing some areas, without going through to many apparatuses.  Didn't really notice many swings around though too, but maybe I didn't look hard enough. Either way, it had pretty walkways and garden surroudnings.  Definately wish I has something nearby my home like this.

This park was okay  all the kids were different ethnicity I loved how they got along and play all together.. the park is quite simple there is not much to do... my kids got done with it within 15-20 mins I just made them use there imagination as they should to stay longer.
This was a fun place to hang out with the kids. We stopped by to play on our way up the coast. Both of our boys, 6 and 9, had a blast!

Homeless everywhere I didn't really feel comfortable with my kids alone, there were no other children or families and maybe about 20 homeless. I have nothing against them but I'd rather not. The playground area is nice though so we stayed for about 15 minutes.

This is a fantastic park directly across from the beach.  My son had a blast here playing on all the equipment.  There are no dogs or food allowed in the playground area, but plenty of grass to have a picnic and bring your fur baby outside of this area.  

The playground equipment is divided between younger kids and older kids, as is standard.  There is a grassy, raised area for parents to sit and relax while older kids play.  My hubby chose to chase my son around on the bigger equipment for safety and fun.  I relaxed outside of this area with our dog and took in the gorgeous ocean views.

The park's walking path is similar to being in a tiny marsh.  

Bottom Line:  Very cute park that is worth checking out while in Santa Barbara for the kiddies.
Nice park for younger kids. Grounds well maintained. Not very busy for a Saturday mid afternoon in leek tourist season.

Love the little man-made pond with the ducks and aquatic turtles!  There was this woman that literally brought a WAGON of bread to feed the ducks, so I know those animals are well-fed here.

The playground itself is fun, just doesn't have sun shades.  Pretty clean place, close to the beach, and well-themed.

Great Park for the kids. Our boys 5 & 6 loved it. They climbed up the ship structure and we spend 1/2 day here, after 2 hours, we walk up one block to the merry- go- around and it's an old carousal from 1916, the boys ride on that and back to the park to do more climbing. There are plenty if trees if you want to sit in the shade.  its right aross from the beach.
Nice local park. Seemed to have new playground equipment. Clean and had a pond area with ducks.

Review for 4 & 6 year olds - they liked it a lot. I liked the clean-ish bathrooms with soap. The mission structure is unique and fun for hide & seek. The kids especially liked the egg (?? weird, IMHO) and the "slanted deck" slide although it seemed relatively dangerous to me with kids sliding down, kids clambering up, and kids pouring sand on it. Somehow my 6 yo bumped his noggin in the melee and had quite a bruise to show for it the next day. It was a fine spot to spend a picnic lunch break until the smell from the adjacent water treatment plant came drifting over. I made the family pack it up then! Expect to park on the street, which has time limits but is free.
A playground so distinct, well kept and child approved I made a special stop in Santa Barbara for my 2 year old son to play here. A great pit stop when on the road with little ones.

It was a nice stop on our day trip. My son-4 loves boats and this park was great for him. My daughter-2 also found it fun. It's just like any other playground but with a boat theme. I liked that there was two playgrounds one was for smaller children. I felt this place was good for toddlers as long as they have someone with them. Nothing wrong with being hand and hand with the little ones. However, if you are taller than 5'5 it might be difficult. I think my only complaint was there is no swings.

I would have liked to go see the ducks and ponds. The walking trail looked nice but I didn't walk it. 

There might be people who are displaced living there but none of them bother us.
Nice bathrooms. There's only 3 parking spaces. The playground is for kids 5-12, so no infant swings for my kid :(

Forget the beach..... we were driving down Cabrillo Blvd. with the Ocean on our right and this park on our left....... what to do.... what to do.....
We chose the park and the toddler and I are glad we did!   
Amazing shipwrecked theme play ground with huge life size sea creature sculptures that mist water every few minutes.   
The playground has foam flooring for cushion or clean soft sand, no kitty litter contents here.  
Beautifully landscaped paths and lawns,  with tropical plants and flowers labeled.   Nice size pond with huge Koi fish and a few ducks.  Picnic tables scattered throughout.
North end of the park holds a wonderful surprise of an Antique Carousel with 5 minute rides for $2.
Clean bathrooms near the playground and carousel.
Great break from the beach for the toddler.
Parking on the street.

Outside of Fess Parker's eyesore (the rainbow arch) and lack of parking there it is nearly impossible to find something wrong with Chase Palm Park. I actually go out of my way to find reasons to visit. Although the playground is designed for toddlers to preteens there is enough beauty for any adult.

Last week I walked here to sit down and read once the beach became a bit chilly. Even with all the water fountains turned off in the drought I had trouble deciding where to sit down. 

Between the concerts at the park and the ornate carousel there truly is something for everyone. In fact, just writing this makes me want to visit again today.
The BEST park I have ever been to! There is so much to do here, your kids will defiantly leave entertained. Highly reccomened!

Great park for our 2 & 4 yr old. They would've stayed longer if it didn't start to get dark on us!  Few different play areas, big sand area with climb on structures, clean restrooms, grass areas great for a picnic. Will be back again for sure! 

Only con - no parking directly connected with the park, all street parking was full, so we had to park about a block down & across the street in a pay for parking lot.

Went with my 2 nieces and nephew, ages 11, 8, and 3. They all had a blast. It's a clean park, somewhat small but not too crowded (we went on a Sunday around 5:00pm). I'd give it 5 stars, but had to take off a star due to the VERY limited parking (there are only about 3 dedicated spaces for the park). Then again, it was a weekend so the limited parking is understandable.

It would be nice if Santa Barbara would post age ranges for their playgrounds on the website so you can tell if it's appropriate for your kid before coming and trying to find parking and all that. There is a play structure for ages 2-5 and over for 5-12 but nothing for really young toddlers, not even baby swings. That said it looks fun for those age ranges, but if your kid is a brand new toddler it's not really worth it unless they're happy just playing in the sand.

Looking for something to do with the family away from LA...head out to Santa Barbara! Make sure to check out the shipwreck playgrounds with the kids. Lots of things to do including multiple playgrounds, carousel ride, creek (with ducks), sand lots, bike/scooter/board paths, etc.. You can easily spend a few hours here. Located right at Cabrillo blvd, you just can't beat the awesome views of the Santa Barbara coastline...a perfect place to cool down during hot days! Even in December, I'm amazed how perfect the area weather can be especially on a nice clear day! You can also take a stroll down the warf, state street, or hang out for cocktails at the Set Bar located in the Hilton SB resort (our personal favorite) all walking distance from the park. Can't ask for a more perfect day!

This was such a good park for the littles! We were staying down the street and had passed by it driving a couple times and it just seemed soo cute, so we decided to make it a priority to let our little one play before we left town. It was themed with little ships and a echo spaceship thing and this little block town you can run around and hide, then sand and also the padded rubber ground, the wood slide was different and fun, we didn't make it to the ducks in the pond, but the different play structures were enough to keep us occupied for about an hour. I am soo glad we came and played here, it was easy access, clean and gave you a variety of activities you could do so the kids are not bored. So awesome

This a mixed review! The actual park was great! My 2 year old loved the wooden slides and the sea themed playground. There is lots of for the little ones to do including, slides, monkey bars, and a whale of course all on a padded floor. Why the four stars?? I would say that the location is less desirable. The area is surrounded by drifters and when I first got there we were the only ones there which made it a bit scary. I'm sure it's fine but personally that put me off. Parking seems to be non existent! We were lucky and parked up the street but crossing a main road like that was pretty daunting. Overall I would say pass the location the park is great for our 2 year old

THE CREW HEADS TO WETTER GROUND!

BACKGROUND:
This long park borders both sides of Cabrillo Blvd. from the East Beach to Stearns Wharf. Much of it has been renovated recently and it has made this area a wonderful place for hanging out on a Sunday afternoon. On the harbor side there is a long bike and rollerblading trail, a skateboard park, a rec. house, and beach access. The long stretch of grass hosts soccer, Frisbee and dancing. 

STATS:
Sunrise-10pm 
Skateboard Park 8am-1/2 hr after sunset

OUR ROUTE:
Just a short walk from the Downtown Santa Barbara train station, me and The Crew make a way through this quaint park that runs parallel to the beach located across the street. 

Meandering through 10-acre park, visitors will see a carousel, fountains, creeks,  WAIT...WHAT?!?!?! CAROUSEL?! Like a  merry-go-round? An amusement ride consisting of a rotating circular platform with seats for riders? Ok, I'll get back to that in a couple lines or so. Park also has a lagoon, and snack bar, as well as Shipwreck Playground, which apparently is modeled after an ocean Schooner.
