We rented 7 electric bikes on Fathers Day to cruise around Santa Barbara. We're not tourists, we live in the area but wanted to try something new and different. Highly recommend trying if you haven't already. It's a great way to see the beautiful city of Santa Barbara and at a good price too. Not only was it affordable but the staff were very helpful and knowledgeable about the sport. They made us feel comfortable and prepared for our adventure. They also have onsite parking and street parking available which makes there location pretty much perfect in our eyes(also minutes from the beach). Thanks guys for a memorable day! Highly recommend this company if your looking to rent bikes, especially electric bikes.

Thank you for a great afternoon! Your staff is amazing & so nice! We booked the day before & prepaid online. When we arrived the bikes were clean & ready to go, with the perfect basket for our puppy! 
Love that you are so close to the bike trail! Easy access in & out. We so appreciate your kindness and quality of your bikes! Can't wait to come back!

Great bike rental shop. Employees are very helpful and knowledgeable. They are happy to get you to the right trail. I rented a specialized high end bike. Worked perfectly. They will be my go to bike shop when ever I come to Santa Barbra.

I had a stand up paddleboard (SUP) lesson with Siena from Cal Coast Adventures and it was amazing. Siena was a great teacher - clear communicator, calm and kind, I felt immediately relaxed - which helps a lot when learning something new! We spent an hour and a half paddling around the harbor and Siena pointed out the sights and shared some history of the harbor. It was absolutely the highlight of my vacation in SB, would highly recommend!

We decided to enjoy the weather in SB and ride around the beach/ state street. There are a lot of options for bike rentals but this place was the best! I can see why they have a 5 star review. Very kind and professional service. I think I asked a ton of questions while making a reservation and they made it super easy. We got the electric bikes we wanted and enjoyed a beautiful day! Definitely recommend booking your ebike online to make sure they don't sell out of the bikes you want. Side note: they have seat attachments for children 2-4 years old.

We booked lessons at Haskells Beach at the Ritz Carlton Bacara. It was super convenient and easy to book. We had 2 instructors (Mac and Ryan) who were great! They came with several wetsuits to make sure we had the right sizes and were very patient and accommodating. We had a 7 year old they kept an eye/hand on the entire time and even got to stand on her board! Great with novice kids and adults! We felt very safe and had a memorable fun time! If staying at Bacara we highly recommend! Easy to walk to and find from the hotel (5 min). Mac and Ryan - 5 stars!!
Great times on the water! Staff was easy-going and friendly. Equipment is well-maintained and in good shape. Easy booking online including signing waivers. Will do it again soon!

The Mrs and I booked a private electric bicycle tour from this organization and glad we did. Or tour guide, Sergio, was a very knowledgeable and personable guide, and, was very clear on the instructions throughout the tour which was about 2.5 hours. Very well worth the $95 per person that we paid. We ended up going to the mission, the presidio, the city college, the beach, and several other locations. Sergio also provided his recommendations, being a lifelong resident of Santa barbara, of good restaurants and other attractions to visit while we were here.. I highly recommend this tour operator, and especially Sergio, should you have the opportunity to do so while in Santa barbara.

I took my daughter on a kayak journey with our fabulous tour guide Emily. We had zero kayaking experience and she made it so easy for us. We enjoyed the gorgeous Santa Barbara ocean, Pier, harbor full of boats. Aside from offering a flawless kayaking experience, spending time with our tour guide was actually my favorite part of the experience. Emily was very personable and super fun to hang out with. She made our experience 100%.

Had a great time with the e bike rental. On line reservation was easy, staff was friendly and very helpful. Appreciated the map and simple instructions as it was my first time using an electric bike. Reasonable prices, relaxed and accommodating vibe. Will be back...

Our family of five had a great mountain bike tour with Sergio yesterday. We've only had one other mountain biking experience so opted for the beginner bluff tour.  Sergio was fabulous! He provided the appropriate number of water breaks and stops for photo ops. Happily, with three teenagers in tow, Sergio was also able to supply enough 'challenge' opportunities to keep it exciting for them.  We definitely felt like it was great value for the money. Lovely scenery, attentive guide, in a fun, challenging, yet safe activity.

Sergio is the best!  We had an amazing time on our tour!  We booked a group tour and it ended up feeling like a private tour with four of us in the group.  For our first time riding e-bikes it was a breeze!  Sergio is a fantastic guide and showed us many spots in Santa Barbara we would have never found on our own.  It was a wonderful tour, highly recommend!
Sergio took us on some awesome trails and we had a ton of fun biking with cal coast! Perfect family activity if you're visiting Santa Barbara.

Had a blast! Sergio started me out slow but soon had me doing some crazy downhills! He gave great instruction and was supportive even after I repeatedly didn't listen and went for stuff out of my skill level! 

He took fantastic pictures, gave us a mini tour of the area and dropped us off at our hotel. 5 star service all around! Can't wait to return and head up to the actual mountains and get more technical! You can't go wrong with Sergio - absolutely recommend him whether you're a beginner or more advanced.

name: Santa Barbara Rock Gym
categories: Active Life, Gyms, Rock Climbing, Fitness & Instruction

yelp_reviews.text: The staff and patrons are so welcoming and inclusive! We love bringing the kids here and will buy a year membership next year for sure!

This is just a great place.  The staff are knowledgable and friendly, and the other members also all seem to have good attitudes.  Everyone who works there....and goes there...loves rock climbing and that makes this place a really fun environment in which to learn.

I started to visit SBRG shortly after it opened last year.  My 9 year old son began climbing there and I would just go down to watch him climb.  Eventually, I decided to try it and now I'm hooked.  I literally had ZERO experience with rock climbing when I started....I mean I never even watched it on television before...or read about it.  But after a very short time, I was able to start climbing and take a few classes and seriously increase my skills.  Its a very social place and it was easy to find several climbing partners close to my skill level who I now climb with almost every week.

SBRG has a wide variety of routes to climb, and these range from "Fun" to seriously advanced.  They also set up new routes frequently, so you don't get bored climbing the same things over and over again.  They offer classes as well, and these are intensive small-group sessions (around 6 students max) where the instructor can really engage every student individually.

There are a couple of nights a week when it can get pretty crowded, but as I mentioned above, everyone who goes there seems to be for lack of a better description....COOL....and will share routes with you etc.

If you are an experiecned climber, or someone who wants to try it for the first time, you will enjoy the SBRG.

Very friendly, courteous staff. I paid for two 11 year old girls to spend time "on the rocks" with Max and he was excellent with them. Encouraging and offering great tips to become more proficient climbers. A great workout when the weather outside is less than ideal.

I'm a beginner climber and this was my first time at this rock gym (I'm a UCSB student so I climb at our recreation center wall). Anyways, it was my first time here and I thoroughly enjoyed it! There is a wide variety of routes with different difficulties. Most are for experienced climbers who can climb those V4s and above, but I found plenty of workout room for my own range of difficulty (Vfun-V2). A lot of their walls are on an upward slant which increases the difficulty of the climb but brings awesome reward upon completion. Will definitely go visit them again as the day use pass is fair priced (they even offer a student discount and more discounts on Tuesdays and Fridays). Parking is easy to find with plenty of 75min free parking all around the area and a full parking lot right outside of REI (that's right, that parking lot is also for the SB Rock Gym!!). The staff is friendly and sets you up really quickly with an orientation and then you are ready to go! The place holds a great community of people and is super family friendly. Make sure you go check it out, you will not regret it.

Took my girlfriend to SB Rock Gym last night as part of her birthday celebrations. As climbing novices, we were apprehensive since we didn't know what to expect. We called the day of one of their 2 hour "Intro to Climbing" classes and they signed us up even though we were technically putting them over class capacity. 

We discovered a fun and friendly staff. The class taught us all the basics that we would need to come back and climb on our own (after we take a safety test) - knots, harnesses, belaying, lingo, etc. 

Our instructor, bearded Chris, was friendly, funny, knowledgeable, encouraging and all around super enjoyable. He deserves a ton of credit for making our first visit awesome. 

SB Rock Gym is a good time - don't let inexperience stop you from checking it out!

Recently checked this indoor rock climbing gym out and fell in love. 

Bouldering? Plenty of options. Lead wall climbs? More limited but still some fun options. Both changed regularly to keep things interesting.

The staff is very friendly. The environment appears to be social between staff and patrons, making for a more fun experience. 

Did the "on the rocks" special which includes 4 lead wall climbs with an instructor and then free use of the gym. For future use, unless you take the Introduction Class, you cannot use the lead walls without a staff member, a safety issue I assume although the gym only has auto-belay.

Offer memberships, day passes, session punch cards, and also it is  1/2 off on Tuesday nights! 

Don't let the lack of height initially turn you off, give it a try and love it too! Perfect for birthday parties, adding to your fitness routine, or just trying out every so often.

I was very excited to find out that there was a new Rock Gym in Santa Barbara. I was extremely impressed with the friendly staff, pricing, and climbing options. It was a large group of us girls including 2 leaders and 8 High Schoolers. It's so nice to have a fun place in town to take kids to. The girls had a blast and enjoyed the freedom to climb with the option of auto belays. There is inexpensive parking across the street and the place is open late. The girls are excited to come back again. I definitely recommend this place!

So when my nephew was crying about hating baseball practice, I told my sister to check out some other hobbies and let him explore his true interests. She started looking for other hobbies for him. That's when we decided to see if he liked the rock gym! This places is very family oriented, safe, and has a good crowd that comes around. I met the manager Wyatt who's an awesome dude I was very impressed with his knowledge of everything rock climbing and my nephew loves this place! Thanks Wyatt for giving him a safe environment to be himself.
Took an intro class to learn the basics and the do's and dont's of bouldering. Everyone was very friendly, there was a kids birthday party while I was there but barely noticed the kids. Great spot.

Awesome rock gym with a good selection of challenging problems. I've seen bigger gyms, but there's a lot of quality in SBRG's limited floor space. The staff is friendly and the culture at the gym is laid back and encouraging. 

They offer a discounted student membership which I'd recommend.

My kids, along with their cousin, did a camp here for a week. This was their second time doing camp at the gym; however, it was their first time doing the outdoor rock climbing. It was fabulous! Cassie and Ian led the trips and made every day wonderful. My kids aren't usually fans of camps and yet they looked forward to each day.  Cassie and Ian not only kept them safe, they made the days fun and they were tolerant and patient and kind. We just wish we had more time in the summer for another session! While the camp might seem expensive at first, they are truly engaged and using their body, learning new skills, and building confidence. It was what every kid hopes for at camp, lots of laughs and memories.
Shout out to Chris for showing my wife and I the ropes. Awesome instructor! Facility was amazing, staff was friendly. Highly recommended!

Love this gym! Sure it's small but if you're a member you get in @ 10 am before all the noobs and tourists. I often have the gym to myself because of this. Staff is friendly and helpful. The only downside is the summer kids camp during the morning hours... Not a fan of kids climbing around and being loud.

My 15 year old son and I were in SB for the day and needed something that we could enjoy together. I found this place through Yelp and used one of their 2 for 1 coupons. Their employees and instructors were all very helpful and answered all our questions and made sure we were safe. I wish there were more easier climbs for the novice, but all in all it was a great bonding experience for the two of us and we will definitely visit again.

Love this place! Setting is both challenging and creative. Fun staff and a great place for kids. I first learned how to climb here and have developed many friendships and connections through climbing here. I would recommend this place to anyone who is looking to start climbing or even if you have been climbing for years. This place has something for everyone.
One of the jewels of Santa Barbara, we just called to come for a class and were greeted by Will,the owner who was just a pleasure to work with - this is one of the highlight about Santa Barbara

Five stars! For being such a small facility, they really do ok for themselves. Great belaying, awesome bouldering! Always lots of fun problems for climbers of all levels. Routes are reset often enough that even the most avid full time members can't get bored. And the staff... It seems to be a thing that the staff at climbing gyms are a little stuck up and typically quite rude about it. But not so here. The staff are awesome, very friendly and professional. They're always willing to give you tips when you ask and all seem to love their jobs. Climb on!

Santa Barbara Rock Gym is such a great place to get excercise and have fun! Not only is it a wonderful family environment, but the staff members are so kind and friendly. Chris and Will were so generous and eager to donate to our organization.
Kids Helping Kids would like to thank Santa Barbara Rock Gym for their generous contribution. All proceed assist in making a positive difference in the lives of youth both locally and globally. It is thanks to companies like Santa Barbara Rock Gym that allow us to make the world a better place.

Great gym and great staff. There's plenty of bouldering available but a bit limited on the lead and top rope climbs and the ceilings aren't very high. All together a solid gym and I will revisit when I'm in Santa Barbara.

I think lots of the reviews are inflated since there aren't really great options for indoor climbing in the area.  UCSB has a climbing wall, but it's not that big (it's taller than SBRG, but less routes in total) - but is a decent option if you also want a membership to a gym as well.  There used to a be gym in Ventura (vertical heaven) that featured both top rope and bouldering, but they've since moved to a new location and only offer bouldering now.  

My major qualms...I mostly TR, so I'll stick to that for my critique.  The walls aren't very tall.  Granted they seemed to do the best they could with the space they have, but the TR routes basically amount to bouldering + maybe 10 feet.  Also, I've seen much more creative route setting elsewhere.  There are a couple interesting routes that make you think, work the problem out, and use some different moves, but the vast majority are straight up, use some flagging and maybe a layback here and there.  

The positives though - decent pricing (especially if catch a membership deal or go on evenings when they're running specials), good place to get into climbing (learning the basics), it's a new facility so while it smells (hey, it's a rock climbing gym), the holds, walls, ropes, autobelays are all in great shape, and because they are growing they are starting to add more benefits to members (yoga classes).

Let me start off by saying this:  There's something special about Santa Barbara Rock Gym.

I've climbed at several different climbing gyms around the country, all of which were taller and larger than SBRG.  What SBRG lacks in height and size, however, they more than make up for in fantastic, flowing top rope and lead routes, challenging bouldering problems -- all of which that are updated frequently -- and a truly awesome staff.  I'm not sure what it is about most rock climbing gyms, where the staff tends to be dry, listless, and totally unenthusiastic.  I mean -- REALLY?  You work at a rock climbing gym... how could you not be pumped?? Not to mention you're surrounded by some of the most chill people in the population... Regardless, SBRG has completely circumvented this issue and is run by a talented, warm, solid group of passionate climbers.  

As I mentioned, the routes are awesome - there's nothing worse than climbing in a gym, and finding that the routes are clunky and lack any flow.  There's a good balance between easier routes and problems and more difficult projects. (And for any lead climbers out there, they've got a great arch that's always got at least a few challenging routes on it).

If you live in the Santa Barbara area or are just passing through, I  definitely recommend you check out SBRG and get your climb on.

If anyone's interesting in starting to climb, this is the place. My daughter came in as a beginner and now climbs better than I do! It's always a great time at the gym, and the instruction couldn't be better.

I started at Santa Barbara Rock gym about two months ago and I am officially hooked. I love to exercise, love working out, and climbing is exercise in a way that I've never experienced before. It's like putting a puzzle together in my mind, then attempting to do it with my body. Watching the more experienced climbers is awesome, and one thing that struck me was how social the environment is and how kind everyone is in the gym. The staff are exceptionally friendly and helpful, which goes a long way, they are great people.

My kid just had her 6th bday party here, and her and her friends had a blast! Kids ranged from 3-8 and the staff there are amazing. It was really reasonably priced for what is put in to making sure the kids are having fun and being safe. Highly recommended for having a awesome bday party and watching the instructors/staff helping the kids overcoming their fears of heights.

The Rock Gym is the greatest place in town! Whether you are 5 years old or 75, crushing 5.12s or struggling up 5-funs, you will love this place. Total positive energy, great staff, the Rock Gym rocks!

This place ROCKS! I have been brand new to the world of rock climbing. The staff at SBRG is great. Chris the Program Director has a great teaching style, and is super-helpful for newbies. He is a passionate instructor that is great with kids. He makes things simple, safe, and fun. Mike, and Dean are also top notch rock climbing instructors that really take an interest in the people they are working with. I have learned a lot, had a lot of fun, and would highly recommend this gym to anyone, of any age looking for a safe, fun place to get exercise. It is perfect for families to do something together. Will has put together a great team for his gym.
I love this place! Has a lot more to climb than your average gym and I always meet the coolest people here. Five dollar Fridays!
It has a wide range of routes and lots for new folks and kids too. Only downside is it can get crazy busy so know that going in. A few auto-belays are available too.

Very nice and clean gym right behind REI. This gym has Auto Belayer so you don't need a partner for top rope. My husband and I were in town for our vacation and decided to check it out for a quick hour of climbing. I love all the climbs. We usually climb 5.10s-11s and found all the 5.9-5.10 climbs here to be quite challenging and requires more core. Each climb has its own name. Very creative. The staff is friendly and attentive. Definitely come back here next time we're in Santa Barbara.
Friendly and helpful staff. Not much of a pro-shop. If you want selection in gear go someplace else. Plenty of walls to choose from. Good times.
This is a wonderful way to spend some time, or have a party.  They have accommodation for kids - parents must be present.  And a great deal on Friday nights!

I've been climbing for over 5 years now and have been to various gyms in Northern California, SoCal, and New York, so I'd like to think I have a decent sense of how gyms are normally set up. 

SB Rock Gym is technically located on State Street, and is a bit hard to find even with the assistance of navigation. it's tucked in a corner near the walkway for the tunnel, and is impossible to see from the road. The best way to get there is to park in REI's parking lot, and walk down the street and turn left onto State until you see it. 

The gym is priced fairly, and the half off Fridays make it a really good deal. Even on fridays, its never too crowded, and you can always get your workout in. The gym utilizes it's limited space well, and the bouldering rooms are one of the best I've ever seen. The bouldering walls have unique terrain and route setters make great unique routes that work with it really well. And its updated pretty often! 

The top rope on the other hand... The top rope is pretty much glorified bouldering with a rope. The entire corner closest to the window is just a mess, with incredibly short routes. The parallel walls in that corner are too close to each other, making it a tight space for belayers. Routes are not updated frequently, which is especially important in a smaller gym like this. Don't get me wrong, the top rope is great for parties and kids who wont mind the shorter height, but for more experienced climbers it really doesn't cut it. 

Overall, an amazing gym for bouldering, but the top rope isn't up to par. 

Also, pictures are incredibly misleading for getting a feel for the size of the gym.
Love the lead routes! The setters are great, I constantly have a project because they reset every week!

I got a groupon for this place because I was going to be in town for a day and wanted to do something fun. It was very easy to use and the staff was very friendly. My boyfriend and I really liked the free climb area. We stayed for hours, basically till our hands were raw. Loved this place, and would most certainly go back!

Nice gym with great staff. My husband and young son recently visited as beginners just looking for an afternoon of fun and something new. The staff was very accomodating and made it a fun experience for everyone. Felt very safe and professional. They made new customers for sure; my son can't wait to go back!!

Not a regular climber but I really enjoyed my time here. On Tuesdays they have a couples discount (doesn't have to be a SO, could be a friend or family) where they give you half off of their original price. If you don't have shoes or a harness you will also have to pay for those additionally. The service is very friendly, laid back, and knowledgeable. Other experienced climbers are also friendly and are not intimidating despite their amazing skills! For beginners they have auto belays that you can hook onto and will catch you on the way down. They have easy routes (even though they were still challenging to me) and they have very difficult routes. They also have bouldering and as long as you are respectful and safe, you can try to easy routes!

Would definitely recommend this to anyone wanting to try rock climbing as this is a good environment to do so!

- J

Decent size gym with lots of interesting boulder problems and some fun sport routes as well. They change the problems often so there is always something new to play with. Prices are reasonable and the staff is friendly with a good focus on safety and comfort. The wall is not as high as you would want for decent length routes, and it tends to get very busy on discounted days.

Our family loves Santa Barbara Rock Gym!  We have a 10 and 12 year old and this place is fun for all ages.  Whether you're a beginner or expert there is a great experience for all levels. The management and staff are friendly and experienced too.

My company went for a team building / celebration event. Our instructor Chris was absolutely on point. He was encouraging for us beginners, genuinely friendly, and kept us all engaged and laughing the entire evening. We all had a wonderful time - I highly recommend.

Thanks Chris & SB Rock Gym!

Got to meet Will, the owner of the place. Super cool, knowledgable, friendly, and honest guy. 
This place has a very friendly, supportive, and cozy ambiance. 
Come out for a climb, bring a friend or two, or meet new ones here!

This place is very accommodating for beginners! Unlike in my hometown where the cost of a few quick lessons costed $80, for just $30 here we were able to do 4 fun and helpful climbs with an instructor and felt like regular 'ole pros by the end of it. It is a smaller facility but you will regardless get your climbing fix! Additionally...the playlist was bumpin' that day.

Perfect location in the heart of downtown, right in front of REI...So many fun climbs and it changes every week. My kids love it here. Great for all ages. I love the set up with the lounge area, comfy sofas and large table to relax at for moms like me who don't climb. The staff is so friendly, Will the owner and all of them really, are very, very helpful. 

Memberships are reasonable, but so is trying it out on $5 Fridays until you figure out that  this is a fun sport or workout for you.  Mountain Sports I think offers discounts for members too if you want to get climbing gear like harnesses, shoes, chalk, etc. REI is also right behind the store and great if you're a member there. 

You can rent equipment at SB Rock Gym too. Harnesses are $5, shoes $2.

They offer discounts for youth memberships so for us it was well worth becoming members and going  whenever they want. And if your whole family are climbers they have a great membership for you too.

This gym is fine for bouldering but their policy for belaying is inexplicably restrictive. I have climbed frequently at about 4 other climbing gyms in major cities (including San Francisco and Philadelphia) and everywhere I've ever been, a climbing gym will allow paying guests to become trained in belaying, pass a test to prove they can operate the equipment safely, and become certified to belay their friends. At SB Rock Gym, they refused to allow my guests to become trained in belaying. I still don't understand why. Their staff members insisted that there is no place in the country that will allow you to become certified in belaying and immediately climb thereafter, but that statement is belied by my own experience at Mission Cliffs (SF), Planet Granite (SF) and Go Vertical (Philly) and a few other places. 

They also inexplicably refused to allow me to bring in my own Vibram 5-finger shoes (which I have used for climbing for the last 5 years at least, without any problem), and made me rent their shoes for $6. It was a real pain and they gave no good reason why I couldn't climb in my own shoes that I prefer for climbing. 

Their bouldering wall is fine but if you're looking to come here with friends to climb and belay, and you're not already certified here, then I don't recommend this place.
Awesome facility, great kids and team programs, knowledgeable and professional staff.

Very fun climbing gym! You can find lots of really interesting boulder problems on vertical wall sections, a cave area and other slightly overhung parts. The building is a kind of short so top roping is shorter than at your average gym. The rating system can sometimes be a little all over the place, but overall really fun! fivedollafivedollafivedollaFRIDAY!!!

This gym was nice, though somewhat smaller than other ones I've been to.  I found the ratings to be really hard - I normally can climb all 5.9's and some 5.10's, and here I struggled with 5.9's.  The staff were really nice and helpful and the atmosphere was relaxed and fun.  There was a good variety of climbs, and climbing there was overall a really nice experience.

Great experience for a family outing.
Though I would have liked a simple return phone call as it took a while to get a hold of someone and left several voice mails.

Fresh Routes!  Though the space is small in comparison to other Rock Gyms, they do a great job keeping it fresh.  The route-setters re-set 5 days a week, WOW, kudos to these guys that work full time to set new routes keeping it fun, challenging and fresh.   Staff are awesome and SBRG is great for everyone from kids to seasoned climbers.  Climb ON!

This is a great place for a local hang out and a good workout. For the size, the gym has a very good amount and very diverse routes. It gets crowded in the 5-7pm, if you want to make friends. The staff are always very friendly and there is an awesome crew around at all times. Excellent bouldering spot, it will train your grip without a doubt.

Accessible, beginner-friendly gym with helpful staff and a great community of regulars.

The emphasis here is definitely on bouldering. There are plenty of top roping routes, but be aware that only 4 of them support auto-belay. There are probably 3-4 times as many top rope routes that require a partner to belay.

It can get busy in the evenings, but I've never had trouble working in enough climbs to go home exhausted. I love it!

Not good for top rope. Walls are only 20 ft tall and set like you're bouldering. Looks great for if you're practice is mainly bouldering. Call first, there were 2 little kid birthday parties here today taking up a significant amount of the small space. Front desk was very helpful. Warning: there is no AC and it was miserably hot.

Maybe I was just tired the day I came in... maybe.

I was in town for my cousin's wedding. Before going home up North I had my husband take me here to check this place out. Upon arrival I was shocked at how SMALL and short this place was. I can literally just boulder every wall here... literally. There taller walls were as tall as the bouldering walls I've bouldered on at other gyms, and high balls outdoors. I sport climb as well and I was shocked to see that this place does indeed have a lead wall... which is surprising considering that this place is pretty short. I'd be scared as heck to test for lead, let alone take a fall. What is my belayer supposed to do?! Keep tension on the entire time, as if I'm going to get pulled off the wall?! NO WAY!

The general price to climb is $15. I decided to pay the $15 because I wanted to pull on plastic that bad. Their rating system is odd, however, I do kind of see their theory behind it: They rate their problems by color and not the usual Vscale because they want to encourage everyone to get on everything and not be fixated on the ratings. TOTALLY UNDERSTAND, but what about those who are new to climbing? They won't understand the Vscale. What about those who want to see how much progress they're making? They can't just be like "Hey I'm a YELLOW COLORED CLIMBER" or "Hey I just crushed that GREEN!".  What does that even mean? Also, the routes are rated accordingly, but what about the subdivisions within that specific rating? Like I said, totally understand, but let's be real... c'mon.

At the end of the day, I ended up only staying for 45 min. and felt like I wasted my money. I forgot to mention, there was uncovered cake left out on the desk. I was thinking it was free game for the members and guests to take? Well whatever the case, it's kind of gross dude. There's chalk flying everywhere. You can't let cake sit out like that!

3 stars though. Customer service was nice.

I've taken my kiddo here a few times, he loves that he can free climb or use the auto belays - he always has a blast so it was a no brainer for us to come again. 

We had a groupon for a lesson and we were taking the kids while the grouponee had their lesson. However I ended up joining the lesson. I was not properly dressed to be a participant but it worked out. Ryan was super patient and made sure that we were safe. Falling 20 ft to my death would be utterly traumatic to my little guy. (This is obviously dramatized and exaggerated as there are a zillion backups in case one thing failed). He taught us to make sure the harness was on right, that the ropes were tied securely and kept reassuring me that I was safe. I'm petrified of heights. Insanely petrified. I hit a mental wall midway through the 2nd climb up and Ryan talked me out of my almost cardiac arrest. He coached me through and wouldn't let me give up. Trust me, I was mid meltdown and ready to call it quits. He was super calm and professional, gave some tips, and I hit the top!!! Then I was stoked to be down on the ground and I made out with the floor until it was borderline humiliating and tragic. Thanks Ryan for making sure I finished so I looked super badass for my kiddo!

I can't even climb a 6 ft ladder without having a panic attack! I'm stoked to have done this - been wanting to forever - grateful to have crossed this off my todo list with my pride and ego intact.

My kiddo loves coming here. You'd think that for amateurs a place like this would be intimidating but it's actually really rad. Everyone is super nice and encouraging and he always has a good time (he's 5) and scaled up 2/3 way on his own with a belay today, a personal best (usually he'll free climb up about 5 ft and jump down). This is a great place to get kids interested in sports and at their own pace. And it's super safe for them to do. The staff is always helpful to answer questions. 

It's nice that decent climbing shoes aren't exp and well worth the investment instead of renting them. 

Will 1 lesson make me comfortable to do it solo - no. But it's encouraged me that I can do almost anything if I put my mind to it and have my peanut gallery to egg me on.

I don't live in this area but decided to stop by on my way home to Orange County. Nice little gym located right off the 101 freeway. Parking is a bit small since it's shared with other businesses. Day passes are decently priced. Nothing crazy. 

Annabelle helped get me set up. She was super patient and sweet. I've climbed at Hangar 18 which is in the OC so I had an idea of what to expect here. Also a tip for future new climbers going to a new gym, try to fill out the waiver online before arriving. Cuts some time out. 

Pretty clean gym and not too busy for a Saturday early afternoon.

Shout out to the gentleman there today. He was super nice, our first time there and he explained it super patiently. I know that's his role, but I'm sure he has to say it a lot every day, he wasn't impatient or irritated, great attitude! 
Once he got us set with our harness and shoes we were able to try the easy course. Lol honestly easy just confirmed that this mom needs to work on her upper body , but oh well it was a great new activity and had a blast falling on my ass a couple of times. Overall had a blast. 
The only thing I suggest, is man oh man can yall please febreeze the hell out of those shoe rentals..... I tried to avoid it but mine didn't have enough grip to climb up.

If you love this rock gym- you either
A) are a long-time climber who knows the people who work there
B) never been to another rock gym and like it because it is a rock gym not because it is SB rock gym.
For one overpriced- charging for shoes and to climb for the day is robbery- especially for beginners who most likely can only climb for an hour before there tendons get tired. 
Secondly the staff is extremely unhelpful to outsiders and new climbers they focus on their friends who climb there which is a bit annoying because you could pay for the new iPhone payments with how much you spend on a membership here.
Thirdly they put up way too many routes with not enough lighting and honestly its just too much. They also don't keep the holds clean enough to keep them distinguishable.
All in all- I have no axe to grind- just wish this place had some competition with reasonable prices and better customer service.

Geez.  I wish I had better things to say about SBRG.  I love that they are supporting the local climbing community but at what cost?  If you are expecting to pay 65 a month for a gym membership your expectations will not be met.  No showers, minimal lockers, not one free weight,  not one pull-up bar, and one hang board tucked away in a corner mounted on a wall that is marked with the previous 100 users sweat.  If you came looking to be challenged as a climber, sure there are a handful of difficult routes.  That being said, 3/4 of the gym is geared towards beginners and the walls for top roping are as high as a standard rock gyms bolder routes.  What's worse is there are no other options in town.  You'd hope with a gym structured towards community building they would at least provide their members (the people who help pay their rent) with industry standards like showers, more lockers, a few free weights, a few hang boards, and wall heights that require some sort of endurance.

The second star I give is because I know the rock gym is doing good things for kids and trying to help the community.  I wish they would just think about their own services, too!  Maybe move a couple blocks off state street, cut the rent in half, have double the space and walls double as high :/

name: Powell Peralta Skate Shop
categories: Surfing, Sporting Goods, Shopping, Art Galleries, Active Life, Arts & Entertainment, Local Flavor, Skate Shops

yelp_reviews.text: They had cool stuff here. Got me a ripper banner and a couple of wall hanger boards. Staff is cool and very helpful. When I walked I felt like a kid in the 80's with all the old school supplies they had. They had nice reissues like a the Tony Hawk, Tommy Guerrero and Lance Mountain. Would definetly take another trip out there.
Very good selection, plenty of cool gear and nice staff to help you out with what you need! Definitely going to shop more here.
Well organized store, very friendly and knowledgeable staff, and they carry great products.  Keep up the good work, guys!

We bought a Powell Peralta skateboard at Lighthouse skate shop in Santa Barbara and later broke it that week because it got ran over by a bus and the guy at Lighthouse skate shop called Powell Peralta shop and talk to Carlos who gladly replace starboard for free! Great customer service! Thank you guys!

We visited yesterday, after another local shop. My 14 yr old son is getting into skating and after doing tons of internet research was excited to be at a bigger shop. He asked some pretty technical questions, and the answers he got were all basically "I'm not sure." And this was from the manager. He was friendly, but I counted at least 5 times that he said "I'm guessing...."

Granted, my son told me that the kind of riding he asked about (free riding) is not their specialty. But it would have been nice if they'd acknowledged it instead of trying to wing it. We went right back to the "A" store a couple blocks away, where he'd had a fantastic experience, and spent some more money there.

I came a while back to buy a skate board for my husband. The 3 guys that helped me were awesome. I knew I wanted to get this death wish board with Mickey Jim Greco and I wanted to carve out a G on the grip tape for our kids name. All 3 guys huddled around and did it for me and they made it look sick bc they carved the G where it said Greco so all u see is a lime green G. They spent like 2 hours helping me pick everything out and putting the board together for me. They definitely went above and beyond and made sure I got the best gear bc I was out of my element. My hubby loved it and he loved the etched out G the most. They made the purchase really fun for me. Thanks!!

Great products and service. Just built an awesome skateboard there and it was great!

Highly recommended

Excellent products, excellent help! Not for locals only, while there saw pilgrims from many lands scooping up new gear - all things skateboard and sweet tees, caps, shoes, stickers, patches, etc. it's all good.

Went in there to get a board and was treated like a V.I.P  The guy who helped me was the only one there because his partner was at lunch.  He was super busy and did an awesome job juggling multiple customers, treating everyone really nice even though he was stretched.  He helped me pick out a board to suit my "stature" and even upgraded the cone bushing to a heavy duty one (also accommodate my "stature") without me asking.  I haven't tried any of the other skate shops in SB but now I know I don't need to!  I hear they are moving to Goleta pretty much off the beaten path but I would suggest its well worth the trip.
Took my daughters board in for repairs. They did it all for free! Super friendly and nice shop. Great customer service too!

I just got back into skating after 10 years
Carlos was very friendly and helpful in helping my choose my new set up. David was also really helpful in building my setup. Come check out Powell for all your skateboarding needs
Very knowledgeable, helpful, and friendly staff. They had a lot of inventory and items that you can only find in the store and not online. Can't wait to return when back in the area.

I whole heartedly recommend this skate shop. From sick clothing and new shoes, decks, etc, to backpacks, socks, and 3rd party gear. No exaggeration all the clothing looks really good. Staff is super friendly and helpful, knowledgable. First time I came in I bought skate bearings, and any skater knows that getting the old ones out to make way for the new can be a big hassle. The only guy working helped me all the way through, without question. He was happy to help. If you're in Santa Barbara and like clothes and/or skateboarding, stop by.

While visiting sunny Santa Barbara and peregrinating down State Street and the other close streets, my younger daughter saw the Powell Peralta skate shop sign and we had to go. 
Upon entering it appeared to be your standard skate shop; decks in vibrant colors align the walls, trucks and wheels, shirts, sweatshirts, shoes, etc.  Pretty cool to see as anyone who knows about the making of this iconic brand was not an easy feat. While I am not a skater, my daughter is a beginner-intermediate level, she has a new trick board and 2 longboards, I do remember a lot of these brands and trademarks from my youth. My hubby also rides a longboard, it's a great dad-daughter 
Service was friendly, they gave my daughter a couple free stickers which was cool.

Stopped by to pick up a new deck because I needed a new one to push around. Decent selection and super nice staff. Helpful, knowledgeable and above all asked the right questions. 

They made sure the board was the right fit for me in size, what I planned on doing with it and made some good recommendations post consultation. 

Will definitely be back to support the local brands.

We went as a family yesterday to hook up some decks we had. Carlos was AMAZING!!! He was friendly, really helpful, had tons of knowledge and when we walked out a relatively short time later, my husband and kids had sick boards to ride and we are STOKED!!! The shop is cool, and totally worth the drive to Goleta. Easy to find and has tons of gear. Do yourself a favor and check it out for sure...but I would call ahead and make sure Carlos was there first, because he's the guy!!!! Thanks again Carlos. The kids (and the husband!!) are sooo happy!

LOVED THIS SHOP! Got my son a flight deck for his birthday and surprised him with a trip to Powell-Peralta. Their selection was huge and Carlos gave us knowledgeable and friendly service. We will be back for sure!!
Really awesome shop. Cool to see the old school style around and to support a local Santa Barbara business. The staff is super nice and helpful.

Top notch skate hub! Cannot go wrong in here. Everything you want and need all in one.

Great workers and customer service. Cooper is the man!

Highly recommended

This place is the legend!

Just being in this shop feels like you're part of the heartbeat of skateboarding.

The residents of santa Barbara are lucky.

Powell skateboarding technology is state of the art!

Can't speak towards the selection of skate boards, but I needed some clear grip tape for a project I'm working on, and the person who assisted me was very helpful and friendly.  Very quick process.  When I got there, the shop was fairly busy and everyone was moved through the line promptly.

Came in looking to get a present for my grandson. I don't know anything about skateboarding but Carlos did a great job helping me find just the right gift. Glad to know there are still small businesses like this one!
Had the typical shop tees. Staff wasn't friendly. Would recommend going on state street and checking out the skate shops there, they have friendly staff and more variety!

I've been wanting to visit Powell since I was 15!  That was over 20 years ago, and I finally made it yesterday!!  I'm not sure if they actually had a shop for the public back then, or if it was just the warehouse.  

Anyway, I felt like a blast to the past.  It's so great to see the old school logos on shirts, sweatshirts, boards, etc...  I was bummed that a lot of the tshirts were XXL, and not Med or Large.  I guess they sell out quickly, but it sucks when I drive from so far away to visit and won't be back for a long time.

A few of the logos that I missed from the 80's are gone.  I guess since those skaters moved on to other companies...  I also was hoping they would have the old school videos/DVDs from the Bones Brigade days...  I had the first 8 videos, but lost them all over time...

I bought 2 shirts and hope they bring back the Bones Brigade ones, as I didn't notice any of them...

Friendly staff and great memories!!

Finally realized that I was much too big for my kids skateboard so I dropped by after work not knowing a thing about modern skateboards. Nice shop. Wonderful mix of the old that I recognized and the new. Super helpful folks there. One of them walked me through a complete build.  So Nice to have the right size board. Very happy I made the decision to treat myself. I'm sure my neighbors will appreciate watching me bust my ass in my driveway.

name: Santa Barbara Beach Horseback Rides
categories: Tours, Event Planning & Services, Beaches, Horseback Riding, Active Life, Hotels & Travel

yelp_reviews.text:
I had a fabulous time on the ride! The guide was knowledgeable about the area, plus he had fun insights about celeb happenings or residents in the neighborhoods we visited. The beach ride was epic and for sure a bucket list type of ride. As someone who grew up riding every now and again, I have to say riding on the beach does beat all! And as a local, I thought I had done everything there was to do in town. Clearly I was wrong ;)

If you're visiting or a local, this is a must do!

My fiancé surprised me for my birthday with a sunset beach ride. And let me say... WOW. This ride was incredible from the start. We told Charlie that we had lots of experience riding (we live on a horse ranch) and he accommodated perfectly to our riding ability! Our horses where gentle yet you still had to give them directions which was perfect! He knew the area like the back of his hand and even gave us pointers if we where ever bring our horses up! The views absolutely breathtaking! Right from the get go. Whether it be alongside the beach with the wave crashing or going up into the hill with all the wild flowers you'll never see a bad view. I loved at the end how we rode through a lemon orchard. It smelt amazing! We will definitely be back and recommend ALL beach rides to Charlie!

My dad came all the way out from Texas to visit me, and we both had it on our bucket list to ride horses on the beach.  We booked with this company and we were very excited to go out riding, UNTIL WE MET CHARLIE.  As soon as we arrived, there was a woman who asked me to move my car , only after I had already parked and put up my sunshade, put everything away and locked the car up.  She could have asked me before I got out of the car but she didnt , and there was no logical reason for me to have to move my car because it was a huge open lot, I wasn't in the way of anything.  I just got back in my car and moved as she asked and didn't say anything although I thought it was weird.  Then charlie pulls up in his jaguar on his high horse. We were having a cigarette and as soon as he got out of the car he didn't greet us, but rather ordered us to put our cigarettes out.  I understand the fact that he didn't want us to smoke on the property but it was the asshole tone that he had , and the way he said it. So we put them out as he asked , and as I was already picking them up , he again in his asshole tone ordered me to put the cigarette butts in the outhouse. You would think when you are paying almost 300 dollars for a ride , you wouldn't be treated like this.  But we still gave it a chance.  A few moments after that he told us to come put our helmets on.  We thought he was joking but he certainly wasn't.  We already have to sign a waiver for liability so I think helmets should be an option. We are both very experienced riders and I wasn't expecting to have to wear helmets on such an adventurous type of ride.  I would have been able to look past the helmet thing but it was his relentless rudeness that turned me off of the whole idea.  I took a moment to call my boyfriend and ask him if he thought we should go or not and while I was on the phone he yelled to me "are you coming riding or not" in that smart ass tone.  It was clear he had better things to do and didn't care for customer service.  My dad and I looked at eachother and decided to get the hell out of there.  THANKS CHARLIE FOR CRUSHING OUR DREAMS OF RIDING ON THE BEACH AND HAVING A GOOD TIME. I'm sure glad you showed your true colors in the first few moments of meeting us, because I would hate to be stuck with that asshole for 2 hours.  Even though he was the way he was , we said thanks anyway and headed out.  Also he has something against Texans because he said "you texans" . I'm sorry your life is so miserable that you have to ruin other people's days.  If you are looking for a laid back ride this is not the place. He even texted us afterward saying how we missed out on the ride of a lifetime, I on the other hand think we dodged a bullet. Don't waste your time or money here.  By the way I live in California so I'm not a "texan" lol and if you had been nice I would have come back and given you more business but I have nothing positive to say about this experience.  Don't do it.

My boyfriend booked a private 1 1/2hr ride for us. We started at the ranch and went across the freeway where we rode along the shore. It was so beautiful. The horse that I was on was very calm but I forgot her name. Charlie, our tour guide, was funny and super sweet. It turns out he and my boyfriend had this secret the whole time- a special proposal. We got engaged while we stopped to take a break half way through. It was emotional and beautiful. I had a wonderful experience and I highly recommend this company.

We had a great time ridding on the beach at Sunset. Poco was a awesome guide. The horses were clean and well taken care of. The view on the beach was beautiful. Highly recommended this ride and I would ask for Poco. He was so nice and had so much to tell us about the area during our ride.

There's nothing quite like riding on the beach at sunset followed by a trot through the lemon groves with Charlie at the lead.  The setting is unbeatable, the horses are very well-trained and Charlie makes the ride a lot of fun.

So much fun! Scarlett the horse was awesome. Loves her! Riding on the beach was so peaceful and fun. Will do again if I am ever back in the area. Wine tasting afterward was also great. Recommend doing the sunset ride.  We called the day of and there happened to be a cancellation and could fit us in. Of course we were not prepared to ride that day and had only flip flops. They were very accommodating in providing so stable boots for us.  Wish I could do this everyday.

I have been horse back riding in various locations throughout the world, and this was by far the most beautiful, most scenic ride that I have ever taken. Charlie was amazing! He was very knowledgeable and ensured that I was safe on my horse at all times. He also took the time to snap a couple of photos of me riding my horse on the beach which was unexpected and very much appreciated. And every person on the beach seemed to know Charlie and would say hello in passing. On our way to the trails, even the passerby's in their vehicles would wave in recognition of Charlie. To me, that clearly illustrates his great character. If you've always wanted to ride in the surf on a beautiful beach with a helpful and experienced guide, look no further than Santa Barbara Beach Horseback Rides. You will not regret it!

I had the best experience! My boyfriend surprised me with this for my birthday and they sure made it an amazing experience! I love riding horses and I have always wanted to ride horses on the beach and IT WAS BEAUTIFUL!! We walked down to the beach and when we got there, we trotted and galloped a bit back and fourth. Our guide took great photos of me and my boyfriend. And then took us back a VERY beautiful way. She said it would be able 90 mins but it turned into about 2 hours which was AWESOME. If you love horses, want to ride on the beach or even surprise your gf/wife with something amazing, I recommend this place for sure!! THE BEST

Fabulous time and lovely people! The best place to experience riding on the beach. Our guide, the owner himself, was so friendly, and FUN! 

The horses are well taken care off, all around a happy experience. Corinna and niece Elisabeth.

Charlie was awesome couldn't have went any better. My girlfriend and I both loved it. I promise you that you won't be dissapointed. The ride through the beach was something special for my girlfriend and I. If your contemplating whether or not to go because you've never riden a horse don't hesitate. I've never riden and Charlie made it very simple and I felt safe at all times. Thanks Charlie for helping out with the special memory.  I would recommend this to anyone with a special someone they will love it.

Just got back from our awesome ride along the beach and through the hillside. Charlie was a great guide for us begginers and made us feel really comfortable. He was happy to take our pictures riding alongside each other. I would definitely recommend the private tour option to any friend. Thanks again Charlie!

We had a great time! I was a first timer so it was nice to be lead for a little bit on the initial trail and then once we hit the beach, I was out on my own. My partner, who has had previous experience really enjoyed being able to open up the horses on the beach. It was a mix of skilled horses from racing, polo and jumping to trail horses; great variety for varying skill levels of riders. The sunset ride has an amazing view! Definitely plan on being back...even talked about some jumping lessons! :)

My wife and I recently had the opportunity for a morning horse ride on the beach when we were visiting SB for our anniversary and it was a lot of fun! It's a nice tour of a very beautiful area of California and we had a lot of fun talking with our tour guide, Charlie. 

I've only been on horses a few times, but my wife used to ride when she was younger. We both felt totally comfortable and had a great time. We even got to canter on the beach, which made my wife really happy. 

After spending 4 days in SB for our anniversary, my wife says this was the highlight of her trip! We will definitely go back again in the future.

When we were about to book this tour, we hesitated a bit.
Turnout, the tour was great.

Even though the weather was a bit cloudy with no sunset.
We still end up with a beautiful ride on the beach with lights coming through the clouds. Booked for a 90 minutes ride, but we got a total of two hours ride, which really worth more than for what we paid for.

Charlie is a great tour guide. 
He not only taught the horses, but he will teach you how to handle your horse. Out of everything he said, some people might not like it, but I found him very professional. 

If you every look for something to do near Santa Barbara.
You should try to book this tour.

My husband and I had a beautiful ride on the beach with Charlie.  We are both fairly experienced, but, I was suprised we were riding to the beach.  The horses were great.  Charlie got us in position to move together and quickly across the street and overpass.  We trotted and cantered on the beach, and it was a beautiful evening.  We took a different way back to the stables, through a lovely garden.  The horses were perfect, and Charlie was wonderful.  We had a great time!

Took my family for our family vacation. My husband along with our two daughters 17 and 21 had never ridden before. They had an amazing time. My daughter's said it was the best vacation ever! They are still talking about it as we drive home. Thank you to Charlie our guide for being hilarious, knowledgeable and helpful. I recommend this beautiful ocean ride to everyone! We'll be back for sure :)

Horseback riding on the beach has been on my bucket list for a while. :)  My husband and I were staying in SB for a few days and decided to give SB Beach Horseback Rides a try and we are glad we did.  Our ride was on Friday, May 13th (chuckle).  We arrived and were greeted warmly by the owner.  He asked our experience and I advised him that my family owned horses and that I rode for over a decade, but not in the last 28 years.  He put me on a horse named Bachi.  He gave me a crop and told me to make sure she doesn't eat a bunch of weeds on the ride.  :)  Well, she tried!  My husband is not an experience rider, so he was put on a very gentle bay named Lucky.  The ride was 90 minutes long and took us over the freeway and down to the beach.  The freeway didn't even phase the horses, so don't worry about that.  On the beach we were told that we could hold the horse back and then canter to catch up with the rest of the riders.  I was able to get Bachi to trot several times, but no canter.  I figure it was the end of the day and she might be tuckered out.  No worries though, I had a great time!  It's so amazingly cool to ride along the shore and the sunset was gorgeous!  Our guide, Paco, took our cell phone and took a whole bunch of pictures of us.  I totally appreciate this and now we have great pictures of this fun time!

Amazing time - truly magical and romantic sunset beach ride with the love of my life who then proposed to me later that night. The horses were beautiful and sweet and the scenery breathtaking. I never wanted to leave. The beach is very protected and special and our guide was really experienced and personable. He made us feel safe. I've since been back and brought friends. Great for coupes or friends! Thanks!
A lovely ride from the stable area to the beach and back. Paco was our guide. The horses did some trotting and cantering on the beach which was fun. Great experience. Would recommend.

We were referred by Ian at the Canary Hotel.  We got in contact with Bob and he gave us some dates/times and recommended a certain day at sunset as it would just be my husband & I.  He sent us a detailed e-mail with directions.

I've never been horseback riding so my husband was excited to have my first time be on the beach at sunset...so beautiful!!  He's had plenty of riding experience but hasn't done so in quite a few years.  We didn't get to meet Charlie but there was another guy who took care of us that day (for the life of us, we cannot remember his name) but he was really knowledgeable and friendly.  Some reviewers mention a dangerous overpass and it's true, you do have to go over it to get to the beach but even someone like me who was scared to even get on a horse had no problem with that part of the trail...I didn't even think twice to be honest.  Once on the beach, it was simply beautiful and quiet.  The only "negative" thing that could not be helped was that my husband's horse, Duke, and my horse, Scarlet, could not walk side by side!!  Scarlet always stayed behind Duke which meant I couldn't hang out with my hubby on our honeymoon horseback ride haha!  At one point, we turned around putting Scarlet in front of Duke & he did not like that & he tried to bite her.  My horse jumped sideways and it scared the crap out of me!  Of course, this could not be helped, they're animals!!  We laughed about it later on and I wasn't scared the entire time.  We just made sure I stayed behind Duke & his ego haha.  Ride was supposed to be 90 minutes but we were easily out there for 2 hours.  Our guide wasn't in a rush and neither were we.  

We got to feed the horses when we got back which was fun.  It was such a great experience for both my husband & I and we'll hopefully do this again the next time we're in SB!

We went out at sunset with Charlie and had a great time. There was 4 of us including an 8 and 10 year old. Charlie led the horse of the 8 year old but once on the beach, he let her ride freely. It was an experience that she will never forget. It was the highlight of their trip.
Very romantic to ride the horses on the beach. A definite bucket item to check off. Staff was very helpful and friendly.

This was an amazing experience. It was my husband and my first time away for a long weekend--and left the highschooler sons home alone---back in Chicago! We needed to chill and connect, and this ride along the beach helped make all that happen. Thank you Charlie for an amazing experience. We will never forget it!

My boyfriend and I were both really excited for this ride and it was even better than we had hoped for! Charlie was a great guide. The horses were awesome! The views were breathtaking, we did the bluff coastal ride. Charlie allowed us to ride at our level, a little more advanced, and this allowed for an extremely fun time! We were the last ride of the day so he even extended our time a bit. I was on cloud nine at the end of the ride which caused me to forget to tip our amazing guide! We will definitely be returning to try out the beach ride and to tip our guide!!

Charlie!!! What more can I say! Between Bobs amazing customer service on the phone and Charlie's awesome sense of humor we had a BLAST! We went as two couples and met Charlie he was an absolute gentleman! He will make sure you are safe and he takes that seriously! You couldn't ask for more in an instructor! I can't say enough about this place, AWESOME experience! We will be back!!!!

A wonderful experience in such an amazing setting -- Santa Barbara really does have it all. We enjoyed taking the horses down to the beach where we enjoyed the waves and sun and Charlie took us back through the lemon orchards where we had incredible views of Summerland and Santa Barbara. I would HIGHLY recommend this to anyone who is looking to try something different than your typical horseback ride! Looking forward to doing this again soon!

Loved the location. Had an experienced trail leader, I'd go around sunset because he will take you to the beach and it's absolutely beautiful. Straight out of a movie scene. Its about 125 a person but worth every minute.

Took a two hour tour while driving back to Cali from Alaska, it was Amazing! Horses were well taken care of, but mine had little bit attitude (he won't go near the ocean), definitely worth the time and money!

Scheduling was easy with Bob & Nedra; paid a bit extra for a private ride with Charlie and it was well worth it. Charlie was very in tune with our abilities and paired us with the right horses. It was several years since my last ride, but Charlie's guidance felt more like an instructor than just a guide which I really appreciated as I was able to learn some new techniques.
During the ride Charlie and I clicked and had many laughs, interspliced with some local knowledge about the plant- and wildlife.
The beach path was also great and I even got to bond with Hayes (wife rode Dakota) upon our return by feeding him some carrots.
Will look up Charlie and crew again next time we're in town.

This was one of the most amazing experiences! We surprised our daughter with the sunset beach ride. I had never even been on a horse and this was excellent for a first timer or beginner. The horses were beautiful and Erica our guide carefully explained everything to us. She was patient and kind and very knowledgeable. This is worth every penny! Don't miss out on this incredible experience!

Booked a ride for my boyfriends birthday and it was a success. The horses are beautiful and well trained, the beach is breath taking and our guide made the entire ride enjoyable. Paco (our guide) was friendly, patient, fun and had loads of cool facts about the area. We had a great time.
Beautiful beach ride today!  Gorgeous weather and great horses.  Thanks Charlie for a great day.
One of the best trail riding businesses in California, can't wait to enjoy more rides this summer! Sweet and well cared for horses. Comfy tack and nice views. Doesn't get any better .

Charlie was a great tour guy, Very funny Guy and pointed out things as we went with the tour. If you're visiting Santa Barbara make sure you don't miss the horseback riding with Charlie, being able to ride a horse at the beach was just incredible and watching the sunset it takes you to another world.
Thank you Charlie.

My husband and I did the sunset ride and had a wonderful time it was the highlight of out trip. Our guide Paco was very patient with me as I have little to no experience, he made me feel very comfortable. The ride down to the beach was great and the sunset ride was spectacular. I cannot say enough great things about this!

WOW! I love this place! Paco was our guide and he was so cool!! My boyfriend surprised me with a horseback ride on the beach for my birthday. It was everything I could ever ask for! So much fun and such a thrill going through the water with the horses. I got to ride Scout! He was so sweet and calm, an absolute joy to ride. 

All of the horses on the ranch look very well taken care of and seem happy. That's something I always look into going to places like this. They appeared to be well fed and groomed regularly - not that I'm a horse expert or anything, haha. There's also mini ponies on site that are super friendly and allow you to play with and pet them! I thoroughly enjoyed that after the ride. They were so cute!

I would love to come here again! Thanks for the amazing ride, Paco!
Beautiful views that were beyond expectations. Pricey but almost worth it. A little hard if you are a first time rider as there isn't much training and explanation

Loved, loved, loved. Would definitely come again. Nice ride through the winery followed by amazing views of Santa Ynez. Lucas and Emily were great riding coaches considering this was me and my husbands first time. Great wine also!

We had a beautiful sunset beach ride.  Truly unique and a spectacular sunset. 
FYI This is not for people wanting to "ride" ie cantor, gallop etc. but is for people 
highly recommend this wonderful experience
Gorgeous ride down to the beach. Charlie knew what he was doing and made sure we did too. Checking riding on the beach off my bucketlist!
Our family rode the bluffs over Memorial Day Weekend with Charlie as our guide. The ride was beautiful and it was obvious the horses are VERY well cared for. I would strongly recommend these guys!

My daughter and I went on the beach trail.  We had a fabulous time. My daughter rides/jumps horses for the past 7 years, so she is has a lot of experience with horses, so we were able to canter on the beach.   She will never forget that experience.  I would highly recommend!!

Our group of 4 had either not ridden at all or not ridden for many, many years.  Charlie got us all set up and perfectly matched us with our horses.  Hanna took us on the 1-hour bluffs ride which was beautiful!  Very scenic views!  We really appreciated their care and cautiousness for our skill levels.  The ranch is located on a gorgeous area.  We would definitely come back again.  Thank you Charlie & Hanna!

It was the highlight of my vacation. At first my family and I were concerned because we typically do not go horseback riding, but we were pleasantly surprised! The trail was breathtaking and the manager Charlie was very entertaining and helpful. If you are in Santa Barbara and looking to horseback I highly recommend doing it through this company.

Had such a blast on our 60 minute trail ride! Scout and Sprinkles were amazing on the trail. Nice and scenic along the mountain as we couldn't do the beach because the tide was high. The houses along the trail were super beautiful and the our guide Paco was excellent as well. Will be back for more horse back riding fun.
Male tour guide was pushy and rude, which put our big group on edge. The  Woman was nice. Would have enjoyed the ride better if there was a tour guide that wasn't so negative. Ride itself was great .

Since Bob accused me of being a fake yelp reviewer, I must update and reiterate. 

Bob and Charlie are a couple of creeps! 

Charlie is a really weird guy and he made me and my girlfriend really uncomfortable. 

It was a really boring trail ride with trail horses. Bob said they were real horses. Not true, they were followers. We had to follow Charlie. :-( Going for a ride with Charlie sucked. It was not fun.  It was creepy.

Bob, you need to learn some humility. Had you responded properly to my review, I might have responded differently, but obviously my review was dead on.  You are the fake. You got my money, but not my review.  I bet you two are a couple of ex-cons. You sure seem like con artists. 

Learn how to respond to a negative review, because you are all wrong.  

Good luck. Tell your little cronies to stop messaging me accusing me of writing a fake review. Yelp keeps businesses accountable. It is the great equalizer. 

Why on earth would someone write a fake review for a horse rental service? Paranoia will destroya.
Really recommend this when visiting Santa Barbara. Paco was our guide and he is excellent. We were really nervous but he assured us we would enjoy it after 20 minutes. He is a great guide.

We went to Santa Barbara for a weekend recently, and horseback riding was on the wife's bucket list. Charlie was a good trail guide, and we enjoyed the ride. It's a beautiful trip through backroads, and some freeway passes, to a nice small beach where the scenery is just gorgeous. My horse bit two others in the rear before we left. He had some personality. Good times!

Ride, trail, and scenery is everything! However, Charlie (the ride manager and owner) is rude, unprofessional, and very temperamental! My horse was moving smaller than the other horses and when I told him I was hitting the horse with my whip and my feet...he proceeds to shout at me saying, "you don't know what you're doing." Mmmmm I know I don't. I never said I did. He then begins to argue with me. He's like "don't tell me what to do". How unprofessional and childish. Long story short...he ended up apologizing but unfortunately the damage was already done. Again, I enjoyed the scenery and experience but I'm never going to this establishment. 

I'm editing this because I forgot to mention, Charlie threaten me during the ride. In the midst of him arguing with me, he says, "I will make you walk back home if you don't stop." Who the heck does he think he is. Even my father never speaks me like that. Charlie seriously needs attend anger management.

I took my kids on a horseback ride for a holiday get together. The horses were incredibly calm, dependable, yet we got to trot and have a lot of fun. The views were absolutely amazing. We saw the ocean, and went through beautiful agricultural ranches, and amazing topography through our two hour ride. I had no idea there were so manyEcological diverse environments that we saw on this ride. Charlie and his staff were wonderful! What a joyful experience, never to be forgotten by me and my millennial children!

Excellent ! My husband and I had a fantastic time . We loved our guide , Mason , and our great horses Scout and Duke . The beach at sunset was beautiful and peaceful ! We did this as a private ride for my birthday.  I ride regularly at home , but not on a beach , so this was special. I believe every level of rider will enjoy this experience. I highly recommend this company. ! AAA+++

It is a good ride but if you have previous extensive riding experience don't tell them cause they'll give you a horse that needs training and spend the whole time telling you how to school the horse instead of you being able to actually enjoy the ride.

Rode with family last night. We had a great time with Paco guiding the way. He was a wealth of knowledge about the area and we learned a little about Polo as well.  We made reservations and everything was ready when we showed up. The ride was beautiful once we got across the highway. It doesn't get much better than beautiful weather, gorgeous sunsets, horseback riding on the beach and friendly people.

This is a merchant without human nature, do not care about the safety of the customer's life, the customer immediately fell from the horse back, he completely ignored, but also scolded the customer, please do not choose this, otherwise you do not know how to die, for your safety considerations, far-sighted this store.

A friend of mine came to visit and we decided to explore Santa Barbara. We are both outdoorsy, athletic types, so when we came across this listing, we knew we had to do it!
We had a nice ride for 90 minutes, and that was plenty for someone like me who's never been on a horse before. Seriously.
I had a mare named Scarlet who was on the slower side, which was good for me, and there were four of us with the guide/trainer, Charlie, who was talkative and gave strong direction. 
The beach was quiet and beautiful. However, my favourite part of the ride was on the way back through the lemon grove. Wow, that was absolutely gorgeous. 
Getting to and from the beach is a little bit unnerving, as you have to cross some of the busier roads to do so. But keep in mind this is a two-exit town, so the roads are not like crossing the PCH.
By the end, you'll be pretty sore if you've never been for a ride before.
Ask about the two-for-one wine tasting for the winery down the road. My friend and I got a coupon for it because we asked, and we loved the rose we tried.

I rode out with Charlie, the proprietor, this morning. It was a perfectly beautiful morning. My horse, Scarlet, is a well trained and strong quarter horse. We rode all along the manicured trails next to creeks and fields, estates, orchards. It was a wonderful hour and a half. We had a great time. Horses are well-kept and willing. Go -- you'll be glad you did!

What I love about this business is the review that is particularly cognizant of the care of the horses. Always be concerned first with how the horses are cared for. As a remote healer of horses, I whole-heartedly concur.
Are these horses happy because of the gratitude rained upon them?

If I could add more stars I honestly would. I own an animal care company in LA as well as having owned my own horse. This was hands down the BEST horseback ride I've ever had.
My friend was also an experienced rider and we had Erica as our guide. She is amazing and really got to know our comfort/experience level. We were scheduled for a group ride but ended up just being the three of us. This was also the first time I was back on a horse after a hip replacement (after a horse injury) so this was a big deal. The horses she brought us were gentle and well trained however, they could move! Not barn sour "tourist" horses.
Riding through lemon tree groves, beautiful CA countryside and ending up on the beach-simply the best!
This was money well spent for a very special memory. Even went to the Summerland Winery to enjoy the two for one wine tasting coupon you receive when you book your ride. Excellent wine and a terrific ending for this horse lovers afternoon.
Thank you Erica for going above and beyond!!

This was definitely a great experience especially if you want to have a "relaxing" adventure.  I enjoyed the scenery, the routes we took, and how simple is was. However, I wish our guide interacted with us more. There were 6 of us total and 2 of them were experienced yet he had the experienced ones right by him while the beginners (like me) were all the way in the back, not getting any guidance. I didn't know what was going on and were just following where the horses were going. I definitely had a complicated and stubborn horse and he even told me that my horse was the slowest but he never mentioned how moody it was! I was told to just kick the belly to make it go faster but when I would do that, it still wouldn't work. It seems that the horse would get mad at me instead and try to look at me. In the end, going back to the ranch, my horse even tried to get rid of me by walking so close to the trees so that I would hit it and FALL. It tried to do that to me 5 times! I ended up having bruises on my knees and legs from trying to stop it. I screamed & asked for help and the guide would just look at me and say I'm fine. I didn't feel safe and was honestly scared to death. 

It's sad that the beautiful experience I was hoping turned out to be a scary one..I really wished I had a great experience as well based on what I've seen on the other reviews but instead I had a complicated time and felt anxious and scared...

Tried to reach Bob three times via text to get information in a ride.   He asked me to send him my name and email, which I did.   Three times!  Never heard anything back.   That's ok,  Bob.  I made other plans!  Thanks.

Our bachelorette party of 7 booked a ride on a Saturday for 4:30. We had a great time and the price seemed reasonable for 2 hours (a little under $120/pp). 

Many in our group had no experience and our guide Paco was patient with all of us and able to make us feel comfortable on our horses.
