The views were pretty epic and I enjoyed riding my horse Sprinkles. The tide was high at the beach, so we didn't spend tons of time there, but it was a pretty nice ride down and we rode on the shore for quite some time. Paco our guide even was kind enough to take some pictures of the group. We also rode through what looked like part of a vineyard. The scenery was really green and looked a bit rustic. Also, there was a lot of pretty flowers and trees on our ride.

Overall, if you are in Santa Barbara, I totally recommend booking a ride here if you want a unique experience and something fun to do in SB.

After the stay at home orders had loosened up, we decided to make a quick trip to Santa Barbara. We wanted to do outdoor activities only and horseback riding fit the bill. We have been horseback riding before but never on the beach. Looked on Yelp and saw this company. I emailed Bob and he promptly responded. Made a reservation and gave my credit card information for payment. Showed up at the stables and everything went smoothly. Erica was our guide and she was great! She pointed out areas of interest, and gave us recommendations on where to go afterwards with our dog. Getting to the beach did take a while, but it was worth it because horseback riding on the beach was awesome! No one was on the beach when we got there so it felt like we had our own private beach. We had a memorable time with great pictures and videos.

The ride was nothing as we expected. Our tour guide was very unhelpful when some of the horses were acting up. My father ended up falling off a horse and we had to take him to Urgent Care right after. The beach view is amazing, but I would have loved a better tour guide and more sympathy. We are not new horse riders either. Wouldn't go back...

Overall the experience felt lacking. From start to finish, the process seemed far too lax and lacked the kind of structure that you'd expect when paying this much. I texted Bob for availability, no response. I tried via email and finally got through only to find out that I would need to send my credit card info via email in order to book. I was a little paranoid about sending this information in such a non-secure but did so anyways. 

Following that exchange, I didn't receive any information about how/when I would be charged. This led me to believe that I would be charged in person, either before or after the trip. However, I ended up being charged the day before the trip, with a merchant name that was only partial appearing as "Santa Barbara Beach Ho." This lead me to believe I might have been scammed. It was only after throughly checking ally my purchases during my stay in SB that I put the pieces together. The charge eventually cleared and appeared as "SANTA BARBARA BEACH HORSESANTA YNEZ CA". Some clear communication about payment would've saved me a lot of worry/hassle. 

As far as the trip goes, it was nice, peaceful, but somewhat boring as the horses travel more slowly than I'd anticipated. Paco took the lead and we followed but didn't really seem interested in engaging the tour members. He would occasionally turn back at us and give a one or two liner fact about the area we were in. But the people in the back were likely too far to hear. Im not sure if this is intentional but the disconnect detracted from my experience personally.

For whatever reason, my girlfriend was paired with a lazy horse that proved problematic throughout the ride. The horse refused to keep up pace and had to be kicked repeatedly throughout the trip. It's hard to look up and enjoy the views when you can't even get your horse to move. We clearly stated that we had never ridden before but were paired this way anyways. Im sure there is some reason why this paring was chosen but I can only comment on what I know.

Overall, the ride was very scenic and peaceful and Is definitely worth checking out. I would recommend it to friends without hesitation but I think there is definitely a lot of room for improvement.

What a wonderful experience. This was truly one of the top things on my bucket list. I loved every second. My guide was awesome! This is certainly a must do. I will definitely be going back to ride again with them!

Been attempting to contact for availability for reservation text, email and phone with no response. 
Disappointing from friends referral.

If I could give negative 5 stars I would.  There were no signs posted for no smoking and when we were told to put them out we simply did that so we did follow directions. My "profane review" only was such because the behavior of your employee charlie warranted such a word. We were not refused service or asked to leave , we left on our own terms because we did not want to promote your unprofessional business or have to deal with your rude employees.  Your employee was rude from the moment he opened his mouth, and you are a liar because we were never asked to leave, we left because we didn't want to be there.  Furthermore proving my point that your business sucks. I will make sure to let all my local friends in Ventura , Ojai, Santa Barbara and Los Angeles areas know to never promote you or your corrupt company . Charlie was the obnoxious one , not us.

Horses were great and the beach ride was awesome! The only complaint is that Charlie the manager came off with a bit of a pretentious attitude when I asked for an "equestrian saddle". He was a bit rude in correcting me abruptly saying it was called an "English saddle" and then gave me a condescending lecture on the proper terms! At another ranch, I asked for an "Equestrian saddle" and they gave it to me no problem. If he had just communicated to me, that they do not have one, instead of shaming and mocking me on what was the correct term of the saddle, that would have been fine! I felt his behavior to be immature and disrespectful.

Took my wife horseback riding for her birthday... something we had not done in decades so I was little terrified but ended it up being a blast and having lots of fun!  

We were scheduled for a Sat 1pm ride and I had checked how long it would take to get there in the morning but by the time we headed out, there was a lot of unexpected heavy traffic which would put us about 15 min late. I texted then spoke to Charlie, the manager, to give him a heads up and he was very accommodating... Part of the group had gone ahead as scheduled but the other part of the group was also running late so they got us a separate guide with Tori which all worked out great!  (FYI - Charlie is direct and has dry humor which we found hilarious so don't be put off... He's really a nice guy!) 

My wife rode Koda and I was on Haze. Haze is a great horse who was kept at the head of the pack as he was fast and liked to run and gallop ahead of the rest which after I figured him out, was a lot of fun! Koda was a little slower than my horse and a bit stubborn so you have to be a little more assertive which my wife had no trouble with and had fun on too. So just go into it knowing that each horse has its own personality and character traits.

Our guide was Tori who's an expert rider with many years experience riding horses and knows how to handle any situation. Her own horse was jittery and got spooked a lot but she handled it like a pro which was amazing to watch. We really liked her; Tori's very knowledgeable and she answered all my questions as we were riding.

The ride was beautiful! My wife especially liked the ride down the road from the stables as it was very scenic and peaceful. And I enjoyed the ride along Loon Point Beach. 

If you'd like to do something fun for a special occasion, I highly recommend these guys! They'll take good care of you!

I went on the beach ride with Paco today and it was an amazing experience.  I highly recommend this experience!  It is beautiful, the horses are wonderful, Paco was extremely friendly and knowledgeable and made the experience very special.  I have only ridden horses one other time ten years ago and he made me feel very comfortable and safe.  We trotted on the beach, rode on beautiful trails, he took wonderful pictures and we saw lots of amazing birds (a huge blue heron), property, and plants.  If you are thinking of booking this trip - definitely do so.  Worth every penny.  Also felt very safe with how they handled things with COVID.  I hope to get back and do it again soon.  I loved my horse Tommy!

Just finished a fabulous ride with Rudy. My group had all beginner riders and Rudy was not only a great instructor, but also was made us all feel welcome and relaxed. We will certainly come back again the next time we are in the area!

Great experience on a beautiful day to walk from the ranch to the beach. Almost chose a different place due to price but it was well worth it. Mason was very helpful and patient with riders of varying experiences. 10/10 would recommend.

The sunset beach ride is a must! This was a bucket list item for me and an anniversary experience for my significant other and I. This ride was one of the highlights of our anniversary trip. I am an inexperienced rider and this was only my second time on a horse. The tour guide Paco was patient and understanding with my nervousness and inexperience. Customer service is above and beyond with this company. I can't wait to go on more rides with them!

We had an awesome 90 min. trail ride with Rudy. It was my first time on a horse and he made me feel so comfortable. He made great conversation and is super knowledgeable about his horses. It was a wonderful trail with an amazing view. Great way to get out and spend our anniversary during Covid-19.

My friend and I were visiting California for the first time. We decided to book a beach horseback riding experience with Santa Barbara Beach Horseback Rides. Unfortunately, we paid a lot of money to go on this ride and left feeling confused and, frankly, disappointed with the ride. 

When we arrived at the ranch, we met Charlie. At first, he seemed friendly. But, very quickly, we realized that he was loud and rude. We told Charlie from the beginning that we were novice riders. He understood this. Yet, we felt like we were led on an intermediate, if not advanced, ride. We were not ready for this and Charlie was not a patient guide. He yelled at me in front of the other riders because I would not hit my horse with a crop riding whip. I did not feel comfortable hitting a horse that hard and it would have been nice to have the instructor show me how to do this. Instead he said, and I quote, "You've tried to hit the horse four times and now you've annoyed me and you've annoyed the horse. You better hit that horse because if you don't it's going to be dangerous for you when we start crossing the highway." Mind you that as novice riders, we were told that we would need to cross a busy highway on horses to get to the beach for the ride. Appalling. 

Charlie was at the front of the line on his horse and barely looked back at us riders. At one point on the beach, my friend's horse wouldn't move and we had to call Charlie for help. She had to beg him to put her horse near his because she was nervous to get back to our location on the ranch. 

I would not recommend this ranch. The beach ride was beautiful but we couldn't enjoy it because of the instructor. There was another woman there named Judy. She should take over as the riding instructor. She seemed much more friendly than Charlie. A note of advice to management: Have patience with customers. We are not all experienced riders. We are paying for you to help us and show us the way. You should remember this and make changes immediately.

I was bucked and fell off Scout during a sunset beach ride where the horses got spooked (I was the second horse going up the hill away from the beach) and several horses went galloping and I fell into the beach right at the water line.  I know that you ride at your own risk, blah blah, but it's not the horse throwing me off that was the worst of it.  It's the way Paco, the lead guy handled it.  He never got off his horse, asked me how I was or assisted in any way.  He left me and my teenage daughter alone in the beach while he took the other three riders back to the stable, without a cell phone (which was trampled by the horses when I fell) and told me that Charlie (supposedly the owner) would come and pick us up.  We waited 35 minutes, until it was almost dark on an isolated beach, with me limping and hands bloody.  He drove us back to my car, never apologized, asked if I needed anything, nothing.  The next day, still nothing.  Not their fault that the horses got spooked, and I fell, but how they completely did not care afterwards is really awful.  Still hurting a week later and grateful I didn't break anything.

I found them on Yelp.

I had two childhood friends come out for a visit and we decided to have an adventurous weekend. I've been on a few trail rides around town, but my friends wanted to opportunity to trot and canter. Bob clarified that if we could prove our riding skills, we would be able to have the experience we wanted, and he was right! 

Charlie led us on an action-packed ride through beautiful Summerland down to the beach and through the orchards and expertly coached us through trotting and cantering safely on the ride. I've been on trail rides where you hardly walk and on a horrific ride out of state with an irresponsible leader where I was bucked off, and this ride was the perfect mix of adventure and safety bearing in mind our riding skills. After our ride, we were lucky enough to have some cuddle time with a 3 week old pony and her parents. It was seriously everything I could have hoped for and more and an experience we'll be talking about for years.

Charlie took tons of great photos and videos with my phone and was an awesome pack leader. I had so much fun I'm going back with my mom in two weeks!

Great horseback riding experience in Santa Barbara. Went with my spouse and we both enjoyed it very much. They require all riders to wear helmets. The lead horse rider has been riding for basically his whole life and he is very knowledgeable. We are somewhat experienced in horseback riding but if you have questions or need help he'll be happy to help you. We didn't just walk either, we trotted & cantered. He said he bases the speeds off of age and experience of the riders. You will ride through the valley, up hills, near the road, through lemon tree fields and have an opportunity to see the ocean from a mountain if you choose to do the "bluff" ride. He said the best time of year is in the spring, but all times are beautiful.

I would recommend the ride due to the great views and how unbelievable riding a horse on the beach is. However, if you book, I would STRONGLY suggest that you ask for any guide other than Paco. Instead of going into every single detail of how inappropriate, rude, disrespectful, and uncomfortable he made me and the person I was with feel, I will just state a couple. It's best to say that he needs to work on his communication skills and patience. There was not a single person in my group that was an avid horse rider including myself. I think it is important for everyone at the facility, especially Paco, to remember that we are not professionals and we are nervous. Yes, we did sign up for the experience. But who would not be slightly nervous to ride a horse on a trail, highway, and beach? Speaking for me specifically, my horse was not happy that day which is understandable and I was having a hard time controlling her. However, when my horse started to act up at any time, Paco decided to escalate the situation with yelling and harsh tones. I felt every time I was more calm than him because I had heard that horses need you to be calm because they pick up on your energy. Of course it made me even more nervous on how upset he would get because I thought If he's getting that nervous about it then is something bad going to happen like I get bucked off? If it wasn't the yelling then it was rude comments, like before we even left the facility and I mounted the horse, he said "you got on way too hard, you need to remember that these are living and breathing animals."  I don't see how I got on hard because I literally got on just like the person I was with but if I did there is a way you can not be condescending and kindly correct me so I'll know what to do in the future. But when the man behind me went to mount the horse, he struggled and almost fell and pulled all on the horse and all Paco said was, woah let's try that again." When we got back and my horse started acting up and I pulled her back he came up to me aggressively and looked me in the eyes and yelled "why would you do that?" Umm.. maybe because you gave me no instruction on what to do and my horse was charging another horse so I didn't know what to do? The whole time this situation happened he was just yelling and I just froze because he got me so nervous and looking back I'm lucky that my horse just stopped and calmed herself down because the man behind me on the horse was yelling because he was scared then Paco was just yelling and here I am, barely ever ridden before, on top of a horse charging another and no one is giving me any instruction. I still do not know what to do if that situation ever occurs to me again because Like I said, Paco never told me what to do or the man behind me who was being charged. Basically in any situation where a horse starts to act up whether it was mine or the other lady that was with us had a temperamental horse, Paco decides to just yell at you. It's like he can't stay calm or think clearly enough on what to do. 

Also, while I was waiting to mount my horse and Paco was checking the saddle, he and a couple other workers decided to discuss right in front of me on how the group before us did not tip. The young girl who did not receive a tip was not upset and actually tried to avoid the conversation but he and another lady were bad mouthing the group that was literally there just 5 minutes ago and who were very nice to them when they left. I understand not getting a tip is frustrating but to talk about that in front of a customer and bad mouth your customers in front of me is super unprofessional and made me uncomfortable. If anyone should have been upset, it would have been the younger girl who did not receive a tip and whether she was or not she did not show it whatsoever and didn't want to talk about. 

Overall, I can tell the horses are cared for and the ride is beautiful. But just because someone is skilled in riding does not mean they are a good fit for giving tours. There has to be a balance of horse knowledge and people skills and unfortunately Paco ruined my whole experience and the person I was with.

My boyfriend and I had such a wonderful time and will be recommending this to our friends and family. We had originally planned for a beach ride, but the tide was too high so we did the scenic trail instead and we were not disappointed!  Gorgeous views, also through the lemon groves of the nearby ranch. Our horses, Tommy and Duke, were very well behaved. Our guide Mason was knowledgeable and great to ride with, although not much time was spent on initial instructions. Unfortunately another young woman in our group got bucked off, I think because her horse got too close beside another horse and freaked out. Mason handled it very well -- apologized, made sure the woman was okay, and switched horses with her. Overall, excellent experience and I would love to go back someday.

They hear it hundreds of times, "It's on my bucket list". I was one of those folks and our guide, 'Tori' and steed 'Tommy' made this part, the crown jewel of my bucket list trip! Beautiful ride, trusted horses,  and a must-do if you've ever wanted to ride the wilds or the beach in Summerland, CA (a burb of Santa Barbara). Thank you Santa Barbara Beach Rides!!!!!
Awesome experience.   Paco was a great docent.  Beautiful ride to the beach and into the estates.

Adults Avoid this boring ride. You are being warned. This is a boring trail ride. Might be fun for a very young child. 

The area where the horses are kept is infested with ticks. I had ticks all over me! 

The guy running things talked way too much. 

Money wasted. Don't do it. Find a reputable place to rent a real horse. This is like a pony ride that could not end soon enough. Lame. 


This was the most boring ride. Booooring. Could not wait for it to end. For children only. Adults Avoid.

Not a fake review.
We called around 12 and was able to book ride same day . Was a lot of fun I would recommend to any one :))

We had a wonderful time! I forget the guy's name who took us on the trail, but he did a great job guiding the tour. You go through the beach and even some lemon trees! Very fun, and romantic. Small group setting which was perfect for our honeymoon adventure. I definitely want to go back and see Sprinkles and Scout again! 

Don't forgot to tip your tour guide, and follow the rules! Some influencer type girl kept getting in trouble not listening.  ...it was kind of funny. I was entertained!! 

Absolutely beautiful view, we went during the week and it seems like no one is on that beach. The guy even took a photo of us which was really nice!!

Hopefully we will be back next year on our anniversary!

Charlie was such an awesome host; he tailored my ride for me as I have more riding experience than the group. Gave me the weekday discount too! He paired me with Erica, and she was a fun guide. We rode down to the beach and there, I was allowed to trot and canter my horse. She took pics of me on my horse, and made it a very memorable experience. I highly recommend this place, and will be back with my kids to share the beauty and fun of riding!

Santa Barbara beach horseback riding facility is like no other place on this earth . Organic farms - orchards -  rolling hills that view the ocean - 100 year old trees everywhere! Charlie (the owner) is one of a kind. My fiance had only been on a horse once before - Charlie made sure he was more then save the entire time. I took English riding lesson last year for some time and this was the most fun I have had on a horse for quite awhile! The beach ride + the ride through the amazingly gorgeous Montecito Hills was beyond!! We will most definitely be back to see Duke Lucky & of course Charlie.
Beautiful ride, a little drama with one of the horses  but overall we owe the great ride to the great guide, and my great horse!  She could have probably used some help though

My hiking partner and I hiked the Jesusita Trail to Inspiration Point. It was a Friday about 11:00 a.m. Finding the trail head was fairly easy, following the trail was fairly easy once you got into it, but we did get off the path a few times and had to ask some other hikers. Even though the trail to inspiration point via Jususita Trail is about 7 miles up and back it seemed a lot longer. A lot of going back and forth to reach the top. We must of only passed a handfull people each time concerned that we had enough water.

When you hit the ending of the Jususita trail it is like what reviews say, you go through a private property gate and continue to Inspiration Point. 

Jususita Trail is definitely a hike worth doing. The day we went, hardly any other hikers were to be seen, the hikers we saw had dogs but not bothersome as some reviewers say. No mountain bikers at all during our entire hike. Do bring wear proper shoes, and bring water.

This is the longer trail to Inspiration point, completely different scenery compared to tunnel trail. Be very careful with your footing and for those rattlesnakes because some parts of the trail next to the cliff are covered with overgrowth and hard to see. I'd wait for the cooler seasons to hike this area because of that. But anyways. Great views. Lots of steep areas. View is worth the hike.

This hike is best done towards the end of the afternoon into the evening. Unless of course you're one of those and like to torment yourself with high heat and bugs.  It is definitely less crowded in the evening. Be prepared for rocks in the trail and elevation. But it is totally worth it. View from the top was amazing!! You could see large stretches of Santa Barbara city and also the coastal waters. And there are lots of trees and vegetation to help provide much appreciated shade.

I just discovered this trail last year.  I love hiking here.  The trail follows the creek most of the way and is pretty secluded once you get far enough back.  The first mile or so of the trail is relatively flat and easy to hike.  The trail meets up with a paved road after a bit and cuts through someone's ranch before heading uphill.  The majority of the trail is shaded until you meet up with a dirt/gravel utility road just before Inspiration Point. This trail is much less crowded than Cold Springs (my favorite) so it makes for a nice alternative.  

A little way into the trail is a fork that is the beginning of the Arroyo Burro trail.  I usually stick to the Jesusita Trail, I think it's more fun.

Beware of the large number of mountain bikers on this one.

Beautiful trail that starts with plenty of tree cover offering a shady, cool hike - at least half way to the top of inspiration point. If you are heading to the point bring lots of water and a snack. The view is spectacular! One of my favorite hikes of all time. 

-Bring water for your dog.
-Bring water for yourself.
-Bring snacks.
-Leave nothing behind.

Partly shaded trail (key in this heatwave) with several different ecosystems along the way.  Nice ascent and what should be a great payoff at the top with ocean views, however not always a guarantee with smog/cloudcover. Alas, the view did not come out for us, but we did have a great time and great workout on the way to the top.  Good for dog-walkers and beginner hikers seeking a 3-4 hour hike.

My main complaint is the trailhead and path weren't that well marked and there are several offshoot paths that fooled us in the first 2 miles and had us taking several unanticipated detours and wandering onto private property.  It is nice though that one of the farms it tramps through has decided to provide a little shade and water to those who wander.

Drought is clearly present here too - no sign of a creek or stream at all in early September.

I really enjoyed this trail. It was easy to find, plenty of parking in front of the trailhead. There are a lot of trees providing shade throughout most of the way. Although it does goes thru a sunny meadow and the top is treeless. I did this around noon and the sun was not unbearable.
It took me about 3 hours to go to the top and back. But I stopped a few times including a short meditation at the top :) the views are just incredible. I have no idea if I reached inspiration point...the road at the top looked to go on for a loong time but I didn't continue on. I felt inspired enough at the point I got to lol
It was a good workout - lots of steep inclines and big steps to conquer. As well as some flat areas for trail running or casual walking to catch your breath.
Ran into quite a few other hikers as well as some bikers...and a horse! 
Must see!

A nicely kept trail good for a moderate hike and some great nature views. The stream wasn't active due to lack of rainfall but I can imagine it to flow fairly strong after a recent downpour. Lots of ancient oak. I started on Franklin Park trail and connected via the Arroyo Burro trail. For parking, I parked on San Roque and entered on the west side of the road.

The hike is easy the first half. Some small inclines but nothing hard. You pass by an avocado farm, grass field and a stream. Then you enter a nice stream which supports a cool wooded forest. Finally, you get to the private property and this is where the fun moderate second part begins. Make sure to fill your bottle. As you go in an s- shaped trail up, you will have plenty of opportunities to see the coast line. Unfortunately my girlfriend and I, were only able to reach the narrow trail before turning back because sunset was coming soon. Love this trail.

This is seriously one of the most well rounded hikes in the area. You can start this hike from Steven's Park, which is what I prefer, or from San Roque road next to the water treatment plant. If you start from the park, it is about a 7.4 mile round trip or if you start on San Roque Rd about 6.2 miles round trip. This hike has it all: a park, a stream which you cross at several points, beautiful views, aggressively inclined switchbacks, competitive distance and heck, it even throws in some horsies. Most of the crossings are easy so don't worry, you might not get wet. I enjoy the crossings and several access points where you can frolic in the water. The last stretch, about half a mile, is pretty sunny and it leads up to none other than Inspiration point. 

If you start at San Roque rd, be careful with the parking lot on the left side when you're facing the mountain because it has a tremendously high curb which will wreck your chassis.

It was a relatively easy hike to the halfway point (water fountain) but then it got a little more challenging as it became steeper.  Only one wrong turn but was able to meet up with the correct trail. It is a 4 mile hike to the top. Once we got to inspiration point we were so glad to have continued on as it was truly a gorgeous sight. However no markers so it was hard to know how much further since we did not know it was 4 miles up. Glad we went. Loved the trail.

This hike is about 5~6 miles long , if you start from the water plant facility. Like what everybody says, park by the water plant facility, its easier and you won't get towed (unless you suck at parking).  Come early if you want good parking! The trail is at the bottom (left side, away from the main road) near the water plant. It's definitely shady and lots of creek crossing. Beware, you might trip on the rocks and fall into the water. But, its soo peaceful and theres not many bugs out if you come early. We didn't see any wild animals, but i hear there is poison oak all around. We went from 8am ~ 12pm. About 4 hr hike round trip, you will cross 3-4 streams along the way and there is a uphill after you pass the gate. I definitely got a good workout and when you reach the power lines, theres this huge gap that you have to jump. Wear good shoes and bring food if you don't want to faint or starve or both. Stay hydrated! There were lots of people with their dogs running up and down this trail. Stay to the main trail, don't veer off into the wilderness, you might not like what you see. XD Anyways, the inspiration point is indeed GORGEOUS and SPECTACULAR and WORTH the 4 hr hike. It's just wow, that feeling of accomplishment and stress-relieving. who woudln't like that?!

This is a great trail and it's in really good shape right now. There are a couple flowing Creeks so just be aware of a little slippery rock crossing here and there. I love that this trail has so much shade and there's always tons of lizards running around. It's really green and lush right now. It's great that it's right in the city and it's perfect when I just want a quick hour hike after work I do the beginning of the trail in and out.

It was my first time hiking in the Santa Barbara area and I really liked this hike.  I think it could have been a 5 start hike if I had hiked it on a clear day.

Parking- we easily found parking along the side of the road on the right side near the trailhead...which is on the left side of the road

Trail- the sign at the start states that the trail is 4.2 miles with 641 ft of elevation gain...not sure what that is based on, but if you are like me and wish to take this trail to Inspiration Point and back it was 7.1 miles for me with an elevation gain of 1,600 ft...so quite a bit of difference there; the trail is easy to follow and the first 1.5 goes north and sticks close to the creek and the water was running with some recent rain; the trail then leaves the trees and heads in a NE direction through a more open sort of grassland area and you will cross a road and some private property nearby and then the incline kicks in for the final mile or so with some switchbacks up to Inspiration Point...which I didn't see any signs but I had my All Trails app to verify we had made it there and there were some other hikers there already but sadly there was no view to enjoy since it was an extremely overcast and foggy morning (4/2/18) and visibility was maybe 200 yards so there was no view of the ocean or island sadly; the hike was green, with the sound of running water for most of the time, and great views of the mountains to the northeast...I just wished we could have seen the ocean...FYI dogs are allowed on this trail off leash which doesn't bother me but might bother some

*the second half of the hike we really worked up a sweat not only because it was steeper but it was very humid and there was a light mist/drizzle

Of course bring plenty of water and hiking shoes or shoes with good traction, trekking poles are not needed for this hike.  Plan on this hike taking about 3-4 hours.  I would rate this as moderately difficult.

Favorite jogging trail in SB- There is great variety going by creeks, beautiful trees and even a meadow- Then uphill to inspiration point with beautiful views there and along the way. Think the the first parts by the creeks and large trees would be fun for kids. Never had a problem with parking at the trailhead- Highly recommend!!

An A1 hiking trail- picturesque views and clear trail markings along the way apart from where it veers off to become Inspiration Point. Good thing there was service up there otherwise we wouldn't have known we reached the peak! 
- Parking was simple. Near the water plant facility, there was a small lot right next to the trailhead, but be careful of the raised edge if your car is low to the floor! You can also park across the street in the dirt.
- Be prepared for rocks, tree roots, puddles, small stream crossings, elevation, and inclines! There was also some poison oak along the trail so be sure to wear some high socks. Bring plenty of water! 
- 3-4 hour round trip 
- Everyone was really friendly (bikers, dogs and dog walkers)

Always a great hike, 4.1 miles. Go as far as you want; you can always stop and turn back. Going up the trail or coming back it's picturesque and a great workout. Right now it is a real treat because you can hear the creek water running and bubbling over the rocks at least half of the way up. You will see lots of green and some flowers right now as well. Have good hiking shoes you do have to cross the creek a few times and the rocks can be slippery. If you go all the way to top the view if Santa Barbara is outstanding. Today we were pressed for time to beat the dark and went around 2 miles up and turned back. A little to many roots and rocks for me to hike this trail in the dark. Starting early in morning is best. See you on the trails

We hiked this trail for the first time on Feb 12, 2011. We didn't know what to expect from an 8.2 mile (rountrip) hike. The trial isn't so bad. The beginning portion is a breeze, crossing over creeks (which was very cool and don't see in San Diego) and small inclines. It's not until midway that the hike starts to pick up. There's a point in the trail where you come to about 4 BIG steps. I'm 5'1" and these steps were considered high for me! Towards the last 30 minutes of the hike up, it's ALL uphill, and the trail swirls back and forth til you reach the towers. 

It took us (a group of 4) a total of ~3 hours to complete the trail (back and fourth).

Jesusita Trail is a solid hike, about 3-4 miles round trip up to Inspiration Point depending on where you start. It's a good enough distance mile wise for beginners & enough of an incline climb for seasoned hikers to get a good workout depending on how hard they push it.

We started our hike a little on the late side around 11am during the weekend so we had to park on the side of the road about a half mile from where the road to the trail starts. The start of this trail also leads to the Tunnel Trail, for the Jesusita Route you'll want to stay to the left once the concrete road ends about a half mile into the hike. There are a few other directions the trail leads to, we took it up to inspiration point, I'll continue the experience of this hike with my review of that site next.

Multiple ways to get up here. 

 It gets hot, so bring water and snacks, its kind of a long hike, with mixed open/shade, and lots of switchbacks with uneven ground and quasi-steep grade.  

*Someone should really make a zipline happen, that shoots back to Santa Barbara.  

Refresher on trail etiquette:
http://blog.rei.com/hike/trail-etiquette-who-has-the-right-of-way/

This is a local well kept trail! Once we got closer to "Inspiration Point", we were definitely feeling the burn having passed all the switch backs. There is enough shade in the beginning of the trail, but towards the end, it can be treacherous. Be sure to stay hydrated and be prepared for this to take about 4-5 hours. Happy Trails!
This trail was nice.  Some good incline but nothing too crazy. Follow the trail because there are some private property that could mislead you in the wrong direction.

We  hiked from the trailhead just north of the Cater Water Treatment Plant up to Inspiration Point and back.  Total mileage was about 7.4 miles, and it was a great hike.

A good amount of trees and shade.  The trail was very pleasant on the eyes most of the way, and you start to see the views of the city and ocean on some of the switchbacks at 2.5mi and 3mi.    

The turn around point is Inspiration Point, and what a great place to have that sandwich you packed with you:   Inspiration Point has expensive views, and the area is nice and flat.

We got two hikes in, while in SB; this one and Romero Canyon.   We both greatly preferred Jesusita (pronounced "Hay Sue See Ta" ) over Romero Canyon.

If you're looking for an easy hike but one that still gives you a good workout and has your legs feeling sore the next day, then you must check out Jesusita Trail. Apparently this trail takes you as far up to Inspiration Point if you want to make it more challenging but we didn't venture that far.

Right now is the time to go since we've had so much rain this year; it's brought out so many beautiful colorful flowers. I haven't seen so much green in our mountains in over a decade! Our creeks have running water again, and the sound of it is so peaceful. It's the simple things like this that make me feel very blessed to live in such a beautiful place.

Directions are simple, just head towards the mountains on San Roque Rd. until you see the Cater water treatment plant. If you get there early enough parking will be easy. 

This hike is 7 miles round trip, and a little steep at times with some rocky trails. I don't have hiking shoes yet but I was still comfortable wearing my running shoes, but if I hiked up to Inspiration Point then that definitely requires hiking shoes.

Most of the trail is shady so you won't be exposed for long periods of time if your hiking on a clear warm day. Bring plenty of water, snacks, sunscreen, and other hiking essentials to get you through. There is one picnic table in the midst of the trail for those that wish to take a break and have a bite to eat.

On our hike we came across a lot of dogs so this is a very dog friendly hike. They especially love jumping into the creeks just like little kids. It was great to see how happy they were splashing in the water. Some of the creeks require crossing the water to get back on the path so be prepared to use your balancing skills while using the rocks to get across.

Overall a must do hike for those that are looking for a fun outdoor adventure! Looking forward to going back soon to trek up Inspiration Point.

name: Mission Historical Park & Rose Gardens
categories: Botanical Gardens, Parks, Active Life, Arts & Entertainment

yelp_reviews.text:
A MUST DO! A FREE PARK WITH A BEAUTIFUL ROSE GARDEN & FREE PARKING. This park is located right across from The Beautiful Mission Santa Barbara. It is operated by the City of Santa Barbara Parks & Recreation Department. It has tall trees, green grass & a wonderful picturesque Rose Garden. The garden is subdivided so all the same color Roses are all together and then it transitions from one color into another color. There are so many varieties & colors here. It's like a box of Crayola Crayons. This is a great place to have your own private picnic  or play a little Frisbee or just chill & read a book. Bring your own blanket & chairs  & something to eat & drink or you can sit on the city benches as well. They have Free Street Parking. You can also go across the street & for $9 you can see The Mission. A great little picnic spot in Beautiful Santa Barbara & it's FREE! 
It used to be, you had to have mega bucks & be really rich to have the Good Life, not anymore. The Game has changed. You've been Upgraded. Now You Can 
Live Like a King on a Limited Budget!

We love picnics and parties in the mission rose gardens. Lots of people bring lawn games and kites and camp out all day. There's not allot of natural shade so you might want to bring a tent or umbrella.

This park is located across the street from Mission Santa Barbara. There is a nice rose garden there as well as benches to sit and relax. 

We walked around to check it out and noticed there is limited shaded areas on the grass, all of which were occupied by picnickers. Several people were tossing balls in the open field which makes up most of the park and several dog owners were there too. None of the dogs were on leash; I didn't see any signs or know what the park policy is so I assume this is okay.

This place is awesome, especially on a beautiful sunny day. Rose garden growing fast, soon to bloom. Wide open grassy space with a few benches and a few trees for some shade. Great place to play lawn games or have a picnic , or both. Right across street from great mission and church. Mission also has a great garden to walk through. Relaxing, family friendly.

I was so happy to find this lovely park. It was my birthday weekend & I was driving around Santa Barbara trying to find a rose garden when Yelp suggested this park. It was delightful. More than half of the roses have name tags below which is always fun. There are open bathrooms here too! 

Such a splendid afternoon smelling all the flowers. I needed that. A decent amount of people around, enjoying life with company. Love whoever takes care of this park, because it shows. 

Santa Barbara birthday rose garden requirement, check!

I appreciate the expansive lawn and the roses across the street from the mission Santa Barbara. The roses always seem to be in bloom. The park is used by many.  For events, for dog walking, for enjoying the afternoon on a stroll, or a spot to have a gathering of lots of friends.  With the Mission as a backdrop, and the beautiful mountains and the view the other direction towards the sea. This is just a wonderful park.  I like it a lot!

Well, well well
Some love Santa Barbara for the ocean/beach
I on the other hand 
love it for the "Mission Historical Park & Rose Garden 
This is the sort of place that can take your mind 
Far beyond everyday reality 
It's just simply beautiful.
The view and the rows of roses
Is an invitation to the eyes.
As long as light exists outside 

This is the perfect place to bring that perfect 
Homemade dish that you just conquered
Whether it was that 30 min dessert 
Or that last minute meal
You definitely want to come to this place
And reserve time to enjoy it

I would like to say
There is just all types of roses 
Some of the memorable varieties include
Barbara Streisand ( yes like the actual actor)
Martha Stewart ( I believe I saw this too)
In the mood
Hot cocoa
Neptune ( a lavender/ pastel purple ) gently folded rose

Neptune was the one that really stained my experience 
It was odiferous and unforgettable 

I didn't check out the surrounding areas 
With the ocean view 
Or even the church like structure across from the roses
I was too busy Smelling roses.

There is lots of areas to sit on the ground with a blanket 
Whether their are kids in your company 
They have room to smile and chase each other


If you are talking about pictures 
Well, this is your opportunity 
Overall just a great place to create memories 

I noticed a stone seated area
Where clever minded individuals can bring a table
Or something to prop things on
And hold a meeting of sorts
Maybe a family dinner for 4


Only thing missing was Julie Andrews 
Singing "These are a few of my favorite things."

I highly recommend every soul visit this place
And create new memories....
 - Ciao

Large, green park next to the Mission with a rose garden. 

We saw the Mission, walked through the art festival, grabbed a cup of wine, and walked over to see the rose garden. People were having picnics, resting, and playing on the grass. The roses were beautiful and of many different colors. We enjoyed our time in the outdoors.

Beautiful spot to go visit while in Santa Barbara. The bookstore/ shop was open during July 2020, masks required & hand sanitizer available. 
  The rose gardens are overwhelming how beautiful they are. All the different roses each with their unique smell.  
  Great place to stroll through, bring a lunch, or bike to from the beach.

Great spot to hang out, picnic, do a photoshoot, etc! There's plenty of grassy areas to chill and the rose gardens occupy just a portion of the plot of land. Also great spot to snap a pic of the roses with the Mission and mountains in the background!

One of my favorite spots in Santa  Barbara is the popular Rose Gardens across from our beautiful Queen Mission.
Take a nice stroll and smell the flowers, over 1500 fragrant roses are spread across this beautiful open space. The blooms are best appreciated during its season from April to November. 
       At any given time whether it's sunny or over cast, you will find visitors enjoying the gardens, meditating on the grass, having a picnic with friends, and may even catch a glimpse of a photogenic wedding party. 
The backdrop of the historic Mission and the beautiful mountains is an oasis for all to enjoy. 
       So, if you're looking for a place to spend your lunch hour, or have a few hours more to enjoy the pristine gardens, stop here to smell the roses. You'll be glad you did!!!

Most people correlate rose gardens with Portland, Oregon, but we too have our own here. Smack dab in front of the gorgeous "Queen of the (21) Missions" Santa Barbara Mission lies this park and rose gardens.

HISTORY: The actual name is A.C. Postel Memorial Rose Garden, but it is known as "The Road Garden." This is home to over 1,500 rose plants which are typically in bloom annually between April and November. The garden serves as one of over 130 recognized competitive demonstration rose gardens in the US, hosting a variety of roses including a number of All-America Rose Selections (AARS) winners. The park and the Mission were designated as a California State Historical Landmark on July 12, 1939, though the first pedigree rose bushes were planted in 1955.

VIEWS: 
To the North-The Mission with its two bell towers and the Santa Ynez Mountains as a backdrop one way.
To the South-The Pacific Ocean and Channel Islands the other way; yes this is one of the only places on the West Coast where the Pacific lies to the south versus the west. You may also seen the oil rigs far into the ocean, but we like to think of them as Spanish galleons.
To the East & West-Luxury houses with a Spanish architecture on the hills and streets surround you; particularly along the scenic Alameda Padre Serra ("APS") Drive.

SCENT: If you're a fan of roses, simply take one deep breath in... The scent is of the Heavens.

FEEL: Peaceful with a well-maintained green grass lawn and a light breeze. Locals and visitors throw down a blanket or pop-up a tent, uncork a bottle of wine, play games, toss a ball, and enjoy this 'Quintessential Santa Barbara' park.

BEYOND THE GARDENS: If you walk toward the mountains a short distance towards the APS Drive, there is a historic olive grove dedicated to World War I veterans, remains of tannery vats, a pottery, gristmill, and a historic reservoir and aqueduct system. You can find a small map and placards along the way.

TIPS:
- It is one of the few public parks that allows public alcohol consumption. Please consider using plastic versus glass for safety and take away what you bring in.
- There are no picnic tables or chairs.
- Dogs are allowed on leashes.
- Hours are sunrise to sunset.

I have overlooked this park for many years, but today when visiting the mission today I took the chance to walk across the street to see this park and rose garden. Most of the park is a large lawn, and very popular with picnickers and sun bathers on the weekend. But I was lucky to be here on a weekday, and found the rose garden part to be much more than I expected! 

The roses are sequestered toward the neighborhood side, and doesn't look like much from a distance. However, walk into them and the world changes. 

There are many beds of roses, most of them 5 to 6 feet tall it seems! The blooms were plentiful on this visit, and the aroma of roses was amazing! Every where I went I smelled the roses, one of my favorite scents. 

The roses are grouped by variety, and there are labels with the common names, should you wish to track down a variety that caught your attention.

And there are benches around here and there so you can just sit and admire the blooms - when in season of course!

If you ARE in the area when they are blooming, take the chance for a leisurely stroll through the beds - you'll be amazed at the potential of a rose garden brought to reality.

Friday night happy hour happens here!  State street is out, the park is in. Bring lawn chairs, dinner, and drinks and watch the kids and dogs run around with the most amazing Mission backdrop.  With that fresh Santa Barbara air and magic sunset light it's a pretty magical place.

One of Santa Barbara's true icons. 

Roses are one of my most favorite flowers, and I most definitely gained my love for them from this very garden. It's simply remarkable. There are hundreds of well-maintained rose bushes in all types of varieties. They are neatly laid out in several large rectangular planter beds, according to color and variety. I love perusing the gardens and viewing the plaque under each section listing the particular plant's name. There are some truly whimsical rose varieties out there!

Santa Barbara hosts an annual pruning workshop here at these very gardens each winter (roses are to be pruned each January/February in California) and I have had the awesome opportunity of attending one. There are experts on hand advising how to cut them back. They are to resemble a vase shape when fully pruned. I have taken these tips and have applied to my 17 rose bushes in my own yard, and they have grown back beautifully the following spring. 

This park also has a wonderful lush grassy knoll which is an extremely popular spot for picnics - especially during covid. Drive past any weekend day and you will see dozens of groups congregated together, enjoying the setting. 

A beautiful spot not to be missed!

name: Lions Park
categories: Hotels, Hotels & Travel, Event Planning & Services, Active Life, Venues & Event Spaces, Parks, Wedding Planning

yelp_reviews.text: Very cute, small park with plenty of parking. They have a main house for events, a playground and a huge grass area. The trees allow a little bit of shade. It's very cute.

On Wednesday night we had the honor of providing DJ Entertainment and LED Uplighting for the Lions Park Open House.  Beautiful Park and Lodge hidden in the Carpinteria foothills.  Cat Chapman the Event Coordinator is super fun to work with, professional, and helpful.  A few months back we DJ-MC'd a Bar Mitzvah in the Lodge, and next month we'll be DJ'ing Ruby and Robert's Wedding, providing a Ceremony DJ System, Cocktail music, Moving DJ Lighting, and a Main DJ-MC System inside the lodge for dinner, speeches, & dancing.  Great venue and a great value for Weddings, Non-Profits, and Special Events to get your Party Started!
beautiful, lush green park. I love how gorgeous and serene it is.

We celebrated a birthday party here with about 80 guests and had an amazing time!  The staff and facilities are really impressive.  It's super easy to make a reservation but I recommend making them in advance, as the park can book up pretty quickly.  

Our event was in March, when there is always a slight chance of rain, but that's where the venue stands out.  We had light rain the day of the event but with the trees, covered decks and indoor outdoor flow everyone was able to enjoy the entire facility with no detriment to the e or excitement and fun.  In fact, we all would have had just as much fun had it rained terribly, since the inside building is so well laid out.  We still enjoyed the kids play structure, horseshoe pits and volleyball net.  Oh my gosh this place is huge on the inside and outside allowing everyone to congregate where they wanted to.

It was really nice that Cat sent us a floor plan so we could design the table layout ourselves.  We really felt like she was working hard to ensure we understood the options available to us.  The bar is large and very well stocked with a good selection of beers and mixed drinks.  And, you can even bring your own selection of wine if you want to with a minimal corkage fee.  The bathrooms are large and clean.  The commercial sized kitchens accommodate all food and drink needs you can imagine.  There is tons of parking and the venue is easy to get to from town.

Comments from our guests were all very good, with everyone having felt like we catered to them; parents, kids and adults all raved about their own unique experiences.  That really made us happy, as bigger events usually have a mixed attendance.  

All in all the park is extremely affordable for what you get and remember, it is a non-profit with proceeds staying locally.  With that in mind, this set-up is superior to a large hotel/resort since you get quality one-on-one caring assistance from local individuals working hard to please, rather than an underpaid employee of a corporation just doing their job.

The venue is gorgeous and we would rather keep it to ourselves as locals, but since the profits stay locally, we cannot voice loud enough how amazing Lions Park is for events of all size, any time of year!  Thank you Lions Park and Lions Club of Carpinteria!

We had my sons wedding there in March and it turned out wonderful.  Cat is so helpful
and made a stressful time much easier.  The bartender guys were great.  Would recommend the lions park.

(Update! I've heard there has been recent upgrades to their bartending staff, I do hope I have made a positive impact & a difference by sharing my experience.)

Original Review.....

Let me start by saying that this review is not only based off of my own personal experience but is also based off of the experience that my guests received on my wedding day at the Lions Park.

I know that everyone had a blast at my wedding because I asked a majority of my guests a few days later. The only negative reviews I heard was about the treatment that my guests received from the bartenders working for the lions park.

I think that at the least when a person is going to work at a wedding that they should try to make this day as special as possible for the soon to be newlywed couple and their guests by being polite and other common social skills that they obviously lack. 

The lions park has their own bar and bartenders. It wasn't good, gosh... We're do I begin. They were dressed as if they were attending a BBQ. Whenever I or a guest ordered a drink they would sigh and make a gesture like "uhhh", as if they didn't want to be there or be doing their job. A job that cost me, the customer, over $3k. 

Where's the customer service?

The only reason I even gave this place two stars is because the Lions Park is in a beautiful town, Carpinteria, and is a beautiful place with with lots of green grass and big beautiful oak trees. The events coordinator for Lions park, Aimee Banks, did a good job and accommodated all of our needs and requirements. She was a pleasure to work with; however, I feel like the customer service was only good up until the wedding day came. That's the day that mattered most. It feels like after they got their money than they didn't care anymore. That may not be the case but that's how it made me feel. Aimee apologized for the bartenders behavior but it doesn't change how I feel much.

It shouldn't be like that. It's the most important day of a persons life, it's supposed to feel special. The guests shouldn't feel like they're not welcome... But unfortunately that was the case.. 

Sorry Lions, you guys have to fix your bartender customer service problems before you host anymore weddings.

It was my great pleasure to coordinate a wedding at Lions Park several weeks ago.  The event contact, Cat, was responsive and super helpful, in all possible ways.  The venue is such a hidden gem!  Lovely and well cared for, the indoor space has everything a wedding party could need.  The outdoors is reminiscent of the Redwoods up north, you'd hardly know you were in a beach town.  I recommend it highly to anyone for events and weddings and I'll be glad to work there again.  

Toby, the Santa Barbara Wedding Coordinator

I was a dinner guest at Lion's Park. The hors d'oeuvre were nice. Dinner was well prepared. The bar patrons were very courteous. Guests were friendly.
Nice venue.

(Update! I've heard there has been recent upgrades to their bartending staff, I do hope I have made a positive impact & a difference by sharing my experience.)

Aimee apologized for the bartenders behavior but it doesn't change how I feel much.

It shouldn't be like that. It's the most important day of a persons life, it's supposed to feel special. The guests shouldn't feel like they're not welcome... But unfortunately that was the case.. 

Sorry Lions, you guys have to fix your bartender customer service problems before you host anymore weddings.

We just had our wedding at Lions park and it was a beautiful day! We used the gazebo adorned with flowers for our ceremony and it was perfect for us, simple and classy. The reception area looked great inside with all of the wood and the beautiful light display on the wall. 

I wish I could give 5 stars but some things need to be addressed. 

Initially, the event manager was very helpful and quick to reply to emails and questions. But as time went on she was slower to reply or did not reply at all. Eventually my wedding coordinator had to get involved and event manager stated she had not seen my emails. Event manager was very apologetic and nice but it is stressful tying to plan a wedding from 3 hours away not to hear back promptly. 

Also, we paid for day before wedding access to the park so we could set up our venue/reception. We had our catering staff delivering linens at a specific time but none of us could get in as the doors were not opened for us as promised. We were waiting around for a while hoping the staff would come but no one arrived. We contacted the bar manager who was able to give us the code to let us in the building and thank goodness because no staff ended up coming to let us in. 

On the day of the wedding the bridal suite was cute and spacious but had no toilet paper in bathroom, hand towel in bathroom or paper towels in kitchen. I searched the premises and found some toilet paper in a storage closet but it would have nice to have been stocked. The white couch was very dirty/stained and we placed a sheet over it so we felt comfortable sitting on it. 

The main thing was my wedding turned out great, the park was beautiful and the lodge area was rustic and charming. The groundskeeper came the morning of the wedding and made the park look wonderful. I would like to say the bar manager Dave is so helpful, friendly and quick to reply to all emails and calls. The bar staff was very friendly on the night of the wedding as well. 

Overall I would recommend this beautiful park if the communication issues improved.

My husband and I recently got married here and it was absolutely perfect! We wanted to go with a rustic and outdoorsie vibe and it did not disappoint.  Cat was wonderful to work with and she made the whole planning process a breeze. Which is great because as we learned planning a wedding isn't always a walk in the park. They have an on site bridal suite which was perfect for getting ready and being on site to oversee the setup of the venue. They also have a full size kitchen, catering parking next to kitchen and it's air conditioned. We really couldn't have asked for anything more that would have made our day any better.

I just shot a wedding at this unique location and I'm absolutely in love with this cute venue. The charming house, the lovable suite to get ready in for the bride, the garden and the big, wooden-paneled room. 

The staff was very sweet and accommodating- unlike the bad review I saw before. I did feel like they were understaffed for sure, but I also felt like they put an extra effort in to make up for it! Everything was on time and well organized. 

The park is surrounded by vineyards and farmers, so it's a very peaceful place-- perfect for weddings due to the picturesque scenery. I hope I get to return for another job soon when it's not as hot and we can do more outdoor pictures! ;)

We had our wedding their last weekend & everything was PERFECT!! I could not of asked for a better day! The venue is so beautiful, the outside area is well kept and perfect for a ceremony! The inside area is great for a reception or any event!  
Not to mention the staff there is wonderful and helpful! 
I highly recommend this venue for any type of event!!

(Update! There has been recent (POSITIVE) upgrades to their bartending staff, I do hope I have made a impact & a difference by sharing my experience.)

Sorry Lions, you guys have to fix your bartender customer service problems before you host anymore weddings.

I got married here July 2017. I wanted an indoor/outdoor venue that was rustic and unfussy, yet sophisticated. This place was PERFECT. I looked at a lot of places and Lions Park was simply the best.  We had our ceremony outside under the trees on the lawn and we had our reception in their lodge (sooo cute!). They are happy to recommend vendors they like to work with and really help to make your day unique and tailored to you. Let me list some of my favorite things: 

1. When your rent this place, it's YOURS for the whole day. It's not open to the public and randoms aren't going to wander onto the property because there's only one wedding each day. 

2. They have bartenders which you DON'T have to rent and they let you bring your own wine. Nuff said. 

3. The staff is so friendly and warm. Cat is superb. She was attentive to my needs, very accommodating and helpful. The whole bar staff was on top of everything and they really made sure to make my day special. 

4. The bridal cottage. All of my bridesmaids were blown away and it was easily the nicest one I looked at. There was one huge main room with a mirror that runs along one wall, a bathroom with a shower, and a bedroom with a full length mirror. More than enough room for my 5 bridesmaids and my mothers It's exactly how I'd pictured getting ready wth my girls, (and more!) 

5. Totally reasonable pricing. They could be charging a lot more for their beautiful venue, so get on it before they increase their fees ;) 

I simply could not have been happier with my wedding venue choice :)

My husband and I were married at Lions Park in March. It was an absolutely amazing experience! The venue has it all, great location, wonderful landscaping, flawless facilities, and an extremely helpful staff that runs it. Cat and David were fantastic assisting us throughout the whole process. 

Lions Park was the perfect setting for us to pledge our undying love towards one another. The outdoor area for the ceremony was picturesque. A sprawling green that was perfectly accented with trees and shrubs. There were ample places for pictures to be taken, and there are many different options as to how you would like to have your ceremony set up. 

The venue also has a lovely lodge that we utilized to hold our reception at immediately following the ceremony. The lodge had a full commercial kitchen, bar, dining area, dance floor, restrooms, and bridal suite. In addition to all of the amenities indoors, there is a lovely deck that wraps around, which was great as it allowed for a lovely outdoor conversation area. 

All around Lions Park was an absolute hit for us, and we cannot sing it's praises enough. Our wedding day was made unforgettably memorable because of Lions Park. We highly recommend Lions Park for any event!

My husband and I absolutely loved this venue! It is absolutely stunning, with a beautiful "nature" and "woodsy" look that made decorating very simple since it didn't really need anything extra! The indoor portion was perfect for the reception, but outdoors was gorgeous the whole time allowing guests to play Cornhole and horseshoes outside, and a ceremony that led up to the "natural arch" of the trees. Our band was also well shaded and plugged in under the gazebo. It was the perfect day for us! The bridal suite is also stunning; our photographer raved about how perfectly set up it was for her. I loved hanging out in there with my bridesmaids. Lions Park provides a lot of amenities too, which eased stress for us. We were able to set up practically everything the night before, making the day of nice and smooth. 100% recommend this venue! Wouldn't change a thing about our wedding day!

We just had our wedding at Lions park and it was a beautiful day! We used the gazebo adorned with flowers for our ceremony and it was perfect for us, simple and classy. The reception area looked great inside with all of the wood and the beautiful light display on the wall. 

I wish I could give 5 stars but some things need to be addressed. 

Initially, the event manager was very helpful and quick to reply to emails and questions. But as time went on she was slower to reply or did not reply at all. Eventually my wedding coordinator had to get involved and event manager stated she had not seen my emails. Event manager was very apologetic and nice but it is stressful tying to plan a wedding from 3 hours away not to hear back promptly. 

Also, we paid for day before wedding access to the park so we could set up our venue/reception. We had our catering staff delivering linens at a specific time but none of us could get in as the doors were not opened for us as promised. We were waiting around for a while hoping the staff would come but no one arrived. We contacted the bar manager who was able to give us the code to let us in the building and thank goodness because no staff ended up coming to let us in. 

On the day of the wedding the bridal suite was cute and spacious but had no toilet paper in bathroom, no hand towel in bathroom nor any paper towels in kitchen. I searched the premises and found some toilet paper in a storage closet but it would have nice to have been stocked. The white couch was very dirty/stained and we placed a sheet over it so we felt comfortable sitting on it. 

The main thing was my wedding turned out great, the park was beautiful and the lodge area was rustic and charming. The groundskeeper came the morning of the wedding and made the park look wonderful. I would like to say the bar manager Dave is so helpful, friendly and quick to reply to all emails and calls. The bar staff was very friendly on the night of the wedding as well. 

Overall I would recommend this beautiful park if the communication issues improved.

Lions Park is one of the most beautiful wedding venues!!! It's secluded, spacious, and in view of the mountains. As well the lodge and bridal suite are absolutely charming. Lions Park seeks to give back to the community so the price is more than reasonable and there isn't a wedding package that you're stuck with. We were able to customize our wedding exactly the way we wanted and only pay for the parts that interested us. Cat, the park's event organizer made the experience a breeze. She responds to email quickly and helped us design exactly what we were looking for. I highly recommend Lion's Park for your wedding or any event!!!

Great venue for a wedding and really enjoyed the gazebo for a backdrop. I loved the fact that we could hold all of the activities outside and that there was a play area for the children and also a relaxed atmosphere for all ages. The bridal suite is really special and there is a great kitchen and bar in the clubhouse. Thanks Cat for your attention to the details!

My husband and I had been coming to Carpinteria for our anniversary every year for the past 6 years. When we got engaged we knew we wanted to get married in Carpinteria but we're worried we wouldn't be able to find an affordable venue. When we found Lions Park it was like hitting a gold mine. We couldn't believe the perfect venue existed in our perfect place, within our budget. Cat was incredible to work with and she truly made the process so easy and was always available whenever we had questions. Our wedding guests were so impressed with our venue and couldn't believe how gorgeous it was with the trees, the lodge, and especially the bridal suite. Lion's Park was the perfect venue for our casual and rustic beach wedding. Thank you Cat and everyone at Lions Park for helping make our wedding everything we could have ever dreamed of.

OK, where do I start? I really wanted to have my wedding here, but my husband (fiancé at the time) Did not. After looking into several different options, he finally decided to let me have my way! Of course, that is the way it should always be... And that is the way it will continue to be! And he was soooooo happy that we did!!

I immediately bonded with Cat, and was extremely excited to get the wedding planning started... Cat was absolutely 100% everything I never knew I needed when it came to wedding planning! I never thought I would get married, I had no idea what I was getting myself into, and she spent countless hours helping me out throughout the process... Anytime I had a question, she immediately answered it... She was precise, responsive and knowledgeable, and made things really really really easy for us. I literally could not imagine having any other venue being so helpful. She made me feel no stress! She was very professional, personable, and all-around knowledgeable about everything that comes with planning a wedding.

The location is perfect.  The staff (Cat, the only one I talked to) is great and all the guests were extremely impressed!!! The price was perfect, too! Don't look elsewhere!!!

Many thanks to Cat and the Lion's Club Park for an amazing day!! We had our daughter's wedding here in August and this setting helped make it a magical day to remember. We really all wanted an intimate outdoor  DIY wedding in a beautiful natural setting, and that's exactly what we got! Due to covid, our first choice of venue was unavailable, but that turned our to be a blessing in disguise as we were all so happy with how the day turned out. 
First, the setting is beautiful. We decorated the on site arch with garlands and placed it under the trees for a perfect ceremony.  We set up tables around a dance floor in front of the lodge for our reception.  We did the flowers ourselves and the vibe was just what we hoped for.  There is also a cute gazebo, an indoor hall if you prefer for the reception, a play area off to the side (our flower girls and their brother enjoyed that) , a full kitchen, and a really nice bridal suite. Plus, plenty of parking. 
Second, Cat was awesome from start to finish. She answered all of our questions and offered helpful suggestions and was just super easy to deal with.
Finally, the price was affordable.
So, we were delighted with how the day turned out! A few tips:
We used Cat's lighting (extra fee but very reasonably priced) and this added so much to the vibe.
We did the flowers ourselves (Mayesh in Oxnard wholesale florist) and we are happy that we did.
We had a taco truck and Mariachi band for dinner and our guests loved this.
For dancing, we skipped the DJ and did our own set list and brought in a rented PA system. It worked great!
We had about 50 guests but the venue can easily accomodate a bigger event.
We hired Cat's son to help with bussing and clean up and that freed us up to enjoy the evening.
We highly recommend Cat and the Lion's Park! Thanks for a day we will always remember!!

We had a company luncheon at Lions Park. With COVID concerns we wanted an outdoor space that could hold us comfortably where people could feel safe. We had such a lovely time. The space and amenities were just perfect for our event. We had approximately 75 guests at our event and the parking lot and picnic table space was very accommodating. The trees were beautiful and the outdoor bathroom facilities were clean. We appreciate Lions Park being an affordable event space in the Carpinteria area!

name: StandUp Paddle Sports ® LLC
categories: Outdoor Gear, Surfing, Shopping, Paddleboarding, Active Life, Education, Specialty Schools, Sporting Goods

yelp_reviews.text: First disclaimer...i will have to finish this review at a later time as I haven't ridden my board yet. I just ordered a mahalo shaka sup from these guys. I have been researching sups for almost a year now wanting to purchase one instead of always renting. I live in the central valley so a lot of lake paddling, but often visit Santa Cruz, and other beaches so wanted a board capable of surfing pretty well. I finally went with these guys after not really seeing anything great from REI. I wasn't able to make the 5 hr trek to santa barbara so had my board shipped. From placing the order online to receiving my board customer service has been awesome. I received a phone call within about 30 minutes of ordering my board. There were some weird issues with my credit card and the guys were patient. They were helpful with paddle and fin selection asking what I would use my board for. Shipping was prompt. I was a little skeptical on how a sup board would do being shipped, but not a scratch on the board. The board is beautiful, pretty light, and nice attention to detain from the board to the paddle to fins. Can't wait to try my new toy out...will post some pics then.

Nick, Deb, and Wardog have been incredibly supportive and friendly.  I was going to buy a Starboard Whopper or Widepoint, but decided to try some similar boards at SUPSports, and loved the Stoke 9'6 33.5.  But Nick suggested since I was moving down from such a big, long heavy board of 11'2, that I also try some others so that I arrive at the right option - so I tried a bunch of others.  All of them were awesome, with different characteristics. Finally, Wardog responded to an email as I was stuck between the Stoked and the Mahalo 10'3 and based on what I was doing (waist to head high waves, some paddling around SB) he recommended the Mahalo.  Final awesome service was Nick helping me through the custom selection process.  They have been incredible!  Albert

Stopped by here Sunday (we were up in Santa Barbara from San Diego for Solstice) to finally check this shop out (I've known about it for a few years) and ended up buying a DVD that I had been interested in for awhile.  Very nice selection of their own brand of boards and they offer rental services which I will use next time I'm up that way.  Had a nice chat with two employees working that day and sorry guys I didn't get your names.  Oh well next time.  Anyway I highly recommend checking this place out and for those who've never SUPed to get some lessons from these guys.

We had a great birthday party for my 12 year-old and her friends.  They taught everyone to paddle board and by the end everyone was standing-up, even me.  I would recommend them as a great activity for preteen/teen groups.

I had stand-up paddled once, and my husband had not yet tried it.  We had a free morning while visiting family, so we took a lesson with Woodie.  Allen was helpful with the simple sign-up process. We appreciated the local, ma and pop shop and service. Woodie tailored the lesson to our skill and adventure levels!  His expertise with stand up and as a firefighter medic trained in water rescue significantly added to the comfort level and overall experience. We will definitely rent from them next time we SUP in Santa Barbara!

Thanks to the staff here.... After a long morning at Mondos I stopped by to buy a new paddle.. They made me the longest one ever, since I am 6 foot 7.. I Drove straight to the beach and did 2 hours of Paddling, working off last nights beer... Thanks again can't wait till I need a new board!!!!  You guys are Rad...

Very knowledgable folks.  I learned a lot from my short visit at the shop.  I wish I would have known about this place a while ago. Had a problem with my paddle, and they fixed it at very little cost to me. Stoked about that.  If you live in SB, I would not buy a SUP without consulting the guys at this shop first.
Last week I bought two replacement SUPS...this time I made sure the dealer would stand behind the products regardless of environmental " events"

I bought two Hammer War Dog carbon boards from these guys. Within a year both boards " melted"... Took them back to shop...was told there must have been an "event" to cause damage....

Amazing lack of explanation. Just posted picture of one of my Hammers with a FREE sign next to FREE washing machine. Gave it away as it was unusable.

Nice guys but they choose to blame TWO faulty boards on a mysterious " event" rather than on crap construction

Buyer beware

A great buying experience!  Exchanged several emails with Warren explaining what I was looking for and asked his advice.  He responded right away and gave me several options along with photos and specs.  Drove down to the really cool shop and met with Ian, who is knowledgeable and really easy to deal with.  Came away with a gorgeous board!  The store is very well stocked and they also do custom builds.  Highly recommend this shop!

My wife and I had purchased paddle boards from REI but couldn't find a paddle for her. We stumbled upon SUP Sports in SB and bought her a paddle that they custom cut to her. Once we saw the quality and care they put into just a paddle we were hooked! We looked at their board options and were so impressed. We took our boards back to REI to buy a lighter and better quality board from SUP Sports. Shop around and then stop by and you will be sold on their quality as well. A great shop and awesome staff!!!

Bought a Wardog from these guys in November 2016.  It is pretty much already on its way to the landfill.  Customer service here is great.  Guys are great.  Boards look cool but don't look too long.  Look at these boards wrong and they will fail.  I have owned many, many surfboards of all types across many sports and these boards are the weakest and cheapest I have ever come across.  Very disappointed in the wasted money.
