What can I say, you want to be Stoked, patron this establishment!  Robert helped me on a Saturday with a problem I had with my paddle!  I was freaking out about losing time on the water for Sunday!  He answered the phone on the second ring, I told him my concern and he said, let me see what I can do.  He took my number and said he would call right back!  I thought, "yeah right", and guess what, he promptly called me right back, with a solution, which made my weekend!!  Who does that anymore, well Robert does, you want top notch service go see Robert and tell him Grandpa Jack sent you!  Robert you rock!!!

Wardog, I have been a customer for almost a decade now, your company has never let us down!  Please pat Robert on the back and tell him many, many, Mahalos from Jack!!!!

What a great place!  I've been wanting to buy a SUP board since trying one for 5 solid minutes while camping last year at Carpinteria.  We've rented a few, and decided to take a lesson that would take us into light surf up at Santa Barbara.  Our big challenge is that we can only afford one board at present, and my husband and I are not the same size people! We've been told elsewhere we'd have to have a 11'6" board or even bigger.

Ian took us out for a fun lesson, beginning in the SB harbor, and then going over to the little beach next to it.  He picked out several boards for us, and my husband was able to do well.  I loved the boards so much that we decided to buy one.  What's amazing is that their Hammer boards are so well designed that we bought a 10'6" 33"wide board, and it is wonderful! They totally went the extra mile....literally...by asking Kelsey to deliver the board down to us in Carpinteria after hours.

As luck would have it, I had a problem that same day, first time out,  with a war between the fin and sand, caused by my complete lack of surfing experience, and I was heartbroken.  We went back up from Carp to SB, and they took great care of me and absolutely made my day.  This shop has the perfect combination of technical expertise and customer service.  Stacy (hope I spelled it right), Ian, and Kelsey ...thanks so much!  I am definitely stoked!

Wonderful Trail in Carp--starts right behind high school and provides a nice incline/climb up to great views of ocean and Carp.

Lots of flowers this time of year!
The trailhead entrance is located right next to Carpinteria high school. You can park at the high school on the weekends and walk to the trail from there.
unbelievable views!  Definately worth the drive.   come at see for yourself.   you will not believe the views.

Okay we did this hike yesterday and really enjoyed it.  We are not hikers but do regularly exercise and like exploring the outdoors.  That said, some people might say this is an easy hike, and it kind of is because the trail path is pretty wide, clear, and you don't have to climb over any boulders or maneuver around obstacles.

But, it is almost all uphill for the first half (once you get around the high school) and even with regular exercise in our routines, we were pretty tired from the climbing up, up, up.  There are a lot of little rocks in the path, so for older people who have trouble with balance (or anyone who has trouble with balance), it would be a little tricky because you would have to watch your stepping.

Beautiful expansive ocean views.

Carpinteria has a new hike and it is great! It starts with a jaunt around the high school, and then you hike up, up, and into some incredible views of this lovely beach community.

The fenced in beginning parts make you feel like a convict, but I promise it gets better.

After the fires there is no shade on the trail. It crosses a fire road at about 2 miles and you can go for many more miles up the road but again, no shade. The trail starts at the high school so make sure to go to the left side and you will begin in a fenced off trail, not where google maps leads you. I walked around for a while looking for the trail head and didn't find it for a while because google maps leads you through an orchard. There are clear markers on the trail showing the distance and markers and the view and wildflowers were beautiful.

This is my FAVORITE hike in Santa Barbara County (so far)! Though it is straight incline upwards and a rocky downpour on the way back I love love love the beautiful scenery each way, and of course the breath taking ocean view at the top. It also helps that there is really close, free parking and a number of craft breweries down the street for afterwards! 

I think the first bench is a perfect distance for those looking for a great outdoor workout that doesn't take up too much of their time/day but gives them that feeling that they went out and worked hard. Great to do with friends, run clubs, mountain biking (somewhat high intensity), and of course our four-legged furry members of the family! 

I used to run or walk this every day when I lived closer and really miss the wonderful feeling I got during and after. I am excited to announce (if you haven't heard already) that the second bench, or the 3.5 mile second phase, is officially open and ready for trekking on. Who's with me? Will definitely be checking that out when I get a free moment and sunny day sometime soon! Pictures to come..
What a beautiful hike. Anyone living in Carp must hike this at least once - even visitors should. Soooo soo beautiful from the top (peak of the Moutain) great pics too

Great views, definitely good for a semi-quick workout (if you just go to the bench) with the switchbacks. This is a no-shade hike so on a hot day it can be strenuous for those not hydrated/used to physical activity. Part 2 (after the bench) is also pretty nice scenic wise and it goes on literally forever!

This is a hidden gem of a hiking trail! Starting behind the high school, the trail has plenty of switch backs in between breaks of flat ground to provide a solid hike. Not to mention there's a gorgeous view of the ocean all the way to the top. The beginning of the trail is a bit tricky to find, but just make your way to the back of the baseball fields and you should be on your way. Not crowded compared to other trails in the area.

A newer, or more accurately stated, recently reopened trail in Carpinteria. Construction is still underway, currently Phase 1 of 3 is the only open section, word is that phase 2 will open in 2016. Eventually the trail will enter the Los Padres National Forest.

Phase 1-
Distance is 2.25 miles each way, making the loop 4.5 miles. It is an easy hike with some areas of incline. There visibility of the city and ocean during 90% of the hike which make for pretty scenery however limited shade. Bring water, wear sunscreen, etc...

Directions-
Easiest is to enter the address for Carpinteria High School as the trailhead starts to the left of the school (when facing it from the from entrance). Parking is at Meadowview Lane as it is prohibited on school property. A short walk on a walking/biking path leads you from parking to school.

name: Mountain Air Sports
categories: Fashion, Active Life, Women's Clothing, Luggage, Ski & Snowboard Shops, Hiking, Rafting/Kayaking, Shopping, Sporting Goods, Outdoor Gear

yelp_reviews.text: These folks are fantastic.  Great selection of gear and very knowledgable staff. They saved my bacon when a large, three letter named outfit didn't get my pack to me on the promised date the day before a week long trip to the Sierras.  They were willing to stay open late and wait for me to arrive.

Excellent outdoors store. They have high-quality gear and very knowledgeable amd friendly staff.
Maybe a bit expensive if you are just starting to get into a sport but definitely the place to go when you want good quality nice gear.

tried to find my dad his xmas present here. nobody would help me. I eventually found something for him, but in the wrong size. since nobody would help me, I had to bring it up to the register to see if they had his size. They did not have his size- i bought the damn jacket anyway, and I was told the return policy was 2 weeks.  so hopefully he will try it on in a few days and it will magically fit, or else i'll return it and they said they can special order the particular size for me. 

I was able to find things, but it's pretty confusing in there, with the men's and women's collections all jumbled together. Maybe it's just me being sensitive, but  this one customer was repeatedly asked if he needed help, while i was floundering!

Great customer service! they spent the time to get me fitted me for the right ski boots and made some custom adjustments for my wide feet. They also put me on a pair of demo skis last season that I loved. I returned before this season started hoping to purchase them. They went out of their way to get ahold of the man in charge to price the skis fairly and adjust them though their shop was not up and ready yet and they had to move some things around to get to the equipment.
I love this store!  Great gear!  Very good range of clothing too!  Very helpful staff!  They know their equipment.   Beautiful art in the store too!

This place has it all for the sports-minded person.  I liked the selection, service, and location.  I didn't purchase anything here, but if my luggage could fit more into it, I certainly would have.  Prices were comparable to other sporting good stores.
High-quality, expensive goods with sales persons that border on Used Car salesmen. They just push too hard, even though they are friendly at it.

My wife and I came here to buy new skis/boots and are very happy we did. Drove up from Oxnard, so it's about equal for us to drive here or to Woodland Hills / Santa Monica where there are other shops. After having bought skis/boots from other shops in LA before, I'm so glad we went to Mountain Air. Worked with Joe, who is apparently a lifer at the store, and he was very generous with his time, totally knowledgable, listened but also lead us to the best choices. He took a lot of time with us to get it right. 

The rest of the staff were all eager beavers to help, but deferred to Joe on the skis. I have no way of verifying this, but it felt like they are NOT commissioned sales staff like some other shops, which I think is good. Either that or they just have a more team spirit culture going on there. 

The only thing I would possibly go down a star on is selection, which is fine, but not huge. We were limited to the choices on-hand. Of course they can special order things for you, but we didn't have time for that. However, I haven't really found much better selection in SoCal, so it seemed unfair to take a star away by comparing them to stores in more ski-enthusiast locales of snowier parts of the country.

These guys are as good as it gets!
I was up in Santa Barbara for a Hobie sponsored 3 day event and someone stole my dagger board off my Adventure Island in the middle of the night so I had to leave early. 
I had to be back in SB two weeks later so I ran to Mountain and asked them to order me one before I left SB. I guess I should have told them there was no rush and it would be awhile before I was back in the area. They had my replacement in two days and called me at least 10 times to let me know it was in.
Outstanding in my eyes!

My only CONS about them is the location. Near impossible to park close unless you are very lucky but a short walk to many public lots. On weekends they are packed so me patient, help will find you. On weekdays, bare bones staff BUT every employee has a specialty and most know about everything they carry so no real worries

Great store with really friendly service on State Street about a block from the wharf entrance.  

They have cool and unique as well as standard camping, exploring, adventuring gear displayed cutely at not too crazy prices.

I recently joined my friend to return a pair of shoes that were hurting his ankles. A friendly salesman called his manager over, who proceeded to aggressively, almost accusingly, criticize us for returning these shoes that were hurting him! I couldn't believe it, actually. 

These guys have some really high quality gear, but some wack customer service. Its almost better to to visit REI, although you will probably be asked to "join the co-op" by every employee you encounter there.

A+ Service staff will go above and beyond to accommodate you.

Logan, specializes in car accessories like roof racks (surf, ski). Logan helped me find exactly what I needed in under 5 minutes and suggested 2 more great products that were available, that I wasn't aware of. I ended up buying what I came for, and one of the products that he suggested too. Happy customer!

I will be saving for third product he suggested and coming back to Mountain Air Sports in Santa Barbara to get it.

Rest assured you will receive first class service at this store from any staff. Each employee seems to specialize in a specific area. Just ask or tell them what you are looking for.

There aren't stores like this anymore.  Personal and expert knowledge from every employee with smiles and helpfulness. We bought two Hobie kayaks and the guys spent a lot of time making sure our racks were on the right way ( they have installed hundreds) and tied it all down to be safe. This store is well worth traveling to (we live 40 minutes away) and by passing all the chain sports stores!

The folks at Mountain Air Sports get five stars for their excellent customer service! It has been an absolute pleasure to work with them over the years!During these challenging times in Santa Barbara following the fire & mudslide, it felt especially good to support a long time local business! #Shop SantaBarbara

I left purchasing one item until the last minute before a backpacking trip. I pretty much knew what I wanted but REI (usually my go-to) don't carry it and weight mattered. It was too late to order anything online so I checked out Mountain Air Sports. It was fantastic! I found EXACTLY what I wanted for a competitive price.

They have a top-notch range of gear. Almost everything that I had been oogling online (and either already purchased or want but don't need just yet) was there and they had it all in plenty of sizes and colours. I would have thought that being a smaller store they might not have had too many options, but I was thrilled! I could have easily spent longer and much more money there. I will definitely return.

Additionally, their staff were all polite and they have a membership program that gives you store credit. It's not the same as the policy at REI, but certainly a great offer if I know that I definitely want an item and don't need to test it first.

And again, I'm always thrilled when a store has Arc'teryx. They have a bunch!

Rad local Mnt Shop - they know their stuff and are VERY helpful. 

I live in Ventura and needed a specific roof rack bike part for a road trip I was leaving for that day. I called [5] local bike shops and no one had this stock part. I walked into this shop and one of the employee's immediately identified my part, went into a spare part bin and got me all that I needed. Interaction took less then [3] minutes.  

Super impressed, thanks again for helping me out! I'm a fan.

I came here in need of some climbing gear since I was going to be unexpectedly stranded in Santa Barbara for the weekend without mine. The service was great and I talked climbing for a bit with one of the sales guys in the store to get some beta on some of the areas around town before picking up some shoes & a chalk bag. One of the sales guys at the register offered to lend me his book and rent his crash pads if I needed them which was really cool of him. The shoe selection was a little small, but they were competitively priced and had enough variety to cover your climbing style.  I'd recommend this place if you're looking to pick up some gear that you might have forgotten while passing through town.

Fun, friendly, and extremely knowledgeable staff! BEST CUSTOMER SERVICE ever!! 
The team here devotes their time to you and does custom boot fitting!  The BEST in Santa Barbara for sure!

backpacking-yes
camping-yes
hiking-yes
rock climbing-yes
travel- yes 
ski-yes
snowboard-yes and a killer selection
car racks-yes and free installations!! who else does that? nobody
FUN-yes!

only the BEST gear companies!
Mountain Air Sports takes care of their customers

Einen guten Laden erkennt man daran, wie reagiert wird, wenn es Probleme gibt.
Drei Artikel im Gesamtwert von 300.-$ hatte ich kurzfristig einkaufen müssen.
Eines davon, eine Camping Matratze von Eureka (Finger weg--chinesische Qualität),
wurde schon nach kurzem leck. Nach einigen Mails und Telefonaten, konnte ich die
Matratze einsenden. Es dauerte zwar etwas über 14 Tage aber letztendlich wurde 
der Kaufpreis per Paypal erstattet.
Mind...blown!  Killer mountain shop!  Vs REI, KO in the first round. REI couldn't put a glove on em. Walking through the door, the first five steps it's obviously a serious gear shop. I'm in!

Mountain Air Sports always has awesome customer service and once again I was thoroughly impressed today. I picked up my second Hobie kayak from them  and received excellent customer service from Joey. I bought my first Hobie from him a couple years ago and though there's a shop that sells hobie kayaks about an hour closer to me I didn't hesitate to make the longer drive to do business with him. He really knows the products and is an avid kayak fisherman so he's able to personally attest to the best kayak for you and best setups for what you're doing. If you are considering getting a Hobie I definitely recommend talking to Joey and setting up a demo in the harbor across the street from the shop. Aside from Hobie kayaks they stock all of the accessories, vehicle racks, and tons of other outdoor gear and clothing. This is a great family owned shop that I also want to mention supports veterans by hosting Heroes On Water Kayak Fishing events in Santa Barbara. Harbor.

The consummate family-run outdoor store. 

Mountain Air's staff doesn't belittle, annoy, or exude the hipper-than-thou attitude I find at many outdoor-retail stores. Absolutely solid gear selection. As an avid climber, trail-runner, and backpacker, they have what I need, not just "filler" products to come off like they know what they're doing. They carry a carefully selected array of fine Patagonia products, and--to my knowledge--are the only purveyors of Arcteryx in SB. To top it off, they give water to my dog, Dale, whenever I come in. 

They stand behind their products, work hard, care about their business (as REALLY care... again, not the I-only-work-here-to-fund-my-ski-season attitude), and remember your name.

Amazing service.

I have the killer combo of weird feet and high expectations about ski boot fit. Dave and Ken at Mountain Air spent hours with me to perfect the fit of my boots so they were just right.

Can't recommend this place enough - from the top-notch products, to the courteous staff to the owner who truly cares about making each and every customer happy. Thanks guys!

This place in incredible! I went to the local REI and was about to purchase my roof rack but something told me I should just check Mountain Air Sports before the big purchase.
I went to Mountain Air and was helped by a super knowledgeable  and fun guy named Dustin! He gave us a thousand options and never tried to oversell us. They have all the baskets, and cargo boxes on display! REI did not have that. We made a decision to get the Thule roof basket and he strapped it up to the car that day with no extra charge ( in the rain !) 
Light rain though because normally if it's heavy rain it's not recommended.
Everyone in the store was so nice, giving our dog treats and made us feel so excited for our new purchase.
Awesome time at Mountain  Air!! 
Thanks guys!

I am writing this review in the parking lot, I'm just SO impressed!!! Just purchased a kayak for my sons birthday and it was a the best customer service experience ever! I can not say enough wonderful things about Dustin he was AMAZING could not have been more helpful did not try to oversell us, was SO honest, thank you so much Dustin!

I needed a winter jacket ASAP and all the other stores had stopped carrying winter stock. After searching desperately, I called Mountain Air Sports up and they had two in stock! Hallelujah. You guys saved me so much money in overnight shipping! 
Plus, they have a great stock of all North Face and Patagonia products. Will return.
I loved the choices for headwear, I found the perfect hat and glasses here. Great customer service, definitely recommend you to stop by and check out the place.

My husband and I were walking by this place and decided to go in. I have been looking at some chaco's and was interested in how they fit, the feel of them, etc. Hailey, one of the employees, was very helpful and gave me some awesome insights on what kind to buy. I was so thankful for her help and really enjoyed my visit! Will definitely visit again!

Mountain Air Sports and the staff are the best!!!  I was trying to find a roof rack for my new 4Runner and was unable to find it at my bike shop, I went to REI only to be told thats all we have and you'll have to order what you want. I went to Mtn Air and not only did they have a much better selection, but the staff member helped me immediately, showing me my options and brought out from their inventory exactly what I was looking for that worked best for my needs. He even set up the locks and all i needed to do was mount them. I found that the quality, style and pricing was even better that what REI showed me. The rest of the staff were also super nice and I highly recommend Mountain Air Sports for your outdoor needs!!!

I just bought a Hobie Eclipse Pedal Board from Mountain Air Sports, and was extremely impressed by the service and attention to detail they offered.  I had been looking to buy the Eclipse for over a year, and finally decided to buy one for my birthday.  I looked all over, online, and no one seemed to have one in stock, or the shipping was crazy money.  I finally called Mountain Air and Joey put in a special order for me.  The board came in stock within a week, which was much faster than any other supplier was offering.  The staff took the Eclipse out of the box to ensure it was in good shape and helped us load it into the truck.  Phenomenal customer service and amazing staff!
They have great winter items here. I always get my snowboard waxed with them before a trip and they always do a great job. Support local businesses!

I bought three electric bikes from Mt Air Sports.  They were on back order but kept me up to date on they're arrival.  They called me as soon as they came in and had them ready to go.  They're really helpful and knowledgeable.

The management made me uncomfortable with transphobic comments being overheard. The equipment itself is overpriced. The person I was being helped by was named Izzy and provided very good customer service, but due to the views of the upper management I will not be returning.
Great store and location. We've been going there for years. Everyone is always super nice and helpful. On our visit a few days ago we noticed employees were not wearing masks. What is that about?

Very good customer service, the boot department was very busy when I was there, but Patty left what she was doing at the counter to help me when she found that all the other employees were helping other customers. Patty was very knowledgable about the various brands of boots, how they fit and which ones would be the best for what I was using them for. I did not feel rushed or pressured and was able to try on several different boots in different sizes and found the perfect boots for me. I typically shop at the San Luis obispo store, which is also has very good customer service, but I happened to be in Santa Barbara and decided to check it out. Great customer service at both stores!

Nice products, but very high priced on some items. Unfortunately, we won't patronize this store after today because of a very negative culture among the store clerks. This includes difficulty getting clerks to bring out shoes, reluctance to verbally "sell" items in case when questions were asked, and rushing back to stand around with other employees and discuss recipes. We are accustomed to friendly, "real" people in the outdoors store we normally make our purchases through - maybe this store is for the wealthy tourists that frequent State Street at the beach - and we did not fit that picture to the workers. Really too bad. The store has potential, but the help is a turn-off.

So we wake up and my husband is all like, "Do you want to go on a leisurely hike in Montecito?"

"Of course!"

3 hours later, this loop is a little less than leisurely.

We had done some quick research and read that the trails are not clearly marked but when in doubt, keep right. Keeping right at times involved short cuts with dramatic inclines and getting lost among the oak trees. 

Don't get me wrong, this hike is fantastic. And the view at the top is well worth the sweat. (270 degrees + full coastline.) But unless you're very familiar with the trail, you may encounter a few wrong turns and big climbs. It'd be great if the town would add some more signage to the route or markings on trees. Nevertheless, if you want to take in the beauty that is Santa Barbara and are feeling adventurous, I highly recommend!

The loop on Cold Spring trail is a nice, relatively easy 2-3 hour hiking trail that has you crossing some cool streams and shaded forests for virtually the entire way. If you want to get better views of the city then I would recommend making a detour to the Edison Catway and climb up to the top to get a good view of SB/Montecito. 

Trailhead starts here on Google Maps:
824 East Mountain Dr Montecito, CA 93108

Didn't go all the way up to Camino Cielo (The Road at the top ridge), but I'll have to try next time. Most of the loop does not have views, but with a steady uphill slope you definitely need some decent legs to go up. If you want to go to see some views take the East Fork or turn right on the trail after the bench. If you want Tangerine Falls, turn left of the bench and cross the creek. Bring an extra pair of shoes because after a rain the ground gets damp and the creeks get filled after the rain. For first timers this is a great place to start if you want to like the SB front country.

This is my go-to trail in the SB area.  I LOVE coming here.  I usually do the East fork.  The entire early portion of the trail is in the shade and follows the creek, making for a cool hike.  Once you pass the electrical towers, the trail is a lot dryer and mostly in the sun but still enjoyable.  For a little variation you could do the West Fork of the trail but it is quite a bit shorter.  The West Fork is across the creek at the bench, about a quarter mile in.  The East Fork is about 9 miles roundtrip while the West Fork is 3 to 3.5 miles roundtrip.

If you are short on time there are some awesome views of SB and Montecito from the electrical towers and it's only about a mile and a half up.  This makes for a quick, relaxing hike.

The only thing I don't enjoy about coming here are the large amounts of people who also enjoy it.  I can't stand going out for a relaxing hike only to be followed by a group of 5 women chatting the entire way.  I generally avoid this trail on the weekends because it can become very busy and there is limited parking.  The best time I have found to start out is early weekday mornings.

This is in a residential area with no parking and no outlet. Extremely hard to find. I think this trail is for the residents of this community. Do not waste your time is you are driving through the area and want a hike.

Tried to do this hike on Friday, but GPS took us to El Camino Cielo, which was spectacular, but NOT the trailhead we were looking for.  On Saturday we followed the directions on santabarbara.com and found our trailhead on Mountain Av.  We headed up the East Fork Trail (hoping to do the loop), but found the trail blocked at the second creek crossing.  Instead we took a left after the first crossing and hiked up to Edison Catway for spectacular views of Santa Barbara and Montecito.  We went right at the Catway and found an alternative way down with the help of other hikers.  On the way down we rejoined the loop trail, went left, stopped at Vista Point and returned to Mountain Av, completing this longer loop in about 2 hours and 20 minutes.

Most important thing I wish I knew for this hike was how to get to the trailhead. Don't follow the directions Yelp or Google maps gives you. Or do, but instead of following Hot Springs Rd to the end like they will say, when going north on it, turn left at E Mountain Dr. Go about 150 ft., and there is a lot of 6 spots on your right just before a small bridge over a creek. park here and at the right corner of the parking spots is the trail entrance.

The hike gets very steep about halfway through. Mostly shady but gets pretty open toward the top. Doesn't take long and the views are great. All around an enjoyable quick hike. Stop at Pressed Juicery (about 1.5 miles away) afterwards!

This place is better to go on a hike in the early morning as Monkey and I learn to not go towards the afternoon on a hot sunny day.  Unless you love the heat and the hot sun.  Also would be a good idea to wear long pants as there are a lot of poison oaks along the trail.  

It's steep up and down and the trail gets small so if you have a hard start then its wise to stop there unless you got heart.  It's a tough trail so it's recommended for a fit person.  Walking sticks would be nice too.  If you are the kind that gets dizzy often then it's not a good place to hike as you can fall off the side of the hill.  

But when you get to the top the view is wonderful as you can see the island, ocean, ugly oil rigs, the whole town of Santa Barbara and the harbor. Also I love that along the trail there are lots of shadows to keep direct sunlight from hitting you as I'm the kind of person whom rather hike in the shade then direct sunlight.  It's nice to hike along a runny stream and see the pretty flowers and different animals and bugs.  My favorite part was going down the hill as it's easy for me and I'm the kind of person who don't like climbing hills.  

For some who likes to dip in the streams, don't forget swimsuits, towels, flip flops.  For those who don't like bugs hanging around them I'd recommend bug spray on yourself. 

I'd go back up here again and recommend checking it out.  If a group of senior citizens can do it then you can too.

Start early if it's your first time. I'm an avid hiker and athlete and it took me almost 2 hours to reach the top of Camino de Cielo (I went on dead end paths every so often and they're not so obvious to me). Sometimes I questioned if I was even on a trail, but that made it fun! The view from the top was just gorgeous and I was lucky enough to find to other gals who could take my picture. Once you get closer to the top, the loose gravel makes it tough but interesting. I love a challenge!

If you're looking for a quick and mostly easy hike, this could be the one!

I hiked this with my friend, and we didn't have much time before sundown, so we were racing to get to the top. We almost turned back, but we trudged on and made it to the Montecito overlook in time before the sun set! It was only about 1.5 mi up to get there, and you see a nice overview of the city and the ocean. A few steep parts but not bad, and there were lots of trees for shade. 

Another review says there's poison oak, but I wasn't aware of this and did the hike in shorts and was okay. But now that you've been warned, you might not want to take your chances!

Oh. Em. Gee.

Stop. Don't read another review. Just go! It's a maaagical place with the greatest views. Park in the parking area on Mountain drive. Follow the wooden signs that say 'trail' with the arrow. Cross the creek and climb to the top...the first part is steep, you'll get a good workout, like climbing stairs. Enjoy the view along the way :)

Easy trail with some steep hills BUT it's still easy. There's some waterfalls and the hike is pretty quick. There's a nice view when you get to the top. I remember the trail being pretty shaded so that's nice.

This is a fun little trail for the entire family in the heart of Montecito. The view when you get to the top is amazing. The odd thing is that you are literally walking between mansions for a good portion if the trail. Still, I recommend it.

Beautiful trail! Really woodsy and shaded, if you are not looking to being exposed in the sun. We had a REALLY hard time finding the location, though, and had to ask several people and keep driving about 10-15 past where we thought it was to find it. Steady incline with lots to see! People on trails were friendly and chatty - we even met fellow Bostonians!! I would definitely go back for a longer hike.

This was a very good trail. There were long trails with shade and some without any shade. A lot of rocks. We hiked from E. Mountain Drive to Montecito Peak. About 9 miles round-trip. It took us a little over 6 hours round-trip, but we took a lot of long breaks. 

We started the Cold Spring Trail on the right "Cold Spring Trail" instead of the left "Cold Spring Trail." The first quarter of a mile was an extremely steep incline. It must have been about an 80 degree incline or something, because I had to literally crawl and claw my way up there with my hands ("gnarly" is how my friend described it). If you're not comfortable with climbing, then you can turn left instead of right.

We hiked for maybe an hour or so and made it to the power lines. From there, you can see a lonely eucalyptus tree on top of one of the mountain peaks. It took us about 45 minutes to reach that tree, which had an excellent view and a cool breeze. This is the perfect spot for a picnic or resting stop. 

From there, we continued for another hour up this very rocky trail to Montecito Peak. Once you reach the bottom of that Montecito peak, you can decide if you want to continue to the top. It's very steep (maybe 75 degrees); it was not as steep as that initial climb on the "Cold Spring Trail."  My friend didn't want to climb up there, so I climbed up there by myself with his dog haha. There's a lot of gravel and I did end up crawling up there with my hands. Once you get to the top, it's flat. The view is awesome! You can see Carpinteria and all of Santa Barbara. There is also a box between some large rocks filled with notebooks where you can write something and sign your name. Climbing down was tough, because it was slippery. I had to stay low to the ground, so I would not slip. I saw a humming bird on my way down :). Hiking shoes are highly recommended.

On our way back, we took the left Cold Spring Trail instead, because we did NOT want to risk climbing down that steep incline we experienced at the beginning of our hike. We saw the carved out bench and then an actual bench. It was a nice route, because there was a creek. There is supposed to be a waterfall, too, but it's dried up. It is too bad that every trail I go on now has a dried up creek or waterfall due to the drought.

I should also mention that there is a lot of poison oak at the beginning or bottom of the trail. Once you get fairly up there, you don't see any...or at least, I don't remember seeing any. I still wore shorts, because I do not like hiking in long pants.

This is my absolutely favorite hike in Santa Barbara.      Sometimes I do the "loop"  it is easy and doesn't take very long.   You just follow the trail and any time another trail goes off to the left, don't take it!   At the top you will have some very nice views of Santa Barbara.    If you want you can do it backwards(start from the trailhead that is 50 feet to the right of the main trail head)   If you do it that direction  the first ten minutes is pretty steep.   

From the Cold Spring Trail you can also go to Tangerine falls by turning left and crossing the creek after you pass the bench.   You will have to take a right turn off of the main trail about 20 minutes later but it is quite obvious.   The hike to tangerine falls is a little tougher but so worth it.

The third hike you can do from this trail is all the way to Montecito Peak or the fire road or beyond.   To do that you go around the loop past the summet and take a left turn, when you get to the big power poles go along the service road until you see a trail on your left going up the mountain.  

If you see a ratlesnake I have named him Rodney.

This hike takes the award for "best scenery" so far among coastal SoCal hikes we've done.  The hike to Montecito Peak is 3.7 miles from the trailhead, and to Camino Cielo Road, 4.5 miles - all of it uphill, but of course, the bonus is that it's all downhill going back.  The amazing views of the coastline from northern Santa Barbara almost all the way to Ventura make the moderate uphill (average 10% grade) trek well worth it!

The last 0.15 mile up the side of Montecito Peak is a bit more challenging - 200 feet of vertical gain - but also worth it.  The view is amazing.  However, be careful coming back down.  The soft soil and gravel makes it easy to slip and fall - consider side-stepping down some of the narrower and steeper sections.

The lower two thirds of the trail is well-shaded - a big plus in the summer.   There is also a fair amount of poison oak in these areas as well, but the trail is wide enough, so if you keep your eyes open and watch where you're going you should be fine.  There is almost none once you get about two-thirds of the way up.

We did the hike on a Friday afternoon in July.  We encountered very few other people on the trail past the first two miles from the trailhead.  In fact, we had the top of Montecito Peak, with its stunning views, to ourselves for the whole half-hour or so we spent up there.  ;-)

Again, a beautiful hike, well worth the time spent. 

We found this site to be quite helpful.  I'd suggest printing it out and bringing it along.

http://www.hikespeak.com/trails/montecito-peak-hike-cold-springs-trail-santa-barbara/

Enjoy!

Fun hike... Cool to check out the over the top mansions. The trail is great for pups... Ours had a blast. 

Though the trails are marked, it's difficult to distinguish which is the 2 mile hike and which is .5 

Next time we are in town, we will try a different trail, but it was worth it to check this one out!

Beautiful woodsy, shaded trail with a gradual incline.  I missed the split to do the loop and ended up doing the Montecito Peak which is beautiful.  It's woodsy through out- you will get no sun on this trail up until the Peak.  It doesn't attract too many people, I ran into a couple of first timers and others that appear to live in the area.  Friendly people in this area, always bring water.  Some first timers on this trail didn't have any water, even if shaded you can die from heat exhaustion.  Driving here from the 101 is really pretty, the area is just filled with big green tress on these beautiful park like estate properties.  

Along this trail you will find a split right where the bench is-  That may have been where I should have turn to do the 3 mile loop that I had planned to do but I kept going straight.  Once I crossed the creek there was a sign posted to the left that said Route Closed.  That may have also been the turn to do the loop, I'm not sure.  There are no clear directions on these signs other than at the entrance listing the names of the trails.  So I kept straight and ended up at the peak with no loop.  I would love to come back and explore the turn off's it's so pretty in here.  The incline to the Peak is easy and worth doing.

GREAT FOR KIDS!
If you have kids, this is a very fun little hike because of the many opportunities it gives the kiddies to find frogs and salamanders along the way! 
I'm not talking the full hike, just the bottom parts that have creek crossings. 
Let's be honest, little legs can only go so far .

Just hiked this trail and it's really beautiful right now. The trail is full of green and yellow with a lot of budding flowers. Next week, after all this rain, it's really going to explode in color. Oh and nice little water falls flowing too. Can't wait to hike it again!

We planned to take the Tangerine falls hike which starts on Cold springs and forks to right a mile or so into the hike. Ive reviewd this trail before, however, due to the rain, the trail looks completely different. 
The trail was crowded(we started around noon on a Sat), especially towards the beginning. The "hazardous..."is no longer there so we missed it and had to backtrack. Look instead for the blue  flags. Towards the end, the trail becomes a lot of rock climbing... wear good shoes with tread. In addition, the whole Trail is covered in poison oak making long pants a necessity if you're allergic.

Cold Springs Trail is about 9 miles (roundtrip) of repetition. This isn't meant to diminish the amazing views because there are great views. It's the terrain that is less impressive it's mostly uneven rockiness. You will feel the repetition 10x more on the way down so keep that in mind as you decide on a stopping point. 

There are some lovely scenes while you begin in a more wooded area - water streams that still exist are very peaceful. At some point you'll see a fallen tree converted into a bench. Cute. The electrical towers are only about a mile or two into the hike and the view is almost good enough to just want to stop there. Don't, it gets more challenging at that point and it's worth it to at least make it to the Eucalyptus tree. (3-3.5 mi in?).

Bugs are rampant after the electrical towers, especially on a hot day. I was fortunate they seemed uninterested in me. Weird. The hike ends with a water tower in a little area where you'll see some parked cars. It's a little risky to climb to the water tower because of the privacy its walls provide. Listen first, people could totally get frisky up there. 

The view from the water tower isn't nearly as good as the views before. That's a little disappointing. Return the way you came but be careful it's punishingly repetitive.

Love love love this hike. It's a very easy hike to do in a hurry if you have work later. It's quick and works you out as well as getting amazing views of Santa Barbara. This hike is located in Montecito. I agent been up there since the fire and I'm terrified it was destroyed. This is one of my favorite hikes to go to. And my dog loves it as well since it's not very strenuous. Once u get to the top you get an amazing view and there are always rocks on top in nice like designs you can do yourself. If this hike is still in place after the fire. I would recommend checking it out!!

Beautiful view of the sunset every single night. The water also seemed warmer than other beaches in the area. It was very clean with very few people. Definitely one of the best beaches I've ever been to and will definitely go again!

Shh!  Tell nobody about this beautiful beach.  It is clean and quiet.  Free parking.  Bring an umbrella and watch the local surfers.
Several dogs wandering around...shared our shade until the owners tracked them down.
Really was a 'friendly' beach.
Best kept secret.......this beach is amazing. You can bei g ur dog ur kids ur beer ur cooler. Its great. Views are also great. Water was cold vut expected

We stopped here on our way from Santa Barbara to Los Angeles and what a good thing we did. Apart from the fact that Santa Claus beach is a very clean and pleasant place to go for a swim and lie in the sun, we also were lucky enough to see dolphins very near to the shore. It was an experience I wouldn't like to have missed.

I guess I should probably start of saying that this is definitely one of the nicest beaches I've seen in SoCal; and saying I come from Hawaii, hopefully adds some gravity to that statement.  Haha.   

We were there on a Saturday afternoon and stumbled upon it completely by mistake. The sand is nice, soft, white and clean, the waves were actually somewhat breaking along the shore, it was nice peaceful and quite, with hardly anyone there.  Parking is free and along the railroad tracks, and the beach is right there on the other side of the tracks.  If you  find yourself getting hungry, there are a lot of great restaurants a stone's throw away at the end of the beach.

I guess one more thing to note, while we were there a wedding party showed up and started taking wedding pictures there along the train tracks.  Maybe that can also attest to its photogenicity?  Hahah.

I would definitely recommend checking this place out, but shhhh, let's keep it our little secret.

Absolutely gorgeous, and a great place to swim in the otherwise freezing cold water of SoCal. This beach is nice and sandy, and very clean. The water is great for swimming and surfing, as the waves are quite large. There's a train track right as you enter, which makes it even more picturesque. Be careful though, it's active with Amtrak running service every hour or so. Parking can be a problem, but we got lucky and found a place right away. A great find.

After visiting Padaro Beach Grill,  we wondered about its namesake beach.  Checked it out online and found that access is via the road.  No set parking, and not well marked, there is a pathway just beyond the new houses on Santa Claus Lane,  where a chemical toilet is placed.

You carefully (!!!) cross the railroad tracks, scamper over big rocks, then land on sugar-fine powdery sand as the beach begins.

We were there after dinner as twilight was coming on.  There were still kids frolicking in the waves on a warm Saturday evening.

There are no lifeguards, and you should plan to take out everything you take in, including your trash.  The only toilet is the one out by the road.

This beach has a serene setting at the northwest end of Carp, with mountains as a backdrop if you turn away from the ocean.

Visit this beach!  Clean, good waves for boogie boarding, easy access, and free street parking.  I like the steeper slope of the beach near the water because once I set up my beach mat and umbrella at the top of the slope, I didn't have to worry about high tide coming in and making me relocate.  I also prefer this beach to East Beach because there were minimal sand flies and no washed up seaweed drying out attracting the flies.  Con- a few tar globs out there.  Olive oil worked to get it off, but it's still yucky dealing with it.

The best beach in Santa Barbara County, if not all of Southern California.

Beautiful walk up and down! 
Perfect for a first date little excursion. Pack your blanket and a bottle of wine (boys take notes).
Always an amazing sunset.
Right off the freeway. 
Little waves but enough to get a little surf/boogie on. 

Always a good time! Please check it out.

One of the best beaches I have visited. Take the Santa Claus exit on 101 south and you end up parallel to the railway tracks. Just park along the tracks or the small parking spot. We were there around 8.30 am and few parking spots available and beach was empty. We spend about 30 minutes walking along the beach. We passed the same beach around 11 am and it was crowded. So get early.
We ate lunch at Padaro Grill today and then swung by this nearby beach to experience the water! The sand was super hot but the beach was clean. Very busy!!

This beach is epic, I didn't want to review because I was trying to keep it to myself. I didn't want to be sefish lol. It's a beautiful beach every angel you get a perfect picture. Whether it's the mountains, beach or the boats out in the distance. Not too many people was there when I went which made it perfect. The people were super nice and the trip to this beach was peaceful.

One of the hidden gems in Santa Barbara. There's not a lot of people and the place is quiet and very relaxing. I absolutely love this beach. Since it's not a "touristy" type beach, parking is quite a chore. You park on the side of a road by the bushes and then across the bushes is the Amtrak tracks which is fully functioning. Across the tracks is where the beach is. There's a bit of a dune that you have to get through. Like a really tiny one but once you get there , it's so pretty. I still don't know why it's called Santa Claus. Have fun relaxing!

The beach is epic...love Carpenteria I have so many good childhood memories with my family hanging out at Santa Claus beach.
Just make sure you bring your big hat and plenty of sunblock:) 
 Have fun Sharon Van Ert

This is a very beautiful beach! The Amtrak goes by. Right next to restaurants. Parking right in the street and the beach is right there. Alit kids on boogie boards. No lifeguards though on end section where we were. Very peaceful.

Favorite beach in Santa Barbara area. The sand is always so clean witch a beautiful flat area. This is definitely a local favorite and it's always cool to cross the railroad tracks to get there. There's a little rock wall that offers some nice places to sit if you forgot your towel. Busy on the weekends but so peaceful on weekday mornings. 

Gorgeous views of Santa Barbara as well as the mountains. And despite it being right of the freeway, no traffic noise - what more could you ask for?

Best secluded beach (on weekdays lol). During the weekday this beach barely has any people on it which is great because social distancing. I came on the weekend a few weeks ago and it was kinda busy but not as busy as the more popular beaches in the area. I've been coming every week just to enjoy the sunset. I believe this beach is dog friendly. I brought my dog and other people had their dogs.

The beach is actually called Padaro Beach but the locals nicknamed it Santa Claus Beach. Whatever you call it, it seems to be more of a locals beach. It's not too big such as miles worth of sand, but it's a decently sized spot; nearly a mile along I would think. 

We checked out the beach for a couple hours while headed to Santa Barbara and it was nice. You'll have to cross some railroad tracks, walk on a path with huge rocks around, and then you'll be on the beach. The waves seemed to be pretty mild for all sorts of fun. 

To be honest, I don't know if there's a restroom around, there might be a porta potty or two however. Parking is street only and along the shoulder

There's some restaurants, shops, and rental places in the locale. For some food, check out the nearby Padaro Beach Grill!
Hands down the best beach to bring your dog to. It's beautiful, clean and so peaceful. The locals are really nice and there are little shops/eateries right next door.

Came back here a year later on one of the busiest holiday weekends (Labor Day). We came on Saturday and Sunday. Saturday we arrived at noon and we had to park far down past the little strip of shops and restaurants. But the beach was actually not too crowded. 

We went back on Sunday at 11 and parking was much easier this time. So I do recommend if you are coming on a weekend to aim for 10-11 for best parking. 

Be careful about parking near the beginning parking with the first outhouse bathroom because when we left, we observed that people decided to park behind the cars that parked there first (perpendicular parking to the railroad track) which was blocking cars in front of them in. 

There was also a man there selling fresh tropical fruit cups that looked delicious. 

Such a beautiful day. There were a lot of people boogie boarding this day and luckily there is a Surf Shop a short walk down the beach where you can rent one for the day. Next time we are gonna rent them. The waves seemed perfect for boogie boarding

We also saw dolphins both days we were there and yes, even 2 sharks on the second day. The sharks didn't seem to be remotely curious to the beach goers. They were just swimming a straight line south of the beach shore about 20-30 feet past most of the swimmers which was also on the same swimming route we saw the dolphins at.

Incredible.

Spent a week nearby in Santa Barbara and came here 5 dif days. Swimming was incredible in EARLY JANUARY. Kids were sunbathing and building sand castles while I was boogie boarding and celebrating the new year. Other guys were surfing and playing etc.

Right near by... walking distance... from delicious Padaro Beach Grill. Dope beach all day thru sunset. Total gem.

We ALL know where Santa resides during the cold winter months..

But where does he go after cozying up next to the fire place sippin on hot cocoa all winter long??


If you said Montego Bay, Jamaica...

You're close! 

The jolly ol' Saint Nick actually heads to Carpinteria, CA and relaxes on his very own 'Santa Claus Beach'. 


Located in Santa Barbara County, this beach is a unique spot where locals and tourists alike come together to enjoy the very best of Southern California. 

What I love about this beach is the way you access it. In order to get onto the beach itself, you must cross over an unprotected rail road track that has actual Amtrak trains go by at various times of the day. 

My Recommendation:

Sure, you can pack a picnic and enjoy the waves as you eat your peanut butter & jelly, but my suggestion and trust me, you will Thank Me Later is first finding a parking spot, then eating at Padaro Beach Grill (see my review of PBG) then making your way to Santa Claus beach which is literally right next door. 

Bring a book, bring your sunscreen & shades and prepare for a relaxing day at the beach. 

I will say that Parking can be a challenge especially on the weekends, so plan ahead and get there early. 

And remember, if you happen to see Santa chillen on the beach, remember to remind him you've been good this year and you can't wait to see what he leaves you under your tree.

I found this to be my favorite beach near Santa Barbara! There's beautiful houses lining it in case you want to dream of being rich ;) this beach is not as packed as all the other Santa Barbara ones I had been at. Overall a great time :)

We liked that this beach was a bit different in how we got there; park on the side of the road and walk across the train tracks.

Not a lot of people on this beach. Thats a huge plus.

Definitivamente would make another stop here if ever passing through.

This is a great FREE beach. You will park across the railroad tracks and hop, skip and jump to the beach. 

This is a dog friendly beach so expect to hear random dogs barking and frolicking in the ocean chasing a ball or two. 

The beach was pretty clean with an occasional abandoned dog poop bag left behind but I actually found people are more respectful and clean up after their dogs here than on Arroyo Burro beach. 

We came here late in the afternoon, about 4pm and stayed until 7pm. It was just enough for us to catch some sun and play for a bit. There was a slight breeze but it didn't make me cold or anything. I was able to withstand wearing just my swim suit the entire time. I would come back here again. Especially after paying $10 for Carpinteria state beach. This beach is just as nice only there is some seaweed washed up on the beach. But no bugs. 

Kind of interesting to see the trains go by every 30 min or so. The conductors always toot at the beach goers to say hello. 

There are port-a-potties by where you park. We didn't use them on our visit though.
Very nice and wild, it doesn't feel at all like u are basically in the city. When low tide, mixed of volcanic black rocks (very beautifully shaped!) And sand. Not for swimming though.

This is a nice little trek to take on a sunny California day at low tide. It is accessible from a side street off Shoreline Drive ( Santa Cruz Blvd). There are a few parking spots on this side street dead end but it  might require a walk.
The pathway down isn't 1000 steps. Though I didn't count, I'd say maybe 150 and the lower 50 can be slippery as they are wet and covered with moss. At the end however you are rewarded with a deserted beach and a great quiet spot to enjoy the view. We went at low tide and there was a huge expanse of sand. We walked toward Santa Barbara and around a bend found a Princess cruise ship docked just off the nearby marina.
Only one other group was on the beach; it was very peaceful! I really enjoyed this hidden treasure!

This spot is awesome. I love to walk along the beach here. It's not as crowded as Hendry's with all the dogs running around. If you hit this place at low tide you can actually walk for miles. There's a hidden little ledge under the stairs where I have spent sone time watching the ocean and people watching. And you get a killer workout just by climbing up and down.
Great workout just go up and down a few times and they should do it. Not really a thousand steps. Definitely a good workout and you have the ocean to cool off in

I do not think this beach is named for the number of stairs to walk down to it.   There are only 232 stairs,  however it is steep and quite a workout going both up and down especially when carrying all your junk.  There is some nearby street parking if you get there early.  I got there at 1130 on a Saturday and parked across the street in hospital parking lot for $5...

There is a free trolley in the summer ending 08/30/15 that you can take into town.  Going into town I waited 5 minutes for the trolley and it made about 16 stops on the way toward Main Beach or Laguna St.  Coming back I waited 20 minutes then gave up and called an Uber who showed up in 3 minutes and was $6 back to my car.  I still never saw the Trolley.

Now back to the beach.  My guess is the beach is named because it takes 1000 steps to walk from one end to the other.  I found the beach very nice.  It was not crowded at 11:30 when I arrived but was crowded at 4:30 when I left.  

The water is not calm but had decent sized frequent waves to play in.  There were tons of people in the water and lots of guys doing that skim board surfing thing.

I found it to be clean.  There is one bathroom and a shower at the bottom of the stairs..

There are some large exposed rock formations that made for some good sights of waves splashing into the shore.

There were lifeguards on duty.  There are some caves at both ends of the beach and there are lots of houses on the bluff overlooking  the beach.

I drove 1:45 to get to this beach and would do it again even though there are dozens of beaches closer to my house.
It is 1000 steps. Skip your stair climber if your going to hit this beach.  It was a quiet change from the crowd scene at sunset on Shoreline Park. Clean, quiet and comfortable.

It's a nice, secluded place to spend some time relaxing. I saw little to no trash scattered on the beach. People say to wear shoes when walking on the beach, but we were able to walk, and even run a little bit while barefoot! There were lots of smooth stones to skip. The waves weren't too high, but the tide was so we didn't get to see the tide pools (if there were any.) Saw lots of dead lobsters that were probably eaten by other animals too! Pretty cool if you like those types of things.

This place is magical. Once you find parking (tip - arrive early to get one of the few spots on the street directly connected to the staircase) the staircase is easy to find and the walk down is quick. In my opinion, the staircase is smaller than some of the other cliff side beaches. It's important to note that the staircase can be slippery when wet. 
The actual beach itself is immaculate and the view is stunning. Many people come here to walk their dogs and let them play so do not be surprised of the leash less friends.
The stairs are so dirty and slimy..I been to Better beaches ..getting down the beach is only bad part...once u get down all see the pipes of the houses where the dirty water goes in to the ocean.

What a hidden gem! Coming from out of state, we wanted to hit up a beach that was out of the way so we can take pictures. It was absolutely gorgeous. It was very small as far as the sand width, but went down for miles. I was so happy we found this place, our pictures were priceless.

Nice hidden private beach area located right after the Shoreline Park. Look for the sign and thats where the steps start!

Not really a thousand steps but its close to a hundred. As you get closer to the bottom the steps get more slippery so be careful. 

Very clean and dog friendly area.

Cool spot. Thankfully the steps are not actually one thousand but there are several long flights ( for those who may be physically limited). Makes for great pictures. Beach immediately near steps  is narrow and rocky, though there may be more space further down. Generally though Id say this is a spot for pictures and a short walk, not for an actual beach hang out.

Tip: steps are eroded and wet so be prepared and step carefully.

This was a great hidden gem to find! I don't think this is a popular beach especially since there is barely any sand to lay and relax.  The ocean meets the shoreline pretty close. Street parking is available and free! My friends and I went during sunset and it was beautiful! The "thousand" steps are to your left (when facing the ocean) and once you reach the bottom, the ocean is literally right there. We found a spot to the right after walking over many rocks and leaned back and enjoyed the sunset.

Santa Barbara has many wonderful beaches with people and dogs, but this is not one of them. From trash, to seaweed, to garbage, this is one of the most disgusting beaches California's Mediterranean has to offer. If you have a pet, then it might be a nice place to stop on by, but if not, then visit one of Santa Barbara's other beaches. My personal feelings would not recommend this beach, but as many people do enjoy it's views, I can't advice all to not stop by.
Great small beach to chill, get a tan, or walk your dog. Parking is scarce, but just look around the neighborhood.

I came here to eat some poke and drink green tea for lunch and it was perfect. There wasn't a lot of people, but the people that were there all brought their dogs!! So I thought that was great. 

Oh and it's not really a thousand steps, so don't get intimidated! :)

Not sure if it was one thousand steps, but there sure was a lot. Going down the stairs is easy, a bit slippery so be cautious. Going back up was a mission for some of us, but kids enjoyed it. It's a private little section and kept well cleaned. This is why we liked it a lot.

Beautiful beach to walk on. Very quiet and not a ton of people. 152-ish steps down. The parking was less than to be desired, no marked parking, had to park on busy neighborhood street in front of a house. Otherwise I would visit again.

It was nice.  I would say close to 1000 steps Whew!  I went down once to look.  Came up to get my chair and drinks. Went down again and stayed around till sunset. Beautiful view lot of doggies too. Parking after 4 is probably more available. 

No bathrooms here so be sure to do your business prior.
1000 steps beach was one of the prettiest beaches I've ever been to. It's not actually One Thousand steps so the views at the bottom were well with it!
Love this beach. Not too many people. Stairs going down to the beach  were slippery and needs repair.

The name is deceiving... There's only 150 steps.  Or 300 if you count going down then climbing back up...  (I counted).  Nonetheless, it's worth every heart-pumping step.

One Thousand Steps Beach is one of my favorite secluded gems in Santa Barbara to take visitors for a sandy stroll.  You have the beautiful Mesa bluffs covered in sprawling plants and vines with a direct view of the Channel Islands in the distance.  If you walk all the way east, you can also see downtown Santa Barbara given that it's very low tide.

Hints & Tips:
-To find it, head up the Mesa and turn toward the ocean onto Santa Cruz Boulevard.  You can only park on the right side of the cul-de-sac street (three cars worth), so if it's already full, park on Cliff Drive instead.  Look for the wooden sign and stairs.
-Be sure to check the tide schedule.  If it's high, the beach barely exists.
-Bring solid shoes in the steps and rocks are very slippery.
-You come here to stroll, not to swim, unless you're an experienced swimmer or surfer.

Cute beach where you can walk a long way along the shore. However, it's highly recommended you check on the tides since high tide can come really far up the shoreline. Luckily, I came on a low tide day, and the water was receding into the ocean. I peeked into some of the tide pools, but only saw a lot of kelp. 

It was overcast and rare super foggy day in Santa Barbara (https://www.yelp.com/user_local_photos?select=Jrqc-oYBw0Ua8cgkFtjwoA&userid=76vIFZcC7owykCBjMfGVXg, https://www.yelp.com/user_local_photos?select=5uUXjhjYjJESkysRXKdxzQ&userid=76vIFZcC7owykCBjMfGVXg) and a lot was obscured by fog, but it was still relaxing to watch the waves and the ocean.

There aren't 1,000 steps to walk down to reach the beach (https://www.yelp.com/user_local_photos?select=O6KO9CdKcL44ae1iRzyveg&userid=76vIFZcC7owykCBjMfGVXg), and it's more like a few flights of stairs. The steps are really wet halfway down, and you'll need to be careful walking down since it's slippery. The last step is also super high off the ground, and a small jump for me to reach the beach. However, I thought the effort was worth it, and I really enjoyed walking along the beach.

Parking in this area can be tough, but there was street parking available in the residential area.

1000 Step Beach that is not really a 1000 steps, LOL, but is definitely worth the trip to walk and explore. It is beautiful, clean, peaceful and not a lot of people (at least the day we went). Here's a couple helpful hints that may be useful.

1) A MUST, check when High tide and Low tide are, because you can't get walking access on the beach when it is high tide.
2) If you miss the street to turn down to park OR if you can't find parking because it is residential street parking, just drive down the road to Shore Line Park which is beautiful too, and park for free in a lot. There is also step access down to the beach near the bathrooms.

My friend and I missed the 1000 stair street, drove to Shore Line Park, parking area, walked toward the bathrooms, found stairs down to the beach, walk on the beach to the 1000 stairs and walked up the 1000 stairs back to the car. 

Just simply beautiful!
Lovely spot. Not a workout at all...our two year old walked it. 149 steps to be exact but a fun experience anyway.

Not much of a beach, nor much parking, but street parking is easy to find. There are about 100-150 steps to get down to the strip of sand. It was a shame I came during high tide and there was a wedding happening when I arrived so I waited until the ceremony was done before heading down. Check the tide tables before coming! It does get super crowded on the weekends so don't expect much of this place.

If you haven't been to this historical area then I suggest you bookmark it and make it a point to visit! Just because it's called One Thousand Steps Beach doesn't mean there are that many steps so don't be discouraged. It's a little over 100 steps. My suggestion would be to wear shoes you don't care to ruin but are sturdy enough to withstand the mossy, slimy, wet steps as you venture down to the beach. The steps are slippery so be careful! 

Once you get to the end of the steps you will have to be extra cautious and use your balancing skills to get down. There's a ton of rocks below that you can step on so take your time and don't injur yourself! Don't mean to scare anyone here just keeping it real but I've seen grandmas trek down the steps onto the beach so if they can do it anyone can! 

The location is at the dead end of Santa Cruz Blvd just off Shoreline Drive but the parking is very limited on this street so I recommend parking on Shoreline Drive where there is plenty of street parking.

If the tide is high you won't be able to get down to the beach but if you're lucky to go at low tide then you will be able to explore everything this hidden beach has to offer. Tide pools, sea shells, beautiful rocks, star fish, crustaceans and much much more! 

There aren't many people who sunbathe here but that's not to say you couldn't. You will see lots of dogs here but they are happy dogs running along the beach enjoying themselves just as we do. 

Overall this beach is definitely a hidden gem and great place for taking pictures. Bookmark it!

During my mini getaway to Santa Barbara, I noticed this on my map and decided to make an early morning walk to see this beach, and it was stunning! I went early morning so it was high tide, and the water was all the way up to the steps, but I didn't mind. I would suggest checking when high/low tide is if you plan on walking along the beach. The view from the top of the stairs was breathtaking. It was super quiet. I only saw 1 other person coming down to see it, but I went at dawn. The next day I went at dawn, I saw someone doing stretches/yoga at the top of the stairs. 

While it wasn't exactly 1000 steps, there are plenty of steps, a little slippery from water. You'll definitely feel the burn in your legs as you make your way back up the steps. 

Parking is super limited as it's entrance is a small street in a residential area. 

Would I come again? Next time I'm staying in Santa Barbara.

Nice little beach to visit, but nothing in particular to do there. You walk down the 150 steps and...there's the beach. I read that I should come at low tide to see tide pools, and according to the internet I was there at low tide, but I didn't see any tide pools. A nice beach for kids to run around at... quiet, no sunbathers, etc.

Wow! It is beautiful down there! Very few people so no mass gatherings. My wife and I enjoyed the sunset and the walk on the sand. It's false advertising as there was only 151 steps! What's that 15.1% of the stated total! But not fret as the view and exercise () going up was all worth it! It's at the end of Santa Cruz blvd. Easy to locate. We then walked to Shoreline park a few blocks away. Breath taking or maybe it was the stairs or both!

This beach is worth the walk down the steps. Once you find the beach, which Google Maps will take you to if you plug in "2 Santa Cruz Blvd, Santa Barbara," just head down the steps. 

The steps can be slippery  -- as they were when we went. Many of the steps were wet and muddy and slick. And the steps at the end that lead you onto the rocky beach are crumbled/eroded. I wish I had sneakers instead of flip flops because I slid on the rocks. 

BUT, it is a gorgeous beach to walk. Many dogs running around. Kids LOVE it. There are tide pools and a spectacular sunset. Not crowded at all. 

It's not 1000 steps as other Yelpers have said & definitely pay attention to the tides or you'll miss out!

Was looking for some good stairs in the area to keep up my exercise routine while away and wow!! These are such a fun alternative!! I'm so glad I found them and will definitely be back! So, the name is a tad misleading - there aren't one thousands steps - there are around 150 stairs down to the beach....still a decent amount! 

Up at the top is a nice viewing area that has safety fencing. The entrance is obvious and marked with a sign as well. There's a decent sized platform about halfway down...but be cautious bc it was at about that point where the stairs were absolutely soaked with water all the way down. They weren't terribly slippery but it's definitely an issue if you're trying to exercise. You can't go at any kind of fast pace. 

I was there right at high tide and the waves were crashing on the bottom couple of stairs, so it's definitely a good idea to check the tide tables before going - plan ahead. I went ahead and visited during high tide bc the timing of low tide  was crazy for me. But you can tell there's a really nice beach at low tide! 

The top half of the stairs and the midway platform are shaded nicely ...but the bottom half of stairs are exposed to full sun. The shade is provided by some lovely trees - such a nice area! The entrance is in a rather residential area and parking is all street parking but I had no issues finding a spot right by the entrance - however I was there on a Wednesday morning. I imagine weekends/holidays get packed.

It is not literally "One Thousand Steps" it was pretty short...

This beach was pretty empty when I went on a Tuesday during Spring Break. It's a very clean beach with a lot of rocks & pebbles. 

Parking is located in a neighborhood so it's free.

I was on an adventure. A Mini-Adventure. I didn't have much money, but yet I wanted to see something new and fun. So, I plotted a trip out to Santa Barbara to see a beach. This one was way cool. Especially the trek down to it from the parking area.

     I had been poking around downtown Santa Barbara when I suddenly decided I wanted to walk in the water. I plugged "Beach" into Yelp and the nearest one was this one.  I drove the short distance, and managed to find a parking spot just over the stairs leading doward. I took a moment to enjoy the vista from this high point, and then began to carefully climb down the stairs. Carefully. They're slick. They're wet. There's fuzzy moss that covers some of it. It's a challenge for sure. 
     My foot slipped out from under me at one point, and for a split second, I saw myself comically falling down the complete length of the staircase bonking and owing my way down.  Thankfully, I landed solidly on my butt. Where there was enough padding and square area to secure myself from taking a long and painful tumble. (this is where the star falls off....because Owtch!)
     I carefully and cautiously regained my footing, and resumed the descent on the thousand steps to the shoreline below. The steps do seem ancient; and their base has been eroded by however many decades of ocean waves lapping at it. I got down to the bottom and looked around. This beach is nice! It's not very long, and there are barely any people at it.    
    I was greeted by a pair of young beauties in skimpy bathing suits, and I asked if they could help me out with a photograph. But beyond them, I only ran into a few more souls down here. There seemed to be natural lil alcoves formed by erosion and wear on the rocks. How fascinating!
    I walked along the waters edge for a lil while and admired the scenery. The water washing my feet every few moments, the creatures that scurried about, the salty smell wafting in the air. It was a good time. Relaxing. Serene.  After an hour or so of walking along the short beach, I returned to the stairs.
     I carefully stepped up them. One at a time. Careful. Being extra careful on my ascent back up to my car. Climbing up took more time, but once I got back up to the top I took another moment to enjoy the view. This beach is super cool. I had a ton of fun exploring it. If you're in the area, I highly suggest you check it out. 

          And remember to be careful on those steps down!

One Thousand Steps Beach isn't the beach you should head for when you want to take a swim; swimming is far too dangerous here!  Also, you really need to be careful if you come here at high tide.

That said, if you want to see an incredible sunset, this is totally the right place to go!  The vistas at any time of day are World Class, too :-)

Whether you grab a little bite here on a lunch break, come for a walk, or just to watch the sunset, it's a beautiful place to enjoy the wonders of nature and to see the ocean at it's best.

Tip: Sometimes you can watch whales and dolphins from the steps :-)

This beach is kind of a hidden find under the arch, in the middle of Shoreline Park. There's tons of parking in the two lots that frame Shoreline Park and street parking available in the neighborhoods across the street, as well. Once you park and walk to the center you will see a tall wooden arch with about 100 something cement stairs going downwards - You have arrived.

This beach is great for long walks on low tide days, tide pool adventures on very low tide days, shell hunting, beautiful ocean pictures, sunsets, watching the sail boats, and running both north and south when the tide generally allows. I wouldn't recommend it as a great beach for swimming or fishing as it is very rocky and the currents tend to be rough. 

I personally love stopping by this beach at the end of the day on my way home from work to have a quick walk with some fresh air. I love how easy parking is, how it's never that crowded, and the sunsets are usually to die for. If you're looking for another close romantic sunset spot check out the Shoreline Park Yelp page and view the pictures of the picnic benches overlooking the ocean and sunset!

Well I guess I need to go back to give it a fair rating. We arrived at 10:30am and it was high tide already. So we did not have the opportunity to go down to the beach but just go down the stairs. View was beautiful regard less. Parking is on the street so pretty simple. How many steps are there really? Shh! Only 150! But you don't have to tell your friends that when you take your selfie ; )   Yes stairs can be slippery. But very easy to climb. Now the thousand step beach by Dana Point is a different story. That is an amazing place but yes it felt like a thousand steps. This one is quite easy.

This place is such a cute quaint find if you're in the Mesa area!  We came when there was high tide so we didn't get to walk on the beach, but the views from the bottom are absolutely stunning.  

I wouldn't say there are 'one thousand steps' but there are quite a bit ~ maybe 200 or so.  Be careful though!  These steps are pretty wet esp with the surfers walking up/down.  

This place is truly a hidden treasure!  I'm so glad we went!

Summary: Not actually 1000 steps, nice beach with great view but not the typical amenities of a bathroom, shower, etc at beachside.

Direct your GPS to the address on Yelp, and you'll have no issue finding this little beach. First things first, it's NOT REALLY 1000 STEPS, so don't be deterred from coming here based on the name. Parking is easy as well, I just parked on the residential street. 

I went on a rainy day, so there was no crowd at all, I'm not sure how it gets on a nice day. It was a bit later in the evening, so it was fun watching the tide get closer and closer to me. At high tide, the water seems to rise above the steps, so there is some erosion at the very bottom, making the steps somewhat slippery. It's a wonderful place to watch the sunset!
Nice place to visit. Check the tide schedule. We were there when there wasn't much beach.  Still it was a nice little cardio boost to start your day.

Gorgeous, gorgeous, gorgeous beach!!! Don't be fooled its really not a thousand steps however it would be a nice workout if it was but no its not. Maybe like 100 steps. The sucky part is that midway down the steps its all wet and muddy omg there is so much mud its kinda gross especially for those who are wearing sandals or walking barefoot. This was my only pet peeve!

Other than that when we came here this place wasnt too packed. There were a lot of joggers, walkers walking their dogs but they let their dogs loose.  I didn't see anyone holding their dog on the leash. They were some dogs barking at each other but thankfully no dogs attacking humans. Because mind you I'm scared of dogs!!! :( 

The water was nice the sand was pretty the beach was pretty. I definitely would come back. Not the best beach I've been to but definitely one I enjoyed and so did my son.
