Seeing him for the 1st time actually playing in the water was the greatest thing for me to watch. He really enjoyed himself and that makes me sooo happy!!! You can get some killer pictures of the sunset and just the overall beach too.

Google maps will take you straight to the entrance. First of all, don't get intimidated--there aren't really 1,000 steps. Probably a little less than 100. Secondly, the steps leading to the beach are uneven so please be careful. And lastly, the view on the steps is HEART EYES EMOJIS for days. I'm so glad we got to the beach early because it was completely EMPTY. I would highly recommend doing the same. It was SO quiet and relaxing. All you hear are waves.

Originally constructed in the 1920s, the stairs have never been restored and are marked by aged patina and slow decay. The lower you go the more pools of water are gathered on the steps but the stairs are so well worn there are uneven surfaces with more grip.

Steps go right up to the water. My wife tried counting and lost track but it's definitely not a thousand. It's easy going down when excited but after roasting in the sun the climb back up will have you sweating. 

Check tides for the day, at 1:30pm it was pretty high and it would be a bummer if you visited and there was nowhere to walk or you end up walking too far and had to slog through water on the way back. It's a narrow beach but not too many people come here so very peaceful.

Google maps will take you right to where they are but you can't park on the cul de sac where the stairs are loc-lactated, plenty of street parking can be found close by.

This beach is great for exploring and taking a walk.  The address on Yelp is more accurate than the one Trip Advisor uses.  2 Santa Cruz Blvd is actually someone's house...oops!  The entrance of the beach is next to this house.  There are some parking spots in this cul de sac, but there is not much space.  The best parking is probably at the nearby park or on the street.  

There are actually not 1,000 steps leading to the beach.  I counted only 150.  It rained the night before my visit, so the steps were extremely slippery and muddy.  I'm so glad I wasn't barefoot - it is really gross and mossy.  I saw many dog owners with their pets on this beach, but it was empty for the most part.  I didn't stay late enough for sunset, but I'm sure it would have been beautiful.  The beach is very rocky and full of small pebbles.  I worried about high tide prohibiting me from exploring the beach, but it was not bad.  There's a bunch of small pools and enough rocks to walk on if you don't have sandals.

Not a swimming beach, and be careful about high tide. However... sunsets from here are amazing, and the views during the day are simply world class. I've gone here for lunch breaks, breakfast walks, sunset, everything. It's just incredible. I've watched whales, dolphins, etc from the steps. Seriously- very very beautiful. Just go and have a walk. You'll be happy you did. You'll leave relaxed.
Beautiful easy hike/walk to the beach. The scenery and nature are breath taking. Great day out with the family, friends or by yourself.   Dogs are welcome are welcome on this hike.

What an awesome sight!  The seals sat sunning themselves while we on Carpinteria Bluffs watched gleefully, it cheered us to see those so much like we with our breakfast bellies and hearts full of love.

A beautiful place to come to see nature!

Great spot to hike and see the seals. Very scenic cliff overlooking the ocean. It's along a beautiful hiking trail great for exercise or fun. Lots of people, so you feel safe. Bring pennies to put on the oft-used train tracks! :)

This is a beautiful place but PLEASE DON'T GO CLOSE TO THE SEALS!! There are signs that say you can't go near them or pass the sign and you will be federally prosecuted if you violate this. During mating season they cannot be disturbed, otherwise their babies WILL DIE. The babies and the mom's need to bond otherwise the mom will not care for them and they will die of starvation. Whenever there's a disturbance the seals will move and go in the water and the babies and the moms will get lost from each other. So please please look at them from the cliff, but don't go too close to it for your own safety. There are volunteers that make sure people aren't going near the seals, they're great!
What a privilege to witness the beauty of the seals in their natural habitat. There is a volunteer at the edge of the cliff with binoculars you may borrow and so much wonderful information.
Great walk and scenery.  Very peaceful.  We saw 3 seals swimming about.   You can visit the tar pits and also a beach.

Currently the parking lot near the beach is marked as private property and is patrolled by security, but parking along Carpinteria Bluff Trail Drive is only about a quarter mile walk away. 

It's definitely worth a visit to see mother nature in all her glory and only a few minutes off the freeway. 

I highly recommend binoculars if you have them.  Luckily a super nice woman volunteer allowed me to use hers. She also was a wealth of information about the wonderful creatures living in this area that deserve preservation and our respect.

I read reviews before visiting this location so I did not expect to see any seals. I heard the best time to see a lot of seals sunbathing on the beach is December through May. We went there today and were happy to see two seals playing in the water. No seals were seen on the shore. It was still fun to see the two seals splashing around, having fun. 
Easy walk and beautiful coastal views.

One of the better places with a view that I've visited while I was here in Santa Barbara. We were hoping to see some seals, and we did! Although you couldn't go down there (I think because it was mating season when I was there), you can still catch a good glimpse from the top. 

On the way there from the parking lot, depending which direction you walk, there's a train track too, so it's a perfect place to capture some nice photos with an awesome view there. Definitely a huge plus since one of the main points of my trip was to take some stunning photos.

A fun little look out spot along the Carpinteria Bluffs. Do not follow the address on this Yelp page, instead drive toward Viola Field and you will see the turn on the right into a parking lot to take the trail. Less then a 10 minute walk to get there. You will have to cross the train tracks. So much fun watching the seals!

SEALS! 

Mother nature deserves 5 stars.

I only wish you can go all the way down to the beach and play with or pet them

WE couldn't see any seals, but we sure could hear every last one of them barking away somewhere off in the distance.  Darn.  It's hit or miss, but they are there.

Yet and still, 

the view is gorgeous, so make the short trek from the parking lot anyway to get a bit of exercise and sun if nothing else.  Be careful bringing small kids.  Make sure you watch them.  Parts of the view has steep drops if you're not careful.

A beautiful hike to a wonderful sanctuary of watching seals lay and do seal things.  We watched the seals lay there, watch us, scuttle, roll, bark, and even saw little babies.  In another corner we saw a gang of cranes, crows, and seagulls.  The views from the bluffs were gorgeous. 

The hike itself is around 1 mile round trip and flat.  You do have to cross the railroad tracks, so it is not wheelchair accessible.  Easy for elderly who are able to walk with a cane for that distance and children, but be careful with the bluffs.  There are viewing benches provided to take in the serene views.

What a lovely stop with my 3 young kids along HWY 101! 

Parking was tricky as WAZE took us to a private lot and we were directed by a worker. Public lot is 1/2 mile further east on Carpinteria near the next stop sign.

The walk is flat, initially very sunny but then shady and lovely with beautiful views of the water. Watch for trains as the tracks are active; a short train passed as we approached. 

The bluff overlooking the seals is small, but easy to socially distance. 

There is also a trail down to the beach along the path. It is uneven, but has some form of asphalt, so isn't slippery. It narrows and gets rocky towards the bottom, but leads to a nicely secluded rocky beach. 

Overall a really easy and lovely short outing with the kids.
I was there in February after seeing the reviews on this place. I didn't see any seals. I'm wondering if they are still seen in this area during that time of year. The views are still pretty though.
We didn't see any seals but there's a beautiful trail to walk on that's well maintained and has a great view of the ocean and the railroad.

Very easy stop off the freeway in Carpenteria. You will have to walk about 1/4 mile because they area above the seals is private property.
As you get close to the cliff you can smell them...Mmmmmm. Its cool to see the seals laying out in the sun and just laying around. I understand February and March is when you get to see all the new pups.

Don't follow Yelp or google maps address. Use carpenteria bluffs trailhead (6145 Carpinteria Ave, Carpinteria, CA 93013). Small lot right outside of 101 freeway entrance/exit.

If you follow Yelp or google maps, it'll take you to private property/lots where security guard will re-direct you.

We went around 1:30pm on a Saturday. Only a few people on trail and plenty of parking (dirt path). Sunny weather and lots of seals on the beach basking.

We were on the way to Solvang and Santa Barbara and made this quick exit when I was yelping to see what was in the area and discovered we were within minutes of pasting this place. Having seen the sea lions during my Big Sur trip, I couldn't wait to get a chance to peak at the seals and especially since I read online that it was around the time that they are mating/having puppies with them on the shorelines of Carpinteria. 

We exited the freeway and drove for another 5 minutes or so until we passed some train tracks and ended up being near an oil drilling area near the shores of the beach. We were a bit confused so we decided to park the car and venture out on foot to see where the area designated for seal sighting was.

Head up the trail (as there are no signs directing you to the seal sighting location) and then probably 5-10 steps later, venture a bit to the right hand side and you'll spot the sign that tells you this is the area for the "Carpinteria Seal Sanctuary" and basically the rules of the sanctuary. If the beach is closed for them to mate, as long as you don't head down onto the beach (which should be gated off anyways), you should be fine.

From atop of the roped off area, you can also still see the seals from down below as they play in the water, waddle onto the shore and jump towards each other and make barking noises at each other.

Simply a beautiful place to just observe them in their natural habitat.

My family and I had a day trip to the Seal Sanctuary this past summer. We have two boys and at the time I was pregnant. The seal sanctuary was a stop on our list while there. With two little kids we had to gage if the hike was worth it. The hike wasn't too bad we made to the cliffs but didn't walk down, that was a pretty intense hike down. We didn't see any seals either but it was still a nice visit. I was surprised to see the Amtrak train run through the hike. I don't remember seeing any posts that had a warning so watch out for the train, don't cross the tracks. Other than that, enjoy !!

The Carpenteria Seal Sanctuary is along the bluffs. You will have to cross over a railroad track but I love the walk around the area because there's plenty to see like wildlife and the lush greenery if you are not there for just the Seal Sanctuary. 

It can get crowded so keep your distance from others. Wait your turns. Little kids get really excited and don't want to leave the viewing area too soon. Make sure to wear face masks. There are pockets of spaces where you can see below from the viewing area on the bluffs however, be careful as not to disturb shrubs or plant specimens beyond barriers. 

There are trails and other nearby of you are up for more adventure.

It's a quick and easy walk to visit the Seal Sanctuary if you are visiting the Carpinteria Bluffs. It's about a half mile or less from the main parking lot at the bluffs to the Seal Sanctuary overlook. During this time of year with pups and their moms hanging out, the beach below is closed. However from the top of the bluffs you get a great view of them laying on the beach below or playing in the small pools. We saw at least 3 babies and several more adults intermittently playing and relaxing. Definitely bring binoculars if you want a closer view but they are still visible with the naked eye. Volunteers are there to answer questions from visitors during pup season and to take "seal counts." Great stop and free to visit local Carpinteria treasure.

We were very disappointed. There is no signage from the bluffs parking lot to give any indication which way to go. With two little ones and a checkout time we didn't have time to wander around looking for the seal lookout point. A simple map from the parking lot would have solved all problems.

Nice little spot to take a few hours to see a quiet beach and some sea life.  Since this is a sanctuary you won't be able to get terribly close to the seals, but that is a good thing for them and for you.   You can see them on an overlook not too far down, it would be good to have binoculars but we couldn't find ours that day.  Getting down to the beach can be a bit challenging.  We ran into a local who told us that there used to be a wooden pier located here and the trail down to the beach was to get people down there to the pier.  The trail is a bit worse for wear but if you are careful you can make it down and up.  Its a nice little beach area for watching the waves.
My daughters and I walked to the seal sanctuary. It was a nice walk. We did go down to the beach where the seals were.

name: Paddle Sports Center
categories: Sports Clubs, Sporting Goods, Recreation Centers, Surfing, Paddleboarding, Shopping, Outdoor Gear, Rafting/Kayaking, Active Life

yelp_reviews.text:
Kayak trip on Santa Cruz Island from Scorpion to potato Harbor.  What a fabulous experience!  About 3 miles or so each way.  Our guide gave us a detailed description of the wildlife, the rock formations, we ate kelp, kayaked into a number of caves.  Eventually we arrived at Potato Harbor among the barking of seals.  We stopped and had lunch and then snorkled around the bay.  

The trip back is a straight shot and it's a good workout on your arms, shoulders and back.  If you're thinking about heading out there, you should not miss this this little adventure.  Really fun and SB Paddle Sports was awsome.

If you're looking to get out of LA and drive on up to Santa Barbara for a day to relax, kayaking with Paddle Sports might be something you want to try out.

Located at the harbor, this outfitter arranges different types of kayaking trips and it's a lot of fun. I went on  the "Santa Barbara Waterfront Kayak and Bike Tour" which was relaxing and a great way to check out how beautiful Santa Barbara really is. This particular tour is a 2 mile paddle down the coastline and a bike ride back to the harbor. You get to see all kinds of sea mammals and learn about the local wildlife from a guide. It's not very strenuous and perfectly fine for beginners. One word of caution though, if you get even a little bit seasick, you'll want to take some dramamine when you go kayaking. I didn't think kayaking would make me seasick but the constant movement amongst the waves made me feel queasy and I ended up jumping into the water to cool off and feel better mid-kayak. Our guide was super laid back and relaxed and let me swim around as long as I wanted, even jumping in after to keep me company. One thing to remember, even in the summer, the water can get COLD.

The bike ride back to the harbor is amongst lovely bike trails and it's very safe. I'm an incredibly inexperienced bike rider but I was fine doing this. Well, except for the part when I rode straight into the bushes and skinned both my knees. But that had more to do with my own clumsiness than anything else. 

It's a great way to spend the morning and they have other types of trips available such as day trips where they take you out to the nearby islands. You can check it out on their website. http://www.kayaksb.com/calendar.asp

You can really work up an appetite kayaking and there are tons of little restaurants around the harbor. Try the little sushi place on the docks- a great quick bite after a leisurely paddle and bike ride along the beautiful Santa Barbara coast... Have fun!

We decided to go kayaking spontaneously and got an reservation online. The people working there were very friendly in explaining how to use the equipment, where to drive and other important information. 

We went on a trip through the harbor and under the eastern docks. On the way we saw sea lions and pelicans up close. It was sometimes a little bit scary when the waves created by jet propelled boat engines hit, but fun nonetheless. Prices are reasonable and equipment appeared to be in a good shape. 

Thank you.

I went to Paddle Sports Center and had an amazing experience. The staff is so friendly and they made the process of getting signed up and out on the water so easy. The instructor made me feel comfortable, especially because I'm not the strongest swimmer and sometimes get nervous in the ocean. Our group had people of all levels and we had so much fun. The instructor gave me tips that helped me get on the paddle board and stay balanced. This is also such an beautiful beach and the views from the water are amazing. I would definitely recommend coming here - kind staff, fun activity, beautiful place!!

I have never been kayaking in the ocean before coming here but I was out at ease by the people working (especially Jordan). atmosphere is very welcoming and the prices for rentals are impossible to beat! I will definitely be coming here again soon--and I would recommend this experience to anyone who enjoys the ocean and seeing gorgeous wildlife!

My first experience paddle boarding ever and I'm so glad I decided to try it here with two lovely new lady foodie friends I got to bond with Santa Barbara. :)

Renting out paddle boards was a simple, fast and affordable! Who could ask for anything better.

One of the nice employees there was instructing us on how to actually paddle board before we strapped up and walked off to the pier. One of my girlfriends is a paddle boarding pro so we told the dude we got it covered. Plus...when I'm learning something, it's best to just throw me in the water and let me learn on my own versus having to listen to someone show me how (why I quit college earlier in my life and worked my way up the corporate ladder by simply experimenting and doing sh*t on my own).

The weather was uber summery and HOT that day. So I knew my ass...and tatas were gonna be burntylicious after our one hour rental. I can honestly say I was freaking out the first 15 min and refused to stand up and paddle, but once I realized it's a lot harder to fall off the board, I stood up ad rowed from side to side like a paddle boarding sexy pro. ;)

We got to see lots of amazing SB scenery, including Bob the sea lion. On top of that, my upper body muscles were on FIRE after that paddle boarding experience. Note to self, next time don't train arms before going on an excursion like this!

You can watch my full paddle boarding adventure here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=z7Lz6k-tXhI&list=UU5YaBtOxOYqeFEEQ_aW6JiA

Great experience here! There was 6 of us and we got 2 tandem kayaks and 2 paddle boards for an hour and 15 minutes it was so fun and relaxing! Customer service was great! Not sure of the guy that helped us but he was so helpful! My only issue is that no one was there to help us get back from the water and I personally was really struggling someone on a boat offered to help me. Also we were told it was going to be different pricing, however it was not but honestly it was worth is! I will definitely be back!!!

Great customer service and easy to book online.  I was able to book the day of the rental for 2 paddle boards and a kayak. An hour later, I showed up for my appointment. They provided lockers for your personal item, got you outfitted quickly and gave recommendations on where to go based on the conditions.   
Super friendly and helpful. 

The equipment was in excellent condition.  They use a nifty timing bracelet which you clock out and in when you leave and return to the shop.   Easy!   

Our group ended up paddling out to the buoy that has sea lions on it.  A little workout but definitely worth it.
Love this place! Melanie was super helpful and friendly, and the views in the harbor were incredible. We went kayaking and will definitely be back

Had a couple of days in Santa Barbara and wanted to spend as much of it on the water as I could. PSC made it happen! Great staff, equipment and prices. I saw seals, sea lions, and dolphins. Kayaked day one in the late afternoon down past the Mesa and paddle boarded day two first thing in the morning. I had a dolphin literally dive under my board. So beautiful!

Choose another kayak/sup boarding rental company.

My 5 girlfriends and I went standup paddle boarding. All the equipment was fine and we got assistance getting on the boards. However when we finished and needed to get back on land, there was no employee present to assist us.  Some of my girlfriends had never SUP boarded before and had difficulty coming back onto the dock on their own.  One fell into the water with her head and neck falling dangerous close to the dock.  This company is either very understaffed or lazy and unwilling to assist their customers.  One female employee walked by and ordered us to pick up the boards and shelve them, not offering any assistance.  If I was the management, I'd better train the staff so they could avoid legal repercussions from injury on site.

PSC is awesome.  They set me up in Goleta so I could paddle aside a friend swimming the Semana Nautica Six Mile Ocean swim and then picked up the kayak at Hendry's at the end of the race.  It could not have been easier.
We had such a great time paddle boarding. The staff is super nice and Fraser is such a great teacher. If you're looking to paddle board look no further. This place is awesome.
Had lots of fun on my paddle board! Pretty easy to do and the staff was great with instruction!
I was very apprehensive about kayaking, but the staff made me feel at ease as they patiently worked with me. I can't wait to go out again.

If they had a 10 star option I would have chosen that.

I hosted a Kayaking birthday party for 10 of my friends.
Clay and Blake were fantastic in helping me organize it all.

I had the kayaks dropped off a few miles north of the Harbor - which was great -we just followed the current and a few sea lions and seals. We did pull over once for a delicious picnic-that I packed for everyone.

Kayaks: They were the red sit on top with back rests type. Very comfortable.
Life preservers: New looking and comfortable -well for a life jacket that is. We all felt safe.
Wet bags: they loaned us one for our lunch items which was great!
We didn't need a guide or someone to give a clinic -but that was an option if we needed it.  

A man of wisdom delights in water.-- Confucius

Enjoy!

Great little break with the family. They were very friendly and helpful. We went out 2 adults  & 2 kids for an impromptu kayak outing after lunch. Note to self : next time me bring a change of pants because getting wet is probable. Luckily the location had a cute pair of shorts on their sales rack  ...
I recommend this as a fun family, couple or friends activity.

The customer service was not good. It was very uncomfortable, they made us feel unwelcomed. You can get a better price on paddle boarding father down lead better by the bbq pits. They are way friendly. I would definitely not come back to this paddle sports center based solely on the interaction with the staff. It put a damper on the entire trip.

My bf and I decided to take a kayak trip whilst visiting S.B. and decided to go with Paddle Sports.  We took the Island Kayaking all day trip out to Santa Cruz Island.  One of the largest of the "Channel Islands".  It's a 1 hour boat ride out to the island which is where we met our guide.  Our guide Sean, who was great by the way, gave us some kayaking instruction and safety tips.  We paddled out as a group of 7, mostly in double kayaks and it was mostly a pretty easy trip with larger caves to start out on.  Our guide was excellent at judging whether our group could handle going into smaller caves or not.  We ended up going in to a few smaller caves, which was exciting.  The swells were really huge on one side of the island and my bf got pretty sick off the back of the kayak, as well as the guy in front of me.  I felt queasy, but was not as affected thank goodness.  
Sean also gave us some information on the environment and the wildlife and also the history of the island .  Over half of our group were beginners and we all did fine.  We also had about 2 hours to eat lunch (which you pack yourself) and explore the island.  It's a very primitive island, which is amazing considering it's so close to the mainland.  The inhabitants and visitors are all dedicated to keeping it a pristine environment, so we were all required to bring anything back with us that we had brought onto the island.  All in all a very fun and adventurous trip.   I would do it again!

Great people and service.  Clay took our group of five out.  He gave us a quick lesson in paddling and then got us each into our own kayaks.  We paddled around the harbor and then out to buoy covered in sea lions (not too close - but close enough for great pictures).  He was really friendly while still paying attention to our safety.   I've ocean kayaked much further north, so I wasn't expecting the open kayaks - I definitely should've worn a swimsuit.  Luckily, they had an outdoor shower and then a private changing room.  Highly recommend!
Grant was super helpful. A fun place fir a nice paddle into the harbor.  Eary reasonably priced as well!
Decent prices,  although it'd be great if they offered prices per hour and not per every 2 hours. No set course, though, which is awesome for a rental place.

Took out a tandem Kayak for one hour, staff were very friendly and helpful.  Easy to navigate your way around, they clearly mark out your trip on a map.  Equipment was in good condition, sea conditions on the day we went were nice and calm.   No qualms about recommending this place.
MY FAVORITE KAYAKING PLACE!!!!!! Super chill employees, they teach you how to properly handle the kayak and it was a great experience.

Had the pleasure of renting two single kayaks for an hour for $24, what a steal. The employees are very helpful and friendly, shout out to Jordon for helping us out! Will definitely be back, thank you so much :)

As enthusiastic kayakers, we had planned on renting kayaks and taking them to the Channel Islands ourselves but when we arrived at the ferry we found out that due to moderate waves the rental place canceled all rentals that day. Fortunately JD (head guide and part owner of Paddle Sports) was kind enough to let us join his tour on the spot. He hosted a fantastic day of paddling and saved our Honeymoon. We heartily recommend his tour and hope to join them again sometime!

Friendly, knowledgable staff. Made my boyfriend's and my first time paddle boarding a great experience. Will be back to do again or try other activities.

Renting paddle boards = $30 for 1 hour, $40 for 2 hours, and $65 for 3 hours. Staff gives you all equipment and walks you down to the harbor where they get you on your paddle board.

Hose outside their shop to rinse off. Can rent a locker out to hold your stuff for $1/day.

Check in for 10% off!

"what should we do today" husband: "let's try paddle boarding!" me: "you just married me. Are you trying to kill me already?"

Husband won out, and I am so glad he did.  These guys here at paddle sports of SB put this nervous nelly to rest by explaining everything, and making sure I felt comfy. They give really in depth instructions, and are quick to clarify the smallest question.   I really felt right at ease before getting into the water.  The whole two hours on the paddle boarding was so fun and exhilarating I didn't want to come back to shore. We loved it!

You can't beat this place in terms of expertise, price (really affordable!! They could charge a lot more) and equipment. Also, if you make your reservation online they give an extra 10% off. :)

Walked in looking to rent 2 SUPS and had to ask staff for assistance.  They explained wind was blowing 5mph so they would recommend kayaking instead.  The harbor was as calm as a pool so I insisted I came to stand up paddle and have experience.  Had fun on the beat up boards but the staff was lame and $60 for 2 boards for just under a hour is a joke.
Super nice and helpful. Pretty good deal, and great student discounts! If it is your first time to Santa Barbara be sure to ask them about good restaurant and bar suggestions.

We had a wonderful time during our short kayak excursion. We were delighted to hear about the chance to make a longer adventure by taking the ferry from Ventura and adding camping and snorkeling to the mix. The staff was very attentive and professional, Jordan exceeded our expectations.
So glad I decided to book a tour rather than renting a kayak on my own. Jordan was our guide. He was awesome!! Great experience.
This was so much fun and the people were very efficient. I really liked the seals cruising by us and the service we recieved.

Super friendly staff, also very knowledgeable. Quick lesson for us newbies at no charge. Very patient with my questions and concerns. Really, first rate service. Can't say enough about how friendly they are.  I was not a fan of all the boat traffic, but it is a harbor after all! The only negative I can think of is they didn't explain until we got back that we had to get the boards out of the water and put them away ourselves when we were done. But again, newbies, so maybe that's the norm. Will know for next time.  Great prices, too!  We will be back!

Nice staff and good service! Good quality on the surfboards. Really helpfull. We had an amazing time. Santa barabara is a perfect place to surf, and paddle sport center made the experience even better.

Had an incredible day on the water yesterday thanks to the efforts of the friendly folks at the Paddle Sports Center. My paddleboard rental was easy, accessible and fun. The guys at the shop provided a detailed rundown of paddleboarding techniques, and hooked me up with all the gear I'd need to enjoy my time on the water. Being right in the harbor, we had so many options to explore the SB coastline. Our day at Ledbetter was perfect.

Great way to spend a sunny afternoon in Santa Barbara.  Kayaking with friends.  We went with a group of 10 for an hour... and got a great workout.. Kayaks for $30 an hour (for 2 ppl).. Time flies by pretty fast and your arms will be sore the next day.  We just kayaked around the area and raced each other..luckily none of us fell into the water.. but our butts were pretty drenched..Other than that, kayaking was a great way to spend the afternoon..

Paddle Sports Center is by far the best place to rent paddle boards in Santa Barbara! The staff in the store are super helpful/friendly and they set you up with everything you need, whether it's your first time out or you're a seasoned pro. I went for the first time this month and was a bit nervous, but took a class from Fraser (the owner, super nice guy) who explained the technique clearly and put me at ease right away! I had a wonderful time out on the water in the harbor and will certainly be back.
Love this spot! I was regularly renting a kayak and loving going out , I miss it and am looking forward to making the trek up there soon.

We came to rent 4 paddle boards.  The staff were nice and friendly and the bay was calm with many things to see.  To me this is better than in the open sea with nothing to see.  When we returned and paid, there were other charges on top of the rental rate.  One is something about "walking" and the other is for gratitude.  We have rented water sport equipments at many other places but i have never seen anyone automatically charge tip.

Jordan and Jordan were fantastic.  They made the rental process very smooth and provided outstanding customer service.  They made sure I had everything I needed before I went out and showed me some great tips to help me get to know the harbor and surrounding area.  It was like I was talking to old friends when I came back to the dock after renting my kayak. I would go here again and again! Top notch for sure.

Friendly staff, fast service, easy equipment rental. They showed us a route to see the sea lions. The route they recommended was perfect since I get tired after about an hour. Also I liked that they already had the kayaks in the water so i didnt have to carry mine down. Hourly price was reasonable and 10% discount for checking in!

Had a great time with a SUP lesson this morning. Fraser does an excellent job teaching everyone the basics of paddling, standing and how to fall properly. He's super knowledgeable about the ocean and the local area and had lots of interesting items to show us. He made the paddle more than just an out and back paddle. Really enjoyed it and highly recommend it. Takes about 2 hours total but it's worth it.

Simple, fun, great pricing, no brainier. Not much to it really. Get a deal when you reserve ahead of
Time online or use your axxess card. Lockers
For personal items. You walk down, grab a board, and get in! First timer today and it won't be my last! Fun little workout adventure that allows you to enjoy and appreciate our little harbor and pier. Loved it!

Fantastic experience for myself and my 7 year old granddaughter. We were given excellent instruction, lots of help selecting our paddles and boards.
Highly recommended.
Jordan was awesome and hooked us up with a great rental! Bathrooms are in the next building. Paid parking.

First time kayaking in Santa Barbara and this company is great all around! The equipment provided was in good condition, the staff provided a quick introduction on how to kayak and safety procedure. Before heading out they informed us with different routes to take and areas to avoid, this we liked because we got to spend our time at our own pleasure. We chose to visit the sea lions and pier, our trip was near a hour and 20 minutes. We also got to use a "student discount". We would defiantly be back!

Came here for a weekend bachelorette party activity! They took such good care of our group of 14 from booking the reservation to getting us out of the kayaks and returning the gear. The crew is friendly, knowledgeable, and helpful. They will give you a map of the surrounding area and warn that paddling towards the pier means much stronger currents coming back. First timers and experienced kayakers/paddleboarders alike will have a good time. Don't forget your sunscreen and if you get seasick take the medication beforehand.

We had a great time kayaking last weekend thanks to the guys at Paddle Sports Center. They were very patient answering our questions, everything went smoothly. It was my first time and they explained everything I needed to know beforehand. 

Make sure you bring your student ID for a walk-in discount! We did not know this until we got there.
Seriously its worth it to come here just for the amazing customer service. Prices and location are great too

This place is awesome! The people are so nice, ready to help you out with all your paddling needs and the gear is very reliable and top notch. Definitely worth the money! Even if it's your first time on a kayak or SUP they welcome you and show you how to do it. There is enclosed water harbor access for the kayaks and SUPs or also beach access for the SUPs to launch through the waves at Leadbetter. Kind people, great gear and good vibes!

The staff at Paddle Sports Center is Awesome!! I had a little bit of nerves about going out into the open ocean in just a Kayak, but they put my nerves to ease. They were soo helpful and informative about things we could see while we were out on the water, and things we could and could not do. They had a place on the dock to keep out shoes, so we didn't have to wear them on the water to avoid getting soggy shoes. Overall GREAT experience and they accept the AXXESS card for a 2 for 1! We were out on the water for about an hour and 20 min and it was soo enjoyable, just being able to be out there cruising along without a care in the world! Thanks Paddle Sports Center for making our visit soo special!
Great time with my wife for our anniversary. Excellent customer service and reasonable prices. Highly recommend!!

Kayaking the sea caves on the channel islands is awesome! Actually the whole place is amazing, the island itself (went to santa cruz island) is really beautiful ..breathtaking views.. so make sure you leave some time to hike as well. You meet early in the morning and take a ferry over to the islands.. BEWARE waters are VERY CHOPPY and many people got seasick, though I think I was in the worst shape. Ive been on boats before, and while I do get seasick in the " oh I feel so queasy" way, ive never actually thrown up .. TILL the ride to the island. OMG I seriously thought I was dying, never have I felt that sick in my life..worse than my worst hangover. So word of advice, take something strong! Dramamine wont cut it. The guide recommended taking  a pill ( I forget the name) the night before and then again in the morning so it's still in your blood stream. Anyway, amidst throwing up several times, fevering, and turning so pale I was almost blue, I was able to see a few dolphins and other sea animals that like to play with the boats, they knew we were coming and seriously created a little show for us. 

I was the first one to get off the boat.. one of the workers literally picked me up and carried me to get me out first. (and yes, it was quite embarrassing ) however once I got onto solid land and kept drinking sips of water, I started to feel a biiit better. There we met our guide JD. HE IS AWESOME. Request him when you call Paddle Sports, though Im sure all the guides are just as awesome. But he was so knowledgeable about the island, kayaking, just everything in general and was really laid back and cool.  Before you get out on the water, he teaches you how to kayak properly. Did you know you use your legs to do most of the work? Well I didn't, it made kayaking long distances sooo much easier! He takes you through the sea caves and you feel completely safe with him as your guide. He also will take pictures for your group and posts them on picasa so you can download them for yourself. =) 

After kayaking you have the rest of the day ( about 2-3 hours) to eat and do whatever you 'd like. You must bring your own lunch. I packed sandwich stuff for me and my friends with HI-C, soda, carrots, and chips.  Being a Korean girl who is turning into her mother more and more every day, naturally I brought enough food to feed an army =) so we offered some food to our guide and ate lunch together. I think he enjoyed our "gourmet" sandwiches lol cuz while the other groups brought bread, bologna, and cheese, I brought mayo, mustard, lettuce, tomatoes, avocados, turkey, ham,  cheese, and pickles on yummy potato bread everything packed individually of course so as not to have soggy sandwiches =) After lunch, we did a nice hike of the island and embraced the clean air and spectacular views before heading back to the boat.

Even though I threw up a week's worth of food and felt like death on the ride over, I still had so much fun and I can't wait to go back and kayak the other islands.. will definitely go with paddle sports again, ill just make sure to take LOTS and LOTSSS of anti nausea medicine first !

Hello awesome sauce! I just had the most amazing sunset kayak adventure led by the guys of Paddle Sports Center! I've rented from these guys for years to go out solo, but this was my first guided group experience with them. I LOVED IT! Great instruction before hopping in the kayaks if you're a beginner. You couldn't feel any safer with these guys leading the way. The gear is in tip top shape. The guides are fun and knowledgeable. I highly recommend the sunset tour. Stunning views. I saw dolphins, harbor seals, brown pelicans, plenty of other sea birds, and an entire school of fish scurrying right underneath my kayak. What a spectacular way to end the work week. If I could give ten stars, I would. If you're a local or an out-of-town guest, Paddle Sports Center Should be the first thing on your list if you ever find yourself wondering what you should do with some free time. A kayak tour from Paddle Sports would be a great date-day/night idea if you're tired of the dinner and a movie rut. I know I'm tired of dinners and movies. These guys are great. Tell your friends.

My wife wanted to go sea kayaking while in SB, so I called one or two places before getting a positive response from Paddle Sports Center. I made a ressie for that Satuday morning about 9:00.

The staff was not unfriendly; but I'd say they were barely above the threshold of "acceptable" customer service. (They seemed tired and distracted, despite having only a few customers). At least the logistics and paperwork were easy and quick.  Convenient lockers made storing our gear, phone, wallets, etc. easy as well.

After donning life vests and doing a quick review of the local map, we made the short walk to our tandem kayak. Then we were off through the maze of docked boats before finally making our way around the breakwater and onto the ocean proper.  Afterwards was a relaxing, 2-hour paddle north and into the kelp forest and back.  The main critters we saw were cormorants, pelicans and one or two seals.

Four stars, but I'll have to take one star off for the terrible recommendation PSC gave us about going under Stearn's Wharf. It turns out hundreds of people fish off the wharf on a Saturday, so we were met with several, muttered curses; and we had to dodge and untangle ourselves from a few fishing lines. No bueno. (Sorry folks).

Just went on my first and definitely NOT last kayaking trip with Paddle Sports of Santa Barbara out to Santa Cruz Island.
First thing i noticed that was unlike other customers going out to the island that day, since we had booked with Paddle Sports, it wasn't necessary to wait in the loooong line to receive boarding and return passes to the ferry, our Paddle Sports guide already had them!
On the boat ride across the channel we stopped a few times to see different wildlife! (on the way back from the island the ferry even stopped to pick up a Mylar balloon that was floating around in the water!)
Once we got to the island there was a ranger who introduced a few things about the island we probably would have never known and got to be on our way! 
We met at the red kayaks and had a prep talk about the do's and dont's of ocean kayaking.
We got suited up and headed out! (I was also surprised to find that our wetsuits were almost brand new and in great condition!)
We spent a bit of time getting used to being in the kayak, the first cave we went through got our blood pumping! It was a blast, the guide went through first and stayed at the exit while each group went through, it was encouraging knowing there was someone waiting for you on the other side!
We explored many sea caves, and went into different coves, all the while our guide kept us in tune with the different happenings and history of the island. My favorite was a cave called the "green room".
Only first hand experience could truly express the beauty once inside this "green room" the reflection from the sun, the rocks, and crystal clear water, gave the cave the most magnificent green color! it was truly an experience not to forget!

Thanks Paddle Sports of Santa Barbara, we look forward to seeing you again at Santa Cruz Island, and checking out all of the other trips you guys have to offer!
Great time out the water! Professional staff! Reasonable rates! Would recommend and do again!

Great quick service by upbeat staff. Decent quality boards and ample selection. The staff also did a good job describing where to go and how their system worked.  We will rent our boards with these guys again.
This place is awesome and Fraser rocks! Kind staff, easy access to the harbor and West Beach by SUP or kayak...you can't go wrong!

Came here for a spontaneous kayak adventure. Customer service was great, they have lockers for your backpack and shoes, and life vests! We paddled out into the ocean and saw seals up close. 5/5 for a great time.
It was really fun and was my first time kayaking in SB. There are seals you can go check out too. Would definitely recommend others to come here!

Easy online rental which is cheaper than booking in the store!  Also, I enjoyed the staff here.  They were really helpful and everything you may need (boards, oars, wetsuit, emergency inflation device) was included in a rental of choice.  It's nice their rental time is determined by an RFID chip so you only pay for the time you use and there's no uncertainty of how long you were actually out.  

There are lockers for rent. 

Before we went out, the staff showed us all the nifty spots on a map and gave us the estimated route times.  There is a seal hang out spot nearby that you can kayak/paddle to.  Also, there is the pier nearby which is also known to have wildlife.

My family and I drove up to Santa Barbara for the day and wanted to rent kayaks.  We found Paddle Sports right near the pier. We rented 2 kayaks one for my husband and myself and one for our  2 daughters. The price was very reasonable for 2 hours of kayaking and the sites were beautiful.

Rented SUP through Groupon which was very easy. Remember to go to their website for registration after you purchase the package via Groupon. Called them to check the reservation, a super helpful guy named Max picked and gave all the helpful information needed. He even told me if I cannot make it at the time that I chose. I can even come at a later time. Showed up after few hours, Max helped me again to check in, provided all the safety rules and get me going at the Harbor. I was alone due to which I was a little scared because I had never taken SUP on a beach before. It's a little different than the lake because of the wind. Had such a wonderful time at the Harbor. Highly recommended.

First things first- check in and get 10% off your total!

Tried my first time stand up paddle boarding here. I always get a little nervous before trying out a new physical activity but everyone here is super nice and makes sure you are well prepared and ready to go. The price is very affordable too for a fun day out on the water. They've got lockers in the shop to store your stuff so you don't have to worry about leaving it out on the dock. The area surrounding area for paddle boarding and kayaking is nice too; you can stay closer to shore/dock if you don't want to go too far out in the ocean. And if you do want to be farther out in the ocean, there's plenty of room for that too. It is a somewhat busy area so there will be larger boats going past you but luckily there's plenty of space so you won't feel like you're going to crash into something especially if you're a first timer. If you're in Santa Barbara and want a fun activity, definitely stop by and rent a kayak or paddle board!

Went during the weekend when the weather was nice for kayaking on a tandem kayak. Pricing was fair and the service is nice. I had a really nice afternoon with my boyfriend and my dog. It's nice that they have a discount for students. I'd be happy to come again.

We took a morning kayaking tour as two small families with Adam as our guide. It was myself and my boyfriend, my two kids (10 + 7 yrs) and our friend her daughter (11). We are Santa Barbara natives so this was a staycation experience for us.  Adam was patient, friendly, helpful and incredibly knowledgeable both about paddle boarding & Santa Barbara's harbor. Our kids are very active and ask a lot of questions and Adam rolled with it with a sense of humor and lots of respect. 
Each of us had very little experience with kayaking but Adam helped us to feel safe, confident and capable immediately. We saw sea lions, tons of birds, and learned a lot about the Santa Barbara Harbor. HIGHLY recommend this to anyone!

What a wonderful experience! A group of 6 of us went kayaking. We each had a tandem kayak; kids in the front, adults in the rear. Our guide Adam, was awesome. Very informative and helpful. We launched from the harbor (right in front of Brophy Bros. Restaurant) and kayaked through the harbor and out to the buoy. We saw tons of beautiful birds and sea lions. I was a bit worried about going so early at 8:30am in the morning, but it was incredible! The water was gorgeous and refreshing. The adults and kids all enjoyed the adventure. Highly recommend. 

Tips: be sure to apply plenty of sunscreen on your thighs, shins and feet, along with full arms and face. Wear a hat and tight-fitting sunglasses. The company provides life vests which you wear over your swim suit. There are lockers inside the shop for your personal effects and a hot outdoor shower for afterwards! Bring a towel and icy water for afterwards. Don't park in the 90-min free parking lot, as the total time was over 90 mins. Enjoy!!

These kids were SO nice to us. We were tired of doing the same old routines for date night, and kayaking seemed like a great thing to try out. We stopped in, and the process was easy! within minutes we were shown what do do for safety, given a paddle, life vest. And we were off! Very reasonable pricing!!! This is place is staying in our date night rolodex!! what a fun day!!

We rented kayaks for a 1 hour trip. We had a lot of fun and the water was very calm. The tour begins and ends in the harbor, so it is not as rough as launching at the beach. Lots of photo ops of the seals. The suggested route was good and the hot shower afterwards was nice.

Wow!!
We had an amazing time on the sunset paddle trip with Adam.  Where do I begin.  It had been 20 years since the last time that my daughter and I had been kayaking. We had  a terrible experience in the Caribbean when she was eight.  This was a fear that she wanted to overcome and wanted me along for the ride.  It was her Bday, so I couldn't say no. To say that I was a nervous wreck, would be an understatement. Adam was the best teacher any student could ask for as he little by little helped us let go of our fears.

We returned to the dock with smiles on our face and in two hours we resolved a fear that we had held on to for twenty years. We can't wait to go back!!!!

So go!!
We had a fantastic time. The guys were outstanding, Max and Drew. They gave up a great paddle board  adventure in the ocean. I did get motion sick and Max towed me in!  Embarrassing but a good

Been renting boards from this location for a while now and everyone that works there, including the owners are so nice that I now consider them my friends. Great people and customer service for our local and tourist population. The rental process is super easy and well organized.

We sure had a great time kayaking and paddle-boarding! The staff were friendly and helpful, and the harbor and breakwater were lots of fun to paddle around. The weather was just perfect for a day on the water. Prices are just fine. 

Love that the kayaks have back supports - for us older folks this is a real help.
Had a great time renting the tandem kayaks, great customer service too. Will return next time in town.

High adventure, little real risk, wide open spaces, low price -- if this is what you seek in a weekend getaway, then you cannot beat a Paddle Sports kayak tour of the Santa Cruz Island sea caves.

Our instructor was subtly expert, constantly judging the abilities and limitations of our particular group.  This is important in kayaking, because it's easy to get in over your head (so to speak) in high winds and chop.  We had two complete beginners on the trip, and they both had a great time.  I have years of kayak experience, but I was not bored at all.  Something for everyone.

Paddle Sports offers a convenient and astonishingly cheap package deal:  ferry, kayak & gear, campsite and park admission.  Just bring food and camping equipment, and you're ready.

We had a great morning trip paddling around the harbor.  It was pretty foggy, so we didn't go beyond the breakwater.  I wish I can remember the name of the guy who helped us, but he was awesome.  He was a one-man operation, and even gave us directions to a local hike when we thought we wouldn't go out b/c of the fog.  We decided to go ahead with it, and he was helpful, friendly, not condescending, and attentive.  I hope to go back to try the channel islands trip.

Good deal and great access through the harbor. Broke my paddle when a wave tipped me over. I have no idea how it snapped but it did. I ended up paddling all the way back with half a paddle and had to tie up the kayak myself. The manager or owner just told me I was responsible. Didn't care to ask what happened and rung me up for an extra 75$ for the broken used paddle. I was happy to pay for the broken paddle but 75$ was twice as much as I was expecting.
Every time I am out in Santa Barbara I go here! The first time I went kayaking I was afraid but they made sure that we knew how to work the kayak. It was an amazing experience!

I had kayaked before but not in the ocean.   They guide was great, made me feel at ease and we enjoyed the trip immensely! I would recommend them to beginners and anyone who just wants to go have some fun!!

I love this place! Fraser is an awesome guy with a super fun and friendly staff! We love to rent kayaks and tour around the harbor, or paddle board on a sunny day. What a great way to enjoy Santa Barbara- from the water! Don't waste your time anywhere else- these guys are pros.
Fraser could not possibly be more accommodating.  Good price, fantastic service.

My friends and I decided to do something adventurous together for once, so we decided on kayaking! May P. found Paddle Sports online and we signed up right away.

For $165 [no taxes or fees!], we got a ferry ride to the island, wetsuits, helmets, lifevests, 2 instructors that gave us our own orientation, and an awesome experience! I was placed in JD's group and he did a great job getting us through caves and educating us on the wildlife - it wasn't boring at all, quite interesting actually! After kayaking, we had the freedom to roam around the island until it was time to get back on the ferry.

We had so much fun - I would definitely love to take other people on this adventure!

We rented a tandem kayak and it was an amazing experience to go around the Santa Barbara Marina. Drew and Jordon were very helpful in giving us a stable kayak and guiding us through the map for the best experience.

I was down in SB for work and brought wife and kids for a little weekend getaway. We wanted to find an activity that took us outside to enjoy the weather but also social distance a bit. The 2 hours we spent kayaking around Sterns Wharf area was fantastic and Paddle a Sports Center made it so easy and fun for our boys. Austin, our guide, was friendly and gave us direction when we needed to have a great time on the water. 

I would recommend booking ahead of time online as it made it really efficient to get us into the water almost as soon as we checked in.

If you want superb SUP, stand up paddle board, instruction, in a beautiful setting, with patient and professional instructors, look no further.  Adam thanks we had a ball.  They also have kayak rentals as well!!

We saw a deal in the Axxess that says "2 for 1 kayaks".  After renting 2 doubles, we get 1 seat free when it comes time to pay. Maybe advertise 2 for 1 seats... 2 for 1 kayaks is misleading.  We rented 2 kayaks. One should be free according to your advertising.
Love this place (and I live in Ventura)!   Tried stand up paddle boarding (SUP) at this place and it was a great experience.  Friendly knowledgeable staff and great prices for renting equipment!
The online reservations were very easy, the parking convenient, the staff super friendly and efficient, and prices very reasonable.  A fun get-away-from-it-all trip, well worth it

Highly recommend the kayaking tour as a perfect social distance activity!  Our two kids (ages 6 and 8) didn't stop paddling the entire 2 hours, the weather was amazing, the tour guide was friendly and helped take pictures for us, and it was one of the highlights of our weekend getaway.  Would definitely book through Paddle Sports Center again!

Awesome little spot to rent surf boards, kayaks, or SUPs. The workers are super friendly, I believe we were assisted by Jordan and Taylor. We called about an hour ahead after finding them on Groupon and they had availability but said they would honor the Groupon rates if we purchased in store so I recommend doing that to support local. Once you get geared up you can paddle out to the pier, the shore, or the little island. You're pretty much guaranteed to see sea lions resting on the dock which is lovely. Highly recommend!

Hello amazing experience here a few weekends ago, and now I'm back with a girlfriend and we had another great experience. If you want to rent a kayak or paddleboard, look no further because this is your place! Friendly service reasonable rates and great location! Will for sure be back!

This crew is family to me. I get great exercise, the staff is so friendly and they always play good music. There's a huge range of equipment to choose from. If you can't find it here, it doesn't exist.
Had an awesome time for SUP yoga. Amazing experience great customer service and guides were amazing!

I've been open ocean kayaking twice, and each time have come here to rent out a boat with my husband. It was such a beautiful experience, seeing the shore, the beaches, the boats, the open ocean scenery, and the seals sunbathing. Not to mention, it is also great exercise! We were pretty tired after an hour of touring around. Just be careful not to snag fishing lines or hit motor boats. 

The service this place gave was great. Super chill workers who were on top of the whole rental and gave us clear concise directions. Will definitely come back!

My usual go to rental place for kayaks in the SB. The staff is very friendly and knowledgable. Check-in is really easy, and if you're a return customer it's even quicker. There are tons of equipment you can purchase at their store to keep  your goods safe and dry. Sadly the lockers are gone due to precautions, but I was able to leave our things at the dock, and kept my valuables in a dry bag I got at the store. When you get back you can hose down outside or use the bathrooms in the building next door. The equipment is reliable and clean. Put on some sunblock and make sure you give the boaters right of way! Enjoy! I'll be back as usual.

Wonderful spirit of service. Fun place to do business. Very helpful to new clients. Got us checked in quicly, andwered all our questions, explained our options. Once we were checked in, showed us what we neede to know to use paddle boards. Showed us where to go and not to go in harbor. Good experience.

I've been coming here as a member for about three years, and every time it's a great experience.  The employees give me a friendly welcome by name, and I'm down at the dock and underway without any other formalities.  Kayaking is my favorite workout, and seeing the occasional whale, dolphins, and ever-shifting crew of seabirds reminds me that wilderness begins at the waterline.

We came here to rent Kayaks and Jordan was extremely helpful and friendly! He gave detailed instructions on how to be safe even when it was extremely windy. Definitely come here if you are looking to rent water sports equipment!

I had an amazing time for my first kayaking alone! Very great price for the exhilarating kind of work out kayaking is! Kind handsome young men helped us, 1 walked us to the kayak and gave us some basic tips how to hold the oars. We went at 2 pm and think the morning would be nicer. 

It's a huge workout on your upper core and arms, so it's best to be fit or you won't get anywhere. 

Can't wait to do it again and try paddle boarding!

I had a great time on the stand up paddle board exploring the harbor. Fraser was a great tour guide! He explained clearly how to paddle and stand up on the board, I felt very safe. It was a beautiful trip! The clothes in the store are also very cute!!

I've rented Kayaks from a bunch of different places, but these guys definitely like their paddle boarding and kayaking. Their online waiver system is a huge time saver and the bracelet for check-out keeps the timing honest. 

Another overlooked detail: their kayaks have actual, rigid seat backs, which means that when you're paddling, you feel supported. Best way to prevent your lower abs from hurting over time.

Have been taking day trips to Santa Barbara for years, usually biking, hiking, and enjoying State Street. When we decided to try kayaking and paddle boarding, we reserved a few days in advance with Paddle Sports Center. Renting from Paddle Sports was easy from start to finish. The on-line reservations and waiver completion really expedited the process. Max and the staff exceeded our expectations, great customer service! Super organized Covid precautions, the lockers were convenient. And the instruction of the equipment and Harbor guidelines & directions they gave helped to make it an enjoyable, fun and beautiful day.

This is my second visit to PSC and I've had a great time during both visits. The staff are generally courteous and helpful. The overall experience of kayaking on the ocean was super fun. It was around $60 to rent a tandem kayak for around 1 hour and 45 mins.
Great experience kayaking here! Staff is SUPER friendly, well organized and knowledgeable. Even on a busy holiday weekend, they have no problem accommodating everyone. Will definitely come back!

This place is awesome! My boyfriend and I went to Paddle Sports as walk-ins and got our rental kayak and gear within minutes. Not only are Jordan and Jordan both awesome, kind and friendly Paddle S. employees, but their also helpful and considerate of the customers. Jordan (at the counter) gave us a student discount which made the trip super affordable. I mean I can't argue for a 2-hour kayak rental for $24 that's a straight steal! And, Jordan who instructs customers on the dos and donts of SB kayaking, was funny, short and to the point. If you are visiting SB, I highly recommend this place.

Found this place thanks to a voucher for 1 hour kayaking that cam with the purchase of our hotel room (West Beach Inn). The kayaking in the harbor and into the sea was so fun. We are not only experienced in lakes, but this was easy to due and the weather was perfect, sunny and 80's, hardly any wind. They gave great instructions, we felt safe and truly enjoyed our time. Also if we didn't have a voucher it is very reasonably priced. We did tandem 2 person and had the best time. Life vests provided! Great staff and neat little shop attached!

Great way to kill some time with the kids before school starts. Staff was friendly and treated us like we were family (joking around while staying professional with what matters). Went kayaking with a larger group and had the time of our lives. Very affordable and the location couldn't be better as you can stay behind a breakwater or go into the ocean if you choose. This is a must stop if you're in Santa Barbara for a weekend.
We rented a tandem kayak last weekend and had a great time. The staff here was very friendly and helpful. Highly recommended!

Service was god awful. Had a 5pm reservation and we showed up at 4:58, and an employee had left when we got there and there was no one else there. We waited 10 minutes before someone showed up. I asked if we could still get the hour and he just said we close at 6 but you could still kayak, basically just avoided answering the question. I said you guys showed up 10 minutes late, so now we have a shorter time because no one was actually here? And his response was we close at 6. We Kayaked for 30 min because my gf and I were more worried about returning by 6 than actually enjoying the hour. I asked if we could do a discount because of the time they weren't there to help us and the girl who originally left said "no, you guys didn't come on time." Where I then replied that we were there at 5, we literally saw you leave, and she said, "oh well you should've said something". So it's my fault that no one was there to help to begin with? I assumed that she wasn't the only one in the shop. If you know you have a reservation at a certain time there should be someone there to help. None of them gave a shit and were just in a rush to go home.

These guys were awesome. I did a 6 mile swim from Goleta Beach to Hendry's Beach. They brought the kayak to the beach and picked it up at Hendry's and made everything really easy. Great customer service!!
The handsome young man who helped us was so sweet and kind. We will definitely be coming back here soon!!! (:

This place is located right on the pier. The process of renting a kayak was super easy. The staff in the shop is so friendly and helpful! They have lockers which I highly recommend using since we did accidentally flipped our kayak. They also have a shower you can rinse off after with. Super fun day adventure!

Incredibly affordable as a walk-in day trip, their monthly membership is an AWESOME deal! The membership is less than a gym's dues, you get 2 hours of water time, paddleboard or kayak as preferred--top notch equipment, changing rooms, towel service, a place to rinse off, and free lockers. 

The staff is so upbeat, too, knowledgeable about the sports and equipment, and quick to help with any and all details. I could not recommend it more. It is stellar, and I am so glad I joined. Tip: sign up online and get a few extra bucks off.

Two Words: First Timer

First timer, but certainly won't be the last.

The staff is quite knowledgeable and they made my first experience more enjoyable. 
Paddles are 30 for an hour, but only 40 for 2 hours. I would suggest doing 2 hours because it is such a good deal! They currently have a check in deal for 10% off, I will be using that next time.

In case you have valuables, they let you rent out lockers for only a buck! 

It was my first time, and I got such a workout. This is such a fun way to be active and to get a tan. I actually had trouble turning and my friend was trying to help me. I ended up falling off! But, like a champ I got up and kept on paddling.

What is nice about getting two hours is that you can stop by the beach and just lay there(rest your arms). My friend and I paddled to the middle of the beach and laid on our paddle boards - it was like meditation.

I would definitely recommend putting on sunscreen before heading out!

Please try this when you are in Santa Barbara - it is too fun!

We had a kayak tour through PSC that was setup by our trip with Trek Travel.  It was first class.  Our guide Melanie was wonderful, very sweet, so fun and interesting to talk to.  Just a simple 1.5 hour paddle tour around the harbor.  Really loved it!!!!
We had a great time. Staff was welcoming and warm. It was so much less expensive than I thought, this is my new favorite weekend activity!
A little hard to find even with all the signs but we found it nonetheless. Staff was helpful and friendly. Overall, had a great time!

We rented kayaks from Paddle Sports Center and had a great time paddling around the marina. The staff was really helpful and friendly. They showed us where to go, how to get back safely, and they even gave us cool stickers. 

Kayaking was a great activity to enjoy on a sunny day - highly recommend. 

They also have a little shower in the back in case you need to rinse off after your adventure.
Rented some kayaks at the end of the day. Great location with all the restaurants and shops close by. Professional and friendly service by the entire team. Would highly recommend!

These guys could improve on customer service/safety. My daughter and her friend rented a tandem Kayak and were basically just told to jump in the Kayak and go. No questions as to whether or not they knew what to do or where to go. They didn't offer a waterproof bag, no help getting out when they got back. We have Kayaked other places where the staff was more helpful and organized.

This place is awesome, super friendly staff. Always have answers for any questions you may have. Jordan was great helped my family have an amazing morning out on the water. Definitely considering becoming members.

I love Kayaking and Paddling in Santa Barbara thanks to Paddle Sports. Great time to workout outside, practice meditation and view wildlife! I've encountered 2 species of seals, aquatic birds and even crossed paths with a school of silver fish energetically jumping out of the water around my kayak like mini dolphins :)

A sunset kayak is a special treat and great reminder of how incredibly accessible kayaking can be for most people, even those with limited mobility on their feet. Paddle sports is my yoga!! As a lifetime member for over 3 years, nothing beats the sanctuary this place has given me. A regular retreat from the office and work, I can come in the middle of the day or late morning and completely recharge my batteries out on the water. Paddle sports provides the community with a safe space for; Exploration, Adventure, Exercise, Meditation, the Great Outdoors, and Fun. 

Due to my status as a regular visitor, I do not know how it would feel coming here as a "rookie" or beginner paddler but the staff id very knowledgable. Sometimes the younger staff will assume you know what you are doing and how to find the kayaks on the dock but I encourage newbies to speak up and ASK for guidance and help with your take-off to the ocean. Why not? doesn't hurt to have a staff member walk you down. Also, it's a good idea to have them explain the safety hazards (boats, wildlife, fishermen) you may encounter and how to communicate with harbor patrol officers if you cross paths. It's good to know the basics before getting out onto the water.  PS offers kayaks, SUP boards and other beach equipment to maximize your ocean enjoyment. 

This is an EASY and AFFORDABLE treat and a must do if you are visiting Santa Barbara. A fun time with paddle sports generally takes about 2 hours and can easily be scheduled into your day trip to SB. Just remember to bring a change of clothes because you never know when you'll get a splash!

Had a great time with our kayak rental! I enjoyed the ease of checking out equipment and I like that they provide life jackets (not all kayak rental places do). When grabbing our gear I appreciated them showing us a recommended kayaking route, especially the best place to view the sea lions!! *heart eyes emoji* I would definitely rent from here again! Tell Jordan we sent you!

Everyone here was friendly and informative. They have great equipment. Best kayak renting experience ever. My wife and I could not have had a better time we will definitely be coming back next time we're in Santa Barbara.

We took the guided kayak harbor tour. Excellent service from start to finish. Our tour guide was Molly - she was outstanding. We had never kayaked before and we were a bit anxious about what to expect, Molly was extremely helpful and did a great job looking out for everyone. I would recommend  the Paddle Sports Center to people of all ages and levels of expertise.
Their staff was warm and friendly and made sure we had an awesome time.  We took our dogs and they were very helpful in making sure our experience was a great one.

This place has been my go to for paddle boarding for several years whenever I visited Santa Barbara.  I have been going there more recently as in the area.  It is great how they are managing COVID totally feel safe knowing they have got a good methods to ensure your safety. 
I would like to give shout out to Laura who has been caring and diligent in making my experience more enjoyable with helping me get boards off and on the racks. 
Thank you,

I  really fell in love with kayaking because of this place! We called in the morning to check on availability and rented a 2 person kayak when we arrived. The employees got us our life jackets and paddles and then showed us over to a map of the harbor. They carefully explained areas where we should go, rules to follow, and where we could see sea lions. They also answered all of our questions and helped us get in our kayak and head out.

The harbor is beautiful and you can stay and paddle around there for a relaxing activity. You can ask paddle out to the first buoy for a bit more of a workout and head back towards the other side of the pier. There's nothing like the view from the water! You pay when you get back to the center, so you can stay out a little longer than expected if you want to spend extra time with the seals!

This place has excellent customer service, reasonable rates, and friendly and knowledgeable staff members teach you the basics to stay alive and have fun whether paddle boarding or kayaking.

The area you paddle or kayak from this rental center is very serene and calm, so first-timers, like myself, have no need for fear. We even saw a seal checking us out. A real seal, not a sea lion!

Summary: strongly recommend a SUP / paddle board lesson with Adam. if you have never paddle boarded before. He is patient, informative, zen nature, and great for families and kids. Rental location ideal. Boards and equipment in very good condition.

My daughter saw paddle boarding in the wharf when we had lunch about a year or so ago.  Did my research, selected Paddle Sports Center, and scheduled a lesson for us, an adult, 15 yr old, 13 yr old, and 10 yr old. Conditions perfect on Aug 4 at 10:00 AM, water like glass. Adam was patient with us getting ready, getting into the water, and learning the basics of the sport. He does take his time with at the beginning, which can be a bit long for kids. But his zen nature, inclusion of everyone, focus on the basics, provided a great lesson. You don't get a ton of practice time, more lesson time, but was great for four people and a little one who was trying the sport for the first time. Their location is ideal, totally protected in the marina / wharf area. Will go back if not for a lesson, definitely for a rental. The kids enjoyed it, even the boys who were complaining before our trip to Santa Barbara.  Adam made paddle board converts out of them!  Awesome time.

Max was our excellent guide during our two hour  kayak tour of the Santa Barbara harbor.  He was fun, funny, laid back  and informative.  We kayaked around the harbor, under the wharf and went  a bit past the sand break to see some sea lions resting on a buoy.  We  learned a lot of history and saw some amazing sights, yachts, wildlife,  etc., and God bless him for towing me and my son's kayak when we fell behind the group.   This was a great tour and it exceeded our expectations.  Highly  recommend.

We came here looking for a place to rent kayaks for ourselves and our pups. The staff members here are great -- very personable, professional, and friendly! They hooked us up with two double kayaks so we would have enough room for one dog on each kayak. They even have dog life jackets!

If we come back to SB, we will def return!

ProTip: They comp an hour on the kayak for your birthday :)
Cat was an awesome tour guide in our wildlife kayak tour! 5 stars for her putting up with our pup and taking pics of us!

If you like your birds with a backdrop of highway traffic, this is the bird refuge for you! It's basically a big, dirty-looking lake ringed by a busy, traffic-jammed road. Yes, there's a reasonably maintained woodchip path,and a couple of small docks jutting into the lake so you can look for birds, but we didn't see much in the way of birds.  And the path is pretty dreary, as is the setting. Give this one a miss.

I bike or rollerblade here on every visit to Santa Barbara; it's a beautiful, peaceful destination, and enjoy seeing the plants, trees and birds.  I agree with Douglas M that last summer, it was very stinky.  Articles said it was due to poor water circulation and a rapid algae bloom and decay, resulting in noxious odors.  Don't know about that, but I'd agree w/ the smelly part.

They must have come up with a solution, because when I visited most recently, it was just fine. Check it out!

My first time here and being from Las Vegas found it to be a peaceful and serene setting.  I saw several different types of birds while here including humming birds.  A nice area to take your children and walk the little paths.

Oh Bird Refuge,   You are so beatiful but what has happened....  I know you are getting a little old and tired, you are filled with silt and so many years of a bad diet seems to have your arteries clogged and your digestive system overwhelmed.    Because I am a good friend I am going to tell you something, not to embarass you but because someone has to let you know.    You kind of stink these days.  Yes I have said it!   And I can assure you that behind your back others are saying it too.   We can smell you on the 101, throughout montecito and even as far away as Alston Road sometimes.    

I don't know the solution, not my area of expertise but something needs to be done!   Your role in life it is to make Santa Barbara a better place and usually you do that so well, but these days not so much.   I hope that the pond Dr. has paid you a visit and has a perscription to bringing you back to you your former fresh scented glory!

I love bird refuges. There's always something to see. The huge lagoon gives the birds a place to hide away from the people on the two islands. The tract /path around the lake gives for plenty of opportunity to view the birds. However on the track are mostly runners and bike riders giving the birds no more than a glance. I have only found one small parking lot around the corner on what I would call the far side. The edge of the water is pretty grungy. Amazing how adaptable the birds are. Oh well.

I actually found this park while making a wrong turn trying to find the freeway entrance. I sat on the bench that overlooked the pond covered in different species of birds. Lots of families rode by on their bicycles, or were on their Sunday run/walk. Very peaceful place if you're looking for some "me time" like I was to read my book

I like the bird refuge for some peace and quiet, but do I agree with the other comments that it is "grungy," "old," and sometimes "stinky?" Yes. 

It's a nice getaway if you are on the East Side. A bike trail goes around part of the brackish water, which drains through East Beach, only minutes away by foot.

You can also park your car at the bird refuge. It's right in front of the Montecito event center and very close to Stella Mares restaurant.
