Also a great spot to bring a six pack and a boombox for some outdoor, nature loving communal bonding! 

Tourists, have you tried Fig Mountain Brewing yet? One of their main beers is actually named after the gorgeous spot. My recommendation is grabbing a six pack of Lizard's Mouth Fig Mountain beers and heading up to the Lizard's Mouth "hike" at sunset to take a totally trendy social media pictures that all your buddies back home with be jealous of - Makes for a great time and an awesome memory! Don't forget to find the rock that looks most like a Lizard!

Short hike, not really a mountainous trail per say, but rather a crap load of huge rocks that go for miles that are great for exploring. Really a fun time and sort of a workout! Come with a few friends and make it an adventure. The only tricky part is getting there, but just know that if you're at least near the gun club you're in the correct vicinity. Make sure you wear the right shoes and bring sunscreen, and of course PLENTY of water. My friends and I always work up an appetite after this hike so you can always look forward to grubbing down on whatever you feel like afterwards :)

Just like the other reviews said, Lizzards Mouth isn't really much of a hike mileage wise. Depending how much you walk/climb around you can probably travel over a mile while you're here. The trail that leads in here is maybe about half a mile until you get to the rock area of the mountain. You can find Lizzards Mouth pretty quick, most of the area you'll can pretty much rock climb/hike.

Up here you have an amazing view of the mountains in the distance, as well as the ocean. If you decided to venture around the rest of the area try to keep try of where you are because there are a ton of paths on the lower end of the mountain and you can get lost pretty easy since some of the paths lead you around in circles.

Beautiful gorgeous hike. Very short trail so don't worry about the distance. It's perfect during any time. Not too crowded. Great for dogs. Careful there is a good amount of rock climbing. But even a grandma can do it. There's a shooting range next door. Easy to find. Definitely worth it. You can go any route and you'll end up at the lizards mouth. Great for pictures. My top 5 hikes. 

Explore the architecture and look at the different rock patterns and caves. Not many lizards LOL

I think everyone should come to Lizard's Mouth at least once. It is as easy or difficult as you want since there isn't much of a trail but lots of boulders for rock climbing. I would like to come back here with a tri-tip sandwich from Cold Springs and watch the sun set.

Came here on Saturday for a Yarn Bombing event!

This isn't so much a trail hike as it is a labyrinth of trails! Pretty exciting, because you just adventure around looking at all sorts of stuff!, jumping on rocks and checking out the view! Heard its a great spot for bouldering or rock climbing. Took my dog and let her roam around. HOWEVER, this seems like prime rattlesnake territory so I would keep your dog on a leash for their own safety!

There is graffiti on some boulders, which is thoroughly disappointing, but you can't do much about that. :[

The journey to get to this place is just that, a journey. The yelp directions did nothing, but get me lost so I had to get creative in looking for directions online.

Here's how I got there, off the 101 you hop onto the 154 a few miles down the road (about 7) turn left onto West Camino Cielo and take this one lane road for about 3 miles. This road seems like you don't belong on it because it seems to only be used by residents, but once you drive down all the way you'll see the entrance to the trail head, which is a brown pipe in the ground and a wooden billboard just before the gun club.

I put these into my gps (34.503055, -119.865691) and got there much more easily than the yelp directions.

Once you see the pipe you just park along the side of the road.

When you see the sign for the hike, go to the RIGHT of it. We went to the left and wandered aimlessly for quite a while. : / Anyway, the hike is straight after that and pretty easy. After a short amount of time, you'll get to the cliff with great views! To get to the actual Lizard's Mouth Rock, it's a bit more treacherous and we decided not to go with a toddler. But the view is good at the end of the easy part!

Short hike but Awesome place to explore and do some amateur rock climbing! Gorgeous views of Santa Barbara, the Pacific Ocean and the Channel Islands! 

Directions to LIZARDS MOUTH
From the 101 Freeway west of downtown Santa Barbara, exit on Highway 154 and head north into the Santa Ynez Mountains. After 7 miles, turn left onto West Camino Cielo (if you reach the East Camino Cielo or the top of the mountain pass, you have gone to far). Off Highway 154, West Camino Cielo makes an immediate right before winding up to the top of the ridge. Take this road for 3.5 miles and enjoy the views over Santa Barbara. The trailhead is on the left side of the road a few hundred feet before the Winchester Canyon Gun Club. When you reach the gun club or the point where the road becomes unpaved, turn around and backtrack to the roadside parking near the trailhead. A wooden board next to a tree (and a brown pipe on the ground) mark the start of the trail to Lizard's Mouth.
Trailhead address: West Camino Cielo (Forest Route 5N12), Los Padres National Forest, Santa Barbara, CA 93105
Trailhead coordinates: 34.503055, -119.865691)

If you're looking for a couple hours of fun for family with kids, consider this place. Kids would love climbing around the rocks. Getting there is not very difficult but the road is narrow. GPS will not take you to the entrance but it'll stop in the middle of the road. Keep on driving up. The entrance is on the left before the gun club. If you reach the gun club, you already go too far and must turn around. When you drive down, not far from the gun club, you'll see the entrance with the sign on the right side. The sign is not curbside and it's easier to spot when driving down than driving up. Parking is on the street. There are a few large areas off street nearby the entrance. Just park in one if those areas. With a short walk on the trail, you'll teach the rocks area like a vista point with the views of Santa Barbara and the Pacific Ocean. It's windy up there so bring a light jacket or a windbreaker even in summer. Wear comfortable shoes, hat, put on sunscreen, bring water & snacks to enjoy the day. Kids will love climbing around the rocks. This place is not suitable for people with mobility problems.

The silhouette of lizards mouth during sunset is gorgeous!!! It gets really windy though and it will require some rock climbing. It's not difficult at all to get to, but don't wear non-sensible shoes like heels/sandals because it's not a nice paved trail.

One of the best views in Santa Barbara! On the All Trails app it says it is a hard hike but it really isn't! It's only 0.3 miles but there is a lot of rock climbing! I recommend bringing a backpack with a blanket, some food, a speaker, and food company! Definitely a place you can spend all day at. For those that want to practice bouldering/ rock climbing there's a few you can practice on.

Getting to Lizard's mouth from the road is just a short walk. You do have to climb rocks and jump around since there isn't much of a trail. I did notice some arrows that people drew. It is a great place to look out over SB. I loved watching the sunset from here. For awhile, I didn't know we were on top of Lizard's Mouth. I had to climb down to see it.

Lizard's mouth is the perfect get away from the hustle and bustle of everyday life. The drive up is about 40 minutes coming from downtown Santa Barbara and is curvy, forested, and fun! 
There a sign that marks the trailhead and a good amount of parking on the side of the road. It's only a 5 minute walk up from the trailhead to Lizards mouth.
There are a plethora of rock formations: ones to climb inside, ones to jump to and from like Lilly pads, and ones perfect for picnicking. There's a safer, flatter area that's great for kids and dogs and a little further on is a more advanced climbing area with larger boulders and crevices.
Don't be alarmed if you hear gun shots in the background. There is a private gun range nearby, but they keep that area safe and secure.
If you live in Santa Barbara and haven't gone up to Lizard's mouth yet, I highly recommend for a fun 1/2 day adventure.

Good hike.... Although the path is wild. You have to do quite a bit if jumping and climbing and stuff like that.  Definitely not ADA accessible, unfortunately... Takes not too long to find the lizzards mouth.  Great views of the sunset and ocean

name: Surf Country
categories: Surfing, Shopping, Surf Shop, Active Life, Fashion, Skate Shops, Sporting Goods

yelp_reviews.text: My son and I wanted to take surfing lessons and we got a whole lot more.  Doug was terrific with both the 7 year old and the 41 year old.  I would highly recommend him for anyone looking to get a taste of what surfing is all about.

Been shopping here since the beginning. Best in town, you just can't beat surf country.

That should say something that Surf Country sticks out as the best in a surf town.

Great place to catch some stoke , rather in the shapers bay doing first or fifth board or in shop for some local knowledge..its a genuine shop you can feel.. I shaped my first board here and Doug was awesome, helping anyone who popped in while keeping me scraping away at my Sweeet mcfishy.  .thnx again  Doug

On our first night in SB we asked the bartender at Longboard's where we could get surf lessons for our 3 boys.  He gave us Doug's #. Within 24 hours of our phone call we had 3 boys suited up and learning the basics on the beach right near UCSB. Doug did a GREAT job assessing the boys' skill levels for 13, 10 and 8.   He took them all out and by the end of the lesson they all got up!! Doug cheered just as much as we did. He had a great attitude and the boys really liked him. PS he had more energy than the kids getting them back into the water ASAP for the next round. It was great. Definitely look into Surf Country for lesson with a local surfer- the experience was fantastic.

Doug is awesome. I bring all of my boards here for repair. Doug has brought a few boards back to life that I was not sure if the would be fixable. He does great work and has the best repair prices out there. He will make sure to get you back in the water really quick. Thanks Doug!

Limited time to write my review, so I'll update it later this week.

It's right off Hwy 101 and there's plenty of parking.

But in a few words; friendly, laid back, knowledgeable, informative and honest.

You can take your time and not feel pressured while you're looking around.

The boards very from new to used and from 9'6 long boards to youth sized thrusters.

Highly recommend you drop by, especially if you're in the UC Santa Barbara area.

I love to support local but the guy working there was a compete jerk when I came in to pick up some resin. Acted like I was wasting his time. Next time definitely going to progressive surf where they appreciate their customers

Doug is a truly spectacular instructor!  He gave private lessons to my daughter (13) and my boyfriend.  He was extremely knowledgeable and extraordinarily patient, 13 year old girls can be a little testy at times :).  He was super encouraging and kind. We will can't wait until next time!!!

I've been in here a handful of times. Each time the same guy is very rude. You should learn how to treat CUSTOMERS. Amazing how you can work in a surf shop and be so un welcoming and disrespectful. Definitely will not be returning . And recommending to everyone I meet and know to not go there.
Pretty sure the guy is Doug. Long hair
I took lessons with Doug and it was a great experience. I had zero experience surfing and Doug had me riding waves within the first lesson. He is very knowledgeable and answered all our questions.

As a surfer, you should always shop in a store that supports the local surfers...period. Surf County is exactly this, a local shop run by local surfers. Where this shop differs, is that you are welcomed when you walk in the door and won't catch a sharp eye from behind the counter. You will be welcomed with a smile. Doug and his staff are knowledge, helpful, and professional. They have got you covered from apparel and accessories to ding repair and boards. Their board selection is top notch and most importantly shaped by the best local shapers. Go see Doug and his guys and start riding a locally made board (as you should be) and quit riding the mass produced "pop outs" that are clogging up the lineup.
Found this shop here on Yelp! Awesome find... Went looking for a wetsuit for my 10 year old, walked in, asked, they had one, bought. I'll return for any wetsuit needs in the future.

Went in for some info and to possibly buy some fins for and older Infinity twin fin I picked up at a garage sale.   The wisdom the owner and eventually another guy that was there (who happened to be an Infinity team surfer from a better time) shared was unreal.   I know it's hard to articulate feelings, but these guys truly made my day.   Thanks
Solid surf shop. Helped us find the right booties and other surf swag. Rented me a long board on short notice at a fair price. I'll be back.

Surf Country is my go-to shop in the Santa Barbara area because you don't get stinky-eye surf-punk attitude here that you might at other shops.  Some highlights:

1) The owner Doug is down to earth and really wants to get you what you need without up-selling you.  I've been in there and he's talking to a mom who wants to buy a board for her son to learn on, and instead of unloading a $600 potato chip the kid is gonna struggle on, he's steering her towards a cheaper learner board and lessons.

2) You will get a lot of tips in there.  If you want a wetsuit they will let you try it on and help you get the right size.  If you want to know where to surf to learn they will let you know where.  If you need advice on ding repair, they will repair it for you or tell you how to do it yourself.  

3) The surf lessons are the best I've seen.  I see them out at Campus Point a lot doing surf lessons, and I have never seen Doug fail to get someone to stand up on a board.  People ask me to teach them and I tell them, you can struggle with me for 2-3 sessions and maybe get up, or you can schedule one lesson with Doug and most certainly stand up.  Case closed.

Anyway, a great shop with great lessons.  The best from Goleta to Carpinteria.

When you walk in, you might be surrounded by the little surf-rascal-groms that work (hang out?) here after school. They're really nice and helpful. This is a full-service shop, well stocked with boards, accessories, and clothes.

When I took my board in here for repairs (big bad Baja rock, bad bad steering skills) the kid surprised me. He said "we could definitely fix it for you, but we have to charge a minimum of $30 and that's not really a $30 job. You could probably do it at home for cheaper, and this would be a good project for you to learn on." If only I could find a mechanic like this kid!

Surf country went above and beyond. They did an amazing repair on the surfboard and now it's good as new. The service was friendly, professional, and affordable. I recommend this spot if you ever need advice or repair. Thanks!

Great surf shop that Ive been going to for the last 14 years. The owner and staff are all very friendly and always down to chat with you or give you good tips on surf spots.
As well as sales they also have a repair shop for anything that may have happened to your board and they have a small selection of skateboarding stuff for sale as well.

I hit a rock on my brand new board at Rincon and brought it to Surf Country for repair.  It pushed the fin box into the board, cracked the glass job on the rail, and broke the tip off the center fin.  Blake was working behind the desk and was very helpful with getting me set up for the repair.  He explained that the fin didn't need to be replaced, and showed me how to repair it with sun-cure.  The repair was less than I expected and looks amazing.

My go to surf shop from here on out.
Doug helped me find a perfect wet suit to rent for my 5 year old nephew that was visiting. Great experience!

The equipment sold at Surf Country (boards, gear, etc.) is overpriced and shoddy at best. Doug does decent repairs and knows his stuff. However, his demeanor is impatient and at times surprisingly rude. I went in to buy a used shortboard. I found one I liked, and wanted to ask him some questions about it. Thus began some impromptu interrogation from him regarding my purpose with the board. I've been surfing for about a year and have made enough progress to get onto a shortboard. Doug essentially saw fit to treat me like a kook and acted rather immature and aggressive. He is a good salesman, but needs to work on his customer service.

I went to Channel Islands in downtown Santa Barbara instead and bought a better used shortboard for less $.

Doug, if you're reading this, you lost a sale and a future customer because of your behavior.

Surf Country = junk sold to unsuspecting customers!

Awesome surf shop. The guys that work here were very upfront with what approach would work best for a cheap beater board I bought off craigslist. Helped me figure out the most cost efficient way to fix my board, as well as being upfront about recommending me to stay away from a particularly band delam, which saved me some money. No upselling here, just good business. Thanks guys.
Very helpful and informative. Got used board and repairs here. Love the board he recommended and the repairs look great.

Doug is a surf guru that is welcoming and freely shares his knowledge on all things surf. We recently moved to the area and needed a shop to outfit my kids. He was able to guide us on getting the right boards, rack for the car, wetsuits and booties. We will be back often to support local business and learn from Doug.

To UCSB students, 
If you're looking for a "local" surf shop with jacked up ding repair appraisal (fairly certain they raise the price for students and have a $30 min.) , stock boards with worse glass jobs than Jack's, and an owner who manages to have the worst customer service while also being demeaning, Surf Country is the place for you! 

Highly encourage you all to check out Bob Haakenson fiberglass for quality ding repair or support your local IV surfers who will have undoubtedly lower prices (i.e maxdoesdings). For used boards, check out Craigslist or CI downtown for higher quality used boards/better deals. If you're thinking of buying new boards here, don't. There's a reason you won't see many people surf Surf Country boards out in the water. Plus you'll save yourself from interacting with doucher doug!

Personalized Great knowledgeable resource
Board ding or disaster repair is amazing and affordable

Took two surf lessons from Doug, once with my wife and the next day with my groomsmen. Doug is an amazing instructure! He teaches you the basics and works super hard throughout the 2 hour lesson to get you standing and surfing. I had zero experience so he positioned me at the best part of the wave and told me when to paddle and when to stand. Also gave me a nice lesson on how to read bouy maps and surf reports. Great guy, super fun lesson!!! I recommended him to all of the guests at my wedding last weekend.

Came from NY and needed a board for my son to use while here. The guys were great. Friendly and accommodating. Rates were fair and they even took into account we took it out the first day late in the day and returned it before 5 PM a few days later. Took money off due to both situations. Highly recommend

A friend and I had come to visit SB for the weekend and wanted to try surfing on our own. The service was very friendly and fast. Rentals are very affordable and the employees are lenient as to when you bring back the equipment. Best surf rental that I've been to in the SB area!

Best surf shop I've been to. Nice selection of new and used surfing equipment. Owner is very knowledgeable about a wide variety of surf products. Past two years I've been coming here, never had a bad experience. They also have lessons, rentals, and skateboarding equipment too.

My 3 adult kids had a fantastic time surfing with Doug last week.  Firstly he was very flexible as he called to let us no the waves were not great on the day we had booked.  We rescheduled for the next day.  The abilities were varied which was not a problem for Doug.  He was helpful, funny and all round great guy.
Best place for ding repairs in SB! Doug knows so much about surfing, and he genuinely enjoys teaching or just helping people learn more about any aspect of the sport.

Nothing but positive things to say. Moved here from Florida and Doug gave me the run down on the local breaks, how it'll be different from what I may be used to, and recommended a great board. Hit the water immediately and it's been serving me well.

I've been here twice, each time was a different person working, but neither of them had masks on! Just suck it up and wear the damn mask. I won't support such idiotic behavior and will not be coming back during or after covid.

WEAR A MASK for god's sake. All your costumers have to wear masks, cause, you know, we're in a hotspot of an exponential surge of a global pandemic, but the guy who works here thinks he's special and doesn't have to.
My 17 year old daughter had her second lesson with Doug while I watched. It was excellent and we hope she gets accepted at UCSB and can have more lessons with Doug. Thanks Doug!

Dirty and unkept. We forgot a Bikini and stopped​ in to grab one so we could hit the beach on such a beautiful​ Santa Barbara day; however, the selection was spotty and dated, the prices high for what the had and the bikinis were dirty. We left and went to Channel Islands which was clean and had an excellent selection with a variety of prices.

name: Cloud 10 Jump Club
categories: Trampoline Parks, Active Life, Party & Event Planning, Event Planning & Services, Gymnastics

yelp_reviews.text: This company doesn't really want customers or else they would have such a ridiculous online waiver process. What makes it ridiculous is that it doesn't work and you can't submit, at least from my browser. When you call the store, they can't help you. Printing it out and bringing it in would make A LOT OF SENSE, but unfortunately, their ridiculous system doesn't allow you to do that and tells you you must SUBMIT the form online. Catch 22 or just plain bad business? When I called and asked for the manager for a work-around solution, I was told that he's "in a meeting".

Purchase some tickets on Groupon to take my kids out for a great evening here.. It was amazing they had a blast I highly recommend it..and I got to say it is great that they have amazing lounging chairs for parents. They had great air conditioner so it does not smell sweaty which was great they do have a little snack bar. And the people there were great. Definitely come give them a try your kids will love it.

Brought the kids here today. They had a good time which is what mainly counts. Price was right and it wasn't so crazy busy that they were able to enjoy it. It does need a good clean and the supervision is a little lacking. Watch your own kids and you'll be fine

Great way to spend an hour or two and will certainly get your little ones to nap! We had a great time with our 3 and 2 year old boys. Khary (sp?) was an awesome employee. He could not have been any nicer to my kids. He had such a nice way of telling my boys the rules, but was still fun.

Bottom line, the kids love this place for basic jumping, we hate it for birthday parties. If you're interested, story below:

We booked and paid for our 3 year old's birthday party online 1-2 months in advance. My husband often takes the boys there during the week to jump, so while they were there at least 3 times afterwards, he checked in with the front desk to confirm our reservation. The guy at the front checked the calendar and said we were booked, and we would be hearing from the event coordinator. When we didn't hear from anyone, my husband checked again. He was told that we would get a call a week before the party. We were never contacted by anyone.

The day before the party, I called and left a message (it is not possible to call and speak to a human), saying that I would like to add a fruit salad to our party. I also said that despite the fact that the website says no outside food/drink, I would be bringing juice boxes because I am not about to give 3-year-olds Coke. Also please call me back to confirm that this is all okay. No call back.

The website says to have your guests arrive for the party 15 minutes early to sign waivers. Since our party was starting at 10 and they opened at 10, we arrived just a few minutes early. There was one other mom there (a high school classmate of mine) with her kids and several of their friends. Doors opened exactly at 10, but the guy at the front wouldn't deal with us because he said we needed to wait for our party coordinator. There was another party booked at the same time, so the only party coordinator I saw started dealing with them and refused to help us. At 10:20, still no party coordinator, but finally the guy at the front let our kids in to at least start jumping. Keep in mind that they are 3 years old, and they'd already been waiting for 20 minutes.

Our party coordinator did not even speak to me until 10:45. I had no idea who he even was. He was actually very polite, but by then the damage was done. They gave us 2 free pizzas for our inconvenience, a couple of free jumping vouchers, let us stay past noon (obviously since we didn't get our party room until 10:45), but the fruit salad was "picked up by someone else." In the end he was very apologetic that things were so disorganized, and thanked me for being so understanding, but I was actually furious and only keeping my cool so our guests would have a good time. No one likes a momzilla at a birthday party.

Communication is huge for me, and this place has none. Never being contacted by anyone is beyond unacceptable. IF you want to have a party here, show up with your group, pay for them to jump, and go eat somewhere else afterwards. This is what my high school classmate did - genius. You couldn't pay me to have a party here again.

Ok, so here's the situation: I planned a birthday party here, not one of the packages but just a large group of people. I called a few days in advance to let them know the day and time I was planning on being there and the duration (2 hrs). I asked if they gave group discounts, they said yes, for groups of 10 or more it would be $8/person for the first hour. The person I talked to said that they put up a note at the front desk to make sure that we would get the agreed rate. I called back the next day to ask if people showed up late to the party (not on the hour) would they still be allowed to jump, and the front desk said yes, that jumpers get a full hour regardless of when they show up. I was really looking forward to the party. 

The day comes and people are showing up, quite a few people are running 10-15 minutes late so we wait for them. At about 15 minutes after the hour we buy our tickets. They would not let us purchase tickets for two hours, because they said they might be closed depending on who shows up, but that we could go back to the counter and purchase a second hour later on. We brushed this comment off and got jumping. 

They only had two staff working the jump floor and there was our group and one other group there, so we only got to use one area at a time. They gave us a warning when 15 minutes, then 5, and then we were told our hour was up and that they were closing (3 hrs early?!?). We got dressed and got our phones and stuff out of the lockers, and milled around for 10-15 minutes deciding where to eat and using the restroom. When I got into my car, I realized it was only 7:10, which means we were only given 40-45 minutes of jump time. Added up, that is almost 3 hours of jump time we were shafted split across my group. This, added to the fact that we weren't allowed to use enough areas for all of us because there was no one "supervising" the other areas when the people working there were just going on their phones and talking to the front desk people the whole time anyway, rubbed me the wrong way. I wouldn't go back here for free jump again. 

The good: The trampolines appear to be in good repair, the building is clean and doesn't smell. There are wipes in the bathroom you can use to clean your feet. They also have free combination lockers you can use while jumping. There is plenty of free parking. 

Overall, I would probably stick to weekends for free jumping so that they have enough staff and you can use all the areas. Scheduled classes or some of the special events during the week that they offer are probably your best bet to have a good experience.

Bottom Line; An awesome place, especially for kids!

My wife and son have been going here for quite a long time to learn how to bounce, tumble, climb....the staff is awesome and so are the facilities.

They have toddler times for the littler kids and the worst part about that, is I cant go participate as I have to be working during those times!

BLUF: We will never come here again and ask for a tour of any trampoline park we visit next before paying to "play" because of this place. It was that bad.

Overall there are 6 trampoline play areas.
1 is reserved for classes only (gymnastics, parkour area)
1 is reserved for kids under 7 (basic play area)
3 are for solo jumping only (basic jump area, half pipe, bball court)
1 allows kids to play together, as long as they stay on their own trampoline (dodge ball)

Cloud 10 is not a trampoline park for kids, it is a gymnastic gyms that have additional areas for kids who are not signed up in their classes. Because of this focus, or maybe an extreme worry over injury, kids aren't allowed to play with each other outside the Dodge ball gym. Rambling aside,  here is our adventure from the start.

We came in on Friday an hour before closing and were told Cloud 10 was closed due to a party. We explained it was our first time and they were awesome to us and gave us 50% off coupons for our first hour. 

We came in the next day to jump and spent 5 minutes each on their iPad signing liability waivers. The iPads were slow and required signatures on each level. It is standard for this sort of place, but took much longer than at SkyZone (an awesome trampolinr park) and was extremely frustrating to use.

After this we were able to pay and get into the gym. With 50% off, we were able to pay for 2 adults, 1 kid, and 1 pair of socks for $25. (Socks are optional). Thank God this is all we paid.

After entering the gym, we wanted to go on their half pipe. Despite its' large size,  we were told only one person is allowed at a time. Frustrating, but we waited. The boy had fun playing on it for about 30 seconds, but was bored quick as he was the only one jumping. To get out of the half pipe, you have to jump on this large inflatable bag - cool, right? Not so much. You can't get close enough to the edge of the half pipe to jump properly. Don't get me wrong, it is still a fun jump, just disappointing for what it looks like.

Next we went to the left where they have four mediun rows of trampolines set out. Kids again are not allowed to play with each other so the boy became bored quickly.

So, we moved on to the Dodge ball court. This court was actually enjoyable as it was the first (and only trampoline) thing we could do as a family. It is laid out with four (i think?) trampolines on each half of the court. Hypothetically, you are supposed to stay on your single trampoline, not cross other trampolines, and play the game. Thankfully, there was only 4 people playing and the kid manning the game did not seem to mind if we had fun. 

Next, we moved onto the trampoline basketball court. Again, only one person was allowed on a trampoline (one trampoline covered a small half court) and the jumpers were not allowed to play with each other. The girl manning the station did see that one of the hoops were low and raised it for us, which was great. Thankfully, the kids manning the stations began flirting with each other soon after so we were able to play a mock game before we were told to stay on our own sides.

We then tried the gymnastics center and were soon told that the section was off limits unless you paid for a class. 

The only thing left was the younger kids area, which we were not allowed to play in because the boy was older than 7 despite no one being in the area.

That left us the bathrooms. For a place that requires jumpers to go barefoot or wear socks, you think they would have exceptionally clean bathrooms, right? The boys said that there was urine on the floor in every stall and it was overall disgusting.

Cloud 10 also has an air hockey table and Foosball table for people to play at.

Overall, this place was not worth their discounted cost of $25. We were extremely disappointed with our experience and that this place is advertised (or appears online) as a fun activity place when the reality is, you can't play on most things as a family and, despite the lengthy liability forms you fill out, you are overly bombarded with rules. We will never go back here again.

If you think this is a great place, visit a Sky Zone. You will soon realize how a trampoline park is supposed to be!

Would be great, but the staff allow teenagers to mess things up for the kids.
Apparently its a thing for teens to come in to the dodgeball area to do smack little kids in the face. Never seen so many tears at a "kid friendly" place.
wth???
Would be great otherwise..

Thanks for letting us transform one of your party rooms into a Whole New World (Jasmine theme) for our princess Catalina's 4 year old birthday party last weekend.  Catalina and her guests had a blast thanks to your awesome staff, especially Mark!
Both my grandsons LOVE Cloud 10 Jump Club.   It is a favorite treat when they are visiting.

My daughter attended a birthday party here last week and it was really fun! This place is very clean and all of the employees were friendly and helpful. I had the added bonus of her being exhausted when we left! We can't wait to go back and jump as a family!

THis place is way too expensive for only one hour of bouncing, but children's activities are scarce in Santa Barbara, so I have taken grandkids there a few times.mTonight I was babysitting so went on line to check the hours, they were listed as 3 PM till 10PM. On the entry door, the hours were listed as closing at 9 PM. We arrived 15 pat 7 and were told they closed at 8! I was given a discount, but believe they should stick to their posted hours.

If your goal is to entertain a child anywhere between age 3 to 13, this place is great.  I see parents getting in as well.  We are here on a Saturday and all areas are open, staffed and bustling.  The party rooms are not being used and I see plenty of outside food being brought in.  My guess is that if you are an uppity perfectionist, you'll find something to complain about.  But if you just want to spend a small amount to put a smile on your kids' faces, this place is wonderful.  Clean jump areas and floors, well behaved patrons and engaged staff.  For ten whopping dollars, I'm not expecting the Biltmore, and they've definitely delivered here.  Enjoy!
Super fun place but not as clean as i would have hoped and the check in process is EXCRUCIATINGLY slow, even if you've prepaid. Very annoying when your kid just wants to go play.

My 2 year old loves Cloud 10, the Cloud Kid area is perfect! Very safe and my wife can feel comfortable not having to chase him around.
 Play dates are definitely perfect here during the summer, you can get in early and get them tired before nap time.
The only reason I couldn't give them 5 stars but them thus deserving 4 would be do to an unfortunate miscommunication we had with some of the newer staff members. Management completely rectified everything and I am very satisfied with how everything turned out!

Can't wait for my kids joint 5yr/7yr party when I can rent out the whole place and have a blast!!!

I have no idea why there are so many negative reviews! Obviously I read them, but I strongly disagree! We travel from San Luis Obispo JUST to come here! The price is very fair, the entire place is VERY clean and the staff is amazing!! The staff interacts with the kids and are SO friendly! 
The waiver is a necessary evil, due to their liability. It took about 5 minutes to fill out the first time we came, and now we walk in, get our bands and pay...all taking about 3 minutes before we are on the trampolines! Just an FYI, go at the top of the hour, it just makes things easier! May kids LOVE coming here, and they are exhausted when we leave! I wish we lived closer!!

Gunther was a superstar!! We came at 7:30 and cloud 10 had closed early. So he saw that I brought my brothers and their families from out of town and opened it for 15 min so the cousins could jump together before bedtime. He also gave us a few free passes to come back another time.  Uncles had a blast with the kids!! Thanks for your accommodations to let us play! 

I would suggest calling at 7p if they have no jumpers they sometimes close early.
Thanks for being SO nice Gunther at cloud 10!!

Not worth it to go as an adult or if you have kids u see 5. Never been here and they wouldn't let us do most of the area. Our 3 year old would play in the kids area so we would go play with her and had multiple snotty kids tell us only kids under 7 aloud. We told them thank you and ignored them the first time then the second kid same thing I'm mad now and state excuse me it also stated for parents to watch there kids. The 3 and 4th kid I told to leave is alone now. The 4th was pushy trying to push us out. Then ran to tell on us. No staff ever asked us to leave the area. Then they have this gymnastic area that apparently only accepts campers. No one was in there. At all just the staff member playing on there phone waiting for payed campers to decide to come to the place. Then we tried to go to the big jumping area with all the trampoline and told that we couldn't be there either. It was a complete waist of time and staff and the kids there were all very rude. It was 30$ for an hour .... seriously.... what we could do we could go to micdonalds play house and get an icream for the the crap this place was.

Trampoline Half Pipe was closed down today.  Employees should have posted a sign at entrance. We brought older children ( 11, 12) and they were looking forward to Trampoline Half Pipe.  We did not know and when our kids went to get in line an employee rudely yelled, "hey, that's closed".  It wasn't until I brought it to their attention that they posted a closed sign.  $15.00 each and $3.25 additional for socks.  
Glad it's only an hour! 

We won't be back!

Today was my second time at Cloud 10. Both times I went in the morning when only kids seven and under are allowed to go. I love that they provide specific times for little ones, and the facility is pretty awesome (and clean!). 

My first time I went in I was seriously considering become a member. I was bouncing with my two year old daughter on the big kid trampolines (no one else was there). The employee warned me that that might skyrocket my daughter. I thanked her for her input but said I understood this of trampolines and was being careful. She said ok and that was that. 

Today was my second time and the same thing happened - same trampolines, still just the two of us on the trampolines, both of us jumping. The second we started, two employees immediately told me that was not allowed at the facility and I needed to stop. I want to acknowledge they were nice in their delivery, but having two employees say it at the same time, and each repeating themselves a few times, put me on the defensive. I shared this with them. They said they were just doing their job. I said I understood that and wasn't asking them not to do their job, but their delivery made me feel whoa. I continued that I was surprised I was being told I could not bounce with my daughter on trampolines, especially when it was just the two of us. I asked why this is. They said they didn't know why. I said I thought it was weird they were strictly enforcing rules that they were unsure of why they existed. We left it there and I obliged by no longer jumping. My daughter ran to me and grabbed my fingers so I could bounce her. She's only two so is still learning to jump. I did not jump in any way, but with her holding my fingers was trying to teach her how to jump by bouncing her myself. Again, both employees told me to stop, that this was not allowed. Fed up and bewildered, I asked why a trampoline business would not allow me to jump OR bounce with my daughter or allow me to aid my daughter in learning to jump, especially since not another soul was in our area. They told me they didn't not know, I could ask the manager, and needed to be on a separate trampoline or stand on the side. I was now pretty irritated. If I'm not allowed to bounce with my daughter or teach her to jump, this should be stated before I buy a ticket. She's only two!!! I swore to myself that after today I would not return.

Then I met the employee Jesus at a different trampoline area. He was amazing. I told him what had happened. He actually offered explanations to me, but made me feel good at the same time. He acknowledged that if no other kids are around, we should be able to enjoy ourselves and that he understood I would not do anything that would jeopardize my daughter's safety. He then went above and beyond with his interactions with my daughter and myself. When she threw a tantrum at one point and I explained she's been through a LOT with her health and this maximized how bad her tantrums can get. He seemed to totally care, inquiring about her. I found this to be touching. I left in a MUCH better mood and realized that I can't write this place off completely. This guy deserves a raise. Jesus was the man. He got a customer to go from I'll never return to maybe one day I'll give it one more shot. He understood the balance of following the rules when it was necessary while trusting me as a Mom. 

I spoke with other parents who expressed the same frustrations I was feeling, saying it just depends who's working each area and how crazy each employee can get. They need to be consistent. If they're so strict it's turning off other parents, they should rethink on how to fine tune it so they can provide a safe facility but not go overboard in killing the fun to a point multiple people agreed in frustration. 

I truly believe the managers might want to consider having a sit down with their employees. People who are ringing up customers should make rules clear before payment is collected, because otherwise I wouldn't have paid to go here. Who goes to a trampoline park with a clingy toddler because she was so sick to be told I can't play with her? Employees should also be instructed on how to talk with customers when they feel rules are being broken as to not put customers on the defensive. I hope Cloud 10 listens.

Waste off money! $30 for 2 kids to jump for one hour. Place is very small and the few trampoline stations they have for older kids, are very limited.  One jumper allowed on a trampoline at the time, instead of playing together. Another area was "out of service". Staff at the entrance did not notify us  about the broken equipment before possibly the steep entry fee. Staff at the trampoline stations were not engaged and spent their time hanging out together at a table, playing on their phones. Not worth the money. I'll drive to sky High in Camarillo next time.

Great space and service. The best of both bounce/trampoline sports and jungle gym type places. My 4 year old especially like the play place structure which is a pretty good size. 

They also have a trampoline basketball court if you're feeling like Mike. They have a big foam bag you can jump into, an obstacle course, and several other trampoline areas. I thought the price was right, too. It wasn't too spendy, aside from the food court was a bit pricey.... all in all fn for kids to get wiggles out and super clean, too!

Lots of jumping fun for the littles. We came on a holiday and it was mostly empty compared to other time we've been here. Can be a good thing because no waiting in lines, but also a bad thing because there's nobody to play bounce dodge ball with! Bathrooms are clean. Food prices are a bit high, but perhaps typical for an "arcade type" of play place. They have automatic shiatsu machines that are amazing .

Really great place to take the kids! Very fun and friendly atmosphere. The staff is really great with kids and aren't rude or mean when they enforce the rules. 

They get really busy on the weekends and the place gets a little crowded... We've been to Sky High and they have a lot more stuff and entertainment for parents.

A decent place for kids to get their energy out, but the staff is lacking. Wonderful friendly staff at the front desk, but everyone manning the floor is unhappy that they have to work. They spend most of their time drinking sodas and flirting with high school kids, and any actual supervision is in the form of yelling at kids. 

I understand it's a loud environment and you have to raise your voice to be heard. That's not the issue.  It's more the manner in how they speak to the kids. 

Seems like a sweet job, if you don't like it, quit.

Fun Exercize for kids of all
Ages! The Dodgeball is awesome & my little one loves it!

Signed my daughters up for camp. What an incredible waste of money. The only adult "camp director" ignored everybody but 3 children and had 2 young teens in charge of the rest of the group all day (20ish kids). Two days in a row. I will never return to this place and spend another cent. They need to get certified coaches and not have children in charge of children in my opinion. Dangerous. What if anything happened what is a 13 year old going to do? 
Do not suggest camps to anyone

SO MUCH FUN!!! I have to say, I was particularly impressed by the cleanliness of the place.  Great place to take the whole family.  The staff members are all enthusiastic and invested in the quality of the enjoyment at this place.  I will, without a doubt, be returning in the near future.  Great addition to our Santa Barbara community!

We drove all the way from SLO County to come here.  It's a great place for kids, I'll give it that.  But it's a bummer that there's only TWO stations our kids can play in.  They have a basketball area and a half pipe area they've closed down because "they don't have enough staff" to run them.  Yet, I am sitting here by the Learning Zone station where there aren't any kids in the classes and the staff member is just playing on the acrobatic 'ropes'. Not worth the drive nor the $15 per kid for 2 hours if they can only play in one trampoline area and the Dodgeball area. But then again, when you've got limited options, this would be a cool place to take the kids if the place was staffed properly.
The best toddler activity in town! Great staff super involved with the kids both for safety and play. Thank you guys!

This place is awesome.  There are areas for the little kids and the adults can jump around and have a blast too.  I hear they have a dodgeball league too.  I would recommend this for anyone looking for a party or something fun to do.  I have also heard awesome things about their gymnastic program.

We just moved to Santa Barbara from Hermosa Beach and this place is everything! My daughters are 2 and 4 and 100% obsessed with Cloud 10 Jump Club! We come here at least once a week! Wall to wall huge trampolines and kids play zone, just awesome!

The only reason why I'm giving this place TWO stars is because my party had a great time and the party host was a total sweetheart.

Other than that, I do NOT recommend this place. The price for a party of 15 is just under $400 for two hours of jumping, two pizzas, water, and socks. If you need to cancel your party, tough luck, they keep all your money and you're stuck with a hole in your pocket. No refund for any reason. 

For my party I had a group of 17 people who were 25 years and older. We were shot many dirty looks, had to ask for our backpacks or we would not have received them, and they didn't properly set up our party room. 

Don't waste your money and don't forget to read the fine print all over the website.

One thousand rules, a screaming guy telling you, you can't do this you can't do that ummm aren't we in a place where kids can let lose and have a little bit of fun! Remember fun!!!! Nop can't do anything. And there is a whole section in the middle of the whole place with all this fun stuff you have to pay extra to play with???? Nobody told us that either. For 13 dollars a person I would take your kids to the movies.

My review is for the special days of little kid hours only.  

As of this review, on each Wed morning (and maybe Tues?) only kids 7 and under and their parents are allowed.  Normally little kids can only play in certain areas.  On these special days and hours, they are allowed to try everything.  

My kids (6,3 1yr) loved it!

Staff
Staff was kind, helpful, friendly, seemed concerned for safety, never rude to the kids.  They played with the kids on the dodgeball and basketball court.  No negative staff experience while we were there.  I did call the night before to ask questions about the facility, and the lady on the phone was a bit impatient with me.  I was nervous going in, but thankfully everyone on staff today was great.

Cost
kids $10
kids under two $8
parents following little kids $5

I was nervous paying $8 for my 18month old.  I am used to kids under 2 or 3 being free places.  It was not a problem.  He LOVED it.  He did this cute toddler walk all over the trampolines.  He played with the dodgeballs.  There is a fun huge playstructure.  

We filled out the liability waver on line before coming in.  DO THIS.  Easy forms on the website.  Saved time.

We jumped barefoot.  No high pressure to buy special socks.

I was clean, well lit, great parking
Kids had a blast. Snack section is way more reasonable than the other jump locations.  Very friendly staff. Not packed and easy to keep an eye on your babies.

We love spending time at cloud 10 my boys absolutely love this place 
The only problem I have is that the toilets have no seat protectors so I have to squint to go pee not fun for a girl 
While I get pee on myself I mean really even Ross has them but not cloud 10

This place is awesome for kids! Finally a much needed spot for kids to let off steam and all that energy they carry! 

I had my sons 10th birthday party here and we were very pleased with the staff and hostess that was provided to us to help make sure our whole experience ran smoothly! I'm sorry I can't remember her name but she was very helpful. 

Our pkg came with socks provided to each child; up to 15 max! Plus 2 large pizzas from Rusty's, and a sack pack for each child as a partying gift. They say backpack but it's a sack pack. It didn't come with anything inside so I was glad I made candy bags for each child to put inside. Be sure to bring toys or candy to put inside for a little extra treat! 

I was able to bring in my sons birthday cake and they even provided plates, utensils, napkins, knife and spatula to serve the cake! Just FYI bring your own candles. 

To sum this place up I want to say I highly recommend you try it out! Your child will love it. My son had a blast and that's all that matters.

Giving this place one star only because well, it's a good idea but it has so much to improve on. Customer service was horrible and what i believe to be the supervisors used profanity. What kind of environment am I leaving my child in? It's over priced and the only thing my daughter liked was the worker who actually talked to her and made her feel welcomed.  The owners should be more concerned about customer service then about stealing money out of working parents..... It makes me wonder how any parent could leave their children at this place.

My daughter wanted to do the dodgeball league but it's so expensive so I had to tell her no. She is so disappointed. 

Overall, I am really disappointed with my experience and will not be going back or letting my daughter go again.

Cloud Kid Area VERY DIRTY. I wanted to love this place, but the cloud kid area was very dirty. The under 7 play area is great with cute, pint-sized trampolines that are a lot of fun. But crawling through the play structure, my hands were coated in grime and dirt. I saw hair and trash debris in the netting throughout. Maybe the staff could thoroughly wipe down the play structure nightly? And parents could wash their kids hands after eating?

I can't rave enough about the experience we had at Cloud 10 today. They've extended their hours to jump for $10 on Tuesday and Wednesday from 9-1:00. My son and his friend played hard and laughed with utter glee in the dodge ball section where an attendant named Kharys played with them non-stop with more enthusiasm and energy than I could ever hope to have. He was AMAZING and so sweet, firm when he needed to be. He captured their attention like a pro. As a mother, I appreciated that so much! Going back every Wednesday possible!
Great time, family friendly.  Great support staff that is visible.  Us adults forget how fun it is to jump around

Kids really enjoyed this place...but a bit of a bummer...several stations were closed down due to...as one gentlemen stated...not enough staffing.....but I watched several staff members walk aimlessly around....playing on their phones and not doing their jobs....basketball court was never open in the two hrs we were there...

So I really want to like this place but so many things keep happening that I just am not a fan. The member thing is so confusing and not worth the $$ the equipment is sooo old it barely has shape. They set up a ninja course with old out of shape gymnastic padding. The owners daughter came into the ninja course area and was very unprofessional. She mentioned how old the equipment was and that it was a different shape now then when they bought it years ago. :( so said the stuff is so expensive they don't update it.  I am trying to find an alternative but so far no other trampoline places are within a decent driving range. The bathroom reeks of urine and kids run in and out barefoot. I want to like this place but the germs and smells are making it hard for me. I will give it more opportunities to change my opinion but so far I'm not loving the teenage staff that are either not doing anything or are on a power tri

Great place its fun for kids definitely!  Although they should have a sign to remind adults "You are playing with children here" my niece, who is 7 years old was hit hard in the chest by a mid 40 year old lady. Adults keep in mind it is a children's playground!

My friends and I went to this place to celebrate one of our own's graduation from college. We had a lot of fun and it really is a work-out! None of us had ever gone to a trampoline park before, but we had a lot of fun and the staff was great. I'd definitely at least try it out. :)

Cloud 10 provides a much needed service to our community.  My kids always have a great time when they go.  However, that said, there customer service and response to customer inquiries is TOTALLY lacking or in my case, non existent.  I requested on numerous occasions to have a manager return my call for a very simple request that continued to go unacknowledged.  Absolutely TERRIBLE.  Kid friendly- but definitely not parent friendly.

My sons love going to Cloud 10. Staff is very, very nice and friendly. They are kind to kids especially the ones manning the acrobatics side. Just one comment in the food area, and again, staff there is very nice too. Selection is minimal which is understandable but even with the minimal selection, I've encountered some challenges. In my recent visit, I ordered Nachos - machine wasn't working. When it finally worked, cheese was cold. I ordered pizza, we had to wait for the delivery. Pizza is your most popular so stock up on it. I ordered a hotdog, hotdog was expired so Manager purchased at Vons. I was getting a drink and diet was not available. I then started on the diet dr pepper and it stopped mid way. In the end, we were able to eat just delayed. You have a great place and wonderful staff, improve the food area because there is so much opportunity for revenue there. We will come back, just hoping for a reliable place to eat while watching the kids.

name: J7 Surfboards
categories: Surfing, Shopping, Sporting Goods, Outdoor Gear, Surf Shop, Fashion, Active Life

yelp_reviews.text: Best boards in Santa Barbara. J7 private label clothing and accessories, and a super knowledgeable staff makes this shop one of the best. JJ isn't bad either , the shop chihuahua
I've bought a wetsuit and many t-shirts & trunks. Always a great selection of merchandise. Its my favorite place to buy birthday gifts. EXCELLENT customer service and great prices!
Huge selection. Knowledgeable staff. Killer Axxess card deal! Kid friendly (people and equipment). BEST PART: Want to talk to your shaper? You can here. Direct access to Jason Feist.
This place was awesome. The guy there was really nice and friendly. We rented paddle boards and had a great time. If I'm ever back in Santa Barbara I will 100% come back here.

Hands down the most epic surfboards in the world! Thanks again Jason for shaping us the best wave riding vehicles over the last two decades!!! And thanks Tarik and Tom for the ultimate customer service and product knowledge. These guys know the ins and outs of surf industry and the best in the biz! The J7 crew recently expanded their shop near the beach in Santa Barbara's Funk Zone and it's world class!!! Super convenient location and they got everything you need under the sun!!!

PERFECT 10!!! 5 STARS!!! BEST PLACE IN SB TO GET HELP PICKING OUT A BOARD!
J7 knows how to help kids pick out the best board for their surf level. "Other Shops" suggested boards that would have set my kid back years in surfing, J7 put my son on the perfect board to advance his level. The staff was more then considerate, professional and service orientated when it comes to picking out boards. This is now our Go- To for board purchases in SB.

Just bought a custom pill from J7. Been on the hunt for a groveler for almost a year before settling on that board. The staff was super helpful throughout the process and very down to earth. The board was delivered exactly when it is promised, which really surprised me due to the horror stories I've heard from some of my friends who got custom boards. It's all the more incredible considering the volume of orders they receive at this shop. I've surfed it several times since I've gotten the board and I'm very happy with how it performs.

These guys are great! Super friendly. I picked up a board earlier today and the staff was very knowledgeable and helped me make my choice. I even got to meet Jason himself while he was taking a break from shaping. I'll be back in the future!

I have been riding J7's for more than a decade now and the boards have evolved into amazing shred sleds. Jason is an awesome shaper and it's great to talk to him about boards, come up with dimensions that can improve your surfing, and pick his brain a bit.
There's a great selection of boards in there. I think I have every model currently in my quiver. The new remodeled shop is bigger and better. super stoked to see the business doing so well. These boards are the real deal! Swoop one up or talk to Jason about customs.

name: Chumash Painted Cave State Historic Park
categories: Active Life, Parks, Public Services & Government, Landmarks & Historical Buildings

yelp_reviews.text:
My husband and I came here as part of our tour of the California Coast.  While these caves were really interesting, they were not quite as exciting as I had hoped.  The cave itself is blocked off by a gate so as to not disturb the paintings.  Since it is a cave (and hence dark), it is difficult to see inside.  Nothing a little flash photography couldn't fix!

Finding the cave was fun, and this isn't a very popular attraction so you don't have to worry about hoards of people.  We were here during peak vacation season and we didn't see another soul.

You have to "hike" a bit from a trailhead, but it's not very far or even remotely difficult and the trail is well marked with signs.  You do not have to worry about missing the main cave, as it is well marked and obvious that you have reached it.

Overall, I would not go out of my way to come here, but if it is on your way I would definitely make it a point to stop and check it out!

It's a very windy road to this cave but once you get there you need to park on the one car lane close to the curves. It is quite a sight, it's worth it if you enjoy history and appreciate it. Otherwise, you will see it and say it was a waste of time..,

We are a family of four. 14yr old teen boy and 12yr old girl. They both liked it.  Very, very, narrow road. One car at a time. Very, very high. (Thank goodness I was not driving!). Very short "hike".  More like a walk. The "park" and cave can be seen in 5 minutes. Views are outstanding! The history behind the painting makes it a worthwhile trip. Parking is located across from 2671.   Best to park there and walk the rest of the way.  Or if you want a longer walk, there are two or three other parking areas prior to that.

This is a pretty cool place to check out if you are in the area. The road to get here is very narrow and windy so drive cautiously. There isn't any parking, you have to pull over to the side of the road. You definitely don't need a lot of time here. It's a quick 10 minute stop to admire the cave and area.

The photos you'll find here or elsewhere are actually a great depiction of what you'll find once you get to this cave. I have to admit: the best part was actually getting to the cave itself, because once you're there (this was my experience, at least) you can't really see much inside the cave. 

The drive up is perilous and switch-backful. If you're afraid of heights and narrow, winding roads, then you better let somebody else do the driving!

Once you get into the mountains, you'll see a rocky outcropping and a sign. Off to one side you'll see a small turnout. That is the "parking lot". We didn't realize it, and drive right up into the small town at the top of the hill. Don't bother; you can't get to the cave from there. I suggest driving past the cave sign until you can make a safe three-point turn, then head down; you'll notice an area where two cars can pull over next to the mountainside, then walk a few minutes back to the cave itself.

It's literally just a few steps and a cave barred off to visitors. Don't get too excited! But in terms of something you don't see everyday, it's worth an hour or two out of your way, and getting up the mountain is interesting/exciting with some AMAZING views!
Awesome place.  The drive is beautiful!  It's quiet and secluded.  Great places to stop and take pictures on the way down.   Please preserve this place and don't pollute.
Unfortunately when we went it was closed off. We past it a couple of times bc the sign was covered as well. There's not much to do considering it's just the painted cave there

I heard about the painted cave from a friend who had been here a few times before.  There is only one cave and a path that leads nowhere but why I wanted to visit was because I was told the area is very calm and the energy was great.  It didn't disappoint!

We were excited to come see Historic Painted Caves. After a windy drive up we apparently passed the site and kept driving up the road. Once we finally managed to get reception and look up the exact location we had to double back. We finally found the tiny little space but another car had taken the parking and the rest of the road didn't look to inviting.

The upside to all this is that we came around sunset and on the way down we parked on the side of the road and watched the sun set.

Get ready for the biggest let down of your life since finding out you were adopted. No parking. Shocker for the kids but no Starbucks either. Its a cave with some small paintings that may or may not be from the 1600s. Maybe 3 cars can park here if they are the size of a go-kart. I guess the best part is that its free and so it gets two stars.

I think by "Park" they meant you'll have to park to see the cave.

Heed caution if:

 - Easily car sick
 - Don't handle disappointment well
 - Unable to identify poison oak -- there's a ton of it
 - Viewing 1600's graffiti isn't your thing
 - In a wheelchair -- you're SOL

Yes, the roads are windy and small. The park itself is small. But you get breathtaking views of the ocean, and a somewhat scary experience driving up there. To me, the journey and the experience was worth it. 

Don't expect a huge place where you can park and stuff. You're on a side of a giant mountain; the Parks department isn't going to build a parking lot for you. We parked on the side of the road a bit further than where the park is; didn't want to block traffic.

The cave itself is barred off, but if you look at the paintings, almost every one of them has been defaced, which explains the bars. So blame the people who came before and ruined a piece of archaeological history as to why you can't get closer.

There's one painting that you can theoretically access up on the left side, but it's quite a climb, but it was also a nice surprise.

I would advise coming here and then going to get food at Cold Spring Tavern; it made for a nice half-day trip up the mountains of Santa Barbara.

If you have a spare hour and you are in the Santa Barbara area, visit the Chumash Painted Cave. It quite a bit of a driving experience up a narrow road that becomes a one lane. It's a pretty small area and very easy to miss. When you see the sign, the cave is literally right there. There's no parking lot. I parked right in front of the sign.

I would say the entire park are is about a 30ft by 30ft area. There is a short trail to the cave (and when I say short, I mean like 15 ft long). You can't actually go into the cave because it is blocked off by a gate, and the space needs to be preserved. Some people have tagged the art, and I can see the purpose of the gate. However, I am a big history fan, and this place is still enjoyable... just don't expect some grand park. It's nothing like that. It's a little trinket of history between residential houses that may have otherwise been overlooked had it not been found by whoever found it.

The drive is a nice windy (full of turns) drive all the way up the mountain. Beautiful views along the way. Drive slow and take your time. The painted cave is directly above the sign on the road. You can look inside the cave and see old drawings. It's not spectacular but it's cool enough. Once you're done that keep driving up the road to Knapp's Castle for some nice views.

This is not a day trip, it's an interesting side trip enroute to somewhere else.  

As others have noted, the drive is a little nerve wracking and the cave is covered in iron bars to maintain the integrity of the site.

Still our family enjoyed seeing it, including our five year old.  Crazy to think someone painted these over 400 years ago and they remain intact.

From downtown Santa Barbara, the short drive to the Chumash pictographs provides excitement as Painted Cave Road winds up a mountain providing gorgeous views along the way.

The wonderful destination called Chumash State Park offers some vibrant pictographs easily visible from the heavy metal gate at the entrance of the little cave.

This will be a quick point-and-shoot experience unless you walk across the road then take some time to sit in the shade and cool your feet in the lively stream.  (That's my routine.)

If you've never seen pictographs, these are a good introduction and you get can back to Santa Barbara in time for lunch.

If you're going to the Cold Springs Tavern nearby or are otherwise in the area, this is worth a visit. As others have said, it is small and you may need a flashlight to see the paintings. It's a unique glimpse into the history of the area, and it feels sacred, like a place where people put a lot of good juju over a long period of time. It's probably not worth going well out of your way for, as the road is tricky and windy, but I for one really enjoyed seeing the paintings and being here. Just be aware this is really not a hike or an attraction where you'll spend much time.

Not really a park...more like a stop on the side of the road. There isn't too much to see here, just some cave paintings behind a huge metal gate. I wouldn't drive to make a trip just to see this - pair it with a trip to Knapp's Castle so your long twisty drive will be much more worthwhile. Set your expectations super low. 

- bring a thin flashlight: the cave is completely dark
- there's not really much parking: maybe 3-4 spots on the side of the road. if all those spots are full, you are SOL because the street is so narrow (only wide enough for 1 car to pass)
its is quick stop not much parking on the side of the road but well worth a look see.....

If you are easily car sick, DO NOT GO. We had some queasy people in our car. Wow, what a twisty drive - very beautiful views on the way up.

Alas, very little to physically see when you finally get there (and no, there is no parking lot - just park at the actual sign). 

I honestly recommend looking at the art in a book or online - you'll get to see more details. It is extremely beautiful. 

We got passed by a cyclist on our way up. Hats off to you, Mr. Cyclist.

The name is far more interesting than cave itself. First of all it took us about 30 minutes to find this place (driving up and down unpaved windy roads). Second, there is absolutely no parking. We pulled over on the side of the road where the road was slightly wider. When we finally got to our destination we saw a large fence and a pitch black cave. Don't bother coming without a flashlight (or even with one).

It is surreal to realize that hundreds of years ago, the Chumash people painted beautiful art on the walls of these caves and they are still here.  There is a spot off the side of the road for two cars to park. It would be nice to hear a bit about the history from a docent, but I understand that the park is super small. Nonetheless, it was cool to see the paintings in person.

Definitely worth defying death both on the way up AND down on a narrow mountain road with twists and blind turns...and though the cave is covered by a metal grate, it is still easily viewable. Others mentioned lighting issues, but I went at 1pm  and the cave was completely lit up by natural light.

The cave drawings/art itself is pretty impressive and definitely worth seeing especially since the drawings have been there for quite a few hot minutes. It is also in a nicely forested area that you can take a little hike behind where the actual cave is.

Fair warning, blink and you pass it! I used my GPS and found it just fine. Also, there is absolutely no parking! You have to pull on the side of the road and if there is two small cars or an oversized minivan, then you are SOL til one of them leaves. It's wise to leave your blinkers on when you are parked as well.

This is not a park. It is a tiny cave on the side of a perilous one lane road. Driving up to the spot was quite an experience. Watch out for cars coming the opposite way, the road is so narrow that you cannot avoid them. When you make it to the site, you have a climb up and over a few boulders to see the paintings. A metal gate keeps you from getting too close to the actual paintings. 

Was it worth the effort? Not really....

DON'T RISK YOUR LIFE TAKING PAINTED CAVE ROAD. It's very narrow and many blind turns. On your way up there's cliff at your right hand side. 

I can't image how people deal with cars coming from opposite direction. We were lucky that we didn't encounter any other car on our way up. We took another road, E Camino Cielo down.

I felt very mad afterward that I accidentally got myself and beloved family into this dangerous situation. When I realized it's not a safe road, there's no way to turn back and we had to keep going. Very scary experience. When we finally got to the site there's no parking. 

There's should be warning signs about the road condition somewhere.

Drive up scared me to death but once you get to the cave you realize it was worth it. The location is beautiful and very lush. The painting is remarkable. 

Drive down is much safer and the lookout points are amazing. Great photo opps.

Review 24

I never knew this landmark existed till about 15 years ago and went see the Painted Cave at the following family road trip. Getting there was very easy through the extremely steep hill and switchbacks, the roads weren't as they are now which is great. 

The paintings haven't changed since the first time I visited, the small area is kept very clean by the park and visitors. The community is very quite and neighbors are friendly, my last visit I spent almost an hour talking to cyclist heading up to their homes past the caves. 

I've taught my boys to pick up 1-bag of trash everywhere we go, bought them a cool foldable trash gripper that they still use when traveling. 

WATCH FOR CYCLIST !! it could be on that road


Plan B.

Chumash Painted Cave State Historic Park isn't very large. The roads leading up to Chumash Painted Cave state park are definitely 'advanced' level.  Small tiny windy roads, one lane wide. Barely two cars wide. It's certainly an adventure getting up there. A Proper parking lot is non-existent.  The Parking lot consists of a one car long divot on the side of the road.  The sign marks the park. If you pass the sign, you've got to continue further up the road until you hit a driveway or somewhere to turn around.  I never before thought my car was all that long until I had to turn around on this road.  After turning around several times above and below looking for a park, eventually I squeezed my Camaro past a cute girl in a tiny tiny red car.  I noticed her window was rolled down. 
     While we were passing, I stopped alongside her, and rolled my window down. I asked; "Is there a state park around here?"  She laughed, and said 'Yes, it's right over there, just pull off the road in front of the sign and go check it out."  I thanked cute girl in tiny tiny red car, and did just that. I pulled off the road as best I could, right up behind the sign. I then carefully got out of my car to go and check out the park.  There are some stairs leading up to the cave, and it's been fenced off for its own protection. I greeted a small lizard that was sitting on a log sunning itself. I then took a look around the place, snapped some pictures, and returned to my car. The whole visit took maybe twenty minutes. 
     The paintings are pretty cool, and I wonder what they're about. There's probably a very interesting story here that I'm not getting fully. I hope through careful study will we eventually decipher the paintings. Thankfully, it's caged off to protect it from drunken hoodlums.  It's a neat little free stop, and it's a pretty cool link back to the people and cultures of the past.  I'm glad I've seen it, but it is most defiantly a bitch to get up there to see it.

Very long super narrow road up to an anticlimactic cave. There isn't much places to hike up there that I could see. I think this road can be good for bikers who want to challenge themselves. Otherwise, I was not a fan.

About 30 minutes drive from downtown Santa Barbara lies the Chumash Painted Caves "Park".  Not much of a park, but more of a pitstop along the road.  

The road to get here was very windy and uphill, and on a clear day, you can see SB and the coast from up high above.  I came on a cloudy day when it was about to rain, so the weather was pretty dismal that day. It's actually kind of hard to pinpoint where to go exactly, as the "park" doesn't have a real physical address. If you decide to come out here, use your maps app, follow the directions, and drive slowly until you see boulders with stairs leading up to a gated area +/- cars stopped along the road. There isn't any place for parking except for the side of the road. 

The total time I spent at the park was probably at most 10 minutes.  It's really just the caves, which are locked and gated up, that there is to see. As a fan of art from different cultures, it was pretty cool to see these paintings that were painted hundreds of years ago by the native Chumash people, who still live in the area (they own the Chumash Casino down the 154 highway).  Besides these caves, there really isn't much to see besides that.  I probably spent more time driving to the caves than I had actually spent there.  Oh well.

I wouldn't recommend coming here if it's going to be your sole destination, but would be OK if you're going to see other things along the way like Solvang, Nojoqui Falls, the Chumash Casino, etc.  By itself, the drive isn't worth it.

Pretty neat place that not many people know about and can go to. The way up is a super steep narrow road but it wasn't too bad. There's not really a parking lot and space really, you just park on the side of the road next to the Chumash sign. The cave is unfortunately gated (I'm assuming because of vandalism since in the caves I saw many initials and hearts etc) 

You need to bring some sort of flashlight or use your phones flash to see inside the caves. Bring a jacket also! It's cold since it's in a shaded area.

I really feel horrific not giving 5 stars to a Chumash site and especially since they worked so hard 500 years ago to paint this thing, only for me to come along 500 years later and give them only 4 of 5 stars on yelp. Sorry Chumash! But it is so diminutive--you drive a ways off the 101, along the 154 highway, and then up the painted cave road that is truly scarily narrow at places. It feels like you should come upon Altamira or Chauvet or something--and then it is just a tiny cave, with bars over it and a five foot square foot painting on the ceiling. Okay, it's all history so it's worth it...

This painted cave art by the Chumash people (dating back to the 1600's or earlier) was a unique sight to see, especially since the meaning of the images is already lost. The cave is protected by a heavy iron bars and you probably won't spend more than 5 minutes there. I would recommend you combine Knapp's Castle with the Painted Caves in the same visit since they're only 15 minutes apart. 

The drive up the mountain to the cave is steep, narrow, and has lots of sharp turns. Once there, you'll have to find street parking as there's no parking lot. The cave is only a short 0.1 mile walk away from the road.
