Swung by here today to check out the painted cave. We were already in the area to see Knapp's Castle. It's a small area along the road, so we almost passed it. Parking is along the road, so very limited. You'll probably only spend 5-10 minutes there max. 

The cave is actually walled off by metal bars, which allow you to see the painting but you cannot touch it. Preservation purposes!

This was listed on a hiking website but there is no trail.  There are only two parking spots and a few steps and cave with the opening gated off.  Cool paintings but you have to look from the cave entrance 15 feet away.  Neat place to stop if you are on the way to something else in the area but not worth the drive for this alone, it takes all of 5 minutes.

I was told this is California small state park. It is definitely really small LOL. 

The drive is really winding as well as steep. There are houses on the road and the people that live up there drive really fast. It is important to remember that you have to pull off the road to let people pass. There are few spots to do this, so drive with care and forethought. 

Once you locate the state park, there is only room for 2 cars to pull off the main one lane road. You have to park right against the rock wall. I let the kids out before pulling over. 

The cave is awesome and barred off to prevent vandalism. You can put the camera throw the opening and take pictures with no bars blocking. After the steep winding road, narrow passage, fast moving locals, you can sit in awe of the art work the Chumash have left for us to view. 

It is pretty amazing to think how the Chumash lived before the conquerors assimilated them. Alttle sad also to imagine what had been lost...

Yes, as other reviews has indicated it isn't really worth the drive and to go out of your way, but I really wanted to see it! So we went and it was a gloomy day- but the views from the mountains were phenomenal! If coming from Santa Barbara turn right onto Painted Cave Road and you will drive up quite a lot, about 15 minutes or so. So once getting there, you will drive past the sign a little and park your car in a tiny, tiny area! Then you will walk down ( carefully) to the cave. Inside you will see the paintings, which are amazing by the way! It is a bit hard to see as you are standing behind a metal door, but there is an opening where you can peak through. 
Besides seeing the painted cave, you will have an amazing drive up there and coming back down. 
Beware the road is very narrow and dangerous so please drive with caution! You cannot see the cars on the other side of the hills while going up and coming back down so I advice honking to ensure they know you are there ( great safety measure!). Either way, it is a great drive, as long as it is not totally out of the way- it is worth it! Enjoy.

For anyone who appreciates history and archeology, this is the perfect stop en route to Knapp Castle, Cold Springs Tavern or Los Olivos/Solvang. Why not learn a bit about the Chumash natives who have called this beautiful paradise home for hundreds of years?! As you climb up the twisting hair-pin Painted Cave Road to the cave, take a look back at the Channel Islands where the Chumash would often fish for food while in their canoes covered in local tar from the beaches. 
Side note: The road is very narrow and becomes one single lane; remember the car traveling up has the right away and to drive slowly! Once the path has trees arching over it and the sun disappears temporarily, keep an eye out for the landmark sign on the west side of the road so as not to pass up the cave. There's only space on the side of the road for about 3-4 cars, so drive carefully. The actual cave is protected by a metal gate with bars since people have sadly defaced some of the petroglyphs. There are two main spots with drawings and paintings in red that date back to the 1600's. You'll see a lot of small pockets in the rock which is where the Chumash use to mix and place their paints made of local plants. See Paolo H.'s review for more historical background. It can be dark inside the cave to see the images so I've found myself using my phone's flashlight to peer inside--just don't drop it! It's awesome knowing there are hidden caves like these throughout the area! If you enjoy this experience, take a trip out the Carrizo Plain National Monument and register for a pass to see the Painted Cave there. Stunning!

Super great pictograph. It's a shame they had to protect it with a heavy gate. It's very difficult to get a reasonable photograph.

Worth the stop

Poorly marked roads made it impossible to locate the first time. Found out you have to travel the narrow and steep road much further than we went.
May try a second time.

I think whether you enjoy this or not depends solely upon managing your expectations properly. 

What is this? This is a location with essentially nothing but a small cave with beautiful, ancient paintings from the chumash people thousands of years ago. Walking to the cave takes 10 seconds, and viewing the paintings in the cave probably takes 2 minutes. 

What is this not? It is not an expansive park with long and winding hiking trails with multiple caves for you to go spelunking in. This isn't some extended excursion. 

Also, it is important to understand that the drive to this cave is not exactly easy. You will take the painted cave road, which is long and extremely narrow and winding. There are several blind hairpin turns. To be honest, these are extremely risky, as there are a number of bikers zooming down the hill without regard for their own lives. You cannot see them approaching, and you cannot see other cars approaching. Take it slow, and make some sounds with your horn on particularly sharp turns. Do NOT take a trailer, commercial truck, or anything else of that nature up/down this road. The edges of this essentially one lane road are not protected at all, and you will likely get yourself in a sticky situation.  

Once you reach the cave, which you will know by a large sign, there is no parking lot. The road near the cave has a very small turn out towards the inside that can partially fit maybe two cars. That's your parking spot.  

After you park, you will walk up the stairs for 10 seconds before reaching the cave with the paintings. The cave is barricaded to prevent vandalism, and rightfully so. I saw so many beers, beer boxes, bottles, bottle caps, cigarettes, face masks, and other trash littered everywhere along the road. I'm sure that the same disgusting people would deface the entire cave if allowed the chance. So, this means you will only be able to peer into the cave from the outside. Make sure you bring a good camera and/or flashlight so that you can see well. 

That's pretty much it. You also get nice scenic views of all of santa barbara from some vantage points along the road.

WOW.....I guess I should have read the reviews a bit more closely.  This spot is the size of a small closet and it's on the side of a harrowing two-lane road that is basically a cart path.  Residents zip down in their fancy cars, leaving others to try to avoid them while also trying not to drive their car down a sheer cliff at the side of the mountain.  

The cave itself could be cool, but there isn't much there and the sign isn't very helpful in terms of information. 

The better part of this trip was traveling back down the mountain and stopping at some of the look out points - which were amazing.

I wouldn't make this an integral part of any trip unless you LOVE cave paintings and don't mind heights, minuscule roads, or having to park in a spot that is DEFINITELY not safe....oh - and don't try to turn around on the cart path...not a good idea.

The best part of this experience is the one-lane road to the Cave. Generally speaking the car going up has right of way, but don't expect any yielding from anyone under 30...

As for the cave, I'd recommend that this be a stopping point for the Knapp Castle which is a few miles up the road. While fascinating and a great learning experience it's only a five minute one. Why not visit two cool places?

Tip - Bring a flashlight
Don't waste your time coming up to these caves. They are closed and there is nothing to see. Just a fence covering everything. What a bummer because the entire family was so excited

We just visited here on our way back from Santa Barbara and we are glad we did. Here are a few things to know:
- The drive up is windy and steep and it will take you more than 10 minutes to make it the 3 miles off the mail road to this location.
- Parking is virtually non-existant so you have to pull over where you can. 
- You will probably only need 15-20 minutes here as the trail is right next to the road and just a few steps up. 
- The cave is fenced to keep people out but the painting are in full view
I recommend this to anyone who hasn't been here. We found a lot of other great hikes in the area as well if you are looking for something that takes a bit longer!

Interesting and unique site.  Be warned that it is up a long, narrow road that is winding.  It's about 15 minutes off of the main road and steep at parts.
When you get there it is a roadside stop with no parking lot and nobody is there.  There are 2 narrow parking spots on the side.  The road is so narrow I had to drive further up to find a place to turn around.  Not a fun drive at all.
There are fantastic views at the top.  You can see real Native American art up close.  It's a few minutes stop but when will you be able to see something like this?  
It's free and worth seeing if you are in Santa Barbara.  It was about 45 minutes total.  Just be careful with the drive.

This is such a cool piece of history, I am glad we stopped by to appreciate the painter cave. It's kind of tucked in so easy to miss, but the sign is helpful. Also a great spot to hike when it's not too warm, will definitely visit again in the spring!

It's worthwhile to take some time to visit this small sandstone cave and view the Chumash rock art if you are ever near Santa Barbara. It's located high up a mountain and off the side of narrow winding road. The only place to park is in one of the two spots located right in front of the site.

It was interesting to learn that painted caves were the location where Shamans would go to enter into their supernatural world. The pictographs illustrate their supernatural experiences in their vision quests. 

If you were able to view the main wall straight-on you would be able to make out a rattlesnake and a centipede. The rattlesnake served as a supernatural guardian and spirit helper of the Snake Shaman. The centipede was thought, at the time, to be deadly and dangerous; it was symbolic of the Shaman and the supernatural world. There is some debate about the sunburst and other geometric images. Some say that they are depictions of the sun or an eclipse and others say they are hallucinogenic patterns seen by the Shaman in his altered state. There are also some human figures thought to represent Shaman heads and ghosts floating in the supernatural. 

It was also interesting to learn that other villagers would avoid rock art sites even when located within their own villages. They wouldn't even look at them and they wouldn't dare touch the panels. They feared swarms of insect-like creatures and sickness were inside the sites. 

The protective gate in front of the cave opening helps to deter vandals. There's a small opening that allows you to take pictures. If you visit in the evening you may want to bring a flashlight.

Love it here! It's nesteld on top of a mountain so you get great views when there's lil to no fog. I came here twice with my puppy to bbq, lounge and hike. They have plenty of picnic areas that you don't need to reserve. They also have a fenced off leash dog area. I didn't use it but it looked ok. 

The grounds were big and clean. The hike up to the gazebo was long in the heat but very nice. I kept thinking mountain lions were gonna get us since they had warning signs but we made it out alive :) This park is a must if you're in the area and looking for a nice picnic spot. Just watch out for the ticks :)
Honestly, not much to do here.  It is definitely a small place to ponder.  It is great for dogs.  I wouldn't go alone, it is a little deserted.

Such a beautiful little piece of paradise tucked up in the mountains. Have driven by several times. I decided to finally stop in this afternoon quick since I had a little extra time in my day. Absolutely worth the slight diversion on my trip back to LA. Felt like a best kept secret, not another soul around-probably doesn't help that it was a bit rainy this morning. But really really enjoyed the solitude and beauty, was very peaceful. Food for the eyes and the soul.

Dog friendly!

Address on yelp might leave you confused, so use this one to get to the exact front of the park:
3399 Toro Canyon Park Rd
Carpinteria, CA

Stopped by here to check out the trails on the way to Santa Barbara. We are early hikers (to avoid the heat of the summer) and accidentally came too early, most parks open at sunrise but this park doesn't open until 8am apparently. 

Driving to the park was interesting, there was no traffic, very deserted, in the middle of no where feeling. There seemed to be at least a ranger truck plus some maintenance people so that at least was comforting. 

There is a wire, fenced in area where you can have your dog off leash, it's not very big but its something I suppose.

Plenty of parking. There is a trail sign where the trail starts, we walked all the way to the gazebo and it was only about half a mile. It looks like you might be able to walk further on paved road, but we heard coyotes barking and decided it would be safer if we were not so adventurous. There might be another trail at the end of the parking lot, the path did not look very worn and there was a mountain lion warning sign by the gate, so again we erred towards the side of safety. 

This place really does have that middle of nowhere, wilderness feeling. Its eerily (and peacefully) quiet, but do watch out for fox tails, mountain lions, and coyotes. Due to this place probably not receiving much traffic, I think you might be more at danger here if you're not careful.

Very nice, and very clean park out in Montecito. there are a lot of trees so that means there is quite a bit of shade for those hot summer days when you cue up the BBQ! You can sit there and just enjoy the nature that, that park has to offer, a lot of scenery.
The only downfall is that since it a ways up there, there is no cell reception. For the most part my phone said "no service" so you really don't have much contact with the outside world!

This park has a quick trail that leads to a gazebo with a partial ocean view. It's a simple hike which kids could definitely do. 

The park is great but the real 5 star show stopper is the Toro Canyon Ridge hike up the road from the park. It is just a small pull off with room for about 2 parked cars just up the hill from the park (if you start driving downhill you've gone too far). There is a sign that says no parking after dusk and it's right after a gated drive. The hike is relatively steep at first but not bad after the initial incline. Most of the hike is hiking the perimeter of a beautiful estate. You'll walk around and then at the end have a slight incline and then BAM the most gorgeous sweeping ocean views come out of nowhere. There are few Santa Barbara views that leave me as awestruck as this one. There is a stone bench up top to enjoy the views from. This hike is worth it for sure!

No smoking allowed throughout the entire park. SWEET! Clean flushing bathrooms, running water, soap. Dogs have dog park area fenced off and not allowed in bathrooms. Nice for dog people and those of us that prefer the park experience to be dog free and smoke free.

We've stopped in quite a few times now this summer.  The grandkids love climbing up the huge rocks and enjoying the view from the top. It's a great spot for a picnic, some photo ops or just a short break from driving between LA and Santa Barbara or beyond.

We haven't tried out the creek bed yet, but I'm sure we will on a future trip.

All right, It's my turn to give back to Yelp and share one of my absolute favorite parks in Santa Barbara. My wife and I used to come here when we were dating, and now we share it with our 5 year old daughter.  I have always enjoyed catching frogs in the creek at this park, and sharing it with my daughter feels like a right of passage. 

Located above Summerland in the foothills between Montecito and Carpinteria, Toro Canyon Park is 74 heavily wooded acres of secluded beauty. A creek meanders through this oak filled canyon past playgrounds, horseshoe pits, several large group BBQ areas, a dog park, horse trails, hiking trails and even a sand volleyball court. There is even a trail that starts by a huge sandstone outcropping and takes you up to a gazebo at the top of a hill that overlooks the entire region. It is an easy hike, and well worth the sweeping vistas. 

Although pretty close to several cities and the 101 freeway, we rarely ever run into more than one or two other people at this park. Of course we typically go on weekdays, so perhaps the weekend gets busier.

On past visits we have seen innumerable birds, a few rabbits, plenty of lizards and even a skunk. The area surrounding the park is even wilder.

Just how wild is Toro Canyon Park? Last February we decided to blaze a new trail along a rain run-off ditch that went deep into the chaparral. It had rained the day before, but that afternoon had been a beautifully warm and sunny day. As we hiked up the hill about an hour before dusk I noticed some large dog footprints heading down the hill in the direction we had just came.

Now, mountain lion and dog prints look pretty similar except mountain lion tracks have an "M" shaped pad while dog track's usually imprint claw marks (I was an eagle scout that spent summers as a camp counselor in the mountains of Kern County!) Well, I couldn't see the claw marks or really make out the pad shape, but the ground was hard and really didn't leave the best tracks. I half joked that It might be a mountain lion, sending my wife (who has a mountain lion phobia) into a heightened state of nervousness. :)

Slightly amused, I trekked forward, but made sure to make a bit more noise and keep my daughter close. Next I spotted the scat, but being much better at tracks than scat, I reassured my now noticeably concerned wife that it was probably just a dog returning back down the path (oh yeah, we weren't on a path) from a hike. I told her we would just follow the trail up and around the bend, and then we would head back (after all, whatever left the tracks was already behind us right?) Well, true to famous last words . . . around the bend we come upon a large muddy ravine with a recognizable set of  "M" shaped, no claw tracks that came to the side of the ravine, lept up onto the bank and extended its claws to grip the ground as it pulled itself up. The print was dry, but it was still wet inside the claw marks!!! We headed back down the hill loud and quick!

I have this theory that people who live with wild bears, play with rattlesnakes or perform any other act where they show a disregard for safety, or a lack of respect for nature, deserve what they get. You really can't mourn any loss of intelligence here! Add me to this list, because I then realized that I had a 5 year old (10 minutes up a remote hill) in some fairly dense hard chaparral with a big cat probably between us and our car at the park grounds. It then hit me that if I had really thought that the tracks belonged to a big dog coming back from a hike, where were the shoe prints from his companion. (OK, I became flushed with stupidity!) Well, we made it back safely but upon returning to the park what should I see on the bridge crossing the creek? A mountain lion warning sign . . . really! So whether the cat was already gone or hiding a few feet away from us in the underbrush, we were left alone. 

Rest assured that the park has always been safe, and that there are plenty of rabbits and deer to keep a mountain lion away from people. Stay on the trails, enjoy the park and don't let this brief encounter dissuade you from experiencing one of the finest parks that Santa Barbara has to offer.

Hours are 8 am to sunset. The park boasts some pretty nice restrooms and the drive to get there includes phenomenal views of the coast and the mountains. I personally like taking E. Valley Road from Santa Barbara and Montecito to get to Toro Canyon Park. Lower your windows or drop the top and experience the beauty that makes this the most desirable (and expensive) place to live in America.

name: Carp Sports
categories: Campgrounds, Beaches, Active Life, Bikes, Tennis, Sporting Goods, Hotels & Travel, Fishing, Bike Repair/Maintenance, Shopping, Local Services

yelp_reviews.text: I got an adorable white beach cruiser, basket, helmet, and lock here. The man working was very friendly and helpful with all of my questions. He even helped my sister attach the lights she bought for her bike. Thanks again!

I walked into Carp Sports on Saturday of 4th of July weekend for the first time and was pleasantly surprised to be greeting as I walked in. I found that they have a large selection of various sporting good even though the store is on the smaller side. I was looking at the bikes and accessories they had available and the sales person was very helpful. He was knowledgeable on the products and willing to hold an item for me until later when I could return. I will be a repeat customer!

He was closing & I needed some swim stuff for the hotel pool, since CVS next door didn't have much of anything in stock yet, they suggested Carps. Great customer service from a welcoming local! Stocked with everything from bikes to beach!
The owner is super cool. Stopped to eat on a road trip and was freezing. Found this place and the guy grabbed a perfect for hoodie for me. Good prices too. Cool shop.

Great local sport store. They have a great selection of beach cruisers, perfect for this sleepy beach town. The owner, TV, is really nice and offers superior customer service. His prices are great and they have a lot of different sports supplies. They have skateboarding, tennis, baseball, football, volleyball and much more.
They had exactly what I needed.  Most importantly very helpful owner/employee for help and customer service.  They have a little of just about anything you need
Never disappointed, they always have what I'm looking for and the service is   the best!  They are knowledgeable, helpful, and most of all friendly!

Horrible service. We drove there all the way from SB through Sunday traffic to find the owner closed the shop 45 mins before closing time. We knocked on the door and in the most rude way he said I can let you in only if you are going to buy something. Not a friendly owner, will not be doing business with them in the future.

Carp Sports saved my Fourth of July plans! Hooray! They were open and ready for business when I discovered my beach cruiser had a bum tire-tube. I took it right over and the nice gentleman had it fixed within a half hour! There was hardly a glitch in my plans. He was so nice, explained my price point options really well, didn't try to sell the latest and greatest products, but wanted to make sure I was riding safe. You don't come across honest people like that very often. I've been a customer here before, but I'll be coming back here for all of my future tuneups for my cruiser. I was very impressed, my little dog even liked him too!

name: Channel Islands Outfitters
categories: Whale Watching Tours, Hotels & Travel, Special Education, Specialty Schools, Active Life, Tours, Rafting/Kayaking, Travel Services, Education

yelp_reviews.text: Best tour!  Tour guide was very knowledgable and service was great!  Thank Phil!  Food was really good and prepared by a chef.  Great service and very fun.  Caves were really cool to view and the water was warm and beautiful.  We saw whales and a pod of 100 dolphins on the way over.  Very magical experience.   The trip was fun and exciting from beginning to the end.

I recently booked the 1/2 day kayak tour at Santa Cruz Island for myself, my brother and our parents. We took the 8:00am ferry out of Ventura Harbor and arrived at Santa Cruz island a little over an hour later. We were immediately mezmerized by the sight of the gorgeous caves. Once we arrived, we decided to go for a hike. We found a great spot near a cliff overlooking the water and had a picnic. We felt like we were on a deserted island, it was so beautiful. Our kayak tour wasn't until 1:30 so we met our guides Dave and Mike and they went over everything we would be encountering in detail. Once we got on the water it was just amazing. As we paddled through the caves, we saw several adorable sea lions popping their heads up in the water near us. I group of them evern swam right up to us! It was such an amazing experience. Emerging into the light out of the dark caves was one of the most beautiful sights I had ever seen. We brought a waterproof camera to snap some shots and I'm so glad we did. This is highly recommended for anyone who would like to spend the day taking in nature and getting a fun workout. We took the 5:00pm ferry back to Ventura although there is a campground for anyone looking to stay overnight. On our ferry ride back we encountered a pod of approximately 700 dolphins that surrounded our boat. Unforgettable!!!

We had a wonderful experience.  From my first phone call to book our trip, to our guide helping us out of the kayak, the staff at Channel Island Outfitters is friendly, helpful and diligent.  They made sure that my our food would be acceptable to us after I had said on the form "no onions" for my husband, (including calling and discussing options with him ~ he does not get that good of service at many restaurants!).  Marina, our guide on the island, was super friendly, knowledgable and helpful.  We were completely inexperienced kayakers, and she took extra good care of us.  We did flip over, but she helped us get right back in the kayak, and she kept checking in on us to make sure we were okay and having a good time.  Highly recommended!

This was a wonderful experience. Our guide, Zach, was really knowledgeable about the wildlife and nature and tried to get us to go as far as we could, through as many caves as possible, despite the increasing waves and wind. Great workout and superb post-kayak lunch! Thank you so much!

Our first time out to the channel islands and this group could not have done more to make it a more perfect experience. Starting from planning and booking by phone, to helping us figure out lunch, to getting us back to the ferry on time they helped us by phone with every detail. Once we arrived the check in was easy. We did the snorkeling and cave kayaking packages. Both were spectacular. Our guide JD was patient and knowledgeable and fun to spend the day with. He was both a skilled teacher and naturalist making us feel safe and answering our questions about the islands wildlife. We purchased the packaged meals and they were delicious. Equipment was in great condition, masks were clean, kayaks had nice supportive backs. Most importantly the snorkeling was breath taking and the cave kayaking certainly makes our list of top 10 lifetime adventures. Island Outfitters made it all easy and fun, a great price for the experience.

They were great in every way.  Reservation seamless, Phil the guide was knowledgable and accommodating, boat was on time and made an effort to be engaging with information and service oriented, meal was good.  I love the fact that they are a B Corp which is difficult to achieve and demonstrates a higher level of commitment to environmental and socially responsible policies / practices.  My ONLY caveat is that we paid for a private tour expecting that it would be better / different than the standard one and that was not the case.  Yes we were a small group and got extra attention but not worth the extra money.

These guys are the best. I've now done two caves and coves tours out of Scorpion Harbor in Channel Islands N.P., and both trips have been an excellent combination of fun and knowledge. If you go to Channel Islands, your trip is not complete until you've done a trip with Channel Island Outfitters. I used to be a kayak guide myself, and I can definitely say CIO is one of the best operations I've experienced anywhere.

Channel Island Outfitters is a great company. They really strive to make your experience on the island fun and worry-free. Their guides seem to be very smart and quite knowledgable about all aspects of natural life. I did the snorkeling and kayaking tour and had a spectacular time. The only complaints I have are two. First, they needed to be a bit more organized in getting the right gear available and getting the orientation down and getting out in the water. They had all of our sizes in advance and we were a small group so we should not have been waiting for long periods of time in the hot sun, while our guide helped sort through mounds of gear  to find what we needed. Second, there needed to be a place in the lunch/changing area that offered some shade. And there also needed to be a place where we could leave valuables without worrying if they get stolen. Other than that, they were great and the trip was just amazing.

The trip was amazing from beginning to end! I booked for 6 of us, the online check-in was simple and straightforward. We upgraded our lunches and they were substantial! No extra food needed, plus they were delicious. All six of us were happy with our meals. We were treated by a dolphin show from the boat! My friends were nervous about the kayaking but the guides were wonderful. What a bonding experience it was! We always felt safe and well taken care of. I would highly recommend this adventure to everyone!

My wife and I bid on the trip to Santa Cruz at a charity auction.  I had flown over the islands but never been on them.  Now they are a national park, but it was once a privately owned sheep ranch.  The island is rather desolate and primitive, the sheep having eaten most of the plants, but still interesting.  The waters around the island are the fun part.  The boat ride over was in rough seas.  The Outfitters knew tha sea was rough that day and offered everyone the option of a refund or a rescheduel, but nobody took them up on the idea.  Several did get sea sick, but recovered quickly for the most part.  It was a rough crossing, but fun.  We saw sea lions and one whale.  The sknokeling and the kayaking were well worth the trip.  The staff were young, energetic and helpful.  By the way, we flew up to Oxnard airport the night before and stayed in a "botel" in the harbor.  It was a small yacht and very comfortable.  It was a two minute walk from there to the Outfitters office in the morning.  Great way to do it.  It's an exciting and memorable trip!

If you are going to Santa Cruz Island, Channel Islands California....be sure to book an excursion with the Channel Island Outfitters company so that you can get to enjoy the amazing marine life, especially since this company has thought of everything!  It was a fabulous experience as our guide insured that we were safe and then provided wonderful information re: the history of the island as well as information about the ecosystem.

What an amazing trip!! We only had a few days to plan something and this made our trip to Santa Barbara so much fun!!! The guide (Dave ?)  was great kept us all entertained and lots of info about the islands! We took our 12 year old and 14 year old!! There were a few opening to the caves that made me a little nervous but coaxed by my daughter and our guide going through each one first it was all good!!!!! Thanks for a great time!! Now to see if we can get some of those great photos he took!!! Oh and we saw a baby bat ray... So cool ;)

Channel Islands has the worlds most helpful and awesome staff! I love renting their paddle boards and cruising around the harbor. Additionally, they have an awesome staff working out on the islands that lead super fun kayak tours. There's a guy named Blake who works in the store who is the nicest, most helpful young gent. If you need any help, he's your guy!

Very good experience. Really fun trip. Great guides who keep you safe while still being fun and adventurous. We did the extended caves trip. I'd recommend this trip if you are in reasonably good shape with basic kayak skills. This is a must do trip. Excellent value if you consider the cost of a snowboard trip or even what they charge a theme parks. Best if you can get a group together.

Trip of a lifetime!  I went with 4 girlfriends and we had the pleasure of having Ryan as our guide.  He knew everything about the area, ecology, geography, politics...entertained and educated our nerdy and socially-minded selves.  I was afraid it would be turbulent waters or too difficult, but seas were calm and Ryan was there to help us out of tight spaces. Lunch was great, just the simple one was more than any of us could finish. 

Although CIO does a really thorough job of almost everything, I think they need to really be stronger in their pre-trip messaging about the fact that you should literally plan to bring NOTHING but a power bar and 100% waterproof camera on the kayak. Falling out is REALLY common. They have everything you need, even a long sleeve special top, if you want one. There are bins to leave clothes and things on the island, but I felt uncomfortable leaving my cell phone there unlocked. It was fine, but it would be easy for someone to take things. 

In short, book your trip now!

We used Channel Island Outfitters for a friend's bachelorette party. We did the trip to Santa Cruz Island with sea kayaking. It was awesome! We had a great guide for the 7 of us. We got to go kayaking in sea caves and I think it was a new experience for all of us -- I would definitely recommend it! We had enough time after kayaking to eat lunch and then explore the island and go on a short hike. The only downside was that the ferrying of people on and off the boat took a looooong time (I think an hour or more). I think this is because the pier on the island got washed out in a storm, so they have to ferry about 6 people at a time off the big boat to the island shore. Even with this delay, though, we had a great time and it was an amazing trip.

My wife and I took a paddle boarding lesson with Frazer and it was such a great time. He was the coolest guy and really made the 2 hour trek a great one as he dropped all kind of local and marine-related info on us. Hands down - try these guys out!

Bottom Line:

We had a good time and our guide was great but there were some logistical problems that kept the overall experience from being great. 

Full Story:

My bf planned a surprise Valentine's Day trip to the Channel Islands, complete with lunch and a guided "caves and coves" kayak adventure.  We arrived by ferry at around 11 am and had about an hour to explore the island before meeting up with Channel Islands Outfitters at 12:30 pm for lunch and our kayaking excursion.

We didn't want to be late for the main attraction, so we did a very quick hike and arrived to the kayak area at around 12:15 pm.  We were starving and excited to eat and get going on our adventure.  Marina had left us a note to let us know that they'd be back from snorkeling at 12:30 pm, so we were sure that we were in the right spot.  We knew that the 12:30 pm time was an estimate, and expected to wait 15 - 30 minutes, no problem.  But thirty minutes went by, then 45, then over an hour.  Finally at around 1:40 pm, Marina showed up with a group of 4 people.  She was apologetic for being late, and it is my feeling that it was not her fault, but the fault of the slow moving group that had gone snorkeling.  They proceeded to very slowly get out of their wetsuits, go to the bathroom, and eat their lunch (read: no sense or urgency).  It was past 2 pm by the time that we started talking about kayaking and getting into our wetsuits.  It was at this point that the other couple informed us that they were on the 3:30 pm ferry back.. leaving us less than an hour for our kayak trip.  Marina did everything that she could to remedy the situation, and got the other couple onto the 4 pm ferry with us, but in the end, we only got about an hour on the kayaks. What made the whole thing worse is that we were just sitting around waiting when we could have been exploring the island.

The lunch, when we finally got it, was pretty good - the chicken sandwich was huge and while not the best I've ever had, pretty damn good for a picnic sandwich. I'd definitely recommend getting the Cuevo Chicken sandwich if you go on a tour with this group.

All said and done, the hour that we did get on the kayak was great. Marina was a fun, knowledgeable guide and did her best to make the experience great for us.  We only got to go inside a few caves, and not very far inside of them, but that was largely due to the large waves which is not controllable. She took a couple pictures of us in the kayak, which we were able to get on the website a few days after our trip. We were slowed down a bit by the other couple that seemed to be kayaking tandem for the first time ever, but again, that was not Marina's fault.  We've gone kayaking before, and the equipment - from the kayak to the booties to the helmet to the wetsuit - were of the highest quality that we've experienced.
 
All in all, I feel like we had a subpar experience driven not by the fault of Channel Island Outfitters, but by the other people on the excursion.  We still managed to have a good time, despite feeling a bit screwed over by the entire situation, especially considering that this was our Valentine's Day and it wasn't exactly cheap (note that we did get a discounted price of $100 off the regular price of $140 since we were only joining in the afternoon part of the excursion, but at $200 for two, it's still not cheap).  

TIP: While no money is exchanged on the island and it is recommended to reserve in advance, Marina told us that if there is space and availability once you are on the island, you do have the option of leaving a credit card number and joining a tour.

We spent the day with this amazing group on the beautiful Santa Cruz island. The whole day was breath taking. JD was patient, fun and informative! Everyone was professional from the harbor all the way back. These caves truly are breath taking and if you havent seen them please take time to do so...it's a time u wont forget. If you do please do it with these guys, they'll make it 100x better. Thanks again guys!

I had CIO host our Annual Staff Retreat with a day of kayaking and hiking at Santa Cruz Island.  I have to say this was the most amazing experiences for all of us involved!  We are still reeling from the day's adventures and the beauty of the island and the ocean trip out to the island.  Grey whales and hundreds of dolphins!  Garrett, Kaia and Matt were incredibly organized and we felt completely safe in their expert hands.  They were so accommodating, down to earth and informative about the history, flora and fauna of the island in all it's glory.  We all felt like we were on another country far, far away but yet so close!  And, I highly suggest the food upgrade as it was delicious and all homemade.  Class act and a memory we will all take with us for a lifetime!  Thank you!!!

Rented a paddle board from these folks on recommendation from A-Frame Surf down in Carpinteria. I will definitely come back again, good pricing and friendly staff! I loved that they have a variety of boards to chose from also, not just standard beginner logs. They've got lockers to stow belongings too, so you don't have to tote everything out or leave valuables in the car (YIKES!), I was also fond of the belt style PFD they provided. See you again on my next trip!

As a local, Ive grown up around water sports and, until now, owned all my own gear.  This year I bought a membership at the shop by the harbor - I wanted to try stand up paddle boarding.  The idea of hauling around the over-sized board and storing it would have been a deterrent for me, not to mention the $$ to buy it.

The membership was everything I had hoped for.  The people there are friendly and helpful, and they have anything you could possibly need for an afternoon of fun!  Rash guards, wet suits, timers, lockers, change rooms, life vests, etc.

The equipment is right on the dock, and if you need they will help you get it in the water. 

This membership was an awesome idea!

We had a great time exploring the caves and snorkeling with our guide Chuck. Chuck was a cool guy and so were the rest of the crew including everyone I talked with over the phone while making our reservation.

This was super awesome! Our guide Ryan gave us an amazing experience of the sea caves and put up with all of our bachelorette party shenanigans! Super fun. Can't wait to do it again sometime. Oh and lunch was delicious!

Amazing. Took my girlfriend here for a day trip and it was one of the best outdoor trips we've ever been on. The guides were very knowledgeable and helpful and challenged us on the kayaks (optional challenges). You can go tandem or single kayak and the kayaks were built for the sea and do not tip easily (no one in our group tipped). I had some experience myself and by going through the sea caves we learned different kayaking techniques that helped me be a better kayaker and that were really fun! The sea caves were amazing and the guides kept them safe by going in first and letting us know if the waves were too big for us to follow. They aren't crazy risky outdoorsmen (been on some scary trips with these people before) and are focused on our safety which helped make the trip more enjoyable. We also kayaked alongside seals, found hidden caves with beached whales, saw many starfish, and learned about all the marine life.

The ferry trip was also incredible. We saw grey whales migrating and "megapods" of dolphins -- literally a thousand dolphins. It was absurd and like a dream. The ferry driver was great too as he willingly took detours to show us the marine life instead of just carting us from Point A to Point B as fast as possible. Much appreciated!

Thanks to Channel Island Outfitters for an amazing vacation! My highest recommendation!

Years of dedicated experience from our guide, JD, made me feel safe and well taken care of so I could relax and truly enjoy the full sensory experience of kayaking around this world treasure of an island. I learned a lot about kayaking, the history of the island and it's inhabitants. I left with a deeper respect for the area. Would do it again in a heartbeat.

Excellent experience!  I have done this twice with out of town guests and it had been a bucket list item for them both.  On our most recent trip our guide, Ryan was really knowledgable about the environment and Eco system of the islands and also really fun!  The trip was so much fun and quite adventurous!  Ryan was also obviously very experienced and an EMT so we felt very safe as well.  The food was amazing and the experience is really organized and well run, with high quality guides and staff.  The only complaint was the organization for the ferry boarding but that is a separate company so I give five stars to this Channel Island outfitters.  We had the time of our lives and want to go back again soon!

Had an amazing time on this kayaking trip. I'm sure your experience depends on not only your own mood/energy and the guide that happens to be leading but we went super excited for the caves and had a really incredible guide. We were lucky to have a small group, gorgeous clear water for snorkeling and a guide who was very knowledgeable about everything around us. I won't ever forget this kayaking experience. Was great to have lunch included oh and the wetsuit was great - I felt like i was part seal part invincible human it made the whole experience better as someone super afraid of falling out of kayaks - I really felt well taken care of. I would recommend these folks for sure. Thank you for the unforgettable weekend! Don't forget to pack your sunscreen so you can reapply!

Best experience off our coast. Our girls (10 & 14 years old) had an awesome time with Channel Island Outfitters. We never kayaked before, but our guide was the best ( i can't remember his name).
Thank you Channel Island Outfitters

So I booked this trip on the fly for something to take up time and do for the day. I thought it was just going to be another fun day out on the water in a kayak and paddling around on the water. J.D. our guide made sure that it was anything but that! It was amazing and if you have the chance to do it, DO IT! Everything from the scenery to kayaking with seals to kayaking through the caves was breath taking. The whole experience is (excuse my language) f****ng awesome, it was that good! Ive kayaking in places before and hiked many scenic hikes but this is truly a one of a kind experience that you can't compare to anything. C.I.O. does everything to make your experience as simple as showing up, taking a ferry over to the islands while watching the whales go by, getting off the boat, and having a great time. Thats simple all you do. They even offered a meal service which was in included in the price! Neal makes all healthy organic locally grown food. I'm not, by far, a super health freak but the food was amazing! You could bring your own though if you so choose to do so, but why would you with such amazing food. I could go on forever on how amazing our experience was with C.I.O. and there caves and kayak tour but you just have to see for yourself to believe me. If your reading this you must be wondering, meh should I or shouldn't it? Is this company better than that company? If thats the case theres not a doubt in my mind C.I.O. is top notch and offers the best service around. So, drop everything your doing and book now, cause if you don't you'll be sorry you didn't listen....I promise that!! If I could give this review 10 stars I would unfortunately I can only give 5.
Amazing trip to the caves on Kayaks! The tour guides were friendly and helpful. Beautiful place

A bit expensive to rent paddle boards. Had to wait in line in the cramped shop and fill out a lot of paperwork. Employees seemed disorganized. The day was really windy so we didn't even get to do much paddle boarding and couldn't get a refund.

Dave and chuck were the best kayak guides ever. Awesome time and they made me feel safe with the waves and caves! Highly recommend. It was the best kayaking experience ever and totally worth it to do this on Channel Islands.
Such an amazing adventure! Guides are amazing. Each cave was like a new log ride experience.

Did the 3-hour kayaking tour with Ryan on Santa Cruz Island. Amazing experience, highly recommend. He was very knowledgable and showed us how to ride waves with our kayak, go in and out of tunnels, and much more

Spent a Sunday out kayaking in and around the Painted Cave (Santa Cruz Island) with favorite guide, Craig. This is the second trip we've done with him - and he is.....the best guide ever! The day was fabulous all around - from the boat ride out to the island, the kayaking and going in these incredible caves. It really changes your perspective on nature. Looking forward to saving up for the next great adventure - either multi-island trip, or camping. CIO is a class act company - and definitely wants to provide the most enjoyable, comfortable adventure they can. I highly recommend their services - and ask for Craig - unless he's already spoken for! ;) Thanks CIO!!!

these guys rule. i took a trip with them to santa cruz island and we kayaked the caves. amazing people, great guides, and tons of fun. worth every cent. thank you garrett, mike, fraser, and the rest of the crew!

I went here with a bachelorette group and had a blast! Byron, one of our guides, was very informative, helpful, and engaging person! Overall, the staff was extremely professional and made the experience super fun. They met us at the docking point and guided us through the whole experience - even when we made a last minute change of plans. The equipment and food that they provided really made the whole experience worry free. Definitely recommend everyone give this adventure a try - you get to learn a lot and have loads of fun!

This kayaking trip was the highlight of my summer. I would recommend it to (almost) anyone visiting southern California. Our tour guide was a kind-hearted, funny guy. My fiance and I had only been kayaking once before, and it was challenging to paddle against the wind for 20-30 minutes straight, but if you're in average shape, you can handle it. Because of the intense paddling at times, I wouldn't take small children on it(but that's just me). It's a beautiful hands-on experience of the California coast and the tour guides/ferry captains were very knowledgeable. My one tiny complaint was that I had trouble finding an address on the email confirmation(as viewed on a mobile phone), and when I called the night before/morning of for directions, I had trouble reaching a person. But we did find it and had a great time.

My mom and I went on the Coves and Caves tour. We had so much fun. Very professional and kind people helped us throughout from booking the trip to letting us borrow snorkeling gear. Our tour guide, Marina, was knowledgeable and patient with us as we maneuvered our kayaks, often places they shouldn't have gone. ;) Seriously great company.

We just got back from a weekend getaway to Santa Cruz Island to Scorpion Anchorage. We went on a 3 hour long kayaking tour with Channel Island Outfitters. We were VERY pleased with our guide- Dave. He was very thorough, and his passion for the islands and what he does really shined through. We saw about 10 caves total, which were absolutely amazing. I felt very comfortable with Dave, and really trusted that we knows what he is doing. He would check out the caves first to make sure everything was ok, and then we would follow him in afterward. The water here was a beautiful aqua color, and being inside of the caves is quite a site to see! I highly recommend this company for a tour!
We had a great time.  Our guide was knowledgable, observant, patient and very focused on fun and safety.  Thank you.

Six stars if I could. A group of 4 of us wanted to do our first Channel Islands kayaking/hiking adventure, and we're so glad we found C.I.O. The boat from Ventura to Santa Cruz Island was perfectly easy (even got to see some cool wildlife). 
We signed up for the Caves and Coves kayaking tour, and that's exactly what we got. Luckily it was a great day to be on the water, because our AMAZING guides Mike and Tom were able to take us to all of their favorite tour spots, including some fun "optional" challenges...which our whole group felt super safe taking advantage of! 
These guys knew what they were talking about every step of the way, and made the whole group feel super comfortable. There were a few points at which anyone who wished to do so could've stopped and turned back to base camp, but everyone (in our mini group, at least) stayed for the duration. 
Mike and Tom (in particular, because they were our specific guides, but everyone involved seemed top-notch) were really helpful and friendly and made our first trip to the Islands a great one. 
Thanks guys, we'll be back!!

Kayaking through the sea caves at Santa Cruz Island was an incredible experience! We had sea lions and seals swimming around the kayaks and crystal clear water. Guides estimated visibility at 40ft. The team was also really helpful. Props to Ben and Marina who created a fun, safe, and comfortable atmosphere for the whole group.

My family was vacationing in Santa Barbara for a week. We heard about kayak tours around the Channel Islands. We chose CIO based on Trip Advisor. We called and were assisted by Clay. He was friendly, informative and worked hard to arrange everything that my family of 6 needed. He gave us a group discount and helped prepare us for our adventure.

We arrived at 7:15 am in Ventura Harbor for our day trip to Santa Cruz Island.
We were able to bypass all the Ferry tours and go straight to our CIO rep who was waiting out front with our boarding passes.

After a fun 1 hour Ferry ride to the island, we were greeted by Matt, our tour guide. He really made our time Kayaking fun. He was polite, friendly, informative, adventitious and encouraging. He lead us on our Kayaks for 2.5 hours. During that time he took pictures and told stories of the island. After our tour, he set us up for snorkeling. He was very helpful. Matt made our day incredible.

We can't wait to use CIO the next time we want to visit the Channel Islands. Their customer service was top-notch and their equipment current. It was the highlight of our vacation.

I took my girlfriend on the Caves and Coves Tour for her birthday, and it was a lot of fun. The kayak guides were super nice. We had Ryan, who was very informative about the islands in general, and tried to show us as much as possible on the water despite the increasing wind and huge waves. The lunch that was included was quite good, and far more substantial than I expected for an included meal. 

Overall, the experience was quite pleasant and I would recommend CIO to anyone interested in kayaking the islands.

This company provided the best service I could receive. They are so no nice and knowledgeable about their business. I and my family had so much fun at the Channel Island with them. I totally recommend Channel Island Outfitters.

As a frequent visitor to Santa Barbara, one of the first things I normally like to do is go stand-up paddle boarding. Fraser and his lovely staff at CIO are always friendly, polite, efficient  and very helpful wether we are renting equipment or just looking for general information. I highly recommend these guys for all of your rental or surf shopping needs.

Still thinking about this review, will update later.  
Pros: Good equipment, small group.
Cons: Wasn't told I would be have to carry my own kyak, add says 8 years plus, I can't see an 8 year old transporting a 150 pound kyak. Guide either unwilling or unable to assist.
We had such an amazing time!!!Garrett,Kaia and Matt were awesome!! This was very educational and invigorating, I highly recommend this as a great staff treat!!

I grew up in Santa Barbara and didn't visit the Channel Islands until after I'd lived away from home for 7 years. This was the best way to do it... My 6 family members and I took the 9am ferry to Santa Cruz and met the Outfitters guides. Our guide was Ben, he was THE BEST! We took kayaks through the caves, went snorkeling and hiking. Ben was fun and flexible with our desires for the trip. It was such a blast and I highly recommend the trip!

We recently booked Channel Islands Outfitters for a sea cave kayaking excursion around Santa Cruz Island.  While I was completely delighted with what mother nature provided - stunning close ups of marine sea life, the swaying beauty of kelp beds and rugged allure of the hidden alcoves through which we paddled - I've really come here to review CIO.

Our guide Phillip, was awesome. Engaging, capable and confident, we felt completely secure out on the water with him.  He seemed passionate about what he does for a living, which makes a big difference. I've been on tours where the guides have given a half hearted performance, which consequently makes for a lackluster experience.  We felt grateful to have such a professional to guide us, so thank you, Phillip for doing what you do!

Our entire experience with CIO, from the onset was a cinch.  We wouldn't hesitate to contact them if we're in the area again.

Went on the Caves & Coves tour with my wife and we had a great time.  Ryan Boucher was our guide & we found him to be excellent.  He very knowledgeable about Santa Cruz Island, its geology, bird and sea life, the kelp forest, and of course, safely leading a group of inexperienced kayakers into potentially dangerous caves.  He was great at teaching us how to control the kayaks in unfamiliar terrain aka in caves, and coaching us through the experience in a way that instilled both confidence and fun!  I have done numerous action adventures, from scuba, snorkeling, zip lining, rappelling, ropes courses, etc. and found Ryan to be amongst the best guides that I have ever had.  Am a local and I would definitely go with CIO again in the future.

My first visit to the unspoiled and sumptuous Channel Islands National Park was incredibly enhanced by my excursion with Channel Islands Outfitters. My girlfriend and I camped for 2 days at Santa Cruz Scorpion Ranch. We found every guide on the island to be friendly and accessible. When we needed to adjust the day and type of tour we wanted to take, they were accommodating in a way that made us feel like they were more interested in offering us the experience we most wanted versus trying to up-sale us. They were even complimentary of the other two companies that offer guided tour excursions and encouraged us to compare. What set Outfitters apart is they have the best equipment. And our guide, JD, was very patient, fun and knowledgable about the unique ecosystem of the island and surrounding waters. Some may offer slightly better pricing, but in this case, you definitely get what you're paying for, including a yummy lunch.

Go with CIO, you get what you pay for. This is a top-notch outfitter. The guides, food, and service were outstanding. Great adventure and the islands were beautiful! Just wish I would have explored the underwater environment. If you want top quality, go with CI Outfitters.

They're great! I did a kayak day trip from camping and it was a nice, quiet experience.  The tour went through many sea caves and the guide was great!  He's no longer with CIO (we were his second to last trip) but I get a feeling that this company is about a good experience as well as taking care of the park.

Had an incredible time on the CIO Caves and Coves Kayak Adventure. Our first time kayaking could not have been better. The guides are super friendly and knowledgeable, the gear is clean and high quality, the food is good and Santa Cruz Island is breathtaking. After our kayak tour, we still had enough time to hike up to Cavern Point. An unbelievable experience that I will definitely recommend to friends and family.

We took our 14 year old grandson on the Caves & Kayaks tour and it was a fantastic experience for us to share with him. He absolutely loved it! Phil was our guide for the kayaking. He was helpful, entertaining and full of information about the history of the islands and the interesting things we were seeing -- much of which we might have missed without his help. The morning flew by as he shepherded our group in and out of caves. The lunch provided was really delicious and we had a great time exploring the island with the time we had left before the afternoon ferry arrived to take us back. Don't miss the short walk to the campgrounds to see all the island foxes that hang out there. They are fascinating! On the ferry ride home, we were thrilled to see several humpback whales and hundreds of bottlenose dolphins. The captain slowed the boat to a crawl for viewing and the naturalist on board pointed them out while providing interesting tidbits of information. This is not a day to be missed. We did it once before several years ago and enjoyed it even more this time. We're already talking to friends about a group of us doing a trip.

Kayaking at Channel Islands was incredible. They have single and tandem kayaks and the water was pretty calm and easy to kayak in.You kayak for a few hours and you do not need much experience with kayaking to be able to do the trip. We had a small group that had 4 kayaks and it was a broad range of people. We had a single guy, a mother and daughter and two couple friends.
It was so awesome to be able to go in so many caves and coves. There was a lot of sea life we were able to see also. Every guide on the island and ferry ride to the island were extremely helpful, friendly and knowledgeable. Our guide Marina made sure to get lots of photos of the group and shared them.The lunch they provided was delicious, I recommend the Italian sandwich or the veggie wrap.
Visited September 2015

http://www.tripadvisor.com/ShowUserReviews-g33045-d1847167-r315598598-Channel_Islands_Outfitters-Santa_Barbara_California.html#

We had a wonderful experience kayaking around the Santa Cruz Island. Johnny, Matt, and Garrett were great tour guides and made it one of our most memorable experiences. We had a great time and it is definitely worth every penny.

Review Update:

I'm updating my review to 4 stars, because the business owner seems to really take great pride in his business and keeping his customers happy at all costs.  While we didn't have the best experience (but don't get me wrong - still had a good time), I feel that it was not the norm, and he is going out of his way to make up for it.  He honestly wants all his customers to be happy, and takes it personally when they are not. Satisfaction guaranteed? That definitely makes me a fan!

I was also made aware that joining the trip halfway through the day is not a standard offering but a special arrangement that they made to accommodate us, and at the last minute.  It was a learning experience for them, and unfortunately, we were the guinea pigs.  My advice is to stick with the regular tours, and I'm sure that you will have an amazing time!

Very fun! The channel island outfitters made it really easy to enjoy the day. We checked in with than on the mainland and they gave us for ticket. When we landed on the island, they provided all the gear plus a quick information sesh on kayaking. The kayaking trip was AMAZING. It included several trips into huge caves and was challenging enough to be fun. Snorkeling gear and lunch were provided after with about 30-60 mins available to hike. Great day.

This is absolutely amazing!  Only an hour north of LA and you feel like you are in a completely different world.  You get to see some really beautiful things.  I signed up for the Caves and Kelp Combo.  I could snorkel all day long.  It is so peaceful and fun.  While I proved myself to be a weak player in the kayaking department, I still had a great time going in and out of the caves.  This is definitely a must do, especially if you are looking for a day trip in LA.
This company is great and the Channel Islands are a must see. Had a great time to say the least!!!!

This was a fabulous experience! My husband and I did the Caves and Coves Classic and had a wonderful time. I am not a skilled kayaker and had only kayaked a few times on a lake. I was grateful it was a calm day and felt safe the entire time. There was just the right amount of challenge and adrenaline to leave me completely satisfied. My husband and I did the double kayak. I'm 5'6" and he is 6'7" and we fit just fine. The whole experience was very well organized. They split us up into groups and we were able to be with our entire party which was great. Ben was our tour guide and he was FABULOUS!!!!! I can't say this loud enough. His two best qualities were his low key-demeanor and calmness. We had a few people fall out unexpectedly (including my mom) and he walked her through getting out of the cave without any worry in his voice. He made her feel safe the whole time. Ben is also incredibly knowledgeable about the eco-system of the island. He was a great teacher without overloading you with too much information. I will definitely look into to doing this trip again with some more friends and family. 

*One thing that I wasn't completely satisfied with was the provided lunch. I think they use a vendor for this and it was less than desired. I had the chicken sandwich which had now flavor at all.

Ever since I saw the Sunset Magazine article about the Channel Islands and kayaking and snorkeling, I have wanted to be there. I got this privilege this April when we decided to stay local for this Spring Break. It was everything and more! First we got catered meals which enabled us to dine on Halibut seared with veggies while at our campsite off the grid. No X Box to distract my 12 yo. Just a curious raven who can open zippers and a few island foxes the size of my housecoat. The guides were great! JD the first day and Chuck the second. We were lucky to be among guides who write and edit world famous surf magazines, not an easy thing! We surfed caves and kayaked hard. The scenery was outstanding and the birds awesome. We were warm in our perfectly fitted wetsuits. I never knew the Pacific had such color! This is the only place on the planet where South American birds winter alongside North pole birds from the Bering Sea. A convergence for everyone.

My mom and I had a great time with CIO. We did the sea cave kayaking with option to snorkel afterwards. Our guide was very nice and encouraging and the kayaking was really beautiful and fun. I was the only one who chose to snorkel afterwards and I had to go back twice when there were issues with the equipment. I also think there were a couple of times when the guides could have been more clear about what was going on before and after we were out on the water, but the bulk of the experience was really great.

Amazing Day trip! Even the trip on the ferry to the Islands was incredible. (Saw 6 whales and hundreds of dolphins). The sea caves were super cool to explore and the guides Marina & Zach were very knowledgeable. We loved the day!

Spent my birthday recently on a day trip to Channel Islands with Channel Islands Outfitters (CIO) and what a day it was! We arrived at the marina in Ventura on schedule and promptly checked in. While waiting for the ferry we were approached by one of the guides, Mike (how did he know we were with CIO?), who introduced himself and got to know us in a very genuine way. From that moment I knew it was going to be a great trip. 

We chatted again with Mike when we were on the ferry. After a rough ferry ride - a ride I thought was fun but many people got seasick on - we arrived at Channel Islands. The view was spectacular. We quickly made our way to the CIO area which Mike had told us about. Once there the lead guide, Chuck, introduced himself. Soon after we met Graham, the junior guide. Chuck led the orientation and he and Graham helped get all the clients into their gear. We carried the kayaks to the harbor and soon we were off. 

The tour itself (the Kayak and Caves tour) was so incredible. It was the perfect mix of easy and difficult paddling, historical and educational stops along the way (on water, not land), and several opportunities to pass through caves, waves, a blow hole, kelp forest and other sites. Some of the sites were challenging and intimidating but the guides never put pressure on the clients. We were free to explore, challenge ourselves or just hang back and watch the others. At one of the caves Chuck entered it first to see if it was safe enough for us. It wasn't so Chuck made what might have been a tough call to keep us safe. 

Back on land we removed the gear and settled into a delicious sandwich and snack. We had about 30 minutes to hike around the area before catching the ferry back to Ventura. 

My friend and I talked about what we saw and did the whole afternoon and evening. Then the next day, and the next day. I'm back at work nearly a week later now and still thinking about it. Holy cow!
 
It's very rare for me to experience something where I can't make at least one suggestion for improvement but with this adventure I'm stumped. Channel Islands Outfitters did everything right. From the friendly, knowledgeable and respectful guides to the tour organization, communication and logistics it was all simply awesome.

This place is starting to feel like my home away from home. The always friendly staff always provide just the right level of service and advice. 

I joined the CIO Co-op because I wanted to learn to SUP - it's such a great deal, beats owning and lugging your own equipment and its a much better way to get a workout than going to the gym!  Plus you get a tan, get to hang with seals and the dolphins  with fantastic views of the SB mountains.

Just about to take a trip out to the Islands to do the Caves & Coves kayak trip - can't wait!
I took my mom here on a pedal kayak trip and it was a memorable treat. Great location, great equipment, great service, and fair price. Do it!

My dad and I had a great time. Very fun and every level of kayaker can enjoy the cave tour. Marina was an awesome guide but all the other guides for CIO seemed amazing as well. Would totally recommend it, exceeded my expectations.

Had a fabulous time with CIO. We were out there celebrating my bro's 40th birthday and it was great to check out the Channel Islands. Everything was great from the get go: pre-boarding review, food, etc. JD was our guide and we had lots of fun with him, even as some of us fell into water as we tried new kayaking maneuvers.

Kayaking through the kelp forest was challenging at times and we spent a long time out on the water. By the end, we were all exhausted. The conditions for snorkeling were not ideal, so we skipped it and went on a 2-mile hike instead.

Since the trip was organized by a friend, perhaps they mentioned this, but it would be nice to have long sleeve wet suits or glove since the barnacles are super sharp. I didn't get cut, but a few others who fell out did. Overall, a great day and one to be remembered.

We had a great trip with our guide Chuck G. He was very knowledgeable about the island and the trip was well organized. A highlight was when a baby sea lion jumped on the back of one of our kayaks and stole a ride for at least a half hour. Overall, a great trip and we highly recommend Channel Island Outfitters. A well run outfit!

My family and I just returned from our day trip kayaking with Channel Islands Outfitters. We chose the sea caves option and were blown away with how much fun we had exploring the sea caves and tunnels of Santa Cruz Island. The guides were enthusiastic and informative and made us feel completely safe the entire time. The whole day was an absolute thrill and I've already been telling friends that they need to check out CIO. A big thanks to Emily and Chuck!

I had such a great time from beginning to the end! The process of booking online was very easy and clear. The boat ride to the island can be very adventurous and I recommend to bring extra warm or waterproof clothes. The kayaking guide I got was Dave, he was very attentive, try to respect each person in our group as well as other people enjoying the island. I also interacted some with Ben and another person (sorry I don't remember the name ) they were all very friendly, nice, knowledgeable and willing that the whole experience was just great. Thank you! I will definitely want to go back and try other type of tour.

We did a combination snorkeling and kayaking trip on Santa Cruz Island labor day weekend with Dave.  The group was small, only 8, he was attentive, informative and a great group leader. He emphasized safety. There were children in the other group of 5 and he was very watchful.  The kelp beds were amazing. I would recommend this combination trip without hesitation. The entire trip including the ferry trip was about 10 hours.  A great day.

We just got back from an awesome kayak tour and would recommend it to anyone.

We did the caves and coves tour, and there were a lot more caves than we had expected.  Going into caves was really exciting, and I'm glad we went on a guided tour. 

Our tour guide Matt was fantastic.  He seemed really excited to explore the different caves and tell us about the wildlife we saw on our trip ( Dolphins, urchins, starfish, garibaldi, pelicans and much more).  

I was glad that I had some kayaking experience to help navigate the caves, but I'm sure you'd be fine without it.  Matt was good at gauging our ability and made sure we were all comfortable exploring the caves.

The island is beautiful and I hope you have a great time!

I've always wanted to go Stand Up Paddle boarding.  So jumped at the chance to take a lesson in Santa Barbara.

Channel Islands Outfitters was awesome, they have all the equipment that you need all you have to do is register for a class and show up.

The instructor, Blake, was great.  He took the class through basics while we were still in the shop, giving us a mental picture of what we were going to be doing, how it would feel, and what to expect.  Then we headed straight out to the harbor.  Blake was friendly, easy going, and really made sure that everyone in the class was comfortable before moving forward.

Sincerely thought that I would have fallen in, and I wasn't the only one, others had the same fear, but no one got wet. 

After the 1hr lesson, the students were allowed to wander around the harbor on the boards and play.  We all took that opportunity.

I'll definitely be back on a board soon!

A wonderful experience. Matt took care of us from the beginning of our journey to the end. We never have been kaykaing before. We are an older couple. Matt is amazing. 

Arlene L

I took the snorkeling and kayak trip, and it was wonderful.  Guides were fun, personable and knowledgeable not only about snorkeling and kayaking, but the natural history of the Channel Islands and the flora and fauna.  Very well organized, and we didn't feel rushed (i.e. they made sure we were back for lunch and the ferry back to the mainland).  Having a full dive wetsuit for snorkeling was really nice (though I'd forgotten that putting on a dive wetsuit is an aerobic activity :-).  I have my own kayak, was a scuba diver many years ago, and have done a lot of snorkeling in the tropics, but I was really happy to have good guide for this area that is unfamiliar; high quality equipment as well.  Plus I learned a lot, and my fellow travelers were great fun.  Oh, and perhaps it sounds strange in a snorkel/kayak review, but the red bean/rice pudding I ordered for desert was delicious, and came wrapped in a banana (or ti?) leaf.  Do this adventure, with Channel Islands Outfitters, you won't regret it!

We took the caves and coves tour on Oct 16 as a group of 7, and had an amazing time from beginning to end. Channel Islands Outfitters were professional, informative, and friendly, and they really gave us a fantastic experience! We aren't super experienced kayakers (a few of us had been before, one had never been), but our guide, Dave, did a great job at making everyone feel comfortable and safe. It was nice to get the safety presentation at the beginning of the day, so we knew what to do in case a kayak tipped over. 

The lunches were also really good! All of the equipment we were provided with was clean and comfortable (wet suits, jackets, life jackets, helmets, and they also had booties for the folks in our group that didn't have water shoes). We only got to go into two caves due to the tide/swell conditions, but we still had a blast. The water was a bit choppy for snorkeling, so we had lunch when we finished and took the Cavern Loop trail for a quick 45 minute hike before it was time to go home. We took the 9am ferry out to the island, and the 5pm ferry home, and it was a good, full day. I loved finally getting to see the Channel Islands up close, and I will definitely be doing this tour again.

I have had several great experiences with Channel Islands Outfitters. They just keep getting better. The guide David is experienced, personable, knowledgeable and provides good instruction. Lunch was great!!! Equipment was good. Read up on the area at Santa Cruz Island and you will know what to expect on your trip. This is a great company, committed to making your experience a great one and earning your repeat business. I take at least one trip a year and bring my friends.

A wonderful experience all the way through. Dave and Byron took turns briefing us on what to expect next. I could tell they always had our safety foremost in mind, yet they were relaxed about it, not prison guards. Byron and Dave separately told us about the wildlife (I didn't know cormorants can dive to 100 feet!), the geology, and various anectdotes. I always felt like they were competent, experienced, and never lost sight that we were guests looking for a good adventure.

Added bonus: of the several choices for guided Ocean kayak tours, my wife chose CIO because they include lunch. It's nice to not have to worry about finding & packing food when you're a tourist. But it's extra nice when the food Vendor is clearly passionate about what he does. Neal (?) met us at the dock and told us about the quality of the food, he described each selection with light in his eyes. When I opened my Italian sandwich I was very pleased but not surprised to see it wrapped in a nice simple brown string bow, with a napkin neatly rolled up inside the wrapping, the sandwich dressed with a shredded coleslaw-like garnish, and the sandwich bread infused with quality olive oil. It really was one of the best Italian subs I've ever had, obviously made by someone who loves what they do.

Finally, these guys did a great job of communicating with us, sending multiple, very detailed clearly written email memos leading up to the voyage.

HIGHLY RECOMMENDED.

Great experience, the staff is very attentive and friendly. I highly recommend, I myself had never been kayaking and was a bit afraid going through caves in the dark, but the staff made sure to guide us. I will definitely be back

Where do we begin?

Started an all day guided kayak trip on Santa Cruz island with my wife, a father and son pair Jim and Dillon, and our guide JD. We started with a briefing at 8:30 am which JD kept straight forward but concise, calming us with his motto, "challenge by choice." Long story short, follow his lead or hang back if it looks out of your league.

No pressure, great knowledge of the island, and scouting anything that was questionable was a solid way to keep all of us confident and comfortable. From knowing the geology, marine life, history of the island, and quality snorkeling spots, JD was humble and confident.

At the end of a stellar day we forgot some jewelry that we kept safe with the guide. On our return trip a representative of Channel Island Outfitters was at the dock to return the jewelry by hand. 

If you're looking for an exceptional experience all around, JD and Channel Island Outfitters are as safe and knowledgeable as you'll find anywhere.

Many thanks to the whole staff at CI Outfitters, and most importantly JD,what a class act. Our friends, our family, and the two of us hope to be back for many more memorable adventures.

All the best
Randall and Cecilia Lovell

Let me start with the reason I'm rating CIO down 2 stars...it has nothing to do with the super friendly service!  Their rates are a bit steep given their competitors prices.  Channel Islands charges $15/ person/ hour, regardless of tandem or single kayaks.  So we paid for my friend's 6 year old daughter even though she was in a double kayak.
