Even with the 2-for-1 Axxess deal, it was $30/hour for 2 adults/ 1 child, and the SB Sailing Center charges by the Kayak, so even without a deal we would have saved $5 at the sailing center.  Channel Islands also doesn't offer dry bags for gear or phones.  I'm not sure if any other companies do, but given that they charge 50-100% more than competitors, I think it would be nice to offer these to borrow.  They do sell them however. 

The plus side...the staff is really friendly and easy going.  If there weren't a better deal around the corner, I would totally be back for the friendly faces!

We had an awesome experience kayaking around the Scorpion Beach at Santa Cruz island. All of our questions during the reservation process were patiently answered and our great guide provided a really adventurous yet safe experience. I guess we'll be back!

Channel Island Outfitters is top notch. Great people. Great gear. Great adventure. My 18 year old son and I had dreamed about this trip for six years. They delivered.  Guides were great, briefing was great, educational discussion was great.

Our day began with perfect weather. I must confess, I was a little nervous. I have grown up in Ventura, but never kayaked the islands. The instructions were very well explained by our guide JD and we hit the water. I was wow' in the first 5 minutes. He told us interesting wildlife facts and island history. You could tell he cared about these islands. I'm coming again with my whole family.

This was an amazing trip. I had never kayaked before so it was a real treat to have a guide take us (hubby also) through several types of caves.  Each cave was different and beautiful. We were blessed with a beautiful day, great food, and a fun and friendly guide. I can't wait to go again next summer.

My husband and I recently did the classic caves and coves tour and had a blast.  Our guide was kind, fun, and knowledgeable.  Kayaking through the caves was certainly a unique experience.  I appreciated that our guide made all the activities no pressure and adaptable to everyone's skill and comfort level.
If you do book a tour with Channel Island Outfitters I HIGHLY recommend you get a packed lunch from Channel Island Providers, a company they partner with.  For $25/person we got breakfast (Greek yogurt, honey, nuts, seeds, and dried fruit), lunch (roasted sweet potato, goat cheese, pesto, and red onion jam sandwich with an apple), and a snack (trail mix with crystallized ginger added especially to help with sea sickness on the ferry ride).  It was absolutely money well spent and kept us from having to worry about packing food while on vacation.

Since we knew we'd be visiting Santa Barbara we laid down money on a couple Groupons a while back to go stand-up paddling with Channel Islands Outfitters.  We bought 2 Groupons but 4 of us ended up going, so we weren't all on the cheap.  None of us had ever done this SUP thing but we didn't sign up for a lesson, just a rental.

The shop seemed well-equipped, although the SUP boards are stored in a locker a little ways away by the beach.  That would have been fine except there was one fewer boards in the locker than we needed so I had to go back to the shop and schlep one  a quarter mile down to the beach anyway (the beginner boards are extra long, wide and heavy!)

Aside from that little bit of disorganization (it was the end of a busy summer weekend) we had a very good time playing in the waves and being chased by dolphins for a couple hours.  The CIO folks were slightly overwhelmed but when they could get to us they were attentive, informative and enthusiastic.  Money.  Well.  Spent.
I had a great kayak tour in the caves at Scorpion Anchorage! They were attentive to those who were new to it and for those of us that wanted more, they did everything to accommodate.

Had my first experience with Channel Islands Outfitters (and the Channel Islands in general), and wanted to share some perspectives. First off, kudos to Clay in the office who was very helpful in helping my wife and I not only understand our options, but really "putting together" our two-day trip to Santa Cruz. Honestly, it was a relief to have someone walk us through what we needed to do for a fun mini-vacation.

I will focus on the 1.5 hour kayak excursion which was a mixed bag. The biggest challenge is that there was no distinction in this excursion between first-timers and those of us who had some prior kayaking experience. Also, it as quite crowded with close to 20 kayakers, matched up with 3 guides.  Because of the size, everything took longer and ate into our "fun time" -- the cliffs and caves along the Santa Cruz shoreline are truly worth exploring, but we seemed to spend more time waiting for the group to catch up and for us to be "rounded up" then actually exploring.

So why the 4 stars you might ask? Because I don't believe this was the norm.  We were there on a Saturday of 4th of July weekend -- lets face it, you're ASKING for crowds. A private conversation with one of our guides confirmed that if we had come just about any other day, the experience would be different. We could tell by their professionalism, expertise and quality gear, that this was a top-notch outfitter, and we can't wait to return.

The staff was awesome and so helpful! Experienced Kayaker or beginner this is the trip for you! They make you feel comfortable during the whole trip and make you feel like a pro. It was so fun going through the caves and checking out all the amazing sea life. They teach you about the Island and all the creatures of the island. If you are looking for a good adventure trip with some amazing people and lots of fun, look no further! 

P.S. make sure to tell them if you have any dietary restrictions because they make you a free organic amazing and delicious meal!

We booked a kayaking excursion with Channel Island Outfitters. First of all this National Park is a must see. The day could not have gone better. We meet our guide DJ and he got us prepared. The day was full of amazing sights and experiences including the cliffs, surf, sights, caves, lunch. We highly recommend the outfit. This was a phenomenal experience.

This was a fantastic experience. The guides were knowledgable and fun as well as safety minded.  They really wanted you to enjoy the experience as well as respecting the environment. Very easy to book and find them in the harbor.  The owner Garrett was very helpful in getting my reservation on the ferry changed.  IT WAS AWESOME

My wife and I explored Santa Cruz Island on Sunday, May 3rd with Channel Island Outfitters. We chose the "Caves & Coves Kelp Combo" tour and had a great time. Marina and Zach were awesome guides. While safety was obviously #1, Marina's enthusiasm and knowledge made the entire experience a lot of fun (and educational!). We had a relaxing, enjoyable day and highly recommend the experience. We hope to return - soon!

I have been wanting to take my 12-year old on a mother-son adventure for a while.  I picked Channel Island Outfitters because the reviews were great and I'd been wanting to check out Santa Cruz Island. I really didn't have a specific expectation, but the trip was AMAZING. I've never kayaked before, but our guide Matt -- who was AWESOME - made everything really easy and straightforward. We took the combo kayak/snorkeling tour and I am so glad we did. The water is no joke. It's super cold - but the gear provided worked great. It only takes a little while to get used to it. Seals were swimming around us. SEALS!!!! 

It helps to be in good physical condition, but the guides will accommodate for you. My 12-year old got tired and a little cold, so we just wrapped it up when we had to.

Here's what I recommend: 
Order the lunches. They are delicious.
Bring more snacks than you think you will need. You will be hungry.
Bring a blanket/change of clothes. The boat ride back to harbor is chilly.
Enjoy and have the best day ever!!!!

JD was a great, well-informed guide and Neal was helpful meeting us in the morning and getting our trip headed in the right direction. Santa Cruz island is lovely and amazing. The attention to detail with the food, safety equipment, training, and legit ourdoor adventure is enough to make even the most cynical city slicker want to return to repeat the experience.

Channel Islands has been on my must-visit list for a long time, but it took a little coordination to figure out how to get the island, what to do while I was there, and everything that I needed to bring to make it enjoyable. Luckily, I found Channel Islands Outfitters who did most of that work for me.

Upon booking the Santa Cruz Sea Caves kayaking tour, Clay from CIO sent me a very detailed itinerary with my ferry information, schedule and links that help you prepare for your trip (what to bring, expect, etc). I was thrilled at the organization of the itinerary, it really helped me to feel relaxed before my trip and not worry about missing anything.

The tour itself was also amazing. Our kayaking guide, Chuck was very knowledgeable about the islands and quite adventurous! I was surprised by how many birds and sea life he could identify within a few seconds. 

We tipped over our kayak once, but were rescued very quickly by Chuck. I never felt unsafe, because we were given instructions on what to do if that happened and it was fairly easy getting back in the boat.  If you go during a windy day, it will be a workout but the waves make it more thrilling. 

We had multiple sightings of sea lions, one even surfaced next to our kayak on the way back to shore! After you are finished, you are provided with snorkeling gear to enjoy the beach before the ferry takes you back. Since you also have a wet suit, the water is not that cold and rather enjoyable. 

The island itself, Santa Cruz, is gorgeous and feels completely remote (great for getting away from it all). Definitely worth visiting over Catalina Island. I have to add that the timing for a one-day trip is perfect. You get there around 9 or 10 am, kayak for 4 hours, and then have 2 hours left to snorkel and eat lunch. It didn't feel too short or too long, like other tours I have been on in the past.

CAVES & COVES CLASSIC: about 3 hours of fun kayaking with 5 kids ranging in age from 8 y/o to 14 y/o. All of us had an AMAZING time. So beautiful starting with the ferry trip from Ventura Harbor with a display of dolphins around us (what a nice treat!) on the way out and the 3 seals 'checking us out' while on the kayaks. Our guides (JD and Mara) was so knowledgeable and patient. Tempo was relaxed. Kids enjoyed the snorkeling afterwards and easy enjoyable hiking trip afterwards. This was truly a fun, enjoyable and worth-while trip and we will go back there in few years!

"I believe adventure travel should actually include adventure." This statement by our Channel Islands guide Dave Bleeker, gave us a prelude to a glorious day of kayaking the Santa Cruz coastline (Sea Caves of Coche Point). We were a motley group of mostly desert rats (including  women, children and senior citizens), some who'd never seen the ocean before, so it'd would've been an easy thing for Dave to play it safe and conservative with us. Instead he led us on a paddle that made it feel like he was holding nothing back, that we were taking risks and having a real adventure. Through it all--surging sea caves and 10 foot swells, Dave kept encouraging us that, even though it was getting pretty wild,  it was all "totally do-able." The tour was a splurge for us, but Dave made sure we got our money's worth and an experience we will remember forever.

My husband and I took our two kids kayaking for the first time with CIO. We were very happy with the whole experience. Clay was patient and helpful in booking our sea cave and kayaking around the Channel Islands. They were organized and well equipped. We felt safe and taken care of by our knowledgable and capable guides Mike and Matt.  HIghly recommend it as a family experience.

I had a great experience! I did the sea cave/coves kayak day-trip. I'd been kayaking before on lakes and rivers, but never in the sea. Even for a novice, you'll have a great time and see some amazing sights. Some of the caves we couldn't go into because of the tide/sea level, but the guide gives it to you straight. They also take time to talk about the native flora/fauna which is really interesting. Great, relaxing, exciting, and a unique experience! At the end of the paddle I had time to do a quick hike that the guide recommended. Our guide Chuck was awesome!!!! Two thumbs up!!!
Amazing adventure stop! Great service and paddle board accessibility!! Amazing student rates as well :)

We did the Marine Gardens at Prisoners day - and we loved it!  
Our guide, Chuck G., made sure we (all first time kayakers) understood what we were doing and that we were safe.  The scenery was wonderful and it was a terrific day!
The lunch and snacks delicious and more than enough food.

name: Varsity Bike Shop
categories: Bikes, Sporting Goods, Skate Shops, Shopping, Active Life, Surfing

yelp_reviews.text: The reviews don't lie! Steve is a very accommodating salesman. I told him upfront what I was looking for, but that I am also pretty clueless about bikes in general. He didn't make me feel stupid or like a menace to him; instead, he seemed interested in helping me find the perfect bike for me. He immediately picked out the correct size for me and let me pick which colored wheels wanted. When I didn't like any of the colors options he had in the store, he offered to order the color I wanted. Better yet, he had some gold wheels in storage and said he'd get them for me and have my bike ready the next morning. He threw in a brand-new extra long U-lock for only $8. When I went to pick up my bike, which turned out to be just a few hours after I stopped by instead of the next morning, he mounted the lock holder onto the frame and adjusted the seat and handlebars for me. I couldn't thank him enough for such a pleasurable experience. I highly recommend anyone to pay Steve and his shop a visit. It's a small place with a limited selection, but that doesn't mean you can't find what you want or have a great experience like I did. Also, the small scale of the shop made it a better experience than shopping at IV Bike Boutique. Both times I was in there, I was the only customer, thus Steve gave me all his attention and time, which again helped ease my mood since I was already feeling uncomfortably clueless from the moment I stepped in. Plus, I got the bike, the wheels I wanted, and the U-lock, all for less than the cost of a similar bike at IV Bike Boutique, which I had my eye on for half a week and went to purchase it only to learn they had sold it to someone else.

Came in the Sunday before classes started to get a new front rim/tire since it got stolen and to replace my back tire. I was helped out super quickly by Steve, the owner, who immediately gave me an estimate on how much I'd be spending, which I really appreciated. I left my bike with him for a few days since he didn't have the rims I wanted in stock yet. 

Came back on Wednesday and he hadn't gotten to my bike yet since they had just received the shipment earlier that day and he got to work on it right away. I only had to wait about 15-20 minutes for everything and this was with people coming in and out of the store with their bikes asking him things. Super quick and efficient. Definitely my go-to place for all my bike needs.

I'm also really liking the expanded store because there's more room to move around and it's not as cramped as it was before. 

Tip: He stores bikes during the summer and gives them a free tune up! They're stored in a storage unit too so no worries about it getting stolen or rusty! If I knew about this before I totally would've done this but sadly I'm only finding out about this during my last quarter at UCSB :(

Wish I could give this place 0 stars if I could. Went in today to pick up my bike that had a flat tire. Not only did they not know where my bike was with their unorganized system, they said I needed to pay three times the original quote despite me having told them not to charge anything extra without contacting me first. The owner started sighing and rolling his eyes when I asked for an explanation. Maybe they're friendly when they're selling bikes but definitely not when they're fixing them. I suggest going to A.S. Bike shop instead for maintenance. Wouldn't even have gone in the first place if A.S. Bike shop was open.

In 1983 I bought one of the first reasonably priced, commercially produced mountain bikes ("Black Diamond" brand) at this shop.  It's been to Japan with me and I still ride it around town daily.  I added a photo of it just for fun.

I was introducing my high school daughter to UCSB and IV last week.  I stopped in to see the owner and compliment him on the longevity of his merchandise.  He's still the nice guy he's been since the 60s.

Amazing customer service! We usually don't have that many bike issues, but every time we go Steve is always great and helpful and never charge for small things. About 2 month ago i went to put air and i forgot my helmet there. Thought i lost it and last week went for a question i had for some time, and then Steve asked me .. "hey, did you forget your helmet here a while ago?" I said "i lost my gray helmet and have no idea where" , then he showed it to me and it was that one, scratched on the left side and marked. "I thought it was you that left the helmet , you and your wife usually pass by here and it was worth keep it!" . I was blown away .. he could have toss it , or give it to somebody else, or anything you can imagine but NO, he kept it until i would go by there. Thank you Steve and thanks Varsity. I would recommend it to anyone! AMAZING!

Fantastic service. Steven was by far one of the best salesmen I've ever had the pleasure of meeting. I came in looking for a bike, with no idea what I was looking for and he helped me find one that I feel is perfect for me. He replaced my wheel colors and handlebar grips free of charge, gave me a $10 discount off of a $35 lock, and even threw in a bell for free. Very friendly staff, great guy, excellent service. Would recommend. I'm headed back in a day or two to have the second handbrake installed on my bike.

Update: was looking through my reviews, and wanted to add: sold the first bike I got here (no problems, I loved it, just wanted an upgrade), got a different one from here, and I love it too. Also, I don't come here too much, but every time I do, Steven always refers to me by name, which is really cool because there's literally thousands of students in IV so it feels really personal and it's awesome.

I went for new back tire tube (flat) and bike chain lube. Upon leaving, I realized I also needed my disk brakes tightened, so I went back. Steve did the first maintenance in a timely and inexpensive manner, and George (I think) handled the brakes (for free). They were very nice about everything, and did a great job!

A friend of mine recommended this place. This place is GREAT! The owner was super cool and helped me pick the right bike. I had a lot of questions and he was very knowledgeable and patient with me. Hands down..best service and bike shop in town!!

Fantastic service in this shop. Like many stories, I had also bought a beach cruiser from a random guy on the street and it came with many problems (it was $26 though, so I'm not complaining), and almost every time I brought it to Varsity, it was fixed for free. Oiled up, wheels pumped, screws replaced. He said it was a screw that needed to be recycled anyway. The only thing I paid for here ever was a bike lock, and I was totally willing to do so here, because of the fantastic service.

The best bike shop in Santa Barbara! I called all around to see about getting my bike worked on. Every place I called was anywhere from 55-90 dollars. I called Varsity bike shop in Isla Vista and he only charges $25! Steven the owner of varsity bike ship was SO kind. He did a great job on my bike and he was so helpful. I highly recommend this shop to anyone who needs anything for their bike including maintenance. Very affordable and great service!! Thank you Steven!
Best bike shop in town and I've been to em all.  Since my bike needed a simple fix Steve didn't charge me and his wife was super helpful!  Definitely saving up to get a Raleigh from them. Thank you!

While in the Santa Barbara area on vacation this past August, my wife's bike needed emergency repair to the rear wheel. Somehow it was severely bent while on the bike rack of our truck.
We drove over to Varsity shortly after they opened on a Friday morning. They were wonderful. The man came out to our truck, had a look, and took the wheel off the bike while it was still on the rack so we didn't have to hassle with that. 
Told us to return in an hour. When we got back, it was all fixed better than new. I won't tell you what he charged, but it was incredibly reasonable. We were expecting more, so we gave him enough tip to buy his lunch. 
If we lived in the area and need a new bike, we would buy from them in a second. With service like that you can't go wrong.

Really helpful and honest.  Reasonably priced bikes for college kids. Nice selection and outstanding customer service.  Brought my daughter in and Steve took care of her.  Great people. Highly recommend.

I have gotten two bikes from varsity bike shop, my first one got stolen.. big surprise iv. Anyways it's a cute little store with a tune up deal and air pump and what not. My newer bike was a cheaper model, why splurge if it's just going to get stolen. Anyways he put the basket on and adjusted the seat for me and everything. I prefer this bike shop to the other ones in iv anyday!
If you respond well to friendly people and cheap prices, you'll like this place.

This place is great. I've bought two bikes here and often bring my bicycle by for service when it needs it and I'm nearby, and I've always been impressed. Both bicycles, and everything else I've bought here, were reasonably priced; the owner has sometimes installed a part for free if it involved minimal labor. He always diagnoses problems quickly and never in my experience has he been wrong. Work gets done quickly, too. And he's said something that I wish more places who do skilled labor would say to me: "You don't need what you thought you needed when you came in. Let me do something less expensive that will actually fix the problem."

Really great store, here.

Super nice employees that are eager to assist with any skateboard or bicycle concerns and maintenance. The place is clean and organized well. They always repair my longboard for little to no charge. I recommend all to stop on by and give this place a try!

Highly recommended!!!! I came in looking for a bike but didnt have the funding necessary. i gave them a call a couple of days later and Steven gladly put the bike on hold for nearly a week and a half. Once i came in to finally purchase the bike it was hands down the best service i could ask for. i had a couple of skewers installed as well as a bike lock mount without any hassle. Owners are outstanding and it is a wonderful buying experience.  if youre looking for a bike or bicycle accessories go check them out!!!!

I bought a bike here two days ago, and I absolutely love it. The owner is the nicest and most helpful person I have met here in IV so far. As soon as I walked in the owner helped me. There are not many choices to choose from, but the service is amazing. When I bought my bike he told me that I can go in within 30-60 days and he would make sure everything with the bike is ok.. and if something was wrong he would fix it for FREE! He even was able to put my basket on for me (for free) that I had bought somewhere else. If you need a bike and you live in IV, GO HERE!!

We had a couple bikes that needed to be assembled and brought them to 
Varsity Bike Shop. Steven was more than happy to do this, and at a very fair
price. There was some damages to the bike and had to put in even more time
to do a simple assembly. He made all the adjustments and repairs-and was 
more than happy to do it. He has great customer service and does a lot of
little extras for free. Great guy!

I was wondering around and wasnt sure whats kind of bike i wanted. The staff are very friendly, and after i simply explained my needs, he introduced me a road bike. After I try, i.know this is exactlly what i want in my mind. Great experience!

These guys are seriously awesome! I've gone there for a flat twice, and they are very fast! They changed my entire tire on the spot in less than ten minutes. I was chatting it up with them for a couple minutes, and they are really cool and down-to-earth people. The prices are also very fair. I send all of my friends to Varsity because I love the customer service!
best bike store ever. such great prices & bikes. if you're a ucsb student definitely check this place out

I wouldn't recommend this store, nor ANY store in IV, for actually buying a bicycle, but this place is the best for tune-ups and repairs. 

The owners are so nice and they often don't even charge for small services, such as bending your fender back into place. Even a popped tire cost me around six dollars. They are really fair. The employees are also very friendly and helpful. 

And as far as I know, this place offers the cheapest tune-ups around, which is great for UCSB students since college/ocean spray is really hard on your bike.

Took my bike to Varsity because my bike pedal had popped off. Steve inspected it and explained to me that my crankset had its threads worn out and would require replacement. The fix would take about 45 minutes. When I came back to pick it up a few hours later, I was pleasantly surprised to find that he had made additional fixes too -- at no additional charge to me! 

Overall a very positive experience here at Varsity Bike Shop. Had many friends recommend getting my bike looked at here and I couldn't agree more.

I'm not very knowledgeable about bikes, but when I walked in to the Varsity Bike Shop to find myself a new bike, I felt very welcomed and was guided very well. Steve is quick, knowledgeable, helpful, and creative (we mixed colors for the bike) and now I have a beautiful bike that is my baby. I ride it everywhere and it was very affordable! 5 stars forsure!

I came here a few times and it was ok, but my last trip here made me don't want to come here anymore. I brought my bike in to fix a squeaky brake, but the guy working there wanted to "save" me some money and save him some time so he can work on the next bike so he just twisted the brake pad, took $12 from me for labor fee, and I still have a super loud squeaky brake. I went back and tell them the problem is still there, but, after an hour of waiting in line and fixing the bike, the problem is still there.
Great family owned business.. made me feel right at home! Thanks Max with u on the team.. my baby is in good hands

I got into a bike accident and my handlebars were twisted to the right, chain fell off, and my brakes were offset. I went to Varsity bike shop to get it fixed because it was the closest shop and I always went there to pump my tires for free. They told me to come back in 30-45 minutes to pick up my bike. I went back and they told me that everything was fixed and I was set. I didn't pay yet for the repairs so I asked how much but they said not to worry about it and just to be safe out there. This was really unexpected and I was just so grateful for their kindness. Definitely check this place out if you need repairs, a new bike, or to pump your tires!

My favorite bike shop here.

I found a beach cruiser off craigs list, then bought it to this shop to make sure it's in decent condition. The owner, Steven, re-adjusted my bike, corrected the alignment, & pumped air for free! 
It was a quick fix, but not many places around here are that generous.
He's a super cool dude and he's good at what he does. Totally recommend this place.

Oh, & I just bought a basket from there which he installed - only $23!

I was very pleased with my visit to the shop today. I came in to get my bike adjusted and I was helped right away. I would highly recommend this place! I will be returning if I ever need something on my bike fixed.

First review I've ever done on yelp and for good reason. The place was CLOSED on a Sunday night around 8 pm when he saw me at the door and he hooked me up with a desperately needed bike. Not only is it sexy and affordable, it rides very smoothly, and Steven is always there to help you if you need something. Great guy, amazing person, just a really awesome dude. He's generous, flexible, and very aware of what you need. When you walk in looking for something Steve will go above and beyond and you won't ever want to go anywhere else for your bike needs.
This bike shop is sooo good i bought a bike from here and its so fast sometimes scary like a rollercoaster but really fast its fun hahaha

Had my ENTIRE hydraulic break system stolen off my mountain bike and needed them replaced. I called REI and they told me that it could be anywhere from $60-$120 in labor alone to install them, even for REI members. I ended up going with Varsity. It took awhile to get the job done but they got me super nice breaks on sale and only charged me $30 for labor!!! I thought I'd be lucky to pay less than 100, since hydraulic breaks can be a pain to install.... unbeatable deal

blake was so nice and helpful! i went to the other bike shop in IV and they were helpful, but varsity bike shop went the extra mile. they taught me a lot  about bikes so that i knew what i was buying and their prices are super fair too. they let me test drive in the parking lot and helped me find the best fit. they even stayed open more than a half hour past close just to finish my bike. 
they store bikes over winter & summer breaks for about the same as the UCSB campus but they offer a tune up with the storage. 
you can come back within 30 days to fix anything with the bike and get a tune up 30 days after your purchase date (also free).
so happy with my cute new bike and so thankful for all their help!!! 
will definitely come back for more services and recommend anyone who wants a good priced bike that will last them a long time.
This place is so great! Very fast, high quality, and cheap repairs and the owner is so nice! Would definitely recommend : )

I kept getting flat tires and kept taking my bike to Varsity bikes to get it fixed. I got flat tires two days in a row just biking on campus. I finally took my bike to AS bikes, and I haven't had a flat tire since. I swear they put old used tubes in the bikes. I've seen them take parts off of other bikes to make repairs, it's all pretty sketchy. AS Bikes is better for repairs, so don't waste your time here.

One of the best bike shops I've been to, period. Service is always great, and prices are always fair. My u-lock was rusted shut, and I couldn't get it open despite trying for about an hour. Needed to get to class, so I called up VBS, and Steve drove and CAME TO ME. Unlocked my bike in a matter of minutes, and told me to pay whenever because I didn't have cash on hand. Biked immediately over and the whole deal only costed me $5. 

For all your bike related needs, save yourself the trouble and money and go to Varsity Bike Shop. Easily the best service around.

Take your bike here if you want fast, expert service at a fair price.

I've visited other bike shops in town, and Varsity Bike Shop is my go-to. They don't overcharge or unnecessarily up-sell and will treat you well.

Okay, so it's small and doesn't have an amazing selection, but the owners are really nice!  They are this old couple that have had the shop forever.  They'll also service your bike for free if you bought it here.  

Yay for mom n' pop stores!

I, like many in Goleta, had my bike seat (post and all) stolen from me. I brought my bike to Steve, and he found a post that fit, installed it and a very comfortable seat for a VERY reasonable price. I was overjoyed with the price, the quality of customer service, and the turnaround time. If you have bicycle needs in Goleta, GO HERE! :-)

blake was so nice and helpful! i went to the other bike shop in IV and they were helpful, but varsity bike shop went the extra mile. they taught me a lot  about bikes so that i knew what i was buying and their prices are super fair too. they let me test drive in the parking lot and helped me find the best fit. they even stayed open more than a half hour past close just to finish my bike. 
they store bikes over winter & summer breaks for about the same as the UCSB campus but they offer a tune up with the storage. 
you can come back within 30 days to fix anything with the bike and get a tune up 30 days after your purchase date (also free).
so happy with my cute new bike and so thankful for all their help!!! 
will definitely come back for more services and recommend anyone who wants a good priced bike that will last them a long time.

What else can I say about Varsity Bike other than astounding. The service is always quick and the shop is clean, organized, and convenient. An added bonus is Steve, the owner, who is experienced and knowledgeable. When I first acquired a Bike from Varsity in September, I had visited a few stores in IV, and finally strolled into Varsity. The difference was stark. Upon entering, I was immediately greeted and helped; especially impressive given how packed the shop was with customers. The entire experience was pleasant, and I rode off with my new bike less than an hour after walking in (including a rim switch at my request). Today I went in to get a Presta valve core replaced on my rear tire valve, as it got bent when I was trying to fill the tire with air, and consequently could no longer be shut and maintain adequate pressure. I was not sure exactly what to expect, as some Presta valve cores are not removable, and require new inner tubes. I was in and out in less than a minute, as mine was apparently removable, and for free no less. The level of professionalism, service, and convince make Varsity the go to spot in IV for bikes and skateboards.
This place is AMAZING! The owner, Steve, is super knowledgeable and extremely cool. If you need anything bike related this is the place to go!

My daughter is a new freshman at UCSB and uses her bike daily (no car). When she left class  and found her bike unrideable due to the handlebars becoming misaligned with the front tire, it was bad news! The campus bike shop had a two week waiting list for repairs so that was a no go. Then she dropped in to Varsity Bike Shop. A quick repair had her back on the road at no charge. Thanks so much Varsity! We'll be back!

Steve is a SB local and he's friendly and cares about providing students with quality service at affordable prices.

I came in for a new inner tube and he fixed my bike up in 10-15 minutes. The new inner tube cost about $10. I was surprised how quick he finished my bike. I've been here a couple times for new tires and other things, and his friendly and efficient service never disappoints. The new storefront has lots of new bikes for sale.

He also has free air in the back of his store so you don't have to run all the way to campus if your bike is flat. Support your local businessman!

I love this place. The people there treat you like family. I consider them friends. 3 options to go to get your bike fixed up around here and this is by far the cheapest and quickest. AS took 2 days to fix my flat. Varsity took 30 minutes.

Christmas time the time to reflect on the people that you love and things that you like one of the things my daughter likes is riding bikes we came to the shop in this cute little quaint town and these two gentlemen helped us my daughter got a bike for Christmas we are so stoked with the professionalism & the great prices. That lead  us to the perfect bike for her. I highly recommend this place . We are very satisfied with their service thank you Norma jean

We walked in looking for a used bike for my daughter who is a Freshman at UCSB, they snubbed their noses at us when we asked if they had any used bikes.

I guess you can use these snobs if you want to get overcharged and are not looking for a used bike. Go To Community Bikes instead, they will treat you like a person.

Just wanted to "hop" on here and write a short review to let everyone know HOW AMAZING the staff is here! Always so so helpful and nice! I personally have never gotten my bike fixed here but every time I go pump my tires at the back of the shop, someone is always there to help me.

The bike shop located in Isla Vista near UCSB. 

I regularly use both the University Bike shop and Varsity Bike Shop back and forth. Both great service but I try my best to utilize the shop as often as possible. The shop stand by their service and product. I especially like how they double-check the original part number when replacing parts in their shop.

Blake was extremely helpful, very knowledgeable and they gave us a great deal!!!  We bought a fixie and Blake walked us through the benefits of each bike until my son decided on the one that worked best for him!  I'd recommend anyone looking for a bike, go see Blake!

110% would recommend this place to anybody looking for a quick and affordable fix. The people here are genuinely so kind and eager to help out anybody, but especially so for the college students. They're on top of their trade, working their hardest and making sure everybody walks out their shop satisfied. As a student who has only been living here a week, I'm thankful to have found the Varsity Bike Shop so early on. It's definitely going to be my go-to repair shop!

Best bike shop in IV, steve is awesome and very helpful. He is honest about the products and recommended the best solution to me and a friend who needed help with bike lights. He also filled my tires!

Family owned business that treats their customers like family . They have helped so many kids get acclimated to their first time being away from parents and being honest with how many bikes get stolen and how careful you have to be in to register them with the police department in the hopes of getting your bike back. Great people

Really nice park in the hideaway near the Santa Barbara mission. There was a decent amount of walking trails and trees with a small playground for kids. The atmosphere is quite peaceful and there are even a few picnic tables and bbq grills. Great little park.

Perfect quite place to spend a few hours jumping on rocks or playing in the creek. My toddler had a blast here. The playground isn't age appropriate for her but I'm sure she will enjoy it when she's older. For now its playing in the creek and making mud pies. All in all fun for the family and a great place o kill a few hours.

Zoe W. said everything I'd want to say, but this place is the bomb.com.  It feels very foresty and natureish, like you're out camping somewhere far away, but it's like 500 feet from the touristy Mission.  The creek was awesome with plenty of wildlife and neat little bugs.  Plenty of logs to walk across the river on.  Lots of fun rocks.

It's a well rounded, woodsy park with all the things you look for in a park. Shaded areas, play structures, small barbecues and benches, small trails, a creek, etc. I'd say the only real shortcoming would be parking, but truthfully I'd rather have less parking to maintain the more wooded and natural quality to the park. My family and I finally came here after wanting to drop by for months and my toddler loved it. The play structure is enough modern fun for most kids but following the small trails to the creek and spending a few hours digging through the pebbles, skipping stones, crossing the creek and mashing rocks to make paint and paint the boulders was quite fun. We took a ton of photos, enjoyed the fresh air, the beautiful running creek (after a rainfall), beautiful oaks. In many ways, it's an ideal park for my family and we don't have to travel very far for it.

My husband and I came here for an afternoon to get away from work and every day craziness. Turns out we picked the perfect spot. I'm a huge fan of free parking and on a week day we found lots of it inside the park. We walked down the little pathways and down to the stream. Leaves were starting to fall from the trees and the sound of the water was very relaxing. On our way back to the car a deer ran right up to us. As soon as it saw us it froze and walked down toward the stream to avoid us. Next time we'll grab a picnic lunch and a spot near the stream so we can listen to the water while we eat.
After hearing so many great stories about this park & it's great memories it was nice today to make our own, great park for picnics followed by a nice nature stroll.

Really nice park really fresh air the path is green no trash anywhere it's honestly the most trees I've ever seen a park and it's really different it's a small but yet private public park and they allow dogs that's very nice and they have trash bins and waste products for dogs. Really nice park in Santa bar ara right by the Santa Barbara mission

Last review of this place 2010... the one before that was 2008.  So I guess my review will be the "New" review for the next two years or so!    I guess I better do a good job of it!   

Pretty place, nice Oak trees.   A creek running past it and seemed to have plenty of parking.    

A friend was having a birthday for her 4 year old, so we went to the park.   They reserved an area,  the Parks Department put up a reserved sign with the childrens names, the number of expected guests and that there would be a bouncy jumper thing... Nice enough.     Except that the party was at 11:00 and the power was turned off.    Picture 35 little 4 year olds without a bouncy thing, criminal!    

The parents made do, there was a playground and a couple of kids tried it out but it was in the full summer sun and was a little hot.    They passed around some coloring books and some of the kids enjoyed them... but the the flacid Johhny Jumper lay next to the party as a cruel reminder of what things could be!   

Eventually after lunch and after 2/3 of the children had already left a park ranger arrived.   He unlocked a door and turned on the power... too little too late.  

I seriously hope that the Parks department refunds the reservation fee!   I don't know the parents seemed pretty chill, probably not the type to go after it.

I've lived in Santa Barbara city/county for 27 years and this was my first time ever at that park I've biked past it been in a vehicle and bus past it last weekend we went there for a birthday part for my soon to be godson and I was impressed by it it was cool that day luckily and we all had a good time and I would go back to that park again now I live in Carpinteria and I'd rather go there than toro canyon park.

What a great park. 

I've only been a handful of times, but each has been enjoyable in its own regard. This park is uniquely laid out and offers a small parking area which meanders in a loop. Even just driving in is an awesome experience. You are instantly shaded beneath towering oak trees, and these form the backdrop of the park. 

There are many areas to picnic, a nice playground, or just sit and take in the surroundings. The park butts up to a seasonal creek which is usually dry but still neat to look at. I live only a mile from here and have walked over to enjoy lunch on a few occasions. You just can't beat the views. 

The park does get pretty full on weekends. It's off of narrow Mission Canyon Road and driving in/out of the single entry can be a bit tricky with other vehicles whizzing by. I'd say my only gripe is that there happen to be a large group of rowdy men who congregate here for hours nearly every day. Listening to their yelling and blasting of music takes away from the serenity of the space, but I suppose everyone is allowed the right to this public land. 

Overall, a nice spot worth checking out.

A fun little park with, you guessed it, a lot of rocks--big rocks, little rocks, and some medium ones too! So we stopped by with our friends while in Santa Barbara. The kids loved playing on the playground and climbing on the rocks, while our frenchie followed along. Yea, it's dog-friendly, so you can bring the whole crew. 

We also walked a loop on one of their trails, maybe 10-15 mins total. Though if you want to make it longer, they have a few more options. I think. 

And we noticed a lot of families there setting up for BBQs. Seems like a great place with a variety of activities.

Came here when visiting the area on a road trip to stretch my legs a bit (it was close to Mission Santa Barbara, but they were packed with tourists so I just wanted a few minutes to myself . . . )

Easy access off main road leads to several small parking lots among coast Oaks, and parking was available although the park was busy! 

There are trails leading up the hill, including a horse trail, and there are picnic tables and BBQ stands scattered throughout the dappled shade of the oaks - reminds me so much of where I live north of San Francisco! 

There is also a horseshoe pits area, a gym structure in a kids play area, and clean restrooms which are ADA accessible. A large BBQ area (commercial grill!) and tables are also available for reservations. 

A great place to relax, take a short stroll through the oaks and along the trails, or just be a kid and scramble around on rocks or in the creek bed.

Tucker's Grove is one of our favorite parks in Santa Barbara. I love bringing my kids and extended family to play on the slides, swings, climb around, explore, picnic BBQ, or go for a hike up San Antonio trail. And as another person mentioned, a great place to take a nap in the shade. With the rain today the creek is flowing steadily... Been a long time since I've seen that happen! Thank goodness for the rain!
Family favorite. The hike/bike trail up the canyon is beautiful, serene and a lot of fun on a mountain bike.

I'm assuming this place was once really pretty and covered in grass but it's now a big patch of dirt and sand..quiet and tucked away...it's a Saturday afternoon and I'm the only one here-so my dog is loving it. Lots of shaded areas with tennis balls and picnic tables. Don't know if I would come here again, but it's an alternative to the busy beach
Omg this was a childhood favorite park , awesome park with all the trees and luscious lawns .

GO TO THIS PARK.

Especially if you're visiting. We found it on YELP. Note: the YELP address listed takes you by the park but tells you to keep going. DONT. Turn into the first park you see. Then veer all the way right and down the road for a bit.

You'll find a k9 slice of heaven with dog equestrian type fence things.

Dog friendly!

This review is for the San Antonio Creek trail.

There's plenty of parking space, just make sure to park all the way at the end of the lot (closer to the dog park) to save you from having to walk through boring parking lot.

A mistake I made was I took a parking spot close to the entrance. I saw a brown wooden sign that said "trail" so I thought that was the start of it. It was not. It's part of the park trail, maybe .25 of a mile loop. Save yourself the time and energy and skip it.

The real trail signs you want to look for are white, or the main one at the end of the parking lot that says "San Antonio Creek trail". There are a couple of minor creek crossings (there was still some water) so just be careful when rock hopping. 

Once we reached the end at San Marcos Pass Rd and it looked like there was more trail on the other side... DON'T DO IT. It's basically all in the sun, not scenic at all, and ends in a residential area that is closed off, forcing you to backtrack all the way to get back to your car. Don't make our mistake. 

Overall the San Antonio Creek trail was nice, mostly flat, shady trail. I would recommend it to people visiting with dogs. If you come in the summer, just make sure to bring a lot of water, you can stop at the dog park to refill. Also, the park opens at 8, which is a bummer to early bird hikers like me who like to go before it gets crowded/too hot.

This is a review for the dog park at Tucker's grove park.

When you drive in to the park you want to drive as far back as possible after turning left. You then need to walk through the  Kiwanis Meadows bbq area and you will see the enclosed dog park in the back. There are two secure gates to make sure no dogs can leave as you enter.

This is a nice and unique dog park. Lots of trees and benches for people to enjoy while the dogs do their things. Plenty of random tennis balls laying around. There are natural hills in the park which I think adds to the overall dog exploring experience. 

There is a watering hole with several large bowls. This area typically turns into a mud pit since the whole park is mostly dirt right now (darn drought). My dog leaves absolutely filthy because she loves rolling around in the mud and dirt. I don't mind because she is in heaven there but if you can't handle some dirt, you might want to find an all grass park. I have never had an issue with any of the dogs or owners at this park! 

Many people bring books to relax while the dogs play. It really is a pretty park and I would highly recommend it! I find that the park is pretty empty in the earlier hours on weekends but is  nice and busy from noon onwards. Similarly it is very busy around 5 onwards on weekdays.
Love this quick hike with the furry ones buried between the beautiful homes in SB.

This dog park is really dusty. There is very little grass and not many dogs go there. You turn right and the entrance, drive all the way down, park and then have to walk a bit to get to the actual enclosed off leash section.
I loved it! It's a nice space with cool little trails that is fun to wander on. I still need to check out the dog friendly zone, but what I did get to see was really nice

A wonderfully large dog park but very few dogs and people visit it on the regular so it's a bit of lacking for social playtime.  It also tends to be 5-10 degrees warmer around the park due to it's inland location, so when it's hot it's much to hot for long haired dogs. I would imagine it would an ideal dog park for fetch-obsessed short-haired dogs who like to to run long distances!

Wonderful park with lovely picnic sites. This park is kept immaculate and the picnic sites are always clean and well maintained. The Ranger and park hosts at Tucker's Grove are friendly and very helpful for assisting visitors. The bathrooms are very clean and we love the kids play area with lots of swings and slides. We took this funny picture of one of the park hosts cleaning up after a large baby shower. Great people for sure

TRASHED!
This is a great park during the week when the County workers can stay ahead of all the litter that's generated, but on Friday, Sat and Sun the amount of trash and garbage that's generated on-site is un-realistic!  How can people just throw their garbage, diapers, beer bottles and pinatta waste on the ground?  There are 8 picnic sites that can be reserved for a fee, but no cleaning deposits are required so nobody has anything to lose by just trashing the place.  The restrooms are cleaned every morning before the park opens, and are a major stop for all of those commuters from Santa Maria needing to empty their Starbucks. There's always a line waiting.  

The dog park is a nice feature but people must obey the leash law and not let their dogs run loose outside of the dog park. Loose dogs chase away the Blue Herons, the park's only natural predator for gopher control.  The dog owners think it's cool, but it's not.  Dog owners also need to be more responsible in picking up their dog's doodo as the kiddies will walk in it.  Also, dogs should not be allowed in or on the playground equipment. They pee on things that kiddies come in contact with.  

Better reserve a picnic site on weekends from Memorial Day to Labor day, as the park is absolutely packed on weekends. There are some first-come-first-served sites available but people stake these out early, often before the park opens. 

Lets see: rental folding tables and chairs are not allowed. Must use park picnic tables  only. So don't think you can rent the smallest, cheapest picnic site and expand it's seating capacity.

There is a coke machine on-site. Vendors and pushcarts are NOT allowed unless they have a license from the County Health Department. You try to sell corn-on-the-cob from your un-licensed push-cart and the Park Ranger will HUNT YOU DOWN! 

Portable cooking equipment (propane or electric only BBQ's., chafing dishes, fryers, popcorn makers, griddles, etc.) are allowed. Must use park BBQ's only for charcoal fires. People don't understand: charcoal from portable BBQ's gets thrown in the bushes at the end of the day. Or the BBq's get thrown in the back of the pickup and then the driver drives real fast........  Only three of the biggest sites have electrical.

WATCH OUT FOR TRAFFIC!  KEEP AN EYE ON YOUR KIDS!  PARK WORKERS GET HIT ALL THE TIME!

Park opens at 8am, closes at Sunset (whenever that is). No, you can't use party lights to extend the day. And if you don't keep your music, the neighbors will call the sheriff on you. 

Park is at it's best during the week and early in the morning. 

But the park serves it's most basic use: in providing outdoor breathing space for those peoples who cram themselves into tiny apartments.  

I worked and lived there as a Park Host for almost 8 months.

This is a great park! 

There's lots of bbq spots for parties big and small, there are hiking trails and a nice big for park at the very end. 

Tucker's grove is one of my fav family bbq spots ever since I was a kid. I have lots of nostalgic memories at Tucker's Grove from hikes, church picnics and family bbqs. Every single one of my family dogs has been to Tucker's Grove!

The family area of the park is great.  The dog park, well...

My wife and I run a local dog walking business and used to frequent this park.  Unfortunately we have largely stopped using it due to the high number of dangerous, callous and inappropriate behaviors we have witnessed on a regular basis.  First you have to dodge the high number of car and RV bums to get to the back parking lot.  The dog park is about 400 yards from the parking lot, and it is often very difficult to avoid unpleasant confrontation along the way (pulling and barking).  Antisocial dogs and their uncaring owners dominate the area during most hours of the work day (10-4).  Which then gives way to a regular, larger crowd of individuals who pay little to no attention as to how their dogs are interacting with others.  Other parks such as Tabano Hollow and open spaces like Evergreen and More Mesa are much more preferable.
Just wasted $10 on the dog wash that had no shampoo in it. No one accepts responsibility for who will return my $10. Not the park, the ranger or the company listed on the machine.
Excellent playground area and clean bathrooms. Plenty of parking too. First time here with my kids and we love that it's close to out home.

Pretty sweet park for a campground-ish feel. 

Last weekend I came here because I wanted to barbecue, and Girsh Park as well as Goleta Beach were completely full. Since Tucker's Grove is huge, we easily found parking and a barbecue several yards away from our car.

We forgot to bring drinks but found vending machines as well as a water spigot, which we used to wash off our plates.

I think that tuckers Grove is five stars because all the dogs get to run and have some fun with people and other dogs! I also think that Tucker's Grove is five stars because the dogs there are normally friendly and there's a lot of fun things for your dogs!and there's a water whenever your dogs get thirsty!

Definitely excited to find this place but the address on here WILL NOT WORK THROUGH GPS/GOOGLE MAPS.

 You have to go to the intersection of turnpike and cathedral oaks and you'll be at the park. My navi & google maps took me to a private access road that will not get you to the park. Once in the park make a right and drive into the last parking area there is, then walk past the white gate down the trail and you will reach the park!

My 9mo frenchie has only been to the dog park in Encino, which we love and has small/big dog sections. This park is for all sizes! It was nice giving my puppy some play with the big dogs! I would be careful if your dog is timid though. 

Very beautiful and lots of trees and grass. a few benches and a water fountain but i would recommend bringing your own water bowl as the ones there were very muddy. I would also recommend bringing your own toys if your dog likes to fetch as there were only a few old tennis balls. There are a few trash cans with pooper scoopers but i still noticed a lot of poo, so be careful where you step! 

I plan to bring my pup back here a few times a week, definitely very pleased!

Tuckers Grove off-leash dog recreation area can be reached by entering the park on Cathedral Oaks Road. Follow the parking areas on the right all the way to the back of the park. You will drive over a creek crossing and arrive at a separate parking area. From here, there is a paved path that leads to Kiwanis Meadows. 

As you arrive at the grassy meadow, the fenced dog run is easily recognizable. Surrounded by Coast Live Oaks and Sycamore trees, this dog park meets all expectations. Take the afternoon and relax with a picnic at one of the picnic sites, which include BBQ grills. 

For the kids there is a nice playground, and restroom facilities are within steps from the dog run. This is the most family friendly dog park in the area. This park is open from 8 AM - Sunset.

Amenities:
Dog waste bag dispensers, watering station and picnic benches within fenced area. Public restrooms, picnic area, hiking trails, and playground are located in the immediate vicinity of dog run.

I came to Tucker's grove with my significant other and puppy, and had a fairly negative experience. 

First, let me start off with the good- there is ample parking to get to the dog park. It definitely took a second for me to find the dog park itself, as it's fairly tucked away, but once we got there, it was spacious and fairly well kept. I wish there was more grassy areas as our pup looked like Pigpen from Charlie Brown (dirt dust followed him everywhere).

Now, for the not so good part of this review. We went into the dog park and let our pup go off leash. What first seemed to be dogs saying hi and playing soon after turned into dogs who were obviously bullying my pup even while under the "supervision" of the owners all collectively snickering by the benches (you know who you are).

Now, you may wonder or think that I'm overly protective of my dog- I'm not. I know he can handle his own, but when three aggressive dogs continue to bull rush him and then I see my pup (who normally wags his tail even at the most confusing of times) had his tail between his legs. Surprisingly, when he finally broke free, he came and hid between my legs and these dogs (although they were somewhat friendly to other humans) wouldn't give my pup a break. Not only was it frustrating that out of the 10 people that were there, one person took their dog away and retreated, but I kept hearing snickering and what seemed to be a lack of respect not only towards my pup, but to myself and my significant other.

On top of that, the conversations they were having obviously showed encouragement of dogs fighting one another. I don't get it. SB is an extremely dog-loving place, and I just happened to run into the 10 dog owners of SB who not only had bullies for dogs, but also had the persona of their dogs as well (or probably more vice versa).

My experience really ruined the beauty of the park itself. I may come back to give it a second chance, but honestly, if I were to see the same group of people again, I would immediately turn my back to the park without batting an eye.
Great dog park and a nice little walk through the woods to get to it.

Tuckers grove is a great little park, you can have BBQs let your kids play on the playground or go on a hike in the hills behind. This is one of my favorite quick mountain bike spots to ride at. Side note this is a pretty great place for Pokemon Go because it has 4 Poké stops at the entrance.

During our family's mid-winter break last month, I asked the kind staff at the SB visitors center to suggest a place for a little persons hike that was safe after the previous months mudslides.  They recommended Tuckers Grove and we came here a couple of days later on a fabulously sunny & warm day.  The short hike we took started on one end of the back section of the park and up the hills, next to the homes that border the park to the north side.  It then looped over and descended, bringing us into the other end of the wide park space.  It seems there are longer hike/bike trails that run along San Antonio creek but that is *not* the hike we took due to the kiddo.

I loved all the reservable areas that we passed on our return walk across the parking lots back to our car!  Some serious bbq pit areas and amenities- looks like one can accommodate 400 people.

Seems like a cute park with a nice kids playground.

One of my favorite childhood parks!

I've been here countless times over the years for family and business functions. Easter egg hunts, birthdays, company picnics, family barbecues, you name it. Though fairly close to the freeway, I see it as a true hidden oasis. The entrance is fairly nondescript and the park itself is rather hidden from the main road with its abundance of large trees. It's got a little something for everyone. 

Tucker's Grove has several areas which can be rented for events and let me tell you, these spots surely will be booked most every weekend during the spring and summer months. They can accommodate all sizes of groups. I've reserved group areas a few times through the County of SB and the process was simple. 

There is so much to do here. One can play on their multiple playgrounds, host a large barbecue on one of the many pits, engage in a game of horseshoes, meander off onto one of the upper trails, or simply sit back and take in the beautiful scenery. The parking lot is ample but will get completely full on a sunny weekend day so be prepared to walk a bit from one of the overflow areas. 

Highly recommended if you're looking for a serene yet fun space to check out!

Tori was an excellent wrangler and it's clear they love and take care of their horses. She knew each of their quirks and matched us to horses that fit our experience levels perfectly. 

The ride does not feel like two hours! It goes by so fast with the amazing scenery and ocean breezes. I love that this didn't feel like a "trail ride" because our horses did more than just walk along in a line. They were allowed to trot and gallop, which made it so much more fun! 

I'm definitely glad I chose to go with this group over some of the other places I researched. I wouldn't have felt comfortable with a rude wrangler and this experience was the opposite of that!

We did a bluffs ride with Tori while staying in Montecito. Two hours around the hills of Summerland and over to the bluffs near the ocean on a lovely afternoon. We rode Gringo and Midnight who were so sweet and well behaved. I've done a few trail rides but I'm hardly a pro, but these horses seemed very happy and cared for, and were fun to ride. Tori was great; super fun and friendly. The whole experience was the highlight of our trip!

AMAZING experience !!! I absolutely LOVE horses, and for my anniversary we booked a 2 hour sunset ride with Los Padres outfitters and seriously was the best experience. It was simple to book the trip, and they respond very quickly. I'm from Orange County so I wasn't very knowledgeable of the area, although they were very helpful and sent detailed directions and instructions.
Our ranger, Seth was such a great guide. He educated us on how we should direct the horses, and how we should ride/sit. I love that they consider what horse would be best for a rider. My boyfriend was more experienced and I was a first timer, they assigned us a horse that would best fit our experience and make sure we are comfortable the whole way. Seth made sure we were safe the whole entire way, and we definitely felt safe with him. He made our ride so calming, and fun! 
The trail is beautiful, Seth took us along the mountains and directly to the beach. Riding along the beach was MY DREAM and I couldn't have asked for more, it was an incredible ride. Seth was also so kind to take so many photos of us !! I loved that he shared that with us. It was so nice to meet him and the adorable horses, Buckshot, Ace, and Chino  
Most importantly You can really see that Los Padres Outfitters really care about these horses and the riders!
All in all, I LOVED IT ALL, I'm coming back. I miss it already

Our first trip with Outfitters was very enjoyable and relaxing on the beach.  This was one of my wife's bucket list adventures and we were happy to do it with you guys.  Our guide was very nice and knowledgeable, thanks a bunch!

I highly recommend Los Padres Outfitters for an awesome horseback riding experience!  My wife and I celebrated our 10 year anniversary and wanted to do something different and memorable and Los Padres definitely provided that experience!  A big thanks to Marilyn for taking care of the administrative stuff and also a huge thanks to Tori for introducing us to the coolest horses ever, Howdy and Chubby, who totally took care of my wife and I on a super scenic ride through country trails, across the 101 Freeway, and down to the Pacific Ocean!  We had the time of our lives!  Thank you!!

Did a 5pm trail and beach ride with Megan and Graham and had a blast!! The scenery is beautiful and tranquil, I've never seen that part of Summerland before. Of course riding a horse on the beach is just amazing and beautiful. The horses were perfectly behaved and even galloped a little with us on the beach. I will definitely be doing another tour with LPO :)

Los Padres Outfitters are so organized, fun and friendly, this was the best part of our 4 day trip to Santa Barbara! The horses were well trained and easy going, which was great for a beginner rider like me. But they also catered for my experienced husband, and made sure he got the opportunity to gallop down the beach - he came back beaming like a happy kid! We started the ride at 4pm,  through beautiful tree lined trails and then to the beach, which was empty and amazing. Then we headed into the cliffs just as the sunset and had a glass of wine as the sky turned purple as the sunset....everything was perfectly timed.  Graham, his wife and team were so warm and friendly and you can see how much they love and care for their horses and the area. They cater everything to your needs. Graham is literally the all-American cowboy and he and his wife looked like they stepped out of the wild west! Gorgeous people with big hearts. We did the 4 hour ride but I would also love to try the overnight rides. All in all, an amazing experience I will not forget!

I have taken many trips with Los Padres Outfitters and they are truly the best and only proper outfitter in the southern Los Padres National Forest. Graham really takes care of his guests - he is a great cook and serves up all sorts of tasty treats. He has really calm mules and horses  that know the trails, so you don't have to worry about it. And of course he takes you to the prettiest scenery that the Los Padres has to offer. 
I can't recommend him highly enough!!
A+++

My husband and I did a beach ride for our anniversary and it topped every other organized ride I've ever been on. It certainly helped that we had perfect weather with no wind. 

There were a couple snags leading up to the actual ride, but nothing I couldn't look past. My husband had never been on a horse and was absolutely terrified, but our guide and his horse quickly put him at ease. By the end of the ride he was completely in love with his horse, Gringo, and talking about riding again. My horse, Blackjack, was equally awesome. There was unmatched variety on this ride that really made it special, and the fact that it was private and not with a group of strangers made it perfect for our special occasion. Our guide also took a few high quality photos of us on her phone that she air dropped to me when we finished -- which we are VERY grateful for. 

Highly recommend a ride with this company

Wow!! This was an amazing experience.We did the 2 hour beach trail and it was absolutely BEAUTIFUL !! The horse were sweet and gentle, my mom and I felt safe the whole ride. Tori and B were awesome guides very sweet and knowledgeable about the horses. They took awesome pictures of us as well. Also making an appointment was super easy the lady on the phone was very nice! We'll definitely be going back we had so much fun!

Amazing trail ride to the beach with Graham as our guide. I cannot say enough good things about our day. Graham was a great guy and all his horses were so well behaved and good looking! We will definitely be back for another ride!

I wish I could give more stars! I had the greatest time, it was better than I could have expected.  Tori was a wonderful wrangler and knew the horses inside and out.  You can tell these horses are loved and well taken care of.  I will be a repeat costumer again and again.  Can't say enough nice things about the people, the horses, the view, the comfort, the communication, etc.  Worth the money for an experience like that.  Thanks again & see you soon!

I cannot say enough good things about this company! My cousin and I are advanced riders and our guide Tori completely catered to our ability. The horses are super responsive, incredibly traffic safe and about as bombproof as I've seen. Beach was beautiful, got to run the horses there and the ride to and from offered stunning views. Tori was a fantastic guide and so much fun to ride with! Thank you so much for everything
Really great outfitter. I surprised my husband for our anniversary with a private ride and it was great. Incredible views of the pacific! We really enjoyed the ride and would highly recommend.

The horses were very sweet and easy to ride. We did 2 hours of beautiful trail and beach riding. Tori was knowledgeable and easy to talk with. She took great pictures of us. We loved the entire experience.

This was so fun! Surprised my husband for our anniversary with a beach ride. Beautiful horses, beautiful beach and a great guide! She was great conversation and took tons of pics of us. Would 100% recommend!
This ride was BEAUTIFUL.   We did the beach ride on the am.  Honestly every moment of the ride was breath taking and our guide was wonderful.  Ask for Morgan.  She was helpful and informative.
This was an amazing experience. Our tour guide was very knowledgeable. Liz catered to our every need. Thank you for sharing this day with us.

BEWARE. I booked a horse ride with my friend because we thought what a cute idea horses on the beach. I really think you should go elsewhere. I am a BEGINNER horse rider I.e. I have rode a horse a few times max and made this clear to Morgan (our guide) I was already nervous getting on the horse she said everything was fine when she couldn't even corral the horses in the beginning. My friends horse kept riding off and then the others would follow. This already made me uneasy. She didn't offer or suggest helmets- she said we would be fine and we trusted her. Like Huh???? She didn't properly check the saddle or tell us to put our feet in the stirrups. We literally had to ask. She started our ride and I'm like ok wow this is like going through an actual farm and down little slopes. She gave us NO INSTRUCTION other than to pull on the reigns when we wanted the horse to stop. No safety protocol, nothing. These horses were miserable too, branded on their asses, not the beautiful majestic horses in the promotional photos. All of a sudden, going down a slope my young horse got spooked by a god damn branch that seems like it was common on the trail. It took off, my saddle spun to the side and I was dragged for a full minute while trying to unlatch my boot from the saddle. I get up, bleeding all over, in shock and Morgan is literally unconcerned and not doing much. She didn't even get off her horse to check if I was alright. The horse has already bolted into the yonder. I was literally touched by angel, If I couldn't unlatch I would have been dead or paralyzed or skinned or brain dead take your pick. We got what I can only consider the farmhand, Sebastian to golf cart us back to our car where I then had to go to emergency services. I have road rash, a torn ligament and immense pain all over. No contact from anyone at the place until my friend emailed about it. They make you sign standard liability waivers, but they didn't even meet their duty of care. Absolutely, unequivocally negligent behavior. Do not go here.

This was one of the best experiences of my life. 

Originally planned to do a beach ride but when the tide was too high they called us 2 hours before our ride and suggested we do a trail ride instead. It was BEAUTIFUL.

Our guide Lucy was so sweet and took lots of great pictures for us.  The trail tour takes you through private trails you wouldn't get to see otherwise. 

I cannot recommend this enough!

This was an amazing time! It was my first time horseback riding and it was so special.  Our tour guide made me feel so at ease throughout the process.  The views were amazing.  I would book this again.

This company is amazing. I proposed to my girlfriend on a ride using this company and they were so helpful. The beach ride was beautiful and they helped in every instant to make the moment so special. They provided a professional photographer and a second handler for us to take pictures with the horses. Would definitely go with this company again.
So much fun riding on the beach. Horses were calm and well taken care of. Toni was a blast as our guide. Can't wait to do a pack-trip with them.

Los Padres Outfitters - #1 for Horseback Riding in Santa Barbara, period. 

I've had the pleasure of riding with LPO over the last year and a half and have gone on close to 20 rides. I usually go riding with Tori, but all of the guides are AMAZING.  These girls have all grown up riding and competing so their skill level is very high Also, don't be afraid to ask questions (within healthy reason) the guides are super friendly and have all the answers. 

Whether I have guests in town or just want to go on a solo ride, Los Padres Outfitters never disappoints. If you want to experience unbelievable views of Santa Barbara on the most majestic creatures (and quite honestly, feel like a cowboy / cowgirl) then I highly recommend booking a ride, you will not regret it! 

Something to consider for a first-timer... 

Before approaching your horse, if you feel a little bit nervous, just take a minute to breathe. Your approach to your horse should be warm and relaxed just like it would be when meeting up with one of your friends. 

Think about it, how would you feel if someone was walking towards you with a nervous look on their face while pretending to be totally cool and then reaching out to touch you?! You would instinctually back up and wonder why that person is being weird. So, just be your normal (hopefully) kind self and enjoy!

Riding a horse is the best way to get out of your head, be in the moment, FEEL and have fun!

Best ride ever. Beautiful horses lovely wrangler just the three of us. We did a four hour ride 3 hours traveling through the gorgeous streets and backroads of Montecito and an hour on the beach at sunset. I've been riding for years and wanted my son (11 years) to have a great trail ride when we went to visit Santa Barbara. Los Padres worked magically. Well worth the price. Great value.

Amazing beach horse ride! Blew away all my expectations! 
Was just my siblings and I and our wrangler. She was so awesome! Made us feel so comfortable! She let us walk, trot, canter on the beach! I highly recommend it if you're in the Santa Barbara area! 10 stars!!!!

Just finished up our ride on the beach and we're now dining in Summerland.  What an amazing adventure!  We had a fabulous guide, Tori, and two sweet, sweet horses; Midnight and Abigail.  Thank you for an amazing experience!  It was so amazing, we might be back for another ride this weekend.

I would give this place a 5-star if it wasn't for the fact that I rode a MULE! Not a horse. I paid a lot of money to go HORSE back riding not mule back riding. I made it very clear that I didn't want to ride a mule however they persisted and made it awkward and their were other horses available. It was slow and not fun. I also paid extra money for the valentines bouquet, that was supposed to be tied on the horse when we got there. Instead we were given the bouquet after the ride. Other than that the ride was beautiful and the guide was nice.

We have a very good experience with Los Padres beach trail riding. We have family of five and the younger one is 10 years old. They arrived on time, horses are in a good care condition. The trail to the beach is amazing fun which we didn't expected. The lady take care our group is very patient and she take care well with my 10 years old. We all have blasted and is highlight in our trip. We are highly recommend Los Padres!!

Super fun ride, trail and beach & picnic.  

Based on that experience we're considering the overnight pack rides which should be fun too!

We took a horseback ride for 90 minutes in Santa Barbara and had a wonderful experience. Elena was professional, on time, friendly and sweet. She was very good with the horses and the horses were very well trained. The ride was beautiful - we went down the trail from the mountains to the beach and out was serene and beautiful. Highly recommend Los Padres outfitters!!!

Ridiculous AMAZING! What kind and giving people! This ride today I experienced will go down as one of my best days. What a beautiful day and beautiful horses. We went for my girlfriends 50th birthday, and it was epic. From the meeting of the horses and the crew to the beautiful walk to the beach and Marilyn's (Grahams Mama) casserole I could go a happy girl..... I enjoyed every last minute. I'm taking my friends and family back and am also signing up for the over night horse back as well. Tori paired my husband and I with Midnight (please let me have him) and Black Jack! She couldn't have done a better job. I now want to be a cowgirl and take lessons. This is a great first date idea or Anniversary...... birthday..... just because and so on. Please do yourself a favor and book a ride! You won't regret it! We made friends today for life! Thank you! We appreciate all of you!!!
See you soon!!!!
Highly recommend the beach riding excursion.  The horses were gentle and easy going. Thank you to Eliana and her team for guiding us. We loved it and would ride again with them.

Our guide Lucy was supportive and put our safety first at all times. This is a very chill and gentle ride. If you are looking to cut loose and ride fast this is Not the ride for you. If you are looking for gorgeous scenery and a relaxed afternoon this IS the ride for you!!!  Thanks Lucy!!!
