What an amazing experience!! My husband and I are in SB for our honeymoon and he had never been horseback riding. Our ride to the beach with Los Padres was so special. The guides were sweet and professional-- and they took a ton of photos for us. The horses were also very well trained. Could not recommend this activity enough! Thank you so much!
Great place for some PONY baseball. Dog friendly and good baseball being played for the love of the game and not the $. Future major leaguers for sure.
Great dog park! We go every day! Could use more mulch now but Pixel always has fun even when he doesn't have a play date.

Mackenzie Park is a wonderful environment for adults & kids alike, ball fields & room to play soccer, etc.  The upper level is the lawn bowls club, a great sport for anyone older than 10 and free lessons as well.

MacKenzie Park now has a fenced in small dog park AND a large dog park!! These are brand new enclosures with great fencing, fresh wood chips, clean dog water fountains, and resting rocks. This is a huge upgrade for MacKenzie Park and for the neighborhood! I plan to spend the whole summer there with my dogs. 

We met lots of great dogs and dog parents. It was really funny watching the little dogs run over to the fence to bark at a large dog that got too close to the fence!!

Be careful if you have kids. The park is full of homeless people. Don't let your kids go in the restroom alone, there was one naked bathing.  The big baseball field doesn't have  fence for baseball tournaments, the most you'll get is a double.

Great new playground with a fence surrounding it so children won't escape. The baseball fields are nice. The bathrooms are typical park bathrooms...nothing special. 
They are building an off-leash dog facility in the corner. Should open soon.

Although beautiful and convenient, I would suggest using caution when bringing your dog here. I have a mid size young dog and on at least two occasions he was attacked while the owners took their time retrieving their dogs. One pit-mix had his teeth on my dogs neck in a choke hold and wouldn't release him. His owner said it was because he really likes balls - I brought my chuck-it for my dog and the pit owner didn't, so his dog wanted my dogs ball. Be careful.

Although there are a couple of off leash dog parks much nearer to where I live, this one is worth the extra drive and traffic! My puppy has the best time in the small dog area. The wood chips keep her from getting all muddy, the pets are well-socialized and pet owners are friendly and very responsible. Love it!

Cute little dog park, however if you can't find a spot out front, you have a bit of a trek from the parking lot.  This was a pit stop for us on our drive up to SLO, my Standard Poodle refuses to poop on a walk, so we had to find a park for him to do his business in private.

We checked out the new dog Park they built next to Mackenzie fields. It is the first dog Park in SB with separated big and small dog sections, which is huge! The space is not too small either. It is right next to a busy street on Upper State and near Trader Joe's, so tough parking during popular hours. 

There's wood chips instead of grass/dirt, so much cleaner taking your pups home! There are rules about no treats as well - I wish it also applied to toys, as some dogs are resource guarding. 

Overall a really great addition for the dog community in Santa Barbara. Only thing I'd change is to be in a more secluded area, but hey, can't complain too much!
Convenient location for dog owners to bring their dogs for exercise during their hotel stay. There's an area for small dogs and an area for large dogs.
It's a great small play area but kids loved it.  My 3 boys all enjoyed it.

This is a cool little playground a little ways off the Carpinteria State Beach, back against the train track. It has a Native American theme to it and presumably some educational value, but that was mostly lost on the kids climbing in and on the teepees and canoes. There are also some slides, including an excellent conveyor belt-like slide, which looked like a ton of fun.

One of the coolest features of the playground is that there's a little ramp up to a bridge over the entrance, so kids can literally run around in circles without getting bored. There's a decent amount of seating for parents, and if you get lucky enough to have the train stop at the Carpinteria Station while you're there, everyone gets a nice look at the train while it's stopped.

I don't know what interpretive play means, but this playground was pretty cool.  It had one of those roller slides, which I hadn't seen since I was a kid.  Lots of stuff to climb up, slide down, and check out. My niece enjoyed hanging from and climbing the igloo shaped huts.  Also, it's not a giant place so you can keep an eye on things and not worry too much about getting lost or having an obstructed view. Plus there's several benches for parents and babysitters to rest and watch their kid(s) -- not stare at their smartphones ignoring the children.  The small lizard statues were an interesting decoration too. 

p.s. I could see there's a brewery across from here, so if parents want to take a break maybe grandma and/or grandpa could watch the little ones while mommy and daddy kick back for a bit and enjoy adult beverages :)

Most times when my kids and I come here I'm responsible for rowing the canoe. Today I was appointed a mermaid by my son. I told him I was the only mermaid on my island (the dolphin statue) and that I was lonely. He said "you won't have to be lonely anymore mermaid, I'll get in my canoe and come visit you whenever you want. 

This park is right next to the train tracks, and a big grassy field. It's equipped with a slide modeled after gushing water, a fun and rippled roll bar slide, Chumash hut models, a sea otter statue, and a fun bridge to run, skate, and ride across. Just a few steps away is Carp State beach. 

You can go down the path along side park for a scenic stroll to the  jelly bowl and learn a thing or two about Carp and some native plants. There is no bathroom here, but some nearby. One at The Spot across street and in parking lot up the road a block. Take your kids and have a blast.
What a great park and next to a train and beach!! Happened upon this park on my way up the 101 and my son loved it. Fun equipment, and next to a huge grassy knoll and the beach.

It is a very cool place for children. There is a trail alongside the play area that leads to the Carpinteria State Park. The area trail alone is less than a mile but it is nice . There are so many things to see like birds and also wildlife. 

There are plaques all over the place that tell you about the origins of the place. The path has also solar panels that will light up at night for night walkers. 

It is also very close to so many restaurants, shops, and the big fields if you just want to run around even more freely.

name: Lookout Park
categories: Active Life, Parks, Beaches, Dog Parks

yelp_reviews.text:
This little Clifftop park have a great view of the Pacific and the coastline both north and south.  The park itself as restrooms, some small volleyball area, grassy areas, and a walking trail down to the beach.  The beach itself is a mixture of sand and rocks, seasonal depending on currents, tide, and longshore drift.  Like many beaches in Santa Barbara County, some folks ride horses up and down the coast, although there are time restrictions on equestrian activities on the beach, specifically between May and November.

Due to all the asphalt seeps in the area, you will often get tar on your feet if you visit the beach here.  Pro tip: keep a bottle of baby oil in your car with some cotton swabs or a small rag. The oil will remove the tar from your feet with a little bit of vigorous rubbing... and your skin will be smooth and soft, like a baby's butt.

Anyway, this is the kind of neat place to visit.  Seasonally, parking can be an issue. Best to arrive early, or have a friend in town within walking distance, and use their driveway.

Beautiful view from the top at the park and the beach is a short walk down a hill.  We lucked out and didn't experience any tar on this visit but we have been when the tar was too much to enjoy the beach.  

Go off season and the parking is easy, but I imagine it can get pretty busy during peak times!

The park at top is okay with bathrooms and public shower area.  Unfortunately not everyone picks up after their dog and homeless seem to overrun so much of SB.

All in all....a very nice time.

This beach is absolutely gorgeous, a long slim expanse of sand, with a beautiful park (showers and restrooms) above it on the bluff. The park has tables that I believe you can reserve for events. The beach is clean - I didn't see any dog mess whatsoever, contrary to other reviews.The place is super dog friendly.  I am not sure if its a common thing, but the day we were there the water itself was filled with oil, slicks of gas scented rainbow atop the waves, with long gooey strands of crude wrapping themselves around your legs beneath the waves. It was a sad and disturbing thing, ruining an otherwise gorgeous spot.

I absolutely love Lookout Park. It's also perfect for my dog as it has a large grass area for him to run on as well as the beach. They also have doggie baths. With the tar here, I was glad I brought my blue Dawn although I didn't need it.

In the upper area in the back there are several regulars that gather at a table to drink. They kept to themselves and added to the ambiance here. Nearby is a horseshoe pit as well as grills.

The view over the cliff to the land formations, rocks and ocean make it clear whee this park received its name.

There is also a playground I've always found to be busy with smiling kids.

I can't think of any age or demographic group that wasn't  represented during my two recent visits. 

I deducted one star because an aging lobster cage had washed up onto the beach. I have a feeling this isn't the first time something similar has happened.

Sorry dog owners. Following a nasty attack on a horse, animal control is now patrolling this beach. Too bad because the only folks using it most of the time are dogs and horse riders. One attack by one dog in the five years I have been enjoying the beach, and all dogs are banned from off leash fun. And shame on animal control for the very nasty way they are dealing with people.

YES!  The view is wonderful!

YES!  The little playground must be fun for kids.

YES!  It is a great picnic spot.

YES!  It is easy to get down from the cliff to the shore for a little walk by the beach.

YES!  It is a dog friendly place.  YES!  It has little poop bags available.  However, if the owners of the dogs don't use them, what good does it do the other park users?

This would get 5 stars except for the inconsiderate dog owners.

Had a lovely time walking along the beach, until I had to spend more time looking at where I put my feet than at the view.  It wasn't just one pile of crap, so it just wasn't just one inconsiderate animal owner.  And then, oh, s**t.  What's that SMELL?!?  Oh, yes, dog poo.  Thank you so much to all you jerks who don't pick up after your dogs.  You ruined a lovely trip to the beach.  It took me twenty minutes of cleaning crap out of my running shoes before I could get back in the car.  

Pick up your dog s**t!  And don't tie it up in the little bags and leave the bags, either!

Great dog beach!! Although, last evening, February 24, the beach SHOULD have been CLOSED due to OIL coming on shore. By the time we recognized how bad it was, it was too late. Our two Chessies were slick with oil. Took a big bottle of Dawn to scrub them down.

Hideaway Heaven. Lookout park is a nook that every person wanting to visit the beach yet avoid the crowds will appreciate. The upper park area has a fun playground and grassy area for picnics, parties, and play-days alike. The lower beach area is quiet and uncrowded making for a great romantic walk, beautiful jog, or Frisbee session with your K-9 friend.

Folks will find the park busier on the weekends and that the park closes right at sundown. Also the path leading down to the beach is a little steep which makes it less accessible for some. Other than that, Lookout park is a warm, cozy, slice-of-paradise hideaway with fun for families, friends, and dogs.

This place was perfect when I came with my pup. It was however noon on a Tuesday so there were only a handful of people on the beach.

Sign says dogs on leash but everyone (including me) let our k9's run along the sand :D 

Beach is hidden right off highway 101, always a good stop if your on your way to Santa Barbara.

The beach is very nice, although if you are barefoot expect to get tar on your feet. My dog had a blast playing in the small waves. Dogs can go off-leash at the beach just by the park and at a different no-name public beach down the road past the entrance to this park which had even fewer rocks on the beach, where you have to park on the side of the street and just walk down to the beach. Beach showers were off, with a sign saying it was due to the drought. Dog wash also not working, at least when I went. But the sand was beautiful, it was very peaceful, the dog had a blast, and we all enjoyed watching her.

This was a beautiful dog beach.  Very easy to get to from the freeway.  We were able to get there easily and let our dog run and play.  The entrance to the beach is a little park but has a paved ramp leading to the beach.  At the top of the ramp there is a doggie bag dispenser and also a jar of doggie treats!!!  We were very impressed by that!!!  The beach was clean and the people were very friendly.  It only took us 10 minutes from the beach to El Paseo in Santa Barbara.

This is our favorite park to go to on our way from our home in San Luis Obispo to Southern California, or as a midway point to meet friends and family who don't want to come all the way up to SLO.  

We like that it's small, great views of the Pacific, and that there's always parking.  Dogs are welcomed, and it seems that many Santa Barbara folks come here with their best friends. The playground was revamped a few years back... great for little ones, up to about 10 years old. I'm WAY past childhood, but I like swinging on the swings to stretch my legs before getting back in the car for the rest of our SoCal road trip. 

Easy downhill walk to the beautiful beach below.  BBQ pits near most tables.

Caution:  If you're going on a weekend, get to the park no later than 11 a.m. to grab a table.  

Only downside is that the restrooms can use some revamping.  No paper  towels and the cement floor always seems wet.

This review is mainly for the park/view. 

On the way home from Paso Robles to LA, we exited the 101 fwy to enjoy the beautiful view. We crossed our fingers that we would be able to find some street parking. To our surprise, there was a perfect little park with a small parking lot. The park had some BBQ grills, a small playground and a pathway to the beach down below. This is a great spot for pictures, with the beach/waves, and distant cliffs in the background. 

Definitely bookmark this park and schedule a stop if you're passing through.

This is an attractive park just off highway 101, easy to find in Summerland and with easy parking.  My husband and I stopped here for a short break the other day, and to enjoy the view over the ocean.  There is beach access too but we just hung out at the top of the overlook.  The lawns are well maintained and I was pleasantly surprised to see they have a self serve dog wash station!  It was in great shape and appeared fully functional as someone had recently used it and there was still a short time left on the meter when I checked it out.   It's a really nice spot for a family picnick and that's what many people were doing when we were there.  Great place to stop and stretch your legs if you're on a long car trip or place to come and relax if you don't mind being around other people.  The park isn't really big enough to get away from it all and get away from every body, but it's peaceful for what it is.

gorgeous view and animal friendly, how can you go wrong. Summerland is such a beautiful place the beach is not only clean but is very unpopulated. You feel like a king on your own private beach. love it!

This park is absolutely adorable! I wish I could spend every afternoon here! There's a few benches & plenty of grass to have a pic-nic, & there is also a path that leads down to the beach. Just be sure to be out of the park by closing hours, usually right after sun down.
Great beach, just watch for tar on your feet going in and out of the car, but that goes for all of Santa Barbara.

This is one of my favorite beaches in the Santa Barbara area. It's not crowded and is dog friendly. There is a dog wash machine, park with grass and playground, and it is a great beach that isn't overrun with tourists. 

My dog loved it. Vast sandy beach with seagulls. 

Be aware that there is a steep ramp you have to walk down (and back up). Normally that wouldn't be a problem but I had to carry my dog down and there isn't a smooth transition from the ramp to the sand. 

Also there is a sign warning of oil in the beach. I took my shoes off while walking and some oil got on my feet but nothing I couldn't scrub off. However, I'd advise wearing shoes. 

Nearby via car there is The Summerland Beach Cafe which is dog friendly.

Stopped while travelling in the RV for a rest and to make lunch. Great location, just off the freeway, easy on/off access. Park was very nice, clean, great views and easy walk to the sand and water. Will plan to stop here again when travelling though.

There is calmness at this area and the park. I enjoy trekking it down to the beach at low tide refit a great walk toward Padaro lane. It is a little oily, thus I haven't been in the water here. Yet, definitely a nice place to chill out and think.

Stanky. Beach reeks of petroleum! Beware the brown nasty water and getting tar foot while walking in the sand. Not suitable for swimming, IMO. Only reason I am giving 2 stars is because the bluff view near the parking area is kind of nice. However, the oil rigs in the distance really spoil the view of the ocean for me.

Today we went with the family and while I was excited to get there my excitement quickly turned to dissapointment. I'll start with the park. 
The park area was nice. It had grills and benches and a nice play area for the kids. You have to get there early to get a spot with a grill and parking. The park and beach is also open to dogs. The bad part of the park is that shade is limited so you need to come early or bring your own shade and chairs.
Now for the beach...
From a distance the beach looks nice and clean. However when u get close you can see that's not the case. From the top at the park you can see the brown water splashing on the shore. The shore is full of rocks so bring water shoes. From what we heard those rocks  haven't always been there so those who have been here in the past may not have had to dealt with them. When your up close not only can you see the oil but you can smell it too. The water stinks of petroleum which I'm sure is why you won't see too many people swimming in the water. The oil will stick to your skin and you'll come out of the water with oil blotches on your skin and clothes. As soon as I saw the oil blotches on my feet I immediately went to go wash my feet and stayed away.
Only reason why I'm giving this 2 stars is because the park was ok and we found somewhat close parking up the street. They shouldn't be allowing people near that water. The beach was closed a few weeks ago because it had more oil in the water than it did today and the smell of petroleum was much stronger. The beach should be closed down until it is completely cleaned up. It's a safety hazard to humans and animals. The oil rigs in the distance don't exactly make for a great photo worthy moment either.

On of the nicest beaches we frequent. Because of the point and wind direction, u can pretty much come here any time of the year and have a great beach day. The facilities up top are great for the kids with a nice little park. Years ago there use to be lots of nude beach goers down aways, but recent crack downs u don't see it much. 
Parking can be a bit of a pain at times..but usually fairly easy to get in and out. 
Surf fishing here can be decent in the area, using the Berkeley gulp stuff works, but the beach is covered with sand crabs and pretty easy to dig a few hundred out..

There are lots of dolphins today. 

This is a great park for family and all.  Beautiful view of the ocean and part of the coast, dog friendly with a  dog wash station. Park has bathrooms, volleyball net, picnic benches and BBQ grills. 

Best part is how close is it to the local shops and restaurants. Sweet spot.

Perhaps I'm a bit biased, as my boyfriend proposed to me here, as his favorite beach, at sunset.  The views are beautiful.  The park is well maintained and even has bathrooms!  Parking lot is fairly large, but I could see it getting full on a nice day.
Such a. Lovely stretch of beach! Dog friendly, kids playground and bbq grills for a party.

Fantastic little park right off the 101. Clean restrooms and nice tables for lunch. The park was very clean and had lots of green grass to play with the kids. The playground was nice for kids and we enjoyed the easy access to the beach. Sunset was breathtaking from the park. Highly recommend stopping by this park on your way through Summerland CA.
Excellent park with easy access to the beach. Somewhat isolated. Nice and quiet. They also had a self dog wash ($10) which is something I've never seen before.

Dog friendly!

The address on yelp is not entirely accurate - this is a better address:
15 Lookout Park Rd
Summerland, CA

Parking is free in a lot, came on a Sunday morning with plenty of spots, but not sure how it fills up later on in the day. 

The park itself is pretty average, green grass, small climbing playthings for kids, a dog wash (which is unusual but awesome). It's the beach along here that really shines. (With oil though, probably) 

There are warnings not to swim in the water and to watch for oil, so be cautious, for this reason and due to the fact that my dog is a greyhound, I did not let her off leash. 

However, we still had a good time meeting other people, dogs, and horses! 

I definitely recommend visiting if you're coming from out of town, and want a fun place to bring your dog.

Great little resting spot next to the 101 on the way up north. Has a dog park in the front and has beach access. It even has a doggy shower area in the back in case your pooch needs to rinse off- although you do have to pay for the self service. Worth the detour if you have 20 minutes.. or a few hours to spare.

This is a great place to bring your dog. If your traveling the King's Highway its a must stop to stretch the legs and tire out the pooch before you travel on. 

There is a doggy wash station in good working order that solves the sand all over the car issue. When you pull in find the ranger at the south end of the paring lot to buy a couple of dollars worth of tokens so you can wash the dog on the way out. 

This beach is a fairly long stretch of sand so bad dogs who run off out of your control should not be taken off leash here.  

We ran up from Santa Clarita on more than a few Saturdays this last  summer and stopped here on the way to the Brewhouse in Santa Barbara (A dog friendly micro brewer / restaurant).  Just the thing for a great day.

Tar patties AND Tar balls alert!!!!

It was a beautiful Saturday and what better way to spend it than going to a beautiful beach in Santa Barbara.  We came upon Summerland and figured LookOut Park would be the perfect medium.  The park was small but had a host of parties in progress.  There were benches that looked out onto the beach, some facing the ocean, 1 facing a tree. (Please refer to picture)   There is a playground for kids to monkey around in and a small restroom.  After taking a quick look around the park we headed down a ramp to the beach.  

The beach itself was pretty sad looking.  Sea debris everywhere, to be expected, but there were also brown foam that washed to shore.  Well no matter, we weren't divas and were used to a not so clean looking beach.  After a short walk, I walked on the beach w flip flops on and suddenly my feet felt sticky.  I looked down to see a wad of black goo on my feet.  Alarmed that I'd stepped on poo, I immediately rushed into the water to wash the excrement off.  However, no matter how hard I tried, it wouldn't come off.  At this point I figured the substance had to be something else.  I joked w lover that it was an oil spill since there were so many oil rigs in the ocean and I was lucky enough to step in it.  A couple of steps later, I saw the same black substance on lover's foot.  EEK!   We rushed to the bathroom to get cleaned up and was about to call the city to let them know when we saw a bulletin.  

"Unique to Santa Barbara counties, we have a Naturally (Bold & Italicized) occurring oil seepage.  You may run into tar patties or tar balls that sticks to your feet".   I'm sure people from around the world flock to Santa Barbara for their naturally (Bold & Italicized) occurring tar balls.

Seriously, crude oil cannot be healthy for you and there were babies, adults, kids, and dogs all frolicking on the beach like their swimming in gold.  Gross!

Never again! Like gag me with a spoon.

We walked this beach every day. Each day was a new discovery of views, rocks collected and sea glass found!

The best part was the daily meeting of new folk and their pups. This is a dog friendly beach and we met and made a lot of new friends!

For the view, sound and solitude of a great Pacific Coast beach - we loved it!

Fantastic little park right off the 101. Clean restrooms and nice tables for lunch. The park was very clean and had lots of green grass to play with the kids. The playground was nice for kids and wenjoyed the easy access to the beach. Sunset was breathtaking from the park. Highly recommend stopping by this park on your way through Summerland CA.

The park is perfect for people who enjoy a small yet beautiful park near the ocean. 

This has some bbq pits that are free. There are two spots one could reserve. Among which the area A is quiet big and perfect for a large group. There is a small children's park beside this area A. 

The beach beside is quiet nice and small. The view is very good too. 

There is a volleyball court for adults.
Fun, quiet little beach. Many spring wildflowers down the wooded trail. Enjoyable for children, dog lovers& more.

This is one of my favorite places to stop and stretch my legs enroute from San Luis Obispo County to Los Angeles or Orange County. It makes a perfect rest stop, with easy on and off access to 101. It has good restrooms, a beach, picnic tables, and lovely scenery. It even has a place you can wash your dog. It's as convenient as a state rest stop, but offers a playground and beach access, not just restrooms and picnic tables. We found the parking adequate, but it's busier in the summertime. We've always found a place to park, but I believe only cars, not trailers or RVs are allowed.
Great place to bring the dogs. Nice beach area, friendly people and a convenient dog wash station.

great stop off on a road trip up or down the coast.  Very dog friendly !

Great views and perfect picnic spot in the park. Or head down to the beach...so quiet, and pretty! 

love it.

Ughh the oil smell is awful!!!! But beach and view are beautiful. Many dogs were off leash (mine too at times) which was nice and no one seemed to mind. Awesome pet wash as well! Easy parking and just a short walk down the path.

Went to the beach today with a group of friends and all I can say is....stay away!! Sure it's picturesque, but you'll find it quite empty most days for a reason....the whole area reeks of petroleum!! My friend went for a swim and quickly came out after she noticed oil in the water and all over her skin. We promptly picked up our things because I did not feel comfortable with my son playing in that sand.

Somehow we all missed the very small warning sign at the bottom of the ramp that stated the air/water/sand poses "short term" health risks....whatever that means. This beach should really be closed to the public. 

I don't want to know what all the people rating this place 5 stars are smoking....especially the ones who bring dish washing soap just "in case" their dogs get covered in oil....hmmm.

But don't take my word for it....google it and see for yourself.

WOW what a change!!! This was always a great stop between Carpinteria and Santa Barbara.  We pick up coffee at Red Kettle Coffee shop and head to the park to enjoy the gorgeous view of the Channel Islands.  

Now, a beautiful renovation job that includes barbecues, leisure sitting, bocce ball court and the best of all, ping pong tables makes this a full afternoon stop (we keep a set of paddles and balls in the car for these very opportunities). Bring the dog,  it also has a do-it-your-self dog wash. Bring the kids, it has a terrific playground too.

Intolerable and continuous smell  of oil refinery, complete with warning signs about oil in water and on beach and dead bird sightings....make this a horrible park for kids and adults alike. Drove 40 minutes out of the way to get here, schlepped all the stuff from the car and arrived to this beach nightmare. Had to drag upset kid all the way back to car. What have they done to this beach?

I would give this TEN stars if I could! What a wonderful community park! We were visiting from the bay area and took our dog here. She had the most fun I've seen in a long time! The weather was perfect, beach was clean, and everyone we ran into was so friendly. Lots of dogs out enjoying the nice weather! 

I love all the grassy areas this park has too, nice to be able to sit and enjoy the beach view. There's some great food and coffee options nearby as well, so you can grab food before or after, or bring some to the park and have a picnic! 

But by far the best amenity here is the dog wash station. $10 for 10 minutes is actually a pretty good deal (it's $15 at Pet Food Express). Our dog was a sandy mess after the beach, and it was super convenient to be able to give her a rinse off before getting back to the car. Plus, the proceeds go to community and park upkeep. I cannot wait to come back to enjoy this park and beach; highly recommend!!
Cool little park to have a picnic, play some volleyball, bring the dog to get some exercise or to just relax.

What an incredible spot to come with family, friends, a date, your dog, or even alone and lookout at the breathtaking ocean beauty! This park is right off the freeway and generally has plenty of parking for all. I'd even recommend stopping here on your way up the coast for a nice stretching of the legs and to get some gorgeous shots of the view.

I have been coming here for years to BBQ (yes they have BBQ areas open to the public), to walk my dog, relax on the beach, and so on. I love the new additions they've added in the past few years, such as the dog bath and new playground equipment. The city really makes an effort to upkeep the beauty and safety of the park. In the Spring the views are even better because of the stunning California native plants and wildflowers. 

Couples, this IS your cheap date spot! Arrange a picnic, go for a walk on the beach, enjoy the sunset, then walk a couple minutes up the street to the Summerland bars for a nice drink and live entertainment. More of a fly solo kind of person? That's cool too! Bring your dog here, come watch other people's dogs here, or go up to Red Kettle Coffee grab a latte and come back to sit on a bench and enjoy the views as the caffeine kicks you into gear. 

The moral of this post is - There is no reason not to visit Lookout Park! Young, old, single, married, or four legged there is something for everyone and you will not be disappointed!

The county parks did a great job on the new east end of the park. Ping Pong tables, Bocce ball, fresh new bbqs and beautiful, native plants. Nice stop along the 101 with a great view of the Pacific ocean.

NOT SANCTIONED TO BE OFF-LEASH.  Bring your leash.  Use discretion!  Be responsible!!  Or please don't come at all!!!

This is a gorgeous place to share for people, dogs & horses alike.  What a gift & blessing to have this slice of paradise between the Pacific & PCH.  

Footnote:  PICK UP AFTER YOUR DOGS ;)

We went here because this showed up on a Yelp search under dog parks.     However, all dogs must be on leashes, and the park is tiny and unfenced.        

When I think dogpark, I think leashless.    Hence my 1star review.

The hike to Montecito Peak is one of the loveliest Santa Barbara front country hikes you can do--it takes you through gorgeous creekside scenery and then follows switchbacks through shady mountainside to rocky canyonous terrain to loose shale. The topography changes so much in as little as a few hundred feet and it is just a fascinating walk through Santa Barbara's geological history.

Peak Stats
3200 vertical feet with views of coastline from Goleta to Port Hueneme

Distance
7 miles roundtrip (takes about 3 hours)

Notes
 - The final ascent to the peak (about 500 feet) is literally straight up and in very loose shale. You will need to use your hands to climb and keep balance. 

 - At the top, there's a box hidden near some boulders that contains log books to record your summit. They're fun to read too as they date back some 10 years!

 - Because of the terrain, I recommend wearing proper trail shoes. The hike could be a disaster in sneakers--between the creek crossings and the slippery shale, some solid traction will really make a difference on the hike :-)

 - Keep your eyes peeled for poison ivy/oak (which I got on the hike), snakes, bobcats and mountain lions. There have been a number of bobcat sightings and there's fresh scat all over the trail--it's creepy!

Directions
Start at Cold Spring trail head on East Mountain Drive in Montecito. Park just where the creek crosses the road. There's a trailhead there that's easy to spot because there are a couple of trash bins there. At about 1/10th of a mile there's a huge boulder which is fun to climb. Continue down the main trail. You will cross a creek twice. After the second crossing, make a SHARP RIGHT. Heading to the left takes you onto a very steep trail that eventually gets more and more primitive and requires a machete to get though. To the right, follow switch backs up the mountain. At 1.5 miles you will come to a service road. Go left on the road and then right up a small, steep trail. Follow this trail to the peak.

Great hike and was around 7.4 miles round trip. It was a slow incline except for the final 400 ft elevation gain over 700 ft in distance. It was pretty steep and plenty of loose gravel. Highly recommend hiking poles if you have any, especially when descending. (It was almost like rock surfing on the way down). Views were great, but there were black flies at the top, but totally worth it! Unfortunately there were a lot of clouds covering the ocean but still amazing views.

Otherwise, overall beautiful hike throughout, limited shade so wear sunblock and bring plenty of water. (Highly recommend downloading all trails app to follow the map)

This is one of the most beautiful and challenging hikes I have enjoyed ! Starts out nice and easy and then gradually gets a little more difficult with each mile. The terrain changes often and there is NO time to lose focus as there are rocks and critters that you have to look out for.

The last portion as mentioned is STEEP and loose - be careful it is short and absolutely worth the views just dont go flying down the side on your butt.
Walking back done - you realize how far you went up because it is the mother of all downhills that just goes on and on...

Great workout, gorgeous views, perfect solitude - no one around other than myself and friend !

Although online directions said Mountain Drive was open, it remained closed due to the mud slides. Literally gone, a river runs through the 10-foot deep ditch that used to be the road. Not only is there no bridge for the cars, hikers can't cross if the waters are too high. Drive to the trailhead from the Hot Spring Rd/Olive Mill Rd side to avoid the water. I tried to have Yelp place their map marker at the correct location near 895 E Mountain Dr, but they refused.

Start the hike on the immediate switchbacks of the Cold Springs Trail. The 6.5-mile roundtrip hike passes through grassy woodlands with shade, across a Mediterranean environment, and eventually to cactus fields. The higher up the mountain, the rockier the terrain. Oddly, the 2450-foot ascent didn't create breezes or lower temperatures.

Few signs and multiple trail splits made having a map or GPS highly helpful. With patches of black char from the Thomas fire, the lone tree near Montecito Peak that survived the blaze is a good landmark to follow.

The steepest part was at the very top with fine gravel that could slide. It was not rock scrambling but the trail disappears in spots. The peak is mostly flat and had a pure 360-degree view encompassing Oxnard, the Channel Islands, and Gaviota.

(I am more than confused with what trail(s) are actually located here, and need to state that sadly I hadn't hiked here pre-fire and flood.)

My first trip I was trying to locate Tangerine Falls, which may or may not be located on the other side of the washed out area of E. Mountain Drive. So once I turned my car around and was heading back to Olive Mill/Hot Springs Road I found a very small place to park.  (NOTE:  If that small area is full, you can park on Riven Rock Drive.)

I began the trail, again fully thinking I was heading for Tangerine Falls.  (But even today, as I check Google Maps, they call this: HOT SPRINGS CANYON TRAIL HEAD.) - so doesn't that constitute a name change? 

As a very beginner hiker the trail starts fast.  I had boots and walking poles, which helped my footing immensely,

Once I realized I had no idea where I was headed to I asked a few people for help who were on the trail, too.  They informed me that now (post fire and flood) the way to access Tangerine Falls is via Gibraltar Road.

Lots of dark green trees, lots of black burned trees, too.  There is a place where you cross the road and then continue on the trail.  After about 1/2 mile, I believe, there are 2 signs that gave direction to HOT SPRINGS trail, and I think there may have been a bridge (since washed out) where you could cross the creek to hike another trails?  SEE PHOTO - Not too sure about this though, only inferring from the signs and what *looked like where a bridge may have been.  I wasn't confident crossing over fallen trees and logs to cross the creek to literally "see what was on the other side".  I did hike just a bit past this sign - and the trail is very narrow and drops off steep to the creek.  Since I am a big newbie I turned around from there ASAP, but quite a few people flew right past me.  Again, this review is from a newbie hiker.

I'm in love with this place regardless and am determined to figure out what exactly is up there still and what trails a hiker has access to.  (Cold? Hot? Tangerine? San Ysidro? Saddle Rock? What else?) Since I'm not a local I am putting together piece by piece on my own as I can. Apparently there are hotel ruins up higher as well?

Expect more pictures ASAP!  And bring water, good shoes, and maybe a trekking pole if you're a newbie like me.  :)

She came from out of town and said she wanted to do something fun...

The conversation shifted to the hot springs in this neck of the woods...

From the tone of my voice... 
She said I was unenthusiastic about it.
But then I thought.....

On our way to this place... 
The first thing that came off weird to me was parking..
Their isn't much parking 
This is about the only negative thing I have to say...

I'm not an avid hiker 
I really don't go hiking at all
In the middle of the day I was walking towards 
"The peak"
Out of all days that could be chosen
I found gold
The weather was superb...
Of course it had it's weird breaks (heat and sun) 
For the most part it was great
And if you want a great experience 
Choose a day featuring great weather..

The hike up had lots of elevated climb to it
I loved it...
Their was lots to look at 
I didn't encounter any strange animals/ indigenous people or what have you
I did see a bright blue dragon fly that seemed to follow me
Wherever I went...

The people along the hike we're friendly and nice 
We all seemed to share the same thoughts 
"Lost and confused "

Not sure what hikes are to entail 
But this one would definitely called for strength, calories and hydration...
You will need all that..

There was off beaten paths that led to points of scratching my head..
And in those moments I just turned around and went the way I came...

While all this was happening 
I was reminded how far we have come along as humans 
How tough things were for our ancestors
Just how appreciative I am for the life I have
Closed from the floods as of May 2018, drove to the trailhead and woman was yelling from across the rubble rocky roadway that we'd be fined if we stayed on the hike... bummer  :(

This trail is sporadically open, if you live in Montecito: it's a hop and skip away to double check.  It has rock slide reinforcements on the trail and the road driving over.  It's lovely really, a deep ravine with a creek running through. Minus the Brad Pitt.  There are a handful of switchbacks and the elevation begins to increase, so being a moderately skilled hiker helps.  Finally, their is a fork in the paths: one leads to the waterfalls, the later to a cellphone tower on a mountaintop.
My favorite hiking trail on the Central Coast. It's not too difficult for even a beginner as long as it's not over 90 degrees. Enjoy!

What a rewarding hike. The hike itself follows the Cold Spring Trail for the first part before making a left across the stream right before a bench. Later it gets more difficult towards the end where you have to climb some steep points towards the end, but the end that has a 200 foot waterfall and a Montecito view make up for it. 

To get there, Google Map this address for the trailhead: 824 E Mountain Dr., Montecito, CA 93108

Parking is on the street. Walk up the Cold Spring Trailhead. Continue until you reach the bench then cross the creek. Walk until you see the Tangerine Falls sign and make a right. 

The trail as of January 2017 hasn't been fully maintained since the last rainstorm, so you will have to climb over and under some fallen trees. The last ¼ mile can get pretty steep with all the rocks, but nothing that leaves you hanging on a wall. The falls itself has a few outcrops you can sit on and it's great after a rain. 

The trail is shady for the entire way, which is great for people who don't like the sun. As for difficulty I'd say as long as you can walk and climb waist high rocks, you should be good. 
The hike takes around an hour to get to the waterfall, 2 hours for a round trip.

Given the constant lack of flow, the name is not really Tangerine Falls but Tangerine Wet Rocks. When the sun hits the 100' falls with two tiers, the wet rocks turn into a pretty orange glow. The rest of the hike is basic and often boring with only sporadic and narrow ocean views out of the canyon.

Start from either side of Cold Spring Creek at the washed-out section of Mountain Drive and head upriver. The creek splits as does the trail. Signs clearly mark the East and West Forks. Cross the river to the West Fork.

At 0.55 miles, the creek and the trail split again. The sign is missing but a fresh-looking 4x4 post remains. The left trail continues along a dry creek that is well maintained, popular with mountain bikers, and on most maps but doesn't reach the falls. Go right instead.

The trail descends a few feet and quickly splits again. The left trail loops above the falls and is on some maps. This trail is steep, overgrown, and an overlook of the falls at 1.4 miles from the trailhead (677' to 1714' elevation change). The right trail is on few maps. It is not a constructed trail but a river-bed rock scramble to the base of the falls.

name: East Beach
categories: Parks, Active Life, Swimming Pools, Beaches

yelp_reviews.text: something for everyone at this beach. Very clean especially for an urban beach. Beautiful views and lots of people, but it is so spread out you don't feel crowded. Very pedestrian friendly with biking paths and walking paths. Love all the trees as well!
We always come here with our cat at night, dressed like hobos and without another soul all around us.
lovely beach, lots of things to do here. Beach Volleyball, bike, walk, skate, enjoy the pier, people watch and enjoy the ocean air. I love how this beach always has something exciting going on!

Some great eye candy is on this beach Joan states in her review. 
Well, thanks for the compliment.
I watch what I eat.
I run even when not trespassing.
And I enjoy a good multi-vitamin now and again.
Ok: The beach. It's swell. The sand is great for feet, and the mind.

I made a solitary, single-lady trip for myself to Santa Barbara, and I really loved coming there. Before I left SB, I knew I had to stop by the beach and lay in the sun. Unlike the beaches in San Diego (and don't get me wrong; I LOVE San Diego), it's not crowded here, even on a Saturday. Not only was it super easy to get to (no crazy traffic I had to fight), parking was very easy to find and I could lay my beach blanket and watch the waves and people. 

I only stayed here for a handful of hours, and by the time I had to leave, I felt really sad. I'm hoping that I can make this an annual trip, maybe this time with a couple other people. :)
Stunning sunsets, palm tree-lined paths, a bustling beach...Nice place to just put down a blanket and take a nap.

I bike to this beach all the time. Beach Volleyball is popular here and fun to watch. I love this beach because it isn't super crowded and has plenty of room. There is also a great breakfast spot/lunch spot right next to the parking lot.

If ur one of those people like me, who don't go out to the water or play volleyball on the sand, u can rent a bike (they even have four person bikes!) or a segway to enjoy the beach view while working off some of those calories from eating/drinking on State Street!

It was nice and peaceful here, though you should be more careful in the evenings. It's nice to walk on the sand. It could get a bit dirty (saw lots of flies on some seaweed....) but there are some nice parts to it. Very close to local inns/hotels.

This beach was conveniently located just across the street from our hotel, the Hyatt.  My son found a few broken shells everywhere, and my husband managed to find a tiny sand dollar that was in tact!  

The water was nice, but the beach was a bit dirty.  There was seaweed and litter.  The worst part was one foot of mine was covered in black spots that stuck like crazy glue. It seemed to be like tar bits, but I really have no idea what it was.  My husband and son went in the water more and had "clean feet", and only one of mine got it.  Another couple at the Hyatt said the same thing happened to one of them.  So the problem does not seem to be huge, but it certainly will be a reason to head to another beach.
east beach tacos very tasty food, loved it. didn't go to the batting cages. found it on yeĺp!

This beach has sand volleyball and free, if you hunt, parking.

It's dirty compared to beaches down in Ventura.   The pacific coast highway is lovely, but if a beach is your destination this isn't worth the drive.  


Lots of seaweed.

And noise from the zoo. B

Great location as a Santa Barbara tourist.  Located right at Stearns Wharf, where you can find public restrooms, restaurants, Santa Barbara water taxi, and the Sea Center.  The land and sea shark tour starting point is close by, and even a skate park if you or your kiddos have an interest.  Closest parking lot to the beach requires payment, but if you're lucky, you could find street parking for free.

East beach, itself, is nice enough.  There is washed up seaweed that attracts flies, which is annoying when sunbathing. Plenty of sand to share when large tour groups come to visit.

Beautiful setting and not crowded.  Lots of open beach for walks.  Downside is the homeless have somewhat taken over so it's quite intimidating.  The restaurant there was closed and several homeless men have set up mattresses and appear to be living there.  I will avoid this beach in the future.

This beach is great. It's such a great change from LA County beaches. The beach is very clean and the long strech of sand allows for an uncrowded area. Arrive early to find tons of parking. The water is also very relaxing here. You can catch a good wave here and there, but mostly it is a nice spot to just dip and relax. The drive to Santa Barbara is beautiful, so the hour and change that you spend on the road is very pleasent coming from the San Fernando Valley. I've been to many beaches and this is definately one of the best in the west coast.

In a town filled with beaches, only Butterfly Beach and Hendry's top it at five stars. I've spend many days reading on the beach, taking a stroll with my dog on the the sidewalk. ( not sure if Actual beach is dog friendly). 

In spring and summer (and December 2013 when in the 80s) I've gone down and enjoyed listening to my music laying on my towel. It brings back memories of doing the same in my teen age years. 

Sometimes after visiting my relatives graves at the cemetery I come here to process my feelings in nature. 

Visit, you won't be disappointed.

An absolutely georgeous beach. Most notably for me, it was lined with great, tall palm trees. Coming from San Francisco (and living there right by Ocean Beach), this had a distinctly warm, clean, and "southern California" feel to it. That said, I'm not sure if this still counts as central Calfironia or what, but I just loved this beach. It was gloriously warm early in the morning, people were going for their morning jogs or walks, many with dogs - just a great place to be. There are a number of volleyball nets if you head a bit south towards the zoo. Sand was nice and clean where we were.

The waves weren't big when we were there, but seem good for boogeyboarding.

We stopped here specifically because it has a small playground right at the beach and  a place where we could sit and picnic while on our drive off the 101.  
It was another great find for our son to play.  Stopping every couple of hours on a road trip is a must with our 2-year old, so we scoped out some spots with the help of Yelpers, once again.  
We're glad we found this spot. There's a playground at the very end of East Beach that sits just in front of a covered area with benches (no tables).  There are 2 toddler swings and 2 regular swings. The ocean air has had its effect on this swing - because its starting look rusty and definitely squeaks loudly.  There is also a small play structure for kids to climb and slide down.  We couldn't beat the view. 
The outdoor showers are close by to wash off feet. We gave our son a sand bucket and shovel. He was happy with that until he found the water. There are clean bathrooms at the East Beach House - so it was good for a stop. 
Just an aside -there seemed to be a lot of riff-raff hanging around (made me want to keep an even closer eye out for my son).

Great for volleyball, families, bikers, friends, runners, kids, dogs, etc.
Definitely a nice place to take a nap or watch the sunset and even people.
Parking is free and not that difficult to find just remember to arrive early for best selection.
Sand is very warm, beach is beautiful and waves are mellow.
Palm trees are everywhere which makes it fee & very "Southern California" in the Central Coast & that to me hits the jackpot. :)

Spent a wonderful afternoon there. It was a little breezy, upper 70s to low 80s, not so crowded. The sand was clean err for an occasional tar ball. Waves were good enough for some boogie boarding. I'll be back.

As we were heading down to LA we decided to stop by Santa Barbara. I've never been here before so I was excited to see what it was like. There are so many beaches to choose from. I had read about East Beach, so that's where we were headed. As we were driving I was a bit concerned. It was so foggy that I thought that the beaches would be freezing cold, but nope once we got closer it was gorgeous. All you could see were people walking in the little town, the tall palm trees, & of course the beach. We parked at the pier for a bit. If you're looking for good scenery for your photos you need to come here, but be careful the pier is open. There are no fences to hold you back from falling in the water. But I have to say, I liked it that way. We didn't get to spend too much time here, so I will be heading back for sure.

From afar, this beach looks perfect. Golden sand, deep blue water, kids & adults alike frolicking in the waves. This beach begs you to kick off your shoes & feel the sand between your toes. But beware, there are also tar globs all over this beach and they are nearly impossible to scrape off your feet! 

I was quick to blame the ugly oil rigs off the coast for this unfortunate phenomenon, but found that these are actually naturally occurring tar balls. Apparently there are some pretty major oil seeps right off the coast of Santa Barbara. It is estimated that about 10,000 gallons of oil each day seep into the ocean in the SB area. Yuck! 

So enjoy this beach from afar, or wear some old shoes that can be left outside so tar isn't tracked into your house/apartment/hotel room. If you do go barefoot, be sure to have some nail polish & a scrubber handy.  You'll need it.

They say East Beach is one of the best beaches in California especially for vollyball.  Yes, there easy access.  Yes, it is in the quaint town of Santa B.  But on my particular vacation, we got sticky, black tar all over our feet walking on the beach (natural but still gross) and we were cold.  It could have been better.  
Tips: 1) Watch where you walk. 2) Check the weather and hope for 75 degrees and 3) Bring a vollyball.

Beach is located to the left of the pier. 

Pretty clean but in the morning I saw a lot of homeless people sleeping on the beach. 

Not dog friendly but it was empty so I played a little fetch with my pup.
Yet another victim of the black tar foot phenomenon.

Like any PCH beach, East Beach in Santa Barbara is no exception in terms of beauty except it's also cleaner than most! It'll be a shame not to at least see this beautiful stretch of sand across the Pacific Ocean coast when you are in Santa Barbara. Street parking may be hard to come by at festive times so you may need to park in the paid lot at the nearby Sterns Wharf, but it'll be worth it to at least stop by!

I LOVE the beach, who doesn't?
East beach in Santa Barbara is another great one to stop at.
Not too full, peaceful, serene, clear waters and soft sand.
Take a break from the every day stressful lifestyle and plump down, watch the waves, smell the air, better than therapy!

Found out I had half a day free in Santa Barbara and decided I wanted to spend it on the beach. This was one of the first beaches that came up when I googled for Santa Barbara beaches. It was a nice beach, nothing too special. It had good views of the water and the pier.

This beach was very clean and perfect for a Sunday morning walk.  It was walking distance from where I was staying and it was a great way to get some exercise.   Today, we were able to enjoy the local artists showing off beautiful paintings, crafts, jewelry and much more at Chase Palm Park.  This is a great location for families to come and enjoy the day on the beach, sightseeing, picnicking, and visiting the local businesses.   Pets are welcomed as long as they are on a leash.
great beach for the active person. Volleyball, sand and from time to time good body surfing. Great people watching (not as great as butterfly though) and by people I mean ladies.

Well you drive down the road by the beach and finally, the last area is east beach. The whole beach for miles here in Santa Barbara is wonderful! The sun, the sand, the beautiful people, the tans, the volleyball, and on and on... Calling it crowded or not, call parking hard or not depends on your personal place of origin, and your consciousness. There were plenty of places to park (from my point of view) and lots of people, but still lots of open space. You decide. Come on out and enjoy the sun, the ocean, the beach, and the people!

Not the most pristine of the beaches around Santa Barbara but it and West Beach are right in the heart of SB. East to reach and right by the Wharf and State street.  South side offers a great walk and lots of Volleyball to watch.
Clean, not crowded for a Saturday in June & a good place to relax, swim or play volleyball

Nice! Like any public beach, it has its share of creeps hanging out on benches in the shady park area at the edge, as well as barbecuers toting loud boom boxes and beer coolers. BUT, when you turn your back to that, you are reminded why you pay the high price of living in SB. Truly breathtaking, and a perfect location for volleyball or yoga.

What can I say? If I had a timecard and punched in for every hour I've spent on this beach, I'd have enough for a downpayment on a condo in my beloved town. The finest beach volleyball beach in all of California, which is the mecca of beach volleyball. The only one I've seen with lines, nets and antennas. Often referred to as "the Church of East Beach" since so many of us have spent their Sundays there. Back in the day, you could hear Karch [Kiraly] 's dad, Lazlo screaming down the beach, as he battled it out on a court with his friends. On Fridays, we played our regular games down the beach from Todd and Phil (2008 Olympic gold medal champions in beach volleyball). 

Free parking along the fence (for those who get there early) and a nearby parking lot ($2 for 3 hours). A short walk away is the the East Beach Grill, which serves up a great breakfast. Bathrooms are available in the same building (Cabrillo Bathhouse) as East Beach Grill.

Ah... East Beach. I miss you and will visit you soon!!!!! :)

Our favorite beach in Santa Barbara. There is plenty of parking and hotels with fantastic ocean views. It's an easy walk to the Sunday Art Walk as well as Stearns Wharf. We love hearing sea lions and the ocean when we go to sleep and wake up.

It was Old Spanish Days Fiesta and my friend Jason E. and I arrived about 2 hours after the noon time parade on Friday August 3. The main street State Street was blocked off so traffic couldn't flow in both directions on Cabrillo. It's okay though, we quickly found parking along East Beach on Cabrillo and everything was a-ok.

The beach is cool, good for families and groups of friends. There are pathways just for the bicycles you can rent close to the intersection of Cabrillo and State. We saw a lot of families with the multiple peddler bikes with canopies. You can also get single and tandem bikes as well.

The beach itself seemed pretty clean. Just watch out for the tanbark where the sidewalk and sand meets. There are lifeguard posts for those you with kids or the folks that have yet to perfect swimming.

It's right by the wharf so you can head up that way to see the view. There are local artists along the wharf entrance making sand creations. We saw a man who made and awesome mermaid in 6 hours. There are many public restrooms, but I didn't see any showers. A lot of families were having lunch on blankets/mats.

We ended up just doing a lot of walking around and unfortunately it began to sprinkle a bit. I'd definitely like to rent a bike next time I'm down here.

Until next time, happy relaxing!
I made it to Santa Barbara just in time to watch the sunset. Amazing. Santa Barbara hardly lets me down.

Pro: not crowed, clean for most part, good grill restaurant with good food so it's convenient, bathroom close by,lifeguards, parking is cheap and convenient 
Con: tar world! Bathrooms are not clean, showers are turned off, feels run down. St. Barbara you have plenty of $$ to put back in your  beaches. Embarrassing for tourists to see this to represent such a beautiful town.
It's the beach.  It's Santa Barbara.  If I win the lottery, I shall live right here.  It's really, really awesome.

We came to SB on Memorial Day to see the ocean and relax.  There is a parking lot close to Sterns Wharf that has paid to park (cash or card), it cost $4.00 for two hours for us.

Parking was really close to the sand so that would have been great if we went out to the sand, but we just walked along and then to the wharf.  

Nice enough place, bathrooms were open too so that would be good except for the liquid floor covering...ewwww.

This is one of my favorite beaches in Santa Barbara for many reasons! 

First of all this beach is pretty clean and there is tons of sand. Even when there is high tide in Santa Barbara, you are able to come to this beach because of the large amounts of sand. That being said there is always somewhere to sit since there is so much sand. I came to this beach on the Fourth Of July and there was still alot of places to sit because of the amount of sand it has. 

This beach has volleyball courts which is awesome! There are a ton of them too making it a fun place to come post it up for the day with some friends. There are always people here playing volleyball if you just want to join in on someone's game you might as well just ask them. There are also a few BBQ's and picnic tables if you are looking for somewhere to do that as well. They even have trash bins even by this area as well. 

Right now there is no bathrooms, but there are in the works of putting new ones in. There is also paid public parking nearby and tons of street free parking too. This beach is located near tons of the hotels on the beach so if you're looking for a beach near your hotel this could be the place. In the summertime they do stand up paddleboard rentals here and kayaks too. 

This beach is one of the best in SB because of the location, the cleanness, and the sand! I would highly recommend this beach to anyone.
One of the main beaches off of Cabrillo. Definitely a good spot. The beach has volleyball a sidewalk for riding bikes and walking and a nice grass area before you get to the beach

East Beach is, well, east of Stearns Wharf and offers beautiful, pristine sand and surroundings in a prime spot close to everything else on Santa Barbara. So I was pleasantly surprised to see that it was relatively quiet when I stopped by.
 
Not that I found this to be a problem - more to enjoy with nobody to disturb you. And there's lots for all to enjoy, whether the sand, plenty of room for volleyball, play areas for kids, picnic space for families, clean facilities inside a pavilion that doubles as an arts center (!), and even a long, narrow park that serves as a buffer between the street and the sand.

For some reason, Ledbetter Beach to the west was far more crowded when I stopped by. I'd say: go for East Beach instead, especially if you can find easy parking nearby.
Easy parking, wide beach front, many sand volleyball courts, great place for a walk, also have a playscape for little ones.

This is a great little in town beach. I typically street park and walk to it. It's a wonderful place to catch sunset and walk on the sidewalk path or on the sand. 

It's well maintained and clean. There are bathroom facilities. It's typically busy but quiet overall. This is one of many lovely beaches in Santa Barbara. Be sure to have a visit on your next beach day.

If you're a local, you know that East Beach is the beach for some great eye candy.  All the young volleyball players head out here for beach volleyball, and usually their girlfriends come along to watch and/or play.  There are probably about 10-12 courts and nets set up for everyone, and it's on a first come basis.  And yes, it does count if there is a ball left on the court.  It's saved so don't think about trying to steal it.  

Parking is somewhat of a major issue.  But you can park for free along Shoreline Drive, or on some of the cross streets.  A parking lot is located kind of close for a small fee.  I'm not sure how much it is these days, but paying for parking here is based on the Honor system, so bring cash in small bills.  

The beach itself is really clean and open with plenty of room to spread out.  The sand is way soft and makes the perfect bed for laying out for a tan.  If it gets too hot for you, you can always walk up towards the grassy area and lay under the palm trees.  But with all the athletes around, don't expect it to be quiet or to have much privacy here.  

If I had to choose my favorite beach of all the beaches Santa Barbara has to offer, hands down, no questions asked,.. it would be East Beach.  Okay, so let's review.  If you're heading to East Beach, be prepared for the parking situation, bring lots of water and snacks because there aren't really any cafes close by, and don't forget over sized sunglasses so you can people watch without looking like a creep.  Let the summertime fun commence! :)

An der Waterfront in Santa Barbara hat man die Auswahl zwischen dem East Beach und dem West Beach, die durch Stearns Wharf getrennt werden. Da wir in der Nähe der Tourist Information einen preiswerten Parkplatz gefunden haben, sind wir zuerst am East Beach entlang gegangen.

Santa Barbara wird vom East Beach durch den Cabrillo Boulevard getrennt und davor verläuft noch ein wunderbar angelegter Fußgänger- und Radweg. Am Ende des Strandes kann man das Andree Clark Bird Refuge besichtigen oder das Volleyballfeld am Strand nutzen. 

Kurz danach gibt es einige Parkplätze direkt am Strand und das Cabrillo Pavilion Bathhouse, wo man sich nach dem Strandgang noch duschen könnte. Im weiteren Verlauf trifft man auf den Chase Palm Park, der auf der Landseite mit Brunnen, Bächen, einer Lagune, einem Karussell gestaltet ist. Ein sehenswerter Spielplatz ist nach einem Schiff aus dem 19. Jahrhundert entworfen wurde. Auf einer großen Rasenfläche finden häufig Konzerte im Sommer statt.

Kurz vor dem Erreichen des Pier hat man auf der rechten Seite den "Chase Palm Park, Skater's Point", wo man den Skateboardern bei ihren Kunststücken zuschauen kann. Auf Stearns Wharf hat man einen einzigartigen Blick auf den Strand, das Meer und die sehenswerte Umgebung. Hier kann man auch die ersten 90 Minuten frei parken, falls man ein freies Plätzchen erhaschen kann.

Der Strand machte auf uns einen sehr gepflegten und ordentlichen Eindruck und war nicht sonderlich überlaufen. Allerdings sollte man sich gut eincremen und einen Hut tragen, da durch den kühlen Wind die intensive Sonneneinstrahlung nicht sofort bemerkt wird. Außerdem sollte man genügend Wasser bei sich haben.

Ein wirklich schöner und abwechslungsreicher Strand.

If you're looking for a super clean and quiet beach, this is it!! Definitely one of my favorites. We stopped by here after wine tasting because it was a short walking distance. I was really surprised to see it so empty and clean which made for great pictures. Was awesome that we had a whole section of the beach just to ourselves. A lot of birds were chilling on the sand. Killer sunset and so peaceful~
My 600th Check In . Visiting beautiful Santa Barbara with our grandchildren.  This playground is right on the beach and very clean.  Parking is only $2.00 per hour. Perfect place to enjoy with kids
We hung out at this beach in April since we were staying at the Hilton Resort across the street. It was open during shelter in place but not crowded at all. Relaxing & clean.
My girls and I enjoyed relaxing at East Beach. It was clean and peaceful. There are several hotels and restaurants nearby.

There are so many incredible traits about this beach I don't even know where to start.. 

There's tons of public parking (free and paid options), its right next to the zoo so tourists you can park and walk to both, there's a cafe if you get starving from playing at one of the many beach volleyball courts, they have restrooms if the ocean isn't your port-a-potty of choice, and during Spring/Summer weekends there's SUP & kayak rentals right there on the beach. 

The beach itself is very clean and well up kept. There's vast amounts of sand space so no matter how busy, you and your friends will always find somewhere to set up camp. In the Spring and early summer the flowers are all blooming and the plants are photogenically beautiful. 

If you're looking for a place to start your long walk on the beach or single run along the water, east beach is your babe as you can go in either direction (on low tide) for quite some distance.There's a little market not too far walking distance, a Habit around the corner, and Chase Palm Park is close which is great for dogs to run around.

I, personally, love going for a run after work from East Beach Cafe area to the pier and back. I see a lot of cool dogs, love looking up at the palms, and enjoy the perfect ocean breeze cooling me off as I go. Wish I was there now!

After chowing down hardcore on some McConnell's and Rori's ice cream BOTH (yea...we lost our minds in Santa Barbara going hamm on ice cream), we needed to take a loooooooooong walk.  Why not East Beach?  


We walked out of our cars, straight onto the beach.



-There was no obnoxious crowd.

-No stupid parking fee.

-No annoying bike path with wanna be actors and supermodels

-No chicks twirling on poles/wanna be pole dancers at the beach begging for the attention like, "Look at meeeeeeee!"


None of that.  Just birds, sand, water, sky.  
(as it should be)


Gorgeous!



This place is the anti-Los Angeles.

East Beach is one of the best in Santa Barbara and not as touristy. Parking is somewhat limited but depending on the time of day you should be ok. People are always playing beach volleyball here so there's that as well.

The picnic tables are chill but watch out for strange people.

We wanted to spend the day at the beach; we were traveling through California on our Honeymoon. We chose this part of the beach because our hotel lady recommended it. It's close to the bathrooms, public baths (when California has the water turned on) and a snack bar.

The sand was fine and clean; the view was spectacular!

A great beach in Santa Barbara that's very well maintained and always exceptionally clean. Loads of beach volleyball courts that often hosts tournaments. Access to use the courts is fairly easy on all but the most popular sunny weekends when beach goers flock to the sand and surf. Speaking of surf they water here is very approving you want I get your feet wet or go for a swim. While it may seem odd to talk about the sand those who frequent the beach will understand when I say this stretch of beach has sand that's very kind to bare feet with its smooth texture and the beach itself is comparatively level with no vegetation or dunes to navigate. Public parking lots are fairly affordable but public parking can be found just a couple blocks away if you want to save some cash. Alcohol is not allowed but if you're discreet and aren't making a scene you shouldn't have any issues. Dogs are also allowed but must be on a leash. All in all a great beach that's very large and easy to access.

Clean, expansive beach with a good amount of sand!

I feel that sand-filled beaches in Santa Barbara aren't what they used to be. Over the years, I've noticed that either the tide has raised or the sandy area has just deteriorated. East Beach is an outlier as it has always had a great, large sandy area. It's the ideal spot to sit out and relax without feeling you're too close to anything around. 

This is also one of the cleaner beaches in the area, aside from the slough which is pretty dirty. I feel that the sand is mostly free of debris or seaweed which is a plus if you're trying to lay out or just enjoy your surroundings. 

The beach offers two parking lots, outdoor showers, the use of bathrooms in the renovated bath house, volleyball courts, and great panoramic views. Santa Cruz and Anacapa Islands are very visible on a clear day, and the Chase Palms to the north provide a nice backdrop.

Whenever I come here, I typically park parallel on Cabrillo as there's usually ample spots. The parking will fill up on weekends, especially Sundays when the Art Walk is going on. That said, I've never felt that the beach has ever been overly crowded. Just a mild, flat place to enjoy the ocean breeze and take in the amazing views.
If you are looking to relax at a clean, quite, and surfing environment beach. This is at. I rate this as one of my favorite beaches on the cali coast.
Very pretty and quiet. My new favorite spot! I always feel like the beach is all to myself. Perfect for a family picnic.... I do wish people leashed their dogs.

This small beach area is well known among locals and is a nice quiet seperate little area at the beach. Although it amounts to a small area inbetween the front edges of 2 beach front homes, it does meet up with the adjacent beaches, but it's beautiful, quaint and ive enjoyed the few times I've spent a few hours here with my wife and daughter.

Take the trail with your best friend through a tunnel of greenery over a small bridge and out over soft white sand to a beach with large rocks and stones and plenty of smooth sand for your best friend to run free. Also have a picnic with a loved one.

There is absolutely no beach here at high tide, take note!! People opt to hang out on The stairs and it can get crowded as this seems to be a popular place for people to bring their lunch. There was a couple making out here when I came with my kid. That was gross. Also, lots of dogs, none of which are on leashes. Contrary to other posts this is not a good place to bring children unless the tide is very low.  We sat on the rocks until the tide went out (the people made out for like 2 hour, cmon!!) and we had a nice walk down the beach when the tide was low enough. That was amazing and worth the low tide wait. Not really worth the hassle overall I would say. Probably not going back here.

Beautiful, secluded, quiet, clean.... Come early. What can I say about this spot? It's lovely... Always friendly people with their friendly dogs on the beach. I have never seen a rough crowd here, thankfully. Lots of sea life to explore on the rocks. There is a small parking lot at the end of San Ysidro. If you come early you are almost guaranteed a spot. If not, there is plenty of street parking past the train tracks. Two entrances to the beach. Straight down the stairway or you can choose the short trail to the right of the parking lot that cuts through the homes on the beach. The trail is easier especially if you have small children

Review 25

Hammonds Beach the larger and what I call the "locals Beach" is very nice. I prefer the next smaller beach south of there (end of Eucalyptus) for its rocks and less popularity. 

The beach is protected and mostly ankle waves are to be expected. The beach is kid friendly for its slow sloping terrain, I felt very comfortable letting my kids play in the ocean. Stop by at this little corner next time you're in the area. 

Parking is less than 30 yards from the beach and you'll always find parking. Watch for kids please!!


Plan B.

Nice quiet place to hangout, take a stroll by the water front, run in the sand and or just sit out in the sun for a tan. Close to a residential area thus place seems more private and less crowded compared to other public beaches nearby. Bring your dogs and run in the sand and water, sit and lay down for a tan, etc.

Parking can be a bit tricky because the small narrow street, you just have to try to find parking along 2 sides of the road. But people are always coming and leaving so you should have no problem finding a spot.

I came by to eat a quick lunch and for a short walk. Would love to come back and visit this location.

Aka Tar Beach. So much tar here you can't even walk on the beach without getting your feet or shoes covered. Had to throw out my shoes after. It's gross. Plus the water is brown and polluted. All of these problems caused by the petroleum industry right off the coast. This may be one of the "nicer" beaches in Santa Barbara area, but who wants to swim in petrol filth? I'd rather avoid the whole county.
Another small and beautiful beach in Montecino. Not crowded, dog friendly and has a beautiful view of beach front homes. Perfect place to relax and visualize your future :)
Lost my vigrinity when I was 13. Dope spot

name: Earl Warren Showgrounds
categories: Hotels & Travel, Venues & Event Spaces, Arts & Entertainment, RV Parks, Event Planning & Services, Haunted Houses, Active Life, Parks

yelp_reviews.text: We are currently here attending the AHASC Region 2 Championships. The grounds are quite nice and kept up. However, the dome arena needs a bit a work such as a fresh coat of paint & basic repairs. This is a wonderful place to have a horse show, the footing in the all the arenas is very nice and soft, which makes it comfortable for the horses.
At their Gem Show their chain man is rude and takes his anger out on his customers. He is with Close Out Depot out of Oregon. He sells gold filled chain and wire. Hope you catch him on a good day!

Came here for a gem show last weekend with my sister and had a lovely time. Having spent lots of time in Santa Barbara growing up, I was surprised I'd never been to these showgrounds before. The gem show wasn't highly attended so the majority of the grounds were closed off this day. Everything was well groomed and the lines were well managed, too. Just like other convention centers or fairgrounds, this was a great place to come for an afternoon and see something unique as it traveled through town throughout the weekend.

The bathrooms were a bit of a disappointment, maybe just one step above port-a-pottys, but other than that the place was A-ok. A little dated, but clean. There was ample parking, clear signage, and it didn't hurt that the weather was gorgeous. I read that the gem and mineral show will be back in a few months. I might have to make another trip to check it out again!
Awesome location depending on the event at the fairgrounds. We typically go to dog shows here and really enjoy the layout, restaurants and available parking.

DO NOT ATTEND AN EVENT HERE unless you want to be harrassed and wait hours to get inside the venue. I attempted to go to a concert there this evening and am disgusted with the treatment and lack of organization of this place. Before I go on any further, I did address my issues on site with the concert organizer and was told that the issues were in fact the fault of the venue and their security team hired by the venue whom they were also very disappointed in.

I've been to many concerts in my life and have never seen such poor and inefficient organization. I arrived and there was a gigantic line around the entire parking lot to get through security to enter the show (there was also no separate line for people who had tickets already and will call).
