I waited for 45 minutes with no movement what so ever, there were not any employees regulating the line and droves of teenagers were cutting in groups with their friends. I wish I knew the crowd and type of event this would be, otherwise I would not have attended at all.

I went up to the front and requested a refund and was told no but I could bypass the line at this point and enter the concert. I had my ticket scanned and went through the security check, no where on the showgrounds website, event website, or tickets were prohibited items listed. There were no signs showing prohibited items. I was then checked by security and violated (yes, this is worse than the airport) and as they went through my bag, he found my vaporizer, a legal smoking device - asked me about it, then proceeded. He then made accusations about my non-sharp quartz stone necklace being a weapon?? Seriously??? He then tried to confiscate my LIPSTICK saying that it was a lighter, I had to open it and show him it was lipstick...Then he scanned me with a metal detector, it went off on the button of my jean jacket then asked me to open up my jacket... are you kidding me? This is a local concert. Further, my fiance gets checked after me by a different security guard, who then confiscates his vaporizer, but the other security guard told me they were allowed... He tried to get it back from him and the security guard actually put his hands on him. This is the worst event I have ever been to and would never attend a concert here again. I am disgusted with the security team and where their priorities are for keeping people safe at an event. They should be much more concerned about all the underage kids drinking in the line on their property (they wouldn't know - no security other than at the front entrance) than adults with legal contents in their bag and jewelry. 

Perhaps this place is better during the day for a horse show or other like event, but I know for sure I will never waste the time or money to find out after tonight. Pathetic.

Such a disappointment and waste of time and money. $10 to park, $8 to just enter the fairgrounds and an additional $7 for the bird and reptile show. When I asked the the woman at the entrance of the bird and reptile show "Didn't we already pay an $8 entrance free to get in?" She answered me with attitude " we have no control what they do "  whatever that means! $25 later and 2 tables of reptile supplies and a few snakes. I guess it might have been alright if you were here for the birds. I won't do this again

I attended a Glass Animals concert at Earl Warren on Sunday evening. It was the ABSOLUTE WORST experience I have ever encountered. The concert was supposed to being at 8 o'clock PM. I arrived at 8:15 and did not get inside the doors until 10:20.... the concert had yet to even begin because so LITTLE people had been allowed inside. It was so unorganized I would NEVER recommend it to anyone. Then once you got inside you had to WAIT in another line to get a 21+ wrist band that line didn't even get you into the beer garden.. then it was another 30 minute line to get a drink. Literally have never had such a horrible experience. I have seen an underground rave be more efficient and organized then this.

It may be easier to mention what does not come to the Earl Warren Showgrounds rather than list the Santa Barbara fair, home shows, craft shows, gem shows, dog shows, wedding shows and gun shows. There's flower shows and horse shows too. Horses are what they do best with a large number of stables and the Kramer arena.
There's also great rental opportunities with the Exhibit building and Waren Hall. I've been to a few hosted events in these buildings and enjoyed my time and the location. I even buy my Christmas tree in the lot each year. 
I'm sure you'll find something to do there too.

The Thursday swap meet was awesome today from a sellers point of view. The crowd was not big but the people were buying. Price to setup is only $20 . Not only did we sell quite a bit but we also did some shopping and bought some great produce. Overall it was a very funny day and profitable to boot.

***FOR RV PARK***

Amazing! This place is located at Earl Warren off Las Positas. Very close to exit/ on-ramp, easily accessible. 

I just had to stay at this park for my full 15 days! Excellent location and even better lot manager. She makes sure you are parked and comfortable during your stay. And by park, I mean she helps everyone back in, if needed! And made sure to direct me where the restrooms and showers are located, as well as give WiFi password and unlocking the hookups immediately upon arrival. She was SO helpful from start to finish. I really felt like I should've tipped her. Myself and all visitors to my site were happily greeted by her and given proper instructions, which is hard to come by for RV parks and campgrounds in general. 

This is a parking lot, and would be nothing without the lively and lovely RV manager. Her name is Susan! 

Here are the specs, short and sweet!

+ WiFi, very good I might add. Was able to do work and check emails quickly 
+ Very close to Las Positas exit, easy turn in and back in. It's a large lot 
+ 30/50 amp hookups 
+ Sewage dump hookup 
+ Water hookup 
+ Walking distance to Whole Foods and other markets 
+ 2.5 miles to beach and downtown State st. (Main popular street in Santa Barbara, attractions, food, pier, shopping) 

Would DEFINITELY recommend this park. Quiet stay in Santa Barbara and close to downtown State St. Hidden gem!

I went to the dog show here on a whim a few weeks ago, and had fun! There are a few large showrooms/showrings/arena areas, allowing for quite a few dog rings goin' all at once. We paid $5 for parking near the restaurant area, and walked right into the dog show from there! (Don't tell them, shh). I wish they had more chairs out, seemed like people brought their own.
Showgrounds are always dirty so to have low ratings means something really bad is going on.

Everyone who attended Glass Animals last night can relate to everything I'm about to say. I fortunately arrived early and didn't have to wait in the crazy line, but I knew from the minute I asked the first staff member a question that the people working the event were by far the most unprofessional people I've seen.  There was no structure to the line and it went off into the parking lot. While I waited in line a group of only about 15 security guards came out to help 2,000+ people. One seemed to have a napoleon complex as he went through the line screaming at us like we're toddlers, which I found extremely crazy considering we paid to be there. He went off about no drugs, no alcohol, which is fair -- but then he started telling us to get rid of our bags, e-cigs, etc etc and cigarettes.  Which is ironic because there were 3 staff members about 5 ft from my smoking cigarettes after we were told it was through the "state rules that the venue can't have cigarettes". Not to mention he was screaming and cussing, then let someone cut in line.
So we get in and I noticed it took about 5 minutes to get each person in. He was busy chatting to realize I was ready to be checked, then after forgot to give me my 21+ bracelet and yelled for me to come as I was halfway in. He then had me wait for 5 mins again just to get my bracelet that HE forgot. What I found confusing is why the staff and security gossiped at around 7pm when they could have started checking then. After the opening set came on, two guys nonchalantly came up to the microphone and told us in a rude tone that the show is postponed an hour.. didn't even seem to care. We all sat on the floor sweating our butts of and everyone was getting extremely irritated. 
Glass Animals did eventually come on and made up for the mistakes, but nonetheless it was one of the most ridiculous concerts I've ever been to. I feel bad for Glass Animals because they had gotten the backlash for the BS that Earl Warren pulled. I went on FB and Twitter after the show and saw all the unprofessional things almost every customer got. Even security grabbing them! Not to mention this happened on a Sunday night where people had school the next day or work.  FYI -- telling people to just "get over it" and just giving them no choice other than to leave after they gave you their money probably won't help you out in the future. HIRE BETTER SECURITY. THEY SUCK. If there was ever an event that I could attend here or in LA, or SF, I would go out of my way to drive out and waste gas instead of coming here again. I suggest no one waste a single minute here, especially musicians who want to book shows here. I'd think twice. So unprofessional.

This review is for the RV park located at the Earl Warren Showgrounds. This is a great location! It is a parking lot with full hookups and easy access. The RV park host, Susan, was super helpful and accommodating! It is quiet and you have access to the entire fairgrounds. Not many places to RV camp in the middle of Santa Barbara, so this is a real gem!

**RV Park Review** We've stayed here multiple times now and each stay has been exceptional. The park is located in a clean and safe area that is centrally located and quiet. Also, the park manager, Susan, is one of the most accommodating and friendly managers we've had the pleasure of meeting during our travels. Earl Warren RV Park is definitely our preferred park for this area! Thank you Susan for making us feel at home here.

I am having a hard time putting my experience into words. Putting it mildly, this was absolutely the worst event I have ever been to. Earl Warren Showgrounds is by far the most unorganized and unprofessional company I have every encountered.

After driving about an hour to the Showgrounds we stumbled upon a line that was over 2 hours long and growing (no exaggeration). We thought this was for sure a "will call" line, but to our amazement this was the line for everyone, and it was in fact due to their pathetic excuse for a security team. When finally reaching the front of line, the Security company used at Earl Warren Showgrounds ended up making us feel extremely violated and caused us to leave the show before entering. After illegal search, attempted confiscation of my belongings, illegal touching, pushing, harassment, and name calling, I had to leave the event before even entering. What an absolute nightmare! I wouldn't go to Earl Warren Showgrounds if it was the last dry land on earth after tonights treatment!

I love the events that go on here! From The fair and Bettie's Practice and Roller Hockey! The space is very large so many event could go on, one of the down sides is once parking fills up street parking can be a hassle to deal with, but it's not as bad for the space it gives! They hold swap meets and diamond expos as well! So anyone in town should check out the event page they provide.

Came here to find a cool venue in the SB area to host our next music festival GOOD VIBES featuring indie rock, reggae, electro, and dubstep (3/26/11).

HUGE PROS: 
Close and cheap parking lot
3.5 large stage areas
1 huge outdoor coliseum *5K person
1 interestingly shaped indoor area
WOODY's BBQ on site! (check them out)
lots of bathrooms
lots of grass
Competitively priced

Cons:
Surround community only allows outdoor area till 10, indoor till midnight.
Dirt coliseum 

The showgrounds also hosts rodeos, circuses, and swap meets. A hidden gem in our book! Make sure you go check out HighBirdProductions.com to see what other shows we'll be throwing. Sorry to make it all commercial but an interesting review in my opinion from an event organizer's perspective.
This is a great location. I have enjoyed the property many times for the rodeos and bull riding competitions. The stables are very well kept. Always good times to be had here.

My husband, Art Rodriguez and I love this place. Susan, the RV Manager is so friendly and welcoming. Just reserve your space online and pull in directly to your spot at check in time. It's that easy. Susan is always available if you need her but no interaction needed, which is perfect for these covid times. The spaces are close together, but we don't spend much time at the RV during the day. The fairground is a great place to lay your head down at night because during the day there is plenty to see and do in Santa Barbara. Thank you for making our experience easy Susan. See you soon.

BROADCASTING FROM THE PARKING LOT OF EARL WARREN, 9/20/2015 9:30PM

Me and hundreds of other American citizens are stuck out in the parking lot in a line wrapped around the entire parking lot trying to get into the venue to see Glass Animals.

We have just hit the one hour mark of standing in line and are still moving at snail's pace with more line to be had ahead. We already missed the opening band which could have been my new indie band to not tell anyone about. Now I will never know and I can never get that back. 

This event is run by Night Out but has externally hired security. Said security just came out and gave us a stump speech about rules, laws, following orders, heroine, and stop asking us questions. Not sure who is at fault here or who should be assigned blame or if it is merely a cataclysmic sum of the unavoidable, but this situation is absolutely ridiculous and sees no sign of getting any better.

If I die in this line, tell Night Out to give my refund to my parents.

Over and out.

I've stayed here with the wife and kids multiple times and will again. Full hook ups, close parting for any Toads. Susan is super accommodating there as well.

Safe location, close to downtown and IV. If you're looking for a resort, this isn't it.

If you do want a night's rest or a spot close to everything SB has to offer at a reasonable price, it's awesome!

The only major knock I'd have, and it doesn't bother me, is that spaces are tight. Just enough room for slides.

Dry nice facility, the Sea glass exhibition was very nice
My Grandchildren enjoyed it also
Some very nice jewelry and we all bought something
The equestrian ring is amazing also, we enjoyed watching a show there

This is a great overnight spot when traveling through Santa Barbara. We stayed one night with a 27' rv. Full hook ups are available, power, sewer and water. I believe there was wifi as well but we didn't use it. 

Susan was very friendly and accommodating and is the reason I thought to write a review.

Ghouls, Terrifying Clowns, Crypt Keepers and ALL things that go bump in the night! THREE Haunted Houses at THE HAUNT and Harvest Festival made our hearts pound and scream over and over again.
It was awesome! 
We bought 3-pack tickets to go to all 3 experiences: 
1. The underground Tunnel of Terror (Like movies Saw, Get Out and Texas Chainsaw combined)  
2. Hallway of Darkness (Just red balloons and clowns) and 
3. Creepy Barns (Eerie, dark, headless horseman vibe plus spiders). 
PLUS Live, local band music with Beer Garden, 
Carnival munchiez including turkey legs, 
funnel cakes, deep fried yummy goodness. 
Carnival rides, Jumpers and Painting pumpkins for young kids. 
Many folks came in costume and it was thrilling to see all the creative Halloween fun!

BIG PLUS: FREE Parking, Close to Freeway and Admission was FREE,  do need tickets for Rides and Haunted Houses.

The RV park was a nice surprise.   We looked on google maps so we knew it was basically a parking lot with hookups, but it didn't feel like it.   It was a really nice stay.   Susan is onsite to make sure everything goes smoothly and you have everything you need.   Places to walk the dog and can stroll around the showgrounds.  Convenient location and was quiet.  Easy Lyft into SB and back.  Would recommend for a waypoint or weekend in SB.
We stayed 5 nights at the RV camp grounds.  The price was right and it was very quiet.   Susan was helpful and we had no problems.   No pool no spa and no kids. lots of quiet.

Good Morning . This RV site so close to downtown santa barbara is Awesome.
Make sure to use Gate "A", you cannot find the RV park from Calle Real.
And 2 miles from Hendry,s Beach, which has the Boat Jessup, which is a must. Moses is our favorite waiter at Boat House. We recommend everything on the menu. 
Susan is the manager with her 2 assistant cats, Viola and Alfie,  who live indoors with her at the house at the entrance. She runs this whole Earl Warren Showgrounds. She makes walk abouts in the rv park regularly, but Earl should provide her with a Golf Cart preferably with a full bar like the golf courses in Arizona. 

You will be most happy 

There are  44 Full hook ups, complete with wifi, but book early, it fills up.

This review is for the Earl Warren RV Park at the Showgrounds. My daughter was having orientation at UCSB and we had the choice to camp or get a hotel. It was much cheaper for us to camp and we found this location on google. 

It is just a parking lot, but has full hook up sites. You book your site online, we did it there from the parking lot after we looked at the sites and which ones were available online and booked right then and there - no issues. There is no host, but you can talk to maintenance if you need anything or if there is an emergency their number is listed. Also once you book and receive your confirmation you will be provided with the Wi-Fi user and password as well as the combination for the showers. We did not use, so I can't provide further information on the showers there.

We did have our two dogs with us and there is a dog run, not fenced and grass areas. It is also close to State Street in Santa Barbara and shopping.  I wouldn't mind staying here again for a short stay to see our daughter or overnight when passing though. The price is not bad for a night.

This is a hidden gem.  The campground is part of the parking lot for the Earl Warren Showgrounds.  However the location is incredible.  It's quite and you are right in the middle of Santa Barbara, but it doesn't feel like it.  The views of the mountains are beautiful.  You are close to everything.  And you can't beat the price for this location.  Also, Susan the manger is great.  She is very helpful in all matters.  I have stayed here several times in the last year.  I look forward to it every time.

Its a nice park but it could use some more trees and bigger ones too.
What it has going for it; is that it has 2 basketball courts, several bassball fields and one soccor field. Also it has some BBQ.

girsh park is nice and well kept but there can be alot of people on the weekends and if you are looking for pick up games or to have your own basketball game be sure to get their early or you'll be having trouble getting a chance to play. Also the goleta 4th of july fest is always nice. I just went their for the recent lemon festival which was smaller than usual but is good for kids.

This park is great for the grass, the basketball courts, the baseball fields and the soccer fields. Additionally I really like this park because it hosts a lot of great events - I saw the fire works on 4th of July here (put on by the rotary club of Santa Barbara) were fantastic and in September each year the park hosts a lemon festival in celebration of Goleta's history as a stronghold in the industry.

I love that there is a fenced in off-leash area for dogs. I've never had any negative run ins with "naughty dogs" or inattentive owners. There are bathrooms, lots of parking, bleachers if you just wanna chill, basketball courts, and it's near Costco if your craving a polish dog and soda.

This is a huge park and great for field sports. On a Saturday there were many birthday parties and a soccer tournament going on. There is only a small playground with wood chips, but at least it is near the bathroom.

There is a parking lot but I noticed many people also park across the street in the Costco parking lot. With the many restaurants across the street it's easy to get a bite to eat after a day at the park.

Really peaceful and nice park. Moms bring their kids here during the day. There are two basketball courts.. they were being used by some grammar school when I visited.. I think they were playing dodge ball. There are baseball fields too which is cool. Then there is that new Ice Rink not too far off.  Nice park !

Great park for families and sports.  Best basketball court and soccer fields in the area.  I was there yesterday with my kids  and they are installing a new playground for small children too, will have to check it out.

I needed a place to stop and let my 2 yr old stretch her legs on the way to LA.  I found this park tucked a few minutes off the 101 and it was super easy to get to.  The park was clean had a good playground, wide open spaces and a fairly clean bathroom.  A good place to visit if you need a place to run off some energy for your kiddos.

This park has it all, baseball, soccer, basketball and jungle gym for the little ones.  It's centrally located near Costco, the marketplace, the ice rink, and Isla vista.   Park also features two small parking lots.  During the Weekends the park hosts several little league soccer and baseball games throughout the year.  Also on occasion you may get to witness a dog show practice area.  This park is well maintained with several picnic areas stay can be reserved.  I take my children there several times a week and it is never a let down.

Stayed at Marriott down the street and came here to get the wiggles out. Lovely little park with lots of amenities. I saw baseball and soccer practices, pick up games on the courts, dog walkers, stroller moms, all the usual community park participants. The playground is small and aimed at toddlers but kids will play anywhere there's a slide or stuff to climb on. The grounds were clean and well manicured. 

One star off for broken bathroom stalls. Also the flowering bushes planted outside the bathrooms attracted tons of bees, which is great! Unless you put them right next to the playground. Terrible  horticulture planning IMO.

I love Girsh Park. The basketball courts are very good and there are no issues with nets missing or awkward rims. There's also usually people to start pick-up games with so that is nice as well.

Weekends are jam packed with soccer so be sure to park on the arête somewhere. 

Tip: don't expect to shoot threes, it's windy...drive to the hole

This is a big park that is easily accessible from the neighborhoods surrounding it.  It is kept up in fairly good condition, and the bathrooms are in more than passable condition.  It tends to be fairly crowded, but it's never overly so.  There is unfortunately a lot of grass, but I suppose that is to be expected for any semiurban park.

Honestly, this review centers on the fact that there is a lot to do here for Pokemon Go players.  There are a six gyms in total, all of which are accessible by car (important for playing during the pandemic without leaving your car).  There is a robust raid hour group that comes here, so you'll almost always find a crowd to play with.  In addition, there are several players in the surrounding neighborhoods who can hit the gyms easily.

What a great park in Goleta!
I take my hat off when I see quality in our parks.

Full-size acrylic basketball courts.
Multiple fields for soccer: grass and turf.
A good amount of parking spaces.
Lots of space to move around.

Location is so convenient too.
Right off of Storke and right behind Costco with the plethora of shops and eats.

Very nice job by the city and county.

What I like about Girsh Park: the basketball courts, green grass, fairly clean public restrooms, and family-friendly atmosphere. 

Children are always playing here with watchful parents and the safe environment is the best thing about this park to me. 

My boyfriend likes to play pick up basketball here and I like to do some high intensity sprints because there is plenty of grass and space to do so. 

Girsh is directly across from Camino Real shopping center and is one of my favorite things in Goleta.

A little something for everyone here. 

I have been a fan of this park ever since it was erected. I was in grade school and it offered my peers and me a good space for outdoor activities when our town had few spots to congregate. 

I'd consider Girsh Park one of Goleta's main hubs. You can find the annual 4th of July show and the California Lemon Festival here. The green belt area is huge and expands into several sports courts: baseball, basketball, soccer, a snack bar, a bioswale (natural landscape area), along with clean restrooms and a few small parking lots. 

This park is also landscaped nicely and offers lush, well-maintained greenery. It's a nice and safe park to explore and admire. Definitely worth a visit.

name: Living Vehicle
categories: Mobile Home Dealers, Architects, Professional Services, Real Estate, Home Services, Trailer Dealers, Automotive, RV Dealers

yelp_reviews.text: I saw this tiny home and the Santa Barbara Earth Day Festival and I want to buy one. I have seen numerous tiny Home and this one is the best by far. The shower is so bright and modern. I live that it has a washer dryer. I'm considering getting one for our back yard for my adult children.
The living vehicle really takes camping to the next level. I'm looking into making a purchase here soon!

A unique and well thought out contemporary take on mobile living.  A better Airstream.  Includes a great selection of top shelf finishes and design elements make the Living Vehicle really stand out.  
For us the unit is a bit too big, we would love to see something in a smaller footprint for just two.  Also our current vehicle sleeping arrangement is rooftop which keys us into evening breezes, better views of sun up and having all of our bedding up and out of the way during the day.  But that's personal stuff, and LV gets kudos for a great job right out of the gate !!

I have been closely following the work by Matthew Hofmann and Hofmann Architecture for a number of years with their fabulous Airstream projects. When I heard about their new Living Vehicle last year, I was intrigued to say the least. A person and company I have high regards for coming out with a new concept that addresses the short comings of living full-time in a smaller, movable domicile. Reading about it and seeing photos has been fine but with finding ourselves not too far from Santa Barbara this week, I contacted the company to see if they could accommodate a short notice visit so we could see the Living Vehicle in person. Fortunately, they were fine with us stopping by and we ended up spending about 1  1/2 hours with Matthew.

The Living Vehicle is the most thought out RV for full-timing that I believe is on the market. The details that are needed for practical full timing have been completely flushed out. This includes the aluminum construction, VHB tape instead of leaky rivets, no wood in the floor, double pane windows, and outstanding insulation. Whether dealing with cold weather or hot, the attention to the envelop make it a truly four-season unit. Appropriate sized holding tanks within the insulated heated basement, quality fittings, cleanly executed wiring and plumbing, and so much more are all done the right way. Most people that have lived with the typical RV's know that too much stuff just breaks and doesn't hold up to normal wear and tear. The interior doors, cabinets and other items are all robust and designed for constant, normal use. The floor plan, space utilization and window placements are all outstanding. I especially like the large skylight over the bed to enjoy the stars at night. My wife loved the bathroom details with the teak (being changed to another weather resistant wood in future models) in the shower and the vessel sink. Incorporating a washer/dryer and a small dishwasher are also great time savers when living in the Living Vehicle day-to-day.

The general feel or vibe of the unit is outstanding. My wife is a little bit more old school then I am with design and décor, therefore I thought she might have a little hesitation with the clean, modern esthetic. However, once she saw it, she loved it.

My impression with the company was just as favorable as the Living Vehicle. Matthew has loads of passion for the product and the design. With him living on a boat, he understands the needs of full time living in smaller space. He was very generous with his time and explaining all the details. The other people we spoke with were also very knowledgeable, helpful and pleasant to talk to. Good impressions of a well-run company. Highly recommended.
Wonderful local company! Compact yet has everything you need! Affordable Luxury. Skylights throughout. Shower while looking up at the stars! What a dream!

It appears that many of the reviews of this vehicle are based on people viewing websites rather than an actual visit. These comments are based on my website views.

There seems to be two incarnations of the Living Vehicle: one in 2017 and the in 2018. I will comment on the 2018 version.

This trailer differs from most in that it is oriented toward long-term use without moving for a while. Not aerodynamic, but roomier. It seems more sustainable than most trailers with its 100 gallon fresh tank, large waste tanks, huge LP tanks, and 600W solar. That is, as long as one does not use the dishwasher or washer and dryer. Also, the air conditioning will likely be unusable without hook-ups.

Some omissions:
- No conventional oven. How do I make my muffins in the morning?
- No double sink... Double sink is needed to wash and rinse dishes efficiently.
- No awning. Seems like this would be a cheap solution to reduce solar load. But it may have been omitted because of its limited lifetime.
- No TV?

At $150K, the price is higher than any trailer of its size, even Airstream.

My wife and I traveled down to Bryce Canyon to see the Living Vehicle in person and it was well worth the trip. The design inside and out is sleek, minimal, and very aesthetically pleasing. I initially thought the interior might be too sterile with all that white but it's balanced out perfectly with warm woods and the color choices help to make the interior seem far roomier than the square footage would suggest. It doesn't just look gorgeous though, it's ridiculously functional too. Tiny Home builders often surprise me with their ingenuity and use of interior space but I've yet to run into any builder that goes to this level. The build quality is outstanding and you can tell the home is going to last a long time. The placement of windows is particular appreciated and the skylights really add a luxury feel to the whole thing and make it very hard to look at other tiny homes the same way. I think it's quite accurate to say this feels like the iPhone of tiny homes and it perfectly blends the mobility of an RV with the interior of a luxury home. If you are in the market for a tiny home I strongly encourage you to go see this one before making a decision.

My husband and I visited the RV show out in Pomona today and as we were walking past, I saw this aluminum trailer that caught the corner of my eye. Even from afar I was drawn to it and as I walked up and took a little tour I was BLOWN AWAY. Matt and Joanna were both there and were very friendly, explaining how their company came to be but wow- this is so far from a trailer. It's totally makes you rethink "trailer"- this is comparable to the tiny house race that's been so popular lately but the home is so thoroughly thought through and it's amazing- so sleek and modern, clean and green- solar panels, lithium batteries- self powered and exquisitely beautiful. 
With housing costs so high in California, this is something my husband and I talked about all the way home and after seeing this it makes it easy to think out of the typical housing box- this is absolutely beautiful. Still in a daze after seeing this and I wish I had more time to go thru all the details of this beautiful smart home. You really have to appreciate the thought that went into building and designing this. Matt, Joanna I think you'll be seeing me again real soon ;)
This thing is awesome!   So spacious and modern.  It's incredible. Woo hoo.  But buy buy buy buy this machine

We own Living Vehicle number V.  We are extremely happy and appreciative to Matthew, Joanna & Wally for creating such a well designed vehicle that was very thoughtfully planned out and with such detail to design, sustainability and functionality. We had some very minor adjustments to our LV and Wally & Kevin have given us five star service to make the adjustments that were needed. Being a new company and with such a unique vehicle, it is growing and changing for the better with each new vehicle coming out and the customer service is excellent. We are very satisfied and happy with our purchase. We are excited to meet other LV owners as well. Well done LV team!!!

My husband and I had the opportunity to meet Matt and Joanna who graciously invited us to visit and tour their Living Vehicle earlier this week.  Having spent the last several months researching tiny homes and saving a few favorites, we were elated to be able to actually visit and see this in person.  We had expected it to be something along the lines of an incredibly glorified camper because the videos, pictures and things we'd read about it seemed a little too good to be true.  Due to the price, we almost talked ourselves out of going to check it out.
I am SOOOO happy we convinced ourselves to make the trip to see the Living Vehicle.  This is truly a piece of art.  There is so much thought put into every little detail of this beautiful space, it absolutely blew me away.  The closet in the bedroom has built-in drawers and the W/D is right next to the closet -- an excellent and practical use of space and placement.  The bathroom is functional with adequate storage and has the most inviting shower I've ever come across -- with a teak wall and a skylight, it felt roomy and airy.  The kitchen is well put together though the fridge was a bit smaller than I would prefer.  The slide out pantry is large enough to store anything from canned goods to cereal boxes and there is even more storage above the sink and in the moveable island.  Also of note there is additional storage under the sink (above the dishwasher) and underneath the grill-oven.  Storage doesn't stop there either; there's more beneath the seating in the living room and even more on the outside of the house - a pass through storage compartment and a couple of others.
The elevator bed above the dining area/living room is fantastic for house-guests and it lowers just enough that you can still sit on the couch/at the table when it is fully lowered.  This place is truly accommodating for anyone and everyone.
Another great feature is the technical panel.  The model we saw was integrated with bluetooth technology for the home audio system.  It also integrated cameras for security that could be viewed on the built in television.
My very favorite thing about this house is ALL THE WINDOWS!  There is so much natural daylight in this place yet it still feels cozy and private.  The skylights in the bedroom, shower and kitchen help to really open up the space and make this feel like a home.  The huge sliding glass door that opens to the fold out deck also helps to expand the space and provide a lovely area to barbeque, lounge beneath the oversized umbrella while reading a book and sipping on a mojito, etc.
Also of mention is the solar panel awning.  This place can run on or off grid as it is also equipped with clean, gray and black water tanks.  Did I mention the outdoor shower as well?  Seriously, Matt and Joanna thought of EVERYTHING.
I was so blown away by every detail in this house, it truly is a magnificent creation.  Most of all, I was shocked at how sizeable it felt for how small it was.  Layout is everything and this couldn't be better done.  I would encourage anyone considering moving to a tiny space to visit this the Living Vehicle and get a good handle on just how functional a small space (212 sq ft) can actually be when it is set up with actual living in mind.  Matt's years of experience and expertise are not lost in this.
A huge thanks to Matt and Joanna for sharing their creation and being so inviting and accommodating.  They are two of the most polite, warm and welcoming people I've ever met and they are genuinely excited and happy to talk about living small and practically.

What an amazing concept!! Last weekend we attended a great open house/reveal party for the Living Vehicle. The Living Vehicle truly has everything you would need. It's not just for short term travel, but for long term living. The full bathroom is what sold me. I don't know why trailer makers think people would rather give up full bathroom space!! I'd rather give up space elsewhere to have the luxury of a full bathroom while I'm roaming the world!! Isn't the point of traveling to be immersed in your surroundings and not cooped up inside?! Bring on the full bathrooms!!!

But that wasn't the only innovative part of this concept. I LOOOOVED the drop down wall to give a full floor to ceiling view PLUS a patio deck!! Talk about dinner with a view!! I can imagine cooking dinner lakeside with fish fresh from that very same lake!  

Not to mention the high ceilings! I was really impressed that the Living Vehicle has the full ceiling height so you felt like you were actually standing in a room and not in a tunnel. 

Not only did the Hofmann's come up with a great concept, but they threw on awesome party. I thought for sure with rain in the forecast they would have cancelled, but not the Hofmanns!! They put up tents and brought out the heat lamps and threw an excellent fiesta!! I thought I would stop by for 20 minutes or so, but ended up staying for about 3 hours. They had a great live band, taco bar and local wine and beer. Why would we leave?!

Unlike any other RV or camper-trailer I have ever seen: it glistens with a bright, pure and joyful interior supported by the functionality and layout only an exceptionally gifted and experienced architect could have realized--and did.

Wally and Mathew have an extraordinary vision for their business and the Living Vehicle takes all the work they've done in sustainable spaces to a whole new level.

Watch the videos. Get a sense of how brilliantly they've combined sustainability with a truly wise approach to living mobile. 

I'm looking at the Living Vehicle as a potential guest house and as a road trip enabler. Excited to see the evolution of the Living Vehicle!

My family and I have been living full-time in our Living Vehicle since our house burned down in the Woolsey fires - there are 4 of us, plus two dogs - and the Living Vehicle has surpassed all our expectations. The design is impeccable - and considering that my entire family is extremely tall, being able to stand upright and move comfortably throughout the entire vehicle makes a huge difference. An efficient, spacious layout has obviously been very carefully considered, and the attention to detail and function is very impressive.
 
The LV customer service is tremendous and the whole experience of dealing with Living Vehicle has been superb. Whenever we have had issues (and there really haven't been many), response is immediate, and a solution has been provided with little or no delay. Wally Hofmann is a pleasure to spend time with, and his commitment to customer service is extremely impressive.
 
The LV has enabled us to live in comfort, and has provided a comfortable and fun home for my family while our house is rebuilt. I know we will look back extremely fondly on this time - LV has made it possible to enjoy the aftermath of the loss of our home, and has enabled us to live on-site while it is rebuilt.
 
We're looking forward to towing the LV somewhere on vacation too eventually - but for now, it's home.

We attended the Earth Day Festival in our home town last weekend mainly to check out the new automobiles and solar coming on the scene.  We were blown away when we came across the "Living Vehicle".  We are already in to the "Tiny House" concept, especially since we now live in a 600 sq ft Adobe home (after raising children in a huge four bedroom home) and we are used to traveling with a 34' RV.  When we saw this unit we were so impressed with the workmanship and design.  A lot of thought went into the design and architecture of this unit.  Love the contemporary design and open feel, yet great use of space. So bright!  My husband was all about the aluminum exterior and really cool tilt down patio deck.  If we were on the hunt again for something to tow (or even to downsize in to) we would definitely entertain the Living Vehicle!  The owners were incredibly personable.  A real pleasant surprise to have such a company in our town.

Hands down the best small space concept and build quality that I have found.  If you want turn-key off grid capabilities, 5k cargo capacity, a washer/dryer and a DISHWASHER you will find it all here in a trailer with a dry weight under 8,000lbs. No need for a dually truck!

We purchased Living Vehicle #5 from LV's first fleet. Our experience was great and seamless from start too finish.
Wally was always available to help us or answer questions as we were brand new to the RV world. The entire LV team was professional and we really love our LV experience and highly recommend buying one. The design is perfect for living and or traveling. The design is functional and there was nothing we would change in regards to design and or function. Every time we travel in it every one stops us and wants to learn about it. It truly is a very special experience.

The living vehicle is the best example of innovation and design in a mobile home that I've ever seen! There is so much thought packed into every inch of this trailer. For anyone wanting to live an adventurous and sustainable life surrounded by beautifully designed spaces and high tech resources, you've got to check this thing out. I never imagined you could pack so much into such a small space without it feeling claustrophobic, but the Living Vehicle proved me wrong. The version that I saw, could very comfortably sleep 6, with two entertainment centers, a patio, an indoor and outdoor shower, sky lights, dishwasher, washer dryer, solar panels... and so much more.

Very well designed and executed. Love the spacious feeling, especially the moonroof over the bed!
Reminds me of my Honda element with that feature.

This is the most impressive small home rv situation I have ever seen. This company has thought of everything to make sure your Living Vehicle can be a long term home that is a serious luxury to live in. Beautiful bedroom, larger and sky roof indoor shower, as well as an outdoor shower. I can't believe how they fit all these high end ementies into a RV (that isn't as bulky as I remember my family's to be). I'm certainly into this incredible Luxury Living Vehicle.
Makes my day. I want to buy one of these ASAP once I get the land to bring it on! This is a mobile home that I would just leave somewhere. The best shower with teak and two beds.

Small family business that is leading the RV industry in innovation, self sustainability and quality. We cannot say regarding the overall value of the Living Vehicle and our overall experience with our delivery. This company is committed to excellence and outstanding Customer Service and support that goes way beyond the purchase and delivery. Outstanding company!

We made a special trip to Santa Barbara from Boston to see the Living Vehicle and meet Joanna and Matthew and it was so worth it. Their beautiful headquarters are a reflection of their attention to detail and impeccable taste, and their friendly and approachable demeanor makes the entire experience lovely.  The vehicle itself is so well thought out. High quality, beautiful and very versatile.  We were, and are extremely impressed. I would give ten stars if I could. ( Luca, the Australian Shepard also made us so happy with her playful personality!)

I will try to keep this review concise, but there is so much to say. We purchased a new 2019 LV and spent 2 months traveling and living in it summer 2019 with a toddler, 2 cats and a 100lb dog. 
The purchasing process was so easy and personalized - they even had a cat door installed in the unit for us! The vehicle itself is incredible - so functional, modern and livable. We knew if we were going to attempt this lifestyle it had to be in a vehicle that would feel like home. We especially loved the drop-down deck, adding natural light and more space. 
We definitely tested the limits of our unit in terms of weather and off-road travel, and it performed great. Any time we had an issue, Wally was always available and quick to help us solve the problem. We had our unit in the shop for a bit and Wally handled the entire process. The concierge service is outstanding. 
On a personal note, we have so many amazing memories from our time in the LV that I think back on almost daily. In fact, we have already started discussing getting back into an LV once our kids are a bit older.
When we decided to sell our unit due to a growing family, Matthew, Joanna, and Wally took care of everything, making the resale process incredibly easy and hands-off for us. I should also mention they are wonderful people and a joy to be around! 

Thank you to the LV team for a wonderful experience, beautiful memories, and an incredible vehicle!

I had the opportunity to see a Living Vehicle in person at the open house this past weekend. Man was I impressed! I've watched the tour videos and looked at the photos on the website but seeing it in person blew me away. The build quality is outstanding and the layout is perfectly set up. What stood out the most to me is how spacious the interior felt given the size of the floor plan and the lack of extending sides. I think the numerous windows and skylights made the difference. I've been pondering the thought of living full time in a trailer in a backyard given the absurd rent prices in Santa Barbara but every one I've seen doesn't seem like it would be adequate for full time living. The kitchens don't have a big enough cooking area, there isn't enough storage space, etc. Not to mention how ugly the new ones are. After seeing Living Vehicle in person, I'm convinced. Now comes the hard part... saving up the $$$

Love coming here and catching that far away from the hustle and bustle feeling....  Prepare to pay the $10 at the gate and make sure to put it into the tube if the Ranger is gone or else they mark the car and ticket....  Love just being here in nature!

Very nice for kids. About 10 minute walk from parking. 20 minutes up 154. Right on paradise and 20 minutes up road. There was some standing water just past an open gate and have to drive through. Then drive and you will see red rock day use site then at the very end of road, fire trail. People brought floaties. Water shoes would be useful. Fun jumping from rocks. Worthwhile if not too hot

Don't get me wrong this place is awesome, probably one of the only spots in sb that has a consistent freshwater swimming hole. However it is getting more crowded every year and the wear is starting to show. I really appreciate the company that tries to maintain this place but it's becoming Santa Barbara's new public pool with the lines to show for it. If you go, I would personally recommend going very early in the morning or hiking all the way back toward the damn where the water is deeper and the crowds thin. Otherwise you'll be spending the afternoon shoulder to shoulder with half of Santa Barbara's young families. Still a beautiful place that makes you feel like you're back in Utah or something definetly check it out.
Nice spot, but water is highly stagnant this time of year so it's gotten pretty grody. Overall nice short hike for our dogs and a nice hangout spot.

We did the 7 mile loop, starting on the high ground (walking up to the dam) then heading back toward the swimming holes.  The signage leading back to the trail from the damn is a but difficult to find, so keep an eye out for signs that say "designated rt" as they might be hidden behind foliage.  

The hike is rather intermediate, and has very consistent but low grade change, as the majority of the hike to the dam is on a fire access road.  I hiked the trail in sandals, so it is possible, just not desirable :-)  The second part of the trail has lots of large rocks you have to walk through, making it a bit of a tow jammer in flip flops.

The swimming holes are beautiful and most are easily accessible.  Swimming holes closest to the dam (furthest from access points) rarely have other people in them, so you can have nice private time with your significant other :-) The closer you get to the trail entrance, the more accessible the swimming holes become and more crowded. 

A fantastic hiking option for dog owners, as we had our dog off leash the majority of the trail, and he had a blast jumping into every swimming hole.

About the Trail- 3.5 miles long each way for a 7 mile loop. Intermediate difficulty. The Red Rock trail follows the river and there are several large swimming holes along the trail. The most popular pool "Red Rock Pools" is 1/2 mile from the trailhead and can be crowded on weekends, there are more secluded pools are further up.

Directions- From Santa Barbara Hwy 101, 10 miles NW on Hwy 154, 10 miles East on Paradise Road to the end of the road.

Man, I really lucked out with this trail.  I was on a group camping trip up the road at Lake Cachuma and had this trail scheduled.  They told us it was a 6 mile hike and about 30 minutes into the drive over there I was already ready to go home. lol.  I am not a hiker and it was really hot so I was nervous about being able to hang, and the fact that I was at the mercy of other people was making me sweat.  

Luckily, the swimming hole we found at the end of about a half mile of "hiking" was perfect and the group went swimming and after an hour or so they were ready to go back.  If I can make it out here again, I will try to hike the rest of the trail and find the other swimming holes.  

Be prepared for the $10 fee to get into the park.  There will be a person there to collect it and give you a tag.  This spot is not run by the forest service, so the person is not official looking. There are several river crossings you have to pass just to get to the trail head, which can be driven through in a sedan, but probably better if you have a bigger car.  It's about 5 miles in from the point you pay and it is seriously beautiful.  Be careful, look for other cars and don't get distracted by the view, but do enjoy it!  We stopped in a few places to take pictures because there was no one behind us. 

Highly recommend!!

name: Channel Islands Expeditions
categories: Hotels & Travel, Boating, Tours, Snorkeling, Active Life, Rafting/Kayaking, Whale Watching Tours

yelp_reviews.text: What a amazing adventure Kayaking around Anacapa Island and exploring it's caves with Blue Ocean Kayaking!  It exceeded my expectations.  The crew were so knowledgeable and made our day so enjoyable!  Once in a Life Time Memories!

This is one of my personal Top Ten favorite things to do in Los Angeles.  If you live here, you need to do this at least once.  If you are visiting, this is an excellent way for a family to have fun, get some exercise and see something incredible.  It beats the crap out of contrived fare like Universal Studios.

Kayaking in the Channel Islands is one of the coolest things you can do in Los Angeles.  Blue Ocean Kayaking is a solid choice for several reasons:  friendly and professional staff, good service, excellent gear.  

But most important:  they take you by boat directly to the place where you will begin kayaking.   You launch your kayak directly into the ocean from their boat, and then the boat picks you up when you are done.  Other tours require you to offload your kayak at the pier and then drag it to the sea and, worse, you need to paddle back to the dock at the end.   With Blue Ocean Kayaking you get a much longer trip because there is no need to double back to return to the dock.  They pick you up right in the water and away you go.

Our trip was marred by a few unpleasant things:   we arrived by 8:00 AM as required but our excursion did not actually leave the pier until after 9:30 AM.  Why make everybody get up super early just to sit around on a dock for 90 minutes?   Worse, the toilet was broken on the boat.   That made life a little awkward since there were 40 people on board for a six hour excursion.   

But none of that mattered once we reached Anacapa Island.   It was superb.  Our guide took us into awesome caves and under arches and through narrow gaps in the rock.  It was exciting, beautiful, thrilling, strange.   There is nothing like floating in a kayak through a narrow gap in the rock as the ocean swell lifts you up gently... in total darkness.   That was just an incredible adventure.

You will see lots and lots of wild animal life very close up.  We saw sea lions barking and lots of curious harbor seals and thousands of cormorants, kingfishers and other sea birds.

This is a good workout.  It is not exhausting but it does require a reasonable amount of stamina.   You will feel like you had a decent workout.   I noticed that people in the single kayaks seemed to be more tired than people in the tandem kayaks.   Makes sense:  in the tandem one person can rest or take photos while the other person paddles, and then switch roles.  In a solo kayak, you need to do all the work by yourself.   They have a special offer for tandem kayaks, so most people chose the tandem.

Final note:  weather is unpredictable.  For us, it was surprisingly cold.  In the 60s ... which is really odd for Southern California in mid July.  There can be a 20 degree variation between the temperature in the city and the temperature at sea.  So be prepared:  bring a waterproof windproof shell, some light polar fleece, and a big beach towel.  I strongly recommend quick-dry sports clothing on the kayak.  You will get wet, and you don t want to be sitting for three hours in wet jeans or a sodden sweatshirt.   Be smart: bring polar fleece and quick dry sports gear.   Also: you must have water shoes or river sandals (the kind that strap onto your feet) because you don't want to step out of the kayak in bare feet.   Rocks are razor sharp.

My trainer and Bootcamp group from Kevin's Bootcamp in North Hollywood, CA went Kayaking at Anacapa Islands this past Saturday.  What an absolute BLAST!!!!!  I've never kayaking before and I am now hooked.  
When we arrived at the dock, we were given a quick lesson on how to hold the oar and the proper way to paddle.  G.J. our instructor was thorough and also really funny.  We had an hour long boat ride out to the islands where they lined up the kayaks and got us sorted with life vests and helmets. After a quick tutorial on how to get into the kayak, we were on our way.
Anacapa Islands are absolutely beautiful.  We explored the caves, saw lots of birds, even managed to get pooped on by one in the, "Good Luck Cave."  Seals give you a really funny look when you get less than three feet away from them!  
G.J. was my groups guide and he was quick to point out anything of interest and all the employees are well versed in facts about the island.
Since my group is highly active, we were given a great workout as G.J. kept us out a bit longer.  End result, we kayaked 4 1/2 miles.  Bonus, on the boat ride back in, we saw dolphins!!!  The  captain slowed the boat so we could all see.  They swam right along with us for a bit.  
I cannot WAIT to go back and do this again.  The price is about $180.00 per person and worth every single penny.  My only advice is wear lots of sunblock, bring an extra sweater, some lunch, and more than one camera!  I went snap-happy and ran out of film by the time we hit the really cool stuff.  Darn, I guess I'm going to have to go back.
Thumbs up, Blue Ocean Kayaking!!!!  Thank you for a great adventure!!!!

Kayak through the caves was amazing.  We went on 11/7/15, there was no wind, no waves, and the weather was beautiful.  Can't complaint.  The crews were nice and the logistic to getting in/out of the boat and kayak was well planned.

The email they sent for instructions, location where to meet is incorrect.  The liability form should be attached to the instructions to be completed prior to the trip to save time for the crew.  

Would love to come back for snorkeling.

This is our third time with Blue Ocean Kayak. It is so amazing to kayak in the caves and see all of the wild life that is right off our beautiful coast. The staff is very nice and making sure your trip is perfect. It is an all day adventure and well worth going with this tour company.

Amazing adventure. 

Their setup with the boat and they swim step is the best available. 
Their staff will make you feel comfortable and give you the confidence to try new things. 

We have done the "Sea Cave" kayaking trips in other places in California always thinking we had a great time... Now I am going to be spoiled, nothing will compare to this trip!

Okay, I am unsure why I am the first to review but here we go....

Friendly Customizable Service.

There I said it, "Friendly."  Everyone from the person on the phone (whom returned my call promptly), the captain and kayaking instructor.  Everyone was friendly with requests.  By requests I mean, the kayaking instructor would hold up for the slowest member of the group (no, it wasn't me - I am "caramel thunder" remember?).  Even when someone had to yak into good ol' mother nature, he was efficient in calling in the cavalry.  Additionally, they were knowledgeable in the sea life, ocean life and local economics. 

If there were to be a "knock," (I am reaching really) - is where you have to meet up in the morning.  It is not like you meet at a building - you meet in the landing parking lot where you're instructed to park in 2 hour parking.  But here is the catch, you get a personal cell phone of the owners.  So either Michelle or Andy will respond.

Overall, I would go back to this company again because they have a private boat instead of one of those big oil rig-type ferries.  So they have the freedom to adjust according to the weather.

Again. I will say it again.
"Awesome."

My husband and I took our 14 year old son and his two best friends over July 4th Weekend.  Blue Ocean Kayaking was amazing!!!  From the minute we got there they were professional and so much fun!  We were first instructed with the proper techniques for kayaking.  The boat ride was quick and easy.  We were very lucky with the weather because the ocean was so calm...like a lake!  On the ride over we saw hundreds of dolphins.  The captain even steered the boat in the direction of the dolphins so that we could get a closer look.  When we got over to the islands we were in for such a treat!  The kayaking was a blast!  The sea caves were beautiful and a sight to see!  We even saw Sea Lions on the rocks in the sea caves!  A highlight was kayaking through the Anacapa Arch!  We have always observed the arch from the boats but to kayak through it was very cool and memorable!  At the end of the kayaking experiencing when everyone was getting settled back on the boat and eating lunch they allowed the boys to jump off of the boat and swim in the ocean!  The boys had an awesome time and my husband and I loved every minute of it!  On the way back to we saw  a Hump Back Whale!  Wow!!!  I highly recommend Blue Ocean Kayaking!
We had a great time. All the staff were very friendly. We want to go back soon!  Don't forget to  bring a change of clothes on the boat. there is a changing room onboard the Sunfish.

I wish I could have done this tour because it looks amazing unfortunately I bought a Groupon and booked the excursion three months before on their own website received confirmation emails and the day of I showed up and there was nothing. After walking around the harbor for 45 mins I finally found a phone number and called. The person who answered said they don't have an office and when I explained myself she abruptly yelled at me and said it's. It my fault there are no tours today it was a mistake  and they will rebook me for another one.
It took three months of planning to coordinate schedules take the day off work and find a babysitter for my special needs child and after the way I was talked To by the company I don't want to deal with them again I want my money back. But of course they have a no refund policy.
As for Groupon when I tried to call them you can not get anyone on the phone and they never return their emails so I tried online chat where they just said you have to deal with the company they will not refund me.
So I'm going to lose my 318$ I will never use Groupon again they have terrible customer service how is it possible there is no one to talk to on the phone wasted endless amount of time trying.  
As for the Blue ocean kayaking the customer service is terrible as well they should have been apologizing to me instead of shifting the blame on me after all it was their own website that booked my trip. Extremely disappointed with how I was treated and filed a complaint with the BBB.

We had so much fun kayaking and blue ocean was a big part of making that happen.  Everything was so easy and organized. From a quick checkin we went to a group lesson on how to kayak. They kept the instructions simple and gave good analogies. Plus, all the guides have a great sense of humor. The boat ride took about an hour, including the times they slowed down so we could watch the pod of dolphins next to the boat. Once to the island they quickly unloaded the kayaks and showed us how to get in. I was worried about falling in, but the crew member held the kayak super tight while I lumbered in (hey, I'm middle aged, allowed to be slow).  They have three guides so each only had seven kayaks to watch. It was amazing to go in to the caves and around the island. The guide went first to check it out so I felt safe!  Saw a baby shark, a seal, fish, many baby birds, and a sea lion came up to our kayak. Even ate some kelp after the guide explained it was safe and showed how to get the tender parts.  Kelp turns out to be bland and salty. But, I ate kelp IN the ocean people! That might make me part mermaid now.  Going through the arch at the end of the trip was awesome. I'm doing this again next summer. Totally worth the cost. 

I'm glad I brought cash to leave a tip. You should too.

Wow! I could not have had a better time if I had paid 1 million dollars for my tour! I was guided by the lovely Miss Dawn. She was so helpful, full of knowledge (not just about the kayaks but the wildlife and the islands we passed by) as well as she made sure that everyone on our tour knew exactly what was going on and how to remain safe at all times. She was so confident in the tour that I was as well. The Captain's name was Andy and he showed us the dolphins on the boat ride out to the islands (Anacapa) We brought our own lunches, which tasted so good after the 3 hours we paddled in the kayaks. I have to say I was quite nervous because I am not the strongest person and I thought this would be hard but it was NOT! It was less work then I thought and I had the time of my life. The caves, the wildlife and all the stories I will take with me forever! Thank you so much for the time I spent  on The Sunfish

GJ leads a knowledgable and fun tour! Paddle through sea caves and check out the sea life. I was lucky to be there when the baby sea lions were nursing, and the island and surrounding ocean was full of them.  All told we kayaked for three hours, mostly calm water. There was an option to go under the Anacapa arch for the more adventurous lot. I recommend not missing out. 

The whole group I was with (nine of us) had fun. At one point a couple's camera jumped ship. Our guide dive on for it. That's commitment.

Very fun for a first time to the islands, caves were fun. However, if you are an experienced kayaker the in-experienced ones will wear you out with zig-zag boating skills and ramming speed techniques. Being able to kayak under the arch at Anacapa was awesome and worth it.

First time kayaking the channel islands.  Professional and fun staff. Knew a lot about local marine life and useless trivia as well.  Will definitely be back to do it again.  Great value, for a memorable trip.

my husband and i love adventure sports and when we read about sea cave kayaking, we knew we had to do it. And i would say nothing would even come close to SPECTACULAR and AMAZINGGGGG than these guys at blue ocean kayaking. they took us ( a group) of kayakers through some wonderful sights ( dolphins saiing with our boat, sea lions in their natural retreat at an arm's distance, world's largest pelicans flying with us as we kayaked through some spectacular caves). These guys are friendly, professional and dont miss the friday special price at $149. ABSOLUTELY WORTH IT. go ahead and make some great memories!

I had such an amazing time on the kayaking adventure at anacapa island! Andy was awesome guide and very knowledgeable. The trip is so well organized and the crew is great. We saw dolphins and seals up close. I couldn't have been more pleased with my experience.

Went kayaking with this group yesterday! WOW! Exciting, interesting, SO different than anything I've done before. I have kayaked for about 15 years, but NOTHIING like this. The ride out was awesome & the skipper found a pod of about 700 dolphins that he took us to. Then we proceeded to Anacapa Island. GJ was a wonderful instructor & leader...great sense of humor, very uplifting, gave kudos when you executed tough moves, gave clear, simple instructions. It was thrilling to be part of nature in such an intimate way. The ocean was smooth, but, there were a big  swells which gave an interesting twist to manuevering. The confidence GJ gave in us to pull through was so helpful & appreciated. I would highly recommend this to anyone with an adventurous spirit.It does require upper body strength. Your calves get wet getting into the kayak. They are sit on top kayaks so your pants will get wet. There were a few teenagers that did awesome. One younger gal (about 10) was in a double kayak & she had a blast! Take munchies as you are quite hungry afterwards. Also, we needed warm cloths for the trip back to harbor (I just took sweats & threw them on). My friend & I will definitely be going back.
I took my girlfriend for her birthday in August and we both had an awesome time. Very friendly staff. Overall great experience.

Definitely  worth the investment! Great adventure. Professional and experienced guides that took us into numerous sea caves and provided us with opportunities to look at sea life up close. This is a must do! THANK YOU BLUE OCEAN KAYAKING!!

My Fiance and I went out this past Saturday and had an amazing time. The crew were all wonderful and very knowledgeable. The caves and tour were amazing to see. This is definitely something we will be doing again. Remember the sunscreen!!

Such a great day! Tour guides were so much fun! Had an amazing time kayaking. Saw a bunch of sea lions while kayaking. We went during high tide which made for great kayaking so if you are in the ears for a while check the tides before going. Beautiful views everywhere I want to go again! Parking was super easy!

What a great day & experience! The team of Dawn, Clare and Jenn did an amazing job taking excellent care of us and showing us a good time! Great time, thanks so much.

Susan

My husband and I went out with Blue Ocean Kayaking today~ It was great. Exactly what an experience like this should be. The boat was clean, comfortable and fast. Captain Andy read the group, and stopped for the perfect amount of time for us to enjoy a pod of dolphins that were swimming along side of us. Their system for getting us and the kayaks into the water was incredibly safe and efficient, our guide had a palpable love of nature and was very knowledgeable and accessible. The tour itself unfolded at a perfect pace. We saw a rookery of sea lions, nesting grounds for pelicans, birds galore, incredible sea vegetation... 
Overall it was a relaxing, yet exhilarating, safe adventure. We will definitely go again.

What an amazing day kayaking at Anacapa in the sea caves with Blue Ocean Kayaking Company.  Captain Andy steered us through a mega pod of dolphins with dolphins in all directions that was breathtaking!  The guides Amy and Andy were phenomenal, educated and lots of fun.   Could not have asked for a more perfect day or a more perfect group to lead us.  Definitely doing this again...and again.....

Kelley Bannon

Booked a Groupon deal to take an overseas guest kayaking.  We had a great day out with the crew on a full boat.  The difference between this and other tour operators is Blue Ocean do not land on the island or use a pier so you have more time in the water.  They launch from the back of their vessel and it is extremely easy to get into the kayak using their launching system.  You literally just sit back into the kayak to get in and lean forward and hold onto hand holds to get out, so it is very easy for the young, old and unsure!  The boat is smaller than other operators so expect a decent swell - I took Dramamine - plus you really feel the swell ocean kayaking too.  All kayaks are doubles, and they will pair you up with a partner if you are solo.  The tour leaders are in single kayaks.

Take food and plenty of water as the Channel Islands are under state park rules, so no food is allowed to be sold in the water/islands.  They have a fridge onboard and plenty of space for bags.  Also, take a change of clothes for the return trip so you don't sit in wet clothes, it can be chilly, wind proof jackets are a plus.  Non slip water shoes are a good idea to as it can be slippy onboard, especially when the boat is rocking.

Arrival time is early, but you can grab a last coffee right at the meetup site, or even have breakfast before you go from the cafe next door to the office.  Public restrooms are here too and I recommend you go before getting onboard as there is only one head and one changing room on the vessel.  The leaders will explain how to use a paddle and how to steer a kayak if you are a newbie, and they are there to help you every step, both in and out of the water.

On our way out we encountered a decent sized pod of dolphins in a feeding frenzy with seals and seabirds all competing for the fish.  The boat slowed down so we could watch and take photos.  Amazing to see.  Plenty of seabirds on the kayak trip, plus you will go past a few places where seals are sunning themselves in bays, one seal came out to watch us and give us a lazy wave with his flipper...lol.  The water was very clear the day we visited and we could see glimpses of fish and star fish as we kayaked along.  You will kayak the length of the island, visiting caves and arches, with a more strenuous stretch in the middle where you cross rougher water.

It is a great trip to take.  Much more to see than just pottering around harbours in a kayak which is what we usually do.  Try it out, you won't be disappointed.  Get a Groupon deal and you will be very happy :P

This outfit is great! Well-organized and friendly. Our guide, Dawn, was incredible! She took us to at least ten caves and was wonderful! They split up groups so you don't have too many people with you. I have done the Channel Islands four times now and this was by far the best experience I've had! Great job!
FANTASTIC, very highly recommended!! GJ was so great and the staffs was friendly and informative. We love the tour and everything was fantastic.

This was one of my most memorable California adventures. The Captian, crew, and guides all worked very hard to make sure that everyone was well taken care of. We had an amazing day. We were lucky enough to see several Humpback whales, dolphins, and Sea Lions as well.

Although I wish the sun was out more, it was out of our control. However the day was well spent. Saw a lot of caves and got in a really good arm workout with the bf. Its worth getting the groupon deal if you can!

This trip was a total ripoff. I would never recommend it. Luckily we got the living social discount but still not worth the price. We did get a bad break with the weather but we were on the boat ride over and back a lot longer then we were in a kayak. There was no apologies or explanations about the weather shortening the time on the kayak. The guides were bad. They didn't explain anything about the island when we were kayaking. We went in 1 cave about halfway. Absolute joke!

We had a great kayaking excursion with Blue Ocean. The staff were all very friendly and helpful. Our kayak guide, Amy, was knowledgeable and a lot of fun. She took us into several great caves and arches, and pointed out all the interesting sea life. This was a great adventure that I definitely recommend! One tip: bring a lot of warm clothing. The boat ride to and from the island is really cold.

great experience, great crew.

My wife and i took advantage of a groupon. they were very accommodating.

Garrett and his team were amazing and this is one of the best tours we've ever done. We saw dolphins, gray whales and actually kayaked with seals right next to us jumping over our kayaks. This was so much more then a kayak tour and worth every penny. Loved the wildlife and the caves and the commentary. A definite must if you are looking for a great tour with great people!!!

This was a really awesome 3/4 day trip (1.5 hour boat ride to the island, ~3 hours of kayaking, and 1.5 hours back to the harbor). The captain brought his super cute black French bulldog on the boat, and she pretty much stole the show. We had really nice guides, but it was basically 1 guy trying to direct ~30 people, which made it nearly impossible to hear instructions or interesting tidbits (and he seemed to know a lot about the wildlife and geography after talking with him on the boat ride over). The large group size made it very crowded at many points, which got annoying and stressful.The other guide stayed in the back with the stragglers, so we pretty much never saw him. We went in at least 15 different caves (usually in groups of 5 kayaks) and the best one was one of the last ones we went in called "Good Luck Cave," where the tide was too low for us to get through by ourselves, so our guide actually stood inside the cave and pushed our kayaks over the shallow part (now that is dedication!). We saw lots of harbor seals, sea lions, abalone, seabirds, and garibaldi (sadly, no dolphins)! One of the best parts was at the very end where we kayaked through arch rock--what a great way to end a 4 mile kayak adventure! 
P.S. Be sure to bring a towel and a change of clothes, because you will be wet everywhere except your torso. When getting in/out of the kayak, you will be in about 1.5-2 feet of water and you must have water shoes (flip flops are fine).
P.P.S. Don't forget to bring some cash to tip!!

What a fun time we had!  The boat crew is great, friendly and very knowledgeable.  It is a long day out so make sure to bring enough food and drink. They will split you up in 3 groups, so if you are with a couple of people, make sure to board your kayaks together to stay together on the water.  It can take a while for everyone to get in their kayaks, but the guys make sure everyone boards & de-boards safely. GJ is awesome and tells the best corny jokes!! Dress in layers and bring something to cushion your bottom, it's a long ride to the island.  
Would definitely recommend this to friends! Great group activity. No experience needed!

Signed up with a buddy and our wives. Didn't know what to expect. This experience was one of a kind. Had a hour long trip to the island, saw sea-lions, and dolphins just on the way over there. Once we got to the island, we got paired with guide Amy, who is a fantastic guide. Amy took all of us on the north side of the island. We pass through caves, seen leopard sharks, and rays about 2-3 ft long. My wife and I felt like the whole experience made us closer than we already are, it's the epitome of teamwork and we're already planning on making this, at least, a once a year trip.

After speaking  with Groupon and they reviewed all the confirmation emails and ruled it was not my fault and are reimbursing me the 318$ so I definitely am appreciative of Groupon and have changed my opinion of them!

My husband and I enjoyed ourselves on this full day journey to Anacapa.  It is an interesting and unique place (I'm not sure I would call it particularly beautiful) and the staff was great to provide some cool facts about the area. Fun to go in and out of the caves and the trip was VERY safety oriented...they are a quality company! We didn't snorkel because the water was super cold (no wetsuits), but the people who did said it was just OK.  Maybe it wasn't a great day for that.  It is a bit of a boat ride out to the island, but we saw a large pack of dolphins on the way back which was a fun way to end the trip.

I had the best nature experience while kayaking out at Anacapa Island. We saw incredible kelp forests, marine life, gorgeous water caves and pristine natural coves. Just give yourself an affordable fabulous ADVENTURE!
Had a great kayaking trip at the Anacapa Islands. Great boat ride over, the Captain slowed down for us to enjoy the dolphins swimming with the boat.  It was an awesome experience with awesome guides!

Recently, I went ocean kayaking with Wild Blue Adventures to Anacapa Island. I highly recommend this very safe adventure, it is definitely a 5 star event! While kayaking, our very friendly and highly knowledgeable Wild Blue Adventures guide, Johnny, introduced our small group of kayakers to several beautiful sea caves, and other fun waterways during our four hours of kayaking. I had a great time, found it well worth the time and cost involved, and will take additional tours with Wild Blue Adventures in the future, at least twice a year. it was great fun and extremely enjoyable! Don't wait, sign up to take a tour soon with Wild Blue Adventures!

We had an amazing adventure on a safe, secure trip with great guides who were highly experienced, friendly, and helpful. We experienced caves, canal challenges, and an easy kayaks tour that lasted 3+ hours. They give you the opportunity to try challenges but give zero pressure for those who just want to chill. We did it all and none of it was dangerous. We have kayaked 1-2 times previously on a lake, so even as novices this is enjoyable and doable. Jordan and Johnny were great guides!!
As a BONUS we got into a few pods of dolphins on the ride back to mainland, and this put the trip from Outstanding to simply amazing. 
#iwillgoagain!

Did this with my wife and 4 of our friends. Drove up from the IE , 2 hour drive well worth it!
What a great crew! Very knowledgeable about the flora and fauna of the island and surrounding area. Going in and out of the caves was awesome, though challenging at times but that made it even more enjoyable. If you are considering this trip, DO IT!  We WILL do it again.

I have to say I had a great time kayaking yesterday!  The caves and sea life was incredible. Johnny and Jordan were awesome guides!  I surprised my husband for his birthday, and then I was sadly surprised with motion sickness :(  But I have to say that Johnny and Jordan were very helpful and very PATIENT with me.
