I eventually had to cut my kayaking short and wait on the boat.  I was very grateful that my husband was able to continue the trip on a single kayak.   The whole crew was nice and helpful. My advice is to take a change of clothing and a jacket for the ride back it could get cold. 
Thank you Johnny and Jordan! We are telling all our friends about our wonderful experience.

My 13 year old nephew recently visited my wife and I from Colorado.  He had never kayaked nor been on a boat in the ocean.  I treated him to a trip to Anacapa Island for a kayak tour through Blue Ocean Kayaking.  The guides were so helpful in explaining the correct techniques for paddling along with the safety issues of kayaking through the sea caves.  Getting in and out of the kayak off the back of the boat was so simple and safe.  You basically just stand on a platform and sit down in the kayak with the boat crew assisting you.  Once in the water, we explored numerous sea caves, paddled next to sea lions and seals, and observed bright colored starfish on the rocks along the island wall.  

On this particular day, there was a fog on the top of the island which gave it a "mysterious' feel.  My nephew thought this was very cool!   Thank you Blue Ocean Kayaking for making my nephew's trip so special!

Absolutely terrific.  Got a groupon and it was worth every cent.  We left the dock around 9AM and returned about 3:30.  Our guides were great.  They made the adventure fun while watching out for our safety.  We kayaked about 4 miles and my arms were very tired during parts of the trip.  The caves were great.  Saw some seals, sea lions, brown pelicans, cormorants, and dolphins.  I would highly recommend a change of clothes for the trip back (at least for the bottom).  You are cold and wet on 1.5 hour ride back.  Would definitely do this again!

This tour is so well run and executed. The crew has the complete process down to a science and the best part about them is that the whole crew seems to be totally stoked on their jobs - everyone is so friendly and excited to share their knowledge of such an awesome place with the tour group.
The actual island tour is unique and unforgettable. How often do you get the chance to kayak through sea caves in a national park? At times it seems almost unreal. Some of the caves are quite large, while other cave sections are so small you actually have to lay back on the kayaks in order to be able to get through to the other side! There are also optional sections (if you don't feel comfortable there are short alternate detours you can take around the sections) on the tour where you get to go through areas with surf and lots of moving water - it really gets your heart rate up.
My wife and I were the last ones through the last cave, so we were lagging a bit behind the group at the end of the day. Garrett asked us if we still wanted to see the large arch at the end of the island. When we said yes, he personally guided us through and around it, and then back to the boat - all the while keeping friendly and informative conversation about the island, the park, and ocean life in general. Totally cool guy who just happens to be the head of the company - a fact that shows in the awesome attitudes of the rest of the crew. Can't wait to do another tour with these guys!

This company is THE best!!! 4 great guys who love what they do. They made an already awesome day of kayaking along Anacapa Island even better with their humor and skills. I highly recommend this adventure.

Great time!  At first was a little leary of boarding in the ocean, but it was a breeze.  The staff was wonderful, it was an amazing trip.  Kayaking out there along the island, the caves, the swells, it was a great day. And a very good workout.  Will definitely be doing it again.

AMAZING ADVENTURE! The guides were very knowledgeable and experienced. They made sure you were safe and didn't pressure you to  do anything you weren't comfortable with. I was able to snorkel with a sea lion! I really had the best day and would totally recommend this to anybody who likes to kayak and experience sea life. Well worth the money! I'm going again for sure.

This is a great ocean and wildlife kayaking experience. The crew and kayak guide are super helpful and friendly and laid back. No pressure, and a completely enjoyable experience. I hope to go back and show friends this fantastic part of Southern California. Thanks!

The team does a great job of providing instructions for first-time sea cave kayakers and is very friendly and knowledgeable. Anacapa island is beautiful, and it was a wonderful day enjoying the island's coastline and sea caves.

I went on this excursion in June, did it again this July and plan to go again in August.  It is a world-class aquatic adventure and worth every penny.   

I live in Ventura and used to kayak Anacapa and other islands on my own.  However, on both trips with Blue Ocean I saw great seascape that I had never seen before.  It was a long and moderately challenging paddle for me at age 70.  But the people younger than me seemed to do fine.  Garrett and the crew are wonderful.  They work hard to make sure everyone has a great time.
This was an amazing trip! So beautiful with a great guide. My 10 and 12 year olds had a great time. Such great memories made and beautiful photos taken.
We had the BEST first time family kayaking experience with Blue Ocean! It's a great day trip. Highly recommend!

The absolute best!  Garrett and his staff made sure every single person had an amazing experience, especially JD.  He had a very brave couple who did not know how to swim!!!  JD gave them the confidence to relax and enjoy the ride.   Their setup is seamless and the one way design traveling downwind and with the current makes it an easy trip for all levels.  Highly recommend!!!
#gowildblue
It was more physical than I anticipated. But a totally great day on the water. There were a lot of people on the boat and not very many places to sit. But would do it again in a heartbeat.

Did this with my wife and 4 of our friends. Drove up from the IE , 2 hour drive, well worth it!
What a great crew! Very knowledgeable about the island and surrounding area. Going in and out of the caves was awesome, though challenging at times but that made it even more enjoyable. If you are considering this trip, DO IT!  We WILL do it again.

Had a great time with Captain Garrett and crew!!!
Nicest people that truly love what they do and want everyone to have the best time possible. They went beyond the "extra mile " to ensure everyone was ok and having a good time. Our group guides Jonathan and Jared were awesome.  Very knowledgeable about the island and patience with all in our group. Will do this trip again with them for sure. Highly recommend this company!!!!
Great scenery.  I had trouble keeping up with the pack.  Garrett made sure i got back safely.

Beautiful day yesterday on the Sunfish.  The crew and guides were great.  It's a special thing to kayak these islands and caves.  Wish I could have snorkeled as well. There was time and a great location  - bring your own mask and gear if you're doing this during COVID restriction.
Great experience with this tour company.  Knowledgeable guides. Lots of time on the water. They send you photos of the trip. Awesome.

Prices are competitive, and they were friendly. I would recommend a few things to have when you go. 
1. Water 
2. Lunch
3. A change of clothes 
4. A jacket 
We went to speak with them and found they have no store front. The whole business is run from their boat.

I can't praise Wild Blue Adventures enough for their incredible kayak tour!! The crew was friendly, knowledgeable, and very helpful. Everything was taken care of for us and I felt completely safe and at ease throughout the experience. The trip included tours through gorgeous tunnels and sea caves, along with facts about the geography of the region from our guide. I was blown away by the rugged beauty of the islands and the assortment of birds, seals, and sea lions we passed along the way. On our way home, we even encountered a pod of dolphins that swam right up next to the boat! All and all an amazing experience, and many thanks to Garrett and Amanda for facilitating it!

Great outfit.  Very professional guides, safety and COVID conscious.  I went on a trip to Anacapa Island in mid-November.  The cruise out was pretty quick.  The wildlife on the way was excellent.  The caves and rocks on Anacapa were wonderful to play in.  I will return again when the Gray Whales begin to head back up the coast.  I highly recommend them.

We drove about 2 hours from Irvine, CA to reach the harbor in Oxnard, CA. We were all prone to sea sickness (highly recommend asking your doctor for that motion sickness patch). We did not see dolphins or anything particularly crazy. The trip can be on the expensive side at about $300 per person, including tip). 

It was the BEST kayaking experiencing of my life. 

The boat was loud and if you don't like the smell of marine diesel, you're gonna want to sit near the front. And if you don't like waves and sea travel, then ...I have a lot of other questions for you. But if you can tolerate about an hour and a half of being on a boat, then you're going to be in for a treat. 

Visiting the Channel Islands is like going to California's version of the Galapagos. It's nearly untouched and it's a nature preserve (being a National Park). So there's a lot of wildlife and limited people. I recommend going in October since it's no longer super hot and apparently we went on a 'perfect day' according to the crew. We briefly saw a Blue Whale en route and it was spectacular. 

I go kayaking several times a season and a lot of the kayak rental places are very hit or miss. The crew at Wild Blue REALLY care about your comfort. They swapped kayaks for me to make sure that I had a good time on the water. That is a HUGE deal, especially if you're going to be on a kayak for several hours going through caves and such. 

If you are afraid of the ocean and tight spaces, going ocean kayaking and weaving in and out of sea waves is going to be a bad time. But if you love adventure and you are a capable swimmer, there's almost no chance of dying because everyone has a life vest plus you're on a kayak. 

I strongly recommend bringing a GoPro AND practice mounting / framing it. It's useless if everything is out of frame or you don't have the right mount. I used a shoulder / backpack mount and I was able to strap it on the chest of my life vest. Awesome footage with awesome people. 

Garret, Mark and everyone aboard the Sun Fish made this a once in a lifetime trip.

I wanted to kayak the caves of Channel Islands, but I didn't like how other companies required you to organize your own transportation to the island first and then meet up with the kayaking company.

Wild Blue is an all-in-one package! You meet them at the port in Oxnard, and you're in their hands from 8AM to 4PM.

The experience itself was amazing! I signed up for cave tours and I got cave tours! We kayaked all the way from the east end of Anacapa Island to the west end, exploring at a minimum 5 caves (with the option of more if you're daring enough).

And the caves all were special in their own way. Spoiler alert if you want to be surprised, but some of the caves had a lobster bed, a turquoise pool, or a tiny tunnel of light guiding you through!

My guide Mark was extremely friendly and knowledgeable. I felt safe the entire time, and never once did I feel like I was about to fall off my kayak. Plus the entire crew was hilarious, cracking jokes every minute!

They've also modified their procedures for COVID as well. There was plenty of room on their boat to maintain social distancing, and everyone's temperature was checked and masks were enforced (except for during lunchtime and when you're on your kayak).

Can't wait for the photos to come out! Can't recommend this experience enough!
We had a wonderful time kayaking through the sea caves and Anacapa Arch.  All the on board staff were excellent.  We will definitely come back and do a different tour with them.

Recently went on a 5 night, 6 day liveaboard trip off the Truth. The crew and captains were all very nice and welcoming. The boat has great safety protocols to ensure us divers were safe throughout the entirety of this trip. Chef Tim was fantastic! I have never had better quality food on a boat trip, and he has definitely set the bar high. We had amazing homemade dishes almost every meal, he had vegetarian and vegan options as well. I would 100% recommend this boat to anyone interested in planning a multi-day dive trip to the Channel Islands.

What a fun time 5 STARS!! Overall the scores breakout: boat was a 3.75, equipment 4.5 and service 5.0. 

Service rocks!  First of all, boat driving is excellent. This is not a push the boat as hard as she will run to get you there as quickly as possible. This is a ride that conforms to the tide and swells. Making the ride smooth and consistent. 

Second, for those of you unfamiliar with sea life, they will spend the time to stop, pursuit and teach you about what is available throughout the journey. I'm an experienced diver so I can tell you, my family who is not familiar benefited greatly and it helped build trust in the crew. 

The boat is open air with a cabin for 10 inside. You will ride outside in the open air with most of their seating in the back.

On arrival and for pickup, they will use their drop deck for easy on and off boarding. 

The tour goes in and out of 10 or so caves and advanced options for the risk takers and will traverse about most of the entire south side of the islands. Finishing with the arch. 

Go with confidence. Ill come back again.

Trip of a lifetime!   We had a perfect day.   Light swell and breeze.   The sea caves were amazing.   I have paddled all over the west.   Anacapa tops every other body of water.

The crew is top notch.   They made the trip comfortable and fun.

Had a fabulous day kayaking Channel Islands National Park, specifically Anacapa Island. We were a group of 8 and had the best day ever! Phil our tour guide took us in and through several caves and passageways, explaining all the marine life and bird species in and around the island. Not only was it a great day of outdoor fun and exercise, but we learned so much about the history of the island as well. Don't the let the price scare you off, it was worth every penny! We had an amazing day!! The crew was great as well!

My family was looking for an outside activity during Covid - driving down from northern ca. This trip made our vacation!  We stopped on the way to the islands to see dolphins right next to our boat.  Garrett and Staff  were awesome and kept everyone safe on the water and on the boat!

Fantastic diving, fantastic crew, and doubly fantastic food!  Eager to return to diving, post Covid (nearly), and post Conception tragedy, I booked my daughter and myself on Vision.  A two-day trip sounded great!  Their safety upgrades were impressive, and their safety briefing and the strongly suggested use of the emergency escapes during the briefing did much to put our minds at ease.  Captain Derek gave a very good and thorough safety briefing.  With my 35 years U.S. Coast Guard experience, I was very impressed.  The crew, Beth, Tim, Dan and Jake were also very impressive.  But again, Jake, the cook, was beyond fantastic, far exceeding good "camping weekend" food, the food was gourmet, from Garlic Quinoa, Bacon Quiche to Tri Tip Burritos, and endless between meal snacks and deserts!  Once I get done with Weight Watchers, I will be going back! By the way, the diving was also fantastic!  Let's go Diving!
Channel Island National Park so amazing and beautiful places.  And The Wild Blue Adventurer was professional. We loved it . Thanks You so Mụch for get we the nice trip .

I had an epic day with my son in late  July 2020.  Our guide Phil was a UCSB grad, marine biologist who was in area, as his normal guiding in Antarctica was limited due to Covid.  The boat staff was knowledgeable, friendly and worked hard to make our day both enjoyable and safe.  Only 8 people on a boat that can hold 30-40 and they were very Covid compliant.  Masks in the boat at all times (except lunch - bring your own food) and outdoor seating but could take off masks in the double kayaks.  Really easy and felt safe and compliant.  10 out of 10 and I'm a tough critic.

We were a group of 4- 2 adults and 2 kids ages 10 and 12. We had an amazing day. The crew and guides Mark and Johnny were beyond fantastic. Super friendly, knowledgeable and patient! We had perfect weather conditions. The boat ride was smooth and enjoyable. Kayaking was so much fun. We saw seals, fish, lobster, abalone, pelicans. Everyone had a blast. I highly recommend to everyone - Channel Islands are a must see for locals and tourists alike!

Awesome one of a kind adventure! This trip provided a safe and much needed escape from quarantine life. Boat takes you to right when your adventure begins. No need to get off the boat to find a kayak everything you need is aboard. Complete with a friendly crew and knowledge tour guide to help you learn about the island and its inhabitants. Can't recommend the cave trip enough and hope to come again!

My husband and I went this last Friday with another couple. I would easily give this tour 10 stars if I could. GJ was the best! He made the trip as awesome as it could be. He clearly loves what he is doing and it shows. We saw several whales on the way along with many dolphin and sea lions. Anacapa Island was beyond gorgeous and the 3 hours went by too fast. I think I could do this every week. Yes, I found my new "Happy Place." 
Suggestions would be to bundle up for the boat ride over to the island. Seriously, bring a coat and blanket. Change of clothes are needed for the ride back since you do get at least a little wet. If the tour could ever provide a lunch, it would be an A+++++. This was well worth the money! Just do it! It's an experience not to be missed!

name: Captain Jack's Tours & Events
categories: Active Life, Wineries, Tours, Horseback Riding, Arts & Entertainment, Food, Hotels & Travel, Event Planning & Services, Wine Tours, Rafting/Kayaking

yelp_reviews.text:
Great experience today at Circle Bar B ranch. My husband and I took our three teenage kids horse back riding today sand had an absolute amazing time ! Jimmy was a great host and was a great pleasure to have as a tour guide ! Definitely recommend if your looking for a wonderful outdoor experience with Mother Nature and all of her beauty in Santa Barbara, CA! 

Thanks Jimmy and Circle Bar B ranch!

Can't really say anything about them because I never did end up going on the tour.  They called me up 3 hours before hand to tell me that the dinner cruise I had booked for the night was canceled due to the coast guard checking out their boats for the next 5 days.  The good thing was that they called me with enough time to spare and figure something else out to do.  The bad thing is I still haven't gotten my money credited back (2 days later now) and the fact that the Coast Guard had to check their boats for 5 days is questionable.

I went on the Sunset cruise with a live jazz band.  The staff on the boat was extremely friendly and easy going.  The views were absolutely beautiful, and the sunset perfect!  I was expecting a much older crowd to be joining us on the boat, especially with the jazz band playing, but the majority of the other passengers where pretty young.  I actually enjoyed that though because everyone was social.  Jessica, the bartender/Gilliganess was terrific!  Great with the drinks and extremely friendly!!!  I definitely recommend the sunset cruise, but I could've done without paying extra for the jazz band, since i could barely hear them from the front of the boat anyway.

I would highly recommend this company if you're looking to tour Santa ynez valley. 
I went with captain jacks because they pick you up from your hotel if you're staying in the downtown Santa Barbara area. Always a plus when you get a dd that's knowledgeable and fun to talk to. 
Went on a Monday holiday with my boyfriend, the guide picked us up promptly at 9am from our hotel by the pier. We picked up another couple a few blocks down and we were on our way. The guide (I wish I remembered his name) asks what your interests are and makes small talk, but it quickly turned into talking to someone you've known for years. 
We stopped at 2 vineyards in the valley. Stopped in on a winery in solvang. Had lunch walking distance from the winery. And went to 2 more wineries walking distance from each other (total 2 vineyards, 3 wineries). From what I know, you're only guaranteed 3 wineries and a choice of a 4th winery or an olive oil tasting. Each winery/vineyard had tasting flights from whites to reds (one even had a port to finish) with about 5-6 pours. Basically, we were lucky and were on a pretty good buzz. Haha. The guide never rushed us, and seemed to know the area wineries pretty well. We ended up getting back to our hotel just before 5pm and were in great spirits. 
All in all, if I'm ever in the sb area and looking for an all day wining, I'll book through captain jacks again! Definitely worth the price and safety !!

Captain Jack's wine tour was an amazing experience for us all and it was all due to our fantastic, kind, funny, sincere and knowledgable tour guide AMIR!!!! He began the day by calling us half an hour ahead of time to ask when we would like him to arrive at our door, and being with a group of 5 hung-over young bachelorettes, we asked if he could arrive closer to 11:30 am than 11. He showed up right when we asked him to and then our fun began! We had only paid for the $50 tour, but just so happened to get a private van even though there were only 5 of us. That was only the beginning...Amir drove us through the beautiful Montecito country side and took us to our first winery: Summerland. Unfortunately we had a little too much to drink the night before and one of girls in our party was incredibly sick and needed to sleep in the van pretty much the entire time. Amir was so considerate and understanding and even took it upon himself to buy her anti-nausea medicine! It was so sweet of him! Amir is an amazing artist and took us on a fantastic, unscheduled but incredibly beautiful detour to see his artwork being shown at a lovely outside gallery. His art was great and we wish we could have bought a piece! He also took us on a tour of the oldest estate in Santa Barbara and helped us take the most beautiful pictures of the scenery and our party. Then he took us to the chalk-art festival at the Santa Barbara Mission and gave us time to wander through and take pictures. We continued on to lunch at Fresco Cafe where he designed many of the fixtures and architecture, then olive oil tasting at Il Fustino and more wineries! 

All in all AMIR was a complete gentleman and a very interesting man. He catered our tour completely to our interests and went ABOVE AND BEYOND to make our bachelorette party special and unique. The wine tour was well worth the money and one of the funnest things I have ever done in Santa Barbara. Please request Amir for your tour, he is the best!!

Peter was awesome! It was his "2nd day" which was HIGHLY suspicious since everyone knew him and he was pretty knowledgable. :) Peter got an A+

The wineries were very nice but we didn't love all the wines there. Most of that was our problem, I think, as we are more Pinot Noir fans and I like sweeter white wines and the Santa Ynez valley is not really known for the Pinot Noir. However, it was as we expected and pretty darn fun! They really could have fit another winery into the mix though for the time we were out. 

Shoestring Winery - Family owned and super cool inside. 
Global Gardens - Olive oil tasting was AMAZING - however I can't personally eat any bread so I was sad! I dipped a few of my cashews into it and bought some oil for home!
Bridlewood Winery - Grounds are beautiful, absolutely beautiful. We did find a Reserve Pinot Noir here that smelled AMAZING. 
Trattoria Grappolo - Yummy lunch stop (not included in tour fee) - A few choices for my diet were available! Yea! 

Side note - as we were leaving we saw Fergie and Josh Duhamel ducking into the bar next door! *swoon* 

The last winery I'm totally blanking on... Oops. It had a horse theme, I think though! 

Then, he offered to stop at this little random place on the way back. We stopped to use the restroom but all of us just wanted to go back home and take a nap! 

Waters were offered from a cooler. The group you are with really makes or breaks it... this one was quiet and friendly so we all had a nice time.

I used Capt. Jack's for a team building event for my company this past week, and they were absolutely fantastic!  I wanted to do something very specific for my team that wasn't necessarily on their listed things to do, but they were very accommodating and did a custom trip just for us.  Working with them was very easy and they did their best to accommodate our budget and everything that we wanted to do.  Our guide, Yenz, was really great as well, and he was a professional photographer that took wonderful pictures of our group during all of our events.  I really couldn't have asked for a better experience, and everybody in our group said they had a wonderful time at our teambuilding event.  I highly recommend them for any event in Santa Barbara that you'd like to do!

Fabulous!! Husband and I wanted to take our 2 small kids kayaking for first time ever. Owner, Captain Jack, was our guide. He was sooo great...patient and kind. He was laid back and wonderful with the kids. He was especially good to me who couldn't paddle as fast as my hubby. And, above all else, he's so knowledgeable about the area. Thanks for such a great time!!!!

I just love Captain Jack's Wine Tour. This was the second time my friends and I did the wine tour with them. Very professional, kind and always on time. Every driver has had a wealth of knowledge at each winery we visited. I would certainly recommend Captain's Jack Wine tour for anyone looking to taste away at the wonderful wineries in SB.

My husband and I took the wine tasting tour with Jens. He was so personable and knowledgeable that we had a perfect day. He pointed out points of interest as we drove along. We took one of his restaurant recommendations and had the best ever dinner while watching the sunset at the beach.

Okay so this gal is s great she is semi new but she is an amazing "chauffeur". The wine tasting is a more friendly drive for a  more private party. It not a bus full it more of a car full. Super nice, great with letting you know when time is closing in. Lunch was amazing! So was the vinegtette tasting. Our Person was Amy  praise

We did the Afternoon Delight Tour.  Our tour guide, Amir did a great job putting together the wineries, then Los Alivos and a stop at Solvang and Cold Spring Tavern on the way back.  We didn't want to block out an entire day so this worked out extremely well--and would recommend both the company and our guide.

Had the best time on our wine tour yesterday. Tracy was the best tour guide ever!  She knew exactly where she was going and had the best recommendations for wineries for us. We will definitely be doing another tour with captain jacks and with Tracy!

We did a Santa Ynez wine tour with Captain Jacks and it was a pleasant experience for the cost.  We visited 3 wineries and had an oil tasting in Los Olivos.  The 3 wineries were very different in size and ambience which I liked (medium size, large sprawling winery and a boutique winery).  The olive oil tasting was delicious.  Our driver was pleasant and primarily just there to drive us.  The restaurant we stopped at for lunch was ok but not the best.  I would use them again for my travels.

We did the Santa Rita tour with Peter - fantastic! We were picked up from Amtrak on time, had plenty of time for 4 wineries (6 tastings each!), a leisurely lunch (with a surprise dessert), plus he even found us a delicious bakery near one of the tasting rooms because he knew my friend & I both have a serious sweet tooth! Fabulous - highly recommend.

Last night I parked across the road from Cpt. Jack's main office.   This morning I went outside and saw they had moved my motorcycle across the street so I would not receive a parking ticket due to street sweeping which takes place on Thursday mornings.  Moving the bike across the street was likely no small task as the wheel was locked in place. 
   The fact that their employees or management would go out of their way, and use their own, unpaid time to help out a complete stranger speaks volumes about their business ethic.
   Thank you!

Me and my husband went on the Santa Rita Wine tour Friday Feb. 11, 2011. Our tour guides name was Tracey. We could not have gotten any luckier. Tracey was awesome, friendly, knowledgeable, fun and an all around great person. She made this tour one of the best times me and my husband have ever had. We visited 4 different wineries and ate lunch at a beautiful golf course. The food at the golf course was excellent. My husband had the Club and I had a chicken bacon sandwich. Our favorite winery was called Alma Rosa. This winery is in the movie sideways and the guy who was in the movie was the one who was pouring our wine. The place looks like a shack in the woods. It is very quite, calm and low key. There is seating in the woods next to a creek. I would highly recommend using Captain Jacks for a Wine tour. Me and my husband are new to drinking wine so this was excellent for just starting out. Defiantly request for Tracey she is the BEST of the Best. No doubt about it you will love her.

Tour guide Tanya took us on a Sideways movie inspired tour. It was a blast to say the least. We were lucky in that we were the only two in the deluxe SUV vehicle. The trip started with Fess Parker where a key scene in the movie played out and covered a couple more significant wineries from the movie. We even got to do olive oil tasting in the middle of the tour. The tour ended with dinner at the Hitching Post restaurant which as many Sideways movie aficionados know is the place where Miles meets his dream woman Maya, the waitress. All in all - a fun trip and kudos to Captain Jack's for making the trip memorable!

Our driver Dennis was awesome.  Just a cool guy.  

For $60 bucks a person we went to four tastings, but I will only count it as three tastings because the first stop was a complete waste of time because the wines there were no good.

Dennis picked my friends and I up at the train station (we took the Amtrak from the Union Station in LA).  From there we went to two tastings, the first of which I mentioned above was a complete waste of time.  After the two tastings we went to lunch and then completed the tour with two more tastings.  Aside from the first wine house the other three had good wine and a good atmosphere.

My friends and I needed to leave the group early so Dennis was more than willing to go out of his way to drop us off at the train station while the others were at the last tasting. 

This tour is great for first timers.

Highly recommend this company. They really go out of their way to make you feel at ease. We went on a tour of 3 vineyards, 1 winery and 1 olive oil farm. Everybody was nice and friendly and the wine was really good.
To my surprise, when the driver came to pick us up, we were the only ones doing the tour. We got to experience a private tour for the price of a regular one.
Will definitely use them again...

Bobby. Bobby is the guy you want. The tour was great. He provided a wealth of information with humor and he kept the tour timely and on track to completion. The atmosphere was relaxed and fun the entire time. We finished up at Firestone and Tommy was fantastic. Fair pour for the tastings and a wealth of knowledge about the wines. There are a few tour companies in the area but overall this one is one of the best. Don't know if they let you choose your guide but if they do - Bobby. But don't tell him we said so...keep him humble ; )

What an incredible experience. 

I was with a group of 8 women (shoutout from Ali's birthday group!) and coming from all different parts of California, all we wanted was a relaxing and super fun day filled with tasty wine and great friends. This tour provided us with exactly what we were looking for -- and much more. I can honestly say that I was very hesitant going into this, but I came out very impressed.

We had Tracy as a tour guide. She is FABULOUS. I cannot stress enough how great she is. Tracy is outgoing, personable, easygoing (didn't have a problem chatting with her for hours!) and is just overall a very genuinely kind individual.

Tracy picked us all up around 9:30 AM in a big van and drove us through the hills (beautiful view) to our first stop which was Foley Estates. We then headed over to a ranch where we had an olive oil and vinegar tasting (much needed before headed on). The olive oil was amazing -- almost all the girls on our tour purchased a bottle -- and the dogs on the ranch were adorable. Not to mention, the owners of the ranch were simply delightful.  We headed on to a few more wineries and grabbed lunch as well (excuse me if the names of the wineries started to slip my mind at this point..), but I do know that the overall experience was great. The lunch wasn't included in the tour, but it was cheap and tasty. Most of us ended up buying at least one bottle of wine on the tour and throughout the tour Tracy was motherly, a ball of energy, and super fun. 

We were with Tracy from ~9:30 AM to 4:30 PM and we never tired of her. In fact, that wasn't enough time! She is a fabulous guide and a beautiful person. Looking back, I honestly have no negative feedback. What a great memory!

One thing to note: a lot of women on our tour were not huge wine drinkers and still had a great time, so I would highly recommend this tour for anyone. The wine was tasty, the scenery was beautiful and the guide was great. 

If you're going to be in the Santa Barbara area and want something fabulous to do, check them out. And be sure to ask for Tracy!

Beer Tour.  Nothing beats getting driven around, drinking, bar hopping, and drinking some more.  Or was it beer tasting we were doing?  We were picked up on time and since it was only two of us, we had the run of the place.  Kevin, our tour guide, was outstanding.  He customized the tour around us and places we wanted to see as well as provided great insight into lesser known areas and beweries of Santa Barbara.  He was personable and friendly, getting us a behind the scenes look at Island Brewery and a personal talk through by one of the owners.  Great tour and a great time.
Had such a great time wine tasting with the group and our guide Tracy. It was awesome, chill and def a great value. We will be back!

We went on a wine & olive oil tour this past weekend in Santa Barbara for a Bachelorette Party! From the very beginning, when I first called to make the reservation, I had excellent customer service from the gentleman that works in the office (His name starts with a C I believe, I can't for the life of me remember his name!). He was incredibly friendly and accommodating , I'd called 3 other places and he is the reason I chose Captain Jack's. That excellent customer service continued when we were picked up by Hank for our tour on Saturday! Super cool guy! As we drove around he even gave us some history and facts about Santa Barbara, had ice cold water for us (as we would need!) and took us on a couple spots not on the tour like the mission! He brought us to a park up on the mountain that looked over all of SB, he drove us to the Biltmore right on the water so we could take pictures, he was amazing! Every stop, they were waiting for us, ready to go, tastings & everything all set up, it was really awesome! Hank was an amazing tour guide and had no problem being our personal photographer! While we were at our second stop he even called and made a lunch reservation for us! :) Can't wait to come back and do another tour!

We did the afternoon delight wine tasting tour yesterday with Amir and it was fantastic! We got lucky and were the only people on the tour so Amir was super flexible with us. We went to 3 wineries and an olive oil place, and stopped at a couple of other places to take pictures along the way. Amir was very personable and a great photographer. He went above and beyond showing us a few cool local places as well. We would highly recommend this tour with Amir to anyone considering a wine tour!
Captain Jacks is the BEST!!!! Tracy is the best tour guide and the sweetest! My best friend and I take the wine tour every summer! I wouldn't do a wine tour without them!!!

Our tour guide Jens was the best, definitely request him as your guide!!! We went on a weekday and ended up getting a private tour it was great just the two of us! Jens was really knowledgeable about the wine and everything to do with Santa Barbara. It was our first trip here and it really helped to go on this tour plus Jens picked us up and dropped us off at our hotel no worries about driving after a full day of tasting.

This comapny provides the most fun, relaxing & knowledgeable tour of their local wineries & oil tasting ever! The tour guide Tracy was wonderful, smart, fun & a delight! I totally would recommend them!

On a whim my friends and I decided to venture up to Santa Barbara for my birthday. We were so lucky to be able to book a wine tasting tour with Captain Jack's on short notice, especially because we hadn't done our research and didn't realize that there would be a huge Summer Solstice event in Santa Barbara that day. We visited three wineries and an olive oil tasting around Santa Ynez. Our guide, Guy, was fantastic and very knowledgeable about both wine and the Santa Barbara area.  We had the best time and we're already planning to do this again before the end of the summer.

I did the Santa ynez valley wine tour with a great group and a knowledgable tour guide. What was the most convenient was that the tour guide picked us up from our hotel and dropped us off at the end of the day. My favorite part of the day was at the Gainy winery that gave us a tour of the process of making the wine. I'm a wine lover and I enjoyed the different wines I got to sample. I wish I got to learn more about the wines as I was tasting them. The olive oil tasting was a nice part of the tour as well.

We booked a local brewery tour with Captain Jack's.  The Tour guide was Guy, he was great, super accommodating and nice.  We went to three different breweries, he had us sit down and then went and got our tasting flights set up for us, and brought them out to us!  I didn't set up our lunch ahead of time, but he went and checked out local spots and gave us options, we ended up eating at the brewery we were at (Figueroa Mountain), who also had the best staff and service plus delicious beer.  As the day wound down he was open as to what time we needed to be done and where to drop off.  Also the office staff at Captain Jack's is so flexible and helpful beforehand.  I highly recommend them and will use them again for future needs, I will use them over any other brewery tour service in town, as all the others were not helpful, and expensive without the inclusion of tasting fees and service!

4.5

came to Santa Barbara and joined Captain Jack's tour for a day
it was pretty nice not having to worry about driving while drinking...getting lost...finding parking...etc

we did the the glider ride, Santa Rita Hills wine tour and we ended the tour with the sunset cruise
our tour guide was very friendly and informative..let me plug in my ipod hehe =P  

glider ride was pretty awesome! though i didn't get asked to "steer" the glider...apparently it is offered??
you see majority of santa barbara from a bird's eye view and it feels really majestic =)

Santa Rita Hills tour was pretty good too...unfortunately i dont' remember too much about the actual vinyard and wines...not yet a connoisseur sorry...but i enjoyed the tours...and the sample munchies =P

the sunset cruise well...i think it was bad timing on our part...we took the 7 or 730 PM cruise...and the waves were quite choppy and it was really cold [october]...

explanation for the .5 penalty...but still 5 star. rating..

-1 star = unfortunately our ride told us that since our hotel is really close to the sunset cruise area...he asked if it was ok for him to let us walk back to the hotel...well...obviously we didn't know how far it really was...we got slightly lost...and uhm...not too thrilled about that...we thought it'd be a nice breezy stroll...but then after the cruise we were so cold...i really had no desire to walk....so the car would have been helpful =(

.5 bump back up...few weeks later they found my camera wedged inbetween the seat and mailed it back to me =) 

but they really deserve a 5 overall as everything prior to being stranded was a great experience..our guide intro us to all the venues and made sure we were taken care of...so i would not mind coming back and going on some more tours with the captain's crew =)
and this time...i'm afraid the tour guides needa stay with us until we are safely back at our hotel =X
Had a wonderful time kayaking with them about a year ago... I had instruction on paddling and then we were off to enjoy paddling out to the 1 mile bouy around the wharf and through the marina..

Great tour!!!
Tracy was amazing. She was easy going and knowledgable and just made our trip so fun. The wineries were beautiful. No complaints.

Our tour was amazing! We went to 3 tasting rooms. We had Tracy as our guide & she was amazing!! She really got to know us, made us feel so comfortable & was willing to go above & beyond. I recommend asking for Tracy if you use this company.

We joined the group wine tour with Tracy last Friday through the Santa Ynez/Los Olivos area.  Our itinerary:

Shoestring Winery - Nice but nothing really special
Lincourt Winery - Loved the wine and the grounds
Global Gardens - Olive oil tasting was cool but I would have skipped it
Trattoria Grappolo - Excellent lunch stop (not included in tour fee)
Bridlewood Winery - Grounds are beautiful but wines were just average

Tracy was a great tour guide and I have absolutely no complaints about her - she was really nice and upbeat.  I will not be back to Captain Jack's however.  I have been on both the Wine Line and the Grape Line and consider them to be far superior wine tours in the area, visiting at least 8 wineries each time - AND I was also able to choose some of the wineries we visited.  

With Capt Jacks, they choose the winery for you and further, WAY too much time is spent at each winery.  3 wineries in an 6-7 hour period?  It just isn't enough variety.  I pay for tours so I can really sample different wineries and learn about the area without worrying about driving.  3-4 wineries is about how many I can do by myself in a day!

I have nothing but good things to say a out this place

 Originally we wanted to do the horseback riding followed by wine tasting but they were fully booked . We decided to just do the wine tasting tour . 

Started at 930 am by going to Santa ynez where we went to a few wineries. We then went to lampoc for some more wine tasting followed by a olive tasting ranch in Los Olivos. The owners of the ranch are soooo nice and they have the cutest babies I mean dogs haha very friendly and beg for you to pet them. We then went to an Italian restaurant trattoria grapollo where we had lunch . After lunch we went to the last winery and returned back to our Parking. I will def return but this time will try the horseback riding tour . Just one suggestion to the owners ...please change or wash your vans because they smell.

My friends and I booked a reservation for 7 people for the kayaking tour and it was SO FUN!  Our guide, Guy, made it a fun and relaxing experience for us girls.  We even saw a whale and sea lions while we were touring the harbor.
Had an amazing wine tour with Tracy as our tour guide. Went to amazing wineries featured in the Sideways film. Tracy was very knowledgeable and fun!!!
We had such a nice time. Our tour guide, Christopher, was great. He made our day extra special.

This is a great tour for the price.  

We did the evening $50 wine tasting where it takes you to 5 wineries.  Later on in the evening I wished I had done the afternoon one where they take you to more wineries.  

This service is awesome cause we stayed outside STATE street and they actually came to pick us up from our hotel.  I was surprised because there were other tour services that didn't want to pick us up cause we were too far outside of state street. 

Each place they took us was a huge lesson about wines and HUGE pouring of wine.  

Now granted if you want a limo trip then this is not for you cause it's a VAN that picks you up for the wine tastings. 

The people were great at each winery and I can't wait to go back again and do the afternoon wine tour.

Amazing people to deal with, they really take the time to get to know you and your preferences to take you to the winery you would like. No like some business where they want you in and out and take your money. Totally went above and beyond in service. Highly recommend taking there tours

Lisa I.
Captain Jack's is the BEST!!! 
My friend and I booked the "Wine Country tour" and it was great. Bobby was our driver and he really went out of his way to ensure we had a good time and great experience. Bad jokes aside he was a lot of fun (sorry Bobby ;)). The staff is professional, knowledgeable, and extremely helpful. We visited several different wineries in and around Santa Ynez and had lunch at a great Italian restaurant. It was delicious. I'm still talking about the pizza and salad :) Please don't get discouraged when you read a not so nice review,it is unfortunate. But you cant go wrong with Captain Jack's!!

Mark was our tour guide and it was an awesome tour. We went to 4 different wineries: Foley, LaFond, Melville, and Amor something... It was my first time in Santa Barbara and wine tasting! I thoroughly enjoyed it and thought Santa Barbara was beautiful! I feel like I'm not that knowledgable regarding wine but I have learned a lot from the tour. Mark was very knowledgable. He even drove us to the store to buy more alcohol after the tour and showed us good places to eat on State St. I had a wonderful weekend and experience! Thank you Mark! :)

Had an amazing experience on the Santa ynez wine tour this weekend.  Tracy was an awesome guide and made the experience that much better. Went to 3 wineries and a olive oil tasting.  Weather was perfect and everyone was super nice.  Thank you for such an amazing unforgettable time!!

My sister and I took a tour with Captain Jack's during a weekend getaway for our birthdays during the summer.
We had so much fun! Our guide was Meghan, she was very friendly and knew a lot about the area. She suggested a great lunch spot and places for us to check out during the rest of our stay. 
The whole experience was great! From the booking to the end of the tour. Top shelf service.
I would definitely do this tour again! 
Thanks for the fun!

We took the wine tour from Santa Barbara. It was amazing. We stopped at 3 wineries and one olive tasting place. The tour guide was so nice and she even offered to stop to let us get lunch because we forgot ours. She was very knowledgeable and friendly. We had a blast. You should definitely take a Captain Jack's tour.

Set up a friend's bachelorette party through Captain Jack's.  They were AMAZING.  Price was excellent, most affordable in Santa Barbara (trust me, I SEARCHED.)  We started out as a huge bachelorette party, but due to lots of confusion and scheduling conflicts, our numbers were fluctuating up until literally two days before our tour.  Andrew who answers the phones was nothing but patient (had to keep calling him with questions, cancellations, changing numbers over and over and over and over...)  Chris the tour guide was AWESOME, did a great job showing us around the vineyards, was friendly, helpful.   Captain Jack's even supplies water and snacks, which after the 2nd and 3rd winery is very helpful! Went to 2 wineries in the morning, had lunch at the 2nd winery, then headed to Los Olivos for final tasting/olive oil tasting.  Los Olivos Grocery has waters and sodas, too, and there's a really cute little coffee shop next to the olive oil place.

My husband and I loved the Top Deck Tour with Captain Jack's!  We were picked up right on time at our hotel in the morning and our guide drove us to Refugio Beach for kayaking.  He gave us instruction on how to navigate the kayak and toured us along the coast and around the kelp beds.  The water was really clear and calm.  I was surprised that we could see fish and even had a close up visit from a seal.  The guide was very knowledgeable about the area. It was a very peaceful way to start the day!

Our guide then took us to a nearby resort for a picnic lunch.  The food was delicious, large portioned and very reasonably priced.  We really enjoyed it.  Lunch was followed by a 1 1/2 hr. trail ride at a nearby ranch.  The horses were great and the views were very beautiful.  Definitely comfortable for someone who is a novice rider.  

Next, we were driven up the lovely coast to a couple of very nice wineries.  We felt like the guide took the time to understand our taste in wine and selected the wineries based on what would be most appealing to us.  He really made sure we were taken care of and knew a lot about vineyards and wine in general.  The day was a great balance of adventure and tranquility- it was just what we needed.  We would certainly recommend this tour!

Sounds like the wine tasting tours at Captain Jack's are great BUT DO NOT book a sunset dinner cruise with them. I reserved 2 weekends ago and they called me the day before we were scheduled to go and told me that the cruise was cancelled because of the coast guard. Coast guard? Sounds kind of fishy. Didn't think much about it and booked again for the next weekend. Once again they called the day before and said the cruise was cancelled because of maintenance problems! Doesn't make sense to me 2 weekends in a row! AND if they knew they weren't gonna be able to use the boat why schedule a cruise and have people booked and then disappointed when you call them and say it's cancelled. Pretty frustrating!

WARNING:
This company also sets up the same cruises with Santa Barbara Sail Center (www.sbsail.com) so be aware that if you reserve a cruise with them it will be cancelled as well.

Chris was the best tour guide ever. This tour is really great for first time wine tasters. Chris was really great and even showed us some tricks to make our wine tasting experience better. We made him really late to pick up the other couples, but Chris was still very friendly and did not make us feel uncomfortable at all  we were paired up with two other couples and had such a great time meeting them. Definitely an experience we won't forget.

We did the Santa Ynez Valley Wine Tasting Tour and had such an amazing experience. Our tour guide was Tracey and she was the best. I proposed to my girlfriend at the second vineyard we went to and not only was it beautiful and a great moment, but Captain Jack's and Tracey helped me plan it out. It wasn't just the great service, but the trip itself was so much fun. They picked us up at our hotel, knew a lot about the area, took us to some great places that had great wine as well as an amazing lunch and olive oil tasting. We will definitely be going back soon. Just a great experience with people who cared and made sure that you were well taken care of.

My husband and I celebrated our  5 year anniversary and booked the award-winning Wine Country tour & it was worth every single dime. First of all we had so much fun, our tour group was only six people which was very nice, a small group but still very fun. Melissa who was our tour guide was such a sweetheart she was very professional very sweet and fun, she would talk to us all day and joke around and laugh at our silly jokes. 
We went to 4 wineries and to an olive oil tasting. We were also driven to a restaurant to have lunch. The lunch was okay, we paid about $30 a plate. 
I would recommend that if you don't drink a lot of wine or aren't used to alcohol that you take it easy because by your fourth Winery you will start to get a little bit dizzy but very happy!

My overall experience was great. We had a lot of fun visiting different wineries & bought overpriced souvenirs of course, Got to taste delicious wine as well as learned a lot about wine! we will definitely be going to Captain Jack's for our wine tasting.

I brought my girlfriend up to Santa Barbara for her birthday. We have never been wine tasting so this was our first experience and I have to say, we had Tracy as our tour guide and she was absolutely amazing. She was very friendly and knowledgeable about the wineries and surrounding areas. She gave a courtesy call informing us of her arrival time and she wasn't late. She made the day fun and I would highly reccomend you request her as your guide. I know when we come back she is going to have a request from me. Thank you for making our day memorable Tracy..
My sister and I went kayaking in the Harbor with Matt on Saturday morning and had a blast! He was an awesome tour guide and made our trip really fun! I highly recommend this tour!

Our driver was Amy... She is amazing! We went there for my 22nd birthday. She was prompt when she picked us up and really upbeat. The wine tasting is a more friendly drive with some strangers, who you soon befriend. I would specify if you wanted it for a more private party. It's a really nice air conditioned car, very clean. She was very nice and polite. Amy gives a little heads up before that stop at a winery is done. Lunch was amazing! So was the vinaigrette tasting. This was a great trip!

Traci was an amazing guide!  She was very friendly and fun. We were in a small group of seven, but the amount of fun we had was like that of a group of 20 close friends!  Traci kept us on a good schedule but allowed for plenty of time at each location. We went on the Sideways tour, and ended up at dinner at the Hitching Post. I had some seared Ahi, and that awesome dinner topped off the great all-day excursion. It was very nice to have this door to door service and not have to worry about a thing. The only downfall was that I love cabs, but there weren't any served at any of the 4 tastings, however, other great wines were available. If you can, request Traci!  You will have a blast and gain an unforgettable experience. We will definitely do this again!
Jens (yens)  Is the BEST tour guide!!!! We highly recommend him as your guide, for the wine and horseback ridding tour.

I live in Orange County and was in Santa Barbara for the weekend to celebrate my boyfriend's birthday. I knew we would be doing the Urban Wine Trail (a walk to up to 8 wineries on the street adjacent to State St.) so I knew we would want a break from wine and opted for the beer tour. We were packed like sardines into the SUV they picked us up in, but we were all excited and head on our way on time and with a nice guide. The first stop was in Carpenteria at the Island Brewing Company. This place was awesome - really explained the beers, the process and even took us on a tour of the brewery. This is what I expected of a beer tour.. everything was downhill from there. The driver than took us back to State St. breweries that were all wrong for many reasons: 1) We could of walked there on our own since we were staying on state street 2) Nothing was explained to us about the beer or the brewery 3) They were basically restaurants that happened to have a lot of beer on tap. We told the driver she could leave and take everyone home since we were walking distance to our hotel. I would do this tour again on two circumstances - they take us to places that are NOT walking distance to where we are staying and the places we go to are excited to have us there and tell us about their beer. Side note: the group you go with or happen to ride along with can make or break the trip. Ours unfortunately broke the trip..... Pro's: The guide was nice, car was clean, first brewery was ideal.

Perfect- Tracy was top notch.  Everything was perfect.  Make friends with your fellow tasters as it makes the tour better (obvious statement of the day).  Tracy was on top of it and couldn't have been better.  

If I had any recommendation I would suggest a wine/beer tasting hybrid.  It would be cool to drink wine in the morning, lunch and then hit a craft brewery or two.  The ladies could find a fruity beer.

Anyway- great hostess, great trip.  Would do it again and again and again and again.

If you can request Tracy you will not be disappointed.  We have been on the tour 3 times now and Tracy by far is  the best tour guide we have had. It seems like she knows everything  about Santa Barbara and she took us to only the best spots. She was awesome  and we can't wait until next year to do it again.

Had a great time with my wife. We got to visit 4 wineries and also a olive tasting . Amir the tour Guide was a great,he made our day. I  recommend you ask for him and you won't be disappointed. I am planing my anniversary next month and my wife and I are super exited . Once again Thanks Amir.

My husband and I selected Captain Jack initially because of the huge selection they had for tours and we really enjoyed the detailed description they have on their website. We had never done an adventure tour before and this was the best decision we could have made. The tour was Heaven on Earth Kayak & Horseback Riding and we had the most entertaining guide (Guy) we could have asked for. We will definitely be back next summer and plan on making Captain Jack tours a regular part of our Santa Barbara vacation experience!

Awesome day!  Captain Jack's sideways tour is a fantastic value.

It was my friend Jyl's birthday weekend and 4 of us girls were lookig for a fun and relaxing day of wine tasting in Santa Ynez Valley.

First, we  were impressed that we got lucky and ended up with a private tour. We got picked up from our hotel in SB in an SUV and was able to see wine country in style.  Favorite part was the Alma Rosa stop as well as Sevtap tasting room in Solvang. 

Our wine tour guide, Jens, was absolutely amazing!!!! He was super knowledgeable about wine, very fun and conversational and went above and beyond to accomodate our needs---from multiple picture taking spots to wine purchase recommendations to making the tour flexible to our interests too- we loved it!!!  Jens is a great asset to Captain Jacks. He made this an amazing day for us. Please extend a special thanks for making this birthday celebration special!

We had a blast! My boyfriend and I didn't know what tour to choose and we are so happy to have chosen the Santa Ynez wine tour. Our tour wouldn't have been the same without our awesome tour guide Kevin!! Kevin was so knowledgable and went out of his way to make our experience amazing!

8 friends and I did their Santa Rita Wine Tour this past Sunday.  Everyone of us had a blast, but not just because of the great wine and company.  Our tour guide, Iaon was AMAZING.  He made the day infinitely more fun.  I would suggest just going on whatever drinking tour he's leading and forget about whatever other tour you had your heart set on!  We'll be coming back up for sure to take the Santa Ynez tour and will definitely be requesting Iaon as our guide again!

My best friend and I take an annual trip to Santa Barbara for some "girl time". We decided to take a wine tour through Captain Jacks. The whole experience from the booking phone call to the end of the tour was nothing short of fabulous. Our tour guide Jens was awesome! His knowledge of the wines and the Santa Barbara area was impressive. He was easy going and willing to make an extra effort to make sure our trip was enjoyable. We will definitely make Captain Jacks a part of our annual Santa Barbara trip!

We had an unbelievable amount of fun on this tour.  Captain Jack's Tours took us to 3 incredible vineyards and we had a ball.  Our tour guide, Chris, was a great sport.  He was very knowledgable and a pleasure to be around.  My fiance and I took our mothers on this tour and they LOVED it.

"SPANK THAT BOOTY!! SPANK HIM!!! HE LIKES IT" Yelled our Tour Guide on our horseback riding trip in Santa Barbara. 

Seriously.. no joke! LOL!! THat pretty much summed up my experience on Captain Jack's Horseback Riding experience. We made a reservation for 11:30 a.m. and paid $55 for horse back riding experience. The Ranch is located on the scenic Central Coast of California - approximately 2 hours from Los Angeles, and only 30 minutes from Santa Barbara. I was a little nervous going on a horse back riding trip because first of all, I don't want to get hurt, and second of all.. well .. I dont want to get hurt. LOL!!

All of us got to the ranch and the first thing we noticed was how potent the ranch smelled.. it just smelled very.. uh.. naturey? .. and.. like horses? among other things.... LOL!! We each got to pick our horses.. I think mine was called "Biscuit" .. damn that horse LOVed to eat. It was kind of funny to see how the horses kind of matched the personalities of its horse rider. My friend who is more chill got the really friendly and calmed natured horse. My other friend got the really stubborn one and I got one that just LOVED to eat. Go figure right? Anyways, the whole ride took about an hour and a half and TRUST ME, that is seriously more than enough time of horse back riding.

 I was already over it after an hour cause your ASS seriously hurts like a mother after an hour on a horse. It is not the most comfortable ride and prepare for you to have back-ache and your butt to ache after the ride. My horse was not only a piggy but he was also stubborn and did watever he wanted, including running toward a branch that was heading toward me. I seriously had to pull a Matrix style move in order to avoid getting knocked off of my horse. Seriously holding the lease for dear life!! Since some of the horses lagged, I didn't really like how our tour guide kept telling us to "SPANK ITS BOOTTY!!! HE LIKES IT!! " Seriously? I don't think he likes his booty whipped.. maybe I don't know so much about horses but.. I just didn't feel comfortable in whipping his butt really hard.. So yea.. pretty much a one time deal for me.

 I mean, don't get me wrong, the scenery was super nice and you do get a chance to see the entire ocean view of Santa Barbara which is gorgeous and get to ride across different streams and be in one with nature. Overall it was fun and the people there were friendly. Definitely a unique experience if you have never done horse back riding before. But, I think I'll stick with the wine at Santa Barbara next time. At least wine will listen to me... well, sometimes. =)

*Tip- Take coastal Highway 101 to Refugio State Beach (Exit 120). Exit and turn right, then drive 3.5 miles up the canyon on Refugio Road. For further directions please look up 1800 Refugio Road, Goleta, CA 93117, however, they do not recommend using a GPS to get there, as often times it will lead you the wrong way (depending on what kind of GPS it is).

We did the Santa Rita Hills wine tour and stopped at 4 different wineries. 


Cons: 
The Car was a bit small for 8 adults 

Pros: 
We didn't have to drive 
Our guide Mark was very friendly and knowledgeable about the area and wine. 
We made friends with our fellow passengers.

Went out on a kayak tour with Guy last Saturday. It was a perfect day to be on the water. Guy was awesome. Though this was not a private tour, there was just 3 of us including Guy and we had a blast. He is very knowledgeable about the boats in the harbor and about the area in general. Being out on the tour with him was like hanging with a friend. Would highly recommend the company and the guide..... Thanks for a great time.

PS: To the jerk from another tour company who I simply asked directions of and he told me he had never heard of Capt.Jacks and sent me off elsewhere, be assured I know the name of your company and would never recommend them to anyone.

We arranged a private tour with Captain Jack's for a party of 8 for our dad's 85th.  The picked up and drop off so nobody had to drive from Westlake Village.  Our guide was Tracy and she was great.  We did four wineries and olive oil tasting with lunch at Fess Parker's Ranch.  This gave us the time to relax and just enjoy the wine tasting in a park like atmosphere. I would recommend this tour to anyone who asked as it was reasonably priced.
We had fun . Thanks to Amit . Very professional and caring . Good driver and tour guide . Even gave a free wine glass . Will do it again with friends
We loved our tour and Tracy was wonderful.  She helped us understand the wines we were tasting and also gave us many options.  We were so, so impressed.

Had a wonderful wine tasting tour this weekend.  Our guide, whose name I have forgotten, was great.  He was the perfect mixture of talkative, yet also allowing the individual groups to talk amongst themselves.  One tasting room we went to was outstanding, another pretty good, and then two were not the best, but that isn't the company's fault.  

Would definitely recommend them to anyone looking for a tasting tour.

Went on my first wine tour this weekend as part of a bachelorette party. Captain Jack's was awesome - the tour guides were friendly, courteous, and knowledgeable. The pace of the tour was perfect - we toured three wineries, stopped at a restaurant for lunch, went to cold spring tavern, and were back in Santa Barbara before sundown. Also, we went on a really hot day and were so thankful that the van was covered and had AC when we were driving from one stop to the next! Overall, the experience was awesome and I'd go again with Captain Jacks in a heartbeat!

Again... 

Another wine tour with Tracy. 

Couldn't ask for more... I seriously love this woman !!!!! 

She makes the experience what it is. I'm forever loyal to captain jacks due to Tracy... Her education on wine, insight to the character of each town, and genuine willingness to accommodate you and make your trip as amazing as possible... She is unmatched!!!! Seriously.

Santa Ynez wine tour is an incredible value and a great time. You will leave with irreplaceable memories. 

Thank you Tracy again!

Great tour by Donna around the town to three local breweries! 

Hubby loved this for our one year anniversary trip... $60 gets you a few tastings at three breweries. You pay for lunch at one of the spots. I was drunk by the first stop.... the tasters are HUGE!! 

If you're not from the area, this is the way to go if you want to get a little buzz going off some great local brews and see the sights of Santa Barbara and the surrounding cities.

Peter our tour guide made the day perfect for my friends bday and all the girls. He was very attentive and took us exactly to places we loved. He also educated us on the wineries, wines. I would recommend him to everyone. He will make your day perfect and very enjoyeable

This review is for the afternoon delight tour. We had a great driver and we enjoyed the wineries we went to (LaFond, Roblar, and I can't remember the third) but the vehicle itself made the experience less enjoyable. It was a stuffy van with no windows to open. We started off in the backseat and every bump we went over was like an earthquake. Would recommend if you want to save money (hello $99 for 3 wineries and an olive oil tasting!) but not if you're looking for a higher end experience.

My parents and I really enjoyed an all-day vineyard tour led by Tracy in May 2011.  Tracy made our day so remarkable and memorable.  She was punctual, polite, kind, friendly, genuine, funny, and very knowledgable about the wine and local cultures.  We spent all day laughing, having great conversations while learning about some of the best wineries in the Santa Barbara area.  I had a chance to go back to SB last weekend, and joined a half-day wine tasting tour with some other customers (strangers) led by Tracy again.  She made us feel very comfortable, and the other customers and I were enjoying chatting and laughing together instantly.  If you get a chance to visit SB, be sure to call here and ask for Tracy - you'll be glad that you did!  All the other staff members I've spoken with on the phone (Luis, Ricky, Andrew) are  professional, friendly and helpful as well.  Thank you, Captain Jack!  We'll be back!

So Mark took a group of us out to wine taste on 7/3/2011 and it was the greatest experience of my life. Well pretty close. He was fun, knowledgeable, exciting, entertaining, an trustworthy. If you are looking to hit up some wineries in SB.... Ask for mark :)
A great experience for people who wanna try out horseback riding for the first time!  Great views along the coast!
Great Sideways tour.  Tracy is an excellent guide and lots of fun.

We had a great tour with Amir.  He was incredibly friendly and knowledgable.  He was really flexible and ensured we saw all the amazing things we had intended to see during the day.  He made it fun as well!  I would definitely highly recommend going on a tour with Captain Jack's and ask for Amir.
Great time! Enjoyed the boat, friendly crew & beautiful sunset.  We saw some sea lions & dolphins for an added treat. Cocktails were cold & reasonably priced.

My girlfriend and I took the tour through the Santa Ynez valley back in March. It was our anniversary weekend so we wanted to do something fun. It was our first time doing a wine tour so we didn't know what to expect. However it turned out to be the best time ever!

Tracy was our guide, and we couldn't have been blessed with someone better. She is awesomely funny, and a great resource on wine. If I am able to take this tour again, I am always requesting Tracy.

The Santa Ynez tour was also great too. Pretty much every stop was a quality drink, and good conversation with some random bar strangers. The scenery was nice of the vineyards. Couldn't ask for more.

My mom and I went for our first wine tasting and were beyond satisfied with our experience with captain jacks tours. We had Amir as our personal chauffeur AND photographer and he was more than we could have wished for. So pleasant, funny, and interesting to talk to as well. Was a little nervous since we were the only two doing a tour that day (woohoo for midweek if you're looking for an absence of crowds!!) but he made us feel like queens and was happy to accommodate all of our wishes. He had a lot to offer in knowledge about the wineries and the general area as well. Will definitely be requesting him if we decided to do another, which very well may be in the future.

I am a wine beginner and we used Captain Jack's to help us tour the Santa Barbara wineries. Our tour guide was extremely informative and helped us customize our tour to the taste and personalities of the wineries that we would most enjoy. Our experience was absolutely incredible. The wines were fabulous and the tastings were fun and highly engaging.  We were a small group so the tastings went quicker than normal and our guide was able to help us squeeze in a incredible Danish bakery, a olive oil tasting and probably the best Italian lunch that I have ever had. (No exaggeration on the lunch at all)

I spent way more money than I thought I would during this whole tour, but I could not help myself. When you find treasures, you want to share them with your family and friends. Although I did not buy something at each stop, I cannot wait to share my goodies and stories. 

Thank you captain Jack, I will definitely be back!

Great value! My girlfriend booked 2 tours on the 4th of July holiday. 

On the 4th, we went on the beer tour. Not only were they great spots but it was perfect timing. The credit goes to Tracy. Figueroa Mtn Brewing was our first stop but had we gone toward the end it wouldve been the chaotic party crowd. I was looking forward to that place the most and Im I showed earlier enough to appreciate and relax to some good beer. We had a awkward moment at Brewhaus where for reason the small group that was there wasnt too welcoming. Tracy rescued us and took us to Union Ale we they had some great food along with beer.

On the 5th, we went on the Santa Ynez Tour. Yanez (butchering his name) was great and fun. He is trying to get his wine expert certification, so he knows his stuff. The great thing is he kept things simple in explaining the wine process. Very engaging with our group.

Next time Im in SB, Im using you guys again!

We took the basic wine tour through Captain Jack's and had a great time! Our tour guide was very knowledgable about the area and told us some cool spots in the area to check out on our own time. The wine tour took us to three wine tasting rooms in the Santa Barbara area (Summerland, Carr, and Santa Barbara winery) as well as an olive oil tasting. Carr was our favorite- great atmosphere and great wines! Our only qualm was that we went to the olive oil tasting right after lunch when we were all full; we would have like to switch up the order (I.e wine tasting, olive oil tasting, wine tasting, lunch, wine tasting). Other than that, a wonderful experience!

Amir was a DELIGHT! I was up in Santa Barbara celebrating a girlfriends Bachelorette party this past weekend. The girls and I could have not had a better experience with Amir at the wheel. He picked us up and took through the canyon to a beautiful spot above the lake to take a group photo (he was happy to play the part of photographer). We then went to the first winery on our tour, Lincourt, for a very tasty first round... I recommend the Rose. After snapping a few photos he took us to our next stop, a tasting room in Los Olivos. Lovely! Next was the olive oil tasting. Lovely as well! Amir then gave us our choice for lunch, we decided to drive into Solvang where we had some time to walk around and enjoy the local Dutchy Kitch. Our third winery was Bridalwood (dear lord they did not have the late harvest white the bride was DYING for... not a major problem since we were pretty sauced by that point). Our final stop, my favorite... Tri Tip sandwiches in the middle of the canyon. Amir was polite, jovial, friendly and punctual. He told us all about the local traditions and "to dos." We could not have been happier and even more importantly, the BRIDE could not have been happier. It really was a wonderful trip and our tour guide took our experience to the next level. Wholeheartedly recommend this tour.

What a fabulous day? I had no idea anything about wine tasting. Our daughter turned 21 so we Google for a tour company. Jackson came up, I called and boom here we are. Their price was the best of all companies and the service was five star
 We had lunch in Santa Ynez and it to easily wonderful.
Today was memorable and we made need friends on our tour. Our guide Rick was truly great. He took great care of us and we even stopped at the lavender farm and olive oil farm. A perfect day!!! Highly, highly recommend!!!

My husband and I had the most wonderful weekend getaway and enjoyed our wine tour and hotel package at the La Quinta in Santa Barbara Ca. arranged by Captain Jacks Tours .
Thank you Captain Jack for a memorable weekend where every detail was taken care of beautifully !!

First of all this was the greatest experience ever, Seth you are honestly amazing. You had so much patience with me and my girlfriend. I am now really great at kayaking thanks to you. I will recommend this place to my family and friends and everyone you won't regret it.

Great tour! Tracy was awesome. We just had the best afternoon.
Tracy took us to 3 wineries- one of them wasn't on the initial agenda but she asked us our preference along the way and it turned out that her recommendation was fabulous. We would definitely take this tour again when back in SB.

I HAD SO MUCH FUN GOING ON THE $50 WINE TOUR IN SANTA BARBARA! PLUS THEY HAVE AN AMAZING HOTEL DEAL TO GO ALONG WITH IT! Our tour guide driver person was so funny and really chill. It's so worth the money and they take you to 4 wineries and lunch. It was amazing. I loved every minute of my tour! I really do recommend using Captain Jack's SB Tours!!!

I am still only going to rate it 3 stars because I have yet to partake in a cruise, but they did repay me in a timely manner as my payment came almost right after I wrote the original review.
I commend them for that.

Had a wonderful time doing a private tour with Captain Guy! For only $50 more for a private tour is well worth it. My family and I had a wonderful time going to 3 different vineyards (of our choosing) and going to a olive farm that happened to be next to the last vineyard. Our tour guy was very informative and made our experience so much fun and enjoyable. My family and I HIGHLY recommend GUY as you tour guy.

The beer tour was a lot of fun. The second stop had a great food selection, just don't fill up or it will be difficult to fully enjoy the last spot. All three places were unique and the other people on the tour were fun and great to talk to.

We went on the Beer Tour. We expected something different but we ended having an okay time. The last stop, Island Brewery Co., was the best of all three stops. The "tour guide" didn't really know much of the breweries. You can actually go to the breweries and do the tastings yourself, so we basically payed to be driven to each brewery.

We've done a lot of wine tours and this was one of the best.  Our guide was knowledgable and kept the tour moving without putting pressure on us to stay on schedule.  He took us to great wineries.  Even though it wasn't a private tour (though they do offer those), he built camaraderie between two groups that didn't know each other.

Tracy!!!! I loooove you! My girlfriends and I had a wonderful day, we made new friends, new memories!!! This was the most incredible and fun day EVER!! She knew so much about the area, chose great  music, and was hilarious! We are planning to come back and will TOTALLY come back to see our newest friend! Tracy we love you!!! Thanks for one of the most incredible days ever!!! Xoxo :D so can't wait to see you again! luv, Jenn, Rachel, Lisa 

P.S. We made friends with a couple we met on the wine tour. Not only did we meet up for dinner and drinks later, but they are coming to our wedding! :) such an amazing experience!

As our first wine tour this exceeded all expectations. Our guide Selvin was amazing. He was super friendly, accommodating, very knowledgeable, overall a cool guy, and made our plan more flexible than we expected. We went to three great vineyards and even though it wasn't included in the afternoon delight tour, Sevlin was able to make a pit stop for us to get sandwiches which was awesome. We definitely recommend captain jacks to anyone who is willing to experience wine tasting.

My girlfriend and I were in Santa Barbara for the week, and we had planned on checking out some of the local wineries. Captain Jack's accommodated us on one of their "afternoon delight" tours same day, no problem; the booking process was fast and easy. Tracy, our driver/guide was awesome.
