She gave us a brief overview of the typical winery tasting experience, and had us at each winery on time. She proved to be an invaluable resource along the way, as well as a great source of in-car entertainment! We had a great time, and would re-do in a heartbeat!

My girlfriends and I recently used Captain Jack's to celebrate a bachelorette party earlier this month. Everyone I worked with was extremely friendly and helpful, from the first phone call I made to get more information about their tours. We decided to go with the city tour, which included tastings at 3 wineries and an olive oil tasting. 

Half of our group was driving into Santa Barbara that morning, so we arranged to have a private tour and requested that they pick us up 1 hour later than the normal start time, so no one had to rush in getting there. Our driver Hank was on time, very helpful in answering any questions we had and offered suggestions on where to eat, places to go, etc. I liked that everything was very organized and we never felt rushed. The day flowed really smoothly and Hank would call the next winery to let them know we were on our way, so everything would be ready and set up for our group by the time we arrived. 

I would highly recommend Captain Jack's! We had an awesome experience with them!

Captain Jack should be demoted. Nice enough people but pretty indifferent about not having any tours available for 3 days in a row for the middle of October. Bought a Groupon and, according to the gentleman on the phone, is very limited even though only requires a 14 day notice. Now I have to deal with Groupon for a refund. There is an hour and a half of my life I will never get back. Oh well.

Had a great time on our kayak tour of the harbor and bay.  Guide was knowledgeable and friendly.  Everything went well until the end when we disembarked on a very slippery boat ramp. I slipped, fell and banged up my elbow.  Highly recommend wearing shoes when you exit the boat.  Operator should also provide a rubber mat or safer place to land.

On 5-21-14 my friend and I had a private wine tasting tour. The Santa Ynez Wine tasting tour was awsome. Got picked up around 9:45 in a Ford  Expedition . Our tour guide was Peter. What a great guy , and very pleasant to be around with. Peter was very knowledgeable of the area and knew allot about the wines and vineyards we visited. Our first stop was Lincourt vineyards. From there, we proceeded to Bridlewood vineyrds.  bridle wood had a beautiful views. After that we did the olive oil tasting . I'm not a fan of olive oil . So I sat this one out. But my friend loved it. He said it was the best olive oil in town. Because we skipped lunch we went straight to Cimarone estate wines for some more tasting. By this time the buzz feeling was kicking in. After Cimarone  , and because we had time to spare, Peter took us to what he said was his personal favorite spot.  The place was Imagine wine. Their French Colombard was amazing. From there , it was time to go back and get dropped off. Peter gave us some great tips on where we should go eat. I highly recommend this tour . And you get Peter as your guide, you will ha a great time. And a wonderful experience.

Dear owner, I'm glad to hear you have repaired the van. That does not take away from the poor experience we had. Offering an additional tasting was a nice gesture but it did not remediate the situation- which we made very clear on that day. There should have been some sort of compensation or even an apology on that day. Also a phone call would be nice.

Very affordable and mainly for those who don't know much about wine tasting.
This was my first wine tour on a bus that took you to the wine shops (not wineries) and it wasn't all that I've hoped it would be.
They pick you up and drop you off, which is nice.
You're crammed in a van with strangers, which wasn't all that bad since my group was pretty cool. But I can imagine how bad it could have been if we were stuck with the wrong peeps....oh the horror.
BUT it's only $50, which includes gratuity.

They take you to wine shops, not actual wineries. (Which I didn't expect, b/c I didn't buy the package so that's not really their fault.)
The lunch our driver drove us to, was satisfactory at most.

I was really glad we were the last ones picked up and the first one's dropped off.

Knowing this, I took my gf's on a similar wine tasting event hosted by the one and only me! 

Although the horseback riding sounds interesting.

I love kayaking but my wife does not like kayaking in the ocean. She would however go if we had a guide. I purchased a  90 minute Kayak Tour, which did take you into the ocean. 

From the beginning when I contacted Mark, the owner, he was exceptionally friendly and very accommodating. He helped book us a kayak tour that meet our exact needs. The day of the tour, our guide was friendly and very helpful. During the tour is explained various factoids and made a few jokes. Although it was his first being a sole guide, he did a great job. As a bonus we saw a swarm of dolphins which was a treat. 

Overall, the experience was good and I would fully recommend this tour, safe and fun!

Yo captain jack tours hooked me up. I got the top deck tour to celebrate my 5th anniversary with the wifey. Chris and Brandon took us kayaking, horse back riding and wine tasting.. each event was more memorable than the one before.  They let us set the pace of our day and we will never forget it. Brandon was especially helpful with getting us Aquinted with the wine culture. I recommend his expertise. Chris was super chill and patient with every aspect with our adventure.  If your looking for a quick getaway , look for these guys.

My boyfriend surprised me with horseback riding. We came here and it was amazing, of course it was my first time. The view was amazing and our guide was great and funny. I would defiantly recommend coming here.

We celebrated a friend's birthday with Captain Jack's beer tasting tour. 

Our tour guide Jennifer picked us up promptly at noon from the Best Western. Clearly nothing but the best for me and my friends. Jennifer said she was new to Captain Jack's but she could have fooled us. She was incredibly accommodating and knowledgable of beer making, and because the three breweries we visited were spread along the coast from Carpinteria to Goleta, she gave us an unofficial tour of Santa Barbara along the way.

We started at Island Brewing Co. in Carpinteria where we tasted their Avocado Honey Ale, Blonde Ale, Jubilee Ale, Paradise Pale Ale and Harvest Ale. Wow, that's a lot of ale. Jennifer arranged a tour of the brewery. I was too tipsy to comprehend everything explained but the machines were shiny enough to hold my interest. 

After 2 hours, several trips to the bathroom and at least 10 bowls of pretzels, we headed north to Goleta and Hollister Brewing Co. Here we enjoyed a reasonably priced lunch (not included in Captain Jack's fee) and six individual tastings. I sampled their Table 42 Red, Pumpkin Saison, Bushwick Stout, Pilfers Pale Ale, The Pope IPA, Crosseyed & Painless IPA. Our group was large but they had no problem splitting our meal checks.

Finally, we drove into Santa Barbara for our last stop, but I honestly can't remember where that was or what we had. Mission accomplished.

Jennifer dropped us off safely at the Best Western around 6PM, which gave us plenty of time to hit the hotel happy hour before heading out for dinner. 

Overall, the convenience of an awesome driver and a hassle-free experience made the tour worth the price. Five stars.

Just wanted to second what Jennifer G. wrote. We had an unforgettable tour with Tracy this past weekend. She really went the extra mile to make the experience special and personalized for each of us.

Great wine and good times. Thanks again, Tracy!

CJ Tours offers a great service for vacationing tourists like myself.  Their website is easily accessible and navigable, and their phone personalities are cheerful and helpful.  

Offering a range of events and tours at all prices, I did the Horseback Riding tour.

I must say this tour was PERFECT.  Not only was my weekend getaway in Santa Barbara already enough, but this tour gave me a chance to travel through the countryside where lands are surprisingly uninhabited, vegetation is allowed to grow anywhere, and city stresses are forgotten.  

Our destination was at a ranch, where the guides may as well be real cowboys.  One gets a sense of old country life here.  

Peanut and I followed the guide through the hills, by poison oaks, across streams, and it was all breezy and calm and gentle.  There were stopping points for photo ops and pleasant conversation all around.  

I would definitely recommend this!

If you're small, ask for Peanut!  He's almost programmed so you don't have to control him too much!
Can u say blast and a half?!!! We went on the $50 wine tour and had too much fun with some great people I love how everyone starts as strangers and u leave the best of friends.

Wine Tasting!!  Yay! 

Tracy was the BEST tour guide! So knowledgable! It was our first time to the area and it was really great to have someone to tell us about everything.  She knew the people at the places we visited, so it was kind of like having an "in".  

I highly recommend taking the tour, you will not be disappointed!  We LOVE wine but the highlight was the Olive Oil tasting- you should have heard the Mr. "oooh that's delicious", "OMG! that tastes so Good!" 

We stopped for lunch at Grapolo's, the food was great and we are pretty sure we had a Bo Derek sighting.  It was a perfect day :-)

We did the Horseback Riding & Wine Tasting combo tour on Mother's Day and were extremely satisfied! We were first picked up at our hotel at 10:30am and driven to Circle Bar B for horseback riding, where we were lucky enough to have a private ride. Then we were driven to Cold Spring Tavern for lunch (where Captain Jack's already made a reservation for us so we didn't have to wait at all- awesome!). The afternoon concluded with wine tasting at 2 wineries and a short olive oil tasting as well. Around 5:30pm, we were dropped off at our hotel, which was super convenient because we didn't have to drive at all that day.

One thing to mention was our driver/tour guide, Sam. He was extremely friendly, had great conversations with us, and drove very safely. We really enjoyed spending the day with him. We highly recommend Sam and Captain Jack's!

Nine friends and I went on the Santa Ynez Valley Wine Tour. Our tour guide and driver, Yenz, was awesome! He told us a bit about the history and growing processes at each winery, which was impressive and interesting. He was friendly, knowledgeable, and answered all of our questions about each winery. I highly recommend anyone interested in the tour to request him. 

The tour itself was great! Three local wineries, a pit stop for lunch, olive oil tasting, and we received two souvenir wine glasses. You can't beat door-to-door pick up and drop off either! 

A DEFINITE must and something I will be doing in the future!

Used Captain Jacks for our Girls Getaway.  We did the Sideways tour and had so much fun.  Our guide was super friendly and very knowledgeable.  We had so much fun learning about wine and tasting it as well.  I would recommend Captain Jacks if you want a mix of fun and learning.  We are already planning our next adventure.

My boyfriend bought a Groupon for the Wine Tasting tour. The whole experience was amazing. Very professional driver Peggy was freaking amazing. She told us she also drives a school bus during the week. She had lots of little Santa Barbara stories. I happen to forget my glasses in the van and she called us and brought them to where we were having dinner. Can't say enough good things about Mrs. Peggy.

Great tour door to door that takes you to beautiful wineries and scenery! Loved all of it. We were so happy with our service and worth the cost. Ask for Richard who will serenade you with a show tune, or John who will tell stories about all of the surrounding area with charm and grace. Richard also had a fantastic lunch recommendation that will not soon be forgotten. Thanks guys and cheers!

My boyfriend and I finally got to cash in our (belated) Valentine's Day trip last month, and let me just say that it was definitely worth the wait! We went on the horseback ride + wine tasting tour and had a blast.

Captain Don (he told us he was a sailor himself!) picked us up from our hotel in the morning and was really friendly and knowledgeable. You can tell that he had lots of experience and told a lot of good stories. Definitely kept us interested the entire day! 

After horseback riding we stopped into Cold Springs Tavern for a buffalo burger and a beer before getting to stop in to two wineries. All of the locations we went to were great! Loved all the scenery and got to learn a lot as well. 

We even had time after wine tasting to head up to Los Olivos for some olive oil tasting! That was fantastic.

After a full day we were definitely satisfied. Captain Don even let us nap a little on the way back to the hotel. What a wonderful time! Compliments to Captain Jack's! Maybe next time we'll do a little kayaking ;]

We put together our own adventure for the July 4th weekend with this tour company and were quite impressed.  The first day we rode horses at Circle Bar B Ranch and the ride was great.  I had been on this ride before when I stayed there, but my partner had not.  At the top of the hill, you have an incredible view of the ocean and you are right below the Reagan Ranch.  The horses were in fine shape and our guide Adam was very nice and funny as well.

On day two, we chose to kayak the harbor and this was really an adventure.  We paddled out to the green buoy where seals were sunning themselves.  A couple highlights of this paddle was going under a catamaran and under the Stearn's Wharf.  Our guides were really nice and made sure we had a great time.

I would book with this operation again.

AMAZING!! 

Truth be told...we looked at this company first, then another and came back to Captain Jack's. Why? Great customer service from the get go and what they promised, they did end up delivering and more! The other company would not confirm our reservation even though they were holding my credit card unless they had two more people join the trip. Bad business, in my opinion.

Tracy was our guide and really, you couldn't have asked for a better guide. She was so professional but yet personable at the same time. She gave us so much information, took us to some awesome wineries, took the bags with our purchased items to put them in the trunk, and made us laugh.

It was my birthday weekend so I requested to go to Firestone and it happened. I requested to spend some time at Cold Springs Tavern for a drink and it happened. Tracy went as far as to present me with a dessert at the restaurant we ate at. SO NICE!!

It was the best way to wine taste in the SB region and we couldn't have picked a better company. Thank you Tracy and all the staff at Captain Jack's!

Guy was an amazing guide and he taught us so much about Santa Barbara and the coastline. I had a great time and I highly recommend traveling with Guy for any excursions with Captain Jack's. It's a great company!

Hit up the beer tour with joe hammond, his first beer tour mind you. He kicked ass, took his time talking about the area and his favorite likes in downtown.  Tastings were great, favorite was Figueroa,  and the tour of islands brewery was awesome. Check it out

My girlfriend and I came on this tour for our one year anniversary, and boy was it worth it.  We had Tracy as our tour guide, and were joined by another nice group of girls on the tour.  I personally do not know all that much about wine, so Tracy was a great resource.  She was always willing to answer questions as she drove, and she is a lot of fun too.

The highlight of the tour for me was the olive oil tasting.  It was a very fun change of pace in between the 3 vineyards.  The view driving through the vineyards was fantastic as well.  If you are looking for something fun to do near Santa Barbara, I would highly recommend Captain Jack.

This review is for the Afternoon Delight Wine Tour and the Harbor Kayak Tour.  

Tracy was our tour guide on the wine tour and she was absolutely fantastic.  I enjoyed just chatting with her and hearing about her experiences as a tour guide.  The wineries we went to were wonderful and my boyfriend and I really enjoyed the group experience of the tour.  We returned to SB from the vineyards in the late afternoon very satisfied (and tipsy) from a great day!

We also had a great experience with Mark (and I can't remember the other gentleman's name!) on the kayak tour.  I would highly recommend Captain Jack's - the whole thing was a seamless experience.

My husband and I did the in-town wine tour and sunset cruise. We had a great time on both! 
Our wine tour driver (can't remember his name, but he was from S. Carolina) did a fantastic job. We ended up riding with one other couple, who also had a great time. The door to door service was awesome for only 50 p/person. They say it's a "half day", but you really get your money worth b/c it's pretty much a full day.

The sunset cruise was so nice! Very relaxing, and not too crowded. 
My only "needs improvement" would be the commentary on the cruise. There was a lot of talk  about the wealthy communities in SB. History is nice, but hearing about the local country club doesn't interest me.

This was the best wine tour EVER!!! I could not have asked for more from a wine tour! Our driver/guide was so nice and really filled us in on a lot of stuff that we didn't know about Wine :) We went to several wineries and wine tasting rooms as well as an olive oil tasting and lunch at a great little Italian place! We made friends on the tour that we will have for a lifetime! I would recommend this tour to anyone and everyone!!! Oh and they pick you up and drop you off at your hotel in Santa Barbara OR Solvang.

I went on a wine tour last weekend with Captain Jack's. My party and I had a wonderful experience. Tracey was our tour guide and she was 100% the reason why our day turned out so fantastic. We luckily ended up having a private tour as well which contributed to this great experience - if you're looking for something more private I would recommend paying the extra for that option because it really made our experience enjoyable. Tracey was such a good time and was knowledgable about the wine industry as well. We had an amazing lunch, went to phenomenal wineries, and even did an olive oil tasting!

My girlfriend and I went on captain jacks beer tasting tour for a special occasion. I highly recommend the tour we stopped by all the best breweries in the area including our favorite 'island brew co' in Carpenteria. Tracy our guide was incredible, when appropriate she gave us our space but also joined in on the fun when we asked her to! We were never rushed at any point in the tour. Thanks for the great memories Captain Jacks!
Great time on kayaking tour, Great information on local area and wildlife. Great guy, highly recommended.

Went on a Wine Tour of the Santa Rita Hills and had a blast!
Our driver Jens was awesome - he was very, very knowledgeable about wine and just Santa Barbara in general. He was quick to drop some knowledge but also gave us time to chat amongst ourselves - the perfect balance.

Each of the wineries along the way were awesome, we were picked up on time and then dropped off at our front door afterwards. I got a groupon for the trip for two people, but would have gladly payed full price for the experience.

Will definitely be taking the tour again and will be requesting the same driver!

My friend was in town from Oklahoma and we scheduled a Wine Country tour. It was absolutely amazing! This was the first time either of us had done any kind of wine tasting/tour. We had no idea what to expect but we were instantly laughing and having a great time from the second we got in the van. Don was an awesome tour guide. The day was a perfect mix of spending time as a group, and having the freedom to go and explore on our own. We stopped for lunch at an amazing italian restaurant and ate as a group which gave us the opportunity to get to know the other people on the tour. Don was aware that there was a couple celebrating an anniversary, as well as a birthday, and both received a complimentary dessert. We visited four wineries and one olive oil farm. I do wish we could have spent more time at the olive oil farm but our group preferred to be where the drinking was. ;) Overall - an absolutely perfect day! I can't wait to go back again and try one of the other tours!

The tour was amazing. Peter was fantastic and friendly and fun and smart and totally accommodating and got us back to the train early and sent us home with tiramisu! He went to great lengths to make sure we got to do everything we wanted (we had a small time issue bc of the train schedule) and he was flexible and he was a safe driver. He also knew a good deal about wine making.

We went on the Santa Barbara Beer Tour for my fiance's birthday and it was so much fun.  Our driver was energetic and so knowledgeable of all the breweries we went to and the beer was fantastic!  It was the perfect way to spend a Saturday and I can't wait to do it again!

I was not impressed by the wine tasting tour we took this weekend.  Our guide rushed us through the whole day only to find out its because they had to get to another group.  It also seemed like he was getting kick backs from certain places.  We told him we wanted a casual lunch and he tried to take us to a nice sit down place.  

I would not use them again.

BEST TOUR BY FAR!!!!!
A couple days before our tour, I recieved an email asking if we wanted to upgrade for a steal of a price! I thought wow this is awesome! Luis was very accomidating and nice for our bachelorette party. The tour guide Peter showed up on time and we warned him that we can get a little innapropriate at times with our conversations and he was ok with it. Not only did he have us laughing all day long, we all felt like he was one of us! He was very informative, and we never felt rushed with our tours. He knew where he was going at all times and even took us to an awesome place for lunch where the staff there was just as friendly. We were running a little late with our tour and he never brought it up, in fact offered to take us to an awesome mountain top bar!  
I will always use captain jack's tours for any future adventures I plan on taking. The tour guide was a perfect fit for our personalities and he made a good photographer as well ( sorry peter!! haha )

I booked through Captain Jacks for wine tasting to celebrate my birthday and it was the most amazing time ever. The tour guide was fantastic and went out of her way to make my day special. We were picked up in a nice new SUV and chauffeured around to three different vineyards which were absolutely breathtaking. I highly highly recommend!

I did the group wine tour with Tracy on a Sunday through the Santa Ynez/Los Olivos area.  

The three wineries (Bridlewood, Carhatt, Shoestring) we visited were great and the service was excellent at each.  They were a good mix of wineries.  

The olive oil shop we visited was awesome, such an unique and cool experience at Los Olivos.  

We had lunch at Trattoria Grappolo which was fairly priced with great food and atmosphere!  

Basics about the tour: Tracy picked us up on-time at Buellton.  She was a knowledgeable and fun guide who was very accommodating.  She did all those small things to make the trip better (ex: water bottles, gift storage, pictures).  After visiting one winery, we did some olive oil tasting, then we visited another winery.  Then, lunch.  Then, one last winery.  Price is typical.  All tastings are free.  Lunch is on you.  Tip for tour guide at end.  We were groupon holders, but we were treated like everyone else.  I would recommend this tour to anyone.

As a surprise for my hubby, I booked a tour with Captain Jack and his microbrewery tasting tour. It was $50 plus a small gratuity for three microbreweries, and included pick-up and drop-off from our Santa Barbara B&B and all tasting fees. We went to Island Brewing Co (8 tastes), Brewhouse (9 tastes), and Telegraph (4 tastes plus a free 10 ounce of your choice). 

We had lunch at Brewhouse which was not included in the cost, but offered a large menu with items starting around $7 for a burger and fries. We also got to meet the brewmaster, Pete, who took us on a small and informative tour of the brewery -- he was a great guy who had a story about all his beers. We especially liked his own unique brews, the dark lager Obamanator and his Habanero beer - great fun!

Our guide Marcus was fun, friendly, and informative. He really knew his beers and was able to keep up on the beer talk with my beer loving hubby. He had an ice-filled chest ready for our growler and bottle buys, too -- nice touch!

We're looking forward to our next tour with Captain Jack and his team.  They also offer kayak tours, wine tasting tours and horseback riding.  From start to finish they are a class act.

One word.....TRACY!!!
If you're looking to reserve and experience the best wine tour -ask for Tracy. She made our Santa Ynez tour one of the most memorable days we've ever had in wine country! My wife and I met new people, shared stories, created memories and overall shared a wonderful experience and we couldn't have done it without Tracy as our guide. 
Captain Jack's was amazing and everywhere we went, not only did they know Tracy but we were treated exceptional. 
We will definitely reserve Captain Jack's for any future tour and recommend it but only if Tracy is our driver ;)

P.S. Don't know what the heck people are whining about here. Captain Jack provided me with exceptional service. I called and left a message and was called back first thing the next day. I was a little "picky" because we had already been to certain wineries in Santa Ynez and Luis was awesome in making sure our tour was "customized" as possible. Thank you!

My daughter and I were participants in the vineyard tour with Capt Jack's and it was fantastic.  Tracy, our guide was great and she had tons of trivia type information about the places we went and the area!  Consider going on the vineyard tour -- it was so worth it!  Thank you!

Love this company! My boyfriend planned a surprise belated birthday trip for me since his work schedule is pretty unpredictable. As soon as we got up to Santa Barbara (which was my first time ever going) we checked into our hotel then on to my first surprise of the trip and as soon as we pull up I see a kayak! In my mind I was freaking out but I said F it let's do this! Our guide Ben was an absolute doll. He was very easy going and knowledgeable and took pictures for us and since it was a sunset kayaking tour (which included champagne) was one of  the best experiences I've had. The next day was our wine tasting tour. They come to pick you up in an SUV (Surprise Surprise cause our driver was BEN!) to a few wineries in the Santa Ynez/Los Olivos as well as an Olive oil tasting (I've never done this either or knew that there was such a thing) which was interesting and tasty. The tour also came with lunch at an Italian restaurant that was very quaint the food was pretty good but you pay for the food yourself. Our third day we went horseback riding (another first) just us and our guide, it was a beautiful trail starting from the bottom of the mountain thru the trees and streams then up overlooking the ocean and I had a sweet and gentle horse, our guide (who wasn't Ben) was cool but got freaked out that my boyfriend and I didn't really talk that much thru the tour (hey man I wanted to enjoy the ride along with the peace and quiet) hahaha. The last tour we had was a Beer tasting tour.  I never knew that there was such a thing. Our guide who was Ben came to pick us up in their SUV and drove us to three or four breweries. Loved all the tastings and especially the names on some of them. Good Times!  When I come back up I am sure to get another Kayaking tour, hopefully with Ben. So if you decide to take in a few tours in Santa Barbara you should check out Captain Jack's.

I was looking for the best and cheapest wine tour for a weekend so I searched Captain Jack's. They offer either a wine or beer tour for $50. They had great reviews so I decided to book it. We had a wonderful guide and were driven around in a large SUV with 3 other couples. We did the beer tour and stopped at 3 different "bars". Only one was a brewery and the rest were bars. The fee paid for each tasting and we also stopped for lunch. The only down side is make sure you check with the driver about what tour you're on. We mistakenly got into the wrong car but checked before we could take off but on our tour we picked up the wrong couple. They don't give their drivers names of the people to pick up, only a location (which is at your hotel if you're staying in Santa Barbara).

all in all it was a great experience

We recently went on Captain Jacks Beer tasting tour for a friend's Birthday. Having never been on a beer tasting tour I didn't know what to expect. We were picked up in front of our hotel and our adventure began. Our tour guide Sarah was Awesome to say the least. She was very knowledgeable on the most random subjects, and was friendly and patient. I highly recommend her as a tour guide. On our beer tasting tour we went to three different breweries, Union Brewery, The Brewhouse, and Island Brewery. Our group had lunch at The Brewhouse and in my opinion this was the best beer tasting experience of the three. The tour was very affordable; I highly recommend this if you are a beer lover. we will definitely go on another tour with Captain Jacks!

I was in Santa Barbara for my birthday weekend with some girlfriends. I decided to go with Captain Jack's for our wine tour as it had good reviews, good pricing and variety. We did the 'Afternoon Delight' wine tour which was a great choice for our group. We could casually start the day and have brunch and then the bus picked us up around 11:45am and they called prior to arrival.
We visited 3 wineries up near Buellton and beyond and did and olive oil tasting. We brought our own snacks to enjoy. Overall we loved the tour and it was a fun experience.

We took the $120 Santa Rita Hills Wine Tour.  Toured some good to very good wineries, and had a good time (drinking does that :).  Not sure if come back again though, Our tour guide was more like a chauffuer than an actual guide.  I expected that out of $99 tour.  Would have been Maybe because busy Memorial weekend, and we got less experienced Guide.  But they were professional, picked us up on time, and dropped us off on time.  Also wish we could have seen a barrel room.  Picked us up in Ford Explorer, and we had a party of 8.  Maybe check out the barrel room,

We took the Santa Rita Hills tour with Jens this weekend and had a lovely experience. He was very knowledgeable about the region and the wines. All in all, it was a great way to spend a Sunday. 

I'm definitely gonna check out the other tours and experiences that Captain Jack's offers!

We did the Santa ynez wine tour and it was great!
Tracy, Our driver/guide was very nice, laid back and accomodating, she took us to the cutest little tasting room, carheart (delicious sangiovese) and other great wineries.
I told her I was a vegetarian and she brought me 2 menus to choose from for lunch. The olive oil tasting was surprisingly good too.  Fun time, good deal, and no DUI!

Our tour guide, Peter was amazing! He was super accommodating, I get car sick very easily and he made it his duty to make sure I got better. If a couple people wanted to wander from the tour, he not only told them where they could go but physically walked them over so they could find their way, but not without making sure the rest of the group was happy where they were. The responses I got from the rest of the group were, "I wouldn't want to do that tour w out Peter, it wouldn't be the same." He made us feel as if he were one of us, just hanging out for the day. We almost forgot that he was working and has a life outside of our group and wine tasting and thought we could hang out all night. He made adjustments on our tour as necessary and I just can't say enough about him. He definitely made this day special for my sister's bachelorette party! Thank you Peter and Captain Jacks!!    I would highly recommend this company to friends!

It was my first wine tour, so with nothing to compare it to- my standards were pretty low. We went to 3 wineries, and the tastings were quick, with completely the bare minimum. I know it's a tasting, but it would have been nice to have a reason for the designated driver! lol. If lunch, cheese plates, or maybe even a bottle as a gift were to have been added, I would of rated much differently. Very overpriced.

We booked a wine tour with Captain Jack's and I can't say enough great things about the tour and the customer service!

We took the train to Santa Barbara from LA.  We were scheduled to arrive at 10:19 am and our guide was to pick us up around 10:30.  Unfortunately, our train was delayed and we knew we weren't going to make it on time. We called, explained the situation and were told not to worry at all.  They'd pick us up whenever we did arrive. And sure enough when we arrived, Suzanne was there to meet us!

Suzanne was an awesome guide and gave us a lot of cool info about the town (it was the first time my friend and I had ever been to Santa Barbara).  We visited 3 fantastic wineries for tastings and also experienced an olive oil tasting.  It was so great having someone so friendly and knowledgable take us around town. Overall, an amazing way to spend the afternoon.  HIGHLY recommend!

This was a wonderful wine tasting tour. From the person who scheduled our last minute tour (Andrew) to the driver (Tracy) who picked us up from our hotel, the service was excellent. Will definitely schedule another tour soon. Thank you Andrew and Tracy!

Do you want to have a great time in Santa Barbara?  Make sure you book a package with the Captain, because we had a marvelous tour, but it doesn't stop there.  The Captain also booked our two night stay at the La Quinta, just to make our visit to this town, perfect!

We went on The Afternoon Delight Wine Tasting Tour recently and had so much fun! We went to 3 different vineyards and one olive vineyard. Tanya our guide was excellent, very knowledgeable and super friendly. I can't recommend this wine tasting tour enough, we had such a memorable day. Thank you Captain Jack and Tanya!!

The experience is what you make it.

We were celebrating a birthday with 13 people in tow and Peter made sure we had a great time.

Carr, Summerland, Olive Oil tasting, and Oreana were the stops of the day and we enjoyed every moment of it.

$60 with wine tastings included is a pretty good deal to me. YAY!

I reserved this tour as part of my birthday weekend getaway trip, and our group decided on the $50 wine tasting tour. It was really easy and convenient to book our reservation. The day before our trip, I even got a call asking if I wanted to upgrade to the Santa Ynez Valley tour for no additional cost--that tour is $50 more, so we saved that amount for each person in our group. Don was our tour guide and he was extremely friendly and accommodating throughout the trip. He even drove us back to one of the vineyards since we packed a picnic and thought we'd enjoy eating there. At the end, we got to go to an olive oil tasting too.

Did the Santa Yanez 3 Winery Tour. From the start, Peter was a great tour guide. He called us 10 mins prior to pick up and was right on time. 

We proceeded to pick up a few more couples in Santa Barbara and one of the couples hasn't been back to the area in 40 years since graduating from UCSB. She wanted to show her husband The Cold Springs Tavern which she claimed she requested during booking. Peter was happy to oblige and everyone enjoyed it and we remained on schedule and then hit traffic on the way to Solvang to pick up the remaining couples. He called each couple to let them know he was running behind due to traffic and all was well.

Peter gave great commentary on the area as we drove and was very engaging and entertaining the entire day. 

All three wineries were great (Buttonwood, Bridlewood, and another that escapes me) were amazing. We also stopped for an olive oil tasting that almost stole the show. 

We stopped for lunch at an Italian bistro and to everyone's surprise, they presented one couple with Tira Misu to celebrate their 2nd anniversary. Peter had overheard them telling the other couples that this was their anniversary weekend and he arranged the whole thing.

We will definitely be back for another winery tour with Captain Jack and will most definitely request Peter (aka Jboo) again!

Take the Beer Tour!~

I will say the only downfall is that you have to have at least 4 people.  But the people in the office were exceptional keeping us in contact if someone else signed up.  We had the best time.  Our tour guide was young and maybe not the most experienced in beer, but he was AWESOME!!! We got luck with the group we were with because they were awesome as well.  

I don't remember the first one but it was in town and they have 5 very full tastings

The last two were the best. Island Brew Co was near the water and it was a gorgeous days so we sat outside and the bartenders there combined some tasting for us as to what they would think we would like.  

The Brew House was the best.  The lunch was awesome (Not included but very reasonable).  Pete was FANTASTIC!! Gave us wayyyyyyy more tastings than we need but gave a tour, explained everything.  He was awesome.

I would do this again.  It was Great and I've been wine tasting several times, a great change of pace!!! I guess you have to also get lucky with the guide, weather, and group you are with.  All of these things were perfect for us!!!

When you go wine tasting you expect to have fun. You don't expect to have one of the most epic days of your life.... But thanks to TRACY at Captain Jack's tours, that is what happened yesterday on our Santa Rita hills wine tour. At first sight, you wouldn't expect our group could even make it to the epic status. Too diverse you might say..... Too much of a motley crew... well.... Tracy is a professional people person. She read right into our group, assessed our common interests and our individual wine experience, and carefully manipulated the mood and customized the activities of the tour to deliver an unforgettable experience. Literally unforgettable.... Because even if I lose all my memory in the future, I will still have the most epic (yep... Using it again) set of photographs, all staged and taken by her, to commemorate the day. Many of you probably are interested in coming to this area because of the movie sideways. Amazing movie (just please don't bring it up if you do a wine tour here)! But, as good as it was, if there had been a film crew on hand yesterday, we easily would have blown it out of the water. Thanks Tracy!

I came here yesterday for my birthday!! Best experience! We did the afternoon delight wine tasting tour!! So much fun! Bob is amazing, he made it so much fun! We did the wine tasting, olive oil and honey tasting! The views were so beautiful, I will definitely be coming back.

We did the $50 ($60 with tip included) tour which was a lot of fun! Best part was the olive oil & vinegar tasting...had not done anything like that before :)

Will return!

This was a fantastic tour! We felt VIP the entire time!! We were always being taken care of by Richard and he went up and beyond t give us a fantastic wine country experience!!!
We really enjoyed our day trip!!!

Nous avons fait de l'équitation et dégustation de vin et de chocolat. Notre guide Jeff F. est un guide spectaculaire. Il connaît la région, les viticulteurs et l'art de la vinification. Merci encore, Jeff!
Le Grand Cru...
Napoleon III
We learned some cool stuff, like the following, on this tour.

The Grand cru wine ranking originates from the Wine Classification of 1855 by order of Emperor Napoleon III. He believed that wines of recognition and long-standing reputations should undergo a certain classification.

The term Grand cru refers to the classification of a vineyard which is known for maintaining a consistent reputation year after year for producing quality wines. While other "cru" terminology for classification often refers to specific wines and wine estates, Grand Cru status focuses more on the vineyard and the uniqueness of terroir. The classification began mainly in Bordeaux, however Burgundy and Alsace also utilize this status classification. In some regions, the Grand Cru status can represent the best of an entire region, with Primier Cru status following second, in some regions, this is not always the case.

I do not recommend this company to anyone. Captain Jack's is a highly inconsiderate company lacking any kind of customer service or respect. The first thing that I was unhappy with was the fact that I reserved a tour for 9 people, and the vehicle that showed only had 8 seat belts. Apparently, they don't value the lives or safety of their customers and didn't think it'd be a big deal for one of us to double up a seat belt. Without any other choice, our group continued to the first stop. Shortly after an unexpected family emergency occurred and we needed to return. Our driver was more than willing to bring us back to the hotel and even told us captain jacks would work something out with us since we didn't complete our tour. HE WAS WRONG. Captain Jack (though I doubt he has any captain experience) refused to work with us, was rude and inconsiderate, and even ignored my phone calls. I would not expect a full refund, but I would expect some sort of customer service or maybe a partial refund. His assistant said he'd try and convince Jack to give us some sort of a refund, and even requested I send him hospital documents to prove our emergency. I sent it in, and never heard back from anyone. Finally, I called asking what happened, and the assistant said, "Captain Jack doesn't want to deal with this anymore." Of course he doesn't want to deal with this, because he had all of our money already and is a greedy owner who carelessly sends insufficient seat belts for his tours. I am a firm believer in the value of customer service and do not recommend this company to anyone.

My first wine tour with my best friend!  We had so much fun :)  We took the Santa Barbara Wine Tasting Tour which is $50 (plus tax and gratuity).  The van picked us up at the Santa Barbara Visitor's Center and took us to 4 wineries.  Due to the Labor Holiday weekend, the Il Fustino olive oil & balsamic vinegar store was not open, thus we had the 4th winery added to our tour.  Our guide was Eric and he was very fun and knowledgeable.  He didn't pressure us to race through our wine tastings.  We stopped for lunch at the Roadhouse.  My favorite winery was Summerland - to me it had a slight "shabby chic" vibe and I liked that it was bright and airy.  BUT, all the wineries were good and had their own "personalities".  A majority of the stops were in the main town of Santa Barbara, except for Summerland (just a quick freeway drive), which makes for a nice day outing without the exhaustion of trekking far.  We are going back soon with more friends, for sure!

Please share a few descriptive words about your tour with us:


Obviously we were floored by the experience. It was our first wedding anniversary, first time in Santa Barbara, and first wine tasting! Carina was beyond awesome. A wealth of knowledge, humor, and professionalism. She went above and beyond--I'd have tipped her $100 if we had it like that right now.

We will definitely be back for the beer tasting and kayaking. I also appreciate the last minute booking, and the "private" tour was an added bonus! Great value, great time, great experience! I'm posting this on Yelp.com!

My wife and I booked the "Sideways" wine tour offered by Captain Jacks. This was an amazing experience. We were joined by another young couple we didn't know, but ended bonding with over the course of the tour.

Our tour guide, Mark, was extremely knowledgeable about the region and wine and went out of his way to help the four of us gain a better appreciation and understanding of wine in general. Unlike so many other oenophiles, he did it without the slightest hint of pretense or condescension, remind us that finding wines to enjoy was all about personal preference. 

As part of the "Sideways" tour, we were supposed to visit the four wineries presented in the movie and dine at the Hitching Post. We started out at Alma Rosa, which is a secluded beauty. This was followed by a visit to Fess Parker, which is a bit more mainstream and commercial, but still beautiful and with tasty wines.

After Fess Parker, Mark asked us if we wanted to "stick to the script" or improvise. We were all in agreement that we should veer from the script, so he then took us to Bridlewood and Lincourt, two wonderful wineries.

The tour concluded with dinner at the Hitching Post II where the 5 of us shared a wonderful meal.

This tour definitely exceeded our expectations and we will definitely book with Captain Jack's on our next visit to Santa Barbara.

Santa Ynez Valley Wine Tour. We had an amazing trip with a great guide named Traci. She was very informative and kept us on a great schedule so we were able to enjoy our tour. We visited three amazing wineries. We had such a good time we are planning a trip for next year and of course we will use captain Jacks!!

Yens was our tour guide and he was awesome!!! We did the three local tasting rooms for wine and one olive oil tasting. The tour itself was delightful. 

We took off stars because the wine at the tasting rooms was largely uninspiring. To be fair, we are not Chardoney drinkers and that is what the local wineries pride themselves on.  The wine tasting rooms do not let you pick your wines, they have a flight ready for you. It is not always their best foot forward. If you ask to taste something specific, they may share, they may not. 

All in all if you are a big fan of California wines in general, you will love this tour. Ask for Yens.

used Captain Jack's again for my birthday a while back and I still give them a WOOHOO 5 star rating..

our guide was very friendly and chatty

i've got no complaints here =)

GREAT TIME - CAN't BEAT THE PRICE!!  

My girlfriends and I planned a weekend away in Santa Barbara and called Captain Jack's for a fun day trip.  For $60 a person (including booking fee and tip) we got picked up at our home in Montecito by the wonderful Private Jack!  :)  It was his first tour, and he totally showed us a great time!  The tour was perfectly timed, never rushed and Jack was super patient with us crazy bunch of happy girls with purple mouths!  LOL!

There were 8 of us so we got our own private SUV, hit up some of SB's local wineries, stopped for a wonderful lunch, and dropped us off safely at our door.  

I'm a first timer with this tour company and will definitely be back!  THANKS FOR THE GREAT TIME JACK!

This tour was an awesome experience! I was really impressed with their willingness to accommodate me and my friends. They were willing to let everyone in my large group pay separately and at any time before the tour started, which was really helpful - they understood how hard it was to get a group of people organized, committed and ready to pay. They were also willing to change the established tour times to meet my needs. The wineries were ready for us all when we got there, and Nicole, our tour guide, was wonderful and awesome in dealing with our large group, even when we got a bit drunk and rowdy! I would most definitely recommend booking a tour with Captain Jack!

Wonderful tour. My family and I had an excellent day. Our tour guide was attentive and engaging while also being very professional. This was our third tour in three years and we were not disappointed. WE have found something new to enjoy at each new tour. I was truly impressed with their customer service as we had a couple of challenges since booking and Capt Jack was very accommodating. Their services are really top notch. I would definitively recommend to any person trying to have a safe, fun filled day in Santa Barbara and surrounding cities.

Melissa was the best guide for our wine tours during our girls weekend!  She was knowledgable about the area, flexible to our requests, and so accommodating.  Prices were very reasonable and it was awesome to just enjoy the wine country and tastings without worrying about driving for the day.

Hats off to Capt'n Jack for the best kind of tour...BEER TASTING!

I live in LA and for my boyfriend's birthday I surprised him with a day trip to SB for Capt'n Jack's Beer Tasting Tour.  This was an incredible day and by far my best gift idea.  Our tour guide was super nice.  I forgot her name because it was a year ago since I went on the tour but I'm sure she reflects the personality of the other tour guides.  

Thumbs up because....

- We were running late because of traffic, but the tour guide happily picked up the other couple before meeting us at our rendezvous which bought us just enough time.
- Visited 3 breweries and tasted approx. 5-8 different beers at each location.  Mind you this started at 11am and so I was pretty buzzed after the first stop =)
- Had time to eat lunch at one of the breweries (decently priced restaurant)
- Didn't have to drive at all!!


- Our tour guide & the other couple really made this trip special.  We were all very laid back and joking around most of the time sharing stories about ourselves which was nice.  
- For $50 per person it really isn't a bad deal because you don't have to drive and because of the beer of course!!!

Qualms? NONE! I would definitely go back again for this tour in particular.  On wait, there was one thing...after we were dropped off I had to take a nap in my car because all that day drinking really took a toll on me and neither my bf nor I had the energy to drive back to LA.  

Great tour. 
Great beers.
Great time.

My wife and I received a gift certificate for the Santa Ynez Wine Tour. We booked it in December and turns out we had the bus to ourselves! Dave was our guide and he was the best. He could hear in our voices how beautiful we thought the scenery was so he took us through some back roads so that we could get a better view. He educated us as well as kept us entertained.

We highly recommend this wine tour, it was beautiful, delish and fun. Ask for Dave to be your driver, you won't regret it!

Wife and I took Captain Jacks tour for wine tasting tour while our stay in SB. Our guide was real cool and knew his stuff. We went to 3 winneries and ate lunch at one of them as well. The views were just amazing. My wife really enjoyed them as she loves wine and likes trying different wines. I would highly reccommend taking a wine tour as they drive you around and give you knowledge on the history of each winnery and areas.

We took the $99 Santa Ynez tour and it was superb. We are from out of state, didn't want to drive, didn't know the area and just wanted to chill and have fun. Our guide was Louis, a lovely personable intelligent and highly knowledgable fellow who grew up in the area. 

He picked us up and dropped us off right at our door, provided water and snacks if we wanted any and was a delight to ride with. We visited the Gainey and Bridlewood wineries, the Global Gardens oil & vinegar purveyors (tasted there also) and the Epiphany tasting room, plus a wonderful lunch in Los Olivos (not included in price.) All of the tastings were great and informative and they do let you keep the glass at most of them. We also visited the Cold Springs Tavern, a cluster of very old (1870s) structures that was a stagecoach stopping point.

HIghly recommended for a day of brilliant fun, wine, sights and wine country education.

Amazing. Loved every bit of this wine  tour of the Santa ynez valley! they took us to such diverse wineries - a real variety. Sooo worth it ! And Meredith did it very last minute for us which I so appreciated.

What a great time we had!!! A bunch of us girls came up from Orange County for a bachelorette party. Our wine tour was nothing but great. I was surprised to come home and read any reviews but good ones but there seems to be a few ANNOYING complainers out there even though I didn't feel there was anything to complain about. Our driver Dennis was super fun and helped get our group of 13 set up at each winery and at lunch. He was talkative and made sure to help each one of the girls in and out of the van ALL DAY LONG! We had a great time together and each of the wineries he took us to were professional and we seemed to get a LOT of tastings at each one.... more than I would have expected in comparison to other tastings I have been to in the past. Overall I would completely recommend going on this wine tour and even asking for Dennis if you want someone fun! Captain Jack made sure that we had a great time and made our wine experience a fun party which was exactly what we were aiming for with our brides last big adventure before walking down the aisle... THANKS DENNIS AND CAPTAIN JACK TOURS!

I just had to say, this tour was a real treat. Jens, our tour guide was the best guide I've had for any occasion, hands down. Personable and  knowledgeable, he really made the trip something to remember. I highly recommend asking for him, you wont be disappointed!

We had Tracy as our Guide, she was WONDERFUL, proffesional, kind, friendly, she cared that we had a good experience as it was our first time and my birthday! You dont have to go the extra mile when its your job but Tracy went above and beyond for us, we felt safe and as if we were being driven by a good friend. Captain Jacks! You have a gem in Tracy and we would use this service again just because of her

My family was in from Ohio and we were looking for the quintessential Southern California day trip...we found it with Captain Jack's!  The tour guide Jack (coincidence namesake) was so professional, fun and accommodating. We had an amazing day and we'd recommend the winery tour to anyone looking to get a slice of Santa Barbara at its best. Thanks Jack and Captain Jack's tours!

Groupon:  $50 - Good for a Santa Ynez Valley Wine-Tasting Tour for 1 (a $110 value)

Expiration: 12/20/12

I purchased this groupon back in mid October and have been experiencing overwhelming difficulties in scheduling a wine tour.  I've made several attempts on a weekly basis to schedule a tour and have been replied commonly with "there are not any tours going out anytime soon".  Captain Jack recommended calling back on a regular basis to check if a tour will be scheduled.  I have been regularly calling back, to be met with the same "no outgoing trip" response.  (I understand that the groupon disclaimer states "valid for existing tour", which means that there needs to be an existing non-groupon tour to exist before any groupon customers can latch onto that trip).  Not that there was none offered, of all the times I've been calling, there was one available date offered to me on a weekday in December which did not accommodate my schedule.  

I understand Captain Jack's policy to not let groupon patron initiate a trip, but this deal has been deceiving from the very beginning from the information provided on groupon.  When Captain Jack posts a dec 20th expiration on the groupon deal, the buyer would expect that he can find adequate number of trips to use the groupon prior to that date.  My experience has suggested otherwise, and through my frustrations in constantly calling to check to see if a tour has been scheduled, have been told that the groupon expiration has been extended to end of Jan.  This would bring solace only if there are available tours in the upcoming few weeks.  Yet, from my experience with the lack of available dates, either they will extend the deal expiration again or suggest to substitute for the cheaper Santa Barbara tour (which is cheaper at $50 compared to the Santa Ynez tour at $100 and goes out more frequently).     

My suggestion to Captain Jack is that if you sell the groupon to over 400 people (this info was obtained by looking at the number of quantities purchased on the groupon site), anticipate that users what to go on the trip prior to your stated expiration of dec 20th.  Accommodate the anticipated number of patrons, offer a good experience and these satisfied patrons will come back.  So far, I have been a disappointed and unsatisfied customer.  I simply want to use my groupon as advertised in the info provided by groupon.

Had the most wonderful experience with Captain Jack's Tours. I did the Afternoon Delight Wine Tour. They picked us up around 11:45 at the location of our choice. We choose sambos so we could start the day off with a hearty breakfast. Our tour guide kept me posted with her ETA and was very welcoming and attentive. She gave us some history of the town and we got to enjoy 3 wine tastings, a vineyard tour, and an olive oil tasting. We even got to explore Solvang for a bit after the tour. I would definitely do a tour with Captain Jacks again and recommend it for anyone looking for a great tour guide that makes it easy, memorable, and so much fun!!

My sisters and I took my mother for her birthday and what a great experience we had!! The tour guide was great and very friendly!! He called us when he arrived at our hotel, than we picked up two more groups, not too far from our hotel. The tour got underway at 11 and ended at 5. Two wineries, than an olive oil and vinegar tasting (awesome!), lunch at a cute little Mexican restaurant, and our last winery. All the wineries were nice and very accomodating. The day went by so fast, but we never felt rushed leaving each spot, there was just enough time! The only thing I would have liked is to bring along some snacks like cheese, crackers, dips, but you cant really do that on this tour. Instead we just brought small bags of peanuts and chips that we carried in our purses. Overall, great time!!!

Just finished a girls mini vaca to Santa Barbara. We had a great time. We like wine, so we booked a tour with Captains Jacks. We had the BEST time. Our driver was Bobby, and even though he had 2 extra ppl to bring along who were both in training ( that alone, was an experience) he was always professional, funny and informative. Having a good driver is half the battle. We enjoyed a really good lunch at a Italian restaurant which we talked about how good it was on our drive home. If your looking for a day well spent, take a wine tour with them, you wont be sorry.....Good Job Bobby.
Betty, Lorrie and Connie from San Diego .

I got a giftcard for the sunset cruise. The girlfriend and I went in early November and it was still great! The weather was good and the boat, service and drinks were great. I would recommend this to anybody that wants to sail in Santa Barbara and enjoy a relaxing trip.

A friend booked a wine tour for my birthday and it was a blast! We traveled with a group of like-minded twenty-somethings and that helped, but the wineries were great and catered our tastings to our palates. There was a lot of variation in the wines we tasted and each place had a different feel. The people who poured the wines were really knowledgeable and friendly as well. 

When we got to the place for lunch there was already a table set up for us and we pretty much had the whole patio to ourselves. Awesome. 

They booked the perfect number of wineries for the trip and I felt like we stayed a perfect amount of time at each place. Our tour guide, Ben, was wonderful and a great companion to have on the trip. He was flexible with our schedule so if we wanted to hang out a little longer that was fine. AND he let us blast MC Hammer in van (!!!). 

Overall, I would definitely recommend Captain Jack's Santa Barbara Tours to anyone looking for this type of tour. I may check out some of the other tours as well.

We went on a wine tour today and it was amazing! We had a last minute cancalation of another event that left us open for the day. We called captain Jack's and were fit into a tour immediately. Peggy was very personable and attentive and gave us a lot of information about the area and wineries we visited. The wine was amazing and there was a lot of variety. Definately booking our next trip with captain  jack

Wow wow wow. Super fun, super impressed!!

First time in SB and here for the weekend for my best friends bday.

I truly believe our awesome guide TRACY made this a 5 star experience!! We felt taken care of, she was knowledgable, and just a cool person.

We went to three wineries, olive oil tasting( much needed and yummy), and lunch! We had the option of eating lunch in solvang or at another location but choose SOLVANG which was cute and fun.

I literally have no complaints. 100% positive experience :)

Will contact this company again and ask for TRACY. 

Trust me!

BEST DAY EVERRR! 

I meant to write this sooner... In fact, I think I wrote a review and never posted it... LOL but I did the Santa Ynez wine tour with Tracy back in March. 

First of all, Tracy is phenomenal. That's that. She was so much fun and definitely a huge aspect of this amazing day. 

Secondly, AMAZING WINE! I loved shoestring winery, bridlewood, (can't recall the other) ... But they were all great! ....not to mention the stage coach stop... it was so cool!

Lunch in Los Olivos was outstanding and I was able to order my favorite glass of wine from our first stop, shoestring, at the restaurant. mmmm Sangiovese. ;) 

The olive oil tasting was also incredible. 

Nothing but great things to say for captain jacks. This was a birthday gift to my best friend and hands down, this was top 5 one of the best days of my life, I'm not kidding. My best friend felt the same way! I've also never reaped so much benefit from a birthday gift to someone else haha.  

It was our first time wine tasting and it will be hard to top!

.... SO GOOD, I'm going back dec 15th. I booked the same tour with a new friend that's never been and we booked it with TRACY!!! 

So excited to share this experience with someone else and create new memories.

Look out Tracy! :)

We went out kayaking with joe today. We're from LA and decided to drive up for a fun day out on the water. Not only did he take us all over the harbor and out to see the sea otters and under the pier to see the starfish, but he also recommended us awesome places to check out in the SB. Super chill service. We had a lot of fun and were definitely coming back (:

horseback & wine tour

FUN FUN FUN! 

captain jack's crew member picks us up from our hotel and drove us to the ranch as we enjoy our horseback ride up to the top of the mountain and a beautiful ocean view. After that, our driver drove us to two wine tasting spots.  Our driver drove us back safely to our hotel.

i recommend this tour for everyone. especially for larger groups where you just want to have a good time - going wine tastings - not worrying about which of your friend should be the designated driver. This way you and your friends can all have fun!

I visited Santa Barbara last weekend with a few friends and booked a harbor kayak tour for our group in advance. Booking was easy and seamless online and our tour ended up being private (with the addition of our tour guides Bill and Georgia, a lovely father/daughter duo and longtime Santa Barbara locals.) We were able to explore the Santa Barbara Harbor and piers by way of kayak, and even saw some sea critters along the way -- many seals and 4 dolphins that popped up right in front of us. It was a nice way to start the morning and to enjoy the ocean at a leisurely pace. It was by far the highlight of my weekend and would def recommend to anyone planning to visit SB.

I was recently on a work trip - and a group of 4 of us decided ahead of time to book a wine tour.  We were SO lucky to have picked Captain Jacks--and to have Tracy as our tour guide.
What a day!  Tracy was so friendly, knowledgeable and helpful  she made the whole experience exceptional.  She called before arriving.  Showed up on time.  She found out what types of wines we liked and then fashioned her touring experience around that.  We ended up touring more wineries than we had thought we would, we also stopped for Olive oil tasting (which was awesome) and then we even stopped at a local brewery.  Tracy made our whole day fun and trouble free.  I will refer anyone that I know to call Captain Jacks if they want to do a wine tour in Santa Barbara, and if they ask for Tracy- they won't be sorry!

I live in LA and my family was in town for graduation weekend, so I suggested we go wine tasting as a way to end the weekend. This was my third time with a Captain Jack's tour with friends/family from out of town, and I will continue to recommend them! I've done the wine tasting room in SB and the Santa Ynez Valley tour.

For the latest tour, we went on a Monday and the others on our tour worked at local tasting rooms, so they were mostly doing their own thing on the tour. Our tour guide, Peter, was awesome. He made sure that we were hearing stories of the wineries, and had a chance to tour around to see the sights at each of the wineries we visited. We visited two wineries (Bridlewood and Brander), along with an olive oil tasting at Global Gardens in Los Olivos (a wonderful specialty shop!). We picked up lunch at Panino in Los Olivos (seriously one of the best sandwiches I've ever had) and ate it on the patio at the third winery, Koehler. On our way back, we stopped by Cold Spring Tavern, an old stagecoach stop that is now a bar during the week, with BBQ tri-tip on the weekends. Not too much going on during the week, but it was a nice little stop at the end of our tour.

All in all, it was a fantastic day trip from LA. I (and my family!) would recommend Captain Jack's very highly. Definitely try to get Peter as your tour guide, bless his heart!

Tracy made our day a very memorable one! This was our first time in the city and she kept the day going with great winery recommendations. 

Highly recommend the $115 tour if you're looking to really experience a day in Santa Barbara. The whole process from booking to the day of was seamless. Request Tracy as your tour guide to make your day even more memorable. 

Thank you so much Tracy for all that you do!

Went on a tour on a Friday, decided on the wine tasting and horseback riding. I recommend Captain Jack tours based on my experience. It was just my husband and I booked on that tour, that was nice. Our tour guide was very nice and patient. The horseback ride was spectacular, first time on a horse, I was a little apprehensive about it, but Im glad I did it, the views were absolutely breathtaking from the top, I also felt very safe which was  a big plus. Our cowboy was very nice and friendly, took lots of pictures of us.  They pick you up and drop you right off at your hotel. Thank you for a wonderful experience

The tour was awesome!  Our guide, Tracey went out of her way to make sure we saw everything we wanted to see and knew about everything in the area.  We loved the wine tastings and learning more about what makes that region special.  Gainey Vineyards was my personal favorite.  The Olive Oil tasting was such a different and fun experience, also a highlight of the day.  If we find ourselves back in Santa Barbara we will definitely be taking another one of Captain Jack's Tours, thank you for such a great day/experience!!

Captain Jack's offers different wine tours throughout Santa Barbara and the Santa Ynez valley.  We opted for one that took us all around the town of Santa Barbara and included tastings at 4 different wineries as well as a lunch stop at the Bay Cafe where we were on our own.  Our tour guide and driver, Iaon, picked everyone up at their hotels on time and dropped everyone off at the end of the day leaving us only to worry about the wine.  Unfortunately while I thought our guide was friendly and helpful, he was a bit quiet.  There was no agenda or mentioning of where we would be going.  Also, he did not add any information to any of the places we visited.  So the tour was definitely more functional than informative.  Our stops included the Summerland Winery, the Coastal Winery out on Stearn's wharf, the Santa Barbara Winery, and finally the Kalyra winery.  Summerland was a great stop with a nice boutique feel to it.  It was an added bonus that on our visit Mary Kate Olsen was there.  I also liked the Santa Barbara Winery.  But I was not taken with the Coastal Winery (too touristy), and I was kind of offended by Kalyra where several of us actually threw all of the wine away.  Also it is noted that doing a wine tasting directly is not the same experience as doing it through the tour.  For example, in the case of the Santa Barbara Winery, a free tasting glass is included in the price of the tasting UNLESS you are on the tour, in which case you do not receive a complimentary glass.  Similarly, the Coastal Winery offers a more extensive wine tasting flight than the one we had courtesy of our tour.  This isn't an issue necessarily, but it is clear to offer the tour at a low price point, certain agreements were worked out in advance.  Just be aware.  All in, the tour costs $50 plus a mandatory $10 tip per person.  The tour was fine, but I think our tour guide explained it best:  If you are interested in wine tasting you should do the Santa Ynez valley tour, if you are interested in wine drinking you should do the Santa Barbara tour.  For the low price, it is difficult to complain too much.  


3.5 stars

Where do I begin, very friendly, they were a little early, so that was awesome and Bobby was so informative about Santa Barbara and the guy who is training under him had a lot of great input about the local hang outs as well... It was so much fun to view the harbor and to try some seriously awesome  beers and lunch with great company and an some alone time as well if you wish it, If you visit the lovely Santa Barbara I highly recommend this place!! Such an amazing time!

This is for the Kayak lessons+tour

Great guide! His name was Guy - perfect kind of guide, let us enjoy and relax without either being aloof/disconnected or boring us with too many stories, his instructions, advice and stories were perfectly placed/timed and we enjoyed a lot. We kayaked through the pier, under a catamaran, up and over the waves from a passing ocean liner and out into the deep blue sea.

Of course, it helped that we went on a perfect day when the weather was ideal, water was calm, and we got to see Seals, Sea Lions, Cormorans drying themselves, gulls/pelicans/etc, but most of all DOLPHINS!!! I counted at least 8, maybe there were more.

Good price ($60 without tip for 2), and very good value.

Our tour guide had picked us up at the train station because we decided to make a day trip to Santa Barbara for the wine tour. The wineries she took us to were quaint and very different from one another. The other passengers in the van were fun and friendly. But my cousins and I sat in the back two rows of the van so we couldn't hear our guide very well. So if you want to hear a bit more about the wineries or have any questions for the guide, you should sit closer to the front.
We did the Santa Barbara wine tasting tour. We had a blast getting drove around to multiple wineries w/ a stop out to lunch. Great experience! Highly recommended

Meredith with Captain Jack's Tours made an ordinary business trip truly special! I ended up with a free night to myself and was looking for an outdoor adventure, after failed attempts to book a solo tour with other companies, Meredith was gracious enough to offer a last second, solo evening tour. 
I have to say, kayaking the harbor is the best way to see Santa Barbara and learn about it's history. The wildlife is surprisingly diverse, with little crabs, pelicans, and sea lions all fighting for your attention. The water is  refreshing, even in the evening, and helps keep your body cooled down while paddling all around the harbor. It was so peaceful floating along with ocean at dusk as the rest of the world kept moving on land. 
I highly recommended working with Captain Jack's Tours, don't be shy giving them a call and seeing what is available. You might just end up with a once in a life time tour like I did.

I live in Santa Barbara, but on this tour I experienced our charming town in a wonderful, new light.  Our tour guide, Tanya,  was delightfully, yet not intrusively personable, knowledgeable, and professional.  The vehicle was roomy and clean.  The wineries, picture ops, lovely garden lunch...well, it was what I imagine the "Magically Mystery Tour" would feel like!  Please give yourself a treat with embarking upon this experience!!!

Captain Jack Tours saved my bf bday Louis ROCKS!
SOOOO perfect! Now I am NOT the planning type but this place made my trip with my bf for his bday soooo special and made such great memories and siick pictures! ;) 

I started planning a trip with Gold Coast Limo WHO ARE AWFUL! they messed up dates didnt confirm, even after I had emailed them over 10 times asking for confirm and some details NO CUSTOMER SERVICE!  THEN Gold Coast Tours last min ie hours before we started leaving for Santa Barabra said they couldnt help us out!  But could just get us a limo but we would have to figure the "tour" part out... which was not the deal and wld be impossible bc I NEVER had been to Santa Barbara.... 

ANYWAY im sooo happy Gold Coast Limo fell through bc I could not have asked for better with Captain Jack Tours... I admit I was put off by the name at first for fun and group tours sounding but It was the most romantic day and the price could not be beat. Captain Jack tour guide was sooo informative and nice and even hooked my bf with these siick cupcakes from a local town which my bf LOVED!

We did the "for serious wine lovers" tour and it started at 9:30 and we got back after 5pm 4 wineries that were stunning even an olive oil bar!

Anyway when I go back Im def heading to Captain Jack and I hope Louis is avail he SAVED my weekend surprise and made it a great gift for my bf!!! THANK YOU!!!

not a good idea to respond to a poor review by making fun of the customers name. accept the poor review and move on. the wineries were extremely snooty and catered more to certain people while we were there too.

My wife and I went on the Sideways tour recently and we had a really good time. Bob was a great guide and made the tour a very worthwhile experience. It's a big commitment to hang out with the same people all day, and we felt lucky to have had Bob as our guide. Fully recommend. Thanks

If you are going to Captain Jack's for a horseback riding tour, I would suggest just going straight through Circle Bar B Stables which is where they send you anyway. They are simply charging you $10 more. Save some money!

As far as my review goes, I was happy to hear that they would take our extremely last minute reservations (the day of!!!) because we were celebrating our bride to be! They were very nice about it and still got us in. Thanks, Andrew!

So much fun!!!  We had the best day on the beer tasting tour.  Our driver was a little strange, but at least he was entertaining.  My favorite stop was the Brewhouse in Santa Barbara.  The price was very reasonable and totally worth it.  We will definitely do this again!

RIPOFF! While the employees are perfectly nice, you can just book directly with whatever tour you want. Captain Jack essentially makes reservations for you and then charges a fee for that! Save some money and go directly to the individual tours and book yourself!

Mediocre tour experience. I planned a bachelorette party, working with Don and Mark was AWFUL! Terrible service!! They gave me wrong information SEVERAL times and were rude and unapologetic about their mistakes. When I called and talked to Mark about misinformation Don Had given me he said "do you think I'm defending Don? I'm not. call back tomorrow and tell him....." Because Mark obviously didn't care AT ALL. There's so many great tours in the area, I would NEVER recommend this tour company to ANYONE!!!

Well deserved 5 stars. Our driver don was excellent. Professional courteous friendly and full of information and helped keep our bi group on schedule. 

They stopped at the best places and the price was right. 

Only complaint is that they should upgrade their fleet with Leather seats and a better ac and tinted windows but Im probably being too picky!

We had family in from out of town and did the afternoon delight wine tour, which included 3 wineries and olive oil tasting.  We ask Meredith to customize the tour for a particular style of wine (Italian varietals) and they nailed it!  I was skeptical about the olive oil tasting, but it was a nice break from the wine tasting.  Definitely, worth it and Merf was a wonderful host. Looking forward to our next tour.

Great service throughout the wine regions of Santa Barbara. Your local tour hosts are very personable and knowledgeable of the areas. We loved spending our day with Adrienne who was a ton of fun showing us all the small towns in California wine country. Very accommodating and definitely worth the price.

Mixed feelings. 

Two friends and I did the Santa Ynez wine tour which included 3 wineries, olive oil tasting, and lunch (lunch not included in the price).  Overall, we enjoyed ourselves--beautiful scenery and perfect weather.  

We were not overly impressed with our guide, Emir.  He was disorganized and kept having to consult his agenda and look up directions to where we were going.  He had to turn around several times.  I highly recommend that the 12-passenger vans used for these tours have GPS installed.  Also, his English was poor and he couldn't answer very many questions from us.  Additionally, the last winery we went to in downtown Solvang was clearly a hook up for a friend of his who is the owner, as they are from the same country.  That just seemed a bit shady.  

I would recommend to Captain Jack that instead of going to downtown Solvang that the tour include a stop in lovely, quaint Los Olivos.   That is such a charming little town and not a tourist trap filled with gift stores selling cheap wares like Solvang.

We just had a very bad experience with this company! We chartered a yacht for a specific date and time, I received verbal confirmation, my credit card was charged, and then I proceeded to make the rest of our other arrangements (including train tickets to Santa Barbara) to coincide with the chartered tour. A few minutes later I was called back and informed there was a mix-up with the schedule and they weren't available after all! **UNBELIEVABLE** Unprofessional to say the least! Now we're stuck trying to make other arrangements after we had firmed-up all of our plans! PROCEED WITH CAUTION!!

Wineries were excellent, Los Olivos and Solvang had some superb wine choices. The tour itself was enjoyable. We visited 4 wineries and had enough time to take our time at each stop.
