The only downside was at the end, when we tipped. Our tour guide was pleasant during the tour, but at the end they were rude. This particular guide was insinuating that we hadn't tipped enough. Very unprofessional.

I absolutely LOVED the 3 vineyard wine tasting tour!!! We had the best group of fun-loving people and the wineries were beautiful! The ride was anything but dull and we had the most AWESOME tour guide around! Guy was not only informative and hilarious but kept the tour rolling on an upbeat note. I highly recommend this tour with Guy to anyone and everyone. Believe me, no matter who you are you will have a blast! Can't wait to book our next amazing tour with Captain Jacks:)

Amazing service. I'd highly recommend using Captain Jack's for any type of event or tour in the Santa Barbra area. We used them for an afternoon wine tasting tour and it was great. Meredith is super friendly and informative. We prefer white wine and she did he research to find those wineries with white wine flights. We told her about a winery we wanted to try and, while we were tasting, she called and confirmed it's availability. Thanks again a wonderful experience!

For my sister's bachelorette party, I wanted to find some activities that we don't usually get to do, so when I saw that there was horseback-riding, we were all excited to go! We haven't gone horse-back riding for years, so this was something unique. We stayed in downtown Santa Barbara and drove over to Circle Bar B Stables, it truly only takes about 20 minutes. It was a pleasant drive and there was enough parking. We were able to use the bathroom inside the lodge before heading out on the adventure. 

The gentlemen that helped us get signed in and get onto our horses was very nice and all the other cowboys we met along the way were also very friendly and professional. They have so many horses! I signed up for the 2:00 (1.5 hr) tour and it turns out it was just us in our group. Along the ride, we did pass by some other groups that either started earlier in the day or came after us. The trail has an incline and takes you to the top of the hill where you can see the view - it's nice that the horse can do all the uphill climbing for you! Our horses were sweet and knew what to do, where to go. My sister's horse, Chichi, was the only naughty one that kept wanting to eat and took huge chomps of greenery. Our tour guide/cowboy Jimmy led the way and made sure we were all doing okay. He helped us take pictures when we stopped at some resting points and viewpoints. It's fun to listen to his stories and to learn more about the horses and all the acres of land they have. 

Overall we really enjoyed the ride and seeing the spectacular views from up top. It's a fun activity and great way to enjoy the outdoors!

We had an incredible wedding reception for my daughter and son-in-law on the water at sunset.  Meredith was responsive, caring and thorough, and she even picked up our food beforehand. The crew were amazing, and I highly recommend Captain Jack's for any event. They went above and beyond for us for memories that will last a lifetime.

My husband and I were traveling through California for his birthday and we were delighted to have booked with Captain Jack's for a Sideways Wine Tour during our time in the Santa Barbara area. Having come to relive the movie, Meredith did an amazing job at taking us to 4 wineries from the film and grabbing us a dinner reservation at the Hitching Post II, just like the characters do. We even had time to stop at the ostrich farm! Meredith was very friendly, upbeat, and knowledgable. She kept the car rides between the tasting rooms entertaining with fun Sideways trivia questions. We would definitely recommend this tour service for anyone in the area!

Paid a pretty penny for a horseback ride from Circle Bar B Stables, which I wouldn't have mined if it was actually pleasant. Our "wrangler" guide  felt it necessary to talk about himself and opinions almost the whole tour, I really just wanted to ride the horse not small talk life stresses. Then there was a fire earlier this year that cleared out most of the lush green foliage that was center of this whole tour, so basically rode through naked, half burnt everything that was unappealing. Then I almost got stomped by horses at the end of the tour, good thing I was able to get behind some gate. At least they take your picture at the top -_-

I went on Captain Jack's kayak and hike tour and let me say that it was the worst hiking experience in my life.  The kayak part was great but we had to ride along with the other tour who was going horseback riding so we dropped them off and headed in the other direction which added about 40 minutes on to our tour.  The mode of transportation in a beat-up old van was also less than ideal.  We were promised a hot spring  which we saw but it was a tiny 4 x 4 hole in the ground covered in scum.  We then hiked a little more and the tour guide was actually cutting out a path with a machete as we hiked.  We were covered head to toe in ticks and decided we couldn't go any farther because the the ticks became unbearable.  The hike only lasted about 30 minutes the tour guide had no idea where he was going and the 30 we did hike was horrible.  He then dropped us off back at our car and we had to tip for such a horrible experience.  I couldn't believe how much I just paid to go hiking on a trail that was non-existent.  Never sign up to hike with Captain Jack.

Over the past weekend 5/1, I took the Captain Jacks tour with Meredith and it was my first time in Santa Barbra.  Along with the trip and its ending, when I was asked how was my time in Santa Barbra, I found my self kept mentioning how much fun I had on the wine tasting tour and how hospitable and professional our guide was.   The accommodations and positive and friendly energy made it great and relaxing!  I would highly recommend these services by all mean!  Best TOUR I have ever been on!! exceeded the expectation with the above and beyond attitude and accommodation, and being so kind about my EXPLICIT music hahah.  Luv you Mer!!

we did the $50 wine tasting room tour as part of a girls weekend trip.  (note- it ends up $60 because they add tax & tip. also remember this is tasting ROOMS, not wineries.)

our driver was laid back & not terribly interactive, which was fine w/ us.  the tasting rooms were hit & miss-- one was BAD.  I was pleasantly surprised by the olive oil & vinegar tasting

the scheduled lunch stop was NOT veggie or vegan friendly, our guide was kind enough to drop us at an alternative lunch spot.  SO appreciateed that, otherwise 2 of us would have gone hungry.

there were 8 of us, so I thought we'd score our own van.... however, they have 14 seaters, so we shared with a bachelorette party.  might have been worth it to pay extra for a private tour?

love the door-to-door service.

We had a great time on our wine tour.  The 3 vineyards were fantastic, the olive oil and vinegar tasting was fun, and the lunch was very nice.  Our Tourgide, Tracey, was fantastic.  She was very knowledgeable, incredibly friendly, and made the experience very fun for us.  She was constantly offering to take pictures for us and suggested places where the pictures would come out best.  She definitely was one of the highlights of our trip.  We also very much enjoyed getting to hear from the owner of the Shoe Strings winery.  She was very sweet, and her perspective on wine and the business was very interesting.  We also enjoyed the Gainey Ranch winery because we got the opportunity to tour the winery and taste some of the grapes.  All in all, great experience, and I would definitely recommend this trip and group to anyone interested.

My boyfriend and I decided to do a day trip to Santa Barbara; it was his birthday weekend so we decided to do a wine tour. After searching for several places, my boyfriend called Captain Jack's and made reservations for us a day ahead. We were told we would be picked up and then driven to 3 different wineries and an olive oil tasting too. That Thursday we got a call before the guy came to pick us up, and we were running behind (we were driving from Los Angeles) but he only had to wait a couple of minutes for us. Yens was an awesome tour guide! We got very lucky. For one, our group tour became a private tour because the other group of girls that was supposed to be there missed their train? YAY for us! 

The private tour experience is amazing! This way he was able to take us to the wineries that's more suitable for us, and we can easily ask him questions and have a one-on-one experience. Yens was super knowledgable about wines, and super personable. We laughed and it instead of feeling like a tour, it became a fun activity. He taught us a lot about wines. I knew nothing about wines; how to drink it, how it should taste, the different wines...etc etc. He is also great because he'll leave us alone for a while so my boyfriend and I got time to spend together. As we drove from destination to destination, he told us stories about our surroudnings and I feel like I know a little history behind beautiful Santa Barbara. 

Overall, an amazing experience and I HIGHLY recommend Captain Jack's for wine tours. My boyfriend and I had such a great time, it was fun all the way through. Every winery that we went to was great, we bought a bottle of wine at each one. And the place we went for olive oil tasting is a room of divine magical elixar. OLIVE OIL is amazing, I never knew of such a thing as olive oil tasting...and I'm in love. I'll give separate reviews for each winery and the olive oil place. But Yens did a great job at choosing the wineries for us. 

The wine tour lasted from about 11am-3pm. We opted for a no lunch break so that we can just end it earlier, plus we had breadsticks along the way, It was a stressfree experience, we got dropped off to our hotel room a bit tipsy and enjoyed a nappy! 

Thanks Captain Jack for such a great time! You made our time in Santa Barbara unforgettable!

Let me confess this is not really a review of the tour but of their tour policy.

They have a 2 person minimum to get on their tours - beer or wine tasting tour. You should go on the tour regardless if one person books it or 10 people book it. 

I just think that's "boulderdash". After booking tours to tour Napa by myself, I didn't think "No solo tour" policies would exist. 

I had intended to take this tour during my birthday visit to SB. I called ahead of time, actually thinking they would be overbooked. Not likely. I was told that I had to wait until a couple decides to book a tour. Even if they do, why would I want to be the 3rd wheel to their tour? I then switched over to a beer tour and it was the same policy. 

In the end they called me 2 days before the day to tell me the "good news"  - 3 people had actually booked the tour. At this point, after reading all the reviews on here I decided to do the tour myself (which is totally doable BTW). 

People shouldn't make policies that exclude single people. That's just me, I guess.

Meredith... you're the best!!!! Our Sideways Wine Tour was great, funny, delicious and everything in between. Thank you for your energy, Santa Barbara history, and smile! We will definitely do it again. The vineyards were great and the wines... FABULOUS!

Do not book a wine tour. 

We purchased the wine tasting tour for a friends birthday. The tour guide added absolutely nothing of value to the day. Overall, It was A LOT of driving with 45 minutes between stops which means the second you start enjoying the wine, it's ended by the drive to the next location.  You're better off just picking out wineries yourself and getting a car service/limo. 

The worst part was, once we were having fun and were finally a little tipsy, we had to leave to bring the tour guide back home or be charged more. 

Editing the review to clarify based on "Captain Jacks response" 

We actually paid for the full day tour and were brought to three wineries and the olive oil tasting. Each tasting location charged 15 dollars for anyone to walk in and do the tasting. The olive oil tasting was free, which was simply a tent on a farm road. In the end after a arguing with the tour guide about not receiving what was purchased, he reluctantly brought us to a tasting room in Solvang for a wine tasting. This was not a winery, just a bar. 

All in all, if you want to spend more time driving than tasting wine book with Captain Jack.

This is actually a response to the owner comment that I just received. 

Unfortunately, Yelp lets business owners comment but it does not give the reviewers (which this site was actually created for) a chance to respond to their comments. 

So here it is...

There's no difference in what you said and what I said. You've just gone on to reiterate your "no single people" policy which I still don't  agree with. Policies should not be made that exclude single people. PERIOD. I think it's "boulderdash" -- putting it politely. And to say that your tour is priced for that, well, that merits a curse word on its own. Maybe it's time you priced differently. It's even worse that you have gone on Yelp to play the defensive owner responding to all the negative reviews, how long will you do this, seriously? 

You should have priced your tour to leave with one person or with 10 people. We all didn't show up in the world attached to the hip so we should be able to imbibe in wine tours by ourselves if we choose to. Your business is the only business I've encountered that discriminates against single people. 

And you dare to perpetrate the fact that one can only enjoy the tour in a group, no, because the wine attendants and fellow wine enthusiasts are there to offer you the company and comic relief you need. So that's just B.S. once again. 

If you're reading this, listen to me, you can use the wine fee to rent a car, visit some wineries in Solvang, Santa Ynez and SB, yep. I did it. And I have you to thank for that.

Such a fun day! Meredith is the best and went above and beyond to make sure our day was memorable.  We loved her extensive knowledge of the area and her energy level is amazing.  She was very attentive but equally hands off while we tasted etc.  Can't wait to go back and take another tour!  Thank you Meredith

I decided to take a guest out on one of their Saturday night dinner sunset cruises for what I thought would be a nice, romantic dinner. The cruise was $178 for two people (originally 160 but tax included) for the two hour cruise compared to their normal cruises which are $80 for two. 

To put it simply, the food was awful. The menu included a basic caesar salad, lasagna, and mexican style fajitas. All of this was home cooked by one of the staff and left in aluminum trays for the guests to serve themselves on paper plates and eat with plastic utensils. Essentially, it was a home cooked meal that cost me $40 a person. I have no idea how they can charge so much for this, and will not go back again. The staff was nice and the actual cruise was great, but an $80 meal for two people would be way better spent at a real establishment. I did ask about the menu when I called to make a reservation and the person on the phone responded it is the chef's discretion. This honestly led me to believe that there was a professional chef preparing this food. 

Overall, do not spend the money on the "dinner" cruise, and opt for just a normal $40 cruise.

Don't waste your time. I was told when I booked this you get 3 wine tastings and an olive oil tasting. They picked us up 35 minutes late. When we got to the first winery we couldn't taste because we were late. We went to 2 other wineries and the pours were very small. We went to a bar after the 2 wineries where the pours were equally as small. Because of the late start we never go to do the Olive Oil tasting which was offered over the phone and supposed to be included in the price. They didn't even offer anything to make up for not being able to go other than we have no time. The ride back to drop off all the people in the van is way too long. They should have one drop off and pick up place. You have to sit there like a shuttle bus and wait for everyone to be picked up and dropped off and you can't drink. Not worth the money. Take a limo instead. It's a buzz kill.

Tracy was the best!  My girlfriend and I did a private tour with Tracy and we could not have asked for a better experience.  This tour was the highlight of our vacation in large part because of Tracy.  She was funny, very friendly and had some great stories.  I can't recommend her enough.

You guys BOOK THIS COMPANY!!

The owner, Meredith is AMAZING. We wanted to do a last minute girls' wine tasting trip though Santa Barbara and she accommodated our multiple changes of plans and changing guest number with a great attitude. She took us to two wineries and to an olive oil tasting (so good) in 127 degree heat and was so kind and patient through all of it. She even brought along cold water, towels, and snacks for us to make us more comfortable. She let us stop by her house to use the restroom on the way out and was just genuinely the sweetest human ever and took care of our group all day. We called her "Mama-Mare". So glad we found her and that she was able to accommodate to our group late minute on a holiday weekend. She knows a ton about Santa Barbara and what places were open during Covid. 

I'm always the one to plan events in my friend group and it was so nice to have her handle all the planning this time. Seriously worth every penny. As an added bonus, she dropped us off at dinner and picked us back up later in the evening, way after our tour ended. 

I can't say enough great things about her and my whole group of friends can't wait to book another trip soon!

We do wine tours in different towns across California at least 5x a year and this trip was by far the most fun tour I have ever been on. If you are looking for a great time, you MUST ask for Richard! He absolutely made our entire trip with his entertainment and encouraging attitude. He made sure all of us girls were safe and we will definitely be going back because of him.

Really fun time! I hosted a girl's trip to Santa Barbara last weekend and we stumbled upon Captain Jack's during our research. We signed up for the horse and wine tour and we had such a great day. Meredith is very personable and great with communication and answering questions. The day of, she arrived with a smile and shuttled us around place to place. She even had snacks in the back for us. The girls really adored her. Thank you Meredith for a seamless day in the great outdoors! Highly recommend :)

Truth be told,  I would rather be skinned alive than be out on the ocean.  I have an irrational fear of open water and what's worse, my husband loves it.  So, in an effort to avoid making my husband's life miserable I came up with the bright idea to book a kayaking tour....in the ocean.   It turns out that late November is a great time to accidentally get a private tour because we were the only ones who had a reservation.  Unfortunately for me, I had no one else to act as a distraction for my fear based awkwardness.                          
 
    We met Joe at the harbor and he gave us a brief, on land, lesson.  Then ish got real.  He said it was time to get in so I took a deep breath, said silently to myself "suck it the BLEEP up", and jumped in the front seat of the kayak.  Joe led us between the boats, under a catamaran, and out to where the harbor meets the ocean.  "Now lets head out to that buoy".  I thought "no big deal" until I realized it was the one with the sea lions which also happened to be the the 1 mile off shore mile marker.  He was talking the whole time but I was experiencing a flight or flight response with tunnel vision and muffled hearing.  The hubs said that Joe was awesome and gave tons of historical and anecdotal commentary.  I started gaining back normal vision and hearing once we headed under the pier and back to our starting point.  

     The hubs had a great time and I conquered a fear all while having a good time in the process.  Overall, it was a great way to spend $100 bucks and the afternoon.

Captain Jacks also has wine tours, sailing and sunset sailing tours, and horseback riding tours, and horseback plus kayaking tours.

Last June (2016) I purchased a gift card for my mom for wine tasting with a man named Don. He was very nice, never gave me any trouble, etc. the plan was to use the gift card last yr but life got in the way, we moved, etc. so this year on her birthday we were planning on using it. We set up the reservation and about an hour later my mom received a call from a guy named Mark. He was basically accusing me of duplicating the gift card and it not being legit (without doing any of his research before hand) I had to forward him emails, my bank account summary from last June, as well as the gift certificate. He was very rude, not apologetic, and we no longer felt comfortable giving this company our business. I requested a refund and spoke with a woman named Tanya (I believe) she was very nice, and I received the check. Im not the type of person to ever duplicate a gift card, especially for my mom for her birthday, and maybe next time before Mark just insinuates things before doing his research he should handle things a bit differently. Not a customers fault one of his employees didn't do the job correctly.

Tour name: $50 Santa Barbara Wine Tasting Tour
(Includes all Wine Tasting Fees & Transportation)

Biggest rip off ever!

Took a train into Santa Barbara from LA and a van was there to pick people up. Here's what $50 got (yes, I actually google mapped the distances):

-Van pick up to drive 2 blocks (0.2 miles)  to Oreana Winery.
-Van drives 0.9 miles to Carr Winery.
-Van drives 5.7 miles down the 101 to The Nugget Restaurant.
-The Nugget is western themed with moose heads on the walls. Very classy and  natural restaurant choice for a wine tasting event.
-Van drives 0.0 miles. Told to walk to the next building over for Summerland Winery.
-Van drives another 5.7 miles to Santa Barbara Winery. Told to walk back to the train station by ourselves (2 blocks). 

You can't keep any of the wine glasses from the wineries cuz tour companies have special rates that they work out with the wineries. The most disappointing part of the day was when we were at Santa Barbara Winery and found out that if you just walked in off the street, the $5 tasting fee included a souvenir wine glass. No souvenir for the people on the tour. IF you're coming from LA, drive yourself.

Horseback riding was fun! Our guide was very informative and made sure to answer all our questions. The views were great and the ride lasted a perfect amount, not too short but also not too long. Overall the experience was great!

I agree with the person below... do not book through this company. I emailed them earlier and no response from them yet. 

We were picked up real late, we were driving around for several hours, eliminated the olive oil tasting and one winery due to time. Maybe we would have arrived earlier at the firs winery (we got there at 11:45am) if we would have been picked up on time. We spent sooo much time in the cramped van, it was horrible. The driver wasn't entertaining, nor did she speak to us and kept having to drive back up and down the road and she missed some entry ways. We ended up leaving early, and had to take an Uber since there was no point to stay in the van and have to drop people off. 

Overall, not worth it (and I have been to this area before with different companies), the full day tour is aa total waste of money and time. 

I don't recommend this company for your grouped wine tasting event. Look elsewhere.

I have recommended Captain Jack's to friends that were visiting town when I wasn't around to host them. They had a great time. So when I saw a wine tour on Groupon I decided to give it a go. We had Groupons for a Santa Ynez wine tour. Called 6 times during business hours. Left 3 messages during business hours repeating the phone number twice each time as instructed and never heard back. $110 bucks on nothing. Awesome!

Even though Groupon claims the dollar amount paid is still valid with the company, their cheapest wine tour is 50 bucks and only in SB/3 tasting rooms downtown. Considering most tasting rooms only charge 10, you could easily do the same by yourself for much, much less. Basically a giant waste of money because of their poor customer service. Never buying a Groupon or recommending Captain Jack's again.

Wow. We had so much fun on the wine tasting tour! Our guide, Happy, was amazing. We appreciated the superb wine, local knowledge, and safe driving. There is no better way to tour than with Captain Jacks. Ask for Happy!

We had an amazing experience wine tasting with our guide Tracy. She picked us up right on time, and drove us out to Santa Ynez. We had a good mix of wineries plus olive oil tasting (so delicious), and they have super cute dogs on the premises! We chose the Afternoon Delight tour, since all day wine tasting sounded like a little too long for our group. The tour was mostly young people, which was nice for us. We also brought food for the bus, which i'd suggest doing on this tour since you don't stop for lunch. Tracy was a great guide, and really enhanced our day trip! The trip was professional, yet fun and laid back. I would definitely choose to go on this, or any of the other Captain Jack tours again.

I am so glad I booked this tour! Meredith and Tammy were absolutely amazing hosts! I worked with Meredith to get everything set up and she made everything so easy. My husband and I just got married and wanted our families to be able to be able to spend time together and have fun outside of the hustle and bustle of wedding activities. This was the perfect get away. The stop at the olive oil farm was amazing and different from other tours offered. You can also tell the relationships that each vineyard had with Meredith and the Captain Jack team. From pick up to drop off it was a seamless and super fun experience for everyone.

We went on a Sea Kayaking/Mountain Hiking tour. It was really very fun. The equipment was good and they let us go at our own pace, which was wonderful b/c I have been on trips where the guides don't care about the desired pace of the paying clients.The tour guides were fun and silly and we all really liked them.

My only negative about this company is that the tour guides didn't seem at all trained in anything besides routes and equipment use: No information on the history or biology of any of the places we visit... so really they were just people w/ equipment to follow... fun people albiet.

Great wine tour the day after Labor Day which was good as everywhere we went they said it was crazy over the weekend.  Ask for Tracy---she was the best:  knowledgeable, fun and caring for whatever we needed or wanted to do.  The air in the van went out halfway through and, although we all said we were OK, she called without our knowing and Captain Jack himself brought a new van.
We did Bridlewood, Fess Parker and Cimarone wine tasting + olive oil farm.  All great, nice variety. Finally, loved stopping for lunch at a restaurant.  Most tours include a picnic lunch which are probably fine but we enjoyed the restaurant experience and ordering what we wanted.
Thanks, Captain Jack, for bringing a new vehicle and be good to Tracy...she's a keeper!

Took the horseback ride and winery tour last week.  Our tour guide, Happy  was amazing.  I can see why she goes by Happy.  I recommend you ask for her if you book a tour through Captain Jack's.  When I booked this tour, the owner, Madalyn kept us informed via phone, text and email.  I would definitely book another tour through Captain Jack's.  Thanks Happy for a wonderful experience.

Meredith was a very engaging tour guide, who kept the three hour Santa Barbara city tour very interesting for all of us, including my 9 and 11-year-old daughters. She is a local who grew up in the area, and had an endless number of interesting facts about the area, some historically based and some from her personal experience. In addition to the fascinating tour, during which we drove throughout the city and made several stops, she was happy to give us advice on local restaurants and sent me a separate email with a list of wineries to visit when my family and I head up north to Los Olivos tomorrow. Highly recommend this tour!

Captain Jack tried to run over my dog!!  I was riding my bike down Cota Street to get home  from a few blocks away with my dog when a bloated old man with blood shot eyes (probably drunk) in a Captain Jack's Monster gas guzzler SUV starts riding really slow next to me (practically running over my dog!!) to tell me that it's not safe to ride my bike with my dog on Cota Street.  I explained that I live on Cota Street and I was just trying to get home.  I hoped this would satisfy him and he would leave since my dog was visibly shaken and I was afraid that in his drunken state Captain Jack would kill my dog.  He didn't leave though!!  He continued to harass me for another block insisting that Cota Street is not safe for me and my dog.  Clearly he failed to see the irony of the situation.  Just when I was about to let go of my dog's leash so that she could run home and be free of this mad man, he finally relented and drove away.  Bad form, Captain Jack.

Santa Ynez Valley Wine Tasting Tour

So, I'm far from a wine connoisseur. Actually, I'm not even a wine drinker. In fact, I don't really drink any alcohol. But, THIS was a great experience. 

Tracy - our guide - picked several of us up around 9 AM in a minivan and we drove around the area to pick up the remaining 9 people, bringing the group total to 13. She drove us through beautiful country land and gorgeous vineyards to arrive at our first stop - Shoestring Winery, where we sipped on six wines in the barrel room. This was followed by great little tasting room at Babcock Winery and Vineyards in Lompoc and then we visited Foley Estates Winery, which appeared to be a more formal tasting setting. We also had a chance to visit a ranch in Los Olivos where we tried multiple olive oils and balsamic vinaigrette, which were all excellent. 

Each tasting included about six wines, many of which could be "revisited" if you so desired!

Lunch was at Trattoria Grappolo in the cutest little area in Santa Ynez. Although it wasn't included in the cost of the tour ($79), the lunch prices were very reasonable and it gave us a chance to get something substantial in our stomachs and to chat with the group in a relaxed setting. The bread at this place was incredible!

Tracy was fantastic. If you go, and I hope you do, ask for her. She is personable, energetic and lots of fun, which are all important since you'll be with your guide from about 9-4:30 PM!

This was indeed a great experience even for non-serious wine drinkers. Pinots, Cabernets, Roses, Syrahs and Merlots are plentiful...enjoy! And definitely ask for Tracy!

I booked a kayaking tour a few weekends ago. When I called on the phone they were super helpful. We got there and it was pretty easy to find as long as you pay attention to the directions carefully. The tour guide was a cool guy and had a lot of info. It was a fun adventure and would definitely recommend them for anyone else looking for a fun time in SB.

Jim and I decided to kick off our New Year's Eve with a wine tasting tour of Santa Barbara's local tasting rooms.  We opted for the $50 wine tour ($10 per person gratuity is added to this price as well) provided by Captain Jack's.  They come and pick you up at your hotel and drop you off there after the tour ends. They promptly arrived on time to get us at around 11:30 am and the tour lasted until around 5:00.  We were joined by three other couples.


Our tour guide (Iaon) was really friendly and courteous. He took us to 4 different tasting rooms around town and also we stopped at Santa Barbara Fish Market for lunch. Lunch was not included in the price.  The tastings and gratuity fees are though.

Now, I cannot say I enjoyed any of the wines we tasted very much. They were all unremarkable, but I had a lot of fun on the tour, nevertheless.  Even at our first stop we were wine tasting with Mary-Kate Olsen lol   I keep forgetting how old that girl is, but I digress.  We were not rushed at any of the tastings and all in all, this was a great way to end 2009.  I would recommend taking this tour if you want a taste of the downtown Santa Barbara tasting rooms. This is pretty comprehensive and worth the price.

We booked the "Santa Rita Hills Wine Tour", which Captain Jacks bills as the more serious tour. While it isn't for college groups or Bachelor parties, it was great fun. Our tour guide/driver made it such a great experience. Mark was incredibly knowledgeable, but not in an annoying way. He had a great feel for when it was a good time to disseminate information or pull back because we were in more of a private conversation. He taught us about grape/wine DNA, cloning, barreling, aging, which wines grow well in which kind of weather. All of it was fascinating and we will have a much better idea of what we're drinking in wine for the rest of our lives. The vineyards were wonderful. Our favorites were Foley and Melville. The tasting fees were included in the Captain Jacks fee. We did buy wine at every stop, but the wineries gave us a nice discount. We do recommend doing this tour on a Sunday if possible. The crowds were much less than Saturdays. All and all we loved Captain Jacks, had a fantastic guide in Mark and definitely want to visit the vineyards again. Well done.

We went on a wine tour and our tour guide was Garret? I'm not even sure what his name was because he was so weird and quiet. He didn't provide a tour, he drove us around. 
I went with a bachelorette party of 9. We did a half day trip and visited 3 wineries and a farm where we tasted olive oils. We spent about 35 minutes at each winery. The wines were fine, but it only amounted to 18 1 oz. pours. The tour was $115 without tip. I think this price is excessive considering a wine tasting is $12. So we spent about $900 as a group on a 15 passenger van for 5 hours. 
I would not recommend this wine tour, there are much better values at other places. When I return to Santa Barbara, I'll choose a different activity.

What a fun day! We were picked up in an suv complete with water and snacks. Went to 3 great wineries and olive tasting.  Tammy was a terrific driver and fun to be around. She stopped off and picked up a yummy lunch for us so we could spend as much time trying (too many! ) wines but we didn't have to worry about driving so it was ok that we got a little tipsy!! Meredith the owner was so sweet and pleasant and really helpful with booking and getting details arranged. I'll definitely be calling them again next time I'm I'm town!

My sister and I went on this tour for a sister trip and weekend getaway to Santa Barbara.  We had a blast and enjoyed all the wineries we visited.  It was also a great way to see different parts of Santa Barbara that we wouldn't have known about other wise.  The most enjoyable part of the tour was the group of strangers that joined us.  We all got well acquainted by the first winery.  Couldn't have asked for a better group of people to sip wines throughout Santa Barbara.  Our wine tour guide, Peggy, was very knowledgeable and sweet and kind.  She strived to make sure we were all satisfied and were having a good time.  My sister and I both would go through this company again and would suggest it to all.

Tour: Santa Barbara Wine Tasting Tour
Price: $50 per person

Everything was poised to be a lovely day of wine tasting in Santa Barbara for my friend's bachelorette party. We were scheduled to visit 4 wineries and stop for lunch along the way. Captain Jack's SUV arrived on time to collect us from the hotel and drove us to our first winery. We piled into the car to head to winery #2 (note we were short one seatbelt), and got a phone call that no one should ever get. The bride and maid of honor's parents were in a roll over car accident. They need to get to the hospital that is 3 hours away. The tour guide dropped us back at the hotel, and said he was sure we would get a refund because of the nature of the situation.

After multiple unreturned phone calls and messages, we were left high and dry. No refund. No partial refund. Nothing. Not even in an emergency situation. Let's also consider here that wine tasting costs $10 per person per winery. Since we didn't visit THREE wineries, that's THIRTY DOLLARS per person that Captain Jack pocketed. 

Bottom line: These are not good people. Don't give them your business.

Just returned from a wonderful 1 day wine tour with Captain jack's.  Meredith was beyond accommodating and helped us find a day tour for 4 plus two dogs plus outdoor tastings.  Our driver Happy was also friendly and amazing.  Even though there was a lot of weather and rain the day of our tour, Captain Jack's made it a great experience.  Highly recommmend!

A true highlight from our recent trip to Santa Barbara.  Very kind, genuine and incredibly informative and creative tour guide who customized our tour to fit our family's needs and interests.  Run, don't walk towards this one!  //Raya and the kiddos.

We needed to bring a puppy with us, and Meredith went above and beyond to find three vineyards that allowed pups. Tanya was our host, she made sure we were having the best time. She even fit in a trip to Solvang where we got to do an olive oil sampling, and grabbed some pastries from one of the many fantastic cafes. Overall I couldn't recommend this company more. Truly a fantastic time from start to finish.
Great option if you want to experience multiple wineries and know new places, Meredith was awesome!! She went above and beyond to make our experience definately one to remember.

Tracy is amazing and the best tour guide anyone could ever hope for!!

My friends and I did the horseback ridding and wine tasting tour and it was worth every penny! We have a wonderful time and it was so nice to have everything taken care of and planned out for us.  Though, we were a little sore from the horseback ridding it was magical! You get to see the area in a completely different way and it's an over all wonderful experience! The wine tasting was so much fun and I will for sure request Tracy again for our next tour. She is fun, upbeat, very knowledgeable about the area and really makes the trip even more memorable!

Took the deep sea fishing tour and was highly disappointed. The captain called to reschedule 15 minutes before the launch time due to 'motor problems' and would not accommodate my girlfriend and I for having to spend an extra night and day other then they 'apologize'.  According to them it was a 99% chance they were going to go and the motor would "work" since the captain liked going on Saturdays compared to Fridays....The next day after we rescheduled, after having paid for everything, the captain tells everyone they will not launch unless each person puts in another $10 due to others not showing. As we got to the cashier to pay, knowing that if we cancel noone else can go, they raised the price to $15 per person. In total we ended up paying over $90 each person for a half day of fishing with nothing more then a "we can go or not I don't care" by the captain after asking if we could get a discount for a future trip or longer time out at sea since we were paying for a 3/4 day trip already. This was a total rip off and a terrible first experience deep sea fishing. I will never reccommend this tour to anyone looking for a deep sea fishing experience in Santa Barbara.  Aside from the deckhand being nice and very helpful with the trip, the captain was snotty and could careless if we were on the boat or not.  This type of service irked me even more considering the fact that my girlfriend had never been fishing before and this was the type of service we received.  If i wanted this type of service and spend $90 each I could have fished off the pier and afterwards eaten at an expensive restaurant in Downtown Santa Barbara.

Update: I contacted Captn Jack Tours, and they said they are mailing me the cheque for the extra money we had to pay...  and also providing us with another tour. We are going to try the wine tasting tour, and we will write an update after we attend it and if we received the cheque.
Had such a blast on this wine tasting experience! Would definitely recommend this company... It was such fun and easy, that the time just flew. Can not sing enough praises!

We went on a bachelorette wine tour this weekend and I have to say, if something could go wrong EVERYTHING went wrong. Although the driver was nice, we were instantly greeted with a van that had NO working air conditioning. It's actually 100 degrees in Santa Barbara this weekend, so imagine a full van of 13 people (jam packed actually) in 100 degree heat. We contacted the company and were told we would be compensated with an extra winery. Let's be honest, we loved the winery's, but there is no way an extra stop would compensate for the immense heat and uncomfortability that we endured. One of the girls actually got car sick from it and was ill at our first stop. The saving grace was the wineries. They were wonderful but no one could pay attention because we were all so hot from the van. Also no lunch was planned so we ended up ordering lunch and it took well over an hour and a half. Only to find out that one girl never had her order placed. There was also another mishap then- but I'm uncomfortable posting it since it could cost a job.  Three of the girls couldn't imagine enduring another 40 minute van ride in the heat and actually had paid for an uber to come get them. We understand that things happen, but they never should have sent a van with no air.  It almost ruined the entire experience for us.

Not one to leave reviews really but Meredith and Kathryn exceeded my expectations. The anniversary surprise for my girlfriend could not have gone any better. She won't stop talking about it. From beginning to end it was great, and it's worth every penny. Meredith and Captain Jacks have made us friends/customers for life. Thank you!

Most Wine Tasting tours will run you upwards of $100 per person (or at least thats what I saw when I was planning the last trip.  So then what do we do?  We look to Yelp for help and yelp help is what we got!

Captain Jack's specializes in tours for those who love the outdoors and booze.  We did the horseback ride/wine tasting.  

Horseback Riding was partnered with a ranch that has horses to take you though SB canyon.  They treat you like newbies (which we were) but aren't condescending about it.  They make it fun despite the pain of the trotting horses.  Rocket, I will miss you! (My horse) Until next time!

The wine tasting was only going to 2 different tasting rooms which was kinda of blah but was still fun.  Our driver took us to Kunin Wine tasting room and Coastal Winery.  We ended up buying a bottle of d'Asti from the Coastal winery.  

Our driver was really cool and kept a good conversation going on the whole time without intruding on our own private conversations.  The best part is that he picked us up and dropped us off at our hotel.  A MAJOR plus for those who like to "Taste" a lot of wines.

If you want to do wine tasting in SB, definitely check out Captain Jacks, great prices with knowledgeable guides.

PS, I think they raised the prices since I booked.  I paid $200 and their site now says $300.

My husband and I went on the Santa Ynez wine tasting tour and  we had really great time! The whole experience was simply amazing. 
They came and pick us up at our place and drop you off in this same place after the tour ended. The van was clean and very comfortable. They provided bottled water during the trip. We had really great  guide who was not only friendly and informative but also introduced us to some  interesting historical facts. We also visited couple of the historical sites on the way.  The wine tasting was great, but in addition, we also did some olive oil and vinegar tasting in Los Olivos. Great bonus!
 Overall the whole experience  was easygoing and well organized and certainly worth the price!

Had a blast on this tour! Our tour guide, Happy, lived up to her name and more. She was so kind and informative. We visited LaFond Winery and a cool spot in Solvang that sold local "Old World" wines with a twist. She was very accommodating and seemed to know all the ins and outs of Santa Barbara and the surrounding area. Highly recommend this tour if you're looking for a pleasant, fun and relaxing time on your tour of the chillest place in Southern California

Want to thank the staff at Captain Jack's for organizing a wonderful wine tour.  And a special thank you to Happy, who went out of her way to be sure we had a great time. She was fun, informative and flexible in making adjustments as we went to fit in as much as possible.

We were in Santa Barbara for our anniversary. My wife loves horses, so we decided to take a ride on the beach. It was a perfect day after a lot of recent rain. The sun was out and it was close to 70 degrees. We really enjoyed it. The only problem we found was that we had to cross a major highway overpass and it was quite busy (not that this is newsbreaking in California). Of course I have the horse that gets spooked by cars... just a bit of information that was given to me prior to my ride. I'll be sure to ask more questions. 

They were really accommodating to us. Our guide Charlie, took care of my wife. He felt that she was nervous so he took the lead reign and led her the entire way to the beach and then let her ride on her own. That was really cool of him to do. I think my horse was part Clydesdale. That sucker was huge. Which is not a problem considering what a fatty I am. Overall we had a great time and my wife couldn't wait to print the photo of her riding on the beach. Something she's always wanted to do.

Amazing moments caught during our horseback ride on the beach! Meredith, thank you for being accessible to all my questions and being informative. Elena was a great tour guide. We loved the horses! Osso and Rupert are so docile and smart!

I had planned this for my husband for Father's Day. Meredith was so helpful to make this so special for him/us.

When we meet her at the hotel lobby, she had such a presence, her energy was just amazing and so welcoming. She explained our plans, and off we went to 3 amazing locations (2 breweries and 1 distillery) plus made a few pit stops  to see some history spots!  We felt like VIP's going to the breweries, because the waiters were so attentive , we didn't have to wait, and were informative about the drinks we were drinking.

Best experience ever! Thank you again for everything Meredith , can't wait to do it again and try new spots!!!

This past weekend some friends and I visited this establishment and it became an experience of a lifetime. Our tour guide, the one and only Meredith was the absolute BEST. The tour she set up for my group of friends, was very educational, fun, relaxing, and several memories were created. Meredith was overly accommodating, and because of her we all tried elk for the first time . Later we had the privilege to meet some more of their guides and again, their personalities great you with such a warm welcome. If you are ever in Santa Barbara and wish to wine taste, You simply have to do it with Captain Jacks.

Went on the harbor kayak tour. The business seems to be very... laid-back. While we have kayaked before, we weren't given any instructions on how to kayak in tandem (two people in one kayak). The equipment seemed to be a bit old and tattered. However, our guide was very friendly and the tour of the harbor and underneath the pier and around the sailboats, was fun and enjoyable.

This was my second trip with Captain Jacks wine tasting tour in the last two years. Both experiences were absolutely magnificent and I will definitely be using their services again. Here are a few reasons why this company just does it best... 
1. They are incredibly accommodating and polite when booking. They will work with you and actually pick you up from a location of your choice (Within reason, of course) 
2. Incredibly punctual. They say 9am, they actually mean 9am! 
3. Great, great, great employees! I can't stress this enough! Our tour guide totally made the trip so much more exciting! Guy you da man! Guy was a good driver; he had endless amounts of knowledge about the history of Santa Barbra! Very interesting,  I leaned tons! He is a great asset to the team! I can't remember who I had the first time around but she was awesome too.  
4. We were given ample time to take photos at all stops. Never did I feel rushed or compelled to get going, it was fun and relaxing.

The team at Captain Jack's was amazing. They catered to our wishes and set up a perfect day of tasting in the Santa Ynez Valley. They are knowledgeable and have very good relationships with the vineyards and tasting rooms.

We had a great day on the Wine Country Tour.  The only reason I'm giving it four instead of five stars is the first winery was quite a distance.  If a closer winery had been chosen, we might have been able to go to one more winery.  But then the price would probably be a little higher.

All the wineries and the olive oil farm were expecting us and treated us very nicely, with lots of information about the wines we were tasting.

The restaurant we stopped at for lunch in Santa Ynez was quaint and the food terrific.  (Lunch is not included in the price, which you are told before booking.  No surprises.)

Our tour guide Rick was knowledgeable about the area and loved taking us to his favorite wineries.  We were treated well and felt Captain Jack's appreciated our business.

I was one of the 13 very lucky people who were fortunate to go on the amazing Santa Ynez Valley Wine Tasting Tour this past weekend. Our group had Tracy as our tour leader, who was one of the most amazing people I have ever met. She was totally down to earth, and was so excited about her job. Honestly, having the right tour guide makes all the difference. 

I went on the tour with two of my best friends as a girl trip. We loved the first winery the best, which was Shoestring. It had the "most" vineyard feel, as it was the only one where we could see the actual vines and grapes. 

The weather was absolutely amazing and all of the wine connoisseurs were extremely pleasant and knowledgeable at every stop. They didn't mind answering all types of questions, especially the ones you might be too embarrassed to ask if you are new to wine. 

I would absolutely recommend this tour if you are looking for some good wine and fun. I wouldn't recommend this particular tour if you are looking for an extremely romantic one on one time with a significant other, as you are in a big group the entire time. 

Thank you again Captain Jack's and Tracy for a great memory!

Just came back from a Santa Rita Wine Tour with Captain Jack's and it was fantastic!  We visited 4 great wineries: Lafond, Melville, Foley and Babcock.  Mark, our tour guide, was extremely knowledgeable about wines and the local area which made for an amazing day!  Previously we had taken Captain Jack's Santa Ynez Wine Tour with Tracy.  She also went above and beyond to make sure we all enjoyed ourselves.  In all, we had a fantastic experience both times and will definitely do it again when we're in Santa Barbara. 
Mal & Corinne
Arizona

This was my third trip with the Captain & Crew.

The first two were the three-vineyard tour and the horseback tour about five years ago, and this time was the distillery & two vineyards tour.

All three trips were excellent.

Meredith (Director of Adventures) was rock solid with help selecting, planning and arranging the latest tour and explaining the measures to protect everyone from the covid.  We felt safe the entire time.

Our guide, Tanya, was fantastic and made the trip special.  We really enjoyed our day with her.  She shared so much about where we were at each leg of the adventure and about the community and history.  All very interesting and appreciated.

This tour was a birthday gift to me from my boyfriend and he couldn't have found a better present. We did a horseback riding tour with wine tasting afterwards and we had the best time. We were picked up by Tanya who was our driver for the day, and from the start it was such a smooth experience. We did this trip with one other couple, and each of us had our own cooler with drinks and snacks in the bus. We then went to the ranch were we rode up to a vista point with our wrangler for 1,5hrs. Unfortunately, we didn't have the best weather but it didn't take away from the experience at all. 

After horseback riding, Tanya drove us to the first vineyard were we started a tasting while she picked up lunch for us. We were able to pick our lunch from a pre-selected menu prior to the trip, and it included sandwiches plus salad and dessert. The two wine tasting spots were super cute, and the wine was pretty good too. Overall, we had a really fun day and Tanya was a fantastic tour guide. I'd definitely recommend booking a tour with Captain Jack's.

We put together a trip for my friend's birthday and 8 of us did the beer tasting...We had a blast!!! We got tastings at 3 breweries, the van picked us up from our hotel and dropped us back off, gratuity included we spent $60 per person.  It was well worth it!

Holly mentioned below the 3 breweries and we did the same tour.  I really enjoyed the Brewhouse, we got a tour and got to see how the beers were made as well as a great selection of food.  I got the pork flavored beer  with my burger...They also had a Jalapeno flavored beer and I bought a bottle and brought it back home.

If you're in Santa Barbara you need to do this little excursion, I highly recommend it!!!

Had a wonderful day driving to & around the Santa Ynez valley. We visited wineries & olive groves, all great experiences. Meredith was so friendly, fun & knowledgeable. Highly recommend Captain Jack's to everyone!

We went wine tasting with Captain Jack's and had an absolute blast. They were right on time and kept us on schedule the entire day. Meredith and Tammy were so fun and they brought us all snacks and water for the rides in between wine tastings. The van was brand new and extremely comfortable for a 6 person group. I will only use them going forward for any and all wine tours in the Santa Barbara / Santa Ynez area.

My wife and I booked the horseback riding and wine tasting package.  It was awesome! Tanya came and picked is up and drove us to the ranch.  She was entertaining, personable and extremely knowledgeable about the area!  My wife and I really enjoyed her company.  The horseback riding was fun, safe and just enough time to mot be sore but still wanting more. LOL.  Next it was to Solvang where we ate some serious lunch, walked around and enjoyed the sights.  There was also a wine tasting in town which was perfect.  Next it was the topping: a beautiful vineyard for wine tasting.  All the wines were superb.  We didn't want to leave! Overall, do yourself a favor and book this company!
This company is great. They are very accommodating, professional, friendly, and knowledgeable. We had a great experience all thanks to Meredith and Tanya.

Booked a horseback riding and kayaking tour for Sunday, 5/16/21. I found this place on Yelp and was curious about the different types of tours offered. I was interested by their great reviews and the different tours listed on the website. We wanted to do something fun while celebrating our 6th wedding anniversary for the weekend.

I called the number and Meredith answered. Although she was not in the office, she told me she would give me a call back. She sent me a text a few hours later letting me know that she will call me as soon as she could. She called me back  and I gave her the date we preferred. She told me that they had openings for the date we wanted.

We were contacted the morning of our reservation by Meredith and Christina. Christina picked us up from our hotel and drove us to Circle Bar B Ranch for our horseback ride. It was about 25 to 30 minutes. She even had snacks and water bottles in a cooler for us. She was fun to talk to and super nice during our ride. She hung out for the 1.5 hour horseback ride until we got back then she drove us to the harbor for our kayaking tour.

Be aware that horseback riding does have a weight limit. I found out over the phone after I was charged, but we were fortunate enough to be able to book at a different ranch who would make it work for us. We were so grateful. Everyone at the ranch is so courteous and friendly. They made us feel very welcomed and safe. Jordan was our guide, and he was amazing. We loved our experience with him. I wasn't allowed to take my purse with me in case I accidentally dropped it or the strap would get caught somewhere on the saddle and end up getting myself hurt. Their biggest goal is keeping the riders safe.

Although our guide for the kayaking tour wasn't feeling well the day of our reservation, Meredith was so kind enough to fill in for us. We are so happy that she did. We believe she made it so much more enjoyable for us. She also took these amazing photos of us on her phone on the kayak up until her phone battery died. She is so kind and was considerate during our ride around the harbor. Even though my husband and I weren't in sync and took a while to catch up, Meredith was very patient with us. We really appreciated that we didn't feel rushed or were being a burden even though we bumped into each other a few times. We had a great time and look forward to booking more tours in the future.

Meredith is amazing!   I booked a private premium wine tour purposely to go to those gems that few travel to walk the winery and taste delicious wine grown by passionate owners who actually pick the own grapes, crush and bottle on site.   95% of wineries buy their grapes from other wineries not the ones Meredith shared with me.  From a women owned winery that is a passionate horticulturist with incredible wines to an organic winery.  Meredith is so knowledgeable about the area and full of interesting facts about the areas it was such a pleasurable experience.   I will continue  to book tours with her and her team

BE AWARE!!!! this is not the actual ranch, but a tour operator that charges much more for exactly the same tour when book directly and they are complete thieves. They charged my cc for an additional horse we did not requested and wont return the money, almost $100!!!. Their costumer service (or rather, lack of), in particular, Louise, was extremely rude about the whole thing over the phone. Talking over me no matter what i said. FYI, This is my 2 time I do this Horseback riding but I can ensure you..... it is the last!

This past weekend my boyfriend and I went up to Santa Barbara to celebrate his birthday and attempt to get a glimpse of normal during this pandemic. To our surprise, we were able to make a reservation to go kayaking with Captain Jack's. Something neither of us have done before mind you. The whole process was super easy. We live in Orange County and were able to make reservations and pay in advance which made everything super seamless. On the day of our reservation our guide Meredith was a breath of fresh air. She recommend a place to grab a quick bite to eat before our tour. Not being from the area this was extremely helpful as some restaurants are still closed for social distancing. She made us feel super comfortable and not like a "fish out of water" when it came to being new to kayaking. I think the banter with complete strangers was great escape from the current reality for all parties involved. Meredith was super knowledgeable about the history of Santa Barbara and seems to really enjoy where she lives which made it that much more enjoyable for us. She made such an impact we were urged to go home and purchase a kayak of our very own. I highly recommend Captain Jack's Tours. When you go, say hi to Meredith for us!

We had the best time on our horseback riding/winery tour! We truly have only good things to say. Meredith was so sweet and friendly, and very knowledgeable about the area! We will definitely be booking another tour with Captain Jack's.

We had Suzanne as our guide for a brewery tour and it was fantastic! We were picked up from our place and taken to 3 different breweries. Suzanne gave us tips on what was good and was super friendly the whole time. I definitely recommend this to anyone!

When my sister who's also my Maid Of Honor told me we would be going wine tasting in Santa Barbara for my bachelorette weekend I was overjoyed! We instantly went on Yelp and started hunting for the most cost effective wine tour company with great reviews. We landed at Captain Jacks. My sister called up Jack (who's apparently much easier to get ahold of when you're paying), she booked the tour for nine and we were on our way!

The van picked us up at our hotel and to our surprise there was only room for 8, meaning only 8 seatbelts. Yet Captain Jack gladly took our payment for 9 girls. We get to our first winery and everything was fine and dandy, we all pile in the car to head to the second winery when my sister and I received the scariest phone call of our lives. Our parents had been in a rollover accident and they're both being rushed to the hospital. We immediatelyreturned to the hotel where they were the epitome of class and compassion and only charged us for cleaning fee for that day and refunded our money for the night. 

The driver from Captain Jacks was also very understanding and told us not to worry, that as long as we explained our situation we should get some sort of refund. 

Days later my sister called Captain Jack and explained the situation. He merely offers a $175 gift certificate for a future visit. My biggest question is why on earth would we return to an establishment who's unwilling to work with it's customers during a time of tragedy. In complete disbelief I asked my fiancé to talk to Captain Jack. He spoke to someone named Andrew who brought up Disneyland as an example. He stated that if we were to visit Disneyland and hadan emergency and had to leave, they wouldn't refund our money. He dismissed the example as a pathetic attempt to make a comparison with no validity. He then asked to speak to Captain Jack but Andrew told him he had left for the day. My Fiance called the following day 4 times with no luck getting a hold of Jack. 

When my Fiance spoke to Andrew again he explained that any refund  would've been accepted and appreciated. I'm dissapointed that a business with zero ethics can still be in business.

Please make sure your family is safe and sound in a bubble before giving this company your money because you will be left high and dry in the case of an emergency.

Unbelievable experience! Our guide was fabulous and the combination of a horseback ride followed by wine tasting and a ride back to our hotel was just the right way to tour Santa Barbara!!! I would highly recommend this to any friend or group tour!

We had a wonderful day with Happy.  She was great to hang out with, has tons of knowledge of the area and showed us a great time. We did horseback riding with wine tasting afterwards.  Would highly recommend.

On my birthday on July 4th, 2017, a last minute/spur of the moment horeseback riding tour was scheduled with Captain Jack Tours (a little pricey, but again last minute)!  The overall experience was phenomenal.  The ride from beginning to end was excellent. It was my girlfriend's first ride and our guide (Rick) was incredibly patient, tolerant, informative and the overall epitome of ovearll professional courtesty. Outstanding views and Noteworthy experience; highly recommend this experience to those who appreciate adventure.  Truly enjoyed our horses Buck & Shelby, they are the best.  Additionally the folks (ownership & staff) at the resort on the premises are simply, nothing short of amazing!  

EL S.

We signed up for the "Sideways" Wine Tour into the Santa Ynez valley and it was a wonderful experience. Our driver Meredith was fun and easygoing and shared her years of knowledge about the region. The stops along the tour coincided with the exact scenes from the movie and the provided list of movie tidbits and trivia made for a great time. We highly recommend this tour if you want to kick back, relax and enjoy your time in the wine country without having to worry about driving yourself. Take the tour if you are ever in the Santa Barbara area! Well worth it!

My family booked a tour with Captain Jack's and it was a true pleasure to work with Meredith and Tanya!  Meredith worked with us to customize and tweak a few things for us and it made the day a raging success.   Tanya our guide is knowledgable and fun.  The most tell tale sign of how awesome these folks are was the reception we got at every winery we walked into.  I have been on many tours and most of them when you walk in the winery staff groan or at best are mildly interested in who you are.  When we walked up with Tanya we were met with hugs, laughter and a genuine sense of pleasure.  I highly recommend booking with this company you will not be disappointed.

This tour is amazing. I planned a tour with them for a bachelorette over ten years ago and had a great experience so a month ago when my friends and I were planning a ladies trip I called captain jack. Meredith was awesome. Not only was the whole process seamless but the communication, knowledge, price and experience were beyond my expectations. All I told Meredith was that I wanted three wineries, outdoors and our group wanted to travel together. She took it from there. Not only did she take us on the wine tour but she also graciously offered to take us to our dinner reservation and pick us up when we were done. BOOK WITH THEM!! Do not waste your time looking elsewhere just call captain jack. You won't regret it.

Meredith is the bomb! She gave us an architectural tour of Santa Barbara. Not only is she well-informed with facts and interesting anecdotes, she's a delight and someone I consider a good friend now :) Thank you!

One of the passengers who seem to know the crew and appeared to be a regular, brought his two boys, both around 6.  Total terrors. Running all over the boat jumping over people and knocking things over.  The Dad kept apologizing while he got drunk and worked on picking up the single ladies on board.  Crew walked calmly by and ignored the kids which were causing problems.  Crew just picked up after them.  At the end the Dad even yelled out to the two boys to apologize to the whole boat for ruining their trip.  Very unprofessional that the crew would allow such behavior.  I won't be returning.

Hands down the best wine tasting tour I've ever been on! Meredith was so kind and accommodating to our group. She curated a well rounded tour, picked us up from our hotel in a luxury van filled with snacks and refreshments and gave us many suggestions on other activities to do in Santa Barbara during our stay. ABOVE AND BEYOND CUSTOMER SERVICE! The tour was so much fun, I didn't want it to end. Thank you, Meredith, for making our weekend so special.

My girlfriends and I had the absolute best time on captain Jack's tour! Meredith is so sweet and made us feel like we could just sit back and enjoy ourselves. I will never go wine tasting and not use captain Jack's tour- they made everything seamless and fun. Thank you for making our girls trip so memorable!

name: Pike’s Surf & Sk8 School
categories: Education, Surf Schools, Specialty Schools, Surfing, Active Life

yelp_reviews.text:
The only reason I give 4 stores instead of 5 was the amount of time it took to have my lesson.

The lesson itself was fantastic.  My friend and I had a great time.  We both were standing and successfully catching waves in our first lesson (I had never surfed before).  My arms got tired pretty quickly (lots of paddling), but he was kind enough to paddle us out for the last 45 minutes or so, so we could take full advantage of our lesson time.

I really liked that he taught us how to do things safely.  He was a great coach, and I really feel like I learned a lot.  I think I'll be getting another lesson soon!

My 8 year old son and I took a joint lesson from Pike's.  James spent some time before we got into the water to provide us with some ocean knowledge.  Once in the water, he quickly was able to identify each or our comfort levels in the water.  He helped us each to get up and actually ride a wave on our first time out.  My son rode one wave all the way into the beach and was able to step off onto the sand.  

At the end of the lesson, he offered that I could contact him with any questions about equipment, surf locations, etc.

All in all, a great experience and we will get be repeat customers.

My fiancé and I took a two hour surf lesson and it was AWESOME!  We both had a very good time and are really psyched on surfing now.

Pike was not only patient and kind, but professional and a lot of fun.  By the end of our lesson today, we were both catching waves and having a great time.  

I had 1 previous surf lesson before (on the East Coast) and it pales in comparison to the lesson we had today: we received so much more insight into surfing today, tips, technique and instruction. We are both glad we had the lesson and would highly recommend Pike to tourists and locals alike looking to surf for a bit in the Santa Barbara area.

We just had one of the best family excursions we could have hoped for. We decided to give surfing a try. My wife searched for an instructor and she looked at the bigger business in the area and then came across James Pike. The search stopped there. He was such a friendly guy on the phone who had a ton of passion for surfing she felt he would be a great fit. 
Well he didn't disappoint. We had a BLAST with James. He's super smart and his knowledge of surfing, surf history, the ocean and just being a good person was impressive. 
He provided us with so much knowledge my kids and I felt very comfortable jumping in the ocean to take on some waves. We all got our chance to catch plenty of waves and had so much fun in doing so. 
If I could give James more stars than 5 I would. Do yourself a favor and book an outing with him. You will be stoked you did so!

James is an excellent teacher with a high EQ.  He is able to tailor his approach and teaching style to the experience and confidence level of his students.  We had a great day with James and his assistant Lucas.

Pike and Logan were great! My boyfriend bought me and his two kids (ages 11 and 15) a group surf lesson. We all had a blast! Pike and Logan took the time to explain everything thoroughly and gave us all individual attention. We all successfully got up in the boards and surfed multiple times. Pike was generous with his time, made us all feel really comfortable. We all agreed, we'd definitely recommend Pike and Logan.

Just had a 2hr lesson surfing with my boyfriend this weekend after buying a package on Living Social. Pike was AMAZING! Not at all what we expected (in a good way). He was very patient and helpful the whole way through. We had an amazing day and can't wait to go back! I would definitely recommend taking lessons from Pike!

James took on four of us for our first surfing experience.  Two middle aged (though we think we are cool) parents and two skeptical teenage girls.   3 out of the 4 were talked into it and not really looking forward to getting crushed by waves and getting humiliated after not being able to stand up on the board.  AMAZINGLY, all four of us got up and rode out a wave on OUR FIRST TRY.  James is that good and all four of us can't wait to go out with him again.  Also, no one got crushed or even bumped, he teaches you about safety and goes out with you one on one to help every step of the way.  

We got so much more than a technical lesson, we got the history of the sport and we all benefitted from his gift of teaching and his obvious love of what he does.   He also discounts the second lesson by 50%!  It is a very fun experience and I would (and have) recommended Pike Surf School to my friends.

James is a great person. I'm new to skateboarding and I was at the skatepark going down ramps and skating around while was giving lessons to a group of little grom skaters. After his lesson he introduced himself and helped me out for a solid hour "just because". He said that while he gave lessons he also gives back. He was patient and helped me quite a bit. He convinced me to loosen my trucks (new skaters usually hesitate to do so because tightened trucks make one more stable). WIth time I have gradually loosened them and it has made a substantial difference. Thank you James I appreciate you and I am sure I will take a lesson in the future!! I've improved a lot since then.

My boyfriend and I were on a vacation to Santa Barbara from Houston and the surfing lesson with James was the highlight of our trip.  He brought the wetsuits that fitted us perfectly and the perfect boards to catch the small waves. He's genuine and honest. He told us the waves were small and if we wanted to cancel the session he's totally fine (even though he had already lugged the equipments to the beach). We decided to go ahead and was able to ride a few waves and had a fantastic time. I would highly recommend his service to everyone.  He's a great teacher and passionate about surfing and teaching. If you are visiting SB or Venture, don't miss out on taking a surf lesson with him.

Booked with James for a surfing lesson for our bachelorette. He was not only accommodating with our group (one of our girls wasn't feeling so well that day) but patient and incredibly warm. We could immediately tell we'd be in good hands and were very excited about the lesson within the first 5 minutes!! James provides great feedback and did a great job trying to get us all great waves to practice on. I would absolutely book with him again!! Such a great teacher and person

Yelpers, be warned: this is a long review!

Back in November I went for a surf lesson with James and, halfway through the lesson, I was loving it so much that I asked him if I could also book a second lesson with him the morning after.

James is very professional and fun at the same time. He just makes you get over whatever your fears or limits may be - if you have any, of course :)  He is able to strike the perfect balance between making you feel safe and confident, but also trying to gently push you out of your comfort zone, which is something you have to do if you want to catch waves in a freezing ocean populated by all sorts of fun little beasts and plants. Two huge + compared to most instructors: 1) he is 100% committed and focused on helping you improve as much as possible and give you the best feeling ever from the surf. While many other instructors I previously surfed with are focused on their own ability and just interested in showing off and telling you they too surfed at pipeline. 2) James is always out in the water beside you, and surfing with you, not telling you what to do from a distance or catching his own waves.

James's lessons take place at Mondo's cove, which is this little, wonderful beach with gentle waves. It is safe and mostly sandy. Local surfers and SUP are very  friendly. It is just the perfect place for beginners and improvers.

My two surf lessons with James are probably the best time I spent during my holidays in wonderful California. Out there in the ocean it was just total bliss. Whether you are a complete beginner, or you already surf and want to improve, I totally recommend booking lessons with James. It's more than worth every penny, and he is very generous with time and advice. 

You will see that you will most likely stand up on your first wave, even if it's you're first time out there. You will feel that you are immediately progressing, and each wave coming up will be better than the previous one. And you will want to go back. No matter how nervous or unfit you may feel. 'Cause that's what happens when you find as good an instructor as James, and when the ocean gives you its very best.

I just wish, now that I'm sitting back in my office in grey and cold northern Europe, that I was closer to that wonderful spot - and could go back sooner. And even if I can't be there tomorrow, I'm really grateful for what James taught me, the time spent surfing with him, and for the amazing gifts of Mondo's and mother nature.

Pike is truly great.

Knowledge, attitude, experience, and effort.  Everything you could want in an instructor and more.  His spiritual connection to the sport will also inspire you.  This is just a great guy, making a living doing what he loves (which includes helping others learn), and it shows.  Full recommendation.

James does a fantastic job teaching surfing. I took my 3 teens and we had an amazing lesson. First James goes over the basics on land. Then he took us with his assistant Logan into the waves. James literally put his foot on my board and paddled me over to the waves. Once there he and Logan would pick a wave, get us in position, yell 1, 2, 3, and launch us. My kids got up their first try. I struggled at first because i wasn't getting up right so James gave me a remedial lesson and I got up! James and Logan would ride in and help us get back out. The lesson ended up being about 3 hours because James wanted to make sure we got it and had several runs. 

James went well beyond our expectations. 

Do it!

Pike is a great surf instructor. My sister and I came here for lessons back in August and he was amazing! So patient and encouraging! I loved how easy he made it to learn and he totally loves that people are using his groupon deal. He provided the boards and the wetsuits and is so helpful in getting you suited up for the waves.

I am a so-so swimmer and was so nervous that I might drown, but it was totally not a problem. He guides you to the waters and helps you ride the waves. It was amazing to surf for the first time. I will definitely come back and take more lessons!

Pike is the perfect surf instructor.  Incredibly nice guy, steeped in surf history, knowledgable about muscles & stretching, patient as heck and always positive.  I've tried instructors in many places but Pike's the one I keep coming back to.

James is awesome. Took two surf lessons with him. I've taken surf lessons with about a dozen instructors over the years (yup, I'm that kinda guy) and he's probably the most rigorous and disciplined one, pointing out lots of tweaks, including my paddling. But managed to still keep it fun and positive too. Highly recommend.

We found Pike through a Groupon and have now had 2 private lessons with him.  My son (7) was standing up the first day and is LOVING surfing thanks to James Pike's awesome attitude and instruction.  Pike knows all the best places to go for beginners and is flexible in schedule and location.  He also runs the skate camp for kids and give skateboard lessons so I'm looking forward to signing my son up for some skating lessons with him as well.  SUPER guy...so glad he offered the Groupon so we got a chance to meet him!!
