I am brand new to surfing and just had a lesson with James.  To say he is an amazing instructor is an understatement. Every element of my experience with him exceeded expectations-from his professionalism over the phone when booking the lesson; to his knowledge of surfing and the history behind it; to his ability to teach both on shore and in the water.  He had me up and riding waves almost immediately.  James offered great tips throughout the lesson and was really encouraging.  If you are thinking about lessons, definitely give him a call.  You'll be so glad you did.

It was a cold and rainy Saturday morning and while we waited on the beach for Pike to arrive (perfectly on-time!) I thought, holy crap, what have I gotten myself into...Yet it was the best time I've had in a long time.  Pike spent a great deal of time making sure that we were comfortable with the boards and balance before taking us out into the water.  He's super patient, reassuring, and honestly seems to enjoy introducing people to surfing. He's also pretty funny! 

We had a great time and can't wait to have family and friends visit so we can have Pike teach them the way of the waves!

Just got back from the beach with James Pike. He has a great outlook and knows how to break you into the history and mechanics of surfing.

I was exhausted but I got up on the board and road a wave solid, pretty hooked.

I had other people cancel on my lessons and flake. He was there early, he takes it seriously, has a lot of boards and gear, and is really passionate about the work he does. He takes his time with you and never added a hint of pressure.

He rocks and its solid money well spent, its worth the cash.

Our family took surf lessons from Pike over spring break 2014.  He was patient, kind and fun with our autistic son. When our son had had enough the lesson continued with the rest of the family without skipping a beat. Everyone in the family caught multiple waves and we all had a great time. He even had great recommendations for things to do in Santa Barbara..

My boyfriend and I signed up for our first surf lessons with James Pike this past Sunday. It's immediately obvious that James is very respectful of the history of the sport and makes sure to pass on his knowledge to anyone interested in surfing. I really loved that he is a self-taught surfer and has been surfing since was 13. He has learned a lot of surfing tips along the years and is happy to share them with his students. As a matter of fact he makes it his goal to make sure he instills in his students as much knowledge about the history, the parts of the surf board, and the actual technique needed to surf before they get out into the water.

I've been living in Los Angeles for 14 years and have never really wanted to get into the ocean here (too cold, too murky). I'm used to warm Atlantic waters in which you can see down to the bottom. I admit I was more hesitant about getting in the "freezing" cold ocean than on the surfboard. And I was happily surprised that the water was the perfect temp with the help of a full wetsuit (provided by James along with the boards)!  Once I got over the fear of being cold all day, I was excited to put what James taught us to practice. All worries and hesitation disappeared once I started paddling out. James is a great teacher, patient, encouraging, and truly seems to enjoy teaching his students to surf. He was out there with us the entire time, helping us find the right waves to surf, and teaching us when to paddle out and when to pop up. Both my boyfriend and I got up on our first waves. I was happily riding the board all the way to the shore by the end of the day!

There's no better feeling than being out in the ocean, on a surfboard, and catching a wave! We've definitely caught the surfing bug! We highly recommend signing up for surf lessons with James and hope to be able to take more when we're back up in the Santa Barbara/Ventura area. So grateful we found James and Pike's Surf 'n Skate!
Awesome experience. Pike is a friendly guy with helpful tips

James was a great teacher for a 1-1 instruction.  He does a fantastic job of getting you prepared to get out in the water and then continues to add more info as you progress.  The spot he picked was great with consistent waves and not too much of a crowd.  Would recommend to anyone interested in wanting to learn to surf.

We were in Santa Barbara for my BFF's birthday-bachlorette weekend and the matron of honor scheduled a surf lesson for 4 of us.  We were all newbies to the sport and had no clue what to expect.  Pike was friendly, inviting and most of all helpful.  All we had to do was meet up, follow him to the beach and he provided the wetsuits, boards and instruction.

During our instruction he provided options for going into the water since there were 4 of us.  We opted for the 1-on-1 experience and we each got his full attention and tutoring in the water AND we all caught at least one wave to shore.  So yes, we were pretty stoked....thanx to Pike!!!

I swear, Pike must have been a fish in another life cuz when you see him in action in the water....he is definitely one with it.  Bottom line, I can't recommend anyone better to learn from than James if you want to surf.

I'm really bad at sports, but every day I drive to UCSB, I can see blue ocean right in front of me and I really wanna jump in and surf. I still can't move my first step on learning surfing, since it was so hard to me. However, during the first private lesson with James, I mostly stood up every time he pushed me, which was totally beyond my imagination and expectations! I'll keep taking lessons with James in the following weeks! Have to say, it was the best lesson I've ever taken! If you are hesitated on whether to start learning surfing, just text James and move to your first lesson, then you will love surfing!

So I took surfing lesson (and tried surfing) for the first time with James yesterday and it was epic! I've always wanted to learn but yet I was so worried due to the lack of experience in the ocean. With James, there is nothing to worry about! He's so chill and patient. He knows the ocean so well and makes sure you are having a positive experience. He went over the basic stuff and helped me get used to the ocean, definitely pushed me out of my comfort zone, which is awesome! Also, he cancelled my lesson the other day and asked if we could switch to a different day due to the change in tides (or whatever it was) that day that might not be suitable for a first-timer like me. One important thing I learned from him: It doesn't matter how good of a swimmer you are or even if you have never been in the ocean (like me), you can still make surfing happen and it's all about the process! Kudos to you, sir!

I was visiting Santa Barbara for a vacation. I always wanted to surf and enjoy my life and what it has to offer! Prior to getting a surf lesson, I wanted to do it by myself first with instructors and see if I can do it. Needless to say I didn't succeed! So tuesday, we were walking down state street and we decided to have a drink at opal restaurant. I met a lovely bartender named Christina and she said she had this surf instructor that was amazing and taught her to surf so easily! I said sounds great, can I have his number and the I got mr. Pike's number and all was history! Called him set up an appointment. He had to scoot me in his busy schedule so I was gonna meet him around five at the nest beaches in Ventura to learn the Basics! He told me the history, parts of the board, how to maneuver the board, and most of all to enjoy it while I was doing it! My fourth try, I got to stand and actually surf! IT WAS THE BEST FEELING EVER! I HIGHLY RECOMMEND HIM if you want to really know how to surf! Such a great and funny guy too! great personality!

James gave me a really great surf coaching lesson at Rincon. He's friendly, knowledgeable, has safety and enjoyability in mind, and was really great to surf with. He was able to give me good pointers on my paddling and technique

It was my first time surfing and was a little scared. I was so impressed with every aspect of the surf lesson and I had the best time surfing for my first time. I was able to get up and several times and felt like a pro it was amazing! Pike is such an awesome teacher and I had a better time surfing than I have ever had wake boarding or any other water sport. If you want to learn howe to surf and want an great experience than use this surf school you wont be let down.

We got our reference through living social for 2 people private lesson for 2hrs. We set up a day to do it only a few days prior. He had us meet at a great beginner spot in Ventura. He was on time as well as having wetsuits, boards, and extras to keep us nice and warm. Pike was so knowledgable about surfing and oceanography to explain surf reports for us to continue going on our own as well. Really nice and cool guy. Made it very fun and definitely turned me and my girlfriend into die hard surfers!
Great experience! James was an excellent teacher and my husband and two childen got up easily and caught waves right away! They had a blast!

My boyfriend and I went for surf lessons with James and we had a great time! 

First we booked by calling his number. He was friendly over the phone, answered all of our questions, and gave us good directions on where to meet him. He kept in contact via text and let us know that the time we chose to surf had bad tides so we needed to push it back 1.5 hours. Turns out the tide shifts each day so if you are planning surfing far in advance, prepare to be flexible with your time of day!

Then we had the lesson on a Sunday. We practiced on the beach and then went out one by one to the ocean. Neither of us had surfed before and James was very generous with towing us out to catch waves when our arms were tired (which was a lot, haha). On the second round, we went out together. Even though it was just the two of us, I think we went over time because James wanted to make sure we each got a lot of waves. 

Afterwards, he gave us suggestions on places to eat and hang out in Santa Barbara and we chatted for a bit. He is a really cool guy and I'm glad we got to hang out and learn to surf with him.

Great day for a very reasonable price. I want to go surfing again! With this as the intro lesson, I would feel comfortable renting a board and wetsuit on my own (though I know I would need to practice a lot). Thanks James!

This was the perfect learning experience for us all. Pike was patient and by the end of the lesson we were all able to stand up. It was so fun that my brother and sister are thinking about regular lessons and skateboarding lessons too. Definitely recommended by my whole family!

Pike was a fantastic instructor!  My fiancee and I recently purchased a groupon through Pike and he provided two hours of private lessons at a very reasonable cost.  He was a lot of fun and knew as much as you need to know about surfing.  After sharing a brief history of surfing, we got on the sand and began learning about the board/ocean/how to surf.  He was very knowledgeable and a great teacher.  We then went out into the ocean where Pike helped us find and ride some waves.  We were both able to get up on our boards throughout the lesson and, as we became more comfortable, he helped us go on our own.  We absolutely loved our experience and will definitely go back again!

Pike is awesome! My brother, best friend, and I took a weekend trip to santa Barbara and last minute decided taking a surf lesson would be fun, we gave Pike a ring and he was completely accommodating although he had to rearrange his schedule and we set up a lesson for the same day! He was on time and prepared, and he provided us with everything we needed. As far as the lesson was concerned, he did amazing! Pike gave us some great history and reviewed the basics with us on land before taking us into the ocean. He made sure we were all having fun and were successful. We all had a blast and were able to catch a few waves. Overall, great experience! I'll definitely visit for another surf lesson from Pike.

I bought my son a private lesson with James last weekend, since my son just started at UCSB and needed to learn about Campus Point. It was a quiet day there with no swell, but James made the most of it, showing my son where all of the breaks typically are. He also spent a bunch of time on the shore helping him with his pop-up. I will definitely be getting another lesson when the waves are a bit bigger.

James is truly passionate about surfing and being at one with the beach and ocean. I 
had already had a beginner's experience surfing so he diligently took the time to give me more advanced tips and knowledge.

Mondo's Cove is a perfect beginner's break as it is produces long and gentle waves that can be ridden for a few hundred yards.

James was great at pushing me to catch more waves and even towed me out when I was taking a break from paddling so I could conserve energy to catch more. 

James is a passionate surfer beyond just instructing and knows many spots all up and down the West Coast to catch waves and he chases after them.

The money is well spent, the comfort and experience gained in a short time will make sure you catch the surfing bug.

Highly Recommended.

My friend and I took a surf lesson from James and had an amazing time! He was very patient and professional, and made both of us feel very comfortable in the water.  I would highly recommend him to anyone and we are going to take a second lesson from him.  He provides the wetsuits and boards, and it is so helpful that he goes out in the water and helps you each step of the way! Such a fun experience!

I rarely write reviews on Yelp, but last year Pike gave surfing lessons to my daughter and I, and I have spent 45 minutes trying to figure out how to contact him again to ask if he would be willing to give us lessons again. He was patient, fun, and a talented teacher. Looking forward to getting more lessons from him in the future.

Over a number of years, I have a number of friends who I've gone out with to try and learn to surf, but  not with a lot of success.  They were great surfers, just not great teachers.  Two years ago, I took lessons with a local adventure business that does surf lessons, and while it was good, it just wasn't quite thorough, and I still just wasn't quite getting it.   So a couple of weeks ago I decided to try again with a friend who had taken lessons with me before.   I found Pike's School and we made plans for the next day.  From the first moment you know you're going to have a good time, he is just a great down to earth, friendly guy.   He kind of baby steps you through the whole process, which isn't a bad thing.  I assume as he's used to dealing with kids in his skate camp that he brings that same teaching style to his surf school for older kids, like myself.  Everything is very A-Z...which is great.  So often we want to rush right into things without knowing the basics behind it.   With Pike there is no just rushing into the water with a board.   I would say a good 45mins was on the beach learning fundamentals.  Once we were in the water he was very patient and encouraging, before we knew it we were both getting up and standing.   We had such a great time we went looking for boards and suits later that day.  But I know we will definitely want to take a couple more classes with Pike in the future.  Can't recommend Pike's Surf School enough!

Over a number of years, I have a number of friends who I've gone out with to try and learn to surf, but  not with a lot of success.  They were great surfers, just not great teachers.  Two years ago, I took lessons with a local adventure business that does surf lessons, and while it was good, it just wasn't quite thorough, and I still just wasn't quite getting it.   So a couple of weeks ago I decided to try again with a friend who had taken lessons with me before.   I found Pike's School and we made plans for the next day.  From the first moment you know you're going to have a good time, he is just a great down to earth, friendly guy.   He kind of baby steps you through the whole process, which isn't a bad thing.  I assume as he's used to dealing with kids in his skate camp that he brings that same teaching style to his surf school for older kids, like myself.  Everything is very A-Z...which is great.  So often we want to rush right into things without knowing the basics behind it.   With Pike there is no just rushing into the water with a board.   I would say a good 45mins was on the beach learning fundamentals.  Once we were in the water he was very patient and encouraging, before we knew it we were both getting up and standing.   We had such a great time we went looking for boards and suits later that day.  But I know we will definitely want to take a couple more classes with Pike in the future.  Can't recommend Pike's Surf School enough!

I had friends from San Francisco coming into town and we decided to get a surf lesson with four people. We met with Pike and it was truly a great experience. He is a wonderful instructor who takes the time to teach you the fundamentals. He is very patient and encouraging and all four of us caught a wave the first time we went out! My husband and I had so much fun, we want to book another lesson the following weekend! I highly recommend taking lessons from Pike, you will not feel self conscious and you will actually learn how to surf in two hours!

Tabano Hollow was the first fenced dog run in the area. With its long grassy meadow and benches under a nice shade tree, this park is a great place to spend an afternoon with your cavorting canine. 
The perimeter is secured by a fence to ensure that your pooch won't run off. 
The park can get fairly crowded at times due to its popularity, although this is a great opportunity for dog socialization.
Off-leash hours are limited, Mon-Sun 3 PM-Sunset. With a creek nearby and plenty of paved bike paths and walkways , Tabano Hollow is a great place for the whole family to explore.

Amenities:
Dog waste bag dispensers, seating areas, watering area for dogs and humans in the enclosed area. Public restrooms are not available.

The dog park is wonderful, big open field with trees on the edges. The other dog owners there were nice and their dogs were well trained and socialized. 

It is in a neighborhood so the regulars asked to keep the dogs quiet, but that is how a herding dog plays, so it probably won't be my regular spot. The hours are also weird, 3pm to sunset.

If my dog could write a review, she will use her paws to give it 5 stars. The only reason why it is 4 stars here it's because there are too many holes on the ground  so you should take extra attention when you try to pick up your dog's poop or chasing after your dogs.  Btw, curious creatures sometimes would appear from the hole, they do provide extra excitement for the dogs.....

Good off-leash option in Goleta. Water, pooper scoopers (and bags), and usually a few abandoned tennis balls are readily available for you & your pooch. Most of the dogs (& owners) here are well behaved and get along just fine. There's a nice shade tree when/if it's warm. So, yeah, I'd rather head to one of the off-leash beaches in the area, but if the tide is too high and/or you've got limited time, this is a perfectly pleasant option.
I could not of imagined a better "fun park" for my dog after sitting in the car for hours. It is located conveniently off the PCH for road-trippers!

Really Nice park but as soon as we got there a park maintenance guy came and told us it was closed and only open at certain times???? 
There was no one else there and it's a designated dog park! 
We won't be going back 
Too Bad

It's not an overly impressive space, essentially just a big dirt enclosure (there may have been grass once but alas the drought...). HOWEVER, this is my favorite dog park in Santa Barbara. you can tell the people who frequent this place are really dog people who love their dogs. Quite a few regulars and every once in a while someone will add to the community of dog toys and play things. 

Biggest downside compared to Tucker's Grove is the very limited  hours of operation, 3pm-sunset.

I used to live in Santa Barbara and I came to this dog park all the time. My dog and I love it.

PROS:
*Huge grassy area
*Lots of regular dogs and people
*Lots of seating 
*Very clean- the community takes care of it well
*There is a dog fountain
*Some doggie bags are available but they are limited

CONS:
*There is no size separation- for smaller dogs (not comfortable around big dogs) it is not recommended
*Limited hours 3pm to sunset
*No restrooms for people


My dog has been stung by a bees here in summer time, so I recommend knowing what to do and exercising caution. Just keep an eye on your dog since it is a big park.

So on this road trip to Nor Cal, I looked on Yelp for some dog parks along the way.  We had been driving for about 2 1/2 hours and felt it was time for a stop.  It is in an interesting location, and when we arrived, there were two dogs and a family inside.  One of their dogs wanted to play, which was great.  Things were going well until the other dog started to get a little rough and Cako bared her teeth in a way I've never seen.  

Stupid me just went and separated them and pet both on the head and spoke gently on how we're all friends.  Meanwhile, the family pretty much did nothing, and the young daughter came and called her dog.  We ended up leaving, and they left right after us as I was wiping down our dog from all the other dog's saliva...LOL!

It's a good space....not very big, but adequate.  Cako approves. (I forgot to take any photos.  I was busy moderating...LOL!)

Came at 5:30pm on a Thursday and the park had a lot of dogs! Lots of open space. Shade and plenty of chairs and several benches available. Dogs don't need to wear a leash since there is a fence surrounding the whole park. Great place for a calm afternoon with your dog.

Perfect place to take my dog, the hours are a little weird because you can't be there from 10 am to 3 pm. I wish this would change for at least the weekend, other than that great place for my dog to play with other dogs.

The Tabano Hallow Open Space is a godsend when my pup has been cooped up in the house for most of the day. It's fairly close to where I live, so we'd walk over during a late afternoon. 

The name really says it all- it's an open space. It's gated which is really nice, since I love being able to have my pup off leash. This area is very spacious, which is great for all dogs with different kinds of personalities to all be in one enclosed space. For instance, one side could be filled with the Golden Girls and their little, slightly territorial dogs, while the other is filled with large dogs playing fetch with their humans.

I love this place better than any other dog park. I forget the name of the dog park that's a little ways away, but I absolutely despise that place (my review is on my profile). The dogs here are typically really friendly towards other dogs and people!

The reason why I gave this space 4/5 stars is because the up-keep on it is minimal/nonexistent. I don't think anyone does any sort of maintenance, but I don't blame them (it's a collective action problem). It kind of sucks when it rains out, because it gets soooo muddy in my opinion. However, the dogs probably don't mind the mud AT ALL.

I came at 7 am in the morning and it was empty. 

Pretty clean public beach.

Not dog friendly but it was empty so I let my pup run around a bit.

This is a beautiful beach that is easy to get too.  Street parking is free.  Long stretch of sand that is good for a stroll or jog.  Also a great beach for playing volleyball - plenty of nets available.

The downside is that the beach is extremely windy.  So windy that in 85 degree weather you want to put on a sweater to sit on the beach.  There are better beaches in the area that are protected from the wind.

The beach had oil all over it.  We were walking along the water and could see the black particles washing in an out.  When we decided to walk back to the sidewalk we found that the entire beach had large globules of oil all over it and it got stuck onto our feet for days.  There also seems to be a river that flows on to the beach and creates a little fresh water habitat, it's looks sick and neglected.  There was virtually no one out there too, all of the people were more interested in driving their cars out on the warf to shop or eat.  This was really sad.
Great little section of beach - look at the wharf in one direction, the marina in another, volleyball courts, and if you're lucky, watch the outriggers go in and out.

Nice sunny weather can make any beach beautiful.  But this beach has a couple other things going for it.  Stearn's Wharf on one side and a harbor view on the other.  Beaches on the walkway for people watching.  Mellow currents great for kayaking and standup paddle surfing.

West Beach is located to the right of Sterns Wharf, if facing the ocean. 

It's a decent stretch of sand... space to lay out although the seaweed and associated flies are undesirable. So is the fact that the water looks filthy, what can you expect though with the wharf being so close. I wouldn't go into the water but there is kayak rentals here, which as long as you don't fall in, would be fun. There is also a few volleyball nets perfect to play a few games in the sunshine.

My favorite thing about this beach is the boardwalk. Perfect for taking a stroll and people watching the locals and tourists. Or look out into the ocean at the boats sailing away...

You know I've never been a fan of this beach because of all the trash, seaweed, and crazy amounts of tourists. This is the location the cruise ships, international tour buses, and other over packed forms of transportation for travelers make their drop offs. Therefor it's usually dirty, kind of smelly, and has things everywhere.

The cool part about this being a tourist drop off is the sand castle artists that make awesome sculptures on occasion, the flags that are put up in remembrance of memorial themed holidays, and the awesome restaurants across the street.

The sand itself and especially the ocean shore is not the safest, most pleasant smelling, clean looking beach sight you'll see in town. I'd recommend just heading over to East Beach for a fun, clean, beautiful time for the whole family. If anything you can bike along the path of West Beach and look at all the restaurants, the pier, and the couple volleyball players that post up here, but that's about the extent of things for this area.

We stayed at a hotel across the street from West Beach. This side of the beach is a little more calm and not as crowded as the beach areas by the pier. There is street parking and neighborhood parking, but to avoid the hassle (since the street signs are confusing), I would try to just walk it or take public transportation.

Unless you're staying nearby at a hotel or B&B, the beach is only within a block or 2 distance away. West Beach is walkable to the marina (5-10 min walk) or the opposite direction to the pier (5-10 min walk).

This is one of the best beaches in Santa Barbara area of the Central Coast. I have been coming here for years on 4th of July weekends.  This beach is always nice and clean with a family feel to it. You can rent bikes or if your a local bring your own bike and ride through the bike paths. Ocean has some of the roughest waves and it can get real windy here. I would always pitch a big umbrella and a blanket and just lay on the beach and fall asleep, along with my significant other. Awesome beach, Great people, Volleyball is popular here as well. And you can go check out the battle ships and boats too.

Located, well, west of Stearns Wharf, West Beach is an open expanse full of gleaming, whitish-golden sand. It's a bit hemmed in by the wharf, boat launches, and other harbor activity - whether boats, kayakers, or windsurfers - so it certainly isn't the calmest, quietest place to swim. It's best to stick to the sand where you can hang out all day with friends and family, play some beach volleyball, and wade a bit into the water.

When I walked past, there weren't any facilities on the beach itself, so to visit the restroom you'll have to patronize a business fronting the beach - or walk a few minutes to the swimming pavilion or the main harbor parking lot a few minutes to the west. Talking about which, besides the main harbor lot and whatever parking there is on the wharf, there is no dedicated lot, except for nearby streets.

An der Waterfront in Santa Barbara hat man die Auswahl zwischen dem East Beach und dem West Beach, die durch Stearns Wharf getrennt werden. Santa Barbara wird vom West Beach durch den Cabrillo Boulevard getrennt und davor verläuft noch ein wunderbar angelegter Fußgänger- und Radweg. 

Der West Beach ist vergleichbar klein und wird durch den Hafen und Stearns Wharf begrenzt. Es gibt dort für die Sportbegeisterten ein Volleyballfeld und durch die teilweise Abschirmung zur offenen See ist das Meer durch die geringe Wellenbewegung auch sehr begehrt bei Kayakfahrern und anderen Wassersportlern.
 
Von der nicht weit entfernten Stearns Wharf hat man einen einzigartigen Blick auf den Strand, das Meer und die sehenswerte Umgebung. Hier kann man auch die ersten 90 Minuten frei parken, falls man ein freies Plätzchen erhaschen kann.

Der Strand machte auf uns einen sehr gepflegten und ordentlichen Eindruck und war nicht sonderlich überlaufen. Wie in den USA üblich sind hier Lifeguards am Strand, um für die Sicherheit der Badegäste zu sorgen. Wem der Strand zu langweilig wird kann auf dem Strandweg Skaten und Fahrrad fahren.

Allerdings sollte man sich gut eincremen und einen Hut tragen, da durch den kühlen Wind die intensive Sonneneinstrahlung nicht sofort bemerkt wird. Außerdem sollte man genügend Wasser bei sich haben. Ein wirklich schöner und abwechslungsreicher Strand.
We didn't spend much time on the West Beach, but it was nice. It's a beautiful area. We walked to the Stearns Wharf and enjoyed dinner there.

When I went on a cruise last October, we had to get tendered via lifeboats to and from an area that was right by the West Beach. We had to walk through here to get to downtown and back. It was obvious that the beach was "off-season", and kind of small, but everything looked nice, especially when looking out into the water.

name: Truth Aquatics
categories: Boat Charters, Rafting/Kayaking, Scuba Diving, Event Planning & Services, Paddleboarding, Boating, Fishing, Active Life, Hiking, Diving

yelp_reviews.text:
Just did a 3 day trip to Santa Cruz and Anacapa Islands on the Truth.

It's exactly what you want out of a California dive boat.  Good captain, good crew, good food.  If you don't have fun, then you probably suck.  I would definitely recommend diving with them and the islands we went to were great; a nice change of pace from diving Catalina.  Good stuff.

I have been diving with Truth Aquatics for over 30 years and have always had great service and have appreciated how well run this outfit is in catering to divers. I have never much been into writing reviews but after my last trip on The Truth I feel compelled to write a review, praising Captain Davey and his crew. Dave truly cares about people, works just as hard as his crew and does it with a smile. His jovial nature and love of the ocean exudes from his being. He is right up there with the best of the best in my book!!!! Keep up the great work guys and gals. I wouldn't think of diving with any other outfit for Channel island trips..:) Kent Clemenco

Just had a fantastic 2-day trip aboard the Truth. Captain Davey and his crew were completely on point. Top notch outfit. I would do another trip with them in a heartbeat.
Hot water showers on deck and below were great, warm drying room for wetsuits and a clothes dryer for wet clothing, quick air fills to 3200 psi, great food (as judged by a former chef who is also vegetarian), comfortable bunks, dry changing room, spacious galley, very easy spacious swim step, etc... all great amenities for a boat this size.
A couple of negatives: single bunks are a little tight, so don't bring much baggage, and it can be a little hard to sleep when the crew gets moving around early in the morning. Anyway, I didn't go on a boat to sleep - the diving was great.
We hit many well sheltered coves on Santa Cruz Island. They usually anchor the boat the both bow and stern, so it is very stable for entry and exit. Captain give good briefings of each site on arrival.
Such a great crew and boat - I cant wait to go back for another trip.

I recently went scuba diving at Santa Cruz Island on the Truth, and was very happy with the experience.  The crew were very nice and helpful.  They went out of their way to be friendly and give me a great experience.  They were smart to go to the back of Santa Cruz because there were offshores running and the island blocked the wind.  The food was better than other boats, and the boat was clean.  Truth is easy to dive from - the stern has a good step arrangement.  Look forward to diving with them again.

I was new to diving these boats and had fairly high expectations as they are some of my friends' favorites. They managed to find decent dive spots though I ended up on a boat that when I signed up was not labeled a hunting boat and as a result some of the spots were a little barren. The food was alright, although I wouldn't say it was the best dive boat food I've ever had. I loved that the boat was huge with enough room for everyone to get around safely and without bumping into each other. The staff was friendly and helpful with gear set up. Overall I had a really good experience with this dive outfit! Totally recommend it to anyone looking for some good diving in California!

I was on a 2-day liveaboard on their Conception boat - leaving Santa Barbara for San Miguel the first day and Santa Cruz Island the second day. 

The crew was top notch, very attentive and friendly, always in the right place at the right time and getting the boat, divers, gear and moorings efficiently squared away. They were quite simply, very, very good. 

The food was fantastic, we had tri-tip for dinner with pineapple upside down cake for dessert; french toast with eggs and bacon for breakfast; and chicken curry and penne pasta with sausage for lunch. Everything was well made, they had a soda gun for drinks and coffee and teas - all you can drink. 

They also now pump NITROX! A great addition for multi-day dives and multiple days of diving. The fills are very reasonably priced as well, $6 a fill. 

I would go back in a second, hopefully they spend more time out of a port down in San Pedro or LB in the future.

I was on the same trip as Kristy C. We were out at San Miguel, Santa Rosa and Santa Cruz on the Truth. Everything went incredibly smoothly and the crew were extremely professional and helpful. In particular Davey (the captain) was always very communicative about everything and as a group we all felt very comfortable talking with him. He was very open to considering different locations and very knowledgeable in general. The second captain, Peter, was also extraordinarily competent and friendly towards everyone. He said he normally works on freighter ships so that is very cool!

The food was great and Larissa and Windy in the kitchen were very friendly and accommodating. We had a vegan on the trip and Larissa made special meals for her which she really appreciated. They also were really nice in preparing some of the fish that was caught on the trip and serving that to everyone. 

In general the boat was great and everyone had a really fun time hanging out on the boat, diving or kayaking. We were a more relaxed group and didn't dive much, but the option is definitely there for hard-core divers to do lots of diving. I would highly recommend a trip aboard the Truth but I'm sure the Vision and Conception are equally as well-run and enjoyable. I hear the owner Glen is cool and is very involved in making sure everything runs smoothly. It is very convenient to leave out of the Santa Barbara harbor also. 

I didn't get a chance to write a review at the end of the trip, so hopefully this makes up for it!!

Spent the past 4 days on the Conception after a 29yr hiatus.  The boat and diving are as good as ever and the crew, food and so forth is better than ever.  Truth Aquatics really do it better than anybody else out there.  This is a dive operation for real divers.  I could not be happier.

Dove North side of Santa Cruz Island and got over to San Miguel Monday.  Even got a technical dive on a Sea Mount between Santa Rosa and San Miguel in on Monday Morning.
This is as good as it gets.  These are the best boats on the West Coast and they have some of the best crew.

Spent three nights on Conception. What an amazing experience!!! Captain Jerry was always available, helpful and knowledgeable. He made sure to connect with us as soon as he had info. Matthias, Kyle and Hunter were so ready to assist in any way! Father Mikey/daughter Kelley galley team kept us deliciously sated. Others commented on our luck to get the best crew. The Channel Islands are a rare treasure. Truth Aquatics will help you appreciate this California gem!
(It was really all positive, except I think this is noteworthy...My trip was not like most. I was with Sierra Club. Would take off many stars for my last minute decision to rent a kayak. Total rip off.)

Our three-day trip to the Channel Islands was fantastic. We hiked, kayaked, and had a very jolly time hanging out on the boat. The food was delicious- cooked to order breakfast and even a Thanksgiving dinner! Slept like babies in cozy bunks. This is definitely the way to do the islands. One cautionary note- if you get seasick easily, it might not be the trip for you.

Truth Aquatics the BEST OF THE BEST!!!  Every one of the boats is first class and the crew on each boat is fantastic.  As a dive shop owner it is important that my clients are taken care of.  The last trip we were on was a two day.  I have a Vegan & a Pescatarian (a vegetarian but eats fish) and they were fantastic.  Vegan sausage for breakfast, vegan cheese & plenty of vegetables for them.  Other dive boats fall short - especially at breakfast.  The boats are clean and well cared for.  Comfortable bunks and full showers!  A room to dry your wetsuits and a dryer for your towels and dive skins.  A full five stars for Truth Aquatics!!

Our trip included two days in the Channel Islands on the boat called conception '
I have to tell you I was surprised to see how organized the crew and the staff was The food was excellent the sleeping arrangements was very comfortable and the layout of the boat was just as you would expect ! Most of the people on the boat were students from Mount sac on their final voyage of being a diver ! We did have one minor emergency with the student became up disoriented , One of the deck hands  that we refer to as white lightning was in the water and The student back to the boat in seconds ! 
Yes I would highly recommend this crew this boat and I will be visiting again !

I have been out on Truth Aquatics boats twice.  One time on the Truth to go to Santa Cruz island and once on the Vision to go to San Miguel Island.  The boats themselves are great, big, nice comfortable galleys, good bunk area, lots of heads, showers, good food, a lot to like here.

So, having said some nice things, why the 1 star?  Both times the boat was more concerned with keeping their schedule than allowing the divers to get in a dive.  Yes, it is important for the boat not to stay out super late, but if you head back to harbor 20 or 30 minutes later than originally planned, but everyone was able to get in a third dive, this is a sacrifice the boat crew should make in a customer service industry.

I've been on many dive boats in Southern California, and most of them are willing to say, "Yeah, we'll stay out a half hour or hour later than planned so you guys can dive.  We're here to make sure you have a good time."   This has never been the case on a Truth Aquatics trip.

The San Miguel trip was particularly bad.  The company chartering the boat had booked for 4 dives, but we only got in 2!  The boat captain then blamed this on the divers not gearing up fast enough (somewhat correct), but as the DM from the local shop pointed out, there were 2 classes on the boat, and they lack the training and experience to be super on top of gearing up and getting out.

My group was the first in the water and we weren't in until 9:30am.  I say this is a failure of the dive boat, they left harbor too late, didn't get to the island early enough, and never made an announcement that we were there and people should get up.  I was already awake, but the process of people getting up leading to the dive briefing took over an hour from when we had arrived at the first dive site.   They should have had everyone awake and breakfasted before they dropped anchor.  The first divers could easily have been in the water an hour earlier if things had been run better.  Then moving from the first dive site to the second dive site took about an hour.   End result, they only let us do two dives and then turned around and went back.

There are a lot of great boats in Southern California.  I'd strongly suggest going with one that is more friendly to the divers and more flexible with its planning.  This company is completely not worth it, especially if you are driving up from Los Angeles or San Diego.

WOW WOW WOW!!!  What a great trip!  From start to finish we had the greatest experience.   Although mother nature was against us slightly at first,  this Captain and his crew showed us the most fantastic trip possible.   The food was exceptional and even more impressive given the  space constriction.  Any opportunity to enhance our experience and this crew was more than willing to accommodate us.  I will definitely be back.

Been diving on these boats since 1997. Always a great crew that manages to find great dive spots regardless of the weather or currents. This latest trip on the Vision was no exception. Did a Limited Load Trip, of the Northern (Western) Islands. A mix of camera/video and hunters. We all got along fine and had some great dives. And now they can pump Nitrox!!!

Truth Aquatics runs scuba diving trips, and occasionally kayak trips and hiking excursions to the islands, out of Santa Barbara Harbor.  Their slogan is "The Best Dive Boats Available" which my friends in resort diving think is hilarious.  (Not the best on the coast, or the finest anywhere: just the best available.)  

Run times to the islands are usually 2 hours: longer than from Ventura, but to the more western, wilder of California's Channel Islands. Accommodations are in single and double bunks below.  Meals range from burgers on 1-day trips to pretty elaborate steak dinners on multi-day trips. Diving is Californian: gear up, jump off and you're on your own.  They have a cleaning table and freezer for those who spearfish or grab lobsters.  Also a dryer below, and a rinse tank for cameras.  They are mostly geared toward fish, scallop and lobster gatherers: not so well set up for photographers. 

Caution: TA tends to cancell their trips when they don't get enough people!  This isn't the case for charters.  But, most of the TA trips get cancelled. They'll call and ask if you want to transfer your deposit to the next trip.

This is a reasonable outfit for those who have some idea of what they're doing: say, more than 50 dives with at least 20 or more in water colder than 60F.  Be prepared for cancellation.

What a great trip - these guys are pros.  I just did a three day trip on the Truth and it was calm, clear water and sunny all three days.   We completely lucked out on the weather conditions.  Dove the best spots  (lobsters were hiding away in the cliff wall of Santa Cruz this time though).  The gear was perfect and the crew was cheerful, experienced and had a great sense of humor - and hopefully a great time too.  Tips - when choosing a bunk - go for the mid section of the boat for less hull noise or the bow - forward bunks - for almost no engine noise.  The back bunks near the stairs have the engine noise so put your snorers there ;-)  They can't hear anything anyways.

Outstanding staff! We did a 2 night Discovery Dive for inexperienced but certified divers. The boat was the Truth. Not luxurious, but comfortable and everything worked. Clean bathrooms, hot showers, a dryer for your wet thing, a hanging area for your wet-suit. The food was very good and plentiful. The crew was always cheerful and incredibly helpful. This was a whole new experience for me. I had never been diving in cold water with a thick wet suit. It took awhile but I finally got the buoyancy under control, thanks to the knowledgeable crew who totally ignored my idiocy and just kept helping me. Great trip. Great experience.

I had an incredible experience a year and a half ago on the Conception. The staff were professional and took safety very seriously. They're known to be the best operation in the Santa Barbara region for a good reason. Sending love to those that lost their lives. What a horrible incident.

Just finished a 3 day trip to the north Channel Islands: San Miguel, Rosa, and Cruz aboard the Conception. The Conception is an awesome dive boat from the diving deck & platform, to the sleeping area, to the galley, to the gear drying room. Captain Jerry grew up on the islands and knows the islands like the back of his hands. He has been driving boats and leading trips since the mid-1980s. He has just the right touch of professionalism, humor, and humility. The staff are fun and run a superbly safe and professional diving program. They keep the ship spotless and take good care of you. The meals and snacks are healthy and toothsome. You won't go hungry. The diving was "Big Boy Diving for me: water 49 - 56 degrees; strong currents and surges off and on; kelp beds, pinnacles, walls, and caverns. Correspondingly, the divers on the trip were experienced and looked out for each others. It was JUST AWESOME DIVING.
Been on two dive trips with this outfit on two different boats. Great experiences both times. Helpful courteous staff. Great food and clean facilities. Would recommend to anyone.

After thirty years of diving the world. I can say without any stretch of the imagination that Truth Aquatics are the best diving outfit on the planet. There are no lacking crew members on the Vision and some stand out exceptionally. The deck crew and the galley crew manage my safety and comfort to the highest level. Captain Ian is a superb mariner. Brittney on the deck crew never fails to achieve everything a professional can aspire to. She, by far is the hardest worker who is genuinely desirous of providing friendly empathic effort to make the offshore diving experience complete.

Nice dive shop open until late, does not offer rental to divers not diving with their boat, also not capable of refueling bottles. This is a bit disappointing. Items however are available for purchase.

I went on the two day discovery diving trip. We were on The Truth with Captain Dave! The trip was wonderful, the diving was amazing and Captain Dave and his crew were very knowledgeable about diving and were able to take us to amazing diving locations. The bunks were comfortable and the food was amazing!

Please ignore this review;
It's too good to be true. 
Going diving with Truth Aquatics
Puts a smile on YOU!

To be clear, this is not a five star dive boat if your idea of five stars is private cabins and more crew than guests.  But if your idea is doing LOTS of diving in a diversity of habitats where the crew makes sure everything is taken care of and the food is delicious and plentiful, this is the place.

Three boats: Truth, Conception, and Vision.  That's in order of size.  Sleep in a rack below decks.  Bring your own bedding.  Get up, eat breakfast, dive, eat a snack, dive, eat some lunch, dive, eat a snack, dive, eat some supper, and if conditions are right, do a night dive.

Want a little more space?  Do a limited load trip.  More expensive, but you get more room.  The food is probably better, too.  

There's never a shortage of food.  Always a bowl of fruit and snacks out if it isn't meal time.  Breakfast cooked to order.  Lunch and dinner menu will cater to your dietary restrictions if you have any.  Nitrox now available from a forward osmosis machine.  NICE.

Ok, so it's really a terrible outfit and you'll hate it.  So maybe go on one of their kayak or hiking expeditions.  Or not.

I did my eighth trip with Truth Aquatics two weeks before Conception burned to the waterline.  What happened was a freak occurrence.  I would have been back on Vision the next August if I could have.

My heart still aches for the suffering of those left behind.

The company has new owners.  One of these days I hope to hear they are running as good of an operation as Truth did.  Then I will go join another five-day trip and visit the Giant Black Sea Bass, the Tope sharks and the bat rays.

Lots of diving.  Diverse habitats.  Attentive, hard-working professional if scruffy deck hands.  Friendly and competent captain.  Lots of diving.  Friendly galley crew serves up plenty of delicious food.  Lots of diving.  Kayking if you like.  Kelp.  Walls.  Lots of diving.  Great sun deck.  Affordable.  Common bunk area, not staterooms.  Lots of good diving.  I keep swearing I'm going to take a year off, but I seem to keep showing up for lots of good diving and all that other stuff.

And if you want, you can actually do a decent number of dives.  Be sure to change the batteries in your computer before you leave, because you might be spending lots of time underwater.

Took the Vision Dive Boat for a group dive for two days to Santa Cruz Island and Anacapa. The crew and accomodations were excellent. Appreciate the crew on deck that introduced themselves as we started the first dive and were very helpful throughout the seven dives made over the two days. The food was outstanding with extras included. The bunks were larger than other boats in the area as well as showers and three heads. Thank you to the Captain and crew for a great weekend.

I was aboard the Truth as part of a partially chartered trip to Santa Cruz Island and was so impressed I needed to write a review. The crew was incredibly friendly and attentive. They came around and introduced themselves to the guests while we were underway. The captain was also really nice and gave us a good safety briefing. I was not diving but I could see the crew was very helpful to the divers, at least one of whom was having issues with equipment. The boat is fairly spacious, has a fresh and saltwater shower, nice bunks with pillows and blankets, and good food. I was especially impressed that the captain came and talked to us about his plan for crossing back because it was so rough.
We've been out on the Truth for 3 trips. Captain Davey and the crew are the best.. Going out on any other boat would pale In comparison now..I only wish there was a sixth star for capt. Davey....

I just got back from 3 Days-3 Nights aboard Truth Aquatic's Vision. We did a dive trip to the Northern Channel Islands. This was an amazing experience. I have to start with the crew. These are the most amazing men and women and they make your stay aboard comfortable, fun, and so easy. All you do is eat, dive, eat, dive, eat, dive, eat, dive, sleep. They take care of everything for you. They know all the best dive spots and find the best ones geared for the trip. The food was amazing and abundant. The smells coming from the galley that you would wake up to or return to the ship to were amazing! They served daily specials, but would also make what you wanted. The Vision is very comfortable. I just threw my sleeping bag on my rack and slept like a baby. The showers are good sized. The Salon is large and a great place to hang out with your friends, download your videos, log your dives, etc. The diving was so easy. The crew was there to take off your fins and quickly refill your tank for the next dive. If you're a new diver and got lost and ended up surfacing a long distance away they had a boat ready to go pick you up. Above the main deck, behind the wheelhouse, was another great place to hang out and get some sun. I can go on and on but you get my point. The Vision is awesome and the crew rocks!!! I can't wait for my next trip with Truth Aquatics.

Went on a 3-day lobster trip on the Conception.

Despite the small craft advisory and the weather, turned out to be a good trip! We were supposed to head to Cortez Banks but ended up in the Northern Channel Islands due to conditions. Sparse lobster were found, but we did retrieve lots of fish and scallops. 

Completed nine dives over three days (total of 11 possible). Captain Jerry did a great job trying to get in as many dives as we could and moving spots so we could get ample lobster grabbing opportunities. Bunks were spacious, staff were extremely helpful and food was plentiful. Would definitely consider going again.
If you don't mind walking up a lot of steps, this is such a great beach! Very quaint. Not crowded. Friendly people and dogs! Loved it.
Wonderful few and those steps will kick your butt on the way back up! Nice little work out, thank heavens for those benches ;)

Beautiful view of the ocean! It's a short walk down the steps. We were visiting Santa Barbara and wanted a view of the ocean and this place is great! The tide was high when we went in the morning so don't always expect to walk on the sand.

This is a legally off-leash beach. However, a warning to those of you with small dogs: whomever it was who designed the lower, metal grid part of the steps deserves a slap upside the head. 

It's great that the lower steps were built to resist storms and battering by high tide waves. What's not "grate" is that the design of the metal grids on the lower steps is not the least bit accommodating for tiny dogs. 

Whenever I take my small dog, I must scoop him up and carry him up/down those metal grid steps, otherwise he might break a leg or two on those damnable gaping squares of Swiss cheese holes. 

No big deal, I suppose, but what a lameass 
goof.

I was visiting Santa Barbara looking for a non conventional beach that wouldn't be crowded. This was it!!! It feels exclusive and hidden. Parking was easy, (not sure because it was Tuesday) parking is in residential area. You walk to the end of the street where you will find the most beautiful staircase down to the beach. There was surfers and a few people lounging, and a few dogs too. The tide seems to come out as it gets closer to sunset. I was there until 8pm and there was still space to be on the sand. This beach is ideal to relax lay out and enjoy one of the most beautiful sunsets you will see.  On the way back I ran up and down the staircase a few times for a quick workout! I absolutely love this beach.
best view in town in our opinion.  always love my time at the steps

Great spot, it's easier going down than up stairs but stairs do have sitting benches if needed.  Definitely fun watching the off leash dogs, waves and sunset. Water is cold but doable.  Definitely lots of flies, seeweed, & tar. P.s. tar hard to wash off!

A site for beach access that required parking in a neighborhood with limited spaces and a long staircase dug into a beach cliff. The steps were fun, but for some reason I incorrectly thought they were decorated. The steps made it inconvenient if carrying lots of beach gear but good because this inconvenience meant less people using this section of the beach.
Really neat place to visit.  Definitely hidden but worth finding.  Plan on 1-2 hours, no restrooms nearby so plan accordingly.  Bring a sweater or light jacket.

One of the beaches in SB that isn't crowded on a weekend. Probably cause it's tucked in the neighborhoods. Entering the beach is through some stairs. "1000 steps" so I read. You'll come across some people heading up, out of breath wishing good luck. Lol. The beach it's self appears inviting than most cause there's no one around. You'll get a good walk depending on the tide without a lot of distractions. It's also dog friendly. The walk back up the steps... well, if you don't workout regularly, you'll break a sweat and call it your workout of the month. Worth the visit.

Very nice spot. Beautiful and secluded. Seems sort of out of the way because it's just hidden in the back of a neighborhood. Not many people there while we were there. The beach is nice, lots of little caves to hang out under to get some shade.
please do yourself the favor and come here. this was my favorite part about santa barbara, honestly.

Easily accessible with plenty of street parking. Pretty view. Relaxing. Was only passing through, so I had just enough time to rest on the steps and read a few pages of my book, then get a decent jog back up the steps to the street level.

My boyfriend and I really enjoyed this beach! We found this serendipitously while driving around Santa Barbara on a beautiful sunny day!

There was plenty of free parking in the residential area nearby.

There were about 242 steps down to the beach. You get an awesome view of the coastline from the top of the stairs. 

This seemed like a private beach used by locals. There were lots of couples, families and dogs! So cute! Good place to go for a stroll, play frisbee, or lay out in the sun.

FYI, there are several rocks and trees that have fallen down in the area. 

Overall, a good, quiet beach that isn't too crowded. You can also get a great workout going up and down these steps!

A hidden gem in Santa Barbara, although not too hidden since it's already on Yelp. 

Not much sandy beach, even less at times depending on the tide. The cliffs block out the wind making this beach perfect even on low 70s day (my perfect beach weather is 76-84 degrees Fahrenheit). 

Never too crowded, whether because it's unknown to some or because the steps to get down deter some, either way I always find a spot to claim.

The beach itself isn't too clean with dried up kelp and seaweed everywhere. But I've learned this is the SB way. 

My favorite beach to relax at and not deal with crowds or parking.
This beach is so quiet and always has so many friendly dogs! I love coming here just to sit and watch them play.

5 stars because the beach is absolutely beautiful and it's dog friendly. On this section of the beach dogs are also allowed off-leash. The steps do take your breath away, but with the views you get, that is easy to forget. A warning, there is a lot of tar in the sand. My husband and I had a lovely 2 mile walk, but at the end our feet were completely covered in tar. The amount of tar we had on our feet was pretty concerning considering it's been the worse we've ever experienced. Thankfully we noticed a CVS store on our way here at the corner of Meigs Rd. and Cliff Dr. and that's where we headed to purchase some baby oil, baby wipes, and a pumice stone. All of those three things helped. Unfortunately our shoes had to be thrown in the trash and we ended up purchasing some cheap sandals. This place is truly beautiful, but be cautious of the tar. Bring sandals, shoes you don't mind destroying, or be prepared to take a trip to CVS. Best of luck and enjoy. :)

One of my two favorite beach spots to hang out, especially for a picnic. Beautiful, and hardly any other people, and mostly just locals. There's always plenty of parking in the surrounding neighborhood and you get an awesome thigh, calf and tush workout when you head back up the steps:)

I love this Santa Barbara beach. You do have to go down and up the stairs(250?), so no good if any knee issues. The beach is beautiful and clean. Great for surfing ‍. Dogs seems ok. Parking (streets)is not an issue. Great for long walks. Stunning views.

A lot, a lot of seaweed, everywhere, it makes it hard to get in and enjoy the water. 
However, super neat hidden beach. The waves can hit hard, so you can't really lay because the waves will get to you. 

Friendly dogs around. 
Parking can be tough, but it's worth checking it out and going down those stairs.

One of favorite places to exercise- Perfect number of stairs for work out sets with the most beautiful views. I used to live in SF and loved the Lyon St. stairs for the same reason- And must admit that the Mesa and Hendry's beach ties if not beats the grandiose views of the Pacific Heights & Presidio for me!

Love love love Mesa lane steps and I'm not just saying that bec I live right here. Tons of fun for my fur baby. Plus my kids and I just ove the beach this beach, Beautiful views... and I love the little work put you get front he steps!!

I visited for the first time with my boyfriend on a Sunday afternoon and it was perfect! Parking was easy and each step we took closer to the ocean the more beautiful the view was. The beach was secluded only a few people with their dogs and one or two couples, very open. It's an awesome place to visit hidden in a neighborhood 10 minutes away from Stearn's Wharf.

Such a neat beach! It's an interior designers fantasy and I am not even kidding. With all the natural color combinations within the rock formations, you can discover endless color palettes here. It's natural beauty heaven. 

The rock walls and the layers are what geologists dream of (I'd think!). The strata is so neat to see along the high bluffs. The tar from the naturally occurring offshore oil is present in the rock formations- I've never seen anything like it. There's tons of colored rocks, striped and some speckled for those who like to collect. 

The250 steps down is worth it and are surprisingly well maintained.

If you are into detail photography, this is spot is ideal. Natural beauty abounds.

The Quick: There are a LOT of steps! But if you're in Santa Barbara you might as well wear comfy shoes and walk down to the beach. Great photo opp on the stairs overlooking the cliffs, oceans, and California beauty.

Hidden stairs in a residential part of town that leads to what seems more like a surfers beach. Not much sandy area to layout, tons of seaweed and kelp flies. Best part was feeling our thighs burn on the way up. Prefer cleaner sands and easier access.
A beautiful beach! We had a lovely picnic here. The beach felt very secluded & private. Not crowded at all. Lots of steps to get down, so be prepared! Absolutely beautiful sunset!

Thank you thank you thank you for existing and connecting, in an indirect way through a residential street, Douglas Family Preserve and Arroyo Burro Beach. 

It's existence makes for a nice long looping walk from beach to preserve, to residential and then back to beach instead of having to double back and see the same thing on the way back to where we came from.

And the different view... AH-MHAY-ZING!

very beautiful place, although not for swimming. there is a lot of seaweed and the water is pretty rough. there are many dogs and i didn't see any poop or anything :) there was a cave that me and my partner wanted to explore but it smelled so we weren't sure if it was from the sewer or whatever. very beautiful
Stay the F away from this beach it's not safe there are millions of tourists crowding the narrow stairs and narrow beach. No social distancing possible and the locals hate the tourists.

I grew up in Santa Barbara. The crowds that have developed here are a joke. It's honestly unbelievable. Just like everything else in the world that's become overpopulated and corny, it was once a private gem for locals and now it's  being shit on by lame rich college dorks and their garbage white claw jock pseudo hippie BS. Hey same as it ever was right? My uncle built a house on Mesa lane. If I still had l to live on this street and or it's surrounding streets I'd probably kill myself. There goes the neighborhood!!

This beach is one of my favorites when I'm looking to have a nice beach walk. The beach stretches for a while and when there is low tide there is so much more beach to explore. There is lots of beach to walk and tide pools to see when the tide is low. There is a very sturdy stair structure to help you get down to the beach which makes it easy to get down. There are alot of steps which makes you feel like your getting your workout in. and I think there is around 250 steps. Coming back up is a bit harder and will have your calves burning on the way back up. 

This beach is great for photos and always has the most amazing sunsets that always last till the very end. I love coming here in the summertime for the sunsets because they last so long. This beach has great street parking making it easy to access from the neighborhood it's near and is sort of hidden which makes it special. 

I love coming to Mesa Lane! I will continue coming here for the sunsets along with my beach walks every week! :)

I will not shut up about how I walked here from isla vista because I did do that. It took me 5 hours AND I waded through the high tide from Boathouse at Hendrys to mesa lane steps shore but it was totally worth it. I love this beach so much that I walked here guys. But when I came again with my roommate to watch the sunset, there were literally maybe 50+ other ppl here on a Saturday. And not everyone was wearing a mask. This was stressful to deal with and I do agree that this place has become a very touristy location. 
Would I walk here again? Probably not. I thought the ocean was going to engulf me in it's waves and that would be the end of me. I actually imagined that I would end up swimming in the ocean and I made peace with losing my belongings to the deep blue.  Also there is a homeless man living in a cave between the Hendrys beach and mesa lane steps beach. Stay wary of tar on the shores and unmasked santa barbarians.
The sunset here is definitely unbeatable though. I will not come back on a weekend.

These stairs are a great workout located in a residential area of Santa Barbara. Parking is always easy and the area is clean and safe. The beach at the bottom is clean and a great walk for after your  workout. Dogs are allowed off leash for most of the beach and are welcomed by all. Downtown is about 10 minutes away and it's an easy drive here. Whenever I'm in town for vacation we prefer to come here rather than the hotel gym. Enjoy!

If I lived nearby, I'd be here every day!  
Mesa Lane Steps provides you with a BEAUUUTIFUL view of the ocean & also a GREAT WORKOUT! There's tons of steps to take before you reach the beach, so be prepared. 

Parking here is easy to find (its in a residential area) and the beach isn't crowded at all.  Besides getting a good workout, it also makes a wonderful place to have a picnic, read a book, or watch the sunset. Absolutely beautiful here!

Why I've never reviewed this I can't tell you.. It has been my go to spot for running, exercising, romantic long walks on the beach, and bringing visitors/tourists to since I moved to SB years ago!

Why Mesa Lane? While it's sturdy and aesthetically beautiful stair structure will have your calves and glutes screaming on the way up, it's a wonderful workout and with a view unlike any other - also makes for great pictures! The street parking is always substantial. It's right next to a dog beach (Hendry's) so you get to see adorable puppies running around. The sunsets are visible until the end and make again for great photos. And when tide is low the tide pools are fun for the whole family. 

I would recommend this spot to any and all fitness junkies up who love running and climbing stairs. Would also recommend to tourists looking for a nice stroll along a beautiful beach with nice sunsets and adorable pups.

I walk from Henry's beach to this one. And I finally decided to climb up the stairs to see if they had a trash for me to toss my dog poop, since he likes to poop in the middle of our beach walk.  

The steps down is not bad at all but the steps going up is a cardiac arrest hill to hell.     They have benches for you to rest on but be warned! Nice beach but leaving it is hell.

This beach is great for walking but I wouldn't consider it a very pleasant beach for spending the day at. Especially when there are far more better beaches like Santa Claus Beach and Carpinteria State Beach. 

First of all, the stairs are a great workout. It feels like it's about 5-6 floors worth of stairs. 

But the main issue is there is A LOT of dogs and a lot of dog poop on this beach. The beach itself is very rocky with lots of washed up seaweed.  There are some cool tree trunks and other rock formations that look cool. 

Then there is the wind factor. Since the beach is surrounded by cliff sides, it gets pretty windy here. We arrived at 4 pm which seemed to be a pretty popular time to arrive as parking in the residential neighborhood was still difficult. 

It's a short walk to arroyo burro beach where you will find more dogs.

Such a beautiful place and well worth the steps afterwards.

Not too crowded and parking was pretty easy to find on a holiday weekend.

Very pretty.  Steps are hidden by homes in a residential part of town.  More for surfers than beach loungers.

Perfect photo opp and great exercise.  

You'll probably spend 15-20 minutes at most here.

Parking is easy

Love this beach as it feels private and not crowded. You see locals surfing there but for the most part it's secluded and the steps are a fantastic workout. You can also walk to Hendry's beach and the Boathouse restaurant during low tide. Beautiful place and the Best Sunsets ever!

Steps upon steps, upon steps. 

The namesake does not lie. In order to access the shore, one must walk down from street level a good 300 or so steps. These steps are old but fairly sturdy and do offer hand rails which is quite helpful. There are also a number of landings if you need to take a little breather. There is the singular stairwell for those going and down - and as the beach is popular, you will likely encounter people headed the opposite direction so prepare to squeeze a bit. 

The bottom of the steps opens up to a true oasis. The tide is usually on the higher side so there isn't always a ton of sand to stretch out on, but the area is ample enough to congregate without feeling too close to those around you. The ocean is calm and it's a great, clean section to wade along the water. 

This little beach doesn't have private parking and is in a residential neighborhood, so you must take what you can get in regards to street spaces. While the trek may be strenuous, the end result is quite worth it.

I always like to check out a new trail while on vacation. With the rainy winter we've had in California, a falls hike seemed like the perfect option during my long weekend in Santa Barbara. Now (or soon) is the time to go, since the falls are flowing and there are a lot of small pools and a smaller waterfall to enjoy.
First off, logistics! The trail head is not as hard to find as I thought it would be. You will see cars parked alongside of the road (at least on a weekend morning) and a sign simply marked "Trail" on the right hand side. There is a narrow path you follow that runs along side private property but there are several markers telling you where to stick to the trail. After going around a gate and walking up a wider fire road, you will have the creek to your left and start heading up hill. 
There are a few junctions but for the most part follow the the trail to the right and up. At one point it seemed wrong to head to the right because it briefly led away from the creek, but that's short lived and you will connect back with it soon enough. 

The trail itself is short, but scenic and well shaded. Don't let the tree canopy fool you though-it gets warm out here and I went through about 40 ounces of water in a 2.5 hour hike. Once you arrive at a smaller waterfall, you can rest and enjoy the small pool and for families with children that might just be enough. However, for the 80 foot fall at the back of the canyon, you will see a narrow trail that curves up to the right-follow that and in less than half a mile you will be at the larger falls. 

This is a well traveled trail and later in the morning it seemed to get more crowded. I was glad we started our hike around 10 a.m. because we had the falls nearly to ourselves. By the time we were headed down, we passed several groups heading up. I had worn regular running sneakers but actually could have used something with a good grip-there are a lot of loose rocks on the trail so be careful of your footing. The trail was moderate for experienced hikers but the elevation gain sneaks up on you-you are basically going up hill the first half of the hike. 

I believe the entire out and back hike is 4 miles or less and the 2.5 hour journey included some time relaxing at the falls and plenty of photos stops. So glad I made it over here and perhaps on another trip I would do some more research and try out some of the longer trails. There were definitely connections to other hiking options and one that I believe even leads to Los Padres National Forest. What a nice little free and wonderful find.

This hike is so intense !!! It is about 9 miles round trip and 4 of those miles are uphill. Seriously such a good effin work out !!! So good that I really teared at the very top because I was dying the whole time lol. The only reason I don't give it 5 stars is the scenery on the way to the top of the hike was okay. There were a few trees and plants, but nothing worth taking pictures of lols.

It is not a good idea to do a 5 hour hike over Thanksgiving, then wait a month and then take on another 5 hour hike over Christmas, and yet that's exactly what I did. 

There are 17 miles of hiking trails located next to San Ysidro Ranch. They are beautifully maintained, but not all that well marked.  So my desire to see the waterfall were proceeded by an accidental right turn onto Old Pueblo Trail, with the Buena Vista Loop thrown on top for good measure.

TIP:  when other hikers try and tell you how to get to the trail you want,  LISTEN.  CAREFULLY.  FOLLOW DIRECTIONS.

Thank goodness there was a hot tub waiting for me back at the Ranch.  My legs are still boycotting me.

We had a nice hike up the main San Ysidro Falls Trail - it can get hot here, so this is a good choice to maximize shade.

We only went about 1.5 miles up. Unfortunately, we didn't get sweeping views of Santa Barbera and the ocean below that I was hoping for - the trail winds behind some of the mountains. However, you get a variety of vegetation (cacti, forests, grassy areas) and the path follows running water so you get some little falls. There are some nice views of the other side of the mountain, some exposed rock that look like fun for scrambling/climbing.

FYI, the first half quarter mile or so of the trail goes through some residential areas before it gets to the more remote areas!

First of all June 2017 no water at the tall fall at the end of the hike. Still a nice hike 

2 things I feel other reviews didn't accurately portray:
Difficulty - I'm in pretty good shape and was expecting an easy 1-2 mile hike. Definitely more moderate and four miles to the last big waterfall (or waterless fall in June) and much of it uphill. 
Scenery- much prettier than I expected. Lots of greenery and wild flowers.  Tons of small little water holes to play in. 
Watch out for bugs, almost all shaded.
Overall I recommend checking it out. Gorgeous houses at the beginning of the trail.

The 4.5 mile climb is not easy but the view at the top makes up for it. The second half of the climb is mostly in the sun but the shaded spots are nice places to take short breaks. There are some loose spots on the ground which made me and my friends slip a little bit on the way down. It's very dry up there so be sure to hydrate and wear shoes that can get dirty! 
7/10 (The 5 star system on Yelp sucks)

The trailhead was a bit hard to find at first so look up a map of the area to make sure you know exactly where you need to be. It's located on E Mountain drive to the left of Park Lane. We got lost driving around Montecito looking for it for about 20 minutes and there's poor/no cell reception to get a gps reading up there. Getting lost wasn't so bad considering the estates in the area were breath taking. The locals on the streets were very friendly and gave us directions. Listen to the directions carefully because there are many trails in the area and you might end up following the wrong trail! Do your research before you get there and make sure to print out a map. I found this website useful: http://www.santabarbarahikes.com/hikes/frontcountry/directions/index.shtml?san_ysidro

The San Ysidro Falls trail follows paved road for a slight part of the trail's beginning and becomes quite steep at many points in the trail. The path is well carved by all of the previous visitors so it's easy to follow without losing the tracks. There is a lot of tree cover and shade which is a blessing because you will definitely break a sweat going up. At later points in the trail closer to the waterfall you'll get to extremely large high steps so if you're not into making a climb then this isn't the trail for you. It isn't that difficult though unless you're just plain lazy. The trail does go up pretty high in the mountains so it's slightly tiring but is definitely doable with a few rest stops. Most people of average physical fitness should be able to do it with no problems. The trail is approximately 2 hours upwards and 1 hour to go back down if your ending destination is the waterfall. Make sure to bring a lot of water and a face towel to wipe sweat with. I hiked with one other person and we each carried a backpack of 4 water bottles which we ended up finishing 6 of the 8 bottles. There are some flying insects like ladybugs and butterflys but all of the insects are pretty much harmless. If you don't like them flying near you then make sure to put on some insect repellent.

The waterfall is beautiful and definitely worth the climb. The coloring of the waterfall is much more spectacular in person than it is in the pictures. I walked into the pool of the waterfall and it was amazing. The water was pretty cold but you get used to it. There's plenty of areas and large rocks to sit on and enjoy it.

A moderate level hike that takes about an hour round trip to the falls. After a rainy season, the falls are actually there and parts of the hike are blanketed in a lush green blanket. Wear long pants since there is poison oak everywhere. 

There is also great rock climbing about midway up the hike. 

You need to park in a residential area and walk in. Just looking at the houses in that area is fun in itself. 

There aren't any bathrooms or potable water at the trailhead. 

A good hike to do with kids or if you only have an hour or two.
This definitely a nice hike! My six year old was able to do it with us... a lot of challenges like getting into rocky steep narrow paths, but it was all part of the fun! Will do it again.

Hiked this trail last week. Still one of my favorite hikes but quite muddy right now in the beginning from all the rain. The last bit of the trail before you reach the waterfall is washed out so if you want to get up close to the waterfall you have to hike in the creek bed and climb/jump some rocks.
