I just completed this hike today. The trail up the waterfall was lovely because of the rocks and the little creek running down below; I like climbing over rocks. Unfortunately, the waterfall was dried up, so there was no water running down. Past the waterfall, it is quite a climb up to El Camino Cielo. I think the elevation is 3200 ft? A lot of never-ending zig zag hills. You would walk 100 feet under the sun and then you hit another 100 feet with shade and a cool breeze. Once you get to the top, the view is gorgeous! Coming down was the most difficult part for me, because it was slippery due to loose gravel; I slipped a few times. The entire hike was about 4.5 miles one-way or 9.0 miles roundtrip. It took us almost 3.5 hours to get to the top and then 2 hours to come down, but we stopped several times going up. Overall, I enjoyed the hike. It's my first hike with elevation! Yay!

The trail starts at the end of Mountain Drive with street parking. Signs mark the trailhead that immediately launches into an easement trail - wandering between homes and along paved roads like Park Lane before tracing the western side of San Ysidro creek.

The beginning of the trail was popular with dog owners with too many irresponsible losers that didn't pick up their dog's turds. Dog waste is not good fertilizer but toxic to the environment. Locked into a canyon, hike lacks vistas except for occasionally peeks out to the ocean with the oil platforms and Channel Islands.

The path is easy to follow with only one possible impediment - a single river crossing just before reaching the falls. The established trail goes near San Ysidro Falls but not to the base of the falls. Walking up the river bed is the only way to reach the falls' base. Despite coming after a snow storm, the water flow over the fall was pathetic where the water is only seen when looking closely. With two rivulets meandering over the side of a smooth rock face, the falls trickled like it had a blockage similar to an elderly man's prostate.

Although the trail continues by wandering deeper into the Los Padres National Forest, going to the base of the falls is 3.7 miles roundtrip with a 1100' elevation change. Please note that maps and older Yelp reviews might conflict with my statements but were likely made prior to the Thomas Fire mudslides in January 2018 which altered the landscape.

This trail is a good one. We got cellphone service on some parts of the trail, which is nice in case of an emergency. The main trail leads to multiple hikes. Just make sure you have a or have seen a map of the trail you are looking for. Google maps also has great images of the trails if you get lost. Otherwise it is kind of confusing.

Amazing hike! The water is flowing abundantly! Many waterfalls! Because there is so much water you do have to cross a small river to see the large waterfall at the end. The trail is very muddy right now so be prepared to get a little dirty. We tried going up the mountain a little further after the large waterfall but the trail was washed out by a mudslide about .25 miles into it. Right now is the time to go while there is so much water flowing!

Ahhh!! Perfect time to do this hike and see the waterfall is RIGHT NOW! San Ysidro Falls is a beautiful hike with a gorgeous and totally-worth-it waterfall view at the end. Apparently there are two locations to start the hike since I did read reviews about the 9 mile roundtrip one but our hike was only 3.3 miles roundtrip. It definitely felt like longer since it was mostly uphill the entire time. Took us about 2 hours total. 

We went around 10 am on a Sunday morning. Parking wasn't hard to find at all and the trail leading up to the waterfall was relatively easy to find as well. Stick to the trails that keep going up and there were plenty of friendly people we encountered on the way who were able to point us in the right direction when we were uncertain. 

Keep in mind that if you plan on walking around in the water or taking pictures underneath the waterfall it might be a good idea to bring sandals or another change of shoes since we decided to walk around in the water and ended up getting our shoes wet.

Kind of confusing/ hard to reach the trailhead. I typed in 'San Ysidro trail' on my iPhone, and it took me to the San Ysidro ranch hotel-- which is insanely beautiful.  Once there, the front desk gave me a super helpful map of how to access the trailhead/ the other trails nearby beside San Ysidro waterfall.
You have to drive through a residential neighborhood which dead ends at the trailhead. 
It is 1.9 miles each way to the waterfall, about 4 miles round trip.

Half the walk is shaded through large tree groves, half the walk is open and sunny. 
The entire trail is next to a creek/river, which was affected by the mudslide, so there is some construction at the bottom, but is so beautiful to have the river the whole time next to you. 

The waterfall itself is hidden, so keep your eyes peeled, as you have to make your own trail to the falls the last 1,000 feet - either through  the river itself (a few inches deep) , or up and down large rocks. I had three small dogs, and they were all able to complete this last portion of the hike.  It's not super difficult, just hidden and kind of hard to create your own path to the waterfall when you have no clue if you're heading the right way to it--- but again, this is only for about 1,000 feet, then you end up dead ended at the waterfall.

It about 50 feet fall, and totally going, even in off season. Very peaceful and a perfect spot to spend the entire day- as there is shade, water, great views. 

The trail to/from has awesome views of the ocean/ Channel Islands, along with great lush trees/plants/flowers throughout the whole walk. It is awesome to be the in mountains, and have ocean views!!! 

There are a Lot of ticks, snakes and poison oak. 

I would recommend spending 3-4 hours for a super leisurely time! 

There are other trails off this main San Ysidro trailhead, but I would recommend staying with the main 1.9 mike San Ysidro trailhead.

All Trails rates this hike as moderate by I say it's more towards hard. It is a steady steep incline for 3+ miles. All trails also says it's only 3.5 miles, that is one way! So your looking at 7 miles round trip. 

It's rocky but with shade the first 2 miles. After that bye bye shade and hello dry and dusty trail. You will want to wear hiking boots has it's very easy to loose gripping on the rocks and roll your ankle. 

I called it quits at 3 miles and went back down the trail. I was exhausted from the steady climb. I am not the most in shape person right now but I am fairly active. I just hiked 5 miles last week too. So unless you are in decent shape, be prepared to not make it all the way to the top. 

Bring plenty of water. 

As someone else said, walking through the private property portion until hitting the split off of the McMenemy trail is filled with dog poop and abandoned dog poop bags. It seems people don't take their dogs on the San Ysidro as I no longer says dog poop or bags unless they just kick it off the cliff side. 

On my way back down I saw a Gardner snake just off of the trail path. 

We started at 10:45 am and arrived back at the car at 1:42 pm.
Cute hike!! Hard to find parking, but the drive there and views from the hike are gorgeous. Montecito is a beautiful area!

I am extremely hesitant to say the San Ysidro trail is "open", however, I was able to make it all the way to the dried up falls. It is one of the few hikes besides Rattlesnake Canyon that is shaded. However, I highly recommend only skilled or experienced hikers go. I was able to witness the damage caused by the Montecito mudslides and then Thomas Fire first hand from this trail. The damage was not only devastating to the families and homes but also to the surrounding trails and nature. What use to be a creek is now a widened, carved out river. Many trees are toppled and burnt while the trail is unsteady and has given way to the cliffs. There are multiple sections of the trail that are best traveled along the riverbed versus using the trail along the riverside. Multiple hikers, including myself, have laid stone markers to distinguish the path, however, many confusing and unsafe areas remain. A hiking post from Santabarbara.com has a detailed description of the trail, yet towards the end it does not help much due to the changes.
San Ysidro Falls trail is spectacular yet it is sad to see so much damage. Hopefully volunteers will fix it up with time since it is a diamond in the rough!

name: Sky Ranch Fitness
categories: Trainers, Fitness & Instruction, Hiking, Beauty & Spas, Active Life

yelp_reviews.text: I wasn't sure what to expect from my first experience at a fitness camp. I knew the accommodations would be lovely at the Bacara Resort in Goleta, which they were. I assumed the hiking would be scenic and memorable, which it was. I hoped the food would be delicious, nutritious and adequate enough to sustain the heavy calorie burning and I was satisfied. But what surprised me most was the level of education I received, not only with regards to nutrition but primarily with my biomechanics. Dr. Michael Luan, like a benevolent pest, constantly adjusted our movements and reminded us HOW to stand, walk and move efficiently. By the end of the trip, my body no longer required the verbal suggestions and had adopted a new, more powerful posture. The hikes were long but not overly arduous (I might bring some extra healthy snacks if I do it again). And they were full of fun, lighthearted interesting conversation. The entire group bonded naturally and easily. I credit Stuart, who runs the camp for fostering an upbeat, playful energy. Who needs to combine seriousness with fitness? Not me. The entire staff seemed highly educated and many of them trained by Dr. Luan. Their knowledge was remarkably helpful but their caring and thoughtful dispositions blew me away. I quickly learned to trust and accept their input. I pushed myself beyond old, self-imposed physical limitations without issue. The staff is a special group that clearly enjoys encouraging and inspiring others to move into higher levels of fitness. To all of them, I am grateful. The highlight of the trip may have been a night out to a winery where wine flowed and a scrumptious meal was graciously served by an incredible chef (whose name escapes me). I did sneak out to Chipotle late one night and I only managed to loose a few pounds. But weight loss wasn't exactly my intention. The insights I left with still have me standing tall and smiling up.

i cant say anythoing good about thi camp. I have been to many hiking camps, weight loss camps.  Very disorganized ! The staff is all partime, and has no idea of what they are doing. THere is tremendous lack of organization and none of it makes sense.
I came here because I could not get inot the other programs and I really needed to get a kick start. Thie hiking is good and that is where it ends. No fulltime help no 
cohesiveness. The owner is not organized and does not know what he is coing.
He gives one person one price and another another price. 
This camp is deligated to college part time students. Dinners are sometimes in 
the hotel sometimes out of the hotel with no restriction on eating which is not for me.
I would not come back !

I can't say enough good things about this program. I had such a blast the week I spent here. From the luxurious accommodations at Bacara to the amazing trainers of Sky Ranch, this experience was life changing. 

First, I have never felt so good both physically and mentally. Although the workouts were tough and hikes were extensive, I had a natural energy and high throughout this week that I am not used to (sadly). My body felt strong and mobile as my mind became clear and alert. Overall, I just felt really good eating well, being active, and getting fresh air on the beautiful hikes. What an amazing change this was from my desk job! 

As the week itself was incredible, I also learned a lot about myself and health in general. I feel like we were all given great tools to take home with us on how to make positive changes in our lives and evaluate our bodies effectively. What I got out of this program went beyond the incredible week stay as the knowledge I gained translated into my every day life. 

Now, back the trainers! They were really great about pushing me to work hard safely in a way that made me fee encouraged and motivated as opposed to pressured and obligated. Everyone was so helpful and friendly - I always felt in good hands.

I have the best memories from this experience and cannot WAIT to return soon. It is a must trip I plan on taking a few times a year. 

Thank you Sky Ranch! xx

I have been participating in this "camp" for almost a week (2 more days to go), and already I've achieved my weight loss goal of 5 pounds. But that is a side issue. Every morning there are hikes that will push you to work, and have views that are amazing. In the afternoons, you'll have workouts (yoga, dance, TRX, interval training) and massages. The combination of the Bacara resort luxury, the views, the friendly people, and the qualified, trained staff make this a reputable, trustworthy fitness retreat. As soon as they get some of their details straightened out (shifting schedule, order of classes) I will switch to a 5 star rating. As a new company, I'd say they are going to do really well, as all the components are in place for a well-rounded experience. Oh, did I mention the wine tasting, lectures, personalized gait/ posture assessments, harbor cruise, and more more more??!!!

Stu did a fantastic job customizing a health retreat for a group of 9 overworked, out of shape and stressed out male executives. We needed a reboot and that is what we got. Although our group wasn't typical SRF clientele, Stu perfectly designed the activities, meals, and trainers to fit our groups needs and wants. Everyone walked away sore, stress free and fired up to jump start their own fitness program and place a higher priority on health. The entire SRF team was extremely professional, knowledgeable and accommodating to our group and they all had the personalities to deal with a rowdy group of old guys. Would recommend this for a corporate or family event. The Bacara hotel is a beautiful property and setting for this program. Unfortunately the hotel needs to beef up its service a little more but don't let that deter you form SRF.
in my opinion sky ranch fitness is disorganized, expensive.  my advise is to stay at Terranea (or any hotel) and hire someone to take you on hikes.  you will save a lot of money

Sky Ranch Fitness is great for anyone who wants to take a retreat from "real life" to focus on health and wellness. I am happy to report this is NOT a boot camp that tortures and starves you for a week. Instead, it's a holistic all-inclusive experience that balances fitness, nutrition, and relaxation at a luxury resort in Santa Barbara.

The setting is the Bacara Resort and Spa, one of the nicest resorts in the Santa Barbara area. As a guest here, we had full access to the pool, spa, and other facilities. We enjoyed our meals at the resort's restaurants.

The typical day began with morning stretches or yoga, followed by a smoothie or breakfast at the restaurant. We would then go for a scenic hike at various locations around Santa Barbara, all of which combined a good workout with beautiful views. After eating snacks and lunch during our hikes, we would return to the hotel for the afternoon. Depending on the day, we would spend the afternoon relaxing, getting a massage, or taking a variety of fitness/personal training classes. Finally, for dinner, we would go out to nice restaurants and/or wineries in the area.

All the meals and snacks were wholesome and healthy. The dishes were generally chosen for us, but we had opportunities to choose foods that we enjoy. I never felt hungry after a meal, but I never felt "stuffed" either.

I definitely recommend Sky Ranch Fitness and the Bacara Resort. The program is individually tailored to your personal needs and goals, so you won't feel like this is too intense (or not intense enough). I was definitely tired after the workouts each day, but was a healthy kind of tired that made it easy to rest each night and wake up a feeling refreshed and ready to go again the next day.

I spent a week in August at Sky Ranch Fitness.  I loved it!  We had a wonderful trainer (Selene) who led us in morning stretches, and then we hiked (at our own pace) in several beautiful spots around Montecito, Santa Barbara and Solvang.  We had a friendly, fun group of guests, all of whom bonded really well.  Stuart (who runs the program) has put together a great team of trainers, all of whom really cared about helping the guests achieve their goals.  Our dinners out (at a winery and a trendy SB restaurant in the "Funk Zone") were also highlights.  I hope to return!

From the gorgeous setting of the Bacara Resort & Spa, the food that is delicious, nutritious and surprisingly filling, workouts and hikes that challenge and really burn in the best possible way, and trainers that never cease inspire - not a stone was left unturned during my time at Sky Ranch Fitness. My husband and I left feeling a bit lethargic and cheat meals were becoming the usual but after a week at Sky Ranch Fitness we lost pounds and inches, and gained the knowledge and the confidence to continue the journey we started in Santa Barbara long after we've returned home. Both of us were a bit apprehensive before heading out because I was never really the high endurance fitness kind of girl, but the hikes, while challenging were so beautiful that I hardly (well, that is unless it was a steep hill) realized how far we'd been going. I'd recommend going to anyone and we both hope to go again soon!!

Okay lets combine the best fitness trainers, the best views, the highest amount of luxury ALL IN SANTA BARBARA! I could not have asked for a better fitness retreat to get me back in the shape I want to be in! You work out most of your day with amazing hikes and great personal trainers that you will  become friends with after the retreat is over. You get to hike at different places each day and each one gets better and more challenging with the greatest views! Let's not forget, you get pampered at the Bacara Spa with massages!  They make it easy at Sky Ranch- they prepare the food for you, you don't have to stress about if you're eating "right." I am already planning my next retreat. The weight loss from eating healthy and exercising all week was only one of the many rewards from my time at Sky Ranch.
